text: Ti Yezu ŋmɛregɛ tɔ a zahereba la, gee yele ba bããlam yeti, “Sum boi mɛ la nini se'ehi en nyɛ sɛla ti yamam nyɛ wa.

ref: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Beni en gulehɛ bɔna ze'elego la puan? Fum kãalɛ la beni bini?”

ref: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Fum kɔ'ɔm lebehe la ma'a. Kɔ'ɔm ita bala gee fum wan vɔna.”

ref: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”

ref: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “Buraa kayima en ze'ele Yerusalɛm sigera Yeriko, ti fãareba ta gu' ka sore, sɛgehɛ ka gee ŋmɛ' ka, gee kiŋɛ gee bahɛ ka ti ka gee kum.

ref: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.

ref: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
text: Bala mɛa ti Levi wa'am na paaɛ zi'an la, nyɛ buraa la, gee me lɔkɛ doohe zi'an kayima ha tole.

ref: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
text: Gee Samariya n ta yɛ'a tɔla, wa'am na pɔra buraa la, nyɛ buraa la, ti nimbu'ɔ nyɔkɛ en.

ref: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
text: Ti ka sige paaɛ en, ka'ɛ kaam la daam duuhe en, gee pɔgeleŋɔ la lu', gee ɔ̃begɛ en pagelɛ a mɛŋa buŋa zuo, tari en paaɛ saama ga'aregɔ yire, bihɛ en.

ref: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
text: Ti yiimbeere ti a dikɛ ligeri wuu dabeha ayi yɔɔrɛ bo' se'em en biheri saama ga'aregɔ yire la, gee yele en yeti, ‘Bihera buraa wa suŋa bɔ'ɔra mam, ti n han ta tulege na ti sanɛ se'ere woo bɔna, n wan yɔ.’

ref: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
text: Ba ani batã wa puan, ti fum ti'ihɛ ti a dela buraa wa n lu fãareba nu'uhen wa mabia?”

ref: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
text: Ti yetɔge tɔgera la lerege yeti, “Se'em n zɔ a nimbu'ɔ la.” Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Me kiŋɛ ta iŋɛ bala mɛa.” Yezu kiŋɛ ta bihɛ Maata la Mɛɛri

ref: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
text: Ban boi sore zuo yɛ'a la, ba ta paaɛ la timbila kayima puan, ti pɔka bɔna bini ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Maata, yi' Yezu kule a yire.

ref: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.

ref: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
text: Gee ti Maata yee kiileŋɔ la tuuma. Bala ti a wa'am na soke Yezu yeti, “N Daana, Fum ka nyɛti n suuɔ wa bahɛ mam ti mam kɔ'ɔm kiilena la tuuma wa n ma'a? Yele en ti a ihege na suŋɛ mam.”

ref: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
text: Gee Daana la yele en yeti, “Maata, Maata, la daani fum mɛ, ti fum puti'irɛ ka gã'a tiŋa la tuuma kekeko la zuo,

ref: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
text: gee yele deyina ma'a n pakɛ. Mɛɛri looe la yele suŋa ti nɛra kan ta'am to'e ka a zi'an.”

ref: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Bala, mam yeti ya yeti, soke ya, gee ba wan bo' ya. Ɛ ya gee ya wan nyɛ. Ŋmɛ' ya gee kuleŋa la wan yu'ɛ bo' fum.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
text: Yamam en tugum dɛna be'em duma, han mina yan bo'ori ya kɔma bo'olum suma se'em, Ŋwani ti ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha kan bo' sɛba en suhɛ en Fa'a Sia la gaŋɛ bala.” Yezu dagi Beelezebule (Matu

ref: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”

ref: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
text: Mam han tara Beelezebule paŋa sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani yu'urɛ sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la? Bala zuo, bamam en wa dɛna ya mɛŋa gakereba

ref: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.

ref: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
text: Gee Yezu yele en yeti, “Sum tugum bɔna mɛ la sɛba en wum Yinɛ yelehum wa gee gurɛ bu suŋa suŋa.” Yona dela daalehum (Matu

ref: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.

ref: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
text: Ti a yele ba yeti, “Yele boi yamam yetɔge tɔgereba wa zuo! Se'ere en sɔi la, ya ni ziile la nɛreba zeero en tibegɛ gaŋɛ, gee kan dikɛ ya nu'dibila tebege zeero la suŋɛ ba.

text: Bala, yamam en de aze' nyɛ duma, la sɛba n yuum pa' ya yaabeduma poore ti ba tum ba tuuma la. Bama n ku nɔtɔgeheba la, ti yamam me mɛ ba yɔɔrɔ.

ref: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.

ref: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
text: Yele boi yamam yetɔge tɔgereba wa zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, yamam en dikɛ mi'ilum kparehega la, ya kan kɛ̃ ya mɛŋa, gee me gu'ura sɛba en boti kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la.”

ref: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
text: Yezu en tɔgɛ ba'ahɛ gee yehe na la, ti gɔnmi'ineba la, la Farasiiduma la, iŋɛ suure la en, nyaa sɔgeha en yɛla zo'e zo'e doohe en tɔgɛ se'em la puan,

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
text: Gee Yinɛ han yɛɛlɛ samana zuto mɔpuuro n boi zina ma'a ti beere ti ba dikɛ ba bahɛ bugumen, a nyaa wan yɛɛlɛ yamam gaŋɛ gaŋɛ la ŋwani? Sakerɛ pɔ'ɛ ya duma wa.

ref: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
text: Gee tugum mu'ɛ ya nini la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la, gee ba wan dikɛ yele ana za'a pa'ahɛ ya.

text: “N yeti ya mɛ wa, n zɔduma wa, da zoti ya sɛba en wan ku inganɛ wa ma'a gee kan ta'am iŋɛ se'ere pa'ahɛ la.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
text: Yinɛ mi' ya zuto zonto kãlɛ mɛ. Da tari ya dabeem, gee ya tari tuulum mɛ gana niimbibehi kekeko.

ref: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
text: “Da zoti ya dabeem n pebibehi wa, la dela Sɔ la pupeelum ti a dikɛ sɔ'ɔlum la bo' ya.

ref: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
text: Bala, koohe ya lɔgerɔ za'a dikɛ ligeri la bo' tarenduma. Maahum ya ligeri tapɔtɔ seto n ka aaheni bo' ya mɛhi, dikɛ ya ligeri biŋe saazuo ha, ze'sɛka ti nageyiga ka pɔra bini, bii mɔka wan paaɛ sagum.

ref: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Fa'a Sia la wan bo' ya sankaŋa puan la, sɛla ti ya wan yele.” Buntata makerɛ

ref: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
text: Gee Yinɛ yele en yeti, ‘Gerego wa! Yu'uŋɔ kuna wa, n wan to'e fum vom la fum zi'an, Gee ti fum lɔgerɔ la ti fum lagehɛ biŋe la, tu bihɛ tumam n wan dɛna se'em dinɛ?’

ref: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
text: “N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti se'em woo en ze'ele nɛreba nɛŋan tɔgɛ mam yele suŋa suŋa , Asaala Dayua wa me ta ze'ele Suŋereba nɛŋan me tɔgɛ a yele suŋa suŋa

ref: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.

ref: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.

ref: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
text: Ti nɛra ayima ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan yele en yeti, “Pa'ala, yele n suuɔ wa ti a tɔrɛ tu sɔ lɔgerɔ la mam la eŋa tiŋasuka.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
text: Gee Yezu yele en yeti, “N zɔ, ani n tum mam na ti n wa'am na dɛna gakera, bii yele maala bu fum?”

ref: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
text: Gee nyaa yele a zahereba la yeti, “Tara ya mɛhi suŋa. Gu' ya mɛhi suŋa ti nyika sɛka da kɛ̃ ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra vom dagi la lɔgerɔ la tarega zo'e zo'e.”

ref: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
text: Ti a nyaa ti'ihɛ bo' a mɛŋa yeti, ‘Beni ti n wan iŋɛ? Se'ere en sɔi la, n ka tari zi'an ti n wan biŋe n dia wa.’

ref: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
text: Ti a nyaa yeti, ‘Mam n wan iŋɛ se'em n ŋwana. N wan gbi' n bã wa bahɛ, gee malum mɛ bunkãra, la n biŋe n dia, la n lɔgerɔ wa bini.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
text: Ti a yele a Zahereba la yeti, “Bala, mam yeti ya yeti, da fabele ya vom wa yele, ti beni ti ya wan di, bii ya iŋa wa yele, ti beni ti tu wan yɛ.

text: Se'ere en sɔi la vom wa gani dia mɛ, ti inganɛ wa gana futo.

ref: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
text: Bihɛ ya mɔpuuro wa, bihɛ ya ban zo'ore se'em, ba ka tuni, bii ba wugera tama, gee n yele ya ti, la Naba Solomɔn la a suŋa la za'a ka paari mɔpuuro wa la kuyina me.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tiŋa wa zuo nɛreba n pɛregeri ba mɛhi ɛɛra yele ana woo, gee yamam Sɔ la mi' ti ya boti ba.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
text: Gee da'aberɛ wa han yele ka suuren yeti, ‘N kpɛ'ɛma wa yuure mɛ,’ gee nyaa pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra yamehi sise'ehi, buraahi la pɔgehi za'a, gee dita en boti se'em, nyuura, ta nyuura bugera,

ref: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
text: Ya ti'ihɛ ti mam wa'am na ti n tari la suma'ahum wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na bii? Aayi, n yeti ya mɛ wa, mam tugum tari la welehego na.

ref: Be ready and keep your lamps burning
text: “Bobe ya kankaŋi maahum tuuma. Bahɛ ti ya butolehi dita suŋa.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
text: Sum wan bɔna la da'aba se'ehi ti ba kpɛ'ɛma paaɛ na nyɛ ba ti ba kɔ'ɔm maahum mɛ zɛ'a. Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti kpɛ'ɛma la wan lu' a sɛganɛ, gee bahɛ ti a da'aba la tee zi'a, dɛla taaba ti a wa'am na tuta ba ti ba dita.

ref: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
text: A han wa'am la yu'unsunsua na, bii bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ, nyɛ ba ti ba maahum ya, sum wan bɔna la da'aba sina taaba.

ref: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
text: “Gee mini ya dina wa, ti yidaana han mina gogo kaareŋa sɛka puan ti nageyiga wan wa'am a yire na, a kan bahɛ ti a kɔ' kɛ̃ a yire la puan.

ref: So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
text: Yamam me maahum, se'ere en sɔi la, Asaala Dayua lɛbega na la ye' la gogo kaareŋa sɛka puan ti ya kan baŋɛ.” Da'aberɛ, la Yambe'o (Matu

ref: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
text: Ti Pita yeti, “Daana, fum makere dina wa bɔ'ɔra la tumam bii nɛra woo?”

ref: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: Ti Daana la yeti, “Ani n nyaa de yameŋa n tari yɛm, gee mina tuuma, ti ka kpɛ'ɛma dikɛ Da'aba la woo iŋɛ a nu'uhen, ti a bɔ'ɔra ba dia ligeri de saŋa?

text: Sum wan bɔna la Da'aberɛ sɛka ti ka kpɛ'ɛma ta lebena nyɛ ti ka tuni bala.

ref: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta wa, ti a wan dikɛ la da'aberɛ kana ti a bihera a lɔgerɔ za'a.

ref: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
text: da'aberɛ wa kpɛ'ɛma wa wan wa'am la daan se'ere ti a ka ti'iha ya, la gogo kaareŋa sɛka puan ti a ka mina. A wan ŋma en zɔra zɔra, dikɛ en ta pa'ahɛ sɛba en ka nane la puan.

ref: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
text: Da'aberɛ sɛka en mi' ka kpɛ'ɛma n boti sɛla gee ka maahum a mɛŋa bii a iŋɛ sɛla ti ba bɔta ti a iŋɛ la, wan to'e ŋmɛ'kãtɛ.

ref: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
text: Gee se'em n ka mi' gee iŋɛ sɛla n de ba ŋmɛ' en, eŋa ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ kan zo'e. Se'em woo ti ba bo' en zo'e, ba eere la zo'e zo'e a zi'an. Se'em ti ba dikɛ kekeko iŋɛ a nu'uhen, ba boti gana bala mɛ a zi'an. Yezu welegere mɛ (Matu

ref: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
text: Yamam birikɔgenɛ duma wa. Ya tã bihera tiŋa wa, la sagbanɛ wa baŋera sɛla n wan iŋɛ, gee ŋwani ti ya kan ta'am pa'alɛ sɛla en iti sankana wa puan? Ti'ele ŋmɛ nupu'ɔ (Matu

text: ti ka han wam yuunɛ se'ere en kini na la, la ani suŋa, gee ka han ka wam, fum ta'am ŋmaaɛ ka bahɛ.’ ” Yezu tibɛ pɔka en de kɔɔreŋɔ

text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛba yee la pooren, gee wan ta tole nɛŋa, ti sɛba me tole nɛŋa, gee wan ta yee pooren.” Yezu kaahɛ bahɛ Yerusalɛm zuo (Matu

text: Gee nyaa yele se'em en yi' en la me yeti, “Fum han yi'ira nɛreba ti ba wa'am fum yire na di wuntɛɛŋa, bii zaanuurɛ, da ni yi' fum zɔduma bii fum suurɔ bii fum nɛreba, bii kpɛ'ɛnduma sɛba en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri lɛm fum ma'a, ti ba me wan ta yi' fum me lerege zuo, la ba lebehe yɔ hɔ

text: Ŋwana ti fum wan nyɛ sum, se'ere en sɔi la, bamam kan ta'am lebehe yɔ hɔ. Fum wan ta to'e fum yɔɔrɛ la tuntɔɔduma vo'ore la.” De'eŋo kãtɛ makerɛ (Matu

ref: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”

ref: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Nɛra ayima en daa iŋɛ de'eŋo kãtɛ nyaa yi' nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

text: Ti dia la saŋa ta paaɛ ti a tum a da'aba ti a kiŋɛ ta yele sɛba woo ti a yi' la yeti, ‘Wa'ana gee sɛla woo maahum ba'ahɛ.’

ref: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
text: Gee ti nɛreba la za'a pɔhɛ suhera sore. Yia daana la yeti, ‘Mam nan kɔ'ɔm da' la samanɛ, ti la dɛna n kiŋɛ de puan zina wa. Bala n beleni fum mɛ ti n kan ta'am wa'am na.’

ref: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
text: Ti a yima me yeti, ‘Mam nan kɔ'ɔm da' la n nii kɔɔri i kɔɔhum bunuu, bɔta ti n dikɛ i kɔ bihɛ zina, bala n beleni fum mɛ, ti n kan ta'am wa'am na.’

text: Ti a wa'am Yezu zi'an yu'uŋɔ kuyima na, soke en yeti, “Rabi, tu mi' ti fum dela pa'ala en ze'ele Yinɛ mɛŋa zi'an na. Se'ere n sɔi la, nɛra ka boi wan ta'am tum daalehum sɛ'a ti fum tuna wa han dagena Yinɛ n boi la a daana.”

ref: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa, nɛra ka boi wan bɔna Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan han dagena ba dɔgɛ en la saazuo na.”

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
text: Ti Yezu lebehe en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta hɔ wa, ti nɛra ka boi wan kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan, han dagena ba malum dɔgɛ en mɛ doohe ko'om, la Sia la puan.

ref: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
text: Sɛla ti inganɛ dɔgɛ dela inganɛ, ti sɛla ti Sia dɔgɛ, dela Sia.

ref: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
text: Bala, da bahɛ ti la iŋɛ fum pakerɛ la mam yele fum yeti, ‘Fu dela ba malum dɔgɛ fum saazuo dɔgum la.’

ref: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
text: Sabeeo lobere yɛ'ɛha la kuŋɔ en boti zi'an. Fum yeen wuna la ku dameŋa, gee fum ka mi' kuŋɔ en ze'ele zi'an na, bii kuŋɔ n ye'ehe zi'an. La ani la bala bo' se'em woo ti Fa'a Sia dɔgɛ en.”

ref: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
text: Ti Yezu soke en yeti, “Aa, Fum dagi la Iziraleduma pa'ala, gee ka bɔkera yele ana?”

ref: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
text: “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa, tumam yeti la sɛla ti tu mina, gee tɔgera sɛla ti tu nini nyɛ, gee ya ka sakɛ bo' tɔ.

ref: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
text: Mam han yele fum timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla ti fum ka nam, fum nyaa yeti fum iŋɛ la ŋwani nam, n han yele fum saazuo yɛla?

ref: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
text: Nɛra ka tabelɛ zom saazuo wa ha, han dagena se'em daana en ze'ele de zuo sige na la, dɛna Asaala Dayua la.

ref: And the Son of Man must be lifted up, just as the metal snake was lifted up by Moses in the desert.
text: Wuu Moose n wa zɛkɛ bunsɛla la muɔ la puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti ba wan ta zɛkɛ Asaala Dayua wa saazuo,

ref: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
text: Ti se'em woo en nam en, wan tara vom kamba'ahɛ.

ref: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
text: “Yinɛ nɔŋɛ timpaberɛ wa mɛ paaɛ paaɛ zuo, ti a dikɛ a dayua kayima wa bo' na, ti se'em woo n nam en, kan bahɛ yoo, gee a wan tara vom kamba'ahɛ.

ref: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
text: “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ, Yinɛ ka tum a dayua wa ti ka wa'am na gakɛ timpaberɛ wa, gee timpaberɛ wa tugum nyɛ faarɛ doohe a dayua wa puan.

ref: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
text: Sɛba en nam en kan nyɛ sageŋɔ, gee sɛba en ka nam en la, pun diŋɛ bɔna la sageŋɔ puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ba ka nam Yinɛ dayua kayima wa yu'urɛ la.

ref: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
text: Gakerɛ en de se'em en ŋwana. Niihum wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na, gee nɛreba nɔŋɛ la lika gana niihum la, beni zuo, ba tuni la be'em.

ref: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
text: Sɛba woo n tuni be'em yaaɛ niihum mɛ, kan wa'am bu puan na, ti ba be'em la da niiɛ na.

ref: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
text: Gee sɛba n tuni yelemɛŋerɛ kini niihum la puan me na, ti ba tuuma la niiɛ na, pa'alɛ ti ba tuni ba tuuma dɔla la Yinɛ n boti se'em.” Yezu la Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
text: Dina pooren ti Yezu la a zahereba kiŋɛ Yudiya kinkɛleŋa ha, ti eŋa la bamam ta kɛ̃ bilam dabeha fii wa, bɔ'ɔra nɛreba ko'om sua.

ref: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
text: Malema peere nɔkpe'ene n daa wa lu Yɔn zahereba la, la Yuuduma nɛra tiŋasuka.

ref: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
text: Yɔn zahereba basɛba n wa wa'am Yɔn zi'an na yele en yeti, “Rabi, se'em n daa wa'am fum zi'an na Yɔɔdan yakenɛ ha la, se'em ti fum daa tɔgɛ a yele la n bala ŋmɛregɛ bɔ'ɔra nɛreba ko'om sua, ti nɛreba la za'a nyaa bibigera a zi'an la.”

ref: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
text: Ti Yɔn lerege ba yeti, “Se'em ka boi wan to'e sɛla, han dagena saazuo n dikɛ bo' a daana.

ref: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
text: Yamam mɛhi en de mam aze'nyɛduma, ti n yɛta ti ‘Dagi mam en de Kiresta la, gee Yinɛ tum mam mɛ na, ti n diŋɛ a nɛŋa na.’

ref: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
text: Pɔgedita la en sɔ'e pɔka la, gee a zɔ la n zi' la en la, puurɛ pee mɛ la en wuni pɔgedita la kua la. La ani mam la bala, ŋwana zuo ti mam puurɛ ba' pee paaɛ paaɛ la.

ref: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
text: “Eŋa wan malum pa'ahera, gee ti mam sigera.” Se'em n ze'ele saazuo na.

ref: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
text: Se'em n ze'ele saazuo na la, en gani za'a. Se'em n ze'ele timpaberɛ wa zuo na, dela timpaberɛ wa zuo bunɔ, tɔgera la timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla. Se'em n ze'ele saazuo na la n gani za'a.

ref: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
text: Eŋa tɔgeri la sɛla ti a nyɛ, gee wum, gee nɛreba kelum ka nam bo' a yelehum la.

ref: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
text: Gee se'em woo n nam bo' a yelehum la nam la yele dina wa, ti Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
text: Se'em daana ti Yinɛ tum la, tɔgeri la Yinɛ nuurɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ba' pirɛ en mɛ la Fa'a Sia la.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
text: Sɔ la nɔŋɛ dayua la mɛ, gee dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ a nu'uhen.

ref: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
text: Se'em woo n nam dayua la tari la vom kamba'ahɛ. Se'em me n zagehɛ dayua la ka tari vom, gee Yinɛ sunsunyiim n boi a daana zuo.

ref: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
text: gee la dagi Yezu a mɛŋa, gee a zahereba la en bo'ori nɛreba la ko'om sua la,

ref: Jesus left Judea and started for Galilee again.
text: ti a bahɛ Yudiya, gee yɛ'ɛha lɛbera Galilii.

ref: This time he had to go through Samaria,
text: Gee la dela a doohe Samariya tiŋa puan.

ref: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
text: Ti Samariya tiŋa pɔka wa yele en yeti, “Ŋwani ti fum Yuu nɛra zuhera ko'om mam Samariya pɔka wa zi'an?”

ref: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
text: Ti pɔka la yele Yezu yeti, “N dɛɛma wa, fum ka tari gu'a, bulega wa me kɔ' mɛ ti fum wan nyɛ vom ko'om buna wa la biha?

ref: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
text: Fum gani tumam yaaba Yakɔbe n bo' tumam bulega wa la mɛ, ti a dayɔɔhi la a duhi za'a nyuura ka puan la?”

ref: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
text: Gee sɛba woo n nyu ko'sebo ti mam wan bo' ba la, ko'nyuuro kan le malum tara bamam duma. Gee ko'sebo ti mam wan bo' ba la, wan lebege la bulega ba puan bula vom ko'om yɛ'ɛha vom en kamba'ahɛ puan.”

ref: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
text: Ti pɔka wa yele Yezu yeti, “N dɛɛma wa, yi bo' mam ko'buna wa, ti ko'nyuuro da le makɛ tara mam, bii n kina ka'lam na agera ko'om.”

ref: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “Kiŋɛ ta yi' fum sira na.”

ref: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum ele la sireba banuu, ti se'em ti fum bɔna la en nananawa, pun dagena fum sira. Sɛla ti fum yele la dela asira.”

ref: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
text: Ti pɔka wa yele en yeti, “Naa, mam bihɛ ti fum dela nɔtɔgeha.

ref: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
text: Tumam yaabeduma pu'uheri Yinɛ la zɔya wa zuto, gee yamam yeti, zi'an ti nɛreba wan pu'uha Yinɛ dela Yerusalɛm puan ma'a.”

ref: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “Pɔka wa, nam mam, ti saŋa kini na ti ya kan pu'uha Sɔ la zoore wa zuo, bii Yerusalɛm puan.

ref: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
text: Yamam pu'uheri la sɛla ti ya ka mina, gee tumam pu'uheri la sɛla ti tu mina. Se'ere n sɔi la, faarɛ ze'eti la Yuuduma zi'an na.

ref: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
text: Gee saŋa kini na, kaŋa n ŋwana, ti sɛba n de yelemɛŋerɛ pu'uhereba la, wan pu'uhera Sɔ la, Sia la yelemɛŋerɛ puan. Se'ere n sɔi la, Sɔ la eere la nɛrebana taaba ti ba pu'uha en.

ref: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
text: Yinɛ dela Sia, ti sɛba n pu'uheri en la dela ba pu'uha en Sia la yelemɛŋerɛ puan.”

ref: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
text: Ti pɔka la yele Yezu yeti, “Mam mi' ti Mɛzaaya la,” kini na. “Eŋa han wa'am na, a wan pa'alɛ tɔ sɛla woo.”

ref: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
text: Ti Yezu yele pɔka wa yeti, “Mam n bala. Mam se'em en tɔgera la fum wa.”

ref: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
text: Bilam, ti a zahereba la n bala paara na, ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ la en tɔgeri la pɔka, gee ba se'em ka lakɛ nuurɛ soke Pɔka wa nyaa “Beni ti fum bɔta?” Bii ba soke Yezu nyaa “Beni ti fum tɔgera la en?”

ref: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
text: Ti pɔka la bahɛ ka yoore la bilam, gee lebe tinkãtɛ la puan, ta muulɛ yele nɛreba yeti,

ref: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
text: “Wa'am ya na bihɛ ya, Buraa kayima n yele mam yele sɛ'a woo ti mam tum! Eii!, Ka wan dɛna Kiresta n bala, bii ka kan dɛna?”

ref: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
text: Ti nɛreba la yehe tinkãtɛ la puan, to'e sore yɛ'ɛha Yezu zi'an.

ref: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
text: Bala ma'a la ti Yezu zahereba la beleni en mɛ yeti, “Rabi, di dia fiiwa.”

ref: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
text: Ti a yele ba yeti, “Mam tari dia mɛ wan di ti ya ka mina.”

ref: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
text: Ti a zahereba la yɛta taaba yeti, “Nɛra n tari dia na bo' en ti a di bii’?”

ref: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Mam dia dela n tuna se'em n tum mam na la sumboolum, la n ba'ahɛ a tuunɛ la.”

ref: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
text: Ya ka tari yetɔgum busebo ni yeti, ‘Ŋmarehi sinaahi n gee la tu bum dia’? Gee mam yeti ya mɛ yeti, Bihɛ ya kaaɛ kaaɛ, la ya nyɛ samana la zuto dia n bi'ɛ se'em, bɔta buŋa.

ref: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
text: Buna la pun pɔhɛ tɔ'ɔha a yɔɔrɛ mɛ, gee ka'aha dia la yɛ'ɛha vom kamba'ahɛ puan, ŋwana ti buta, la buna la wan lagum nyɛɛna la taaba.

ref: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
text: Ya yetɔgebuna wa dela asira. ‘Ayima n buti, ti ayima buna.’

ref: I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
text: Mam tum ya ti ya kiŋɛ ta bum la zi'an ti ya ka tum. Basɛba n namehɛ, ti yamam kiŋɛ ta dita ba nyuurɔ.”

ref: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
text: Ti Samariyaduma en yehe tinkãtɛ la puan na la kekeko nam bo' Yezu, doohe la pɔka la n yele se'em yeti, “A yele mam la mam tum yele sɛ'a woo la.”

ref: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
text: Bala zuo, Samariya tinkãtɛ nɛreba wa n wa'am na na la, ba belum en mɛ ti a kɛ̃ la ba. Ti a sakɛ, kɛ̃ bilam dabeha ayi'.

ref: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
text: Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam en, doohe a yelehum la puan.

ref: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
text: Gee nyaa yele pɔka la yeti, “La le dagena fum n yele tu se'em la zuo ti tu nam wa, gee tu mɛhi tuba n wum, ti tu baŋɛ ti eŋa n sirum de timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a faara la.” Yezu lebe Galilii

ref: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
text: En paaɛ Galilii la, Galiliihi la to'e en mɛ, doohe la ban pun nyɛ sɛla woo ti Yezu tum Yerusalɛm de'eŋo la puan la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa lagum bɔna de'eŋo la puan mɛ. Yezu tibɛ kpɛ'ɛma dayua

ref: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
text: Ti Yezu le kiŋɛ Kana n boi Galilii puan, zi'an ti a tabelɛ bahɛ ti ko'om lebege daam la. Kpɛ'ɛma ayima dayua n wa bɛ̃'ɛra, bɔna Kapɛnum.

ref: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
text: Ti kpɛ'ɛma wa n ta wum ti Yezu ze'ele Yudiya wa'am la Galilii na la, ti a kiŋɛ ta belum en ti a wa'am na tibɛ a dayua wa, se'ere en sɔi la, a dayua la kɔ'ɔm yɛ'ɛha la yɔɔen.

ref: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Han dagena ti ya nyɛ la daalehum la nyalema, ya kan malum nam.”

ref: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
text: Ti kpɛ'ɛma wa belum Yezu yeti, “Naa, wa'am kalam na tibɛ n dayua wa gee ka ye' la kum.”

ref: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Kina gee fum dayua la kan ki.” Ti buraa wa nam yelehum la, Yezu n yele en se'em la, gee sige sore yɛ'ɛha kula.

ref: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
text: En boi sore zuo kula la, ti a yamehi zɔ tu'uhɛ en na yele en yeti, “Fum bia la vo'e ya.”

ref: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
text: Ti a soke ba sansɛka puan ti bia la ban'an la bahɛ ka la, ti ba yeti, “Zaam, wuntɛɛŋa gogo kaareŋa kayina wuntɛɛŋa zuo tiŋasuka, ti ka ɔɔrɔ la bahɛ en.”

ref: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
text: Bala ti buraa wa baŋɛ ti sankaŋa mɛa puan ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Kina gee fum bia la kan ki la.” Ti buraa wa nam Yezu, eŋa la a yire za'a.

ref: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
text: Daalehum kana pa'ahɛ la daalehum buyi' ti Yezu tum, eŋa n ze'ele Yudiya wa'am Galilii na la.

ref: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
text: Yele ana pooren ti Yuuduma malema de'eŋo kuyima saŋa paaɛ, ti Yezu kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm.

ref: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
text: Yerusalɛm puan la, nayaŋa kayima ti ba yi'ira ka piihi nayaŋa, ti bɔka gã'a ti ba yi'ira ka Heburu puan ti Bɛti-zata, tara bɔpagehi sinuu.

ref: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
text: Ti buraa kayima bɔna bini bɛ̃' yuuma pihitã la anii.

ref: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
text: ya me ka tari a yelehum la ya puan se'ere n sɔi la, ya ka nam se'em ti a tum na la.

ref: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
text: Yezu n nyɛ en ti a gã' bini gee me mina ti a bɛ̃' yuue mɛ la, ti a soke en yeti “Fum boti tibegɔ?”

ref: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
text: Ti buraa wa lebehe en yeti, “Naa, Suŋera la ni han sige na damɛ ko'om la, mam ka tari se'em ni wan suŋɛ mam ti n lu ko'om la puan yia. Mam ni ta maahena la, ti ayima n pun lu bini.”

ref: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Ihige kilum fum suŋɔ kiŋɛ.”

ref: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
text: Bilam bilam ti buraa la nyɛ ka tibegɔ, vaaɛ ihige saazuo, kilum ka suŋɔ tu' kina. Yele dina wa iŋɛ la vo'ohego daarɛ puan.

ref: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
text: Bala ti Yuuduma la yele buraa wa n nyɛ ka tibegɔ wa yeti, “Zina dela vo'ohego dabeherɛ, la dagi ma'a ti fum ziira suŋɔ.”

ref: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
text: Gee buraa wa lerege ba yeti, “Buraa sɛka n tibɛ mam la n yele mam ti, ‘Kilum fum suŋɔ la kina.’ ”

ref: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
text: Ti ba soke en yeti, “Buraa kani n bala yele fum yeti, ‘Kilum fum suŋɔ kiŋɛ la’?”

ref: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
text: Buraa wa n nyɛ ka tibegɔ wa me ka mi' Yezu n de se'em. Ti Yezu me doohe nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan yehe.

ref: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
text: Pooren ti Yezu ta nyɛ buraa wa pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, yele en yeti, “Bihɛ, fum ban'an la bahɛ ya. Da le tum be'em ti la tari sɛla n gani ŋwana le wa'an fum zuo na.”

ref: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
text: Ti buraa wa kiŋɛ nyaa ta yele Yuuduma la yeti, la dela Yezu n daa tibɛ mam la.

ref: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti Yuda duma la ihige dɔla Yezu dɔlega, eŋa en tuni tuun-ana wa taaba vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan la.

ref: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Mam Sɔ n tuni la, mam me tuni mɛ.”

ref: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
text: Ŋwana zuo, ti Yuuduma wa malum mu'ɛ la nini ɛɛra ba ko' en, beni zuo, a ka galum la vo'ohego daarɛ kiha la ma'a, gee a galum mɛ la eŋa en yi'iri Yinɛ ti a mɛŋa Sɔ, tara a mɛŋa makera la Yinɛ la. Dayua la paŋa

ref: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti Dayua la kan ta'an tuna a mɛŋa tuuma, han dagena sɛla ti a nyɛ ti Sɔ la tuna la. Sɛla woo ti Sɔ la tuna la, bala mɛa ti Dayua la tuna.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
text: Sɔ la nɔŋɛ Dayua la mɛ, gee pa'ala en sɛla woo ti eŋa n de Sɔ la tuna. A me wan pa'alɛ en yele kãra n gani yele ana wa, ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ

ref: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
text: La dela asira ti, wuu Sɔ la n ihigere kum bɔ'ɔra ba vom se'em la, bala mɛa ti Dayua la me bɔ'ɔra nɛreba vom en boti se'em.

ref: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
text: Sɔ la mɛŋa ka gakeri nɛra, gee a dikɛ gakerɛ paŋa la za'a bo' la Dayua la,

ref: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
text: ti bala ti nɛra woo wan na'aha dayua la wuu ban na'ahɛ Sɔ la se'em la. Fum se'em woo n ka na'ahɛ Dayua la, ka na'ahɛ sɔ se'em n tum en na la.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
text: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa. Se'em woo n wum mam yelehum wa, gee nam se'em daana n tum mam na la, tari la vomkanba'ahɛ. Ena daana ka boi gakerɛ puan. A daana tee mɛ kum puan wa'am vom puan na.

ref: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
text: “N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa ti, saŋa kini na, ka pun paaɛ, ti ki'induma wan wum Yinɛ dayua wa kua, ti sɛba n wum wan vɔna

ref: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
text: Wuu sɔ la mɛŋa n tari vom a puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti a bo' Dayua la me sore ti a tara vom a puan,

ref: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
text: gee me kelum bo' Dayua wa paŋa ti a gakɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, a dela Asaala Dayua.

ref: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
text: Da bahɛ ya ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ la yele ana, gee saŋa la kini na ti sɛba ti ba pi' ba yɔɔrɔ puan wan wum Dayua la kua,

ref: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
text: ihige ba yɔɔrɔ la puan yehe na, ti sɛba n tum sum wan vo'e ihige kɛ̃ vomkanba'ahɛ puan, ti sɛba n tum be'em me vo'e ihige kɛ̃ namehegɔ puan. Yezu Aze'nyɛduma

ref: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
text: “Mam ka tuni tuunɛ n ma'a. Mam n wum se'em la, mam gakeri dɔla la bala mɛa. Bala zuo, mam gakerɛ la dela tuuntɔɔ se'ere n sɔi la, mam ka tuni n mɛŋa sumboolum, gee se'em n tum mam la sumboolum.

ref: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.
text: Mam han muula n mɛŋa yele, n muulegɔ la kan dɛna asira.

ref: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
text: Ayima n boi ha muula mam yele, ti mam me mina ti en tɔgeri n yele se'em la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth.
text: Ya daa tum nɛreba mɛ Yɔn zi'an, ti Yɔn me yele ya yelemɛŋerɛ la.

ref: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
text: Mam yeti sɛla wa dagi la mam to'ere la asaala aze'nyɛ yetɔgum, gee mam yeti ŋwana la yamam nyɛ faarɛ.

ref: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
text: Yɔn n daa bala ana wuu butolila la, dita niira, ti ya daa maahum ya suya ti ya lagum nyɛɛm a bugum la puan dabeha ayi.

ref: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
text: Gee mam tari la yelehum n gani Yɔn. Tuunsɛ'a ti n Sɔ dikɛ bo' mam ti n tum ba'ahɛ la, tuun aŋa ti n tuna wa. Ŋwana wa pa'alɛ mɛ ti, Sɔ la n tum mam na.

ref: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
text: Mam Sɔ se'em n tum mam na la mɛŋa pun tɔgɛ mam yele mɛ. Yamam ka tabelɛ wum a kua bii ya nyɛ a naanɛ,

ref: You search the Scriptures, because you think you will find eternal life in them. The Scriptures tell about me,
text: “Ya viiheri pa'alegɔ gɔnɔ la puan la daare woo, se'ere n sɔi la ya ti'ihɛ ti vom kamba'ahɛ yɛla n boi bini, gee gɔnɔ tuna mɛa n bala tɔgera mam yele la.

ref: but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
text: Gee la bala za'a ya zagehɛ mɛ ti ya kan wa'an mam zi'an na la ya nyɛ vom la.

ref: I don't care about human praise,
text: Mam ka eere nɛreba na'ahegɔ.

ref: but I do know that none of you love God.
text: Gee mam mi' ya ti ka tari Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ ya puan.

ref: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
text: Han dɛna mam, mam wa'am la n Sɔ yu'urɛ puan na, gee ya ka to'e mam. Gee se'em han wa'am a mɛŋa na, ya to'ore en mɛ.

ref: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
text: Ya yeti ya iŋɛ la wani nam mam. Ya han tɔ'ɔha taaba na'ahegɔ, gee ka ɛɛra la ninmu'urɛ, na'ahegɔ seko n ze'eti se'em ma'a n de Yinɛ la zi'an na la?

ref: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
text: Da ti'ihɛ ya ti mam n wan ta ze'ele Sɔ la nɛŋan zɛregɛ ya. Se'em n zɛregɛ ya dela Moose, se'em ti ya dikɛ ya puti'irɛ delum la.

ref: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
text: Ya han sirum nam ni Moose, ya wan nam ni mam. Se'ere n sɔi la, Moose gulehɛ la mam yele.

ref: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
text: Gee ya han ka nam Moose n gulehɛ sɛla la, ya yeti ya iŋɛ la ŋwani nam mam yeti ya sɛla wa?”

ref: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
text: Bala ti ba doohe lagehɛ sɛla woo, zɔra la ti n yee boroboro anuu la ti nɛreba la obe tigɛ gee bahɛ la puan la, pirɛ pitɔ pia la tuyi'.

ref: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
text: Ti ban yeti ba ze'ele uureŋɔ la ti a zom la, ba bihɛ ti ba pun bɔna la doone, zɛ'sɛka ti ba ye' ni la. Saazuo Boroboro

ref: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
text: Ti ba soke en yeti, “Beni daalehum ti fum wan tum, ti tu nyɛ gee nam bo' fum. Beni tuunɛ ti fum wan tum?”

ref: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Nɛra kan ta'am wa'am mam zi'an na, han dagena Sɔ la n tum mam na la mɛŋa n ve'e en na. Ti n me wan vo'e nɛre eŋa daana ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la.

ref: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
text: “Bumbilepaalega boi ka'lam ziira boroboro banuu la zim iyi'. Gee bana yeti ba iŋɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ wa la wani?”

ref: The ground was covered with grass, and Jesus told his disciples to tell everyone to sit down. About
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Bahɛ ya ti nɛreba la zi'ire tiŋa.” Muɔ me n zo'e zi'an la puan, ti nɛreba la zi'ire, nɛreba n en paaɛ wuu tuha anuu la zi'ire tiŋa.

ref: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
text: paaɛ zom uureŋɔ ti ba yakɛ Kapɛnum tiŋa. Bala ma'a ti la nyuulum mɛ, gee ti Yezu nan ka lebe ba zi'an na.

ref: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
text: Ŋwana en tole la, ti Yezu yakɛ Galilii tiŋa ko'kãtɛ la yakenɛ deyima la ti ba yi'ira Tiberiyasi la ha.

ref: A large crowd had seen him work miracles to heal the sick, and those people went with him.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ bibigera dɔla en se'ere zuo, ba nyɛ daalehum sɛ'a ti a tuna bɔ'ɔra bã'duma la.

ref: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
text: Ti Filipi lerege en yeti, “Ŋmarehi siyoobe yɔɔrɛ pun kan ta'am da' dia bo' nɛrebɔbegɔ wa ti nɛra woo yeen paaɛ fiifiihe.”

ref: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
text: Ti a zahereba la ayima, Simɔn Pita suuɔ la Anduru, yele Yezu yeti,

ref: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
text: Ti Yezu to'e boroboro la, bo' Yinɛ fara ba'ahɛ, gee bahɛ ti ba tɔrɛ bo' nɛreba la n zi' la. Gee to'e zim la iŋɛ bala mɛa, ti nɛra woo to'e paaɛ en boti se'em.

ref: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
text: Nɛreba la n di tigɛ la, ti a yele a zahereba la yeti, “Doohe ya va ban tigɛ gee bahɛ sɛla woo la za'a, ti sɛla da gee.”

ref: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
text: Yezu n baŋɛ ti nɛreba la maahum mɛ ti ba wa'am na pɛregɛ bobe en na'am la, a bahɛ ba mɛ gee kiŋɛ zom zoore la zuo ha a ma'a. Yezu kini ko'om zuo (Matu

ref: The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day
text: Yiimbeere, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la n zaam boi ateko la yakenɛ deyima ha la, tiɛ ti uureŋɔ zaam ka boi gee gee la uureŋɔ kuyina ma'a n zaam boi . Ba me zaam nyɛ ti Yezu zaam ka doohe a zahereba la uureŋɔ la, gee a zahereba la ma'a n zaam kiŋɛ.

ref: Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.”
text: Da tuni ya ɛɛra disɛka n wan ba'ahɛ, gee tuni ya ɛɛra disɛka n wan bɔna wuuha, bo' ya vom kamba'ahɛ, sebo ti mam Asaala Dayua wa wan bo' ya la. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ma'a ti Yinɛ n de Sɔ la daalehum bɔna a puan.”

ref: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
text: La gulehɛ bɔna nɔtɔgeheba gɔŋɔ puan yeti, ‘Yinɛ mɛŋa wan pa'alɛ ba za'a.’ Se'em woo n wum Sɔ la kua, gee zamehɛ Sɔ la zi'an n ye' mam zi'an na.

ref: They started arguing with each other and asked, “How can he give us his flesh to eat?”
text: Ŋwana ti Yuuduma la pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene tuulɛ la ba mɛhi yeti, “Ŋwani ti buraa kana wa wan dikɛ ka inganɛ bo' tumam ti tu obe?”

ref: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
text: Yinɛ n ta lu la, ti a zahereba la kiŋɛ paaɛ ateko la nɔgbene,

ref: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
text: Ti komi'ihe pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra, se'ere n sɔi la, sabeeo kiŋɔ n lobere ko'om la zuo.

ref: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
text: Bama n mɔm uureŋɔ la kiŋɛ wuu miila atã anaahi la, ba nyɛ ti Yezu yagɛ la ko'om la zuo kina paara uureŋɔ la na, ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba.

ref: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
text: Gee a yele ba yeti, “La dela mam. Da zoti ya dabeem.”

ref: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
text: Ti uurenɔ tuseto ze'ele Tiberiyasi tiŋa wa'am na paaɛ zi'an ti Daana la zaam bo' fara boroboro la zuo gee ti nɛreba la zaam obe la.

ref: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
text: Gee nɛrekuuŋɔ la n nyɛ ti Yezu, bii a zahereba la ka boi zi'an la puan la, ti ba zom uurenɔ la mɛhi kiŋɛ Kapɛnum tiŋa ta ɛɛra en.

ref: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
text: Ban ta nyɛ en ateko la yakenɛ deyima la ha la, ba yele en yeti, “Rabi, wani ma'a ti fum wa'an ka'lam na?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, yan eere mam wa dagi la daalehum la ti ya nyɛ la, gee yan zaam obe boroboro la tigɛ la zuo.

ref: “What exactly does God want us to do?” the people asked.
text: Ti ba soke en yeti, “Beni en de tu iŋɛ, ta'am ita sɛla ti Yinɛ tuuma?”

ref: Jesus answered, “God wants you to have faith in the one he sent.”
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Yinɛ en boti ya tuna se'ere dela, ya nam mam se'em ti a tum na wa.”

ref: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
text: Tumam yaabeduma yuum di la maana muɔ puan la ha. Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, ‘A bo' ba la boroboro n ze'ele saazuo na ti ba di la.’ ”

ref: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, Moose n yuum bo' ya boroboro sɛka la dagi saazuo boroboro, gee la dela mam Sɔ n bo'ori ya boroboro se'ere n sirum ze'ele saazuo na.

ref: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, Yinɛ boroboro dela boroboro n ze'ele saazuo sige na, bɔ'ɔra timpaberɛ wa za'a vom.”

ref: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
text: Ti ba lebehe en yeti, “Naa, yi bɔ'ɔra tɔ boroboro kana wa saŋa woo.”

ref: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Mam n de vom boroboro la. Se'em woo n wa'am mam zi'an na, kan malum nyɛ kom. Se'em woo n nam mam, ko'yuuro kan malum tara en.

ref: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
text: Gee mam pun yele ya yeti, Ya nyɛ mam mɛ, gee la bala za'a ya ka nam bo' mam.

ref: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
text: Sɛla woo ti Sɔ la dikɛ bo' mam, wan wa'am mam zi'an na, ti se'em woo me n wa'an mam zi'an na, mam kan makɛ dige en bahɛ.

ref: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
text: Se'ere n en sɔi la, mam ze'ele saazuo sige na dagi la n tuna n mɛŋa sumboolum, gee se'em daana n tum mam na la sumboolum.

ref: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
text: Ti dina wa n de se'em daana n tum mam na la sumboolum yelemɛŋerɛ. Ti n da bɔregɛ se'em, sɛba woo ti a dikɛ bo' mam la puan bahɛ, gee n ihige ba ba'ahegɔ daarɛ.

ref: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Dina wa n de mam Sɔ la suboolum yelemɛŋerɛ, ti sɛba woo n nyɛ Dayua la gee nam bo' ka la, wan tara vomkanba'ahɛ, ti n ta vo'e ba ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la.”

ref: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
text: Ti Yuuduma la pɔhɛ nyɔna en la en yeti, “Mam n de boroboro n ze'ele saazuo sige na la.”

ref: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
text: Ba nyɔni yeti, “Dagi ena n de Yezu la,Yosefe dayua la, se'em sɔ la a ma ti tu mina wa? Ti la iŋɛ la wani ti a ŋmɛregɛ yɛta ti, ‘Mam ze'ele la saazuo sige na’?”

ref: Jesus told them: Stop grumbling!
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Da gugeleni ya la taaba.

ref: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
text: Dagi ti se'em n tabelɛ nyɛ Sɔ la, han dagena se'em n ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na la. Eŋa daana n nyɛ sɔ la.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
text: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti se'em woo n nam tari la vomkanba'ahɛ.

ref: I am the bread that gives life!
text: Mam n de vom boroboro la.

ref: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
text: Ya yaabeduma yuum di maana dia la mɛ muɔ la puan ha, gee ba za'a ki ya.

ref: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
text: Boroboro en ze'ele saazuo sige na n ŋwana, ti se'em woo n obe de, kan ki.

ref: I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
text: Mam n de vom boroboro la, ze'ele saazuo sige na la. Se'em woo en obe boroboro dina wa wan vɔna wuuha. Boroboro se'ere ti mam yeti n dikɛ bo' timpaberɛ wa ti ba tara vom la dela mam inganɛ wa.”

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta wa, ti han dagena ya obe la mam asaala dayua wa inganɛ, gee nyu mam ziim, ya kan tara vom ya puan.”

ref: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
text: Se'em en obe mam inganɛ, gee nyu mam ziim la n wan tari vomkanba'ahɛ, ti n ihige en ba'ahegɔ daarɛ.

ref: My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink.
text: Mam inganɛ wa dela dia yelemɛŋerɛ, ti mam ziim wa dɛna sɛla en nyuuri yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Se'em en obe mam iŋa wa, gee nyu mam ziim la boi la mam puan, ti mam me bɔna la en.

ref: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
text: Wuu Sɔ la n de vom daana tum mam na ti mam vɔna Sɔ la zuo la, bala mɛa ti se'em woo n obe mam wan vɔna mam zuo.

ref: The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
text: Boroboro dina n ze'ele saazuo sige na, de ka ani wuu ya yaabeduma n yuum di boroboro gee kelum ki la. Gee fum se'em en boroboro dina wan vɔna wuuha.”

ref: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
text: A tɔgɛ yetɔgebuna wa la en daa boi Kapɛnum tiŋa Zamehegɔ yire la puan pa'ala la. Vom kamba'ahɛ Yelehum

ref: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
text: Yezu zahereba la kekeko n wum ti Yezu tɔgɛ yele ana la, ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, “Pa'alegɔ kuna ba' kpe'em mɛ. Ani n wan to'e ku?”

ref: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
text: Gee sɛba boi ya puan ka nam bɔ.” Se'ere n sɔi la, pɔhega na la, Yezu pun mina ba puan sɛba n wan nam bo' en, la se'em n de en wan ta koohe en mɛ.

ref: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going

text: Mam yele ya ti ya wan ki ya be'em la puan. Ya sirum wan ki ya be'em la puan han dagena ya nam mɛ bo' mam ti mam dela mam n de se'em la.”

ref: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
text: Ti ba soke en yeti, “Fum dela ani?” Ti Yezu lebehe ba yeti, “Beni ti n pun le tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya wa?

ref: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
text: Gee mam tari ya yɛla zo'e zo'e wan yele ni ya, gee gakɛ ya. Gee Se'em daana n tum mam na la dela yelemɛŋerɛ, ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra timpaberɛ wa la mam wum sɛla a zi'an la.”

ref: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
text: Ti ba ka bɔkɛ ti Yezu tɔgeri la a Sɔ la yele bɔ'ɔra ba'.

ref: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
text: Ŋwana ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Ya han ta zɛkɛ Asaala Dayua wa saazuo ba'ahɛ, ya wan baŋɛ ti mam n bala, gee me baŋɛ ti mam ka iti sɛla n ma'a, gee mam tɔgeri yele ana wa dɔla la Sɔ la n pa'ali mam se'em.

ref: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
text: Se'em daana n tum mam na la, boi mɛ la mam. A ka bahɛ mam n ma'a, se'ere n sɔi la, mam tuni pɛɛra la a puurɛ paaɛ paaɛ saŋa woo.”

ref: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
text: En yeti yele ana wa woo la, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam bo' en. Sɛba n de Yezu dɔleba

ref: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
text: Ti Yezu yele Yuuduma sɛba n nam bo' en la yeti, “Ya han gurɛ la mam yelehum wa, ya sirum dela mam zahereba.

ref: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
text: Ya wan mina yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti yelemɛŋerɛ la bahɛ ti ya bɔna fai.”

ref: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
text: Ti ba lebehe yele en yeti, “Tumam dela Aberaham yaahi, tu ka tabelɛ dɛna se'em da'aba. De vuurɛ dela deni ti fum yeti, ‘Tu wan bɔna fai’?”

ref: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa. Se'em woo n tum be'em dela be'em da'aberɛ.

ref: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
text: Da'aberɛ ka boi wuuha yire la puan. Gee dayua la, kɔ'ɔm bɔna yire la puan mɛ wuuha.

ref: If the Son gives you freedom, you are free!
text: Bala zuo, dayua la han faaɛ fum bahɛ, fum yeti fum sirum bɔna la fai.

ref: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
text: Mam mi' ti ya dela Aberaham yaahi, gee la bala za'a, ya eere la fole ti ya ku mam, se'ere n sɔi la ya suya ka boti mam yelehum la.”

ref: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
text: Mam yeti ya la sɛla ti mam bɔna Sɔ la zi'an gee nyɛ, gee han dɛna yamam, yamam iti la sɛla ti ya wum ya sɔ la zi'an. Yezu la Aberaham

ref: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
text: Ti ba lerege Yezu yeti, “Aberaham n de tumam sɔ” Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Ya han deni Aberaham kɔma, ya wan iti ni Aberaham itegɔ,

ref: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
text: gee nananawa, ya zigera mɛ ti ya ku mam, mam se'em n yele ya yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere ti n wum Yinɛ zi'an. Dagi bala ti Aberaham iŋɛ.

ref: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
text: Yamam kɔ'ɔm sirum ita la tuunsɛ'a ti ya sɔ la ita la.” Ti ba yele en yeti, “Tumam dagi kɔm-yooro, Tumam sɔ dela ayima, Yinɛ wa.”

ref: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Yinɛ han sirum dɛna yamam Sɔ, ya wan nɔŋɛ ni mam, se'ere n sɔi la, mam ze'ele la a zi'an na bɔna ka'lam wa. Dagi mam mɛŋa n wa'am na, eŋa n tum mam na.

ref: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
text: Wani ti ya ka bɔkera mam yeti se'em wa? La dela, ya ka maahum ti ya nam mam yelehum wa zuo.

ref: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
text: Ya kɔ'ɔm ze'ele la ya sɔ Sagena la zi'an na, gee me looe ti ya tum ya sɔ tuunfi'iha sumboolum la. A dela nɛre kuura pi'ileŋo puan na, ka bɔta yelemɛŋerɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, yelemɛŋerɛ ka boi a puan. A han parum, a tɔgeri dɔla la a naanɛ n bala, se'ere n sɔi la, a dela pumparema, gee dɛna pumpɔreŋɔ woo sɔ.

ref: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
text: Gee mam n tɔgeri yelemɛŋerɛ la zuo, ya ka sakɛ bo' mam.

ref: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
text: Ya ani n wan ta'am pa'alɛ mam n tum be'em sebo? Mam han yɛta ya yelemɛŋerɛ, wani ti ya ka nam mam?

ref: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
text: Se'em woo en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, wuni Yinɛ yelehum mɛ. Se'ere zuo ti yamam ka wuna yelehum la dela, ya ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na.”

ref: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
text: Ti Yuuduma la lebehe en yeti, “Tu ka tari la buurɔ la tu yele fum yeti, fum dela Samariya, ti kulekpa'bɛ'a yaga fum zuo la?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, kulekpa'bɛ'a ka yage mam zuo. Gee mam na'ahɛ n Sɔ la mɛ, gee ti yamam ka na'ahɛ mam.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, “Ti se'em woo n gurɛ mam yelehum wa kankaŋi, kan malum ki.”

ref: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
text: Ti Yuuduma la yele en yeti“, “Tu nyaa baŋɛ ya ti kulekpa'bɛ'a n sirum yage fum zuo. Aberaham ki mɛ, bala mɛa ti nɔtɔgeheba la woo ki, gee la bala za'a, fum yeti mɛ ti, ‘Se'em woo n gurɛ mam yelehum wa kankaŋi kan malum ki?’

ref: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
text: Fum gani tumam sɔ Aberaham n ki la mɛ? Nɔtɔgeheba la me ki mɛ. Fum tugum yeti fum dela ani?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Mam han na'ahera n mɛŋa, n mɛŋa na'ahegɔ kuŋɔ dela yoo. La dela mam Sɔ la n na'aheri mam, Eŋa n bala ti ya yeti, ‘Yinɛ ma'a n de tumam Yinɛ la,’

ref: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
text: la yamam n pun ka mina en la. Gee mam mi' en. Mam han yeti mam ka mi' en, n pareni mɛ wuu yamam wa la. Gee mam sirum mi' en mɛ, gee gurɛ a a yelehum la kankaŋi.

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa. Aberaham n wa bɔna la, ti mam pun bɔna mɛ.”

ref: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
text: Ŋwana ti nɛreba la pi'ihe kuga ti ba lobe en, ti a lile nɛreba la puan yehe pu'uhegɔ yire la puan.

ref: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
text: Yezu n kini la, a nyɛ la buraa n de fua ka biilimen na.

ref: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
text: Ti a zahereba la soke en yeti, “Rabi, ani n tum be'em? Buraa wa bii ka dɔgereba be'em zuo ti ba dɔgɛ ka ti ka dɛna fua wa?”

ref: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “La dagi buraa wa, bii ka dɔgereba be'em zuo, gee ka de fua ti Yinɛ tuuma n niiɛ ka puan na.

ref: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
text: La dela tu tuna n Sɔ la n tum mam na la tuuma, ti wuntɛɛŋa nan boi wa, gee yu'uŋɔ kini na, ti nɛra kan ta'am tum.

ref: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
text: Mam n nan boi timpaberɛ wa zuo wa, mam de timpaberɛ la niihum.”

ref: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
text: En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a tubehɛ la nɔtuurum iŋɛ tuntɔnɔ puan, nɔm tuntɔnɔ la la nɔtuurum la, dikɛ buuɛ buraa la nini la,

ref: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
text: gee yele en yeti, “Kiŋɛ Silom bɔka la puan ta wire.” . Ti buraa wa kiŋɛ ta wire a nɛŋa la. Ti a nini la pikɛ ti a kina lɛbera na,

ref: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
text: ti a mabiihi, la nɛresɛba n ni nyɛta en ti a zi'an suhera la, pɔhɛ sɔkera taaba yeti, “Ena dagi buraa la n ni zi'an suha la?”

ref: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
text: Ti sɛba yeti, en bala, ti sɛba me yeti, “Aayi, a yeen wuna mɛ la en.” Ti buraa wa mɛŋa muula yeti, “Mam n bala, mam n bala.”

ref: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
text: Ti a lerege ba yeti, “Buraa la yu'urɛ n yi'ire Yezu la, n bum tuntɔnɔ yakɛ buuɛ mam nini la, gee yele mam yeti, ‘Kiŋɛ Silom bɔka la puan ta wire.’ Bala ti mam kiŋɛ ta wire nyaa nyɛta wa.”

ref: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
text: Ti ba soke en yeti, “Ka bɛɛ?” Ti a yeti, “Mam ka mi'.” Farasii duma viihɛ tibegɔ la yele

ref: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
text: La dela vo'ohego daarɛ ti Yezu nɔm tuntɔnɔ la buuɛ buraa la nini ti i pikɛ la.

ref: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
text: Bala ti Farasii duma wa me pɔhɛ sɔkera buraa la, lan iŋɛ se'em ti a nini la pikɛ. Ti a yele ba yeti, “A nɔm la tuntɔnɔ buuɛ mam nini, ti mam kiŋɛ ta wire nyaa nyɛta wa.”

ref: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
text: Ti Farasii duma la basɛba yeti, “Burasɛka n iŋɛ ŋwana wa ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, se'ere en sɔi la, a ka na'aheri vo'ohego dabeherɛ la.” Gee basɛba me yeti. “Ŋwani ti nɛra n de be'em daana wan ta'am tuna daalehum buna taaba?” Ti ba welehi taaba.

ref: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
text: Ti Farasii duma wa le soke fua wa yeti, “Fum ti'ihɛ ti buraa wa dela beni? Fum nini ti a pikɛ.” Ti buraa la yele ba yeti, “A dela nɔtɔgeha.”

ref: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
text: Gee Yuuduma kpɛ'ɛnduma la pun ka sakɛ ti buraa la tabelɛ fɔ mɛ gee nyaa nyɛta, han dagena ban yi' buraa wa dɔgereba na la,

ref: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
text: ti ba soke ba yeti, “Yamam dayua n ŋwana, ti ya yeti yamam dɔgɛ ka ti ka dɛna la fua ka biilimen na la? La nyaa iŋɛ la wani ti ka nyɛta wa?”

ref: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
text: Ti ka dɔgereba lerege yeti, “Tumam mi' ti tumam dayua n bala, tu me dɔgɛ en ti a dɛna la fua,

ref: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
text: gee tumam ka mi' lan iŋɛ se'em ti a nyaa nyɛta wa, bii tu mina se'em daana n pikɛ a nini la. Soke ya en gee a dagi bia, a mɛŋa wan tɔgɛ bo' a mɛŋa.”

ref: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
text: Ti ba le yi' buraa wa n tabelɛ dɛna fua wa buyi' puan na yele en yeti, “Pɛgɛ Yinɛ! Tumam mi' ti burasɛka n tibɛ fum wa dela be'em daana.”

ref: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
text: Ti a lerege ba yeti, “Mam ka mi' a de be'em daana, bii a dagi be'em daana. Yele yinɛ ti mam mina dela, mam daa fɔ mɛ, gee nyaa nyɛta.”

ref: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
text: Ti Yuuduma wa le soke en yeti, “A iŋɛ fum la wani? A iŋɛ la wani pikɛ fum nini la?”

ref: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text: Ti a lerege ba yeti, “Mam pun yele ya bala mɛ, gee ya ka boti ya wum. Wani ti ya le ɛɛra ya wum le yɛ'ɛha? Ya nyaa boti ya lebege la a zahereba bii?”

ref: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
text: Ti ba taaɛ en la tuurɛ, yɛta en yeti, “Fum n de a bura kaŋa wa zahera, gee tumam dela Moose zahereba.

ref: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
text: Tumam mi' ti Yinɛ yuum tɔgɛ mɛ bo' Moose, gee han dɛna bura kana wa, tumam ka mi' kaŋa n yehe zi'an na.”

ref: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
text: Ti buraa la lebehe ba yeti, “Yele pakerɛ yaa! Ya ka mi' kaŋa n yehe zi'an na, gee la bala za'a, kaŋa me n pikɛ mam nini wa.

ref: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
text: Tumam mi' ti Yinɛ ka kelehe be'em duma pu'uhegɔ, gee a kelehe la se'em n na'ahɛ en, gee nana a suboolum.

ref: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
text: Tu ka tabelɛ wum dɛ, timpaberɛ wa n pɔhɛ na wa paaɛ zina, ti nɛra n pikɛ nɛra n de fua a biilimen na nini.

ref: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
text: Buraa wa han ka ze'ele ni Yinɛ zi'an na, ka kan ta'am ni looe yele.”

ref: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
text: Ti ba lerege en yeti, “Fum ti ba dɔgɛ hɔ kɔ'ɔm bahɛ be'em zo'e zo'e puan na wa en zigeri ti fum pa'alɛ tumam?” Gee nyaa sa'ɛ buraa la ti ka kiŋɛ. Sia puan fɔɔlum

ref: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
text: Ti Yezu en ta wum ti ba sa'ɛ buraa la mɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti a ta nyɛ ka, nyaa soke ka yeti, “Fum nam asaala dayua la?”

ref: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
text: Ti buraa la soke en yeti, “Naa, a dela ani? Pa'ali mam ti n nam bu n.”

ref: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti‘, “Fum pun nyɛ en mɛ. Se'em n ze' tɔgera la fum wa n bala’.”

ref: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
text: Ti a yeti, “N daana, mam nam ya.” Gee na'ahɛ en.

ref: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
text: Ti Yezu yeti, “Mam wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na ti n gakɛ mɛ, ti sɛba n fɔ la nyɛta, ti sɛba n nyɛti la tee fɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Ya han fɔ ni mɛ, ya kan tari ni be'em, gee yamam n yeti, ‘Tumam nyɛti me wa la,’ ya be'em la yeere la ya puan.”

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
text: “Mam yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ, yelemɛŋerɛ ti se'em woo n ka doohe nayaŋa puan kɛ̃ piihi zɔŋɔ la puan, gee doohe la ze' sɛka sige, dela nageyiga, la se'em n faari.

ref: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
text: Nayangura la ni yu'ɛ bo' en mɛ ti a kɛ̃. A piihi la wuni a kua mɛ. A yi'ire a mɛŋa piihi la mɛ yu'ura, bɔna si nɛŋa tara si yɛha.

ref: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
text: A han tari si yehe na, a boi la nɛŋa ti si dɔla en, se'ere n sɔi la, piihi la mi' a kua mɛ.

ref: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
text: Piihi la kan doohe saana, si tugum wan zɔ mɛ gee bahɛ en se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka mi' saana la kua.”

ref: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
text: Yezu dikɛ la tɔgɛ biŋere buna wa tɔgɛ bo' ba, gee nɛreba la ka bɔkɛ en yeti ba sɛla la.

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
text: Ti Yezu le yele ba yeti, “N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ'e wa, Mam n de piihi la nayaŋa.

ref: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
text: Sɛba woo n diŋɛ mam nɛŋa na la dela nageyigehi, la fãareba, ti piihi la ka kelehe ba.

ref: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
text: Mam n de nayaŋa la, se'em woo n doohe mam zi'an kɛ̃ wan nyɛ faarɛ. A wan kɛ̃, gee yehe ta nyɛ yaama obe.

ref: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
text: Nageyiga ye' na yoo gee a ye' na ti a zu mɛ, gee ku, gee sagum. Gee han dɛna mam, mam wa'am na ti ba nyɛ la vom, vom sebo en seke yabera .

ref: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
text: “Mam dela dunkiinsuŋa la. Dunkiinsuŋɔ la dikeri la a vom biŋera bɔ'ɔra a piihi.

ref: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
text: Gee se'em ti ba dikɛ la, ti a dagena piihi la dunkiina la, ka sɔna piihi la, han nyɛ ti saheŋa kini na, a bahɛ piihi la mɛ gee zɔ, ti saheŋa la wa'am na nyɔgɛ piihi la gee yiregɛ si bahɛ.

ref: Hired workers run away because they don't care about the sheep.
text: Se'em ti ba dikɛ la n wan zɔ la dela, piihi la yele ka pakɛ en.

ref: I am the good shepherd. I know my sheep, and they know me.
text: Mam n de dunkiinsuŋɔ la. Mam mi' sɛba n de n dinɛ, ti mam dinɛ la me mina mam,

ref: Just as the Father knows me, I know the Father, and I give up my life for my sheep.
text: wuu Sɔ la n mi' mam ti mam me mina Sɔ la se'em la. Mam dikɛ n vom mɛ biŋe bo' n piihi la.

ref: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
text: Mam tari n piihi sise'ehi mɛ ti si ka bɔna piihi zɔŋɔ wa puan. N wan ba'ahum ɛ si na ti si me wum n kua. Ti piihi la za'a dɛna buyina, dunkiina ayima.

ref: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
