ref: “The Lord has blinded the eyes of the people, and he has made the people stubborn. He did this so that they could not see or understand, and so that they would not turn to the Lord and be healed.”
text: “Yinɛ mɛŋa n pi' ba nini, gee kãbɛ ba suya, ti ba nini da nyɛta, ti ba suya me da bɔkera yɛla, ti ba da tee ba puti'ira lebena ti n tibɛ ba.”

ref: The next day a large crowd was in Jerusalem for Passover. When they heard that Jesus was coming for the festival,
text: Ti yiimbeere ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la en ka tã ita en wa'am azɔɛtole de'eŋo la na la, wum ti Yezu ye' Yerusalɛm na.

ref: “People of Jerusalem, don't be afraid! Your King is now coming, and he is riding on a donkey.”
text: “Da zɔta dabeem Zaayɔn poyua wa, Bihɛ, fum naba la n bala kina na la, a yaga la bumpaalega zuo.”

ref: At first, Jesus' disciples did not understand. But after he had been given his glory, they remembered all this. Everything had happened exactly as the Scriptures said it would.
text: Yezu zahereba la ka bɔkɛ yele ana wa yia yia ha la, gee Yezu n da ta to'e a suŋa ba'ahɛ la, ti ba nyaa tiɛ ti yele ana wa woo gulehɛ bɔna gɔŋɔ la puan mɛ doohe Yezu puan, ti ba dikɛ a za'a iŋɛ en.

ref: A crowd had come to meet Jesus because they had seen him call Lazarus out of the tomb. They kept talking about him and this miracle.
text: La me dela, nɛreba wum mɛ ti a tum la daalehum kana zuo, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ yehe bibigera tu'uha en.

ref: Some Greeks had gone to Jerusalem to worship during Passover.
text: Nɛresɛba n wa'am na ti ba na'ahɛ azɔɛtole de'eŋo la saŋan la, basɛba dela Giriki duma.

ref: Philip told Andrew. Then the two of them went to Jesus and told him. The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up
text: Bala ti Filipi kiŋɛ ta yele Anduru, ti Anduru la Filipi nyaa paaɛ Yezu zi'an yele en.

ref: Jesus said: The time has come for the Son of Man to be given his glory.
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Saŋa la paaɛ ya ti asaala dayua la nyɛ pɛka.

ref: I tell you for certain that a grain of wheat that falls on the ground will never be more than one grain unless it dies. But if it dies, it will produce lots of wheat.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti beeleho han ka lu tintɔnɔ puan pu'ɛ, bu kelum dɛna la beeleho. Gee bu han lu timpaberɛ pu'ɛ yehe na, bu wan wam biili zo'e zo'e.

ref: If you love your life, you will lose it. If you give it up in this world, you will be given eternal life.
text: Se'em en nɔŋɛ a vom wan bɔregɛ bɔ bahɛ, ti se'em n ka gagɛ a vom zuo timpaberɛ wa zuo, a n tugum wan tara vomkanba'ahɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: Now I am deeply troubled, and I don't know what to say. But I must not ask my Father to keep me from this time of suffering. In fact, I came into the world to suffer.
text: “Nananawa, mam suure sagum mɛ. Gee n wan yeti bu? N yeti ‘N Sɔ, yehe mam namehegɔ saŋa wa puan bii’? Aayi n kan yele bala, namehegɔ wa zuo ti mam wa'am na paaɛ saŋa kana puan wa.

ref: When the crowd heard the voice, some of them thought it was thunder. Others thought an angel had spoken to Jesus.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la n ze' la en gee wum kua la, basɛba yeti, “Saa n damɛ, ti basɛba me yeti, suŋera n tɔgɛ bo' en na.”

ref: Then Jesus told the crowd, “That voice spoke to help you, not me.
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Kua la wa'an la yamam zuo na gee dagi mam.

ref: This world's people are now being judged, and the ruler of this world is already being thrown out!
text: Tiŋa la gakerɛ paaɛ ya, se'em n sɔi timpaberɛ wa bihera la dika saŋa paaɛ ya.

ref: If I am lifted up above the earth, I will make everyone want to come to me.”
text: Gee han dɛna mam, ba han ta zɛkɛ mam saazuo timpaberɛ wa zuo, n wan ve'e nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'am n zi'an na.”

ref: Jesus was talking about the way he would be put to death.
text: Yezu yele ŋwana pa'ala la kum sebo taaba ti a wan ki.

ref: The crowd said to Jesus, “The Scriptures teach that the Messiah will live forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la lebehe en yeti‘, “Tumam wum nɔze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan ti la yeti, Kiresta la wan bɔna la wuuha kan ki. Wani ti fum yɛta yeti, Ba wan ta zɛkɛ asaala dayua la saazuo? Ani n de asaala dayua wa?”

ref: Jesus answered, “The light will be with you for only a little longer. Walk in the light while you can. Then you won't be caught walking blindly in the dark.
text: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, “Niihum la wan bɔna la ya la dabeha fii wa, bala biheri ya kina ti niihum la nan boi wa, ti lika la da ta wa'am na pi' ya. Ya han kina lika puan, ya ka mi' yan ye' zi'an.

ref: Have faith in the light while it is with you, and you will be children of the light.” The People Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus After Jesus had said these things, he left and went into hiding.
text: Niihum wa n nan boi la ya wa, nam ya bo' niihum la, la ya dɛna niihum la kɔma.” Yezu n yele ba ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a kiŋɛ mɛ ta sugɛ a mɛŋa ti ba da nyɛta en.” Nɛreba n bu'uri Yezu yele

ref: He had worked a lot of miracles among the people, but they were still not willing to have faith in him.
text: La Yezu n tum daalehum kekeko nɛreba la ninin la, ba zo'e zo'e ka nam bo' en.

ref: This happened so that what the prophet Isaiah had said would come true, “Lord, who has believed our message? And who has seen your mighty strength?”
text: Gee ŋwana wa iŋɛ ti la doohe yelese'ere n tɔgɛ doohe nɔtɔgeha Azaaya puan se'em yeti, “N Daana, ani n nam tu yelehum la, gee ani ti Daana pa'alɛ a kinkpaŋa?”

ref: The people could not have faith in Jesus, because Isaiah had also said,
text: Ba kan ta'am nam, se'ere n sɔi la Azaaya daa purɛ pa'ahɛ yeti,

ref: Isaiah said this, because he saw the glory of Jesus and spoke about him.
text: Azaaya n yele dina wa la dela a yuum nyɛ la na'ahegɔ seko n de Yezu dinɛ, bala ti a tɔgɛ a yele la.

ref: Even then, many of the leaders put their faith in Jesus, but they did not tell anyone about it. The Pharisees had already given orders for the people not to have anything to do with anyone who had faith in Jesus.
text: Gee la bala za'a, pun ta ta' la kpɛ'ɛnduma la zo'e zo'e, nam bo' en mɛ. Gee Farasii duma la zuo, ba ka ta'am muulɛ dɛ se'ere n sɔi la, ba zoti la dabeem ti ba wan sa'ɛ ba zamehegɔ yire la puan bahɛ.

ref: And besides, the leaders liked praise from others more than they liked praise from God. Jesus Came To Save the World
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, ba ba' nɔŋɛ la asaala pɛka, gana pɛka sɛka n ze'eti Yinɛ zi'an na. Yezu pa'alegɔ la kilihego

ref: In a loud voice Jesus said: Everyone who has faith in me also has faith in the one who sent me.
text: Ti Yezu kaahɛ bahɛ na yeti, “Se'em woo n nam bo' mam, ka nam bo' la mam ma'a gee a nam bo' la se'em n tum mam na la.

ref: And everyone who has seen me has seen the one who sent me.
text: Se'em woo me n nyɛ mam, a nyɛ se'em daana n tum mam na la mɛ.

ref: I am the light that has come into the world. No one who has faith in me will stay in the dark.
text: Mam wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo me na woo niihum la, ti fum se'em woo n nam bo' mam kan yee lika puan.

ref: I am not the one who will judge those who refuse to obey my teachings. I came to save the people of this world, not to be their judge.
text: Mam ka gakeri se'em n wum mam yelehum wa gee ka tara bɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka wa'am na ti n gakɛ timpaberɛ wa mɛ, gee n tugum faaɛ ka bahɛ.

ref: But everyone who rejects me and my teachings will be judged on the last day by what I have said.
text: Se'em n zagehɛ mam gee ka to'e mam yelehum la, tari se'em n wan gakɛ en mɛ. Ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la, yelehum sebo ti mam tɔgɛ wa n wan dɛna gakera.

ref: I don't speak on my own. I say only what the Father who sent me has told me to say.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka tɔgɛ la mam mɛŋa n boti se'em, gee Sɔ la n tum mam na la n bo' mam nuurɛ, mam wan tɔgɛ sɛla, la mam wan yele sɛla.

ref: I know that his commands will bring eternal life. This is why I tell you exactly what the Father has told me.
text: Mam me mi' suŋa ti eŋa n boti n yele se'ere dela vomkanba'ahɛ yele. Bala zuo, mam n tɔgeri sɛla la dela Sɔ la n kɔ'ɔm bɔta ti n tɔgɛ se'em n bala.”

ref: It was before Passover, and Jesus knew that the time had come for him to leave this world and to return to the Father. He had always loved his followers in this world, and he loved them to the very end.
text: Nananawa, gee ti azɔɛtole de'eŋo la wan paaɛ la, ti Yezu pun baŋɛ ti a saŋa la paaɛ ya ti a bahɛ timpaberɛ wa, gee lebe a Sɔ zi'an. Bala ma'a ti Yezu nɔŋerɛ pun kɛ̃ sɛba en de a dinɛ la n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo la mɛ paaɛ paaɛ. A nɔŋɛ ba mɛ ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ.

ref: Even before the evening meal started, the devil had made Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, decide to betray Jesus.
text: Ba zaanuurɛ dia saŋa, ti Sagena la pun diŋɛ kalum Yudasi en de Simɔn Isikariyɔte dayua la puti'irɛ mɛ, ti a koohe Yezu.

ref: Jesus knew he had come from God and would go back to God. He also knew that the Father had given him complete power.
text: Ti Yezu n pun mina ti a Sɔ dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ la eŋa Yezu nu'uhen, gee me mina ti eŋa ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, gee me lɛbera la Yinɛ zi'an la,

ref: So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
text: ti a ihige a zi'irego zi'an la, yɛɛ a fuo seko ti a yɛ dɔgelɛ la biŋe, gee dikɛ duuhega wooe a sia.

ref: He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
text: Nyaa ka'ɛ ko'om iŋɛ peere lɔkɔ puan, pɔhɛ pɛɛra a zahereba la nama, gee duuha ba la duuhega la ti a wooe la.

ref: But when he came to Simon Peter, this disciple asked, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
text: Ti a wa'am na paaɛ Simɔn Pita, ti a yele en yeti, “Daana, fum yeti fum pee la mam nama yoi?”

ref: Jesus answered, “You don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Nananawa, fum kan bɔkɛ mam iti sɛla wa, gee pooren fum wan ta bɔkɛ.”

ref: “You will never wash my feet!” Peter replied. “If I don't wash you,” Jesus told him, “you don't really belong to me.”
text: Ti Pita yele en yeti, “Fum kan makɛ pee mam nama.” Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “Han dagena mam pee fum mɛ, fum ka tara tɔtɛ la mam.”

ref: Peter said, “Lord, don't wash just my feet. Wash my hands and my head.”
text: Ŋwana ti Simɔn Pita yele en yeti, “Daana, yi da pee n nama ma'a, gee pee n nu'uhi la n zuo pa'ahɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Se'em n diŋɛ sɔ iŋa ka le sɔɔra pa'aha han dagena a nama ma'a ti a wan duuhe, a iŋa la za'a ani la filam. Yamam ani la filam, gee dagi ya za'a.”

ref: Jesus knew who would betray him. That is why he said, “except for one of you.”
text: Yezu mi' ba puan se'em n wan koohe en. Bala zuo ti a yeti, “Dagi ya za'a n ani filam la.”

ref: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
text: En pee ba nama la ba'ahɛ la, dikɛ a fuo la yɛ, nyaa lebe zi'ire la ba la, a yele ba yeti, “Ya mi' sɛla ti n iŋɛ ya wa vuurɛ?

ref: You call me your teacher and Lord, and you should, because that is who I am.
text: Ya yi'iri mam la Pa'ala la Daana, Ya ka tuue, se'ere en sɔi la, mam n de se'em n bala.

ref: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
text: Mam iŋɛ biŋe ya mɛ, ti ya me ita wuu mam n iŋɛ se'em la.

ref: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti yameŋa ka gani ka daana, bii bitunehi gana se'em n tum ba la.

ref: I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, “The man who ate with me has turned against me!”
text: N ka tɔgeri ŋwana bɔ'ɔra la ya za'a. Mam mi' sɛba ti N looe la mɛ. Gee la dela la doohe gulehegɔ la n yeti, ‘Mam la se'em n lagum tuna laa kayina puan la n dikɛ mam koohe la.’

ref: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
text: N yeti ya dina wa nananawa ti de nan ka tum wa, de nyaa han ta tum, ya wan nam ti mam n bala.

ref: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
text: Bala ti a zahera wa n zi' dɛla a nyu'ɔ zuo la soke en yeti, “Daana, ani n de en?”

ref: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
text: Yezu n tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a suure sagum mɛ ti a kaahɛ yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa. Yamam puan ayima n yeti a koohe mam.”

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
text: Ti a zahereba la ayima, se'em ti a ba' nɔŋɛ la zi'an dɛla en,

ref: and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant.
text: ti Simɔn Pita ɛbegɛ en yeti, a soke Yezu, se'em n de en ti a tɔgera wa.

ref: No one at the meal understood what Jesus meant.
text: Gee se'em ka boi ban zi' la puan mina se'ere zuo ti Yezu yele en bala.

ref: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
text: Basɛba ti'ihɛ ti, Yudasi n zãli ba bo'olum tapɔɔ la, ti Yezu yɛta en ti, “Da' lɔgeseto ti ba wan bɔta azɔɛtole de'eŋo la puan”; bii a bihɛ sɛla bo' naheduma.

ref: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
text: Bala, en to'e boroboro la ba'ahɛ la, bilam bilam ti a ihige yehe. Bala ma'a ti la dela yu'uŋɔ. Ze'elego paalega

ref: Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
text: Yinɛ han nyɛ pɛka Dayua wa puan, Yinɛ mɛŋa wan bo' Dayua la me pɛka Dayua la mɛŋa puan, gee me iŋɛ de kalam.

ref: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
text: Kɔmbibehi wa, mam yeti n bɔna la ya la dabeha fiiwa. Ya wan ta ɛɛra mam, wuu mam daa yele Yuuduma la se'em la, bala ti n nyaa yɛta yamam me. ‘Mam n ye' zi'an la, ya kan ta'am wa'am bini na.’

ref: Simon Peter asked, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “You can't go with me now, but later on you will.”
text: Ti Simɔn Pita yele en yeti, “Daana, fum yeti fum kiŋɛ la bɛ?” Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Mam ye' zi'an la, fum kan ta'am doohe mam nananawa, gee pooren, fum wan doohe.”

ref: Peter asked, “Lord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!”
text: Ti Pita yele en yeti, “Daana, beni zuo ti mam kan ta'am doohe fum nananawa? Mam wan dikɛ n vom biŋe bo' hɔ.”

ref: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “Fum wan dikɛ fum vom biŋe bo' mam? Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa, ti nɔdɔɔ kan kaaha gura ti fum ta ki'ihɛ mam sugum nuurɛ butã.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
text: “Da bahɛ ya ti ya suya daana, nam ya Yinɛ, gee nam mam me.

ref: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
text: Mam Sɔ yire la ha, deto zo'e mɛ. La han dagi ni bala, mam wan yele ni ya ti n ye' ti n ta maalɛ la bɔŋa zi'an biŋe ya’?

ref: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
text: N han kiŋɛ ta maalɛ zi'an la biŋe ya ba'ahɛ, n wan le lebe n mɛŋa na, tari ya kiŋɛ, ti zi'an ti mam bɔna la, bilam ti yamam me wan bɔna.

ref: You know the way to where I am going.”
text: Ya me pun mina sore se'ere n dole yɛ'ɛha zi'an ti mam yɛ'ɛha la mɛ.”

ref: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
text: Ti Tɔmasi yele en yeti, “Daana, tu ka mi' fum ye' zi'an, tu yeti tu iŋɛ la wani baŋɛ sore la?”

ref: “I am the way, the truth, and the life!” Jesus answered. “Without me, no one can go to the Father.
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Mam n de sore la, la yelemɛŋerɛ la, la nyɔvore la. Se'em kan ta'am paaɛ Sɔ la zi'an, han dagena a doohe la mam puan.

ref: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
text: Ya han mina mam, ya wan mina mam Sɔ la me. Gee nananawa yɛ'ɛha ha, ya mi' en, gee me nyɛ en.”

ref: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.”
text: Ti Filipi yele en yeti, “Daana, pa'alɛ tɔ se'em n de Sɔ la, gee bala wan tigɛ tɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
text: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, “Mam n boi la ya saŋa kana wa woo wa Filipi, ti fum kelum ka mina mam? Se'em woo n nyɛ mam, nyɛ Sɔ la mɛ. Wani ti fum le yɛta mam yeti, ‘Pa'alɛ tɔ Sɔ la’?

ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
text: Fum ka nam ti mam boi mɛ la Sɔ la, ti Sɔ la me bɔna la mam? Yelehum wa ti mam tɔgera wa dagi mam mɛŋa yelehum, gee bu dela Sɔ se'em n boi la mam la n tuni a tuuma mam puan.

ref: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
text: Nam bo' mam ti mam boi mɛ la n Sɔ la, ti n Sɔ la bɔna la mam. Gee fum han pun ka nam, tuuma la mɛŋa zuo, fum dela fum nam bo' mam.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti se'em woo n nam mam, me wan tuna tuuma sɛ'a ti mam tuna wa, a wan tum a pun gaŋɛ bala, beni zuo, mam ye' la Sɔ la zi'an.

ref: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
text: N wan iŋɛ sɛla woo ti ya soke bɔta mam yu'urɛ puan, ti Sɔ la nyɛ pɛka dayua la puan.

ref: I will do whatever you ask me to do. The Holy Spirit Is Promised
text: Ya han soke mam yu'urɛ la puan bɔta sɛla woo, n wan iŋɛ dɛ. Fa'a Sia la nɔ'pu'ulegɔ.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
text: “Ya han nɔŋɛ mam, ya wan gurɛ mam pa'alegɔ kankaŋi.

ref: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
text: Bala ti n wan soke n Sɔ la, ti a wan bo' ya Nu'pu'ŋmɛ'ɛra ayima en kpe'em paaɛ paaɛ ti a bɔna la ya wuuha.

ref: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
text: Eŋa en de Fa'a Sia la n de yelemɛŋerɛ la, se'em ti Timpaberɛ wa zuo kan to'e en, Se'ere n sɔi la, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba ka nyɛti en, bii ba mina en. Gee yamam mi' en se'ere n sɔi la, a boi mɛ la ya, gee wan kelum bɔna la ya.

ref: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
text: “N kan bahɛ ya ti ya ana wuu kiibehi la. N lebere ya zi'an me na.

ref: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
text: La han iŋɛ fiiwa, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba kan le nyɛ mam, gee yamam wan nyɛ mam. Mam vɔi la, yamam me wan vɔna.

ref: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: De daarɛ la, ya wan baŋɛ ti mam boi mɛ la n Sɔ la, ti yamam bɔna la mam, ti mam me bɔna la ya.

ref: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
text: Se'em n mi' mam Pa'alegɔ duma la, gee dɔla a n de se'em n nɔŋɛ mam. Se'em en nɔŋɛ mam la, mam Sɔ la me wan nɔŋɛ en, ti mam mɛŋa me nɔŋɛ en, gee niira n mɛŋa pa'ala en.”

ref: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
text: Ti Yudasi ayima, yele Yezu yeti, “Daana, wani ti fum wan ta niiɛ fum mɛŋa pa'ala tumam, gee dagi timpaberɛ wa?”

ref: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “Sɛba en nɔŋɛ mam la, wan tara mam yelehum la, ti mam Sɔ wan nɔŋɛ ba, ti mam la n Sɔ la wan wa'am na iŋɛ tu bɔŋa zi'an ba puan.

ref: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
text: Se'em n ka nɔŋɛ mam, ka guti mam yelehum. Yelehum wa ti ya wuna wa me dagi mam yelehum, bu dela Sɔ la n tum mam na la dinɛ.

ref: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
text: “N yeti ya yele ana wa woo ti n nan boi la ya wa.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
text: Gee Tɛ'ɛla la kini na, Fa'a Sia la. Se'em ti Sɔ la wan tum en na mam yu'urɛ puan la. Eŋa wan pa'alɛ ya sɛla woo, gee tiiha ya sɛla woo ti mam yele ya la.

ref: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
text: Suma'ahum ti mam dikɛ baha ya. Mam mɛŋa suma'ahum ti mam dikɛ bɔ'ɔra ya. Mam ka bo'ori n suma'ahum wa wuu timpaberɛ zuo nɛreba n bo'ori ba suma'ahum se'em la. Da bahɛ ya ti ya suya daana, gee me da bahɛ ya ti dabeem tara ya.

ref: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
text: Ya wum mam pun yele ya yeti, ‘N yeti n kiŋɛ mɛ, gee le lebe ya zi'an na.’ Ya han nɔŋɛ mam, ya wan nyɛɛna, la mam n ye' Sɔ la zi'an la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Sɔ la gani mam mɛ.

ref: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
text: Nananawa, n pun yele ya mɛ, gee ti yɛla la baŋɛ wa'am na, bala ti yele ana wa han ta wa'am na tum, ya wan nam mam.

ref: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
text: N kan le ta'am tɔgɛ la ya yuue, se'ere en sɔi la, Be'em daana se'em n pɛregeri timpaberɛ wa zuo kini me na. A me ka tari n paŋa,

ref: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
text: gee mam iti la sɛla ti Sɔ la bo' mam nuurɛ ti n iŋɛ la, ti timpaberɛ nɛreba baŋɛ ti mam nɔŋɛ Sɔ la mɛ. Ihige ya ti tu kiŋɛ.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
text: “Mam de yelemɛŋerɛ sinsabega, ti mam Sɔ bihera sinsabega la.

ref: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
text: A ŋmati yile-se'ere woo n naaɛ la mam gee ka wuna baha mɛ. Gee yɔreha yile-se'ere woo n naaɛ la mam gee wuna wala ti de wam zo'e zo'e.

ref: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
text: Yamam pun diŋɛ ana la filam, doohe la yelehum la ti n tɔgɛ bo' ya la.

ref: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
text: Boi ya la mam, ti mam me bɔna la ya. Wuu yile n kan ta'am wam wala de ma'a se'em, han dagena de naaɛ mɛ la ta'aŋa la, bala mɛa ti yamam me kan ta'am wam, han dagena ya naaɛ mɛ la mam.

ref: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
text: Mam n de ta'aŋa la, ti yamam dɛna yila. Sɛba n naaɛ la mam, ti mam me naaɛ la ba la wani zɔ'ɔra mɛ, se'ere zuo, la han dagena la mam puan, ya kan ta'am iŋɛ sɛla.

ref: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
text: Se'em woo n ka naaɛ la mam, ba lobere en baha mɛ wuu yile ti ba ŋmaaɛ bahɛ ti a ku'e se'em la. Yile aŋa, ba ni lagehɛ ba mɛ bahɛ bugum puan ti ba di.

ref: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
text: Ya han bɔna la mam, ti n yelehum la bɔna la ya, soke ya yan boti sɛla, gee la wan tum bo' ya.

ref: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
text: Mam Sɔ pɛka boi la, ya wam zo'e zo'e. Ŋwana ti ya wan dɛna mam zahereba.

ref: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
text: Wuu Sɔ la n nɔŋɛ mam se'em la, bala mɛa ti mam nɔŋɛ ya. Boni ya n nɔŋerɛ la puan.

ref: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
text: Ya han gurɛ mam pa'alegɔ duma la kankaŋi, ya wan bɔna n nɔŋerɛ la puan, wuu mam n gurɛ n Sɔ pa'alegɔ duma la se'em kankaŋi la, bɔna a nɔŋerɛ la puan se'em la.

ref: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
text: N yeti ya yele ana wa woo, ti mam nyɛɛŋɔ wan bɔna la yamam puan, ti ya nyɛɛma pirɛ.

ref: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
text: “Mam Pa'alegɔ n ŋwana. Ti ya nɔŋɛ taaba, wuu mam n nɔŋɛ ya se'em la.

ref: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
text: Se'em ka tari nɔŋerɛ n karegɛ gana dina wa, ti nɛra dikɛ a vom bo' a zɔduma.

ref: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
text: Yamam dela mam zɔduma, ya han ita mam bo' ya nuurɛ ti ya ita sɛla la.

ref: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
text: Mam ka yi'iri ya n yamehi yɛ'ɛha, se'ere n sɔi la, yameŋa ka mi' ka daana n iti sɛla. Gee mam yi'iri ya la n zɔduma se'ere n sɔi la, mam bahɛ ti ya mina sɛla woo ti mam wum n Sɔ la zi'an mɛ.

ref: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Yamam ka looe mam, gee mam n looe ya, gee tum ya ti ya kiŋɛ ta wam wala. Ya wam wala sɛ'a n kan pu'ɛ. Bala ti Sɔ la wan bo' ya sɛla woo ti ya soke en mam yu'urɛ puan.

ref: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
text: N bo'ori ya pa'alegɔ kuna, ti ya nɔŋɛ la taaba. Nɛreba wan yaaɛ ya.

ref: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
text: “Timpaberɛ wa zuo han yaaɛ ya, mini ya ti ka yaaɛ la mam yia, gee nyaa yaaɛ yamam.

ref: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
text: Ya han deni timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, timpaberɛ wa zuo wan nɔŋɛ ya wuu a dinɛ la. Gee yan dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ la, gee mam n looe ya yehe timpaberɛ wa puan la, bala zuo ti timpaberɛ wa zuo yaaɛ ya la.

ref: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
text: Tɛri ya yelehum la ti mam tɔgɛ bo' ya yeti, ‘Yameŋa ka gana ka daana la.’ Ba han mɛlehɛ mam, ba wan mɛlehɛ yamam me. Ba han gurɛ mam yelehum la kankaŋi, ba wan gurɛ yamam yelehum la me kankaŋi.

ref: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
text: Gee ba wan iŋɛ ya yele ana woo mam yu'urɛ wa zuo. Se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka mi' se'em n tum mam na la.

ref: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
text: Mam han ka sige ni na tɔgɛ ni bo' ba, ba ka tari ni tuure. Gee nananawa, ba ka tari tu ka baŋɛ mɛ, ba be'em la puan.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
text: Se'em woo n yaaɛ mam, yaaɛ mam Sɔ la me mɛ.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
text: Mam han ka ze'ele ni ba nɛŋan tum daalehum sebo taaba ti nɛra kan ta'am tum wa, ba ka tari ni tuure. Gee nananawa, ba pun nyɛ mɛ, gee ti ba sina gee yaaɛ mam la n Sɔ la.

ref: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
text: Gee ŋwana woo iŋɛ ti la doohe la gulehegɔ la n boi ba pa'alegɔ gɔŋɔ la puan yeti, ‘Ba yaaɛ mam la yoo la.’

ref: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
text: “Gee Tɛ'ɛla la han wa'am na, se'em ti mam wan tum en ti a ze'ele Sɔ la zi'an na la, se'em n de Fa'a Sia la en de yelemɛŋerɛ, ze'ele mam Sɔ la zi'an na la, a wan tɔgera la mam yele bɔ'ɔra ya.

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text: Yamam me mɛhi dela ya tɔgera mam yele, se'ere n sɔi la, ya boi la mam mɛ n pa'alegɔ wa pi'ileŋo puan na.

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: “N yele ya yele ana wa, ti a gu' ya ti ya da pita luta.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
text: Kelehe ya, Ba wan dige ya zamehegɔ yiya la puan bahɛ. Saŋa kini na, ti sɛba n ku ya, wan ti'ihɛ ti ban iŋɛ bala la, ba tuni bɔ'ɔra la Yinɛ.

ref: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
text: Ba wan iŋɛ ŋwana doohe la ban ka mi' Sɔ la, bii mam la zuo.

ref: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
text: Gee mam diŋɛ yele ya yele ana wa, ti a saŋa han ta paaɛ, ya wan tɛra ti n pun yele ya a yele. N daa ka yele ya yele ana wa pi'ileŋo puan na la, doohe la mam nan boi la ya la zuo. Fa'a Sia la tuuma

ref: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
text: Gee nananawa, mam ye' lɛbera la se'em n tum mam na la zi'an, gee ya se'em ka soke mam nyaa ‘Fum ye' la bɛha?’

ref: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
text: Gee mam n nyaa yele ya yele ana wa la, susageŋɔ n pirɛ ya suya.

ref: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
text: Gee la bala za'a, mam yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti yamam sum zuo, la ani suŋa ti mam kiŋɛ. Se'ere n sɔi la, mam han ka kiŋɛ Tɛ'ɛla la kan wa'an ya zi'an na. Gee mam han kiŋɛ, n wan tum en ya zi'an na.

ref: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
text: A han wa'am na, a wan bahɛ ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba baŋɛ ti ba tuue mɛ la bamam n ti'ihɛ ti be'em, tuuntɔɔ vom, la gakerɛ n de sɛla la.

ref: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
text: Ba ka mi' be'em n de sɛla, beni zuo ba ka nam mam.

ref: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
text: Ba ka mi' tuuntɔɔ vom n de sɛla, beni zuo, mam ye' la Sɔ la zi'an, ti ya kan le nyɛ mam.

ref: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
text: Ba me ka mi' gakerɛ n de sɛla, beni zuo, ba pun gakɛ be'em daana la en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo la mɛ.

ref: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
text: “Yɛla zo'e zo'e boi ti n wan yele ya, gee nananawa la, la wan zo'e gaŋɛ ya tarega.

ref: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
text: Gee se'em en de Sia yelemɛŋerɛ la han ta wa'am na, a wan gu'ura ya ti ya mina yelemɛŋerɛ la za'a. Se'ere n sɔi la, a ka yeti a tɔgera la a mɛŋa yetɔgum, gee a wan tɔgɛ la sɛla woo ti a wum Yinɛ zi'an, ti a wan muulɛ bo' ya yele sɛ'a n wan wa'am na.

ref: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
text: A wan bo' mam pɛka, se'ere n sɔi la, a wan dikɛ la sɛla n de mam dinɛ, muulɛ bo' ya.

ref: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
text: Sɛla woo ti Sɔ la tara dela mam dinɛ. Bala zuo ti mam yeti, a wan dikɛ la sɛla n de mam dinɛ, muulɛ bo' ya la. Susageŋɔ wan lebege Nyɛɛma

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
text: “La han iŋɛ fiiwa, ya kan le nyɛ mam, gee la han le iŋɛ fiiwa ya wan nyɛ mam.”

ref: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
text: Ti a zahereba la basɛba yɛta taaba yeti, “De vuurɛ dela deni ti a yele tu yeti, ‘La han iŋɛ fiiwa ya kan le nyɛ mam, gee la han iŋɛ fiiwa ya wan le nyɛ mam.’ ‘Se'ere en sɔi la, mam lebere la Sɔ la zi'an’?”

ref: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
text: Ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, “Yetɔgebuna wa vuurɛ dela dinɛ ‘La han iŋɛ fiiwa’? Tu ka mi' en tɔgeri sɛla yele.”

ref: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
text: Yezu baŋɛ mɛ ti, ba boti ba soke en la se'ere, bala ti a yele ba yeti, “Ya sɔheri mɛ la taaba, de vuurɛ n de se'em, la mam yeti, ‘La han iŋɛ fiiwa, ya kan le nyɛ mam, gee la han iŋɛ fiiwa, ya wan le nyɛ mam la?’

ref: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti ya wan kele, gee si'im, gee ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba nyɛɛna. Ya suure wan sagum, gee ya susageŋɔ la wan ta lebege nyɛɛma.

ref: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
text: Pɔka han virege, la ni wuna ka mɛ, beni zuo, a dɔgum saŋa n paaɛ. Gee bia la han diŋɛ lu na, a ka le tɛra too la, beni zuo, a nyɛɛni mɛ la eŋa n tari asaala wa'an timpaberɛ wa zuo na la.

ref: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
text: Bala, la wuni ya mɛ nananawa, gee n wan le lebe ya zi'an na, ti ya suya pirɛ la nyɛɛma, ti nɛra ka bɔna wan to'e ya nyɛɛŋɔ la ya zi'an.

ref: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: De daarɛ, ya kan soke mam se'ere. Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, n yeti ya ti, ya han soke sɛla mam Sɔ la zi'an mam yu'urɛ puan, a wan dikɛ la bo' ya.

ref: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
text: Wa'am na na paaɛ zina, ya ka soke en sɛla mam yu'urɛ wa puan. Soke ya gee ya wan to'e, ti ya nyɛɛma la pirɛ paaɛ paaɛ. A zahereba la suma'ahum

ref: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
text: “Mam yeti ya yele ana wa me la yetɔgum biŋere puan. Gee saŋa la kini na, ti n kan le tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya yetɔge biŋere puan, gee n wan kɔ'ɔm tɔgera Sɔ la tuuntɔɔ bɔ'ɔra ya.

ref: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
text: De daarɛ ya wan soke sɛla woo la mam yu'urɛ puan. Mam ka yeti mam wan ze'ele ya na'arɛ soke sɛla Sɔ la zi'an bo' ya,

ref: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
text: se'ere en sɔi la, Sɔ la mɛŋa nɔŋɛ ya mɛ, la yamam n nɔŋɛ mam, gee nam ti mam ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na la.

ref: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
text: Mam ze'ele la Sɔ la zi'an sige timpaberɛ wa zuo na, gee le yeti n bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo, gee lebe n Sɔ la zi'an.”

ref: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
text: Ti a zahereba la yele en yeti, “Ɛ̃ɛ, fum nyaa tɔgeri la tuuntɔɔ, fum ka le tɔgera biŋera.

ref: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
text: Nananawa, tu mi' ti fum mi' sɛla woo, ka ɛɛra nɛra ti a soke fum se'ere. Ŋwana wa ti tumam nam ti fum ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na.”

ref: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
text: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, “Ya nyaa nam ya?

ref: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
text: Saŋa la kini na, ka pun paaɛ mɛ, ti ba wan dige ya ti ya yiregɛ, ti nɛra woo kule a yire a yire, gee bahɛ mam n ma'a. Gee la bala za'a, n kan bɔna n ma'a, beni zuo, Sɔ la n boi la mam.

ref: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
text: N yeti ya dina wa, ti ya tara suma'ahum mam puan. Han dɛna timpaberɛ wa zuo, ya wan nyɛ mɛlehegɔ. Gee da bahɛ ya ti dabeem tara ya, gee mam pun nyaŋɛ timpaberɛ wa mɛ.”

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
text: Yezu n tɔgɛ yele ana ba'ahɛ la, a gorege tɔ la saazuo, gee yeti, “N Sɔ, saŋa la paaɛ ya. Pɛgɛ fum Dayua wa, ti Dayua la me pɛgɛ hɔ.

ref: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
text: Fum n bo' mam kpe'eŋo ti n sɔna asaala woo, la n dikɛ vomkanba'ahɛ bo' sɛba woo ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la.

ref: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
text: Ŋwana n de vomkanba'ahɛ la, ti saaleba baŋɛ fum, fum se'em n de yelemɛŋerɛ Yinɛ wa ma'a, gee me baŋɛ Yezu Kiresta, se'em ti fum tum na la.

ref: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
text: Mam pɛgɛ hɔ timpaberɛ wa zuo, la mam ba'ahɛ tuunse'ere ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la.

ref: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
text: Bala nananawa n Sɔ, bahɛ ti n nyɛ pɛka fum nɛŋan. Pɛka sɛka ti n daa pun tara fum nɛŋan gee ti timpaberɛ wa baŋɛ naam la.

ref: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
text: “Mam dikɛ fum yu'urɛ wa pa'alɛ sɛba ti fum looe ba bo' mam timpaberɛ wa zuo la. Ba dela fum dinɛ, gee ti fum dikɛ ba bo' mam, ti ba me gurɛ fum yelehum la kankaŋi.

ref: They know that you gave me everything I have.
text: Nananawa, ba mi' suŋa ti sɛla woo ti fum dikɛ bo' mam sirum ze'ele la fum zi'an na.

ref: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, yelehum la ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la, mam dikɛ bo' ba mɛ, ti ba me to'e bu, gee mina yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti mam sirum ze'ele fum zi'an na. Ba me nam ya ti, fum n tum mam na.

ref: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
text: Mam pu'uhi bɔ'ɔra la bamam, Gee mam ka pu'uhi bɔ'ɔra timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba. Mam pu'uhi bɔ'ɔra la sɛba ti fum dikɛ bo' mam, ban de fum dinɛ la zuo.

ref: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
text: Mam bunɔ za'a dela fum bunɔ. ti fum bunɔ dɛna mam bunɔ. Bama puan ti mam nyɛ pɛka.

ref: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
text: Nananawa, mam kan le bɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo, gee bamam kelum bɔna la ka puan. Gee ti mam yɛ'ɛha fum zi'an na. N Sɔ Fa'a daana, gura ba fum yu'urɛ se'ere ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la puan, ti ba dɛna buyina wuu tumam n de buyina se'em la.

ref: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
text: Mam n boi la ba la, mam gu'uri ba mɛ la yu'urɛ la ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la. Mam gu'uri ba mɛ, ti ba ayima ka bɔregɛ, han dagena se'em ti la gã'a na ti a bɔregɛ ti gulehegɔ la wa'an asira na la.

ref: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
text: Gee nananawa, mam ye' la fum zi'an na, gee tɔgera yele ana timpaberɛ wa zuo, ti ba tara mam nyɛɛma la ti a pirɛ ba paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
text: Mam bo' ba fum yelehum la, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo yaaɛ ba, la bamam n dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, wuu mam n dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ se'em la.

ref: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
text: Mam ka pu'uheri ti fum fooe ba yehe timpaberɛ wa zuo, gee mam pu'uheri ti fum gu' ba suŋa suŋa be'em daana wa zi'an.

ref: They don't belong to this world, and neither do I.
text: Ba dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, wuu mam n dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ se'em la.

ref: Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours.
text: Pee ba ze'ele la yelemɛŋerɛ. Fum yelehum la n de yelemɛŋerɛ la.

text: Wuu fum n tum mam timpaberɛ wa zuo na se'em la, bala mɛa ti mam me tuna ba timpaberɛ wa puan.

ref: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
text: Bama zuo ti mam pee n mɛŋa, ti bamam me nyɛ peere yelemɛŋerɛ puan.

ref: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
text: “N ka pu'uhɛ bɔ'ɔra la bamam ma'a, gee sɛba woo me n wan ta nam mam doohe ba yelehum muulegɔ puan la.

ref: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
text: Ba za'a me dɛna buyina, wuu fum Sɔ wa n boi la mam se'em la, ti mam me bɔna la fum se'em la, bamam me bɔna la tu bala mɛa, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba nam ti fum n tum mam na.

ref: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.

ref: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
text: Mam bɔna la ba, ti fum bɔna la mam, ti bamam me naaɛ taaba bi'ɛ kahegɛ dɛna buyina, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba baŋɛ ti fum tum mam na, gee me nɔŋɛ ba wuu fum nɔŋɛ mam se'em la.

ref: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
