text: pɔhɛ Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la, tari bala ta paaɛ Yezu n wan zom saazuo la. Bana puan ayima n wan lagum pa'ahɛ tu puan lagum dɛna Yezu vo'ore la aze' nyɛ daana.”

ref: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
text: Bala zuo ti ba ku nuurɛ yehe buraahi siyi, kayima Yosefe, se'em ti ba yi'ira Basabase la, ti a yu'urɛ deyima dɛna Yusetuse la, la Matiyase.

ref: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
text: Gee nyaa pu'uhɛ yeti, “Daana, fum mi' nɛrawoo suure puan. Pa'alɛ tɔ buraahi siyi wa, ba se'em ti fum looe,

ref: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
text: ti a sooe ze'ele fum tuntuneba tuuma la fole la n gã' doohe la Yudasi n bire a poore bahɛ gee kiŋɛ a mɛŋa n boti zi'an la.”

ref: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
text: Ti ba bahɛ maanɛ kuga, ti la looe Matiyase, ti ba dikɛ en pa'ahɛ Yezu tuntuneba pia la ayima la puan.

ref: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
text: Azɔɛtole dapihinuu de'eŋo la, daarɛ la n ta paaɛ la, ba za'a lagehɛ bɔna la bɔbeyinɛ.

ref: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
text: Ba ta yeti ba bihɛ ti sɛla n ze'ele saazuo damena na wuu kusebego kãtɛ la, wa'am na pi' yire se'ere ti ba bɔna bini la tapi.

ref: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
text: Ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ ba za'a, ti ba pɔhɛ tɔgera yetɔgesaahi yima yima, wuu Fa'a Sia n bo' ba paŋa se'em la.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
text: Ti dameŋa kana wa bahɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la en lagehɛ na la, la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ se'ere n sɔi la, nɛrawoo n boi bini wuni ban tɔgeri la a tiŋa yetɔgum.

ref: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
text: Ti la iŋɛ pakerɛ gee ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba ti ba sɔkera taaba yeti, “Nɛrebana wa za'a dagi la Galiliihi?

ref: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
text: Gee ti la iŋɛ la ŋwani ti tumam woo n boi ka'lam wuna ti ba tɔgera a mɛŋa yetɔgum?

ref: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
text: Sɛba n ze'ele Patiya, Mɛdise, Ɛlamduma, la sɛba woo n de Mɛsopotemiya, Yudiya la Kapadosia, Pɔntusi la Esiya,

ref: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
text: Kereteduma la Arebiyaduma za'a yɛta yeti, tu wuni ban tɔgeri tu mɛhi tiŋa yetɔgum puan pa'ala Yinɛ tuunkãra yɛla.”

ref: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
text: Ti la iŋɛ ba za'a pakerɛ ti ba ze'a yɛli yɛta taaba yeti, “Yele dina wa vuurɛ dela bem?”

ref: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
text: Gee ti nɛreba basɛba me giila ba gee yɛta yeti, “Daam paalum ma'a n bala ti ba buge bala.” Pita tɔgɛ bo' nɛrekuuŋɔ

ref: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
text: Gee Pita n ze' la zahereba pia la ayima la n wum bala la, a zɛkɛ la a kua tɔgɛ bo' nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, “Yudiya nɛreba wa, la sɛba woo n boi Yerusalɛm wa, bahɛ ya ti n yele ya yele dina wa. Kelehe ya mam yeti se'ere wa.

ref: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, nɛreba wa ka buge wuu yan yeti se'em la. La nan dela gogo kaareŋa awai bulika.

ref: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
text: Aayi, ŋwana wa dela yele se'ere ti Nɔtɔgeha Yowɛle yuum purɛ biŋe yeti,

ref: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
text: ‘Yinɛ yeti, Ba'ahegɔ dabeha la puan, N wan ka'ɛ Sia la bahɛ saaleba woo zuo, ti ya dayɔɔhi la poyɔɔhi wan purɛ mam yelehum la, ti ya bumbile-paalehi nyɛta lilegere, ti ya buranya'ahi zaahena zaaheŋɔ.

ref: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
text: Pun ta ta' la mam da'aba, buraahi la pɔgehi za'a dabeha aŋa puan, n wan ka'ɛ n Sia ti ba puta.

ref: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
text: Gee n me wan tum daalehum saazuo wa ha, gee tum saŋa la daalehum timpaberɛ wa zuo, Ziim, la bugum la zu'uhi wan pi' tapi.

ref: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
text: Wuntɛɛŋa wan sobege ti ŋmarega dɛna ziim, gee ti Daana la dabehekãtɛ la, en de tɛrega dabeherɛ la nyaa paaɛ.

ref: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
text: La wan wa'am na tole ti fum se'em woo n yi' Daana la yu'urɛ la wan nyɛ faarɛ.’

ref: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
text: “Yamam sɛba n de Izirale buuri wa, kelehe ya mam n tari se'ere ti n yele wa. Yezu n de Nazareti buraa la, dela buraa n tari tuunɛ ti Yinɛ pun maahum en, gee tum daalehum a puan doohe paŋa, nyalema la daalehum ya tiŋasuka, ti la niiɛ pa'alɛ ya ti Yinɛ n sirum looe en wuu ya mɛhi n mi' se'em la,

ref: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
text: Buraa kana wa daa lu ya nu'uhen doohe la Yinɛ mɛŋa n pun maahum a mina de se'em la, ti ya daa kpa' en gee ku en, doohe la yan dikɛ en bu sɛba n ka mi' kiha la ti ba ku en la.

ref: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
text: Gee Yinɛ n vo'e en, gee yehe en kum puan bahɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, kum paŋa kan ta'am bobe en sɔna.

ref: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la Deviti yuum purɛ a yele yeti, ‘Mam nyɛti n Daana la saŋa woo ti a bɔna n nɛŋan. Se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa n boi zuɔ nu'o ti n ka gbɛ'ɛra.

ref: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
text: Bala zuo, mam suure wan nyɛɛna, gee ti mam zɛleŋa tara pɛka. Se'ere n gani za'a dela, mam iŋa wa wan vo'ohe gee tara beere puti'irɛ.

ref: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, fum Yinɛ kan zagehɛ n Sia bo' ki'intiŋa, bii fum bahɛ ti fum Fa'a daana wa wuu'ɛ.

ref: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
text: Fum pa'alɛ mam vom sore la mɛ, Fum wan bo' mam nyɛɛma doohe la fum n boi la mam la.’

ref: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
text: “N suurɔ Izirale buuri wa, mam wan yele ya gee ti dabeem ka tara mam yeti, tu sɔyaaba Deviti me yuum ki mɛ ti ba pi' en, ti a yɔɔ kelum gã'a la zina.

ref: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
text: Se'em ma'a ti a dina nɔtɔgeha la, ti a pun mina mɛ ti Yinɛ pɔ mɛ la pɔtɛ ti a wan bahɛ ti a yaaŋa kayima ta wa'am na sooe zi'ire na'am kuka la zuo.

ref: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
text: Deviti n nyɛti dina la zuo ti a tɔgɛ Kiresta vo'ore yele yeti, ‘A kan ta zagehɛ eŋa bo' ki'intiŋa la. Bii eŋa kɔba ta lagum wuu'ɛ.’

ref: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
text: Yezu kana wa, Yinɛ n vo'e en, yelese'ere ti tu za'a dɛna aze' nyɛ duma ti de sirum iŋɛ mɛ.

ref: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
text: En nyaa paaɛ zi'ire Yinɛ na'am paŋa kuka la zuo la, a to'e Fa'a Sia la ti Yinɛ n de Sɔ biŋe nuurɛ ti a wan bahɛ ti a wa'am na la, bala ti a ka'ɛ ŋwana wa bahɛ tumam zuo, ti ya za'a nini nyɛ ti ya tuba wum

ref: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
text: Se'ere n sɔi la Deviti yuum ka ihege zom saazuo, gee eŋa mɛŋa en yeti, Daana la yele mam Daana la yeti, “Zi'a n zuɔ nu'o wa,

ref: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
text: gura ti n bahɛ ti fum dinduma za'a ta lebege fum nama dafeere.”

ref: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
text: Bala zuo la, Izirale yire za'a, mina ya suŋa ti Yinɛ n iŋɛ en ti a dɛna Daana la Kiresta. Yezu sɛka ti ya kpa' la. Sɛba pɔhɛ tee ba suya

ref: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
text: Nɛreba la n wum ŋwana la, la ve'e ba mɛ ba suya puan ha, ti ba soke Pita la tuntuneba basɛba la yeti, “Tu suurɔ wa, tu nyaa wan iŋɛ la ŋwani?”

ref: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
text: Ti Pita yele ba yeti, Tee ya suya, ya za'a ayima ayima to'e ko'om sua Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan, ti Yinɛ di sugeri bo' ya be'em la, gee ti ya to'e Fa'a Sia la bo'olum la.

ref: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, Yinɛ n biŋe a nuurɛ ti a iŋɛ se'em la dela ya dinɛ la ya kɔma, ta ta' se'em woo n zaaɛ gee ti Daana la yi' en.

ref: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
text: Ti Pita dikɛ yɛla kekeko magehɛ pa'alɛ ba gee kpemehe ba yeti, “Faaɛ ya mɛhi be'em buuri saŋa wa puan bahɛ.”

ref: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
text: Ti nɛrebana wa kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' tuntuneba pa'alegɔ la, la lageŋɔ la taaba, lagum ɔbera boroboro, la pu'uhegɔ. Sakereba la vom la taaba

ref: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
text: Ti nɛra woo yari ya' nɔya, la daalehum la nyalema sɛ'a ti tuntuneba la tuna la.

ref: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
text: Ti sɛba woo n nam la za'a boi la bɔbeyinɛ ita sɛla woo buyina.

ref: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
text: Ba wan ni koohe ban tari sɛla, la ba lɔgerɔ, tari ligeri la wa'am na tɔrɛ bo' nɛra woo doohe la n boti sɛla.

ref: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
text: Beere en yilegeri woo, ba tari la saŋa zo'e zo'e bɔna pu'uhegɔ yire la puan zameha, tɔta boroboro la la taaba, gee dɔla taaba yiya dita la taaba la pupeelum la bu'a suya la taaba,

ref: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
text: pɛgera Yinɛ, ti ba puya pee la nɛra woo n nɔŋɛ ba se'em la. Ti beere n yilegeri woo, Daana la tara nɛreba pa'aha basɛba n nyɛ faarɛ wa lageŋɔ wa puan.

ref: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
text: Daarɛ deyima ti Pita la Yɔn yɛ'ɛha pu'uhegɔ yire la pu'uhegɔ saŋa, woo gogo kaareŋa butã wuntɛɛŋa bɔba.

ref: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
text: Ti nɛreba zi buraa ti ba dɔgɛ ka ti ka dɛna kɔɔreŋɔ ka bilɛɛmen ha tari na. Nɛreba n ni zi buraa wa tari na ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la nayaŋa sɛka ti ba yi'ira ka Nayansuŋa la nuuren, ti a zi'an bilam la a ta'am suha sɛba n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la daare woo.

ref: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
text: Buraa wa n ta nyɛ ti Pita la Yɔn boi bini ti ba kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire wa puan la, ti a suhɛ ba ti ba bo' en sɛla.

ref: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
text: Ti Pita kpa' en biha zii, ti Yɔn me biha en bala, ta yele buraa la yeti, “Biha tu na.”

ref: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
text: Ti Buraa wa kpa' biha, ba la ninmu'urɛ gee ti'iha la a wan to'e sɛla ba zi'an.

ref: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
text: Gee Pita yele en yeti, “Mam ka tari salempɛɛlega, bii salema, gee mam n tari sɛla n ŋwana ti mam dikera bɔ'ɔra fum wa. Yezu Kiresta n de Nazareti Buraa la yu'urɛ puan, ihege la fum kina.”

ref: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
text: Gee nyaa gurɛ kɔɔreŋɔ wa zuɔ nu'o ve'e en ihege ze'ele, ti bilam bilam ti paŋa wa'am kɔɔreŋɔ wa nama la a kigela puan na,

ref: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
text: ti a ɛkɛ saazuo lebe ze'ele, nyaa pɔhɛ kinɛ, nyaa doohe la Pita la Yɔn kɛ̃ Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, kina kaara, gee ɛgera la pupeelum gee pɛgera Yinɛ.

ref: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
text: Ti nɛreba la woo n nyɛ en ti a kini, gee pɛgera Yinɛ.

ref: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
text: Ba baŋɛ ti buraa kaŋa n bala ni zi'a Pu'uhegɔ yire la nayansuŋa la nuuren suhera la. Ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ ti ba zɛ'a yɛli la sɛla n iŋɛ buraa wa la. Pita tɔgɛ Solomɔn paka la puan

ref: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Buraa wa kɔ'ɔm gi' Pita la Yɔn la bala kankaŋi, gee ti nɛreba la za'a zɔta yɛ'a ba zi'an, pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ze'sɛka ti ba yi'ira Solomɔn zinzaka la puan, ti la dɛna yele pakerɛ bo' ba.

ref: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
text: Pita n nyɛ nɛreba wa bala la, a yele ba yeti. “Yamam Izirale buuri wa, ŋwani ti yele dina wa iŋɛ ya pakerɛ, bii ŋwani ti ya biha tumam wuu la dela tumam paŋa bii tu Yinɛ zoolum puan ti buraa wa ihege kina wa?

ref: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
text: La dela Aberaham Yinɛ la, Aziki Yinɛ la, la Yakɔbe Yinɛ la. Tu sɔyaabeduma Yinɛ la en na'ahɛ a dayua Yezu, se'em ti ya daa yehe en tihɛ, ze'ele Palete nɛŋan zagehɛ en, la Palete n daa pun iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a bahɛ en la za'a.

ref: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
text: Yamam zagehɛ la Fa'a la tuuntɔɔ daana la gee tugum soke ti ba bo' ya nɛrekuura,

ref: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
text: ya tugum ku la vom daana la se'em ti Yinɛ vo'e en kum puan la. Yele dina wa ti tumam dɛna aze' nyɛ duma.

ref: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
text: La doohe la sakerɛ a yu'urɛ puan ti buraa wa ti ya nyɛta gee mina ka wa dɛna ka mɛŋa wa. Sakerɛ a yu'urɛ puan la en tibɛ en za'a za'a ya woo ninin wa.

ref: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
text: “Nananawa n suurɔ, mam mi' ti ya daa tum la yɛmka'alum, wuu ya nɛhi duma la me n daa tum yɛmka'alum se'em la.

ref: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
text: Ŋwana puan ti Yinɛ nɔtɔgeheba la en yuum purɛ yeti, a Kiresta la wan namehɛ la wa'am asira na.

ref: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
text: Bala zuo la, tee ya, la ya ŋmɛregɛ ya mɛhi bo' Yinɛ, ti a piiɛ ya be'em woo bahɛ,

ref: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
text: ti fula fula saŋa ze'ele Daana mɛŋa zi'an sige ya zuo, ti a tum Kiresta se'em ti a looe bo' ya la, ti a wa'am ya zi'an na, se'em n de Yezu la.

ref: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
text: Yezu se'em n wan bɔna saazuo gura sɛla woo maalegɔ seko ti Yinɛ tɔgɛ dɛ yuuman yuuman ha doohe a tuulum nɔtɔgeheba puan la.

ref: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
text: Moose yuum yeti, ‘Ya Daana Yinɛ la a mɛŋa wan yehe nɛra ya mɛŋa puan, ti a dɛna a nɔtɔgeha wuu mam wa. Kelehe ya sɛlawoo ti a wan yele ya.

ref: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
text: La wan ta dɛna se'em la dela, fum se'em woo n ka ta kɛleha nɔtɔgeha kaŋa la, ba wan vuuɛ n nɛreba la puan bahɛ.’

ref: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
text: Nɔtɔgeheba wa woo n zo'e se'em la, ze'ele Samuwɛle saŋa ta paaɛ sɛba n wa'am a pooren na la woo yuum purɛ dabeha wa yele mɛ.

ref: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
text: Ya dela nɔtɔgeheba wa yaahi, la nɔpu'ulegɔ la ti Yinɛ yuum pu'ulɛ la ya yaabeduma la Aberaham yeti, ‘Fum yaŋa puan ti buuri woo n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a wan nyɛ sum.’

ref: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
text: Yinɛ n wa looe a yameŋa wa la, a pɔhɛ tum en la yamam zi'an ti ya nyɛ sum, doohe la en wan bahɛ ti yamam ayima ayima tee ya suya gee yehe ya be'em tuuma la puan la.”

ref: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
text: Pita la Yɔn n ze' tɔgera bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la, ti Pu'uhegɔ nɛŋa daana, la kpɛ'ɛma se'em n biheri pu'uhegɔ yire la, la Sadusiiduma wa'am ba zi'an na,

ref: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
text: ti ba suure nyiie la ban pa'ali nɛreba la gee muula yeti Yezu puan a ki gee ihege boi mɛ la.

ref: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti ba nyɔgɛ ba ta pagɛ yiimbeere se'ere n sɔi la yinɛ daa pun lu mɛ.

ref: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
text: Gee sɛba n wum yelehum la zo'e zo'e nam bu mɛ, ti ba kãlɛ dɛna wuu buraahi tuha anuu.

ref: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
text: Ti la iŋɛ ti yiimbeere la, ti naduma, kiinduma la gɔnmi'ineba la lagehɛ taaba Yerusalɛm puan,

ref: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
text: ti Annas n de malema kiima, la Kaifase, Yɔn, la Alɛgezanda la sɛba woo n de malema kiima yireduma la za'a wa'am na.

ref: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
text: Ban yu'ɛ ba na ti ba zɛ'a ba nɛŋan la, ti ba soke ba yeti, “Beni paŋa, bii yu'deni puan ti ya nyaŋɛ tum ŋwana wa?”

ref: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
text: Ŋwana ti Pita n pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la yele ba yeti, “Naduma n sɔi nɛreba, la kiinduma wa,

ref: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
text: Ya han sɔkera tumam mɛ zina wa doohe la yelesunɛ en tum bo' nɛra n bɛ̃'ɛri, ti ya sɔkera lan iŋɛ se'em ti buraa wa nyɛ ka tibegɔ,

ref: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
text: baŋɛ ya zina, yamam za'a, la Izirale buuri za'a, ti buraa wa n ze' ya nɛŋan dɛna ka mɛŋa wa, dela Yezu Kiresta n de Nazareti buraa la yu'urɛ la puan. Se'em ti yamam yuum kpa', gee ti Yinɛ vo'e en a kum la puan la.

ref: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
text: Yezu wa dela ‘Kugere se'ere ti yamam mɛteba la zagehɛ la, n nyaa de ɛ̃berɛ kugere kãtɛ.’

ref: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
text: Faarɛ ka boi se'em puan, se'ere n sɔi la, yu'se'ere ka boi sagbanɛ wa tiŋa bo' nɛresaaleba ti ba wan yi' de nyɛ faarɛ.”

ref: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
text: Nɛreba wa n nyɛ Pita la Yɔn sukpe'ene la, gee me baŋɛ ti ba dagi gɔnmi'ineba gee ba yeen dɛna la nɛreba la, ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ, gee ti ba le baŋɛ ti ba ni bɔna mɛ la Yezu.

ref: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
text: Ban nyɛ ti buraa la n nyɛ ka tibegɔ la ze' la ba la, ba ka tari se'ere ti ba wan yele de puan.

ref: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
text: Bala ti ba bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba yehe kiinduma la nɛŋan fɔregɛ gee ti bamam suhɛ yele la la taaba bihɛ.

ref: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
text: Ti ba nyaa yele taaba yeti, “Beni ti tu wan dikɛ iŋɛ ba? Se'ere n sɔi la, la ka sugɛ nɛrawoo n boi Yerusalɛm wa ninin ti ba tum la daalehum n de yelemɛŋerɛ. Tu kan ta'a sugɛ dɛ.

ref: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
text: Gee tumam n ka boti ti de yiregɛ zi'an woo la, bahɛ ya ti tu ka'am ba la ninmu'urɛ ti ba da le dikɛ yu'dina wa tɔgɛ zi'an, nɛreba tiŋasuka.”

ref: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
text: Ŋwana ti ba nyaa yi' Pita la Yɔn lebena, yele ba la ninmu'urɛ ti ba da le tɔgɛ bii pa'alɛ Yezu yu'urɛ la puan le yɛ'ɛha.

ref: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
text: Gee Pita la Yɔn yele ba yeti, “Lebehe ya bo' ya mɛhi nyaa, la ani suŋa Yinɛ nɛŋan ti tu kɛlehi la yamam bii Yinɛ? Gakɛ ya.

ref: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, tumam kan nyaŋɛ mum tu nɔya kan tɔgɛ sɛla ti tu nini nyɛ, ti tu tuba wum.”

ref: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, buraa wa ti daalehum tum ka puan la gani yuumpihinaahi mɛ. Sɛba en nam la pu'uhi bɔta sukpe'ene

ref: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
text: Ban bahɛ ba la, ti ba kiŋɛ ba pu'uhi taaba la zi'an, ta yele ba malema kiinduma la, la kiinduma la en yele ba se'em.

ref: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: Ba taaba la n wum la, ba za'a zɛkɛ la ba kuuhi buyina, kaahɛ bo' Yinɛ yeti, “Gaŋeza'a Daana se'em n naam saazuo la timpaberɛ wa, ateko la sɛla woo n boi bu puan,

ref: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
text: la dela fum n doohe Fa'a Sia la puan, doohe tu yaaba Deviti n de fum yameŋa la puan yeti, ‘Ŋwani ti sɛba n dagi Yuuduma wa suya nyiie, ti nɛreba la ti'iha yeleyooro?

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
text: Tiŋa wa zuo na'duma vaaɛ ihege mɛ, Tiŋa wa zuo kpɛ'ɛnduma maahum mɛ zɛ'a ti ba zabɛ la Daana la, ti ba zabɛ la a Kiresta la.’

ref: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, tinkãtɛ wa puan, Hɛrɔd la Pɔntiyɔsi Palete ta ta' la sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la, la sɛba en de Izirale nɛreba la lagehɛ la taaba ti ba zabɛ la fum Fa'a yameŋa Yezu, se'em ti fum bi'ihɛ en;

ref: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
text: ti a tum sɛla woo ti fum nu'o la fum puti'irɛ pun diŋɛ maahum ti la tum la.

ref: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
text: Nananawa tu Daana, bihɛ ba vuuhego wa, gee la fum bo' fum yamehi wa sore ti ba muulɛ fum yelehum la sukpe'ene,

ref: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
text: gee ti fum mɛŋa tiɛ fum nu'o la fum tibɛ, gee ti nyalema la daalehum tuna Fa'a yameŋa yu'urɛ Yezu wa puan.”

ref: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
text: Ban pu'uhɛ la, zi'an la ti ba lagehɛ bɔna la miim mɛ, Ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ ba za'a ti ba tɔgɛ Yinɛ yelehum la la sukpe'ene. Sakereba la tɔrɛ ba lɔgerɔ la taaba

ref: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
text: Nananawa, Lan de se'em la dela, sakereba la za'a tari la suyinɛ la putɛ'yinɛ la taaba, ti ba se'em ka sɔna a lɔgeseko bii en tari sɛla, gee ba se'em woo n tari sɛla dela ba za'a bunɔ.

ref: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
text: Ti Yezu tuntuneba la nyɛ pankãtɛ, muulɛ ban nyɛ sɛla la doohe Daana Yezu ihegere la, ti zu'uhegɔ kãtɛ bɔna ba za'a zuo.

ref: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
text: Naha ka boi ba puan, se'ere n sɔi la, basɛba zo'e zo'e n tari samana bii yiya koohe a mɛ tari ligeri la na.

ref: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
text: Ba tari i na biŋe la Yezu tuntuneba la nɛŋan, ti ba tari i tɔta bɔ'ɔra nɛrawoo n ka tari sɛla.

ref: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
text: Gee buraa kayina yu'urɛ n yi'iri Ananiya naaɛ la ka pɔga Safira, koohe ba samanɛ,

ref: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
text: ti a pɔga la mina, ti a welege ligeri la i se'ehi biŋe gee tari se'ehi n gee la na biŋe tuntuneba la nɛŋan.

ref: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
text: Ti Pita soke “Ananiya” yeti, “Ŋwani ti Sagena la pirɛ fum suure paaɛ bala ti fum parum bo' Fa'a Sia, doohe fum aaɛ samanɛ la ti fum koohe la ligeri i se'ehi biŋe la?

ref: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
text: Samanɛ la n nan ka koohe ni la, de dagi ni la fum mɛŋa dinɛ, fum le ta koohe de la, ligeri la ka boi la fum nu'uhen? La iŋɛ la ŋwani ti fum ta'am ti'ihɛ yele dina wa fum suuren? Fum ka parum bo' la asaala, gee Yinɛ.”

ref: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
text: Ananiya n wum yetɔgebuna wa la, a kɔ'ɔm lu mɛ ki. Ti dabeem kãtɛ sãɛ sɛba woo me n wum la.

ref: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
text: Ti budibe paalehi la paaɛ pɔbɛ en, zi en yehe ta pi'.

ref: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
text: Ti la iŋɛ wuu gogo kaareŋa atã la, ti a pɔga la me kiŋɛ kɛ̃ na, ka baŋɛ sɛla en iŋɛ.

ref: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
text: Ti Pita soke en yeti, “Yele mam, fum la fum sira la koohe ya samanɛ la ligeri la ŋwani la ŋwani.” Ti a yeti, “Ɛ̃ɛ, tumam n koohe de se'em n bala.”

ref: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
text: Ti Pita yele en yeti, “La iŋɛ la ŋwani ti yamam ku nuurɛ la taaba ti ya magehɛ Daana la Sia wa bihɛ? Bihɛ, sɛba n pi' fum sira nama n bala bɔna kuleŋa nuuren wa, bamam mɛa n wan zi fum yehe.”

ref: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text: Bilam bilam ti a lu Pita nɛŋan ki, budibe paalehi la n kɛ̃ nyɛ en ti a ki mɛ la, ti ba zi eŋa me yehe ta pi' en lɛm asira la.

ref: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
text: Ti dabeem kãtɛ sãɛ lageŋɔ la, la sɛba woo n wum yele ana wa. Tuntuneba tibɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ

ref: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Daalehum la nyalema la kekeko en tuni nɛreba la puan, doohe tuntuneba la puan. Ti ba za'a lagehɛ bɔna pu'uhegɔ yire la ze'sɛka ti ba yi'ira ka Solomɔn zinzaka la puan.

ref: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
text: Gee nɛreba la, la ayima me ka makɛ dikɛ a mɛŋa pa'ahɛ tuntuneba la puan, gee nɛreba la za'a na'aheri ba mɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
text: Gee nɛreba n tugum malum nam bo' Daana la, ti buraahi la pɔgehi kãlɛ zo'e,

ref: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
text: ti nɛreba ni zi bã'duma ta biŋe sore nuuren la ba kugehi bii ba sunɔ zuto, ti Pita en tole la a nyilenyileho wan doohe ba zuto tole ti ba nyɛ ba tibegɔ.

ref: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
text: Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e me ni ze'ele tinse'ehi n boi Yerusalɛm bɔba, tari bã'duma la sɛba ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi mɛleha ba, ti ba za'a nyɛ ba tibega. Tuntuneba la mɛlehegɔ

ref: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
text: Ti malema kiima la vaaɛ ihege, eŋa la sɛba woo n boi la en la n pirɛ la nyuure la,

ref: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
text: nyɔgɛ tuntuneba la pagɛ ba paka deto puan.

ref: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
text: Gee yu'uŋɔ, ti Yinɛ suŋera wa'am na yu'ɛ paka deo kulehi la tari ba yehe na, gee yele ba yeti,

ref: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
text: “Kiŋɛ ya ta ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la ya yele nɛreba la vom bɔna wa yelehum la za'a.”

ref: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
text: Ban wum ŋwana la, ba kiŋɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la la bulika, ta kɛ̃ pɔhɛ ba yelehum pa'alegɔ. Malema nɛŋa daana la, la sɛba woo n boi la en la n paaɛ na la, ba yi' la kiinduma la za'a, la sɛba woo n de kima Izirale buuri puan la lagehɛ na, gee nyaa tum ti ba kiŋɛ paka deo la puan ta tari tuntuneba la na.

ref: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
text: Gee sɛba n guri pu'uhegɔ yire la n kiŋɛ paka deo la puan la, ba ka nyɛ ba bini, ti ba lebena yele yeti,

ref: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
text: “Tumam paaɛ ti paka deo kulehi la yu' mɛ kankaŋi, ti gureba la me zɛ'a kulehi la nuuren, gee tumam n yu'ɛ kulehi la, tumam ka nyɛ se'em bini.”

ref: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
text: Sɛba n biheri pu'uhegɔ yire la kpɛ'ɛma la, la malema kiinduma la n wum ŋwana wa la, ba maam mɛ la ba, ti'ihera nyaa beni n tugum ita wa.

ref: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
text: Ti nɛra ayima n bala paaɛ na muula yeti, “Bihɛ ya, buraahi la ti ya pagɛ ba la n bala zɛ'a pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ha pa'ala nɛreba la.”

ref: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
text: Ti sɛba n guri pu'uhegɔ yire la kpɛ'ɛma la pu'uhegɔ yire gureba la kiŋɛ ta tari ba bããlam wa'am na, se'ere n sɔi la, ba zoti mɛ ti nɛreba la wan lobe bamam.

ref: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
text: Ban tari ba paaɛ na la, ba bahɛ ti ba ze'ele la malema duma kima la nɛŋan. Ti malema nɛŋa duma la soke ba

ref: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
text: yeti, “Tu daa bo' ya nuurɛ la ninmu'urɛ yeti, ya da le tara yu'urɛ wa pa'ala, gee bihɛ yamam n bala pirɛ Yerusalɛm wa la ya pa'alegɔ wa, kɔ'ɔm lu' ti ya dikɛ buraa wa ziim ka'arɛ pagelɛ tumam zuo.”

ref: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
text: Gee Pita la tuntuneba la lebehe ba yeti, “Tumam wan nam la Yinɛ nyana saala kpe'eŋo.

ref: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
text: Tu yaabeduma Yinɛ la n vo'e Yezu a kum la puan, se'em ti ya daa yule en tia la zuo ku la.

ref: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
text: Yinɛ zɛkɛ en mɛ ta zi'ire a zuɔ nu'o ti a dɛna Nɛŋa Daana, la Faara, ti a bo' Izirale suteere la yelebe'ero sugeri.

ref: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
text: Ti tumam n dɛna aze' nyɛ duma bo' yele ana wa, la Fa'a Sia ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' sɛba n nane en la.”

ref: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
text: Ban wum ŋwana la, ba suya n nyiie gaŋɛ, ti ba ku nuurɛ la taaba ti ba ku ba.

ref: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
text: Gee Farasii ayima n lagum bɔna kiinduma la puan ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Gamaliyɛl, pa'ala Moose pa'alegɔ la, ti nɛreba za'a na'ahera en. Eŋa n ihege ze'ele bo' nuurɛ ti ba bahɛ ti tuntuneba la yehe yiŋa fii wa.

ref: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
text: Ti a nyaa yele ba yeti, “N suurɔ Izirale taaba wa, ti'ihɛ ya suŋa yan yeti ya iŋɛ nɛreba wa se'em.

ref: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
text: Eŋa pooren, ti Yudasi n de Galiliiŋa me yuum ihege na, nɛreba kãalegɔ saŋa la, ti nɛreba me yuum dɔla en, ti eŋa me ta ki, ti a dɔleba la za'a yiregɛ.

ref: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
text: Bala, nananawa, yeledina wa puan, mam yeti ya me wa, ti ya fooe ya nu'o buraahi wa puan gee bahɛ ba ba ma'a. Se'ere n sɔi la, yele dina wa bii tuun-dina wa han dɛna la asaala puti'irɛ, de wan bahɛ yoo.

ref: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
text: Gee de han dɛna la Yinɛ puti'irɛ yamam kan ta'am gu' ba. Ya han yeti ya iŋɛ bala, ya wan ta nyɛ ti ya zaberɛ mɛ la Yinɛ mɛŋa!” Ti ba bɔkɛ a yetɔgum la.

ref: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
text: Ti ban yi' tuntuneba la kɛ̃ na la, ba bahɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' ba mɛ, gee ti ba nyaa bo' ba nuurɛ yeti ba da le tɔgɛ se'ere Yezu yu'urɛ la puan, gee nyaa bahɛ ba ti ba kiŋɛ.

ref: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
text: Ban baheri kiinduma la zi'an la, ba puurɛ pee mɛ ti bamam me ti ba diihe ba vi Yezu yu'urɛ la zuo.

ref: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Ti daarɛ woo, pu'uhegɔ yire la puan bii ba mɛhi yiya puan, ba ka go'ore pa'alegɔ, bii ba muula ti Yezu n de Kiresta la.

ref: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
text: Dabeha aŋa puan la, zahereba la kãlɛ n wa pa'aha la, ti Hiburuduma sɛba n tɔgeri Giriki la la nyɔni mɛ la ba Hiburuduma la mɛŋa, doohe la ban ka bo'ori Hiburuduma sɛba n tɔgeri Giriki la pokɔpa la ba daare woo dia tɔtɛ la.

ref: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
text: Ti zahereba pia la bayi' la yi' sɛba n nam la za'a lagehɛ, yele ba yeti, “La dagi ma'a bo' tumam, ti tu bahɛ Yinɛ yelehum la muulegɔ gee yee dia tɔrega.

ref: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
text: Bala tu suurɔ wa, looe ya buraahi siyopɔi ya puan n de tuuntɔɔ, ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ ba, dɛna yɛmduma, tari na bo' tu, ti tu bo' ba sore ti ba biha yele ana wa,

ref: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
text: gee tumam me, wuu tu tuni n de se'em la, tu wan kaabɛ tu mɛhi saŋa woo bo' pu'uhegɔ, la yelehum muulegɔ.”

ref: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
text: Ti ban yele se'em la pee nɛreba la puurɛ, ti ba looe Siteven, n de buraa n pirɛ la sakerɛ la Fa'a Sia la, gee me looe Filipi, Porokoruse, Nikano, Timɔn, Pamenase, la Nikolayuse se'em n yehe Antiyoke se'em n dagi Yuda buuri gee lebege Yuda buuri la.

ref: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
text: Ti ba bahɛ ti buraahi sina wa ze'ele tuntuneba la nɛŋan, ti ba pu'uhɛ, gee dikɛ ba nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto.

ref: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
text: Ti Yinɛ yelehum la kɔ'ɔm yiregera, ti zahereba la kãlɛ malum pa'ahera Yerusalɛm puan, ti malema duma la zo'e zo'e to'e sakerɛ la. Siteven nyɔkerɛ

ref: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
text: Siteven pirɛ me la zu'uhegɔ la paŋa, tuna daalehum la nyalema kãra bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la.

ref: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
text: Ŋwana ti sɛba n boi zamehegɔ yire la puan ti ba yi'ira a boi fai pa'alegɔ yire la puan la , Sereniduma, Alɛgezanda duma, la sɛba n me yehe Kilikiya, la Esiya na la, daa vaaɛ ihege la nɔkpe'ene la Siteven

ref: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
text: Gee ba ka nyaŋɛ ze'ele la yɛmsebo la Sɛsɛka n boi la en ti a tɔgera la.

ref: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
text: Ti ba iŋɛ bããlam ɛbegɛ buraahi sise'ehi ti ba yeti, “Tumam wum mɛ ti a tɔgɛ tu' Moose la Yinɛ.”

ref: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
text: Ti ba bahɛ ti nɛreba la gbirege, ta ta' kiinduma, la gɔnmi'ineba la, ti la ka yuue ti ba kaaɛ Siteven mum, nyɔkɛ en, tari en wa'am kiinduma zi'an na.

ref: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
text: Gee nyaa maahum aze' nyɛ pumparenduma, ti ba yehe na yeti, “Buraa wa kan malum bahɛ ka tɔgera tuura tuulum zi'an wa la Ze'elego la.

ref: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam wum ti a yeti, Yezu n de Nazareti buraa la wan wa'am na sagum ze'kana wa, gee malum teehum tu malema sɛ'a ti Moose dikɛ bahɛ tu la.”

ref: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
text: Ti sɛba woo n boi kiinduma suhega zi'an la puan la biha Siteven nɛŋa zii, ta nyɛ ti a nɛŋa la tee me ana woo Suŋera nɛŋa la.

ref: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
text: Ŋwana ti malema nɛŋadaana la soke Siteven yeti, “Alaa, yele ana wa za'a dela asira ti fum yele la bala?”

ref: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
text: Ti Siteven lerege yeti, “N suurɔ la n sɔduma, kelehe ya mam. Yinɛ en de suŋa Yinɛ la yuum niiɛ la de mɛŋa bo' tu sɔyaaba Aberaham, se'em ma'a ti a bɔna Mɛsopotemiya, se'em ma'a ti a nan ka nɔgɛ kiŋɛ Haran la, yeti,

ref: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
text: ‘Bahɛ fum tiŋa wa la fum nɛreba, gee kiŋɛ tiŋa kayima ti n yeti n pa'alɛ hɔ.’

ref: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
text: Bala ti Aberaham bahɛ Kalediya duma tiŋa wa, gee kiŋɛ ta zi'ire Haran la. Ti a sɔ en ta ki la, ti Yinɛ ihege en bilam wa'am tinsɛka ti ya bɔna ka puan zina wa puan na.

ref: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
text: Yinɛ yuum ka dikɛ tinkana wa zi'an bɔba bo' Aberaham ti ka dɛna a dinɛ, pun dagena wuu na'arɛ tagelegɔ beene. Gee Yinɛ yuum biŋe la nuurɛ ti a wan dikɛ ka bo' eŋa la a yaahi se'ehi n wan ta wa'am na, la Aberaham n yuum pun ka tara bia la.

ref: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
text: Yinɛ yuum tɔgɛ bo' en yeti, “A yaahi wan ta dɛna saama bɔna nɛreba basɛba tiŋa, ti ba dikɛ ba lebege ba da'aba, mugehe ba yuuma kɔbehi sinaahi.”

ref: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
text: ‘Gee n wan gakɛ tinse'ehi ti ba dɛna ba da'aba la.’ Yinɛ n yele se'em n bala. ‘N han ta gakɛ tihi la ba'ahɛ fum yaahi la nyaa wan ze'ele si puan yehe na, la ba na'aha mam tinkana wa puan.’

ref: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
text: Ti Yinɛ ku ŋmaa nuurɛ la n. Bala zuo ti Aberaham n ta dɔgɛ Aziki la, a ŋmaaɛ bia la dɔgum dabeha anii daarɛ mɛ, ti Aziki me n ta dɔgɛ Yakɔbe, ti Yakɔbe me ta dɔgɛ ba yaabeduma pia la bayi' la.

ref: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
text: “Ti yaabeduma wa looe Yosefe ba puan nyuuna la en, ta koohe en bu Igipiti. Gee Yinɛ boi la en mɛ,

ref: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
text: gee faaɛ en pɔgeleŋɔ woo puan bahɛ, gee bahɛ en ti a nyɛ zu'uhegɔ, gee bo' en yɛm Igipiti naba Faro nɛŋan, se'em n wa looe Yosefe ti a sɔna Igipiti za'a la eŋa naba la yire za'a biha.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
text: Ti la bɔna bɔna ti kom pɛɛlega ta sige Igipiti la Keenan tihi puan, la namehegɔ kãtɛ, ti tu yaabeduma ka nyɛ dia zi'an zi'an.

ref: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
text: Gee Yakɔbe n ta wum ti ki boi Igipiti la, a tum la tu yaabeduma wa ti ba kiŋɛ ba yia kinɛ.

ref: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
text: Ba yi'i puan la ti Yosefe niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ a kiinduma wa, ti naba Faro me baŋɛ Yosefe yire buuri wa.

ref: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
text: Lan iŋɛ se'em ti Yakɔbe la tu yaabeduma basɛba la kiŋɛ Igipiti n bala. Yakɔbe ta ki la bilam, la tu yaabeduma basɛba la me,

ref: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
text: Ti ba zi ba lebe Sɛkɛm na, pi' ba yɔɔ seko puan ti Aberaham yuum da' la salema pɛɛlega Hamɔn dayɔɔhi la zi'an Sɛkɛm puan la.

ref: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
text: “Gee saŋa la n ta pɔra ti Yinɛ tum a nɔbiŋere la ti a biŋe la Aberaham la, ti nɛreba wa en boi Igipiti bala ma'a la ba' wi'ɛ mɛ,

ref: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
text: tara tara ti naba ka yima n ka mi' Yosefe yele, wa'am na zi'ire Igipiti na'am kuka la zuo,

ref: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
text: tara yɛm kirega dugera tu buuri wa, pɛregɛ tu yaabeduma ti ba han ni dɔgɛ bia, ba bahɛ ka ti ka ta ki.

ref: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
text: Sankana puan ti ba dɔgɛ Moose, ti a dɛna bia n sum Yinɛ Nɛŋan. Ŋmarehi sitã ti bia wa bɔna ka sɔ yire,

ref: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
text: ti ban daa ta tari en ta bahɛ la, Faro mɛŋa poyua n nyɛ en, dikɛ en bihera, ti a ta zo'e na dɛna a mɛŋa dayua.

ref: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
text: Bala zuo la, ba yuum pa'alɛ Moose mɛ la Igipiti yɛm la za'a, ti a zo'e na, tara paŋa a yetɔgum puan la a tuuma puan.

ref: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
text: En wa'am na nyɛ ti ba tari ba ayima ŋmɛ'ɛra la, a lerege en mɛ, ŋmɛ' Igipiti nɛra la ku.

ref: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
text: Moose ti'ihɛ ti a buuri la wan baŋɛ ti Yinɛ wan doohe eŋa puan faaɛ ba bahɛ, gee a buuri la ka bɔkɛ de bala.

ref: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
text: Yiimbeere ti a le kiŋɛ na paaɛ ti a buuri la bayi' zaberɛ, ti a zigɛ ti a sugelum ba, nyaa yɛta ba yeti, ‘N suurɔ wa, mini ya ti ya dela suuɔ. Ŋwani ti ya pɔgelena taaba?’

ref: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
text: Ti se'em n ŋmɛ'ɛri ni a tadaana la za'ɛ Moose bahɛ ha, gee yele en yeti, ‘Ani n iŋɛ hɔ ti fum biha tumam, gee gakera tɔ?

ref: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
text: Fum boti fum ku mam me mɛ wuu fum zaam ku Igipiti nɛra la se'em la?’

ref: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
text: Moose n wum ŋwana la, a zɔ mɛ loŋe Midiya, ta dɛna saana bilam. Bilam ti a dɔgɛ dayɔɔhi siyi.

ref: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
text: Ti yuuma pihinaahi ta tole, ti Yinɛ Suŋera ta niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ en Muɔ puan ha, Sinai Zoore la ha, ti tuntuo dita gee ka taara.

ref: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
text: Moose n nyɛ ŋwana la, la iŋɛ en la pakerɛ la sɛla ti a nyɛta la, ti en kiŋɛ paara ti a bihɛ la, ti Daana la kua yi' en yeti,

ref: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
text: ‘Mam n de fum yaabeduma Yinɛ la. Aberaham Yinɛ, Aziki la Yakɔbe Yinɛ la.’ Ti Moose iŋa pɔhɛ gbɛ'ɛra, gee ti a ka le sakɛ ti a le bihɛ.

ref: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
text: Ti Daana la yele en yeti, ‘Pirege fum tagera la ti fum pire la, se'ere n sɔi la, ze'sɛka ti fum zɛ'a wa dela Tuulum zi'an.

ref: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
text: Mam nini sirum nyɛ mɛlehegɔ seko ti mam nɛreba wa bɔna Igipiti mɛleha la, gee me wum ba uhega, bala ti n sige na ti n faaɛ ba. Wa'am na nananawa na, n wan tum hɔ Igipiti.’

ref: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
text: “Moose kana n bala ti ba daa ka nam gee soke en yeti, ‘Ani n iŋɛ hɔ ti fum biha tumam gee gakera tu la?’ Moose kaŋa n bala ti Yinɛ tum en ti a sɔna biha gee dɛna faara, doohe Suŋera la n niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ en muuen ha la.

ref: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
text: Ti a tari ba yehe na, se'em ma'a ti a damɛ Igipiti, la mɔgemɔlegɔ la puan, la kpaahenɛ la puan la daalehum nyalema yuuma pihinaahi.

ref: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
text: Moose kana n bala yuum yele Izirale buuri wa yeti, ‘Yinɛ wan looe a nɔtɔgeha ya mɛŋa buuri wa puan bo' ya wuu en looe mam se'em la.’

ref: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
text: Eŋa n bala wa bɔna Izirale lageŋɔ la puan muuen ha la, la suŋera se'em n yuum tɔgɛ bo' Moose Sinai Zoore la zuo la, gee daa bɔna la tu yaabeduma, ti a to'e vom pa'alegɔ na bo' tu.

ref: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
text: Tu yaabeduma la yuum ka maahum ti ba nana en, gee tugum za'ɛ en bahɛ kinkɛleŋa, gee ti ba suya puan, ba yuum lebe la Igipiti puan,

ref: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
text: Sankaŋa ti ba kure nagebila, kɔrum duhi kaabɛ ka, gee na'ahɛ sɛla ti ba dikɛ ba nu'uhi kure la.

ref: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
text: Ŋwana ti Yinɛ ŋmɛregɛ gee bahɛ ba, gee dikɛ ba bo' saazuo ŋmarebibehi wa kaabegɔ, wuu lan gulehɛ nɔtɔgeheba gɔŋɔ la puan yele se'em la. La yeti, ‘Yamam yuum kɔreni ya duhi la gee kaabera la mam yuuma pihinaahi muɔ la puan ha la, Izirale yire duma wa?

ref: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
text: Aayi, yan daa yeheri la, ya daa tari Mɔlɛki bagerɛ la mɛ la ya, gee tara ŋmarebila bagerɛ la n yi'iri Arafan la, bage se'ere ti ya kure na'aha la. Bala la, n wan bahɛ ti ya kiŋɛ zaaɛ gana Babilɔn.’

ref: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
text: “Tu sɔyaabeduma la yuum ziiri tuulum sugerɛ la mɛ la ba muuen la ha, wuu Yinɛ n yuum pa'alɛ Moose se'em la, gee yuum bo' en nuurɛ ti a maalɛ tuulum sugerɛ la wuu eŋa n pa'alɛ en se'em la.

ref: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
text: Tu yaabeduma la me yuum tari tuulum sugerɛ wa doohe la Yosua kɛ̃ na, se'em ma'a ti ba yuum ta nyaŋɛ tihi se'ehi woo ti Yinɛ yuum dige si nɛreba tu yaabeduma wa ninin la. Ti tuulum sugerɛ wa yuum bɔna la bilam tara tara ta paaɛ Naba Deviti saŋa,

ref: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
text: se'em ti Yinɛ yuum zu'uhɛ en ti a yuum soke Yinɛ ti a bahɛ ti a mɛ Yinɛ bɔŋa zi'an, Yakɔbe Yinɛ wa yu'urɛ purega zi'an.

ref: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
text: Gee Solomɔn en yuum ta wa'am na mɛ yire bo' en.

ref: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
text: Gee Gaŋɛ Za'a Daana wa ka ki'ire yiya ti saaleba nu'uhi mɛ puan, wuu nɔtɔgeha la n yele se'em la.

ref: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
text: ‘Saazuo n de mam na'am kuka zi'an, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo dɛna mam nama dafeere Yire deni taaba ti fum wan ta'am mɛ bo' mam, Daana n yele. Bii ze'kani n wan dɛna mam vo'ohego zi'an?

ref: You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did.
text: “Tube-kpirega duma wa, ya suya digelum mɛ ti ya tuba li', saŋawoo yamam kan malum nam bo' Fa'a Sia la, ya kɔ'ɔm ita mɛ wuu ya yaabeduma en yuum iti se'em la.

ref: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
text: Nɔtɔgeha kani ti ya yaabeduma ka mɛlehɛ? Ba yuum ku sɛba woo en yuum puti Fa'a Daana wa kinɛ yele la mɛ, ti nananawa, yamam nyaa lebege ŋma'areba la kuureba.

ref: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
text: Ya dela sɛba n to'e ze'elego la, ti ku ze'ele la Yinɛ Suŋereba ze'a na, gee ya ka dole ku.” Ba lobe Siteven

ref: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
text: Nɛreba la n wum yele ana wa la, ba suure n nyiie, ti ba ɔbera nyɛna Siteven nɛŋan.

ref: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
text: Gee ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ en, ti Siteven gorege bihɛ saazuo ha, nyɛ Yinɛ suŋa, ti Yezu zi'a Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o.

ref: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
text: Ti a yele nɛreba la yeti, “Bihɛ ya, mam nyɛti la saazuo n yu'ɛ, ti Asaala Dayua la zɛ'a Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o.”

ref: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
text: Ti nɛreba la li' ba tuba, gee kaaha tagurega, lagum zɔ uue en ha.

ref: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text: Nyaa vuue en yehe tiŋa la kinkɛleŋa ha, pɔhɛ lɔbera en la kuga. Ti aze' nyɛ duma la yɛɛhɛ ba futo biŋe budibele paalega ti ba yi'ira ka Sɔɔl nɛŋan.

ref: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
text: Ban lobere Siteven la, ti a pu'uhɛ yeti, “Daana Yezu, to'e n sia wa.”

ref: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
text: Gee kpa' duma kaahɛ supi yeti, “Daana, da dikɛ be'em buna wa bahɛ ba zuo.” En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a ki mɛ.

ref: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
text: Gee Sɔɔl mu'ɛ la nini ba'ahera Lageŋɔ la, doohe la en dole yire yire vɛ'ɛra sɛba n nam la, buraahi la pɔgehi, tara kina ti ba pagera ba. Filipi muulɛ Yelehum Samariya

ref: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
text: Lan iŋɛ bala la, sɛba en nam la nyaa zɔ yiregɛ mɛ, lomehe zi'ihi, muula yelehum la.

ref: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
text: Filipi kiŋɛ sige la Samariya tinkãtɛ puan, ta muulɛ Kiresta yele bo' ba.

ref: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
text: Ban wum gee nyɛ daalehum zo'e zo'e sebo ti Filipi tum la, nɛrekuuŋɔ la naaɛ nuurɛ kelehe a yelehum la suŋa suŋa.

ref: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛfi'iha kaahi kaahedɔɔrɔ gee zɛ'ɛta nɛreba kekeko en tari ba la puan yɛhera gee baha ba, ti kɔɔrenɔ la wɔbehi zo'e zo'e ihegera kina.

ref: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
text: Ti nyɛɛma kãtɛ sige tinkãtɛ la puan, yele ana zuo.

ref: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
text: Buraa kayima yu'urɛ en yi'iri Simɔn se'em n ni tuna a tiim tuuma Samariya tiŋa la puan, yuum Samariya nɛreba la, yɛta ti eŋa bi'e mɛ.

ref: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
text: Ti Samariya tiŋa puan, bia ta paara bunkutɛ woo kelehe en mɛ suŋa suŋa, gee yɛta yeti, “Burakana wa dela yinɛ se'ere ti ba yi'ira Paŋa Yinɛ la, n sige na.”

ref: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
text: Ti ba ni ana kɛli gee kɛleha n suŋa suŋa se'ere n sɔi la, saŋa zo'e zo'e ti a ni ita a tiim tuuma la yɔɔna ba.

ref: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
text: Gee lan nyaa wa'am na ti nɛreba la nam Filipi en muuli faarɛ la doohe Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, ba to'e la ko'om sua, buraahi la pɔgehi za'a.

ref: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
text: Nananawa, Yezu tuntuneba la n boi Yerusalɛm la n ta wum ti Samariyaduma to'e Yinɛ yelehum la me la, ba tum la Pita la Yɔn ba zi'an.

ref: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
text: Ti ba bayi' la sige Samariya, ta paaɛ pu'uhɛ bo' ba, ti ba ta'am to'e Fa'a Sia la

ref: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
text: Ti Pita la Yɔn digehɛ ba nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto ti ba to'e Fa'a Sia la.

ref: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
text: Gee Simɔn n ta nyɛ ti tuntuneba la dikɛ la ba nu'uhi pagelɛ ti Fa'a Sia la sige nɛreba zuo la, a tiɛ la nu'o ti a bo' tuntuneba la ligeri,

ref: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
text: gee yeti, “Bu' ya mam me pankana wa, ti se'em woo ti n dikɛ n nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo, Fa'a Sia sige a daana zuo.”

ref: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!

ref: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
text: Pita la Yɔn n yele bamam n mi' Yezu yele se'em, gee muulɛ Daana la yelehum la ba'ahɛ la, ba lebe la Yerusalɛm, ban ye' lɛbera la, ba muuli yelehum la bala bɔ'ɔra timbibehi zo'e zo'e se'ehi n kaaɛ Samariya tiŋa la nɛreba. Filipi la Itoopia kpɛ'ɛma

ref: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”

ref: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
text: Ti Filipi ihege kiŋɛ. Bala ma'a la ti Itoopia bulɛ n de kpɛ'ɛnkãtɛ, n de ba yetɔgum kpɛ'ɛma, tuna bɔ'ɔra Itoopia Pɔgenaba Kandase, sɔna a pɔgenaba wa tarega za'a biha. A wa'am Yerusalɛm na ti a na'ahɛ la Yinɛ,

ref: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.

ref: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
text: Ti buraa wa lerege en yeti, “N yeti n iŋɛ la ŋwani bɔkɛ, han dagena nɛra n suŋɛ mam?” Gee nyaa yi' Filipi ti a kɛ̃ na zi'ire lɛm en.

ref: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
text: Ti Bulɛ wa soke Filipi yeti, “N soke fum se'ere, Nɔtɔgeha wa tɔgeri la ani yele? A tɔgeri la a mɛŋa yele n bala bii ayima me ha?”

ref: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.

ref: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”

ref: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.

ref: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.

ref: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.

ref: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
text: Gee ihege kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan, gee nɛra wan yele fum sɛla en de fum iŋɛ.”

ref: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
text: Mam mɛŋa wan pa'alɛ en, en de a namehɛ namehɛ kãra sɛ'a mam yu'urɛ wa muulegɔ zuo.”

ref: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
text: Sɔɔl n wa'am na paaɛ Yerusalɛm la, maahum a mɛŋa ti a lagum Yezu zahereba la, gee ba za'a me zoti en me, se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka nam ti a dela Yezu zahera.

ref: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
text: ti la lilege bo' en ti a nyɛ ti buraa yu'urɛ en yi'iri Ananiya kɛ̃ a zi'an na, dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo ti a lebe nyɛta.”

ref: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.

text: Ŋwana woo en daa iti la, Sɔɔl me daa kelum mu'ɛ la nini tara nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega la kua yɛla dɔla Daana la zabera la, nyaa pun kiŋɛ malema nɛŋadaana la zi'an,

ref: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.

ref: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Ŋwana ti Ananiya kiŋɛ, ta paaɛ kɛ̃ yire la puan. Dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo, gee yele en yeti, “N suuɔ Sɔɔl, Daana Yezu se'em n niiɛ a mɛa pa'alɛ hɔ fum boni sore zuo yɛ'a ka'lam na la, n tum mam ti n wa'am na ti fum nini la le nyɛta, gee ti Fa'a Sia la me pirɛ hɔ.”

ref: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.

ref: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
text: gee zahereba la basɛba tari en la yu'uŋɔ bahɛ ti a sige pi'ɔ puan dikɛ doohe nangoone zuo sigehera pi'ɔ la fifihe ti ku ta ze'ele tiŋa. Sɔɔl boi Yerusalɛm

text: Ti Sɔɔl tɔgera gee ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma la Hiburuduma sɛba en tɔgeri Giriki la, ti ba maahum ti ba ku en.

ref: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
text: Ti sakereba la n ta baŋɛ la, ba tari en kiŋɛ la Kasariya, ta bahɛ ti a tole Tasuse.

ref: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
text: Gee ŋwana ma'a saŋa la, ti Lageŋɔ la n boi Yudiya, Galilii, la Samariya tihi puan la za'a nyaa tari la suma'ahum, gee ze'ele suŋa. Ti ba kina la Daana la zoolum Fa'a Sia la sumbugehum puan, ti ba zɔ'ɔra ba kãlɛ puan. Aniyase nyɛ tibegɔ

ref: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
text: Pita n daa dole kaara la, a ta kiŋɛ la nɛresunduma sɛba n boi Lida la.

ref: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
text: Bilam ti a nyɛ buraa ti ka yu'urɛ n yi'iri Aniyase, n bɛ̃' gã'a ka suŋɔ zuo yuuma anii, dɛna kɔɔreŋɔ.

ref: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
text: Ti Pita yele en yeti, “Aniyase, Yezu Kiresta tibɛ hɔ mɛ. Ihege la fum kilum fum suŋɔ la.” Ti bilam bilam, ti a ihege.

ref: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
text: Ti nɛresɛba woo n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri Lida la Saron tihi puan n nyɛ buraa wa la, ba ŋmɛregɛ la ba suya bo' Daana la. Pita boi Lida la Yopa

ref: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
text: Yopa puan, Yezu zahera ayima n boi bini ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Tabita ti de vuurɛ dɛna la Dɔkasi. A pirɛ itesuŋɔ la bu'a.

ref: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
text: Sankaŋa puan la, ti a lu bã'a, ki. Ban sɔ en ba'ahɛ la, ba tari en ta biŋe la bɔ'ɔ n tugelum saazuo ha puan.

ref: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
text: Lida tiŋa wa n ka zaaɛ la Yopa la, ti zahereba sɛba n boi la n wum ti Pita wa'am bini na la, ti ba tum buraahi siyi a zi'an na suhera en yeti, “Wa'am tu zi'an kalum na, da gu'um.”

ref: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
text: Bala ti Pita sirum ihege doohe la buraahi la ti ba wa'am na paaɛ. Ti ba tari en zom bɔ'ɔ la n tugelum bɔna saazuo la. Ti pokɔpa la woo zɛ'a la en kɛla, gee pa'ala lɔge kãra la lɔge bibehi seto ti Dɔkasi pɛ en daa nan boi la ba la.

ref: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
text: Ti Pita bahɛ ti ba za'a yehe, gee ti a kpa' duma pu'uhɛ, gee ŋmɛregɛ tɔ kum la gee yeti, “Tabita, ihege.” Ti kum la pikɛ bu nini, ti en nyɛ Pita la, ti a ihege zi'ire.

ref: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
text: Ti Pita tiɛ a nu'o gurɛ en, suŋɛ en ti a ihege ze'ele. Ti a nyaa yi' nɛresunduma la la pokɔpa la, dikɛ en voto pa'alɛ ba.

ref: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
text: Ti yele dina wa to'e Yopa za'a, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam Daana la.

ref: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
text: Ti Pita kɛ̃ Yopa puan dabeha kekeko la buraa kayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Simɔn n de gbanzaba.

ref: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
text: Buraa kayima n boi Kasariya puan, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'iri Koneliyose, bihera pɛntiireba kɔbega ti ba yi'ira ba Itali tansugere.

ref: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
text: A dela nɛra suŋɔ paaɛ paaɛ, dɛna Yinɛ zɔta eŋa la a yire za'a, gee bɔ'ɔra nɛreba yabi yabi gee pu'uha Yinɛ saŋa wuu.

ref: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
text: Ti daarɛ deyima wuntɛɛŋa gogo kaareŋa atã bɔba, ti a nyɛ ti lilegere ti de yu'ɛ, bo' en, ti a nyɛ ti Yinɛ Suŋera ayima kini na gee yɛta en yeti, “Koneliyose.”

ref: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Ti a bihɛ Suŋera la ti dabeem kɛ̃ en paaɛ paaɛ, gee soke en yeti? “La dela beni Daana?” Ti la lerege en yeti, “Fum pu'uhegɔ, la fum bu'a la za'a zom paaɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan mɛ dɛna tuulum tiihegɔ.

ref: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
text: Bala zuo la, tum nɛreba Yopa ti ba ta ɛ nɛra ayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Simɔn ti ba yi'ira Pita la zi'an.

ref: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
text: A kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la Simɔn se'em n de gbanzaba la yire n boi ateko nuuren la.”

ref: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
text: Suŋera la n tɔgɛ bo' en ba'ahɛ gee kiŋɛ la, a yi' la a yire la yamehi siyi, la pɛntiira suŋɔ se'em kpe'eŋo en zuni la sɛba en biheri en daare woo la,

ref: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
text: yele ba sɛla woo, gee tum ba Yopa.

ref: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
text: Ti yiimbeere wuu wuntɛɛŋa zuo tiŋasuka, ti ban boi sore zuo pɔra tiŋa la puan la, bala ma'a ti Pita me zom gɔhegɔ zuo ti a pu'uhɛ.

ref: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
text: Sankaŋa puan la, ti a kom duna ti a bɔta sɛla ti a di, ti ban boi bini dugera la, ti a zɛɛ guum zaahum.

ref: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
text: A nyɛ mɛ ti saazuo yu'ɛ ti sɛla ana wuu ganɛ n karegɛ, yirege ti sɛla gurɛ a nɔya anaahi la ze'ele saazuo sigehera en fiifiihe tiŋa wa zuo na.

ref: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
text: Ti duhi nama anaahi anaahi, bunvuula, la saazuo niihe en boi ku puan.

ref: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Ti a wum ti kua yɛta en yeti, “Pita, ihege la fum ku ba obe.”

ref: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Gee Pita yeti, “La kan ta'am iŋɛ bala n Daana, se'ere n sɔi la, mam ka tabelɛ di galeŋɔ bii kihere sɛla.”

ref: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Ti kua le la yele en yi'i yeti, “Sɛla ti Yinɛ pee, da yi' la kihere bunɔ.”

ref: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
text: Lilegere dina wa iŋɛ la bala sugum nuuren butã, ti n yeti a bihɛ la, ti ganɛ la zom la saazuo.

ref: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
text: Pita n ze' yɛli ti'iha lilegere vuurɛ en de se'em la, a yeti bihɛ la ti buraahi la ti Koneliyose tum la n bala paara na, zɛ'a Simɔn yire zanɔren, gee sɔkera de yele.

ref: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
text: Pita n kelum bɔna bini ti'iha lilegere la yele la, ti Fa'a Sia la yele en yeti, “Bihɛ, buraahi sitã n eere fum.

ref: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
text: Nananawa, ihege sige tu'uhɛ ba, la fum doohe la ba kiŋɛ, da bɔ'ɔra, se'ere n sɔi la, mam tum ba.”

ref: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
text: Ŋwana ti Pita sige ta paaɛ buraahi la yele ba yeti, “Bihɛ ya, Mam n bala ti ya ɛɛra la, beni zuo ti ya kina na?”

ref: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
text: Ti ba lerege yeti, “Koneliyose n de pɛntiireba kɔbega nɛŋadaana, dɛna nɛre tuuntɔɔ, dɛna Yinɛ zɔta la, ti Yuuduma la za'a yi'ira en yu'sunɛ la, ti Fa'a Suŋera pa'alɛ en ti a tum yi' fum ti fum wa'am a yire na ti a wum fum tari se'ere ti fum yele.”

ref: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
text: Ŋwana ti Pita nyaa tari ba kɛ̃, ta bo' ba ga'aregɔ zi'an. Ti yiimbeere ti a ihege doohe la ba, ti Yopa suurɔ basɛba, lagum kɔlegɛ en.

ref: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
text: Yiimbeere ti ba wa'am na paaɛ Kasariya puan. Koneliyose n pun gura ba la zuo, a yi' a mabiihi la sɛba n ba' dɛna a zɔduma lagehɛ na.

ref: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
text: Pita n wa'am na paaɛ la, ti Koneliyose tu'uhɛ en, dɔbelɛ a nɛŋan na'ahɛ en.

ref: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
text: Gee Pita yele en mɛ yeti, “Ihege, mam me yeen dɛna la asaala.”

ref: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
text: Ti Pita n tɔgɛ la en la, a kɛ̃ deen ta nyɛ ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e n lagehɛ,

ref: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
text: ti a yele ba yeti, “Ya mɛhi mi' ti la dela kihere bo' Yuda nɛra, ti a lagum bii a wa'am na kaaɛ sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la, gee Yinɛ n lilege pa'alɛ mam yeti, n da yi' asaala ti kihere bii galeŋɔ lɔkɔ.

ref: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
text: Bala zuo ban tum yi' mam la, n ka de n zagehɛ. Gee nyaa bahɛ ti n soke se'ere zuo ti ya tum yi' mam la?”

ref: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
text: Ti Koneliyose lerege yeti. “Dabeha anaahi n ŋwana zina, ti mam wa pu'uha n yire, wuu sankana wa, gogo kaareŋa atã bɔba, mam yeti n bihɛ ti buraa n yɛ lɔgerɔ ti tu nyagera bala zɛ'a mam la.

ref: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Yele mam yeti, ‘Koneliyose, fum pu'uhegɔ la fum bu'a la paaɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan mɛ dɛna tuulum tiihegɔ.

ref: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
text: Bala, tum nɛreba Yopa, ti ba ta soke Simɔn se'em ti ba yi'ira Pita la yele. A kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la Simɔn la n de gbanzaba yire n lɛm la ateko la puan.

ref: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti n kɔ'ɔm tum ba fum zi'an na, ti fum me iŋɛ sum zo'e zo'e wa'am na wa. Bala zuo la, tu za'a ze' la Yinɛ nɛŋan ka'lam ti tu kelehe sɛla woo ti Daana bo' fum nuurɛ ti fum yele tu.” Sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri wum Yelesuma la

ref: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
text: Ti Pita pɔhɛ ya'ɛ a nuurɛ tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ba yeti, “Mam nananawa yelemɛŋerɛ bɔkɛ ya ti Yinɛ ka gaheri nɛreba,

ref: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
text: gee tiŋa woo puan, se'em n zoti en, gee tuna sɛla n de ma'a ti Yinɛ bɔta.

ref: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
text: Ya mi' yelehum sebo ti a yuum tum bo' Izirale buuri la, muula suma'ahum yelehum Yezu Kiresta puan. Eŋa n de sɛla woo Daana la.

ref: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
text: Ya mi ti Yelehum buna wa to'e Yuda za'a mɛ, pɔhɛ Galilii, Yɔn en yuum muulɛ ko'om sua yelehum ba'ahɛ la.

ref: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
text: Yinɛ n bi'ihɛ Yezu en yehe Nazareti na la, la Fa'a Sia, la paŋa se'em, la en yuum eere tuna sum se'em, gee tibera sɛba woo ti Sagena la mugeha ba, se'ere n sɔi Yinɛ en boi la en zuo.

ref: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
text: Tumam dela aze' nyɛ duma bo' en tum yele sɛ'a woo Yudiya la Yerusalɛm tihi puan, ti ba ku en doohe la ban yule en tia zuo la;
