ref: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
text: gee ti Yinɛ vo'e en dabeha atã daarɛ, gee bo' en fole ti a niiɛ a mɛŋa.

ref: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
text: A yuum ka niiɛ a mɛŋa bo' la nɛra woo, gee tumam sɛba ti Yinɛ pun looe ti tu dɛna aze' nyɛ duma bo' en la ma'a, tumam sɛba n di gee nyu la en, en ihege a kum la puan la.

ref: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
text: Ti a bo' tu nuurɛ ti tu tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba, gee muulɛ ti eŋa n de se'em ti Yinɛ looe en ti a dɛna vɔpa la ki'induma gakera.

ref: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
text: Nɔtɔgeheba la woo yuum yele la eŋa yele ti, se'em woo n nam en, a be'em wan saalum Yezu yu'urɛ la zuo.” Fa'a Sia la sige sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la zuo

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
text: Pita n boi bini tɔgera ŋwana la, ti Fa'a Sia la sige sɛba woo n boi bini wum yelehum la zuo na.

ref: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
text: Ti sakereba la en ŋma en doohe Pita na la zɛ'a yɛli, la Fa'a Sia bo'olum la n lagum sige sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la zuo.

ref: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, ba wum nɛreba la n tɔgeri yetɔgum buuri buuri, gee na'aha Yinɛ se'em la. Ŋwana ti Pita yeti,

ref: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
text: “Se'em boi wan ta'am gu' nɛreba wa ti ba da sɔ ko'om, sɛba n to'e Fa'a Sia la bo'olum wa wuu tumam wa?”

ref: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
text: Ŋwana ti Pita nyaa bo' sore ti ba sɔ nɛreba la ko'om Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ puan. Ti nɛreba yele Pita ti a kɛ̃ la ba yuue fii wa.

ref: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
text: Nananawa,Yezu tuntuneba la, la sakereba la za'a n boi Yuda tiŋa la wum ti, sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la me nam Yinɛ yelehum la mɛ.

ref: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
text: Bala, Pita n lebe zom Yerusalɛm la, sakereba sɛba n ŋma la zuuheri en mɛ

ref: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
text: yeti, “Ŋwani ti fum kiŋɛ sɛba n ka ŋma la zi'an, pun ta di la ba?”

ref: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
text: Ti Pita pɔhɛ yele yele pa'ala ba yeti,

ref: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
text: “Mam wa bɔna la Yopa puan pu'uha, ta zɛɛ guum, nyɛ lilegere. Lilegere la puan, mam nyɛ sɛla n karegɛ wuu ganɛ la ti ba dikɛ sɛla lu' ku nɔya anaahi la ze'ele saazuo ha bahera ku fiifiihe na, wa'am na lɛm mam.

ref: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Ti n wum kua, ti ka yɛta mam yeti, ‘Ihege, Pita; la fum ku ba obe.’

ref: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Ti mam yeti, La kan ta'am iŋɛ n Daana, se'ere n sɔi la, sɛla n de galeŋɔ bii kihere lɔkɔ ka tabelɛ tole mam nuurɛ wa puan.

ref: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Gee yi'i puan, kua la ze'ele saazuo lerege mam na yeti, ‘Sɛla ti Yinɛ pee, da yi' la galeŋɔ lɔkɔ.’

ref: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
text: Bunɔ wa iŋɛ la butã gee ti ba lebehe ve'e sɛla woo zom saazuo ha.

ref: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
text: Bihɛ, sankaŋa mɛa puan, ti buraahi sitã ti ba tum ba ti ba ze'ele Kasariya na, paara tumam n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri yise'ere puan la.

ref: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
text: Ti Sia la yele mam yeti n doohe en kiŋɛ gee da gahera bamam la tumam tiŋasuka. Ti tu suurɔ tuyoobe wa n doohe mam ti tu kiŋɛ ta paaɛ kɛ̃ buraa la yire.

ref: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
text: Ti buraa wa, yele tu eŋa n nyɛ Suŋera ti a zɛ'a a yire la puan gee yele eŋa yeti, ‘Tum nɛreba Yopa, ti ba kiŋɛ ta tari Simɔn, se'em yu'urɛ n yi'iri Pita la na,

ref: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
text: a wan bo' ya yelehum, ti fum la fum yire wa za'a wan nyɛ faarɛ.’

ref: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
text: Ti mam n pɔhɛ tugum bini la, ti Fa'a Sia la sige ba zuo na, wuu kaŋa n yuum sige tumam zuo se'em pɔhega saŋa la.

ref: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Ti mam tiɛ tu Daana yelehum la, en daa wa yeti, ‘Yɔn sɔɔri ya mɛ la ko'om, gee ba wan ta sɔ yamam mɛ la Fa'a Sia la.’

ref: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
text: Bala zuo,Yinɛ mɛŋa han bo' ba bo'olum bumɔ mɛa, sebo ti a daa tumam n daa nam bo' Daana Yezu Kiresta la, mam dela ani wan ta'am gu' Yinɛ?”

ref: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
text: Nɛreba la n wum ŋwana wa la, ba yee la suram. Gee pɛgera Yinɛ yeti, “Yinɛ mɛŋa n bo' sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la me teere se'ere n bo'ori vom la.” Lageŋɔ seko n boi Antiyoke

ref: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
text: Nananawa, sɛba woo n zɔ yiregɛ doohe la ban mɛlehɛ Siteven se'em la zuo la, yiregɛ mɛ kiŋɛ zaaɛ zaarɛ ta paaɛ Fonisiya, Sapuruse, la Antiyoke tihi, ka muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la bo' nɛra nɛra han dagena Yuda buuri la ma'a.

ref: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
text: Gee ba puan, buraahi sise'ehi n de Sapuruse, la Saarin tihi wa'am Antiyoke na tɔgɛ bo' sɛba n de Hiburuduma gee tɔgera Giriki la, me muulɛ Daana Yezu bo' ba.

ref: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
text: Ti Daana la nu'o paga ba zuo, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e sakɛ, dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' Daana la.

ref: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
text: Ti yele ana wa woo lu Yerusalɛm lageŋɔ la woo tuban, ti ba tum Banabase Antiyoke.

ref: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
text: Banabase n wa'am na paaɛ nyɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la n sige nɛreba la zuo se'em la, a puurɛ n pee, ti a kpemehe ba za'a yeti, ba bɔna ba sakerɛ la puan la Daana la, la ba kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' en saŋa woo;

ref: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
text: se'ere n sɔi la, Banabase wa dela nɛresuŋɔ, pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la, la sakerɛ, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'am Daana la zi'an na.

ref: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
text: Ti Banabase ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ Tasuse ti a ta ɛ Sɔɔl.

ref: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
text: En nyɛ Sɔɔl la, a tari en wa'am la Antiyoke na. Ba boi bini la bala yuum mune, lagena la Lageŋɔ la, pa'alɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ. Antiyoke puan ti nɛreba pɔhɛ yi' Yezu zahereba la yu'urɛ ti “Kiresta kɔma.”

ref: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
text: Sankaŋa puan, ti nɔtɔgeheba ze'ele Yerusalɛm sige Antiyoke na.

ref: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
text: Ti ba puan ayima yu'urɛ n yi'ira Agabuse, ti Sia la bo' en paŋa ti a vaaɛ ihege ze'ele purɛ yeti, kompɛɛlega wan sige timpaberɛ zuo zi'an woo. Ti la sirum wa'am na iŋɛ bala Kulodeyose Kaiza saŋa puan.

ref: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
text: Ti Yezu zahereba la kɔ'ɔm lu' ba puti'irɛ ti ba woo wan bo' Sakereba la en boi Yuda puan la suŋɛ doohe la nɛra woo paŋa n wan ta'am se'em bo'.

ref: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
text: Ti ba sirum iŋɛ yele dina wa, dikɛ ba suŋɛ bo' Banabase la Sɔɔl ti ba tari ta bo' kiinduma.

ref: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
text: Saŋa kaŋa mɛa puan ti naba Hɛrɔd tiɛ a nu'uhi la gbirege pagelɛ lageŋɔ nɛreba basɛba.

ref: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
text: Bahɛ ti ba kɔregɛ Yemesi en de Yɔn la la su'a.

ref: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
text: En nyɛ ti la pee Yuuduma la puurɛ paaɛ paaɛ la, a kiŋɛ la nɛŋa, bahɛ ti ba nyɔkɛ Pita me. Ŋwana ma'a wa dela boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo la saŋa la

ref: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
text: En bahɛ ti ba nyɔkɛ en la, a tari en me na pagɛ la paka deo puan, dikɛ en bo' pɛntiireba tansuga anaahi n tari pɛntiireba ba anaahi banaahi ti ba gura en. Gee iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti Azɔɛtole de'eŋo la han tole, a yu'ɛ en yehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan na.

ref: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
text: Ban pagɛ Pita paka deo la puan la, lageŋɔ la mu'ɛ la nini la pu'uhegɔ saŋa woo bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ a zuo. Pita yehe paka deo la puan

ref: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
text: Hɛrɔd n wa ti'iha ti a tari en yehe na la, yu'uŋɔ kuŋɔ mɛa ti ba ba' Pita mɛ la bãhi, ti a giha pɛntiireba bayi' tiŋasuka gee ti pɛntiireba basɛba me zɛ'a yiŋa kuleŋa gura paka deo la.

ref: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
text: Ba yeti ba bihɛ ti Daana suŋera n bala paka deo la puan, ti bugum niiɛ bini piim. Ti a paaɛ ŋmɛ' Pita lugerɛ yele en yeti, “Ihege kalam.” En tɔgɛ bala la, ti bãhi la n ba' Pita nu'uhi la zɔgɛ tiŋa.

ref: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
text: Ti Suŋera la yele en yeti, “Lu' fum sɛganɛ, lu' fum tagera la.” Ti Pita iŋɛ bala. Ti a nyaa yeti, “Lobe fum fuo la lu' suŋa la fum dɔla mam na.”

ref: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
text: Ban kiŋɛ gaŋɛ gura se'em n ze' yia la, le wa'am na kiŋɛ gaŋɛ yi'i gura la, ba wa'am na na paaɛ la kuto nayaŋa la n dole yɛha yɛ'a tiŋa la puan la. Ti kuto nayaŋa la mɛŋa yu'ɛ ka mɛŋa bo' ba, ti ba kiŋɛ yehe sige sore zuo kina, Pita ta yeti a bihɛ la ti suŋera la mɛlum ya gee bahɛ en.

ref: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
text: Bilam ti Pita yɛm nyaa lebena, ti a yeti, “Nananawa Mam mi' ti yelemɛŋerɛ Daana n tum a Suŋera ti a wa'am na faaɛ mam Hɛrɔd nu'uhen, la Yuuduma wa woo n ti'ihɛ ni ti yele se'ere paaɛ ni mam la.”

ref: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
text: Pita n baŋɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃ Mɛɛri n de Yɔn se'em yu'urɛ deyima n de Maki la ma Mɛɛri yire, zi'an ti sakereba zo'e zo'e lagehɛ pu'uha la.

ref: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
text: En paaɛ ŋmɛ' yire la yiŋa kuleŋa la, pɔgesɔɔ yu'urɛ en yi'iri Aroda n wa'am na sakɛ ŋmɛ'a la.

ref: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
text: En baŋɛ ti kua la dela Pita la, ti a puurɛ ba' pee gaŋɛ, ti en wan yu'ɛ ni kuleŋa la, a tugum bahɛ mɛ gee zɔ lebe ha ta muula yeti, Pita ze' kuleŋa nuuren ha.

ref: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
text: Ti ba yele en yeti, “Fum zuo ka tuni. Ti pugela wa sina gee sɛ zɛ'a kankaŋi ti la dela bala.” Ti ba yele en yeti, “Fum ye nyɛ la a suŋera.”

ref: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
text: Sankana woo la, ti Pita kelum ŋmɛ'ɛra kuleŋa la mɛ, ti ban yu'ɛ la, ba nyɛ en mɛ. Ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ .

ref: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
text: Ti Pita yibehɛ ba la nu'uhi ti ba da vɔɔla, gee nyaa pa'alɛ ba Daana la en iŋɛ se'em tari eŋa paka deo la puan yehe na. Gee pa'ahɛ yeti, “Yele ya Yemesi yele dina wa suurɔ la za'a me.” Gee nyaa bahɛ bilam gee tee kiŋɛ zi'an.

ref: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
text: Beere n ta yilegɛ la, ti gbiregeri kãtɛ lu pɛntiireba la, doohe la beni n iŋɛ Pita.

ref: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
text: Hɛrɔd bo' sore ti ba ɛ Pita la ninmu'urɛ ti ba ka nyɛ en la, ti a malum viihɛ gureba la, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba ku ba. Gee nyaa ze'ele Yuda kiŋɛ sige Kasariya, ta bɔna bilam. Hɛrɔd kum

ref: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
text: Ti Hɛrɔd suure ba' nyiie la Taya la Sidɔn tihi nɛreba. Bala ti ba naaɛ taaba la nɔyinɛ tum ba nɛreba na ti ba wa'am na ŋmɛ' nu' pu'ɔ. Ti ba paaɛ na nyaŋɛ tɔgɛ ti Balastuse se'em en biheri naba bungã'ala la bɔkɛ ba. Ti ba nyaa suhɛ suma'ahum, se'ere n sɔi la, ba tihi la dele la naba tihi la nyɛta dia.

ref: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
text: Ti suhega la dabeherɛ ta paaɛ, ti Hɛrɔd bagɛ a na'am lɔgerɔ, tole zi'ire a na'am kuka la zuo, nyaa tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba la woo n lagehɛ na la.

ref: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
text: Ti nɛreba la vɔɔla yeti, “Ɛhɛɛ, yinɛ de mɛŋa kua n ŋwana gee dagi asaala.”

ref: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
text: Ti bilam bilam doohe la Naba wa n ka dikɛ na'ahegɔ wa bo' Yinɛ la zuo, ti Daana Suŋera lobe en la ba'a ti a gã'a ti zunzuya dita en tara tara ti a ta ki.

ref: God's message kept spreading.
text: Gee Yinɛ yelehum la zo'ore mɛ yiregera.

ref: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Ti Banabase la Sɔɔl n ta ba'ahɛ tuunɛ se'ere ti ba wa'am bini na ti ba tum la, ba lebe la Yerusalɛm, ta tari Yɔn se'em yu'urɛ deyima n yi'ira Maki la lebena.

ref: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
text: Antiyoke sakereba lageŋɔ la puan, nɔtɔgeheba la pa'aleba n boi bini. Banabase, Simeyɔn se'em ti ba yi'ira Naiga la, dikɛ Lusiyuse n yehe Saarin, Manayin n de Naba Hɛrɔd kiima ayima la, nyaa dikɛ Sɔɔl.

ref: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
text: Daarɛ deyima ti ba bɔna Daana la na'ahegɔ puan la nɔlurɛ, ti Fa'a Sia la tɔgɛ yeti, “Yehe ya Banabase la Sɔɔl ze'ele bo' tuunse'ere ti mam yi' ba ti ba tum la.”

ref: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
text: Ti ban ta ba'ahɛ nɔlurɛ la pu'uhegɔ la, ba digehɛ la ba nu'uhi pagelɛ Banabase la Sɔɔl zuo gee bahɛ ba ti ba kiŋɛ. Yezu tuntuneba la muulɛ Yelehum la Sapuruse

ref: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
text: Bala, Fa'a Sia la n tum ba ti ba yehe la, ba kiŋɛ Kilikiya, ze'ele bilam zom uureŋɔ kaaɛ Sapuruse.

ref: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
text: Ban ta paaɛ Salamise tiŋa la, ba muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la mɛ Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire la puan. Ti Yɔn bɔna lagum suŋɛ ba.

ref: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
text: Ban doohe tiŋa la n boi ko'om tiŋasuka la za'a ta paaɛ Pafose la, ba tu'uhɛ la tiim daana ayima n de Yuda buuri nɔtɔgehe pumparema, ti ba yi'ira en Baa-Yezu.

ref: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
text: A boi mɛ la nɛŋa daana Segiyose Pɔɔlusi, en de mi'ilum daana yi' Barabase la Sɔɔl, bɔta ti a wum Yinɛ yelehum la.

ref: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
text: Gee tiindaana Elymas , eŋa ka sakɛ bo' ba, gee zigera ti a tee nɛŋadaana wa puti'irɛ yehe sakerɛ la puan.

ref: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
text: Gee Sɔɔl me, se'em ti ba tee yi'ira Pɔɔl la, pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la bihɛ en zii

ref: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
text: gee yele en yeti, “Sagena dayua wa, fum se'em n zaberɛ la tuuntɔɔ vom wa. Fum pirɛ mɛ la pa'ahegɔ la ŋmɛlema ma'a. Fum kan bahɛ fum dole ŋmɛlena Daana tuuntɔɔ sɔya wa?

ref: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
text: Nananawa kelehe, Daana la nu'o n zaberɛ la hɔ, fum wan fɔ pun kan nyɛ yintule wa ta paaɛ saŋa kayima.” Bilam bilam ti puuhego la lika pi' en ti a tu'una, ɛɛra nɛra gurɛ a nu'o.

ref: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
text: Gee nɛŋadaana wa n nyɛ sɛla n iŋɛ la ti a nam yelehum la se'ere n sɔi la Daana la pa'alegɔ la iŋɛ en la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ. Pɔɔl la Banabase boi Pisidiya Antiyoke

ref: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
text: Ti Pɔɔl la a tuntuna tadaana wa zom uureŋɔ Pafose wa'am na paaɛ Piga en boi Pamfiliya puan. Gee Yɔn bahɛ ba mɛ gee lebe Yerusalɛm,

ref: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
text: gee ti bamam ze'ele Piga wa'am na paaɛ Antiyoke n boi Pisidiya puan. Ti vo'ohego daarɛ ti ba kɛ̃ zamehegɔ yire la puan, ta zi'ire.

ref: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
text: Ban kãalɛ Moose la nɔtɔgeheba ze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan ba'ahɛ la, ti zamehegɔ yire la puan kpɛ'ɛnduma la tum yele ba na yeti, “Tu suurɔ wa, Ya han tara kpemehego seko ti ya bo' nɛreba wa, bo' ya na.”

ref: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
text: Ŋwana ti Pɔɔl ihege ze'ele suŋa, gee pɔhɛ tugum yeti, “Yamam Izirale buuri, la yamam sɛba woo n zoti Yinɛ, kelehe ya.

ref: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
text: Yinɛ se'ere n sɔi Izirale buuri wa la, yuum looe la tumam sɔyaabeduma, gee iŋɛ Izirale buuri wa ti ba dɛna nɛrekãreba ban yuum nan boi Igipiti puan la. Ti Yinɛ mɛŋa zɛkɛ a nu'o tari ba ze'ele Igipiti la puan yehe na,

ref: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
text: Bala zuo, bihɛ ya suŋa ti sɛla ti nɔtɔgeheba la purɛ biŋe la da sige ya zuo. La yeti,

ref: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
text: Ti Yinɛ ta sagum tihi siyopɔi nɛreba za'a, Keenan sɔ'ɔlum puan, gee nyaa dikɛ ba tihi la bo' Izirale buuri wa ti la dɛna ba sɔ'ɔlum,

ref: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
text: Yinɛ n ta fooe Sɔɔl la, a tee dikɛ la Deviti zi'ire na'am kuka la zuo. Yinɛ n wa yeti a tɔgɛ Naba Deviti yele la, a yeti, ‘Mam nyɛ Yesi dayua Deviti wa ti a dɛna la se'em ti mam suure kɛ̃ paaɛ paaɛ, a wan ta'am tum mam sumboolum n de se'em la.’

ref: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
text: Buraa kana wa yaahi puan ti Yinɛ doohe tari faara la n de Yezu wa'am Izirale puan na wuu en yuum biŋe a nuurɛ se'em la.

ref: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
text: Faara la kinɛ na la, ti Yɔn pun diŋɛ nɛŋa wa'am na tɔgɛ suteere ko'om sua yele bo' Izirale buuri la mɛ za'a.

ref: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: Yɔn n wa pɔra a tuuma la ba'ahegɔ la, a yele a nɛreba la yeti, ‘Yamam ti'ihɛ mam dela ani? Mam dagi se'em ti ya gura la. Aayi, eŋa ye' la mam pooren na. Mam ka seke n zãla a nama tagera.’

ref: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
text: “N suurɔ wa, Aberaham yireduma yaahi wa, la yamam sɛba n zoti Yinɛ wa, Yinɛ tum faarɛ yelehum buna wa bo' la tumam na.

ref: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
text: Gee sɛba n ki'ire Yerusalɛm la la ba nɛheduma la daa ka baŋɛ en, bii nɔtɔgeheba la yelehum la ti nɛreba daa kãala pa'ala ba vo'ohego dabeherɛ woo puan la. Ba tugum daa bahɛ ti yelehum buna wa niiɛ la asira na, doohe la ban gakɛ en gee ku en la.

ref: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
text: Ban doohe tum sɛla woo n gulehɛ a yele se'em ba'ahɛ la, ba yakɛ en mɛ tia la zuo, tari en ta laaɛ yɔɔ puan.

ref: But God raised him from death!
text: Gee Yinɛ vo'e en mɛ kum la puan,

ref: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
text: Ti a niiɛ a mɛŋa dabeha kekeko pa'alɛ sɛba n doohe la en ze'ele Galilii ta paaɛ Yerusalɛm, bamam n nyaa de aze' nyɛ duma boi a nɛreba.

ref: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
text: Tumam tari la yelesuma la wa'am na, ti sɛla ti Yinɛ yuum biŋe a nuurɛ la tu yaabeduma la,

ref: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
text: a tum dɛ bo' la tumam n de ba kɔma la, doohe la n ihege Yezu a kum la puan wuu lan gulehɛ se'em bɔna yuuma gɔŋɔ zuo buyi' puan se'em la. La gulehɛ yeti, ‘Fum dela mam Dayua, Zina mam dɔgɛ hɔ.’

ref: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
text: En tɔgɛ se'em doohe en wan vo'e en a kum la puan ti a kan le lebe pu'urɛ puan. ‘N wan bo' fum tuulum yɛla la sum sebo ti n biŋe nuurɛ la Deviti ti n bo' la.’

ref: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
text: Bala zuo la, a le yele yuuma gɔŋɔ la puan ze'a kayima yeti, ‘Fum kan bahɛ ti fu tuulum nɛra la pɔ'ɛ.’

ref: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
text: Naba Deviti n daa ta tum a Yinɛ la n boti a tum a mɛŋa saŋa la puan ba'ahɛ la, a ki mɛ, ti ba pi' en lɛm a yaabeduma, ti a pɔ'ɛ.

ref: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
text: Gee eŋa se'em daana ti Yinɛ vo'e en kum puan la ka makɛ pɔ'ɛ.

ref: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
text: Bahɛ ti n yele ya ti ya baŋɛ zina n suurɔ, ti la doohe la buraa kana Yezu Kiresta wa puan, ti tu muula sugeri dia yelehum wa bɔ'ɔra ya wa la.

ref: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
text: ‘Bihɛ ya, Yamam sɛba duma n ka dikɛ Yinɛ yelehum yi' sɛla wa, Bahɛ ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ ti ya bahɛ yoo, se'ere n sɔi la, mam tuni mɛ ya vom dabeha wa puan. N tuni la tuunsɛ'a ti ya kan malum sakɛ, nɛra han pun yele ya.’ ”

ref: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
text: Pɔɔl la Banabase en ta yɛ'a yɛha la, ti nɛreba la bɛlena ba yeti, ba daan tɔgɛ yele ana mɛa bo' ba vo'ohego dabeherɛ se'ere n kini na la.

ref: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
text: Ban ta ba'ahɛ lageŋɔ la zamehegɔ yire la puan la, Ti Yuda buuri la zo'e zo'e la sɛba n kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' Yuda pu'uhegɔ la dɔla Pɔɔl la Banabase poore, ti ba tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ba gee kpɛmeha ti ba kelum bɔna Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la puan.

ref: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
text: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ la en le ta paaɛ la, la ŋwani ti tiŋa la za'a nɛreba n wa'am na ti ba kelehe Daana la yelehum.

ref: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
text: Gee Yuuduma la n nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la, nyuure n kɛ̃ ba, ti ba pɔhɛ tuura, gee zɛregera Pɔɔl n tɔgɛ se'em la.

ref: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
text: Ti Pɔɔl la Banabase tɔgɛ la buraanɛ yele ba yeti, “La nari mɛ ti ba muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la bo' yamam yia. Gee yamam n zagehɛ ka nam bu la, gee gakɛ ya ti yamam ka nari vom kamba'ahɛ wa la, tu nyaa ŋmɛregera lɛbera la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la zi'an.

ref: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
text: Ŋwana wa la zuo, Daana pun bo' tumam a nuurɛ mɛ yeti, ‘Mam iŋɛ ya ti ya dɛna la niihum bo' sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la, Ti ya bahɛ ti faarɛ paaɛ timpaberɛ wa zi'an woo.’ ”

ref: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
text: Sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la n wum ŋwana la, ba pee puurɛ mɛ, ti ba pɛgera Daana la yelehum la. Bama sɛba ti ba pun maahum ti ba nyɛ vomkan ba'ahɛ la bamam en nam.

ref: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
text: Yinɛ yelehum la n yiregɛ zi'an la puan se'em n bala.

ref: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
text: Gee Yuuduma la doohe mɛ dɔrɛ pɔge se'ehi en kaabɛ si mɛhi bo' pu'uhegɔ la, mina si nɛŋa si poore la, la buraahi se'ehi n de zuto duma tiŋa la puan la, ti ba gbirege Pɔɔl la Banabase, sa'ɛ ba yehe tiŋa la puan.

ref: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
text: Ŋwana ti Pɔɔl la Banabase kpa'ahɛ ba nama puuhego bahɛ ti la dɛna yele bo' tiŋa la, gee nyaa kiŋɛ Ikonium tiŋa.

ref: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
text: Ti Yezu zahereba la pirɛ la nyɛɛma la Fa'a Sia la.

ref: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
text: Bala mɛa n le iŋɛ, Ikonium puan, zi'an ti Pɔɔl la Banabase kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃' Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire la puan, muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la paaɛ paaɛ ti Yuuduma la Girikiduma zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la.

ref: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
text: Gee Yuuduma sɛba n ka nam yelehum la dɔrɛ sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la, tu'ule ba puti'ira la suurɔ la.

ref: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
text: Ŋwana zuo, ti Pɔɔl la Banabase yee tiŋa la puan yuue, tɔgera Daana la yelehum la ninmu'urɛ, ti Daana la mɛŋa bahɛ ti a tuntuneba la tuna daalehum la nyalema la ba nu'uhi ti la pa'alɛ ti zu'uhegɔ yelehum la dela eŋa yelehum.

ref: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
text: Gee tiŋa la nɛreba zo'e zo'e welehi mɛ buyi, ti basɛba pa' Yuuduma la n ka sakɛ yelehum la poore, ti basɛba me pa' Yezu tuntuneba la poore.

ref: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
text: Ti sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la Yuuduma la ba kima za'a, la n ta maahum ti ba iŋɛ ba sɛla en ka ani suŋa gee lobe ba la kuga la,

ref: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
text: Lisetira puan, buraa n boi bini dɛna kɔɔreŋɔ ka dɔgum puan na, ka malum kiŋɛ.

ref: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
text: Ti buraa kana mɛa wa zi'a kɛleha Pɔɔl n tɔgeri la kpiri. Ti Pɔɔl ta biha en zii, ta baŋɛ ti buraa wa tari sakerɛ ti a wan nyɛ a tibegɔ,

ref: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
text: ti Pɔɔl yele en paŋa paŋa yeti, “Ihege ze'ele fum nama zuo.” Ti buraa wa ɛkɛ ze'ele, pɔhɛ kina kaara.

ref: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
text: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la n nyɛ Pɔɔl en tum se'em la, ti ba woole kpim Likoniduma yetɔgum puan yeti, “Tingama n lebege nɛreba wa'am tu zi'an na.”

ref: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
text: Ti ba sigɛ Barabase ti Zeyusi, gee sigɛ Pɔɔl ti Hɛmese, se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa n boi nɛŋa la tugum la.

ref: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
text: Ti tindaansɛka n kaaberi Zeyusi wa la bagerɛ bɔ'ɔ la kɔ'ɔm pɔra la tiŋa la kinkɛleŋa, ti a tari lɔlehi la mɔpuuro na, ti eŋa la nɛrekuuŋɔ la maahum ti ba kaabɛ ba.

ref: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
text: Yezu tuntuneba Banabase la Pɔɔl n ta wum ŋwana la, ba aahum la ba futo, gee zɔ gɛregɛ nɛreba la puan ha, tahera yeti,

ref: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: “Tu zɔduma wa, ŋwani ti ya ita ŋwana? Tumam dela nɛresaaleba wuu yamam la, tara la yelesuma wa'am na muula bɔ'ɔra ya ti ya ŋmɛregɛ ya puti'ira yehe yeleyotuna wa puan, gee wa'am Yinɛ vua wa ze'a na, se'em n naam saazuo la tiŋa wa, la ateko wa la sɛla woo n boi ba puan la.

ref: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
text: Buuri saŋa se'ehi n tole ha la, ti Yinɛ yuum bahɛ ti tihi woo ita ban boti se'em,

ref: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
text: gee la bala za'a, aze' nyɛ duma boi mɛ Yinɛ itesuŋɔ seko ti a ita doohe n baheri ti saa zɛ'ɛta saazuo na niira bɔ'ɔra ya, ti yuuma dɛna yuumsuma, ti a tigeha tɔ tu dia ti ya suya pirɛ la nyɛɛma.”

ref: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
text: La Pɔɔl duma n pun tɔgɛ yetɔge buna za'a la, bu ka nyaŋɛ la pika pika me, ta'am gu' nɛrekuuŋɔ la ti ba da kaabɛ ba.

ref: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
text: Gee Yuuduma basɛba n ze'ele Antiyoke la Ikonium tihi puan na, tɔgɛ nyaŋɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la ti ba pa' ba poore. Nyaa lobe Pɔɔl, vuue en yehe tiŋa la yiŋa ha ta bahɛ ti'iha ti a ki mɛ.

ref: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
text: Gee zahereba la n lagehɛ kaaɛ en la, a ihege mɛ kiŋɛ kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan, ti yiimbeere ti eŋa la Banabase kiŋɛ Dɛɛbi. Antiyoke lɛbega.

ref: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
text: Ban muulɛ yelesuma la tiŋa la puan ba'ahɛ la, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la, ba lebe la Lisetira, ze'ele bilam tole Ikonium nyaa ta tole Antiyoke.

ref: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
text: Ba doohe malum kpe'em zahereba la gee kpemehe ba ti ba bɔna ba sakerɛ la puan, yɛta ba yeti, “Tu wan doohe la namehegɔ zo'e zo'e puan gee kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.”

ref: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
text: Ban doohe lageŋɔ la woo puan lu' nuurɛ gee pu'uhɛ gee loohe kiinduma bahɛ lageŋɔ woo puan ba'ahɛ la, ba dikɛ ba woo iŋɛ la Daana se'em ti ba sakɛ bo' en la nu'uhen.

ref: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
text: Ti ba nyaa ze'ele bilam doohe Pisidiya tole, wa'am na paaɛ Pamfiliya.

ref: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
text: Ban tɔgɛ yelehum la Pɛga puan la, ba ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ sige la Atalia.

ref: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
text: Ba ze'ele la bilam le zom uureŋɔ lebe Antiyoke, ze'a ti ba daa lagehɛ dikɛ ba iŋɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ puan la tuuma la ti ba tum ba'ahɛ la.

ref: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
text: Ban wa'am na na paaɛ la, ba yi' lageŋɔ la za'a mɛ lagehɛ na, nyaa yele sɛla woo ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba tum la ba, la Yinɛ n yu'ɛ sakerɛ kuleŋa se'em bo' sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la se'em.

ref: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
text: Ŋwana pooren, ba yee mɛ la Yezu zahereba la yuue fiiwa.

ref: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
text: Ti nɛreba basɛba ta ze'ele Yudiya wa'am na pa'ala sakereba la yeti, “Han dagena fum ŋma mɛ wuu Moose pa'alegɔ la n yele se'em la, fum kan ta'am nyɛ faarɛ.”

ref: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
text: Ban ta ba'ahɛ la, Pɔɔl la Banabase ŋmɛ' la nɔkpe'en tuulɛ la ba, Pɔɔl la Banabase la basɛba ti ba looe ba ti ba tari yele ana wa kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm ta sɔhɛ la Yezu tuntuneba la la kiinduma la.

ref: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
text: Ti lageŋɔ la tum ba, ti ban dole Fonisiya la Samariya la, ba yeti sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la teere mɛ, ti sakereba la za'a n boi ze'se'ehi la puurɛ pee.

ref: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
text: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Yerusalɛm la, lageŋɔ la za'a la Yezu tuntuneba la, la kiinduma la za'a to'e ba mɛ, ti ba me yele ba Yinɛ n doohe bamam puan tum yele sɛ'a woo.

ref: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
text: Gee sakereba la basɛba n boi Farasiiduma wele la ihege ze'ele mɛ yeti, “La dela de sore ti ba ŋma ba, gee ti ba me bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba dɔla Moose ze'elego la.”

ref: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
text: Ti Yezu tuntuneba la, la Lageŋɔ la Kiinduma la lagehɛ taaba zi'ire ti ba bihɛ sɔhega wa puan.

ref: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
text: Ban ta ŋmɛ' nɔkpe'ene la zo'e la, ti Pita ihege ze'ele saazuo yele ba yeti, “N suurɔ wa, ya mi' ti kuremin ha la, Yinɛ yuum looe la loore ya puan, ti mam n wan dɛna se'em puan ti Sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la wan doohe wum yelesuma wa wa gee lebege sakereba.

ref: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
text: Gee Yinɛ se'em n mi' asaala suuren la, pa'alɛ yeti ba nari mɛ doohe la n bo' ba a Fa'a Sia la, wuu en yuum bo' tumam se'em la.

ref: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
text: En duuhe ba suya la bahɛ doohe ban nam la puan la, pa'alɛ yeti, yima yima ka boi tumam la bamam tiŋasuka.

ref: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
text: Bala zuo la, ŋwani ti ya makera Yinɛ bihera, doohe la yan le dikera yɔgehega yɔgera a zahereba wa, yɔgelehi, yɔgehega sɛka ti tumam yaabeduma bii tumam mɛhi kan ta'am zi wa?

ref: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
text: Lan tugum dɛna se'em dela, tumam sakɛ ya ti tu wan nyɛ faarɛ doohe la zu'uhegɔ seko n doohe Yezu puan na la, wuu bamam me n wan sakɛ se'em la.”

ref: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
text: Ti lageŋɔ la za'a yee suram, gee kɛleha sɛlawoo ti Banabase la Pɔɔl yɛta ba doohe daalehum la nyalema sɛ'a ti Yinɛ tum doohe ba puan, sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la tiŋasuka la.

ref: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
text: Ban ta tɔgɛ ba'ahɛ la, ti Yemesi lerege yeti, “N suurɔ wa, kelehe ya mam.

ref: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
text: Simeyɔn . pun pa'alɛ Yinɛ en pɔhɛ zɔ sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri wa nimbu'ɔ se'em, gee yeti a yehe nɛreba ba puan ti ba yi'ira a yu'urɛ la.

ref: This agrees with what the prophets wrote,
text: Dina wa doohe mɛ la nɔtɔgeheba tugum la ti ba gulehɛ yeti,

ref: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
text: ‘Yele ana pooren n wan lebe na, la n maalɛ Deviti bɔŋa zi'an la lu la. N wan vaaɛ nangoone gira la le mɛ en, la n sɛ di ze'ele saazuo,

ref: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
text: ti nɛrawoo han nyɛ bala, ba wan ɛ Daana la. Sɛba n pun dagena Yuda buuri gee ti mam yi' ba la za'a. Daana la n yele se'em n baheri ti

ref: I promised it long ago.”
text: nɛreba bɔkera yele ana yuuma yuuma ha la.’

ref: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
text: Bala zuo, mam n gi' putɛ'se'ere dela, tu da daam sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri gee ŋmɛregɛ yɛ'a Yinɛ zi'an na wa,

ref: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Gee tu tugum gulehɛ bo' ba yeti, ba yehe ba mɛhi baga dia puan, la yaluntuuma, la sɛla woo ti ba kigɛ ku, la ba da dita ziim.

ref: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, tihi wuu puan, buuri sansɛka n tole, Moose tari sɛba n muuli a yele me, se'ere n sɔi la, ba kãalɛ a yele vo'ohego dabeherɛ wuu zamehegɔ yiya la puan.” Kiinduma gɔŋɔ ti ba gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra sakereba sɛba n dagi Yuuduma

ref: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
text: Ti Yezu tuntuneba la lageŋɔ kiinduma la, la lageŋɔ la za'a ku nuurɛ ti ba looe nɛreba ba puan pa'ahɛ Pita la Banabase puan tum ba ti ba kiŋɛ Antiyoke, ba tum Yudasi se'em ti ba yi'ira Barabase, la Silase se'em n de suurɔ la nɛŋa daana la,

ref: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
text: ba gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa yeti, “Tu suurɔ wa, Yezu tuntuneba la, la Kiinduma la n gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam sakereba sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri ya tuuhum puan na, bɔna Antiyoke, Siriya la Kilikiya tihi puan la, tu pu'uheri ya na.

ref: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
text: Tumam n wum ti nɛreba basɛba ze'ele tumam zi'an yehe, gee me ka to'e kã'aŋɔ tumam ze'a la, wa'am na yele ya yɛla a sɛ'a ti daam ya suya, gee gbirege ya puti'ira ti a ka gã'a bɔbeyinɛ la zuo,

ref: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
text: ti tumam ku nuurɛ deyina tum nɛrebana wa ya ze'a na, ti ba doohe la tu nɔŋerɛ suurɔ Banabase la Pɔɔl,

ref: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: sɛba n pun pɔ̃hɛ ba nyuurɔ tu Daana Yezu zuo.

ref: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
text: Tu tum la Yudasi la Silase sɛba n wan tɔgɛ yele sɛ'a n boi gɔŋɔ wa puan la ba nuurɛ bo' ya.

ref: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ la dagi Fa'a Sia la, la tumam me puti'irɛ ti tu pɛregɛ zeero le pa'ahɛ zeseto n pun bɔna ya zuo wa, han dagi yele sɛ'a en pakɛ wa.

ref: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
text: Ya da dita sɛla woo n dikɛ kaabɛ bo' bagerɛ, ya da dita ziim, bii sɛla ti ba kigɛ ku gee yehe ya mɛhi yaluntuuma puan. Ya han dɔla ŋwana, la ba'ahɛ mɛ, Yinɛ suŋɛ ya.”

ref: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
text: Ŋwana ti ba tum nɛreba wa, ti ba kiŋɛ Antiyoke. Ti ba ta paaɛ, lagehɛ lageŋɔ la za'a, ba bo' ba gɔŋɔ la.

ref: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
text: Lageŋɔ la nɛreba la n kãalɛ gɔŋɔ la, ba puurɛ pee mɛ la kpemehego seko n boi bini la.

ref: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
text: Ti Yudasi la Silase en de nɔtɔgeheba la tɔgɛ yele sɛ'a n wan kpemehe gee sɛ sakereba la ze'ele suŋa.

ref: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
text: Ban kɛ̃ bini yuue fii la, sakereba la bahɛ ba mɛ la suma'ahum ti ba lebe sɛba n tum ba na la zi'an. [

ref: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
text: Bala ti Pɔɔl la Banabase yee Antiyoke, la ba nɛreba basɛba zo'e zo'e, pa'ala nɛreba la, gee muula Daana la yelehum la bɔ'ɔra ba. Pɔɔl la Banabase welegere

ref: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
text: Dabeha n iŋɛ fii wa la, ti Pɔɔl yele Banabase yeti, “Wa'ana ti tu lebe doohe bihɛ sakereba sɛba woo ti tu doohe tihi la woo puan pa'alɛ gee muulɛ Daana la yelehum bo' ba la, la tu bihɛ ban ani se'em.”

ref: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Ti Banabase bɔta ti Yɔn se'em yu'urɛ deyima n yi'iri Maki la doohe ba.

ref: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
text: Gee Pɔɔl ku a puti'irɛ mɛ ti ba kan bahɛ ti se'em n tabelɛ zagehɛ ba Pamfiliya gee ka doohe ba kiŋɛ tuni la doohe ba.

ref: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
text: Ti ba nɔkpe'ene la ba' karegɛ gaŋɛ ti ba welehi taaba, ti Banabase tari Maki, ti ba zom uureŋɔ kiŋɛ Sapuruse.

ref: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
text: Gee ti Pɔɔl me tari Silase ti bamam me yeti ba yehe, Ti Sakereba la pu'uhɛ dikɛ en iŋɛ Daana la zu'uhegɔ puan.

ref: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
text: Ti Pɔɔl doohe Siriya la Kilikiya tihi puan za'a puan, sɛɛra Lageŋɔ duma la woo zɛ'ɛta.

ref: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
text: Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ta paaɛ Dɛɛbi la Lisetira tihi puan, bilam ti ba nyɛ zahera ayima yu'urɛ en yi'iri Timoti, ti a ma dɛna Yuda buuri puan pɔka, dɛna sakereba la ayima, gee ti a sɔ dɛna Giriki nɛra.

ref: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
text: Timoti wa dela se'em ti sakereba la n boi Lisetira la Ikonium la tɔgera a yele suŋa.

ref: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
text: Ŋwana ti Pɔɔl bɔta ti Timoti wa doohe en, nyaa bahɛ ti a ŋma, Yuuduma la n boi zɛ'se'ehi puan la zuo. Se'ere n sɔi la, ba daa mi' ti Timoti sɔ dela Giriki buuri.

ref: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
text: Ti ban dole kaara tiŋa tiŋa la, ba yeti nɛreba la ze'elego seko ti tuntuneba la kiinduma la en boi Yerusalɛm ku nuurɛ bu ba ti ba dɔla a suŋa la.

ref: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
text: Ba doohe se'em kpemehe lageŋɔ la nɛreba ba sakerɛ puan n bala, ti nɛreba me malum kɛ̃ lageŋɔ la pa'ahɛ daarɛ woo. Pɔɔl nyɛ la Masedoniya buraa lilegere puan

ref: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
text: Ti ba doohe Firigiya la Galatia sɔ'ɔlum la puan za'a kaaɛ, doohe la Fa'a Sia la n ka bo' ba sore ti ba muulɛ yelehum la Esiya tiŋa puan la zuo.

ref: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
text: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Misiya tiŋa beene zuo la, ba iŋɛ ba puti'irɛ ti ba kɔ'ɔm kɛ̃ Bitiniya tiŋa puan, gee Yezu Sia la ka bo' ba sore,

ref: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
text: bala ti ba doohe Misiya kinkɛleŋa sige, ta paaɛ Torowase.

ref: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
text: Ti yu'uŋɔ ti Pɔɔl ta nyɛ lilegere, ti buraa n yehe Masedoniya tiŋa n bala zɛ'a la en bɛlena yeti, “Wa'am Masedoniya na suŋɛ tɔ.”

ref: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
text: Eŋa n nyɛ lilegere dina wa la, ti tu ihege bilam bilam, zigera ti tu yakɛ kiŋɛ Masedoniya, se'ere n sɔi la tu sakɛ ya ti Yinɛ n yi' tu ti tu kiŋɛ ta muulɛ faarɛ la bo' ba . Lidiya teere

ref: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
text: Ti tu zom uureŋɔ, ze'ele Torowase, kɔ'ɔm kiŋɛ tuuntɔɔ ta paaɛ Samotirasi, yiimbeere ti tu paaɛ Niyapolise,

ref: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
text: ze'ele bilam tole Filipi, kaŋa n de tinkãtɛ Masedoniya tiŋa la puan, me dɛna Aromduma sɔ'ɔlum. Tu yee la tinkana wa puan dabeha fii wa.

ref: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
text: Ti vo'ohego dabeherɛ la ta paaɛ, ti tu yehe tiŋa la yiŋa, kiŋɛ ta pɔra kulaa la, zi'an ti tumam ti'iha ti pu'uhegɔ ze'a wan bɔna la. Tu zi'ire la bilam na la, pɔhɛ tɔgera bɔ'ɔra pɔgehi n lagehɛ na.

ref: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
text: Ti pɔka bɔna ba puan kɛleha tɔ, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Lidiya, dɛna pɔka dɛna Yin-zɔta, yehe Tayatira na, kɔɔha fumɔlɔ. Ti Daana la yu'ɛ ka suure ti ka kɛleha Pɔɔl en yeti sɛla la suŋa suŋa.

ref: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
text: Kaŋa la ka yire nɛreba la za'a n ta to'e ko'om sua la, ka bɛlena tumam yeti, “Ya han sirum nyɛ mam ti mam dela sakera Daana la puan, wa'am ya na kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra mam yire.” Ka tɔgera la bala tara tara ta nyaŋɛ tɔ. Ba pagɛ Pɔɔl la Silase

ref: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
text: Daarɛ deyima ti tu yɛ'a pu'uhegɔ zi'an la, tu tu'uhɛ la pugela n de yameŋa, ti kulekpa'bɛ'a dɔla ka, ti ka dɛna sɔputega, tari ligeri kina na bɔ'ɔra a daana la doohe la en yeti nɛreba ba yɛla la.

ref: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
text: Pugela wa n dole Pɔɔl la tumam la, ka ni ta kaahɛ mɛ bahɛ na yeti, “Nɛrebana wa dela Gaŋɛ za'a Yinɛ la Yamehi, muula faarɛ sore la bɔ'ɔra ya.”

ref: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
text: A iti ŋwana wa la dabeha zo'e zo'e. Ti a itegɔ la ba' nyiie Pɔɔl susuya, ti a ŋmɛregɛ yele kulekpa'bɛ'a la yeti, “Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan ti n bo' fum nuurɛ yɛta fum tole yehe a puan na.” Ti sankaŋa mɛa puan la, ti sɛbɛ'a yehe pugela la puan.

ref: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
text: Gee pugela la daan duma la n nyɛ ti ban tari puti'irɛ ti ba wan nyɛ ligeri la tole ya la, ti ba nyɔkɛ Pɔɔl la Silase, vɛ' ba kɛ̃ da'a puan na, tari ba wa'am kpɛ'ɛnduma nɛŋan na.

ref: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
text: gee wa'am na pa'ala tu nɛreba wa kiha sɛ'a n dagi ma'a bo' tumam n de Arome duma wa ti tu to'e a dɔla.”

ref: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la lagum pa' ba poore, zabera la Pɔɔl duma, ti yelegakereba yɛɛhɛ ba lɔgerɔ, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' ba.

ref: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
text: Ti ban ŋmɛ ba sagum sagum ba'ahɛ la, ba vu ba ta bahɛ la paka deo puan, gee bo' sɛba n biheri paka deo la nuurɛ ti ba gura ba suŋa.

ref: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
text: Doohe la ban bo' en nuurɛ se'em la zuo, ti eŋa me tari ba kɛ̃ paka deo la saraa puan ha, ta dikɛ kuto ba' ba nama tabelɛ dɔkãtɛ.

ref: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
text: La ta paara me woo yu'uŋɔ sunsua bɔba, ti Pɔɔl la Silase pu'uha gee yuuma bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ, ti sɛba ti ba pagɛ la ba la kɛleha.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
text: Ba yeti ba bihɛ, ti tiŋa la tinmiiŋo kãtɛ n miim, ti paka deo la ɛ̃berɛ la gbiregera, ti bilam bilam ti paka kulehi la za'a yu'uhɛ, ti zɔtɔ la n ba' nɛreba la za'a lorehe.

ref: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
text: Gura la en ta ihege nyɛ ti paka deo kulehi la yu'ɛ mɛ gã'a la, ti a fooe a su'a ti a ku a mɛŋa se'ere n sɔi la, a ti'ihɛ ti sɛba ti ba pagɛ ba la za'a yehe zɔ mɛ.

ref: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
text: Gee Pɔɔl tahɛ en mɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “Da pɔgelum fum mɛŋa gee tu woo boi mɛ.”

ref: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
text: Ti gura wa yi' ti ba tari bugum kɛ̃ na, ti a lu tiŋa Pɔɔl la Silase nɛŋan gbi'ira.

ref: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
text: Gee nyaa tari ba yehe yiŋa na yele ba yeti, “Naa, Beni ti n wan iŋɛ la n nyɛ faarɛ?”

ref: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
text: Ti ba lerege en yeti, “Nam Daana Yezu, gee fum wan nyɛ faarɛ, fum la fum yire za'a.”

ref: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
text: Ti ba nyaa tɔgɛ Daana la yelehum la bo' eŋa la sɛba woo n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri a yire la puan la.

ref: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
text: Ti yu'uŋɔ kuŋɔ sankaŋa mɛa puan, ti gura la tari ba kiŋɛ ta di'ihe ba nɔra la, gee ti eŋa la a nɛreba za'a to'e ko'om sua kalam ka gu'um.

ref: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
text: Ti a nyaa tari ba wa'am a yire na, bo' ba dia, gee ti eŋa la a nɛreba za'a nyɛɛna la bamam n lebege sakereba bo' Yinɛ.

ref: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
text: Beere n ta yilegɛ la, ti sɛba n gakeri yɛla la tum gureba yeti, “Kiŋɛ ya ta bahɛ ti buraahi la kiŋɛ.”

ref: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
text: Ti gura la to'e sɔɔleŋa la ta yele Pɔɔl yeti, “Yelegakereba la tum na yeti, tu bahɛ ti ya kiŋɛ. Bala zuo la, ya ta'am yehe nananawa la inma'ahum.”

ref: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
text: Gee Pɔɔl lerege en yeti, “Ba ŋmɛ tumam mɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ nɛŋan, gee ka nyɛ tu tuure, gee ti tu me dɛna Arome tiŋa wa nɛreba, ti ba le pagɛ tu, gee nyaa bɔta ti ba bahɛ tɔ bããlam? La kan iŋɛ bala. Bahɛ ti ba mɛhi wa'am na tari tu yehe.”

ref: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
text: Ti gureba la tari yetɔgum la ta bo' yelegakereba la, ti dabeem ke ba, la ba wum ti ba dela Arome tiŋa duma la.

ref: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
text: Ti ba nyaa wa'am na belum ba, gee nyaa tari ba yehe, gee nyaa yele ba ti ba bahɛ tiŋa la puan.

ref: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
text: Ban yehe paka deo la puan na la, ba kiŋɛ la Lidiya yire. Ban paaɛ nyɛ gee kpemehe sakereba la ba'ahɛ la, ti ba bahɛ tiŋa la puan.

ref: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
text: Pɔɔl la Silase n wa'am na gaŋɛ Amfipolisi la Apolonia tihi tole la, ba wa'am na paaɛ la Tɛsalonika, zi'an ti Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire bɔna bini.

ref: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
text: Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃ bini wuu en ni pun ita se'em la, vo'ohego dabeha atã ti eŋa la Yuuduma la tɔgera yɛla gulehegɔ la puan,

ref: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
text: ti a pa'ala ba, gee niira bɔ'ɔra ba ti la de la Kiresta la namehɛ gee ihege kum puan. Gee nyaa yele ba yeti, “Yezu kana wa, se'em ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra ya wa en de Kiresta la.”

ref: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
text: Ti ba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la, dikɛ ba mɛhi pa'ahɛ Pɔɔl la Silase puan, ti sɛba me n de Giriki sɛba en de Yinɛ zɔteba la, me zo'e zo'e ta ta' la pɔgese'ehi n de zuo duma la me ka pɔ'ɛ.

ref: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
text: Gee nyuure n kɛ̃ Yuuduma la basɛba, ti ba me nyɛ nɛreyaalehi sise'ehi n boi da'a, ti ba pa' ba poore, ti ba nyaa lagehɛ taaba bahɛ ti gbiregeri kãtɛ sige tiŋa la puan. Ban eere Pɔɔl la Silase ti ba tari ba na ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ tiŋasuka la, ba kiŋɛ ta zabɛ mɛ la Yason yire.

ref: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
text: Ban ka nyɛ Pɔɔl la Silase bini la, ti ba vɛ' Yason la sakereba basɛba wa'am tiŋa la kpɛ'ɛnduma nɛŋan na, gee vɔɔla yeti, “Nɛreba wa en dole tuleha timpaberɛ wa kpabera la, nyaa paaɛ ka'lam me na,

ref: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
text: ti, Yason to'e ba ki'ihɛ a yire. Ba za'a iti la sɛla n ka dole Kaiza ze'elego la. Ba yeti, naba ayima me n boi ha ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Yezu.”

ref: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
text: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la kpɛ'ɛnduma la n wum ŋwana la, la daam ba mɛ.

ref: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
text: Ti ban to'e bahega ligeri Yason la sɛba ti ba lagum nyɔgɛ ni la zi'an ba'ahɛ la, ba bahɛ ba mɛ ti ba kiŋɛ. Pɔɔl la Silase boi Beroya

ref: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
text: Yu'uŋɔ kuŋɔ mɛa puan ti sakereba la tari Pɔɔl la Silase ta bahɛ sore ti ba kiŋɛ Beroya tiŋa. Ban ta paaɛ bini la, ba kiŋɛ la Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire puan.

ref: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
text: Yuuduma bana to'e ba gana Yuuduma sɛba n boi Tɛsalonika puan la mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, ba to'e yelehum la ti ba muulɛ bo' ba la mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, gee viihɛ gulehegɔ la puan daarɛ woo ti ba bihɛ nyaa yɛla la ani la bala bini.

ref: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
text: Ŋwana zuo la, ba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la mɛ ta ta' la Giriki pɔgehi la buraahi zo'e zo'e n de zuoduma.

ref: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
text: Gee Yuuduma sɛba n boi Tɛsalonika la n ta wum ti Pɔɔl le muulɛ yelehum la me, ba ɛ Beroya tiŋa puan mɛ, ti ba ihege wa'am bilam me na, ti ba gbirege gee tu'ule nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

ref: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
text: Bilam bilam ti sakereba la tari Pɔɔl kalam kiŋɛ ateko ko'om nuuren, gee ti Silase la Timoti yee pooren.

ref: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
text: Sɛba n tari Pɔɔl kina la, tari en kiŋɛ la zaaɛ zaarɛ ta paaɛ Atense, ti en pa'alɛ ba ba wan iŋɛ se'em bahɛ ti Silase la Timoti wa'am na paaɛ en ka'lam ba'ahɛ la, ti ba keehe en gee lebe. Pɔɔl boi Atense

ref: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
text: Pɔɔl n boi Atense gee gura Silase la Timoti la, a suure sagum mɛ paaɛ paaɛ, la tinkãtɛ la n pirɛ la baga la.

ref: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
text: Bala a pɔhɛ yetɔgum mɛ la zamehegɔ yire la puan la Yuuduma la la Yinɛ zɔteba la, la da'ahi puan daare woo la sɛba en wan bɔna bini.

ref: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
text: Ti Epikureya la Sitoyiki yɛmduma wa'am na ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene la en. Ti sɛba yeti, “Gerego kuna me boti ku pa'alɛ la bem?” Ti sɛba me yeti, “A tɔgeri la tihi sise'ehi tingama yɛla.”

ref: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
text: Ti ba tari en wa'am Areopagus tugum zi'an la puan na, nyaa soke en yeti, “Bahɛ ti tu baŋɛ pa'alesaaŋa wa ti fum pa'ala vuurɛ en de se'em?

ref: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum tari la pa'alesaanɛ wa'am tu tuba puan na, bala ti tu bɔta ti tu baŋɛ ku vuurɛ.”

ref: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
text: Atenseduma woo, la saama n ki'ire ka puan, ka tari ba saŋa ita sɛla gana ba yɛta bii ba kɛleha sɛla n de yele paalɛ.

ref: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
text: Ti Pɔɔl ihege ze'ele Areopagus wa nɛŋan yeti, “Atenseduma wa, mam nyɛ ti ya ba dɛna la Yinɛ pu'uheba sadɔseko woo puan paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, mam n doohe ya tiŋa wa za'a kaaɛ mum la, gee ma'ɛ n mɛŋa bihera ya lɔgeseto ti ya na'aha la, mam nyɛ la kaabegɔ bimbine deyima ti gulehegɔ gulehɛ iŋɛ de zuo yeti, ‘Yinɛ se'ere ti tu ka mina.’ Bala zuo la, yinɛ se'ere ti ya na'aha gee ka mina la, Yinɛ dinɛ n bala ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra ya wa.

ref: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
text: Yinɛ se'ere n naam timpaberɛ wa la sɛla woo n boi de puan la. Yinɛ se'ere n de Saazuo la timpaberɛ wa zuo Daana la ka kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri zɔnɔ ti saaleba dikɛ nu'uhi mɛ puan.

ref: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
text: Bii nɛreba tuna bɔ'ɔra en wuu a boti la sɛla, se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa en bo'ori saala woo vom, la sɛla woo vo'ohum.

ref: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
text: Yinɛ iŋɛ ŋwana wa, ti nɛreba wan ɛɛra en. Ba wan tu'una ɛɛra en, ta nyɛ en, gee ti Yinɛ me pun ka zaaɛ la tu se'em.

ref: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, ‘Eŋa zuo ti tu vɔna, gee kina gee dɛna nɛreba wa,’ wuu ya mɛŋa mageha guleheba en yele se'em yeti, ‘Se'ere n sɔi la, tumam me dela a kɔma la.’

ref: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
text: Tumam n yi de yinɛ kɔma la, la dagi ti tu ti'iha ti Yinɛ naanɛ wan ani mɛ wuu salema, bii salempɛɛlega bii kugere bii naanɛ se'ere ti ba gulɛ bii naanɛ bii ti'ihegɔ seko en yehe asaala na.

ref: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
text: Saŋa daa boi ti Yinɛ daa ka dikɛ saaleba yɛmka'alum ti la dɛna daaŋɔ. Gee nananawa, a bo' nɛrawoo n boi ze'sɛka woo la nuurɛ ti ba tee,

ref: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, a looe la dabeherɛ biŋe, ti daan dinɛ daarɛ ti a wan gakɛ nɛrawoo n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo tuuntɔɔ. A wan doohe la buraa kayima ti a mɛŋa looe la, doohe eŋa puan ti putɛ'kɔ'ɔrɛ bɔna, eŋa n ihege en kum puan la.”

ref: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
text: Ban wum ti a purɛ a ki gee ihege la, ti basɛba pureha, gee basɛba yeti, “Tu boti fum malum tɔgɛ yele ana wa pa'ahɛ mɛ ti tu kelehe.”

ref: When Paul left the council meeting,
text: Lan paaɛ ŋwana beene la, Pɔɔl bahɛ ba mɛ.

ref: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
text: Gee ba puan basɛba yehe me na pa' Pɔɔl poore dɛna sakereba, Diyonisuse n de Areopagus lageŋɔ nɛra la, la pɔka ti ba yi'ira Damaris la basɛba n lagum bɔna la ba lagum sakɛ.

ref: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
text: Ŋwana wa n tole la, ti Pɔɔl bahɛ Atense gee kiŋɛ Korinti.

ref: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
text: Bilam ti ba ta nyɛ Yuda buuri la nɛra ayima ti ba ba yi'ira en Akuila, yehe Pɔntusi tiŋa na, nan kɔ'ɔm ze'ele Itali lebena la a pɔga Pirisila, se'ere n sɔi la, Kaiza Kulodeyose bo' la nuurɛ ti Yuuduma woo bahɛ Arome. Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ti a ta bihɛ ba,

ref: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
text: gee ba za'a n tuni tuunɛ deyina la zuo ti Pɔɔl kɛ̃ la ba, ti ba lagum tuna. Ba za'a lagum maala la gana pagehi.

ref: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
text: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ woo, Pɔɔl ni wan kɛ̃ zamehegɔ yire la puan, zigera ti a nyaŋɛ Yuda la Girikiduma la.

ref: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
text: Silase la Timoti n ta ze'ele Masedoniya paaɛ en na la, ti Pɔɔl boi la yelehum la muulegɔ puan mimi, yɛta Yuuduma la ti Kiresta la dela Yezu.

ref: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
text: Ban ka sakɛ bo' en, gee tuura en la, ti a kpa'ahɛ a futo puuhego bahɛ, gee yele ba yeti, “Ya taala dɔla ya mɛhi, mam ka tari taalɛ. Yehe zina yɛ'a ha, n nyaa wan kiŋɛ la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la zi'an.”

ref: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
text: Ti Pɔɔl bahɛ zamehegɔ yire la puan, gee yehe kiŋɛ buraa ka yima zi'an ti ka yu'urɛ yi'iri Taitusi Yusetuse, dɛna se'em n na'aheri Yinɛ, ti a yire kɔ'ɔm du' la zamehegɔ yire la.

ref: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
text: Kirisipuse n de kpɛ'ɛma zamehegɔ yire la puan la, to'e yelehum la lebege Daana la sakera, eŋa la a yire nɛreba za'a, ti Korintiduma sɛba n wum Pɔɔl yelehum muulegɔ la zo'e zo'e to'e lebege sakereba, to'e ko'om sua.

ref: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
text: Yu'uŋɔ kuyima ti Daana la yele Pɔɔl lilegere puan yeti, “Da tara dabeem, tɔgera, da sina.

ref: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
text: Ti Pɔɔl yee tiŋa la puan yuunɛ la ŋmarehi siyoobe, pa'ala nɛreba la Yinɛ yelehum la.

ref: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
text: Gee Galiyo n wa biha Akaiya tiŋa la, ti Yuuduma wa lagehɛ taaba gbirege la Pɔɔl, tari en wa'am tugum yire puan na.

ref: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
text: Nyaa yeti, “Burakana wa dole pa'ala nɛreba paaɛ paaɛ ti ba pu'uha Yinɛ sadɔɔ seko n ka dole la ze'elego la.”

ref: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
text: Pɔɔl n boi bini ti a ya'ɛ nuurɛ la, ti naba Galiyo pun yele Yuuduma la yeti, “La han deni la a tuue la se'ere bii a galum la se'ere n wan tari ni sore ti n to'e yamam Yuuduma wa suhega la.

ref: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
text: Gee kan de tugum la yu'ura la yamam mɛŋa ze'elego wa la, ya mɛhi bihɛ tɔgɛ dɛ, gee mam ka boti n dɛna gakera yele ana wa puan.”

ref: Then he sent them out of the court.
text: Gee sa'ɛ ba tugum deo la puan ti ba yehe.

ref: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
text: Ti Yuuduma la ŋmɛregɛ gurɛ Sositenese n de zamehegɔ yire la kpɛ'ɛma ayima la, ŋmɛ' en gakereba la nɛŋan, gee la bala za'a Galiyo ka be'e bihɛ ba yɛla wa puan. Pɔɔl lebe Antiyoke

ref: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
text: Pɔɔl en kɛ̃ bilam yuue fii la, a keehe sakereba la za'a mɛ, gee zom uureŋɔ kiŋɛ Siriya tiŋa, ti Pirisila la Akuila doohe en. Ti a wa'am na paaɛ Sinkireya, bahɛ ti ba kɛ̃ a zuo bilam se'ere n sɔi la a biŋe la nuurɛ.

ref: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
text: Ban wa'am na na paaɛ Ɛfisu la, a bahɛ ba la bilam, gee yia la, a kɛ̃ la zamehegɔ yire puan, ta tɔgɛ yɛla la Yuuduma la.

ref: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
text: Gee ban yele en ti a yee la ba dabeha ayi la, a ka sakɛ,

ref: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
text: En ta paaɛ sige Kasariya puan la, a kiŋɛ zom la Yerusalɛm ta pu'uhɛ lageŋɔ la, ze'ele bilam kiŋe sige Antiyoke.

ref: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
text: En kɛ̃ bilam yuue fii wa la, a bahɛ bilam mɛ gee dɔla kaara tihi la n boi Galatia la Firigiya sɔ'ɔlum puan la, kpɛmeha zahereba la za'a en boi bini la. Apolos tuuma

ref: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
text: Nananawa, Yuda buuri la nɛra ayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Apolos, wa'am Ɛfisu na. Ba dɔgɛ en la Alɛgezandiriya, ti a dɛna buraa n mi' tugum, gee me mina gulehegɔ la suŋa.

ref: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
text: A zamehɛ bi'ɛ mɛ Daana sore la puan, gee tɔgera la ninmu'urɛ, gee me pa'ala Yezu yɛla la ma'a ma'a. Gee a mi' la Yɔn ko'om sua la ma'a yele la.

ref: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
text: Ti buraa wa pɔhɛ tɔgera la buraanɛ zamehegɔ yire la puan, gee Pirisila la Akuila n kelehe en ba'ahɛ la, ba tari en yehe kiŋɛ la kinkɛleŋa ha, ta pa'alɛ en Yinɛ sore la ma'a ma'a.

ref: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Buraa wa ta maahum ti a kiŋɛ Akaiya la, suurɔ la za'a daa pa' la a poore kpɛmeha en ti a kiŋɛ, gee nyaa gulehɛ gɔŋɔ bo' zahereba la n boi bilam la yeti ba to'e en suŋa . En ta paaɛ la, a nyaŋɛ kpemehe sɛba woo ti zu'uhegɔ zuo ba lebege sakereba la mɛ.

ref: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la a kɔ'ɔm tɔgɛ mɛ la buraanɛ nyaŋɛ Yuuduma la, dɔla gulehegɔ la puan pa'alɛ ba ti Kiresta la dela Yezu.

ref: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
text: Apolos n boi Korinti la, ti Pɔɔl doohe la tinkãra zo'e zo'e puan tole wa'am na paaɛ Ɛfisu, zi'an ti a nyɛ zahereba basɛba.

ref: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
text: Ti a yele ba yeti, “Yan lebege sakereba la, ya to'e Fa'a Sia la?” Ti ba lerege yeti, “Aayi, tumam pun ka wum ti Fa'a Sia boi.”

ref: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
text: Ti Pɔɔl soke ba yeti, “Gee ba daa sɔ yamam ko'om la la ani puan?” Ti ba lerege yeti, “Yɔn ko'om sua la puan.”

ref: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
text: Ti Pɔɔl yele ba yeti, “Yɔn ko'om sua la dela suteere ko'om sua. A daa yeti nɛreba la yeti, ba nam se'em n wan wa'am eŋa pooren na la. Yezu n bala.”

ref: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Nɛreba la n wum ŋwana la, ba nyaa sɔ ba ko'om la Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la puan.

ref: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
text: Ti Pɔɔl n dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto la, ti Fa'a Sia la sige ba zuo, ti ba tɔgɛ zɛlempaala, gee purɛ.

ref: There were about twelve men in this group.
text: Ba za'a de wuu nɛreba pia la ba yi'.

ref: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
text: Ti Pɔɔl kina zamehegɔ yire la puan tara tara ŋmarehi sitã, tɔgera yelehum la la ninmu'urɛ, gee ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene se'ere n wan bahɛ ti nɛreba la bɔkɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum yɛla la.

ref: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
text: Gee basɛba kɔ'ɔm kãbɛ suure zagehɛ yelehum la, gee yaala Sore la nɛreba la nɛŋan la, ti Pɔɔl bahɛ ba, gee tari sakereba la kina zamehegɔ yire ti ba yi'ira Tairanuse la puan, ta pa'ala pa'ali seko ti nɛreba wan bɔkɛ ba daare woo.
