ref: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
text: A iti la bala tara tara la yuuma ayi', ti la ŋwana wa bahɛ sɛba woo n boi Esiya dɛna Yuda buuri bii Giriki buuri la woo wum Daana yelehum la. Seva dayɔɔhi

ref: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
text: Ti Yinɛ dɔla Pɔɔl puan tuna paŋa tuunkãra sɛ'a n boi si tɔka,

ref: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
text: ti ba nyaa tara Pɔɔl nɛnduuhega bii a fuo tanɛ n kalum a iŋa kina pagela bã'duma zuo ti ba bã'ahi bahera ba, ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi ɛgera yɛhera na gee bahera nɛreba.

ref: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
text: Ti Yuda buuri la puan sɛba eere sa'aha sɛbɛ'ɛhi me zigɛ ti ba dikɛ Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la sa'ahɛ kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi n boi sɛba puan la, yɛta yeti, “N sa'ari fum mɛ Yezu se'em ti Pɔɔl muula a yele la puan.”

ref: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
text: Budibeto tuyopɔi n de Yuuduma pu'uhegɔ malema nɛŋadaana ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Seva, en zigeri ita ŋwana wa.

ref: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
text: Gee Kulekpa'bɛ'a la lerege ba yeti, “Han dɛna Yezu mam mi, la Pɔɔl mam mi' gee yamam dela bani?”

ref: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
text: Ti Buraa la ti kulekpa'bɛ'a la bɔna ka puan la ɛkɛ ba' ba, gbirege la ba ta nyaŋɛ ba za'a, ti ba zɔ yire la puan yehe na bɛɛla gee pɔgelum.

ref: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Yele dina wa n lu sɛba woo n boi Ɛfisu puan tuban la, Yuda buuri la Giriki buuri la woo n boi Ɛfisu puan ani la suram la dabehekãtɛ, gee ti ba zɛkera Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la.

ref: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
text: Ti zo'e zo'e la n lebege sakereba la me purɛ ba be'em gee pa'alɛ bamam n ni tuna se'em la.

ref: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
text: Ti sɛba me n ni tuna tiim tuuma la me kekeko me va ba tiim gɔnɔ la na nyɔ' tɔ nɛreba nɛŋan. Ban magehɛ gɔnɔ la bihɛ la, tu wan koohe ni paaɛ wuu salempɛɛlega tuha pihinuu.

ref: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
text: Ŋwana ti Daana yelehum malum zɔ'ɔra la paŋa gee tɔ'ɔra zi'an woo. Ɛfisu gbiregeri

ref: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
text: Yele ana wa woo n iŋɛ ba'ahɛ la, Pɔɔl maahum mɛ Sia la puan ti a doohe Masedoniya la Akaiya tihi puan gee nyaa tole Yerusalɛm. A yele bo' a mɛŋa yeti, “N han paaɛ bilam, n wan ba'ahɛ paaɛ Arome.”

ref: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
text: Bala ti a tum a suŋereba bayi', Timoti la Erastuse, ti ba kiŋɛ Masedoniya, gee ti eŋa mɛŋa yee Esiya yuue fii wa.

ref: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
text: Sankaŋa puan la ti gbiregeri kãtɛ en sige, doohe Sore la zuo.

ref: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
text: Buraa yu'urɛ n yi'iri Demetereyose, en de salempɛɛlega kuta, nyaa dikɛ salempɛɛlega kure Atemis tinganɛ la, ti tuun-dina wa tari ligeri tuunkãtɛ na bo' tuntuneba.

ref: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
text: Ti a kã'ahɛ nɛrebana wa woo, la nɛresɛba woo n lagum tuna tuun-ana taaba lagehɛ taaba na yele ba yeti, “N taaba wa, ya mi' ti tu tarega wa yehe la tuun-ana wa puan.

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: Ya me nyɛ ya, gee me wum ti, dagi Ɛfisu ma'a puan, gee la ŋwani ti Esiya wa puan za'a, Pɔɔl wa doohe pã'ahɛ mɛ gee vɛ' nɛrekuuŋɔ yehe, doohe la en tɔgeri yeti, yina sɛ'a ti ba dikɛ nu'uhi maalɛ la dagi yina.

ref: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
text: Ŋwana wa tari la nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega kina na, dagi la tu tuuma wa ma'a n wan bahɛ yoo, gee tinganɛ kãtɛ Atemis en de bage kãtɛ zɔŋɔ la wan lebege kɛhega lɔkɔ, ti ba faaɛ a pansɛka ti a tara ta'am lagehɛ Esiya tiŋa wa, la timpaberɛ wa za'a ti ba kina na'aha en la.”

ref: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Ban wum ŋwana wa la, la wuŋɛ ba mɛ ti ba suya nyiie, ti ba kaahɛ tagurega yeti, “Ɛfisuduma Atemis n gani za'a.”

ref: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
text: Ti gbiregeri sige tiŋa la puan, ti nɛreba zɔ kalam kiŋɛ de'eŋo yire la puan, gee vɛ'ɛra Gayɔse la Aristakuse n de Masedoniya, dole Pɔɔl ɛɛra la.

ref: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
text: Ti Pɔɔl bɔta ti a gɛregɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la ha, gee ti a zahereba la ka sakɛ bo' en.

ref: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
text: Ti Esiya kpɛ'ɛnduma sɛba n de Pɔɔl zɔduma la tum sɔɔleŋa bo' en na ti a da makɛ nɛ na'arɛ de'eŋo yire la puan.

ref: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
text: Gee bala ma'a la ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la nyaa yabera bura bura. Bana yɛta sɛla, ti bana me yɛta sɛla ha, se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka mi' ban boi zi'an, gee ti ba zo'e zo'e me ka mina se'ere zuo ti ba lagehɛ na.

ref: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la basɛba ka'ana Alɛgezanda la ti Yuuduma la za'ɛ en bahɛ na. Ti Alɛgezanda wa doohe yibehɛ nɛreba la ti nɛreba la go'e, gee nyaa zigera en wan iŋɛ se'em tɔgɛ suŋɛ a mɛŋa nɛreba la nɛŋan.

ref: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Gee nɛreba la n ta baŋɛ ti a dela Yuda buuri la, ti ba dikɛ gogo kaareŋa ayi' la, kaahɛ la nɔyinɛ baha na yeti, “Ɛfisu yinɛ Atemis n gani za'a.”

ref: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
text: Gee tiŋa la gɔnguleha en ta bahɛ ti nɛreba la go'e la, a yele ba yeti, “Ɛfisu tiŋambiihi wa, ani n gee ka mina ti Ɛfisu wa n biheri Atemis se'em n gani za'a la zɔŋɔ la, la bage se'ere saazuo ha lu na la?

ref: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
text: Yele ana wa n kan ta'am sugɛ la zuo, la dela ya ana bããlam gee da kabelɛ tum tuunse'ere yoo.

ref: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
text: Yan tari buraahi wa wa'am na ka'lam na wa, si ka fa' tu zɔŋɔ wa puan sɛla bii tu' tu bagerɛ wa.

ref: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
text: Ya han le bɔta ti ya baŋɛ la se'ere pa'ahɛ, ba paaɛ sɛba n biheri nɛreba yɛla la zi'an.

ref: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, tu ka zaaɛ ba kɛɛ tɔ zina ti tu gbiti mɛ. Se'ere n sɔi la, tu ka tari se'ere ti tu wan yele doohe gbiregeri dina puan.”

ref: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
text: En tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a yele nɛreba la mɛ ti ba yiregɛ.

ref: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
text: Gbiregere la n ma'ɛ la, ti Pɔɔl tum yi' a zahereba la na, kpemehe ba, gee keehe ba, gee yɛ'a Masedoniya.

ref: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
text: En ta paaɛ bilam doohe kaaɛ tihi la za'a kpemehe sakereba la suŋa suŋa ba'ahɛ la, a kiŋɛ la Girise,

ref: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
text: Bilam ti a ta kɛ̃ ŋmarehi sitã. A ta maahum mɛ ti a kiŋɛ Siriya, ti Yuuduma la maahum gbirege la en, ti bala la zuo, ti a iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a lebe Masedoniya.

ref: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text: Ti Faruse dayua Sopata n ze'ele Beroya la, kɔlegɛ en, nyaa dikɛ Aristakuse, Sekunduse n yehe Tɛsalonika na, Gayɔse n yehe Dɛɛbi, la Timoti nyaa dikɛ Takikuse la Tirofimuse n yehe Esiya.

ref: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
text: Bana woo diŋɛ la nɛŋa ta gura tu Torowase,

ref: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
text: gee ti tumam zom uureŋɔ kiŋɛ Filipi tiŋa, ti boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo la tole, ti dabeha anuu daarɛ ti tu paaɛ ba Torowase, ti tu kɛ̃ bilam dabeha ayopɔi. Pɔɔl keehe ti a kiŋɛ Torowase

ref: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
text: Dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ, ti tu lagehɛ na ti tu di Daana tuulum dia la, ti Pɔɔl nyaa suhera suhega la ba, la eŋa n maahum ti a bahɛ yiimbeere la. A tɔgɛ la bala ta paaɛ yu'uŋɔ sunsua.

ref: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
text: Ti ba lamehɛ bugum zo'e zo'e bɔ'ɔ la n boi saazuo ha ti ba bɔna ku puan suhera la puan.

ref: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
text: Ti budibele paalega ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Yutikuse, zi'a lɛm takole nuuren, ti guum zɛɛ en ti a giha gee ti Pɔɔl kelum tɔgera wɔgera. Ti budibela wa gihi tole nyaa ta ze'ele yitugema butã puan la, lu ta paaɛ tiŋa, ti ba ɔ̃begɛ en ti a ki mɛ.

ref: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
text: Gee Pɔɔl sige mɛ, ta nyigele bia la zuo, ɔ̃begɛ en a nu'uhen, gee yeti, “Da bahɛ ya ti la dɛna daaŋɔ gee a vom ka kiŋɛ zi'an.”

ref: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
text: Ti Pɔɔl le zom saazuo la ha, ti ban ta di Daana tuulum dia la ba'ahɛ la, ti a le pɔhɛ suhega la ba tara tara ti beere ta yilegera, ti a nyaa bahɛ ba gee kiŋɛ.

ref: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
text: Gee budibela la n lu na la, vo'e mɛ ti ba tari en kiŋɛ la pupeelenkãtɛ. Torowase yɛ'a Militusi

ref: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
text: Ti tu kiŋɛ ta zom uureŋɔ yɛ'a Asɔsi, ti'iha ti tu wan paaɛ Pɔɔl bilam dikɛ en, se'ere n sɔi la, a pun iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a kan doohe ko'om puan.

ref: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
text: En tu'uhɛ tu Asɔsi la, ti tu dikɛ en pa'ahɛ kiŋɛ Mitilene.

ref: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
text: Tu ze'ele la bilam dikɛ uureŋɔ, ti yiimbeere ti tu paaɛ Kiyose. Ti yiimbeere le yɛ'ɛha ti tu paaɛ Samose, ti dabehe se'ere n tagelɛ la, ti tu paaɛ Militusi.

ref: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, Pɔɔl iŋɛ a suure ti a mɔm zo'e Ɛfisu mɛ la a da gã'arɛ Esiya la, a nini ba' mu'ɛ la a paaɛ Yerusalɛm, la han wan ta'am ni iŋɛ, a paaɛ ni dabeha pinuu de'eŋo daarɛ la. Pɔɔl tɔgɛ bo' Ɛfisu kiinduma

ref: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
text: Militusi puan, ti Pɔɔl bɔna gee tum sɔɔleŋa bo' Ɛfisu, yɛta ti a boti a zi'ire mɛ la lageŋɔ la kiinduma la.

ref: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
text: Kiinduma la n wa'am na paaɛ en la, ti a yele ba yeti, “Yamam mɛhi mi' mam daa boi la ya se'em, ta paaɛ daan se'ere daarɛ ti mam pɔhɛ nɛ Esiya puan.

ref: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
text: N tuni bɔ'ɔra Daana la la sigehe mɛŋa la nintam, ŋmibera mageha sɛ'a ti Yuuduma wa ni maahum ti a paaɛ mam.

ref: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
text: N ka zagehɛ ti n tum se'ere n wan tara suŋɛ. N muuli yelehum la mɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, gee pa'ala ya nɛrekuuŋɔ puan, dɔla yire yire,

ref: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
text: gee yɛta Yuuduma la, la Girikiduma ti ba tee ba suya lebe Yinɛ zi'an, gee sakɛ bo' Yezu

ref: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
text: Ti nananawa, mam Fa'a Sia la da'aberɛ wa ye' la Yerusalɛm, n ka mi' sɛla n wan ta iŋɛ mam bini,

ref: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
text: han dagena Fa'a Sia la mɛŋa n yeti mam tiŋa woo ti n paara ka puan yeti, ti paka la mɛlehegɔ en ga' gura mam bini.

ref: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Gee mam ka biheri n vom la ti bu tara vuurɛ bo' mam, n han wa nyaŋɛ ni ba'ahɛ n tuunɛ wa, la tuunse'ere ti n to'e tu Daana Yezu ze'a ti n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa, n de Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ yɛla wa.

ref: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
text: “Nananawa, mam mi' ti yamam sɛba woo ti n tari ya doohe muula Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa yɛla la, ya ayima kan le nyɛ mam la ya niho.

ref: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
text: Bala zuo ti mam yɛta ya zina wa la ninmu'urɛ ti ya se'em han bɔregɛ, ya taala ka dole mam,

ref: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
text: se'ere n sɔi la, mam daa ka zagehɛ ti n yele la ninmu'urɛ, Yinɛ puti'irɛ za'a n de se'ere.

ref: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
text: Biheri ya, ya mɛhi suŋa, gee me biha piihi la ti Fa'a Sia la dikɛ ya ti ya biha la. Ya biha Yinɛ lageŋɔ la ti a dikɛ a mɛŋa Dayua ziim to'e ku la.

ref: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
text: Mam mi' ti mam han tole ba'ahɛ, sahi n obere wan wa'am na lagum ya, kan bahɛ piihi la.

ref: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
text: Basɛba wan pun yehe yamam mɛŋa puan gɛ̃ta yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti ba ta'am pã'ahɛ zahereba la basɛba ti ba doohe ba poore.

ref: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
text: Bala zuo la, ani ya wire, gee tɛra ti yuuma atã ti mam ka go'e n tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa, ka'ana ya ayima ayima la ninmu'urɛ la nintam.

ref: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
text: Gee nananawa, mam dikɛ ya ita la Yinɛ nu'uhen la a zu'uhegɔ yelehum la puan. Yelehum sebo n wan ta'am mɛ ya ze'ele, gee bo' ya tɔtɛ la sɛba n pee ba mɛhi la.

ref: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
text: N ka fã nɛra salempɛɛlega, bii salema bii futo.

ref: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
text: Ya mɛhi la mi' ti mam daa tuni mɛ la n nu'uhi suŋera n mɛŋa la sɛba n tuni la mam.

ref: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
text: Yele ana wa woo puan la, mam iŋɛ mɛ pa'ali ya, ti tu han tuna bala tu wan ta'am suŋɛ sɛba n ka kpe'em la, doohe la tumam n tɛri tu Daana Yezu yelehum la. Se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa yeti, ‘Sunkãtɛ boi mɛ la se'em n bo'ori, gana se'em n to'ohe.’ ”

ref: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
text: En tɔgɛ ŋwana woo ba'ahɛ bo' ba la, a kpa' la duma la ba za'a suhɛ bo' ba.

ref: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
text: ti ba suya sagum mɛ yele yele woo en yele ba yeti ba pun kan le malum nyɛ en la niho le yɛ'ɛha la. Ti ba nyaa tari en na ki'ihɛ uureŋɔ.

ref: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
text: Tumam n bahɛ nɛreba la gee zom tu uureŋɔ yɛ'a la, tu kɔ'ɔm kiŋɛ la tuuntɔɔ ta paaɛ Kose, ti yiimbeere ti tu paaɛ Arodisi, ze'ele bilam tole Patara.

ref: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
text: Ti tu nyɛ ti uureŋɔ boi bilam maahum ti ku kiŋɛ Fonisiya, ti tu zom, ti ku yɛ'a.

ref: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
text: Ti tu ta nyɛta Sapuruse, ti tu bahɛ tiŋa la tu gɔbega bɔba gee kiŋɛ ta paaɛ Siriya kiŋɛ ta sige Taai, se'ere n sɔi la uureŋɔ la zɛ la lɔgerɔ bɔta ti ba yehe lɔgerɔ la bilam.

ref: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
text: Tu kɛ̃'a dabeha la bilam n ba'ahɛ la, tu bahɛ bilam mɛ gee le to'e tu sore. Ti sakereba la za'a, ba pɔgeba la ba kɔma beele tu ta yehe tiŋa la puan. Bilam ti tu za'a kpa' duma mɔgerɛ la nuuren pu'uhɛ,

ref: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
text: gee keehe taaba, ti tumam kiŋɛ ta zom uureŋɔ ti bamam me lebe kula ba yiya.

ref: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
text: Tumam n yehe Taai tiŋa na daarɛ la, tu kelum kina la bala ta paaɛ Pitolemayese, pu'uhɛ suurɔ gee kɛ̃ la ba daanyinɛ.

ref: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
text: Yiimbeere ti tu wa'am na paaɛ Kasariya, ta kɛ̃ Filipi n de ayehemuulɛ daana, se'em n de ba bayopɔi ayima la yire, kɛ̃ la en.

ref: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
text: Filipi wa tari la pɔgesa'kaha anaahi, en puti.

ref: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
text: Tumam n wa bɔna la ba dabeha zo'e zo'e la, ti nɔtɔgeha ayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Agabuse ze'ele Yudiya wa'am na.

ref: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
text: Wa'am tumam zi'an na, paaɛ na dikɛ Pɔɔl sɛganɛ lu' a mɛŋa nama la a nu'uhi, gee yeti, Fa'a Sia la yeti, “Ŋwana ti Yuuduma la n boi Yerusalɛm la wan ta lu' burasɛka n sɔi sɛganɛ wa, dikɛ en bu sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la.”

ref: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
text: Tumam n wum ŋwana la, tumam la sakereba la n boi bilam la tɔgɛ mɛ la en ninmu'urɛ ti a da kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm.

ref: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
text: Ti Pɔɔl lerege ba yeti, “Ya iti la bem n bala, ya kele mɛ Sagena mam suure? N yele ya ti, mam ka maahum ti ba bobe mam ma'a, gee n pun ki Yerusalɛm, Daana Yezu yu'urɛ wa zuo.”

ref: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
text: En ka maahum ti a tee a puti'irɛ la, tu yee la suram, han dagena tu yeti, “Daana la sumboolum tum.”

ref: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
text: Dabeha ana n ta tole la, ti tu maahum, to'e sore yɛ'a zɔna Yerusalɛm.

ref: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
text: Ti zahereba la basɛba n yehe Kasariya la, lagum doohe tu, tari tu na paaɛ Neeson n yehe Sapuruse tiŋa la yire, se'em en de yia yia zahera la ayima la. Se'em ti tu wan kɛ̃ en la en la, en bala. Pɔɔl bihɛ Yemesi Yerusalɛm

ref: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
text: Tumam n paaɛ Yerusalɛm la, suurɔ la n boi bini la to'e tu mɛ suŋa.

ref: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
text: Yiimbeere ti Pɔɔl tari tu kiŋɛ ti a ta bihɛ Yemesi, a ta paaɛ la ti kiinduma la za'a boi mɛ.

ref: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
text: Ti en pu'uhɛ ba ba'ahɛ la, a pɔhɛ mɛ lɔɔha yele yele doohe sɛla ti Yinɛ tum bo' sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la eŋa tuuma la puan.

ref: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
text: Ban wum yɛla la ba'ahɛ la, ba pɛgɛ la Yinɛ. Gee nyaa yele en yeti, “Fum nyɛ ya, tu suuɔ wa, bihɛ sakereba en de tuha tuha bilam se'em nyaa pa'ahɛ Yuuduma la puan, gee ti ba za'a me nini kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ la ze'elego la.

ref: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
text: Ba pun yele ba mɛ, fum yele, ti fum pa'ali Yuuduma sɛba woo n boi la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la mɛ ti ba zagehɛ Moose, gee le yeti fum yeti ba ti ba da ŋmata ba kɔma, gee me da dɔla malema la.

ref: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
text: Beni n de tu iŋɛ? Ba wan ba'ahum wum ti fum wa'am na.

ref: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
text: Bala zuo la, doohe sɛla ti tu wan yele la. Tu tari la buraahi sinaahi n biŋe ba nuurɛ la pɔta.

ref: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
text: Bala, pa'ahɛ ba puan la fum lagum malum peere malema la la ba, gee yɔ ti ba siie ba zuto la. Bala nɛrawoo wan baŋɛ ti sɛla ka boi nɛreba n yele fum yele se'em la, gee ba wan nyɛ ti fum mɛŋa dole gee nana ze'elego la mɛ suŋa suŋa.

ref: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Gee han dɛna sɛba n dagi Yuuduma gee lebege sakereba la, tumam tum gɔŋɔ bo' ba ha yeti, ba da dita sɛla ti n kaabɛ bo' bagerɛ, ba da dita ziim, la ba da dita sɛla ti ba kɛge ku, gee yehe ba mɛhi yaluntuuma puan.”

ref: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
text: Ti Pɔɔl tari buraahi wa, eŋa n pee a mɛŋa ba'ahɛ la yiimbeere, kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la ba, ta malum peere malema dabeha la za'a nɛrekuuŋɔ la nɛŋan, ta paaɛ se'em ma'a ti ba wan kaabɛ kabegɛ bo' ba woo ayima ayima la. Ba nyɔkɛ Pɔɔl Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan

ref: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
text: Dabeha ayopɔi la n pun ta wuna ti a ba'ahɛ me la, ti Yuda buuri sɛba n ze'ele Esiya na, gee nyɛ Pɔɔl pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la, gbirege nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a bahɛ. Ti ba nyɔkɛ Pɔɔl,

ref: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
text: gee tahera yeti, “Tu Izirale taaba wa, suŋɛ ya mam. Burakana wa n bala pa'ala nɛrawoo zi'an woo ti ba da nana tumam nɛreba, tu ze'elego, la ze'kana wa. Sɛla n pun gana bala dela a tari la Giriki nɛreba wa'am na kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire wa puan, galum tuulum zi'an wa.”

ref: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
text: Se'ere n sɔi ŋwana wa la dela, nɛreba la pun diŋɛ nyɛ la Tirofimuse n de Ɛfisu nɛra la ti Pɔɔl dɔla la en tiŋa puan ha la, ti ba kɔ'ɔm ti'ihɛ ti a tari en kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan me na.

ref: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
text: Ti tiŋa la za'a gbirege, ti nɛreba la zɔ lagehɛ taaba kalam. Gurɛ Pɔɔl vuue en yehe pu'uhegɔ yire la yiŋa, gee yu' kulehi la bilam bilam.

ref: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
text: Ban zigeri ti ba ku en la, ti sɔɔleŋa paaɛ pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana la yeti, Yerusalɛm za'a gbiregeri mɛ

ref: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text: Ti bilam bilam ti a yehe pɛntiireba la ba nɛheduma en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega kɔbega zɔ paaɛ zi'an la puan. Nɛrekuuŋɔ en nyɛ kpɛ'ɛma la la a pɛntiireba la, ti ba go'e Pɔɔl ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ.

ref: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
text: Ti pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana wa paaɛ nyɔkɛ Pɔɔl gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba lu' en la zɔtɔ tuyi' Gee nyaa viiha eŋa n de se'em la en eŋa sɛla.

ref: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la basɛba vɔɔla ti ŋwana, ti basɛba me vɔɔla ti ŋwana ha. Ti eŋa n kan baŋɛ sɛla n iŋɛ doohe la voole la zuo la, ti a bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari en wa'am pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la na.

ref: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
text: Pɔɔl n ta zɔna kura la zuo la, nɛreba la malum gbirege gaŋɛ la bala, ti pɛntiireba la ɔ̃begɛ Pɔɔl buke.

ref: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la n dole la kɔ'ɔm tahera mɛ yeti, “Ba'ahɛ ya en ha Ba'ahɛ ya en.” Pɔɔl tɔgɛ suŋɛ a mɛŋa

ref: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
text: Ban ta pɔra ti ba tari Pɔɔl kɛ̃ pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la, ti Pɔɔl yele nɛŋadaana la yeti, “N wan ta'am tɔgɛ se'ere bo' fum?” Ti nɛŋadaana la soke en yeti, “Fum tã tɔgera Giriki mɛ?

ref: “Aren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led
text: Fum ye dagi Igipiti tiŋa nɛra la n gbirege, tari zabe-zabereba tuha anaahi yehe kɛ̃ muuen ha la?”

ref: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
text: Ti Pɔɔl lerege en yeti, “Mam dela Yuda buuri, yehe Tasuse n boi Kilikiya sɔ'ɔlum puan la. Mam dela tiŋa n en tari yu'kãtɛ tiŋambia. N beleni fum mɛ bo' mam sore ti n tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba wa.”

ref: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
text: Ti nɛŋadaana wa bo' en sore, ti Pɔɔl zom yagelɛ kura la zuo, yele nɛreba la yeti, ba yeen go'e, ti ban iŋɛ bããlam la, a tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba la mɛ Hiburu yetɔgum puan yeti:

ref: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
text: Ban wum ti a tɔgeri la Hiburu yetɔgum puan bɔ'ɔra ba la, ti ba malum iŋɛ bããlam pa'ahɛ. Ti a nyaa yeti:

ref: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
text: “Mam dela Yuda buuri, ti ba dɔgɛ mam Tasuse tiŋa n boi Kilikiya sɔ'ɔlum puan la, gee mam zo'e la tinkana puan, ti mam zi'ire Gamaliyɛl nama tiŋa, ti a pa'alɛ mam gee, zamehi mam nima nima ti yaabeduma pa'alegɔ la, ti mam mu'ɛ la nini boi Yinɛ woo yamam za'a n ana se'em zina wa.

ref: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
text: Mam pɔgelum Sore wa mɛ, pun ta paaɛ n kuura ba, doohe la mam yuum ni bobe sɛba n boi Sore wa puan ba buraahi la ba pɔgehi, tari ba ta pagɛ paka deto puan,

ref: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
text: malema nɛŋadaana la la pu'uhegɔ yire kiinduma la woo wan tɔgɛ mam yele pa'alɛ ya. Bama zi'an ti mam to'e gɔŋɔ ta bo' tu suurɔ n boi Damasekuse. N kiŋɛ bini ti n ta bobe la sakereba la n boi bini, gee tari ba lebe Yerusalɛm ti ba mɛlehɛ ba. Pɔɔl tɔgɛ a teere yele (Tuuma

ref: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
text: “Mam n boi sore zuo kina ta pɔra Damasekuse la, wuu wuntɛɛŋa zuo tiŋasuka, ti mam ta yeti n bihɛ la, ti bugum kãtɛ n ze'ele saazuo ha nyakɛ kaaɛ mam ze'ele.

ref: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Ti mam lu tiŋa, gee wum ti kua yi'iri mam yeti, ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, beni zuo ti fum mɛleha mam?’

ref: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
text: Ti mam lerege yeti, ‘Fum dela ani n Daana?’ Ti kua la yele mam yeti, ‘Mam dela Yezu n yehe Nazareti na la, Mam n bala ti fum dɔla mɛleha la.’

ref: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
text: Lan iŋɛ ŋwana la, yelemɛŋerɛ sɛba n dole la mam la nyɛ bugum la, gee ka wum se'em n tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra mam la kua.

ref: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
text: Ti mam soke yeti, ‘Beni ti n nyaa wan iŋɛ n Daana?’ Ti Daana la yele mam yeti, ‘Ihege la fum kɛ̃ Damasekuse. Bilam ti ba wan ta yele fum, sɛla woo ti mam looe fum ti fum tum.’

ref: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
text: Mam ka nyɛti, doohe la bugum la n ba' nyakɛ ku n nimbaahi la zuo, ti sɛba n dole mam la yari gurɛ n nu'o ve'e mam ta kɛ̃ Damasekuse puan.

ref: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
text: “Buraa kayima n de Ananiya kaabɛ ka mɛŋa bo' ze'elego la, ti Yuda buuri la woo n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri Damasekuse puan la yi'ira ka yu'sunɛ la,

ref: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
text: Kaŋa n wa wa'am na, zɛ'a lɛm mam gee yeti, ‘N suuɔ Sɔɔl, lebe nyɛta.’ Sankaŋa ti mam lebehe nyɛta gorege saazuo nyɛ en.

ref: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
text: Ti a nyaa yele mam yeti, ‘Ti yaabeduma Yinɛ n looe fum ti fum baŋɛ a suboolum, fum nyɛ Tuntɔɔ Daana wa, gee wum a kua.

ref: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
text: Bala, nananawa, ŋwani ti fum gu'una? Ihege ze'ele, la fum sɔ ko'om, ti ba saalum fum be'em bahɛ, fum yi'iri a yu'urɛ la.’ Ba tum Pɔɔl sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la zi'an

ref: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
text: “Mam lebe Yerusalɛm na ba'ahɛ la, ti daarɛ deyima ti n bɔna bini pu'uha, pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti guum zɛɛ mam,

ref: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
text: ti n nyɛ en ti a yɛta mam yeti, ‘Iŋɛ kalam la fum yehe Yerusalɛm wa tɔtɔ, se'ere n sɔi la, ba kan to'e fum yeti fum tɔgɛ mam yele se'em wa.’

ref: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
text: Ti mam yeti, ‘N Daana, ba mɛhi mi' ti, mam doohe zamehegɔ yiya woo puan me, ŋmɛ'ɛra gee pagera sɛba woo n nam bo' fum la mɛ.

ref: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
text: Ti sansɛka ti ba ta ka'ara fum Aze' nyɛ daana Siteven ziim bahera la, mam mɛŋa n ze' bini pa' ba poore ti ba ku, gee to'ohe ba n lobere en kuura la futo zãla.’

ref: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
text: Ti a yele mam yeti, ‘Kiŋɛ, gee n yeti n tum fum la zaaɛ zaarɛ, sɛba n dagi Yuuduma zi'an.’ ” Pɔɔl la Arome tugum duma

ref: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
text: Ŋwana wa beene woo la, ti nɛreba la kelehe en mɛ, gee kɔ'ɔm malum tahera baha na yeti, “Ba'ahɛ ya nɛre-ina taaba yehe timpaberɛ wa zuo. Se'ere n sɔi la, a pun dagena ba bo' en sore ti a vɔna.”

ref: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
text: Ban taheri bala la, gee yɛɛha ba futo la fɔmbera tiŋa gee vaara tintɔnɔ lɔbera saazuo la,

ref: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
text: ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la bo' sore ti ba tari en lebe pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la puan, la ba viihɛ en gee ŋmɛ' en ti a baŋɛ se'ere zuo ti kaahi dɔɔrɔ tuna sigera a zuo wa.

ref: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
text: Gee ban lu' en ta' la gu'uhi la, Pɔɔl yele pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana se'em en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la yeti, “La de de sore ti fum ŋmɛ' Arome tiŋambia sɛka ti ba ka nyɛ ka tuure?”

ref: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
text: Pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega wa wa n wum ŋwana la, a kiŋɛ la pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la zi'an ta soke ba yeti, “Fum boi beni ti fum iŋɛ la bem n bala? Buraa wa dela Arome wa tiŋambia.”

ref: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
text: Ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la wa'am na soke Pɔɔl yeti, “Alaa, yele mam, fum dela Arome tiŋambia?” Ti Pɔɔl yeti, “Ɛ̃ɛ.”

ref: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
text: Ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la yeti, “Mam yɔ la kɔɔba zo'e zo'e gee nyaŋɛ to'e n tiŋambiine gɔŋɔ wa.” Ti Pɔɔl yele en yeti, “Ba nuun dɔgɛ mam la Arome tiŋa wa.”

ref: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
text: Bilam bilam ti sɛba woo n ze' ni ti ba viihɛ en la fɔ lebe pooren gee bahɛ en, ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la me zɔ dabeem, se'ere n sɔi la, a baŋɛ ti Pɔɔl dela Arome tiŋambia gee ti a sina gee ba' en. Pɔɔl paaɛ Kiinduma nɛŋan

ref: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
text: Pɛntiireba kɛ'ɛma la n boti a baŋɛ sɛla ti Yuuduma tara zuuha Pɔɔl la zuo la, ti yiimbeere ti a yu'ɛ en yehe na, gee bo' malema kiinduma la ba kima la za'a nuurɛ ti ba lagehɛ. Ti a nyaa tari Pɔɔl sige ba ze'a na, bahɛ ti Pɔɔl zɛ'a ba nɛŋan.

ref: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
text: Pɔɔl n ze' biha kiinduma zii la, a ta yeti, “N suurɔ, wa'am na paaɛ zina, mam vɔi n vom mɛ tuuntɔɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan.”

ref: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
text: Ŋwana ti Ananiya n de malema nɛŋadaana la kiima la, tahɛ sɛba n ze' lɛm Pɔɔl ti ba ŋmɛ' a nuurɛ la.

ref: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
text: A yetɔgebuna ti Pɔɔl me yele en yeti, “Yinɛ wan ŋmɛ' fum nangoonpeele wa. Fum zi' bilam ti fum gakɛ mam doohe ze'elego la, gee la bala za'a fum galum ze'elego mɛ nyaa ta bo' nuurɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' mam?”

ref: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
text: Ti sɛba n ze' lɛm Pɔɔl la yele en yeti, “Fum dela ani tuura Yinɛ malema kiima?”

ref: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
text: Ti Pɔɔl yeti, “Mam ka baŋɛ n suurɔ, ti a dela malema kiima. Se'ere n sɔi la, gulehegɔ la yeti, ‘Da tɔgɛ fum nɛreba nɛŋa daana be'em.’ ”

ref: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
text: Pɔɔl n ta baŋɛ ti nɛreba la basɛba dela Sadusii duma ti basɛba dɛna Farasiiduma la, ti a yi' kiinduma la buki yeti, “N suurɔ, mam dela Farasii, mam dela Farasii dayua. Mam boi tugum wa puan wa dela, beere puti'irɛ n ihegere la puan.”

ref: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
text: Pɔɔl n yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, ti nɔkpe'ene lu Farasiiduma la, la Sadusiiduma la tiŋasuka, ti lageŋɔ la welege buyi'.

ref: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
text: Ti nɔkpe'en kãtɛ ihege, ti Farasii gɔnmi'ineba basɛba ihege yeti, “Tumam ka nyɛ tuure se'ere buraa wa puan. Gee la han dɛna la sira ti Sia bii Suŋera n sirum tɔgɛ bo' en?”

ref: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
text: Nɔkpe'ene la n nyaa ta lebege zaberɛ la, ti dabeem kɛ̃ pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma doohe la ba ka gagɛ ba aahum Pɔɔl zɔra zɔra la, ti a bo' pɛntiireba la nuurɛ ti ba sige ta pɛregɛ Pɔɔl wa'am pɛntiireba bɔŋa ze'a la puan na.

ref: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
text: Yu'ukuŋɔ puan la, ti Daana la wa'am na zɛ'a lɛm Pɔɔl, gee yele en yeti, “Tara sukãberɛ, wuu fum n muulɛ mam yele se'em Yerusalɛm puan la, bala mɛa muulɛ mam yele Arome wa puan.” Ban maaheni se'em ti ba ku Pɔɔl

ref: The next morning more than
text: Ba me gaŋɛ nɛreba pinaahi n ku nɔdina wa.

ref: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
text: Ti ba kiŋɛ malema kiinduma la yizuto kiinduma la zi'an ta yele ba yeti, “Tumam kɔ'ɔm pɔ la nuurɛ ti dia kan tole tumam nuuren han dagena tu ku la Pɔɔl.

ref: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
text: Bala la, nananawa, yamam la kiinduma la bahɛ ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma mina ŋwana, la a bahɛ ti ba tari Pɔɔl wa'am ya zi'a na, wuu ya boti ya malum viihɛ la a yele la paaɛ paaɛ la. Gee tumam pun maahum mɛ ti tu wan ba'ahɛ en ti a pun kan paaɛ yamam nɛŋan na.”

ref: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
text: Gee Pɔɔl taya dayua wum ban yeti ba sugɛ se'em gura Pɔɔl, me kiŋɛ ta nyaŋɛ nyɛ sore kɛ̃ pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la, dikɛ ŋwana yele Pɔɔl.

ref: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
text: Ti Pɔɔl yi' pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega yele en yeti, “Tari budibele paalega wa kiŋɛ pɛntiira kpɛ'ɛma wa zi'an gee a tari se'ere ti a ko'ohe en.”

ref: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
text: Bala ti kpɛ'ɛma wa pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana se'em en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega pɛntiireba wa, to'e budibela wa, tari en kiŋɛ pɛntiireba kɛ'ɛma la ze'a na yele en yeti, “Pɔɔl n boi paka deo puan la n yi' mam, yele mam yeti n tari budibela wa wa'am fum ze'a na, gee ka tari se'ere ti ka yele bo' fum.”

ref: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
text: Ti pɛntiira kpɛ'ɛma gi' budibela wa nu'o, tari ka lebe kinkɛleŋa ba ma'a, ta soke en yeti, “Beni n de la ti fum tara ti fum yele mam?”

ref: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
text: Ti budibela la lerege yeti, “Yuubuuri la ku la nuurɛ ti ba beere tum nɛreba fum zi'an ti fum bahɛ ti Pɔɔl wa'am ba zi'an wuu ba boti ba malum viihɛ la a yele la paaɛ paaɛ la.

ref: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
text: Ŋwana ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma wa bahɛ budibela wa ti ka kiŋɛ, gee yele en yeti, “Da pun yele zugɔ yiŋa me ti fum yele mam yele dina wa.” Ba ta'ahɛ Pɔɔl timbihega nɛŋa daana Felikise zi'an

ref: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
text: Maahum yire me bo' Pɔɔl ti a ba', ti fum tari en la inma'ahum ta bo' timbihega nɛŋadaana Felikise.”

ref: The commander wrote a letter that said:
text: Gee nyaa gulehɛ yele la n de se'em, gɔŋɔ yeti:

ref: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
text: “Mam Kulodeyose Lisiyase n gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra fum zuo daana, timbihega nɛŋadaana Felikise na. N pu'uhi fum na.

ref: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
text: Buraa wa ti Yuuduma la n boi ka'lam la nyɔkɛ ka, zigera ti ba ku ka, gee mam n baŋɛ ti ka dela Arome tiŋambia la, ti mam doohe la pɛntiireba ta to'e ka bahɛ.

ref: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
text: Mam zigeri ti n baŋɛ yelese'ere ti ba dikɛ zuuha buraa wa, mam bahɛ ti ba tari en kiŋɛ ba kiinduma la nɛŋan mɛ.

ref: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
text: Mam nyɛ se'ere dela, ba zuuhe en mɛ doohe ba ze'elego yɛla puan, gee ba mam ka nyɛ sɛla ti a tuue seke ba ku' en bii ba pagɛ en.

ref: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
text: Mam wam ti ba maaheni mɛ ti ba ku en, ti mam ta'ahɛ en fum zi'an nananawa na, gee me bo' sɛba n zuuheri en la nuurɛ yeti, ba wa'am fum ze'a na, la ba yele fum ban tari se'ere la buraa wa la.”

ref: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
text: Bala ti pɛntiireba la n to'e a nuurɛ la, tari Pɔɔl bala wa'am na paaɛ Antipatirise tiŋa yu'uŋɔ.

ref: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
text: Ti Yiimbeere, ti ba bahɛ ti pɛntiireba sɛba n tari yire zabera la tara Pɔɔl yɛ'a gee ti basɛba la tulege lebe pɛntiireba ba bɔŋa ze'a la.

ref: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
text: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Kasariya puan la, ba dikɛ gɔŋɔ la mɛ bo' timbihega nɛŋadaana la, gee dikɛ Pɔɔl me iŋɛ a nu'uhen.

ref: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
text: Zuo daana wa n kãalɛ gɔŋɔ la ba'ahɛ la, a soke Pɔɔl en yehe lugese'ere na, ti en baŋɛ ti Pɔɔl yehe la Kilikiya na la,

ref: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
text: ti a yele en yeti, “Sɛba n zuuheri fum la han paaɛ na, n wan bahɛ ti fum tɔgɛ fum nuurɛ.” Gee nyaa bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari en kiŋɛ Hɛrɔd yikãtɛ la puan ti a ta bɔna bilam gee ti ba gura en.

ref: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
text: Ŋwana wa dabeha anuu puan, ti malema nɛŋadaana Ananiya sigena la kiinduma basɛba, la nɛra ayima en de yetɔgetɔgera ti ba yi'ira en Tetulose, ti ba dikɛ Pɔɔl yele biŋe timbihega nɛŋadaana la nɛŋan.

ref: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
text: Ban sam Pɔɔl ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, ti Tetulose wa pɔhɛ Pɔɔl zɛregerɛ, yɛta yeti: Naa sukpe'ene, “Fum zuo, suma'ahum boi tu mɛ wa'am na paaɛ zina, ti teere tee bo' tiŋa wa nɛreba wa, fum mi'ilum la zuo.

ref: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
text: Tu nini ba' tigɛ la yele ana wa ti tu pɛgera a sadɔɔ woo puan la zi'an woo, la pupeelum.

ref: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
text: Gee mam ka boti n gu'um la zuo, mam beleni fum mɛ ti fum kelehe tu suhega wa gi'a gi'a la fum zu'uhegɔ la.

ref: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
text: Tumam n nyɛ buraa wa, yelemɛŋerɛ, a dela nɛrebe'o n de nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega bo' tiŋa wa. A dela se'em n baheri ti gbiregeri sigera Yuda buuri wa n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo zi'an woo, dɛna lageŋɔ seko ti ba yi'ira ba Nazarenduma la nɛŋadaana.

ref: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
text: Gee yamam mɛhi la han viihɛ bihɛ, ya wan wum a mɛŋa zi'an, yele sɛ'a woo ti tumam dikɛ zɛregera en wa.”

ref: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
text: Ti Yuuduma la me pa'ahɛ ba nuurɛ yeti, sɛla woo ti ba yele la n kɔ'ɔm bala ti ba tɔgɛ la. Pɔɔl tɔgɛ gu' a mɛŋa Felikise nɛŋan

ref: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
text: Timbihega nɛŋadaana la ta yele Pɔɔl ti a me tɔgɛ a nuurɛ la, ti Pɔɔl yeti: “Mam puurɛ pee mɛ la fum bo' mam sore ti n tɔgɛ n nuurɛ wa. Mam mi' ti fum bihɛ tiŋa wa la ka nɛreba dagi zina.

ref: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
text: Gee fum mɛŋa n wum se'em la, la ka gaŋɛ dabeha pia la ayi' n ŋwana, ti mam kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm ti n ta pu'uhɛ.

ref: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
text: Ba ka nyɛ mam ti mam ŋmɛ'ɛri nɔkpe'ene la se'em pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, bii n gbirege nɛreba zamehegɔ yire puan bii tiŋa la puan.

ref: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
text: Ba me ze' nananawa, ba pun kan tara se'ere ti ba pa'alɛ yeti bamam n zuuheri mam se'ere zuo wa la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
text: Gee se'ere n de yelemɛŋerɛ ti mam wan yele fum dela, la doohe la Sore la ti bamam yeti, de dela be'em tigere, gee mam pu'uhi la tu yaabeduma Yinɛ la, gee nam sɛla woo en gulehɛ biŋe doohe ze'elego la nɔtɔgeheba gulehɛ biŋe la.

ref: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
text: Mam tari la beere puti'irɛ Yinɛ zi'an. Beere puti'irɛ se'ere ti bamam mɛhi pun sakɛ ti, tuntɔɔduma la be'em duma ihegere kum puan la wan tum.

ref: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
text: Ŋwana zuo la, mam kpeŋere la n mɛŋa saŋawoo ti n tara putɛ'sunɛ bo' Yinɛ, la nɛreba za'a.

ref: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
text: Yuuma n tole ha, ti mam yuum tari n bo'olum wa'am na ti n bo' n tiŋa nɛreba, gee me tara n kaabegɔ bo'olum na ti n kaabɛ.

ref: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
text: Mam wa'am na bɔna malema ana puan la, ti ba nyɛ mam Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti n boi la peere malema la ba'ahegɔ puan. Nɛreba daa ka zo'e bini, bii daaŋɔ seko sige bini.

ref: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
text: Gee Yuuduma basɛba n daa ze'ele Esiya wa'am na, bamam me pun suni ti ba me boni fum nɛŋan ka'lam, la ba yele bamam me n tari se'ere la mam.

ref: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
text: Bii bahɛ ti nɛrebana wa yele fum n tuure n daa de se'ere ban daa tari mam kiŋɛ kiinduma la nɛŋan la,

ref: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
text: Han dagena yele dina wa ma'a ti n daa zɛ'a ba nɛŋan gee yele la. ‘De dela nɛreba han ki ba wan le ihege la. Yele dina wa zuo ti mam zɛ'a fum nɛŋan zina wa.”

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
text: Gee Felikise n pun mina Sore la yele la, malum dikɛ yetɔgum la ze'ele la dabeherɛ se'ere yeti, “Lasiya n de pɛntiira kiima la han sige na, n wan bihɛ fum yele wa puan n de se'em.”

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: Gee bo' pɛntiira nɛŋa daana se'em en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la nuurɛ yeti, guri ya en, gee bahɛ ya en ti a tara fai fii wa, da gu'uri ya a zɔduma ti ba da kina na bihera en gee bɔ'ɔra en sɛla ti a bɔta. Pɔɔl boi pɛntiireba nu'uhen

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Ti la iŋɛ dabeha fii wa, ti Felikise doohe la a pɔga Durusila n de Yuda buuri la wa'am, tum ti ba yi' Pɔɔl na, ti a bahɛ en ti a tɔgɛ bu en ti wum a sakerɛ Kiresta Yezu puan n ani se'em.

ref: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
text: Ti Pɔɔl n ta tɔgera pa'ala tuntɔɔnɛ, tana fum mɛŋa tara, la gakerɛ la n kini se'em na la, ti dabeem kɛ̃ Felikise, ti a yele Pɔɔl yeti, “Kiŋɛ ti la iŋɛ fii, n han le ta nyɛ fole, n wan tum ti ba yi' fum na.”

ref: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
text: Gee ŋwana woo la, a puti'irɛ dela Pɔɔl wan bo' en ligeri. Yele dina wa me zuo ti a ni makɛ yi' Pɔɔl na zi'a la en sɔha.

ref: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
text: Yuuma ayi' n ta wa'am na tole, ti Pɔkiyose Fesetuse sooe Felikise, gee Felikise wa de gakɛ n boti a iŋɛ ti Yuuduma la puurɛ pee la, a bahɛ ti Pɔɔl kelum bɔna la paka deo puan.

ref: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
text: Naba Fesetuse n wa sooe tiŋa la, dabeha atã n tole, ti a ze'ele Kasariya kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm,

ref: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
text: ta paaɛ ti malema kiima la, la Yuda kiinduma la lagehɛ yele en Pɔɔl yele, ba belum en

ref: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
text: gee suhɛ ti a bahɛ Pɔɔl, la a bahɛ ti ba tari Pɔɔl lebe Yerusalɛm na. Gee ba pun maahum mɛ ti ba wan sugɛ gura en la ba ku en sore la zuo.

ref: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
text: Ti Fesetuse lerege ba yeti, ba pagɛ Pɔɔl la Kasariya tiŋa puan, la me kan yuue la eŋa mɛŋa n kiŋɛ bini.

ref: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
text: “Bala zuo” a yele ba yeti, “Ya nɛheduma la sige paaɛ mam Kasariya na, ti ban tari se'ere la buraa wa la, ba wan zɛregɛ ka.”

ref: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
text: Fɛsetuse ka kɛ̃ la ba gaŋɛ dabeha anii bii pia, a bahɛ bilam me gee kiŋɛ sige Kasariya, ti yiimbeere ti a kɛ̃ gakerɛ yire la puan ta zi'ire gakerɛ kuka la zuo, ti a gakɛ, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari Pɔɔl na.

ref: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.

ref: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
text: Ti Pɔɔl me tɔgɛ a nuurɛ yeti, “Mam ka tuue Yuuduma ze'elego la, bii Pu'uhegɔ yire la, bii Kaiza la fii me.”

ref: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
text: Gee Fɛsetuse n boti a pee Yuuduma la puurɛ la zuo ti a soke Pɔɔl yeti, “Fum wan bɔta ti ba tari fum lebe Yerusalɛm ti n ta zi'ire bilam gakɛ hɔ yele sɛ'a ti ba zuuha fum wa?”

ref: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
text: Ti Pɔɔl lerege yeti, “Mam ze' la Kaiza gakerɛ kuka wa nɛŋan. Ka'lam n de mam yetɔgum wa zi'an. Mam ka tum se'ere tuue Yuuduma wa wuu fum mɛŋa n mi de se'em suŋa suŋa la.

ref: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
text: La han deni la mam tum la se'ere tuue ti la dɛna n ki, mam ka zigeri ti n li'ihɛ kumbumɔ. Gee n han ka tara tuure bɔna ba zuuhego la woo puan, nɛra ka boi me wan ta'am dikɛ mam iŋɛ ba nu'uhen. Mam n yeti se'em wa dela, n bahɛ ti Kaiza gakɛ n yele wa.”

ref: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
text: Ti Fesetuse tɔgɛ la a gakereba kiinduma la, gee lerege Pɔɔl yeti, “Fum beleni la Kaiza ti a bihɛ fum yele la bo' fum, la me han dɛna Kaiza, fum ye' la Kaiza.” Fɛsetuse tɔgɛ la naba Agiripa

text: Gee la ka yuue, ti kusebekãtɛ ti ba yi'ira kɔ zanɔya dapɔya bɔba kusebego en bala ze'ele Kerete puan na.

ref: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
text: Kusebego wa n lure uureŋɔ la ti ku kan ta'am ŋmɛregɛ tu'uhɛ ku la, tu yari bahɛ mɛ ti kusebego la za'ara tu yɛ'a.

ref: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
text: Ti tu dɛ ko'om la zuo bala doohe ko'om la tiŋasuka tinpika kayima en boi gulengukɔ puan ti ba yi'ira ka Kawuda. Ti tu mɛ gee nyaŋɛ uureŋɔ la uurenɔ bibehi la en yule ku la.

ref: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
text: Ban nyaŋɛ ve'e uurembibehi kɛ̃ uureŋɔ la puan na, gee dabeem en tari ba ti uureŋɔ la wan wɛgɛ la, ba dikɛ la mi'ihi lu' uureŋɔ pulin na ta taaba. Gee ve'e sɛla en ze'eti uureŋɔ la dooe na, gee ti kusebego la kelum za'ara uureŋɔ kina.

ref: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
text: Ti kusebego wa ba vɛgelum tu bala ti beere ta yilegɛ, ti ba pɔhɛ gbɛ'ɛra lɔgerɔ la lɔbera baha ko'om la puan,

ref: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
text: ti dabeha atã daarɛ la, ti ba dikɛ ba mɛhi nu'uhi ɔ̃bɛ lɔgeseto en de uureŋɔ la lɔgerɔ la lobe bahɛ ko'om la puan.

ref: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
text: Wuntɛɛŋa bii ŋmarebibehi en ka le niiɛ na dabeha kekeko la, ti ko'mi'ihi la me ka malum sigeha la, tumam vo'ore puti'irɛ woo nyaa kɔ'ɛ mɛ.

ref: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
text: Ban ka di dia ti la yuue la, ti Pɔɔl nyaa ihege saazuo ba puan yeti, “Buraahi wa, la daa nari ni ti ya kelehe ni mam n daa yele ya se'em la, la daa dagi ni tu ze'ele Kerete la kiŋɛ ni, sageŋɔ la sɛla woo en bɔregɛ wa kan boni.

ref: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
text: Mam yeti ya nananawa, ti nɛrawoo kpeŋe a suure, gee ya puan se'em kan koŋe a nyɔvore, gee uureŋɔ la ma'a n wan sagum.

ref: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, zaam yu'uŋɔ, Yinɛ se'ere ti mam dɔla gee na'aha wa suŋera en zaam wa'am na ze'ele mam sakpikɔ,

ref: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
text: gee yele mam yeti, ‘Da zɔta dabeem Pɔɔl, gee fum wan ba'ahum ze'ele Kaiza nɛŋan; la dela asira, ti Yinɛ bo' sɛba woo me n boi uureŋɔ wa puan la fum wa piirɛ.’

ref: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
text: Gee tu wan ba'ahum kiŋɛ ta ŋmɛ' ko'om puan tiŋa ka yima doone.”

ref: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
text: Ŋwana ti ba ɔ̃begɛ tibehegɔ seko en ze'eti uureŋɔ la, bahɛ ko'om la puan, gee lorum mi'se'ehi en lu' tara mɔna uureŋɔ la me bahɛ, gee nyaa bire uureŋɔ la poore bahɛ kusebego la, ti sabeeo la nyaa za'ara ba yɛ'a doone na.

ref: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
text: Gee bala ma'a la, sɛba n mɔni uureŋɔ la maaheni mɛ ti ba zɔ gee bahɛ uureŋɔ la, pun dikɛ uureŋɔ bila bahɛ ko'om la puan la, gee ita wuu ba yeti ba dikɛ la uureŋɔ ze'elego tibehum la bahɛ uureŋɔ la nɛŋa bɔba ko'om la puan la,

ref: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
text: Ti dabeem en tari tɔ ti uureŋɔ la wan ta ŋmɛ' tahi la zuo, ti ba dikɛ ze'elego tibehum la n lu' la mi'ihi la banaahi bahɛ ko'om la puan, gee kɔ'ɔm pu'uha ti beere yilegɛ kalam.

ref: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
text: ti Pɔɔl waaɛ pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana la en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la a pɛntiireba la yeti, “Nɛreba wa han ka yee uureŋɔ wa puan ya kan piiɛ.”

ref: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
text: Ti pɛntiireba la ŋmaaɛ mi'se'ehi en gurɛ uureŋɔ bila la bahɛ, ti ko'mi'ihi la tari uureŋɔ pika la kiŋɛ.

ref: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
text: Bala zuo la, mam beleni ya woo mɛ ti nɛra woo di sɛla la a nyɛ paŋa. Se'ere n sɔi la, ya se'em zonko kan bɔregɛ ya zuin.”

ref: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
text: En tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, gee looe boroboro, bo' Yinɛ fara ba za'a nɛŋan, gee gi'ɛ dɛ pɔhɛ ɔbega.

ref: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
text: Ti la kpemehe ba za'a, ti ba woo me di dia.

ref: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
text: Ba woo en di ma'ɛ ba kom ba'ahɛ la, ba pɔhɛ gbɛ'ɛra la mui bɔra en boi uureŋɔ la puan la lɔbera baha ko'om la puan, ti la bahɛ ti uureŋɔ la ana faga faga. Uureŋɔ la wɔ̃regerɛ

ref: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
text: Beere en ta yilegɛ la, sɛba n mɔni uureŋɔ la ka baŋɛ ti doone n bala gã'a la, gee tugum ti'ihɛ ti la yeen dɛna la ko'om la kinkɛleŋa, bala ti ba iŋɛ ba puti'irɛ yeti, ba wan kɔ'ɔm bahɛ ti uureŋɔ la ze'ele la bilam, ba han wan nyaŋɛ.

ref: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
text: Gee uureŋɔ la wa'am na ŋmɛ' la kulenkãtenɛ, zɔ ta sãɛ tiŋa ha bɛgerɔ puan ha, yari firɛ zɛ'a bilam kan ta'am vurege, ti ko'mi'ihi kãra la me ŋmɛ uureŋɔ la paŋa paŋa ti ku tahɛ.

ref: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
text: Ti pɛntiireba la me maahum ti ba ku sɛba woo en boi paka deen bɔna uureŋɔ la puan la, ti ba se'em da bum piiɛ.

ref: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
text: Gee pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la n boti a faaɛ Pɔɔl bahɛ la zuo, a ka bo' sore ti pɛntiireba la tum ban ti'ihɛ ba tum se'em la, gee a tum bo' la a nuurɛ yeti, sɛba en ta' ko'om la, diŋɛ nɛŋa lu ko'om la puan bum dooe yia,

ref: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
text: gee ti sɛba n gee la me digehɛ uureŋɔ la dɔparebalagehi la zom tu zuo me bum yakɛ. La iŋɛ la ŋwana, ti ba tari nɛrawoo dooe doone na la inma'ahum.

ref: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
text: Gee tumam n paaɛ doone suŋa ba'ahɛ la, ti tu nyaa baŋɛ ti timbila la en boi ko'om tiŋasuka la yu'urɛ en yi'iri Malita.

ref: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
text: Ti tiŋa la nɛreba pa'alɛ tɔ ba itesuŋɔ en ka tari makerɛ. Lan wa dɛna sanire saŋa ti saa pɔhɛ niia ti la ma'ara la, ba tuurɛ la bugum gee yi' tu za'a ti tu zi'ire kaaɛ bɔ ɔɔha.

ref: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
text: Ti Pɔɔl me lagehɛ dɔpu'uha na biŋe lɔɔha ita bugum la puan, ti kampia ɛkɛ yehe na, bugum la wɔlegɔ la zuo, vile Pɔɔl nu'o kankaŋi yula.

ref: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
text: Tiŋa la nɛreba en nyɛ ti bunsɛla wa yule a nu'o la, ti ba waahena bɔ'ɔra taaba yeti, “Burakana wa ba'ahum dɛna la nɛrekuura; bala en sɔi ti ka pun piiɛ ko'om puan kum la, buurɔ ka bo' ka sore ti ka vɔna.”

ref: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
text: Gee ti Pɔɔl me mɛhegɛ kampia la bahɛ bugum la puan gee ti be'em ka paaɛ en.

ref: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
text: Ti nɛreba la guri mɛ ti a iŋa fuuhum, bii a lu ki bilam bilam. Gee ban gu'uhi yuue, ta nyɛ ti sɛla sɛla ka ita Pɔɔl la, ti ba tee ba puti'ira le yeti a dela bagerɛ.

ref: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
text: Nananawa zɛ'kana ti nɛreba kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la, mɛa puan, samana zo'e zo'e en boi bini dɛna tiŋa la n boi ko'om tiŋasuka la naba yu'urɛ en yi'ira Pabiliyuse dinɛ, se'em n to'e tumam, bihɛ tumam suŋa dabeha atã.

ref: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
text: Ti la iŋɛ, ti Pabiliyuse wa sɔ lu bã'a gã'a la ɔɔrɔ la nyɛ'a. Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ta bihɛ en, gee tibɛ en doohe la a dikɛ la a nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo.

ref: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
text: Dina en iŋɛ tole la, ti tiŋa la puan nɛresɛba woo en gee tara bã'ahi yima yima la, me wa'am na, ti Pɔɔl tibɛ ba.

ref: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
text: Ti nɛreba wa na'ahɛ tumam paaɛ paaɛ la ba bo'olum. Pooren ti tumam ta yeti tu kiŋɛ la, ti ba tari tumam en boti sɛla woo la na iŋɛ uureŋɔ seko ti tu yeti tu zom la puan. Pɔɔl paaɛ Arome

ref: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
text: Ŋmarehi sitã en tole, ti tumam ihege la uureŋɔ la. Ku wa yee la tiŋa la en boi ko'om tiŋasuka la puan. Ku wa bɔna bini la ɔɔrɔ saŋa la puan, dɛna Alɛgezandiriya uureŋɔ, ti liiba paga la ti ba na'aha la bɔna ku puan.

ref: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
text: Tu pɔhɛ la Sirakuse, ze'ele bilam dabeha atã;

ref: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
text: Gɔlum na paaɛ Aregium, dikɛ daarɛ deyima bilam, ti kusebego ze'ele tiŋanuurɛ bɔba vuke na, ti yi'i daarɛ la ti tu wa'am na paaɛ Puteyoli.

ref: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
text: Bilam ti tu nyɛ suurɔ tuseto, ti ba me yi' tu ti tu wa'am na kɛ̃ la ba dabeha ayopɔi. Tumam n doohe se'em wa'am na paaɛ Arome na n bala.

ref: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
text: Ti suurɔ la n boi Arome n wum ti tu kini na la, yehe la zaaɛ zaarɛ Apiyosi Da'a bɔba ha la, la Saama Vo'ohego Zi'ihi Sitã la puan ha tu'uhɛ tu na. Pɔɔl en ta nyɛ ba la, a bo' la Yinɛ fara gee ti a iŋa kpeŋe.

ref: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
text: Tumam n wa'am na na kɛ̃ Arome puan la, ba bahɛ Pɔɔl mɛ ti a bɔna a ma'a, la pɛntiira sɛka en guri en la. Pɔɔl la Yuuduma kiinduma Arome

ref: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
text: Dabeha atã daarɛ, ti Pɔɔl yi' kiinduma la en biheri Yuda buuri wa la. Ban lagehɛ na ba'ahɛ la, ti a yele ba yeti, “N suurɔ, la mam en pun ka iŋɛ se'ere tuue tu nɛreba Yuuduma wa, bii tu yaabeduma kiha wa la, gee la bala za'a, ba nyɔkɛ mam mɛ Yerusalɛm, dikɛ mam ta'ahɛ Arome duma ha.

ref: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
text: Ban ta viihɛ mam bihɛ la, Arome duma wa daa boti ba bahɛ mam mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, vuurɛ daa ka boi kua la ti ba daa bɔta ti ba ku' mam la puan.

ref: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
text: Gee Yuuduma la en ka sakɛ la zuo, la daa pɛregɛ mam mɛ, ti n suhɛ timbihega kpɛ'ɛma la ti a bahɛ ti n paaɛ timbihega nɛŋadaana Kaiza nɛŋan, la mam pun ka tari zɛregerɛ se'ere la n mɛŋa tiŋa duma la.

ref: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
text: Yele dina wa zuo, ti mam suhɛ sore ti n nyɛ ya, la n tɔgɛ la ya, lan de Izirale beere puti'irɛ wa zuo ti ba ba' mam la bãhi wa.”

ref: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
text: Ti ba lebehe en yeti, “Tumam ka to'e gɔŋɔ Yudiya, doohe fum puan. Tu suuɔ la kuyina me n kini na saŋa woo la me ka tabelɛ yele fum yele be'o ti tu wum.

ref: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
text: Gee tumam wan bɔta ti tu wum la fum mɛŋa zi'an, fum ti'ihɛ se'em doohe sadɔkuna wa ti tu mina ti zi'an woo ba zɛregera ku me wa.” Pɔɔl muulɛ Faarɛ yelehum la Arome

ref: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
text: Ban daa ta ze'ele dabeherɛ ti ma le tu'uhɛ la ena la, ba wa'am la a ga'aregɔ zi'an na, ba nɛrekuuŋɔ. Ze'ele bulika tara tara la zaanuurɛ, ti a tu' yele la n ani se'em bo' ba, yɛta ba Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la n ani se'em, zigera ti a tee ba puti'ira la Yezu, ze'ele Moose ze'elego la, la nɔtɔgeheba la puan.

ref: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
text: Ti basɛba sakɛ doohe la Pɔɔl n yele se'em la, gee basɛba me zagehɛ ti bamam kan nam.

ref: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
text: Bala, ba ka sakɛ bo' taaba, bala zuo la, ban yiregerɛ la, Pɔɔl tɔgɛ yele deyima ma'a yeti: “Fa'a Sia la yuum tari la buurɔ la a yele ya yaabeduma doohe nɔtɔgeha Azaaya puan yeti,

ref: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
text: ‘Kiŋɛ nɛrebana wa zi'an ta yele ba yeti, Ya sirum wan kelehe, gee ya kan malum bɔkɛ, ya me sirum wan bihɛ, gee ya kan malum nyɛ.

ref: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
text: Se'ere n sɔi la, nɛrebana wa suya ba' bukɛ mɛ, ti ba tuba li' ka wuna, ti ba mum ba nini, ti ba da biha la ba nini la, gee me da kɛleha la ba tuba, gee me da bɔkera la ba suya la ba ta'am ŋmɛregɛ na ti n tibɛ ba'.’

ref: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
text: En tɔgɛ yele ana wa ba'ahɛ la, ti Yuuduma la yiregera gee nyɔna taaba la ninmu'urɛ.]

ref: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
text: Pɔɔl kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la bilam yuuma ayi', bihera a mɛŋa, gee tɔ'ɔha sɛba woo en kini a zi'an na la,

ref: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
text: gee muula Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yele, gee pa'ala Yezu Kiresta yele la sukpe'ene, ti sɛla ka gu'ura en.

ref: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
text: Mam Pɔɔl, Yezu Kiresta da'aberɛ, ti Yinɛ ayi' mam ti n dɛna tuntuna, yehe mam ze'ele bo' Yinɛ Faarɛ yelehum la ma'a,

ref: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
text: yelehum sebo ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ wunse'eren ha, doohe a nɔtɔgeheba puan, Fa'a gɔnɔ la puan la.

ref: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
text: Doohe tuuntɔɔ sia puan, ba looe en mɛ ti a dɛna Yinɛ dayua, la paŋa, doohe en ihege a kum puan la, Ena daana dela Yezu Kiresta, tu Daana la.

ref: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Se'em puan ti tumam to'e zu'uhegɔ, la tuntuneba yɔ'ɔ, ti tu bahɛ ti timpaberɛ wa nɛreba za'a me nam, gee tara sakerɛ a yu'urɛ la puan.

ref: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
text: Yamam mɛhi ti Yinɛ yi' ti ya dɛna Yezu Kiresta dinɛ wa pa'ahɛ bini mɛ.

ref: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: Mam Pɔɔl n gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la yamam sɛba woo ti Yinɛ nɔŋɛ ya, ti ya bɔna Arome tiŋa puan, ti Yinɛ yi' ya ti ya dɛna nɛresunduma la. Zu'uhegɔ dɛna ya dinɛ, ti suma'ahum en ze'eti Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ, la Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an la dɛna ya dinɛ. Pɛka Pu'uhegɔ

ref: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
text: Yia, mam bo'ori Yinɛ fara Yezu puan ya za'a zuo, beni zuo, ya sakerɛ la yele to'e tiŋa wa zuo za'a mɛ.

ref: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
text: Yinɛ wa ti mam tuna bɔ'ɔra dɛ la n sia za'a, doohe mam muuli a dayua Yezu yelehum wa, en de mam aze'nyɛdaana, ti mam ka malum tamena ya n pu'uhegɔ puan saŋa woo.

ref: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
text: Sɔkera ti Yinɛ suboolum en de se'em la, n wan ba'ahum nyɛ sore ba'ahegɔ puan ta'am wa'am ya zi'an na.

ref: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
text: N ba' boti ni n nyɛ ni ya mɛ, la n tɔrɛ zu'uhegɔ puan bo'olum la ya, ti la kpemehe ya Sia puan.

ref: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
text: Bii tu lagum bugelɛ taaba la tu sakerɛ. Yamam sakerɛ, la mam sakerɛ.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
text: Mam boti ya mina dina wa n suurɔ, ti saŋa zo'e zo'e ti n ni maahum ti n wa'am ya zi'an na, . N boti ni ti n wa'am ni na, la n bum sɛla ya puan bo' Yinɛ, wuu mam n bum se'em tihi sise'ehi nɛreba puan bo' Yinɛ la.

ref: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
text: Gee me le mu'ɛ nini ti n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' yamam sɛba me n boi Arome tiŋa wa. Faarɛ yelehum wa Paŋa

ref: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Vi ka tari mam la faarɛ yelehum wa. Beni zuo, bu dela Yinɛ faarɛ paŋa, bo' se'em woo en tari sakerɛ, Yuuduma yia, gee nyaa bɔ'ɔra Girikiduma.

ref: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
text: Bo puan ti Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la niira, dɔla sakerɛ puan, wuu lan pun gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Se'emdaana en de tuuntɔɔ wan vɔna mɛ la sakerɛ la.” Asaala tuure

ref: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
text: Yinɛ sunsunyiim ze'ele la saazuo ha niiɛ bahɛ nɛre sɛba be'em la ba tuunfi'iha en gu'uri yelemɛŋerɛ la.

ref: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ban suni ti ba mina Yinɛ yele sɛ'a la, niiɛ bo' ba mɛ kpaii. Beni zuo, Yinɛ mɛŋa en niiɛ a bo' ba.

ref: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
text: Pɔhɛ Yinɛ n naam tiŋa wa se'em, a paŋa la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ, la a naanɛ la, la ban pun sugɛ la, gee asaala bɔkɛ a mɛ, gee nyɛ ba kpaii, doohe sɛla woo ti Yinɛ naam la puan. Ŋwana zuo, asaala ka tari a yeti eŋa ka baŋɛ mɛ.

ref: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
