text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la ban pun mina Yinɛ wa la, ba ka na'aheri en wuu a dela Yinɛ, bii ba bɔ'ɔra en fara fara, gee ba tugum lebege la nɛrefɔgerɔ ba ti'ihegɔ puan. Ti ba putɛ'vɔya la en ti'ihɛ yele yaalehi la pirɛ la lika.

ref: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
text: Ba yeti bamam dela yɛm duma, gee ba tugum lebege la geto,

ref: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
text: nyaa tugum tee dikɛ na'ahegɔ seko en de Yinɛ vua wa na'ahegɔ la bo' naanɛ sɛ'a en ŋwani la asaala en kiiri, bii niihe, bii duhi en tari nama anaahi, bii bunvuula.

ref: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
text: Ŋwana zuo, ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba bo' ba kunkoma'ahum sebo ti ba suya bɔta la, dɛgerɔ tuuma la, ti ba tuna zufagehum tuuma la taaba.

ref: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Ba tee Yinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la en de se'em la mɛ bo' pumpɔreŋɔ, nyaa tugum na'aha gee malena malema bɔ'ɔra naanɛ, gee bahɛ naana la mɛŋa, se'em en nari pɛka saŋa woo la. Taari.

ref: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
text: Ŋwana zuo, ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba bo' zufagehum kunkoma'ahum, ti ba pɔgehi tee pɔka la buraa en naari taaba se'em la, bo' sadɔɔ seko en ka nari.

ref: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
text: Bala mɛa ti buraahi me tugum bahɛ ba naara la ba pɔgeba, gee tugum tara kunkoma'ahum la taaba, ti buraahi tuna zufagehum tuuma la ba burataaba, lageha namehegɔ kãtɛ biŋera ba mɛhi zuto ba tuure wa puan.

ref: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
text: Ban pun kelum ka bihɛ nyɛ ti la nari ba baŋɛ Yinɛ la, ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba bo' putɛ'ŋmɛlema dikɛ ba bo' yele yaalehi se'ehi en dagi ba tuna ma'a.

ref: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
text: Ba kɔ'ɔm pirɛ mɛ la tuunfi'iha yima yima, be'em, fãa, ti'iha be'em bɔ'ɔra nɛreba. Pirɛ la nintɛrega, kua, zaba, pã'ahegɔ, sɔtuuma, ba dela yelebibehi duma,

ref: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
text: Sagena nɛreba yu'ura, yaaɛ Yinɛ, tuura, ukerɛ, pa'ali mɛŋa, be'em maaheŋɔ, ka nana dɔgereba,

ref: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
text: geto, nɔkɔ'ɔreba sukãberɛ, ka tara nɛreba giile

ref: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
text: Ba mi' ti Yinɛ yeti, sɛba woo en iti yele ana wa dela ba ki, gee ba pun ka ita a ba ma'a, gee ba pɛgeri la sɛba me en ita a la.

ref: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
text: Bala zuo, fum ka tari fum yeti n ka baŋɛ mɛ, fum de nɛre se'em woo, fum han gakɛ basɛba. Fum n gakɛ ayima la, fum sagum la fum mɛŋa n bala. Beni zuo, fum se'em en de gakera la, iti la yele aŋa mɛa.

ref: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
text: Gee tu mi' ti, “Yinɛ en gakeri nɛre sɛba en iti yele aŋa la, dole la yelemɛŋerɛ suŋa suŋa.”

ref: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
text: Fum bihɛ de la ŋwani, fum de se'em za'a, ti fum gakera nɛreba en iti yele sɛ'a, gee ita yele aŋa mɛa fum mɛŋa, fum ti'ihɛ ti fum wan piiɛ Yinɛ gakerɛ la?

ref: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
text: Bii fum tugum ka nam la Yinɛ itesuŋɔ la n ka tari tɛka la, la a ŋmibega, la a sugeri la? Fum ka baŋɛ ti Yinɛ itesuŋɔ la dela la bahɛ ti fum tee?

ref: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
text: Gee fum sukãberɛ la, la fum n ka malum tɛɛra lagehɛ la sunsunyiim pagela fum zuo, gura sunsunyiim dabeherɛ la ti Yinɛ tuuntɔɔ gakerɛ la wan niiɛ fum zuo na.

ref: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, a yeti a yɔ nɛra woo la en tum se'em.

ref: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
text: Sɛba en ŋmibe tuna sum la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba nini mu'ɛ mɛ ɛɛra suŋa, na'ahegɔ, la sɛla en ka pu'uri. Yinɛ wan bo' ba vom kamba'ahɛ.

ref: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
text: Sɛba me en eere ba mɛhi en boti se'em la, ka nana yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee nana la tuunfi'iha la, Yinɛ sunsunyiim, la sunsunyiim tuulɛ en de ba dinɛ.

ref: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, wuŋerɛ en ka tari makerɛ la susageŋɔ wan bɔna bo' se'em woo n tuni be'em, pɔhera Yuuduma, gee nyaa paara Girikiduma.

ref: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Gee suŋa la na'ahegɔ, la suma'ahum wan bɔna bo' se'em woo en tuni sum la, pɔhera Yuuduma, gee nyaa paara Girikiduma me.

ref: God doesn't have any favorites!
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ka lugehe.

ref: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
text: Sɛba woo en tum be'em gee ka mina ze'elego la, ba wan bɔregɛ ti ze'elego la ka pa'ahɛ, ti sɛba woo me en tum be'em gee mina ze'elego la, ba wan gakɛ ba mɛ la ze'elego.

ref: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ ba pa'alɛ yeti, ze'elego la en boti se'em la, gulehɛ iŋɛ la bamam suya puan, ti ba mɛhi puti'ira dɛna ba aze'nyɛduma, ti ba mɛhi itegɔ la me wan ta zuuhe ba, bii bahɛ ba fai,

ref: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
text: daan se'ere daarɛ, wuu vom yelehum la en yele se'em yeti, Yinɛ wan doohe Kiresta Yezu puan gakɛ sɛla woo en sugɛ nɛre woo suuren la. Yuuduma, la Ze'elego la

ref: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
text: Gee fum han yi'ira fum mɛŋa Yuda nɛra, dɛla Ze'elego la, ŋmɛ'ɛra fum nyu'ɔ la fum en lɛm Yinɛ se'em,

ref: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
text: gee mina Yinɛ sumboolum se'em, gee mina sɛla en de ma'a se'em, doohe la ban pa'alɛ hɔ Ze'elego la puan yɛla la zuo,

ref: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
text: gee han mina ti fum pa'ali la fɔɔhi, dɛna niihum bo' sɛba en boi lika puan,

ref: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
text: dɛna se'em n pa'ali geto, dɛna se'em n pa'ali kɔmbilɛɛhi, bi'ihɛ fum mɛŋa la mi'ilum la yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere n boi Ze'elego la puan,

ref: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
text: Bala la, fum se'em n pa'ali basɛba la, fum ka pa'ali fum mɛŋa? Fum han muula ti zua ka ani suŋa, gee ti fum mɛŋa la zuura?

ref: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
text: Fum se'em n yeti kabohi dela kihere la, gee ti fum mɛŋa la tuna kabohi? Fum se'em n yaaɛ baga paaɛ paaɛ la la, gee tara kunkoma'ahum la pu'uhegɔ yire lɔgerɔ?

ref: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
text: Fum se'em n ŋmɛ'ɛri fum nyu'ɔ la Ze'elego la, gee nyaa diihe Yinɛ vi la fum galum ze'elego la?

ref: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
text: Wuu lan pun gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Yamam zuo, ti nɛreba basɛba tuura Yinɛ yu'urɛ la.”

ref: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
text: Buraahi ŋmaa tari tuulum dela, fum nane Ze'elego la. Gee fum han galena Ze'elego la, fum ŋmaa la lebe ta dɛna la se'em n ka ŋma.

ref: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
text: Bala, sɛba n ka ŋma la han dɔla Ze'elego la n boti se'em la, la bamam pun ka ŋma la, ba kan biha nyɛta ba ti ba dɛna sɛba n ŋma?

ref: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
text: Ŋwana ti sɛba n pun ka ŋma ba inganɛ, gee nana Ze'elego la, wan zɛregɛ yamam sɛba n ŋma, zãli Ze'elego la ti ba gulehɛ la, gee galena ku yɛla la.

ref: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
text: Nɛra dagi Yuda buuri doohe la a yiŋa, bii yelemɛŋerɛ ŋmaa dɛna yiŋa bii ingɔŋɔ yele.

ref: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
text: Gee yelemɛŋerɛ se'em n de Yuda buuri paaɛ paaɛ dela puan ha. Ŋmaa yelemɛŋerɛ dela suure puan. La dela Sia puan, dagi inganɛ. Nɛre-ina taaba ka to'ore pɛka nɛreba zi'an, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa zi'an.

ref: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
text: Ŋwana zuo la, beni yɔ'ɔ ti Yuda nɛra tara? Bii beni tuulum n boi ŋmaa puan?

ref: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
text: La zo'e mɛ zi'an woo. Yia dela, Yuuduma la en pɔhɛ to'e Yinɛ yele sɛ'a en sugɛ la.

ref: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
text: Gee Yuuduma wa basɛba yuum han ka tara sakerɛ? Ban ka tari sakerɛ la wan gu' Yinɛ en tari sakerɛ se'ere la ba la me yoi?

ref: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
text: Sore ka boi! La asaala woo n pun dɛna pumparema la, Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana wuu lan gulehɛ se'em la, “Bala ti fum wan tara buurɔ fum tugum puan, gee di mi'a gakerɛ woo puan.”

ref: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
text: Gee tumam ŋmɛlema la han bahɛ ti Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la niiɛ na, tu wan yeti bu? Yinɛ ka tari buurɔ a han taaɛ a sunsunyiim bahɛ tu zuo?

ref: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
text: Sore ka boi! La han dɛna bala, Yinɛ wan iŋɛ la ŋwani gakɛ tiŋa wa?

ref: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
text: Gee la han doohe la mam pumpɔreŋɔ la zuo ti Yinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la niiɛ na bɔ'ɔra en suŋa, beni zuo ti ba wan sagum mam ti n dela be'em daana?

ref: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
text: Ŋwani ti tu yi kɔ'ɔm kan yele wuu nɛreba basɛba n sageni tumam yu'ura se'em ti tumam yeti, “Tu kɔ'ɔm tuna be'em ti la bahɛ ti sum wa'am na”? Nɛrebana duma wa sageŋɔ gã' gura ba mɛ. Tuntɔɔ daana kai'

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
text: La nyaa dela ŋwani wa? Tumam Yuda buuri wa nyani basɛba me bii? Aayi, la fii wa me. Tumam pun diŋɛ yeti, nɛra woo, Yuuduma la Giriki buuri za'a boi la be'em paŋa puan,

ref: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
text: wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Se'em ka boi dɛna tuuntɔɔ, la ayima me.

ref: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
text: Ba se'em ka bɔkeri, Se'em ka boi ɛɛra Yinɛ.

ref: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
text: Ba woo bire la ba pɔya, ba naaɛ la taaba bahɛ yoo. Se'em ka boi ita itesuŋɔ, Ayima me ka boi.”

ref: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
text: “Ba koohe dela yɔɔrɔ kãra, ti ba tari ba zɛlehi pã'aha.” “Kampia yabega en boi ba nɔya puan.”

ref: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
text: “Ba nɔya pirɛ mɛ la pɔrɛ la yele tooro.”

ref: These people quickly become violent.
text: “Ba nama kabelɛ ɛɛra la ziim ka'ahegɔ,

ref: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
text: sageŋɔ la nɔyalegerɛ en ni yee ban doohe zi'an woo tole,

ref: They don't know how to live in peace.
text: gee suma'ahum sore la, ba ka mi.”

ref: They don't even fear God.”
text: “Yinɛ zoolum la fii wa ka boi ba ninin.”

ref: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
text: Tumam mi' ti ze'elego la n yeti sɛla woo la, yeti bɔ'ɔra la sɛba n dole ku la, ti nɛra da ta tara n ka baŋɛ mɛ, tiŋa wa za'a wan ta yele ban daa iŋɛ sɛla la sɛla.

ref: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
text: Nɛra ka boi wan ta to'e buurɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan doohe la yele sɛ'a en boi ze'elego la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la puan ti be'em puti'irɛ bɔna. Sakerɛ puan tuntɔɔnɛ

ref: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
text: Gee nananawa, ze'elego la pooren, Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la niiɛ mɛ, ti nɔtɔgeheba la pa'alegɔ la tɔgɛ la yele,

ref: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
text: ti Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ dole la sakerɛ Yezu Kiresta puan, bɔ'ɔra sɛba woo en nam, ti yima yima ka bɔna,

ref: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
text: Ba nyɛ buurɔ doohe la zu'uhegɔ la en de bo'olum la, doohe faarɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la,

ref: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
text: Beni zuo ti tu nyaa ŋmɛ'ɛra tu nyu'ɔ la? Sɛla ka boi. Ze'elego seko bii? Bii tuunsɛ'a? Aayi. La dela sakerɛ, gee dagi itegɔ.

ref: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam n gurɛ se'ere dela, Nɛra nyɛti buurɔ, la sakerɛ puan gee dagi a dole la kiha en gulehɛ bɔna ze'elego la puan.

ref: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
text: Abunɔ, bii Yinɛ dela Yuuduma ma'a Yinɛ? A dagi sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la me Yinɛ? Ɛ̃ɛ, la dela bala, a dela sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la me Yinɛ,

ref: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
text: Yinɛ dela ayina, gee wan bo' sɛba en ŋma la buurɔ ba sakerɛ zuo, gee bo' sɛba en ka ŋma la buurɔ ba sakerɛ me zuo.

ref: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
text: Ŋwana zuo, tu lobe ze'elego la bahɛ sakerɛ la zuo? Sore ka boi bala la fii me. Gee lan tugum dɛna se'ere dela, tumam en tugum zãla ze'elego la.

ref: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
text: Beni ti tu wan ta'am yeti Aberaham en de tu yaaba inganɛ puan la, yuum nyɛ a mɛŋa tuuma puan?

ref: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
text: Aberaham yuum han di buurɔ la a tuuma puan, a tari sɛla ti a wan ta'am dikɛ ŋmɛ' a nyu'ɔ, gee dagi Yinɛ nɛŋan.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
text: Gee Gɔŋɔ la yeti boo? “Aberaham nam la Yinɛ, ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ ti a dɛna tuuntɔɔ.”

ref: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
text: Se'em woo en tum, ba ka bihɛ nyɛ a yɔɔrɛ la ti de dɛna bo'olum, gee la dela sɛla ti a tum to'e.

ref: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
text: Gee se'em en ka tum, gee yeen nam la se'em n bo'ori be'em duma buurɔ la, sakerɛ dinɛ taaba ti ba bahɛ nyɛ ti a dela tuuntɔɔ daana.

ref: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
text: Bala mɛa ti naba Deviti tɔgɛ doohe sum sebo en boi bo' sɛba ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ ti ba dɛna tuuntɔɔ duma gee dagi la Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ la ba tuuma la yeti,

ref: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
text: Sum boi mɛ la se'em ti Daana la ka bihɛ nyɛ a be'em la.”

ref: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
text: Sum buna wa purɛ bo' la sɛba en ŋma la ma'a, bii bu ta' sɛba en ka ŋma la me mɛ? Se'ere en sɔi la tu yeti,“Sakerɛ zuo ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ Aberaham ti a dɛna tuntɔɔdaana.”

ref: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
text: Aberaham daa to'e ŋmaa daalehum la mɛ ti la dɛna sɛla n pa'alɛ a tuntɔɔnɛ la ti a to'e la sakerɛ la, ti a nan ka ŋma. Iti kuna wa dela, la iŋɛ ti a dɛna yaaba bo' sɛba woo en nam gee ka ŋma la, n yeti la sɛba ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ ba ti ba dɛna tuuntɔɔ duma la.

ref: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
text: Gee me kelum dɛna yaaba bu sɛba en ŋma la, dagi ba ŋmaa la ma'a, gee sɛba en tari sakerɛ se'ere taaba ti tu yaaba Aberaham yuum tara gee nyaa baŋɛ ŋma la. Yinɛ nɔbiŋere la en boi sakerɛ puan se'em

ref: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
text: Nɔbiŋere la n biŋe ti Aberaham la a yaahi ti ba wan sɔna timpaberɛ wa za'a la yuum ka tɔgɛ bo' Aberaham bii a yaahi la la doohe la ze'elego la puan, gee la doohe tuntɔɔnɛ en ze'eti sakerɛ puan.

ref: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
text: La han dɛna la sɛba en dole ze'elego la n wan ta dɛna asooesɔna duma, sakerɛ yi dela zaŋa, ti nɔbiŋere la me bahɛ yoo.

ref: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la tari la Yinɛ sunsunyiim kina na. Gee zi'an ti ze'elego ka bɔna, galeŋɔ me pun ka bɔna.

ref: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
text: Ŋwana zuo, la dela sakerɛ en bahɛ ti nɔbiŋere la, doohe zu'uhegɔ puan paaɛ Aberaham yaahi woo, dagi la sɛba ma'a n dole ze'elego la, gee sɛba me en tari Aberaham sakerɛ la taaba la. (A dela tu za'a sɔ,

ref: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
text: wuu lan gulehɛ gɔnɔ la puan se'em yeti, “Mam iŋɛ hɔ ti fum dɛna la tihi zo'e zo'e sɔ”) — Yinɛ se'ere ti Aberaham nam la nɛŋan. Yinɛ se'ere en bo'ori kum vom, gee baha ti sɛla en ka boi bɔna la.

ref: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
text: La en pun bɔna bɔnsɛka en ka tare beere puti'irɛ la, gee doohe beere puti'irɛ puan a nam mɛ ti eŋa wan ta wa'am na dɛna “tihi zo'e zo'e sɔ,” wuu eŋa en wum se'em yeti, “Fum yaahi yeti ba wi'ɛ la bala la.”

ref: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
text: Bumbu'um yuum ka boi bahɛ ti a miim la Yinɛ nɔbiŋere la, gee a tugum malum kpe'em mɛ la a sakerɛ, pɛgera Yinɛ.

ref: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
text: Kɔ'ɔm lu' a suure kankaŋi, ti Yinɛ wan ta'am tum en biŋe nuurɛ ti a tum se'em la.

ref: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
text: Gulehegɔ en yeti, “Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ en mɛ la,” ka gulehɛ la eŋa ma'a zuo,

ref: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
text: gee tumam me. Yinɛ wan bihɛ nyɛ tumam me ti tu dɛna tuntɔɔduma, tumam sɛba woo en nam se'em daana en vo'e Yezu tu Daana kum puan la.

ref: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
text: Se'em ti Yinɛ dikɛ en tihɛ na ti a ki tumam tuure zuo, gee daa vo'e en tumam buurɔ zuo la.

ref: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
text: Bala zuo, tumam en to'e buurɔ doohe sakerɛ puan la, tu tari suma'ahum mɛ la Yinɛ, suma'ahum buna wa ze'ele la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan na.

ref: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
text: Se'em puan ti tu doohe nyɛ yɔ'ɔ doohe puan ti tu tara zu'uhegɔ seko ti tu yaga ku zuo wa, gee me ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la tumam beere wan lagum nyɛ Yinɛ suŋa la.

ref: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
text: La dagi bala ma'a, gee tu me ŋmɛ'ɛri nyu'urɔ mɛ la tu namehegɔ la, gee mina ti namehegɔ wa bo'ori tu la ŋmibega,

ref: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
text: ti ŋmibega bɔ'ɔra tu aŋa en bi'ɛ, ti aŋa en bi'ɛ la bɔ'ɔra tɔ beere puti'irɛ,

ref: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
text: ti beere puti'irɛ wa me kan diihe tu vi, beni zuo, Yinɛ ka'ɛ la nɔŋerɛ bahɛ tu suya puan, doohe Fa'a Sia la ti a bo' tu la puan.

ref: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
text: Se'em ma'a ti tumam daa nan dɛna tɔregɔ duma la, sansɛka en daa nari la puan, ti Kiresta ki be'em duma wa zuo.

ref: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, la kpe'em mɛ ti tu ti'ihɛ ti nɛra wan ki bo' nɛre tuuntɔɔ. Han dɛna la nɛresuŋɔ, daanse'ere nɛra ta'am sirum maahum ti a ki.

ref: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
text: Gee Yinɛ pa'alɛ tumam la a nɔŋerɛ, la tumam en daa kelum bɔna tu be'em puan la, ti Kiresta ki bo' tu.

ref: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
text: Sɛla en gani bala dela, tumam en nyaa to'e buurɔ Kiresta ziim la puan la, tu nyɛ la faarɛ a puan, yehe Yinɛ susuntuulega la puan.

ref: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam nyaa daa han tugum dɛna Yinɛ dinduma, gee ti a dikɛ tu naaɛ la a mɛŋa, doohe a dayua kum la puan, sɛla en nyaa gani bala dela, en dikɛ tu naaɛ a mɛŋa la, tu wan nyɛ faarɛ a vom la puan.

ref: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
text: Gee sɛla en pun gana bala dela, tu ŋmɛ'ɛri la nyu'ɔ Yinɛ puan, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zuo, se'em zuo ti tu nyaa nyɛ naarɛ la Yinɛ wa. Adam la Kiresta

ref: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
text: Bala zuo, wuu be'em en wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na, doohe buraa kayima puan na se'em, ti kum me doohe be'em na, ti kum taaɛ nɛra woo se'em la, beni zuo, nɛra woo tum la be'em.

ref: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
text: Be'em pun bɔna tiŋa wa zuo mɛ gee ti ze'elego la baŋɛ wa'am na, gee ba ka bihɛ nyɛta be'em zi'an ti ze'elego ka bɔna.

ref: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
text: Gee la bala za'a, kum pa'alɛ a paŋa mɛ ze'ele Adam, ta paaɛ Moose, pun ta ta' sɛba be'em en ka ani wuu Adam tuure la, Adam kana wa ani wuu se'em en ye' na la.

ref: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
text: Gee se'em ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' tu zaŋa la ka ani wuu se'em en tari tuure la. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba kekeko la han ki doohe la nɛre yinɛ tuure la zuo, yelemɛŋerɛ, Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la, la a bo'olum ti a bo' zaŋa la, doohe la zu'uhegɔ seko ti a zu'uhɛ buraa kayima Yezu Kiresta wa la, zu'uhegɔ kuna wan bɔna zo'e zo'e bo' nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

ref: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
text: Bo'olum la ti a bo' tu zaŋa la, ka tari too seko taaba ti nɛre yinɛ tuure la tara la. Se'ere en sɔi la, gakerɛ la doohe la tuure deyina zuo tari sageŋɔ wa'am na, gee zaŋa bo'olum la en bo' doohe tuure zo'e zo'e zuo la, tari la buurɔ wa'am na.

ref: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
text: La han dɛna la nɛre yinɛ tuure la zuo ti kum doohe bilam sɔna sɛla woo la, yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla en gani bala dela, sɛba en to'e zu'uhegɔ kãtɛ la, la tuuntɔɔ zaŋa bo'olum kãtɛ la tari la vom paŋa, doohe buraa kayima Yezu Kiresta wa puan.

ref: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
text: Bala zuo, wuu nɛre yinɛ tuure en tari sageŋɔ wa'am saaleba woo zuo se'em na la, bala mɛa ti nɛre yinɛ tuuntɔɔ tuunɛ la me bo' nɛreba woo buurɔ, la vom.

ref: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
text: Wuu nɛre yinɛ pobiriga zuo ti zo'e zo'e dɛna be'em duma se'em la, bala mɛa ti nɛre yinɛ naŋa la zuo, ti zo'e zo'e wan dɛna tuntɔɔduma.

ref: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
text: Gee ze'elego wa'am na ti ku bahɛ ti tuure kekeko en malum niiɛ pa'aha , gee zi'an ti tuure malum niira pa'aha la, zu'uhegɔ me malum niira pa'aha gana la bala,

ref: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
text: bala, be'em n pa'ali a paŋa kum puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti zu'uhegɔ wan pa'alɛ a paŋa doohe buurɔ, yu'ura sore bɔ'ɔra vom kamba'ahɛ sebo en boi Yezu Kiresta tu Daana puan la.

ref: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
text: Beni ti tu nyaa wan yele? Tu kɔ'ɔm kelum tuna be'em, ti la bahɛ ti zu'uhegɔ malum pa'aha?

ref: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
text: Sore ka boi bala la fii me! Ŋwani ti tumam sɛba en ka le bɔna be'em tuuma puan la, wan le lebena vɔna bu puan?

ref: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
text: Ya ka mi' ti tumam sɛba woo en sɔ tɔ Kiresta Yezu puan la, ba sɔ tɔ ki'ihɛ la a kum la puan?

ref: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
text: Bala zuo, ba pi' tu mɛ la Kiresta, doohe ban sɔ tɔ ki'ihɛ a kum la puan la, ti Kiresta en ihege kum la puan a Sɔ paŋa suŋa puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti tumam me wan kina la vom paalum.

ref: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
text: Tumam han naaɛ la en mɛ a kum buna taaba wa puan, tu wan ba'ahum ta naaɛ la en a ihegere dina taaba wa me puan.

ref: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
text: Tu mi' ti ba daa kpa' tu naanɛ kɛka la mɛ la Kiresta, ti tu be'em naanɛ la sagum, ti tu da le dɛna da'aba bo' be'em.

ref: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en ki, boi la fai ti be'em ka le tana en.

ref: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
text: Gee tumam han ki mɛ la Kiresta, tu nam ya ti tu me wan ta vɔna la en.

ref: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
text: Tu mi' ti Kiresta en vo'e kum puan na la, kan le makɛ ki. Kum paŋa ka le ta'na en.

ref: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
text: En ki kum sebo la, a ki keehe la be'em, buyina za'a. Gee vom sebo ti a vɔna wa, a vɔi bo' la Yinɛ.

ref: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
text: Bala, yamam me biha nyɛta ya mɛhi ti, ya ki keehe be'em mɛ, gee nyaa vɔna bo' Yinɛ Kiresta Yezu puan.

ref: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
text: Bala, da bo' ya fole, ti be'em sɔna ya ingana wa en ki la, ita ya ti ya nana ya kɔrehi la.

ref: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
text: Da le dikɛ ya ingana la ze'sɛka bo' be'em, ti la dɛna be'em lɔkɔ tara tuna be'em tuuma. Gee tugum dikɛ ya mɛhi bo' Yinɛ, wuu nɛreba ti ba tari ya kum puan wa'am vom puan na la. Gee dikɛ ya ihe la bo' Yinɛ, ti si dɛna tuuntɔɔ vom tuuma lɔgerɔ.

ref: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em kan le sɔna ya, yan ka boi ze'elego la puan, gee bɔna zu'uhegɔ puan la. Tuntɔɔ vom yamehi

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
text: Beni en gee? Tu tuna be'em ti tu ka dole ze'elego la, gee tu boi la zu'uhegɔ puan zuo? Sore ka boi la fii me!

ref: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
text: Ya ka mi' ti ya han dikɛ ya mɛhi bo' se'em ti ya wan nana en, ya dela se'em ti ya nana la da'aberɛ? La dɛna be'em en tari ya yɛ'a kum puan, bii la dɛna naŋa en tari ya yɛ'a tuuntɔɔ vom puan.

ref: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
text: Gee pɛka dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ, ti yamam sɛba en tabelɛ dɛna be'em da'aberɛ wa, nyaa dela sɛba en nane ya suya puan ha, doohe la pa'alegɔ seko taaba en dikɛ bo' ya la,

ref: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
text: la yan nyɛ faarɛ be'em puan la, ya nyaa dela tuuntɔɔ vom da'aberɛ.

ref: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
text: N tari la asaala yɛla mageha, ya bɔkerɛ en ka zo'e la zuo. Wuu yan tabelɛ dikɛ ya ihe wa lɔgerɔ bo' dɛgerɔ tuuma se'em la, malum galena kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra puan puan se'em la. Bala, nananawa, dikɛ ya ihe la za'a tihɛ, ti si dɛna da'aberɛ bo' tuuntɔɔ vom, ti Yinɛ pee ya.

ref: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
text: Yan daa de be'em da'aberɛ la, ya daa ka tari sɛla ita la tuntɔɔnɛ.

ref: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
text: Bala, beni sum ti ya nyɛ yele sɛ'a en nyaa tari ya vi wa puan? Yele aŋa za'a ba'ahegɔ dela kum.

ref: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
text: Gee nananawa ti ba nyaa faaɛ ya be'em puan bahɛ fai, gee ti ya dɛna Yinɛ da'aberɛ wa, sum sebo ti ya nyɛ dela sakerɛ. Ti de ba'ahegɔ dɛna la vom kamba'ahɛ.

ref: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em yɔɔrɛ dela kum, gee Yinɛ zaŋa bo'olum la ti a bo' la, dela vom kan ba'ahɛ, Kiresta Yezu tu Daana la puan.

ref: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
text: Ya ka mi, n suurɔ wa, ti mam tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la sɛba en mi' ze'elego la. Ti ze'elego la ta' nɛra la a vom saŋa ma'a?

ref: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
text: Bala, pɔka en ele, ze'elego la lu' pɔka la mɛ ta' la ka sira la, en nan vɔi la. Gee ka sira la han ki, ba yeheri ka sire-ele ze'elego la puan bahera mɛ.

ref: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
text: La dela bala mɛa n zɔduma wa, yamam ki mɛ keehe ze'elego la, Kiresta inganɛ la puan, ti ya dɛna ayima dinɛ, ya dɛna se'em daana ti ba ihege en kum puan, ti tu ta'am wam wala bo' Yinɛ la.

ref: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
text: Tumam en daa nan boi tumam en boti se'em puan la, tu be'em kɔrehi la en boi tu puan la daa ihegere mɛ, ze'elego la zuo, tuna tu inganɛ wa puan, wuna kum wala.

ref: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
text: Gee nananawa, ba yehe tumam ze'elego la puan mɛ. Tumam ka le vɔna sɛla en yuum tari tumam ti tu dɛna yamehi la puan, ti tu da dɛna yamehi bo' diimen diimen gulehegɔ la, gee vom paalum la en de Sia puan vom la. Ze'elego la, la Be'em

ref: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
text: Tu wan yeti bu? Ti ze'elego la dela be'em bii? Sore ka boi la fii me! Gee la bala za'a, la han dagi ni la ze'elego la zuo, mam kan mini be'em. Mam kan mini ti piŋa dela sɛla, ze'elego la han ka yele yeti, “Da pim.”

ref: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
text: Gee be'em la en nyɛ yɔ'ɔ nuurɛ bu'a la puan la, nyaa tari la piŋa yɛla kekeko kina n puan na. Ze'elego la han ka boni, be'em ki ni mɛ gã'a.

ref: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
text: Saŋa ka yima, mam tabelɛ vɔna mɛ, ti ze'elego la ka boi. Gee nuurɛ bu'a la n wa'am na la, be'em vo'e mɛ na,

ref: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
text: ti mam ki, ti nuurɛ bu'a de mɛŋa en wan bo' ni mam vom la, nyaa lebege dɛna kum bo' mam.

ref: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em en nyɛ yɔ'ɔ nuurɛ bu'a la puan la, bu pã'ahɛ mam mɛ, doohe bilam ku mam.

ref: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
text: Bala, ze'elego la dela fa'a ti nuurɛ bu'a la dela Fa'a dɛna dɔ'ɔ, ana suŋa.

ref: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
text: Bala, sɛla en ani suŋa la, en tari kum na bo' mam yoi? Sore ka boi la fii me! La dela be'em en tuni, ti a ku mam, dɔla sɛla en ani suŋa la puan, ti la pa'alɛ ti be'em dela be'em, gee wan doohe nuurɛ bu'a la puan yalegɛ nuurɛ gaŋɛ bala. Zaberɛ en boi mam puan

ref: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
text: Bala zuo, tu mi' ti ze'elego la dela Sia puan tuunɛ, gee mam en ŋwana dɛna asaala, ti ba koohe mam ti n dɛna da'aberɛ bo' be'em.

ref: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
text: N ka bɔkeri n mɛŋa itegɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, n ka iti mam boti n ita sɛla la, gee n tugum ita la yele sɛ'a ti n ka bɔta la.

ref: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
text: Bala, mam han ita sɛla ti n ka bɔta la. Mam sakɛ ya ti ze'elego la ani suŋa mɛ.

ref: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Gee yelemɛŋerɛ la dela, la dagi la mam en iti bala, gee be'em la en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri n puan la en iti bala.

ref: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
text: Mam mi' ti yelesunɛ ka boi n puan. N yeti la n inganɛ wa puan n bala. Mam wan ti'ihɛ sɛla en ani suŋa bo' n mɛŋa, gee n kan ta'am tum dɛ.

ref: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Bala, mam han tuna sɛla ti n ka bɔta, la yi dagi mam en tuni, gee be'em la en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri n puan la.

ref: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
text: Bala, mam biheri dɛ ti la nyaa dɛna la ze'elego en boi mam puan dɛna ti, n han bɔta ti n tum sum, tuunfi'iha la en ba' lɛm mam.

ref: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
text: Mam nyɛ ti mam boti Yinɛ ze'elego la mɛ zo'e zo'e n puan puan ha,

ref: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
text: gee mam nyɛti la ze'elego kuyima n inganɛ wa puan en zaberɛ la ze'elego seko en boi mam puti'irɛ puan la, bahɛ ti mam dɛna da'aberɛ bo' be'em ze'elego la en boi mam iŋa wa puan la.

ref: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
text: Bihɛ mam n de nɛra vom n tari nimbaalega se'em. Ani en wan faaɛ mam insɛka n tari kum kina wa puan bahɛ?

ref: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
text: Gee Yinɛ n sɔi fara doohe Yezu Kiresta tu Daana la puan. Bani zuo, mam suure puan, mam dela da'aberɛ bo' Yinɛ ze'elego la, gee n inganɛ puan, mam dela da'aberɛ bo' be'em ze'elego la.

ref: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
text: Bala zuo, nananawa, sageŋɔ ka boi sɛba en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la zuo.

ref: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
text: Beni zuo, ze'elego seko en boi Fa'a Sia la en de vom Kiresta Yezu puan la, faaɛ ya bahɛ la fai, ze'elego seko en tari be'em la kum la nu'uhen.

ref: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ tum sɛla en daa kpe'em bo' ze'elego la ti ku ka nyaŋɛ tum, doohe la inganɛ wa en tari tɔregɔ la, a iŋɛ dina doohe la en tum a mɛŋa dayua ti ka tara inganɛ en ani wuu asaala be'em inganɛ wa, ti ka zabɛ be'em, sagum be'em paŋa, la inganɛ wa,

ref: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
text: ti ze'elego la en boti yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere la tum tu puan suŋa suŋa, tumam sɛba en ka vɔi wuu tu inganɛ la en boti se'em, gee Fa'a Sia la en boti se'em la.

ref: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
text: Sɛba en vɔi ba inganɛ en boti se'em la, dikɛ ba puti'ira delum la inganɛ wa yɛla, gee sɛba en vɔi Fa'a Sia la en boti se'em la, dikɛ ba puti'ira delum la Fa'a Sia la en boti se'em.

ref: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
text: Fum dikɛ fu puti'irɛ delum inganɛ wa la dela kum, gee fum dikɛ fum puti'irɛ delum Fa'a Sia la dela vom, la suma'ahum.

ref: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
text: Bala zuo, sɛba en dele ba inganɛ la kan ta'am tigɛ Yinɛ nini.

ref: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
text: Gee yamam wa la ka boi inganɛ puan. Ya boi la Fa'a Sia la puan, Yinɛ Sia la han sirum bɔna la ya. Gee se'em woo en ka tari Kiresta Sia la a puan, dagi Kiresta dinɛ.

ref: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
text: Gee Kiresta han bɔna ya puan, la ya ingana la en pun ki be'em zuo la, gee Sia la en bo'ori vom doohe tuntɔɔnɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ bo' tu la zuo.

ref: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
text: Se'em daana Sia en vo'e Yezu kum puan la han kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra ya puan, Se'em daana en vo'e Kiresta kum puan mɛa wan bo' ya vom ya ingana wa n wan ki wa puan , doohe a Sia la en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la ya la.

ref: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
text: Ya han vɔna inganɛ la en boti se'em la, ya wan ki. Gee ya han vɔna Fa'a Sia la puan, ku inganɛ tuuma la, ya wan vɔna.

ref: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
text: Sɛba woo ti Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la gu'ura ba ti ba kina tuuntɔɔ la, en sirum de Yinɛ kɔma.

ref: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
text: Fa'a Sia kana mɛa en de tu aze'nyɛdaana la tumam sia la, ti tu dela Yinɛ kɔma,

ref: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
text: ti tu han dɛna Yinɛ kɔma, tu dela sɛba en wan sooe sɔna, tu dela Yinɛ kɔma wan sooe Yinɛ lɔgerɔ sɔna. Tu naaɛ mɛ la Kiresta sooe sɔna Yinɛ lɔgerɔ, tu han sirum namehɛ la en, tu me wan ta lagum nyɛ suŋa la en. Beere Suŋa

ref: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
text: Mam mi' n suure puan suŋa suŋa ti namehegɔ seko ti tu nameha saŋa kana wa puan wa la, ti ku dagi sɛla en wan dikɛ makɛ la sunsɛka en maahum ti ka niiɛ bo' tu na wa.

ref: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
text: Timpaberɛ wa za'a ani la kɛli, gee gura Yinɛ kɔma wa niirɛ na.

ref: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
text: Timpaberɛ wa za'a bɔberɛ se'ere en ka tari vuurɛ puan wa, dagi la ka sumboolum, gee se'em n iŋɛ ka bala ti la zi'im wan ba'ahɛ suŋa,

ref: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
text: ti timpaberɛ wa za'a wan faaɛ ka mɛŋa fai yehe ka bobere wa en ye' pu'urɛ puan wa, gee nyaa to'e Yinɛ kɔma suŋa fai bɔŋa wa.

ref: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
text: Tu mi' ti timpaberɛ wa za'a uheri mɛ viregere too wa'am na paaɛ zina.

ref: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
text: Dagi la timpaberɛ wa ma'a, gee tumam mɛhi en tari Fa'a Sia didiina tu puan la me uheri mɛ puan ha, gee gura ti Yinɛ to'e tu bihera, gee bo' tu inganɛ wa faarɛ.

ref: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
text: Tu beere puti'irɛ zuo tu tari faarɛ. Puti'irɛ se'ere en diŋɛ niiɛ na, le dagena puti'irɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, ani en tari putɛ'se'ere en diŋɛ niiɛ na?

ref: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
text: Gee tu beere puti'irɛ han dɛna la sɛla ti tu nan ka nyɛ, tu wan gura dɛ suŋa suŋa la sugeri.

ref: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
text: Bala mɛa ti Fa'a Sia la suŋera tɔ tu tɔregɔ puan, se'ere en sɔi la, tu ni pun ka mina tu wan suhɛ Yinɛ lan suni se'em, gee Fa'a Sia kaŋa mɛa en ni ŋmɛ'ɛra nupu'ɔ bɔ'ɔra tɔ, la si'iŋɔ kãra en gani yetɔgum.

ref: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
text: Ti Yinɛ en biheri suure puan la, mi' sɛla en boi Fa'a Sia la puti'iren mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Fa'a Sia la sooe ŋmɛ'ɛra la nupu'ɔ bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la, dɔla Yinɛ sumboolum.

ref: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
text: Tu mi ti sɛla woo tuni naara taaba tara la sum bɔ'ɔra la sɛba en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, sɛba ti Yinɛ yi' doohe a puti'iren la.

ref: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba ti a pun mina la, a pun maahum ba ze'ele mɛ, ti ba tara a dayua la naanɛ, ti a dayua la ta'am dɛna zuodaana suurɔ kekeko puan.

ref: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
text: Bala zuo, beni ti tu wan yele, yele ana wa puan? Yinɛ han sɔna tɔ, ani en wan ta'am zabɛ tɔ?

ref: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
text: Yinɛ se'ere en daa ka bahɛ a mɛŋa dayua, gee dikɛ ka tihɛ tumam za'a zuo la, a kan doohe a dayua mɛa puan bo' tu sɛla woo ti tu bɔta me yoi?

ref: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
text: Ani n wan ta'am tari se'ere na dɔgelɛ sɛba ti Yinɛ looe la zuo? La dela Yinɛ mɛŋa en bo' tu buurɔ.

ref: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
text: Ani en wan ta'am sagum tɔ? La dela Kiresta Yezu en ki, ɛ̃ɛ, se'em ti Yinɛ vo'e en la, ti a bɔna Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o la. Eŋa en sirum de nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra bɔ'ɔra tumam.

ref: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
text: Ani en wan ta'am bɔkɛ tumam Kiresta nɔŋerɛ wa puan? Wuŋerɛ bii, susageŋɔ bii, namehegɔ bii, kom bii, beele bii, nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega bii sɔ'a?

ref: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
text: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em la, “Yamam zuo, ba kuuri tumam la daare woo. Ba bihɛ nyɛ tumam ti tu ana mɛ wuu piihi ti ba tara yɛ'a kɔregerɛ la.”

ref: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
text: Gee yele ana woo puan, tumam gani nyaŋereba mɛ, doohe se'em daana en nɔŋɛ tu la puan.

ref: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam sakɛ n suure puan suŋa suŋa, ti kum bii vom, bii suŋereba, bii bihereba, bii sɛla n boi zina , bii sɛla n wan wa'am beere na, bii pahi,

ref: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
text: bii sɛla en boi zuin zuin ha, bii sɛla en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha, bii sɛla bɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a wan ta'am bɔkɛ tumam Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ wa n boi Kiresta Yezu tu Daana puan wa.

ref: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
text: Mam tɔgeri la yelemɛŋerɛ Kiresta puan, Mam ka pareni. Fa'a Sia la yele mam puti'irɛ puan ti la dela bala.

ref: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
text: Mam tari la susageŋɔ kãtɛ, la daaŋɔ en ka tari ba'ahegɔ n suuren wa.

ref: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
text: Mam ti'ihɛ yeti, nɔpɔtɛ wan pɔ ni bahɛ mam mɛŋa zuo, ti ba ŋmaaɛ ni mam Kiresta zi'an bahɛ ni, mam mɛŋa nɛreba wa zuo. Mam suurɔ inganɛ puan wa zuo.

ref: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
text: Ba dela Izirale buuri wa. Bama ti Yinɛ to'e ba biha, pa'alɛ ba a suŋa, nɔnaarɛ, ze'elego, na'ahegɔ, la a nɔbiŋere la dɛna ba dinɛ.

ref: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
text: Bama en de Hiburuduma kansanhi yaahi, gee ti inganɛ puan, Kiresta la yehe ba puan na, se'em en gani za'a la. Sum dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

ref: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
text: La dagi ti Yinɛ yelehum la koŋe mɛ. Dagi Izirale buuri la za'a en de Izirale buuri yelemɛŋerɛ,

ref: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
text: Ŋwana pa'alɛ ti, dagi kɔma woo ti ba dɔgɛ ba inganɛ puan en de Yinɛ kɔma, gee nɔbiŋere kɔma la ti ba kãala ba yaahi.

ref: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɔbiŋere la en yele se'em en ŋwana, “Saŋa kana wa puan n wan lebe na, ti Saara wan pɛga bia en de budibela.”

ref: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
text: ti la dagena la nɛra tuuma zuo, gee Yinɛ yi'a la), ti ba yuum yele en yeti, “Kiima la wan tugum dɛna bɛ'a la yameŋa.”

ref: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
text: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Mam nɔŋɛ la Yakɔbe, Gee yaaɛ Esɔ la.”

ref: Are we saying God is unfair? Certainly not!
text: Beni ti tu wan yele? Ŋmɛlema en boi Yinɛ puan bii? Sore kai' boi fii me.

ref: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
text: Bala, la dagi la asaala suboolum, bii a tiregerɛ, gee la dela Yinɛ mɛŋa se'em n tari nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
text: Gulehegɔ la gulehɛ Faro yele yeti, “Mam yɛɛlɛ fum na'am la yele dina wa zuo, ti n pa'alɛ n paŋa fum puan, ti muulɛ n yu'urɛ wa timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a.”

ref: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
text: Bala zuo, Yinɛ tari nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bɔ'ɔra la se'em woo ti a looe, gee me kãbera se'em woo suure ti a bɔta. Yinɛ sunsunyiim la a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ

ref: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
text: Ya wan yele mam yeti, “Ŋwani ti Yinɛ sina gee bihera saaleba tuure? Ani en wan ta'am zagehɛ Yinɛ suboolum?”

ref: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
text: Gee ani en de fum, asaala, wan ta'am ŋmɛ' nɔkpe'ene la Yinɛ? Yagumbaherɛ wan ta'am soke yagumbaha la yeti, “Ŋwani ti fum yagum mam ŋwana?”

ref: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
text: Yagumbaha la ka tari yɔgerɔ la paŋa, wan ta'am dikɛ yɔgerɔ tunɔ mɛa yagum lɔkɔ kuyima, ti kuŋɔ dɛna tuulum lɔkɔ, gee yagum kuyima ti kuŋɔ dɛna tuunse'ere yoo lɔkɔ?

ref: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
text: Ŋwani, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa han bɔta ti a pa'alɛ a sunsunyiim, ti a paŋa niiɛ, gee me bɔta gee ŋmibera la sugeri, la lɔgeseto ti a suure nyiie la tɔ, lɔge seto en maalɛ bo' sageŋɔ la.

ref: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
text: Gee ŋwani, a han le iŋɛ bala ti a pa'alɛ a suŋa bo' sɛba en de a zɔ ba nimbu'ɔ la, sɛba ti a pun maahum biŋe a ti a pa'alɛ ba a suŋa kãtɛ la,

ref: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
text: Wuu lan yele Hozeya gɔŋɔ la puan se'em yeti, “Sɛba en daa dagi mam nɛreba la, n wan yi' ba ‘n nɛreba.’ Eŋa se'em ti ba yuum ka nɔŋɛ la, n wan yi' en ti ‘n nɔŋerɛ bunɔ.’ ”

ref: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
text: “Zɛ'se'ehi ti ba daa yi' ya ti, ‘Ya dagi mam nɛreba la.’ Bilam mɛa ti ba wan yi'ira ya Yinɛ vua la kɔma.”

ref: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
text: Azaaya yuum kaahɛ Izirale yele yeti, “La Izirale buuri la kɔma en zo'e wuu kulega tintɔnɔ la, ba fii wa ma'a en wan nyɛ faarɛ.

ref: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
text: Daana la wan tum a gakerɛ tuunɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo la ninmu'urɛ, gee ba'ahɛ de ŋwana kalam, iŋɛ de ŋwana girega girega.”

ref: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
text: Ŋwana ti Azaaya tɔgɛ biŋe yeti, “Pɛntiireba nɛŋa Daana wa han ka bahɛ ni tu buuri wa fii wa bo' tu, Tumam me wan piiɛ ni wuu Sodom la. Tu wan ani ni wuu Gomora la.” Izirale buuri bumbu'um

ref: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
text: Beni ti tu nyaa wan yele? Sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la en daa ka zigeri tuuntɔɔ vom wa yele la, nyɛ tuuntɔɔ vom la mɛ. Tuntɔɔ vom sebo en dole sakerɛ puan la.

ref: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
text: Ŋwani ti ba ka nyaŋɛ? Ba daa ka zigeri ɛɛra dɛ sakerɛ puan, gee ba daa eere de mɛ wuu la dela tuuma puan la. Ba ŋmɛ' ba nama mɛ la kuge se'ere en piti la,

ref: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
text: wuu lan gulehɛ yele se'em la. “Bihɛ ya, n biŋere la kugere Zaayɔn, en wan pita nɛreba, Taŋa en wan bahɛ ti nɛreba luta, Gee fum se'em woo en nam en, nyanɛ kan di fum.”

ref: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
text: N suurɔ, mam suure en boti sɛla, la mam pu'uhegɔ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ ba zuo dela, ba nyɛ faarɛ.

ref: I know they love God, but they don't understand
text: Mam wan ta'am yeti, ba ba' tara ninmu'urɛ mɛ paaɛ paaɛ bo' Yinɛ, gee la ka dole bɔkerɛ.

ref: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la ba ka mi' tuuntɔɔ vom sebo en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na la, gee zigera ti ba tum ba mɛŋa tuntɔɔnɛ la, ba ka dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la.

ref: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta en de ze'elego la ba'ahegɔ, ti tuuntɔɔ vom la nyaa bɔna bo' se'em woo en nam en. Faarɛ dela nɛra woo dinɛ

ref: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
text: Moose gulehɛ tuuntɔɔ vom sebo en dole ze'elego malema puan na la yele yeti, “Se'em en iti ze'elego malema la dela a vɔna ze'elego la yɛla la za'a.”

ref: But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?”
text: Gee tuuntɔɔ vom sebo en ze'ele sakerɛ puan na la, a yeti, Da yele ya suya puan yeti, “Ani en wan zom saazuo ha?

ref: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
text: Gee gulehegɔ la yeti boo? “Yelehum la lɛm ya mɛ, kɔ'ɔm bɔna ya nɔgbamen la ya suyan.”

ref: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
text: Beni zuo, ya han purɛ la ya nɔya, ti Yezu en de Daana, gee nam la ya suya ti Yinɛ vo'e en kum la puan mɛ, ya wan nyɛ faarɛ.

ref: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra ni nam mɛ la a suure, ti Yinɛ bo' en buurɔ, gee me ni purɛ la a nuurɛ nyɛ faarɛ.

ref: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
text: Gɔŋɔ la puan yeti yaa, “Se'em ka boi nam Yezu, ti vi di a daana.”

ref: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
text: Yima yima ka boi Yuda la Girikiduma tiŋasuka. Daana ayima en de ba za'a Daana, gee me bɔ'ɔra ti la sɛkera se'em woo en yi' en.

ref: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, “Se'em woo en yi' Daana la yu'urɛ wan nyɛ faarɛ.”

ref: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
text: Gee nɛreba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani yi' se'em ti ba ka nam en? Gee ba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani nam se'em ti ba ka tabelɛ wum a yele? Gee ba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani wum, se'em han ka muulɛ bo' ba?

ref: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
text: Gee ba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani muulɛ, han dagena ba tum ba mɛ? Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Bihɛ sɛba en tari faarɛ yelehum la kina na la nama en ani se'em suŋa suŋa.”

ref: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
text: Gee dagi nɛra woo en nam bo' faarɛ yelehum la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Azaaya yeti, “Daana, ani en nam tu yelehum wa?”

ref: No one can have faith without hearing the message about Christ.
text: Bala zuo, sakerɛ ze'eti la wuŋa puan na, ti sɛla ti ba wum la me ze'eti la Kiresta yelehum la puan na.

ref: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
text: Gee mam sokere dela, Ba ka wum? Yelemɛŋerɛ, ba wum ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ gɔŋɔ la puan yeti, “Ba kua yehe paaɛ timpaberɛ wa zi'an woo mɛ, ti ba yelehum la paaɛ timpaberɛ wa luga za'a.”

ref: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
text: Ti mam le soke yeti, “Izirale yuum ka bɔkɛ me bii?” Yia, Moose yeti, “N wan bahɛ ti ya nini tɛta la sɛba en pun ka zo'e paaɛ tinmune. N wan bahɛ ti ya suure nyiie la tinyaalega.”

ref: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
text: Azaaya me yuum kãbɛ la suure yele ya yeti, “Sɛba en ka eere mam la, en tugum nyɛ mam. N niiɛ n mɛŋa bo' la sɛba en ka soke n yele.”

ref: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
text: Gee a tɔgɛ Izirale yele yeti, “Daarɛ woo, mam tiɛ n nu'o la bala bo' nɛreba en ka nane, sɛba en ka dole mam n boti sɛla la.”

ref: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
text: Mam soke nyaa, Yinɛ zagehɛ a nɛreba la mɛ n bala bii? Sore ka boi la fii me. Mam mɛŋa dela Izirale buuri, Aberaham yaaŋa, yehe Benyamin yizuo puan na.

ref: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
text: Yinɛ ka zagehɛ a nɛreba sɛba ti a pun mina ba na la. Ya ka mi' Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la en tɔgɛ Elia yele se'em, en yuum ŋmɛ'ɛri nupu'ɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan Izirale yele se'em la?

ref: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
text: “Daana, ba ku fum nɔtɔgeheba la la za'a, ba gbirege kaabegɔ zi'ihi la mɛ, ti mam ma'a en gee, ti ba ɛɛra mam me ti ba ku.”

ref: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
text: Beni en nyaa boi? Izirale koŋe ba to'e sɛla ti ba ɛɛra la mɛ. Sɛba ti Yinɛ pun looe la ma'a en nyɛ dɛ, gee bana la woo kãbɛ la ba suya,

ref: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
text: Bala mɛa, saŋa kana wa puan, fii wa boi ti Yinɛ looe ba a zu'uhegɔ la zuo.

ref: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Gee la yi han dɛna la zu'uhegɔ zuo, la le dagena tuuma, han dagena bala, zu'uhegɔ yi dagi zu'uhegɔ.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
text: wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Yinɛ bo' ba la sia sɛka en ka bɔkeri, nini en ka nyɛti la tuba en ka wuni wa'am na paaɛ zina beere wa.”

ref: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
text: Naba Deviti yeti, “Bahɛ ti ba dia zi'ihi ta lebege bɛregɔ nyɔgɛ ba, gee dɛna pirega pita ba, gee dɛna namehegɔ, nameha ba.

ref: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
text: Bahɛ ti ba nini pãarum, ti ba da nyɛta, bahɛ ti ba uma da malum ihegera.” Sɛba en dagi Yuuduma faarɛ

ref: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
text: Ti mam soke yeti, bala ba gɛ̃rɛ mɛ ta lu? Sore ka boi la fii me. Gee ba gɛrega la bahɛ ti faarɛ la paaɛ la sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la, ti la bo' Iziraleduma la nintɛrega.

ref: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
text: Bala, ba gɛrega la han bahɛ ti tarega paaɛ tiŋa wa zuo, ti ba tɔregɔ la bahɛ tarega paaɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la, gee ba nyaa han lebehe ze'ele suŋa suŋa la nyaa wan ana la ŋwani?

ref: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
text: Nananawa mam tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la yamam sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri wa, se'ere en sɔi la, mam de Yinɛ tuntuna bo' yamam. Mam na'aheri n tuunɛ la mɛ,

ref: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
text: ti daanse'ere n bahɛ ti nyuure kɛ̃ mam mɛŋa nɛreba la, ti n ta'am faaɛ ba basɛba bahɛ.

ref: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
text: Bama en zagehɛ Yinɛ la han bahɛ ti naarɛ wa'am tiŋa wa zuo, la Yinɛ tiŋa suka na, gee ba Yinɛ to'ore nyaa wan ana la ŋwani, gana kum vo'ore.

ref: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
text: Zom sebo en dikɛ kaabera yia, dɛna yia wala bo'olum la, han dɛna tuulum bunɔ, zom la za'a yi dela tuulum bunɔ. Nyɛga la han dɛna tuulum bunɔ, yila la me dela tuulum bunɔ.

ref: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
text: Gee yila la a sɛ'a daa han kɔ' mɛ, ti ba dikɛ yamam en de olive tiyɛɛhɛ tubera na wa wa'am na toŋe yila la zi'ihi la, ti ya lagum nyuura olive nyɛga la ko'sumɔ la,

ref: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
text: ya da ŋmɛ'ɛra ya nyu'urɔ la yila sɛ'a en kɔ' la. Ya han ŋmɛ'ɛra ya nyu'urɔ, ya tɛra ti, dagi yamam en suŋeri nyɛgerɛ la, gee tile la en tugum suŋera yamam.

ref: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
text: Ya ta'am yeti, “Yila en aahum, ti ba ta'am dikɛ fum toŋe.”

ref: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
text: Bala dela asira. Gee yila la kɔ' doohe la ba bumbu'um zuo, gee yamam ze' doohe la sakerɛ zuo. Bala, da ŋmɛ'ɛra ya nyu'urɔ gee tum ze' ya la dabeem.

ref: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ daa han ka bahɛ bo' yelemɛŋerɛ yila la, daanse'ere a kan bahɛ bo' yamam me.

ref: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
text: Bala, mini ya Yinɛ itesuŋɔ, la a kpe'eŋo seko ti a tara la. A nini mu'ɛ mɛ la sɛba en lu la, gee tara Yinɛ itesuŋɔ dɔla yamam, wuu ya han bɔna a sum la puan, bii a ŋmaaɛ yamam me bahɛ.

ref: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
text: Izirale nɛreba la han ka kelum bɔna ba bumbu'um la puan, Yinɛ wan dikɛ ba naaɛ le yɛ'ɛha. Se'ere en sɔi la,Yinɛ tari paŋa ti a le dikɛ ba naaɛ le yɛ'ɛha.

ref: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa han ŋmaaɛ yamam olive tiyɛa puan na naaɛ la timɛnka puan, ŋwani ti bana en tugum dɛna timɛnka yila wa kan le ta'am dikɛ naaɛ ba mɛŋa olive mɛnka yila la puan yɛ'ɛha. Izirale za'a wan nyɛ faarɛ

ref: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
text: Ŋwana ti ya kan ta'am yɛta ti, yamam ba' mina mɛ, n suurɔ. N boti ya bɔkɛ yele se'ere en sugɛ wa mɛ, ti Sukãberɛ en lu Izirale buuri la basɛba zuo, gura ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la kãlɛ en de se'em wan nyɛ faarɛ la ta kɛ̃ na ba'ahɛ suŋa suŋa.

ref: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
text: Bilam ti Izirale za'a nyaa wan nyɛ faarɛ wuu lan gulehɛ yele se'em ti, “Zaayɔn puan, ti Faara la wan yehe na, A wan sagum tuunfi'iha za'a Yakɔbe buuri la puan.”

ref: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
text: “Mam nɔnaarɛ en ŋwana ti en naaɛ la ba wa, en han ta saalum ba be'em la za'a bahɛ.”

ref: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
text: Han dɛna la yelehum la, ba dela Yinɛ dinduma yamam zuo. Gee han dɛna loohego, bamam ti Yinɛ nɔŋɛ, ba yaabeduma zuo.

ref: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ bo'olum, la a yi'a ka lukere.

ref: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
text: Wuu yamam en daa nan ka nane Yinɛ, gee nananawa nyaa nyɛ Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bamam ka nane la zuo la,

ref: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
text: bala mɛa ti bamam me ka nana, ti la bahɛ ti Yinɛ en zɔ yamam nimbu'ɔ se'em la, bamam me to'e nimbɔ'zɔrɛ dinɛ mɛa.

ref: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ pagɛ ba za'a la akanane puan, ti a ta'am zɔ ba za'a nimbu'ɔ.

ref: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
text: Bihɛ,Yinɛ tarega, la a yɛm la a mi'ilum pirɛ paaɛ mɛ ka tara tɛka. A gakerɛ gaŋɛ bɔkerɛ mɛ, ti a sɔya la ba' dɛna nyalema gaŋɛ bɔkerɛ!

ref: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
text: “Ani en tabelɛ mina Daana la puti'irɛ? Bii ani en de a ka'ana?”

ref: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
text: Bii ani en tabelɛ bo' Yinɛ bo'olum, “Bii tabelɛ bo' en sɛla, ti a to'e gee lebehe en?”

ref: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan, la eŋa zi'an ti sɛla woo zɛ'ɛta na, gee dɛna a dinɛ. Eŋa ti na'ahegɔ dɛna a dina saŋa woo. Taari.

ref: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
text: Bala zuo, n yeti ya mɛ n suurɔ, ti Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la zuo, ya dikɛ ya ihi la bo' wuu kaabɛ vua la, dɛna tuulum kaabegɔ, gee tigɛ Yinɛ nini, dina en de ya sia puan na'ahegɔ.

ref: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
text: Da dikɛ ya mɛhi naaɛ la tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, gee tugum malum sɛ ya mɛhi, ya puti'ira teere puan, la ya viihɛ baŋɛ sɛla en de Yinɛ sumboolum, la sɛla en ani suŋa, nara gee ba'ahɛ za'a.

ref: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
text: Zu'uhegɔ seko ti Yinɛ zu'uhɛ mam la zuo, mam yeti se'em woo en boi ya puan yeti, se'em da biha nyɛta a mɛŋa gana en sunti a ti'iha a mɛŋa yele se'em la, gee a sigeha a mɛŋa a ti'ihegɔ puan, ti nɛra woo bɔna wuu Yinɛ en makɛ a sakerɛ se'em bo' en la.

ref: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
text: Wuu iŋa en tara luga yima yima se'em, ti luga la za'a me ka lagum tuna tuunɛ deyina se'em la,

ref: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
text: bala mɛa ti tumam en zo'e se'em wa, dɛna iŋa kayima Kiresta puan. Gee la tu yima yima mɛa, tu dela iŋa kayima lɔgerɔ la taaba.

ref: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
text: Tu tari la zu'uhegɔ bo'olum en boi yima yima, doohe la ban bo' tu, tu zu'uhegɔ se'em la. Purega puan, tu dela tu puta ti la zuna la tu sakerɛ la,

ref: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
text: tuntuna dela a tuna, ti pa'ala me dɛna a pa'ala,

ref: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
text: Kpemehego, daana me dela a kpɛmeha, ti se'em en bo'ori, me bɔ'ɔra yabi yabi, Nɛŋadaana mu'ɛ nini, ti nimbɔ'zɔta me tuna la pupeelum. Yelemɛŋerɛ Kiresta bia daalehum

ref: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
text: Tari ya yelemɛŋerɛ nɔŋerɛ. Yaaɛ ya sɛla en de be'em, gee gurɛ sɛla en de sum kankaŋi.

ref: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
text: Nɔŋɛ ya taaba la giile. Pageri ya taaba la na'ahegɔ tuuma.

ref: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti ya inkirega kɔ'ɔhɛ. Kpe'em ya mɛ sia puan, bɔta ti ya tuna bɔ'ɔra Daana la.

ref: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
text: Nyɛɛni ya mɛ la beere puti'irɛ, tara ya sugeri ya namehegɔ puan, gee me ŋmibera ya pu'uhegɔ puan.

ref: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
text: Lageheri ya suŋera Yinɛ nɛresunduma la, gee tiɛ ya nu'uhi la itesuŋɔ paara saama.

ref: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
text: Ka'ari ya sum bɔ'ɔra sɛba en nameheri ya la, ka' sum, gee da pɔti ya bɔ'ɔra ba.

ref: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
text: Nyɛɛni ya la sɛba en nyɛɛni, gee kɛla la sɛba en kele.

ref: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
text: Boni ya la suma'ahum la taaba, da pa'ala ya mɛhi, gee boni ya la naheduma, da ti'iha ti ya wikɛ gana yan de sɛba.

ref: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
text: Da yɔ ya be'em bo' se'em en eŋa ya be'em, gee ni ti'ihɛ ya sɛla en ani suŋa nɛra woo ninin.

ref: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
text: Gee la han wan ta'am iŋɛ, lan de yamam la, boni ya la suma'ahum la nɛra woo.

ref: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
text: N suurɔ, da makɛ ya lerege nɛra ya mɛhi, gee bo' ya Yinɛ sunsunyiim la sore ti bu tum. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, “Leregere dela mam dinɛ, Mam en wan lebehe yɔ. Daana la en yele.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
text: Aayi, “ya dinduma kom han duna, diihe ya ba, ko'nyuuro han tara ba, bo' ya ba sɛla ti ba nyu. Ya han ita ŋwana, ya ka'ari la vi bugum pagela ba zuto.”

ref: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti be'em nyaŋɛ ya, gee tugum nyaŋɛ ya be'em, la ya sum la.

ref: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
text: Bahɛ ya ti nɛra woo sigehe a mɛŋa bo' timbihega kpɛ'ɛnduma la, se'ere en sɔi la kpe'eŋo ka boi han dagena Yinɛ. Kpe'eŋo seko woo en boi wa, Yinɛ en sɛ ba ze'ele.

ref: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
text: Bala zuo, fum se'em en zaberɛ kpe'emseko, zaberɛ la Yinɛ en looe sɛla, sɛba en zaberɛ la kpe'eŋo seko wan nyɛ la gakerɛ.

ref: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, kpɛ'ɛnduma dagi dabeem lɔkɔ bo' sum, gee be'em. Fum ka boti fum tara dabeem la kpɛ'ɛnduma? Yi ita sɛla en de ma'a, gee ba wan pɛgɛ hɔ.

ref: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, a dela Yinɛ yameŋa fum sum zuo. Gee fum han ita sɛla en dagi ma'a, fum dela fum zɔta dabeem, se'ere en sɔi la, kpɛ'ɛma la ka tari a paŋa daalehum la la yoo. La dela Yinɛ yameŋa la tuunɛ ti a namehɛ se'em en tuni tuunyooro.

ref: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
text: Bala zuo, nɛra dela a sigehe a mɛŋa, dagi la ba wan namehɛ fum la ma'a, gee fum suure la en gakeri fum la me.

ref: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
text: Bala mɛa zuo ti fum me yɔɔra kuba la. Se'ere en sɔi la, kpɛ'ɛnduma la dela Yinɛ yamehi, mu'ɛ nini la tuun-ana wa.

ref: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
text: Da tara se'em sanɛ, han dagena ya nɔŋɛ taaba ma'a. Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em en nɔŋɛ a tadaana, tum ze'elego la en boti se'em woo ba'ahɛ mɛ.
