ref: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Ze'elego seko en yeti, “Da tum kabohi, da ku nɛra, da zu, da pim”; la ze'elego seto woo en boi la za'a, kilehe bɔna la yele dina wa puan, “Nɔŋɛ fum mabia wuu fum nɔŋɛ fum mɛŋa se'em la.”

ref: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
text: Nɔŋerɛ ka tuni mabia be'em. Bala, se'em en tari nɔŋerɛ, doohe la ze'elego la za'a. Ninmu'urɛ muulegɔ

ref: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
text: Dina pooren, ya mi' sansɛka puan ti tu bɔna nananawa, la nyaa paaɛ sansɛka en de ya niiɛ ya guum la puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, faarɛ nyaa lɛm tu nananawa gana tumam n daa nan nam Yezu la.

ref: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
text: Yu'uŋɔ la pun tole mɛ, ti beyiine pɔra. Bala, bahɛ ya ti tu biŋe lika puan yɛla kinkɛleŋa, gee dikɛ niihum zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la yɛ.

ref: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
text: Bahɛ ya ti tu vɔni ya na'ahegɔ vom, wuu tumam n de niihum kɔma la, gee dagi tu dɛna sɛba en dole dita dikãreyooro la de'em yooro, bugera, tuna dɛgerɔ tuuma, la yetɔgeyooro, dagi zaba la nintɛrega yɛla.

ref: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
text: Gee tugum dikɛ ya Daana Yezu Kiresta yɛ wuu fuo la gee da bo' ya inganɛ wa sore ti de dikɛ tu doohe tu kunkoma'ahum la yɛla la.

ref: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
text: To'ohe ya sɛba en ka kpe'em ba sakerɛ puan la, gee dagi ya ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkɛ'ɛma la ba, yɛla zuo.

ref: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
text: Sɛba nam la ba dita ban paaɛ sɛla woo, ti sɛba me en ka kpe'em la, dita vuurɔ ma'a.

ref: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
text: Fum dela ani gakera ayima yamehi? Ba mɛŋa Daana la nɛŋan ti ba wan ba zɛ'a bii ba lu. Ba me wan zɛ'a, se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la wan bahɛ ti ba zɛ'a.

ref: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
text: Sɛba bihɛ la dabeherɛ deyima ti de dɛna tuulum dabeherɛ gana dabeherɛ deyima, ti sɛba me biha dabeha la za'a ti a dɛna buyina. Bahɛ ya ti ba woo tari ba suya puan ban ti'ihɛ se'em la.

ref: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
text: Sɛba en looe dabeherɛ se'ere la, iti bala bɔ'ɔra la Daana la, ti sɛba me en diti sɛla woo la me diti na'aha la Daana la, doohe la ba ni bo' Daana la fara gee di la. Ti sɛba me en ka diti sɛla woo la me lɔkeri na'aha la Daana la, la ban ni bo' Yinɛ fara de zuo la.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
text: Tu ka vɔi bo' la tu mɛhi, tu ka kiiri tu mɛhi kum.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
text: Tu han vɔna, tu vɔi bo' la Daana la, tu han le ki mɛ, tu dela Daana la dinɛ. Bala, tu vɔi mɛ, bii tu ki mɛ, tu dela Daana la dinɛ.

ref: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti Kiresta ki, gee le vo'e, ti a dɛna Daana bo' ki'induma la vɔpa.

ref: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
text: Ŋwani ti fum gakera fum suuɔ? Bii ŋwani ti fum zɛɛna fum suuɔ? Tu za'a wan ze'ele Yinɛ gakerɛ kuka la nɛŋan mɛ.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, “Mam en vɔi wa, Daana la n yeti, dune woo wan kpa' tiŋa bo' mam, ti zɛleŋa woo wan dikɛ pɛka bo' Yinɛ.”

ref: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
text: Bala zuo, tu za'a wan ta yele tu yele tu yele bo' Yinɛ . Da bahɛ ti ayima pirɛ lu

ref: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
text: Bala zuo, da bahɛ ya ti tu gakera taaba, gee tu ku puti'irɛ ti tu kan dikɛ sɛla beŋe, bii gu' se'em sore.

ref: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
text: Mam mi', gee ti Daana Yezu me bahɛ ti n baŋɛ ti, sɛla ka boi dɛna galeŋɔ lɔkɔ ku mɛŋa, gee ku de galeŋɔ lɔkɔ bɔ'ɔra la se'em daana en ti'ihɛ ti ku dela galeŋɔ lɔkɔ la.

ref: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
text: Fum suuɔ han nyɛ pɔgeleŋɔ doohe fum en diti sɛla puan, fum ka le kina la nɔŋerɛ. Da bahɛ ti fum diti sɛla sagum se'em ti Kiresta ki bo' en la.

ref: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
text: Bala zuo, da bahɛ ti sɛla en ani suŋa bo' fum la, ba ta tɔgera bu be'em sore.

ref: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan dagi la dia la nyua, gee tuuntɔɔ vom, suma'ahum la nyɛɛma Fa'a Sia la puan.

ref: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
text: Se'em woo en tuni dɔla sadɔɔ kuna bɔ'ɔra Kiresta, Yinɛ nini tigɛ la a daana mɛ, ti saaleba me to'e en .

ref: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
text: Bala, bahɛ ya ti tu dole ya la ninmu'urɛ yele sɛ'a en bo'ori tu suma'ahum, gee mɛta tɔ.

ref: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti dia zuo, sagum Yinɛ tuuma. Sɛla woo sirum dela tuulum, gee la me dela tuure bo' ya ti ya bahɛ ti nɛra lu doohe yan diti sɛla la.

ref: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
text: La ani suŋa ti tu da di dia, bii nyu daam, bii iŋɛ sɛla en wan bahɛ ti fum suuɔ lu.

ref: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
text: Sakerɛ se'ere ti fum tara la, tara dɛ fum ma'a, fum la Yinɛ tiŋasuka. Sum boi mɛ la sɛba en ka nyɛ se'ere zuo ti ba wan gakɛ ba mɛhi doohe bamam en bihɛ ti sɛla dagi be'em.

ref: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
text: Gee sɛba en bu'uri la, sageni la ba mɛhi, ba han di diihe sise'ehi, beni zuo, ba ka tuni la sakerɛ. Sɛla woo en ka ze'ele sakerɛ puan na, dela be'em.

ref: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
text: Tumam sɛba n kpe'em tu sakerɛ puan la dela tu ŋmibera la sɛba en tari tɔregɔ ba sakerɛ puan la, gee dagi tu kɔ'ɔm biha tu mɛhi nini tika ma'a.

ref: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
text: Tu ayima ayima dela tu tigera tu suurɔ nini, tu putɛ'sunɛ en de tu mɛ tu mabia la zuo.

ref: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta daa ka biheri la a mɛŋa nini tika ma'a yele; Gee wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Tuurɛ sɛ'a taaba ti sɛba tuura ya la, sigera la mam zuo.”

ref: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
text: Yinɛ se'ere en bo'ori sɛ ze'ele kankaŋi la kpemehego la, iŋɛ ti ya bɔna la suyinɛ la taaba, wuu Kiresta Yezu en boti se'em la,

ref: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
text: bala ti ya wan naaɛ taaba la putɛ'yinɛ la kɔyiŋa, pɛgera Yinɛ en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la. Faarɛ yelehum la dela Yuuduma, la sɛba en dagi Yuuduma dinɛ

ref: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
text: Bala zuo, to'oheri ya taaba, wuu Kiresta en to'e ya se'em la, ti la dɛna na'ahegɔ bo' Yinɛ.

ref: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
text: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti Kiresta lebege la yameŋa bo' ya sɛba en ŋma la, Yinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la zuo, ti a bahɛ ti Yinɛ en biŋe nɔse'ere bo' yaabeduma la tum,

ref: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
text: gee me bahɛ ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la me pɛgɛ Yinɛ a zu'uhegɔ la zuo. Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Bala zuo, n wan purɛ fum yu'urɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la tiŋasuka, gee yuum pɛka yuuma pɛgɛ fum yu'urɛ.”

ref: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
text: Gee le yele yeti, “Nyɛɛni ya mɛ la a nɛreba, yamam sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri wa.”

ref: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
text: Gee le yele yeti, “Pɛgɛ ya Daana wa, yamam sɛba woo en dagi Yuda buuri wa, gee bahɛ ti nɛra woo pɛgera en.”

ref: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
text: Azaaya yele pa'ahɛ yeti, “Yesi puan, nyɛgerɛ kini na, Se'em en wan ihege na bihera sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la. Eŋa puan ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la wan tara beere puti'irɛ.”

ref: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
text: Nananawa,Yinɛ se'ere en sɔi beere puti'irɛ la, pirɛ ya la nyɛɛma woo la suma'ahum yan nam la zuo, ya pirɛ la beere puti'irɛ ti bu yabera doohe Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan. Pɔɔl gulehɛ la sukpe'ene

ref: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
text: Mam mɛŋa nam ya mɛ n suurɔ, ti ya mɛhi pirɛ mɛ la itesuŋɔ, pirɛ la mi'ilum za'a, wan ta'am ka'am taaba.

ref: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
text: Gee la bala za'a, yeledaahi a sɛ'a ti n gulehɛ la sukãberɛ bo' ya na la dela, n tiihɛ ya, doohe zu'uhegɔ la ti Yinɛ bo' mam,

ref: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
text: ti n dɛna Kiresta Yezu tuntuna bo' sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la, malema daana tuuma Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum wa puan, ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri wa la, Yinɛ to'e ba kaabegɔ, ti Fa'a Sia la pee ba.

ref: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta Yezu puan, mam tari se'ere zuo ti n pagera la mam tuni bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ wa mɛ.

ref: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam kan makɛ ti n tɔgɛ sɛla, han dagena Kiresta en ba'ahɛ tuunsɛ'a doohe mam puan, nyaŋɛ sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri ti ba nam Yinɛ, doohe mam tugum bii tuunse'ere woo puan,

ref: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
text: doohe paŋa kirehi, la nyalema, en tum doohe Yinɛ Sia paŋa la puan, pɔhɛ Yerusalɛm ta paaɛ Ilirikum, mam tɔgɛ Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la za'a za'a.

ref: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
text: Ŋwana en bahɛ ti mam lu' n suure ti, n kan muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la zi'an ti ba tabelɛ purɛ Kiresta bini, la n da ta mɛ dɔgelɛ ayima ɛberɛ zuo,

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
text: gee woo lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Sɛba ti ba ka tabelɛ yele ba Kiresta yele la, wan nyɛ en, ti sɛba en ka tabelɛ wum a yele la wan bɔkɛ en.” Pɔɔl maaheni ti a kiŋɛ Arome

ref: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
text: Dina zuo ti saŋa zo'e zo'e mam ka nyɛta fole wan wa'am ya zi'an na la.

ref: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
text: Gee nananawa, mam n ka le tara zi'an tinsina wa woo puan ti n kiŋɛ la, n suure nyaa kɔ'ɔm bɔta mɛ wuu yuuma zo'e zo'e ti en bɔta se'em la, ti n wa'am ya zi'an na,

ref: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
text: n han ta paaɛ Sipeen tiŋa, n boti n nyɛ ya mɛ n sore kinɛ la puan, ti ya te'ele mam ti n tole, n han wa'am na di pupeelum la ya dabeha fii wa.

ref: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Masedoniya, la Akaiya tihi sakereba la puurɛ pee mɛ ti ba tɔrɛ ba tarega la sɛba en de naheduma nɛresunduma la puan la Yerusalɛm puan la.

ref: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
text: Ba puurɛ pee mɛ ti ba tum dina wa. Yelemɛŋerɛ bamam mɛhi la tari la ba sanɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yuuduma la han tɔrɛ ba sia puan maalegɔ bo' sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la, bamam me dela ba tum inganɛ puan lɔgerɔ maalegɔ tuunɛ me bo' ba.

ref: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
text: Bala, n han ta paaɛ tum tuun-dina ba'ahɛ, dikɛ ban bo' ba sɛla la bo' ba ba'ahɛ, n wan doohe ya bɔba na, tole Sipeen tiŋa.

ref: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
text: N me mi' ti n han ta yɛ'a na, n ye' mɛ na, pirɛ la Kiresta maalegɔ la puan.

ref: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
text: N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ n suurɔ, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan, la Sia la nɔŋerɛ puan, ti ya naaɛ la mam la ninmu'urɛ pu'uhegɔ bo' Yinɛ n zuo,

ref: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
text: ti n piiɛ sɛba en ka nam Yudiya puan la nu'uhen, gee ti n tuunɛ Yerusalɛm puan la me, nɛresunduma la wan to'e bu.

ref: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
text: Bala ti Yinɛ sumboolum la puan, n wan wa'am ya zi'an la nyɛɛma, gee tu za'a lagum me tara pupeelum la taaba.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
text: Nananawa, suma'ahum Yinɛ la bɔna la ya za'a. Taari.

ref: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
text: N dikeri la tu taya Fobi en de nɛŋadaana suŋera Sinkireya tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la pa'ala ya,

ref: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
text: ti ya to'e en Daana la puan wuu lan nari se'em bo' nɛresunduma la, la ya suŋɛ en la sɛla woo ti a wan bɔta ya zi'an. A dela suŋɛ bo' nɛreba zo'e zo'e, ta ta' la mam mɛŋa.

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Pirisika la Akuila, sɛba en tuni la mam Kiresta Yezu puan la,

ref: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
text: la bamam en pɔ̃hɛ ba vom mam zuo la. La dagi mam ma'a en pu'uheri ba na, gee sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri lageŋɔ duma la za'a pu'uheri ba na.

ref: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya lageŋɔ seko en lageheri ba yire puan la bo' mam. Pu'uhɛ ya mam sunɔŋerɛ suuɔ Epanetuse se'em en de yia daana Esiya sɔ'ɔlum la woo puan ti a tee a suure bo' Kiresta la.

ref: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Mɛɛri, se'em en tum zo'e zo'e ya puan la.

ref: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Anduronikus la Yuniya en de mam buuri, ti ba daa lagum pagɛ ba la mam la. Bama en de sɛba ti ba ba' mina Yezu tuntuneba la puan. Bama pun dɔla Kiresta mɛ gee ti mam baŋɛ wa'am na.

ref: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Ampiliatus en de mam nɔŋerɛ suuɔ, Daana la puan.

ref: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Yurebanuse mam tuntuna tadaana Kiresta puan, la Sitakise ti n nɔŋɛ la.

ref: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Apeles, se'em tuuma n tigɛ Kiresta nini. Pu'uhɛ ya Aristabulus yire duma la.

ref: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya n buuri Hɛrɔdiyɔn. Pu'uhɛ ya sɛba en dole Daana la, dɛna Nasisu yire duma la.

ref: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya sɛba en de Daana la tuntuneba dɛna, Tirefaina la Tirefosa. Pu'uhɛ ya n sunɔŋerɛ, Pɛsisi en tum paaɛ paaɛ Daana la puan la.

ref: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
text: Pu'uhɛ Arufuse se'em ti ba looe en Daana tuuma puan la, gee pu'uhɛ a ma la en de mam me ma la.

ref: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Asinkrituse, Felegɔn, Hɛmese, Patirobase, Hɛmase, la suurɔ la za'a en boi la ba la.

ref: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya Filologuse, Yuliya, Nɛruse la a taya la, la Olimpase, la nɛresunduma la za'a en boi la ba la.

ref: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya taaba gee kɔkera taaba la pupeelum. Kiresta lageŋɔ la woo pu'uhi ya na. Ba'ahegɔ pa'alegɔ

ref: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
text: N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ n suurɔ, ti ya bahɛ ti ya nini bɔna sɛba en tari welegere la daaŋɔ kina ya tiŋasuka na, ban ka sakɛ bo' pa'alegɔ seko ti ya zamehɛ la, lɔkeri ya ba.

ref: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛre bamam taaba ka tuni bɔ'ɔra tu Daana Kiresta, gee ba tuni bɔ'ɔra la ba mɛhi puya. Ba tari la tɔgɛ malemale la belegere, pã'aha susɛ'a en ka mi' la.

ref: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
text: Ya naŋa la en yehe zi'an woo se'em la, en bahɛ ti mam nyɛɛna ya zuo, n boti ya tara la yɛm, sɛla en ani suŋa puan, gee yehe ya mɛhi sɛla en de be'em puan.

ref: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: La kan yuue la Suma'ahum Yinɛ la en wɔ̃regɛ Sagena la biŋe ya nama tiŋa. Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya.

ref: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
text: Timoti, mam tuntuna tadaana la pu'uheri ya na, la Lukiyuse la Yason, la Sosipata en de mam buuri la pu'uhi ya na.

ref: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
text: Mam Tɛtiyuse, se'em nu'o en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa la me pu'uhi ya Daana la puan na.

ref: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
text: Nananawa wa, pɛka dɛna Yinɛ se'em en wan ta'am kpemehe ya la yelehum la ti n bo' ya la, la mam n muulɛ Yezu Kiresta bo' ya se'em, yele se'ere en sugɛ gã'a diimen diimen ha se'em,

ref: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
text: gee nananawa, de nyaa niiɛ na, doohe nɔtɔgeheba en gulehɛ se'em la, de niiɛ bo' sɛba woo en dagi Yuda buuri la, Yinɛ en boi wuuha wa n bo' a nuurɛ se'em, ti a bahɛ ti nɛra woo nam gee sakɛ la.

ref: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
text: Pɛka dina Yinɛ, se'em ma'a en de yɛm za'a Yinɛ la dinɛ, doohe Yezu Kiresta puan wuuha wuuha Taari.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
text: Pɔɔl, se'em ti Yinɛ yi' ti, a dɛna Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, doohe Yinɛ mɛŋa suboolum la, la tu suuɔ Sositenese

ref: To God's church in Corinth. Christ Jesus chose you to be his very own people, and you worship in his name, as we and all others do who call him Lord.
text: en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ Lageŋɔ la en boi Korinti tiŋa, la sɛba ti ba soke ba, Yezu Kiresta puan, gee yi ba ti ba dɛna nɛresunduma, ta pa'ahɛ la sɛba woo en boi ze'sɛka woo yi'ira Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, ti a dɛna bamam la tumam Daana la.

ref: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Zu'uhegɔ dɛna ya dinɛ, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an na, bɔna la ya.

ref: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
text: Mam bo'ori n Yinɛ fara mɛ saŋa woo ya zuo, la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ kãtɛ se'ere ti a ka'ɛ bahɛ ya zuo yoo Kiresta Yezu puan la,

ref: who helps you speak and understand so well.
text: beni zuo, sadɔɔ seko woo puan, ya pirɛ mɛ a puan, la tugum, la mi'ilum woo,

ref: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
text: doohe la Kiresta yɛla la en ba'ari nyɛga ya puan se'em suŋa suŋa la,

ref: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
text: bahɛ ti ya ka koŋe zu'uhegɔ bo'olum sebo ya puan, yan guri tu Daana Yezu Kiresta niirɛ na wa.

ref: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
text: A me wan sɛ ya ze'ele suŋa suŋa ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ, ti ya kan tara gbelegere, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta dabeherɛ la puan.

ref: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
text: Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana. Eŋa puan ti ba yi' ya ti ya tara Lageŋɔ la a Dayua Yezu Kiresta en de tu Daana la. Bɔgehegɔ en boi Korinti Lageŋɔ la puan

ref: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
text: Nananawa, n yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ n suurɔ, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan, ti ya za'a sakera bɔ'ɔra taaba ti bɔgehegɔ da bɔna ya puan, gee ya tugum naaɛ taaba dɛna buyina, la putɛ'yinɛ

ref: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
text: N suurɔ wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Kuloo yire nɛreba yele mam ti nɔkpɛ'ɛma en boi ya tiŋasuka.

ref: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
text: De vuurɛ dela, ya sɛba yeti, “Mam dole la Pɔɔl,” bii “Mam dole la Apolose,” bii “Mam dole la Sefase,” bii “Mam dole la Kiresta.”

ref: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
text: Aa! Kiresta bɔgehi mɛ yoi? Pɔɔl ti kpa' bo' ya la? Bii ya daa sɔ ko'om la Pɔɔl yu'urɛ puan?

ref: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
text: Mam bo' Yinɛ fara ti, mam ka sɔ ya mina ko'om, han dagena Kiresepusi la Gayɔse ma'a,

ref: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
text: ti se'em da ta'am ta yeti, ba daa sɔ ko'om fum la mam yu'urɛ puan.

ref: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta ka tum mam ti n sɔɔra nɛreba ko'om, gee n muula Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la, dagi asaala yɛm kirega puan, ti Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ paŋa la da bahɛ yoo. Kiresta paŋa la Yinɛ yɛm

ref: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Pumpuuredɔɔ yelehum la dela gɔɔnyɛla bo' sɛba en baheri yoo la. Gee han dɛna tumam sɛba en nyɛ faarɛ wa, bu dela Yinɛ paŋa.

ref: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, “Mam wan sagum yɛm duma yɛm, gee burum baŋereba baŋerɛ.”

ref: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
text: Yɛm daana la bɛɛ? Gɔnmina la bɛɛ? Sankana nɔkpe'en ŋmɛ'ɛra la bɛɛ? Yinɛ ka iŋɛ ti tiŋa wa zuo yɛm la dɛna la gɔɔnɛ?

ref: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
text: Ze'ele Yinɛ yɛm puan na, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba ka mi Yinɛ ba mɛhi yɛm puan, Yinɛ en iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a doohe tumam gɔɔnɛ muulegɔ wa puan, la a faaɛ sɛba en nam la.

ref: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yuuduma boti ba nyɛ la kirehi, ti Girikiduma me bɔta yɛm ti ba ta'am nam.

ref: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
text: Gee han dɛna tumam, tumam muuli la Kiresta ti ba kpa' la, yelese'ere en de pirega lɔkɔ bo' Yuuduma, gee dɛna gɔɔn yelehum bo' sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la.

ref: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
text: Gee sɛba ti Yinɛ yi' la, Yuuduma, la Girikiduma za'a, Kiresta dela Yinɛ paŋa, la Yinɛ yɛm bo' ba.

ref: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba en nyɛ sɛla ti'iha ti Yinɛ gɔɔnɛ n bala la, nyani asaala yɛm, ti nɛreba en nyɛ sɛla ti'iha ti Yinɛ tɔregɔ en bala la, nyana asaala paŋa.

ref: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
text: N suurɔ wa, pun bihɛ ya mɛhi puan ti ba yi' ya se'em wa, Dagi ya zo'e zo'e en daa de yɛm duma asaala gakerɛ puan, dagi ya zo'e zo' en de pahi duma, dagi ya zo'e zo'e en daa de nabiihi ya dɔgum puan.

ref: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
text: Gee Yinɛ looe la sɛla en de gɔɔnɛ tiŋa wa zuo, ti a diihe yɛm duma la vi. Yinɛ looe la sɛla en ka tari paŋa tiŋa wa zuo, ti a diihe paŋa duma la vi.

ref: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
text: Yinɛ looe la tarema, la sɛla en ka tari zuo tiŋa wa zuo, yelesɛ'a en pun dagena sɛla la, ti a sigehe yelekãra duma la ti ba lebege zaŋa,

ref: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
text: Ŋwana ti nɛra kan ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan.

ref: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
text: Yinɛ en de ya vom nyɛgerɛ, Kiresta Yezu puan, se'em en de tu yɛm, ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, dɛna tuuntɔɔ la, se'em en sokere la, la ayɔto'e daana la,

ref: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
text: ti wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Se'em woo en ŋmɛ'ɛri a nyu'ɔ, a ŋmɛ' kɔ Daana la puan la.”

ref: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
text: Mam n wa'am ya zi'an na la n suurɔ, mam ka wa'am na muula Yinɛ sugelum yelehum la la yɛm kirega sɛka bii nɔmaalum puan bɔ'ɔra ya.

ref: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
text: Beni zuo, mam daa iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti n ka boti n baŋɛ se'ere ya puan, han dagena Yezu Kiresta, la ban en kpa' en la ma'a.

ref: At first, I was weak and trembling with fear.
text: N wa'am ya zi'an mɛ na la tɔregɔ, la dabeem la gbi'a zo'e zo'e.

ref: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
text: Mam tugum, la n muulegɔ la daa dagi pã'ahegɔ yetɔgum, gee Sia la tuuma, la paŋa,

ref: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
text: ti la bahɛ ti ya sakerɛ la da yaga asaala yɛm zuo, gee Yinɛ paŋa puan. Yinɛ yɛm

ref: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
text: Gee la bala za'a, sɛba en bi'ɛ sakerɛ puan la, ti tari yɛm tɔgera mɛ, gee dagi zina beere yɛm wa puan, bii pahi se'ehi en sɔi zina beere wa biha, gã'a gura sageŋɔ paaɛ si wa.

ref: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
text: Gee tumam tɔgeri la Yinɛ yɛm en sugɛ, yele sɛ'a ti nɛreba ka mina, ti Yinɛ yuum pun tɔgɛ a yele ti tiŋa wa nam pun ka naam, tumam tibehum zuo.

ref: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
text: Se'em ka boi sankana na'duma wa puan bɔkɛ yele ana wa; Ba han bɔkɛ ni mɛ, ba kan kpa' ni Tibehum Za'a Daana la.

ref: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
text: Gee wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Sɛla ti niho ka tabelɛ nyɛ, bii tuberɛ wum, bii asaala suure tabelɛ bɔkɛ dɛ, Sɛla ti Yinɛ maahum biŋe sɛba en nɔŋɛ en la” —

ref: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
text: Yele ana ti Yinɛ niiɛ pa'alɛ tumam doohe Sia la puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, Sia la mi' sɛla woo mɛ, pun ta ta' yele sɛ'a en sugɛ Yinɛ zi'an.

ref: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
text: Asaala ini en wan ta'am mina sɛla en sirum de asaala, han dagena asaala sia la en boi a puan la? Bala mɛa ti se'em ka bɔna wan bɔkɛ sɛla en sirum de Yinɛ yele, han dagena Yinɛ Sia la.

ref: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
text: Nananawa, han dɛna tumam, tumam ka to'e la tiŋa wa zuo sia la, gee Sɛsɛka en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, ti tu ta'am bɔkera bo'olum sebo ti Yinɛ ka'ɛ bahɛ tu zuo la.

ref: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
text: Ti tumam tɔgera yele ana wa mɛ dagi la asaala pa'alegɔ yɛm puan, gee Sia la pa'alegɔ. Lɛregera Sia puan yɛla pa'ala sɛba en tari Sia la.

ref: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
text: Sɛba ti Sia la ka bɔna la ba la, ka to'ore Yinɛ Sia bo'olum la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba ka tari vuurɛ bo' ba, ti ba me kan ta'am bɔkɛ ba, se'ere en sɔi la, ba dela Sia puan yɛla.

ref: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
text: Sɛba ti Sia la bɔna la ba la bɔkeri sɛla woo mɛ, ba ka boi nɛra nu'uhi ti a bihera ba.

ref: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, “Ani en mi' Daana la puti'irɛ, wan ta'am pa'alɛ en?” Gee tumam tari la Kiresta puti'irɛ.

ref: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
text: Bala la, n suurɔ, mam ka ta'am ni tɔgɛ bo' ya wuu sɛba ti Fa'a Sia la bɔna la ba, gee en tugum tɔgɛ bo' ya wuu sɛba ti Sia la ka bɔna ba puan la. Wuu ya dela kɔma Kiresta puan la.

ref: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
text: Mam bo' ya la bi'ihum, gee dagi dikpe'ene, se'ere en sɔi la, ya daa ka maahum ti ya di dikpe'ene. La nananawa me, ya pun kelum ka maahum,

ref: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
text: beni zuo, ya kelum bɔna la ingɔŋɔ puan. Nyuure la nɔkpɛ'ɛma en kelum bɔna ya puan wa, ya ka boi la ingɔŋɔ puan wa, ita saaleba itegɔ?

ref: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
text: Beni zuo, ya ayima han yeti, “Mam dole la Pɔɔl,” ti ayima yeti, “Mam dole la Apolos,” ya kɔ'ɔm ka ita la nɛresaalenɛ en bala?

ref: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
text: Ani en de Apolos? Ti ani dɛna Pɔɔl? Ba za'a dela yamehi se'ehi ti ya doohe ba puan baŋɛ Yinɛ, wuu Daana la en bo' ba ayima ayima se'em la.

ref: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
text: Mam burɛ mɛ, ti Apolos bahɛ ko'om. Gee Yinɛ en bahɛ ti la yehe na.

ref: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
text: Bala, dagi se'em en burɛ la, bii se'em en bahɛ ko'om la en de sɛla, gee Yinɛ ma'a, se'em en bahɛ ti la yehe la.

ref: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
text: Se'em en burɛ la, la se'em en bahɛ ko'om la dela buyina, ti ba ayima woo wan to'e a yɔɔrɛ doohe la ba ayima woo tuuma en de se'em.

ref: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
text: Tumam dela Yinɛ yamehi, tuna la taaba; Yamam en de Yinɛ va'am, dɛna Yinɛ yire.

ref: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
text: Doohe la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ ti a zu'uhɛ mam la, mam ani mɛ wuu se'em en mi' mɛa, iibɛ ɛ̃berɛ la biŋe, ti ayima me wa'am na mɛ dɔgelɛ. Mɛta wuu dela a bihɛ suŋa en wan mɛ se'em dɔgelɛ de zuo.

ref: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
text: mɛta woo tuunɛ wan ta niiɛ na, se'ere en sɔi la, dabeherɛ la daarɛ wan niiɛ dɛ na, beni zuo, bugum en wan ta bahɛ ti de niiɛ na, bugum la wan magehɛ tuunse'ere taaba ti nɛra woo tum la bihɛ.

ref: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
text: Ti sɛla en dikɛ mɛ dɔgelɛ ɛ̃berɛ la zuo la han ka di, mɛta la wan to'e yɔɔrɛ.

ref: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
text: Gee mɛta la tuunɛ la han di bugum, mɛta la namehɛ bahɛ la yoo. A mɛŋa wan nyɛ faarɛ, gee la wan ana en wuu a doohe la bugum puan tole la.

ref: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
text: Yamam ka mi' ti ya dela Yinɛ yire, ti Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la bɔna ya puan?

ref: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
text: Ya se'em han sagum Yinɛ yire wa, Yinɛ me wan sagum a daana. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yire la dela fa'a, ti yamam en dɛna Yinɛ yire la.

ref: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
text: Da pã'ahɛ ya mɛhi. Ya han ti'ihɛ ti yamam en de yɛm duma buuri sankana wa puan, ya tugum lebege geto, la ya ta'am nyɛ yɛm.

ref: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tiŋa wa zuo yɛm dela gɔɔnɛ Yinɛ zi'an. Beni zuo, la gulehɛ yeti, “Yinɛ nyɔgere yɛm duma mɛ ba mɛhi nyaleŋɔ puan,”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
text: le yɛ'ɛha, “Daana la mi' yɛm duma ti'ihegɔ mɛ, ti ba ka tari vuurɛ.”

ref: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
text: la dɛna Pɔɔl, bii Apolos, bii Sefase, bii timpaberɛ wa, bii vom, bii kum, bii zina, bii beere yele, la woo dela ya dinɛ,

ref: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text: ti yamam me dɛna Kiresta dinɛ, ti Kiresta me dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ.

ref: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
text: Bala, yeen dikɛ ya tu ŋwana, ti tu dela Kiresta yamehi, tu dela sɛba en biheri Yinɛ sugelum yɛla la.

ref: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
text: Se'ere en gani bala dela, la dele sɛba en biheri Yinɛ lɔgerɔ la ti ba dɛna sɛba ti nɛreba wan nam ba.

ref: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
text: Gee han dɛna mam, la dela yelepika bo' mam, ti yamam gakɛ mam, bii saaleba tugum zi'an gakɛ mam. Mam mɛŋa pun ka gakera n mɛŋa.

ref: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
text: Mam ka nyɛ se'ere ti mam tuue n mɛŋa, gee la bala za'a, mam ka piiɛ. La dela Daana la ma'a en gakeri mam.

ref: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
text: Bala, da gakɛ ya gee ti saŋa la nyaa paaɛ, gee ti Kiresta nyaa wa'am na, eŋa se'em daana en wan bahɛ ti sɛlawoo en sugɛ lika puan nananawa niiɛ peelumen, gee lilege suure puan yɛla la. Bilam ti nɛrawoo nyaa wan to'e a pɛka Yinɛ zi'an.

ref: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
text: Mam bahɛ ti Apolos la mam mɛŋa dɔla yele ana mɛ yamam sum zuo, n suurɔ wa, ti ya zamehɛ tumam puan, la ya se'em da vɔna zɔɛ sɛla en gulehɛ la. Ŋwana ti ya se'em kan ukera a mɛŋa la eŋa en boti se'em gana se'em la zuo.

ref: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
text: Beni zuo, ani en nyɛ sɛla en boi la tɔka fum puan? Beni ti fum tara gee ka to'e de Yinɛ zi'an? Fum han to'e la Yinɛ zi'an, ŋwani ti fum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ wuu la dagi la bo'olum ti ba dikɛ bo' fum?

ref: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
text: Bala! Yamam pun tara sɛla woo ti ya bɔta la mɛ! Yelemɛŋerɛ ya pun dɛna la buntateba? Ya pun lebege la naduma ti tumam ka te'ele ya? Mam boti ni ti ya sirum lebege ni la naduma, ti tumam me deni naduma la ya.

ref: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
text: Mam ti'ihɛ ti Yinɛ ka kãalɛ tumam tuntuneba wa pa'ahɛ nɛreba puan, beni zuo, tu ani wuu ba maahum tu yɛ'a la kua la, beni zuo, ba dikɛ tu diilɛ mɛ bo' timpaberɛ wa, la suŋereba, la saaleba.

ref: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
text: Bala! Tumam nyaa dela geto Kiresta zuo, gee ti yamam dɛna yɛm duma Kiresta puan. Tumam tari la tɔregɔ, gee yamam dɛna paheduma. Yamam dela zuto duma, gee tumam dɛna fagehum duma.

ref: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
text: Wa'am na paaɛ tumam en kɔ'ɔm zɛ'a wa, kom la ko'nyuurɔ en kuuri tɔ, tu yɛ la lɔge-ãahema, ti ba ŋmɛ'ɛra tɔ, tu ka tari yire wan kɛ̃,

ref: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
text: tu taregerɛ mɛ la tu mɛhi nu'uhi tuuma. Ba han ni to' tɔ, tu ni ka' la sum bo' ba duma. Ba han mɛlehɛ tɔ, tu ni ŋmibe mɛ,

ref: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
text: ba han ni zɛregɛ tɔ, tu ni lebehe mɛ la kɔsuŋa. Tumam ani wuu tu dela tiŋa wa zuo sɔgerɔ la, dɛna sɛla woo bunkɛhela wa'am na paaɛ zina. Sɔ kã'aŋɔ

ref: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
text: Mam ka gulehɛ ŋwana ti n bahɛ ti vi en nyɔkɛ ya, gee n ka'am ya, wuu yan de n sunɔŋerɛ kɔma la.

ref: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
text: Ya ta'am tara bihereba tuha pia Kiresta puan, gee ya ka tari sɔduma zo'e. Yelemɛŋerɛ, Kiresta Yezu puan, ti mam lebege ya sɔ, faarɛ yelehum wa zuo.

ref: and I want you to be like me.
text: Ŋwana zuo, mam beleni ya mɛ, ti ya tɔgeha mam.

ref: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
text: Dina wa zuo ti n tum Timoti, se'em en de mam sunɔŋerɛ la n sakerɛ dayua Daana la puan, ti a tiihɛ ya mam n dole Kiresta Yezu yɛla la se'em, wuu mam en pa'ali a se'em zi'an woo, la Lageŋɔ woo puan se'em la.

ref: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
text: Gee ya sɛba en ti'iheri ti mam kan le wa'am ya zi'an na la, lebege la nɛrewureba.

ref: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
text: Gee n wan wa'am ya zi'an nananawa na, la han dɛna Daana la suboolum, la n baŋɛ, dagi la nɛrewureba wa yetɔgum la ma'a, gee ba paŋa la me.

ref: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la dagi la tugum ma'a, gee paŋa.

ref: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
text: Beni ti ya wan bɔta? N wa'am ya zi'an na la kpa'aherɛ bii, nɔŋerɛ la sigehe mɛŋa?

ref: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
text: Ba kɔ'ɔm yele mam mɛ ti galehi en boi ya puan. Galehi sɛ'a taaba en pun ka bɔna sɛba en ka dole Yinɛ puan. Beni zuo, buraa en boi ya puan gã'a la ka sɔ pɔga.

ref: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
text: Gee ti ya tugum ukera. La dagi ni la ya tugum kɛla, gee ti se'em en tum tuun-dina wa la, ya fooe ni en ya puan bahɛ?

ref: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
text: Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la puan bahera buraa sɛka en tum tuun-dina wa taaba wa zuo. Bala ya han ta lagehɛ, ti n sia bɔna la ya, la Daana Yezu paŋa la,

ref: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
text: ya dela ya dikɛ nɛre-ina daana bo' Sagena la ti a sagum a inganɛ la, gee ti a sia ta nyɛ faarɛ Daana dabeherɛ la puan.

ref: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
text: Ya pa'ali mɛŋa la ka tari vuurɛ, yamam ka mi' ti dabelɛ fii wa en ni bahɛ ti zom la za'a ihegera?

ref: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
text: Pee ya dabelɛ kɛka la bahɛ, la ya ta'am dɛna dabelɛ paalum ma'a, wuu dabelɛ en sirum ka boi ya puan se'em wa la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba kaabɛ tu azɔɛtole pebila la en de Kiresta la mɛ.

ref: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
text: Bala zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu de'em ya de'eŋo wa, dagi la dabelɛ kɛka la en de pa'ahegɔ, la be'em dabelɛ la, gee tu de'em ku la boroboro zaŋa la en de sira, la susunɛ. Galeŋɔ dela ba gakɛ

ref: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
text: Mam daa gulehɛ n gɔŋɔ la puan yeti ya da dikɛ ya mɛhi lagum la sɛba en tuni galeni ba mɛhi la,

ref: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
text: ka pa'alɛ la fii me ti sɛba en ka dole Kiresta tiŋa wa zuo la, bii sɛba en nyigere, la fãareba, bii bage kaabereba. La han dɛna bala, la yi dela ya bahɛ tiŋa wa zuo en bala.

ref: I was talking about your own people who are immoral or greedy or worship idols or curse others or get drunk or cheat. Don't even eat with them!
text: Gee nananawa, mam en gulehɛ yɛta ya se'em dela, ya da dikɛ ya mɛhi naaɛ la se'em en yi'iri a mɛŋa ya suuɔ, gee yaala, nyigera, bii kaabera baga, bii nɛreba tua, dabugere, bii fãara. Da pun naaɛ la ena daana di.

ref: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, “Chase away any of your own people who are evil.”
text: Yinɛ mɛŋa en wan gakɛ sɛba en ka dole Kiresta la. “Fooe ya be'em daana la ya puan bahɛ.”

ref: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
text: Ya se'em han tara se'ere la a tadaana, a wan sakɛ tari dɛ kiŋɛ sɛba en ka dole Yezu la zi'an ti ba ta gakɛ ya, gana a tari kiŋɛ nɛresunduma la zi'an?

ref: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
text: Yamam ka mi' ti nɛresunduma la en wan ta gakɛ timpaberɛ wa? Yamam han wan ta gakɛ timpaberɛ wa, la iŋɛ la ŋwani ti ya kan gakɛ yele-pigehi wa?

ref: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
text: Yamam ka mi' ti tumam en wan gakɛ suŋereba, nyaa benti yele ana wa?

ref: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
text: Bala, ya han ni nyɛ yele pigehi ana wa, la dela ya digehɛ gakereba sɛba en ka boi lageŋɔ la puan ti ba gakɛ?

ref: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
text: N yeti ya ŋwana ti vi en nyɔkɛ ya. La wan ta'am dɛna ti, nɛra yinɛ me ka boi ya puan tara yɛm wan ta'am maalɛ sakereba bayi tiŋasuka,

ref: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
text: gee ti sakera tari sakera kiŋɛ tugum zi'an, sɛba en ka dole Yezu nɛŋan yele ana wa zuo?

ref: When one of you takes another to court, all of you lose. It would be better to let yourselves be cheated and robbed.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya pun tara yɛla la taaba pun pa'alɛ ti ya koŋe mɛ en bala. Ŋwani ti yamam tugum ka bahɛ ti ba tuue ya? Ŋwani ti yamam tugum ka bahɛ ti ba fa' ya?

ref: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
text: Yamam ka mi' ti be'emduma kan kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan? Da pã'ahɛ ya mɛhi. Yalum duma, bage kaabereba, sɛba en tuni kabohi, pɔgenindɔɔrɔ la buraahi bii pɔgehi en gã' taaba,

ref: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
text: nageyigehi, nyigereba dabugereba, tuureba, la fãareba, bana wa ayima kan kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

ref: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
text: Gee bala ti ya sɛba daa ana. Gee ba pee ya mɛ, ba sukɛ ya mɛ, ba bo' ya la buurɔ tu Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, la tu Yinɛ Sia la puan. Na'aha Yinɛ, inganɛ la Sia puan

ref: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
text: “Sɛla woo ka kihi bɔ'ɔra mam,” gee dagi sɛla woo en tari nyuurɔ. “Sɛla woo ka kihi bɔ'ɔra mam,” gee mam kan lebege da'aberɛ bo' sɛla.

ref: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
text: “Dia dela puurɛ dinɛ, ti puurɛ me dɛna dia dinɛ,” gee Yinɛ wan sagum ba za'a. Inganɛ la ka boi la yaluntuuma zuo, gee de boi bo' la Daana la, ti Daana la me bɔna bo' inganɛ la.

ref: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
text: Yinɛ en vo'e Daana la kum puan, gee me wan vo'e tumam me a paŋa la puan.

ref: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
text: Ya ka mi' ti ya ihe la dela Kiresta dinɛ? Bala, n dikɛ Kiresta lɔgerɔ lebege yaalegɔ lɔgerɔ? La kan tum.

ref: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
text: Ya ka mi' ti se'em woo en dikɛ a mɛŋa naaɛ la yalema, dela inganɛ deyina la en? Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, “Bayi' la wan lebege inganɛ deyina.”

ref: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
text: Gee se'em woo en dikɛ a mɛŋa naaɛ la Daana la dela buyina la en Sia puan.

ref: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
text: Zagehɛ yaluntuuma, se'ere en sɔi la, be'em sebo woo ti nɛra tum ni dɛna la iŋa la yiŋa na. Gee yaluntuuma daana galum la iŋa la za'a.

ref: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
text: Bii ya ka mi' ti ya iŋa la dela pu'uhegɔ yire bo' Fa'a Sia la en boi la ya la, se'em ti ya daa to'e Yinɛ zi'an la. Bala, ya ka le sɔna ya mɛhi?

ref: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba da' ya mɛ la da'alum, bala zuo, na'ahɛ ya Yinɛ la ya inganɛ la.

ref: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
text: Nananawa, han dɛna yelesɛ'a ti ya gulehɛ sɔkera la, yan yeti, “La ani suŋa ti buraa da kalum pɔka.”

ref: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
text: Gee yaluntuuma la zuo, buraa woo di a suure pɔga, ti pɔka woo me ele ka suure sira.

ref: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
text: Sira la dela a naara la a pɔga lan nari se'em la , ti pɔka me naara la ka sira lan nari se'em la.

ref: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔka la ka sɔi ka mɛŋa iŋa la, gee sira la en sɔi, bala mɛa ti sira la me ka sɔna ka mɛŋa iŋa la, gee pɔka la en sɔi.

ref: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text: Da zagehere ya taaba, han dagena ti ya ku la nuurɛ la taaba saŋa ka yima puan, ti ya kaabɛ ya mɛhi bo' pu'uhegɔ. Gee bala han tole, ya lebe naaɛ taaba, ti Sagena la da makɛ ya bihɛ, yan ka ta' ya mɛhi tara la zuo.

ref: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
text: Mam en yeti ya sɛla wa dela n yeen yele mɛ gee dagi nuurɛ ti n bo'.

ref: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
text: Mam boti ni ti ya za'a ani ni wuu mam wa, gee nɛra woo tari la a bo'olum sebo ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' en. Ayima tari la bo'olum buna, ti ayima me tara bo'olum buna.

ref: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
text: Yamam sɛba en ka di pɔgeba bii ele sireba la, la pokɔpa la, mam yeti, la ani suŋa ti ya kelum bɔna bala ya ma'a woo mam n boi se'em wa.

ref: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
text: Gee ba han ka zameha ba mɛhi ti ba tana ba mɛhi tara, ba di pɔgeba, bii ba ele sireba. Se'ere en sɔi la, la ani suŋa ti ba di pɔgeba bii ba ele sireba nyana kunkoma'ahum en kuuri ba.

ref: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
text: Sɛba en tari pɔgeba la sireba la, mam bo' ya la nɔdina wa, dagi mam gee Daana la, ti pɔka da bahɛ ka sira.

ref: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
text: Yamam sɛba en gee la me, mam n yeti se'ere n ŋwana gee dagi Daana la. Ti sakera han tara pɔga ti ka ka sakɛ bo' Yezu, gee bɔta ti ka bɔna la ka sira la, sira la da lebehe ka.

ref: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
text: Pɔka han tara ka sira en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu, gee ti buraa la bɔta a bɔna la pɔga la, pɔka la da bahɛ buraa la.

ref: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sira wa en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu la lebege la tuulum nɛra, a pɔga wa zuo. Bala mɛa ti pɔgesɛka en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu la me wan lebege tuulum nɛra a sira la zuo. Han dagena bala, ba kɔma la kan dɛna tuulum kɔma. Gee lan de se'em la, ba dela tuulum kɔma.

ref: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
text: Gee sɛba en ka dole Yezu la han bahɛ taaba, de dɛna bala, la han iŋɛ bala, buraa la bii pɔka la ka boi bobere puan. Suma'ahum zuo ti Yinɛ yi ya.

ref: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
text: Gee ani n mi', Pɔka wa, fum ta baŋera la ti fum faaɛ fum sira la mɛ, sira wa, fum ta baŋera la ti fum faaɛ fum pɔga la mɛ. Vom sebo ti Daana bɔta

ref: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
text: Lan pun dɛna se'em woo, nɛrawoo vɔna vomsebo ti Daana la dikɛ bo' en la, vom sebo ti Yinɛ yi' ya ti ya vɔna la. Mam nɔze'elego en ŋwana bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ duma la za'a.

ref: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
text: Ŋmaa dagi sɛla, a ka ŋma me dagi sɛla, gee Yinɛ nɔze'elego la naŋa en de sɛla woo.

ref: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
text: Bahɛ ya ti nɛra woo ana wuu en daa boi se'em gee ti Yinɛ yi' en la.

ref: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
text: Fum daa dela da'aberɛ gee ti Yinɛ yi' fum? Da bahɛ ti la daana hɔ, fum han pun wan nyaŋɛ faaɛ fum mɛŋa bɔna fai, dikɛ fum boi se'em zina wa tum gaŋɛ sɛla woo.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
text: Ba da' ya mɛ la da'alum, da deni ya da'aba bo' saaleba kpɛ'ɛnduma.

ref: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
text: Nananawa, pɔgesakaha la me yele en ŋwana, Daana la ka bo' mam nuurɛ, gee mam bo'ori ya la n mɛŋa puti'irɛ, wuu se'em ti Daana la zɔ' n nimbu'ɔ ti n dɛna se'em ti ya wan nam la.

ref: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
text: Mam ti'iheri yeti, tumam en nyɛti daaŋɔ seko en boi la zuo, la wan ana suŋa ti ya kɔ'ɔm bɔna yan boi se'em la.

ref: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
text: Fum di la pɔga? Da zigera ti fum bahɛ ka. Fum ka tari pɔga? Da ɛɛra pɔga.

ref: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
text: Gee fum han di pɔga bii fum ele la sira, fum ka tum be'em. Pɔgesakãherɛ me han ele, a ka tum be'em. Gee sɛba en ele bii di pɔgeba la, ba wan nyɛ ɛregɔ, gee n boti n faaɛ ya mɛ yele ana wa puan bahɛ.

ref: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
text: Mam n yeti se'em wa la, n suurɔ, dela, sansɛka ti Yinɛ looe la ba'aheri mɛ n bala, bala nananawa yɛ'a ha, bahɛ ya ti sɛba en pun tara pɔgeba la, bɔna wuu ba ka tari la pɔgeba la,

ref: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
text: ti sɛba en kele, ana wuu ba ka kele me la, ti sɛba en nyɛɛni, ana wuu ba ka nyɛɛni me la, ti sɛba en da'ari kɔɔhera ana wuu ba ka tari mɛ la,

ref: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
text: ti sɛba en tuni tiŋa wa zuo ana wuu ba ka tuni mɛ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, tinkana wa ba'aheri mɛ n bala.

ref: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
text: Mam boti n faaɛ ya mɛ ninmu'urɛ puan bahɛ. Se'em n ka tari pɔga nini mu'ɛ mɛ la Daana la, en wan iŋɛ se'em ti Daana nini tigɛ.

ref: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
text: Gee se'em n tari pɔga nini mu'ɛ mɛ la tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, a biheri la en wan iŋɛ se'em pee a pɔga puurɛ,

ref: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
text: a puti'irɛ la dela buyi. Gee pɔge se'ehi en ka ele, la pɔgesakaha la nini mu'ɛ mɛ la Daana la, ti ba dɛna tuulum nɛreba inganɛ la Sia puan. Gee pɔge se'ehi en ele nini mu'ɛ mɛ la tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, eŋa n wan iŋɛ se'em pee a sira puurɛ.

ref: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
text: N tɔgeri yele ana wa woo la ya sum zuo, dagi la n gu'uri ya mɛ se'ere puan, gee n bahɛ ti sɛla woo dɔla ma'a ma'a, gee ti sɛla da gu' ya Yinɛ sore dɔlega la puan.

ref: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
text: Se'em han ta bihɛ ti a kan ta'am a mɛŋa tara la pɔgesakãherɛ la ti a bɔna la de la, a kunkoma'ahum han ba' zɔ'ɔra la pɔka la, wuu lan suni ti la ana se'em la, a di ka wuu en boti se'em la, la dagi be'em. Bahɛ ya ti ba di taaba.

ref: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
text: Bala la, se'em en di a pɔgesakãherɛ iŋɛ suŋa, gee fum se'em en ka di a pɔgesakãherɛ en gani za'a.

ref: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
text: Pɔka en ele boi la sire ele kiha puan, a sira n nan vɔi la. Gee a sira la han ki, a tari sore ti a malum ele eŋa en boti se'em, gee a ele doohe Daana sumboolum puan.

ref: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
text: Gee mam puti'irɛ puan, sum wan boni la pɔka la, ka han zi' ni bala ka ele. Mam ti'ihɛ ti mam me tari Yinɛ Sia la n puan mɛ.

ref: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
text: Nananawa, disɛka ti ba dikɛ kaabɛ bo' baga sɔhega la. Tu mi' ti, “Tu za'a tari mi'ilum mɛ.” Mi'ilum bo'ori la pa'alɛ mɛŋa gee nɔŋerɛ en mɛti.

ref: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
text: Se'em han ti'ihɛ ti eŋa mi' sɛla, nan kelum ka tara mi'ilum sebo en de a tara.

ref: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
text: Gee se'em en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, Yinɛ mi' en mɛ.

ref: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
text: Bala, han dɛna disɛka en dikɛ kaabɛ baga sɔhega la, tu mi' ti “Bagerɛ ka boi tiŋa wa zuo dɛna bunvua,” ti yinɛ ka bɔna han dagena “Yinɛ deyina wa ma'a.”

ref: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla ta'am bɔna ti ba yi'ira ba yina ti ba bɔna saazuo bii tiŋa wa zuo, wuu lan de sira se'em ti yina la daanduma boi mɛ la.

ref: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
text: Gee han dɛna tumam, Yinɛ dela ayima, Sɔ la, se'em zi'an ti sɛla woo ze'ele na la, la se'em zuo ti tu za'a vɔna la, Daana ayima, en de Yezu Kiresta la, se'em puan ti sɛla woo ze'ele na, la se'em zuo ti tu vɔna la.

ref: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
text: Dagi nɛra woo en tari mi'ilum buna wa, gee yelemɛŋerɛ nɛreba basɛba boi mɛ ti ba suya ba' lu' la baga, tara tara la zina, ba kelum ti'iha dia ti ba di ti ba dikɛ ka kaabɛ bo' la baga, ti ba puti'ira la en ka kpe'em la nyaa galum ba.

ref: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
text: “Dia kan ta'am tari tu lɛm Yinɛ.” Tu ka sagum tole tu han ka di ka, tu me ka nyani se'em tu han di ka.

ref: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
text: Gee bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa ti ya fai yɔ'ɔ wa da dɛna pirega lɔkɔ bo' sɛba en ka kpe'em la.

ref: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, basɛba han nyɛ yamam sɛba en de mi'ilum duma wa ti ya boi baga zɔŋɔ puan dita, la kan bahɛ ti bamam sɛba puti'ira en ka kpe'em la me iŋɛ ba puti'irɛ ta paaɛ ze'sɛka ti ba me bɔta ti ba di dia n dikɛ kaabɛ bagerɛ?

ref: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
text: Gee fum han tuna fum yire duma be'em, pɔgelum ba puti'ira la en nan ka kpe'em la, fum tuni la Kiresta be'em.

ref: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
text: Bala zuo, dia la en han sɔna ba lua, mam yi kan makɛ obe nɛŋɔ, la n da bahɛ ti ba ayima lu.

ref: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
text: Mam ka boi la fai yoi? Mam dagi la Yezu tuntuna yoi? Mam ka nyɛ la Yezu tu Daana la yoi? Dagi yamam en de mam tuuma Daana la puan?

ref: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
text: Mam han pun ka dɛna Yezu tuntuna bo' basɛba, gee la se'em se'em me, n de mɛ bo' yamam, se'ere en sɔi la, yamam en de mam daalehum pa'ala ti n dela Daana la tuntuna.

ref: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
text: Tu ka tari sore ti tu dita gee nyuura tumam en boti sɛla?

ref: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
text: Tu ka tari sore ti tu pɔgeba en de tu sakereba la dɔla tɔ wuu Yezu tuntuneba basɛba la, la Daana suurɔ la, la Sefase en iti se'em la?

ref: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
text: Bii Banabase la mam ma'a n kɔ'ɔm ka tari sore ti tu me bahɛ da tuna diiha tu mɛhi?

ref: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
text: Ani n boi, ni yɔɔra a mɛa pɛntɛrɛ tuuma puan? Ani n sɛ tiihi gee ka dita si wala, bii ani en dole piihi gee kan sũna si iilum?

ref: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ Moose nɔze'elego puan yeti, “Da lu' lɔlega nuurɛ gee tara ka tulegera ki.” La dela lɔlemɛŋehi ti Yinɛ yɛta wa?

ref: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
text: Gee Yinɛ ka tɔgeri ŋwana za'a la tumam zuo, yelemɛŋerɛ, ba gulehɛ dina wa la tumam zuo? Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em han kɔɔra, a kɔɔri gee tara la beere puti'irɛ, ti se'em me han tɔɔra ki, a tɔɔri gee tara la puti'irɛ ti a wan to'e a tɔtɛ.

ref: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
text: Tumam han burɛ la sia sum ya puan, tu nyaa han kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra ya ingɔŋɔ puan nyuurɔ, la ba' gaŋɛ mɛ yoi?

ref: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
text: Basɛba han tara sore ti ba to'e sɛla ya zi'an, tumam ka nari gaŋɛ bala? Gee aayi, tumam ka makɛ dikɛ yɔ'ɔ kuna wa tum, gee tumam ŋmiberi mɛ sɛla woo puan, nyana tu dikɛ sɛla beŋe Kiresta faarɛ yelehum wa puan.

ref: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
text: Yamam ka mi' ti sɛba ti ba dikɛ ti ba tuna pu'uhegɔ yire, nyɛti ba dia la pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti sɛba me n tuni kaabegɔ bimbine zuo la me ni to'e ba dinɛ la sɛla ti ba dikɛ kaabɛ la puan?

ref: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
text: Bala mɛa, Daana la bo' nuurɛ ti sɛba woo n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la, nyɛta ba nuurɛ dia faarɛ yelehum mɛa puan.

ref: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
text: Gee mam ka dikɛ yɔ'ɔ kuna wa la kuyina me tum, bii n nyaa guleha ŋwana ti ya dikɛ de tum sadɔseko puan. Yelemɛŋerɛ, mam tugum ki nyani nɛre se'em gu' mam se'ere zuo ti mam ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ la.

ref: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
text: Mam han muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa, dina ka bo' mam sore ti n ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, la dela tuunɛ gã'a n zuo, ti yele n bɔna n zuo, n han ka muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa.

ref: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
text: Beni zuo, mam han tuna dina wa la n mɛŋa suboolum, mam tari yɔɔrɛ mɛ, gee la han dagena mam mɛŋa suboolum, ba ye dikɛ tuunɛ la bo' mam mɛ ti n tum ti ba yɔ mam.

ref: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
text: Beni en nyaa de mam yɔɔrɛ la? La dela n muulegɔ la, n bahɛ ti faarɛ yelehum wa paaɛ nɛreba zaŋa la, ti n da ta'am dikɛ mam tari yɔ'ɔ seko faarɛ yelehum wa puan la tum.

ref: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
text: La mam pun bɔna fai la, mam na'ahera nɛra woo mɛ, dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege da'aberɛ bo' nɛra woo, ti n ta'am nyaŋɛ ba zo'e zo'e.

ref: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
text: Yuuduma la zi'an, mam ni dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege la Yuda nɛra, ti n ta'am tee Yuuduma la, Sɛba en dole kiha la me zi'an, mam ni dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege la bamam en ani se'em la, , ti n ta'am tee sɛba en boi kiha puan la.

ref: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
text: Sɛba en ka dole kiha la, mam ni dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege la sɛba en ka dole kiha la , ti n ta'am tee sɛba en ka boi kiha puan.

ref: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
text: Sɛba en tari tɔregɔ, mam ni lebege la tɔregɔ daana, ti n ta'am tee tɔregɔ duma la. Mam lebege la sɛla woo bo' nɛra woo ti sadɔseko woo puan, n ta'am faaɛ basɛba.

ref: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
text: Mam iti ŋwana za'a la faarɛ yelehum la zuo, ti n ta ta'am tara tɔtɛ bu sum la puan.

ref: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
text: Yamam ka mi' ti zua puan, zɔteba la woo ni pagera la taaba, gee ba ayima ma'a en ni ta di mi'a? Zɔ ya yan wan zɔ se'em la ya ta'am di.

ref: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
text: Zɔteba kpemehere ba mɛhi la ba tana ba mɛhi tara sɛla woo puan. Ba iti dina wa ti ba to'e la muŋa sɛka en ta' Sagena, gee tumam dela muŋa sɛka en ka ta' sageni.

ref: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
text: Bala, mam ka zoti yoo yoo, bii n ŋmɛ'ɛra kunkura yoo yoo,

ref: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
text: Gee mam kpemehe n iŋa wa mɛ ti ka dɛna da'aberɛ, ti n han ta muulɛ bo' basɛba, mam mɛŋa kan bahɛ yoo.

ref: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
text: Mam ka boti ya bɔna aliko n suurɔ, ti tu sɔyaabeduma la za'a yuum boi sagbanɛ la tilen la mɛ, ba za'a yuum doohe ateko la puan tole mɛ,

ref: This was like being baptized and becoming followers of Moses.
text: ba za'a yuum to'e Moose dɔlega ko'om sua la mɛ, sagbanɛ la, la ateko la puan,

ref: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
text: ti ba za'a di sia puan diyiŋa la,

ref: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
text: gee ti ba za'a nyu sia puan ko'yimɔ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba nyu ko'om la la sia puan taŋa la en yuum dole ba la, tansɛka en de Kiresta la.

ref: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
text: Gee la bala za'a, Yinɛ nini da ka tigɛ la ba zo'e zo'e, ti ba kɔrum ba muuen la ha.

ref: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
text: Yele ana wa iŋɛ ti a dɛna la yɛm nyɔka bo' tumam, ti tu kɔrehi da le bɔna be'em puan wuu bamam en da iŋɛ se'em la.

ref: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
text: Da lebege ya bageditiba wuu ba basɛba n da iŋɛ se'em la, wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Nɛreba la zi'ire mɛ di, gee nyu gee ihege wa'ara wa'yooro la.”

ref: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
text: “Ya yeti, fum ta'am tari sore ti fum ita sɛla woo” gee ya me mina ti dagi sɛla wuu en tari nyuurɔ, “Ya yeti, fum ta'am tara sore ti fum ita sɛla woo,” gee ya me mina ti dagi sɛla woo n mɛti.

ref: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
text: Tu ka de tu mageha Kiresta biha wuu basɛba en daa iŋɛ se'em ti kampiihi kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra ba kuura la.

ref: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
text: Tu me da nyɔna wuu ba puan basɛba en yuum iŋɛ se'em ti Sagena la sagum ba la.

ref: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
text: Yele ana wa iŋɛ ba ti la dɛna la makerɛ, gee ti ba gulehɛ a biŋe ti a pa'alɛ tumam sɛba en boi ba'ahegɔ buuri saŋa wa puan la.

ref: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
text: Bala, fum se'em en ti'ihɛ fum ze' mɛ, bihɛ suŋa la fum da lu.

ref: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
text: Makerɛ ka boi paaɛ ya gee ka tabelɛ paaɛ nɛrese'em. Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana, a kan bahɛ ti makerɛ paaɛ ya gaŋɛ ya paŋa tɛka. Gee makerɛ la puan, a wan pa'alɛ ya sore ti ya ŋmibe de puan.

ref: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
text: Bala zuo, n zɔduma wa, ɛkɛ ya baga kaabegɔ puan yehe.

ref: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
text: Mam mi' ti mam tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la yɛm duma. Gakɛ ya bo' ya mɛhi mam en yeti ya sɛla wa.

ref: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
text: Sum wanɛ la ti tu ni ka' bahɛ de puan la, tu ni ka nyuura la Kiresta ziim la n bala de puan la? Boroboro la me ti tu ni gi'ihɛ la, tu ni ka tɔta la Kiresta iŋa la en bala?

ref: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
text: Boroboro la en de deyina la zuo, tumam en zo'e se'em la dela inganɛ deyina, se'ere n sɔi la, tu lagum obe la boroboro deyina la.

ref: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
text: Bihɛ ya Izirale nɛreba wa, ba sɛba en diti kaabegɔ dia la, dagi la taaba kaabegɔ bimbine la zuo?

ref: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
text: Beni ti n dikɛ ŋwana makera wa? Dia la en dikɛ kaabɛ baga la en de sɛla bii, baga la en de sɛla.

ref: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
text: Aayi, mam yeti sɛla wa dela, sɛla ti bage kaabereba la ni kaabɛ bo' la, ba ni kaabɛ bo' la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi gee dagi Yinɛ. Mam ka boti ya dikɛ ya mɛhi naaɛ la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi.

ref: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
text: Ya kan ta'am nyu Daana wanɛ la puan, gee le nyu kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi wanɛ puan. Yamam kan ta'am naaɛ la Daana di, gee le naaɛ la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi di.

ref: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
text: Bii tu boti tu ihege la Daana la nyuure? Bii tumam kpe'em gana en mɛ? Ita sɛla woo pɛgera Yinɛ

ref: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
text: Da eere ya, ya mɛhi sum ma'a, gee eere ya suurɔ me sum.

ref: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
text: Diti ya sɛla woo en koohe da'a puan, gee da soke ya doohe ya puti'ira en iti ya se'em la.

ref: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, “Timpaberɛ wa, la kaŋa en pirɛ se'em gã'a wa dela Daana la dinɛ.”

ref: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
text: Se'em en ka dole Yezu sore han yi' fum ti fum wa'am na di, ti fum iŋɛ fum puti'irɛ ti fum kiŋɛ, fum han ta paaɛ, di sɛla woo ti ba tari na ze'ele fum, gee da soke sokere doohe la fum puti'irɛ en iti fum se'em.

ref: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
text: Mam yeti sɛla wa dela, nɛre-ina wa puti'irɛ zuo, dagi fum mɛŋa puti'irɛ. Beni zuo, ŋwani ti mam fai bɔŋa wan bahɛ ti ayima puti'irɛ gakera en?

ref: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
text: Mam han bo' Yinɛ fara gee di dia, ŋwani ti ayima wan zɛregera mam disɛka ti mam bo' Yinɛ fara ka zuo gee di?

ref: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
text: Bala, ya diti mɛ, ya nyuuri mɛ, bii sɛla woo ti ya ita, iti ya sɛla woo ti Yinɛ yu'urɛ nyɛta pɛka.

ref: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
text: Da boni ya bunsɛka en wan tara daaŋɔ wa'am Yuuduma bii Girikiduma, bii Yinɛ lageŋɔ la puan na.

ref: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
text: Wuu mam muuri ti n tigɛ nɛra woo nini sɛla woo ti n ita puan se'em la, n ka eere n mɛŋa sum gee en eere la zo'e zo'e sum, ti ba ta'am nyɛ faarɛ.
