ref: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
text: Tɔgehi ya mam, wuu mam tɔgehɛ Kiresta se'em la. Zuo pi'a

ref: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
text: N pɛgɛ ya, la yan tɛri mam sɛla woo puan se'em la, gee me tara malema suŋa suna wuu mam dikɛ a se'em bo' ya la.

ref: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
text: Gee n boti ya bɔkɛ yeti, Kiresta en de buraa woo zuo daana, ti sira me dɛna a pɔga zuo daana, ti Yinɛ dɛna Kiresta zuo daana.

ref: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
text: Burasɛka woo en pu'uhi, bii puta gee ti sɛla pi' ka zuo, diiheri la a zuo daana la vi.

ref: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
text: Gee pɔgesɛka en pu'uhi, bii puta gee ka pi' ka zuo, diihe la ka zuo daana la vi, la ŋwɔni mɛ la ka siie ka zuo la mɛ.

ref: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔka han kan pi' ka zuo, ka yi siie ku bahɛ, gee la han dɛna vi ti pɔka kɛ̃ bii siie ka zuo, ka yi vuge sɛla.

ref: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, buraa dagi a pi' ka zuo, eŋa en de Yinɛ naanɛ, la Yinɛ suŋa la. Gee pɔka dela buraa suŋa.

ref: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, ba ka naam buraa yehe pɔka puan, gee pɔka ti ba naam yehe buraa puan.

ref: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
text: Ba me ka naam buraa pɔka zuo, gee ba naam la pɔka buraa zuo.

ref: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
text: Dina wa zuo, la suŋereba la zuo, pɔka dela ka pi' ka zuo ti la dɛna daalehum ti nɛra en sɔi en.

ref: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
text: La ka boi la fii me Daana la sore puan, ti pɔka sɔna ka mɛŋa buraa zi'an, buraa me sɔi ka mɛŋa pɔka zi'an.

ref: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
text: Wuu pɔka en ze'ele buraa puan na se'em la, bala mɛa ti buraa me zɛ'ɛta pɔka puan na, gee sɛla woo ze'eti la Yinɛ zi'an na.

ref: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
text: Gakɛ ya bo' ya mɛhi nyaa, la de ma'a ti pɔka pu'uha Yinɛ gee ka ti sɛla ka pi' ka zuo?

ref: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
text: gee pɔka han tara zomwogero, la dela a suŋa? Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ bo' zomwogero la ti la dɛna la sɛla en pi' ka zuo la.

ref: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
text: Gee se'em han ka sakɛ gee ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene, tumam ka tari malema aŋa bii Yinɛ Lageŋɔ la. Daana tuulum dia la galeŋɔ

ref: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
text: Nananawa, n kan pɛgɛ ya yele sɛ'a en kini na wa puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya han ni lagehɛ taaba na, la ni dagena sum, gee la ni tum dɛna la sageŋɔ.

ref: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
text: Mam yeti n pɔhɛ sɛla dela, ya han ni lagehɛ na wuu Yezu lageŋɔ la, mam wum ti yelehego en boi ya puan, ti mam n bihɛ dɛ ta paaɛ zi'an la, mam nam ti la dela bala.

ref: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
text: La de sira, yelehego wan ba'ahum bɔna ya puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, yele dina ma'a ti la wan niiɛ peelumen sɛba n de yelemɛŋerɛ Yinɛ pu'uheba ya puan.

ref: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, dia la saŋa han ni ta paaɛ, nɛrawoo zugeri la a zuka dia la puan, ti ayima kule kom, ti ayima me buge.

ref: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sansɛka woo ti ya ɔbera boroboro wa, gee nyuura wula wa puan, ya tiiheri la Daana la kum gura a lɛbega na. Daana tuulum dia, la be'em

ref: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba woo en diti gee nyuura gee ka ti'iha Daana iŋa la tuulum yele, diti gee nyuura la gakerɛ baha ba mɛhi zuto.

ref: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
text: Bala mɛa ti a dikɛ wula, ban di yu'uŋɔ ba'ahɛ la, yeti “Wula wa dela mam ziim la nɔpu'ulegɔ paalega. Ita ya dɛna sansɛka woo ti ya yuure de puan, la ya tɛra n yele.”

ref: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
text: Mam to'e se'ere Daana zi'an la, dinɛ ti mam dikɛ bɔ'ɔra ya. Ti Daana Yezu, yu'uŋɔ seko ti ba wan koohe en la, a dikɛ la boroboro,

ref: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
text: en bo' fara ba'ahɛ la, a figehe de mɛ gee yeti, “Dina en de mam iŋa ti n dikera bɔ'ɔra ya. Iti ya dɛna la ya tɛra n yele.”

ref: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
text: Bala la, fum se'em woo en obe boroboro wa, gee nyu Daana wula la puan ti la ka nara, wan yele se'ere zuo ti fum galum Daana iŋa la la a ziim.

ref: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
text: Bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa ba'ahɛ, gee ta'am obe boroboro la gee nyu daam wula la puan.

ref: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
text: Dina wa zuo ti ya zo'e zo'e ihe buge ti ya bɛ̃'ɛra, ti ya sɛba ki.

ref: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
text: Gee tu han gakɛ tu mɛhi suŋa suŋa, ba kan gakɛ tɔ.

ref: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
text: Gee Daana la han gakɛ tɔ, a dɔlege la tu tuba ti tu da lagum nyɛ sageŋɔ la tiŋa zuo nɛreba.

ref: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
text: Bala n suurɔ, ya han ni lagum taaba ti ya di Daana dia la, ni gu'uhi ya taaba.

ref: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
text: Ya kom han duna, ni di ya yire, ti ya han ta lagehɛ taaba, la da dɛna sageŋɔ bo' ya. Han dɛna yɛla asɛ'a la yele, n han ta wa'am na, n wan suŋɛ ya ti ya maalɛ.

ref: My friends, you asked me about spiritual gifts.
text: Nananawa, han dɛna la Sia yɛla la, n suurɔ, mam ka boti ya bɔna aliko.

ref: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
text: Ya mi' ti, yan daa nan de bageditiba la, ba daa pã'ahɛ ya mɛ, tara ya ti ya ta bɔregɛ, dɔla baga en ka tã tɔgera.

ref: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
text: Bala zuo, n boti ya bɔkɛ mɛ ti, nɛra ka boi tɔgera Yinɛ Sia la paŋa puan tabelɛ, “Puta baha Yezu zuo.” Bii nɛra ta'am yeti “Yezu en de Daana,” han dagena Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan.

ref: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
text: Lan de se'em n ŋwana: Zu'uhegɔ bo'olum la boi la yima yima, gee Saa kayima mɛa en bo'ori,

ref: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
text: ti tuuma la me bɔna yima yima gee ba tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana ayima mɛa.

ref: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
text: Ti tuuma yima yima tuna, gee Yinɛ deyina mɛa n bo'ori ba za'a paŋa, ti ba tuna nɛrawoo puan.

ref: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
text: A bo' nɛra woo la Sia tuunɛ Sia tuunɛ, ti nɛra woo nyɛta, nɛra woo sum zuo.

ref: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
text: Ayima, Sia la bo' en la yɛm tugum puan, ti ayima me, Sɛkaŋa mɛa n bo' en mi'ilum tugum puan,

ref: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
text: ti ayima me, Sɛkaŋa mɛa bo' en la sakerɛ, ti ayima me tibegɔ bo'olum yima yima,

ref: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
text: ti ayima me, Sɛkaŋa mɛa bo' en la kirehi tuuma bo'olum, bo' ayima me purega bo'olum, gee bo' ayima me sɛɛhi baŋerɛ bo'olum, bo' ayima tugum buuri buuri bo'olum, bo' ayima me tugum leregere bo'olum.

ref: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
text: Nananawa, bo'olum buna wa za'a, Sia kayima wa n bahɛ ti ba tuna. Sia kayima mɛa n bo'ori nɛra woo a bo'olum a bo'olum wuu Sia la mɛŋa n boti se'em la. Iŋa kayina, luga zo'e zo'e

ref: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Sɛyiŋa mɛa puan ti ba sɔ tu za'a ko'om, ki'ihɛ inyiŋa puan. Yuuduma, la Girikiduma, da'aba, la sɛba en bo' fai, gee bahɛ ti tu za'a nyu Sɛyiŋa la.

ref: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, iŋa la dagi lɔge yiŋɔ ma'a, gee lɔgerɔ zo'e zo'e.

ref: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
text: Ti na'arɛ han wan ta'am yeti, “Mam n dagi nu'o la zuo, mam ka po pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan,” bala kan bahɛ ti a kan pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan.

ref: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
text: Tuberɛ han wa ta'am yeti, “Mam dagi niho la zuo, mam ka po pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan,” bala kan ta'am bahɛ ti a kan pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan.

ref: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
text: Iŋa la za'a han deni la niho, sɛla n wan wuna bɛɛ? Iŋa la za'a han deni la tuberɛ, sɛla n wan nyuuhera nyuuŋo bɛɛ?

ref: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
text: Gee lan de se'em la, Yinɛ en tigehe lɔgerɔ la za'a iŋa la puan, ayima ayima wuu en boti se'em.

ref: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
text: Ba za'a han deni la lɔgeyiŋɔ, iŋa wan boni la bɛ?

ref: It takes many parts to make a single body.
text: Gee lan de se'em la nananawa la, luga la zo'e mɛ, gee dɛna inyiŋa.

ref: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
text: Bala la, niho kan ta'am yele nu'o yeti, “Mam ka eere fum,” bii zuo yele na'arɛ yeti, “Mam ka eere fum.”

ref: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
text: Gee lan tugum dɛna se'em dela, iŋa la lɔgesɛ'a en ani tɔregɔ la yele pakɛ mɛ,

ref: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
text: tu iŋa la lɔgesɛ'a ti tu ti'iha ti ba ka tari tuni la, tu biheri ba mɛ suŋa la na'ahegɔ. Tu iŋa lɔgeseto en ka tari nimpiilum la, tu biheri ba mɛ la na'ahegɔ kãtɛ.

ref: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
text: Gee tu ihe lɔgesɛ'a en ba' tari na'ahegɔ la ka eere yele ana. Gee Yinɛ tigehe iŋa la la sadɔseko ti lɔgeseto ti tu ka ba' tɛra tu yele la en tugum nyɛta na'ahegɔ kãtɛ.

ref: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
text: A iŋɛ ŋwana ti bɔgehegɔ da bɔna iŋa luga la puan, gee luga la tugum suŋera taaba.

ref: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
text: Iŋa la lugerɛ deyima han nyɛ wuŋerɛ, iŋa la luga la za'a ni lagum nameha mɛ; iŋa la lugerɛ deyima han nyɛ na'ahegɔ, iŋa la za'a ni lagum nyɛɛna mɛ.

ref: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
text: Nananawa, ya dela Kiresta iŋa, ti ya ayima ayima dɛna iŋa la luga.

ref: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
text: Ti Yinɛ en looe sɛba lageŋɔ la puan n ŋwana, yia, tuntuneba, yi'i, nɔtɔgeheba, ta'a pa'aleba, nyaa bo' kirehi, nyaa bo' tibegɔ bo'olum yima yima, suŋɛ yima yima, nɛheduma yima yima, la tugum buuri buuri bo'olum.

ref: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
text: Nɛrawoo dela tuntuna yoi? Bii Nɛrawoo en de nɔtɔgeha? Nɛrawoo dela pa'ala yoi? Bii Nɛrawoo en tuni kirehi?

ref: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
text: Nɛrawoo tari la tibegɔ bo'olum yima yima? Bii Nɛrawoo en tɔgeri zɛlempaala? Nɛrawoo en tã lɛregera zɛlempaala yoi?

ref: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
text: Gee tugum bahɛ ya ti ya nini mu'ɛ la bo'olum sebo en tari suŋɛ bo' nɛra woo. Gee n wan pa'alɛ ya sadɔseko en de ma'a gana za'a pa'ahɛ.

ref: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text: Mam han pun tana tɔgera saaleba tugum buuri buuri, bii tana tɔgera Yinɛ suŋereba yetɔgum, gee ka tara nɔŋerɛ, n ani wuu luŋa en yeen vɔɔla, bii kpankpaŋi en ŋmɛ'ɛri la.

ref: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
text: N me han tara purega bo'olum paŋa, bɔkera sɛla en sugɛ, gee tara sɛlawoo mi'ilum, tara sakerɛ woo wan ta'am vɔ zɔya, gee ka tara nɔŋerɛ, n dagi sɛsɛla.

ref: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
text: N han pun dikɛ n tarega za'a bo' nɛreba, gee ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ dikɛ n iŋa tihɛ ti ba nyɔ', gee ka tara nɔŋerɛ, nyuurɔ ka boi bini.

ref: Love is patient and kind, never jealous, boastful, proud, or
text: Nɔŋerɛ dela sugeri, nɔŋerɛ dela itesuŋɔ. Nɔŋerɛ ka tari nintɛrega bii pa'alɛ n mɛa, bii nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a,

ref: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
text: bii wurebere, nɔŋerɛ dagi mam boti se'em ti n ita, nɔŋerɛ ka eere n ma'a sum, a ka iti suure tɔtɔ, a ka biŋere be'em a suuren.

ref: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
text: Nɔŋerɛ ka nyɛɛni sɛla en dagi ma'a, gee a tugum nyɛɛna mɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
text: Nɔŋerɛ ka bã'ahi. Gee han dɛna purega, ka wan wa'am na ba'ahɛ. Han dɛna yetɔgum buuri buuri, bu wan wa'am na ba'ahɛ. Han dɛna mi'ilum me, bu wan wa'am na ba'ahɛ.

ref: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
text: Tu yeen mina la fii wa, tu puti la fii wa.

ref: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
text: Gee mɛ'ɛ la han diŋɛ wa'am na, fii wa la kan le bɔna.

ref: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
text: Mam n daa de bia la, n daa tɔgeri la biine, ti'iha wuu biine, bɔkera wuu biine, gee mam nyaa de bunkutɛ la, mam bahɛ biine mɛ.

ref: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
text: Nananawa, tu biheri la biheŋa puan ti la ka niiɛ suŋa, gee la kini na, ti tu wan ta nyɛta kpaii. Nananawa, tu nan mi' la fii, gee la kini na, ti tu wan ta mina za'a, wuu mam ti ba mina mam za'a za'a se'em la.

ref: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
text: Gee nananawa, sɛla en pakɛ dela, sakerɛ, beere puti'irɛ la nɔŋerɛ. Bana n wan bɔna wuuha, gee sɛla en gana za'a dela nɔŋerɛ.

ref: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
text: Ɛ ya nɔŋerɛ ninmu'urɛ, gee me mu'ɛ nini ti ya nyɛ Sia yɛla, yele yele wuu purega bo'olum.

ref: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba n tɔgeri buuri buuri yetɔgum la ka tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la nɛreba gee Yinɛ. Beni zuo, nɛra ka boi bɔkera ba. Ban tɔgeri sɛla en sugɛ Sia puan la zuo.

ref: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
text: Gee zi'an kayima me, puteba la, tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la nɛreba, ti la mɛ ba, gee kpemehe ba, gee bugelɛ ba suya.

ref: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
text: Se'em en tɔgeri buuri buuri yetɔgum la mɛti la a mɛŋa, gee se'em en puti la mɛti la lageŋɔ la.

ref: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
text: Lan de se'em dela, mam wan boti ni ti ya za'a tɔgera yetɔgum buuri buuri la, gee n wan ba' boti ni ti ya puta. Se'em en puti la gani se'em en tɔgeri yetɔgum buuri buuri la mɛ, han dagena nɛra en boi lerege a yetɔgum la, ti bu mɛ lageŋɔ la.

ref: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text: Bala, n suurɔ, mam han wa'am ya zi'an na tɔgera buuri buuri yetɔgum, n wan suŋɛ ya la ŋwani, han dagena n lilege la se'ere pa'alɛ ya bii mi'ilum bii purega bii pa'alegɔ puan?

ref: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
text: La dela buyina la bumŋmɛ'ɛla, wuu talitiŋa bii kɔ̃legɔ. Limehego han ka limehe, nɛra wan iŋɛ la ŋwani baŋɛ yuunɛ se'ere ti ba ŋmɛ'ɛra, bii pɛɛbera?

ref: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
text: Le yɛ'ɛha me tintanɛ han ka peebe gɔlegɛ, ani n wan maahum yehe pɛntɛrɛ?

ref: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
text: La ani la bala la ya. Fum han bɔna tɔgera buuri buuri puan, ti nɛra ka bɔkera bɔ, nɛra yeti a iŋɛ la ŋwani baŋɛ fum yeti sɛla? Fum tɔgera bahera la sabeeo puan yoo.

ref: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
text: La han dɛna ti mam ka mi' yetɔgesebo vuurɛ, mam dela saana bo' tɔgera la, ti tɔgera la me dɛna saana bo' mam.

ref: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
text: La ani la bala mɛa la ya, yan kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ nini ɛɛra Sia yɛla la. Zigɛ ya suŋa suŋa la ya dikɛ bu tum suŋa ti la mɛ lageŋɔ la.

ref: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
text: Bala, se'em en tɔgeri yetɔgum buuri buuri, a pu'uhɛ nyɛ paŋa la a ta'am lerege de.

ref: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam han pu'uha la yetɔgum buuri buuri, mam sia la en pu'uhi, gee mam puti'irɛ ka bɔkeri.

ref: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
text: Beni ti n nyaa wan iŋɛ? N wan pu'uha la sia la puan, gee me pu'uha la n puti'irɛ la, gee yuuna pɛka yuuma la n sia la, gee yuum pɛka yuuma la n puti'irɛ la me.

ref: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
text: Han dagena bala, fum han ka'ara sum sia la puan, ŋwani ti yiŋa daana wan ta'am yeti “Taari” bo' fum fara bu'a la, yiŋa daana la n en ka bɔkerɛ fum n yeti sɛla la?

ref: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, han dɛna fum, fum ta'am pɛgɛ Yinɛ mɛ paaɛ paaɛ, gee la ka mɛti ayima.

ref: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
text: Mam bo' Yinɛ fara ti n tɔgeri yetɔge buuri buuri gana ya za'a,

ref: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
text: gee la ka boi la fii me, ti lageŋɔ puan, mam wan tugum tɔgɛ la yɛla anuu la n puti'irɛ ti la ta'am ka'am basɛba, nyana mam tɔgɛ yɛla tuha pia buuri buuri yetɔgum puan.

ref: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
text: N suurɔ, da deni ya kɔma ya ti'ihegɔ puan, gee tugum deni ya kɔmbilɛɛhi be'em tuuma puan, gee ti'ihegɔ puan deni ya bunkureba.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
text: Yinɛ Gɔnɔ la puan, la gulehɛ yeti, “Doohe la nɛreba en tɔgeri buuri buuri yetɔgum puan, Doohe saama nɔgbama puan, Ti n wan tɔgɛ bo' nɛrebana wa, gee la bala za'a, ba kan kelehe mam,” Daana la n yele.

ref: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
text: Bala yetɔge buuri buuri yi dagi daalehum bo' sakereba, gee sɛba en nan de saama sakerɛ la puan, gee purega dagi sɛba en de saama sakerɛ puan la dinɛ, gee sakereba la dinɛ.

ref: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
text: Han dɛna bala, la han iŋɛ ti lageŋɔ la za'a lagehɛ na, ti nɛra woo tɔgera buuri buuri, yiŋa nɛra bii sɛba en dagi sakereba kɛ̃ na, ba kan yeti ya zuto ka tuni?

ref: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
text: Gee ya za'a han puta, ti se'em en dagi sakera bii yiŋa nɛra kɛ̃ na, ya za'a n bahɛ ti a nyɛ teere, ya za'a en bahɛ ti a mina en de se'em.

ref: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
text: Yelesɛ'a en sugɛ a suure puan la han niiɛ na, nɛra la wan kpa' duma, lu tiŋa aliko Yinɛ nɛŋan, na'ahɛ en, gee muulɛ yeti, “Yinɛ sirum boi mɛ la ya.” Ma'a ma'a pu'uhegɔ

ref: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
text: Beni en nyaa de ya iŋɛ n suurɔ wa? Ya han ni lagehɛ taaba na, ya ayima ayima tara yuunɛ, tara pa'alegɔ, tara lilegere, tara buuri yetɔgum yetɔgum bii tara yetɔge buuri buuri leregere. Iŋɛ ya sɛla woo suŋa ti la mɛ lageŋɔ la.

ref: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
text: Se'em ya puan han wan tɔgera yetɔge buuri buuri, ba dɛna bayi bii ba han pun wan zo'e batã. Ba tɔgera sɔɔhena, ti nɛra bɔna ya puan lɛregera ba yetɔgum la.

ref: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
text: Gee nɛra han ka bɔna wan lerege yetɔgum buuri buuri la, ba ana suram lageŋɔ la puan, gee tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ba mɛhi la Yinɛ.

ref: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
text: Bahɛ ya ti sɛba en puti la, bayi bii batã tɔgɛ, ti basɛba me bɔna ti'ihɛ ban tɔgɛ sɛla la bihɛ.

ref: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
text: Gee Yinɛ han lilege sɛla bo' se'em me en zi' bilam, bahɛ ya ti se'em en puti yia la yee bããlam.

ref: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya woo ta'am puta ayima ayima, ti ya woo zamehɛ ti la kpemehe ya.

ref: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
text: Purega paŋa la boi la puteba la nu'uhi.

ref: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ dagi Yinse'ere n gbiregeri, gee a dela suma'ahum Yinɛ. (Wuu nɛresunduma lageŋɔ la woo puan la iti se'em la,

ref: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
text: pɔgehi dela ba ana bããlam lageŋɔ la woo puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ka bo' ba sore ti ba tɔgɛ, gee ba biŋe ba mɛhi tiŋa wuu pa'alegɔ la n yele se'em la.

ref: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
text: Se'ere han bɔna ti ba bɔta ti baŋɛ, ba soke ba sireba yire. La dela nyanɛ ti pɔka tɔgera lageŋɔ puan.

ref: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
text: Benii! Bii Yinɛ yelehum la pɔhɛ la yamam zi'an na? Bii yamam ma'a n de sɛba ti yelehum la paaɛ?)

ref: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
text: Se'em en yeti a dela nɔtɔgeha bii a tari Sia pahi, dela a mina ti mam en guleheri sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya wa dela Daana la nuurɛ.

ref: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
text: Se'em woo en ka dikɛ yele ana wa yi sɛla, se'em me da dikɛ en yi sɛla.

ref: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
text: Bala n suurɔ, mu'ɛ ya nini la purega bo'olum la, da gu'uri ya yetɔge buuri buuri la me.

ref: But do everything properly and in order.
text: Gee sɛla woo dela ya ita dɛ suŋa, gee ita dɛ ma'a ma'a.

ref: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
text: Nananawa n wan tiɛ ya n suurɔ, faarɛ yelehum la ti n muulɛ bo' ya la, sɛ'a ti ya to'e, gee sɛ zɛ'a a puan la,

ref: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
text: yelehum sebo puan ti ya nyɛ faarɛ, doohe ya han gurɛ yelehum la ti n muulɛ bo' ya la kankaŋi, han dagena ti ya yeen sakɛ la yoo.

ref: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam bo' ya la sɛla n de ninmu'urɛ yele yia ti mam to'e de. De dela, Kiresta ki bo' la tumam be'em, doohe gulehegɔ la,

ref: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
text: ti ba laaɛ en, ti a vo'e dabeha atã daarɛ, wuu lan gulehɛ se'em la,

ref: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
text: Gee nyaa le ta niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ suurɔ en gani kɔbehi sinuu bɔbeyinɛ, ti ba puan basɛba kelum vɔna la zina, ti basɛba me ki.

ref: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
text: Ti a nyaa ta niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ Yemesi, gee niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ a tuntuneba la za'a.

ref: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
text: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, a niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ la mam se'em en ani woo nɛra en ka bi'ɛ gee ti ba dɔgɛ en la.

ref: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam n pɔ'ɛ Yezu tuntuneba la puan, ka nara ti ba yi'ira mam a tuntuna, se'ere n sɔi la, mam n daa dole mɛleha Yinɛ lageŋɔ la.

ref: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
text: Gee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la zuo, mam dela mam en de se'em la, ti a zu'uhegɔ la ti a dikɛ bo' mam la me ka bahɛ yoo. Gee mam tugum tum mɛ paaɛ paaɛ gaŋɛ ba za'a. La dagi mam mɛŋa paŋa, gee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la n boi la mam la.

ref: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
text: Bala zuo, la dɛna mam bii bamam, tumam en muulɛ se'em n bala, ti bala mɛa ti yamam me wa'am na nam. Kum Vo'ore

ref: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
text: Nananawa, ba han muula ti Kiresta vo'e mɛ a kum puan, ŋwani ti ya basɛba wan yeti, vo'ore ka boi bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ki la?

ref: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
text: Vo'ore han ka bɔna bo' sɛba n ki la, Kiresta yi ka vo'e.

ref: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
text: Ti Kiresta me han ka vo'e, tu muulegɔ la ye dela yoo, ti ya sakerɛ la me dɛna yoo.

ref: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
text: Tumam pun nyaa dɛna la pumparenduma bo' Yinɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam yeti, Yinɛ vo'e Kiresta mɛ, gee ti a me ka vo'e en, la han sirum dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ ti sɛba n ki, ka vo'ore.

ref: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba n ki la han ka vo'e, Kiresta mɛ ka vo'e.

ref: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
text: Kiresta han ka vo'e, ya sakerɛ la yi dela zaŋa, ti ya kelum bɔna la ya be'em la puan.

ref: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
text: Ti sɛba n ki Kiresta puan la me, bahɛ yoo.

ref: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
text: La han dɛna la tiŋa wa zuo vom ma'a zuo ti tu tara puti'irɛ Kiresta puan, tumam n yi de nimbaalega duma gana nɛra woo.

ref: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
text: Gee yelemɛŋerɛ la dela, Kiresta vo'e mɛ kum la puan, dɛna kum vo'ore walediinɛ bo' sɛba woo en ki la.

ref: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
text: Wuu kum n doohe asaala puan wa'am na la, bala mɛa ti vo'ore me doohe asaala puan wa'am na.

ref: Adam brought death to all of us, and Christ will bring life to all of us.
text: Se'em woo n yehe Adam puan na n kiiri se'em la, bala mɛa ti se'em woo n yeheri Kiresta puan na wan vɔna.

ref: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
text: Gee nɛreba la woo tari la ba sore ba sore ti ba wan vo'e. Kiresta n de walediinɛ la, a lɛbega na, sɛba n de a dinɛ la wan vo'e.

ref: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
text: Gee ti ba'ahegɔ la nyaa wa'am na, Kiresta han ta dikɛ sɔ'ɔlum la bo' Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, bala ma'a ti a pun sagum bihereba woo, la kpe'eŋo woo, la paŋa woo en zaberɛ la Yinɛ mɛ.

ref: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta ba'ahum dɛna naba, gura ti “Yinɛ dikɛ a dinduma za'a biŋe a nama tiŋa.”

ref: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
text: Dima sɛka ti a wan sagum ba'ahegɔ za'a dela kum.

ref: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, “Yinɛ dikɛ sɛlawoo biŋe la Kiresta nama tiŋa.” Gee lan yeti, “Sɛla woo boi la a nama tiŋa la,” la niiɛ mɛ ti, se'em daana en dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ Kiresta nama tiŋa la mɛŋa ka lagum pa'ahɛ bini.

ref: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
text: Sɛla woo han dikɛ bo' Kiresta, Kiresta en de Dayua la mɛŋa me wan bɔna se'em daana en dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ a nu'uhen la me nu'uhen, ti Yinɛ nyaa sɔna sɛla woo, gee bɔna sɛla woo puan.

ref: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
text: Han dagena bala, nɛrebana wan iŋɛ la ŋwani? Bama sɛba en to'ohe ko'om sua bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ki la? Han dɛna ti sɛba n ki la kan vo'e, ŋwani ti nɛreba tɔ'ɔha ko'om sua bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ki la?

ref: And why do we always risk our lives
text: Gee ŋwani ti tumam pɔ̃hera tu nyuurɔ saŋa woo?

ref: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
text: Mam kiiri la daarɛ woo. La dela yelemɛŋerɛ n suurɔ, wuu mam ŋmɛ'ɛri n nyu'ɔ ya zuo se'em la. Nyɔ'ɔ ŋmɛ'sɛka ti mam ŋmɛ'ɛra Kiresta Yezu en de tu Daana la puan.

ref: Don't fool yourselves. Bad friends will destroy you.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti se'em pa'ahɛ ya. “Nɛreyaalehi sageni la vom sumɔ.”

ref: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
text: Sigehe ya mɛhi, gee nyɛ putɛ'sunɛ, la ya da le tum be'em. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba basɛba ka mi' Yinɛ. N yeti dɛna wa ti la dɛna la vi bo' ya. Vo'ore iŋa

ref: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
text: Gee basɛba wan soke yeti, “ Kum ta vo'ore la ŋwani? Beni ihe taaba ti ba wan tara?”

ref: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
text: Gerego wa, sɛla ti fum burɛ kan ta'am yehe na, han dagena la pu'ɛ mɛ nyaa bule yehe na.

ref: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
text: Gee Yinɛ en bo'ori naanɛ la eŋa en boti de se'em. A bo'ori biileho woo mɛ la bu naanɛ.

ref: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
text: Dagi naanɛ woo en ŋwani taaba, gee saaleba tari la iŋa kayima, ti duhi me tara iŋa kayima, ti niihe me tara naanɛ deyima, ti zim me tara iŋa kayima.

ref: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
text: Saazuo ihe boi mɛ, ti tiŋa wa zuo ihe me bɔna. Gee saazuo ihe la suŋa boi mɛ ti tiŋa wa zuo ihe suŋa me bɔna.

ref: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
text: Wuntɛɛŋa suŋa boi mɛ, ti ŋmarega suŋa bɔna, ti ŋmarebibehi me suŋa bɔna. Yelemɛŋerɛ ŋmarebibehi suŋa boi la yima yima la taaba.

ref: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
text: Bala mɛa ti la wan ana la kum en wan ta vo'e la. Sɛla en burɛ pu'uri mɛ, gee sɛla ti ba vo'e ka pu'uri.

ref: These ugly and weak bodies will become beautiful and strong.
text: Ba buti la dɛgerɔ puan, gee vɔ'ɔra suŋa puan. Ba buti mɛ la tɔregɔ, gee vɔ'ɔra la paŋa.

ref: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
text: Ba buti la ingɔŋɔ iŋa puan, gee ba vo'ore la sia iŋa puan. Ingɔŋɔ puan iŋa han bɔna, sia puan iŋa me boi mɛ.

ref: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
text: Bala ti la gulehɛ yeti, “Yia buraa, Adam la, lebege la asaala”, gee pooren Adam la lebege la sɛsɛka en bo'ori vom.

ref: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
text: Gee la dagi sia iŋa la en de yia, gee ingɔŋɔ iŋa la. Nyaa bo' sia iŋa la.

ref: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
text: Yia buraa la dela la timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, a dela tintɔnɔ nɛra. Yi'i buraa la dela saazuo nɛra.

ref: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
text: Wuu tintɔnɔ nɛra la n ani se'em la, bala mɛa ti sɛba n de tintɔnɔ nɛreba la ana. Wuu saazuo nɛra la n ani se'em la, bala mɛa ti sɛba n de saazuo nɛreba la ana.

ref: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
text: Wuu tumam n tari tintɔnɔ nɛra la naanɛ se'em la, bala mɛa ti tu wan tara saazuo nɛra la me naanɛ.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
text: Sɛla ti mam yɛta wa n suurɔ, dela dina wa: Ingɔŋɔ la ziim kan ta'am to'e Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum. Bii sɛla en pu'uri to'e sɛla en ka pu'uri.

ref: I will explain a mystery to you. Not every one of us will die, but we will all be changed.
text: Kelehe ya, n wan yele ya sɛla en sugɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ tu za'a kan ki, gee tu za'a wan ta tee,

ref: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
text: saŋa pika pika puan, wuu niho en kpamehɛ la puan, ba'ahegɔ tintanɛ la peebego puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, tintanɛ wan ta peebe, ti sɛba n ki wan vo'e kan le pu'ɛ, gee han dɛna tumam tu inganɛ wa wan tee.

ref: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
text: Ingɔŋɔ seko en pu'uri wa han tee yɛ ingɔŋɔ seko en ka pu'uri, ti ingɔŋɔ seko n kiiri wa ta tee yɛ ingɔŋɔ seko en ka kiiri la, bala ti yetɔge sebo ti ba gulehɛ la nyaa wan wa'am yelemɛŋerɛ na. Ba gulehɛ yeti, “Ba vole kum mɛ, la nyaŋerɛ.”

ref: Where is its victory? Where is its sting?”
text: “Kum, fum nyaŋerɛ la bɛɛ? Kum, fum duŋa la bɛɛ?”

ref: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
text: Kum duŋa dela be'em, ti be'em paŋa dɛna kiha la.

ref: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
text: Gee pɛka dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ, se'em n bo'ori tu nyaŋerɛ doohe tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan.

ref: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
text: Bala, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ze' ya kankaŋi, da bikeri ya, kɔ'ɔm boni ya Daana la tuuma puan saŋa woo malum pa'ahera, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti Daana la puan ya tuunkãra la kan bahɛ yoo.

ref: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
text: Nananawa, lige lagehegɔ la ti ya yeti ya lagehɛ bo' nɛresunduma la, doohe ya mam en pa'alɛ Galatia lageŋɔ duma la se'em la.

ref: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
text: Dabeha ayopɔi woo pɔhega daarɛ, nɛra woo ni yehe en nyɛ nyuurɔ se'em la puan biŋe, ti la de ta dɛna ya lagehɛ, n han ta wa'am na.

ref: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
text: N han ta paaɛ na, n wan tum se'em ti ya wan looe la, la gɔnɔ seto n pa'alɛ ti tu mi' en, ti a tari ya bo'olum la kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm.

ref: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
text: La han le nara ti n me kiŋɛ, ba wan kɔlegɛ mam. Sore kinɛ maaheŋɔ

ref: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
text: gee daanse'ere n wan bɔna la ya, bii n pun ta bɔna la ya ɔɔrɔ saŋa la puan, ti ya bahɛ mam ti n kiŋɛ mam boti n kiŋɛ zi'an woo.

ref: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
text: N ka boti n nyɛ ni ya nananawa n tɔlega wa puan, se'ere en sɔi la, n boti ni ti n kɛ̃ ni la ya dabeha ayi', Daana la han bo' sore.

ref: I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
text: Gee n wan bɔna Ɛfisu ta paaɛ dabeha pinuu de'eŋo la,

ref: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, kulenkãtɛ en yu'ɛ bo' mam ti n tum tuuma n tari zuo, gee dinduma en zo'e.

ref: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
text: Timoti han ta wa'am na, da iŋɛ ya sɛla ti a zɔta ya dabeem, se'ere en sɔi la, a kɔ'ɔm tuna Daana tuuma la mɛ wuu mam la.

ref: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
text: Bala la, da bahɛ ya ti se'em zeem en. Suŋɛ ya en ti a kiŋɛ a sore la la suma'ahum, ti a ta'am wa'am n zi'an na, se'ere en sɔi la, mam guri en mɛ la suurɔ tuseto la.

ref: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
text: Nananawa, han dɛna tu suuɔ Apolos yele, mam yele en mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti a wa'am na bihɛ yamam, la suurɔ tuseto la, gee a pun ka bɔta la fii me ti wa'am na nananawa na. A han ta nyɛ yɔ'ɔ a wan wa'am na. Ba'ahegɔ yelehum, la pu'uhegɔ

ref: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
text: Biheri ya suŋa, ze' ya kankaŋi ya sakerɛ la puan, kpemehe ya ya mɛhi, gee kpe'em.

ref: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
text: Nananawa, n suurɔ, ya mi' ti Stefanase yire duma n de yia duma ti ba tee Akaiya tiŋa puan, gee me kaabɛ ba mɛhi tuna bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la.

ref: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
text: Mam ka'ani ya mɛ ninmu'urɛ ti ya dikɛ ya mɛhi tuna tuun aŋa taaba bɔ'ɔra nɛrebamam taaba, la sɛba woo en tuni gee wɔbegera muregere la ba.

ref: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
text: Mam puurɛ pee mɛ la Stefanase, Fotunatuse la Akaikus kinɛ na la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba sooe la ya na'arɛ.

ref: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
text: Ba bugelɛ la mam suure la yamam suure. Bala, to'e ya nɛrebana wa la na'ahegɔ.

ref: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
text: Lageŋɔ seto n boi Esiya pu'uhi ya na. Akuila la Pirisika ta ta' la lageŋɔ la en boi ba yire puan la pu'uhi ya suŋa suŋa Daana la puan.

ref: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
text: Suurɔ la za'a pu'uhi ya na. Pu'uheri ya taaba la yelemɛŋerɛ kɔkerɛ.

ref: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
text: Mam Pɔɔl n gulehɛ pu'uhi kuna la n mɛa nu'o pu'uhera ya na.

ref: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
text: Bahɛ ya ti ka'be'o dɔla se'em woo n ka nɔŋɛ Daana la. Maranata!

ref: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Daana Yezu zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya.

ref: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
text: Mam nɔŋerɛ bɔna la ya za'a Kiresta Yezu puan.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
text: Mam Pɔɔl, n de Yezu Kiresta tuntuna, doohe la Yinɛ sumboolum, la Timoti tu suuɔ la en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ lageŋɔ la n boi Korinti puan la, la nɛresunduma la woo n boi Akaiya tiŋa puan za'a la.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
text: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, la suma'ahum n ze'ele Yinɛ n de Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya. Pɔɔl pɛka, a namehegɔ la pooren

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
text: Sum dɛna Yinɛ n de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la dinɛ, nimbu'ɔ zɔta za'a Sɔ la, Yinɛ se'ere n de sumbugela woo daana la.

ref: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
text: Se'em n bugelɛ tu suya tu namehegɔ wuu puan, ti tumam me ta'am bugelɛ sɛba n boi namehegɔ puan, la sumbugelum sebo ti Yinɛ dikɛ bugelɛ tumam mɛhi la.

ref: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
text: Wuu Kiresta namehegɔ la n yage tumam zuo zo'e zo'e se'em la, bala mɛa ti tumam sumbugehum la me wan yaga ya zuo zo'e zo'e Kiresta puan.

ref: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
text: Tumam han nyɛta namehegɔ, la dela yamam sumbugehum la ya faarɛ zuo. Tu han nyɛta sumbugehum, la dela yamam sumbugehum, sebo ti ya nyɛ doohe yan ma'ɛ ya mɛhi, gee ŋmibera namehegɔ seko ti tumam namehera ku puan la.

ref: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
text: Tumam n tari beere putɛ'se'ere la ya ka miini. Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam mi' ti, yamam n nameheri la tɔ wa, bala mɛa ti ya wan nyɛ tumam sumbugehum la tɔ.

ref: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
text: Tumam ka boti ya bɔna aliko n suurɔ, la tumam n nyɛ namehɛ seko Esiya tiŋa la, se'ere en sɔi la, la daa ba' wuŋɛ tu mɛ gaŋɛ, ta wɔ̃regɛ tɔ, ti tu pun ka le ta bɔta ti tu vɔna.

ref: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, la daa ani tɔ woo ba gakɛ tu mɛ ti ba ku la, gee ŋwana wa en iŋɛ la, daa bahɛ ti tu ka dɛla tu mɛhi, gee Yinse'ere n vo'ore kum la.

ref: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
text: Se'em en faaɛ tumam nɔyalegerɛ kum wa puan bahɛ la, kelum faara tu mɛ, eŋa puan tu puti'irɛ dela, ti a wan le faaɛ tɔ bahɛ,

ref: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
text: yamam en naaɛ la tɔ, suŋera tɔ la nupu'uŋmɛ'a la, bala zuo ti a bu tu sum doohe nɛreba zo'e zo'e nupu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la puan. La bahɛ ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e bo' Yinɛ fara bo' tu. Pɔɔl sore kinɛ gu'a

ref: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tumam ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'urɔ mɛ, gee me tara yelemɛŋerɛ tu puti'ira puan ti Tumam tum timpaberɛ wa zuo mɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ, la Yinɛ zoolum, gee dagena tiŋa wa zuo yɛm, gee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la, ti ŋwana wa dɛna la yamam wa zuo.

ref: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka guleheri sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya gana sɛla ti ya wan ta'am kãalɛ gee me bɔkɛ. Mam puti'irɛ dela ya wan bɔkɛ bala ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ.

ref: just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
text: Wuu yan pun bɔkɛ tɔ zi'ihi sise'ehi la, ti Daana Yezu daarɛ la, tumam n wan dɛna ya nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a wuu yamam n wan dɛna tumam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a se'em la.

ref: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
text: Mam mi' dina wa la zuo, n boti ni ti n wa'am la yamam zi'an yia na, ti ya ta'am nyɛ zu'uhegɔ sugum nuuren buyi'.

ref: once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea.
text: Mam boti ni ti n kaaɛ ya bihɛ mɛ n han ta yɛ'a Masedoniya tiŋa, gee n han ta ze'ele Masedoniya lɛbera na, n le kaaɛ ya bihɛ, gee ti ya bahɛ mam ti n kiŋɛ Yudiya.

ref: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
text: N daa yeen yɛta mɛ, mam daa yeti ya dina wa la mɛ bii? Mam maaheni n maaheŋɔ dɔla la asaala yoi? Ti n yeen yeti “Ɛ̃ɛ ɛ̃ɛ” ŋmɛregɛ bilam yeti “Aayi aayi?” Ze'yiŋa?

ref: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
text: Wuu Yinɛ n kɔ'ɔm dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ daana wa la, tumam en yeti sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya ka tabelɛ dɛna “Ɛ̃ɛ la Aayi.”

ref: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ Dayua la, Yezu Kiresta, se'em yelehum ti tumam muulɛ bo' ya la, Silevanuse la Timoti la mam daa ka tari “Ɛ̃ɛ la Aayi.” Gee a puan, saŋa woo la kɔ'ɔm dɛna la “Ɛ̃ɛ.”

ref: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan, Yinɛ nɔbiŋe a woo dela “Ɛ̃ɛ.” Dina wa zuo, la dole la eŋa puan ti tu ni yeti “Taari la,” Yinɛ sɔna na'ahegɔ.

ref: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
text: Gee la dela Yinɛ n sɛ' tumam la yamam Kiresta puan, gee bi'ihɛ tu,

ref: and put his Spirit in our hearts to show that we belong only to him.
text: doohe la en dikɛ a daalehum la daalɛ tɔ, gee bo' tu a Fa'a Sia tu suya puan ti la dɛna a yia lɔkɔ en sɛ tɔ ze'ele.

ref: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
text: Gee mam yi' la Yinɛ ti a dɛna mam aze'nyɛ daana, ti ya da nyɛ susageŋɔ zuo, ti n daa ka le wa'am Korinti na la.

ref: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
text: Mam yeti se'em wa dagi la n boti n ba' dikɛ la zeero pa'ahɛ ya sakerɛ la puan, gee la tugum dɛna la tu dela tuntuneba la ya, ya tara nyɛɛma zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, ya ze' mɛ kankaŋi ya sakerɛ la puan.

ref: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
text: Bala, mam iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti, n kan le wa'am na bihɛ ya la susageŋɔ.

ref: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam han sagum ya suya, ani n boi wan bo' mam pupeelum, han dagi se'em ti mam sagum a suure la?

ref: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
text: Mam gulehɛ mam gulehɛ se'em wa la ti, la suŋɛ ti n han ta wa'am na n da nyɛ susageŋɔ sɛba n wan bo' ni mam pupeelum la zi'an. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam nam ya za'a ti, mam pupeelum wan dɛna ya za'a pupeelum.

ref: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
text: Mam n gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa la nimbaalega, la susageŋɔ, la nintam zo'e zo'e wa dagi la n bahɛ ti ya suya sagum, gee n bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ se'ere ti mam tara bɔ'ɔra ya. Sugeri

ref: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
text: Gee se'em han tari susageŋɔ wa'am na, a ka sagum la mam ma'a suuren, gee fum han bihɛ, dagi la n ba' pa'alɛ gaŋɛ mɛ, gee la lagum paaɛ ya za'a mɛ.

ref: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
text: Bala, tuberɛ dɔlegerɛ se'ere ti ya za'a naaɛ nuurɛ dɔlege nɛre-ina taaba tuberɛ wa la seke ya.

ref: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
text: Bala nananawa, tugum di ya sugeri bo' en, gee bugelɛ a suure, ti a susageŋɔ la da ba' gaŋɛ en.

ref: You should make them sure of your love for them.
text: Bala, n kɔ'ɔm bɛlena ya mɛ ti ya malum pa'alɛ en ya nɔŋerɛ.

ref: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
text: Mam n daa gulehɛ ya la yele dina wa zuo. N makɛ ya bihɛ, gee baŋɛ nyaa ya nane sɛla woo puan.

ref: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
text: Se'em woo ti ya di sugeri bo' en, mam me di sugeri bo' en mɛ. Mam n di sugeri bo' sɛla la, wuu n han sirum tari se'em yele n suure wa puan gee di sugeri bo' en, la dela yamam sum zuo, Kiresta nɛŋan.

ref: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
text: Tu iti dina wa ti Sagena la n da nyaŋɛ tɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka bu'uri a nyaleŋɔ la. Pɔɔl susageŋɔ Torowase

ref: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
text: Mam n paaɛ Torowase tiŋa ti n muulɛ Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la, kuleŋa n yu'ɛ bo' mam Daana la puan,

ref: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
text: gee mam puti'irɛ daa ka gã'arɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, n ka nyɛ n suuɔ Taitusi bini. Bala ti mam keehe ba, gee kiŋɛ Masedoniya.

ref: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
text: Gee pɛka paaɛ Yinɛ, se'em n dole Kiresta puan saŋa woo bahɛ ti tu kina nyaŋerɛ kinɛ, gee dɔla tumam puan bahɛ ti tu yiregera nyuuŋo suŋɔ seko en boi Kiresta baŋerɛ puan zi'an woo la.

ref: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
text: Sɛba n bɔregɛ la, nyuuŋo suŋɔ la han ŋmɛ'ɛra bamam, ba malum kiira mɛ. Sɛba n nyɛ faarɛ la me, nyuuŋo suŋɔ la han ŋmɛ'ɛra bamam, ba malum vɔna mɛ. Ani n seke wan ta'am tum tuunɛ wa?

ref: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam ka bureni Yinɛ yelehum la, wuu sɛba zo'e zo'e en iti se'em la. Gee Kiresta puan, tumam tɔgeri mɛ yelemɛŋerɛ, wuu nɛreba ti Yinɛ tum tɔ, gee ti tu me zɛ'a a nɛŋan saŋa woo la.

ref: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
text: Tumam pɔhɛ ti tu pɛgɛ la tu mɛhi le yɛ'ɛha? Yelemɛŋerɛ, dinɛ ka pakɛ tumam wuu sɛba en iti se'em la. Tu gulehɛ pɛka gɔnɔ bo' yamam, bii yamam gulehɛ pɛka gɔnɔ bo' tumam na?

ref: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
text: Yamam mɛhi en de tumam gɔŋɔ, gulehɛ bɔna tumam suya puan, ti nɛra woo wan mina, gee kãalɛ.

ref: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
text: Ya mɛhi la mɛ pa'alɛ ya ti ya dela Kiresta gɔŋɔ, ti tumam en de sɛba en maahum kɔ. Tu ka dikɛ zigemɔlegɔ gulehɛ, gee tu dikɛ la Yinɛ vua wa Fa'a Sia la gulehɛ ya. Tu ka gulehɛ iŋɛ la kugelama puan, gee tu gulehɛ iŋɛ la saaleba suya puan.

ref: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
text: Tumam en nam Yinɛ se'em en bala Kiresta puan.

ref: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
text: Dagi la tumam mɛhi en ta' sɛla ita, wan ta'am yeti ŋwana la ŋwana tumam mɛhi zi'an na; tumam tã'alum ze'eti la Yinɛ zi'an na.

ref: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
text: Yinɛ en de se'em en iŋɛ tumam ti tu dɛna tuntuneba a nɔpu'ulepaalɛ la puan, dagi la pa'alegɔ yoo puan, gee Sia la puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, pa'alegɔ yoo la kuuri mɛ, gee Sia la bo'ori la vom.

ref: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
text: Nananawa, kum tuuma yɛla la ba daa kɔrum a iŋɛ labelɛ kugelama puan la, han daa wa'am na na la niihum en kpigeri Iziraleduma la nini ti ba ka tana bihera Moose nɛŋa, kan daa ba' nyagera la zuo, dɛna niihum sebo en nyaa gbe'e biŋe pooren wa, nyaa bihɛ!

ref: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
text: Niihum kãtɛ boni taaba n wan dɔla Sia tuuma yɛla wa!

ref: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
text: Suŋa kana taaba daa han dɔla sageŋɔ tuuma yɛla la, suŋa kãtɛ en gani bala wan dɔla buurɔ tuuma yɛla.

ref: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla n tabeli tara suŋa, suŋa la ni ta bɔregɛ mɛ, doohe la suŋa kãtɛ kayima n wa'am na zuo.

ref: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla en kan bɔna wuuha daa han tara niihum buna taaba, sɛla n wan bɔna wuuha la niihum wan gana bala mɛ.

ref: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
text: Tumam n diŋɛ tara beere putɛ'dina wa la, tu tuni mɛ la sukpe'ene.

ref: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
text: Tu ka ani wuu Moose, n daa dikɛ fuo pi' a nɛŋa ti, Izirale nɛreba la da ba' biha niihum sebo n kan bɔna wuuha la.

ref: The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
text: Gee ba suya yuum ba' kãbɛ mɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ, wa'am na paaɛ zina, ba han wum nɔpu'ulegɔ kɛka la kãalegɔ, sɛla n daa pi' ba nini la kelum pi' ba mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta ma'a puan ti sɛla n pi' ba la wan pilege bahɛ.

ref: When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, la zina me, ba han kãalɛ Moose pa'alegɔ la, tanɛ la kelum pi' ba puti'ira la bala.

ref: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
text: Gee se'em n ŋmɛregɛ wa'am Daana la zi'an na, tanɛ la ni pilege mɛ.

ref: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
text: Lan de se'em dela, Daana la n de Fa'a Sia la, ti zi'an ti Daana la Sia bɔna, fai n boi bini.

ref: So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
text: Gee tumam za'a pilehe tu nɛhi bahɛ mɛ, gee biha Daana la suŋa la, wuu biheŋa puan la, ti la tee tu, ti tu tara Yezu naanɛ, zɛ'ɛta suŋa puan malum paara suŋa puan. Yele dina wa ze'eti la Daana la n de Fa'a Sia la zi'an.

ref: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
text: Bala, lan diŋɛ dɛna Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ zuo ti tu bɔna tuun-ana wa puan la, tu yɛm ka yɔ'ɔri.

ref: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
text: Tumam zagehɛ nyanɛ tuunsɛ'a ti se'em sugera la mɛ. Tumam zagehɛ mɛ ti tu kan tuna pã'ahegɔ tuuma bii tu burum Yinɛ yelehum la. Gee tumam kɔ'ɔm tɔgera la yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ, nɛrawoo nɛŋan la Yinɛ nɛŋan.

ref: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
text: Tumam yelehum wa han pun pi' mɛ, bu pi' la sɛba en baheri yoo la zi'an.

ref: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
text: Ba yele la dela, tiŋa wa zuo yinɛ la n pi' sɛba n ka nam Yezu la puti'ira, ti a gu ba ti ba da nyɛta faarɛ yelehum niihum la n de Kiresta suŋa la. Kiresta se'em n de Yinɛ mɛŋa naanɛ la.

ref: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam ka muuli la tu mɛhi yele, tu muulɛ la Yezu Kiresta se'em n de Daana la yele. Tumam mɛhi dela yamam yamehi Kiresta zuo.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela Yinse'ere n daa yeti, “Bahɛ ti niihum ze'ele lika puan niiɛ na la,” Yinɛ dinɛ mɛa n niiɛ tumam suya puan, ti a bo' tu a mi'ilum niihum la, n de Yinɛ suŋa niira Yezu Kiresta nɛŋa puan la.

ref: We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us.
text: Gee tumam tari tuulum lɔgerɔ tuna wa la yɔgerɔ kaleŋa puan, ti la niiɛ kpaii ti pankãtɛ kana wa n gani za'a wa dela Yinɛ paŋa gee dagi tumam paŋa.

ref: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
text: Tu nyɛ namehegɔ mɛ bɔberɛ woo gee ku ka ku tɔ. Tu nɔya maam mɛ gee tu suya ka gu'uhi.

ref: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
text: Tu nyɛ mɛlehegɔ, gee ba ka bahɛ tɔ, ti ba ŋmɛ' ta lobe gee ba ka sagum tɔ;

ref: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam n vɔ wa, saŋa woo tumam ka gagɛ kum Yezu zuo, ti Yezu vom la wan ta'am niiɛ tu ihe wa puan.

ref: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
text: Ŋwana wa bahɛ ti kum n zabera tumam daare woo, gee ti vom bɔna la yamam.

ref: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
text: Wuu tumam en lagum tara sia kayima tu sakerɛ puan doohe la gulehegɔ la n yeti, “Mam nam mɛ, ti n tɔgɛ.” Bala mɛa ti tumam me nam gee me tɔgɛ.

ref: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam mi' ti se'em n vo'e Daana Yezu la wan vo'e tumam me na naaɛ la Yezu, gee tari tumam la yamam paaɛ a nɛŋa.

ref: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla woo dela yamam zuo, ti zu'uhegɔ la, n pa'aherɛ se'em la paaɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e, ti pɛka malum zo'e pa'ahɛ ti Yinɛ yu'urɛ nyɛ pɛka. Vɔna la sakerɛ

ref: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
text: Bala la, ti ka kɔ'ɛ suure, la tu inganɛ wa n namehɛ ta ana wuu ka pu'uri me wa, tu puan naanɛ la lebegere la paalega beere yilegeri woo.

ref: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, namehegɔ pika wa n kan yuue wa maaheni tu mɛ ti tu ta'am to'e vom kamba'ahɛ sunsɛka n tibegɛ ka tara makerɛ la,

ref: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
text: Tumam n ka biheri sɛla n nyɛti, gee tu biheri la sɛla n ka nyɛti zuo, sɛla n nyɛ zina ka yuure, gee sɛla n ka nyɛti ka tari ba'ahegɔ.

ref: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
text: Tu mi' ti tiŋa zuo sugeri wa ti tu bɔna de puan wa han ta sagum ba'ahɛ, tu tari yire n ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, yise'ere ti nu'uhi ka mɛ, ka tari ba'ahegɔ saazuo ha.

ref: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu tiŋa zuo sugerɛ wa ti tu bɔna de puan tu uheri mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, kɔ'ɔm boti ni ti tu yɛ ni tu saazuo fuo la.

ref: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu han yɛ tu saazuo fuo wa, tu kan zɛ'a beele.

ref: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
text: Tumam en nan boi tiŋa wa zuo inganɛ wa puan la, ti uheri mɛ la too tu zeero puan, se'ere n sɔi la, tu ka boti tu zɛ'a beele, gee ba tum malum bagɛ tɔ la lɔgerɔ pa'ahɛ. Bala ti Yinɛ vom la wan vole sɛla n pu'uri la.

ref: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
text: Bala, saŋa woo tu tari puti'irɛ mɛ, la tum pun mina ti tu boi inganɛ wa puan ka'lam wa la, tu zaaɛ mɛ la Daana la.

ref: But we live by faith, not by what we see.
text: Tu vɔi me la sakerɛ gee dagi sɛla ti tu nini nyɛta.

ref: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tumam nam ya ti tu bahɛ inganɛ wa gee ta bɔna la Daana la.

ref: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
text: Bala, tu bɔna inganɛ wa puan, la tu ka boi inganɛ wa puan, tumam lu' tu suya ti tu tigɛ la a nini.

ref: After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies. Bringing People to God
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu za'a wan ze'ele Kiresta gakerɛ kuka la nɛŋan, ti nɛra woo to'e a yɔɔrɛ en tum sɛla a inganɛ wa puan. La dɛna sum bii be'em. Naarɛ tuuma

ref: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
text: Bala, tumam n mi' tu zɔta Daana la vuurɛ n de sɛla la, tu tiregerɛ mɛ ti tu bahɛ ti basɛba me baŋɛ en. Gee han dɛna tumam mɛhi, Yinɛ mi' tumam mɛ suŋa suŋa, n me tari puti'irɛ ti yamam me mi' tu suŋa suŋa ya suya puan.

ref: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
text: Tumam ka pɛgeri tu mɛhi bɔ'ɔra ya le yɛ'ɛha yoo, gee tu bo'ori ya la yɔ'ɔ ti ya ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ tu zuo, la ya ta'am lebehe sɛba n ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ nyu'urɔ ba yiŋa gee dagi ba suure puan la.

ref: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu iŋɛ ya gaafara, tumam zuto han ka tuna, la dela Yinɛ zuo, tu han le dɛna tu mɛhi la dela yamam zuo.

ref: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
text: Kiresta nɔŋerɛ la en bo'ori tɔ paŋa, se'ere zuo, tu za'a sakɛ ya ti nɛra ayima n ki bo' nɛrawoo, bala la, nɛrawoo ki mɛ.

ref: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
text: Yezu ki bo' la nɛra woo, ti sɛba n vɔ kan le vɔna bɔ'ɔra ba mɛhi gee se'em n ki gee ihege bo' ba la.

ref: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
text: Yehe nananawa yɛ'ɛha ha, tu ka le bɛha nɛra inganɛ puan, la tumam n pun tabelɛ mina Kiresta inganɛ puan la, tu ka le malum mina n en sadɔkuŋɔ puan.

ref: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
text: Bala, se'em han bɔna Kiresta puan, a dela naanɛpaalega, sɛla woo n de kɛka tole ya, bihɛ, sɛla woo nyaa lebege la paalega.

ref: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
text: Dina wa za'a ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an, se'em n dikɛ tu naaɛ la a mɛa doohe Kiresta puan, gee bo' tumam naarɛ tuunɛ wa.

ref: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
text: Dina dela, Kiresta puan ti Yinɛ dɔla dikera tiŋa wa za'a naara a mɛa, ka dikera ba be'em taala pagela ba zuo, tum dikɛ naarɛ sɔɔleŋa wa iŋɛ tumam nu'uhen.

ref: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
text: Bala, tumam dikɛ la Kiresta na'arɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, Yinɛ beleni ba dɔla la tumam puan. Bala tu yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ Kiresta zuo, ti ya lebe naaɛ la Yinɛ

ref: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Wuu tumam n lagum tuna la en la, ti beleni ya mɛ ti ya da to'e Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la yoo.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yeti, “To'ore saŋa la n paaɛ la, mam kelehe ya mɛ. Dabeherɛ se'ere en de n faaɛ ya la, mam faaɛ ya mɛ.” Bihɛ, to'ore saŋa la n ŋwana. Bihɛ, zina n de faarɛ dabeherɛ la.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
text: Tumam ka dikeri sɛla bɛŋera se'em sore ti, tuure da bɔna tu tuunɛ wa puan.

ref: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
text: Gee tumam en de Yinɛ yamehi la, tu nyɔkɛ tu mɛhi mɛ yɛla zo'e zo'e puan. Ŋmibega kãtɛ, namehegɔ, wuŋerɛ, nɔyalegerɛ,

ref: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
text: yelemɛŋerɛ tugum, la Yinɛ paŋa, la tuntɔɔnɛ zaberɛ lɔgerɔ ti tu zãla zuɔ la gɔbega,

ref: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
text: na'ahegɔ la fakerɛ yɛla, bã'ahi, imma'ahum puan. Ba ita tu wuu tu dela pumpã'ahum duma, gee ti tu dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ duma,

ref: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
text: tu ani wuu nɛra n ka mi' tu la, gee nɛreba mi tɔ mɛ. Tu ani wuu tu kiiri mɛ la gee bihɛ, tɔ vɔ mɛ. Tu ani wuu ba namehɛ tu mɛ la gee ba ka ku tɔ.

ref: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
text: Tu ani wuu tu suya n sagum la, gee tu nyɛɛni mɛ saŋa woo. Tu ani mɛ wuu naheduma la, gee me iŋɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba dɛna buntateba, tu ani wuu tu ka tari la sɛla la, gee me tara sɛla woo.

ref: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
text: Ti tɔgɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ bo' yamam Korintiduma wa. Tu suya kɔ'ɔm yu'ɛ mɛ bo' ya.

ref: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
text: Gu'a ka boi tumam n nɔŋɛ ya se'em la puan, gee la boi la yamam ma'a puan.

ref: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
text: Mam n boti sɛla ya zi'an dela, n yeti ya mɛ wuu n kɔma la, ti ya me yu'ɛ ya suya za'a. Yinɛ vua la pu'uhegɔ yire

ref: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
text: Da tari ya mɛhi naara yoo yoo la sɛba n ka dole Yezu. Se'ere en sɔi la, beni nɔyinɛ en boi la sɛla n de tuuntɔɔ la sɛla n dagi tuuntɔɔ? Beni sɔhega n boi lika la niihum tiŋasuka?

ref: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
text: Beni sakerɛ ti Kiresta tara la pumparema? Bii beni ti sakera tara la se'em n dagi sakera?

ref: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
text: Beni nuurɛ ti Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la wan ku la baga zɔŋɔ? Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam dela Yinɛ vua wa pu'uhegɔ yire la, wuu Yinɛ mɛŋa en yele yeti, “Mam wan bɔna ba puan, kina la ba, n wan dɛna ba Yinɛ, ti ba me dɛna n nɛreba.

ref: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
text: Bala, yehe ya ba puan wa'am na, la ya bɔna ya tɔka la ba. Daana la n yele, da kalum ya dɛkɔ lɔkɔ, gee n wan to'e ya.

ref: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
text: N wan dɛna ya Sɔ, ti ya dɛna n kɔma. Daana la en de Gani Za'a Daana la en yele.”

ref: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
text: Tumam n diŋɛ tara nɔbiŋe-ana wa la, n suurɔ, bahɛ ya ti tu pee tu mɛhi ingɔŋɔ la sia puan galeŋɔ woo puan. Tu bahɛ ti tuulum vom pirɛ, doohe tu Yinɛ zoolum puan. Pɔɔl pupeelum

ref: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
text: Bo' ya tu zi'an ya suya puan. Tumam ka tuue ya se'em, tu ka galum se'em, tu ka bihɛ ya se'em yoo.

ref: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
text: Mam ka yeti ŋwana ti n zɛregɛ ya mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, mam pun diŋɛ yele yeti, ya boi la tumam suya puan, ti tu wan lagum ki la taaba bii tu lagum vɔna la taaba.

ref: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
text: Mam tari ya saŋa woo ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ. Mam tari ya pagera mɛ. Buga buga nyaa pirɛ mam mɛ. Nyɛɛma n pirɛ mam tu namehegɔ la woo puan.

ref: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
text: La tumam n daa pun ta kɛ̃ Masedoniya tiŋa na la, tu ihe wa daa ka nyɛ vo'ohego, gee tu daa kɔ'ɔm nyɛta la namehegɔ zi'an woo. Nɔkpɛ'ɛma la sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ tu puan, la dabeem sebo n boi tumam mɛhi tiŋasuka.

ref: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
text: Gee Yinɛ, se'em n bugelɛ se'em suure n sagum la, bugelɛ tu suya mɛ la Taitusi paaɛ na.

ref: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
text: Gee dagi Taitusi kinɛ la ma'a, gee subugehum sebo ti yamam daa bugelɛ a suure, ti a wa'am na yɛta tɔ yan boti ya nyɛ mam se'em, yan kele se'em, yan mu'ɛ nini se'em la. Ti la bahɛ ti mam me nyɛɛna, kɔ'ɔm kelum nyɛɛna yɛ'a ha.

ref: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
text: La ha pun dɛna la mam sagum la ya suya doohe la n gɔŋɔ la, mam ka lebere pooren, .

ref: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
text: Nananawa, mam nyɛɛni mɛ, ti la dagena la ya suya n sagum la, gee ya susageŋɔ la bahɛ ti ya nyɛ la suteere. Ya nyɛ Yinɛ zoolum susageŋɔ, bala ti ya susageŋɔ la ka daam tɔ sadɔɔ seko puan.

ref: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ zoolum susageŋɔ bo'ori la suteere se'ere en bo'ori faarɛ ti pooren lɛbega ka bɔna. Gee tiŋa zuo susageŋɔ bo'ori la kum.

ref: Just look what God has done by making you feel sorry! You sincerely want to prove you are innocent. You are angry. You are shocked. You are eager to see that justice is done. You have proved that you were completely right in this matter.
text: Bihɛ ya yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ zoolum susageŋɔ wa iŋɛ ya puan. Beni ninmu'urɛ ti ya ka mu'ɛ ti ya yehe ya mɛhi, beni sigehe mɛŋa, beni pakerɛ, beni boolum, beni zika, beni namehegɔ. Sadɔɔ seko woo puan ya pa'alɛ mɛ ti ya ka tari tuure yele la puan.

ref: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
text: Bala, la mam en pun gulehɛ bo' ya na la, la dagi tu guleheri la se'em n tuue la yele, bii se'em ti ba tuue la yele, gee tu boti tu bahɛ ti ya ninmu'urɛ se'ere ti ya tara la tumam la n niiɛ bo' ya Yinɛ nɛŋan.

ref: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
text: Dina puan ti tu nyɛta suma'ahum. Sɛla en pa'ahɛ tumam mɛhi subugehum la puan dela, tu kelum kɔ'ɔm nyɛɛna mɛ la Taitusi. Se'ere en sɔi la, a suure gã'arɛ la ma'ahum, yamam za'a zuo.

ref: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
text: Mam han daa dikɛ ya ŋmɛ' la n nyu'ɔ sadɔɔ seko puan boi en, bu nyanɛ ka di mam. Gee sɛla woo ti tu tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya en de yelemɛŋerɛ la, tum ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ se'em bɔ'ɔra Taitusi la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
text: Ti a ka'ɛ a suure za'a za'a bɔ'ɔra ya na, en ti'iheri ya za'a n nane se'em, la yan to'e en se'em la Yinɛ zoolum dabeem, la gbi'a se'em la.

ref: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
