text: La bahɛ ti mam puurɛ pee mɛ la ya, se'ere en sɔi la, mam ba' nam ya mɛ za'a za'a.

ref: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Sɛla me en boi dela tu boti ya baŋɛ mɛ tu suurɔ, doohe la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la ti a bo' lageŋɔ seto woo en boi Masedoniya tiŋa la.

ref: Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously.
text: La, ban daa pun nyɛ namehegɔ kãra, nyɛ nɔŋɔ pɛɛlega la, ba nyɛɛma kãtɛ la ti ba tara la bahɛ ti ba bo' mɛ zo'e zo'e

ref: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
text: Mam nini en nyɛ se'ere la, ba za'a iŋɛ la ba suure la bu'a, bɔ'ɔra ban tari sɛla, pun ta bo' gaŋɛ ban tari sɛla la.

ref: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
text: la yele dina me, dagi ti tumam en yeen daa ti'iha, gee ba dikɛ la ba mɛhi bo' Daana la yia, gee nyaa dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' tumam me wuu Yinɛ suboolum n de se'em la,

ref: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
text: ti tu ta'am tɔgɛ la Taitusi yeti, wuu eŋa n pun pɔhɛ la, a kɔ'ɔm ba'ahɛ pupeelum bo'olum tuunɛ wa ti ya dikɛ wa.

ref: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
text: Nananawa, yamam en zo'ore sɛla woo puan se'em la, sakerɛ, tugum, mi'ilum, yiŋa yɛla ninmu'urɛ, la yan nɔŋɛ tu se'em la, bala mɛa ti tu bɔta ti ya me zo'e la pupeelum bu'a wa puan.

ref: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
text: Mam ka yeti dina ti la dɛna la n bo' ya la nuurɛ, gee n boti n dikɛ la ya yelemɛŋerɛ nɔŋerɛ la makɛ la basɛba ninmu'urɛ.

ref: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, yamam mi' pupeelum bo'olum sebo ti tu Daana Yezu Kiresta bo' doohe la en pun dɛna buntata la, gee yamam zuo ti a lebege naha, ti a nɔŋɔ la puan yamam wan dɛna buntateba.

ref: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
text: Bala, kɔ'ɔm tum ya de ba'ahɛ, ti ya ninmu'urɛ la doohe la yan tum de ba'ahɛ se'em la yan tari sɛla la.

ref: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ninmu'urɛ han bɔna, ba to'ore nɛra woo n tari sɛla ti a bo' la mɛ, gee dagi nɛra n ka tari sɛla.

ref: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
text: Mam yeti se'em wa dagi la n bahɛ ti ya sɛba zɛ'a fai gee ti la wuna ya sɛba, gee sɛla woo dɛna ma'a ma'a bo' ya

ref: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
text: yamam tarega nananawa wan bɔ'ɔra bamam ti bamam me tarega wan ta tee bɔ'ɔra yamam me, ti sɛla woo ti sɛla woo dɛna ma'a ma'a.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
text: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, “Se'em n tari zo'e, ka ba' tara gaŋɛ, ti se'em n tari fii me ka ba' tara ti la pɔ'ɛ” la. Taitusi yele

ref: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
text: Gee fara bo'ori la Yinɛ, se'em n kalum Taitusi suure, ti a nini mu'ɛ la ya wuu mam mɛŋa nini en mu'ɛ la ya se'em la.

ref: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi la a yeen to'e la tumam suhega la ma'a, gee a nini mu'ɛ mɛ gana saŋa woo, ti a mɛŋa daa pun maahum wa'am ya zi'an na la.

ref: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
text: Tu tuni Taitusi mɛ la tu suuɔ kuyima en de nɛre kãtɛ tara zuo lageŋɔ la woo puan, muula faarɛ yelehum la mɛ.

ref: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
text: Gee dagi bala ma'a, gee lageŋɔ la looe en mɛ ti a lagum ɛɛra la tɔ, tumam en dole ka'ahera nɔŋerɛ bo'olum tuuma wa, ti la dɛna na'ahegɔ bɔ'ɔra tu Daana la mɛŋa, gee me pa'alɛ tu susunɛ wa.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
text: Tu iŋɛ tu puti'ira mɛ ti se'em da zɛregɛ tɔ doohe pupeelum bo'olum wa ti tu ka'ahera wa.

ref: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
text: Tumam iŋɛ tu puti'ira ti tu tum la sɛla n de ma'a, dagi la Daana la ma'a nɛŋan, gee saaleba nɛŋan me.

ref: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
text: Bama en ye' na la, tu tuni la tu suuɔ seko ti tu viihɛ en saŋa zo'e zo'e puan, nyɛ ti a dela ninmu'urɛ daana yɛla zo'e zo'e puan, gee a nini nyaa pun mu'ɛ gana yia la mɛ la en nam ya paaɛ paaɛ se'em la.

ref: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
text: Han dɛna Taitusi, eŋa dela mam zɔ la mam tuntuna tadaana ya tuuma la puan, gee han dɛna tu suurɔ seto en dole la, bamam dela lageŋɔ la bitunehi, ti Kiresta nyɛta pɛka.

ref: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
text: Bala zuo la, ze'ele ya lageŋɔ la nɛŋan la ya pa'alɛ ba ya nɔŋerɛ, la se'ere zuo ti tu tari ya ŋmɛ'ɛra tu nyu'ɔ la.

ref: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
text: Nananawa, la ka pakɛ ti n gulehɛ Nɛresunduma tuuma la yele bo' ya.

ref: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam mi' ti ya ninmu'urɛ la n de mam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la ti mam tara ya ŋmɛ'ɛra bɔ'ɔra Masedoniya tiŋa nɛreba yeti, Akaiya pun maahum mɛ yuunɛ n tole ha la, ti yamam ninmu'urɛ la n gbirege nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

ref: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
text: Gee mam tuni tu suurɔ wa na ti, tu nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la ti tu tara ya ŋmɛ'ɛra la kan dɛna zaŋa dina wa puan. Bala la, ya wan maahum, wuu mam n pun yele se'em ti ya wan maahum la.

ref: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
text: Han dagena bala, Masedoniya nɛreba basɛba han doohe la mam wa'am na nyɛ ti ya ka maahum, la wan dɛna vi bo' tu, mam pun kan yele ya se'ere yele dina puan.

ref: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
text: Bala ti mam ti'ihɛ ti la nari ti n yele suurɔ wa ti ba diŋɛ nɛŋa wa'am ya zi'an na, la ba te'ele maahum bo'olum kãtɛ wa ti ya biŋe nuurɛ ti ya bo' wa, ti bu dɛna pupeelum bo'olum gee dagi pɛregerɛ bo'olum.

ref: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
text: Lan de se'em la dela, Se'em daana n buti yaara yaara, kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la yaara yaara, ti se'em me n buti yabi yabi me kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la yabi yabi.

ref: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
text: Nɛrawoo bo' wuu en maahum a suure ti a bo' se'em la. Dagi la a bɔ'ɔra la susageŋɔ bii a bɔ'ɔra la pɛregerɛ. Beni zuo, Yinɛ nɔŋɛ la se'em en bo'ori la pupeelum.

ref: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
text: Ti Yinɛ wan ta'am bo' ya maalegɔ sɛlawoo puan ti la zo'e gaŋɛ, bala ti saŋa woo ya wan tara sɛla woo ti la seke, ti ya ta'am tɔrɛ ti la zo'e ya itesuŋɔ wuu puan.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
text: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em ti, “A bo'ori la yabi yabi, a bo'ori naheduma mɛ, a tuuntɔɔ tuuma la boi la wuuha.”

ref: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
text: Yinɛ se'ere en bo'ori bumbuura ti ba buta, gee bɔ'ɔra buum ti ba dita la, wan bo' ya, gee nɔbɛ ya bumbuura ti ya wan burɛ, gee nɔbɛ ya tuuntɔɔ vom wala la ti ya kɛ̃' zo'e zo'e.

ref: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
text: Yamam za'a wan pãrɛ mɛ sɛla woo puan, la ya pupeelum bo'olum kãtɛ wa n wan bahɛ ti tumam ze'ele tu nama zuo bo' Yinɛ fara fara.

ref: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, yamam en tum tuun-dina wa la, dagi la ya bo' la nɛresunduma la ma'a, gee bu ɔlegeri mɛ la fara bu'a zo'e zo'e bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ.

ref: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
text: Doohe la tumam en makɛ ya bihɛ la tuun-dina wa taaba la, ya bo' Yinɛ yu'urɛ pɛka mɛ la yan nam gee sakɛ bo' Yezu faarɛ yelehum la se'em la, la ya pupeelum tɔrega la ti ya tɔrɛ la ba, la basɛba me se'em la.

ref: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
text: Ba nɔŋɛ ya, gee pu'uha bɔ'ɔra ya la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ seko en ka tari makerɛ ti Yinɛ ka'ɛ bahɛ ya la zuo.

ref: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
text: Fara fara dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ la a bo'olum sebo n ka tari makerɛ wa.

ref: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
text: Mam mɛŋa Pɔɔl, mam beleni ya mɛ doohe la Kiresta ma'ma' la a uhega la puan, mam se'em n ni sigehe n mɛŋa n han ni zɛ'a ya nɛŋan, gee kpe'em bo' ya n han ni zaaɛ la ya.

ref: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
text: Mam beleni ya mɛ ti mam han ni bɔna la ya, n dagi n kpe'em la ya, la n bɔta ti n da sakɛ bo' sɛba n ti'ihɛ ti tu iti sɛla woo la nɛresaalenɛ puan la.

ref: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu dela nɛresaaleba, gee tumam ka zaberɛ woo nɛresaaleba n zaberɛ se'em la,

ref: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
text: se'ere n sɔi la, tumam zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la yeen dagena la nɛresaala zaberɛ lɔgerɔ, gee tu tari la Yinɛ paŋa en wan ta'am sagum pahi zi'ihi.

ref: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
text: Tu sageni nɔkpɛ'ɛma mɛ la pirega lɔgeseko woo n zaberɛ la Yinɛ mi'ilum la, gee nyɔgera putɛ'sɛ'a woo n boi ti ba nana Kiresta.

ref: And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
text: Tu maahum mɛ ti tu namehɛ sɛba woo n ka nane la, yamam han nam ba'ahɛ.

ref: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
text: Biheri ya la sɛla n ze' ya nɛŋa wa, yamam han nam ti ya dela la Kiresta dinɛ, yi tiiheri ya ya mɛhi yeledina wa, ti wuu yan n de Kiresta deni la, tumam me dela bala mɛa.

ref: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
text: Nananawa, mam han pun ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ zo'e fii wa, la pan-sɛka ti tu tara, ti tu Daana la dikɛ bo' tu ti ka mɛta tɔ gee dagi ka aahum tɔ wa la, mam kan tara vi la ka.

ref: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
text: Mam ka boti la ana wuu n zigeri ti n vuuhe ya mɛ dɔla n gulehegɔ wa puan.

ref: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba yeti, “A gɔnɔ tibegɛ mɛ gee kpe'em, gee a han zɛ'a ya nɛŋan, a ka tari paŋa ti a yetɔgum la me ka tara tibehum.”

ref: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
text: Bahɛ ya ti nɛrebana wa baŋɛ ti, tum yeti ya sɛla gɔŋɔ puan, tu han ka bɔna la ya la, tu wan yele la bala mɛa tu han bɔna la ya.

ref: We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves.
text: Tumam ka sakɛ dikɛ tu mɛhi pa'ahɛ bii makɛ la sɛba n pɛgeri ba mɛhi la. Gee bamam han mageha ba mɛhi, tara ba mɛhi giila la taaba, ba ka pa'alɛ ti ba tari yɛm.

ref: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
text: Tumam en de se'em la, tu ka ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ zɔ'ɔra tu tɛka beene, gee tumam wan bɔna la ze'sɛka beene ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' tu la, tu dela tu paaɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ta ta' la yamam.

ref: We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
text: Tumam ka yakɛ tu beene la tumam n daa paaɛ yamam la Yinɛ yelehum la. Tumam n de yia duma ze'ele zi'an n zaaɛ wa'am ya zi'an na la Kiresta faarɛ yelehum wa.

ref: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
text: Tumam ka ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ zɔ'ɔra tu tɛka beene, de vuurɛ dela, tu ka ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ la basɛba tuuma, gee tumam puti'irɛ dela, wuu ya sakerɛ la n zo'ore la, tumam n tuni zi'an la ya la, wan yalegɛ pa'ahɛ,

ref: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
text: ti tu ta'am muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa tinse'ehi en zɔɛ yamam, gee kan ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la tunsɛ'a n pun tum ayima tuuma zi'an.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.”
text: “Bahɛ ya ti se'em en ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ nyu'ɔ a ŋmɛ'ɛra kɔ Daana la puan.”

ref: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi sɛba en pɛgeri ba mɛhi ti Yinɛ to'e ba, gee sɛba ti Yinɛ mɛŋa pɛgera la.

ref: I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ.
text: Mam tari la Yinɛ nyuure ya zuo paaɛ paaɛ se'ere n sɔi la, mam biŋe la nuurɛ ti n dikɛ ya elehe sira ayima. N dikɛ ya wuu pɔgesa'kaherɛ la bo' Kiresta.

ref: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
text: Gee dabeem n tari mam. Wuu kampia la n yuum pa'ahɛ Eva la a wikerɛ la, bala mɛa ti yamam me puti'ira wan yehe, ti ya kan le tara ninmu'urɛ la susunɛ pu'uha Kiresta.

ref: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em han wa'am na muulɛ Kiresta kayima n dagi sɛka ti tumam muulɛ bo' ya la, bii ya to'e la sia kayima n dagi sɛka ti ya pun to'e la, bii faarɛ yelehum sebo en dagi sebo ti ya pun to'e la, ya me ni sirum to'e a mɛ la ninmu'urɛ.

ref: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
text: Mam ti'ihɛ ti mam dagi bia tuntuneba sɛba n iŋɛ ba mɛhi ami' za'a duma wa nɛŋan.

ref: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
text: Daanse'ere n ka mi' la tugum gee dagi mi'ilum. Yelemɛŋerɛ, sadɔɔ seko woo puan, la sɛla woo puan, tu tum pa'alɛ ti tu mi' mɛ.

ref: Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge?
text: Mam tum la be'em la mam sigehe en mɛa, ti yamam tee zom saazuo la, la mam n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' ya zaŋa la?

ref: I robbed other churches by taking money from them to serve you.
text: Bala, Mam fa' lageŋɔ tuseto, doohe la mam sakɛ to'e suŋɛ bamam zi'an ti n ta'am wa'am na tum bo' yamam la.

ref: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
text: Mam n daa boi la ya ti nɔŋɔ tara mam la, mam daa ka dikɛ n mɛŋa ziile ya se'em, se'ere en sɔi la, mam n daa boti sɛla woo la, tu suurɔ seto n daa ze'ele Masedoniya tiŋa na la en daa bo' mam. Bala, mam yehe n mɛa gee wan kelum yehe n mɛa, kan le dikɛ n mɛa ziile ya sadɔseto woo puan.

ref: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
text: Wuu Kiresta yelemɛŋerɛ la n boi mam puan se'em la, mam yele dina wa n de mam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a wa kan yee suram Akaiya tiŋa nɛreba puan.

ref: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
text: Ti beni zuo? La dela mam ka nɔŋɛ ya mɛ bii? Yinɛ ma'a en mi'.

ref: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
text: Mam n iti se'em la, n wan kelum ita la bala mɛa, la n da bo' sore ti sɛba n boti ba nyɛ sore bahɛ ti ba baŋɛ ba ti mam la bamam zuni me la sore.

ref: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
text: Bama sɛba n ŋmɛ'ɛri ba nyu'urɔ bala la dela pumparenduma. Ba dela pa'ahegɔ tuntuneba, ba yeen iŋɛ la ba mɛhi ti bamam dela Kiresta tuntuneba.

ref: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
text: La dagi pakerɛ, Sagena la mɛŋa iŋɛ a mɛŋa la bala ti eŋa dela yelemɛŋerɛ suŋera.

ref: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
text: Bala, la dagi pakerɛ, a tuntuneba la me han iŋɛ ba mɛhi ti bamam dela tuuntɔɔ vom la tuntuneba. Ba ba'ahegɔ wan doohe mɛ la ba tuuma la. Pɔɔl nyɛti namehegɔ

ref: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
text: Bahɛ ti n le takɛ pa'ahɛ. Da bahɛ ti ya se'em ti'ihɛ ti mam dela gɔɔŋɔ. Gee ya han sina gee iŋɛ bala, yi yeen to'e ya mam bala ti n dɛna gɔɔŋɔ, ti mam me ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ fii wa.

ref: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
text: Mam yeti nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a seko wa, seko ti mam sɛ zɛ'a ku puan wa dela, n yeti dina wa mɛ dagi la Daana la paŋa puan, gee gɔɔnɛ puan.

ref: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
text: Nɛreba en diŋɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra ba nyu'ɔ nɛresaalenɛ puan la, mam me wan ŋmɛ' la bala mɛa.

ref: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya pee la puurɛ ŋmibera la gɔɔnɔ, gee dɛna yɛmduma ya mɛhi.

ref: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
text: Ya ŋmiberi mɛ la se'em woo en dikɛ ya lɛbegera a da'aberɛ, bii se'em n yeti a obe ya, bii a bihɛ ya yoo, bii zɛkera a mɛŋa, bii lɔbera ya pɛgehi.

ref: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
text: La tari mam la vi, la mam n yeti ya dina wa. Tu ka kpe'em gee bahɛ ti yele ana wa ita tu puan. Gee yelese'ere woo puan ti se'em wan ŋmɛ' a nyu'ɔ, n yeti la gɔɔnɛ yɛla la puan, mam me wan ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ a zuo.

ref: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
text: Ba dela Hiburu duma? Mam me dela bala. Ba dela Iziraleduma? Mam me dela bala. Ba dela Aberaham tuuhum? Mam me dela bala.

ref: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
text: Ba dela Kiresta tuntuneba? N nyaa kɔ'ɔm tɔgera mɛ wuu zɔlegɔ la, han dɛna bala mam yi gani ba. Namehɛ gana ba, kɛ̃ paka deto puan gana ba, to'ohe ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ en ka tari kãlɛ gana ba, saŋa zo'e zo'e, pɔ̃hɛ n nyuurɔ gana ba.

ref: Five times my own people gave me
text: Sugum nuurɛ bunuu ti n to'ohe Kpa'aha deyina ka boi kpa'aha pihinaahi,Yuuduma la nu'uhen.

ref: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
text: Nuurɛ butã ti ba ŋmɛ' mam la kuto kpa'aha. Buyina ti ba lobe mam la kuga. Butã' ti uureŋɔ wɔ̃rɛ la mam ko'kãtɛ puan, ti n bɔna ko'om la zuo bala yu'uŋɔ la beyiine.

ref: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
text: Saŋa woo, mam boi kinɛ zuo, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ kulaahi puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la nageyigehi, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la n mɛŋa nɛreba, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la sɛba n dagi Yuuduma, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ tinkãra puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ muɔ puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ ko'kãtɛ puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la suurɔ seto n de pumparenduma.

ref: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
text: Namehegɔ la tuunkãra yu'uŋɔ, gɔgara dabeha zo'e zo'e, kom la ko'nyuuro, saŋa zo'e zo'e ka tara wan di, ɔɔrɔ la beele puan.

ref: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
text: Gee bala gã'arɛ kinkɛleŋa, mam en de se'em ti mugehego gã'a n zuo mam mu'ɛ nini la lageŋɔ duma la za'a la zuo.

ref: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
text: Ani n de tɔregɔ daana, ti mam dagena tɔregɔ daana? Ani ti ba pirɛ ti a lu, ti bugum ka dita mam?

ref: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
text: Mam han wan ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ, mam ŋmɛ'ɛri mɛ la yele sɛ'a en bahɛ ti mam tɔregɔ niira la.

ref: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
text: Se'em en de Yinɛ, la Daana Yezu Sɔ la, la, mi ti mam ka pareni.

ref: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
text: Damasekuse tiŋa puan, Aretase en wa dɛna timbihega nɛŋa daana saŋa la, ba wa bahɛ ti gureba n gura Damasekuse tiŋa nayahi la za'a, ti ba ta'am nyɔkɛ mam.

ref: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
text: Gee ba yuum dikɛ mam iŋɛ la pi'ɔ puan, dikɛ mam doohe yoko pika puan labelɛ la nangoone la bala ti mam ta paaɛ tiŋa piiɛ a nu'uhen.

ref: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
text: La ani suŋa ti tu ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ, gee nyuurɔ ka boi bini tɔ'ɔra. Gee mam wan kelum ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ mɛ la mam en nyɛ sɛla, la Daana la en lilegere sɛla la puan.

ref: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
text: Mam n ŋwana, maahum ti n wa'am ya zi'an na sugum nuuren butã wa. N me kan dɛna zeero bo' ya, se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka boti sɛla en de ya dinɛ, gee n boti la yamam mɛhi. Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi kɔma en de ba bɔ biŋe ba dɔgereba, gee dɔgereba n de ba bɔ biŋe ba kɔma.

ref: As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time.
text: Gee mam mi' ti nɛre-ina taaba wa, a boi la inganɛ puan bii sia puan, mam ka mi'. Yinɛ ma'a n mi'.

ref: But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell.
text: Sɛla en yuum zɛ en zom zi'suŋan ha , ti a ta wum yɛla n dagi ba tɔgɛ bii asaala tɔgera a yele.

ref: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
text: Buraa kana wa zuo ti mam wan ze'ele ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ, gee han dɛna mam mɛŋa ma'a, mam kan ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ, han dagena n ti'iha n tɔregɔ yɛla ma'a.

ref: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
text: Gee mam han bɔta ti n ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ, mam kan dɛna gerego, se'ere en sɔi la, mam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la wan dɛna la yelemɛŋerɛ, tɔgera la yelemɛŋerɛ la. Gee mam zagehɛ mɛ ti n kan ŋmɛ nyu'ɔ, ti se'em da ti'iha mam yele gana en nyɛti sɛla bii en wum se'ere mam puan la.

ref: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
text: A daana han pun biha mam nyɛ lilegere se'ere en boi deyima la. Bala, sɛla n wan gu' mam ti mam kan ba' zɛkera n mɛŋa la dela, gũ'a n dikɛ lu' mam inganɛ puan, dɛna Sagena la bituneŋa, ti a nameha mam, la a gu' mam ti n da ba' zɛkera n mɛŋa.

ref: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
text: Sugum nuuren butã ti mam suhɛ Daana la yele wa zuo ti de bahɛ mam,

ref: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
text: gee Daana la yele mam yeti, “Mam zu'uhegɔ la seke bo' fum, se'ere en sɔi la, paŋa niiri ka mɛŋa la tɔregɔ puan.” Bala, mam wan tum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ mɛ la nyɛɛma n tɔregɔ la puan, bala ti Kiresta paŋa la wan bɔna n puan.

ref: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
text: Bala zuo la, mam nini tigɛ ya la tɔregɔ, tuurɛ, wuŋerɛ, mɛlehegɔ, la nɔyalegerɛ Kiresta zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, sansɛka woo ti tɔregɔ kɛ̃ mam, mam ni nyɛ la paŋa. Pɔɔl ti'ihɛ lageŋɔ la yele

ref: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
text: Mam dela gerego. Yamam n pɛregɛ mam kɛ̃ bini. Yelemɛŋerɛ, yamam n tugum deni sɛba en wan pɛgeri ni mam, se'ere en sɔi la, mam dagi bumbila sadɔɔ seko puan bo' sɛba en zɛkeri ba mɛhi ti bamam dela tuntuneba wa zi'an, la mam pun dagena sɛla wa.

ref: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
text: Daalehum sebo n pa'ali ti nɛra dela Yezu tuntuna yelemɛŋerɛ la daa tum ya puan mɛ la nyɔkɛ n mɛŋa. Nyalema la kirehi la tuunkãra.

ref: You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
text: Mam tum la be'em boni bo' ya lageŋɔ wa gana lageŋɔ seto woo en boi la, han dagena mam n ka dikɛ n mɛŋa ziile ya wa ma'a? Dina wa han dɛna la tuure, yi di ya sugeri bo' mam de zuo.

ref: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
text: Mam wan yuregɛ n lɔgerɔ la pupeelum, gee bahɛ ti ba yuregɛ mam mɛŋa pa'ahɛ bo' ya. Mam han nɔŋɛ ya zo'e ŋwana, yamam dela ya nɔŋɛ mam fii yoi?

ref: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
text: Yi mini ya suŋa, ti mam daa ka dikɛ n mɛŋa ziile ya. Gee la bala za'a, mam wikɛ mɛ, n daa pa'ahɛ zu ya mɛ.

ref: Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you?
text: Mam daa fa' ya mɛ doohe sɛba ti n tum ya zi'an na la puan yoi?

ref: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
text: Mam daa belum la Taitusi ti a wa'am na, gee tum tu suuɔ la pa'ahɛ a puan. Taitusi daa ka fa' ya, bii a daa fa' ya mɛ? Tu daa ka tari tu mɛhi mɛ sia kayima puan? Tu daa ka tari la putɛ'yinɛ?

ref: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
text: Yamam ni tugum ti'iha ti tumam tɔgeri pɔ'ɔra la tu mɛhi wa? Tumam tɔgeri la yelemɛŋerɛ Kiresta puan, Yinɛ nɛŋan. Sɛla woo ti tumam ita, n suurɔ wa, la dela ya zo'ore zuo.

ref: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
text: Dabeem en tari mam, ti n han wa'am na, n kan nyɛ ya ti ya ana wuu mam boti ni ya ana se'em la, ti yamam me kan nyɛ mam me ti n ana wuu yamam n boti se'em la. Dabeem n tari mam ti daanse'ere gaka, nyuure, sunsunyiim nyika, ukerɛ, la gbirege wan bɔna ya puan.

ref: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
text: Dabeem n tari mam ti, n han le wa'am na, mam Yinɛ wa wan bahɛ ti nimbu'ɔ nyɔkɛ mam, ti n kele nintama'aha bo' sɛba zo'e zo'e en daa tum be'em gee ka tee ba dɛgerɔ, ba yaalegɔ la ba nyanɛ tuuma la ti ba ni pun tuna la puan la.

ref: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
text: Nuurɛ butã n ŋwana ti n yɛ'a ya zi'an na wa. “Zuuhego woo suni ti ku tara Aze' nyɛduma bayi' bii batã.”

ref: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
text: N daa pun ka'am sɛba n daa tum be'em la mɛ, la ya za'a la ninmu'urɛ. Gee kelum ka'ana ba la mam n pun ka bɔna la ya wa. Wuu mam daa boi la ya n kinɛ buyi' puan la. N daa yele ya yeti, n han le lebe na, n ka yeti n tara ninkɔrɛ la se'em.

ref: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
text: Yan diŋɛ bɔta sɛla ti la pa'alɛ ti Kiresta tɔgeri dɔla n puan la, a dagi tɔregɔ daana kan ta'am iŋɛ yan en boti se'em, gee a paŋa karegɛ mɛ ya puan.

ref: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
text: Ba daa kpa' en mɛ a tɔregɔ puan, gee a vo'e mɛ Yinɛ paŋa la puan. Tumam dela tɔregɔ a puan, gee tumam han tuna la yamam, tu vɔi mɛ la en, Yinɛ paŋa la puan.

ref: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
text: Magehɛ ya mɛhi bihɛ nyaa ya vɔi sakerɛ la puan. Magehɛ ya mɛhi bihɛ, ya ka baŋɛ ti Kiresta boi ya puan? Han dagena ti ya sirum koŋe la ya magehɛ ya mɛhi bihɛ.

ref: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
text: Mam puti'irɛ dela, ya wan baŋɛ ti tu ka koŋe.

ref: We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
text: Gee tu pu'uhi Yinɛ ti ya da tum se'ere tuue. Dagi la tu nyaŋɛ magehegɔ la mɛ, gee la dela ya ita sɛla n de ma'a, tu han pun koŋe magehegɔ la mɛ.

ref: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu kan ta'am iŋɛ sɛla tuue yelemɛŋerɛ la, han dagena tu tuna yelemɛŋerɛ la ma'a.

ref: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
text: Tumam nyɛɛni mɛ, tu han tara tɔregɔ gee ti yamam kpe'em. Tumam n pu'uhi se'ere dela, ya ze'ele suŋa, ba'ahɛ suŋa suŋa.

ref: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
text: Bala, mam gulehɛ gɔnkuna wa bɔ'ɔra ya ti n ka boi la ya, bala ti n han ta wa'am na, n kan dikɛ sukpe'ene dikɛ pansɛka ti Daana la dikɛ bo' mam ti n mɛ ya la, sagum ya. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ la kã'aŋɔ

ref: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
text: Ba'ahegɔ za'a n suurɔ, nyɛɛni ya. Maahum ya sɛla woo suŋa, kpemehe ya taaba suya, sakerɛ ya bo'ori ya taaba, boni ya la suma'ahum, ti Yinɛ En de nɔŋerɛ la suma'ahum Yinɛ la wan bɔna la ya.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya taaba la tuulum suma'ahum kɔkerɛ.

ref: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele tu Sɔ Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya,

ref: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
text: eŋa se'em en dikɛ a mɛŋa bo' tumam be'em zuo, ti a faaɛ tɔ be'em buuri sankana wa puan bahɛ, doohe tu Yinɛ n de tu Sɔ la sumboolum en de se'em la,

ref: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
text: eŋa se'em daana ti suŋa dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari. Faarɛ yelehum ka le bɔna zi'an

ref: I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message,
text: La iŋɛ mam la pakerɛ la yamam en pilum zageha se'em en yi' ya doohe Kiresta zu'uhegɔ puan la, gee ŋmɛregɛ dɔla sɛla ti ba yi'ira faarɛ yelehum sebo en boi bu tɔka,

ref: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
text: dagi ti faarɛ yelehum bu sebo en boi ha, gee basɛba en boi, tulena ya puti'ira, bɔta ti ba tee Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la.

ref: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
text: Gee la han pun dɛna la tumam, bii Yinɛ suŋera en ze'ele saazuo ha sige na, muulɛ faarɛ yelehum sebo en boi bu tɔka la tumam en muulɛ sebo bo' ya la, ka' be'o sige a daana zuo!

ref: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
text: Ɛ̃ɛ, wuu tumam en pun yele ya ni se'em la, n le yele ya pa'ahɛ yeti, se'em han wa'am na muulɛ faarɛ yelehum sebo n boi bu tɔka la yan pun to'e sebo la, ka' be'o sige a daana zuo!

ref: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
text: Nananawa, mam nyaa eere la saaleba sakɛ bo' mam yoi? Bii n eere la Yinɛ sakɛ bo' mam? Bii n tugum nyaa zigera ti n pee la saaleba puya? Mam han kelum dole ni la n pɛɛra saaleba puya, mam kan deni Kiresta yameŋa. Pɔɔl di buurɔ ti a dela Yinɛ tuntuna

ref: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, n boti ya baŋɛ mɛ, n suurɔ, ti faarɛ yelehum la ti n muulɛ bo' ya wa, ka ze'ele la asaala zi'an na.

ref: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
text: Mam ka to'e bɔ asaala zi'an, bii n zamehɛ bu mɛ, gee mam to'e bu la Yezu Kiresta en niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ mam la zi'an.

ref: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
text: Ya wum ya, gee me ka bu'ura mam en daa vɔi se'em Yuuduma pu'uhegɔ la puan. Mam daa gbiregeri mɛ, mɛleha Yinɛ lageŋɔ la, zigera n sagum kɔ.

ref: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
text: Mam daa ba' tole la nɛŋa Yuuduma pu'uhegɔ la puan n nɛreba tiŋasuka, gana mam la sɛba en de zuo taaba zo'e zo'e. Se'ere en sɔi la, n daa ba' mu'ɛ la nini la n yaabeduma pa'alegɔ la.

ref: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
text: ti a lilege a Dayua la pa'alɛ mam, ti n ta'am muulɛ Kiresta yele bo' tihi. Mam daa ka sɔhɛ bo' asaala sɛka,

ref: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
text: bii n zom Yerusalɛm sɛba en pun dɛna Yezu tuntuneba mam yia la zi'an, gee mam yehe mɛ bilam bilam kiŋɛ Arebiya tinkãtɛ puan, ti pooren ti n lebe Damasekuse tinkãtɛ.

ref: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
text: Yuuma atã en tole, ti n zom Yerusalɛm ti n ta limehe Kaifase guum, gee kɛ̃ la en dabeha pia la anuu.

ref: The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother.
text: Gee n ka nyɛ tuntuna se'em bini, han dagena Yemesi en de Daana suuɔ la.

ref: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
text: Mam guleheri sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya wa, Yinɛ nɛŋan ti n zɛ'a wa, n ka pareni!

ref: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
text: Pooren ti n kiŋɛ Siriya la Kilikiya sɔ'ɔlum puan,

ref: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
text: gee bala ma'a ti n nan dela saana bo' Yudiya lageŋɔ la en boi Kiresta puan la.

ref: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
text: Ba yeen wum la nɛreba en yeti, “Se'em en yuum nameheri tu la, nyaa muuli la sakerɛ se'ere ti a yuum zigera a sagum la.”

ref: And because of me, they praised God.
text: Ti ba pɛgɛ Yinɛ mam zuo.

ref: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
text: Ti yuum pia la anaahi ta tole, ti n le zom Yerusalɛm la Banabase, tara Taitusi pa'ahɛ.

ref: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
text: Mam kiŋɛ la dela n nyɛ la lilegere. Ti mam paaɛ dikɛ sɛla woo biŋe ba nɛŋan doohe faarɛ yelehum la ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la, ti n baŋɛ ti mam tuni sɛla la, gee pun tum sɛla la, kan bahɛ yoo.

ref: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
text: Gee Taitusi se'em en yuum pun bɔna la mam la, ba ka pɛregɛ en ti a ŋma, la en pun dɛna Giriki la.

ref: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
text: Gee doohe la pumpɔreŋɔ sakereba ti nɛreba li'ihɛ tari ba na la, sɛba en iŋɛ bããlam kɛ̃ ti ba bihɛ fai sɛka ti tumam tara Kiresta Yezu puan, la ba ta'am bahɛ ti tu lebege yamehi la,

ref: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
text: tu pun ka nam bo' ba la fii me, ti faarɛ yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ ta'am bɔna la ya saŋa woo.

ref: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
text: Gee la daa dagi bala, nɛheduma wa en nyɛ ti Yinɛ en dikɛ faarɛ yelehum wa bo' ti n muulɛ sɛba en ka ŋma la, wuu Pita ti Yinɛ dikɛ faarɛ yelehum wa bo' en ti a muulɛ bo' sɛba en ŋma la,

ref: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
text: Bala, Yemesi, Kaifase, la Yɔn, sɛba ti ba mina ba ti ba dela tiŋasuka nyuuhe la, en ta baŋɛ zu'uhegɔ seko ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' mam la, ba daa kɔkɛ mam la Banabase mɛ, pa'alɛ ti tu dela buyina tuunɛ la puan. Gee nyaa sakɛ ti tumam kina sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la zi'an, ti bamam me kina Yuuduma la zi'an.

ref: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
text: Ba yele tu la yele yinɛ ma'a, ti tu tɛra naheduma yele, sɛla ti mam pun mu'ɛ nini ti n iŋɛ en bala. Pɔɔl zɛregɛ Pita

ref: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
text: Gee Kaifase en ta wa'am Antiyoke tinkãtɛ na la, mam yuum ze'ele la a nɛŋan zɛregɛ en, se'ere en sɔi la, a tuue mɛ.

ref: He used to eat with Gentile followers of the Lord, until James sent some Jewish followers. Peter was afraid of the Jews and soon stopped eating with Gentiles.
text: Han dagena sansɛka ti nɛreba basɛba ze'ele Yemesi zi'an wa'am na la, Pita ni lagum naaɛ me la sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan wa dita. Gee nɛreba la en paaɛ na la, a fooe a mɛŋa lebe la pooren a ma'a. Se'ere en sɔi la, a zoti la sɛba en ŋma la.

ref: He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled.
text: Ti Yuuduma basɛba me lagum pa'ahɛ a birekɔgenɛ wa puan, ti la bahɛ ti Banabase me lagum pa'ahɛ ba birekɔgenɛ wa puan.

ref: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
text: Gee mam en ta nyɛ ti ba itegɔ la ka dole faarɛ yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti n yele Kaifase ba za'a nɛŋan yeti, “La fum en pun dɛna Yuu nɛra wa, han vɔna wuu se'em en dagi Yuu nɛra, gee ka vɔna wuu se'em en de Yuu nɛra, ŋwani ti fum wan ta'am pɛregɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma ti ba vɔna wuu Yuu nɛreba?” Sakerɛ en bo'ori Yuuduma la sɛba en dagi Yuuduma faarɛ

ref: We are Jews by birth and are not sinners like Gentiles.
text: Tumam mɛhi dela Yuu buuri tu dɔgum puan, gee dagi be'em duma sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma buuri la puan.

ref: But we know that God accepts only those who have faith in Jesus Christ. No one can please God by simply obeying the Law. So we put our faith in Christ Jesus, and God accepted us because of our faith.
text: Gee la bala za'a, tu mi' ti nɛra de ma'a la Yinɛ dagi la en tuni ze'elego la, gee en tari sakerɛ Yezu Kiresta puan. Ti tumam wa'am na nam Yezu Kiresta, ti tu dɛna ma'a la Yinɛ tu sakerɛ Kiresta puan, gee dagi tumam en tuni ze'elego tuuma se'em. Beni zuo, nɛra ka boi n wan ta'am dɛna ma'a la Yinɛ eŋa en tuni ze'elego tuuma la.

ref: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
text: Gee tumam han tiregera ti tu dɛna ma'a la Yinɛ doohe Kiresta puan la han dɛna be'emduma, Kiresta nyaa dela be'emduma yameŋa bii? La kan ta'am dɛna bala!

ref: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
text: Gee mam han le mɛta yele sɛ'a ti mam tabelɛ gbi' bahɛ, n yi pa'alɛ ti n dela be'em ita.

ref: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la puan, mam ki mɛ bo' ze'elego la, ti n ta'am vɔna bo' Yinɛ. Ba kpa' mam mɛ la Kiresta,

ref: I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me.
text: La le dagena mam mɛŋa en vɔi, gee la dela Kiresta en vɔi mam puan. Ti vom sebo ti n vɔna inganɛ puan nananawa, n vɔi mɛ la sakerɛ Yinɛ Dayua la, se'em en nɔŋɛ mam gee dikɛ a mɛŋa kaabɛ bo' mam la puan.

ref: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
text: Mam ka zagehɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra han ka tara tuure doohe ze'elego la puan, Kiresta kum la yi dela yoo.

ref: You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you?
text: Yamam Galatia gɔɔnɔ wa! Ani n yuum ya? Bihɛ ya, dagi ya ninin, la nɛrekuuŋɔ la nini ti ba daa kpa' Yezu Kiresta la?

ref: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
text: Yele yinɛ se'ere ma'a ti mam bɔta ti n baŋɛ ya zi'an dela, Yamam to'e Fa'a Sia la doohe la yan tuni ze'elego la yɛla se'em bii, yan nam sɛla ti ya wum la?

ref: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
text: Yamam ba' gaam la bala? Yamam en pɔhɛ la Fa'a Sia la, ya nyaa ba'aheri mɛ la inganɛ?

ref: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
text: Yamam en nyɛ yɛla wa zo'e se'em wa dela yoo? Wuu la han sirum dɛna la yoo.

ref: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
text: La yi han dɛna bala, Yinɛ bo' ya Fa'a Sia la, gee tum kirehi ya puan la doohe la ze'elego la tuuma bii yan nam sɛla ti ya wum la zuo?

ref: The Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith.
text: Wuu Aberaham yuum nam, “Yinɛ, ti Yinɛ bo' en tuntɔɔnɛ,”

ref: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
text: bala ya nyɛ ya, sɛba en nam la en de Aberaham yaahi.

ref: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
text: Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la en pun bihɛ nyɛ ti, Yinɛ wan dikɛ sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la naaɛ la a mɛŋa ba sakerɛ zuo la, a te'ele muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa bo' Aberaham mɛ yeti, “Sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la woo wan nyɛ sum la fum puan.”

ref: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
text: Dina wa zuo, sɛba en nam la nyɛti sum mɛ la Aberaham se'em en daa nam la.

ref: Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.”
text: Gee sɛba woo en dikɛ ba mɛhi delum ze'elego tuuma la, nɔpɔtɛ en dole ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ biŋe yeti, “Nɔpɔtɛ en dole se'em woo en ka dole gee ka nana sɛla woo n gulehɛ biŋe ze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan la.”

ref: No one can please God by obeying the Law. The Scriptures also say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.”
text: Nananawa, la kɔ'ɔm niiɛ mɛ gã'a ti nɛra ka boi ka tara tuure Yinɛ nɛŋan ze'elego la dɔlega puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, “Se'em en de tuntɔɔdaana, wan vɔna la sakerɛ puan.”

ref: The Law isn't based on faith. It promises life only to people who obey its commands.
text: Gee ze'elego la ka yagɛ sakerɛ zuo. Gee lan tugum dɛna se'em la dela, “Fum se'em woo en dole ze'elego la tuuma la wan vɔna mɛ la yele aŋa.”

ref: But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse.
text: Kiresta yɔ to'e tɔ mɛ ze'elego pɔtɛ la puan bahɛ, doohe eŋa ti nɔpɔtɛ dɔla en tumam zuo la. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ biŋe yeti, “Nɔpɔtɛ en dole se'em woo en yule tia zuo.”

ref: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
text: La iŋɛ ŋwana ti Yezu Kiresta puan, Aberaham sum la wan paaɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la, ti tumam me to'e Fa'a Sia nɔbiŋere la sakerɛ puan. Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ la Aberaham

ref: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
text: N suurɔ wa, n bo'ori ya la vom puan makerɛ. Nɛra han diŋɛ biŋe a nuurɛ, se'em ka pa'aheri se'ere bii zageha dɛ.

ref: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
text: Nananawa, Yinɛ yuum biŋe nɔya la bo' la Aberaham la a yaaŋa. La ka yeti, “La a Yaahi” wuu ba zo'e mɛ. Gee la yeti, “La fum yaaŋa,” de vuurɛ dela, nɛra ayima, se'em en de Kiresta la.

ref: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ bo'olum la han ze'eti ni la ze'elego la puan na, la yi' ka le ze'ele nɔbiŋere la puan. Gee Yinɛ dikɛ bu bo' la Aberaham doohe nɔbiŋere la puan. Ze'elego la vuurɛ

ref: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
text: Ŋwani ti ze'elego la yi' bɔna? Yinɛ dikɛ ku pa'ahɛ la tu be'em zuo, gura ti Aberaham yaaŋa la ta wa'am na, se'em ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ la en la. Ku me ze'ele la suŋereba la zi'an doohe nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra la puan.

ref: There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him. Slaves and Children
text: Nananawa, nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra gani nɛreyinɛ mɛ, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa dela ayima.

ref: Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law.
text: Ze'elego la yi' ka sakɛ bo' Yinɛ nɔbiŋere la bii? La kan dɛna bala! Se'ere en sɔi la, ba han bo' ni ze'elego seko en wan bo' ni vom, tuntɔɔnɛ yi' wan ze'ele la ze'elego la puan na.

ref: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
text: Gee Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la yeti, sɛla woo boi la be'em paŋa la puan, ti sɛla ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ doohe sakerɛ n boi Yezu Kiresta puan la, wan dikɛ bo' la sɛba n nam la.

ref: The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith.
text: Nananawa, sakerɛ saŋa n daa nan ka paaɛ la, ze'elego la daa pagɛ tu mɛ, gee gura tɔ, tara tara ti sakerɛ saŋa la nyaa ta paaɛ.

ref: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
text: Bala, ze'elego la en daa de se'em en guri tu, tara tara ti Kiresta ta wa'am na, ti tu nyaa naaɛ la Yinɛ dɔla tu sakerɛ puan.

ref: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
text: Gee nananawa ti sakerɛ wa'am na la, tu ka le bɔna se'em en ni guri tu la nu'uhen,

ref: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yezu Kiresta puan, ya za'a dela Yinɛ kɔma doohe sakerɛ puan.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
text: Beni zuo, wuu ya zo'e zo'e en to'e ko'om sua Kiresta puan la, ya pi' ya mɛhi mɛ la Kiresta.

ref: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
text: Dinɛ zuo, Yuuduma buuri bii Giriki buuri ka le bɔna, da'aberɛ bii fai daana ka le bɔna, buraa bii pɔka ka le bɔna. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya za'a dela buyina Kiresta Yezu puan.

ref: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
text: Ti fum se'em n de Kiresta dinɛ, fum yi dela Aberaham yaaŋa, dɛna asooe sɔna daana wuu nɔbiŋere la en de se'em la.

ref: Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs.
text: Mam n yeti se'ere n ŋwana, asooe sɔna duma la han nan dɛna kɔma, ba ka nyanɛ yamehi, la bamam pun sɔna lɔgerɔ la za'a la.

ref: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
text: Ba kelum bɔna la bihereba la pa'aleba nu'uhen, gura daan se'ere ti Sɔ la looe la ta paaɛ.

ref: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
text: La ani la bala mɛa bo' tumam. Tumam en daa nan de kɔma la, tu daa dela da'aba bo' sɛɛhi se'ehi en sɔi timpaberɛ wa zuo wa.

ref: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
text: Gee Yinɛ saŋa la en daa ta pirɛ paaɛ la, Yinɛ tum la a Dayua, ti pɔka dɔgɛ ka, dɔgɛ ka doohe ze'elego la,

ref: so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children.
text: ti a ta'am yɔ to'e sɛba en boi ze'elego la puan bahɛ, ti Yinɛ ta'am to'e tu ti tu dɛna a kɔma.

ref: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
text: Tumam en de a kɔma la zuo, ti Yinɛ tum a Dayua Sia la tu suya puan na, ti tu tana kaahɛ yeti, “Aba! N Sɔ!”

ref: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
text: Bala, fum le dagena da'aberɛ, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa bia. Ti fum han dɛna la a bia, fum dela la asooe sɔna daana Yinɛ puan. Pɔɔl fabele Galatiyaduma yele

ref: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
text: Wunse'eren ha la, yan daa nan ka mi' Yinɛ la, ya yuum dela da'aba bo' nɛreba en dagi yina.

ref: But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers?
text: Gee Nananawa, ti ya nyaa wa'am na baŋɛ Yinɛ la, bii n yeti, Yinɛ n baŋɛ ya la, ŋwani ti yamam wan le ŋmɛregɛ dɔla tɔregɔ la, nɔŋɔ sɛɛhi se'ehi en sɔi timpaberɛ wa zuo wa? Ŋwani ti ya le bɔta ti ya dɛna da'aba bo' ba le yɛ'ɛha?

ref: You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years.
text: Ya na'aheri la dabeha asɛ'a, ŋmarehi sise'ehi, sahi sise'ehi, la yuuma a sɛ'a.

ref: I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
text: Dabeem en tari mam, ti mam tum sɛla bo' ya la wan bahɛ yoo.

ref: My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me
text: N suurɔ, n beleni ya mɛ, ani ya wuu mam la, se'ere en sɔi la, mam ani mɛ wuu yamam la. Ya ka iŋɛ se'ere se'ere tuue mam.

ref: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
text: Ya mi' ti n daa ka paaɛ n mɛŋa mam daa muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' ya yia la.

ref: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
text: La mam bã'a la en daa makɛ ya bihɛ la, ya daa ka keheri mam, bii ya zagehɛ mam. Gee ya daa to'e mam mɛ wuu Yinɛ suŋera la, to'e mam wuu Yezu Kiresta la.

ref: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
text: Beni en iŋɛ ya putɛ'sunɛ la ti ya daa tara la? Se'ere en sɔi la, mam yeti mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti la han wan ta'am ni iŋɛ ni, ya wan kpigɛ ni ya nini bo' ni mam.

ref: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
text: Mam nyaa lebege la ya dima la mam yele ya yelemɛŋerɛ wa?

ref: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
text: Ba yeen ita mɛ gee ba tari la puti'irɛ se'ere ha. Ba boti ba yelege ya mɛ, ti ya bɔna la ba.

ref: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
text: La ani suŋa ti ya nɔŋɛ mam la putɛ'sunɛ saŋa woo, gee dagi sansɛka ti n bɔna la ya ma'a.

ref: My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you.
text: N kɔmbibehi wa, yamam sɛba zuo ti n le bɔna wuŋerɛ puan wuu n viregere mɛ la, gura ti Kiresta naanɛ ta to'e ya wa.

ref: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
text: Mam boti ni ti n boni mɛ la ya nananawa, gee tee ni n kua, se'ere en sɔi la, mam maam mɛ la ya. Hega la Saara makerɛ

ref: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
text: Yele ya mam, yamam sɛba en boti ya dɛna ze'elego la da'aba wa, dagi ze'elego la ti ya wan kɛleha?

ref: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ biŋe yeti, Aberaham yuum tari la dayɔɔhi siyi, ti kayima yuum dɛna a pɔge da'aberɛ la bia, ti kayima la me dɛna a mɛŋa pɔga, en dagi da'aberɛ la bia.

ref: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
text: Kayima la, se'em en de da'aberɛ la bia la, ba dɔgɛ kaŋa doohe la inganɛ puan, gee kayima la me a mɛŋa pɔga la n dagi da'aberɛ la bia la, ba dɔgɛ kaŋa doohe la nɔbiŋere puan.

ref: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
text: Yele ana wa dela makerɛ. Pɔgesina wa ze'ele bo' la nɔpu'ulegɔ buyi'. Pɔka kayima, yelemɛŋerɛ, dela Hega, ze'ele Senai Zoore la zuo, dɔgera kɔma en de da'aba.

ref: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
text: Hega dela Senai Zoore en boi Arebiya timpaberɛ. La ze'ele bo' la zina Yerusalɛm wa, Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔka wa boi la da'aberɛ puan la ka kɔma.

ref: But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave.
text: Gee pɔka kayima la me ze'ele bo' la Yerusalɛm sɛka en boi saazuo ha la. A dagi da'aberɛ gee me dɛna tu ma.

ref: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, “Nyɛɛna, kundɔgerɛ wa, fum se'em en ka dɔgɛ kɔma wa, kɛ̃ baaŋa puan la fum voole, fum se'em en ka tabelɛ nyɛ dɔgum too wa. Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔgesɛka en de ze'kpaahenɛ la kɔma zo'e mɛ gana pɔgesɛka en ele la kɔma.”

ref: My friends, you were born because of this promise, just as Isaac was.
text: Nananawa, yamam n suurɔ wa, ya dela nɔbiŋere la kɔma, wuu Aziki la.

ref: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
text: Gee wuu bisɛka ti ba dɔgɛ doohe inganɛ puan la, en yuum mɛlehɛ bisɛka ti ba dɔgɛ doohe Sia puan la se'em la, la dela bala mɛa nananawa.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
text: Gee Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la puan yeti bu? La yeti, “Dige ya da'aberɛ la, la ka bia la ti ba yehe. Se'ere en sɔi la, da'aberɛ la bia la kan lagum tɔrɛ lɔgerɔ la, la pɔgesɛka en de a mɛŋa pɔga la en dagi da'aberɛ la bia.”

ref: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
text: Bala, n suurɔ, tumam dela kɔma, dagena pɔgesɛka n de da'aberɛ la kɔma, gee tu dela pɔgesɛka en dagi da'aberɛ la kɔma.

ref: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
text: Han dɛna fai, Kiresta en faaɛ tu bahɛ fai. Ze' ya kankaŋi bala zuo la, gee da le dikɛ ya mɛhi bo' da'aberɛ yɔgehega la. Kiresta kɔma fai naanɛ

ref: I, Paul, promise you that Christ won't do you any good if you get circumcised.
text: Kelehe ya! Mam Pɔɔl yeti ya mɛ ti, ya han bahɛ ti ba ŋma ya, Kiresta yi kan tari nyuurɔ bo' ya.

ref: And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Yamam sɛba n boti ya naaɛ la doohe ze'elego puan la, ŋmaaɛ ya mɛhi mɛ Kiresta zi'an bahɛ. Ya boi la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la yiŋa.

ref: But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ.
text: Gee Fa'a Sia la, la sakerɛ puan, tumam mu'ɛ la nini gura beere tuntɔɔnɛ.

ref: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
text: Beni zuo, Kiresta puan, fum ŋma ya yoo, fum ka ŋma yoo, la ka tari sɛla tɔ'ɔra. Yele se'ere ma'a en tari sɛla tɔ'ɔra dela, sakerɛ en dole nɔŋerɛ puan.

ref: You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth.
text: Ya daa zoti la suŋa suŋa. Ani en gu' ya ti ya da nam yelemɛŋerɛ la?

ref: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
text: Pã'ahegɔ kuna taaba ka ze'ele Yinɛ se'ere n yi' ya la zi'an na.

ref: A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough,
text: Dabelɛ fii wa n ihegere tizom za'a.

ref: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
text: Mam mi' ya suŋa suŋa Daana la puan ti ya ka ti'ihɛ buyi' buyi'. Gee se'em daana en tuleni ya puti'ira la wan nyɛ a namehegɔ paaɛ paaɛ la en pun dɛna se'em me.

ref: My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised.
text: Gee n suurɔ, wa, ŋwani ti ba kelum nameha mam, mam han kelum muulɛ ni ŋmaa yɛla? La han de ni la bala, Kiresta kum pumpuuredɔɔ zuo la muulegɔ la kan tara ni daaŋɔ.

ref: I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
text: Mam boti ni ti nɛresɛba en pɛregeri ya wa la, ba pun kɔ'ɔm lage ni ba mɛhi!

ref: My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love.
text: Ba yi' ya wa'am la fai puan na, n suurɔ, gee da dikɛ ya fai kana wa galena ya mɛhi. Gee tugum doohe ya nɔŋerɛ puan, tuna bɔ'ɔra taaba suŋa suŋa.

ref: All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la za'a ninmu'urɛ dela “Fum Nɔŋɛ fum suuɔ wuu fum mɛŋa la.”

ref: But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves. God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
text: Gee la han dɛna ti ya duni la taaba, gee yi'ira taaba, mini ya suŋa ti la kan yuue la yan n ba'ahɛ taaba lipi. Ingɔŋɔ tuuma

ref: If you are guided by the Spirit, you won't obey your selfish desires.
text: Mam yeti ya yeti, voni ya Sia la n boti se'em la, gee sore da bɔna la fii me ti ya tigehɛ ya ingɔŋɔ kɔrɛ la.

ref: The Spirit and your desires are enemies of each other. They are always fighting each other and keeping you from doing what you feel you should.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ingɔŋɔ la n boti sɛla la, dagi sɛla ti Sia la bɔta. Sia la me en boti sɛla la dagi sɛla ti ingɔŋɔ la bɔta. Bana bayi' wa ka sakerɛ bɔ'ɔra taaba, ti la gu' ti fum da ta'am ita fum boti se'em.

ref: But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
text: Gee Sia la han pa'ala ya, ya dagi yamehi bo' ze'elego la.

ref: People's desires make them give in to immoral ways, filthy thoughts, and shameful deeds.
text: Ingɔŋɔ tuuma la ka sugɛ. Ba dela, yaalegɔ, dɛgerɔ, inganɛ kɔrɛ,

ref: envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
text: nintɛrega, buka, punya'anɛ, la yelesɛ'a n ani ŋwana. N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ wuu mam pun yele ni ya la ninmu'urɛ yia se'em la, ti sɛba en iti yele ana taaba kan lagum kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la. Sia walɛ

ref: God's Spirit makes us loving, happy, peaceful, patient, kind, good, faithful,
text: Gee Sia la walɛ dela, nɔŋerɛ, nyɛɛma, suma'ahum, ŋmibega, pupɛɛla itesuŋɔ, la sakerɛ,

ref: gentle, and self-controlled. There is no law against behaving in any of these ways.
text: zulaam, la nyɔkɛ n mɛŋa. Ze'elego ka boi gana yele ana.

ref: And because we belong to Christ Jesus, we have killed our selfish feelings and desires.
text: Sɛba n de Kiresta Yezu deni la, kpa' ba inganɛ la mɛ, la de kunkoma'ahum la de kɔrɛ la.

ref: God's Spirit has given us life, and so we should follow the Spirit.
text: Tu han vɔna la Sia la vom, bahɛ ya ti Sia la me pa'alɛ tɔ.

ref: But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti tu dɛna pumpã'aheduma, pagera taaba, tara nintɛrega la taaba.

ref: My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself.
text: N suurɔ wa, ya han ta baŋɛ ti be'em sebo en nyɔkɛ ya se'em, yamam sɛba en boi Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan la, malum sɛ en ze'ele, gee iŋɛ ya dɛ la sigehe n mɛŋa. Gee bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa ti yamam me da ta lu pa'ahegɔ be'o puan.

ref: You obey the law of Christ when you offer each other a helping hand.
text: Ziiri ya taaba zeero, ŋwana ti Kiresta ze'elego la wan wa'am asira na.

ref: If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong.
text: Sɛba en dagi sɛsɛla la han ti'iha ti ba dela sɛla, ba pã'aheri la ba mɛhi.

ref: Do your own work well, and then you will have something to be proud of. But don't compare yourself with others.
text: Nɛra woo makɛ a mɛŋa tuuma bihɛ, gee dagi ayima tuuma. Bala en wan bahɛ ti fum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ.

ref: We each must carry our own load.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra woo ziira la a mɛŋa zeero.

ref: Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
text: Sɛba ti ba pa'alɛ ya yelehum la dela ya tɔrɛ sɛla woo en ani suŋa la se'em en pa'alɛ ya la.

ref: You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant.
text: Da pã'aha ya mɛhi, nɛra kan pim Yinɛ. Nɛra en burɛ sɛla la, bala mɛa ti a kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra.

ref: If you follow your selfish desires, you will harvest destruction, but if you follow the Spirit, you will harvest eternal life.
text: Fum han burɛ la ingɔŋɔ yɛla, fum kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la ingɔŋɔ sageŋɔ yɛla. Gee fum han burɛ la Sia puan yɛla, fum kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la vom kamba'ahɛ Sia la puan.

ref: Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up.
text: Bala, da bahɛ ya ti tu taregɛ la tu ita sɛla en de ma'a. Se'ere sɔi la, tu wan ta kɛ̃', kɛ̃'a saŋa la han paaɛ, ti tu ka bahɛ tu ita sɛla en de ma'a la.

ref: We should help people whenever we can, especially if they are followers of the Lord. Final Warnings
text: Bala zuo, sansɛka woo puan, bahɛ ya ti tu ita sɛla en ani suŋa bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo, yele yele wuu sɛba en de yire puan, Kiresta sakerɛ la puan. Ba'ahegɔ kã'aŋɔ, la ka'a

ref: You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
text: Bihɛ ya gɔnɔ kãra seto ti n ni gulehɛ la n mɛŋa nu'o bɔ'ɔra ya na!

ref: Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ.
text: La dela sɛba en boti ba ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la ba ingɔŋɔ yɛla la, n zigeri ti ba pɛregɛ ya ti ya ŋma la. Ba iti ŋwana ti ba da nyɛ namehegɔ Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ la yɛla muulegɔ puan.

ref: They are circumcised, but they don't obey the Law of Moses. All they want is to brag about having you circumcised.
text: Sɛba en pun ŋma la mɛhi ka dole ze'elego la, gee ba boti ya ŋma mɛ ti ba ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ la ya ingɔŋɔ la.

ref: But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
text: Mam da makɛ ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ yele se'ere puan, han dagena tu Daana Yezu Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo ma'a, yele se'ere zuo ti ba kpa' timpaberɛ wa labelɛ mam, gee kpa' mam labelɛ timpaberɛ wa la.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are circumcised or not. All that matters is that you are a new person.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ŋmaa, la a ka ŋma, dagi sɛsɛla, gee dɔgum paalemɔ en de sɛla woo!

ref: If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
text: Gee sɛba en wan dɔla pa'ali kuna wa la, suma'ahum, la a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bɔna la ba, gee bɔna la Yinɛ Izirale paalega la.

ref: On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
text: Yehe zina yɛ'ɛha, se'em da ɛ daaŋɔ bo' mam. Se'ere en sɔi la,Yezu yiha en bala to'e mam iŋa wa za'a wa.

ref: My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
text: Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ya sia puan, n suurɔ wa. Taari.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. To God's people who live in Ephesus and are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
text: Mam Pɔɔl, en de Kiresta Yezu tuntuna,Yinɛ sumboolum zuo la. N gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra la nɛresunduma en boi Ɛfisu, dɛna sakerɛ duma Kiresta Yezu puan la.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
text: Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum en ze'ele tu Sɔ Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya. Sia sum sebo en boi Kiresta puan

ref: Praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ for the spiritual blessings that Christ has brought us from heaven!
text: Sum dɛna Yinɛ, se'em en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la dinɛ. Se'em en bo' tu sum Kiresta puan, Sia puan sum sebo woo en boi tinsuŋan ha la,

ref: Before the world was created, God had Christ choose us to live with him and to be his holy and innocent and loving people.
text: wuu en looe tu se'em Kiresta puan gee nyaa baŋɛ naam timpaberɛ wa la, ti tu dɛna tuulum nɛreba, ka tara tuure a nɛŋan, a nɔŋerɛ la puan la.

ref: God was kind and decided that Christ would choose us to be God's own adopted children.
text: A daa pun maahum tu mɛ ti a to'e tu biha, ti tu dɛna a kɔma Yezu Kiresta puan, wuu sum, la boolum sebo en boi a sumboolum puan la,

ref: God treated us with undeserved grace because of the Son he dearly loves, and so we should praise God.
text: ti pɛka paaɛ a suŋa zu'uhegɔ la ti a ka'ɛ saarum tu zuo na yoo a dayɔba'arɛ la puan la.

ref: Christ sacrificed his life's blood to set us free, which means our sins are now forgiven. Christ did this because of God's gift of undeserved grace to us. God has great wisdom and understanding,
text: ti a ka'ɛ bu zo'e zo'e bahɛ tu zuo na la. Bo tari la yɛm la mi'ilum za'a,

ref: and by what Christ has done, God has shown us his own mysterious ways.
text: ti a iŋɛ tɔ ti tu baŋɛ a sumboolum sugelum yele, wuu sum la boolum sebo ti a pun maahum biŋe Kiresta puan

ref: Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
text: ti la dɛna sɛla en maahum gura saŋa la ta paaɛ, ti a kã'ahɛ sɛla woo bo' a mɛŋa. Sɛla en boi saazuo, la sɛla en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, ti iŋɛ Yezu dɛna nɛŋa daana.

ref: God always does what he plans, and this is why he appointed Christ to choose us.
text: Kiresta puan, tu tari tɔtɛ mɛ, ban pun maahum tɔ bo' eŋa se'em daana en ba'aheri sɛla woo a mɛŋa kã'aŋɔ la a sumboolum puan la.

ref: He did this so we Jews would bring honor to him and be the first ones to have hope because of him.
text: Bala ti tumam sɛba en de yia duma dikɛ tu puti'irɛ delum Kiresta la, wan vɔna pɛgera a suŋa la.

ref: Christ also brought you the truth, which is the good news about how you can be saved. You put your faith in Christ and were given the promised Holy Spirit to show you belong to God.
text: Eŋa puan ti yamam me en wum yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum la en de ya faarɛ yelehum la, me daa nam en, ti ba di'e ya la daalehum sebo en de Fa'a Sia la ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ ti a wan bo' la.

ref: The Spirit also makes us sure we will be given what God has stored up for his people. Then we will be set free, and God will be honored and praised. Paul's Prayer
text: Yele dina en de daalehum, pa'alɛ ti tu tari tɔtɛ en yɔ to'e tu ti tu dɛna Yinɛ mɛŋa nɛreba la dɛna pɛka bo' a suŋa la. Pɔɔl pu'uhegɔ

ref: I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people.
text: N wum yan tari sakerɛ Daana Yezu puan se'em, la yan tari nɔŋerɛ se'em bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la za'a. Ŋwana zuo,

ref: So I never stop being grateful for you, as I mention you in my prayers.
text: mam ka malum bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ fara ya zuo gɔ'ɔra, mam tɛri ya yele mɛ n pu'uhegɔ puan.

ref: I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God.
text: N pu'uheri mɛ ti tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Yinɛ la, se'em en de zu'uhegɔ za'a Sɔ la, wan bo' ya a yɛm, la lilegere Sia, yan wa'am na ti ya baŋɛ en wa.

ref: My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
text: Bala ti ya suure wan yu'ɛ, ti ya nyɛ niihum la, la ya baŋɛ beere puti'irɛ se'ere zuo ti a yi' ya na la, la tarega sɛka en boi suŋa tarega la puan bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la,

ref: I want you to know about the great and mighty power that God has for us followers. It is the same wonderful power he used
text: la a pankãtɛ sɛka en ka tari makerɛ bɔ'ɔra tumam sɛba en nam en, a pankãtɛ tuuma la puan la.

ref: when he raised Christ from death and let him sit at his right side in heaven.
text: Yinɛ dikɛ pankana wa tum la Kiresta puan, en vo'e en a kum la puan, tari en ta zi'ire a zuɔ, tinsuŋan ha la,

ref: There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well.
text: ti a gana bihereba za'a, kpe'eŋo la pahi za'a, la na'am woo n boi, gee gana yu'ura woo en tabelɛ sigɛ, dagi la zina buuri saŋa wa puan ma'a, gee buuri saŋa se'ehi woo en kini na la.

ref: God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything.
text: Yinɛ dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ la a nu'uhen, gee bahɛ en ti a dɛna zuodaana sɔna sɛla woo en de lageŋɔ la dinɛ.

ref: The church is the body of Christ and is filled with Christ who completely fills everything.
text: Lageŋɔ la dela Kiresta iŋa, kɔ'ɔm pirɛ mɛ la eŋa se'em daana en boi sɛla woo puan la.

ref: In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God.
text: Ya yuum ki mɛ ya tuure la ya be'em la puan,

ref: You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God.
text: ti ya yuum bɔna bu puan, dɔla timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla la, dɔla se'em daana paŋa n boi kusebego wa puan la. Sɛsɛka n boi nananawa tuna la sɛba n ka nane Yinɛ nuurɛ la.

ref: Once we were also ruled by the selfish desires of our bodies and minds. We had made God angry, and we were going to be punished like everyone else.
text: Tu za'a yuum nan vɔi mɛ la ba, dɔla tu inganɛ kɔrɛ, la tu mɛhi puti'ira. Tu yuum dela nɛresɛba ti Yinɛ sunsunyiim dɔla tɔ, wuu kɔma woo en ani se'em la.

ref: But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you.
text: la tumam n daa pun ki tu be'em la zuo la, Yinɛ bahɛ ti tu vo'e mɛ la Kiresta. Zu'uhegɔ zuo ti ya nyɛ faarɛ.

ref: God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven.
text: Yinɛ vo'e tu mɛ la Kiresta, gee bahɛ ti tu zi'ire la en saazuo ha, Kiresta Yezu zuo.

ref: God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: Bala ti saŋa la han ta paaɛ, a wan pa'alɛ a zu'uhegɔ la n ka tari makerɛ la, la a sum sebo ti a tara bɔ'ɔra tɔ Kiresta Yezu puan la.

ref: You were saved by faith in God, who treats us much better than we deserve. This is God's gift to you, and not anything you have done on your own.
text: La dela zu'uhegɔ zuo ti ya nyɛ faarɛ, doohe sakerɛ puan. Dina dagi ya mɛhi en iŋɛ sɛla, gee la dela Yinɛ bo'olum.

ref: It isn't something you have earned, so there is nothing you can brag about.
text: La dagi ya tum mɛ nyɛ, ti se'em wan ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ.

ref: God planned for us to do good things and to live as he has always wanted us to live. This is why he sent Christ to make us what we are. United by Christ
text: Tumam dela sɛba ti a iŋɛ tɔ ti tu dɛna la. A naam tu la Kiresta Yezu puan, ti tu tuna tunsuma sɛ'a ti Yinɛ pun maahum a ti a dɛna tu vom sore la. To dela buyina Kiresta puan

ref: Don't forget that you are Gentiles. In fact, you used to be called “uncircumcised” by those who take pride in being circumcised.
text: Bala zuo la, tɛri ya ti saŋa kayima ha, yamam sɛba n dagi Yuuduma buuri ya dɔgum puan na la, sɛba n ŋma la yi'ira ya la “Sɛba n ka ŋma.” Ŋmaa kana wa dela ingɔŋɔ ŋmaa, ti nɛreba nu'uhi ŋmata.

ref: At that time you did not know about Christ. You were foreigners to the people of Israel, and you had no part in the promises God had made to them. You were living in this world without hope and without God,
text: Tɛri ya ti sankaŋa puan la, ya yuum ka mi' Kiresta, dɛna zanku'uma Izirale timpaberɛ, dɛna saama bo' nɔpu'ulegɔ nɔbiŋere la, ka tara beere puti'irɛ, gee me ka mina Yinɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo.

ref: and you were far from God. But Christ offered his life's blood as a sacrifice and brought you near God.
text: Gee nananawa, Yezu Kiresta zuo, yamam sɛba ti saŋa yuum bɔna ti ya zaaɛ zaaɛ ha la, ba nyaa tari ya lɛm me na, Kiresta ziim la zuo.

ref: Christ has made peace between Jews and Gentiles, and he has united us by breaking down the wall of hatred that separated us. Christ gave his own body
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa mɛŋa n de tu suma'ahum. A inganɛ puan, a bahɛ ti yelehego buyi' n lebege buyina, gee gbirege nangoone se'ere en beŋere la, diŋo seko n boi tu tiŋasuka la bahɛ.

ref: to destroy the Law of Moses with all its rules and commands. He even brought Jews and Gentiles together as though we were only one person, when he united us in peace.
text: A saalum ze'elego la, la ku pa'alegɔ, la ku malema za'a bahɛ mɛ, ti a malum naam asaala paalega bo' a mɛŋa ti a tee ze'ele bo' bayi' la, ti suma'ahum bɔna.
