ref: On the cross Christ did away with our hatred for each other. He also made peace between us and God by uniting Jews and Gentiles in one body.
text: Ti a ta'am bahɛ ti bayi' wa naaɛ taaba bo' Yinɛ, ti ba dɛna iŋa kayima, doohe pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo, nyaa ku diŋo la, doohe a pumpuuredɔɔ la.

ref: Christ came and preached peace to you Gentiles, who were far from God, and peace to us Jews, who were near God.
text: Bala, a wa'am na muulɛ la suma'ahum bo' yamam sɛba n daa zaaɛ zaaɛ ha la, gee bo' yamam sɛba n lɛm la.

ref: And because of Christ, all of us can come to the Father by the same Spirit.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan, ti tu za'a tara Sia kayima dɔla paara Sɔ la zi'an.

ref: You Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens with everyone else who belongs to the family of God.
text: Bala zuo, ya ka le dɛna saama bii zanku'uma, gee ya lagum dela tiŋambiihi la nɛresunduma, gee me dɛna Yinɛ mɛŋa yire nɛreba,

ref: You are like a building with the apostles and prophets as the foundation and with Christ as the most important stone.
text: ti ba mɛ ya dɔgelɛ tuntuneba, la nɔtɔgeheba ɛberɛ la zuo, ti Kiresta Yezu mɛŋa dɛna ɛberɛ kugere la.

ref: Christ is the one who holds the building together and makes it grow into a holy temple for the Lord.
text: Eŋa puan ti yire la za'a naaɛ taaba, lebege Tuulum pu'uhegɔ yire, Daana la puan.

ref: And you are part of that building Christ has built as a place for God's own Spirit to live.
text: Eŋa puan ti yamam me mɛ naaɛ taaba Sia puan, dɛna Yinɛ bɔŋa zi'an.

ref: Christ Jesus made me his prisoner, so I could help you Gentiles.
text: Ŋwana wa zuo ti mam Pɔɔl bɔna paka deen bo' Kiresta Yezu, yamam sɛba n dagi Yuuduma wa zuo.

ref: You have surely heard about God's gift of undeserved grace in choosing me to help you.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya pun wum Yinɛ en doohe a zu'uhegɔ puan dikɛ tuunɛ iŋɛ mam nu'uhi yamam zuo,

ref: In fact, this letter tells you a little about how God has shown me his mysterious ways.
text: la sugelum yele la ti a daa bahɛ ti mam baŋɛ dɛ doohe lilegere puan, mam gulehɛ yelesɛ'a fii wa zuo ha la.

ref: As you read the letter, you will also find out how well I really do understand the mystery about Christ.
text: Ku wan dɛna gɔŋɔ ti ya han kãalɛ, ya wan baŋɛ mam n bɔkɛ Kiresta sugelum yele la se'em.

ref: No one knew about this mystery until God's Spirit told it to his holy apostles and prophets.
text: Buuri sahi se'ehi n tole la, sugelum yele dina wa ka lilege de bo' saaleba wuu dinɛ n nyaa lilege se'em bo' a tuulum tuntuneba, la a nɔtɔgeheba doohe Fa'a Sia puan wa la.

ref: And the mystery is this: Because of Christ Jesus, the good news has given the Gentiles a share in the promises God gave to the Jews. God has also let the Gentiles be part of the same body.
text: De vuurɛ dela, sɛba n dagi Yuuduma wa nyaa lagum dɛna la sɛba en tari tɔtɛ, lagum dɛna iŋa kayima la nɛresɛba n wan lagum to'e nɔbiŋere lɔkɔ la Kiresta Yezu puan, faarɛ yelehum la zuo.

ref: God treated me with kindness. His power worked in me, and it became my job to spread the good news.
text: Faarɛ yelehum buna zuo, ti mam lebege yameŋa, Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ bo'olum la ti a dikɛ bo' mam doohe a paŋa tuuma la puan.

ref: I am the least important of all God's people. But God was kind and chose me to tell the Gentiles that because of Christ there are blessings that cannot be measured.
text: Gee la mam n pun pɔ'ɛ nɛresunduma la za'a puan la, Yinɛ dikɛ la zu'uhegɔ kuna bo' mam, ti n tari Kiresta tarega la n ka tari makerɛ la faarɛ yelesuma la na bo' sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la,

ref: God, who created everything, wanted me to help everyone understand the mysterious plan that had always been hidden in his mind.
text: ti nɛra woo baŋɛ putɛ'se'ere n boi sugelum yele wa en sugɛ yuuma yuuma ha, Yinɛ se'ere n naam sɛla woo puan la,

ref: Then God would use the church to show the powers and authorities in the spiritual world that he has many different kinds of wisdom.
text: ti la doohe lageŋɔ la puan, ti Yinɛ yɛm la n ka tari ba'ahegɔ la wan nyaa niiɛ bo' timbihereba, la kpɛ'ɛnduma n boi saazuo ha.

ref: God did this according to his eternal plan. And he was able to do what he had planned because of all Christ Jesus our Lord had done.
text: Ŋwana wa iŋɛ doohe la a putɛ'se'ere n ka tari ba'ahegɔ, ti a tum dɛ tu Daana Kiresta Yezu puan la,

ref: Christ now gives us courage and confidence, so we can come to God by faith.
text: se'em puan ti tu wan paaɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan la sukpe'ene ti dabeem ka bɔna, doohe tumam n sakɛ bo' en se'em.

ref: This is why you should not be discouraged when I suffer for you. After all, it will bring honor to you. Christ's Love for Us
text: Bala zuo, mam pu'uhɛ bɔ'ɔra ya mɛ, ti ya puti'irɛ da kɔ'ɔhɛ mam nameheri se'em ya zuo wa. Ba dela ya sum. Pɔɔl pu'uhɛ bo' kãaleba

ref: I kneel in prayer to the Father.
text: Ŋwana zuo, mam kpa' la duma Sɔ la nɛŋan,

ref: All beings in heaven and on earth receive their life from him.
text: se'em puan ti nɛra woo n boi saazuo ha la timpaberɛ wa zuo tara ba yu'urɛ.

ref: God is wonderful and glorious. I pray that his Spirit will make you become strong followers
text: Mam pu'uhegɔ dela, a wan ze'ele a tarega suŋa la puan, bo' ya sore ti ya tara paŋa ya puan, doohe Sia la puan,

ref: and that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. Stand firm and be deeply rooted in his love.
text: ti Kiresta bɔna ya suya puan, sakerɛ zuo, wuu yan ba' nyɛga gee tara nɔŋerɛ puan se'em la.

ref: I pray that you and all God's people will understand what is called wide or long or high or deep.
text: Mam pu'uhegɔ dela, ya tara paŋa n wan ta'am bɔkera la nɛresunduma la za'a, ya faarɛ la yaleŋa, de girega, de woko la de kɔ'ɔlum,

ref: I want you to know all about Christ's love, although it is too wonderful to be measured. Then your lives will be filled with all that God is.
text: gee me mina Kiresta nɔŋerɛ la n gani mi'ilum woo la, la ya ta'am pirɛ la Yinɛ naanɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: As a prisoner of the Lord, I beg you to live in a way that is worthy of the people God has chosen to be his own.
text: Dinɛ zuo, mam en boi paka deen Daana la tuuma zuo beleni ya mɛ, ti ya vɔna vom sebo en nari la yi'sɛka ti ba yi' ya kɛ̃' ka puan la.

ref: Always be humble and gentle. Patiently put up with each other and love each other.
text: Voni ya ya vom za'a la sirege n mɛŋa, zulaam, gee ŋmibera la taaba nɔŋerɛ puan.

ref: Try your best to let God's Spirit keep your hearts united. Do this by living at peace.
text: Kpeŋe ya mɛhi la ya tara Sia la puan nɔyinɛ la suma'ahum sebo en de bu dikɛ ya naaɛ taaba.

ref: All of you are part of the same body. There is only one Spirit of God, just as you were given one hope when you were chosen to be God's people.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Iŋa kayina, la Fa'a Sia kayima ma'a en boi, wuu ban yi' ya beere putɛ'yinɛ wa puan se'em la.

ref: We have only one Lord, one faith, and one baptism.
text: Daana ka ayima, sakerɛ deyina, la ko'om sua buyina.

ref: There is one God who is the Father of all people. Not only is God above all others, but he works by using all of us, and he lives in all of us.
text: Yinɛ deyina, la Sɔ ayima en boi bo' sɛla woo, gana sɛla woo, tuna la sɛla woo, gee bɔna la sɛla woo.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “When he went up to the highest place, he led away many prisoners and gave gifts to people.”
text: Ŋwana zuo ti yelehum la yeti, “En zom saazuo ha la, a nyɔkɛ sɛla en nyɔgere la mɛ a mɛŋa, gee bo' a nɛreba bo'olum.”

ref: This also means that the one who went deep into the earth is the same one who went into the highest heaven, so he would fill the whole universe.
text: Se'em daana n sige na la, eŋa en bala en zom saa zuin zuin ha la, ti a sɔna sɛla woo biha.

ref: Christ chose some of us to be apostles, prophets, missionaries, pastors, and teachers,
text: Bo'olum la ti a bo' la dela, basɛba wan dɛna tuntuneba, ti basɛba dɛna nɔtɔgeheba, ti sɛba dɛna ayehemuulɛduma, ti basɛba dɛna dunkiinduma la pa'aleba,

ref: so his people would learn to serve and his body would grow strong.
text: ti a kpemehe nɛresunduma la ti ba tuna ba tuuma, la ba mɛ lageŋɔ la n de Kiresta iŋa la,

ref: This will continue until we are united by our faith and by our understanding of the Son of God. Then we will be mature, just as Christ is, and we will be completely like him.
text: gura ti tu za'a ta wa'am na naaɛ taaba la sakerɛ deyina, zo'e Yinɛ Dayua la mi'ilum puan, zo'e dɛna bunkureba ti Kiresta naanɛ la to'e tu za'a.

ref: We must stop acting like children. We must not let deceitful people trick us by their false teachings, which are like winds that toss us around from place to place.
text: Tu da le dɛna kɔma le yɛ'ɛha, kaara kina nɛŋa la pooren, ti pa'alegɔ en ani wuu sabeeo ziira ya ɛɛra zi'an woo, pa'ali seko n de nɛreba mɛhi nigeŋɔ la nyaleŋɔ la pã'ahegɔ pã'ahegɔ la.

ref: Love should always make us tell the truth. Then we will grow in every way and be more like Christ, the head
text: Gee tu dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ duma tu vom puan, la nɔŋerɛ, tu dela tu zo'e sɛla woo puan ta paaɛ se'em n de zuodaana, Kiresta la n ani se'em la.

ref: of the body. Christ holds it together and makes all of its parts work perfectly, as it grows and becomes strong because of love. The Old Life and the New Life
text: Eŋa puan ti iŋa la za'a nyɔkɛ taaba suŋa suŋa dɛna inyiŋa doohe la giile woo n tuni de tuunɛ suŋa se'em bahɛ ti inganɛ la zɔ'ɔra doohe la dinɛ n mɛti de mɛŋa nɔŋerɛ puan la. Vom kɛka la vom paalega

ref: Their minds are in the dark, and they are stubborn and ignorant and have missed out on the life that comes from God. They no longer have any feelings about what is right,
text: Lika n pi' ba bɔkerɛ, yehe ba mɛhi Yinɛ vom sum la puan, la ban ka mi' sɛsɛla, gee ti ba suya dagelum la zuo.

ref: and they are so greedy they do all kinds of indecent things.
text: Ba zuto ki mɛ ti ba dikɛ ba mɛhi kɔ'ɔm bo' kunkoma'ahum, be'ero, ita dɛgerɔ tuuma woo.

ref: But this isn't what you were taught about Jesus Christ. He is the truth, and you heard about him and learned about him.
text: La de asira ti ya wum a yele, gee ti ba me pa'alɛ ya a yele ti yelemɛŋerɛ boi la Yezu puan.

ref: You were told that your foolish desires will destroy you and that you must give up your old way of life with all its bad habits.
text: Ba pa'alɛ ya ti ya bahɛ ya vom kɛka sore la, ya naanɛ kɛka la, n de ŋmɛlema naanɛ ti kunkoma'ahum pirɛ dɛ la,

ref: Let the Spirit change your way of thinking
text: gee malum tee ya mɛhi sia puan la ya puti'ira puan.

ref: and make you into a new person. You were created to be like God, and so you must please him and be truly holy. Rules for the New Life
text: Gee yɛ ya mɛhi la vompaalega naanɛ la, naam se'ere n naam ŋwana la Yinɛ mɛŋa la, dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la tuulum la. Vompaalum sore

ref: We are part of the same body. Stop lying and start telling each other the truth.
text: Bala zuo, tumam n baheri pã'ahegɔ vom la, bahɛ ya ti tu za'a tɔgera yelemɛŋerɛ bɔ'ɔra tu suurɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, tu za'a dela iŋa kayima.

ref: Don't get so angry that you sin. Don't go to bed angry
text: Fum suure ta'am ihege gee da tum be'em. Da bahɛ ti yinɛ ma'a la fum sunsunyiihum la,

ref: and don't give the devil a chance.
text: me da bo' Sagena la yɔ'ɔ fum puan.

ref: If you are a thief, quit stealing. Be honest and work hard, so you will have something to give to people in need.
text: Nageyigehi bahɛ zua, gee tugum bahɛ ya ba ti ba tuna la ninmu'urɛ, ba tuna yelemɛŋerɛ la ba mɛhi nu'uhi, la ba ta'am tara, gee me suŋera naheduma.

ref: Stop all your dirty talk. Say the right thing at the right time and help others by what you say.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti yetɔgepu'uha zɛ'ɛta ya nɔya puan yɛhera na. Gee yetɔge sebo en tari vuurɛ gee mɛta ma'a, sansɛka n de ya tɔgɛ. Bala ti yelehum la wan tara zu'uhegɔ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n wum la.

ref: Don't make God's Spirit sad. The Spirit makes you sure that someday you will be free from your sins.
text: Me da dɛgɛ ya Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la suure, sɛ sɛka ti Yinɛ di'e ya, daalɛ ya a mɛŋa daalehum gura faarɛ daarɛ la.

ref: Stop being bitter and angry and mad at others. Don't yell at one another or curse each other or ever be rude.
text: Dina zuo, yehe ya, ya mɛhi yaarɛ woo puan la sunsuntuulɛ la sunsunyiihum, nyabega, yu'sageŋɔ, ta ta' la pɔgeleŋɔ woo.

ref: Instead, be kind and merciful, and forgive others, just as God forgave you because of Christ.
text: Gee iti ya sum bo'ori ya taaba, gee zoti ya taaba nimbu'ɔ, gee dita sugeri bɔ'ɔra taaba wuu Yinɛ n di ya be'em sugeri bo' ya se'em Kiresta zuo la.

ref: Do as God does. After all, you are his dear children.
text: Dina zuo, tɔgehi ya Yinɛ wuu kɔma ti a nini tigɛ la ya la,

ref: Let love be your guide. Christ loved us and offered his life for us as a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: gee vɔni ya la nɔŋerɛ wuu Kiresta n nɔŋɛ tɔ se'em, dikɛ a mɛŋa bo' tu se'em, lebege nyuuŋo suŋɔ kaabegɔ bo'olum ti ba kaabɛ bo' Yinɛ la. Zagehɛ ya baga kaabegɔ sadɔtɔ

ref: You are God's people, so don't let it be said that any of you are immoral or indecent or greedy.
text: Gee yaluntuuma la dɛgerɔ tuuma woo, bii nyika tuuma da pun puta ya puan, wuu la nari se'em nɛresunduma tiŋasuka la.

ref: Don't use dirty or foolish or filthy words. Instead, say how thankful you are.
text: Bahɛ ya ti nyanɛ tugum, gɔɔm tugum la yetɔgum yooro kɔ'ɔm yehe ya zi'an. Gee tugum bahɛ ya ti ya pɛgera Yinɛ.

ref: Being greedy, indecent, or immoral is just another way of worshiping idols. You can be sure people who behave in this way will never be part of the kingdom that belongs to Christ and to God. Living as People of Light
text: Mini ya dina suŋa suŋa, ti se'em n tuni yaluntuuma, bii galeŋɔ daana, bii se'em n bɛ' , kan tara tɔtɛ kɛ̃' Kiresta la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

ref: Don't let anyone trick you with foolish talk. God punishes everyone who disobeys him and says foolish things.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti se'em pã'ahɛ ya la gɔɔm yetɔgum. Se'ere en sɔi la, yele ana zuo ti Yinɛ sunsunyiihum wan sige sɛba n ka nane la zuo.

ref: So don't have anything to do with anyone like that.
text: Dinɛ zuo, da tari ya yelese'ere ita la nɛrebamam taaba.

ref: You used to be like people living in the dark, but now you are people of the light because you belong to the Lord. So act like people of the light
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya daa nan boi la lika puan, gee nananawa Daana la puan, ya dela la niihum. Bala Vɔni ya wuu kɔmsɛba n boi niihum puan la.

ref: and make your light shine. Be good and honest and truthful,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, niihum wa wala boi sɛla woo n ani suŋa, gee dɛna tuuntɔɔ, la yelemɛŋerɛ puan.

ref: as you try to please the Lord.
text: Zigeri ya ti ya ɛ gee zamehɛ sɛla n tigeri Daana la nini.

ref: Don't take part in doing those worthless things that are done in the dark. Instead, show how wrong they are.
text: Da lagum tuna ya lika puan tuunsɛ'a n ka tari walesuma la, gee tugum viie ya ba fihi.

ref: It is disgusting even to talk about what is done in the dark.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la pun dɛna la vi ti fum tɔgera yelesɛ'a ti nɛrebana taaba sugera ita a la yele.

ref: But the light will show what these things are really like.
text: Gee sɛla woo ti bugum niiɛ ze'ele, la ka tari suka.

ref: Light shows up everything, just as the Scriptures say, “Wake up from your sleep and rise from death. Then Christ will shine on you.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla woo n ka sugɛ dela niihum n niiɛ bahɛ la zuo. Bala ti la gulehɛ yeti, “Gɔgihera wa, ihege! Ihege kum la puan, ti Kiresta niiɛ bahɛ fum zuo.”

ref: These are evil times, so make every minute count.
text: Ya tuna tuunsuma saŋa woo puan, se'ere sɔi la, dabeha la dela dabeha tooro.

ref: Don't be stupid. Instead, find out what the Lord wants you to do.
text: Bala, da deni ya geto gee tugum bɔkeri ya Daana la sumboolum n de se'em.

ref: Don't destroy yourself by getting drunk, but let the Spirit fill your life.
text: Da lɛɛra ya mɛhi la daam bugera,bala dela yaalegɔ. Gee tugum pirɛ ya mɛhi la Fa'a Sia la,

ref: When you meet together, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, as you praise the Lord with all your heart.
text: tari yuum suma tɔgera bɔ'ɔra taaba, kpemehego yuuma la na'ahegɔ yuuma, la Sia yuuma sɛ'a n boi ya puan. Yuuni ya la pupeelum ya suya puan bɔ'ɔra Daana la.

ref: Always use the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to thank God the Father for everything. Wives and Husbands
text: Bo'ori ya Yinɛ n de Sɔ la fara saŋa woo puan, la sɛla woo puan tu Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan. Kiresta kɔma yire

ref: A wife should put her husband first, as she does the Lord.
text: Pɔgeba, sigehe ya mɛhi bo' ya sireba wuu yan ani se'em bo' Daana la.

ref: A husband is the head of his wife, as Christ is the head and the Savior of the church, which is his own body.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, asira n de a pɔga zuodaana wuu Kiresta n de lageŋɔ la zuodaana se'em la. Lageŋɔ seko n de a iŋa la, ti a mɛŋa dɛna ka faarɛ la.

ref: Wives should always put their husbands first, as the church puts Christ first.
text: Wuu Lageŋɔ la en boi Kiresta nu'uhen se'em la, bala mɛa ti pɔgehi me wan sigehe ba mɛhi bo' ba Sireba sɛla woo puan.

ref: A husband should love his wife as much as Christ loved the church and gave his life for it.
text: Sireba, nɔŋɛ ya pɔgeba, wuu Kiresta n nɔŋɛ lageŋɔ la se'em, gee dikɛ a mɛŋa vom bo' ku se'em la.

ref: He made the church holy by the power of his word, and he made it pure by washing it with water.
text: A iŋɛ ŋwana ti lageŋɔ la dɛna la tuulum lageŋɔ doohe en duuhe ko'om en de a yelehum la,

ref: Christ did this, so he would have a glorious and holy church, without faults or spots or wrinkles or any other flaws.
text: wuu n boti a dikɛ lageŋɔ la, la ku suŋa la bo' a mɛŋa, ti sɛla da ti'ɛ, bii lirum, bii sɛla kalum kɔ la. Ɛ̃ɛ, ŋwana ti lageŋɔ la wan dɛna tuulum lɔkɔ ka tara dɛkɔ.

ref: In the same way, a husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself. A husband who loves his wife shows he loves himself.
text: Bala mɛa ti sireba tara ba pɔgeba nɔŋerɛ sanɛ wuu ban nɔŋɛ ba mɛhi ihe se'em la. Fum se'em n nɔŋɛ fum pɔga, nɔŋɛ la fum mɛŋa.

ref: None of us hate our own bodies. We provide for them and take good care of them, just as Christ does for the church,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra ka boi yaaɛ a mɛŋa iŋa. Gee a tugum diiha ka mɛ la dinu'uma, gee bihera ka suŋa, wuu Kiresta n iŋɛ se'em bo' lageŋɔ la.

ref: because we are each part of his body.
text: Se'ere zuo, tumam dela a iŋa la luga a sɛ'a.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother to get married, and he becomes like one person with his wife.”
text: “Yele dina wa zuo, buraa wan bahɛ ka Sɔ la ka ma gee dikɛ ka mɛŋa naaɛ buyina la ka pɔga ti ba bayi' la lebege bunyinɛ.”

ref: This is a great mystery, but I understand it to mean Christ and his church.
text: Yele dina wa dela yelekãtɛ se'ere n sugɛ ti mam dikɛ dɛ makera Kiresta la Lageŋɔ la.

ref: So each husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself, and each wife should respect her husband.
text: Nɛra woo, lan pun ana se'em me, dela a nɔŋɛ a pɔga wuu a mɛŋa la, ti pɔga la me dɛna ka na'aha ka asira.

ref: Children, you belong to the Lord, and you do the right thing when you obey your parents. The first commandment with a promise says,
text: Kɔma, nane ya ya dɔgereba, se'ere en de ma'a en bala Daana sore la puan.

ref: and you will have a long and happy life.”
text: “Bala ti la wan ana fum suŋa, gee ti fum nyuura wugɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo.”

ref: Parents, don't be hard on your children. Raise them properly. Teach them and instruct them about the Lord. Slaves and Masters
text: Sɔduma, da daani ya ya kɔma ti ba suure nyiira, gee tugum bihɛ ya ti ba zɔ'ɔra la tube-dɔlegerɛ la Daana la sore puan kã'aŋɔ. Da'aba la ba kpɛ'ɛnduma

ref: Slaves, you must obey your earthly masters. Show them great respect and be as loyal to them as you are to Christ.
text: Da'aba, nane ya ya timpaberɛ wa zuo kpɛ'ɛnduma, la dabeem la gbi'a, la suyinɛ wuu yan nane Kiresta se'em la.

ref: Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. You are slaves of Christ, so with your whole heart you must do what God wants you to do.
text: Da iti ya ŋwana ba nɛŋan ma'a ti ya yeen pee ba suure, gee ya dela Kiresta yamehi, tuna Yinɛ sumboolum la ya suya za'a za'a.

ref: Gladly serve your masters, as though they were the Lord himself, and not simply people.
text: Tuni ya ya tuuma la pupeelum kãtɛ wuu ya tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana la, gee dagi asaala.

ref: You know that you will be rewarded for any good things you do, whether you are slaves or free.
text: Tu mina ti, sum sebo woo ti tu iŋɛ tu wan lebehe to'e la bala mɛa tu Daana zi'an, tu dɛna yamehi bii tu bɔna fai.

ref: Slave owners, you must treat your slaves with this same respect. Don't threaten them. They have the same Master in heaven that you do, and he doesn't have favorites. The Fight against Evil
text: Yamam kpɛ'ɛnduma me, iti ya ya yamehi bala mɛa. Da vuuheri ya ba, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti yamam la bamam za'a tari la Daana ayima ti a bɔna saazuo ha, se'em ti yelehego ka bɔna a puan. Yinɛ zaberɛ lɔgerɔ za'a

ref: Finally, let the mighty strength of the Lord make you strong.
text: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, kpe'em ya mɛ Daana la, la a pankãtɛ la puan.

ref: Put on all the armor that God gives, so you can defend yourself against the devil's tricks.
text: Bobe ya mɛhi la Yinɛ zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la za'a, la ya ta'am ze'ele zabɛ la Sagena la vuuhego la za'a.

ref: So put on all the armor that God gives. Then when that evil day comes, you will be able to defend yourself. And when the battle is over, you will still be standing firm.
text: Bala zuo la, bobe ya mɛhi la Yinɛ zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la za'a, bala ti ya wan ta'am sɛ' ze'ele dabehebe'o la daarɛ kankaŋɛ, dabehebe'o daarɛ la han ta paaɛ ya na. Ti ya me han ta zabɛ paaɛ de ba'ahegɔ, ya wan kelum ta'am sɛ' ze'ele kankaŋɛ.

ref: Be ready! Let the truth be like a belt around your waist, and let God's justice protect you like armor.
text: Bala zuo, sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋɛ. Gee bahɛ ti yelemɛŋerɛ ana wuu sɛganɛ la lu' ya sɛɛhi la, gee me bahɛ ti tuntɔɔnɛ dɛna ya nyu'ɔ tɛ'ɛlega.

ref: Your desire to tell the good news about peace should be like shoes on your feet.
text: Tagera sɛ'a ti ya wan pire ni, pirɛ ya sɛla woo n wan ta'am maahum ya ti ya muulɛ suma'ahum faarɛ yelehum wa.

ref: Let your faith be like a shield, and you will be able to stop all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
text: Lɔge tuna wa za'a la, dikɛ ya sakerɛ ti la dɛna zaba tɛ'ɛlega, ti ya wan ta'am kiihera yabega pɛɛma la woo ti be'emdaana la tɛɛra la.

ref: Let God's saving power be like a helmet, and for a sword use God's message that comes from the Spirit.
text: Tara ya faarɛ ti la dɛna ya zaberɛ zuo wanɛ, la Sia puan su'a la n de Yinɛ yelehum la.

ref: Never stop praying, especially for others. Always pray by the power of the Spirit. Stay alert and keep praying for God's people.
text: Bala pu'uheri ya Sia la puan saŋa woo, ya pu'uhegɔ la ya suhega woo puan. Ti ba'ahegɔ puan, da ga' ya gihera gee sɔheri ya saŋa woo bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la za'a.

ref: Pray that I will be given the message to speak and that I may fearlessly explain the mystery about the good news.
text: Pu'uheri ya bo'ori ya mam mɛ, ti n han ni yeti n tɔgɛ, Yinɛ bo' mam yelehum, ti n tɔgɛ la sukpe'ene ti nɛreba baŋɛ sugelum yele se'ere n boi faarɛ yelehum wa puan,

ref: I was sent to do this work, and this is the reason I am in jail. So pray that I will be brave and will speak as I should. Final Greetings
text: sebo zuo ti mam sooe Kiresta na'arɛ ti ba ba' mam la bãhi. Pu'uheri ya Yinɛ bɔ'ɔra mam ti n ta'am muulɛ dɛ la sukpe'ene, wuu lan de n tɔgɛ se'em la. Ba'ahegɔ kã'aŋɔ

ref: I pray that God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will give peace, love, and faith to every follower!
text: Suma'ahum bɔna la lageŋɔ la nɛreba za'a, la nɔŋerɛ n dole la sakerɛ, ze'ele tu Sɔ Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya.

ref: May God be kind to everyone who keeps on loving our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ bɔna la yamam sɛba woo n tara nɔŋese'ere n ka tari ba'ahegɔ la bɔ'ɔra tu Daana Yezu Kiresta la.

ref: From Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus. To all God's people who belong to Christ Jesus at Philippi and to all your bishops and deacons.
text: Mam Pɔɔl la Timoti en de Kiresta Yezu yamehi la en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa, bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ nɛresunduma la za'a en boi Kiresta Yezu puan Filipi timpaberɛ la, la lageŋɔ nɛheduma, la kiinduma la.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul's Prayer for the Church in Philippi
text: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an, bɔna la ya. Pɔɔl pu'uhi bɔ'ɔra Filipiduma

ref: Every time I think of you, I thank my God.
text: Mam bo'ori n Yinɛ fara mɛ, saŋa woo ti n ni ta tiɛ ya yele,

ref: And whenever I mention you in my prayers, it makes me happy.
text: pu'uheri la nyɛɛma n pu'uhegɔ woo puan bɔ'ɔra ya za'a.

ref: This is because you have taken part with me in spreading the good news from the first day you heard about it.
text: Se'ere zuo, ya lagum suŋɛ mam mɛ faarɛ yelehum wa puan, ze'ele pɔhega daarɛ la wa'am na paaɛ zina.

ref: God is the one who began this good work in you, and I am certain that he won't stop before it is complete on the day that Christ Jesus returns.
text: Bala, mam tari sakerɛ la yele dina wa puan yeti, se'em daana en pi'ilum tunsunɛ dina wa ya puan la, wan tari dɛ bala ta ba'ahɛ, Yezu Kiresta lɛbega dabeherɛ la puan.

ref: You have a special place in my heart. So it is only natural for me to feel the way I do. All of you have helped in the work God has given me, as I defend the good news and tell about it here in jail.
text: La dela ma'a bo' mam ti n ti'iha ya za'a yele ŋwana, beni zuo, ya tari mam mɛ ya suya puan, la yamam za'a en lagum tɔrɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la mɛ la mam, n paka wa puan, la mam en ka sakerɛ faarɛ yelehum wa zuo, gee sɛɛra bɔ zɛ'ɛta se'em la.

ref: God himself knows how much I want to see you. He knows I care for you in the same way Christ Jesus does.
text: Yinɛ en de mam aze'nyɛ daana, mam n mu'ɛ nini ba' bɔta ti n nyɛ ya za'a se'em, la Kiresta Yezu nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la.

ref: I pray that your love will keep on growing and you will fully know and understand
text: Mam pu'uhegɔ en de se'em en ŋwana, ti ya nɔŋerɛ wan zo'e yabera, ti ya mi'ilum malum pa'ahɛ, ti ya pirɛ la bɔkerɛ,

ref: how to make the right choices. Then you will still be pure and innocent when Christ returns. And until that day,
text: ti la suŋɛ ya, ti ya zamehɛ mina sɛla en ani suŋa gana za'a, bala ti Kiresta lɛbega dabeherɛ na la, ya wan dɛna Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba, ka tara dɛkɔ.

ref: Jesus Christ will keep you busy doing good deeds that bring glory and praise to God. What Life Means to Paul
text: Yan diŋɛ wam tuuntɔɔ wala sɛ'a en ze'eti Yezu Kiresta zi'an na, tara na'ahegɔ la pɛka bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ la. Pɔɔl zina vom

ref: My dear friends, I want you to know that what has happened to me has helped to spread the good news.
text: N boti ya baŋɛ mɛ sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ti sɛla en iŋɛ mam la, tugum suŋɛ mam mɛ ti n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa,

ref: The Roman guards and all the others know I am here in jail because I serve Christ.
text: ti la niiɛ bo' gureba la za'a, la nɛra woo en boi bilam, ti ba baŋɛ yeti, mam en boi pakadeen wa dela Kiresta zuo.

ref: Now most of the Lord's followers have become brave and are fearlessly telling the message.
text: Ŋwana n bahɛ ti tu suurɔ zo'e zo'e nyɛ inkpeŋere Daana la puan mam, paka wa puan ka zɔta ti ba tɔgɛ yelehum la, la sukpe'ene ti dabeem ka bɔna.

ref: Some are preaching about Christ because they are jealous and envious of us. Others are preaching because they want to help.
text: Sɛba muuli Kiresta mɛ, nintɛrega la nyuure zuo, gee sɛba me muulegɔ mɛ la putɛ'sunɛ.

ref: They love Christ and know I am here to defend the good news about him.
text: Nɛrebana wa muuli Kiresta la nɔŋerɛ puan, gee mina ti ban tari mam wa'am ka'lam na wa dela mam en ka sakerɛ faarɛ yelehum la zuo.

ref: But the ones who are jealous of us are not sincere. They just want to cause trouble for me while I am in jail.
text: Gee sɛba me muuli Kiresta la nyika puti'ira puan, dagi la ba susunɛ puan, gee ba malum pa'ahɛ mam namehegɔ, n paka deo bɔŋa wa puan.

ref: But that doesn't matter. All that matters is that people are telling about Christ, whether they are sincere or not. This is what makes me glad. I will keep on being glad,
text: Gee sɛla en pakɛ dela, yele dina wa ma'a. Ti ba yeen muulɛ Kiresta sadɔtɔ seto woo puan, pumpɔreŋɔ puan, bii yelemɛŋerɛ puan, mam nyɛɛni mɛ ŋwana puan. Ɛ̃ɛ, mam wan kelum nyɛɛna la bala,

ref: because I know that your prayers and the help that comes from the Spirit of Christ Jesus will keep me safe.
text: se'ere en sɔi la, mam mi' ti ya pu'uhegɔ zuo, la Yezu Kiresta Fa'a Sia la suŋɛ zuo, la wan ta tee dɛna mam faarɛ.

ref: I honestly expect and hope I will never do anything to be ashamed of. Whether I live or die, I always want to be as brave as I am now and bring honor to Christ.
text: La dela sɛla ti mam kɔ'ɔm ana kɛli, gee me tari puti'irɛ, ti vi kan di mam sadɔɔ seko woo puan, gee doohe mam en muuli la sukpe'ene wa zuo, Kiresta yu'urɛ wan nyɛ pɛka nananawa, wuu la ni pun ani se'em mam iŋa wa puan la, mam vom bii n kum la.

ref: If I live, it will be for Christ, and if I die, I will gain even more.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, han dɛna mam wa, mam vom dela n tuna bɔ'ɔra Kiresta, ti n kum dɛna n nyuurɔ.

ref: I don't know what to choose. I could keep on living and doing something useful.
text: N han yeti n vɔna n ingɔŋɔ wa puan, la dela tuunsuma n tari wala bɔ'ɔra mam. La bahɛ ti mam ka mina mam wan looe sɛla.

ref: It is a hard choice to make. I want to die and be with Christ, because this would be much better.
text: La kpe'em bo' mam mɛ yele ana ayi' wa puan. Mam puti'irɛ puan, mam boti ni ti n bahɛ vom buna wa mɛ, gee kiŋɛ ta bɔna la Kiresta, bala kɔ'ɔm nyana mɛ.

ref: But I know that all of you still need me. This is why I am sure I will stay on to help you grow and be happy in your faith.
text: Mam n diŋɛ mina yele dina wa suŋa suŋa la, mam mi' ti n wan vɔna, la n tuna la ya za'a, ya nɛŋa tɔlega, la ya nyɛɛma ya sakerɛ puan zuo.

ref: Then, when I visit you again, you will have good reason to take great pride in Christ Jesus because of me.
text: Ŋwana ti n wan ta'am lagum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ paaɛ paaɛ la ya Yezu Kiresta puan, n han ta le wa'am ya zi'an na.

ref: Above all else, you must live in a way that brings honor to the good news about Christ. Then, whether I visit you or not, I will hear that all of you think alike. I will know that you are working together and are struggling side by side to get others to believe the good news.
text: Yamam yeen vɔna ya vom, sadɔɔ seko en naaɛ la Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la, ti n han nyaŋɛ wa'am na bihɛ ya, bii n han pun ka bɔna la ya gee wum ya yele, n wan mina ti ya ze' la kankaŋɛ, Sia kayima puan, naaɛ taaba la putɛ'yinɛ, zigera la taaba ti ya tara sakerɛ faarɛ yelehum la puan.

ref: Be brave when you face your enemies. Your courage will show them that they are going to be destroyed, and it will show you that you will be saved. God will make all of this happen,
text: Da bahɛ ya ti ya dinduma bihɛ ya yoo. Han dɛna bamam, ŋwana dela aze'nyɛ daana bo' ba sageŋɔ la, gee tugum dɛna yamam faarɛ. Ŋwana wa dela Yinɛ en iŋɛ dɛ.

ref: and he has blessed you. Not only do you have faith in Christ, but you suffer for him.
text: Yinɛ ka doohe a zu'uhegɔ la puan bo' tu la yɔ'ɔ ti tu nam la Kiresta ma'a, gee tu nameheri a zuo me,

ref: You saw me suffer, and you still hear about my troubles. Now you must suffer in the same way.
text: yan diŋɛ zigera zika sɛka ti ya nyɛ ti mam zigeri la, gee wum ti n kelum zigera ka la zuo.

ref: Now make me completely happy! Live in harmony by showing love for each other. Be united in what you think, as if you were only one person.
text: yi bahɛ ya ti mam nyɛɛŋɔ pirɛ, doohe ya tari putɛ'yinɛ, nɔŋerɛ deyina la taaba, nɔyinɛ la suure deyina.

ref: Don't be jealous or proud, but be humble and consider others more important than yourselves.
text: Da iti ya sɛla nyika puti'irɛ puan, bii pa'alɛ n mɛŋa yooro puan, gee sigehe n mɛhi puan, tugum bihɛ ya nyɛta sɛba gana ya mɛhi.

ref: Care about them as much as you care about yourselves
text: Da bahɛ ya ti ya se'em kɔ'ɔm biha a mɛŋa ma'a sum yele, gee ya biha basɛba sum yele me.

ref: and think the same way that Christ Jesus thought:
text: Bahɛ ya ti putɛ'se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la, bɔna yamam me puan.

ref: Instead he gave up everything and became a slave, when he became like one of us.
text: gee a daa bihɛ a mɛŋa la zaŋa, dikɛ a mɛŋa lebege yameŋa, ti ba dɔgɛ en ti a dɛna asaala. Tara asaala naanɛ,

ref: Christ was humble. He obeyed God and even died on a cross.
text: sigehe a mɛŋa, nam Yinɛ ta paaɛ kum, kum sebo en pun dɛna pumpuuredɔɔ zuo kum.

ref: Then God gave Christ the highest place and honored his name above all others.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ me zɛkɛ en saazuo gee bo' en yu'se'ere en gani yu'ura za'a,

ref: So at the name of Jesus everyone will bow down, those in heaven, on earth, and under the earth.
text: ti Yezu yu'urɛ la, ti duma woo wan kpa'ara bɔ'ɔra, saazuo ha, la timpaberɛ wa zuo, la timpaberɛ timpaberɛ ha,

ref: And to the glory of God the Father everyone will openly agree, “Jesus Christ is Lord!” Lights in the World
text: ti zɛleŋa woo puta yeti, Yezu Kiresta en de Daana, ti la dɛna na'ahegɔ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ en de Sɔ la. Niiri ya wuu bugum timpaberɛ wa zuo

ref: My dear friends, you always obeyed when I was with you. Now that I am away, you should obey even more. So work with fear and trembling to discover what it really means to be saved.
text: Ŋwana zuo, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, wuu yamam en nane mam saŋa woo se'em la, ti la dagena la mam ni bɔna la ya la ma'a, gee nam ya mam gana bala nananawa, ti n ka bɔna la ya wa. Tuni ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ pa'alɛ ti ya nyɛ faarɛ, la dabeem la gbi'a.

ref: God is working in you to make you willing and able to obey him.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela Yinɛ en boi ya puan tuna la ninmu'urɛ, bɔ'ɔra ya paŋa ti ya tana sakera gee tuna bɔ'ɔra a yu'urɛ la.

ref: Then you will be the pure and innocent children of God. You live among people who are crooked and evil, but you must not do anything they can say is wrong. Try to shine as lights among the people of this world,
text: bala ti ya kan tari gbelegere la taalɛ, ya wan dɛna Yinɛ kɔma en ka tari dɛkɔ nɛre ŋmɛlema, la fãareba buuri saŋa nɛreba wa tiŋasuka. Niiri ya ba puan timpaberɛ wa zuo wuu ya dela bugum la.

ref: as you hold firmly to the message that gives life. Then on the day when Christ returns, I can take pride in you. I can also know that my work and efforts were not useless.
text: La dela yamam en tari vom yelehum wa kankaŋi la zuo, ti n wan ta ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ Kiresta lɛbega daarɛ na, ti n daa ka zɔ bahɛ yoo bii n tum bahɛ yoo.

ref: Your faith in the Lord and your service are like a sacrifice offered to him. And my own blood may have to be poured out with the sacrifice. If this happens, I will be glad and rejoice with you.
text: Gee ba han pun ka'ɛ mam wuu buum kaabegɔ la bahɛ kaabegɔ bo'olum la zuo, yamam sakerɛ zuo, la de mam la pupeelum, ti n nyɛɛni la yamam za'a.

ref: In the same way, you should be glad and rejoice with me. Timothy and Epaphroditus
text: Bala mɛa ti yamam me dɛna ya nyɛɛna la pupeelum la mam. Timoti la Epafurodituse

ref: I want to be encouraged by news about you. So I hope the Lord Jesus will soon let me send Timothy to you.
text: Mam tari puti'irɛ Daana Yezu zi'an ti n tum Timoti ya zi'an kalam na, la n nyɛɛm doohe mam wan wum yelesuma sɛ'a ya zi'an la.

ref: I don't have anyone else who cares about you as much as he does.
text: Mam ka tari se'em en ani wuu eŋa, ba' tara ya giile bala.

ref: The others think only about what interests them and not about what concerns Christ Jesus.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, bana la za'a eere la ba mɛhi ma'a sum yele, dagi Yezu Kiresta yelehum la sum yele.

ref: But you know what kind of person Timothy is. He has worked with me like a son in spreading the good news.
text: Gee ya mɛhi mi' Timoti tibehum en ani se'em. A ani wuu dayua la ka Sɔ la, lagum tum la mam faarɛ yelehum tuuma wa puan la a ziim za'a.

ref: I hope to send him to you, as soon as I find out what is going to happen to me.
text: Bala zuo, mam ti'ihɛ ti n tum en mɛ kalam na, wuu mam n nyɛ ti la ani mam se'em la.

ref: And I feel sure the Lord will also let me come soon.
text: Gee mam nam Daana la, ti mam me wan wa'am ya zi'an tɔtɔ na.

ref: I think I ought to send my dear friend Epaphroditus back to you. He is a follower and a worker and a soldier of the Lord, just as I am. You sent him to look after me,
text: Le pa'ahɛ, mam ti'ihɛ ti la nari ti n tum Epafurodituse ya zi'an na. A dela n suuɔ, la n tuntuna tadaana, la n pɛntiira tadaana en de ya bituneŋa, gee dɛna tuntuna bo' mam en boti sɛla.

ref: but now he is eager to see you. He is worried, because you heard he was sick.
text: A ba' bɔta ti a nyɛ ya za'a mɛ, gee a suure sagum mɛ la yan wum ti a bɛ̃'ɛri mɛ la.

ref: In fact, he was very sick and almost died. But God was kind to him, and also to me, and he kept me from being burdened down with sorrow.
text: A daa sirum ba' bɛ̃' mɛ, gee la a ki. Gee ti Yinɛ zɔ a nimbu'ɔ, gee dagi la eŋa ma'a ti Yinɛ daa zɔ a nimbu'ɔ, gee mam me, ti n susageŋɔ la da tagelɛ taaba.

ref: Now I am more eager than ever to send Epaphroditus back again. You will be glad to see him, and I won't have to worry any longer.
text: Mam ba' bɔta ti n tum en mɛ ya zi'an kalam na, ti ya nyɛɛm la yan le nyɛ en la, gee ti mam me susageŋɔ la sige.

ref: Be sure to give him a cheerful welcome, just as people who serve the Lord deserve.
text: To'e ya en Daana la puan la ya pupeelum za'a, gee na'ahɛ ya nɛrebana taaba.

ref: He almost died working for Christ, and he risked his own life to do for me what you could not.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, a dela se'em en daa gee la a ki' Kiresta tuuma zuo, pɔhɛ a vom, tum tuunse'ere ti yamam kan nyaŋɛ ni tum bo' mam la.

ref: Finally, my dear friends, be glad that you belong to the Lord. It doesn't bother me to write the same things to you that I have written before. In fact, it is for your own good.
text: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, n suurɔ wa, nyɛɛni ya Daana la puan. N gulehɛ yele sɛ'a ti n pun gulehɛ bo' ya yia la le bo' ya, dagi daaŋɔ bo' mam. Han dɛna yamam me, la suŋeri ya mɛ.

ref: Watch out for those people who behave like dogs! They are evil and want to do more than just circumcise you.
text: Mini ya baahi la yele, mini ya be'em tuntuneba la yele, mini ya sɛba ti inganɛ wa ŋmaa dɛna ba ninmu'urɛ la yele!

ref: But we are the ones who are truly circumcised, because we worship by the power of God's Spirit and take pride in Christ Jesus. We don't brag about what we have done,
text: Bihɛ ya, tumam en de sɛba n sirum ŋma la, tumam en de sɛba n na'aheri Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la puan, gee ŋmɛ'ɛra tu nyu'ɔ Yezu Kiresta puan, ka dikɛ tu mɛhi delum inganɛ malema,

ref: although I could. Others may brag about themselves, but I have more reason to brag than anyone else.
text: la mam en pun tara sore ti n ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la inganɛ la. Se'em han bɔna ha tara se'ere zuo ti a wan ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la a inganɛ, mam yi tari gana bala.

ref: I was circumcised when I was eight days old, and I am from the nation of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a true Hebrew. As a Pharisee, I strictly obeyed the Law of Moses.
text: Ba ŋma mam la dabeha anii daarɛ, mam dela Izirale buuri, yehe Benyamin yizuo puan, dɛna Hiburu, ti Hiburu pɔga la buraa naaɛ taaba dɔgɛ. La han dɛna la ze'elego la, mam dela Farasii paaɛ paaɛ,

ref: And I was so eager I even made trouble for the church. I did everything the Law demands in order to please God.
text: la han dɛna la ninmu'urɛ, mam en daa dole mɛleha lageŋɔ la, la han dɛna la tuuntɔɔ vom doohe ze'elego la puan, mam ka tari tuure.

ref: But Christ has shown me that what I once thought was valuable is worthless.
text: Gee ba han pun tara nyuurɔ seto woo, mam bihɛ ba za'a ti ba dela zaŋa Kiresta zuo.

ref: Nothing is as wonderful as knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. I have given up everything else and count it all as garbage. All I want is Christ
text: Sɛla en gani bala dela, mam bihɛ sɛla woo ti ba dela zaŋa beni zuo, tibehum sebo en gani za'a en boi mam n baŋɛ Yezu Kiresta en de mam Daana la. Eŋa zuo, ti mam bɔregɛ sɛla woo bahɛ, gee biha ba ti ba dɛna sɔgerɔ, ti n ta'am tara Kiresta,

ref: and to know that I belong to him. I could not make myself acceptable to God by obeying the Law of Moses. God accepted me simply because of my faith in Christ.
text: la n vɔna a puan, da tara n mɛŋa tuntɔɔnɛ en yehe ze'elego la naŋa puan na, gee tuntɔɔnɛ en yeheri n sakerɛ Kiresta puan na. Tuntɔɔnɛ se'ere en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an dɔla sakerɛ puan.

ref: All I want is to know Christ and the power that raised him to life. I want to suffer and die as he did,
text: Mam boti n baŋɛ Kiresta mɛ, la a vo'ore paŋa la, la n lagum namehɛ a namehegɔ la, doohe la n ana wuu en, a kum la puan,

ref: so that somehow I also may be raised to life. Running toward the Goal
text: wuu ma beere puti'irɛ en de n ti Yinɛ wan ta ihege mam kum puan la. N zigeri yɛ'ɛha la nyaŋerɛ

ref: I have not yet reached my goal, and I am not perfect. But Christ has taken hold of me. So I keep on running and struggling to take hold of the prize.
text: Dagi la mam pun tara ŋwana mɛ, bii n pun di la mi'a. Gee mam kɔ'ɔm zigera mɛ ti la dɛna mam dinɛ, beni zuo, Kiresta Yezu iŋɛ mam ti n dɛna la a dinɛ.

ref: My friends, I don't feel I have already arrived. But I forget what is behind, and I struggle for what is ahead.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, mam ka biheri ti de pun dɛna la mam dinɛ, gee yele dina wa ma'a ti mam ita. Mam tameni sɛla woo en boi mam pooren, gee tiregɛ kina nɛŋa ti n paaɛ sɛla en boi n nɛŋan ha la.

ref: I run toward the goal, so I can win the prize of being called to heaven. This is the prize God offers because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: N tiregeri mɛ ti n di mi'a, la n to'e yɔɔrɛ la en de saazuo yi'a wa yɔɔrɛ, ti Yinɛ yi' mam doohe Kiresta Yezu puan la.

ref: All of us who are mature should think in this same way. And if any of you think differently, God will make it clear to you.
text: Bahɛ ya ti tumam sɛba en bi'ɛ la, tara puti'irɛ deyina, gee se'em puti'irɛ han bɔna deyima yelese'ere puan, la han dɛna bala, Yinɛ wan lilege dɛ bo' ya.

ref: But we must keep going in the direction that we are now headed.
text: Yeen bahɛ ya ti tu zãla sɛla ti tu tara la kankaŋi.

ref: My friends, I want you to follow my example and learn from others who closely follow the example we set for you.
text: N suurɔ, naaɛ ya taaba la ya tɔgeha mam, gee la ya bihera sɛba en vɔi dɔla tumam n iŋɛ sɛla pa'alɛ ti yamam nyɛ la.

ref: I often warned you that many people are living as enemies of the cross of Christ. And now with tears in my eyes, I warn you again
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba zo'e zo'e vom dela dinduma la Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ la. Mam ni pun yɛta ya ba yele, gee n nyaa yeti ya mɛ la nintam.

ref: that they are headed for hell! They worship their stomachs and brag about the disgusting things they do. All they can think about are the things of this world.
text: Ba ba'ahegɔ dela sageŋɔ. Ba yinɛ dela ba puya, ti ba suŋa bɔna ba vi la puan, ba puti'ira boi la timpaberɛ wa zuo lɔgerɔ puan.

ref: But we are citizens of heaven and are eagerly waiting for our Savior to come from there. Our Lord Jesus Christ
text: Gee tumam timbiine la boi la saazuo ha. Bilam ti tu gura ti tu faara la wan ze'ele na, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta la.

ref: Dear friends, I love you and long to see you. Please keep on being faithful to the Lord. You are my pride and joy. Paul Encourages the Lord's Followers
text: Dinɛ zuo, n suŋɛ duma ti n nɔŋɛ, gee bɔta ti n nyɛ ya paaɛ paaɛ wa, mam nyɛɛma la mam na'am muŋa wa. Ze' ya kankaŋi Daana la puan, sadɔɔ kuna puan, n sunɔŋerɛ wa. Kpemehego

ref: Euodia and Syntyche, you belong to the Lord, so I beg you to stop arguing with each other.
text: N beleni Eyodia, la Sintike, ti ba dɛna putɛ'yinɛ Daana la puan.

ref: And, my true partner, I ask you to help them. These women have worked together with me and with Clement and with the others in spreading the good news. Their names are now written in the book of life.
text: Gee yɛta fum se'em ti mam la fum bɔna yɔgehega kayima puan wa yeti, fum suŋɛ pɔgehi wa. Se'ere en sɔi la, si lagum zabɛ mɛ suŋɛ mam faarɛ yelehum wa tuuma puan. Bamam la Kilɛmɛnti la basɛba la en de mam tuntuneba taaba la, sɛba yu'ura en boi vom gɔŋɔ la puan la.

ref: Always be glad because of the Lord! I will say it again: Be glad.
text: Nyɛɛni ya Daana la puan saŋa woo. Le yɛ'ɛha, n yeti, nyɛɛni ya.

ref: Always be gentle with others. The Lord will soon be here.
text: Bahɛ ya ti ya sigehe n mɛŋa la niiɛ bo' nɛra woo. Daana la pɔri na.

ref: Don't worry about anything, but pray about everything. With thankful hearts offer up your prayers and requests to God.
text: Da fabele ya sɛsɛla yele, gee sɛla woo puan, doohe ya pu'uhegɔ, gee suhega, la fara bu'a puan, bahɛ ti Yinɛ baŋɛ yan boti sɛla la.

ref: Then, because you belong to Christ Jesus, God will bless you with peace that no one can completely understand. And this peace will control the way you think and feel.
text: Ti Yinɛ suma'ahum la n gani bɔkerɛ za'a la, gu'ura ya suya la ya ti'ihegɔ sɛ ti zɛ'a kankaŋi Yezu Kiresta puan.

ref: Finally, my friends, keep your minds on whatever is true, pure, right, holy, friendly, and proper. Don't ever stop thinking about what is truly worthwhile and worthy of praise.
text: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, n suurɔ, sɛla woo en de yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla woo en tari na'ahegɔ, sɛla woo en de ma'a, sɛla woo en ka tari dɛgerɔ, sɛla woo en peere puurɛ, sɛla woo n nari. Sɛla han bɔna di mi'a, sɛla en boi nara pɛka, ti'iheri ya yele ana wa yele.

ref: You know the teachings I gave you, and you know what you heard me say and saw me do. So follow my example. And God, who gives peace, will be with you. Paul Gives Thanks for the Gifts He Was Given
text: Tara ya yele sɛ'a ti ya zamehɛ, gee to'e, gee wum, gee nyɛ mam puan la tuna, ti suma'ahum Yinɛ la, wan bɔna la ya. Pɔɔl bo' fara Filipiduma fara la ba bo'olum la

ref: The Lord has made me very grateful that at last you have thought about me once again. Actually, you were thinking about me all along, but you didn't have any chance to show it.
text: Mam nyɛɛni la nyɛɛmkãtɛ Daana la puan nananawa, ti ba'ahegɔ za'a, ya malum ihege yan daa ti'iheri n yele se'em la mɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya yuum ti'iheri n yele, gee ya yuum ka nyɛ yɔ'ɔ pa'alɛ dɛ.

ref: I am not complaining about having too little. I have learned to be satisfied with whatever I have.
text: Dagi mam n yeti ŋwana wa dela, n boti la sɛla ya zi'an. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam zamehɛ mɛ ti sadɔɔ seko woo puan, n nini tigɛ la sɛla ti n tara mɛ.

ref: I know what it is to be poor or to have plenty, and I have lived under all kinds of conditions. I know what it means to be full or to be hungry, to have too much or too little.
text: Mam mi' mam tari n mɛŋa se'em mɛ, n han ta sige, gee mina mam tari n mɛŋa se'em, n han le ta zom paaɛ paaɛ. Yele ana wa, de se'ere puan, la yɛla za'a puan, mam zamehɛ la sugelum yele se'ere en boi putigerɛ puan, la kom puan, tara zo'e zo'e puan, la ka tari puan.

ref: Christ gives me the strength to face anything.
text: Mam ta'am iŋɛ sɛla woo se'em daana en bo'ori mam paŋa la puan.

ref: It was good of you to help me when I was having such a hard time.
text: Gee sadɔɔ seko woo puan, ya daa iŋɛ la itesuŋɔ, la yan lagum di mam nimbu'ɔ la.

ref: My friends at Philippi, you remember what it was like when I started preaching the good news in Macedonia. After I left there, you were the only church that became my partner by giving blessings and by receiving them in return.
text: Yamam Filipiduma sirum mi' ti, faarɛ yelehum pɔhega dabeha la puan ha la, mam en bahɛ Masedoniya la, lageŋɔ daa ka boi lagum tum bo' mam bu'a bii to'ore yɛla wa puan, han dagena yamam ma'a.

ref: Even when I was in Thessalonica, you helped me more than once.
text: Mam a daa pun ta bɔna Tɛsalonika timpaberɛ ha la, ya bo' mam suŋɛ me na gaŋɛ buyina.

ref: I am not trying to get something from you, but I want you to receive the blessings that come from giving.
text: Dagi la n eere la bo'olum la, gee mam eere la nyuurɔ seto n wan kã'ahɛ zo'e zo'e beŋe bo' ya la.

ref: I have been paid back everything, and with interest. I am completely satisfied with the gifts you sent with Epaphroditus. They are like a sweet-smelling offering or like the right kind of sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Ya kɔ'ɔm yɔ mam la za'a za'a, ti n tara gaŋɛ. Ya kɔ'ɔm tigehɛ mam mɛ suŋa suŋa, yele yele wuu mam nyaa to'e ya bo'olum la ti ya dikɛ tum Epafurodituse na la. Bo dela nyuuŋo suŋɔ kaabegɔ bo'olum. Bo dela kaabegɔ n tigeri Yinɛ nini.

ref: I pray that God will take care of all your needs with the wonderful blessings that come from Christ Jesus!
text: Bala zuo, Mam Yinɛ wa wan tigeha ya sɛla woo ti ya bɔta, doohe la a tarega sum sebo n boi Yezu Kiresta puan la.

ref: May God our Father be praised forever and ever. Amen. Final Greetings
text: Pɛka dɛna tu Yinɛ, la tu Sɔ la dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ la ka'a

ref: Give my greetings to all who are God's people because of Christ Jesus. The Lord's followers here with me send you their greetings.
text: Pu'uhɛ nɛresunduma la za'a en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la. N suurɔ n boi la mam ka'lam pu'uheri ya na.

ref: All of God's people send their greetings, especially those in the service of the Emperor.
text: Nɛresunduma la woo pu'uheri ya na, yele yele wuu timbihera nɛŋadaana la yireduma la.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless your life!
text: Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya Sia puan.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is also a follower.
text: Mam Pɔɔl en de Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, doohe la Yinɛ suboolum, la tu suuɔ Timoti en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa.

ref: To God's people who live in Colossae and are faithful followers of Christ. I pray that God our Father will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: Tu gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la nɛresunduma, la sakereba en de tu suurɔ Kiresta puan, bɔna Kolose tiŋa la. Zu'uhegɔ dɛna ya dinɛ, la Yinɛ en de tu sɔ la suma'ahum. Pɔɔl fara bu'a

ref: Each time we pray for you, we thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Tumam en ni pu'uhera bɔ'ɔra ya la, saŋa woo tu kɔ'ɔm bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la la fara,

ref: We have heard of your faith in Christ and of your love for all God's people,
text: se'ere en sɔi la, tumam wum ya sakerɛ en ani se'em Kiresta Yezu puan, la nɔŋerɛ se'ere ti ya tara bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la za'a la,

ref: because what you hope for is kept safe for you in heaven. You first heard about this hope when you believed the true message, which is the good news.
text: beere putɛ'se'ere en boi saazuo gura ya la zuo. Ya pun tabelɛ wum beere putɛ' dina wa yele mɛ, gee baŋɛ wum yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum en de faarɛ yelehum la,

ref: The good news is spreading all over the world with great success. It has spread in this same way among you, ever since the first day you learned the truth about God's wonderful kindness
text: n wa'am ya zi'an na la. Woo bumɔ en wani wala gee zɔ'ɔra se'em tiŋa wa zuo za'a se'em la, bala mɛa ti bu wuna wala ya puan, pɔhɛ daan se'ere daarɛ ti ya wum, gee bɔkɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la.

ref: from our good friend Epaphras. He works together with us for Christ and is a faithful worker for you.
text: Dina ti ya zamehɛ Epafaras zi'an, tu yameŋa tadaana, se'em ti tu nɔŋɛ la. A dela Kiresta yelemɛŋerɛ tuntuna ya zuo,

ref: We have not stopped praying for you since the first day we heard about you. In fact, we always pray that God will show you everything he wants you to do and that you may have all the wisdom and understanding his Spirit gives.
text: Dina wa zuo, daan se'ere daarɛ ti tu wum dɛna wa la, tu ka go'e tu pu'uha bɔ'ɔra ya, gee suhera ti ya pirɛ la Yinɛ suboolum mi'ilum, la Sia puan yɛm woo, la bɔkerɛ woo,

ref: Then you will live a life that honors the Lord, and you will always please him by doing good deeds. You will come to know God even better.
text: ti ya vɔna vom sebo en nari bɔ'ɔra Daana la, ti ya vom za'a tigɛ a nini wuu yan wan wuna wala tuunsunɛ woo puan se'em la, la yan zo'ore Yinɛ mi'ilum puan se'em la.

ref: His glorious power will make you patient and strong enough to endure anything, and you will be truly happy.
text: Ya kpe'em la paŋa sɛka woo en ze'ele Yinɛ paŋa puan na, ya dɛna sɛba en maahum ti ya ŋmibera sɛla woo puan la sugeri, gee nyɛɛna

ref: I pray that you will be grateful to God for letting you have part in what he has promised his people in the kingdom of light.
text: la pɛka bɔ'ɔra Sɔ la fara. Sɔ se'em en iŋɛ ya ti ya lagum tara tɔtɛ la nɛre sunduma la en boi niihum puan la.

ref: God rescued us from the dark power of Satan and brought us into the kingdom of his dear Son,
text: Yinɛ faaɛ tu mɛ yehe lika paŋa puan bahɛ, gee tee dikɛ tu ki'ihɛ a Dayɔba'arɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan,

ref: who forgives our sins and sets us free.
text: se'em puan ti tu nyɛ faarɛ, la yele be'ero sugeri dia. Kiresta Kpe'eŋo

ref: Christ is exactly like God, who cannot be seen. He is the first-born Son, superior to all creation.
text: Kiresta dela Yinɛ se'ere ti tu ka nyɛta wa naanɛ, kiima naaŋɔ wa woo puan.

ref: Everything was created by him, everything in heaven and on earth, everything seen and unseen, including all forces and powers, and all rulers and authorities. All things were created by God's Son, and everything was made for him.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan ti sɛla woo en boi saazuo la timpaberɛ wa zuo naam. Sɛla ti tu nyɛta, la sɛla ti tu ka nyɛta, ba dɛna na'am, pahi, bihereba bii kpe'eŋo, sɛla woo naam doohe la eŋa puan, gee dɛna eŋa dinɛ.

ref: God's Son was before all else, and by him everything is held together.
text: Kiresta mɛŋa pun bɔna mɛ gee ti sɛla woo nyaa baŋɛ naam, eŋa puan ti naaŋɔ wa za'ana bɔna suŋa.

ref: He is the head of his body, which is the church. He is the very beginning, the first to be raised from death, so that he would be above all others.
text: Eŋa en de zuo daana, lagehegɔ la puan. Eŋa en de pi'ileŋo, dɛna kiima kum vo'ore puan yia, ti a ta'am wa'am na dɛna yiadaana sɛla woo puan.

ref: God himself was pleased to live fully in his Son.
text: Eŋa puan ti Yinɛ nini tigɛ ti a za'a za'a bɔna a puan,

ref: And God was pleased for him to make peace by sacrificing his blood on the cross, so that all beings in heaven and on earth would be brought back to God.
text: eŋa ti Yinɛ nini tigɛ ti a doohe a puan dikɛ sɛla woo naaɛ taaba bo' a mɛŋa, sɛla woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, bii saazuo, doohe la Yinɛ en iŋɛ suma'ahum doohe la a ziim la pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo la.

ref: You used to be far from God. Your thoughts made you his enemies, and you did evil things.
text: Wunse'eren ha la ya daa nan zaaɛ mɛ, gee dɛna dinduma ya puti'ira puan, tuna be'em tuuma la,

ref: But his Son became a human and died. So God made peace with you, and now he lets you stand in his presence as people who are holy and faultless and innocent.
text: e a nyaa dikɛ ya naaɛ mɛ, doohe amɛŋa saala ingɔŋɔ kum la puan, ti a bahɛ ti tu dɛna tuulum nɛreba, ka tara dɛkɔ bii geere a nɛŋan,

ref: But you must stay deeply rooted and firm in your faith. You must not give up the hope you received when you heard the good news. It was preached to everyone on earth, and I myself have become a servant of this message. Paul's Service to the Church
text: wuu ya han kelum gu'ura ya mɛhi suŋa, zɛ'a kankaŋi ya sakerɛ la puan, ka vuregera ya beere puti'irɛ se'ere ti faarɛ yelesuma la biŋe nuurɛ ti ya wum la, se'ere en muulɛ bo' bunvua en boi tiŋa wa zuo wa. Mam Pɔɔl dela se'em ti ba looe bo' faari yelesuma wa. Pɔɔl suure boi Kolosiduma puan

ref: I am glad I can suffer for you. I am pleased also that in my own body I can continue the suffering of Christ for his body, the church.
text: Nananawa, mam nyɛɛni mɛ n namehegɔ la puan yamam zuo, mam inganɛ wa puan, bahɛ ya ti n yele de ŋwana yeti, mam ba'aheri la namehegɔ seko en gee Kiresta namehegɔ la puan, a iŋa la en de lageŋɔ la zuo.

ref: God's plan was to make me a servant of his church and to send me to preach his complete message to you.
text: Mam lebege la lageŋɔ la yameŋa, doohe la Yinɛ en bo' mam tuunse'ere ya zuo la, ti n bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ Yinɛ yelehum la suŋa suŋa,

ref: For ages and ages this message was kept secret from everyone, but now it has been explained to God's people.
text: sɛla en sugɛ asaaleba zi'an yuuman yuuman, la buuri sahi puan ha, gee nyaa lilege bo' a tuulum nɛreba la.

ref: God did this because he wanted you Gentiles to understand his wonderful and glorious mystery. And the mystery is that Christ lives in you, and he is your hope of sharing in God's glory.
text: Bama ti Yinɛ looe ti a doohe ba puan bahɛ ti ba baŋɛ Yinɛ suŋa en zo'e se'em yele se'ere en sugɛ wa puan bo' sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la, yele wa dela Kiresta n boi ya puan, en de ya beere puti'irɛ la suŋa.

ref: We announce the message about Christ, and we use all our wisdom to warn and teach everyone, so all of Christ's followers will grow and become mature.
text: La dela eŋa ti tu muula wa, ka'ana nɛra woo gee pa'ala nɛra woo yɛm puan, ti tu wan bi'ihɛ nɛra woo Kiresta puan.

ref: This is why I work so hard and use the mighty power he gives me.
text: Dina zuo ti mam zigera la pansɛka woo ti a dikɛ iŋɛ mam puan la paŋa la inkpeŋere la.

ref: I want you to know what a struggle I am going through for you, for God's people at Laodicea, and for all of those followers who have never met me.
text: Mam boti ya baŋɛ mam mu'ɛ nini tuna se'em ya zuo, la sɛba en boi Laodisiya, la ya sɛba za'a en pun ka nyɛ mam la nini.

ref: I do it to encourage them. Then as their hearts are joined together in love, they will be wonderfully blessed with complete understanding. And they will truly know Christ. Not only is he the key to God's mystery,
text: Mam boti ba suya nyɛ la kpemehego, naaɛ taaba nɔŋerɛ puan, gee nyɛ bɔkerɛ se'ere en gani za'a, tara tibehum, gee me tara mi'ilum doohe Yinɛ sugelum yele la en de Kiresta a mɛŋa la.

ref: but all wisdom and knowledge are hidden away in him.
text: La dela Kiresta puan ti yɛm la mi'ilum tarega za'a bɔna.

ref: I tell you these things to keep you from being fooled by fancy talk.
text: Mam yeti ŋwana la dela, nɛra da pa'ahɛ ya la nɔmaalum.

ref: Even though I am not with you, I keep thinking about you. I am glad to know that you are living as you should and your faith in Christ is strong. Christ Brings Real Life
text: La mam en ka boi la ya ingɔŋɔ puan bilam la, gee n boi la ya sia puan, gee nyɛɛna, bɔta ti n nyɛ yan ze' se'em kankaŋi la ya sakerɛ Kiresta puan. Vom za'a boi la Kiresta puan

ref: You have accepted Christ Jesus as your Lord. Now keep on following him.
text: Wuu yan to'e Kiresta Yezu en de Daana la, kelum vɔni ya ya vom la a puan,

ref: Plant your roots in Christ and let him be the foundation for your life. Be strong in your faith, just as you were taught. And be grateful.
text: ba' ya nyɛga, gee mɛ ya mɛhi yagelɛ a zuo, gee sɛ' ya ze'ele kankaŋi sakerɛ la puan, wuu ban pa'alɛ ya se'em la, pirɛ yabera la fara bu'a.

ref: Don't let anyone fool you by using senseless arguments. These arguments may sound wise, but they are only human teachings. They come from the powers of this world and not from Christ.
text: Bihɛ ya suŋa ti nɛra da dikɛ ya da'aba doohe gɔŋɔ wogerɔ la pã'aheyooro doohe asaala malema, la sɛse'ehi en sɔi timpaberɛ wa bihera la, gee dagi Kiresta en boti se'em la.

ref: And you are fully grown because you belong to Christ, who is over every power and authority.
text: Yamam n wa'am a zi'an na wa, sɛla woo wan ze'ele tuuntɔɔ bo' ya. Eŋa en de se'em en de zuo daana, sɔna pahi, la kpe'eŋo za'a.

ref: Christ has also taken away your selfish desires, just as circumcision removes flesh from the body.
text: Eŋa puan ti ba ŋma yamam me sia puan ŋmaa, yan wan ŋmaaɛ ni ya ingɔŋɔ la zi'an bahɛ ni la, ya ŋmaa dɛ la Kiresta ŋmaa la puan.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was the same as being buried with Christ. Then you were raised to life because you had faith in the power of God, who raised Christ from death.
text: Ban daa laaɛ ya la Kiresta ko'om sua puan la, ba daa lagum vo'e ya mɛ la en ya sakerɛ puan,Yinɛ paŋa la puan, Yinɛ se'em en vo'e en kum puan la.

ref: You were dead, because you were sinful and were not God's people. But God let Christ make you alive, when he forgave all our sins.
text: Yan daa ki ya tuure puan, ya ingɔŋɔ la en ka ŋma la puan la, Yinɛ bahɛ ti ya vo'e mɛ la en, eŋa en daa di tu za'a tuure sugeri bo' tu.

ref: God wiped out the charges that were against us for disobeying the Law of Moses. He took them away and nailed them to the cross.
text: Saalum gulehegɔ seko en daa gulehɛ gã'a gura tɔ, la ku namehegɔ seko en nari tu la. A daa uue ku bahɛ la kinkɛleŋa ha, kpa' kɔ pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

ref: There Christ defeated all powers and forces. He let the whole world see them being led away as prisoners when he celebrated his victory.
text: A yɛɛhɛ pahi duma la kpe'eŋo mɛ, gee diihe ba nyanɛ peelumen, nyaŋɛ ba za'a za'a pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

ref: Don't let anyone tell you what you must eat or drink. Don't let them say you must celebrate the New Moon festival, the Sabbath, or any other festival.
text: Bala la, da bahɛ ya ti se'em zɛregɛ ya doohe dia bii nyua bii kiha dabeha, bii ŋmarehi paalehi, bii de'eŋo bii vo'ohego dabeha.

ref: These things are only a shadow of what was to come. But Christ is real!
text: Bana wa daa dela yele sɛ'a n wan wa'am na la yinyiligo, gee Kiresta en de yelemɛŋerɛ la.

ref: Don't be cheated by people who make a show of acting humble and who worship angels. They brag about seeing visions. But it is all nonsense, because their minds are filled with selfish desires.
text: Da bahɛ ti nɛra sigehe fum, doohe eŋa en boti se'em doohe sigehe n mɛŋa yooro, suŋereba pu'uhegɔ, zaaheŋɔ yɛla, banaduma ukeri mɛ la asaala puti'irɛ la pa'alɛ mɛŋa,

ref: They are no longer part of Christ, who is the head of the whole body. Christ gives the body its strength, and he uses its joints and muscles to hold it together, as it grows by the power of God. Christ Brings New Life
text: gee ka gi' Kiresta en de zuo la ti iŋa la za'a nyɛta diihum, gee naaɛ taaba doohe la a giila la innaanɛ la, zɔ'ɔra Yinɛ puan la. Pumparum pa'aleba

ref: You died with Christ. Now the forces of the universe don't have any power over you. Why do you live as if you had to obey such rules as,
text: La han dɛna ti Kiresta puan, ya ki mɛ la pansɛka en sɔi tiŋa wa la, ŋwani ti ya vɔna wuu ya kelum dɛna la tiŋa wa zuo dinɛ la? Ŋwani ti ya nana malema en de,

ref: “Don't handle this. Don't taste that. Don't touch this.”?
text: “Da gurɛ, da lem, da kalum”?

ref: After these things are used, they are no longer good for anything. So why be bothered with the rules that humans have made up?
text: Malema ana za'a tari bɔ'ɔra la sɛla en ni tum ta kuregɛ sagum. Ba yeen dɛna la asaala ze'elego la pa'alegɔ.

ref: Obeying these rules may seem to be the smart thing to do. They appear to make you love God more and to be very humble and to have control over your body. But they don't really have any power over our desires.
text: Ba tari la naanɛ en ani wuu yɛm duma la, ba ta' zɛkera la ba mɛhi en boti se'em, sigehe n mɛŋa, ta'na nameha ba ihe, gee ba ka tari nyuurɔ bo' nɛra, la han wa'am yaalegɔ yɛla puan na.

ref: You have been raised to life with Christ. Now set your heart on what is in heaven, where Christ rules at God's right side.
text: Bala, la han dɛna ti ya vo'e mɛ la Kiresta, ye eere ya yele sɛ'a en boi tinsuŋan ha la, zi'an ti Kiresta zi'a Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o bɔba la.

ref: Think about what is up there, not about what is here on earth.
text: Bahɛ ya ti ya puti'ira bɔna la yele sɛ'a en boi tinsuŋan ha la, dagi yele sɛ'a en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo,

ref: You died, which means that your life is hidden with Christ, who sits beside God.
text: se'ere en sɔi la, ya ki mɛ, ti ya vom la sugɛ la Kiresta puan, Yinɛ puan.

ref: Christ gives meaning to your life, and when he appears, you will also appear with him in glory.
text: Kiresta en de ya vom la han ta niiɛ na, yamam me wan lagum niiɛ la en, la na'ahegɔ.

ref: Don't be controlled by your body. Kill every desire for the wrong kind of sex. Don't be immoral or indecent or have evil thoughts. Don't be greedy, which is the same as worshiping idols.
text: Bala zuo, ku ya sɛla woo en boi ya puan dɛna tiŋa dinɛ zuo bunɔ wuu, yaluntuuma, dɛgerɔ tuuma, kunkoma'ahum, be'em kɔrehi, la nyika .

ref: God is angry with people who disobey him by doing these things.
text: Yele ana wa zuo ti Yinɛ sunsunyiim yɛ'a sɛba en ka nane la zuo na la.

ref: And this is exactly what you did, when you lived among people who behaved in this way.
text: Wunse'eren ha la, Yele ana mɛa ti yamam me yuum ita, yan yuum nan vɔi vom bumɔ taaba la.

ref: But now you must stop doing such things. You must quit being angry, hateful, and evil. You must no longer say insulting or cruel things about others.
text: Gee nananawa, la dela ya bahɛ yele aŋa woo, sunsunyiim, putuulum, nɛreba yu'ura sageŋɔ, kɛhega yetɔgum da zɛ'ɛta ya nɔya puan.

ref: And stop lying to each other. You have given up your old way of life with its habits.
text: Da pareni ya bɔ'ɔra taaba, yan diŋɛ yɛɛ naanɛ kɛka la, la de itegɔ la,

ref: Each of you is now a new person. You are becoming more and more like your Creator, and you will understand him better.
text: gee yɛ ya naanɛ paalega la pi' ya mɛhi, naanɛ se'ere en teere de mɛŋa saŋa woo ti de baŋɛ de Naana la mi'ilum la paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are a Greek or a Jew, or if you are circumcised or not. You may even be a barbarian or a Scythian, and you may be a slave or a free person. Yet Christ is all that matters, and he lives in all of us.
text: Teere dina puan la, Giriki ka boi ti Yuda bɔna; sɛba en ŋma ka boi, ti sɛba en ka ŋma bɔna; nɛreyooro ka boi ti nɛreyaalehi bɔna; da'aba ka boi ti sɛba en boi fai bɔna. Gee Kiresta en de za'a gee bɔna ba za'a puan.

ref: God loves you and has chosen you as his own special people. So be gentle, kind, humble, meek, and patient.
text: Wuu Yinɛ en looe ya la, ti ya dɛna tuulum nɛreba ti a nɔŋɛ la, bagɛ mɛhi la ya nɛreba giile, itesuŋɔ, sigehe mɛa, ma'ma', la sudi'ihego

ref: Love is more important than anything else. It is what ties everything completely together.
text: Se'ere en gani za'a dela, bagɛ ya mɛhi la nɔŋerɛ, sɛla en bobere ya lagena taaba, ti ya tara nɔyinɛ sunɛ la taaba en bala.

ref: Each one of you is part of the body of Christ, and you were chosen to live together in peace. So let the peace that comes from Christ control your thoughts. And be grateful.
text: Gee me bahɛ ti Kiresta suma'ahum pirɛ ya suya, yele se'ere zuo ti ba yi' ya na pa'ahɛ inganɛ deyina lageŋɔ la puan en bala. Gee la ya me bo'ori Yinɛ fara.

ref: Let the message about Christ completely fill your lives, while you use all your wisdom to teach and instruct each other. With thankful hearts, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs to God.
text: Bahɛ ya ti Kiresta yelehum la bɔna ya puan seke. Pa'ali ya gee ka'ana taaba la yɛm, gee ti nyɛɛma en boi ya suya puan la, tari ya saaŋɔ la baaŋa, la Sia puan yuuma ya suya puan bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ.

ref: Whatever you say or do should be done in the name of the Lord Jesus, as you give thanks to God the Father because of him. Some Rules for Christian Living
text: Gee ti sɛla woo ti ya ita, la dɛna tugum bii tuuma, iti ya sɛla woo Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la puan, bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ en de Sɔ la fara dɔla Yezu puan. Kiresta kɔma yiya puan ze'elego

ref: A wife must put her husband first. This is her duty as a follower of the Lord.
text: Pɔgeba, sigeha ya mɛhi bɔ'ɔra ya sireba, wuu lan nari se'em Daana dɔlega puan la.

ref: A husband must love his wife and not abuse her.
text: Sireba, nɔŋɛ ya pɔgeba, gee de makɛ iti ya ba vɛgela vɛgela.

ref: Children must always obey their parents. This pleases the Lord.
text: Kɔma, nane ya ya dɔgereba yele se'ere woo puan, gee yele dina en tigeri Daana la nini.

ref: Parents, don't be hard on your children. If you are, they might give up.
text: Sɔduma, da nyiiha ya kɔma suya gee bala wan bahɛ ti ba sia yehe ya.

ref: Slaves, you must always obey your earthly masters. Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. Honor the Lord and serve your masters with your whole heart.
text: Da'aba, nane ya sɛba en sɔi ya tiŋa zuo wa la sɛla woo puan, dagi la ban boi ya nɛŋan la ma'a, ti ya ita ti ba puurɛ, gee ita ya bala la ya suure za'a, la Daana la zoolum.

ref: Do your work willingly, as though you were serving the Lord himself, and not just your earthly master.
text: Fum tuunɛ en de se'ere woo la, dikɛ fum mɛŋa bo' de wuu fum tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana la gee dagi fum pee asaala puurɛ,

ref: In fact, the Lord Christ is the one you are really serving, and you know he will reward you.
text: yan diŋɛ mina ti Daana la puan ya wan ta to'e tɔtɛ ti la dɛna ya yɔɔrɛ la. Ya tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana Kiresta la.

ref: But Christ has no favorites! He will punish evil people, just as they deserve.
text: Se'em en ka tuni sɛla en de ma'a la ba wan lebehe yɔ en sɛla woo ti a ka tum ma'a la, ti lugehego ka bɔna.

ref: Slave owners, be fair and honest with your slaves. Don't forget that you have a Master in heaven.
text: Kpɛ'ɛnduma, bo'ori ya ya da'aba buurɔ, la sɛla en de ma'a, gee ya mi' ti yamam me tari kpɛ'ɛma mɛ en boi tinsuŋan ha. Pa'ahegɔ kã'aŋɔ

ref: Never give up praying. And when you pray, keep alert and be thankful.
text: Boni ya pu'uhegɔ puan saŋa woo gurɛ yan pu'uheri se'ere zuo la ya suya puan suŋa suŋa la fara bu'a.

ref: Be sure to pray that God will make a way for us to spread his message and explain the mystery about Christ, even though I am in jail for doing this.
text: Gee me pu'uha bɔ'ɔra tumam me, ti Yinɛ yu'ɛ kuleŋa bo' yelehum la, ti tu muulɛ Kiresta yele sɛ'a en sugɛ la, yele sɛ'a zuo ti mam bɔna paka deen wa,

ref: Please pray that I will make the message as clear as possible.
text: ti n ta'am lilege de suŋa wuu mam suni ti n muulɛ de se'em la.

ref: When you are with unbelievers, always make good use of the time.
text: Tara ya mɛhi suŋa la yɛm yiŋa duma zi'an, tari ya sansɛka woo ti ya nyɛ tuna suŋa suŋa.

ref: Be pleasant and hold their interest when you speak the message. Choose your words carefully and be ready to give answers to anyone who asks questions. Final Greetings
text: Bahɛ ya ti ya tugum ana suŋa saŋa woo, ti saalum bɔna bu puan, ti ya mina yan wan lebehe nɛra woo se'em. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ

ref: Tychicus is the dear friend, who faithfully works and serves the Lord with us, and he will give you the news about me.
text: Takikuse wan yele ya mam yɛla woo. A dela nɔŋerɛ suuɔ, sakerɛ yameŋa, gee me dɛna mam da'aberɛ tadaana Daana la puan.

ref: I am sending him to cheer you up by telling you how we are getting along.
text: Mam tum en ya zi'an na la dina wa zuo, ti ya mina tumam ani se'em, gee me kpemehe ya suya.

ref: Onesimus, the dear and faithful follower from your own group, is coming with him. The two of them will tell you everything that has happened here.
text: A kini me na la Onesimuse, en de sakerɛ daana, tu suuɔ ti tu nɔŋɛ, a dela yamam ayima. Ba wan yele ya sɛla woo en iti ka'lam.

ref: Aristarchus is in jail with me. He sends greetings to you, and so does Mark, the cousin of Barnabas. You have already been told to welcome Mark, if he visits you.
text: Aristakuse ti ba lagum pagɛ la mam la pu'uheri ya na, bala mɛa ti Maki en de Banabase taya bia la, se'em ti tu yɛta ya a yele wa me pu'uha ya na. A han wa'am ya zi'an na, to'e ya en suŋa.

ref: Jesus, who is known as Justus, sends his greetings. These three men are the only Jewish followers who have worked with me for the kingdom of God. They have given me much comfort.
text: Yezu se'em ti ba yi'ira Yusetuse la pu'uhi ya na. Bana ma'a en de sɛba n ŋma bɔna mam tuntuneba tadaanduma la puan Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum tuuma la puan. Bana en de sɛba en bugelɛ mam suure.

ref: Your own Epaphras, who serves Christ Jesus, sends his greetings. He always prays hard that you may fully know what the Lord wants you to do and that you may do it completely.
text: Epafaras, en de ya ayima la, dɛna Kiresta [Yezu] da'aberɛ pu'uhi ya na. A mɛɛrɛ saŋa woo pu'uhegɔ puan ya zuo, ti ya wan ze'ele bi'ɛ suŋa, gee ka bu'ura ya suure puan la Yinɛ sumboolum en de se'em.

ref: I have seen how much trouble he has gone through for you and for the followers in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
text: Mam tɔgɛ a yele mɛ, doohe en mu'ɛ nini tuna se'em bɔ'ɔra yamam la sɛba en boi Laodisiya la Hirapolise tihi puan la.

ref: Our dear doctor Luke sends you his greetings, and so does Demas.
text: Luki en de sunɔŋerɛ tiba la, la Demase pu'uhi ya na.

ref: Give my greetings to the followers at Laodicea, especially to Nympha and the church that meets in her home.
text: Pu'uhi ya n suurɔ seto en boi Laodisiya la, gee pu'uhɛ ya tu taya Nyimfa, la lageŋɔ la en boi a yire la.

ref: After this letter has been read to your people, be sure to have it read in the church at Laodicea. And you should read the letter that I have sent to them.
text: Ba han ta kãalɛ gɔŋɔ wa bo' ya ba'ahɛ, bahɛ ya ti ba kãalɛ kɔ bo' lageŋɔ la en boi Laodisiya tiŋa puan la me, gee la ya me kãalɛ gɔnseko en gulehɛ bo' Laodisiya tiŋa lageŋɔ la me.

ref: Remind Archippus to do the work that the Lord has given him to do.
text: Gee me yele Akipus yeti, “Bihɛ suŋa la fum ba'ahɛ tuunse'ere ti fum to'e Daana zi'an la.”

ref: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL. Don't forget that I am in jail. I pray that God will be kind to you.
text: Mam Pɔɔl en gulehɛ pu'uhegɔ wa la n mɛŋa nu'o. Tɛri ya mam boi paka deen la. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya.

ref: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God the Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ. I pray that God will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Mam Pɔɔl, Silevanuse, la Timoti en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra sakereba Lageŋɔ la en boi Tɛsalonika la, Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Daana Yezu Kiresta. Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum bɔna la ya. Tɛsalonikaduma sakerɛ en de makerɛ

ref: We thank God for you and always mention you in our prayers. Each time we pray,
text: Tumam daarɛ woo bo'ori Yinɛ fara mɛ ya za'a zuo, gee puta ya yu'ura tu pu'uhegɔ puan saŋa woo.

ref: we tell God our Father about your faith and loving work and about your firm hope in our Lord Jesus Christ. The Thessalonians' Faith and Example
text: Tu tɛɛra tu Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la nɛŋan ya sakerɛ tuuma, la ya nɔŋerɛ tuuma, la yan ze' se'em kankaŋi ya beere puti'irɛ la puan se'em tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan la.

ref: My dear friends, God loves you, and we know he has chosen you to be his people.
text: Tu mi', tu suurɔ wa en de Yinɛ sunɔŋerɛ nɛreba wa, ti eŋa en looe ya,

ref: When we told you the good news, it was with the power and assurance that come from the Holy Spirit, and not simply with words. You knew what kind of people we were and how we helped you.
text: beni zuo, tu faarɛ yelehum muulegɔ la ka wa'am ya zi'an na dɛna la yetɔgum ma'a, gee bu wa'am mɛ na la paŋa, la Fa'a Sia, la suteere paŋa. Wuu yamam en mi' tumam en pa'alɛ ya tumam en de nɛresɛba taaba bɔna la ya yamam zuo la.

ref: So, when you accepted the message, you followed our example and the example of the Lord. You suffered, but the Holy Spirit made you glad.
text: Ti yamam me tɔgehɛ tumam, la tu Daana la, se'ere en sɔi la, la yan pun nyɛta namehegɔ la, ya to'e yelehum la mɛ la nyɛɛma sɛ'a ti Fa'a Sia bo' la mɛ.

ref: You became an example for all the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia.
text: Bala ti ya dɛna makerɛ bo' sakereba la woo en boi Masedoniya la Akaiya tihi la.

ref: And because of you, the Lord's message has spread everywhere in those regions. Now the news of your faith in God is known all over the world, and we don't have to say a thing about it.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana yelehum la doohe ya puan yehe mɛ, dagi la bu paaɛ la Masedoniya la Akaiya tihi ma'a puan, gee zi'an woo, ya sakerɛ se'ere tara Yinɛ puan la niiɛ mɛ, ti la pun ka pakɛ ti tumam le tɔgɛ de yele.

ref: Everyone is talking about how you welcomed us and how you turned away from idols to serve the true and living God.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛre sɛba en boi tihi sina puan la, puti tu yele mɛ yan to'e tu se'em, la yan ŋmɛregɛ baga kaabegɔ puan se'em lebe Yinɛ zi'an na, ti ya tuna bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ vua, la yelemɛŋerɛ Yinɛ wa se'em,

ref: They also tell how you are waiting for his Son Jesus to come from heaven. God raised him from death, and on the day of judgment Jesus will save us from God's anger.
text: gee gura a Dayua la en wan ze'ele saazuo na la, se'em ti a ihege en kum puan la. Yezu, se'em en faaɛ tɔ Yinɛ sunsunyiim sebo en kini na la.

ref: My friends, you know our time with you wasn't wasted.
text: Yamam mɛhi mi', n suurɔ, ti tu kinɛ ya zi'an na la, ka bahɛ yoo,

ref: As you remember, we had been mistreated and insulted at Philippi. But God gave us the courage to tell you the good news about him, even though many people caused us trouble.
text: gee la tumam en pun nyɛ namehegɔ, la zufagehum sebo itegɔ seko Filipi timpaberɛ wuu yan mi' de se'em la, tumam kelum tara inkpegere mɛ muula Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la, la sɛba en pun ka sakɛ bo' tu la en zo'e se'em la.

ref: We didn't have any hidden motives when we won you over, and we didn't try to fool or trick anyone.
text: Tumam en yeti ya sɛla la ka ze'ele la pa'ahegɔ bii putɛ'yoo, bii nigeŋɔ puan na,

ref: God was pleased to trust us with his message. We didn't speak to please people, but to please God who knows our motives.
text: gee wuu Yinɛ en bihɛ nam tɔ ti a dikɛ faarɛ yelehum wa iŋɛ tu nu'uhen se'em la, bala ti tumam ka tɔgera ti tu pee saaleba suya, gee tu pee Yinɛ suure, se'em en makere tu suya bihɛ la.

ref: You also know we didn't try to flatter anyone. God himself knows what we did wasn't a cover-up for greed.
text: Wuu yan mi' se'em la, ti Yinɛ me dɛna tumam aze'nyɛ daana se'em la, tu ka tabelɛ wa'am ya zi'an na la nɔmaalum yetɔgum, bii la kunkonyiim puti'irɛ,

ref: We were not trying to get you or anyone else to praise us.
text: bii tu ɛɛra pɛka saaleba zi'an, la dɛna yamam zi'an bii basɛba zi'an,

ref: But as apostles, we could have demanded help from you. After all, Christ is the one who sent us. We chose to be like children or like a mother nursing her baby.
text: tu ta'am soke ya sɛla yele, wuu tumam en de Kiresta tuntuneba la. Gee tu daa ani la ma'ma la ya, wuu pɔgesɔɔ en biheri ka mɛŋa kɔma la giile se'em la.

ref: We cared so much for you, and you became so dear to us, that we were willing to give our lives for you when we gave you God's message.
text: Tu ba' tara la ya giile gaŋɛ zuo, ti tu iŋɛ tu puti'irɛ ti tu tɔrɛ la ya, dagi la Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la ma'a, gee tu mɛhi vom. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya ba' bɔna la tu suya puan.

ref: My dear friends, you surely haven't forgotten our hard work and hardships. You remember how night and day we struggled to make a living, so we could tell you God's message without being a burden to anyone.
text: Ya tɛri tumam n daa tuni gee nameha se'em, n suurɔ, tu daa tuni la bala yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa za'a, ti tu da dɛna zeero bo' ya, tumam n muulɛ Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la bɔ'ɔra ya la.

ref: Both you and God are witnesses that we were pure and honest and innocent in our dealings with you followers of the Lord.
text: Yamam dela aze'nyɛduma, la Yinɛ me, tumam en de tuulum, la tuuntɔɔ, ka tara tuure tu itegɔ puan se'em bɔ'ɔra yamam sɛba en nam la.

ref: You also know we did everything for you that parents would do for their own children.
text: Wuu yan mi' se'em la, tumam iti ya woo mɛ, wuu Sɔ en iti a kɔma se'em la,

ref: We begged, encouraged, and urged each of you to live in a way that would honor God. He is the one who chose you to share in his own kingdom and glory.
