text: ka'ana, gee kpɛmeha ya, gee me bɛlena ya ti ya vɔna vom sebo taaba n nari bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ, se'em en yi' ya ki'ihɛ a mɛŋa sɔ'ɔlum, la a suŋa puan la.

ref: We always thank God that you believed the message we preached. It came from him, and it isn't something made up by humans. You accepted it as God's message, and now he is working in you.
text: Tu me kelum bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ fara mɛ saŋa woo yele dina wa zuo. Ti yan to'e Yinɛ yelehum la ti ya wum tu zi'an la, ya nam bu mɛ, ti bu dagi asaala yelehum, gee bu sirum dela Yinɛ yelehum sebo en tuni la yamam sɛba en nam la suŋa suŋa.

ref: My friends, you did just like God's churches in Judea and like the other followers of Christ Jesus there. And so, you were mistreated by your own people, in the same way they were mistreated by their people.
text: Gee yamam n suurɔ wa, ya dela sɛba en tɔgehi Yinɛ lageŋɔ la en boi Kiresta Yezu puan bɔna Yudiya la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya namehɛ la bala mɛa ya mɛhi nɛreba zi'an, wuu ban yuum iŋɛ Yuuduma puan se'em la.

ref: Those evil people killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and they even chased us away. God doesn't like what they do and neither does anyone else.
text: Sɛba en ku' Daana Yezu, la nɔtɔgeheba la, gee sa'ɛ tu bahɛ yiŋa la. Ba ba' tum sagum la Yinɛ suure, gee ka sakera bɔ'ɔra se'em se'em,

ref: They keep us from speaking his message to the Gentiles and from leading them to be saved. They have always gone too far with their sins. Now God has finally become angry and will punish them. Paul Wants To Visit the Church Again
text: doohe la ban pɛregeri tɔ ti tu da tɔgera yelehum la bɔ'ɔra sɛba en dagi Yuuduma, ti ba nyɛ faarɛ la. Ŋwana ti saŋa woo, ba be'em la malum pa'ahera, gee ti Yinɛ sunsunyiim wan sige ba zuo mɛ ba'ahegɔ puan. Pɔɔl le bɔta a bihɛ Tɛsalonikaduma

ref: My friends, we were kept from coming to you for a while, but we never stopped thinking about you. We were eager to see you and tried our best to visit you in person.
text: Gee han dɛna tumam n suurɔ, saŋa fii wa tumam han lebege kiibehe la tumam en welege tu mɛhi ya zi'an ingɔŋɔ wa puan la, gee dagi suure puan. Tu ba' bɔta mɛ la sunɔŋerɛ ti tu nyɛ taaba la tu nini.

ref: After all, when the Lord Jesus appears, who else but you will give us hope and joy and be like a glorious crown for us?
text: Beni en de tumam beere puti'irɛ, bii nyɛɛma, bii nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a muŋa tu Daana Yezu nɛŋan, a lɛbega daarɛ na la? La dagi la yamam?

ref: You alone are our glory and joy!
text: Ɛ̃ɛ, yamam en de tumam na'ahegɔ la tu nyɛɛma!

ref: Finally, we couldn't stand it any longer. We decided to stay in Athens by ourselves
text: Ŋwana, tumam en daa kan le ta'am ŋmibe la zuo, tu daa iŋɛ tu puti'irɛ ti tu yee la tu ma'a Atense timpaberɛ puan,

ref: and send our friend Timothy to you. He works with us as God's servant and preaches the good news about Christ. We wanted him to make you strong in your faith and to encourage you.
text: gee tum Timoti tu suuɔ, la tu tuntuna tadaana Yinɛ puan, se'em en muuli Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la, ti a wa'am na bo' ya paŋa, gee kpemehe ya ya sakerɛ la zuo,

ref: We didn't want any of you to be discouraged by all these troubles. You knew we would have to suffer,
text: ti ya se'em da miina mɛlehegɔ kuna wa zuo. Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya mɛhi mi' ti tumam n lu' tu suya ti tu paaɛ zi'an n bala.

ref: because when we were with you, we told you this would happen. And we did suffer, as you well know.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tumam n daa boi la ya la, tu pun daa yele ya mɛ, gee ti la nan ka paaɛ, ti tu wan nyaa mɛlehɛ, ti la sirum wa'am na dɛna bala, wuu yan mi' de se'em la.

ref: At last, when I could not wait any longer, I sent Timothy to find out about your faith. I hoped Satan had not tempted you and made all our work useless.
text: Yele dina wa zuo, mam daa kan le ta'am ŋmibe la zuo, ti n tum na, ti n baŋɛ ya sakerɛ la en ze' se'em. Mam zoti la dabeem ti daan se'ere mageha la magehɛ ya bihɛ mɛ, ti tu namehegɔ tuuma la bahɛ yoo. Timoti inkpegere sɔɔleŋa

ref: Timothy has now come back from his visit with you and has told us about your faith and love. He also said that you always have happy memories of us and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
text: Gee Timoti nan kɔ'ɔm ze'ele la ya zi'an wa'am tu zi'an na wa. A tari la yelesuma na bo' tu doohe ya sakerɛ, la ya nɔŋerɛ en ani se'em. A me yele tu ti ya kɔ'ɔm tɛra tu yele mɛ suŋa suŋa, gee mu'ɛ bɔta ti ya nyɛ tɔ, wuu tumam en kɔ'ɔm bɔta ti tu nyɛ ya se'em la.

ref: My friends, even though we have a lot of trouble and suffering, your faith makes us feel better about you.
text: Ŋwana zuo, n suurɔ, tumam susageŋɔ, la tu mɛlehegɔ wa puan za'a, tumam ihe kpeŋe mɛ yamam zuo, doohe la ya sakerɛ la.

ref: Your strong faith in the Lord is like a breath of new life.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam wan vɔna, ya han kelum sɛ zɛ'a kankaŋi Daana la puan.

ref: How can we possibly thank God enough for all the happiness you have brought us?
text: Tu wan iŋɛ la ŋwani bo' Yinɛ fara yamam zuo la ti la seke, la nyɛɛma sɛ'a ti tumam tara Yinɛ nɛŋan yamam zuo wa?

ref: Day and night we sincerely pray that we will see you again and help you to have an even stronger faith.
text: Yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa, tu pu'uhi mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti tu nyɛ ya la tu nini, la tu malum lebehe sɛla woo en pɔ̃hegɛ ya sakerɛ puan la.

ref: We pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus will let us visit you.
text: Nananawa, tu Yinɛ, la Sɔ la mɛŋa, la tu Daana Yezu pa'alɛ tu sore, ti tu wa'am ya zi'an na.

ref: May the Lord make your love for each other and for everyone else grow by leaps and bounds. This is how our love for you has grown.
text: Gee ti Daana la iŋɛ ya ti ya zo'e, gee pirɛ la nɔŋerɛ bɔ'ɔra taaba la nɛra woo, wuu tumam en pirɛ la nɔŋerɛ bɔ'ɔra ya se'em la.

ref: And when our Lord comes with all his people, I pray he will make your hearts pure and innocent in the sight of God the Father.
text: A wan kpemehe ya suya la Yinɛ tuulum vom puan, ti ya ka tara tuure tu Yinɛ, la tu Sɔ la nɛŋan, tu Daana Yezu, la a nɛresunduma la kinɛ puan na.

ref: Finally, my dear friends, since you belong to the Lord Jesus, we beg and urge you to live as we taught you. Then you will please God. You are already living that way, but try even harder.
text: Ba'ahegɔ puan, n suurɔ, tu yeti ya mɛ gee ka'ana ya Daana Yezu puan ti, wuu yan zamehɛ tu zi'an, yan wan vɔna se'em tigɛ Yinɛ nini la, , malum iŋɛ ya bala paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: Remember the instructions we gave you as followers of the Lord Jesus.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' pa'alegɔ seko ti tu dikɛ bo' ya doohe Daana Yezu puan la.

ref: God wants you to be holy, so don't be immoral in matters of sex.
text: Ŋwana en de Yinɛ sumboolum, ti ya sukɛ ya mɛhi. Ya ayima ayima yehe ya mɛhi yaluntuuma puan.

ref: Don't be a slave of your desires or live like people who don't know God.
text: Dagi la ya tara kunkoma'ahum wuu sɛba en dagi Yuuduma en ka mi' Yinɛ la.

ref: You must not cheat any of the Lord's followers in matters of sex. Remember, we warned you that he punishes everyone who does such things.
text: Ti ya se'em da tuue, bii fã a suuɔ yele ana wa puan, beni zuo, Daana la mɛŋa en wan lerege yele ana woo puan, wuu tumam en pun te'ele yele ya gee ka'am ya la ninmu'urɛ se'em la.

ref: God didn't choose you to be filthy, but to be pure.
text: Yinɛ ka yi' tu na bahɛ dɛgerɔ tuuma puan, gee tu dɛna Yinɛ tuulum duma.

ref: So if you don't obey these rules, you are not really disobeying us. Instead, you are disobeying God, who gives you his Holy Spirit.
text: Ŋwana zuo, se'em woo en zagehɛ yele ana wa, ka zagehɛ la asaala kpe'eŋo gee Yinɛ, se'em me en bo'ori tu a Fa'a Sia la.

ref: We don't have to write you about the need to love each other. God has taught you to do this,
text: Nananawa, han dɛna suurɔ nɔŋerɛ yele, ya ka eere se'em gulehɛ bo' ya na. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en pun pa'alɛ yamam ti ya nɔŋɛ taaba mɛ.

ref: and you already have shown your love for all his people in Macedonia. But, my dear friends, we ask you to do even more.
text: Ti la me sirum dɛna asira ti ya nɔŋɛ ya suurɔ woo en boi Masedoniya timpaberɛ puan mɛ. Gee tumam ka'ani ya mɛ n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ti ya malum iŋɛ bala paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: Try your best to live quietly, to mind your own business, and to work hard, just as we taught you to do.
text: Ya kpeŋe ya mɛhi bɔna bããlam, biha ya mɛhi yɛla, gee sakɛ ya tuna la ya mɛhi nu'uhi wuu tumam en pa'alɛ ya se'em la,

ref: Then you will be respected by people who are not followers of the Lord, and you won't have to depend on anyone. The Lord's Coming
text: la ya ta'am tara ya mɛhi suŋa, ita sum bɔ'ɔra yiŋa duma, gee da dikɛ ya mɛhi delum se'em. Daana la lɛbega na

ref: My friends, we want you to understand how it will be for those followers who have already died. Then you won't grieve over them and be like people who don't have any hope.
text: Gee tu ka boti ya bɔna aliko n suurɔ, doohe sɛba en ki la yele, ti ya suya da ba' sagum wuu sɛba en ka tara beere puti'irɛ la.

ref: We believe Jesus died and was raised to life. We also believe that when God brings Jesus back again, he will bring with him all who had faith in Jesus before they died.
text: Tumam en diŋɛ nam ti Yezu ki' mɛ gee ihege la, bala mɛa ti Yezu puan, Yinɛ wan tari sɛba en ki la en la wa'am na.

ref: Our Lord Jesus told us that when he comes, we won't go up to meet him ahead of his followers who have already died.
text: Ŋwana zuo, ti tu yɛta ya Daana yelehum la puan yeti, tumam sɛba en vɔi wa, la sɛba en yee gura Daana kinɛ na la, sore ka boi ti tumam wan tole sɛba en ki la nɛŋa.

ref: With a loud command and with the shout of the chief angel and a blast of God's trumpet, the Lord will return from heaven. Then those who had faith in Christ before they died will be raised to life.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la mɛŋa wan sige na, doohe la nuurɛ ti ba bo' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa, ti suŋereba nɛŋa daana la yi' na, ti Yinɛ tintanɛ la pɛɛbera, ti sɛba en ki' Kiresta puan la ihege yia,

ref: Next, all of us who are still alive will be taken up into the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the sky. From that time on we will all be with the Lord forever.
text: ti tumam sɛba en vɔi wa, la tu sɛba en yee wa, wan naaɛ la ba sagbama puan, ti tu to'ohe Daana la kusebego puan, bala ti tu wan bɔna la Daana la wuuha wuuha.

ref: Encourage each other with these words.
text: Bala, kpemehe ya taaba la yelehum buna wa.

ref: I don't need to write you about the time or date when all this will happen.
text: Nananawa, han dɛna saŋa la bii dabeherɛ la, n suurɔ, ya dagi ba gulehɛ sɛsɛla bo' ya.

ref: You surely know that the Lord's return will be as a thief coming at night.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mɛhi mi' suŋa ti Daana la dabeherɛ la, wan wa'am na wuu yu'uŋɔ nageyiga la.

ref: People will think they are safe and secure. But destruction will suddenly strike them like the pains of a woman about to give birth. And they won't escape.
text: Ba han ta yeti, “Suma'ahum bii fai nyaa boi.” Bilam bilam ti sageŋɔ wan sige ba zuo na, ti too la ana wuu pɔka n virege la, ti piirɛ kan bɔna!

ref: My dear friends, you don't live in darkness, and so that day won't surprise you like a thief.
text: Gee yamam n suurɔ, ya ka boi lika puan, ti de daarɛ la ta iŋɛ ya pakerɛ wuu nageyiga la.

ref: You belong to the light and live in the day. We don't live in the night or belong to the dark.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya za'a dela niihum kɔma, dɛna beyiine kɔma. Tumam dagi yu'uŋɔ, bii lika puan kɔma.

ref: People sleep during the night, and some even get drunk.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba n gihere, gihere la yu'uŋɔ, ti sɛba n bugeri, bugera yu'uŋɔ.

ref: But we belong to the day. So we must stay sober and let our faith and love be like a suit of armor. Our firm hope that we will be saved is our helmet.
text: Gee tumam en de beyiine kɔma la, bahɛ ya ti tu tara tu mɛhi suŋa, gee yɛ sakerɛ la nɔŋerɛ ti la dɛna tu nyu'ɔ tɛ'ɛlega, Gee han dɛna tu zuo wanɛ, tu vuge faarɛ en de tu beere puti'irɛ la.

ref: God doesn't intend to punish us, but wants us to be saved by our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ka maahum tu ba'ahegɔ zi'an bahɛ la sunsunyiim puan, gee tu nyɛ faarɛ doohe tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan,

ref: Christ died for us, so we could live with him, whether we are alive or dead when he comes.
text: se'em en ki bo' tu, ti tu pige woo, tu gihi woo, tu ta'am vɔna la en.

ref: This is why you must encourage and help each other, just as you are already doing. Final Instructions and Greetings
text: Ŋwana zuo, kpemehe ya taaba gee mɛta taaba wuu yan pun ita se'em la. Ba'ahegɔ kã'aŋɔ, pu'uhegɔ la ka'a

ref: My friends, we ask you to be thoughtful of your leaders who work hard and tell you how to live for the Lord.
text: Gee tu beleni ya mɛ tu suurɔ, na'aheri ya sɛba en tuni ya puan la, sɔna ya biha Daana la puan, gee ka'ana ya la.

ref: Show them great respect and love because of their work. Try to get along with each other.
text: To'ohe ya ba suŋa suŋa la nɔŋerɛ, ba tuuma la zuo. Boni ya la suma'ahum la taaba.

ref: My friends, we beg you to warn anyone who isn't living right. Encourage anyone who feels left out, help all who are weak, and be patient with everyone.
text: Tu yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ tu suurɔ, ti ya ka'ana sɛba en iti bura bura la, la ninmu'urɛ, gee kpɛmeha sɛba puti'ira en kɔ'ɛ la. Suŋeri ya tarenduma, tari ya sugeri bɔ'ɔra ba za'a.

ref: Don't be hateful to people, just because they are hateful to you. Rather, be good to each other and to everyone else.
text: Bihɛ ya suŋa ti ya se'em da dikɛ be'em yɔ be'em. Gee saŋa woo, eere ya ti ya ita sum bɔ'ɔra taaba, la nɛra woo.

ref: Always be joyful
text: Nyɛɛni ya saŋa woo,

ref: and never stop praying.
text: pu'uheri ya da gɔ'ɔra,

ref: Whatever happens, keep thanking God because of Jesus Christ. This is what God wants you to do.
text: bo'ori ya Yinɛ fara yelese'ere woo puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sumboolum n bala bo' ya Yezu Kiresta puan.

ref: Don't turn away God's Spirit
text: Da kiiheri ya Fa'a Sia la.

ref: or ignore prophecies.
text: Da zɛregerɛ ya nɔtɔgeheba yelehum la,

ref: Put everything to the test. Accept what is good
text: gee magehere ya sɛla woo biha. Gurɛ ya sɛla en ani suŋa la mɔpi.

ref: and don't have anything to do with evil.
text: Yehe ya mɛhi be'em woo puan.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will make you completely holy. And may your spirit, soul, and body be kept healthy and faultless until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
text: Suma'ahum Yinɛ la mɛŋa wan sukɛ ya za'a za'a. Ya sia za'a, la ya puti'irɛ za'a, la ya iŋa za'a, la ya inma'ahum za'a, maahum ya biŋe gura tu Daana Yezu Kiresta kinɛ na.

ref: The one who chose you can be trusted, and he will do this.
text: Se'em en yi' ya la dela sakerɛ daana, a wan iŋɛ ŋwana wa.

ref: Friends, please pray for us.
text: N suurɔ, pu'uheri ya bo'ori tɔ.

ref: Give the Lord's followers a warm greeting.
text: Pu'uheri ya suurɔ woo la tuulum kɔkerɛ.

ref: In the name of the Lord I beg you to read this letter to all his followers.
text: Mam ka'ani ya mɛ Daana la puan, ti ya kãalɛ gɔnkuna wa bo' suurɔ la za'a.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
text: Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ wan bɔna la ya.

ref: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God our Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Mam Pɔɔl, Silevanuse, la Timoti, en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra Tɛsalonikaduma lageŋɔ la n boi doohe Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta paŋa puan la.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! When Christ Returns
text: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ tu Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an na bɔna la ya. Fara bu'a

ref: My dear friends, we always have good reason to thank God for you, because your faith in God and your love for each other keep growing all the time.
text: Tu dela tu bo' Yinɛ fara saŋa woo ya zuo n suurɔ, wuu lan nari se'em. Beni zuo, ya sakerɛ la zo'ore mɛ yabera, ti ya woo en nɔŋɛ taaba se'em la malum pa'aha.

ref: This is why we brag about you to all of God's churches. We tell them how patient you are and how you keep on having faith, even though you are going through a lot of trouble and suffering.
text: Ŋwana zuo, tumam mɛhi ŋmɛ'ɛri la nyɔ' ya zuo, Yinɛ lageŋɔ la puan, la yan sɛ ze'ele kankaŋi gee tara sakerɛ ya mɛlehegɔ a too seko puan ti ya ŋmibera wa. Kiresta gakerɛ la kini na

ref: All this shows that God judges fairly and is making you fit to share in his kingdom for which you are suffering.
text: Ŋwana en de daalehum bo' Yinɛ tuuntɔɔ gakerɛ la, a maahum ti a bahɛ ti ya nara mɛ la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la, ti ya me namehera bu zuo la.

ref: It is only right for God to punish everyone who is causing you trouble,
text: La dela ma'a bo' Yinɛ ti a lerege pɔgeleŋɔ bo' sɛba en pɔgelum ya la,

ref: but he will give you relief from your troubles. God will do the same for us, when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven with his powerful angels
text: gee bo' sɛba ti ba pɔgelum la me fai, ta ta' la tumam me, Daana Yezu han ta ze'ele saazuo niiɛ na, la a suŋereba bumpahi la.

ref: and with a flaming fire. Our Lord Jesus will punish anyone who doesn't know God and won't obey his message.
text: Kina na la bugum en diti zigi zigi, lɛregera sɛba en ka mi' Yinɛ, la sɛba en ka nam Daana Yezu faarɛ yelehum la.

ref: Their punishment will be eternal destruction, and they will be kept far from the presence of our Lord and his glorious strength.
text: Bana n wan ta nyɛ namehegɔ n ka tari ba'ahegɔ sageŋɔ la. Sɛba ti ba yelege ba Daana la nɛŋan, a suŋa, la a paŋa la puan.

ref: This will happen on the day when the Lord returns to be praised and honored by all who have faith in him and belong to him. This includes you, because you believed what we said.
text: De daarɛ la, a han ta wa'am na ti a Niiɛ a suŋa la, a suŋa la wan niiɛ a nɛresunduma la puan, ti sɛba woo en nam zɛ'a yɛli a suŋa la zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, ya nam tumam aze'nyɛ muulegɔ la mɛ.

ref: God chose you, and we keep praying that God will make you worthy of being his people. We pray for God's power to help you do all the good things you hope to do and your faith makes you want to do.
text: Lan paaɛ ŋwana beene la, tu pu'uheri saŋa woo mɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, suhera ti Yinɛ wan bahɛ ti ya nara la a yi'a la ti a yi' la. A wan doohe a paŋa la puan bahɛ ti ya putɛ'suma la ya sakerɛ tum,

ref: Then, because of the undeserved grace of God and our Lord Jesus Christ, you will bring honor to the name of our Lord Jesus, and he will bring honor to you.
text: ti tu Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la nyɛ na'ahegɔ ya puan, ti yamam me nyɛ na'ahegɔ a puan, doohe tu Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan.

ref: When our Lord Jesus returns, we will be gathered up to meet him. So I ask you, my friends,
text: Gee han dɛna tu Daana Yezu Kiresta kinɛ na la, la tumam n wan ta lagehɛ taaba bɔna la en la, tu beleni ya mɛ, tu suurɔ,

ref: not to be easily upset or disturbed by people who claim the Lord has already come. They may say they heard this directly from the Holy Spirit, or from someone else, or even that they read it in one of our letters.
text: ti ya da bu'ura ya puti'ira puan, bii ya pabegɛ Sia puan bii yelehum bii gɔŋɔ seko en zi'im ze'ele tumam zi'an na yɛta ti, Daana dabeherɛ la pun paaɛ mɛ.

ref: He will brag and oppose everything holy or sacred. He will even sit in God's temple and claim to be God.
text: A ka sakɛ bo' se'em, gee zɛkɛ a mɛŋa gana sɛla woo ti ba yi'ira yinɛ bii na'ahegɔ daana woo, la a nyaa ta'am tara a kuka Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire puan, dikɛ a mɛŋa lebege Yinɛ.

ref: Don't you remember I told you this while I was still with you?
text: Yamam ka tɛri ti mam pun yele ya yele ana wa, mam daa kelum bɔna la ya la?

ref: You already know what is holding this wicked one back until it is time for him to come.
text: Ya mi' sɛla en gu'uri be'em daana wa kinɛ nananawa mɛ, gura a ta niiɛ a mɛŋa, a saŋa la han ta paaɛ.

ref: His mysterious power is already at work, but someone is holding him back. And the wicked one won't appear until this someone is out of the way.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em daana en ka kihi yele la nyaleŋɔ la pun tuna mɛ, gee la wan niiɛ la se'em ma'a ti se'em en gu'uri a paŋa nananawa la ta bahɛ.

ref: Then he will appear, but the Lord Jesus will kill him simply by breathing on him. He will be completely destroyed by the Lord's glorious return.
text: Bala ma'a ti se'em daana en ka kihi yele wa nyaa wan niiɛ a mɛŋa na, se'em ti Daana Yezu wan sagum en la a nuurɛ sabeeo, diihe en vi doohe a lɛbega la puan na.

ref: When the wicked one appears, Satan will pretend to work all kinds of miracles, wonders, and signs.
text: Se'em daana en ka kihi yele la kini na niiɛ mɛ Sagena la tuuma puan, se'em en tari pahi woo, kirehi, la pumpɔreŋɔ nyalema tuuma,

ref: Lost people will be fooled by his evil deeds. They could be saved, but they will refuse to love the truth and accept it.
text: la pa'ahegɔ kãra woo, bɔ'ɔra sɛba en wan bahɛ yoo, ban zagehɛ ba nɔŋɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la ba nyɛ faarɛ la.

ref: So God will make sure they are fooled into believing a lie.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ tum pa'ahegɔ paŋa na, ti ka bahɛ ti ba nam bo' pumpɔreŋɔ,

ref: All of them will be punished, because they would rather do evil than believe the truth. Be Faithful
text: ti la bahɛ ti sɛba woo n ka nam yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee dikɛ la kunkɔma'ahum la dɛgerɔ tuuma la nyɛ sageŋɔ. Ba looe ya bo' la Faarɛ

ref: My friends, the Lord loves you, and it is only natural for us to thank God for you. God chose you to be the first ones to be saved. His Spirit made you holy, and you put your faith in the truth.
text: Gee tumam dela tu bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ fara saŋa woo ya zuo, n suurɔ ti Daana la nɔŋɛ wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en looe ya ti ya dɛna wala diina faarɛ puan, doohe Fa'a Sia la en pee ya la, la yan nam yelemɛŋerɛ la.

ref: God used our preaching as his way of inviting you to share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Dina zuo, ti a yi' ya doohe tumam n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' ya la, ti ya ta'am tara tu Daana Yezu Kiresta suŋa la.

ref: My friends, this is why you must remain faithful and follow closely what we taught you in person and by our letters.
text: Bala zuo, n suurɔ, sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋi, gee gurɛ ya pa'ali seko ti tu pa'alɛ ya la. La dɛna tu nuurɛ pa'alegɔ, bii tu gulehɛ la gɔŋɔ bo' ya na.

ref: God our Father loves us. He treats us with undeserved grace and has given us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope. We pray that our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father
text: Nananawa, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta mɛŋa, la Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ se'em en nɔŋɛ tɔ gee doohe a zu'uhegɔ puan, bo' tu sumbugehum la beere putɛ'sunɛ en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la,

ref: will encourage you and help you always to do and say the right thing.
text: bugelɛ ya suya, gee kpemehe ya suya tunsuma woo la yetɔge sumɔ woo puan.

ref: Finally, our friends, please pray for us. This will help the message about the Lord to spread quickly, and others will respect it, just as you do.
text: Ba'ahegɔ puan, n suurɔ, pu'uheri ya bo'ori ya tɔ, ti Daana yelehum la yiregɛ kalam gee nyɛ na'ahegɔ zi'an woo, wuu bumɔ en ani se'em ya puan wa,

ref: Pray that we may be kept safe from worthless and evil people. After all, not everyone has faith.
text: gee ti tumam me piiɛ putɔpa, la nɛre ŋmɛlema duma nu'uhen. Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi nɛra woo en tari sakerɛ.

ref: But the Lord can be trusted to make you strong and protect you from harm.
text: Gee Daana la dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana. A wan sɛ ya ze'ele, gee gu' ya nɛrebɛ'a la zi'an.

ref: He has made us sure that you are obeying what we taught you and that you will keep on obeying.
text: Tumam nam Daana la mɛ ya zuo, ti ya iti, gee wan kelum ita yelesɛ'a za'a ti tu bo' ya nuurɛ ti ya ita la.

ref: I pray that the Lord will guide you to be as loving as God and as patient as Christ. Warnings against Laziness
text: Daana la bahɛ ti ya suya kiŋɛ Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ, la ŋmibega Kiresta puan. Zinyageduma ka'agɔ

ref: My dear friends, in the name of the Lord Jesus, I beg you not to have anything to do with any of your people who loaf around and refuse to obey the instructions we gave you.
text: Nananawa, tu bo'ori ya la nuurɛ tu zɔduma, Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ puan ti, ya zaaɛ la sakereba sɛba en vɔi zinyagerɛ vom, ka dɔla la pa'aleŋɔ la ti ba to'e tumam zi'an la.

ref: You surely know that you should follow our example. We didn't waste our time loafing,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mɛhi mi' yan de ya tɔgehɛ tumam se'em. Tu daa dagi zinyage duma tumam n daa boi la ya la,

ref: and we didn't accept food from anyone without paying for it. We didn't want to be a burden to any of you, so night and day we worked as hard as we could.
text: tu daa ka di se'em dia gee ka yɔ. Gee tu daa tuni mɛ gee wɔbegera muregere yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa, ti tu da dɛna zeero bo' ya se'em.

ref: We had the right not to work, but we wanted to set an example for you.
text: Ŋwana dagi la tu ka tari la sore ti tu soke ya tumam en boti sɛla, gee tu boti tu iŋɛ biŋe mɛ ti ya tɔgehɛ.

ref: Now we learn that some of you just loaf around and won't do any work, except the work of a busybody.
text: Tumam wum ti ya sɛba vɔi la zinyagerɛ vom, yeen kiilena, ka tara tuun vua.

ref: So, for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ, we ask and beg these people to settle down and start working for a living.
text: Nananawa, nɛre bana taaba wa, tu bo'ori ya nuurɛ, gee ka'ana ya Daana Yezu Kiresta puan, ti nɛra woo tum ti a nu'o paaɛ a nuuren .

ref: Dear friends, you must never become tired of doing right.
text: N suurɔ, da taregɛ ya la itesuŋɔ.

ref: Be on your guard against any followers who refuse to obey what we have written in this letter. Put them to shame by not having anything to do with them.
text: Mini ya sɛba en ka nam sɛla ti tu yɛta ya gɔnkuna wa puan wa. Da tari ya sɛsɛla ita la ba, bala ti vi wan di ba,

ref: Don't consider them your enemies. Instead, speak kindly to them as you would to any other follower. Final Prayer
text: gee la bala za'a, da bihɛ ya ba wuu ba dela ya dinduma, gee ka'ani ya ba la ninmu'urɛ wuu ya suurɔ la. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ, la ka'a

ref: I pray that the Lord, who gives peace, will always bless you with peace. May the Lord be with all of you.
text: Nananawa, suma'ahum Daana la mɛŋa wan bo' ya suma'ahum saŋa woo, sadɔɔ seko woo puan. Daana la bɔna la ya za'a.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to all of you.
text: Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya za'a.

ref: From Paul. God our Savior and Christ Jesus commanded me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, who gives us hope.
text: Mam Pɔɔl en de Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, doohe la nuurɛ ti Yinɛ en de tu faara la, la Kiresta Yezu en de tu beere puti'irɛ la bo' mam la.

ref: Timothy, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind and merciful to you. May they bless you with peace! Warning against False Teaching
text: N gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra la Timoti, en de mam mɛŋa dayua sakerɛ la puan. Zu'uhegɔ, nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Kiresta Yezu en de tu Daana la zi'an na la dɛna fum dinɛ. Mini ya pumpã'ahum pa'aleba la yele

ref: When I was leaving for Macedonia, I asked you to stay on in Ephesus and warn certain people there to stop spreading their false teachings.
text: N yeti fum mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, wuu mam daa yele fum se'em mam daa wa yɛ'a Masedoniya la, ti fum yee Ɛfisu, la fum pa'alɛ nɛreba basɛba ti ba da pa'ala pa'alegɔ en boi kuyima,

ref: You needed to warn them to stop wasting their time on senseless stories and endless lists of ancestors. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help anyone to do God's work that can only be done by faith.
text: ba da Sagena saŋa la yele yooro la yaabeduma tuuhum wogerɔ seto en yeen tara nɔkpɛ'ɛma kina na, ka bahera ti nɛra bɔkera Yinɛ suboolum la en dole sakerɛ puan la.

ref: You must teach people to have genuine love, as well as a good conscience and true faith.
text: Gee pa'alegɔ kuna wa tuunɛ dela, la bahɛ ti ba tara nɔŋerɛ se'ere en ze'ele susunɛ puan na, putɛ'sunɛ, la yelemɛŋerɛ sakerɛ.

ref: There are some who have given up these for nothing but empty talk.
text: Nɛreba basɛba bɔregɛ mɛ yele ana puan, gee dikɛ ba mɛhi ta bo' yetɔge yooro en ka tari vuurɛ,

ref: They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses. But they don't know what they are talking about, even though they think they do.
text: bɔta ti ba dɛna ze'elego la pa'aleba, gee me ka bɔkera ba mɛhi en yeti sɛla la, bii sɛla ti ba tɔgera la yele la.

ref: We know the Law is good, if it is used in the right way.
text: Tu mi' ti ze'elego la ani suŋa mɛ, se'em han dɔla dɛ nima nima.

ref: We also understand it wasn't given to control people who please God, but to control lawbreakers, criminals, godless people, and sinners. It is for wicked and evil people, and for murderers, who would even kill their own parents.
text: Ŋwana vuurɛ dela, fum bɔkɛ yeti, ze'elego la ka ze'ele bo' sɛba en ka tari tuure, gee sɛba en ka dole kihere, la sɛba en ka nane, la sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ, la be'em duma, sɛba en dagi tuuntɔɔ, la dɛgerɔ duma, sɛba en ku ba ma bii ba sɔ, la nɛre kuureba,

ref: The Law was written for people who are sexual perverts or who live as homosexuals or are kidnappers or liars or won't tell the truth in court. It is for anything else that opposes the correct teaching
text: yalum duma, buraahi en gã' taaba, sɛba en koohe nɛreba, pumparenduma, pumparum gakereba, la sɛla woo en ka dole yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ la,

ref: of the good news the glorious and wonderful God has given me. Being Thankful for God's Kindness
text: en dole faarɛ yelehum la suŋa en de sum Yinɛ wa dinɛ, ti a dikɛ bo' mam la. Pɔɔl pupeelum la Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ

ref: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me the strength for my work because he knew he could trust me.
text: Mam puurɛ pee mɛ la Kiresta Yezu tu Daana, se'em en bo' mam paŋa, beni zuo, a bihɛ nyɛ ti n nari mɛ, ti a looe mam ti n tuna bɔ'ɔra en,

ref: I used to say terrible and insulting things about him, and I was cruel. But Christ had mercy on me because I didn't know what I was doing, and I had not yet put my faith in him.
text: la mam daa pun tuura, dɔla sakereba la nama mɛleha ba, dɛna zabe-zaberega. Gee Yinɛ zɔ mam nimbu'ɔ mɛ, beni zuo, mam daa ka mi' mɛ gee tuna bala, mam ka nam la zuo,

ref: Christ Jesus our Lord treated me with undeserved grace and has greatly blessed my life with faith and love just like his own.
text: Daana zu'uhegɔ la ɔkɛ mam mɛ, la sakerɛ la nɔŋerɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la.

ref: “Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners.” This saying is true, and it can be trusted. I was the worst sinner of all!
text: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ, dɛna tu to'e bu nu'uhi siyi, ti Kiresta Yezu wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na ti a faaɛ la be'em duma, sɛba ti mam be'em gana ba za'a.

ref: But since I was worse than anyone else, God had mercy on me and let me be an example of the endless patience of Christ Jesus. He did this so that others would put their faith in Christ and have eternal life.
text: Gee dinɛ zuo ti a zɔ mam nimbu'ɔ, ti mam puan, be'em nɛŋa daana wa puan, ti Yezu Kiresta wan pa'alɛ a sugeri en ka tari makerɛ la, ti mam dɛna makerɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n wan wa'am na nam en ti ba nyɛ vom kamba'ahɛ.

ref: I pray that honor and glory will always be given to the only God, who lives forever and is the invisible and eternal King! Amen.
text: Na'kãtɛ sɛka en boi wuuha, se'em en ka kiiri, Na'liliŋɔ se'em ma'a en de Yinɛ la, sɔna na'ahegɔ la pɛka wuuha wuuha. Taari.

ref: Timothy, my son, the instructions I am giving you are based on what some prophets once said about you. If you follow these instructions, you will fight like a good soldier.
text: Pa'alegɔ kuna ti n dikɛ ita fum nu'uhen n bia Timoti, doohe la puresɛka en pun purɛ bahɛ fum zuo la. Ti fum han dɔla a, fum wan zabɛ zabesunɛ,

ref: You will be faithful and have a clear conscience. Some people have made a mess of their faith because they didn't listen to their consciences.
text: fum tara sakerɛ la putɛ'sunɛ. Ban zagehɛ ba suya en pa'alɛ ba sɛla la, basɛba sakerɛ wɔ̃rɛ mɛ wuu yoore en lu wɔ̃rɛ la.

ref: Two of them are Hymenaeus and Alexander. I have given these men over to the power of Satan, so they will learn not to oppose God.
text: Ba puan dela Hameneyɔsi la Alɛgezanda, sɛba ti mam dikɛ ba bo' Sagena la, ti ba nyɔgɛ yɛm da tuura Yinɛ.

ref: First of all, I ask you to pray for everyone. Ask God to help and bless them all, and tell God how thankful you are for each of them.
text: Sɛla en de yia, mam yeti, ya tara suhega, pu'uhegɔ, nupu'ɔ la fara bu'a pu'uha bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo.

ref: Pray for kings and others in power, so we may live quiet and peaceful lives as we worship and honor God.
text: Pu'uhi ya bɔ'ɔra naduma, la sɛba woo en boi kpe'em kãra puan. Bala ti tu wan bɔna bããlam la suma'ahum, la Yinɛ zoolum, la tibehum.

ref: This kind of prayer is good, and it pleases God our Savior.
text: Dina en de tuuntɔɔ gee tigɛ Yinɛ en de tu Faara la nini,

ref: God wants everyone to be saved and to know the whole truth, which is,
text: se'em en boti nɛra woo nyɛ faarɛ gee wa'am na baŋɛ mi'ilum sebo en boi yelemɛŋerɛ la puan la.

ref: There is only one God, and Christ Jesus is the only one who can bring us to God. Jesus was truly human, and he gave himself to rescue all of us.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ dela deyina, nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra me dela ayima, Yinɛ la saaleba tiŋasuka Ena daana la Kiresta Yezu, se'em en wa'am na dɛna asaala a mɛŋa la,

ref: This is why God chose me to be a preacher and an apostle of the good news. I am telling the truth. I am not lying. God sent me to teach the Gentiles about faith and truth.
text: Dina zuo ti a looe mam ti n muula, gee dɛna tuntuna , mam dela sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la pa'ala, sakerɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ puan.

ref: I want everyone everywhere to lift innocent hands toward heaven and pray, without being angry or arguing with each other.
text: Mam yi boti ni ti, lageŋɔ woo puan, buraahi pu'uha, gee zɛgera tuulum nu'uhi saazuo, ti susuya bii nɔkpɛ'ɛma da bɔna ba puan.

ref: I would like for women to wear modest and sensible clothes. They should not have fancy hairdos, or wear expensive clothes, or put on jewelry made of gold or pearls.
text: Bala mɛa ti pɔgehi la me dɛna ba yɛɛra lɔgerɔ seto en nari, makɛ ba suŋa, dɛna futo en ani suŋa. Dagi zuto pã'aŋɔ nyalema nyalema, bii salema lɔgerɔ, bii peranini, bii futo da'a en ba' kpe'em, gee sɛla en tari tibehum gani bala dela,

ref: Women who claim to love God should do helpful things for others,
text: ba nɔna la tuunsuma, wuu lan nari se'em bo' pɔge se'ehi en mi ban na'ahɛ Yinɛ se'em la.

ref: and they should learn by being quiet and paying attention.
text: Bahɛ ti pɔgehi zameha bããlam la sigehe n mɛŋa paaɛ paaɛ.

ref: They should be silent and not be allowed to teach or to tell men what to do.
text: Mam ka bo' sore ti pɔka pa'ala bii a tara kpe'eŋo gana buraa, a dela a ana bããlam.

ref: After all, Adam was created before Eve,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Adam ti ba naam yia gee nyaa naam Eva,

ref: and the man Adam wasn't the one who was fooled. It was the woman Eve who was completely fooled and sinned.
text: la me dagi Adam ti ba yuum pã'ahɛ la, gee pɔka la ti ba yuum pa'ahɛ ti a lebege be'em daana.

ref: But women will be saved by having children, if they stay faithful, loving, holy, and modest.
text: Gee la bala za'a, a wan nyɛ faarɛ doohe kɔma dɔgum puan, ba han kelum bɔna sakerɛ, nɔŋerɛ, tuuntɔɔ vom, la narehum.

ref: It is true that anyone who desires to be a church official wants to be something worthwhile.
text: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ, ti, se'em woo en boti a dɛna Yinɛ Lageŋɔ puan nɛŋa daana, a boti la tuunsunɛ.

ref: That's why officials must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. They must be self-controlled, sensible, well-behaved, friendly to strangers, and able to teach.
text: Bala, Yinɛ Lageŋɔ nɛŋa daana dela a zɔɛ tuurɛ, a dɛna pɔge yiŋa daana, a ana ma'ma', a tara puti'irɛ, a tara zuo, a tɔ'ɔha nɛreba, a tana pa'ala,

ref: They must not be heavy drinkers or troublemakers. Instead, they must be kind and gentle and not love money.
text: a da dɛna bugera, a da dɛna nɛre gbiri gbiri, gee a ana bããlam, a da dɛna zabe-zaberega, a da ba' nɔŋɛ ligeri.

ref: Church officials must be in control of their own families, and they must see that their children are obedient and always respectful.
text: A dela a tana tara a mɛŋa yire suŋa, bahɛ ti a kɔma sigehe ba mɛhi suŋa gee na'aha nɛreba sadɔɔ seko woo puan.

ref: If they don't know how to control their own families, how can they look after God's people?
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em han ka ta'am tara a mɛŋa yire suŋa, a wan iŋɛ la ŋwani ta'am tara Yinɛ lageŋɔ la?

ref: They must not be new followers of the Lord. If they are, they might become proud and be doomed along with the devil.
text: A da dɛna bia sakerɛ la puan, gee a wan ta pirɛ la pa'ali mɛŋa, lu Sagena la sageŋɔ puan.

ref: Finally, they must be well-respected by people who are not followers. Then they won't be trapped and disgraced by the devil. Church Officers
text: Se'ere en gani bala dela, a dɛna se'em ti yiŋa duma nam en, ti a da ta lu zufagehum puan, la Sagena la bɛregɔ puan. Kiimbibehi aŋa

ref: Church officers should be serious. They must not be liars, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money.
text: Yinɛ lageŋɔ Kiimbibehi me dela ba tara ninmu'urɛ, ba da dɛna lebe ke, lebe ke duma, ba da yuura daam zɔ'ɔra, ba da ba' dɛna bu n bɔteba,

ref: And they must have a clear conscience and hold firmly to what God has shown us about our faith.
text: ba gurɛ sakerɛ yelehum la en gaŋɛ asaala bɔkerɛ la kankaŋi, la putɛ'sunɛ.

ref: They must first prove themselves. Then if no one has anything against them, they can serve as officers.
text: Ba magehɛ ba yia bihɛ, ti ba han ze'ele ti tuure ka bɔna ba puan, bahɛ ya ti ba tuna kiimbibehi tuuma.

ref: Women must also be serious. They must not gossip or be heavy drinkers, and they must be faithful in everything they do.
text: Pɔgehi me dela ba tara ninmu'urɛ, ba da Sagena nɛreba yu'ura gee ba ana ma'ma', ba dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ nɛreba sɛla woo puan.

ref: Church officers must be faithful in marriage. They must be in full control of their children and everyone else in their home.
text: Kiimbibehi wa woo dela ba dɛna pɔge yiŋa duma, tana ba kɔma, la ba yiya biha suŋa.

ref: Those who serve well as officers will earn a good reputation and will be highly respected for their faith in Christ Jesus. The Mystery of Our Religion
text: Sɛba en tum suŋa ban de kiimbibehi la zuo la, ba nyɛ la ze'ele suŋɔ bo' ba mɛhi, gee tara buraanɛ sakerɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la. Pu'uhegɔ wa sugelum yele

ref: I hope to visit you soon. But I am writing these instructions,
text: Mam ti'ihɛ ti n wa'am ya zi'an me na nananawa, gee n gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra ya ti,

ref: so if I am delayed, you will know how everyone who belongs to God's family ought to behave. After all, the church of the living God is the strong foundation of truth.
text: n han yuura, ya wan baŋɛ lan nari ya ita se'em la taaba Yinɛ yire la en de Yinɛ vua wa pu'uhegɔ yire la, en de yelemɛŋerɛ la ziirɛ, la ɛ̃berɛ.

ref: Here is the great mystery of our religion: Christ came as a human. The Spirit proved that he pleased God, and he was seen by angels. Christ was preached to the nations. People in this world put their faith in him, and he was taken up to glory.
text: Bu'a ka boi, tumam pu'uhegɔ wa puan yele sɛ'a en gani asaala bɔkerɛ la, dela yele kãra, A daa niiɛ a mɛŋa la inganɛ puan, di buurɔ Sia la puan, suŋereba nyɛ en mɛ, ba muulɛ a yele mɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la puan ti tiŋa wa zuo nam en, a zom la zuin zuin ha la na'ahegɔ.

ref: God's Spirit clearly says that in the last days many people will turn from their faith. They will be fooled by evil spirits and by teachings that come from demons.
text: Nananawa, Fa'a Sia la yele ti la niiɛ mɛ, ti ba'ahegɔ saŋa la, sɛba wan zagehɛ ba Kiresta kɔma sakerɛ la, doohe la ban kelehe pã'ahegɔ sɛɛhi, la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi pa'alegɔ,

ref: They will also be fooled by the false claims of liars whose consciences have lost all feeling. These liars
text: doohe la birekɔgen-pumparenduma sɛba zuto en ka ŋmɛ'ɛri kibegera la.

ref: will forbid people to marry or to eat certain foods. But God created these foods to be eaten with thankful hearts by his followers who know the truth.
text: Ba pa'alegɔ la yeti pɔgedire kihi mɛ, gee pɛregera nɛreba ti ba da dita diihe sise'ehi, ti Yinɛ naam ti sɛba en nam gee mina yelemɛŋerɛ la to'e la pɛka dita la.

ref: Everything God created is good. And if you give thanks, you may eat anything.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛlawoo ti Yinɛ naam, ani la suŋa, sɛla ka de tu zagehɛ, tu han to'e de mɛ la pɛka.

ref: What God has said and your prayer will make it fit to eat. Paul's Advice to Timothy
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sukɛ de mɛ la yelehum la, la pɛka. Yezu tuntuna suŋa

ref: If you teach these things to other followers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus. You will show you have grown up on the teachings about our faith and on the good instructions you have obeyed.
text: Fum han dikɛ pa'ali kuna biŋe suurɔ la nɛŋan, fum wan dɛna Kiresta Yezu tuntuna suŋa, se'em n tigehɛ a mɛŋa la yelehum sebo en bo'ori sakerɛ la, la pa'ali suŋɔ seko ti fum dɔla wa la.

ref: Don't have anything to do with worthless, senseless stories. Work hard to be truly religious.
text: Da tara sɛla ita la yetɔge yooro la pɔgenya'ahi sɔlema, gee zamehɛ fum mɛŋa Yinɛ zoolum,

ref: We have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of everyone, but especially of those who have faith. This is why we work and struggle so hard.
text: Tara tara la zina, tu nameheri mɛ gee zigera, beni zuo, tu dikɛ tu beere puti'irɛ delum la Yinɛ vua wa en de asaala woo Faara wa, yele yele wuu sɛba en nam la.

ref: Teach these things and tell everyone to do what you say.
text: Yele ana ti fum wan mu'ɛ nini muula, gee pa'ala a.

ref: Don't let anyone make fun of you, just because you are young. Set an example for other followers by what you say and do, as well as by your love, faith, and purity.
text: Da bahɛ ti nɛra nɛra zeem fum bumbile paalum la zuo, gee tugum dɛna makerɛ bo' sakereba la, fum tugum, fum aŋa, nɔŋerɛ, sakerɛ, la fum filam vom.

ref: Until I arrive, be sure to keep on reading the Scriptures in worship, and don't stop preaching and teaching.
text: Gee ti n ta paaɛ na la, dikɛ fum mɛŋa bo' fum kãala yelehum la bɔ'ɔra nɛrekuuŋɔ, la kpemehego, la pa'alegɔ.

ref: Use the gift you were given when the prophets spoke and the group of church leaders blessed you by placing their hands on you.
text: Da zagehɛ Yinɛ bo'olum sebo en boi fum puan la. Sebo ti ba dikɛ bo' fum doohe purega, ti kiinduma la daa dikɛ ba nu'uhi pagelɛ fum zuo pu'uhɛ la.

ref: Remember these things and think about them, so everyone can see how well you are doing.
text: Tuna yele ana, kaabɛ fum mɛŋa bo' a, ti nɛra woo wan nyɛ fum tole nɛŋa se'em.

ref: Be careful about the way you live and about what you teach. Keep on doing this, and you will save not only yourself, but the people who hear you.
text: Bihɛ fum mɛŋa suŋa, la fum pa'alegɔ la. Kɔ'ɔm ita yele ana, se'ere en sɔi la, fum han ita yele ana, fum wan faaɛ fum mɛŋa, la fum kɛleheba la.

ref: Don't correct an older man. Encourage him, as you would your own father. Treat younger men as you would your own brother,
text: Da tahɛ bunkutɛ, gee tɔgera bɔ'ɔra en wuu sɔ la, tɔgera bɔ'ɔra budibeto la wuu fum suurɔ la,

ref: and treat older women as you would your own mother. Show the same respect to younger women that you would to your sister.
text: tɔgera bɔ'ɔra pɔgenya'ahi la me, ba wuu maduma la, pɔge sa'ara la me wuu, fum tapa la, la susunɛ.

ref: Take care of any widow who is really in need.
text: Na'aha pokɔpa sɛba en sirum de pokɔpa.

ref: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should learn to serve God by taking care of her, as she once took care of them. This is what God wants them to do.
text: Pokuurɛ han tara kɔma bii yaahi, yia dela ba zamehɛ tuna ba pu'uhegɔ tuuma la ba yiya puan, ba me lebehe bɔ'ɔra ba dɔgereba la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ nini tigɛ mɛ la yele aŋa.

ref: A widow who is really in need is one who doesn't have any relatives. She has faith in God, and she keeps praying to him night and day, asking for his help.
text: Gee se'em en sirum de Pokuurɛ ka tara bihera, dikɛ a mɛŋa delum la Yinɛ, bɔna suhega la pu'uhegɔ puan yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa.

ref: A widow who thinks only about having a good time is already dead, even though she is still alive.
text: Gee pɔkɔ-se'ere en diti nyɛlum pun ki mɛ gee vɔna la.

ref: Tell all this to everyone, so they will do the right thing.
text: Mu'ɛ nini bo' ba yele ana wa me pa'ahɛ, ti ba vom da tara zɛregerɛ.

ref: People who don't take care of their relatives, and especially their own families, have given up their faith. They are worse than someone who doesn't have faith in the Lord.
text: Se'em woo en ka biheri a yire, yele yele wuu a yire duma, a zagehɛ sakerɛ la mɛ, a be'em gani se'em en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu.

ref: For a widow to be put on the list of widows, she must be at least
text: Pokuurɛ se'ere yu'urɛ en wan bɔna pokɔpa yu'ura puan la dɛna yuuma pihiyoobe beene yɛ'a ha, gee me ele la buyina.

ref: She must also be well-known for doing all sorts of good things, such as raising children, giving food to strangers, welcoming God's people into her home, helping people in need, and always making herself useful.
text: A dɛna se'em ti nɛreba wan tɔgera a sum yɛla, se'em en bihɛ a kɔma ti ba zo'e suŋa na, a tɔ'ɔha saama, tɔ'ɔha nɛresunduma, suŋera sɛba en boi namehegɔ puan, gee kaabɛ a mɛŋa bo' itesuŋɔ woo.

ref: Don't put young widows on the list. They may later have a strong desire to get married. Then they will turn away from Christ
text: Gee da digeha pɔkɔbibehi yu'ura pa'aha yu'ura la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ba kunkoma'ahum la han ni ta ihege, ve'e ba Kiresta zi'an, ba ni bɔta la ba le ele,

ref: Besides, they will become lazy and get into the habit of going from house to house. Next, they will start gossiping and become busybodies, talking about things that are none of their business.
text: Gee dagi bala ma'a, gee ba zamehɛ la zinyagerɛ, ɛɛra lɔ'ɔha yiya kaara, ba ka ze' la yoo ma'a, gee ba dela yelebibeheduma, la sɛba n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra nɛreba yɛla puan, tɔgera sɛla en dagi ba tɔgɛ.

ref: I would prefer that young widows get married, have children, and look after their families. Then the enemy won't have any reason to say insulting things about us.
text: Bala, mam wan bɔta ti pɔkɔbibehi ele, dɔgɛ kɔma, bihera ba yiya suŋa, la ba da bo' dima la yɔ'ɔ ti a dɛgɛ tɔ.

ref: Look what's already happened to some of the young widows! They have turned away to follow Satan.
text: Se'ere en sɛ la sɛba pun ŋmɛregɛ mɛ dɔla Sagena la.

ref: If a woman who is a follower has any widows in her family, she should help them. This will keep the church from having that burden, and then the church can help widows who are really in need. Church Leaders
text: Pɔge sɛka en nam Yezu han tara pokɔpa yelemɛŋerɛ a yire, a suŋɛ ba, bala ti zeero kan bɔna lageŋɔ la zuo, ŋwana ti lageŋɔ la wan ta'am suŋɛ sɛba me en sirum de pokɔpa la.

ref: Church leaders who do their job well deserve to be paid twice as much, especially if they work hard at preaching and teaching.
text: Kiinduma sɛba en tuni lageŋɔ tuuma suŋa la nari la na'ahegɔ en ka tari makerɛ, yele yele wuu sɛba en mu'ɛ nini muula gee pa'ala Yinɛ yelehum la.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “Don't muzzle an ox when you are using it to grind grain.” You also know the saying, “Workers are worth their pay.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, “Da lu' lɔlega nuurɛ gee tara ka tulegera ki.” “Tuntuna dela ba yɔ en.”

ref: Don't listen to any charge against a church leader, unless at least two or three people bring the same charges.
text: Da malum sakɛ zɛregerɛ ti ba zɛregɛ kiinsɛka, han dagena aze'nyɛduma n paaɛ bayi' batã.

ref: But if any of the leaders should keep on sinning, they must be corrected in front of the whole group, as a warning to everyone else.
text: Han dɛna sɛba en kɔ'ɔm bɔna be'em puan la, tahɛ ba nɛreba nɛŋan, ti sɛba en gee la, dabeem wan kɛ̃ ba.

ref: In the presence of God and Christ Jesus and their chosen angels, I order you to follow my instructions! Be fair with everyone, and don't have any favorites.
text: Yinɛ nɛŋan, la Kiresta Yezu nɛŋan, la suŋereba sɛba ti ba looe la nɛŋan, n ka'ani fum mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti fum to'e yele ana wa za'a, da kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra se'em, ti lugehego da bɔna bini.

ref: Don't be too quick to accept people into the service of the Lord by placing your hands on them. Don't sin because others do, but stay close to God.
text: Da kabelɛ tara fum nu'uhi pagela nɛra zuo, da lagum naara la sɛba tuna be'em, tara fum vom la ti bu ka tara dɛgerɔ.

ref: Stop drinking only water. Take a little wine to help your stomach trouble and the other illnesses you often have.
text: Da le yuura ko'kahegɛ ma'a, gee nyuura daam fii wa, fum puurɛ la, la fum bã'a la en gbirehe la zuo.

ref: Some people get caught in their sins almost at once, even before the time of judgment. But other people's sins don't show up until later.
text: Nɛreba basɛba be'em ba' niiɛ mɛ gee wan tari gakerɛ wa'am ba zuo na, gee ti sɛba be'em me wan niiɛ la pooren.

ref: It is the same with good deeds. Some are easily seen, but none of them can be hidden.
text: Bala mɛa ti tuunsuma ba' niiɛ na, gee la han pun dagena bala, ba kɔ'ɔm kan sugɛ gã'a.

ref: If you are a slave, you should respect and honor your owner. This will keep people from saying bad things about God and about our teaching.
text: Bahɛ ti sɛba woo en de da'aba na'aha ba kpɛ'ɛnduma sɛla woo puan, ŋwana ti Yinɛ yu'urɛ, la pa'alegɔ la kan nyɛ tuurɛ.

ref: If any of you slaves have owners who are followers, you should show them respect. After all, they are also followers of Christ, and he loves them. So you should serve and help them the best you can. False Teaching and True Wealth These are the things you must teach and tell the people to do.
text: Sɛba kpɛ'ɛnduma en de sakereba la, ba da zɛɛna ba, se'ere zuo, ba dela lageŋɔ la puan nɛreba. Gee ba tugum tuna suŋa suŋa bɔ'ɔra ba, se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba en diti ba tuuma la nyuurɔ dela sakereba, en de ba suurɔ ti ba nɔŋɛ. Pumpã'ahum pa'alegɔ, la yelemɛŋerɛ tarehum Pa'ala gee ka'ana ba yele ana wa la ninmu'urɛ.

ref: Anyone who teaches something different disagrees with the correct and godly teaching of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Se'em han pa'ala sɛla en boi la tɔka gee ka sakɛ bo' Daana Yezu Kiresta yelehum, la pa'alegɔ seko en dole Yinɛ zoolum yɛla la,

ref: Those people who disagree are proud of themselves, but they don't really know a thing. Their minds are sick, and they like to argue over words. They cause jealousy, disagreements, unkind words, evil suspicions,
text: a pã'ahɛ la a mɛŋa, ka bɔkera sɛla, gee bɔta la gaka la nɔkpe'en yaalehi doohe yu'ura puan. Bana puan nyuure, welehego, tuurɛ, dagebo',

ref: and nasty quarrels. They have wicked minds and have missed out on the truth. These people think religion is supposed to make you rich.
text: ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma la sɛba en ka ti'ihɛ suŋa, ka tara yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti'iha ti Yinɛ zoolum dela bɔɔbɔ sore.

ref: And religion does make your life rich, by making you content with what you have.
text: La de sira, tarehum kãtɛ boi Yinɛ zoolum puan mɛ, ti la seke.

ref: We didn't bring anything into this world, and we won't take anything with us when we leave.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka tari sɛla wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na, bala ti tu kan dikɛ sɛla lebe,

ref: So we should be satisfied just to have food and clothes.
text: gee tu han tara dia la lɔgerɔ, tu bahɛ ti la seke tu.

ref: People who want to be rich fall into all sorts of temptations and traps. They are caught by foolish and harmful desires that drag them down and destroy them.
text: Gee sɛba en boti ba ba' tara la luti la magehegɔ puan, ti yeleyaalehi zo'e zo'e la kunkoma'ahum tooro ni nyɔgɛ ba, seto en ni za'ɛ nɛreba bahɛ wuŋerɛ la sageŋɔ puan.

ref: The love of money causes all kinds of trouble. Some people want money so much they have given up their faith and caused themselves a lot of pain. Fighting a Good Fight for the Faith
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ligeri nɔŋerɛ en de be'em woo nyɛgerɛ, ti ban mu'ɛ nini ti ba tara la zuo, basɛba zagehɛ sakerɛ la mɛ, lu' ba mɛhi la wuŋerɛ zo'e zo'e. Sakerɛ zabesunɛ

ref: Timothy, you belong to God, so keep away from all these evil things. Try your best to please God and to be like him. Be faithful, loving, dependable, and gentle.
text: Gee han dɛna fum, Yinɛ tuntuna wa, zagehɛ yele ana wa woo, dɔla tuuntɔɔ vom, Yinɛ zoolum, sakerɛ, nɔŋerɛ, ŋmibega, la ma'ma' bɔŋa.

ref: Fight a good fight for the faith and claim eternal life. God offered it to you when you clearly told about your faith, while so many people listened.
text: Zabɛ sakerɛ zabesunɛ la, gurɛ vom kamba'ahɛ la, se'ere zuo ti ba yi' fum la, la se'ere ti fum purɛ yelemɛŋerɛ purega la nɛreba zo'e zo'e nɛŋan la.

ref: Now I ask you to make a promise. Make it in the presence of God, who gives life to all, and in the presence of Christ Jesus, who openly told Pontius Pilate about his faith.
text: Yinɛ nɛŋan, se'em en bo'ori sɛla woo vom la, la Kiresta Yezu, se'em en yuum tɔgɛ dinɛ n de se'em Pɔntiyɔsi Palete nɛŋan, gee purɛ yelemɛŋerɛ purega la nɛŋan, ti n bɔ'ɔra hɔ nuurɛ wa,

ref: Promise to obey completely and fully all that you have been told until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
text: ti fum tara ze'elego la, ti dɛkɔ bii zɛregerɛ da bɔna bini ta paaɛ Yezu Kiresta lɛbega na,

ref: The glorious God is the only Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords. At the time that God has already decided, he will send Jesus Christ back again.
text: se'em ti Yinɛ wan bahɛ ti a wa'am sansɛka en nari la puan na la. Eŋa se'em en de sum daana, la se'em ma'a en gani za'a la, Naduma Naba, la Daanduma Daana la.

ref: Only God lives forever! And he lives in light that no one can come near. No human has ever seen God or ever can see him. God will be honored, and his power will last forever. Amen.
text: La dela eŋa ma'a en ka kiiri, gee bɔna niihum sebo en ka kaleni puan. Se'em ti nɛra ka tabelɛ nyɛ en bii wan nyɛ en, eŋa se'em ti na'ahegɔ, la pansɛka en ka ba'aheri dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha la. Taari.

ref: Warn the rich people of this world not to be proud or to trust in wealth that is easily lost. Tell them to have faith in God, who is rich and blesses us with everything we need to enjoy life.
text: Han dɛna zina beere buntateba wa, ka'am ba ti ba da pa'ala ba mɛhi, bii ba dikɛ ba puti'irɛ delum sɛla en kan bɔna wuuha, gee ba tugum dɛla Yinɛ, se'em en bo'ori tu sɛla woo zo'e zo'e ti tu dita la pupeelum.

ref: Instruct them to do as many good deeds as they can and to help everyone. Remind the rich to be generous and share what they have.
text: Ba dela ba ita itesuŋɔ, ba dɛna buntateba itesuŋɔ puan, ba bɔ'ɔra, ba dɛna sɛba en maahum ti ba tɔta,

ref: This will lay a solid foundation for the future, so they will know what true life is like.
text: Ŋwana ti ba wan ka'aha lɔgeseto en wan bo' ba beere ɛ̃berɛ sunɛ la biŋera saazuo ha, la ba beere tara vom sebo en sirum de vom la. Pɔɔl kã'aŋɔ la ka'a

ref: Timothy, guard what God has placed in your care! Don't pay any attention to godless and stupid talk that sounds smart but really isn't.
text: Timoti, gura sɛla en dikɛ iŋɛ fum puan la suŋa. Zagehɛ yetɔge yooro, la zaba nɔkpɛ'ɛma, la sɛla ti ba parum yi'ira mi'ilum la.

ref: Some people have even lost their faith by believing this talk. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: Ban ti'ihɛ ti bamam mi'ilum la zuo, basɛba buregɛ ba sakerɛ la puan mɛ. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la hɔ.

ref: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus. God himself chose me to be an apostle, and he gave me the promised life that Jesus Christ makes possible.
text: Pɔɔl, Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, Yinɛ suboolum puan, doohe nɔbiŋere la en biŋe doohe vom sebo en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la zuo.

ref: Timothy, you are like a dear child to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Christ Jesus will be kind and merciful to you and will bless you with peace! Do Not Be Ashamed of the Lord
text: N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la Timoti en de mam sunɔŋerɛ dayua. Zu'uhegɔ, nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la, la Kiresta Yezu tu Daana la zi'an na, bɔna la hɔ. Fara bu'a la kpemehego

ref: Night and day I mention you in my prayers. I am always grateful for you, as I pray to the God my ancestors and I have served with a clear conscience.
text: Mam suure pee mɛ la Yinɛ, se'em ti mam pu'uha la putɛ'sunɛ, wuu mam sɔyaabe duma en yuum iti se'em la, wuu mam en tɛri fum yele saŋa woo se'em n yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa pu'uhegɔ puan.

ref: I remember how you cried, and I want to see you, because this would make me truly happy.
text: Mam en tiiheri fum nintam la, n kɔ'ɔm bɔta ti n nyɛ fum mɛ, ti n poore pee.

ref: I also remember the genuine faith of your mother Eunice. Your grandmother Lois had the same sort of faith, and I am sure you have it as well.
text: La tiiheri mam mɛ fum yelemɛŋerɛ sakerɛ la yele, sakerɛ se'ere en yuum boi fum mayaaba Lois puan yia, tagelɛ fum ma Yunise, ti nananawa, mam mina ti de boi fum me puan.

ref: So I ask you to make full use of the gift God gave you when I placed my hands on you. Use it well.
text: Dɛna zuo ti mam tɛɛra hɔ ti fum malum gbirege Yinɛ bo'olum sebo en boi fum puan, ti mam daa dikɛ n nu'uhi pagelɛ fum zuo pu'uhɛ la,

ref: God's Spirit doesn't make cowards out of us. The Spirit gives us power, love, and self-control.
text: se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ka bo' tu la dabeem Sia, gee a tugum bo' tu la Sia en tari paŋa, la nɔŋerɛ, la fum mɛŋa tarega.

ref: Don't be ashamed to speak for our Lord. And don't be ashamed of me, just because I am in jail for serving him. Use the power that comes from God and join with me in suffering for telling the good news.
text: Bala zuo da tara valum la tu Daana yɛla la muulegɔ, bii mam se'em en boi paka deen a zuo la, gee naaɛ la mam, namehɛ faarɛ yelehum wa zuo, dɛla Yinɛ paŋa la,

ref: God saved us and chose us to be his holy people. We did nothing to deserve this, but God planned it because he is so kind. Even before time began God planned for Christ Jesus to show kindness to us.
text: se'em en faaɛ tɔ, gee yi' tu tuulum yi'a, dagi la tu tuuma zuo, gee eŋa mɛŋa en boti se'em, la a zu'uhegɔ zuo. Zu'uhegɔ kuna wa dikɛ bo' tu doohe la Kiresta Yezu puan, diimen diimen ha,

ref: Now Christ Jesus has come to offer us God's gift of undeserved grace. Christ our Savior defeated death and brought us the good news. It shines like a light and offers life that never ends.
text: gee de nyaa niiɛ mɛ na, doohe tu Faara Kiresta Yezu niirɛ na la puan, se'em en wa'am na sagum kum paŋa, gee tari vom la sɛla en ka kiiri, ti la niiɛ peelumen na, doohe faarɛ yelehum la puan.

ref: My work is to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher.
text: Faarɛ yelehum buna wa ti ba looe mam ti n dɛna bu muula, bu tuntuna, la bu pa'ala,

ref: This is why I am suffering now. But I am not ashamed! I know the one I have faith in, and I am sure he can guard until the last day what he has trusted me with.
text: dina wa zuo ti mam nameha wuu mam en namehɛ se'em wa. Gee vi ka tari mam, se'ere en sɔi la, mam mi' se'em ti mam delum en la mɛ, N me mi' suŋa suŋa ti a wan ta'am gura mam ta paaɛ de daarɛ, la sɛla ti a dikɛ iŋɛ mam puan la.

ref: Now follow the example of the correct teaching I gave you, and let the faith and love of Christ Jesus be your model.
text: Gurɛ yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ la suŋa, sebo ti fum wum mam zi'an la, la sakerɛ la nɔŋerɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la.

ref: You have been trusted with a wonderful treasure. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit, who lives within us.
text: Tara tuulum yelesɛ'a en dikɛ iŋɛ fum puan la suŋa, doohe la Fa'a Sia la en boi tu puan la.

ref: You know that everyone in Asia has turned against me, especially Phygelus and Hermogenes.
text: Fum mi' ti sɛba woo en boi Esiya tiŋa la zagehɛ mam mɛ, bamam basɛba dela, Figeluse la Hɛmogenese.

ref: I pray that the Lord will be kind to the family of Onesiphorus. He often cheered me up and wasn't ashamed of me when I was put in jail.
text: Daana la wan zɔ Onesiforuse yire duma za'a nimbu'ɔ, beni zuo, a dela se'em en baheri ti mam puti'irɛ gã'a tiŋa, ti vi ka tara en la mam bãhi wa.

ref: Then after he arrived in Rome, he searched everywhere until he found me.
text: Eŋa paaɛ Arome tiŋa la, a ɛ mam mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ta nyɛ mam,

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will ask God to show mercy to Onesiphorus on the day of judgment. You know how much he helped me in Ephesus.
text: Daana la bahɛ ti a ta nyɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ Daana la zi'an de daarɛ. Fum me mi' en tum zo'e se'em Ɛfisu puan.

ref: Timothy, my child, you must let Christ Jesus make you strong by his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Nananawa, fum n bia wa, kpe'em mɛ la zu'uhegɔ seko en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la,

ref: You have often heard me teach. Now I want you to tell these same things to followers who can be trusted to tell others.
text: la sɛla ti fum wum mam zi'an nɛreba zo'e zo'e nɛŋan la. Dikɛ bo' yelemɛŋerɛ duma sɛba en wan ta'am pa'alɛ basɛba me.

ref: As a good soldier of Christ Jesus you must endure your share of suffering.
text: Lagum nameha wuu fum de Kiresta Yezu pɛntiira suŋa la.

ref: Soldiers on duty don't work at outside jobs. They try only to please their commanding officer.
text: Nɛra ka boi tuna pɛntiireba tuuma, gee le gɛ̃ta a mɛŋa la daare woo yɛla. Pɛntiira puti'irɛ dela a pee a daana la puurɛ.

ref: No one wins an athletic contest without obeying the rules.
text: Gee mina en zoti puan, nɛra ka boi zɔta dita mi'a gee ka zɔ doohe zua ze'elego la.

ref: And farmers who work hard are the first to eat what grows in their field.
text: Kaara se'em n kɔ la, eŋa en de a to'e a tɔtɛ va'am dia la puan yia.

ref: If you keep in mind what I have told you, the Lord will help you understand completely.
text: Ti'ihɛ mam en yele fum sɛla wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la wan bo' fum bɔkerɛ sɛla woo puan.

ref: Keep your mind on Jesus Christ! He was from the family of David and was raised from death, just as my good news says.
text: Tɛra ti Yezu Kiresta ihege mɛ a kum la puan, a dela Deviti yaaŋa, mam faarɛ yelehum en bala.

ref: And because of this message, I am locked up in jail and treated like a criminal. But God's good news isn't locked in jail,
text: Dina zuo ti mam nyɛ namehegɔ too, pun ta paaɛ ba ba' mam wuu tuunfi'iha daana la. Gee Yinɛ yelehum ka ba'ari.

ref: and so I am willing to put up with anything. Then God's special people will be saved and given eternal glory because they belong to Christ Jesus.
text: Bala zuo, mam ŋmiberi mɛ sɛla woo puan, sɛba ti ba looe la zuo, ti ba me nyɛ faarɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan, la na'ahegɔ seko en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la.

ref: Here is a true message: “If we died with Christ, we will live with him.
text: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ, ti tu han ki mɛ la en, tu me wan vɔna la en,

ref: If we don't give up, we will rule with him. If we deny that we know him, he will deny that he knows us.
text: Tu han ŋmibe, tu me wan di na'am la en, Tu han zagehɛ en, a mɛ wan zagehɛ tɔ,

ref: If we are not faithful, he will still be faithful. Christ cannot deny who he is.” An Approved Worker
text: Tumam han ka tara sakerɛ, eŋa nuun dela a sakerɛ daana. Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa kan ta'am zagehɛ a mɛŋa. Tuntuna sɛka ti Yinɛ looe

ref: Don't let anyone forget these things. And with God as your witness, you must warn them not to argue about words. These arguments don't help anyone. In fact, they ruin everyone who listens to them.
text: Tiɛ ba dina wa, gee kagɛ ba la ninmu'urɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan, ti ba bahɛ ba tara yɛla ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma, sɛla en ka tari suŋɛ, gee Sagena kɛleheba la.

ref: Do your best to win God's approval as a worker who doesn't need to be ashamed and who teaches only the true message.
text: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa la fum kaabɛ fum mɛŋa suŋa bo' Yinɛ wuu se'em ti Yinɛ mɛŋa nam la. Tuntuna sɛka en ka de a zɔta valum, pa'ala yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum la tuuntɔɔ.

ref: Keep away from worthless and useless talk. It only leads people farther away from God.
text: Lɔkera sɛba en tɔgeri yetɔge yaalehi, se'ere en sɔi la, bu wan tari nɛreba malum kɛ̃ dɛgerɔ yɛla puan.

ref: That sort of talk is like a sore that won't heal. And Hymenaeus and Philetus have been talking this way
text: Ba tugum la wan taaɛ wuu kɔhi kɛka la. Nɛre bana wa basɛba dela Hɛmenayɔse la Filetuse,

ref: by teaching that the dead have already been raised to life. This is far from the truth, and it is destroying the faith of some people.
text: sɛba en buregɛ yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ la puan, yɛta ti ihegere la pun iŋɛ mɛ. Ba dole tulena la basɛba sakerɛ.

ref: But the foundation God has laid is solid. On it is written, “The Lord knows who his people are. So everyone who worships the Lord must turn away from evil.”
text: Gee Yinɛ ɛ̃berɛ la kɔ'ɔm zɛ'a la kankaŋi, tara daalehum buna wa: “Daana la mi' sɛba en de a dinɛ.” Bala la, bahɛ ti “Sɛba woo en yi'iri Daana la yu'urɛ la yehe ba mɛhi be'em puan.”

ref: In a large house some dishes are made of gold or silver, while others are made of wood or clay. Some of these are special, and others are not.
text: Yikãtɛ puan, lɔgerɔ boi bini mɛ, gee dagi la salema la salempɛɛla lɔgerɔ ma'a, gee dɔɔrɔ la yɔgerɔ lɔgerɔ. Basɛba dela la tuulum lɔgerɔ, basɛba mɛ dela tuni woo lɔgerɔ.

ref: This is also how it is with people. The ones who stop doing evil and make themselves pure will become special. Their lives will be holy and pleasing to their Master, and they will be able to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: Sɛba woo en pee ba mɛhi yehe yele sɛ'a ti n yele wa puan, wan dɛna tuulum lɔgerɔ en yehe ze'ele, tari tuunɛ bɔ'ɔra yire la daana. Maahum gura tuunsunɛ woo.

ref: Run from temptations that capture young people. Always do the right thing. Be faithful, loving, and easy to get along with. Worship with people whose hearts are pure.
text: Zɔ yehe bumbile paalumen kɔrehi puan, gee dɔla tuuntɔɔ vom la ninmu'urɛ, sakerɛ, nɔŋerɛ la suma'ahum la sɛba en pu'uhi Yinɛ la susunɛ la.

ref: Stay away from stupid and senseless arguments. These only lead to trouble,
text: Da tara sɛla ita la geto la nɔkpe'en yooro. Fum mi' ti, ba tari la zaba kina na.

ref: and God's servants must not be troublemakers. They must be kind to everyone, and they must be good teachers and very patient.
text: Daana la tuntuna ka de a dɛna zabe-zabera, gee a ana ma'ma' la nɛrawoo, a tana pa'ala gee tara sugeri.

ref: Be humble when you correct people who oppose you. Maybe God will lead them to turn to him and learn the truth.
text: A pa'ali sɛba en ka sakeri bɔ'ɔra en la, la sigehe mɛŋa. Ani en mi,Yinɛ ta'am bahɛ ti ba tee gee baŋɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la,

ref: They have been trapped by the devil, and he makes them obey him, but God may help them escape.
text: la ba ta'am piiɛ bumbɛ'a bɛregɔ la, bamam sɛba ti a pun nyɔkɛ ti ba dɛna a da'aba ti ba tum a puti'irɛ la.

ref: You can be certain that in the last days there will be some very hard times.
text: Fum dela fum mina dina wa, ti, ba'ahegɔ dabeha la, sukpɛlehi saŋa wan wa'am na.

ref: People will love only themselves and money. They will be proud, stuck-up, rude, and disobedient to their parents. They will also be ungrateful, godless,
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba wan nɔŋɛ ba mɛhi, nɔŋɛ ligeri, nyɔ'ŋmɛ'ɛreba, nɛre wureba, kɛhega, ka nana dɔgereba, nini ka tigera, dɛgerɔ vom,

ref: heartless, and hateful. Their words will be cruel, and they will have no self-control or pity. These people will hate everything good.
text: ka tara giile, ka dita sugeri, yu'sageneba, ka tana nyɔkera ba mɛhi, sukãberɛ, kiha sum,

ref: They will be sneaky, reckless, and puffed up with pride. Instead of loving God, they will love pleasure.
text: taaba ŋma'a, bura bura, pa'alɛ mɛŋa, nɔŋɛ nyɛlum gana a nɔŋɛ Yinɛ,

ref: Some men fool whole families, just to get power over those women who are slaves of sin and are controlled by all sorts of desires.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba puan basɛba dela sɛba en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri yiya puan pã'aha pɔgese'ehi en de geto, pirɛ la be'em, ti kɔrehi woo tana ba,

ref: These women always want to learn something new, but they never can discover the truth.
text: ti ba pa'ala ba saŋa woo gee ba kan malum baŋɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la.

ref: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, these people are enemies of the truth. Their minds are sick, and their faith isn't real.
text: Wuu Yenesi la Yamberese en yuum ka sakɛ bo' Moose se'em la, bala ti nɛrebana wa en tari putɛ'be'o la sakerɛ se'ere en dagi yelemɛŋerɛ wa, me ka sakɛ bo' yelemɛŋerɛ la.

ref: But they won't get very far with their foolishness. Soon everyone will know the truth about them, just as Jannes and Jambres were found out. Paul's Last Instructions to Timothy
text: Gee ba kan ba' nyɛ nɛŋa tɔlega bala, beni zuo, wuu lan daa ani se'em buraahi siyi la puan la, bamam gɔɔn tuuma la me wan niiɛ peelumen ti nɛra woo nyɛ. Pɔɔl kagɛ Timoti

ref: Timothy, you know what I teach and how I live. You know what I want to do and what I believe. You have seen how patient and loving I am, and how in the past I put up with
text: Nananawa, fum Timoti, mi' mam pa'alegɔ, la mam aŋa, mam puti'irɛ en de se'em vom puan, n sakerɛ, n sugeri, n nɔŋerɛ, mam n ze' kankaŋi se'em,

ref: trouble and suffering in the cities of Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. Yet the Lord rescued me from all those terrible troubles.
text: n mɛlehegɔ la n namehegɔ, la yelesɛ'a en paaɛ mam Antiyoke, Ikonium la Lisitera tihi, namehegɔ seko ti n ŋmibera ku puan! Gee la bala za'a, Daana la faaɛ mam ba za'a puan bahɛ mɛ.

ref: In fact, anyone who belongs to Christ Jesus and wants to live right will have trouble from others.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛba woo en boti ba vɔna Yinɛ zoolum vom Kiresta Yezu puan, wan nyɛ mɛlehegɔ.

ref: But evil people who pretend to be what they are not will become worse than ever, as they fool others and are fooled themselves.
text: Gee tuunfi'iha duma, la pumpã'ahum duma wan ze'ele sageŋɔ paaɛ nɔyalegerɛ. Ba wan pã'aha basɛba, ti basɛba me pã'aha ba.

ref: Keep on being faithful to what you were taught and to what you believed. After all, you know who taught you these things.
text: Gee han dɛna fum, bɔna sɛla ti fum zamehɛ la puan, gee nam suŋa suŋa, mina se'em daana zi'an ti fum zamehɛ a la,

ref: Since childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures that are able to make you wise enough to have faith in Christ Jesus and be saved.
text: ze'ele fum biilemen na, fum mi' fa'a gulehegɔ wa, en wan ta'am pa'alɛ fum ti fum nyɛ faarɛ doohe sakerɛ Kiresta Yezu puan.

ref: Everything in the Scriptures is God's Word. All of it is useful for teaching and helping people and for correcting them and showing them how to live.
text: Gulehegɔ la za'a ze'ele la Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la puan na, ana suŋa ti ba tara pa'ala, tahera, dɛmeha, la tuuntɔɔ vom kpemehego,

ref: The Scriptures train God's servants to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: bala ti se'em woo en de Yinɛ dinɛ wan seke, bi'ɛ wan ta'am tum tuunsunɛ woo.

ref: When Christ Jesus comes as king, he will be the judge of everyone, whether they are living or dead. So with God and Christ as witnesses, I command you
text: Yinɛ, la Kiresta Yezu nɛŋan, se'em en wan gakɛ vɔpa la ki'induma la, la a lɛbega na, la a sɔ'ɔlum la zuo, n kageri fum mɛ la ninmu'urɛ.

ref: to preach God's message. Do it willingly, even if it isn't the popular thing to do. You must correct people and point out their sins. But also cheer them up, and when you instruct them, always be patient.
text: Muula yelehum la, kɔ'ɔm zɛ'a mɛ kankaŋi, sum saŋa bii be'ero saŋa, tɔgera tɛɛra, tahɛ, kpɛmeha, la pa'alegɔ sugeri zo'e, zo'e.

ref: The time is coming when people won't listen to good teaching. Instead, they will look for teachers who will please them by telling them only what they are itching to hear.
text: Gee saŋa la kini na, ti nɛreba kan bɔta yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ, gee ban tari tuba en zakeni la, ba wan kã'ahɛ pa'aleba bo' ba mɛhi, wuu ba kɔrehi la en boti se'em la.

ref: They will turn from the truth and eagerly listen to senseless stories.
text: Gee wan biri pɔya, kan kɛleha yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee tuuna yɛ'a solum yooro puan.

ref: But you must stay calm and be willing to suffer. You must work hard, telling the good news and to do your job well.
text: La han dɛna fum, saŋa woo ta'na fum mɛŋa tara suŋa, ŋmibera namehegɔ puan, tuna ayehemuulɛ daana tuuma, tum fum tuunɛ ba'ahɛ suŋa suŋa.

ref: Now the time has come for me to die. My life is like a drink offering being poured out on the altar.
text: Nananawa, la han dɛna mam, ba pun ka'ɛ mam mɛ wuu kaabegɔ ko'om la, mam ye'ehego saŋa paaɛ ya.

ref: I have fought well. I have finished the race, and I have been faithful.
text: Mam zabɛ la zabesunɛ, mam ba'ahɛ n zua la, mam gurɛ en sakerɛ la mɛ.

ref: So a crown will be given to me for pleasing the Lord. He judges fairly, and on the day of judgment he will give a crown to me and to everyone else who wants him to appear with power. Personal Instructions
text: Nananawa yɛ'a ha, sɛla en dikɛ biŋe gura mam dela, tuuntɔɔ vom muŋa, sɛka ti Daana la en de tuuntɔɔ gakera la, wan dikɛ bo' mam de daarɛ, gee dagi mam ma'a, gee sɛba woo en me guri Yezu lɛbega na la. Pɔɔl mɛŋa kã'aŋɔ

ref: Come to see me as soon as you can.
text: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa gee wa'am n zi'an kalam na,

ref: Demas loves the things of this world so much that he left me and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
text: se'ere en sɔi la, Demase en nɔŋɛ zina beere tiŋa wa la, bahɛ mam gee kiŋɛ la Tɛsalonika tiŋa. Gee Kiresensi kiŋɛ Galatia, ti Taitusi me kiŋɛ Dalematiya tiŋa.

ref: Only Luke has stayed with me. Mark can be very helpful to me, so please find him and bring him with you.
text: Luki ma'a en boi la mam ka'lam. Ɛ Maki la fum doohe en na, se'ere en sɔi la, a tari tuulum mam la tuuma wa puan.

ref: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
text: Mam tum Takikuse la Ɛfisu.

ref: When you come, bring the coat I left at Troas with Carpus. Don't forget to bring the scrolls, especially the ones made of leather.
text: Fum han kina na, tari n fuwoko la ti n bahɛ la Kapuse en boi Torowase tiŋa la, la gɔnɔ la, yele yele woo gɔnɔ seto ti n kilum la.

ref: Alexander, the metalworker, has hurt me in many ways. But the Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
text: Alɛgezanda se'em en kuti la ba' pɔgelum mam mɛ, Daana wan lebehe yɔ en a be'em tuuma la.

ref: Alexander opposes what we preach, so you had better watch out for him.
text: Yamam me dela ya mina a yele, se'ere en sɔi la, a kɔ'ɔm mum la nini zagehɛ tumam yelehum la.

ref: When I was first put on trial, no one helped me. In fact, everyone deserted me. I hope it won't be held against them.
text: Mam wa kiŋɛ yia ti n tɔgɛ n nuurɛ la, nɛra daa ka ze'ele ti a suŋɛ mam, gee ba za'a zagehɛ mam mɛ. Yinɛ bahɛ ba taalɛ bo' ba.

ref: But the Lord stood beside me. He gave me the strength to tell his full message, so that all Gentiles would hear it. And I was kept safe from hungry lions.
text: Gee Daana la en ze'ele la mam, gee bo' mam paŋa, ti doohe mam puan, yelehum la wan muulɛ suŋa, ti sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la wan wam dɛ. Bala ti a faaɛ mam gbigenɛ nuuren la.

ref: The Lord will always keep me from being harmed by evil, and he will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. Praise him forever and ever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: Daana la wan faaɛ mam be'em zaberɛ woo puan bahɛ, gee tara mam kɛ̃ a saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan. Eŋa en sɔi na'ahegɔ wuuha wuuha. Taari. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ la ka'a

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
text: Pu'uhɛ Pirisila la Akuila, la Onesiforuse yire duma.

ref: Erastus stayed at Corinth. Trophimus was sick when I left him at Miletus.
text: Pu'uhɛ Erastuse en yee Korinti, la Tirofimuse en ka paaɛ a mɛŋa ti mam bahɛ en Militusi la.

ref: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, and Claudia send you their greetings, and so do the rest of the Lord's followers.
text: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa la fum wa'am na gee ti ko'oro saŋa paaɛ. Yubuluse pu'uhi fum na, bala mɛa ti Pudɛnsi, Linuse, Kulodiya la tu suurɔ za'a pu'uha fum na.

ref: I pray that the Lord will bless your life and will be kind to you.
text: Daana la bɔna la hɔ sia, zu'uhegɔ bɔna la hɔ.

ref: From Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. I encourage God's own people to have more faith and to understand the truth about religion.
text: Mam Pɔɔl en de Yinɛ da'aberɛ, gee dɛna Yezu Kiresta tuntuna, sɛba ti Yinɛ looe la sakerɛ la zuo, la yelemɛŋerɛ mi'ilum sebo en dole Yinɛ zoolum la,

ref: Then they will have the hope of eternal life God promised long ago. And God never tells a lie!
text: beere puti'irɛ en de vom kamba'ahɛ la, ti Yinɛ en ka pareni wa biŋe nuurɛ gee baŋɛ naam tiŋa wa la.

ref: So, at the proper time, God our Savior gave this message and told me to announce what he had said.
text: Sansɛka en nari la puan, ti a niiɛ a yelehum la doohe muulegɔ puan, muulegɔ seko en dikɛ bo' mam, doohe Yinɛ en de tu faara la nuuren.

ref: Titus, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and Christ Jesus our Savior will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! What Titus Was To Do in Crete
text: Mam en guleheri bɔ'ɔra fum Taitusi en de mam sunɔŋerɛ dayua, sakerɛ se'ere ti tu lagum dɔla la puan. Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Kiresta Yezu en de tu Faara la bɔna la ya. Taitusi kiŋɛ Kerete

ref: I left you in Crete to do what had been left undone and to appoint leaders for the churches in each town. As I told you,
text: Mam bahɛ hɔ Kerete tiŋa puan la dina wa zuo, ti fum maalɛ yelesɛ'a en gee dɛna a tum la, gee loohe kiinduma tiŋa woo puan, wuu mam en pa'alɛ fum se'em la.

ref: they must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. Their children must be followers of the Lord and not have a reputation for being wild and disobedient.
text: Ba dɛna sɛba en ka tari zɛregerɛ ba vom puan, sire-sɛka en di pɔgeyiŋa, sɛba kɔma en nam Yezu, sɛba en dagi kɔm-wureba, bii tube-kpirehi.

ref: Church officials are in charge of God's work, and so they must also have a good reputation. They must not be bossy, quick-tempered, heavy drinkers, bullies, or dishonest in business.
text: Wuu eŋa en de Yinɛ lageŋɔ nɛŋadaana la, se'em en biheri Yinɛ lageŋɔ la, a tara zɛregerɛ zi'an. A da dɛna nɛre wurebere, bii sunsuya daana, bii a koohe a mɛŋa bo' daam, bii gbiregeri daana, bii a ba' nɔŋɛ ligeri.

ref: Instead, they must be friendly to strangers and enjoy doing good things. They must also be sensible, fair, pure, and self-controlled.
text: Gee a dɛna se'em en to'oheri nɛreba a yire, nɔŋɛ itesuŋɔ, yɛm daana, dɛna tuuntɔɔ ka tara dɛgerɔ, tana a mɛŋa tara.

ref: They must stick to the true message they were taught, so their good teaching can help others and correct everyone who opposes it.
text: A dela a gurɛ yelehum la en dole pa'alegɔ la, ti la bahɛ ti a tana pa'ala pa'ali seko en de ma'a la, gee tana valegera sɛba en ka pa'ali dɔla la.

ref: There are many who don't respect authority, and they fool others by talking nonsense. This is especially true of some Jewish followers.
text: Ba zo'e mɛ dɛna tube-kpirega duma, tɔgɛ fai duma, la pumpã'ahum duma, yele yele wuu sɛba en ŋma la puan.

ref: But you must make them be quiet. They are after money, and they upset whole families by teaching what they should not.
text: Ba dela ba ŋmɛkɛ ba nɔya, la ban gbiregeri nɛreba yiya la ba pa'alegɔ la, doohe ban pa'ali pa'alegɔ seko en bo'ori bamam ma'a nyuurɔ, gee dagi na sɛla en dagena ba pa'ala.

ref: It is like one of their own prophets once said, “The people of Crete always tell lies. They are greedy and lazy like wild animals.”
text: La yuum dela ba ayima, ba mɛŋa nɔtɔgeha en yuum yeti, “Kereteduma dela pumparenduma saŋa woo, ana wuu muuen dumbɛ'ɛhi, zinyagehi, gee dɛna punya'anduma.”

ref: This surely is a true saying. And you should be hard on such people, so you can help them grow stronger in their faith.
