text: Ban tɔgɛ sɛla la dela asira. Dina zuo la, tahɛ ba la ninmu'urɛ, ti ba lebe bɔna sakerɛ la puan suŋa suŋa.

ref: Don't pay any attention to any of those senseless Jewish stories and human commands. These are made up by people who won't obey the truth.
text: Da kɛleha Yuuduma yetɔge yooro la, bii tɔ'ɔha sɛba en zagehɛ yelemɛŋerɛ nɔze'elego.

ref: Everything is pure for someone whose heart is pure. But nothing is pure for an unbeliever with a dirty mind. That person's mind and conscience are destroyed.
text: Sɛba suya en de tuuntɔɔ la, sɛla woo dela tuuntɔɔ bɔ'ɔra ba, sɛba en ŋmɛleni gee ka nam Yezu la, sɛla ka boi dɛna tuuntɔɔ bo' ba. Ba mɛhi puti'ira la ba zuto ki mɛ.

ref: Such people claim to know God, but their actions prove they really don't. They are disgusting. They won't obey God, and they are too worthless to do anything good.
text: Ba iŋɛ ba mɛhi ti bamam mi' Yinɛ, gee ba zagehɛ en mɛ la ba itegɔ la. Ba dela nɛre fi'iha, tube-kpirega duma, ka nara tuunsunɛ se'ere woo puan.

ref: Titus, you must teach only what is correct.
text: Gee han dɛna fum, pa'ala sɛla en dole la ma'a pa'alegɔ la.

ref: Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
text: Yele buraa kureba la ti, ba da dɛna susuya duma, gee ba dɛna ninmu'urɛ duma, yɛm duma, kpe'em sakerɛ puan, nɔŋerɛ la ŋmibega puan.

ref: Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper,
text: Bala mɛa, yele pɔge kureba la ti ba tara na'ahegɔ ba vom puan, ba da dɛna sɛba en sageni nɛreba yu'ura, bii da'aba bo' daam-nyuure. Ba pa'ala sɛla en ani suŋa,

ref: so the younger women will be loving wives and mothers.
text: ti la nyaŋɛ kpemehe pɔge paalehi la ti ba nɔŋɛ ba sireba, nɔŋɛ ba kɔma,

ref: Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
text: ba tana ba mɛhi tara, nyɔkera ba mɛhi, tana bihera ba yiya, tara itesuŋɔ, sigeha ba mɛhi bɔ'ɔra ba sireba, ti Yinɛ yelehum la da nyɛ zɛregerɛ.

ref: Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
text: Bala mɛa, pa'alɛ bura paalehi la ti ba nyɔkera ba mɛhi.

ref: Always set a good example for others. Be sincere and serious when you teach.
text: Bahɛ ti naŋa bɔna fum puan zi'an woo, ti fum dɛna tuunsuma daana ti ba wan tɔgehɛ, fum pa'alegɔ puan, pa'ala la yɛm la paŋa,

ref: Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
text: la yɛm yetɔge sebo en ka tari sokere, ti se'em woo en ka sakɛ la, vi wan di en ti a kan nyɛ be'em sebo tɔgɛ tɔ.

ref: Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners
text: Yele da'aba ti ba sigehe ba mɛhi bo' ba kpɛ'ɛnduma gee bɔ'ɔra ba na'ahegɔ en seke. Ba da lɛbeha ba kpɛ'ɛnduma la,

ref: or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior. God's Kindness and the New Life
text: ba da zuura, gee ba pa'alɛ ti ba dela nɛresuma suŋa suŋa, bala ti sɛla woo puan, ba wan ana wuu nunɔm zɔtɔ la bo' ma'a pa'alegɔ la en ze'ele Yinɛ tu Faara zi'an na la.

ref: God has shown us undeserved grace by coming to save all people.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la wa'am na tari la faarɛ na bo' nɛra woo,

ref: He taught us to give up our wicked ways and our worldly desires and to live decent and honest lives in this world.
text: pa'ala tɔ ti tu bahɛ be'em woo, la tiŋa wa zuo nyɛlum yooro, la saŋa kana wa, tu vɔna vom sebo en de fum tana n mɛŋa tara, tuntɔɔnɛ la Yinɛ zoolum,

ref: We are filled with hope, as we wait for the glorious return of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ.
text: tumam n guri beere putɛ'sunɛ wa, la Yinɛ kãtɛ wa, la tu Faara Yezu Kiresta niirɛ na la na'ahegɔ la.

ref: He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
text: Eŋa en kaabɛ a mɛŋa bo' tu, ti a yɔ to'e tu tuure woo puan, gee pee tu bo' a mɛŋa, ti tu dɛna a mɛŋa nɛreba, sɛba en mu'ɛ nini tuna tuunsunɛ.

ref: Teach these things, as you use your full authority to encourage and correct people. Make sure you earn everyone's respect.
text: Muula yele ana wa, kpɛmeha, gee tahera nɛra woo la paŋa. Da bahɛ ti se'em bihɛ zeem fum.

ref: Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful
text: Tiihɛ ba ti ba sigehe ba mɛhi bo' nɛhi duma, la kpɛ'ɛnduma, ba nana ba. Ba maahum ba mɛhi bo' tunsunɛ woo,

ref: and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone.
text: ba da tɔgera be'em bɔ'ɔra nɛra, ba bahɛ nɔkpɛ'ɛma, ba ana ma'ma, gee tara nɔsunɛ la nɛrawoo.

ref: We used to be stupid, disobedient, and foolish, as well as slaves of all sorts of desires and pleasures. We were evil and jealous. Everyone hated us, and we hated everyone.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam mɛhi tabelɛ dɛna la geto, ka nana, bɔregɛ, dɛna da'aba bo' kɔrehi la nyɛlum yima yima, dikɛ tu dabeha tum pa'ahegɔ tuuma, nintɛrega, zɛregera nɛreba, yaaɛ taaba.

ref: God our Savior showed us how good and kind he is.
text: Gee Yinɛ se'em en de tu Faara la itesuŋɔ la a nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ la en niiɛ paaɛ nɛra woo la,

ref: He saved us because of his mercy, and not because of any good things we have done. God washed us by the power of the Holy Spirit. He gave us new birth and a fresh beginning.
text: a faaɛ tu mɛ, dagi la tu tuntɔɔnɛ tuuma ti tu tum zuo, gee a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la, doohe ko'om dɔgum paalemɔ la, la teere se'ere ti Fa'a Sia la tee tu la.

ref: God sent Jesus Christ our Savior to give us his Spirit.
text: Sɛkana wa ti a ka'ɛ bahɛ tu zuo zo'e zo'e doohe Yezu Kiresta tu Faara la puan,

ref: Jesus treated us much better than we deserve. He made us acceptable to God and gave us the hope of eternal life.
text: ti, en bo' tu buurɔ a zu'uhegɔ la zuo la, tu beere wan sɔna vom kan ba'ahɛ la ti tu tara bu puti'irɛ la.

ref: This message is certainly true. These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds.
text: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ. Mam suure boti fum tɔgera yele ana wa suŋa, ti sɛba en wa'am na nam Yinɛ la wan kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' tuunsuma. Yele ana wa dela ma'a, gee tara nyuurɔ bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo.

ref: But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
text: Gee yehe ya mɛhi nɔkpe'en yooro, yaabeduma yɛla, welehego, la nɔze'elego la zaberɛ yɛla, ba ka tari nyuurɔ, gee me ka tara vuurɛ.

ref: Warn troublemakers once or twice. Then don't have anything else to do with them.
text: Fum han diŋɛ ka'am nɛra yia, le pa'ahɛ yi'i, da le tara sɛla ita la se'em en welegere nɛreba,

ref: You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are. Personal Instructions and Greetings
text: fum diŋɛ mina ti nɛre-ina taaba ka boti yelemɛŋerɛ, dɛna be'em daana, pun sagum a mɛŋa. Ba'ahegɔ sɔhega, la ka'a

ref: I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
text: N han tum Atemas bii Taikikuse fum zi'an na, kpeŋe fum mɛŋa gee wa'am n zi'an Nikopolisi tiŋa na, se'ere en sɔi la, n iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti n bɔna la bilam ɔɔrɔ saŋa la.

ref: When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
text: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa la fum tum Zenase en de yetɔge tɔgera, la Apolos na. Gee la fum kpeŋe fum mɛŋa ti sɛla da koŋe ba ba sore kinɛ la puan.

ref: Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: Sɛba woo en boi la mam la pu'uheri ya na. Pu'uhi sɛba woo en nɔŋɛ tɔ sakerɛ la puan la. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya za'a.

ref: From Paul, who is in jail for serving Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is like a brother because of our faith. Philemon, you work with us and are very dear to us. This letter is to you
text: Pɔɔl, se'em ti ba page en Kiresta Yezu zuo, la Timoti tu suuɔ. N guleheri bɔ'ɔra la Filemɔn tu zɔ ti tu suure nɔŋɛ, la tu tuntuna tadaana,

ref: and to the church that meets in your home. It is also to our dear friend Apphia and to Archippus, who serves the Lord as we do.
text: n kelum guleha bɔ'ɔra la Afiya, tutã la Arekipus en de tu pɛntiira tadaana, la lageŋɔ seko en boi fum yire la.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Philemon's Love and Faith
text: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ tu Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an na. Filemɔn nɔŋerɛ la sakerɛ

ref: Philemon, each time I mention you in my prayers, I thank God.
text: Mam han ta tiɛ fum yele n pu'uhegɔ puan, mam ni bɔ'ɔra la Yinɛ fara saŋa woo,

ref: I hear about your faith in our Lord Jesus and about your love for all God's people.
text: se'ere en sɔi la, mam wum fum nɔŋɛ Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba la woo se'em, la fum sakerɛ Daana Yezu puan.

ref: As you share your faith with others, I pray they may come to know all the blessings Christ has given us.
text: Mam pu'uheri mɛ ti fum wan tara fum sakerɛ la tuna, fum han bɔkɛ tuunsuma sɛ'a za'a ti tu tara Kiresta puan la.

ref: My friend, your love has made me happy and has greatly encouraged me. It has also cheered the hearts of God's people. Paul Speaks to Philemon about Onesimus
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, mam puurɛ ba' pee mɛ la kpemehego seko en ze'ele fum nɔŋerɛ puan, beni zuo, tuulum nɛreba la suya malum gã'a la timpaberɛ fum puan, n suuɔ. Pɔɔl suhega

ref: Christ gives me the courage to tell you what to do.
text: Dina wa zuo, la mam n pun tara paŋa Kiresta puan, ti n bo' fum nuurɛ ti fum tum fum tuunɛ,

ref: But I would rather ask you to do it simply because of love. Yes, as someone in jail for Christ,
text: gee mam wan tugum suhɛ fum la nɔŋerɛ puan, se'ere en sɔi la mam Pɔɔl nyaa kuregɛ mɛ, gee me le bɔna paka deen Kiresta Yezu zuo.

ref: I beg you to help Onesimus! He is like a son to me because I led him to Christ here in jail.
text: Mam sɔheri fum mɛ n bia Onesimuse zuo, mam dela a Sɔ n paka saŋa la.

ref: Before this, he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
text: A daa ka tari vuurɛ bo' fum, gee nananawa, a tari vuurɛ bo' fum, ta ta' la mam me.

ref: I would like to keep him here with me, where he could take your place in helping me while I am here in prison for preaching the good news.
text: Mam boti ni ti a boni mɛ la mam, la a sooe ni fum na'arɛ tuna bɔ'ɔra mam ban daa pagɛ mam faarɛ yelehum wa zuo la;

ref: But I won't do anything unless you agree to it first. I want your act of kindness to come from your heart, and not be something you feel forced to do.
text: gee n ka boti n iŋɛ sɛla gee ti fum ka mina, ti fum tuunsuma la wan dɛna la fum suure en boti gee dagi sɛla en de pɛregerɛ.

ref: Perhaps Onesimus was taken from you for a little while so you could have him back for good,
text: Daanse'ere bala en sɔi ti a daa yehe fum zi'an saŋa fii wa, gee a wan lebe na bɔna la hɔ wuuha,

ref: but not as a slave. Onesimus is much more than a slave. To me he is a dear friend, but to you he is even more, both as a person and as a follower of the Lord.
text: a han lebe na a kan le dɛna da'aberɛ, a gana da'aberɛ mɛ, a dela sunɔŋerɛ suuɔ bu — yele yele mam, gee la wan gana bala fum zi'an, inganɛ puan, la Daana la me puan.

ref: If you consider me a friend because of Christ, then welcome Onesimus as you would welcome me.
text: Bala la, fum han nam ti tu lagum tuna mɛ, to'e en wuu fum wan to'e ni mam se'em la.

ref: If he has cheated you or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
text: A han tuue fum se'ere puan, bii a tari la fum sanɛ, bahɛ ti la dɛna mam sanɛ.

ref: With my own hand I write: I, PAUL, WILL PAY YOU BACK. But don't forget you owe me your life.
text: Mam Pɔɔl mɛŋa n gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa la n nu'o. Mam wan lebehe yɔ dɛ. Mam ka yele se'ere la fum mɛŋa en tari mam sanɛ la pun ta ta' fum mɛa.

ref: My dear friend and follower of Christ our Lord, please cheer me up by doing this for me.
text: Ɛ̃ɛ n suuɔ, bahɛ ti n nyɛ nyuurɔ dina fum puan Daana la zuo! Bahɛ ti n suure gã'a timpaberɛ Kiresta puan.

ref: Please get a room ready for me. I hope your prayers will be answered, and I can visit you.
text: Yele Yinɛ en gee — maahum saama bɔ'ɔ bo' mam, mam tari puti'irɛ ti, fum pu'uhegɔ puan n wan bɔna la hɔ. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ la ka'a

ref: Epaphras is also here in jail for being a follower of Christ Jesus. He sends his greetings,
text: Epafarase, se'em ti ba lagum pagɛ en la mam Kiresta Yezu zuo la, pu'uhi fum na,

ref: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, who work together with me.
text: bala mɛa ti Maki, Aristakuse, Demase la Luki, n tuntuna tadaanduma la me pu'uhi fum na.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
text: Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la fum sia.

ref: Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors.
text: Diimen diimen ha la, Yinɛ da tɔgɛ bo' tu yaabeduma mɛ saŋa zo'e zo'e, la sadɔtɔ zo'e zo'e, dɔla nɔtɔgeheba puan,

ref: But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son.
text: gee ba'ahegɔ dabeha wa puan, a tɔgɛ bo' tu doohe la a dayua puan, sɛka ti a looe ti ka sooe sɔna sɛla woo, se'em puan ti Yinɛ doohe naam sɛla woo en boi wa.

ref: God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together. After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven.
text: A dela Yinɛ suŋa niihum paaɛ paaɛ, dɛna Yinɛ vore vore, bahɛ ti sɛla woo bɔna, doohe a yelehum paŋa puan. Eŋa en duuhe saaleba be'em ba'ahɛ la, a zi'ire la Gani za'a Daana la zuɔ nu'o saazuin ha,

ref: He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs. God's Son Is Greater than Angels
text: A gani suŋereba mɛ, wuu a yu'urɛ se'ere ti a to'e la en de yu'kãtɛ gana suŋereba yu'ura se'em la.

ref: God has never said to any of the angels, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!” Neither has God said to any of them, “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son!”
text: Suŋera kani ti Yinɛ tabelɛ yele en yeti, “Fum dela mam Dayua, zina, mam dɔgɛ hɔ”? Bii a tabelɛ yeti, “N wan dɛna a sɔ, ti a dɛna n Dayua”?

ref: When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
text: Le yɛ'ɛha, Yinɛ en yeti a ta bahɛ ti a dayɔkiima wa wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na la, a tɔgɛ bala yeti, “ Bahɛ ti Yinɛ suŋereba la za'a na'aha dayua wa.”

ref: And when God speaks about the angels, he says, “I change my angels into wind and my servants into flaming fire.”
text: Yinɛ tɔgɛ a suŋereba yele yeti, “A iŋɛ a suŋereba ti ba dɛna la sabeeo, gee iŋɛ a yamehi ti ba dɛna bugum zɛlehi.”

ref: But God says about his Son, “You are God, and you will rule as King forever! Your royal power brings about justice.
text: Gee a dayua la, Yinɛ tɔgɛ eŋa yele yeti, “Fum na'am ka tari ba'ahegɔ, Oo Yinɛ wa, ti fum na'am doore la dɛna tuuntɔɔ doore.

ref: You loved justice and hated evil, and so I, your God, have chosen you. I appointed you and made you happier than any of your friends.”
text: Fum nɔŋɛ la tuntɔɔnɛ, gee yaaɛ be'em. Bala zuo, Yinɛ wa, fum Yinɛ la so fum zuo mɛ la nyɛɛma kaam gana fum taaba.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “In the beginning, Lord, you were the one who laid the foundation of the earth and created the heavens.
text: Gee le yeti, “Pi'ileŋo puan na la, Daana, fum n biŋe timpaberɛ wa ɛ̃berɛ, ti saazuo wa dɛna fum nu'uhi tuuma;

ref: They will all disappear and wear out like clothes, but you will last forever.
text: gee ba woo wan mɛlum gee fum boi mɛ. Ba woo wan ki ŋwana nuga wuu tanɛ la;

ref: You will roll them up like a robe and change them like a garment. But you are always the same, and you will live forever.”
text: wuu fuo ti ba ni kilum se'em la, fum wan kilum ba la bala, wuu futo en pelegere se'em la, bala mɛa ti ba wan tee. Gee fum kɔ'ɔm ana la fum ani se'em la, ti fum yuuma ka tara ba'ahegɔ.”

ref: God never said to any of the angels, “Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you!”
text: Gee suŋera kani ti Yinɛ tabelɛ yele en yeti, “Zi'ire n zuɔ nu'o na, gura ti n ta bahɛ ti fum dinduma lebege fum nama dafeere?”

ref: Angels are merely spirits sent to serve people who are going to be saved.
text: Suŋereba la za'a dagi la sabeeo Sia tuuma la puan, ti Yinɛ tuna ba ti ba tuna bɔ'ɔra sɛba en wan ta sooe vaaɛ faarɛ la?

ref: We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away.
text: Bala, tu dela tu iŋɛ tube wuŋa la sɛla ti tu wum la, la tu da bɔregɛ yehe de puan.

ref: The message spoken by angels proved to be true, and all who disobeyed or rejected it were punished as they deserved.
text: Ti, yelehum sebo en yuum muulɛ doohe suŋereba puan la yuum han tara tibehum, ti bu tuure, la tube-kpirega woo tɔ'ɔra namehegɔ kãtɛ,

ref: So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true.
text: ŋwani ti tu wan ta'am piiɛ, tu han zagehɛ faarɛ kãtɛ dina wa? Tu wum bu yele yia la Daana la mɛŋa zi'an, ti sɛba en wum bu a mɛŋa nuuren la me yele tu ti bu dela bala,

ref: God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to. The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
text: ti Yinɛ mɛŋa en de aze'nyɛ daana la ba, la nyalema la kirehi, la paŋa tuuma yima yima, doohe Fa'a Sia la bo'olum la ti a tɔrɛ bo' doohe a mɛŋa sumboolum la. Se'em en tari to faarɛ

ref: We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels.
text: Lan de se'em la dela,Yinɛ ka dikɛ tinsɛka en ye' na, ti tu tɔgera ka yele wa, iŋɛ suŋereba la nu'uhen.

ref: Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God, “What makes you care about us humans? Why are you concerned for weaklings such as we?
text: Gee nɛra ayima en tɔgɛ gɔŋɔ la puan zi'an kayima yeti, “Asaala dela beni ti fum ba' tɛra ba yele bala, Bii a dela ani me, ti fum ba' tara ba bala?

ref: You made us lower than the angels for a while. Yet you have crowned us with glory and honor.
text: Fum iŋɛ en ti saŋa fii wa, a da zuna la suŋereba. Fum vugele en la suŋa, la na'ahegɔ muŋa,

ref: And you have put everything under our power!” God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control.
text: dikɛ sɛla woo biŋe a nama tiŋa.” Yinɛ en dikɛ sɛla woo biŋe a nama tiŋa la, a ka gee sɛla bahɛ ti la ka bɔna a nu'uhen. Gee lan de se'em la dela, tu nan ka nyɛ ti sɛla woo bɔna a nu'uhen,

ref: What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
text: gee tu nyɛ Yezu, se'em ti saŋa fii wa, Yinɛ iŋɛ en ti a bɔna suŋereba la tiŋa, gee nananawa nyaa vuge suŋa la na'ahegɔ muŋa doohe a namehegɔ kum la zuo, ti Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la zuo,Yezu wan lem kum bo' nɛra woo.

ref: Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory.
text: La daa dela ma'a, ti Yinɛ se'em en sɔi, la se'em puan ti sɛla woo bɔna la, en boti a tari nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'am suŋa puan na la zuo, a bahɛ ti se'em en de ba faarɛ nɛŋa daana la bi'ihɛ a mɛŋa kahegɛ namehegɔ puan.

ref: Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters.
text: Beni zuo, se'em en peere la, la sɛba be'em ti a pee la, za'a ze'ele la zi'an kayina na. Beni zuo, a ka tari valum, ti a yi' ba a suurɔ,

ref: He even said to God, “I will tell them your name and sing your praises when they come together to worship.”
text: bala ti a yeti, “Mam wan muulɛ fum yu'urɛ bo' n suurɔ la, “Lageŋɔ la puan, mam wan pɛgɛ hɔ.”

ref: He also said, “I will trust God.” Then he said, “Here I am with the children God has given me.”
text: Le yɛ'ɛha, a yeti, “Mam wan delum la eŋa.” A me le yeti, “Mam en ŋwana, la kɔma sɛba ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' mam la.”

ref: We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death.
text: Lan yi de ti, kɔma la za'a dela inganɛ la ziim la, eŋa Yezu mɛŋa me wa'am na ana mɛ wuu bamam la, ti a ta'am doohe kum puan sagum se'em en tari kum paŋa la. Sagena la en bala,

ref: But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying.
text: gee faaɛ sɛba woo vom saŋa woo en boi yamenɛ puan doohe ban zoti kum la zuo.

ref: Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants.
text: La kɔ'ɔm niiɛ mɛ gã'a, ti Yezu ka wa'am na ti a suŋɛ la suŋereba, gee a tugum wa'am na ti a suŋɛ la Aberaham yaahi.

ref: He had to be one of us, so he could serve God as our merciful and faithful high priest and sacrifice himself for the forgiveness of our sins.
text: Bala zuo,Yezu dela a lebege ana wuu a suurɔ la sɛla woo puan, la a dɛna nimbɔ'zɔta, la sakerɛ malema kiima, Yinɛ tuuma la puan, la a kaabera be'em duuhego kaabegɔ bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la.

ref: And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.
text: Beni zuo, eŋa mɛŋa nyɛ la makerɛ, a namehegɔ la puan, a wan ta'am suŋɛ sɛba woo en nyɛti makerɛ la.

ref: My friends, God has chosen you to be his holy people. So think about Jesus, the one we call our apostle and high priest!
text: Bala zuo n suurɔ, n tuulum nɛre tadaanduma Yinɛ saazuo yi'a wa puan wa, bihɛ ya suŋa suŋa Yezu en de tuntuna, gee dɛna malema kiima bo' tumam sakerɛ yelehum la wa la,

ref: Jesus was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in serving all God's people.
text: a daa tari sakerɛ mɛ la se'em en looe en la, wuu Moose en yuum “de sakerɛ daana la pu'uhegɔ yire tuuma la za'a se'em la.”

ref: But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house.
text: Gee la bala za'a, Yezu tari zuo la suŋa gana Moose mɛ, wuu yire daana en tari zuo gana yire la mɛŋa se'em la.

ref: Moses was a faithful servant and told God's people what would be said in the future.
text: Yi bihɛ, Moose yuum dela sakerɛ daana Lageŋɔ tuuma la za'a, dɛna de yameŋa, tɔgera yele sɛ'a ti ba wan tɔgɛ beere.

ref: But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope. A Rest for God's People
text: Gee Kiresta en de se'em la, yuum tari la sakerɛ la Yinɛ lageŋɔ la, dɛna dayua, ti tumam dɛna yire la, tu han gurɛ tu inkpeŋere wa la tu nyu'ɔ ŋma'a sɛka en de tu beere puti'irɛ zuo wa kankaŋi. Bumbu'um tahega

ref: It is just as the Holy Spirit says, “If you hear God's voice today,
text: Bala zuo, wuu Fa'a Sia la en yele se'em ti, “Zina, ya han wum Yinɛ kua,

ref: don't be stubborn! Don't rebel like those people who were tested in the desert. *
text: da kãbɛ ya suya wuu ya yaabeduma pobiriga la, wuu ban yuum boi makerɛ dabeherɛ puan kpaahenɛ puan ha la,

ref: For forty years your ancestors tested God and saw the things he did.
text: zi'an ti ya yaabeduma la yuum makɛ mam bihɛ, la bamam en pun nyɛ mam tum se'em, yuuma pihinaahi la.

ref: “Then God got tired of them and said, ‘You people never show good sense, and you don't understand what I want you to do.’
text: Bala ti mam yuum iŋɛ suure la sankaŋa buuri la, yele ba yeti, bamam saŋa woo kɔ'ɔm bɔregera mɛ ba suya puan, ba ka makɛ mina mam sore la.

ref: God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ”
text: Mam sunsunyiim la puan, mam pɔ mɛ yeti, ba kan kɛ̃ mam vo'ohego la puan.”

ref: My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God.
text: Tara ya mɛhi suŋa n suurɔ, ti ya se'em da tara be'em suure, suse'ere en ka nane, gee ŋmɛregera de mɛŋa yɛha Yinɛ vua wa zi'an.

ref: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
text: Gee tugum kpemehe ya taaba daare woo wuu ban yi'iri se'em ti “zina” wa la, ti ya se'em da bɔna ti be'em pã'ahɛ en ti a kãbɛ a suure.

ref: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam nyaa dela taaba la Kiresta, tu han gurɛ tu yia sakerɛ la kankaŋi ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn like those who rebelled.”
text: Wuu lan yele se'em yeti, “Zina ya han wum Yinɛ kua, da kãbɛ ya suya wuu ya yaabeduma pobiriga puan la.”

ref: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
text: Nananawa, bani en bala yuum wum gee bire pɔya la? Dagi sɛba woo en yuum bahɛ Igipiti ti Moose tari ba yehe na la?

ref: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
text: Gee bani ti Yinɛ yuum pɔ nuurɛ ti ba kan kɛ̃ eŋa vo'ohego la puan la, han dagena sɛba en yuum ka nane la?

ref: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
text: Bala, tu nyɛ ya ti ba yuum ka nyaŋɛ kɛ̃, doohe la ban ka nam la zuo.

ref: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
text: Bala zuo, vo'ohego puan kɛ̃'a nɔbiŋere la en kelum bɔna la, bahɛ ya ti tu biha tu mɛhi suŋa, ti ya se'em da ta yaa koŋe a paaɛ vo'ohego la.

ref: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
text: Asira, faarɛ yelehum la wa'am tumam zi'an na wuu bumɔ en yuum wa'am bamam zi'an se'em na la. Gee yelehum la ti bamam yuum wum la, yuum ka bo' ba nyuurɔ, beni zuo, ba yuum ka naaɛ taaba ba sakerɛ puan, la sɛba en yuum kelehe yelehum la.

ref: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam sɛba en sakɛ la, kɛ̃ vo'ohego la puan mɛ wuu Yinɛ en yele se'em yeti, “Wuu mam sunsunyiim puan ti n pɔ yeti, Ba kan makɛ kɛ̃ mam vo'ohego la puan” la, la eŋa en pun tum sɛla woo ba'ahɛ tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan ha la.

ref: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Zi'an kayima gɔŋɔ la puan, la tɔgɛ dabeha ayopɔi la yele yeti, “Ti Yinɛ vo'ohe dabeha ayopɔi daarɛ la, a tuuma la woo puan.”

ref: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
text: Le yɛ'ɛha, ze'kana mɛa puan yeti, “Ba kan kɛ̃ mam vo'ohego la puan.”

ref: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
text: Bala vo'ohego la en kelum yu'ɛ bo' basɛba ti ba kɛ̃ la, ti sɛba en yuum pɔhɛ to'e faarɛ yelehum wa yia la koŋe ba kɛ̃ mɛ ban ka nane la zuo,

ref: Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn!”
text: bala zuo ti a le yehe la dabeherɛ ze'ele, yi'ira dɛ “Zina”, yuum tɔgɛ de yele doohe Deviti puan, pooren pooren na Yetɔge sebo en yuum pun tɔgɛ gã'a yeti, “Zina, ya han wum Yinɛ kua, Da kãbɛ ya suya.”

ref: If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yosua yuum han bo' ni nɛreba wa la vo'ohego, Yinɛ kan le tɔgeri ni pooren, dabeherɛ deyima yele.

ref: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
text: Bala, vo'ohego dabeherɛ kelum bɔna mɛ bo' Yinɛ nɛreba.

ref: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
text: Beni zuo, Sɛba en kɛ̃ Yinɛ vo'ohego la puan la, me ni vo'ohe ba tuuma puan mɛ wuu Yinɛ en vo'ohe se'em a tuuma za'a puan la.

ref: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
text: Bala, bahɛ ya ti tu tiregɛ ya suŋa suŋa la tu ta'am kɛ̃ vo'ohego wa puan, ti se'em da bɔna ya puan lu doohe ka nam wuu bamam en yuum ka nam se'em la.

ref: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, Yinɛ yelehum la vɔi mɛ gee tuna, dita gana su'a en tari nɔya ayi' la, ni ŋmaaɛ vo'ohum la ta paaɛ sia la, la inganɛ zi'an woo, ta ta' la kɔba, la a tomehego zi'ihi woo la sɛla en boi kuba la puan ha za'a. Yelehum la mi' suure puan ti'ihegɔ woo mɛ la de maaheŋɔ woo.

ref: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
text: A nɛŋan, sɛla ka boi sugɛ, gee sɛla woo ze' la beele, gã'a kpaii, se'em daana ti tu wan ta yele en tu yɛla za'a la ninin. Yezu dela malema kiima kãtɛ

ref: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
text: Lan de ti tu tari la malema kiima kãtɛ en kɛ̃ tinsuŋan la, dɛna Yezu, Yinɛ dayua la, bahɛ ya ti tu gurɛ tu sakerɛ yelehum la kankaŋi.

ref: Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin!
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka tari la malema kiima sɛka en ka zoti tu tɔregɔ nimbu'ɔ, gee tu tari la se'em en nyɛ mageha sadɔseko woo puan wuu tumam la, gee a ka tum be'em.

ref: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
text: Bala zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu paaɛ ya Yinɛ na'am kuka la nɛŋan ti zu'uhegɔ bɔna la, la sukpe'ene, la tu ta'am to'e a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la, nyɛ zu'uhegɔ, ti la suŋɛ tɔ sansɛka woo n nari.

ref: Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins.
text: Malemaduma kiima woo ti ba looe en saaleba puan, ba ni bahɛ ti a sɔna la yelesɛ'a en de Yinɛ yɛla biheri bɔ'ɔra saaleba. A kaabera bo'olum, gee kaabera be'em zuo.

ref: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
text: A dela nɛre ma'ma', ana bããlam gee maala ba yɛmka'alum, la ban kini buta la, eŋa mɛŋa en boi tɔregɔ nu'uhen la zuo.

ref: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
text: Ti ŋwana zuo ti la dɛna a ni kaabɛ a mɛŋa be'em zuo yia, gee nyaa kaabɛ bo' nɛreba la me.

ref: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
text: Nɛra ka dikeri na'ahegɔ kuna bɔ'ɔra a mɛŋa, gee a ni to'e de mɛ Yinɛ han yi' en wuu Aron ti a yi' se'em la.

ref: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
text: Bala mɛa ti Kiresta yuum ka dikɛ na'ahegɔ kuna bo' a mɛŋa ti a dɛna malema kiima, gee se'em en looe en la en yeti, “Fum dela mam Dayua, Zina mam de fum sɔ”;

ref: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
text: wuu en le tɔgɛ gɔŋɔ la zi'an kayima se'em yeti, “Fum dela malema daana kɔ'ɔm yɛ'a ha, doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la.”

ref: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
text: Yezu en yuum boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, a inganɛ puan la, a yuum dikeri la pu'uhegɔ, la suhega la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa la nintam bɔ'ɔra se'em daana en wan ta'am faaɛ en kum puan bahɛ la, ti a ni kelehe en, a Yinɛ zoolum la zuo.

ref: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
text: La en pun dɛna Dayua la, a zamehɛ naŋa mɛ, en namehɛ sɛla woo puan.

ref: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
text: Ŋwana ti Yinɛ en yehe en ti a dɛna malema kiima doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki. Lua ninmu'urɛ kã'aŋɔ

ref: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
text: Yele deni wa puan, tu tari la yɛla zo'e zo'e en wan yele, gee a pa'alegɔ en tɔi, yan ka bɔkerɛ kalam kalam la zuo.

ref: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
text: La sankana ti ya deni ya dɛna pa'aleba la, ya kelum bɔta la nɛra le pa'alɛ ya pɔhega yele sɛ'a en boi Yinɛ yelehum la puan. Ya boti la bi'ihum gee dagi di kpe'ene.

ref: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en mɔgeri bi'ihum kelum dɛna la bilia, ka mina tuuma tuuntɔɔ vom yɛla puan.

ref: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
text: Gee dikpe'ene dela sɛba en bi'ɛ kahegɛ bunɔ, sɛba en nyɛ kpemehego tuuma puan, tana wɛleha be'em la sum la.

ref: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
text: Bala zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu kiŋɛ nɛŋa bi'ihɛ suŋa suŋa, tu bahɛ pi'ileŋo pa'alegɔ ti ba pun pa'alɛ tu yia Kiresta puan pooren, da le bahɛ ya ti tu le tagehɛ ɛberɛ iibegɔ, la en de tu tee kum tuuma, tu tara sakerɛ Yinɛ puan,

ref: Let's grow up, if God is willing.
text: Tumam wan iŋɛ sɛla en bala, Yinɛ han bo' tu sore.

ref: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
text: Samanɛ se'ere en nyuuri saa ko'om en niira baha de zuo saŋa woo la, gee maala disuŋa bɔ'ɔra sɛba en kɔɔri de la, to'ore la maalegɔ Yinɛ zi'an.

ref: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
text: Gee samanɛ la han yɛhera gu'uhi la tuntuuro, de ka tari vuurɛ, ka zaaɛ ba pɔ bahɛ de zuo, ti de ba'ahegɔ ta dɛna la ba nyɔ' dɛ.

ref: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
text: La tumam en pun tɔgera ŋwana wa la, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, tu mi' suŋa suŋa ti yelesuma en gani ŋwana boi ya puan mɛ. Yelesuma sɛ'a en de faarɛ yɛla.

ref: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
text: Yinɛ dagi se'em n ka bo'ori buurɔ. A kan mum a nini la ya tuuma la, la ya nɔŋerɛ la ti ya pa'alɛ a yu'urɛ zuo la, doohe la yan bihɛ nɛresunduma la se'em, wuu yan kelum ita se'em wa la.

ref: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
text: Tu boti ya ayima ayima woo me pa'alɛ la ninmu'urɛ dinɛ taaba, la ya ta'am nyɛ beere puti'irɛ za'a za'a, ta paaɛ de ba'ahegɔ.

ref: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
text: Bala ti se'em kan bukɛ, gee ya wan tɔgehɛ sɛba woo en doohe sakerɛ, la ŋmibega puan ta to'e Yinɛ en biŋe nuurɛ ti a iŋɛ se'ere la. Yinɛ nɔbiŋere ka yuure

ref: No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham,
text: Yinɛ en yuum biŋe nuurɛ la Aberaham la, en yuum ka tari se'em en gani eŋa mɛŋa ti a wan pɔ a daana la, a yuum pɔ la a mɛŋa,

ref: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
text: yeti, “ Yelemɛŋerɛ, n wan iŋɛ fum sum, n wan bahɛ ti fum wi'ɛ.”

ref: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
text: Bala ti Aberaham me yuum tari sugeri ŋmibera la, a yuum sirum ta to'e nɔbiŋere la mɛ.

ref: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
text: Saaleba ni pɔ la se'em n gani ba. Pɔrɛ han diŋɛ pɔ, ti ba dikɛ sɛla taaɛ pɔtɛ la, nɔkpe'ene woo ba'ahɛ ya.

ref: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
text: Bala mɛa ti Yinɛ en daa ta bɔta ti a pa'alɛ ti la niiɛ bo' sɛba en wan ta sooe sɔna a nɔbiŋere la en de a naanɛ se'ere en ka malum tɛɛra la, a pɔ la pɔtɛ ti de dɛna daalehum,

ref: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
text: ti la doohe yele sɛ'a ayi' en ka teere puan, yele sɛ'a puan ti deyima dɛna Yinɛ kan ta'am parum la, tumam sɛba en sugɛ a zi'an la wan nyɛ inkpeŋere kankaŋi ta'am to'e putɛ'se'ere ti a dikɛ biŋe tu la.

ref: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
text: Tu tari la putɛ'dina, ti de ana wuu ɛberɛ kãtɛ, la tiŋasuka nyuuŋa la bo' tumam sɛɛhi. Beere puti'irɛ se'ere en kɛ̃ saraa la puan ha, tama la en lu yula la pooren ha la,

ref: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
text: zi'an ti Yezu dɛna nɛŋa daana bini tumam zuo, kɛ̃ bini, dɛna malema kiima kɔ'ɔm yɛ'a ha, doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la.

ref: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
text: “Naba Mɛlekizɛdɛki wa n yehe Salem na wa, dɛna Gani za'a Yinɛ malema daana la, wa tu'uhɛ Aberaham ti a ze'ele la en kiŋɛ ta zabɛ nyaŋɛ naduma gee lɛbera na la, ti a purɛ sum bahɛ a zuo.”

ref: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
text: Ti Aberaham yehe lɔgeseto woo ti a nyɛ la puan, “Seko woo en pa'aheri pia la bo' en.” Yia, a yu'urɛ la vuurɛ dela, “Tuntɔɔ naba” Buyi', a dela Salem tiŋa naba, ti de vuurɛ dɛna la, “Suma'ahum naba.”

ref: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
text: A ka tari sɔ, a ka tari ma, a ka tari buuri tuuhum. A ka tari pi'ileŋo dabeha, bii vom ba'ahegɔ dabeha. Gee a ani wuu Yinɛ Dayua la, dɛna malema daana yɛ'a ha.

ref: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
text: Bihɛ ya en de nɛre kãtɛ se'em. Ti Aberaham n pun dɛna yaaba la, ta bo' en pia pia bo'olum a lɔgerɔ la woo puan.

ref: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
text: Sɛba en de Levi buuri, tuna malema tuuma la, Yinɛ bo' ba la nɔze'elego, ti ba tɔ'ɔha pia pia bo'olum nɛreba la zi'an, ba suurɔ la zi'an, la bamam en pun dɛna Aberaham yaahi la.

ref: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
text: Gee buraa kana wa en dagi yaaŋa wa, to'e pia pia bo'olum mɛ Aberaham zi'an, gee purɛ sum bahɛ Aberaham, se'em en pun to'e nɔbiŋere.

ref: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
text: Nɔkpe'ene ka boi bini, tarema en to'ore sum kpɛ'ɛma zi'an.

ref: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
text: Yele wa zi'an kayima dela, saaleba en kiiri to'ohe pia pia bo'olum, gee zi'an kayima me, se'em ti ba mina ti a pun bɔna mɛ na la me to'ohe pia pia bo'olum la mɛ.

ref: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
text: Se'em wan ta'am pun yeti, Levi mɛŋa la en yuum to'ohe pia bo'olum la, me yuum bo'ori pia bo'olum mɛ a yaaba Aberaham puan na.

ref: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, a yuum boi a yaaba Aberaham iŋa puan mɛ, gee ti Mɛlekizɛdɛki tu'uhɛ en la. Malema daana ayima en doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki.

ref: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
text: Han dɛna ti vom sebo en bi'ɛ kahegɛ han daa bɔna nɛreba la puan doohe la Levi buuri malema duma tuuma la, beni ti la le nari ni ti ba le tɔgera malema daana ayima yele, se'em en doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la, gee ka tɔgera ti a doohe la Aron?

ref: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
text: Teere han wa'am malema duma la puan na, teere me wan ba'ahum wa'am nɔze'elego la me puan na.

ref: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
text: Se'em daana zuo ti ba tɔgera yele ana wa la yehe la buuri deyima me puan na, buuri se'ere nɛra en ka tabelɛ tum kaabegɔ bimbine la zuo.

ref: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
text: La niiɛ mɛ gã'a ti tu Daana la yehe la Yuda buuri puan na, buuri dina wa puan, Moose ka tɔgɛ malema duma yele de puan.

ref: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
text: La pun kɔ'ɔm ba' niiɛ mɛ gã'a, ti malema daana ayima han ihege na doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la,

ref: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba purɛ a yele mɛ na yeti, “Fum dela malema daana ka tara ba'ahegɔ, doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki.”

ref: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
text: . Zi'an kayima me pa'ali ti, beere putɛ'sunɛ se'ere boi gana ŋwana, ti tu wan dɔla pu'uha Yinɛ.

ref: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
text: gee malema daana ena wa la, pɔ mɛ gee lebege malema daana, doohe se'em daana en yele en yeti, “ Daana la pɔ mɛ, gee kan tee a puti'irɛ, ti ‘Fum dela malema daana ka tara ba'ahegɔ;’ ”

ref: This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God.
text: lan de se'em la, Yezu ze'ele bo' la nɔpu'ulegɔ n gani bala.

ref: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
text: Gee malema daana ena kɔ'ɔm bɔna a malema tuuma la puan mɛ wuuha wuuha, se'ere en sɔi la a boi la wuuha.

ref: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
text: Dina zuo la a nyaŋeri mɛ faara, nananawa la saŋa woo sɛba en dole a puan paara Yinɛ la baha, eŋa en vɔi wuuha la zuo, a ŋmɛ'ɛra nupu'ɔ ba zuo la.

ref: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
text: La dela ma'a ti tu tara malema kiima kana taaba, en de fa'a, ka tara gbelegere, ka galum, ti ba yehe en ze'ele a ma'a ti a ka pa'ahɛ be'em duma puan, ti ba zɛkɛ en zom zuin zuin ha gaŋɛ saazuo wa.

ref: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
text: Eŋa ka ani wuu malema duma bana la, a ka de a ni kaabera daare woo, ni kaabɛ a mɛŋa be'em zuo yia, gee nyaa kaabɛ bo' nɛreba la be'em. A kaabɛ la kaabegɔ buyina za'a, en daa dikɛ a mɛŋa kaabɛ la.

ref: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
text: Nɔze'elego la daa loohe la sɛba en de tɔregɔ duma la ti ba dɛna malema kiinduma, gee nɔpɔrɛ la en wa'am ze'elego la pooren na la, looe la Dayua sɛka en bi'ɛ kahegɛ, saŋa woo la.

ref: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
text: Yeledaa sɛka en boi tumam en yeti sɛla wa puan dela dina wa. Tu tari la malema kiima en boi a tɔka, se'em en zi' Gani za'a Daana la na'am kuka la zuɔ nu'o saazuo ha,

ref: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
text: dɛna Yinɛ tuntuna saraa la puan, yelemɛŋerɛ kaabegɔ paka sɛka ti Daana la mɛŋa, gee dagi saaleba ti' la.

ref: Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer.
text: Ba loore Malema kiima woo ti a kaabera la bo'olum kaabegɔ, gee ti la me dɛna ti malema kiima kana wa mɛŋa me ni tara sɛla na kaabɛ.

ref: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
text: La han deni la timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla, a pun kan ta'am deni malema daana, se'ere en sɔi la, malema duma pun bɔna mɛ kaabera bo'olum kaabegɔ la wuu ze'elego la en pa'alɛ se'em la.

ref: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
text: Bama na'aheri Yinɛ la tanɛ deo seko puan ti asaala mɛŋa ti', gee dɛna saazuo pu'uhegɔ yire la yinyiligo la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Moose en yuum ta maahena ti a ti' pu'uhegɔ tanɛ deo wa la, Yinɛ yuum kagɛ en mɛ yeti, “Bihɛ suŋa la fum ti' de sɛla woo wuu mam magehɛ de se'em pa'alɛ hɔ zoore la zuo la.”

ref: Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
text: Gee nananawa Yezu to'e la tuunɛ en gani ŋwana, pun nyaa ta dɛna nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔpu'ulegɔ en gani ŋwana paaɛ paaɛ la puan, nɔpu'ulegɔ en yage nɔbiŋere en gani ŋwana zuo.

ref: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la yuum han ka tari ni tuure, la ka le pakɛ ni ti ba le ɛɛra nɔpu'ulegɔ buyi.

ref: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
text: Yinɛ yuum nyɛ la tuure la yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la puan en sɔi ti a yuum yeti, “Dabeha sirum kini na, Daana la en yele, ti n wan pu'ulɛ nɔpaalɛ la Iziraleduma wa, la Yuuduma wa,

ref: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
text: ti de kan ana wuu mam yuum pu'ulɛ nɔse'ere la ba yaabeduma la, daan se'ere daarɛ ti mam tari ba la n mɛŋa nu'o ze'ele Igipiti tiŋa yehe na la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba yuum ka dole mam nɔpu'ulegɔ la, bala, ba yele yuum ka pakɛ mam. Daana la en yele se'em n bala.

ref: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Nɔpu'ulegɔ kuna ti mam wan pu'ulɛ la Iziraleduma wa, dabeha aŋa han ta tole, Daana la en yele, “N wan dikɛ n nɔze'elego la iŋɛ ba puti'ira puan, gee gulehɛ a iŋɛ ba suya puan, dɛna ba Yinɛ, ti ba dɛna mam nɛreba.

ref: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
text: Ti ba kan le pa'ala taaba, bii yɛta ba mabia yeti, ‘Baŋɛ Daana la,’ se'ere en sɔi la, ba woo wan mina mam, ze'ele ba bumbila ta paara ba nɛre kãtɛ.

ref: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, n wan zɔ ba tuure la nimbu'ɔ, gee kan le tɛra ba be'em la.”

ref: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
text: Yinɛ en tɔgeri “nɔpu'ulepaalɛ” yele la, pa'alɛ ti, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la dela kɛka. Ti sɛla en kuregɛ ki nuga, kan yuue gee mɛlum.

ref: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
text: Nananawa, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la yuum tara la malema, ti ba dɔla na'aha Yinɛ, timpaberɛ zuo pu'uhegɔ zi'an.

ref: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ti' yia tanɛ la mɛ, ti yia bɔ'ɔ la puan butolela zɛ'ɛlega bɔna de puan, ti teebula ti boroboro se'ere en kaaberi bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ la paga de zuo; ti ze'kana wa yu'urɛ yi'iri fa'a zi'an.

ref: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
text: Ti sɛla en ze' bini dela nyuuŋo digelum nyɔ'a daka la, la nɔpu'ulegɔ tuulum daka la, ti salema dikɛ kaaɛ ka luga la za'a la, salema kaleŋa bɔna ka puan la maana dia la, la Aron kurego doore la en yuum puue puuro la me bɔna ka puan, ti kuge lama la ti ba gulehɛ ze'elego la iŋɛ a zuo la me bɔna ka puan;

ref: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
text: ti tuulum daka wa zuo, kelumpagehi duma en nyagere en yage ka zuo, tɔ taaba pi' sugeri dia zi'an la. Yele ana wa tu kan ta'am pa'alɛ a yɛla vuurɛ paaɛ paaɛ nananawa.

ref: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
text: Ban maahum yele ana ba'ahɛ la, ti malema duma la kɛ̃'ɛra daarɛ woo yia bɔ' la puan na, ti ba malena ba malema la;

ref: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
text: gee malema kiima la ma'a en ki'ire saraa la puan gee buyina yuunɛ woo puan, tara ziim ti a kaabɛ bo' a mɛŋa, la nɛreba la be'em sebo ti ba tum gee ka mina la.

ref: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
text: Ŋwana ti Fa'a Sia la pa'alɛ yeti, sadɔɔ seko en dole kɛ̃'ɛra saraa la puan la nan ka yu'ɛ, yia tanɛ deo la en nan kelum zɛ'a la.

ref: This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices.
text: Ŋwana dela daalehum bɔ'ɔra zina beere wa, ti bo'olum bu'a, la kaabegɔ kaabera kan ta'am bahɛ ti na'ahereba la puti'ira bi'ɛ kahegɛ,

ref: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
text: gee la yeen dɛna la dia la nyua, la ko'om sua malema yima yima, la kiha sɛ'a en dikɛ bo' ingɔŋɔ, gura sansɛka en wan ta ze'ele sɛla woo suŋa la.

ref: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
text: Gee Kiresta en wa'am na la a dela malema kiima bo' yelesuma sɛ'a n wa'am na bɔna doohe tanɛ yire n ka tari makerɛ dɛna yire n ba'ahɛ yire ,

ref: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
text: a kɛ̃ la buyina za'a za'a Fa'a Saraa wa puan, ka tari buuhi la nagebibehi ziim, gee a mɛŋa ziim, n ze'ele bo' faarɛ en ka tari ba'ahegɔ.

ref: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
text: Han dɛna ti buuhi la lɔlehi ziim, la nagepole tampɛgelum la en tari miiha sɛba en galum la zuo, ti la pɛɛra ba ingana, duuhe ba la,

ref: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
text: Ŋwani ti Kiresta ziim la kan tum gaŋɛ bala zo'e zo'e se'em en doohe Sia sɛka en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la puan, kaabɛ a mɛŋa fa'a kaabegɔ bo' Yinɛ la. Ziim buna wan sirum pee tu puti'ira yehe kum tuuma puan, tari tu lebe ti tu na'aha Yinɛ Vɔa wa!

ref: Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings!
text: Dina wa zuo ti a dɛna nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra bo' nɔpu'ulepaalɛ la, ti bala ti sɛba ti ba yi' la wan ta'am to'e nɔbiŋere lɔkɔ la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, kum n ki to'e ba, ba tuure la en daa boi yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la puan la.

ref: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
text: Zi'an ti nɔpu'ulegɔ biŋe, la dela nɔpu'ula la ki gee ti nɔpu'ulegɔ la nyaa tuna.

ref: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɔpu'ulegɔ la wan pɔhɛ tuna la nɛra la en ki, de ka tuni se'em ma'a ti nɔpu'ulegɔ la nan vɔna la.

ref: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
text: Ŋwana zuo, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la yuum ka makɛ tuna, ti ziim ka ka'ɛ.

ref: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Moose yuum muulɛ nɔze'elego la woo mɛ bo' nɛreba la doohe pa'alegɔ la en de se'em la ba'ahɛ, gee nyaa dikɛ nagebibehi, la buuhi ziim, la ko'om la gunguno mɔlegɔ, la nyunhunyuuŋa tuhe bini, nyaa dikɛ miihi gɔŋɔ la mɛŋa, gee miihi nɛreba la za'a,

ref: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
text: gee yɛta yeti, “Nɔpu'ulegɔ la ziim en ŋwana ti Daana la maahum bo' ya.”

ref: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
text: Bala mɛa ti a dikɛ ziim la miihi tanɛ deo la, la lɔge seto woo ti ba tari na'ahera la.

ref: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, pa'alegɔ la puan, sɛla woo peere mɛ la ziim, ti ziim han ka ka'ɛ, sugeri dia ka boi bo' be'em. Kiresta kaabegɔ la en saalum be'em

ref: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
text: Bala ti la daa nara ti saazuo lɔgerɔ la ti ba tɔgehɛ maalɛ la, ba duuha ba la malema ana, gee saazuo lɔgerɔ la mɛŋa nari la kaabegɔ seko en gani ŋwana.

ref: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
text: Se'ere ni sɔi la, Kiresta ka kɛ̃ la pu'uhegɔ deo seko ti saaleba nu'uhi mɛ, yeen dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ deo la ti ba tɔgehɛ, gee a kɛ̃ la tinsuŋan mɛŋa puan ha, ti a bɔna Yinɛ nɛŋan nananawa, tumam zuo.

ref: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
text: Bii a kaabera a mɛŋa tulena wuu malema kiima la en ni kɛ̃ Fa'a Saraa la puan buyina yuunɛ woo, tara ziim en dagi a mɛŋa ziim se'em la;

ref: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
text: la ha deni bala, a ye wan nameheri mɛ tulena, pɔhera tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan na la. Gee lan de se'em la, a wa'am la buyina za'a za'a ba'ahegɔ saŋa buuri wa puan, ti a fooe be'em bahɛ, doohe a dikɛ a mɛŋa kaabɛ.

ref: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
text: bala mɛa ti Kiresta, en kaabɛ a mɛŋa buyina bo' nɛreba zo'e zo'e be'em la ba'ahɛ, a wan le niiɛ na buyi' puan na, dagi a le zabɛ la be'em, gee a faaɛ sɛba en ani kɛli gura en la.

ref: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
text: Bala, ze'elego la en yeen dɛna yinyiligo bo' yelesuma la en kini na la, gee dagi yelesuma la yelemɛŋerɛ naanɛ n bala la, kaabegɔ yiŋɔ la en kaaberi yuunɛ woo kan ta'am bahɛ ti sɛba en dole ku la dɛna nɛreba en bi'ɛ kahegɛ.

ref: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
text: Han dagi ni bala, ba kan bahɛ ni ba kaabegɔ, sɛba en na'aheri ku la, han duuhe ba mɛhi buyina za'a za'a, ka le tari ni be'em puti'ira?

ref: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la kan ta'am iŋɛ ti lɔlehi la buuhi ziim ta'am saalum be'em.

ref: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
text: Lan vuti fifihe ti Kiresta ta wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na la, a yeti, “Kaabegɔ, la bo'olum fum ka boti, gee iŋa ti fum maahum bo' mam.

ref: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
text: Nyɔ'a bo'olum, la be'em kaabegɔ bo'olum, fum suure ka boti ba.

ref: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
text: Bala ti mam yeti, Yinɛ bihɛ, mam wa'am na ti n tum la fum sumboolum Oo Yinɛ, .”

ref: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
text: En yele se'em zuin wa yeti, “Fum suure bii fum nini tika ka boi kaabegɔ, la bo'olum bii nyɔ'a bo'olum la be'em duuhego bo'olum puan la” ,

ref: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
text: gee nyaa pa'ahɛ yeti, “Bihɛ, mam wa'am na ti n tum la fum sumboolum.” Bala, a saalum yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la bahɛ mɛ, ti a ta'am sɛ yi'i nɔpu'ulegɔ la.

ref: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
text: Ti la dɛna la Yinɛ mɛŋa sumboolum zuo, ti ba pee tumam doohe Yezu Kiresta inganɛ la en dikɛ kaabɛ buyina za'a za'a bo' nɛra woo la.

ref: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
text: Gee malema daana woo ze' mɛ beere en yilegeri beere a tumam la puan, kaabera kaabegɔ yiŋɔ mɛa tulena, kaabegɔ yiŋɔ mɛa en kan malum pee be'em bahɛ la.

ref: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
text: Gee Kiresta kaabɛ la buyina bo' saŋa woo, kaabegɔ kuyina ma'a la bo' be'em ba'ahɛ la, a nyaa zi'ire la Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o,

ref: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
text: pɔhɛ bala ma'a ha, “a guri mɛ sansɛka ti a dinduma wan lebege a nama dafeere.”

ref: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, kaabegɔ kuyina ma'a puan, ti a bahɛ ti ba bi'ɛ kahegɛ saŋa woo, bamam sɛba en kɔ'ɔm dɛna filam la.

ref: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
text: Fa'a Sia la dela aze'nyɛdaana bo' dina wa, en yele tu yeti,

ref: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
text: “Nɔpu'ulegɔ kuna ti n wan pu'ulɛ la ba, dabeha aŋa la han ta tole. Daana la n yele. N wan dikɛ n nɔze'elego iŋɛ ba suyan, gee gulehɛ a iŋɛ ba puti'ira puan,”

ref: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
text: a me pa'ahɛ yeti, “N kan tɛra ba be'em, la ba ka fabele se'ere tuuma la le yɛ'ɛha.”

ref: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
text: Zi'an ti sugeri dia bɔna bo' yele ana, be'em duuhego kaabegɔ ka le bɔna. Ŋmibega yi'a

ref: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
text: Bala zuo n suurɔ, tumam n diŋɛ tari puti'irɛ ti tu wan kɛ̃ Fa'a saraa la puan Yezu ziim la zuo la,

ref: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
text: doohe la sadɔɔ paalega vua la ti a yu'ɛ bo' tu, doohe la en ahegɛ tanɛ se'ere n beŋe la bahɛ la, ,

ref: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
text: la tumam en tari malema kiima kãtɛ Yinɛ yire la puan la,

ref: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
text: bahɛ ya ti tu vurege du' la yelemɛŋerɛ suure, tari puti'irɛ n pirɛ la sakerɛ, ti tu suya miihi ti ba duuhe a be'em puti'irɛ, ti tu ihe pee la tuulum ko'om.

ref: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
text: Bahɛ ya ti tu gurɛ suŋa suŋa tu beere puti'irɛ sakerɛ yelehum la gee da miina, se'ere en sɔi la se'em daana en biŋe nuurɛ la dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana.

ref: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
text: Gee bahɛ ya ti tu biha tumam n wan iŋɛ se'em pagera taaba la nɔŋerɛ la itesuŋɔ,

ref: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
text: da zagehera ya lagena taaba, wuu basɛba itegɔ en de se'em la, gee ya kpɛmeha taaba, malum ita de gana bala, wuu yan nyɛ ti dabeherɛ la pɔri la.

ref: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
text: Gee tu han kɔ'ɔm mina gee bɔna be'em puan tumam en to'e yelemɛŋerɛ mi'ilum la ba'ahɛ la, kaabegɔ ka le yee wan kaabɛ bo' tu be'em,

ref: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
text: gee sɛla en wan wa'am na dela dabeem gakerɛ, la sunsunyiim bugum la en wan di dinduma la.

ref: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
text: Se'em woo en tuue Moose pa'alegɔ la, kiiri mɛ ti nimbu'ɔ ka bɔna, “Aze'nyɛ duma bayi' batã han diŋɛ tɔgɛ.”

ref: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
text: Bala, namehɛ kãtɛ deni taaba ti yamam ti'iha ti ba en wan dikɛ bo' sɛba en ka dikɛ Yinɛ Dayua la yi' sɛla, tuura nɔpu'ulegɔ ziim la n dikɛ pee ba la, gee nyiiha zu'uhegɔ Sia la?

ref: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu mi se'em Daana en yeti, “Leregere dela mam dinɛ, N wan lebehe yɔ la.” Gee le yeti,“Daana la wan gakɛ a nɛreba” la.

ref: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
text: La dela dabeem yele ti fum lu Yinɛ vua wa nu'uhen.

ref: Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light.
text: Gee tiihɛ dabeha aŋa ha la, yan daa nyɛ niihum la, ya daa ŋmiberi mɛ la zika kãtɛ namehegɔ puan,

ref: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
text: saŋa se'ehi ba ni ba' diihe ya la vi nɛreba nɛŋan, la mɛlehegɔ, ti sahi se'ehi, ya naaɛ la sɛba ti ba ba' mɛleha.

ref: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya tari nimbu'ɔ mɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba en boi paka deto puan, gee sakɛ la nyɛɛma ba han fãara ya lɔgerɔ, gee mina ti yamam mɛhi tari la sɛla en ani suŋa gee bɔna wuuha gana bala.

ref: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
text: Bala zuo, da bahɛ ya puti'irɛ dinɛ taaba, de tari la yɔɔrɛ kãtɛ kina na.

ref: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
text: Bala zuo, ya boti la ŋmibega, ti ya han ta tum Yinɛ sumboolum la ba'ahɛ, ya wan to'e nɔbiŋere la.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
text: Gee la kelum wan, “iŋɛ fii wa, ti se'em daana en ye' na la wan sirum wa'am na, gee kan gu'um;

ref: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
text: Gee mam tuuntɔɔ daana wa wan vɔna mɛ la sakerɛ. Mam sia ka nyɛɛni la se'em en lebere pooren.”

ref: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
text: Gee tumam ka pa'ahɛ sɛba en lebere pooren, ni ta bɔregɛ la, gee sɛba en tari sakerɛ, bala zuo nyɛ faarɛ la puan.

ref: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
text: Nananawa, sakerɛ dela suure en ku gura beere puti'irɛ, suure en kɔ'ɛ gura sɛla ti nini ka nyɛ.

ref: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sakerɛ zuo, ti nɛreba yuum tɔgera ba yele.

ref: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti tu bɔkɛ ti Yinɛ naam saazuo la timpaberɛ wa mɛ la a yelehum ma'a, bala, sɛla ti tu nyɛta wa ze'ele la sɛla ti tu ka nyɛta puan na. Abɛl, Enɔki la Nowa makerɛ

ref: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Abɛl bo' Yinɛ bo'olum sebo en tigɛ nini gana Keen bo'olum la. Doohe ŋwana zuo, ti ba tɔgera a yele ti a dela tuuntɔɔ nɛra. Yinɛ mɛŋa en de aze'nyɛ daana bo' a bo'olum la. La en pun ki la, gee la bala za'a, a sakerɛ la zuo, a kelum tɔgera mɛ.

ref: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yinɛ tee Enɔki kiŋɛ ti a ka makɛ lem kum. “Ba yuum ka nyɛ en se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en dikɛ en kiŋɛ.” Gee ti teere wa wa'am na la, ba pun tɔgɛ a yele mɛ ti, “a tigɛ la Yinɛ nini.”

ref: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
text: Bala, sakerɛ han ka bɔna, la kpe'em mɛ ti fum tigɛ Yinɛ nini, se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en wa'am a zi'an na, dela a mina ti Yinɛ boi mɛ, gee yɔɔra sɛba woo en eere en la ninmu'urɛ.

ref: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Nowa, Yinɛ kagɛ en doohe yele sɛ'a en wan wa'am na, ti Nowa nam yetɔgum la ninmu'urɛ, gee kpa' daka kãtɛ la ti a faaɛ a yire. Yele dina ti Nowa yuum dikɛ gakɛ timpaberɛ wa, gee nyaa sooe vaaɛ tuntɔɔnɛ en dole sakerɛ puan la. Aberaham sakerɛ

ref: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Aberaham nam en yuum yi' en ti a nɔgɛ kiŋɛ zi'an ti a wan ta sooe ti ka dɛna a sɔ'ɔlum la. A yuum sirum ihege yɛ'a mɛ, gee ka mina en ye' zi'an la.

ref: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Aberaham ta zi'ire saŋa fii wa, tinsɛka puan ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ ti a wan dikɛ bo' en la, wuu en de saana ka puan la, a ki'ire la suga puan, bala mɛa ti Aziki la Yakɔbe me iŋɛ, bamam sɛba la eŋa en lagum vaaɛ nɔbiŋere sɔ'ɔlum bumɔ mɛa la.

ref: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Aberaham yuum guri la tinkãtɛ en tari ɛ̃berɛ, ti ka gula, la ka mɛta dɛna Yinɛ.

ref: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti a to'e dɔgum paŋa nɔbiŋere, la en daa pun ba' kuregɛ la, ti Saara me ka dɔgera la, beni zuo, a yuum nam mɛ ti se'em en biŋe nuurɛ la dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana.

ref: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Bala, ze'ele nɛra ayima wa puan na, nɛre se'em en ani wuu a ki mɛ la puan na, ti yaahi wa'am na, “zo'e wuu ŋmarebibehi en zo'e se'em sagbama zuo ha la, zo'e kan ta'am kãalɛ wuu kulaa tintombi'ihego la.”

ref: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
text: Bana woo wa'am na ki mɛ la ba sakerɛ la, gee ka to'e Yinɛ en biŋe nuurɛ ti a bo' ba sɛla la, gee ba nyɛ la mɛ ba suyan zaaɛ zaarɛ ha ti la kini na, ti ba to'e la. Ba daa yeti bamam dela saama, dɛna ɛɛreba timpaberɛ wa zuo.

ref: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
text: Nɛresɛba en tɔgeri ŋwana la, la niiɛ mɛ ti ba eere la ba mɛŋa tiŋa ti ba zi'ire.

ref: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ ba han ti'ihɛ ni la ba tiŋa ti ba bahɛ pooren ha la yele, ba tari ni sore ti ba lebe.

ref: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
text: Gee nananawa lan de se'em la dela, ba suure kɛ̃ mɛ la tinsɛka en gani bala, tinsɛka en ze'eti saazuo na la. Bala zuo, Yinɛ ka tari vi la ba yi'ira en ba Yinɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ, a sirum maahum la tiŋa biŋe ba.

ref: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
text: A dayɔsɛka ti Yinɛ yele en yeti, “Aziki puan ti fum wan nyɛ yaahi ti ba dikɛ sigɛ hɔ la.”

ref: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
text: Aberaham yuum ti'ihɛ nyɛ ti la de sira, ti Yinɛ wan ta'am ihege nɛra kum puan lebena, tɔgɛ biŋere puan a yuum sirum lebe to'e en mɛ.

ref: Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Aziki ka' sum bahɛ Yakɔbe la Esɔ beere puan.

ref: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yakɔbe en wa pɔra kum la, a ka' sum bahɛ Yosefe dayɔɔhi zuo, “Nyiga a doore zuo na'ahera.”

ref: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yosefe ta pɔra kum gee tɔgɛ bo' Iziraleduma, ba yɛhega yele, gee pa'alɛ ban wan pi' en se'em. Moose sakerɛ

ref: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Moose dɔgereba yuum sugɛ en ban yuum dɔgɛ en ti a ta paaɛ ŋmarehi sitã la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba yuum nyɛ ti bia la sum mɛ, ti dabeem ka tara ba la naba la nuurɛ la ti a bo' la.

ref: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Moose en yuum ta zo'e la, a zagehɛ ti, ba da yi'ira eŋa Faro poyua bia,

ref: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
text: gee tugum looe ti a nameha la Yinɛ nɛreba la nyana a dita nyɛlum be'em puan dabeha fii wa.

ref: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
text: A yuum nam ya ti Kiresta zuo a wan to'e kɛhega la namehegɔ, ti la dɛna tarega kãtɛ bo' en, nyana Igipiti tarega la, se'ere en sɔi la, a yuum biheri la yɔɔri kãtɛ la en boi nɛŋa ha la.

ref: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti a bahɛ Igipiti, ka zɔta dabeem la naba la sunsunyiim la. A yuum kɔ'ɔm ŋmibera mɛ kina wuu a nyɛ la se'em daana ti ba ka nyɛta la.

ref: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti a doohe azɔɛtole malema la, la ziim miihego, ti Sagena la en dole kuura bikiinduma la da ta'am kalum Izirale bikiima. Izirale sakerɛ puan gendɔɔrɔ

ref: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti nɛreba la daa sige mɔgemɔlegɔ la puan kiŋɛ yakɛ wuu la dela kɛ'ɛŋa la, gee Igipitiduma la en se'em ti ba me iŋɛ bala la, ba yee la ko'om la puan.

ref: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yeriko nangɔɔma la lu, Izirale nɛreba la en kaari dɛ muna ta paaɛ dabeha ayopɔi daarɛ la.

ref: Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
text: Sakerɛ zuo ti Arahab en de yalema yuum ka lagum nyɛ sageŋɔ la sɛba woo en yuum ka nam la, se'ere en sɔi la, a yuum to'e Izirale viihereba la mɛ la suma'ahum.

ref: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
text: Beni ti n wan le yele pa'ahɛ? Saŋa kan bɔna bo' mam ti n yele Gidiyɔn, Barake, Samsin Yɛfita, Deviti la Samuwɛle, la nɔtɔgeheba la,

ref: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
text: sɛba en yuum ze'ele sakerɛ puan nyaŋɛ tinkãra, gakɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ, ze'ele to'e sɛla ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ la, mum gbigema nɔya,

ref: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
text: kiihe bugum en taaɛ dita ka tara makerɛ, piiɛ kuura sɔ'a nuuren, nyɛ paŋa tɔregɔ puan lebege kansanhi pɛntɛrɛ puan, bahɛ ti pɛntiireba en de saama zɔ zɔpɛɛlega.

ref: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
text: Ti pɔgehi nɛreba en tabelɛ ki, le ihege ti ba to'ohe ba. Ba mɛlehɛ basɛba mɛ, gee ti ba zagehɛ ti bamam kan yehe paka deo la puan, ti ba to'e ihegere sunɛ se'ere n gani bala.

ref: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
text: Basɛba to'ohe la kɛhega la ŋmɛ'kãtɛ, pun ta paaɛ kuto zɔtɔ ba'a, la paka deto puan.

ref: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
text: Ba lobe basɛba mɛ la kuga ku, aahum basɛba wele wele, kɔrum basɛba la sɔ'ɔhi. Ba yɛ la piihi la buuhi gana ɛɛra la nimbu'ɔ, kua, la mɛlehegɔ,

ref: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
text: ba eere nyaara la kpaahenɛ zi'ihi, zɔya zuto, tahi leŋen, tiŋan yogero puan. Tiŋa wa ka ana suŋa bo' ba.

ref: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
text: Gee yele ana za'a la, la ban pun pɛgɛ ba la ba sakerɛ la, ba ka nyaŋɛ to'e sɛla ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ ti a bo' la,

ref: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
text: se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ maahum la sɛla en gani bala biŋe, ti tumam la bamam lagum taaba bi'ɛ kahegɛ.

ref: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
text: Bala, aze'nyɛ wa en zo'e se'em wuu sagbanɛ wa kaaɛ tu mum wa zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu dikɛ tibehegɔ seko woo, la be'em sebo woo en ba' tabera, gee ana la tɔ kankaŋi la biŋe kinkɛleŋa, gee bahɛ ya ti tu zɔta la ŋmibega, zɔ sɛka en gã' tu nɛŋan wa.

ref: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
text: Tu biha suŋa suŋa Yezu en de sakerɛ nɛŋa daana, se'em daana n de sakerɛ la ba'ahegɔ daana la. Se'em ti nyɛɛma sɛ'a en dikɛ biŋe en la zuo, a daa ŋmibe mɛ ta paaɛ pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo, ka fabela dɔɔ zuo kum la nyanɛ la, nyaa wa'am na zi'a a zi'a zi'an Yinɛ na'am kuka la zuo, zuɔ nu'o.

ref: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
text: Ti'ihɛ ya bihɛ Yezu en daa ŋmibe se'em namehegɔ seko taaba ti be'em duma daa dikɛ namehɛ en la, bala ti yamam me kan taregera bii ya suure sagum.

ref: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
text: Yan zigeri la be'em la, ya nan ka zigɛ paaɛ ze'sɛka en de ya ziim ka'ɛ.

ref: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
