text: Ya me pun tamɛ kpemehego seko en gulehɛ pa'alɛ ti ya dela Yinɛ kɔma la. La yeti, “N bia, da bihɛ Daana tube-dɔlegerɛ la yoo, bii fum suure sagum, a han dɔlegɛ fum tuberɛ.

ref: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la nameheri la sɛba ti a nɔŋɛ, gee dɔlegera bisɛka woo ti a to'e.”

ref: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
text: Ya ha ŋmibera makerɛ puan tube-dɔlegerɛ zuo. Yinɛ iti ya mɛ wuu a kɔma la. Bikani en boi ti ka dɔgera ka dɔlegera ka tuberɛ?

ref: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
text: Ya han ka to'e tube-dɔlegerɛ se'ere ti kɔma woo tɔ'ɔha la, ya dela kɔm-yooro, gee dagi Yinɛ kɔma.

ref: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
text: Le yɛ'ɛha, tu tari tu dɔgereba mɛ, ti ba dɔlegera tu tuba, gee ti tu nana ba. La tugum dagena tu malum bɔta ti tu dikɛ tu mɛhi iŋɛ Sɔ sɛka en sɔi sɛɛhi la nu'uhen la tu vɔna?

ref: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
text: Tu dɔgereba ni dɔlegɛ tu tuba la saŋa fii wa wuu bamam en bihɛ ti la nari se'em la, gee Yinɛ dɔlegeri tu tuba la tumam mɛhi sum zuo, ti la bahɛ ti tu ta lagum tara a tuulum la.

ref: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
text: Tube dɔlegerɛ woo tɔi mɛ, gee la nyani nyɛlum en wan bɔna saŋa fii wa. Ba'ahegɔ za'a, tube-dɔlegerɛ la ni tari la suma'ahum wala sɛ'a en de tuuntɔɔ vom bɔ'ɔra sɛba ti tube-dɔlegerɛ bi'ihɛ ba la.

ref: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
text: Bala, zɛkɛ ya nu'uhi wa en ki yula wa, kpemehe ya duma wa en tɛteni wa,

ref: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
text: maalɛ fum sore la suŋa bo' fum nama, ti sɛla en de wobete da sugehe, gee a tugum naaɛ taaba. Da zagehɛ ya Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la

ref: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
text: Eere ya suma'ahum ninmu'urɛ nɛra woo, la fa'a vom sebo en han ka bɔna, nɛra kan ta'am nyɛ Daana la.

ref: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
text: Bihɛ ya suŋa, ti ya se'em da koŋe Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la. Da bahɛ ya ti too nyɛgerɛ yehe ya puan na, tari daaŋɔ wa'am na, ti ya doohe de puan galum nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

ref: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
text: Bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa suŋa ti ya se'em da ana wuu Esɔ, se'em en ka ta' a mɛŋa, ka zɔta Yinɛ la, se'em en yuum koohe a kinɔ dia buyina ma'a zuo la.

ref: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
text: Ya nan ka paaɛ zi'an ti ya wan ta'am kalum la na, bugum peelum la puan, la lika gbareti la zi'an na, la ze'sɛka en gbiregeri la,

ref: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
text: la tintanɛ peebego la, la kua sɛka tugum ti ka kɛleheba bɛlena ti ka pun da le tɔgɛ yele yinɛ pa'ahɛ la.

ref: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
text: (Se'ere en sɔi la ba kan nyaŋɛ tara en bo' a nuurɛ la se'em la, ti “Duŋa han pun kalum zoore la, ka dela ba lobe ka ku” la.

ref: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, zi'an la yuum ba' tara la dabeem gaŋɛ ti Moose yeti, “Mam gbi'iri mɛ la dabeem.”)

ref: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
text: Gee yamam paaɛ la zoore se'ere yu'urɛ en yi'iri Zaayɔn la puan na, tinsɛka en de Yinɛ vua wa tiŋa la puan, tinsɛka en de Yerusalɛm sɛka en boi saazuo ha la, ya paaɛ la suŋereba en zo'e kan ta'am kãalɛ la lageŋɔ kãtɛ en boi kuyima,

ref: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
text: ya wa'am la lageŋɔ seko en de kɔmdiina sɛba yu'ura en gulehɛ bɔna tinsuŋan puan la na, ya wa'am la Yinɛ se'ere en de sɛla woo gakera la zi'an na, Ya wa'am la tuuntɔɔ duma sɛɛhi la n bi'ɛ kahegɛ la zi'an na.

ref: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
text: Ya wa'am la Yezu en de nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔpu'ule paalega la puan la zi'an na, la ziim sebo en dikɛ miihi, gee tɔgera yelesuma gana Abɛl ziim la zi'an na.

ref: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
text: Bihɛ ya suŋa la ya da zagehɛ se'em en kageri ya wa. Nɛreba daa han ka piiɛ ban da zagehɛ se'em en daa kagɛ ba tiŋa wa zuo la, tumam yeti tu iŋɛ la ŋwani piiɛ la fii me, tu ha zagehɛ se'em en kageri tɔ saazuo na wa.

ref: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
text: Sankaŋa ha la, a kua ma'a en yuum miim timpaberɛ wa, gee nananawa, a biŋe la nuurɛ ti “Le yɛ'ɛha, n wan le miim, gee dagi timpaberɛ wa ma'a, gee la saazuo za'a.”

ref: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
text: Yetɔge buna wa “Le yɛ'ɛha wa,” pa'ali ti, a yeti a vuɛ la sɛla en miini la bahɛ bana dela naaŋɔ lɔgerɔ n bala, ti sɛla woo n ka miini la yee.

ref: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
text: Bala zuo, tumam en yeti tu to'e sɔ'ɔlum sebo en ka miini la zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu bo' ya Yinɛ fara, doohe la na'ahegɔ kaabegɔ seko taaba en tigɛ nini, ti duma kpa'a, la dabeem bɔna bini.

ref: Our God is like a destructive fire!
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu Yinɛ la dela bugum sebo en diti kãbera.

ref: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
text: Bahɛ ya ti suuɔ nɔŋerɛ yee saŋa woo.

ref: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
text: Da zagehɛ ya tɔ'ɔha saama, gee sɛba iŋɛ la bala ta to'ohe suŋereba gee ka baŋɛ.

ref: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
text: Tɛri ya sɛba en boi paka deen la yele, wuu ya lagum bɔna la paka deen la ba la, sɛba ti ba mɛleha ba la, wuu yamam mɛhi ti ba mɛleha la.

ref: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
text: Bahɛ ya ti pɔgedire la sire-ele tara na'ahegɔ ya za'a zi'an, ti pɔgedire la sire-ele suŋɔ la da galum, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ wan gakɛ yaluntuuma duma, la kabohi duma mɛ.

ref: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
text: Tari ya ya vom suŋa ti bu da kɛ̃ ligenɔŋerɛ puan, gee bahɛ ya ti ya nini tigɛ la sɛla ti ya tara. Se'ere en sɔi la, a yele tu yeti, “N kan malum bahɛ ya, bii n zagehɛ ya.”

ref: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
text: Ŋwana zuo la, tu wan ta'am yele gee ti dabeem ka tara tɔ yeti, “Daana la en de mam suŋera, N kan zɔta dabeem. Beni ti nɛra wan ta'am iŋɛ mam?”

ref: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
text: Tɛra ya nɛheduma, sɛba en tɔgɛ Yinɛ yelehum la bo' ya la. Bihɛ ya ban daa vɔi ba vom se'em, la ya tɔgehɛ ba sakerɛ la.

ref: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
text: Yezu Kiresta dela bala mɛa zaam, zina, la saŋa woo.

ref: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti pa'ali yooro yima yima ziira ya kaara zi'an woo, se'ere en sɔi la, la ani suŋa ti zu'uhegɔ kpemehe suure la, nyana dia kihega yɛla, yele sɛ'a en ka suŋɛ sɛba en dole a la.

ref: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
text: Tumam tari la kaabegɔ bimbine se'ere ti sɛba en ze' tanɛ deo la puan kaabera la ka tari sore ti ba dita kaabegɔ dia la.

ref: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, duhi se'ehi ziim ti malema kiima la tari kɛ̃ Fa'a saraa la puan ti bu dɛna be'em duuhego kaabegɔ la, ba ni nyɔ' si la tiiheŋɔ la yiŋa ha.

ref: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
text: Bala zuo ti Yezu namehɛ tiŋa la yiŋa ha, ti a pee nɛreba be'em la a mɛŋa ziim.

ref: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
text: Bahɛ ya ti tu kiŋɛ ya a zi'an tiiheŋɔ la yiŋa la ha, la tu ta lagum di nyanɛ se'ere ti a ŋmibe de puan la.

ref: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
text: Tiŋa wa zuo, tu ka tari tinsɛka n wan bɔna wuuha, gee tu biheri la tiŋa sɛka n ye' na la.

ref: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
text: Doohe eŋa puan, yi bahɛ ya ti daarɛ woo, tu bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ tu na'ahegɔ kaabegɔ en de tu nɔgbama wala ti tu puta a yu'urɛ la.

ref: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Da zagehɛ ya ti ya tuna sum, gee tɔta yan tari sɛla la taaba, gee kaabegɔ kuna taaba en tigeri Yinɛ nini.

ref: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
text: Nane ya ya nɛhi duma, gee sigehe ya mɛhi bo' ba, gee bamam en biheri ya vom, gee wan ta yele ban tuni se'em. Bo' ya ba sore ti ba tuna ba tuuma la nyɛɛma gee dagi ba si'ina. La han ana bala, ya kan nyɛ nyuurɔ.

ref: Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right.
text: Pu'uhi ya bɔ'ɔra tɔ, gee tumam mi' ti tumam tari la putɛ'sunɛ, ti tu suure bɔta ti tu tuna la yelemɛŋerɛ sɛla woo puan.

ref: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
text: N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, ti ya tuna ŋwana wa gana sɛla woo, ti n ta'am le lebe ya zi'an kalum na. Pu'uhegɔ

ref: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
text: Nananawa, Yinɛ se'em en de suma'ahum daana la, se'em daana en tari a piihi dunkiin kãtɛ wa ze'ele kum puan lebe na la, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta doohe a ziim la nɔpu'ulegɔ la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la puan,

ref: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
text: wan bahɛ ti ya bi'ɛ tunsunɛ woo puan, ti ya ta'am tum a sumboolum, doohe la en boi tu puan tuna la tɔ, tuunsɛ'a en tigeri a nini Yezu puan, se'em ti suŋa dɛna a dinɛ saŋa woo. Taari.

ref: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
text: N beleni ya mɛ n suurɔ, ŋmibe ya la n kpemehego yelehum wa, gee n yeen gulehɛ la girega girega bo' ya na.

ref: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
text: N boti ya mina ti ba bahɛ tu suuɔ Timoti, ti a han wa'am tɔtɔ na, a wan doohe la mam na ti n nyɛ ya.

ref: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
text: Pu'uhɛ ya nɛheduma, la nɛresunduma la woo bo' tu. Sɛba woo en boi Itali tiŋa pu'uheri ya na.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
text: Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya za'a.

ref: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
text: Yemesi en de yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la Daana Yezu Kiresta la, en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra yizuto pia la tuyi' la en nɔgɛ bɔna zi'an woo la. Mam pu'uheri ya na. Sakerɛ la Yɛm

ref: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
text: N suurɔ, ya han ni ta nyɛta maketooro seto woo, da dikɛ ya ku yi' sɛsɛla, gee tugum nyɛɛni ya.

ref: You know you learn to endure by having your faith tested.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti ya sakerɛ makerɛ bo'ori ya la ŋmibega.

ref: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
text: Bahɛ ya ti ya ŋmibega la pirɛ, ti ya ta'am bi'ɛ kahegɛ ti sɛla da koŋe ya.

ref: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
text: Ya se'em han pɔ̃hegɛ yɛm, a suhɛ Yinɛ, se'em en bo'ori yabi yabi gee ka fabela la, gee a wan bo' fum.

ref: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
text: Gee suhɛ en la sakerɛ, gee da bu'ura. Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em n bu'uri ani wuu Ateko ko'mi'a ti kusebego tara ŋmɛ'ɛra kaara se'em la.

ref: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
text: Bahɛ ya ti suuɔ seko en sigehe ku mɛŋa la, ŋmɛ' a nyu'ɔ, la Yinɛ en zɛkɛ en la,

ref: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
text: ti buntata me sigehe a mɛŋa, beni zuo, tarega wan mɛlum wuu mɔpuuro la.

ref: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, wuntɛɛŋa zoni mɛ na la tuulega, ku'ura vatɔ la, ti bu puuro woo luta, ti bu suŋa la Sagena. Bala mɛa ti buntateba wan ta bahɛ yoo ban mu'ɛ nini ba bumbɔrɛ la puan se'em la. Magehegɔ la pã'ahegɔ

ref: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
text: Sum boi mɛ la se'em en ŋmiberi makerɛ puan. Ena daana nyaŋɛ magehegɔ la mɛ, wan to'e vom muŋa la, ti Daana la biŋe nuurɛ ti a bo' sɛba en nɔŋɛ en la.

ref: Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others.
text: Se'em da bɔna ti makerɛ se'ere han paaɛ en, ti a yeti, “Yinɛ en makerɛ mam biha.” Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em kan ta'am makɛ Yinɛ bihɛ, Yinɛ mɛŋa me ka makerɛ se'em la be'em biha.

ref: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
text: Gee la han ta iŋɛ bala, nɛra mɛŋa kunkoma'ahum en ni pã'ahɛ en, ve'e en kɛ̃' makerɛ la puan.

ref: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
text: Ti kunkoma'ahum buna wa nyaa han ta nyɔkɛ puurɛ, bu dɔgeri la be'em, ti be'em buna wa han ta bi'ɛ kahegɛ, bu dɔgeri la kum.

ref: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
text: Da bahɛ ya ti se'em pã'ahɛ ya, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa.

ref: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
text: Beni zuo, abo'yabiyabi woo, la bo'olum sumɔ woo ze'eti la saazuo, Sɔ la en de niihum daana la zi'an sigera na. Se'em puan ti ŋmɛlema la lika ka bɔna doohe teere se'ere puan.

ref: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
text: Doohe a mɛŋa puti'irɛ en de se'em la zuo, a dɔgɛ tu mɛ doohe a yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum la puan, ti tu dɛna wala diina a naaŋɔ la puan. Kelehego la tuuma

ref: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
text: Ya dela ya bɔkɛ dina wa, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, bahɛ ya ti nɛra woo kɛleha kalam kalam, gee da tɔgera kalam kalam, bii ita suure kalam kalam.

ref: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya sunsunyiim la ka ŋwɔni Yinɛ tuuntɔɔ vom la.

ref: You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
text: Dinɛ zuo, yehe ya mɛhi yaalegɔ woo puan, la tuunfi'iha kãra puan, gee sigehe ya mɛhi to'e yelehum la en burɛ ya puan tara paŋa wan ta'am faaɛ ya sɛɛhi bahɛ la.

ref: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
text: Gee me iti ya yelehum la en de se'em la, gee da yeen kelehe ya kelehego ma'a pã'ahɛ ya mɛhi.

ref: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba han yeen kɛleha yelehum la ma'a, gee ka tari bu tuna, ba ani mɛ wuu sɛba en ni bihɛ ba mɛhi biheŋa puan,

ref: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
text: ba han bihɛ ba mɛhi gee kiŋɛ, bilam bilam ba ni tamɛ ban ani ni se'em la mɛ.

ref: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
text: Gee sɛba en bihɛ nɔze'ele seko en ka tari gbelegere la, fai nɔze'elego la en ka tari gbelegere la, tara kɔ, dagena kɛleheba sɛba en tameni, gee sɛba en tari tuna, ba wan nyɛ sum ba itegɔ la puan.

ref: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
text: Sɛba han ti'ihɛ ti bamam pu'uheri Yinɛ, gee ka tana ba zɛlehi tara, gee pã'aha ba suya, ba pu'uhegɔ la dela yoo.

ref: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
text: Pu'uhegɔ seko en de fa'a, ka tara galeŋɔ Yinɛ en de Sɔ la nɛŋan en ŋwana. Tu bihera kiibehi, la pokɔpa ba nimbu'ɔ puan, gee ka galena ya mɛhi la timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla.

ref: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
text: N suurɔ wa, yamam en tari ninkɔɔra se'em wa sirum nam Daana Yezu Kiresta, en de suŋa daana?

ref: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra han wa'am ya lageŋɔ puan na, pire salema bãhi, yɛ fusunɔ, ti naha me en ka yɛ lɔgesunɔ me kɛ̃' na,

ref: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
text: ti ya han bihɛ nyɛ se'em daana en yɛ fusunɔ la, yele en yeti, “N beleni fum mɛ, zi'ire ka'lam,” gee yele naha la yeti, “Zɛ'a ke ha,” bii “Zi'ire tiŋa,”

ref: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
text: ya ka lugehe la ya puan en bala, lebege gakereba en tari putɛ'be'ero?

ref: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
text: Kelehe ya, n sunɔŋerɛ duma. Yinɛ ka looe la naheduma timpaberɛ wa zuo ti ba dɛna buntateba sakerɛ puan, la ba ta sɔna sɔ'ɔlum la ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ bo' sɛba en nɔŋɛ en la?

ref: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
text: Gee yamam ka nane naha. Dagi buntateba la en bala mugehera ya la? Dagi bamam en dati ya kina tugum zi'ihen la?

ref: Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
text: Dagi bamam en tuuri yu'sunɛ se'ere en dikɛ sigɛ ya la?

ref: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
text: Ya wan iŋɛ suŋa, ya han sirum tum na'am nɔze'elego la wuu gulehegɔ la en yele se'em yeti, “Fum nɔŋɛ fum tadaana wuu fum mɛŋa la.”

ref: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
text: Gee yamam han lugeha nɛreba, ya tum la be'em, ti ze'elego la nyɔkɛ ya ti ya tuue mɛ.

ref: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum se'em woo en dole ze'elego la za'a, gee tuue zi'an kayima, tuue ze'elego la za'a mɛ.

ref: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em daana en yeti, “Da tum kabohi la,” eŋa mɛa en yeti, “Da ku nɛra.” Bala, fum han ka tum kabohi, gee ku nɛra, fum tuue ze'elego la mɛ.

ref: Speak and act like people who will be judged by the law that sets us free.
text: Bala, tɔgera gee ita wuu sɛba ti ba wan gakɛ la fai bɔŋa ze'elego la.

ref: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
text: Se'ere en sɔi la gakerɛ kan ta tara nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bo' se'em woo en ka tari nimbɔ'zɔrɛ. Nimbɔ'zɔrɛ ta' gakerɛ mɛ. Sakerɛ en ka tari tuna

ref: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
text: Beni nyuurɔ en boi bini n suurɔ, se'em han yeti a tari la sakerɛ gee ka tari tuna? Fum sakerɛ la wan faaɛ hɔ bahɛ yoi'?

ref: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
text: Fum suuɔ bii taya han ɛɛra beele, ka tara a nuurɛ dia,

ref: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
text: ti ya ayima yele en yeti, “Kina la suma'ahum. Ɔɔrɔ kan kɛ̃ hɔ, ti fum di tigɛ,” gee ka bo' en, en a inganɛ la en boti sɛla la, beni sum en boi bini?

ref: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
text: Bala, sakerɛ ma'a, han ka tara tuna ki mɛ.

ref: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
text: Gee ayima wan yeti, “Fum tari la sakerɛ, ti mam me tara tuuma.” Eii! Yi pa'alɛ mam fum sakerɛ la ti tuuma ka bɔna bini, gee mam me wan dikɛ la n tuuma la pa'alɛ hɔ n sakerɛ.

ref: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
text: Ya nam ti Yinɛ dela ayima. Ya mɔ ya. Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi me pun nam la bala, gee gbi'ira.

ref: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
text: Ya boti ba pa'alɛ ya mɛ? Gɔɔŋɔ wa, ti sakerɛ en ka tari tuuma, ki mɛ?

ref: Well, our ancestor Abraham pleased God by putting his son Isaac on the altar to sacrifice him.
text: Tu sɔyaaba Aberaham yuum ka di buurɔ a tuuma puan, eŋa en yuum kaabɛ a dayua Aziki kaabegɔ bimbine la zuo la?

ref: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
text: Ya nyɛ ti sakerɛ la yuum vɔi mɛ la a tuuma la, ti a tuuma la bahɛ ti sakerɛ la bi'ɛ.

ref: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
text: Bala ti gulehegɔ la wa'am asira na, gulehegɔ seko en yeti, “Aberaham nam Yinɛ mɛ, ti Yinɛ bo' en tuntɔɔnɛ,” ti ba yi'ira en Yinɛ zɔ.

ref: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
text: Ya nyɛ ti nɛra diti buurɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan la a tuuma puan gee dagi sakerɛ ma'a zuo.

ref: For example, Rahab had been a prostitute. But she pleased God when she welcomed the spies and sent them home by another way.
text: Dagi bala mɛa ti Arehabe, pɔge yalema la yuum di buurɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan a tuuma puan, la eŋa en yuum to'e viihereba la gee bahɛ ti ba doohe sadɔɔ kuyima yehe la?

ref: Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
text: Wuu iŋa en ka tari sia ki se'em la, bala mɛa ti sakerɛ en ka tari tuuma me ki.

ref: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
text: Dagi ya zo'e zo'e en wan dɛna pa'aleba n suurɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti tu sɛba en pa'alɛ la, ba wan gakɛ tu la nima nima.

ref: All of us do many wrong things. But if you can control your tongue, you are mature and able to control your whole body.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu za'a tuure mɛ. Se'em en ka tuure a tugum puan dela tuuntɔɔ, tana a a iŋa za'a tara la gareŋenɛ.

ref: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
text: Tu han lɛ' Yire nɔya la gareŋenɛ ti tu tana ba, tu nyaŋɛ ba iŋa la za'a mɛ, ŋmɛreha ba tumam n boti zi'an.

ref: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
text: Bii bihɛ ya uurenɔ, ban tibegɛ se'em ti kusebego ma'a za'ara ba kina la. Gee la bala za'a, zugɔ pika ti uuren-mɔneba la dikɛ yule uureŋɔ la pulin, tari ku kina bamam en boti se'em.

ref: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
text: Bala mɛa ti zɛleŋa dɛna iŋa la lɔge pika, gee bahɛ ti asaala ŋmɛ'ɛra nyɔ' la yele kãra. Bihɛ goo en karegɛ se'em, gee ti bugum pãreŋa ni taaɛ kɔ di filam!

ref: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
text: Zɛleŋa dela bugum. Zɛleŋa en boi tu iŋa wa puan la, dela be'em timpaberɛ. Ka ni galum la inganɛ wa za'a, nyaa taaɛ asaala naaŋɔ la za'a di ta ba'ahɛ gee nyaa taaɛ a mɛŋa dita, bugum timpaberɛ la puan.

ref: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, goo duhi woo, niihe, bunsɛlenduma, ko'om puan bunvɔɔhi, ta'am nyɛ malehegɔ mɛ, ti asaala en sirum malehɛ ba,

ref: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
text: gee nɛra ka boi ta'am malehɛ a zɛleŋa wa. A dela bumbe'o en ka tuni vɔ'ɔha, pirɛ la yabega.

ref: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
text: Nɔdinɛ mɛa puan ti sum, la ka' be'o zɛ'ɛta na. N suurɔ, la dagi la ana bala.

ref: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
text: Buleniho wan ta'am bula ko'sumɔ la ko'be'ero me yoi?

ref: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
text: N suurɔ wa, Kinkaŋa wan ta'am wam ta'ama? Bii sinsabega wam mu'uro? La kan malum iŋɛ ti ko'om en tari yaarum, bule ko'sumɔ. Yɛm buyi'

ref: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
text: Ani en tari yɛm, tara bɔkerɛ ya puan? Pa'alɛ bɔ doohe fum vom sumɔ la puan, ti fum tuni fum tuuma la ma'a ma'a la yɛm.

ref: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
text: Gee ya han tara nintɛrega en ba' gaŋɛ, nyika puti'ira ya puan, da dagum ŋmɛ'ɛri ya nyu'ɔ, gee parena yelemɛŋerɛ la nɛŋan.

ref: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
text: Yɛm buna taaba ka ze'ele saazuo na, gee bu dela timpaberɛ wa zuo, ka dɛna sia puan, gee kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi yɛm.

ref: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, zi'an ti nintɛrega la nyika puti'irɛ bɔna, gbiregeri la tuunfi'iha yima yima me wan bɔna.

ref: But the wisdom that comes from above leads us to be pure, friendly, gentle, sensible, kind, helpful, genuine, and sincere.
text: Gee yɛm en ze'ele saazuo na, yia bu dela fa'a, tara suma'ahum, ma'ma, tara waŋa suboolum, pirɛ la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, tara walesuma, ti fum kan nyɛ lugehego, bii birikɔgenɛ bu puan la fii me.

ref: When peacemakers plant seeds of peace, they will harvest justice.
text: Ba burɛ la tuntɔɔnɛ biili suma'ahum puan, bo' sɛba en boti suma'ahum la.

ref: Why do you fight and argue with each other? Isn't it because you are full of selfish desires that fight to control your body?
text: Zaba la nɔkpɛ'ɛma sɛ'a en boi ya puan wa, ze'ele la bɛha na? Ba ka ze'ele la ya kunkoma'ahum la en zaberɛ ya puan la?

ref: You want something you don't have, and you will do anything to get it. You will even kill! But you still cannot get what you want, and you won't get it by fighting and arguing. You should pray for it.
text: Yan boti sɛla gee kan nyɛ la zuo, ti ya kuura.Ya nini en tɛti la sɛla gee kan nyɛ la zuo, ti ya ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma gee zabera taaba. Ya ka tari, beni zuo, ya ka soke.

ref: Yet even when you do pray, your prayers are not answered, because you pray just for selfish reasons.
text: Ya soke ya gee ka to'e, beni zuo, ya ni soke tuue mɛ, ti ya ta'am di ya ma'a nyɛlum.

ref: You people aren't faithful to God! Don't you know if you love the world, you are God's enemies? And if you decide to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
text: Bunyaalehi wa! Ya ka mi' ti ya nyɔgɛ zɔtɔ la timpaberɛ wa, dela ya dɛna dinduma la Yinɛ? Ŋwana zuo, fum se'em woo en boti fum dɛna timpaberɛ wa zuo zɔ, lebege la Yinɛ dima.

ref: Do you doubt the Scriptures that say, “God truly cares about the Spirit he has put in us”?
text: Bii ya ti'ihɛ ti la dela yoo ti gulehegɔ la yeti, “Yinɛ si'ini mɛ la nyuure la a Sia la ti a bahɛ ti ka bɔna la tɔ la?

ref: In fact, God treats us with even greater kindness, just as the Scriptures say, “God opposes everyone who is proud, but he blesses all who are humble with undeserved grace.”
text: Gee a malum bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo la zu'uhegɔ zo'e zo'e. Ŋwana zuo ti la gulehɛ yeti, “Yinɛ zagehɛ pa'alɛ n mɛŋa duma mɛ, gee bɔ'ɔra la sigehe n mɛŋa duma zu'uhegɔ.”

ref: Surrender to God! Resist the devil, and he will run from you.
text: Sigehe ya mɛhi ŋwana zuo, bo' Yinɛ. Da bo' ya kulekpa'bɛ'a la sore, gee a wan ɛkɛ gee bahɛ ya.

ref: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Clean up your lives, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you people who can't make up your mind.
text: Vurege ya du' Yinɛ, gee eŋa me wan vurege du' ya. Pee ya nu'uhi, be'em duma wa, gee pee ya suya, puti'ira ayi' ayi' duma wa.

ref: Be sad and sorry and weep. Stop laughing and start crying. Be gloomy instead of glad.
text: Fabele ya, si'ini ya, gee kɛla. Bahɛ ya ti ya lɔ'ɔrɔ lebege kuunɔ, ti ya pupeelum dɛna susageŋɔ.

ref: Be humble in the Lord's presence, and he will honor you. Saying Cruel Things about Others
text: Sigehe ya mɛhi Daana la nɛŋan, gee a wan zɛkɛ ya. Taaba gakerɛ

ref: My friends, don't say cruel things about others! If you do, or if you condemn others, you are condemning God's Law. And if you condemn the Law, you put yourself above the Law and refuse to obey either it
text: Da tɔgera ya be'em bɔ'ɔra taaba, n suurɔ wa. Fum se'em woo en tɔgɛ be'em bo' fum tadaana, bii gakɛ fum tadaana, tɔgɛ la be'em bahɛ ze'elego la zuo, gee gakɛ ze'elego la. Gee fum han gakɛ ze'elego la, fum ka tuni ze'elego la tuuma, gee fum dela ku gakera.

ref: or God who gave it. God is our judge, and he can save or destroy us. What right do you have to condemn anyone? Warning against Bragging
text: Nɛra ayima ma'a en bo'ori ze'elego la, gee dɛna ku gakera, se'em en wan ta'am faaɛ gee sagum. Bala, fum dela ani ti fum gakɛ fum tadaana? Nyɔ'ɔ ŋmɛ'a

ref: You should know better than to say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to the city. We will do business there for a year and make a lot of money!”
text: Kelehe ya, yamam sɛba en yeti, “Zina bii beere tu wan kiŋɛ ke la ke ha, ta kɛ̃' bini yuunɛ, lɛɛbera nyɛta ligeri la.”

ref: What do you know about tomorrow? How can you be so sure about your life? It is nothing more than mist that appears for only a little while before it disappears.
text: Gee ya pun ka mina sɛla ti beere wan tari na. Ya vom dela bem? Se'ere en sɔi la, ya dela mɛgelum en lu yee fii wa, gee mɛlum.

ref: You should say, “If the Lord lets us live, we will do these things.”
text: Gee yan tugum ni wan yele se'em dela, “La han dɛna Daana la putɛ ɛrɛ, tu wan vɔna, gee tum ŋwana la ŋwana.”

ref: Yet you are stupid enough to brag, and it is wrong to be so proud.
text: Gee lan de se'em la, ya ŋmɛ'ɛri nyu'ɔ la ya pa'alɛ mɛhi puan. Nyɔ'ɔ ŋmɛ'a kana taaba woo dela be'em.

ref: If you don't do what you know is right, you have sinned.
text: Gee se'em woo en mi' sɛla en de ma'a ti a tum, gee koŋe a tum dɛ, tuue mɛ.

ref: You rich people should cry and weep! Terrible things are going to happen to you.
text: Kelehe ya, yamam buntateba wa, kele ya gee si'ina la nimbɔ'seko en ye' ya zuo na la.

ref: Your treasures have already rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
text: Ya tarega la pu'ɛ mɛ, ti bunmɔgela mɔgɛ ya fukãra la.

ref: Your money has rusted, and the rust will be evidence against you, as it burns your body like fire. Yet you keep on storing up wealth in these last days.
text: Ya salema, la salempɛɛla la di la tiŋanziim, ti ba tiŋanziim la wan dɛna aze'nyɛ daana zɛregɛ ya, bu wan di ya iŋa wuu bugum la. Bihɛ ya, yamam tugum lagehɛ la lɔgerɔ biŋe ba'ahegɔ dabeha wa puan?

ref: You refused to pay the people who worked in your fields, and now their unpaid wages are shouting out against you. The Lord All-Powerful has surely heard the cries of the workers who harvested your crops.
text: Kelehe ya! Yɔɔrɛ se'ere en de tuntuneba sɛba en tum ya vatɔ tuuma la ti ya fa' biŋe la kaahi baha me na, ti buneba la kaahega me lu pɛntiireba za'a nɛŋa Daana la tuban.

ref: While here on earth, you have thought only of filling your own stomachs and having a good time. But now you are like fat cattle on their way to be butchered.
text: Ya vɔi timpaberɛ zuo mɛ la tarega la nyɛlum. Ya di mɛ karegɛ gura ya kum daarɛ.

ref: You have condemned and murdered innocent people, who couldn't even fight back. Be Patient and Kind
text: Ya ni gakɛ mɛ, gee ku tuuntɔɔ daana, se'em en ka zigɛ la ya. Suure di'ihego namehegɔ puan

ref: My friends, be patient until the Lord returns. Think of farmers who wait patiently for the autumn and spring rains to make their valuable crops grow.
text: N suurɔ wa, tari ya sugeri gee gura Daana la lɛbega na. Ti'ihɛ ya bihɛ Kaareba en ni tara sugeri se'em gura ba bumbuura suma la ti sigesaa la burega saa ta wa'am na ni.

ref: Be patient like those farmers and don't give up. The Lord will soon be here!
text: Yamam me tara sugeri bala, gee da kɔ'ɛ ya puti'irɛ. Daana la kinɛ na paaɛ mɛ na en bala.

ref: Don't grumble about each other or you will be judged, and the judge is right outside the door.
text: N suurɔ wa, da gɔgeni ya la taaba, bala ti ba kan gakɛ ya. Bihɛ ya, Gakera la en bala ze'a kuleŋa nuuren wa!

ref: My friends, follow the example of the prophets who spoke for the Lord. They were patient, even when they had to suffer.
text: La han dɛna la namehegɔ la sugeri yɛla, n suurɔ wa, dole ya sɛla ti nɔtɔgeheba sɛba en tɔgɛ Daana la yu'urɛ puan la daa doohe namehɛ seko woo puan tole la.

ref: In fact, we praise the ones who endured the most. You remember how patient Job was and how the Lord finally helped him. The Lord did this because he is so merciful and kind.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu yi'iri sɛba en sakɛ ŋmibega la, la sunduma. Ya wum Yobe ŋmibega la yele, ya me baŋɛ Daana la puti'irɛ yele dinɛ, Daana la en tari giile la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ se'em.

ref: My friends, above all else, don't take an oath. You must not swear by heaven or by earth or by anything else. “Yes” or “No” is all you need to say. If you say anything more, you will be condemned.
text: Se'ere en gani za'a n suurɔ wa, da pɔti ya saazuo, bii timpaberɛ wa, bii pɔ yele se'ere. Bahɛ ya ti ya “Ɛ̃ɛ” dɛna ɛ̃ɛ, ti ya “Aayi” dɛna aayi', ti ya da lu sageŋɔ puan. Sakerɛ Daana Pu'uhegɔ

ref: If you are having trouble, you should pray. And if you are feeling good, you should sing praises.
text: Sɛba boi ya puan nameha? Ba pu'uhɛ. Sɛba boi ya puan tara pupeelum? Ba yuuna pɛka yuuma.

ref: If you are sick, ask the church leaders to come and pray for you. Ask them to put olive oil on you in the name of the Lord.
text: Sɛba boi ya puan bɛ̃'ɛra? Ba yi' lageŋɔ kiinduma ti ba pu'uhɛ bo' ba, gee sɔ' en tuulum sua la kaam Daana la yu'urɛ puan.

ref: If you have faith when you pray for sick people, they will get well. The Lord will heal them, and if they have sinned, he will forgive them.
text: Sakerɛ pu'uhegɔ la wan faaɛ bã'duma la bahɛ, ti Daana la ihege ba, ti ba se'em en tum be'em wan nyɛ sugeri dia.

ref: If you have sinned, you should tell each other what you have done. Then you can pray for one another and be healed. The prayer of an innocent person is powerful, and it can help a lot.
text: Ŋwana zuo, puti ya ya be'em bɔ'ɔra taaba, gee pu'uha bɔ'ɔra taaba bala ti ya wan nyɛ ya tibegɔ. Tuntɔɔ daana pu'uhegɔ tari la paŋa, gee tuna.

ref: Elijah was just as human as we are, and for three and a half years his prayers kept the rain from falling.
text: Elia yuum dela asaala wuu tumam la, gee yuum pu'uhɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti saa da ni, ti la dikɛ yuuma atã la ŋmarehi siyoobe ti saa sirum ka tɔkɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo.

ref: But when he did pray for rain, it fell from the skies and made the crops grow.
text: Ti a le pu'uhɛ, ti saazuo ha bo' saa na, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo maalɛ dia.

ref: My friends, if any followers have wandered away from the truth, you should try to lead them back.
text: N suurɔ, se'em han bɔna ya puan bɔregɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa puan, ti se'em tari en lebena,

ref: If you turn sinners from the wrong way, you will save them from death, and many of their sins will be forgiven.
text: ya mina ti, se'em woo en tari be'em daana a tuure puan lebe na la, wan faaɛ be'em daana la sia kum puan bahɛ, gee ti Yinɛ pi' be'em daana la be'em zo'e zo'e.

ref: From Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. To God's people who are scattered like foreigners in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
text: Mam Pita en de Yezu Kiresta tuntuna, en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra sɛba en nɔgɛ bɔna zi'an woo, Pɔntusi, Galatia, Kapadosia, Esiya, la Bitiniya tihi puan la.

ref: God the Father decided to choose you as his people, and his Spirit has made you holy. You have obeyed Jesus Christ and are sprinkled with his blood. I pray that God will be kind to you and will keep on giving you peace! A Real Reason for Hope
text: Sɛba ti Yinɛ en de Sɔ la daa pun te'ele looe ba, maahum ba, gee duuhe ba la a Fa'a Sia la, ti ba nana Yezu Kiresta, gee ti a miihi ba la Yezu ziim la. Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum zo'e zo'e dɛna ya dinɛ. Beere putɛ'vua

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is so good, and by raising Jesus from death, he has given us new life and a hope that lives on.
text: Sum dɛna Yinɛ en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la dinɛ! A nimbɔ'zɔrɛ kãtɛ la zuo, a bo' tu la dɔgum paalemɔ ti tu tara beere putɛ'vua doohe Yezu Kiresta vo'ore a kum la puan.

ref: God has something stored up for you in heaven, where it will never decay or be ruined or disappear.
text: A yi ya mɛ ti ya to'e lɔgerɔ seto en ka sageni, ka galum, ka kuregera, en maahum biŋe saazuo ha gura ya.

ref: You have faith in God, whose power will protect you until the last day. Then he will save you, just as he has always planned to do.
text: Yamam sɛba ti Yinɛ paŋa gu'ura ya doohe ya sakerɛ puan la, ya faarɛ la wan niiɛ ba'ahegɔ saŋa la na.

ref: On that day you will be glad, even if you have to go through many hard trials for a while.
text: Dina puan ti ya nyɛɛna, la han pun dɛna ti nananawa, saŋa fii wa, ya namehɛ kãra yima yima doohe mageha yima yamam puan

ref: Your faith will be like gold that has been tested in a fire. And these trials will prove that your faith is worth much more than gold that can be destroyed. They will show that you will be given praise and honor and glory when Jesus Christ returns.
text: ti ya yelemɛŋerɛ sakerɛ la en tari tibehum gana salema, sakerɛ wa en tari tibehum gana salema wa, en pun tana Sagena la, gee ba wan kelum magehɛ bu la bugum bihɛ, bala mɛa ti ba wan magehɛ ya sakerɛ la bihɛ, gee de wan tara pɛka la suŋa, la na'ahegɔ, Yezu Kiresta han ta niiɛ na.

ref: You have never seen Jesus, and you don't see him now. But still you love him and have faith in him, and no words can tell how glad and happy
text: La yan pun ka nyɛ en la, ya nɔŋɛ en mɛ. La yan pun ka nyɛ en nananawa la, ya nam en mɛ, gee nyɛɛna mɛ la nyɛɛma kãtɛ sɛ'a en ka tari tɛka pirɛ suŋa.

ref: you are to be saved. This is why you have faith.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya to'ore la ya sakerɛ yɔɔrɛ en de ya sɛɛhi faarɛ la.

ref: Some prophets told how God would treat you with undeserved grace, and they searched hard to find out more about the way you would be saved.
text: Han dɛna faarɛ dina wa yele, nɔtɔgeheba sɛba en yuum purɛ zu'uhegɔ la en wan dɛna ya dinɛ la, ma'ɛ ba mɛhi mɛ, viihɛ, gee ɛ bu paaɛ paaɛ,

ref: The Spirit of Christ was in them and was telling them how Christ would suffer and would then be given great honor. So they searched to find out exactly who Christ would be and when this would happen.
text: viihera ɛɛra nɛresɛka, la sansɛka ti Kiresta Sia la en boi ba puan la ta pa'alɛ ba, doohe la Sia la en te'ele yele ba namehegɔ seko taaba en maahum gura Kiresta, la na'ahegɔ seko en dole ku poore.

ref: But they were told that they were serving you and not themselves. They preached to you by the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven. And their message was only for you, even though angels would like to know more about it. Chosen To Live a Holy Life
text: La yuum niiɛ pa'alɛ ba yeti, ba ka tuni bɔ'ɔra la ba mɛhi, gee tumam, doohe yelesɛ'a en nyaa muulɛ bo' ya nananawa, doohe sɛba en tari faarɛ yelehum wa na bo' ya la, doohe Fa'a Sia la ti Yinɛ tum en saazuo ha bahɛ na la.Yele sɛ'a ti suŋereba mu'ɛ nini ti ba nyɛ la. Fa'a vom yi'a

ref: Be alert and think straight. Put all your hope in how God will treat you with undeserved grace when Jesus Christ appears.
text: Ŋwana zuo, maahum ya puti'ira ti ya tum. Tana ya mɛhi tara. Bahɛ ya ti ya puti'ira za'a bɔna zu'uhegɔ seko ti Yezu Kiresta wan tari na bo' ya, a han ta niiɛ na la puan.

ref: Behave like obedient children. Don't let your lives be controlled by your desires, as they used to be.
text: Wuu yan de kɔma en nane la, da naaɛ ya la kunkoma'ahum sebo taaba ti ya yuum pun tara, yan yuum nan ka mi' la.

ref: Always live as God's holy people should, because God is the one who chose you, and he is holy.
text: Gee wuu se'em en yi' ya la, en de fa'a se'em la, deni ya Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba ya mɛhi, ya itegɔ woo puan.

ref: That's why the Scriptures say, “I am the holy God, and you must be holy too.”
text: La gulehɛ yeti, “Deni ya Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba, se'ere en sɔi la mam dela Fa'a.”

ref: You say that God is your Father, but God doesn't have favorites! He judges all people by what they do. So you must honor God while you live as strangers here on earth.
text: Yamam han yi'ira en Sɔ, se'em en gakeri nɛra woo ka tara ninkɔɔra, gee nɛra woo la a tuuma en de se'em la, voni ya la dabeem ya nɔka saŋa wa puan.

ref: You were rescued from the useless way of life you learned from your ancestors. But you know you were not rescued by such things as silver or gold that don't last forever.
text: Ya mi ti Yinɛ yɔ mɛ to'e ya yehe vom yoo sebo ti ya to'e ya yaabeduma zi'an la. A ka yɔ mɛ la lɔgeseto en sageni, ana wuu salempɛɛla bii salema,

ref: You were rescued by the precious blood of Christ, that spotless and innocent lamb.
text: gee Kiresta tuulum ziim en ani wuu kaabegɔ pebila en ka bɛ̃' bii tara dɛkɔ se'em la.

ref: Christ was chosen even before the world was created, but because of you, he did not come until these last days.
text: Yinɛ yuum pun maahum en mɛ gee ti timpaberɛ wa baŋɛ pi'ilum, gee ta niiɛ en ba'ahegɔ saŋa wa puan, yamam zuo.

ref: And when he did come, it was to lead you to have faith in God, who raised him from death and honored him in a glorious way. This is why you have put your faith and hope in God.
text: Doohe eŋa puan ti ya wa'am na nam Yinɛ, se'em en ihege en a kum puan la, gee bo' en na'ahegɔ, ti ya sakerɛ, la ya beere puti'irɛ la bɔna Yinɛ zi'an.

ref: You obeyed the truth, and your souls were made pure. Now you sincerely love each other. But you must keep on loving with all your heart.
text: Nananawa ti ya pee ya sɛɛhi doohe yan nam yelemɛŋerɛ la, nyaa tara yelemɛŋerɛ nɔŋerɛ la, nɔŋɛ ya taaba paaɛ paaɛ ya suya puan ha.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Humans wither like grass, and their glory fades like wild flowers. Grass dries up, and flowers fall to the ground.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, “Asaala woo ani mɛ wuu muɔ la ti ba suŋa za'a ana wuu mɔpuuro la. Muɔ ku'uri mɛ, ti mɔpuuro zɔgera,

ref: But what the Lord has said will stand forever.” Our good news to you is what the Lord has said.
text: gee Daana yelehum la kɔ'ɔm bɔna mɛ wuuha wuuha.” Yelehum buna wa dela faarɛ yelehum sebo ti ba muulɛ bo' ya la.

ref: Stop being hateful! Quit trying to fool people, and start being sincere. Don't be jealous or say cruel things about others.
text: Bala, yehe ya mɛhi, yele ana wa puan. Putoo, wikerɛ, birekɔgenɛ, nintɛrega, la yu'sageŋɔ.

ref: Be like newborn babies who are thirsty for the pure spiritual milk that will help you grow and be saved.
text: Wuu yan de bilɛɛhi la, kɔ'ɔm leebere ya mɛ la Sia puan bi'ihum sumɔ la, bala ti bu wan bahɛ ti ya zɔ'ɔra ya faarɛ la puan,

ref: You have already found out how good the Lord really is.
text: ya han sirum lem mɛ nyɛ Daana la en ani suŋa se'em.

ref: Come to Jesus Christ. He is the living stone people have rejected, but which God has chosen and highly honored.
text: Wa'am ya a zi'an na, eŋa en de vom ɛ̃berɛ kugere la, la saaleba en pun zagehɛ en la, gee eŋa ti Yinɛ looe, ti a dɛna tuulum lɔkɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan.

ref: And now you are living stones being used to build a spiritual house. You are also a group of holy priests, and with the help of Jesus Christ you will offer sacrifices that please God.
text: Wuu yan de vom kuga en dikɛ ya mɛhi mɛ Sia yire, deni ya tuulum malema duma, kaabera sia puan kaabegɔ en tigɛ Yinɛ nini doohe Yezu Kiresta puan.

ref: It is just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a choice and precious cornerstone. No one who has faith in this one will be disappointed.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la boi gulehegɔ la puan yeti, “Bihɛ, n biŋere la ɛ̃berɛ kugere Zaayɔn, ɛ̃berɛ kugere se'ere ti ba looe, gee ti de dɛna tuulum kugere. Se'em woo en nam en, vi kan di en.”

ref: You are followers of the Lord, and this stone is precious to you. But it isn't precious to those who refuse to follow him. They are the builders who tossed aside the stone that turned out to be the most important one of all.
text: Ti fum se'em woo en nam, a dela tuulum lɔkɔ bo' fum, gee sɛba en ka nam la, “Ɛ̃berɛ kuge se'ere ti mɛteba zagehɛ la, dinɛ en de kuge sunɛ, tara ɛ̃berɛ la,”

ref: But you are God's chosen and special people. You are a group of royal priests and a holy nation. God has brought you out of darkness into his marvelous light. Now you must tell all the wonderful things he has done. The Scriptures say,
text: Gee yamam dela buuri se'ere ti Yinɛ looe, ya dela malemaduma nabiihi, dɛna tuulum timpaberɛ, Yinɛ mɛŋa nɛreba, ti ya muulɛ tuunkãra sɛ'a en de se'em daana en yi' ya lika puan yehe niihum kãtɛ puan na la dinɛ.

ref: “Once you were nobody. Now you are God's people. At one time no one had mercy on you. Now God has treated you with kindness.” Live as God's Servants Should
text: Ya daa nan ka de a nɛreba, gee nananawa, ya dela Yinɛ nɛreba. Ya daa nan ka to'e nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, gee nananawa, ya to'e nimbɔ'zɔrɛ. Vɔni ya wuu Yinɛ yamehi

ref: Dear friends, you are foreigners and strangers on this earth. So I beg you not to surrender to those desires that fight against you.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ duma mam ka'ani ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, wuu yan de saama, la nɔgereba la, ti ya yehe ya mɛhi ingɔŋɔ wa kɔrehi sɛ'a en zaberɛ la ya sia la puan.

ref: Always let others see you behaving properly, even though they may still accuse you of doing wrong. Then on the day of judgment, they will honor God by telling the good things they saw you do.
text: Tara ya mɛhi suŋa sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la zi'an, bala ti ban pun dikɛ ya yi' be'em duma la, ba wan nyɛ ya tunsuma la, gee pɛgera Yinɛ, a han ta wa'am na ti a gakɛ.

ref: The Lord wants you to obey all human authorities, especially the Emperor, who rules over everyone.
text: Daana la zuo, nane ya asaala tigere woo kpe'eŋo, yele yele wuu Timbihega nɛŋa daana se'em en sɔi ya za'a bihera la,

ref: You must also obey governors, because they are sent by the Emperor to punish criminals and to praise good citizens.
text: bii timbihega duma ti a tum ba, ti ba namehɛ tuunfi'iha duma, gee pɛgera sɛba en tuni ma'a la.

ref: You are free, but still you are God's servants, and you must not use your freedom as an excuse for doing wrong.
text: Yamam en de Yinɛ yamehi la, vɔni ya fai, gee la bala za'a, da dikɛ ya fai bɔŋa la tuna be'em.

ref: Respect everyone and show special love for God's people. Honor God and respect the Emperor. The Example of Christ's Suffering
text: Na'ahɛ ya nɛra woo. Nɔŋɛ ya suurɔ seto en de sakereba la. Zoti ya Yinɛ. Na'aheri ya timbihega nɛŋa daana la. Kiresta namehegɔ makerɛ

ref: Servants, you must obey your masters and always show respect to them. Do this, not only to those who are kind and thoughtful, but also to those who are cruel.
text: Yamehi, nane ya ya kpɛ'ɛnduma kpe'eŋo woo la na'ahegɔ, dagi la sɛba en iti ya suŋa, gee ana ma'ma la ya la ma'a, gee sɛba me en vɛgeleni ya.

ref: God will bless you, even if others treat you unfairly for being loyal to him.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela yɔɔrɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, yan mi' Yinɛ la, ya han ŋmibera too, gee nyɛta namehegɔ seko en dagi tuuntɔɔ.

ref: You don't gain anything by being punished for some wrong you have done. But God will bless you, if you have to suffer for doing something good.
text: Ya han ŋmibera ban ŋmɛ'ɛri ya sɛla ti ya tuue puan, beni nyuurɔ en boi bini? Gee ya han ŋmibera yan tum ma'a gee ti ba namehɛ ya de zuo, Yinɛ nini tigɛ mɛ la ya.

ref: After all, God chose you to suffer as you follow in the footsteps of Christ, who set an example by suffering for you.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ yi' ya la, beni zuo, Kiresta namehɛ mɛ bo' yamam ti ya biha bala niira a nama la.

ref: Christ did not sin or ever tell a lie.
text: “A ka tari be'em, pã'ahegɔ ka tabelɛ ze'ele a nuuren na.”

ref: Although he was abused, he never tried to get even. And when he suffered, he made no threats. Instead, he had faith in God, who judges fairly.
text: Ban wa tu' en la, a ka leregere la tuurɛ. Ban wa nameha en la, a ka lebehe ba. Gee a dikɛ a mɛŋa bo' la se'em en gakeri tuuntɔɔ la.

ref: Christ carried the burden of our sins. He was nailed to the cross, so we would stop sinning and start living right. By his cuts and bruises you are healed.
text: Eŋa mɛŋa en zi' tu be'em a iŋa puan pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo. Be'em la en ki tumam puan la, tu nyaa wan vɔna tuuntɔɔ vom. A pɔgeleŋɔ la puan, ti ya nyɛ tibegɔ.

ref: You had wandered away like sheep. Now you have returned to the one who is your shepherd and protector.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya daa bɔregɛ mɛ wuu piihi la, gee nananawa, ya ŋmɛregɛ lebe la dunkiina sɛka en guri ya sɛɛhi la zi'an na.

ref: If you are a wife, you must put your husband first. Even if he opposes our message, you will win him over by what you do. No one else will have to say anything to him,
text: Pɔgeba, bala mɛa, sigehe ya mɛhi bo' ya mɛhi sireba, ti basɛba han pun ka nam yelehum la, ba wan nyaŋɛ ba, gee pun ka yele se'ere, gee ba pɔgeba aŋa la ma'a,

ref: because he will see how you honor God and live a pure life.
text: ba han nyɛta ya zulaam vom la, la ya na'ahegɔ seko en boi ya vom puan la.

ref: Don't depend on things like fancy hairdos or gold jewelry or expensive clothes to make you look beautiful.
text: Da yeen nɔni ya ya mɛhi yiŋa, doohe ya zuto gulega, la salema lɔgerɔ yɛa, bii futo da'a en kpe'em,

ref: Be beautiful in your heart by being gentle and quiet. This kind of beauty will last, and God considers it very special.
text: gee tugum bahɛ ya ti ya nunɔm la bɔna ya puan, ya ana ma'ma, gee tara bããlam sia vom la en de sunsɛka en ka tari ba'ahegɔ, tara tuulum kãtɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan la.

ref: Long ago those women who worshiped God and put their hope in him made themselves beautiful by putting their husbands first.
text: Sadɔɔ kuna ti diimen diimen tuulum pɔgeba la en yuum tari beere puti'irɛ Yinɛ puan la, yuum nɔna. Nana ba sireba en tari kpe'eŋo seko woo la.

ref: For example, Sarah obeyed Abraham and called him her master. You are her true children, if you do right and don't let anything frighten you.
text: Bala ti Saara nam Aberaham, pun ta yi'ira en a daana.Ya me dela a poyɔɔhi, wuu ya han ita sɛla en ani suŋa, gee ka bahɛ ti dabeem sãɛ ya.

ref: If you are a husband, you should be thoughtful of your wife. Treat her with honor, because she isn't as strong as you are, and she shares with you in the gift of life. Then nothing will stand in the way of your prayers. Suffering for Doing Right
text: Sireba, bala mɛa, me zu'uhi ya ya pɔgeba, yan boi la taaba la, na'ahera fum pɔga, la eŋa en pun dɛna tɔregɔ lɔkɔ la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba wan ta to'e Yinɛ vom bo'olum la mɛ. Iti ya bala ti sɛla da gu'ura ya pu'uhegɔ. Itesuŋɔ namehegɔ

ref: Finally, all of you should agree and have concern and love for each other. You should also be kind and humble.
text: Ba'ahegɔ, ya woo tara Sia la nɔyinɛ vom la, zɔta taaba nimbu'ɔ, nɔŋɛ ya taaba, tari ya taaba giile, gee sigehe ya mɛhi ya suya puan bo' taaba.

ref: Don't be hateful and insult people just because they are hateful and insult you. Instead, treat everyone with kindness. You are God's chosen ones, and he will bless you. The Scriptures say,
text: Da dikɛ be'em yɔ be'em, bii kɛhega yɔ kɛhega. Gee lan de se'em dela, lɛbehera sum. Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ yi' ya, ti ya wa'am na to'e sum la.

ref: “Do you really love life? Do you want to be happy? Then stop saying cruel things and quit telling lies.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, “Sɛba en boti vom, gee bɔta ti ba nyɛ dabeha suma la, ba ta'na ba zɛlehi da tɔgera be'em, ba da tara ba nɔya pã'aha.

ref: Give up your evil ways and do right, as you find and follow the road to peace.
text: Ba bire ba pɔya bahɛ be'em, gee ita suŋa. Ba ɛɛra suma'ahum, gee mu'ɛ nini dɔla bɔ.

ref: The Lord watches over everyone who obeys him, and he listens to their prayers. But he opposes everyone who does evil.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la nini gã' la tuuntɔɔ duma zuo, ti a tuba ana kɛli la ba pu'uhegɔ. Gee Daana la nɛŋa ka loregere la sɛba en tuni be'em.”

ref: Can anyone really harm you for being eager to do good deeds?
text: Nananawa, ani n wan daam ya, ya han mu'ɛ nini ti ya ita sɛla en ani suŋa?

ref: Even if you have to suffer for doing good things, God will bless you. So stop being afraid and don't worry about what people might do.
text: Gee ya han pun nameha yan iti sɛla en de ma'a zuo, sum boi mɛ la ya. Da zoti ya ba, gee me da bahɛ ti la daana ya,

ref: Honor Christ and let him be the Lord of your life. Always be ready to give an answer when someone asks you about your hope.
text: gee ya suya puan, tugum malum gurɛ ya Kiresta ti a dɛna daana. Saŋa woo, maahum ya ti ya tɔgɛ po'e ya mɛhi, se'em en soke ya ti ya pa'alɛ en ya beere puti'irɛ wa, lerege ya en bããlam la Yinɛ zoolum.

ref: Give a kind and respectful answer and keep your conscience clear. This way you will make people ashamed for saying bad things about your good conduct as a follower of Christ.
text: Tari ya susunɛ, ti ba han pun yi'ira ya yu'yooro, sɛba en keheri ya itesuŋɔ Kiresta puan la, vi wan di ba.

ref: You are better off to obey God and suffer for doing right than to suffer for doing wrong.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, la ani suŋa ti fum nyɛ namehegɔ fum itesuŋɔ zuo, namehegɔ la han dɛna Yinɛ sumboolum, nyana fum nyɛ namehegɔ be'em tuuma zuo.

ref: Christ died once for our sins. An innocent person died for those who are guilty. Christ did this to bring you to God, when his body was put to death and his spirit was made alive.
text: Kiresta me wa namehɛ mɛ buyina, tumam za'a be'em zuo. Tuntɔɔ daana en namehɛ bo' sɛba en dagi tuuntɔɔ duma, ti a tari ya na bo' Yinɛ. Ba ku en mɛ ingɔŋɔ wa puan, gee ba vo'e en mɛ Sia puan,

ref: Christ then preached to the spirits that were being kept in prison.
text: bilam ti a kiŋɛ ta muulɛ bo' sɛɛhi la en boi paka deo puan la,

ref: They had disobeyed God while Noah was building the boat, but God had been patient with them. Eight people went into that boat and were brought safely through the flood.
text: sɛ se'ehi yuuman yuuman ha, en yuum ka nam, Yinɛ en yuum gu'uhi la sugeri Nowa dabeha la puan, uureŋɔ la maalegɔ saŋa ha la, ti nɛreba fii wa, en de nɛreba ba nii ma'a yuum nyɛ faarɛ ko'om la puan la.

ref: Those flood waters were like baptism that now saves you. But baptism is more than just washing your body. It means turning to God with a clear conscience, because Jesus Christ was raised from death.
text: Ko'om sua wa ti yele dina wa yuum ze'ele bo' la, en faaɛ ya bahɛ nananawa. Dagi la bu yehe la dɛgerɔ ya iŋa puan, gee bu dela Ko'om sua sɛka ti tu suhera Yinɛ ti a bo' tu putɛ'sunɛ doohe Yezu Kiresta vo'ore la puan.

ref: Christ is now in heaven, where he sits at the right side of God. All angels, authorities, and powers are under his control.
text: Kiresta, en bala zom saazuo ha, ta bɔna Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o bɔba, la suŋereba, kpɛ'ɛnduma, la pahi se'ehi ti ba dikɛ biŋe a nama timpaberɛ.

ref: Christ suffered here on earth. Now you must be ready to suffer as he did, because suffering shows that you have stopped sinning.
text: Lan de ti Kiresta namehɛ mɛ ingɔŋɔ wa puan la, me bobe ya mɛhi la zaba lɔgerɔ, la putɛ' dinɛ mɛa. .

ref: It means you have turned from your own desires and want to obey God for the rest of your life.
text: Bala yan vɔi vom dabeha sɛ'a en gee timpaberɛ wa zuo wa, da voni ya bu la asaala kunkɔma'ahum, gee Yinɛ suboolum vom.

ref: You have already lived long enough like people who don't know God. You were immoral and followed your evil desires. You went around drinking and partying and carrying on. In fact, you even worshiped disgusting idols.
text: Ya pun diŋɛ dikɛ la saŋa zo'e zo'e tuna sɛla ti sɛba en dagi Yuuduma ita la. Zufagehum, kunkɔma'ahum, buka, dikãra, yaalegɔ, la baga kaabegɔ.

ref: Now your former friends wonder why you have stopped running around with them, and they curse you for it.
text: La iŋɛ nɛreba la pakerɛ ti yan ka le lagena la ba tuna tuunyooro tunɔ taaba la, bala ti ba yaala ya la.

ref: But they will have to answer to God, who judges the living and the dead.
text: Gee ba wan ta yele bo' eŋa se'em en maahum ti a gakɛ vɔpa la ki'induma la,se'ere zuo ti ba yaala ya la.

ref: The good news has even been preached to the dead, so that after they have been judged for what they have done in this life, their spirits will live with God.
text: Ŋwana zuo, ti ba muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la, pun ta paaɛ ki'induma la, ti la bahɛ ti ban pun gakɛ ba ingɔŋɔ puan wuu nɛra woo ti ba gakɛ se'em la, ba wan vɔna Sia puan wuu Yinɛ en ani se'em la.

ref: Everything will soon come to an end. So be serious and be sensible enough to pray.
text: Sɛla woo ba'ahegɔ pɔri mɛ. Bala, mu'ɛ ya nini, gee ta'na ya mɛhi tara suŋa, ya pu'uhegɔ zuo.

ref: Most important of all, you must sincerely love each other, because love wipes away many sins.
text: Sɛla en gani bala, tari ya nɔŋerɛ saŋa woo bɔ'ɔra taaba, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔŋerɛ pi'ire la be'em zo'e zo'e.

ref: Welcome people into your home and don't grumble about it.
text: To'oheri taaba suŋa ya yiya puan, gee da gɔgeni ya.

ref: Each of you has been blessed with one of God's many wonderful gifts to be used in the service of others. So use your gift well.
text: Wuu yan de tuntuneba suma, ti Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ pirɛ ya la, tuni ya bɔ'ɔra taaba, la bo'olum sebo woo ti yamam ayima ayima to'e la.

ref: If you have the gift of speaking, preach God's message. If you have the gift of helping others, do it with the strength that God supplies. Everything should be done in a way that will bring honor to God because of Jesus Christ, who is glorious and powerful forever. Amen. Suffering for Being a Christian
text: Fum se'em en tɔgeri, dela a tɔgera wuu Yinɛ mɛŋa en tɔgeri la. Fum se'em en tuni bɔ'ɔra, dela a tuna la pansɛka za'a ti Yinɛ bo' en la, bala ti Yinɛ wan nyɛ na'ahegɔ sɛla woo puan, doohe Yezu Kiresta puan. Eŋa en sɔi na'ahegɔ la paŋa, wuuha wuuha. Taari. Kiresta kɔma namehegɔ

ref: Dear friends, don't be surprised or shocked that you are going through testing that is like walking through fire.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, da bahɛ ya ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ, la namehɛ tuulɛ wa en boi ya puan ti bu makɛ ya bihɛ wa, ti la ana wuu yele saanɛ bo' ya.

ref: Be glad for the chance to suffer as Christ suffered. It will prepare you for even greater happiness when he makes his glorious return.
text: Gee nyɛɛni ya, la yan lagum tuta Kiresta namehegɔ la, bala ti ya me wan ta vɔɔla la nyɛɛma, a han ta niiɛ na la a suŋa la.

ref: Count it a blessing when you suffer for being a Christian. This shows that God's glorious Spirit is with you.
text: Ba han tuura ya Kiresta yu'urɛ zuo, sum en dole ya, beni zuo, sia sɛka en de suŋa la, dɛna Yinɛ Sia la, en boi la ya.

ref: But you deserve to suffer if you are a murderer, a thief, a crook, or a busybody.
text: Gee da bahɛ ya ti ya se'em nyɛ namehegɔ, ti a ku mɛ, bii nageyiga, tuunfi'iha, bii se'em en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra nɛreba yɛla puan.

ref: God has already begun judging his own people. And if his judgment begins with us, imagine how terrible it will be for those who refuse to obey his message. The Scriptures say,
text: Saŋa la paaɛ mɛ ti gakerɛ la pɔhɛ Yinɛ yire la puan yia, ti la han pɔhera tumam, la wan ba'ahɛ la ŋwani la sɛba en ka nam Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la?

ref: “If good people barely escape, what will happen to sinners and to others who don't respect God?”
text: Gee “La han kpe'em bo' tuuntɔɔ duma ti ba nyɛ faarɛ, la wan ana la ŋwani bo' putɔpa, la be'em duma la?”

ref: If you suffer for obeying God, you must have complete faith in your faithful Creator and keep on doing right.
text: Bala, bahɛ ya ti sɛba en nameheri dɔla Yinɛ sumboolum la, dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' Naana la en de sakerɛ daana la, gee kelum ita ba sum la.

ref: Church leaders, I am writing to encourage you. I too am a leader, as well as a witness to Christ's suffering, and I will share in his glory when it is shown to us.
text: Nananawa, mam mɛŋa en de kiima, gee dɛna aze'nyɛ daana bo' Kiresta namehegɔ la, gee me dɛna se'em en wan ta nyɛ tɔtɛ suŋa la en wan ta niiɛ na la puan. Mam kpemehe kiinduma en boi ya puan la

ref: Just as shepherds watch over their sheep, you must watch over everyone God has placed in your care. Do it willingly in order to please God, and not simply because you think you must. Let it be something you want to do, instead of something you do merely to make money.
text: yeti, ya gura Yinɛ piihi la en boi ya nu'uhen la suŋa, dagena pɛregerɛ puan gee la susunɛ, wuu Yinɛ en wan boti ni ti ya ita se'em la, dagi kunkonyiim puan gee ya kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ la nini la tuuma la.

ref: Don't be bossy to those people who are in your care, but set an example for them.
text: Da dikɛ ya zeero ziile sɛba en boi ya nu'uhen la, gee deni ya makerɛ bo' piihi la.

ref: Then when Christ the Chief Shepherd returns, you will be given a crown that will never lose its glory.
text: Ti dunkiin naba la han ta niiɛ na, ya wan di suŋa muŋa sɛka en ka pelegere la.

ref: All of you young people should obey your elders. In fact, everyone should be humble toward everyone else. The Scriptures say, “God opposes proud people, but he helps everyone who is humble.”
text: Bala mɛa, ti yamam bumbile paalehi la me dɛna ya nana ya kiinduma la puan. Ya za'a yɛ sigehe mɛŋa ti a dɛna ya fuo, ya bɔŋa la taaba. Se'ere en sɔi la, “Yinɛ zagehɛ zɛkɛ n mɛŋa daana mɛ, gee bɔ'ɔra sigehe n mɛŋa daana zu'uhegɔ.”

ref: Be humble in the presence of God's mighty power, and he will honor you when the time comes.
text: Bala, sigehe ya mɛhi Yinɛ nu'uhen, ti a zɛkɛ ya, saŋa la han ta paaɛ.

ref: God cares for you, so turn all your worries over to him.
text: Dikɛ ya ninmu'urɛ za'a bahɛ Yinɛ nu'uhen, beni zuo eŋa en biheri ya.

ref: Be on your guard and stay awake. Your enemy, the devil, is like a roaring lion, sneaking around to find someone to attack.
text: Tana ya mɛhi tara, gee me maahum. Gee ya dindaana la en de Sagena la kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ la nini nyaara wuu gbigenɛ la, ɛɛra nɛra ti a obe.

ref: But you must resist the devil and stay strong in your faith. You know that all over the world the Lord's followers are suffering just as you are.
text: Da bo' ya en sore, sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋɛ ya sakerɛ la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti ya suurɔ en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, me dole la namehegɔ kuna mɛa puan tɔla.

ref: But God shows undeserved kindness to everyone. That's why he appointed Christ Jesus to choose you to share in his eternal glory. You will suffer for a while, but God will make you complete, steady, strong, and firm.
text: Ti ya han namehɛ yuue fii wa, Yinɛ en de zu'uhegɔ za'a daana la, se'em en yi' ya ti ya wa'am na bɔna sunsɛka en ka tari ba'ahegɔ Kiresta puan la, a mɛŋa wan lebehe ze'ele ya, te'ele ya, kpemehe ya, gee sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋi.

ref: God will be in control forever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: Eŋa ti paŋa dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ la ka'a

ref: Silvanus helped me write this short letter, and I consider him a faithful follower of the Lord. I wanted to encourage you and tell you how kind God really is, so you will keep on having faith in him.
text: Doohe Silevanuse, se'em ti mam nyɛ ti a dela suuɔ en de sakerɛ daana, ti mam gulehɛ gɔŋɔ girega wa, ti n kpemehe ya, gee pa'alɛ ti Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ yelemɛŋerɛ en ŋwana. Sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋi de puan.

ref: Greetings from the Lord's followers in Babylon. They are God's chosen ones. Mark, who is like a son to me, sends his greetings too.
text: Ya lageŋɔ suurɔ en boi Babilɔn, ti Yinɛ lagum looe ya la, pu'uhi ya na. Bala mɛa ti mam dayua Maki me pu'uha ya na.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. I pray that God will give peace to everyone who belongs to Christ.
text: Pu'uhi ya taaba la suurɔ kɔkerɛ. Suma'ahum bɔna la ya woo en boi Kiresta puan la.

ref: From Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ. To everyone who shares with us in the privilege of believing that our God and Savior Jesus Christ will do what is just and fair.
text: Mam Simɔn Pita, en de yameŋa, dɛna Yezu Kiresta tuntuna la, en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba en doohe Yinɛ la Faara Yezu Kiresta tuntɔɔnɛ la puan to'e sakerɛ en de tuulum lɔkɔ wuu tumam dinɛ la.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to you and will let you live in perfect peace! May you keep learning more and more about God and our Lord Jesus. Living as the Lord's Followers
text: Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum en pirɛ paaɛ dɛna ya dinɛ doohe yan mi' Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta se'em la. Kiresta kɔma yi'a, la ba loore

ref: We have everything we need to live a life that pleases God. It was all given to us by God's own power, when we learned he had invited us to share in his wonderful goodness.
text: Yinɛ sia puan paŋa la bo' tu sɛla woo ti tu bɔta, ti tu ta'am vɔna gee tara Yinɛ zoolum doohe tumam mi' se'em daana en yi' tu a mɛŋa suŋa, la a itesuŋɔ puan la.

ref: God made great and marvelous promises, so his nature would become part of us. Then we could escape our evil desires and the corrupt influences of this world.
text: Bala ti a doohe yele ana wa puan, bo' tu a tuulum nɔbiŋe kãra la, ti bamam puan, ya wan ta'am piiɛ ŋmɛlema sɛ'a en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, kunkoma'ahum zuo la, gee tugum lagum tara a sia puan naanɛ la.

ref: If you keep growing in this way, it will show that what you know about our Lord Jesus Christ has made your lives useful and meaningful.
text: Yele ana han sirum bɔna ya puan, gee malum pa'aha ya puan, la wan gu', ti ya ka tara tɔregɔ, gee gu' sɛla en wan bahɛ ti ya kan wuna yan mi' tu Daana Yezu Kiresta la.

ref: But if you don't grow, you are like someone who is nearsighted or blind, and you have forgotten that your past sins are forgiven.
text: Se'em woo en koŋe yele ana wa, ka nyɛti zaara, dɛna fua, gee me tamena ti Yinɛ duuheri a be'em sebo en tole la bahɛ mɛ.

ref: My friends, you must do all you can to show God has really chosen and selected you. If you keep on doing this, you won't stumble and fall.
text: Ŋwana zuo, n suurɔ, kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ ya nini ti ya pa'alɛ, ti Yinɛ en yi' ya gee looe ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya han ita yele ana, ya kan malum sɛregɛ.

ref: Then our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will give you a glorious welcome into his kingdom that will last forever.
text: Bala, ya han ita ŋwana, ya kɛ̃'a tu Daana, la tu Faara Yezu Kiresta sɔ'ɔlum la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la puan, wan yu'ɛ yɔlaam bo' ya.

ref: You are holding firmly to the truth you were given. But I am still going to remind you of these things.
text: Ŋwana zuo, mam ku n puti'irɛ mɛ ti n tiihera ya yele ana wa, la yan pun mina a, gee sɛ zɛ'a yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere en wa'am ya zi'an na la.

ref: In fact, I think I should keep on reminding you until I leave this body.
text: Mam ti'ihɛ ti la de la ma'a ti mam nan boi timpaberɛ zuo sugerɛ wa en kan yuue wa puan la, n malum tiihɛ ya,

ref: And our Lord Jesus Christ has already told me that I will soon leave it behind.
text: la mam pun mi' ti n kum dabeha ka zaaɛ, n wan yehe sugerɛ wa puan wuu tu Daana Yezu Kiresta en niiɛ de se'em bo' mam la.

ref: This is why I am doing my best to make sure that each of you remembers all of this after I am gone. The Message about the Glory of Christ
text: N me wan kpeŋe n mɛŋa sadɔɔ seko woo puan, ti n han ta bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo, ya wan ta'am tiiha yele ana wa, sansɛka woo puan. Aze'nyɛduma en nyɛ Kiresta suŋa la se'em

ref: When we told you about the power and the return of our Lord Jesus Christ, we were not telling clever stories someone had made up. But with our own eyes we saw his true greatness.
text: Tumam ka yeen kɔ'ɔm dɔla la pumpɔreŋɔ kãra sɔlema, tumam en bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ tu Daana Yezu Kiresta paŋa, la a lɛbega na la, gee tumam dela aze'nyɛduma bo' a pankãtɛ la.

ref: God, our great and wonderful Father, truly honored him by saying, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, a to'e la na'ahegɔ la pɛka Yinɛ en de Sɔ la zi'an. Doohe la kɔsɛka en wa ze'ele Pɛntiireba za'a Daana la zi'an tɔgɛ bo' en na yeti, “Mam Dayua en ŋwana, mam Dayɔba'arɛ, se'em ti mam nini tigɛ.”

ref: We were there with Jesus on the holy mountain and heard this voice speak from heaven.
text: Tumam mɛhi en daa wum kua wa en ze'ele saazuo ha tɔgɛ bahɛ na. Tumam en daa boi la en tuulum zoore la zuo.

ref: All of this makes us even more certain that what the prophets said is true. So you should pay close attention to their message, as you would to a lamp shining in some dark place. You must keep on paying attention until daylight comes and the morning star rises in your hearts.
text: Bala, tumam en tari purega yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ. La wan ana ya suŋa, ya han kpeŋe ya mɛhi kɛleha yelehum wa en de butolela niira lika puan wa, gura ti beere ta yilegɛ, ti bulika belum ŋma'arɛ niira ya suya puan.

ref: But you need to realize that no one alone can understand any of the prophecies in the Scriptures.
text: Yia, ya mina ti purega ka boi gɔŋɔ la puan dɛna asaala mɛŋa puti'irɛ,

ref: The prophets did not think these things up on their own, but they were guided by the Spirit of God.
text: beni zuo, purega ka tabelɛ ze'ele asaala sumboolum puan na, gee saaleba, buraahi la pɔgehi en ze' zulaam, ti Fa'a Sia la tɔgɛ Yinɛ zi'an doohe ba puan.

ref: Sometimes false prophets spoke to the people of Israel. False teachers will also sneak in and speak harmful lies to you. But these teachers don't really belong to the Master who paid a great price for them, and they will quickly destroy themselves.
text: Gee nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma en yuum ihege na bɔna nɛreba la puan, wuu pumparenduma pa'aleba en wan bɔna ya puan se'em la, sɛba en wan li'ihɛ tari sageŋɔ puti'ira wa'am ya puan na. Ba wan pun ta zagehɛ ba Daana se'em en da' ba la, tari sageŋɔ wa'am ba mɛhi zuo na kalam.

ref: Many people will follow their evil ways and cause others to tell lies about the true way.
text: Gee bala za'a, zo'e zo'e wan dɔla ba yaalegɔ sadɔtɔ la. Pumpɔrum-pa'aleba wa zuo, nɛreba wan tuura yelemɛŋerɛ sore wa.

ref: They will be greedy and cheat you with smooth talk. But long ago God decided to punish them, and God doesn't sleep.
text: Ba nyika la zuo, ba wan fa' ya la ba pã'ahegɔ yelehum la. Ba namehegɔ la en purɛ bahɛ ba zuo yuuman yuuman ha la, dagi yoo, ba sageŋɔ la kan gã'arɛ.

ref: God did not have pity on the angels that sinned. He had them tied up and thrown into the dark pits of hell until the time of judgment.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yuum han ka bahɛ suŋereba, ban yuum tum be'em la, gee lobe ba bahɛ bugum timpaberɛ, ba' ba la bahɛ lika puan ha, ti ba bɔna bilam gura gakerɛ,

ref: And during Noah's time, God did not have pity on the ungodly people of the world. He destroyed them with a flood, though he did save eight people, including Noah, who preached the truth.
text: Yinɛ yuum han ka bahɛ diimen diimen saŋa la, la eŋa en yuum pun faaɛ Nowa, se'em en muulɛ tuuntɔɔ vom yele la, la nɛreba bayopɔi basɛba la, gee yuum bahɛ ti ko'om di sankaŋa be'em buuri la.

ref: God punished the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah by burning them to ashes, and this is a warning to anyone else who wants to sin.
text: En yuum bahɛ ti Sodom la Gomora tihi lebege tampɛgelum, sagum ba puga puga ti maalegɔ ka bɔna la, ti ba dɛna makerɛ pa'alɛ sɛla en wan wa'am be'em duma zuo na la.

ref: This shows that the Lord knows how to rescue godly people from their sufferings and to punish evil people while they wait for the day of judgment.
text: bala, Daana la mi' en faari tuuntɔɔ duma magehegɔ puan se'em bahɛ mɛ, gee bahɛ ti be'em duma bɔna namehegɔ puan gura gakerɛ dabeherɛ la,

ref: The Lord is especially hard on people who disobey him and don't think of anything except their own filthy desires. They are reckless and proud and are not afraid of cursing the glorious beings in heaven.
text: yele yele wuu sɛba en tari ba ihi tuna yaalegɔ tuunsɛ'a taaba, la sɛba en ka zoti kpe'eŋo seko woo la. Ba kpe'em mɛ, ti ba suya kãbɛ, ti ba ka zɔta ba sagum Saazuo pãkara duma yu'ura,

ref: Although angels are more powerful than these evil beings, even the angels don't dare to accuse them to the Lord.
text: ti suŋereba en tugum kpe'em, dɛna paŋa duma, ka malum tara tuurɛ yɛla gakeri ba Daana la nɛŋan.

ref: These people are no better than senseless animals that live by their feelings and are born to be caught and killed. They speak evil of things they don't know anything about. But their own corrupt deeds will destroy them.
text: Nɛrebana wa ani mɛ wuu duhi gɔɔnɔ la, ba yeen dɛna la bunvɔɔhi en ka tari vuurɛ, ti ba yeen dɔgɛ ba ti ba nyɔgɛ ba ku la. Ba tuurɛ la sɛla ti ba ka bɔkɛ, ti ba han ta sagum bunvɔɔhi sina la ba'ahɛ, ba wan sagum bamam me.

ref: They have done evil, and they will be rewarded with evil. They think it is fun to have wild parties during the day. They are immoral, and the meals they eat with you are spoiled by the shameful and selfish way they carry on.
text: Ba namehɛ la ba tuure namehegɔ. Ba dikɛ dɛ ti la dɛna la nyɛlum bo' ba, ti ba ɛɛra dita dikãra peelumen peelumen. Ba dela nɛrefi'iha, la zufagehum duma, nɔŋɛ ba dita ba dikãra mɛa la fagehum ya nɛŋan.

ref: All they think about is having sex with someone else's husband or wife. There is no end to their wicked deeds. They trick people who are easily fooled, and their minds are filled with greedy thoughts. But they are headed for trouble!
text: Ba nini kɔ'ɔm biha la yaalegɔ yɛla ma'a, ka tigera be'em. Ba ta' pa'aha la sɛɛhi se'ehi en ka ze' suŋa la. Ba zamehi ba suya la nyika. Nɔpɔrɛ kɔma en de ba!

ref: They have left the true road and have gone down the wrong path by following the example of the prophet Balaam. He was the son of Beor and loved what he got from being a crook.
text: Ba bahɛ tuuntɔɔ sore la mɛ gee tuuna, dɔla Balaam, Boso dayua la sore la, se'em en nɔŋɛ be'em yɔɔrɛ,

ref: But a donkey corrected him for this evil deed. It spoke to him with a human voice and made him stop his foolishness.
text: ti a mɛŋa be'em wa tahɛ en la. Boŋa en ka ta' tɔgera en wa tɔgɛ asaala kua bo' en, gu' nɔtɔgeha wa a zɔlegɔ tuunɛ la puan.

ref: These people are like dried up water holes and clouds blown by a windstorm. The darkest part of hell is waiting for them.
text: Nɛre bana wa dela bulekɛ'ɛhi, dɛna pika ti kusebego pɛ'ɛra kina. La dela bana ti lika paaɛ paaɛ pun maahum biŋe gura ba la.

ref: They brag out loud about their stupid nonsense. And by being vulgar and crude, they trap people who have barely escaped from living the wrong kind of life.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ta' pageri la page yooro, tara ba yaalegɔ kunkoma'ahum la, pa'ahɛ nɛresɛba en nan kɔ'ɔm piiɛ sɛba en boi be'em puan la.

ref: They promise freedom to everyone. But they are merely slaves of filthy living, because people are slaves of whatever controls them.
text: Ba biŋere ba nɔya, ti fai wan bɔna, gee ba mɛhi dela ŋmɛlema tuuma da'aba. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba dela da'aba bo' sɛla woo en ta' ba.

ref: When they learned about our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they escaped from the filthy things of this world. But they are again caught up and controlled by these filthy things, and now they are in worse shape than they were at first.
text: La han dɛna ti, ban piiɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo galeŋɔ la, doohe ban mi' tu Daana, la tu faara Yezu Kiresta la, ba le lebe lu la ba puan, ti la nyaŋɛ ba, ti ba'ahegɔ puan, ba pun nyaa yalegɛ nuurɛ gana ba yia la mɛ.

ref: They would have been better off if they had never known about the right way. Even after they knew what was right, they turned their backs on the holy commandments they were given.
text: La ani ni suŋa bo' ba, ti ba pun da baŋɛ ni tuuntɔɔ sore la, nyana ban baŋɛ dɛ gee biri ba poore bahɛ fa'a ze'elego la ti ba yuum dikɛ bo' ba la.

ref: What happened to them is just like the true saying, “A dog will come back to lick up its own vomit. A pig that has been washed will roll in the mud.”
text: La iŋɛ ba doohe la yelemɛŋerɛ tɔgɛ biŋere la en yeti, “Baa lebere la ka tiiro puan la,” la, ba sɔɔri kurikuri ti ka lebe ta lirum la bɛgerɔ puan.

ref: what God's prophets said would happen. You must never forget what the holy prophets taught in the past. And you must remember what the apostles told you our Lord and Savior has commanded us to do.
text: ti ya tɛra yelehum la en tɔgɛ bo' ya yuuman yuuman ha doohe tuulum nɔtɔgeheba la puan la, la nɔsɛ'a ti tu Daana, la tu Faara la tɔgɛ doohe a tuntuneba puan la.
