ref: But first you must realize that in the last days some people won't think about anything except their own selfish desires. They will make fun of you
text: Yia, ya za'a dela ya bɔkɛ ti, ba'ahegɔ dabeha la puan, sinsɛhi wan wa'am na, sɛna la ba mɛhi kunkoma'ahum,

ref: and say, “Didn't your Lord promise to come back? Yet the first leaders have already died, and the world hasn't changed a bit.”
text: gee yɛta ti “A lɛbega na nɔbiŋere la yee la bɛ? Tu sɔyaabe duma en tole na ki ba'ahɛ la, sɛla woo kelum bɔna la lan yuum boi se'em timpaberɛ wa naaŋɔ puan na la.”

ref: They will say this because they want to forget that long ago the heavens and the earth were made at God's command. The earth came out of water and was made from water.
text: Ba kɔ'ɔm mina mɛ gee zagehɛ yelemɛŋerɛ dina wa, ti Yinɛ yelehum la en naam saazuo wa yuuman yuuman la timpaberɛ wa, la ko'om wa en gã' wa,

ref: Later it was destroyed by the waters of a mighty flood.
text: ko'bumɔ mɛa en yuum di timpaberɛ wa, ti ka za'a sagum la.

ref: But God has commanded the present heavens and earth to remain until the day of judgment. Then they will be set on fire, and ungodly people will be destroyed.
text: Yelehum bumɔ mɛa en tɔgɛ biŋe, ti zina beere saazuo la timpaberɛ wa gura ba sige bugum puan la. Ba gã' gura la gakerɛ dabeherɛ la, la sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ la sageŋɔ dabeherɛ la.

ref: The Lord isn't slow about keeping his promises, as some people think he is. In fact, God is patient, because he wants everyone to turn from sin and no one to be lost.
text: Daana la ka gu'uni a nɔbiŋere la, wuu sɛba en ti'ihɛ gu'uŋɔ se'em la, gee a di'ihe la suure la ya, ka bɔta ti se'em bahɛ yoo, gee ya za'a wa'am na tee.

ref: The day of the Lord's return will surprise us like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a loud noise, and the heat will melt the whole universe. Then the earth and everything on it will be seen for what they are.
text: Gee Daana dabeherɛ la yeti la wa'am me na wuu nageyiga la, ti saazuo wa mɛlum la voole kãtɛ, ti bugum nyɛlegɛ lɔgerɔ, ti sɛla woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo nyɛlegɛ la bugum.

ref: Everything will be destroyed. So you should serve and honor God by the way you live.
text: Yele ana za'a en wan ta nyɛlegɛ ŋwana la, yan wan dɛna nɛre sɛba taaba dela, ya vɔna Yinɛ tuulum vom, la Yinɛ zoolum vom,

ref: You should look forward to the day when God judges everyone, and you should try to make it come soon. On that day the heavens will be destroyed by fire, and everything else will melt in the heat.
text: gura, gee bɔta ti Yinɛ kinɛ na dabeherɛ la wa'am kalam na, beni zuo, de daarɛ ti saazuo wan taaɛ bugum mɛlum, ti sɛla woo wan nyɛlegɛ la bugum la?

ref: But God has promised us a new heaven and a new earth, where justice will rule. We are really looking forward to this!
text: Gee, doohe a nɔbiŋere la puan, tu guri la saazuo paalega, la timpaberɛ paalega, sɛka puan ti tuuntɔɔ vom bɔna la. Ba'ahegɔ kã'aŋɔ

ref: My friends, while you are waiting, you should make certain the Lord finds you pure, spotless, and living at peace.
text: Bala, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, yan guri yele ana wa la, zigɛ ya mɛ ti ya nyɛ en la suma'ahum, ti dɛkɔ ka lama ya, bii tuure,

ref: Don't forget that the Lord is patient because he wants people to be saved. This is also what our dear friend Paul said when he wrote you with the wisdom God had given him.
text: gee biheri ya tu Daana sugeri la ti bu dɛna tu faarɛ zuo. Bala mɛa ti tu sunɔŋerɛ suuɔ Pɔɔl guleha bɔ'ɔra ya, la yɛm sebo ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' en la,

ref: Paul talks about these same things in all his letters, but part of what he says is hard to understand. Some ignorant and unsteady people even destroy themselves by twisting what he said. They do the same thing with other Scriptures too.
text: tɔgera yele ana wa yele wuu en iti se'em a gɔnɔ woo puan la. Yɛla asɛ'a boi a gɔnɔ la puan ti a bɔkerɛ kpe'em, ti sɛba en ka mi' la, la sɛba en ka ze' bɔbeyinɛ la ŋmɛreha a, tari sageŋɔ kina ba mɛhi zuto na, wuu ban pun ita vom gɔŋɔ la luga a sɛ'a se'em la.

ref: My dear friends, you have been warned ahead of time! So don't let the errors of evil people lead you down the wrong path and make you lose your balance.
text: Ŋwana zuo la, yamam n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, mam en te'ele ka'am ya la zuo, bihɛ ya suŋa ti yalum duma wa tuure la da ve'e ya kiŋɛ ti ya da ta'am ze'ele ya mɛŋa.

ref: Let the gift of undeserved grace and the understanding that come from our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ help you keep on growing. Praise Jesus now and forever! Amen.
text: Gee tugum zo'ore ya zu'uhegɔ, la ya mi'ilum tu Daana la tu faara Yezu Kiresta puan. Eŋa ti pɛka za'a dɛna a dinɛ, nananawa ta paara dabeherɛ se'ere en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la.

ref: The Word that gives life was from the beginning, and this is the one our message is about. Our ears have heard, our eyes have seen, and our hands have touched this Word.
text: Tumam muuli la se'em en daa pun bɔna pi'ileŋen ha yele, se'em yele ti tumam wum, se'em ti tumam nyɛ la tu nini, se'em ti tu bihɛ, gee gurɛ en la tu nu'uhi. Yelemɛŋerɛ, eŋa en de vom yelehum la.

ref: The one who gives life appeared! We saw it happen, and we are witnesses to what we have seen. Now we are telling you about this eternal life that was with the Father and appeared to us.
text: Vom buna wa, Yinɛ daa niiɛ bu mɛ na, ti tumam nyɛ bɔ, dɛna bu aze'nyɛduma, muula bu bɔ'ɔra ya, Yinɛ vom sumɔ en kan ba'ahɛ wa en daa boi la Yinɛ en de Sɔ la nyaa niiɛ bɔ bo' tu.

ref: We are telling you what we have seen and heard, so you may share in this life with us. And we share in it with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text: Le yɛ'ɛha tumam muuli la sɛla ti tu nyɛ gee wum bɔ'ɔra ya, ti yamam me bɔna la tɔ kankaŋi, gee yelemɛŋerɛ, tu boi mɛ la Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la a Dayua Yezu Kiresta la kankaŋi.

ref: We are writing to tell you these things, because this makes us truly happy. God Is Light
text: Tu gulehɛ yele ana wa, ti la bahɛ ti tu nyɛɛma en pirɛ. Yinɛ dela niihum

ref: Jesus told us God is light and doesn't have any darkness in him. Now we are telling you.
text: Sɔɔleŋa sɛka ti tumam wum a zi'an n ŋwana ti tu muula bɔ'ɔra ya wa ti, Yinɛ dela niihum, ti a puan, lika ka boi la fii wa me.

ref: If we say we share in life with God and keep on living in the dark, we are lying and are not living by the truth.
text: Tu han yeti tumam boi mɛ la en kankaŋi, gee bɔna lika puan, tu pareni mɛ, gee ka tuna yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: But if we live in the light, as God does, we share in life with each other. And the blood of his Son Jesus washes all our sins away.
text: Gee tu han vɔna niihum la kankaŋi, wuu eŋa mɛŋa en boi niihum puan se'em la, tu wan bɔna taaba kankaŋi, ti a Dayua Yezu ziim la pɛɛra tɔ, tuure woo puan bahera.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we are fooling ourselves, and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Tu han yeti tu ka tari tuure, tu pã'ahɛ la tu mɛhi, ti yelemɛŋerɛ ka bɔna tu puan.

ref: But if we confess our sins to God, he can always be trusted to forgive us and take our sins away.
text: Gee tu han purɛ tu tuure la, eŋa se'em daana en de yelemɛŋerɛ la tuuntɔɔ daana la, wan di sugeri bo' tu tuure la gee pee tu yehe sɛla woo en dagi ma'a puan.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we make God a liar, and his message isn't in our hearts.
text: Tu han yeti tumam ka tari tuure, tu iŋɛ en la pumparuma, ti a yelehum la ka bɔna tu puan.

ref: My children, I am writing this so you won't sin. But if you do sin, Jesus Christ always does the right thing, and he will speak to the Father for us.
text: N kɔmbibehi wa, n gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra ya ti, la bahɛ ti ya da tara tuure. Gee se'em han tuue, tu tari nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra mɛ en boi la Sɔ la, Yezu Kiresta, tuuntɔɔ daana la.

ref: Christ is the sacrifice that takes away our sins and the sins of all the world's people.
text: Eŋa en de akaabɛ to'e kaabegɔ bo' tumam tuure la, dagi tumam ma'a tuure la, gee tiŋa wa zuo za'a tuure.

ref: But if we claim to know him and don't obey him, we are lying and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Se'em woo en yeti, “Mam mi' en”, gee ka nana a nɔze'elego la, dela pumparema, ti nɛre-ina daana, yelemɛŋerɛ ka boi a puan.

ref: We truly love God only when we obey him as we should, and then we know we belong to him.
text: Gee se'em woo en nane a yelehum la, yelemɛŋerɛ, nɛre-ina daana puan, Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ la bi'ɛ paaɛ mɛ a puan. Dina ti tu wan baŋɛ ti tu boi a puan.

ref: If we say we are his, we must follow the example of Christ. The New Commandment
text: Se'em woo en yeti, “Mam boi a puan.” Dela a ita itegɔ wuu eŋa en iti se'em la. Nɔze'elego paalega

ref: My dear friends, I am not writing to give you a new commandment. It is the same one you were first given, and it is the message you heard.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, n ka guleheri nɔze'elego paalega bɔ'ɔra ya, gee nɔze'elego kɛka la ti ya pun tara de pi'ileŋen na la. Nɔze'elego kɛka la dela, yelehum la ti ya pun wum la.

ref: But it really is a new commandment, and you know its true meaning, just as Christ does. You can see the darkness fading away and the true light already shining.
text: Gee n kelum guleha la nɔze'elepaalega bɔ'ɔra ya, en de yelemɛŋerɛ a puan, la yamam me puan, beni zuo, lika la ye' tɔla mɛ, gee ti yelemɛŋerɛ niihum la pun niira na.

ref: If we claim to be in the light and hate someone, we are still in the dark.
text: Se'em woo en yeti, “Mam boi la niihum la puan,” gee yaaɛ a suuɔ, kelum bɔna la lika la puan.

ref: But if we love others, we are in the light, and we don't cause problems for them.
text: Se'em woo en nɔŋɛ a suuɔ, boi la niihum la puan, ti nɛre-ina daana, sɛla ka boi wan bahɛ ti a gɛ̃ta.

ref: If we hate others, we are living and walking in the dark. We don't know where we are going, because we can't see in the dark.
text: Gee se'em woo en yaaɛ a suuɔ boi la lika la puan, kina lika la puan, gee ka mina sose'ere ti a wan doohe, beni zuo, lika la tari la fɔɔnɛ na ki'iha a ninin.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because your sins have been forgiven in the name of Christ.
text: N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la yamam, n kɔmbibehi wa, beni zuo, Yinɛ bahɛ ya tuure bo' ya a yu'urɛ la zuo la.

ref: Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you have defeated the evil one.
text: N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam, sɔduma, beni zuo, ya mi' en, se'em en pun bɔna pi'ileŋen na la. N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam bumbile-pɔɔlegɔ, beni zuo, ya nyaŋɛ be'em daana la mɛ.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because you have known the Father. Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you are strong. God's message is firm in your hearts, and you have defeated the evil one.
text: N gulehɛ bo' yamam kɔma, beni zuo, yamam mi' sɔ la. N gulehɛ bo' yamam sɔduma, beni zuo, yamam mi' se'em en pun bɔna pi'ileŋo puan ha la. N gulehɛ bo' yamam bumbile paalehi, beni zuo, yamam tari paŋa, ti Yinɛ yelehum la bɔna ya puan, ti ya me nyaŋɛ Sagena la.

ref: Don't love the world or anything that belongs to the world. If you love the world, you cannot love the Father.
text: Da nɔŋɛ ya tiŋa wa zuo, bii yelesɛ'a en boi ka puan wa. Se'em han nɔŋɛ tiŋa wa zuo, Sɔ la nɔŋerɛ ka boi eŋa daana puan.

ref: Our foolish pride comes from this world, and so do our selfish desires and our desire to have everything we see. None of this comes from the Father.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla woo en boi tiŋa wa zuo la, ingɔŋɔ wa kɔrehi, nini wa kɔrehi, vom wa puan ya'anɛ, ka ze'ele Sɔ la zi'an na, gee tiŋa wa zuo.

ref: The world and the desires it causes are disappearing. But if we obey God, we will live forever. The Enemy of Christ
text: Tiŋa wa zuo la ka kɔrehi la ye' tɔla mɛ, gee sɛba en tuni Yinɛ suboolum wan bɔna wuuha. Kiresta dima daana la saŋa

ref: Children, this is the last hour. You heard that the enemy of Christ would appear at this time, and many of Christ's enemies have already appeared. So we know the last hour is here.
text: N kɔma wa, la dela ba'ahegɔ saŋa. Yan pun wum ti Kiresta dima daana la kini na la, bala mɛa ti nananawa, Kiresta dima duma zo'e zo'e wa'am na. Ŋwana puan ti tu baŋɛ ti la dela ba'ahegɔ saŋa la.

ref: These people came from our own group, yet they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But they left, which proves they did not belong to our group.
text: Ba ze'ele la tumam zi'an yehe, gee ba daa dagi tumam. Ba han deni la tumam puan, ba wan yee ni bɔna la tɔ. Gee ban yehe la, ba bahɛ ti la niiɛ ti ba se'em ka de tumam puan.

ref: Christ, the Holy One, has blessed you, and now all of you understand.
text: Gee Fa'a Daana la en sɔi yamam, ti ya za'a mina dina wa.

ref: I did not need to write you about the truth, since you already know it. You also know that liars do not belong to the truth.
text: Mam en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra ya wa, dagi la ya ka mi' yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee ya mi' de mɛ, gee mina ti pumpɔreŋɔ ka ze'eti yelemɛŋerɛ puan na.

ref: And a liar is anyone who says Jesus isn't truly Christ. Anyone who says this is an enemy of Christ and rejects both the Father and the Son.
text: Ani en de pumparema la? Han dagena se'em en zagehɛ ti Yezu dagi Kiresta la. Ena daana en de Kiresta dima la, se'em en zagehɛ Yinɛ en de Sɔ, la Dayua la.

ref: If we reject the Son, we reject the Father. But if we say we accept the Son, we have the Father.
text: Se'em woo en zagehɛ Dayua la, kan tara Sɔ la. Se'em woo en sakɛ bo' Dayua la tari Sɔ la me mɛ.

ref: Keep thinking about the message you first heard, and you will always be one in your heart with the Son and with the Father.
text: Gee han dɛna yamam bahɛ ya ti sɛla ti fum wum pi'ileŋen ha la bɔna fum puan. Sɛla woo ti fum wum pi'ileŋen ha la han bɔna fum puan, fum wan bɔna la Dayua la, la Sɔ la.

ref: I am writing to warn you about those people who are misleading you.
text: N gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra ya la sɛba en wan pã'ahɛ ya la zuo.

ref: But Christ has blessed you with the Holy Spirit. Now the Spirit stays in you, and you don't need any teachers. The Spirit is truthful and teaches you everything. So stay one in your heart with Christ, just as the Spirit has taught you to do. Children of God
text: Han dɛna yamam, bi'ihegɔ seko ti ya to'e a zi'an la, boi ya puan mɛ, bala, ya ka eere nɛra ti a pa'alɛ ya. Gee a bi'ihegɔ la en pa'ali ya sɛla woo la, ti la me dɛna sira gee dagena pumpɔreŋɔ, wuu a bi'ihegɔ la en pun pa'alɛ ya se'em la, boni ya a puan.

ref: Children, stay one in your hearts with Christ. Then when he returns, we will have confidence and won't have to hide in shame.
text: Gee Nananawa n kɔmbibehi wa, boni ya a puan, ti a han ta niiɛ na, tu wan tara inkpeŋere ti vi ka tara tɔ a nɛŋan, a han ta wa'am na. Yinɛ kɔma

ref: You know Christ always does right and everyone who does right is a child of God.
text: Ya han mina ti Yinɛ dela tuuntɔɔ, ya wan mina ti se'em woo en iti sɛla en de tuuntɔɔ, eŋa en dɔgɛ en.

ref: Think how much the Father loves us. He loves us so much that he lets us be called his children, as we truly are. But since the people of this world did not know who Christ is, they don't know who we are.
text: Bihɛ ya nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ se'ere en boi de tɔka ti Sɔ la dikɛ bo' tu, ti ba yi'ira tumam Yinɛ kɔma. Ɛ̃ɛ tumam en sirum de sɛba n bala. Se'ere en sɔi ti tiŋa wa ka mina tu la dela, tiŋa wa yuum ka ka mi' en.

ref: My dear friends, we are already God's children, though what we will be hasn't yet been seen. But we do know when Christ returns, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, tu dela Yinɛ kɔma nananawa. Tumam n wan ta ana se'em nan ka niiɛ. Tumam n mi' sɛla dela dina wa, Kiresta han ta niiɛ na, tu wan ana wuu en la, tu wan nyɛ en wuu en ani se'em la.

ref: This hope makes us keep ourselves holy, just as Christ is holy.
text: Se'em woo en tari putɛ'dina Yezu Kiresta puan, peere la a mɛŋa wuu eŋa Kiresta en de fa'a se'em la.

ref: Everyone who sins breaks God's law, because sin is the same as breaking God's law.
text: Se'em woo en tuue me galum mɛ, tuure dela galeŋɔ.

ref: You know Christ came to take away our sins. He isn't sinful,
text: Ya mi' ti Kiresta niiɛ na ti a dikɛ la tuure, ti eŋa puan ti tuure ka bɔna.

ref: and people who stay one in their hearts with him won't keep on sinning. If they do keep on sinning, they don't know Christ, and they have never seen him.
text: Nɛra ka boi bɔna a puan gee tuura. Se'em en tuure ka nyɛ en, bii a mina en.

ref: Anyone who keeps on sinning belongs to the devil. He has sinned from the beginning, but the Son of God came to destroy all that he has done.
text: Se'em woo en tari tuure dela Sagena la bia, se'ere en sɔi la, Sagena la pun tuura mɛ la bala pi'ileŋen na. Yinɛ Dayua la niiɛ na la yele dina zuo, ti a lorege Sagena la tuuma za'a za'a.

ref: God's children cannot keep on being sinful. His life-giving power lives in them and makes them his children, so they cannot keep on sinning.
text: Se'em woo ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ en kɔ'ɔm ka bɔna tuure puan, beni zuo, Yinɛ naanɛ en boi a puan, a kan ta'am bɔna tuure, beni zuo, Yinɛ en dɔgɛ en.

ref: You can tell God's children from the devil's children, because those who belong to the devil refuse to do right or to love each other. Love Each Other
text: Yinɛ kɔma, la Sagena la kɔma en niiɛ ba mɛhi se'em en ŋwana. Se'em woo en ka tuni sɛla en de tuuntɔɔ ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, bii fum se'em woo en ka nɔŋɛ fum suurɔ. Nɔŋɛ ya taaba

ref: From the beginning you were told we must love each other.
text: Bala, yelehum la n ŋwana ti ya wum pi'ileŋo na la, ti tu nɔŋɛ taaba.

ref: Don't be like Cain, who belonged to the devil and murdered his own brother. Why did he murder him? He did it because his brother was good, and he was evil.
text: Tu da ana wuu Keen se'em en ze'ele Sagena la zi'an na, ku a mɛa suuɔ la. Beni zuo ti a ku en? Se'ere zuo, a mɛŋa tuuma la dela be'em, gee ti a suuɔ la tuuma dɛna tuuntɔɔ.

ref: My friends, don't be surprised if the people of this world hate you.
text: Da ze' ya yɛli n suurɔ, la tiŋa wa zuo en yaaɛ ya la.

ref: Our love for each other proves we have gone from death to life. But if you don't love each other, you are still under the power of death.
text: Tu mi' ti, tumam zɔɛ la kum paaɛ vom, beni zuo tu nɔŋɛ la taaba. Se'em woo en ka nɔŋɛ, ŋwɔni ti a ki mɛ n bala.

ref: If you hate each other, you are murderers, and we know murderers do not have eternal life.
text: Bala se'em woo en yaaɛ ba suuɔ dela kuureba, ya mi ti kuureba ka tari Yinɛ vom sumɔ en kan ba'ahɛ la.

ref: We know what love is because Jesus gave his life for us. This is why we must give our lives for each other.
text: Tu mi' nɔŋerɛ la dina wa puan ti, a dikɛ la a vom biŋe bo' tumam, ti tumam me dɛna la tu dikɛ tu vom biŋe bo' tu taaba.

ref: If we have all we need and see one of our own people in need, we must have pity on that person, or else we cannot say we love God.
text: Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ boi se'em daana en tari tiŋa wa lɔgerɔ la ŋwani gee nyɛ a suuɔ en boti suŋɛ, gee kɔ'ɔm kãbɛ suure zagehɛ ti a suŋɛ en.

ref: Children, you show love for others by truly helping them, and not merely by talking about it.
text: Kɔmbibehi wa, da bahɛ ya ti tu nɔŋɛ taaba la nuurɛ bii tugum ma'a, gee tu nɔŋɛ ya taaba la, yelemɛŋerɛ la tuuma.

ref: When we love others, we know we belong to the truth, and we feel at ease in the presence of God.
text: Dina puan ti tu wan, mina ti tu ze'ele la yelemɛŋerɛ puan na, ti tu han wa'am a nɛŋan na, tu suya wa ma'ɛ lɔge lɔge.

ref: But even if we don't feel at ease, God is greater than our feelings, and he knows everything.
text: Ti suya han ni ta zɛregɛ tɔ, Yinɛ gani tu suya la mɛ, gee mina sɛla woo.

ref: Dear friends, if we feel at ease in the presence of God, we will have the courage to come near him.
text: Sunɔŋerɛ duma, tu suya han ka zɛregɛ tɔ, tu wan ta'am ze'ele Yinɛ nɛŋan ti dabeem kana,

ref: He will give us whatever we ask, because we obey him and do what pleases him.
text: gee to'e a zi'an sɛla woo ti tu soke, beni zuo, tu nane a nɔze'elego la mɛ, gee ita sɛla n peere a puurɛ.

ref: God wants us to have faith in his Son Jesus Christ and to love each other. This is also what Jesus taught us to do.
text: A nɔze'elego la n ŋwana, ti tu nam a Dayua Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, gee nɔŋɛ taaba, wuu eŋa en bo' tu nuurɛ se'em la.

ref: If we obey God's commandments, we will stay one in our hearts with him, and he will stay one with us. The Spirit he has given us is proof that we are one with him.
text: Se'em woo en nane a nɔze'elego la boi la a puan, ti eŋa me bɔna a puan. Dina puan ti tu mina ti a boi la tu mɛ, doohe la a Fa'a Sia la ti a bo' tu la.

ref: Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world,
text: Sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, da nam ya sia woo, gee magehɛ sɛɛhi la bihera nyaa, ba ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔtɔgehe pumparenduma zo'e zo'e en kɛ̃ tiŋa wa zuo.

ref: and you can know which ones come from God. His Spirit says that Jesus Christ had a truly human body.
text: Dina puan ti ya wan mina Yinɛ Sia la. Sɛsɛka woo en purɛ ti Yezu Kiresta wa'am me na la ingɔŋɔ, ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na,

ref: But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
text: gee sɛsɛka woo en ka purɛ ti Yezu [Kiresta wa'am me na la ingɔŋɔ] ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, sɛkana en de Kiresta dima la sia la ti ya wum ti ka kini na la, ka pun diŋɛ bɔna tiŋa wa zuo mɛ.

ref: Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world.
text: Kɔmbibehi wa, yamam ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, gee nyaŋɛ ba, se'ere en sɔi la, se'em n boi ya puan la gani se'em en boi tiŋa wa zuo la mɛ.

ref: These enemies belong to this world, and the world listens to them, because they speak its language.
text: Ba ze'ele la tiŋa wa zuo na, bala la, ban yeti sɛla dela tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, ti tiŋa wa zuo me kɛleha ba.

ref: We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
text: Gee tumam ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, se'em woo en mi' Yinɛ kelehe tumam mɛ, se'em woo n ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na ka kelehe tu. Dina puan ti tu mina yelemɛŋerɛ sia, la pã'ahegɔ sia la. Yinɛ dela nɔŋerɛ

ref: My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him.
text: N Sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, bahɛ ya ti tu nɔŋɛ ya taaba, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔŋerɛ ze'eti la Yinɛ zi'an na. Se'em woo en tari nɔŋerɛ, Yinɛ en dɔgɛ en, ti a mina Yinɛ.

ref: God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him.
text: Se'em woo en ka tari nɔŋerɛ ka mi' Yinɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ dela nɔŋerɛ.

ref: God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life.
text: Yinɛ niiɛ a nɔŋerɛ pa'alɛ tɔ la sadɔɔ kuna puan. Yinɛ tum la a mɛŋa dayua ba'arɛ bahɛ tiŋa wa zuo na, ti tu wan vɔna vom sumɔ en de Yinɛ vom, dɔla a puan.

ref: Real love isn't our love for God, but his love for us. God sent his Son to be the sacrifice by which our sins are forgiven.
text: Dina wa puan ti nɔŋerɛ bɔna, dagi ti tumam en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, gee eŋa en nɔŋɛ tɔ, gee tum a Dayua ti a wa'am na dɛna akaabɛ to'e kaabegɔ bo' tumam tuure.

ref: Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
text: N Sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, Yinɛ en ba' nɔŋɛ tumam ŋwana wa la, tumam me dela tu nɔŋɛ taaba.

ref: No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
text: Se'em nan ka tabelɛ nyɛ Yinɛ. Gee tu han nɔŋɛ taaba, Yinɛ boi tu puan mɛ, ti a nɔŋerɛ la niiri mɛ suŋa tu puan.

ref: God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us.
text: Dina puan ti tu mina ti, tu boi la a puan, ti eŋa me bɔna tu puan, se'ere en sɔi la, a bo' tu a Fa'a Sia la.

ref: God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him.
text: Tu me nyɛ de mɛ, gee muula ti, Yinɛ en de Sɔ la tum la a Dayua ti a dɛna Faara bo' tiŋa wa zuo.

ref: God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God
text: Yinɛ boi mɛ la se'em woo en purɛ ti Yezu dela Yinɛ Dayua, gee ti a daana me bɔna Yinɛ puan kankaŋi.

ref: and are sure God loves us. God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us.
text: Bala tumam mi' gee me nam nɔŋerɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ tara bɔ'ɔra tu la mɛ. Yinɛ dela nɔŋerɛ, se'em woo en boi nɔŋerɛ puan, boi la Yinɛ puan, ti Yinɛ me bɔna a daana puan.

ref: If we truly love others and live as Christ did in this world, we won't be worried about the day of judgment.
text: A nɔŋerɛ la niiɛ suŋa suŋa tu puan la ŋwana. Ti dabeem kan ta bɔna tu puan la fii me gakerɛ daarɛ la, beni zuo wuu eŋa en ani se'em la, tumam me ani la bala tiŋa wa zuo.

ref: A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
text: Dabeem ka boi nɔŋerɛ puan, gee nɔŋerɛ en pirɛ sa'ahɛ la dabeem. Dabeem dole mɛ la sɛla en de tuberɛ dɔlegerɛ, ti se'em woo en tari dabeem nan ka paaɛ nɔŋerɛ en pirɛ ba'ahɛ.

ref: We love because God loved us first.
text: Tumam tari nɔŋerɛ, beni zuo, eŋa en nɔŋɛ tu yia.

ref: But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see?
text: Se'em han yeti, “Mam nɔŋɛ Yinɛ” gee yaaɛ a suuɔ, dela pumparema. Se'ere en sɔi la, ŋwani ti eŋa ka nɔŋɛ a suuɔ ti a nyɛta la nini, gee wan ta'am nɔŋɛ Yinɛ se'em ti a ka nyɛta?

ref: The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
text: Nɔze'elego la ti tu to'e a zi'an la dela dina wa. Ti se'em en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, dela a nɔŋɛ a suuɔ me.

ref: If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children.
text: Se'em woo en nam ti Yezu en de Kiresta la, Yinɛ en dɔgɛ a daana, se'em woo en nɔŋɛ dɔgera la, me nɔŋɛ se'em ti a dɔgɛ la mɛ.

ref: If we love and obey God, we know we will love his children.
text: Dina puan ti tu mina ti, tu nɔŋɛ Yinɛ kɔma, tu han nɔŋɛ Yinɛ gee nana a nɔze'elego la.

ref: We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
text: Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ en de se'em ŋwana, ti tu nana a nɔze'elego la. A nɔze'elego la me ka tibegɛ vilegera,

ref: Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory.
text: se'ere n sɔi la, se'em woo ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ nyaŋeri tiŋa wa mɛ. Nyaŋerɛ se'ere en nyaŋɛ tiŋa wa zuo n ŋwana, tu sakerɛ.

ref: No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God. Who Jesus Is
text: Bala, Ani en de en nyaŋɛ tiŋa wa zuo, han dagena fum se'em nam ti Yezu dela Yinɛ Dayua? Yinɛ dayua la aze'nyɛ yɛla

ref: Water and blood came out from the side of Jesus Christ. It wasn't just water, but water and blood. The Spirit tells about this, because the Spirit is truthful.
text: Yezu Kiresta kana en bala doohe ko'om la ziim puan wa'am na na la. Dagi la a doohe la ko'om la ma'a puan wa'am na, gee ko'om la la ziim. Fa'a Sia la en de aze' nyɛ daana, se'ere en sɔi la, Sia la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: They are the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and they all agree.
text: Ti ba dela batã en de aze'nyɛduma tiŋa wa zuo. Fa'a Sia la, ko'om la, la ziim la, bana batã wa naaɛ la taaba.

ref: We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son.
text: Tu han tɔ'ɔha nɛreba en iŋɛ se'em yɛla, Yinɛ en iŋɛ sɛla la yɛla gani bala, Yinɛ en tɔgɛ a Dayua la yɛla se'em n ŋwana.

ref: If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar.
text: Se'em woo en nam Yinɛ Dayua la dela aze' nyɛ daana a mɛŋa suure puan. Se'em woo en ka nam bo' Yinɛ, iŋɛ Yinɛ ti a dɛna la pumparema, la ban ka nam yelesɛ'a ti Yinɛ tɔgɛ doohe a Dayua la.

ref: God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son.
text: Yinɛ en iŋɛ sɛla la yele en ŋwana. Yinɛ bo' tu la vom sumɔ en kamba'ahɛ, ti vom buna wa boi la a Dayua la puan.

ref: And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life. Knowing about Eternal Life
text: Se'em woo en tari Dayua la, tari vom mɛ, se'em woo en ka tari Yinɛ Dayua la, ka tari vom. Ba'ahegɔ

ref: All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life.
text: Mam gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra la yamam sɛba en nam Yinɛ Dayua la yu'urɛ la, ti ya mina ti ya tari Yinɛ vom sumɔ en kamba'ahɛ la.

ref: We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him.
text: Buraanɛ se'ere ti tu tara a puan dela, ti tu han soke en sɛla doohe a suboolum la, a kelehe tu mɛ.

ref: And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
text: Ti tu han mina ti a wum sɛla woo ti tu soke, tu mi' ti, tu to'e sɛla ti tu soke en la.

ref: Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly.
text: Fum han nyɛ ti fum suuɔ tuue tuue se'ere en dagi a ki, fum suhɛ Yinɛ bo' en, ti a bo' a daana vom, sɛba tuure en dagi ba ki la. Tuure boi mɛ dɛna fum ki. Mam ka yeti ya suhɛ tuure dinɛ.

ref: Everything that is wrong is sin, but not all sins are deadly.
text: Sɛla en dagi tuuntɔɔ woo dela tuure gee tuure boi mɛ ka kuura.

ref: We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
text: Tu mi' ti, se'em woo ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ la kɔ'ɔm ka bɔna tuure puan, se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta se'em ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ la en gu'uri en, ti Sagena la ka kalena ba.

ref: We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
text: Tumam mi' ti tu dela Yinɛ kɔma, gee be'em en ba' zo'e tiŋa wa zuo za'a.

ref: We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
text: Tu me mi' ti, Yinɛ Dayua la wa'am na bo' tu la bɔkerɛ, ti tu mina eŋa se'em en de yelemɛŋerɛ la, tu me boi la eŋa se'em en de yelemɛŋerɛ la puan, Yelemɛŋerɛ dina wa dela Yinɛ Dayua Yezu Kiresta. Eŋa en de Yinɛ de mɛŋa mɛŋa, la Yinɛ vomsumɔ kamba'ahɛ.

ref: Children, you must stay away from idols.
text: Lan de ŋwana la, N kɔmbibehi wa, gu' ya mɛhi suŋa suŋa baga puan.

ref: From the church leader. To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth.
text: Kiima la en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra pɔgesɛka ti Yinɛ looe la, la ka kɔma, ti mam nɔŋɛ ba yelemɛŋerɛ la puan. Dagi mam ma'a, la sɛba woo en me mi' yelemɛŋerɛ la.

ref: We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
text: Yelemɛŋerɛ la en boi tu puan la zuo, gee wan bɔna la tu wuuha la zuo.

ref: I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love. Truth and Love
text: Zu'uhegɔ, nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Yezu Kiresta en de Sɔ la dayua la zi'an na wan bɔna la tɔ, yelemɛŋerɛ la nɔŋerɛ puan. Yelemɛŋerɛ la nɔŋerɛ

ref: I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do.
text: Mam puurɛ ba' pee mɛ la mam en nyɛ fum kɔma la basɛba en kinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la puan, wuu Sɔ la en bo' tu nuurɛ se'em la.

ref: Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do.
text: Gee nananawa, pɔge mɛnka wa, mam yeen suhera fum mɛ gee dagi la n gulehɛ la nɔze'elego paalega bɔ'ɔra hɔ, gee se'ere ti tu tara pi'ileŋo na la yeti, bahɛ ya ti tu nɔŋɛ taaba.

ref: Love means we do what God tells us. And from the beginning, he told you to love him.
text: Nɔŋerɛ n ŋwana, ti tu vɔna dɔla a nɔze'elego la, nɔze'elego la dela, wuu fum wum de se'em pi'ileŋo na la, dɔla a.

ref: Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ.
text: Pumpã'ahum duma zo'e zo'e en wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na, sɛba en ka sakɛ ti Yezu Kiresta wa'am inganɛ puan na. Nɛre-ina daana dela pumpã'aha, la Kiresta dindaana la.

ref: So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward.
text: Biheri ya mɛhi suŋa, la ya da bɔregɛ sɛla ti tumam tum bo' ya ti ya tara la, gee ya wan to'e yɔɔrɛ la za'a.

ref: Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you.
text: Se'em woo en ka dole Kiresta pa'alegɔ la gee zɔɛ ku, ka tari Yinɛ a puan. Se'em woo en dole pa'alegɔ la, gee bɔna ku puan, tari Sɔ la mɛ, la Dayua la mɛ a puan.

ref: If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them.
text: Da to'e se'em en wa'am fum yire na, bii fum kã sum bo' se'em en wa'am fum zi'an na gee ka tara pa'alegɔ kuna.

ref: Greeting them is the same as taking part in their evil deeds. Final Greetings
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum han kan' sum bo' nɛre-ina daana, fum lagum tuna la a daana be'em la mɛ. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ

ref: I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
text: La mam en pun tara yɛla zo'e zo'e ti n gulehɛ bo' ya la, n kan gulehɛ a gɔŋɔ puan, gee n iŋɛ puti'irɛ ti n wa'am ya zi'an na tɔgɛ bo' ya, ya nini, ti tu pupeelum la pirɛ.

ref: Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
text: Fum suuɔ kɔma sɛba ti Yinɛ looe la pu'uhi fum na.

ref: From the church leader. To my dear friend Gaius. I love you because we follow the truth,
text: Kiima la, en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra n sunɔŋerɛ Gayɔse, se'em ti n nɔŋɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ.

ref: dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ daana wa, mam pu'uhegɔ dela sɛla woo kina suŋa bɔ'ɔra hɔ, ti fum tara inma'ahum wuu lan ani suŋa se'em la fum sia la.

ref: It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth.
text: Mam puurɛ ba' pee mɛ tole, n zɔduma la basɛba en paaɛ na tɔgɛ fum en tari sakerɛ se'em yelemɛŋerɛ la puan, yele yele wuu fum boi yelemɛŋerɛ la puan se'em la.

ref: Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth. Working Together
text: Mam ka tari pupeelum kãtɛ gana ŋwana, ti n wum ti n kɔma boi yelemɛŋerɛ la puan.

ref: Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ daana wa, fum tuni mɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ puan, sɛla woo ti fum ita bɔ'ɔra zɔduma la, la ban pun dɛna saama bo' fum la.

ref: They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve.
text: Ba yele fum nɔŋerɛ la yele mɛ lageŋɔ la nɛŋan. Fum wan iŋɛ suŋa, fum han wan iŋɛ ba suŋa ti ba tole, wuu Yinɛ en boti de se'em la.

ref: When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba pɔhɛ ba sore wa la Kiresta zuo, ka tɔ'ɔha suŋɛ sɛba n ka pa' ahɛ tu puan la zi'an.

ref: We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
text: Bala, tu dela tu suŋera nɛrebamam taaba, ti tu lagum dɛna tuntuneba yelemɛŋerɛ tuuma la puan. Diyoterefise la Demetereyose

ref: I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us.
text: Mam gulehɛ sɛla bo' lageŋɔ la. Gee Diyoterefise en boti a dikɛ a mɛŋa iŋɛ ba nɛŋa daana la ka bihɛ nyɛ tumam kpe'eŋo la.

ref: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
text: Bala, n han wa'am na, n wan bahɛ ti a baŋɛ en iti sɛla la, en dole yiregera pumpɔreŋɔ seko bɔ'ɔra tumam la. Ti bala ka seke en, ti a zagehɛ ti a kan to'e zɔduma la, gee gu'ura sɛba en boti ba iŋɛ bala la, pun ta sa'ahɛ ba lageŋɔ la puan.

ref: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ daana wa, da tɔgeha sɛla en de be'em, gee tugum tɔgeha sɛla en ani suŋa. Se'em woo en iti sum ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, se'em woo en tuni be'em nan ka nyɛ Yinɛ.

ref: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
text: Nɛra woo tɔgeri la sum yɛhera Demetereyose yɛla, bala mɛa ti yelemɛŋerɛ sore la me tɔgera a yele. Tumam me tɔgera a yele la bala, fum me mi' ti tumam en tɔgeri se'ere la dela yelemɛŋerɛ. Ba'ahegɔ pu'uhegɔ

ref: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
text: N tari la yɛla zo'e zo'e wan gulehɛ bo' fum, gee n tugum kan gulehɛ a iŋɛ gɔŋɔ puan,

ref: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
text: gee n iŋɛ la n puti'irɛ ti n kan yuue gee nyɛ hɔ, ti tu nyɛta taaba gee sɔhɛ.

ref: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
text: Suma'ahum bɔna la hɔ. Zɔduma la pu'uhi hɔ na. Pu'uhɛ zɔduma la en boi bilam la, nɛra woo a yu'urɛ a yu'urɛ.

ref: From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James. To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
text: Mam Yude en de Yezu Kiresta da'aberɛ la, dɛna Yemesi suuɔ la en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam sɛba ti Yinɛ yi' la, yamam sɛba ti Yinɛ en de Sɔ la nɔŋɛ, gee dikɛ ya sugɛ biŋe bo' Yezu Kiresta la.

ref: I pray that God will greatly bless you with mercy, peace, and love! False Teachers
text: Nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, la suma'ahum, la nɔŋerɛ en ka tari makerɛ dɛna ya dinɛ. Gɔŋɔ wa vuurɛ

ref: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
text: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, wuu mam en ma'ɛ n mɛŋa maahena ti n gulehɛ faarɛ se'ere ti tu tara wa yele bo' ya na la, mam bihɛ ti la nari mɛ ti n gulehɛ yele ya ti, nɛra woo zabɛ a sakerɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' tu buyina la zuo.

ref: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛre be'ero tuseto en wa'am na zu gɛregɛ ya puan, nɛre sɛba ba'ahegɔ zi'an en boi sageŋɔ puan ban ka zoti Yinɛ la zuo, sɛba en tee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la ti ku dɛna yaalegɔ tuuma, ka nam tu kpɛ'ɛma ayima ma'a en de Daana Yezu Kiresta la. Pumparum pa'aleba gakerɛ

ref: Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them.
text: Nananawa, n ba' bɔta mɛ ti n tiɛ ya yele dina wa, la yan pun mina de suŋa suŋa la. Ti, Daana se'em en yuum faaɛ nɛreba buyina za'a za'a yehe Igipiti nu'uhen la, pooren a yuum sagum ba puan sɛba en yuum ka nam en la mɛ.

ref: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
text: Gee ti suŋereba sɛba me en yuum ka ze' ba zɛ'a zi'an, gee yuum bahɛ ba zɛ'a zi'an la, a bobe ba mɛ la bãhi dikɛ ba bahɛ lika kãtɛ puan gura gakerɛ dabehekãtɛ daarɛ la.

ref: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
text: Bala mɛa ti Sodom la Gomora, la timbibehi la za'a en kaaɛ ba la me en yuum ani wuu bamam la, kɛ̃ yaluntuuma puan, bɔta buraa en gã' la taaba ti pɔgehi me gã'a la taaba la, dela sɛla en pa'alɛ nɛreba en nyɛ namehegɔ se'em bugum en diti ka kiŋera la puan.

ref: The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels.
text: Gee la bala za'a, bala mɛa ti zaahum zaaheneba wa galena ba ingɔŋɔ, ka nana kpɛ'ɛnduma, gee tuura saazuo pãkara duma.

ref: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
text: Gee Yinɛ suŋereba nɛŋadaana Maikɛl en yuum ta zigera la Sagena la, ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene la en Moose iŋa zuo la, a yuum ka tu' en, bii sagum a yu'urɛ, gee a yuum yele en yeti, “Daana en tahɛ hɔ la.”

ref: But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings.
text: Gee nɛrebana wa tuurɛ la sɛla ti ba ka bɔkɛ, ti yele aŋa mɛa en Sagena ba. Ba ani mɛ wuu duhi la, ba ka zamehi sɛla.

ref: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
text: Yele boi ba zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba dikɛ la Keen sore la, gee dikɛ ba mɛhi luhɛ Balaam tuure la en yuum de ligeri zuo la puan, ba bahɛ la yoo wuu Kuura pobiriga la en yuum iŋɛ en se'em la.

ref: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
text: Ba dela galeŋɔ ya nɔŋerɛ lageŋɔ la puan, ban diti la ya ti dabeem ka tara ba, gee ti ba yeen ɛɛra ba pirɛ ba puya ma'a la. Ba dela sagbama yooro en ka tari ko'om, ti sabeeo ziira ba kaara zi'an woo. Ba dela dɔwalega tiihi en ka tari wala la, ba ki la buyi', ba vuuɛ ba mɛ ta ta' la nyɛga.

ref: Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
text: Ba dela komi'ihe yooro, yɛhera ba nyanɛ fulenfuka na, ba dela ŋmarebibehi en kaari bura bura, sɛba ti lika kãtɛ gã'a gura ba wuuha.

ref: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
text: Bana en bala ti Enɔki en de Adam buuri saŋa buyopɔi puan la yuum purɛ ba yele yeti, “Bihɛ ya, Daana la kini mɛ na la a tuulum nɛreba tuha pia,

ref: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
text: ti a gakɛ sɛla woo, gee bahɛ ti nɛra woo nyɛ ban ka zoti Yinɛ gee tum tuunfi'iha sɛ'a taaba la, la yetɔge yooro seto taaba ti sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ tɔgɛ la ba nɔya tuue Yinɛ la.”

ref: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
text: Ba dela gɔgeleŋɔ duma, sɛba en nyɛti nɛreba tuure. Ba dole la ba mɛhi kunkoma'ahum, tɔgera ka du'ura, tara nɔmaalum la nɛreba, suŋera ba mɛhi ma'a. Ninmu'urɛ kã'aŋɔ la Pɛka

ref: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Gee han dɛna yamam n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ya dela ya tɛra tu Daana Yezu Kiresta tuntuneba la en daa pun diŋɛ tɔgɛ se'em biŋe la.

ref: They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God.
text: Ba daa yele ya yeti, “Ba'ahegɔ saŋa, nɛre kɛhereba wan bɔna, dɔla ba mɛhi en ka zoti Yinɛ la kunkoma'ahum.”

ref: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
text: La dela tiŋa zuo nɛrebana wa n ka tari Fa'a Sia la, n tari welehego kina ya puan na.

ref: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
text: Gee yamam n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, mɛ ya mɛhi yagelɛ tuulum sakerɛ la zuo. Pu'uheri Fa'a Sia la puan.

ref: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
text: Dikɛ ya mɛhi iŋɛ Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ la puan, gee la ya biha tu Daana Yezu Kiresta nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la en dole yɛ'a vom kamba'ahɛ puan la.

ref: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
text: Gee zoti ya sɛba en bu'uri ba sakerɛ puan la nimbu'ɔ.

ref: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
text: Faaɛ ya basɛba bahɛ wuu ya tum la bugum puan zɛ ba fooe na la. Gee me tari ya Yinɛ zoolum nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba. Gee kiha ba futo ti ba yaluntuuma la galum la. Sum ka'a

ref: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
text: Yinɛ deyina ma'a wa en de tu faara doohe Yezu Kiresta tu Daana puan la, sɔna na'ahegɔ la kpe'eŋo la paŋa la asɔeza'a, saŋa se'ehi en pun tabelɛ tole la, la nananawa, la wuuha wuuha. Taari.

ref: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
text: Yezu Kiresta lilegere, ti Yinɛ lilege bo' en, ti a pa'alɛ a da'aba, sɛla en wan ba'ahum iŋɛ nananawa. A niiɛ dɛ doohe la en tum a suŋera, a yameŋa Yɔn zi'an na la.

ref: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
text: Yɔn wa dela se'em en muuli Yinɛ yelehum, gee pa'ala Yezu Kiresta yele, ta ta' la eŋa en nyɛ sɛla woo se'em la.

ref: God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
text: Sum boi mɛ la fum se'em en kãalɛ yelehum wa yɛla la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa, ti sum bɔna la sɛba woo me en kelehe bu gee tara sɛla woo en gulehɛ bɔna gɔŋɔ wa puan la. Se'ere en sɔi la, saŋa la pɔri mɛ.

ref: From John to the seven churches in Asia. I pray that you will be blessed with kindness and peace from God, who is and was and is coming. May you receive kindness and peace from the seven spirits before the throne of God.
text: Mam Yɔn en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ tuyopɔi la en boi Esiya tiŋa puan la. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele se'em daana en de, gee daa dɛna, la se'em en ye' na la, la sɛɛhi siyopɔi la en ze' a na'am kuka la nɛŋan la,

ref: May kindness and peace be yours from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness. Jesus was the first to conquer death, and he is the ruler of all earthly kings. Christ loves us, and by his blood he set us free from our sins.
text: la Yezu Kiresta en de yelemɛŋerɛ aze'nyɛ daana la, dɛna ki'induma bikiima la, dɛna timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma naba la. Eŋa se'em en nɔŋɛ tɔ, gee faaɛ tɔ tu be'em puan bahɛ, la a mɛŋa ziim la,

ref: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
text: gee iŋɛ tɔ ti tu dɛna sɔ'ɔlum, gee dɛna malema duma, tuna bɔ'ɔra a Yinɛ la Sɔ la, eŋa ti na'ahegɔ la kpe'eŋo dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

ref: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
text: Bihɛ ya! Eŋa en bala bɔna sagbama puan kina na la. Niho woo wan nyɛ en pun ta ta' sɛba en daa lu' en la. Eŋa en kinɛ na la zuo, buuri woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo wan kele. Lan yeti la ana se'em en bala. Taari.

ref: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
text: “Mam en de Alifa la Omega”, Daana Yinɛ la en yele. Se'em en de, se'em en daa de, la se'em en wan wa'am na la. Gani za'a Daana la. Kiresta lilegere

ref: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
text: Mam Yɔn, ya suuɔ, se'em la yamam en lagum bɔna Yezu puan, lagum nyɛ namehegɔ, a sɔ'ɔlum la, la a sugeri ŋmibega la, n wa bɔna la tiŋa en boi ko'om tiŋasuka ti ba yi'ira ka Patimose, mam muuli Yinɛ yelehum la, la mam en de Yezu aze'nyɛ daana la zuo.

ref: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
text: Mam wa bɔna la Sia puan Daana dabeherɛ la puan, gee wum n pooren, ti kua kɛ'ɛŋa en ani wuu tintanɛ en peeberi la,

ref: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
text: yɛta mam yeti, “Gulehɛ iŋɛ gɔŋɔ puan, sɛla ti fum nyɛ wa, la fum bo' lageŋɔ tu yopɔi la. Bo' Ɛfisu, bo' Semirina, bo' Pɛgamum, bo' Tiyatira, bo' Sadise, bo' Filidelefiya gee bo' Laodisiya.”

ref: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
text: Ti mam ŋmɛregɛ ti n bihɛ nyaa ani kua en bala tɔgɛ bo' mam la, ti mam ŋmɛregɛ la, mam nyɛ la salema butolela ze'elehi siyopɔi,

ref: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
text: ti ze'elehi wa tiŋasuka, mam nyɛ la nɛra ayima ti a ana wuu Asaala Dayua la, yɛ fuwoko, ti salema tanɛ gabegɛ a nyu'ɔ.

ref: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
text: Ti a zuo, zonto kɔ'ɔm pelege, fari wuu gunguno la, pelege wuu sakuga la, ti a nini ana wuu bugum peelum la,

ref: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
text: ti a na'tala wa nyakera wuu nyɔgum ti ba sɛ' bugum puan paaɛ paaɛ ti ba nyakera se'em la, ti a kua ana wuu kulaa kãtɛ ko'om en sũni la.

ref: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
text: A zuɔ nu'o puan, a zãli la ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi, ti a nuurɛ puan, su'a en diti, tara nɔya ayi' en yeheri na, ti a nɛŋa kpikera wuu wuntɛɛŋa en ba' kpikera se'em la.

ref: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
text: Mam nyɛ en la, mam lu la a nama tiŋa wuu n ki mɛ la. Gee ti a dikɛ a zuɔ nu'o pagelɛ mam zuo gee yeti, “Da zɔta dabeem, Mam en de Pɔhega, la Ba'ahegɔ.

ref: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
text: Mam de se'em en vɔi la. Mam yuum ki mɛ, gee bihɛ, n vɔi mɛ wuuha wuuha. Mam en tari kum, la ki'intiŋa kparehega la.

ref: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
text: Nananawa, gulehɛ sɛla ti fum nyɛ, sɛla en iŋɛ wa, la sɛla en wan iŋɛ ŋwana pooren.

ref: I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
text: Han dɛna sugelum yele se'ere en boi ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi wa ti fum nyɛ n zuɔ nu'o wa puan wa, la salema butolela ze'elehi siyopɔi la, ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi la ze'ele bo' la Lageŋɔ tuyopɔi la Suŋereba.Ti ze'elehi siyopɔi me ze'ele bo' Lageŋɔ tuyopɔi la.

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
text: “Suŋera se'em en boi Ɛfisu lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna dela se'em daana en zãli ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi a zuɔ nu'o puan la, se'em en eere salema butolehi siyopɔi ze'elehi la tiŋasuka la yetɔgum.

ref: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
text: “Mam mi yan tuni se'em, ya namehegɔ la yan tari sugeri se'em ŋmibega puan. Mam mi' ti ya ka ta' ŋmibera la be'em duma, ya magehɛ sɛba en yeti bamam dela Yezu tuntuneba gee dagena la, baŋɛ ti ba pareni mɛ.

ref: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
text: N me kelum mina ti ya ŋmiberi mɛ la sugeri, gee uhera mam yu'urɛ la zuo, gee me mina ti ya ka taregeri.

ref: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
text: Gee mam tari la yele dina la ya. Ya bahɛ yan daa tari nɔŋerɛ se'ere la mam yia la mɛ.

ref: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
text: Tiɛ ya sɛla en bahɛ ti ya lu la, la ya tee, la ya tuna tuunsɛ'a ti ya daa pun tuna yia la. Han dagena bala, n wan wa'am ya zi'an na dikɛ ya butolela zɛ'ɛlega la ka ze'elego zi'an la, han dagena ya tee mɛ.

ref: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
text: Gee la bala za'a, ya tuunsuma en ŋwana. Ya ka boti Nikolatan duma tuuma la ti ba tuna la, tuunsɛ'a ti mam mɛŋa me pun ka bɔta la.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden. The Letter to Smyrna
text: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha Fa'a Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ duma la. Se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, N wan bo' en sore ti a di vom tia la wala la en boi Yinɛ vo'ohego zi'an la. Semirina lageŋɔ sɔɔleŋa

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
text: “N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la Suŋera se'em en boi Semirina tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la. Yetɔgebuna dela se'em en de Pɔhega gee dɛna Ba'ahegɔ, se'em en daa ki gee le vɔna la yelehum.

ref: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
text: “N mi' ya pɔgeleŋɔ, la ya nɔŋɔ, la yan pun tara la za'a. N mi' yu'sageŋɔ seko n paari ya, doohe sɛba en yeti ba dela Yuda buuri gee dagena, gee dɛna Sagena la lageŋɔ yire la.

ref: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
text: Da zoti ya dabeem la yan boi bini ti ya namehɛ namehegɔ seko wa. Mini ya suŋa ti bumbe'o la maahum mɛ ti a bahɛ ti ba pagɛ ya sɛba paka deto puan, ti la dɛna makerɛ bo' ya, dabeha pia ti ba wan dikɛ pɔgelum ya. Gee tari ya sakerɛ ta paaɛ kum, gee ti n bo' ya vom muŋa la.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death. The Letter to Pergamum
text: Bahɛ ti se'em woo n tari tuba, a kɛleha Fa'a Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la. Se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, kum buyi' la kan paaɛ en. Pɛgamum tiŋa lageŋɔ sɔɔleŋa

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
text: “Suŋera se'em en boi Pɛgamum tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la, ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna dela se'em daana n tari sɔ'ditega en tari nɔya ayi'.

ref: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
text: “N mi' ya bɔŋa zi'an, la dela zi'an ti Sagena la na'am kuka la bɔna. Gee la bala za'a ya kɔ'ɔm gurɛ mam yu'urɛ la mɛ kankaŋi, gee ka zagehɛ ya sakerɛ la mam, pun ta paaɛ zahera Antipas se'em en daa de mam aze'nyɛ daana, tara sakerɛ la mam, ti ba daa ku en ya tiŋasuka, Sagena la bɔŋa zi'an la.

ref: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
text: Gee mam tari la yele dina la ya. Ya tari basɛba ya puan ti ba dɔla Balaam pa'alegɔ la mɛ, se'em en daa pa'alɛ Balake ti a dikɛ sɛla en piti beŋe Izirale buuri la ti ba dita baga dia gee tuna yaluntuuma la.

ref: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
text: Bala mɛa ti yamam basɛba me dɔla Nikolatan pa'alegɔ kankaŋi la.

ref: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
text: Tee ya. Han dagena bala, n wan wa'am ya zi'an nananawa na, la n zabɛ la ba, la sɔ'ditega la en ze'ele mam nuuren na la.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
text: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuberɛ, a kɛleha Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la. Fum se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, n wan bo' en maana dia la basɛba en dikɛ sugɛ la, gee bo' en maanɛ kugepeele, ti kugere wa zuo, ba gulehɛ la a yu'paalɛ iŋɛ de zuo, yu'se'ere ti se'em ka mina, han dagena fum se'em daana en to'e de la ma'a. Taitira lageŋɔ

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
text: “Suŋera se'em en boi Taitira lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna dela Yinɛ Dayua la, se'em nini en ani wuu bugum peelum la, la se'em nama en nyagere wuu nyɔgum la dinɛ.

ref: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
text: “N mi' ya tuuma, ya nɔŋerɛ, ya sakerɛ, ya tuuma, ya sugeri la ya ŋmibega. N mi' ti ya pooren tuuma wa karegɛ gana ya yia tuuma la.

ref: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
text: Gee mam tari la dina la ya. Ya to'e Yezebɛle mɛ, pɔgesɛka en yi'iri ka mɛŋa nɔtɔgeha la, se'em pa'alegɔ en bahɛ ti mam yamehi bɔregɛ, tuna yaluntuuma, gee dita dia ti ba dikɛ kaabɛ baga la.

ref: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
text: Mam bo' en saŋa mɛ ti a tee, gee a zagehɛ mɛ ka tee a yaluntuuma la puan.

ref: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
text: Mini ya suŋa, ti n lobere en baha la suŋɔ zuo, ti sɛba me en naaɛ la en tuna kabohi la me, n wan lobe ba bahɛ susageŋɔ kãtɛ puan, han dagena ba tee mɛ yehe ba mɛhi a tuuma la puan.

ref: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
text: N wan damɛ a kɔma, ku ba, ti mam lageŋɔ la woo baŋɛ, ti mam en de se'em en mi' puti'ira la suya puan, gee wan bo' nɛra woo a tuuma yɔɔrɛ en nari se'em.

ref: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
text: Gee ya sɛba woo en gee bɔna Taitira la, ka dɔla Yezebɛle pa'alegɔ la, ka zamehɛ sɛla ti ba yi'ira ti Sagena la tuunkãra la, n yeti yamam yeti, n kan dikɛ zeero seto pa'ahɛ ya,

ref: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
text: yeen gurɛ ya yan tari sɛla la kankaŋi, gura ti n ta lebe na.

ref: I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end.
text: Se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, gee kelum tuna mam tuuma la ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ, N wan bo' en paŋa ti a biha tihi.

ref: I will give each of them the same power my Father has given me. They will rule the nations with an iron rod and smash those nations to pieces like clay pots. I will also give them the morning star.
text: Wuu mam en to'e paŋa se'em n Sɔ zi'an la, bala mɛa ti se'em daana woo n nyaŋɛ, n wan bo' a daana bulika beŋma'arɛ.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuberɛ, a kɛleha sɛla ti Fa'a Sia la tɔgera bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la.

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
text: “Suŋera se'em en boi Sadise tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna ze'eti la se'em n tari Yinɛ Sɛɛhi siyopɔi la, gee zãla ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi la zi'an na. “Mam mi ya tuuma, ya tari la vɔpa yu'urɛ, gee ya ki mɛ.

ref: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
text: Niiɛ ya, la ya kpemehe sɛla en gee, gee me yɛ'a kum la. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka nyɛ ya tuuma la ti a tigɛ Yinɛ ninin.

ref: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
text: Bala, tɛri ya yan to'e sɛla, la yan wum sɛla la, la ya tara di kankaŋi, gee tee. Ya han ka ihege, n wan wa'am na wuu nageyiga la, ti ya me kan mina sansɛka ti n yɛ'a ya zi'an na.

ref: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
text: Gee la bala za'a, ya kelum tara yu'ura fii wa Sadise tiŋa la puan en ka lirum ba lɔgerɔ. Bama en wan kina la mam, yɛ ba lɔgepeelo, se'ere n sɔi la, ba nari mɛ.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
text: Ya han nyaŋɛ, ba me wan bagɛ ya la fupɛɛla, ti n kan saalum ya yu'ura vom gɔŋɔ la puan bahɛ. N wan purɛ ya yu'urɛ n Sɔ nɛŋan, la a suŋereba nɛŋan.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Philadelphia
text: Bahɛ ti se'em en tari tuba, a kɛleha Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la woo la. Filadelefiya tiŋa lageŋɔ sɔɔleŋa

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
text: “Suŋera se'em en boi Filadelefiya tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna ze'ele la Fa'a daana la, se'em en de yelemɛŋerɛ daana la, zi'an na. Se'em en tari Deviti yire kparehega la, Se'em en yu'ɛ ti se'em ka bɔna yu'ura la, Se'em en yu' ti se'em ka bɔna wan yu'ɛ la.

ref: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
text: “N mi' ya tuuma. Bihɛ yaa, N yu'ɛ la kuleŋa biŋe ya, ti se'em ka bɔna wan yu' ka. Mam mi' ti ya tari paŋa fii wa, gee la bala za'a, ya tari mam yelehum la mɛ suŋa suŋa, ka zagehɛ mam yu'urɛ la.

ref: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
text: Bihɛ ya, n wan iŋɛ Sagena lageŋɔ en yeti bamam dela Yuuduma gee dagena, gee parena la, n wan iŋɛ ba ti ba wa'am na kpa' duma ya nɛŋan, la ba zamehɛ bɔkɛ yeti, mam sirum nɔŋɛ ya.

ref: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
text: Yamam en tari mam yelehum la en de sugeri, la ŋmibega la zuo, n wan gu' ya makerɛ se'ere en ye'ehe timpaberɛ wa zuo na ti de makɛ tiŋa wa zuo nɛreba za'a la puan.

ref: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
text: N ye' la nananawa na. Gurɛ ya yan tari sɛla la kankaŋi, ti se'em da ta faaɛ ya muŋa la.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
text: Ya han nyaŋɛ, n wan iŋɛ ya ti ya dɛna ziirɛ mam Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, kan malum yehe de puan. N wan gulehɛ n Yinɛ la yu'urɛ labelɛ ya, gee gulehɛ n Yinɛ tiŋa la yu'urɛ labelɛ ya, Yerusalɛm paalega la en ze'ele mam Yinɛ la zi'an saazuo ha na la, la mam mɛŋa yu'paalɛ labelɛ ya.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Laodicea
text: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha sɛla ti Fa'a Sia la yɛta bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la. Laodisiya tiŋa sɔɔleŋa

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
text: “Suŋera se'em en boi Laodisiya tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna ze'ele la se'em en de “Amɛn” la zi'an na. Sakerɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ aze'nyɛ Daana la, se'em en de Yinɛ naaŋɔ la za'a pi'ileŋo daana la zi'an na.

ref: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
text: “N mi' ya tuuma. Ya ka ma'ɛ bii ya tula. Mam boti ni ti ya ma'ɛ ni mɛ, bii ya tole ni.

ref: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
text: Gee lan de se'em la, yan de ko'bugere la zuo, ka ma'ɛ, gee me ka tula la zuo, mam boi bini ti n tubehɛ ya mɛ n nuuren bahɛ na.

ref: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya yeti, ‘Tu tari mɛ, tu pãrɛ mɛ, ka bɔta sɛla sɛla le yɛ'ɛha.’ Gee ya ka baŋɛ ti ya ku'urɛ mɛ, dita nimbu'ɔ, nameha, fɔ, gee ɛɛra beele.

ref: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
text: Bala zuo, mam ka'ani ya mɛ ti, ya wa'am na da' salema sɛ'a ti bugum bi'ihɛ a paaɛ la mam zi'an, la ya dɛna buntateba. Da' ya futo pɛɛla mam zi'an bagɛ pi' ya nyanɛ la, ti nɛreba da nyɛta ya beele la, gee da' tisumɔ mam zi'an tigɛ ya nini, la ya nyɛta suŋa.

ref: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
text: Mam taheri gee dɔlegera la sɛba ti n nɔŋɛ la tuba. Bala, mu'ɛ ya nini gee tee.

ref: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
text: Kelehe ya, mam ze' la kuleŋa la nuuren ŋmɛ'ɛra, se'em han wum mam kua gee yu'ɛ kuleŋa la, n wan kɛ̃ a zi'an na, di la en, ti a me di la mam.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
text: Se'em daana woo en nyaŋɛ, n wan bo' a daana zi'an ti a bɔna la mam, n na'am kuka la zuo, wuu mam mɛŋa en nyaŋɛ, gee ta zi'a la n Sɔ a na'am kuka la zuo se'em la.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha Fa'a Sia la en yeti sɛla bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la.”

ref: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
text: Ŋwana en tole la, ti mam biha, ti tinsuŋan ha, kuleŋa yu'ɛ bahɛ. Ti yia kua sɛka ti mam pun wum ni ti ka tɔgera bɔ'ɔra mam ana wuu tintanɛ en peeberi la, yele mam yeti, “Zom ka'lam na, ti n pa'alɛ hɔ sɛla en wan iŋɛ ŋwana wa pooren.”

ref: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
text: Kalam ti mam pun bɔna Sia la puan, paaɛ bini. Tinsuŋan ha la, mam nyɛ ti na'am kuka kãtɛ en ze', ti nɛra ayima zi'a ka zuo.

ref: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
text: Ti se'em en zi' ka zuo la nyagere mɛ wuu tuulum kuga en de dɛlemadɛtɔ la mɔlegɔ, ti sɛla en kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ wa mum la dɛna, gumatia tahɔ en ani biin wuu tuulum kuge sabela la.

ref: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
text: Ti sɛla en ze' kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ wa mum, dela na'am kugehi pihiyi' la sinaahi, ti sɛba en zi' na'am kugehi sina wa zuto dɛna kiinduma pihiyi' la banaahi en bagɛ la na'am fupɛɛla, ti salema muhi vuga ba zuto.

ref: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
text: Ti sɛla en ze'eti na'am kuka kãtɛ la puan na, dela saa bugum en nyagere, la dameŋa la saa tahega, gee ti na'am kuka kãtɛ wa nɛŋan na, bugum zɛlehi siyopɔi en ze' dita, dɛna Yinɛ sɛɛhi siyopɔi la.

ref: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
text: Na'am kuka kãtɛ mɛa nɛŋan, sɛla en gã' wuu salema ateko la, ana yilaam. Ti sɛla en kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, ka luga anaahi la za'a, dela bunvɔɔhi banaahi, ti ba zi'an woo tara nini, ba nɛŋa la ba poore za'a .

ref: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
text: Ti se'em en boi yia la ana wuu gbigenɛ, ti se'em en tagelɛ la me ana wuu lɔlega, ti ta'a daana la tara nɛŋa en ani wuu asaala nɛŋa la, ti naahi daana la nɛŋa ana wuu tugeho en ɛgeri la.

ref: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
text: Ti bunvɔɔhi sinaahi wa ayima woo tari la kelumpagehi siyoobe, pirɛ la nini ba yiŋa, la ba puan. Ti bulika la yu'uŋɔ za'a, ba kɔ'ɔm yuuna mɛ ka malum gɔ'ɔra. Ba yuuni yeti, “Fa'a, Fa'a, Fa'a, Daana Yinɛ, Gani za'a daana, se'em en daa de, gee kelum dɛna, gee wan wa'am na.”

ref: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
text: Ti saŋa woo, bunduma wa han ni na'ahera, gee pɛgera, gee pu'uha se'em daana en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la, se'em en boi wuuha wuuha la,

ref: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
text: “Fu nari mɛ, tu Daana la tu Yinɛ, ti fum to'e pɛka la na'ahegɔ la paŋa, se'ere en sɔi la, fum naam sɛla woo , ti fum sumboolum zuo ti ba bɔna, ti fum en naam ba.”

ref: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti se'em en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la a zuɔ nu'o puan, ti gɔŋɔ ti ba gulehɛ ku puan, la ku pooren za'a, pɔbɛ ku la daalehum buyopɔi.

ref: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera en de paŋa daana, muula la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “ Ani en boi nara ti a lorege gɔŋɔ wa, la a lakɛ daalehum la gee bihɛ ku puan?”

ref: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Ti se'em ka bɔna tinsuŋan la, bii timpaberɛ wa zuo, bii timpaberɛ wa tiŋa tiŋa ha, wan ta'am lakɛ gɔŋɔ la bihɛ ku puan.

ref: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Ti mam pɔhɛ kɛla ninmu'urɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, se'em ka boi nara a yu'ɛ gɔŋɔ la, bii a bihɛ ku puan.

ref: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
text: Ti kiinduma la ayima yele mam yeti, “Da kɛla. Bihɛ, Gbigenɛ la en yehe Yuda buuri puan na la, se'em tuŋo en yehe Deviti puan na la nyaŋɛ ya, wan ta'am yu'ɛ gɔŋɔ la, la ku daalehum buyopɔi la.”

ref: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la bunvɔɔhi banaahi la, la kiinduma la tiŋasuka, ti Pebila ze', ana wuu ba tabelɛ kɔregɛ ka mɛ la, tara iila ayopɔi la nini iyopɔi en de Yinɛ sɛɛhi siyopɔi la, ti Yinɛ tum si timpaberɛ wa zuo zi'an woo na la.

ref: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
text: Ti a kiŋɛ paaɛ to'e gɔŋɔ la se'em en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la zuɔ nu'o la puan.

ref: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
text: Ti ba za'a yuum yuumpaalɛ yeti, “Fum nari mɛ ti fum to'e gɔŋɔ la la fum lorege daalehum se'ere en sɔi la, ba yuum kɔregɛ fum mɛ, ti fum dikɛ fum ziim yɔ to'e nɛre sunduma, buuri woo, tugum woo, nɛreba woo la tihi woo puan bo' Yinɛ.

ref: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
text: Fum iŋɛ ba ti ba dɛna la malema duma buuri sɔ'ɔlum, tuna bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ,” ba wan sɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo biha.

ref: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
text: Ti mam bihɛ, wum suŋereba kuuhi zo'e zo'e en kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la bunduma la, la kiinduma la. Ti ba kãlɛ kɛ̃'ɛra tuha pia la tuha tuha,

ref: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
text: yuuna la ba kuuhi za'a yeti, “ Pebila la ti ba kɔregɛ la nari mɛ ti ka to'e paŋa, tarega, yɛm la kpe'eŋo la na'ahegɔ, la suŋa, la sum.”

ref: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
text: Ti mam wum ti bunvua woo en boi tinsuŋan, timpaberɛ wa zuo, la tiŋa tiŋa ha, la ateko puan, la sɛla woo en boi ba puan, ti ba za'a yuuna yeti, “ Se'em daana en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la, la Pebila la, sum, la na'ahegɔ, suŋa la kpe'eŋo dɛna fum dinɛ wuuha wuuha.”

ref: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
text: Ti bunvɔɔhi banaahi la yeti, “Taari.” Ti kiinduma la za'a lu tiŋa la na'ahegɔ.

ref: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti Pebila la lorege daalehum bayopɔi la a yima, ti mam wum ti bunvɔɔhi banaahi la a yima yi' bahɛ na la kua en ani woo saa dameŋa yeti,“Wa'ana.”

ref: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
text: Ti mam bihɛ, ti yeeho pɛɛlega en ze', ti bu ba'ara zãla tahɔ, ti ba dikɛ muŋa bo' en, ti a yehe na, dɛna nyaŋera, ti a nini mu'ɛ la nyaŋerɛ.

ref: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Pebila la en lorege yi'i daalehum la, ti mam wum ti bunduma la, yi'i daana la yi' bahɛ na yeti, “Wa'ana.”

ref: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
text: Ti yeeho buyima me yehe na, mu'ɛ ziŋiliŋ, ti ba bo' bu ba'ara la paŋa, ti a doohe kã'ahɛ suma'ahum timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a yehe, ti la bahɛ ti nɛreba kɔrum taaba. Ba me bo' en la sɔ'kãtɛ.

ref: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
text: En lorege daalehum butã la, mam wum ti bunduma la, ta'a daana la, yi' bahɛ na yeti,“Wa'ana.” Ti mam bihɛ ti yeeho sɔbelegɔ en ze', ti bu ba'ara la zãla magehega bayi' a nu'uhen,

ref: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
text: ti mam wum ti sɛla ana wuu kua la, ze'ele bunvɔɔhi banaahi la zi'an na yɛta yeti, “Makɛ ki la wula ti la dɛna daanyinɛ tuuma yɔɔrɛ, gee dikɛ wambila makɛ kemɔlega ti la dɛna daanyinɛ yɔɔrɛ, gee da sagum olive kaam la, la daam la.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
text: En lorege daalehum sebo en pa'ahɛ bu naahi la, mam wum la bunduma la bunaahi daana la en yi' bahɛ na yeti,“Wa'ana.”

ref: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
text: Ti mam bihɛ ti yeeho wugelegɔ en ani biin zɛ'a. Ti bu ba'ara la yu'urɛ yi'ira Kum, ti sageŋɔ dɔla la en. Ti ba bo' ba paŋa, ti ba sɔna timpaberɛ wa puregerɛ bunaahi, la ba kɔrum la su'a, kom, la bã'ahi, la muuen duhi en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo.
