ref: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
text: En lorege daalehum bunuu la, mam nyɛ la kaabegɔ bimbine la tiŋa, ti sɛɛhi en de sɛba ti ba kɔrum ba Yinɛ yelehum la zuo la, la ban muulɛ ban nyɛ sɛla la zuo la.

ref: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
text: Ti ba kaahɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “Gaŋeza'a Daana, Fa'a la Yelemɛŋerɛ Daana, la yeti la yuue la ŋwani gee ti fum gakɛ gee lerege timpaberɛ zuo duma wa en ka'ɛ tumam ziim wa se'em la?”

ref: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
text: Ti ba bo' ba za'a fupɛɛlega fupɛɛlega, gee yele ba yeti, ba lebe ta gã'arɛ fii wa, gura ti kãlɛ se'ere ti ba yeti ba kɔrum ba tuntuneba tadaanduma la, la ba suurɔ la ti ba yeti ba ku ba nananawa la paaɛ.

ref: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
text: En lorege daalehum buyoobe la, mam bihɛ ti tinmiiŋo kãtɛ en sige, ti wuntɛɛŋa wa sobege wuu susageŋɔ fuo la, ti ŋmare pɛɛlega mu'ɛ wuu ziim la,

ref: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
text: ti ŋmarebibehi en boi sagbama zuo ha luta timpaberɛ wa zuo na wuu kinkaŋa ti sabeeo lobe ti ka mu'uro zɔgera se'em la,

ref: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
text: ti sagbanɛ wa mɛlum wuu gɔŋɔ en kilum ku mɛŋa se'em la, ti zoore woo, la ko'om tiŋasuka tiŋa wuu mɛlum ka bɔŋa zi'an la.

ref: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
text: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo na'duma, nɛrekãreba, pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛnduma, nɛrezura, la nɛra woo, da'aberɛ bii se'em en boi fai, za'a zɔta sugera yogero puan, tahi leŋen la zɔya tilan,

ref: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
text: gee yi'ira zɔya, la tahi yeti, “Lu ya tu zuo na, lile tu, ti tu da nyɛta se'em daana en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la nɛŋa. Tu da nyɛ Pebila la sunsunyiim la,

ref: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
text: se'ere en sɔi la, ba sunsunyiim dabehekãtɛ la paaɛ ya.Ti ani n wan ta'am ze'ele?”

ref: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
text: A yele ba yeti, “Da sagum ya timpaberɛ la, bii ateko la, bii tiihi la, gura ti tu daalɛ tu Yinɛ yamehi la daalehum busebo ba gbɛ'ɛra ba'ahɛ.”

ref: Then I heard how many people had been marked on the forehead. There were
text: Ti mam wum ti sɛba ti ba wan daalɛ ba la dela tuha kɔbega la tuha pihinaahi la a naahi, Izirale buuri woo puan.

ref: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
text: yuuna yeti, “Taari, Sum, la suŋa la yɛm la fara fara la na'ahegɔ la paŋa la kpe'eŋo dɛna tumam Yinɛ la dinɛ wuuha wuuha, Taari.”

ref: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
text: Ŋwana en tole la, mam nyɛ la nɛrekuuŋɔ ti nɛra kan ta'am kãalɛ ba, en ze'ele tihi woo, buuri woo, nɛreba woo, la tugum woo puan na, zɛ'a na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la Pebila la nɛŋan, yɛ salema lɔge pɛɛla, zãla kunkɔɔreŋɔ vɔtɔ ba nu'uhen.

ref: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
text: Kaahɛ bahɛ na la kɔ kɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “Faarɛ ze'eti la tumam Yinɛ wa, se'em en yage na'am kuka kãtɛ wa zuo wa, la Pebila wa zi'an na.”

ref: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
text: Ti suŋereba za'a ze'ele kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la kiinduma la, la bunvɔɔhi banaahi la, ti ba za'a lu aliko na'am kuka kãtɛ la nɛŋan, na'ahɛ Yinɛ,

ref: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
text: Ti kiinduma la ayima nyaa yele mam yeti, “Bani en de ba yɛ fupɛɛla wa, ba ze'ele la bɛha na?”

ref: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
text: Ti mam yele en yeti, “Naa, fum en de se'em en mi'.” Ti a nyaa yele mam yeti, “Bana dela sɛba en ze'ele namehegɔ kãtɛ puan yehe na. Ba pee ba futo mɛ, ti tu pelege kirikiri Pebila la ziim la puan.

ref: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
text: Ŋwana zuo ti ba ta'am paaɛ Yinɛ na'am kuka wa nɛŋan na wa, gee na'aha en beere en yilegɛ woo, la yu'uŋɔ a pu'uhegɔ yire wa puan wa, ti se'em en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ wa zuo la wan gura ba.

ref: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
text: Ba kan mina kom, bii ko'nyuuro le yɛ'ɛha Wuntɛɛŋa ka le si'ira ba, bii wɔlegɔ ŋmɛ'ɛra ba.

ref: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Pebila la en boi na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la en wan dɛna ba dunkiina, wan tari ba kina vom ko'sumɔ kulaa la puan ti ba nyuura, gee ti Yinɛ wan duuhe ba nintam woo ba ninin bahɛ.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
text: Pebila la en lorege daalehum buyopɔi la, la ani la suram tinsuŋan la, paaɛ wuu gogo kaareŋa girego.

ref: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋereba bayopɔi la en ze' Yinɛ nɛŋan la, ti ba bo' ba tintana a yopɔi.

ref: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
text: Ti suŋera ayima zãla zu'uhi nyɔ'a dugela en de salema, wa'am na ze'ele kaabegɔ bimbine la zi'an, ti ba bo' en nyuuŋo suŋɔ digelum zo'e zo'e, ti a dikɛ kaabɛ pa'ahɛ nɛresunduma la woo pu'uhegɔ la, kaabegɔ daka la en de salema la zuo, na'am kuka kãtɛ la nɛŋan.

ref: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
text: Ti nyuuŋo suŋɔ digelum zu'uhi la, la nɛresunduma pu'uhegɔ la ze'ele suŋera la nu'uhen ihegera saazuo, Yinɛ nɛŋan

ref: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
text: Ti suŋera wa dikɛ zu'uhi nyɔ'a dugela wa, mu'ɛ bugum kaabegɔ daka la zuo, pirɛ ka, dikɛ dugela la lobe bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na, ti saa tahega, saa dameŋa, saa nyaka, la tinmiiŋo kɔ'ɔm gbiregera. Tintana ayopɔi

ref: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
text: Ti suŋereba bayopɔi la en zãli tintana ayopɔi la, maahum ti ba peebe tintana la.

text: Ti yia suŋera la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti sakuga la bugum en gɛregɛ la ziim, ti ba ka'ɛ bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na, ti timpaberɛ wa buyina puregerɛ butã puan taaɛ bugum di. Tiihi buyina puregerɛ butã di, ti mɔ ma'aha za'a di.

ref: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
text: Ti yi'i suŋera la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti sɛla en ani wuu zoore kãtɛ la, taaɛ bugum dita ti ba za'ɛ bahɛ n ateko puan na.

ref: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
text: Ti ateko la buyina puregerɛ butã puan, lebege ziim, ti bunvɔɔhi en boi ko'om la puan la buyina puregerɛ butã puan ki, ti uurenɔ en boi ko'om la zuo buyina puregerɛ butã puan, sagum.

ref: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
text: Ti suŋera butã daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti ŋmarebili kãtɛ ze'ele saazuo lu timpaberɛ wa zuo na, nyagera la bugum. Ka me lu la kulaahi la kulehi woo buyina puregerɛ butã puan.

ref: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
text: Ŋmarebila wa yu'urɛ yi'iri la buntoo. Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a ko'om, buyina puregerɛ butã puan tɔna lanlaŋi, ti zo'e zo'e ki, ko'om la nyua puan, bu too la zuo.

ref: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
text: Ti suŋera bu naahi daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti wuntɛɛŋa niihum buyina puregerɛ butã kiŋe, ti ŋmarega niihum buyina puregerɛ butã puan kiŋe, ti ŋmarebibehi me buyina puregerɛ butã puan me kiŋe, ti la bahɛ ti ba woo niihum buyina puregerɛ butã puan kiŋe, ti wuntɛɛŋa ka niira dabeherɛ la buyina puregerɛ butã puan, ti yu'uŋɔ me dɛna bala mɛa.

ref: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
text: Ti mam bihɛ, wum ti tugeho kaaheri kɔkɛ'ɛŋa gee ɛgera saazuo ha, yɛta yeti, “Yele kãtɛ, Yele kãtɛ, Yele kãtɛ boi timpaberɛ wa nɛreba zuo, suŋereba batã wa han peebe ba tintana la ti ba bɔna bini ti ba peebe la!”

ref: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
text: Ti suŋera bunuu daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti mam nyɛ ti ŋmarebila la en ze'ele saazuo ha lu timpaberɛ wa zuo na la, ti ba bo' ka kparehega, ti ka yu'ɛ boko kãtɛ en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha.

ref: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
text: Ti ka yu'ɛ boko la en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha la, ti zu'uhi tu'ura boko kãtɛ la puan na wuu bugum kãtɛ en taaɛ dita zu'uhi la, ti zu'uhi la gu' wuntɛɛŋa, la sabeeo wa.

ref: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
text: Ti sunwa'ara ze'ele zu'sɔbelegɔ wa puan sige timpaberɛ wa zuo na, ti ba bo' ba kpe'eŋo ti ba kpe'eŋo la ana wuu nahi kpe'eŋo la timpaberɛ wa zuo.

ref: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
text: Ti ba yele ba yeti, ba da sagum timpaberɛ wa muɔ, bii bumbula, bii tiihi, gee ba sagum nɛre sɛba woo en ka tari Yinɛ daalehum la ba gbɛ'ɛran.

ref: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
text: Ba bo' ba sore ti ba mɛlehɛ nɛrebana wa taaba ŋmarehi sinuu, gee ba da ku ba. Gee bunduma wa duŋa too ani mɛ wuu naŋa duŋa la, ba han dum nɛra.

ref: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
text: Dabeha aŋa puan, nɛreba wan ɛɛra kum, gee ba kan nyɛ. Ba wan bɔta ti ba ki, gee kum wan ɛkɛ gee bahɛ ba.

ref: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
text: Fum han bihera sunwa'ara wa, ba ani mɛ wuu yire en bagɛ, maahum yɛ'a pɛntɛrɛ la. Ba zutin, sɛla en vuge ba wuu salema muhi la, ti ba nɛhi ana wuu saaleba nɛhi la,

ref: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
text: ti ba zuo zonto ana wuu pɔgehi zuo zonto la, ti ba nyɛna ana wuu gbigema nyɛna la;

ref: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
text: ti sɛla ti'ɛ ba nyu'urɔ wa wuu zaba kuto tɛ'ɛlega la, ti ba kelumpagehi la dameŋa ana wuu zaba yiiri toto kekeko en zoti yɛ'a pɛntɛrɛ damena se'em la.

ref: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
text: Ba tari la zuya wuu nahi la, ti duŋa zi'an bɔna, ti ba paŋa bɔna la ba zuya la puan, ti ba mɛlehɛ nɛreba ŋmarehi sinuu.

ref: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
text: Ba tari la naba ti a biha ba, suŋera se'em en tari tiŋa tiŋa boko kãtɛ la kparehega la. A yu'urɛ Hiburu puan dela Abadon. Giriki puan a yu'urɛ dela Apoliyon.

ref: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
text: Yia namehegɔ la tole ya, bihɛ, namehegɔ buyi' en wan wa'am na.

ref: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
text: Ti suŋera buyoobe daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti mam wum ti kua ze'ele iila a naahi la en boi kaabegɔ salema daka la en ze' Yinɛ nɛŋa la,

ref: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
text: yɛta suŋera buyoobe daana la n tari tintanɛ la yeti, “Lorege suŋereba banaahi la ti ba bobe ze'ele Yufaratesi kulaa kãtɛ la zi'an la bahɛ.”

ref: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
text: Ban bahɛ suŋereba banaahi la se'em n bala. Suŋereba sɛba ti ba gurɛ ba ze'ele gura sansɛka, dabeherɛ se'ere, ŋmare sɛka, la yuunɛ se'ere, ti ba ku nɛresaaleba buyina puregerɛ butã puan timpaberɛ wa zuo la.

ref: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
text: Mam nyɛ yire la se'em lilegere la puan en ŋwana. Yire la ba'areba la yɛ la tɛ'ɛlega kuto futo n mu'ɛ wuu bugum, la tuulum kuge sɔbele puuhego, kuge dɔboolum, ti yire wa zuto ana wuu gbigema zuto la, ti bugum la zu'uhi la bugum peelum yɛhera ba nɔyan na.

ref: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
text: Tooro tuna tutã' wa en ku timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba buyina puregerɛ butã. Bugum la, zu'uhi la, la bugum peelum la en ze'eti ba nɔyan yɛhera na la.

ref: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, yire wa paŋa boi la ba nɔyan la ba zuyan. Ba zuya la ani mɛ woo bunsɛlenduma la, tara zuto, ti ba tara ba mɛleha saaleba la.

ref: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
text: Gee nɛre sɛba woo en gee, ti too kuna wa ka ku ba la, ka makɛ tee ba mɛhi ba nu'uhi en iti sɛla puan, bii ba bahɛ ba na'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, la baga ti ba dikɛ salema, salempɛɛlega, la nyɔgum, la kuga, la dɔɔrɔ maalɛ ba ti ba ka nyɛta, bii wuna, bii kina la.

ref: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
text: Ba me ka tee ba mɛhi ba nɛre kua la, bii ba sɔ̃niniduma la, bii ba yaluntuuma, bii ba nageyigum la puan.

ref: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera paŋa daana ayima ze'ele saazuo ha sigera na, ti sagbama pi' en, ti gumatia tahɔ yula a zuin, ti a nɛŋa nyagera wuu wuntɛɛŋa la, ti a nama ana wuu bugum la.

ref: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
text: Ti a zãla gɔŋɔ pika a nu'en ti ku yu'ɛ. Ti a dikɛ a zuɔ na'arɛ tagelɛ ko'kãtɛ wa zuo, gee ti a gɔbega na'arɛ bɔna doone na, tinkɛ'ɛŋa zuo.

ref: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
text: Ti a tahɛ la paŋa wuu gbigenɛ en taheri la. Ti en tahɛ la, ti saahi siyopɔi uhe bahɛ na.

ref: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
text: Ti saahi siyopɔi la en uhe bahɛ na la, ti mam bɔna bini ti n gulehɛ, gee mam wum la kua saazuo na en yeti, “Sugɛ saahi siyopɔi wa en yele sɛla la, gee da gulehɛ de biŋe.”

ref: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
text: Ti suŋera sɛka ti mam nyɛ ti a na'arɛ deyima bɔna ko'kãtɛ puan ti deyima bɔna doone na la, Zɛkɛ a zuɔ nu'o tɔ saazuo ha,

ref: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
text: gee pɔ se'em en boi wuuha wuuha ha wa se'em en naam saazuo la sɛla woo en boi ba puan ha, la timpaberɛ wa la sɛla woo en boi ka zuo, la ko'kãtɛ la puan, la sɛla woo en boi bu puan yeti, “ Gu'uŋɔ ka le bɔna,

ref: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
text: gee dabeha sɛ'a ti suŋera buyopɔi daana la wan peebe a tintanɛ la, Yinɛ yele se'ere en sugɛ la wan wa'am yelemɛŋerɛ na, wuu en muulɛ de se'em doohe a yamehi la en de nɔtɔgeheba puan se'em la.”

ref: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
text: Ti kua sɛka ti mam pun wum ni ti ka tɔgɛ saazuo bahɛ na la, le tɔgɛ bo' mam na yeti, “ Kiŋɛ la fum ta to'e gɔŋɔ la en lakɛ bɔna suŋera se'em na'arɛ deyima en boi ko'kãtɛ la puan ti deyima me bɔna doone la zi'an.”

ref: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
text: Bala ti mam kiŋɛ suŋera wa zi'an, ta yele en ti a bo' mam gɔŋɔ pika la, ti a yele mam yeti, “To'e la fum obe. Ku wan tɔna fum puuren, gee za'ahum wuu seero la fum nuuren.”

ref: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
text: Bala ti mam to'e gɔŋɔ pika la suŋera la nu'en obe ku, ti ku za'ahum wuu seero la mam nuuren, gee mam obe ku vole la, ku tɔi mɛ mam puuren ha.

ref: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
text: Ti ba nyaa yele mam yeti, “ Fum dela fum le purɛ bahɛ nɛreba kekeko zuo, la tihi, la yetɔgum buuri buuri, la na'duma zuto.”

ref: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
text: gee da makɛ zinzaka la en boi pu'uhegɔ yire la yiŋa ha la. Bahɛ bilam, se'ere en sɔi la, ba dikɛ bilam bo' la sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri nɛreba, ti ba niira tuulum tiŋa wa puan ŋmarehi pihinaahi la siyi.

ref: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
text: Bana bayi' wa en de olive tiihi siyi, la butolela ze'elehi siyi la en ze' Daana se'em en sɔi timpaberɛ wa nɛŋan la.

ref: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
text: Ti se'em han yeti a daam ba, bugum wan ze'ele ba nɔyan na di ba dinduma. Se'em woo en yeti a daam ba, wan ki la kum buna taaba.

ref: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
text: Ba tari kpe'eŋo n wan yu' sagbama ti saa da ni, ba purega saŋa la puan, gee me tara kpe'eŋo la ko'om woo, wan ta'am bahɛ ti ko'om woo lebege ziim, gee bahɛ ti tooro yima yima to'e timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, bamam en boti se'em ma'a.

ref: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
text: Ba han purɛ ba yelehum la ba'ahɛ, bunsilemasio la en ze'ele tiŋa tiŋa yoko la puan dooe na la, wan kɔ' zaberɛ la ba, nyaŋɛ ba, ku ba,

ref: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
text: ti ba ki ti ba ihi la gã'a sore zuo, tinkãtɛ sɛka ti ba ze'ele ti ba yi'ira ka Sodom la Igipiti la, zi'an ti ba tabelɛ kpa' ba Daana la bini la.

ref: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
text: Dabeha atã la girego, ti nɛreba, la ba suurɔ, buuri yima yima, la tugum yima yima, la tihi nɛreba wan kina limeha kum la, gee zagehɛ ti ba kan pi' ba yɔɔ seko puan.

ref: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
text: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba wan nyɛɛna la ba kum la, bɔ'ɔra taaba bo'olum, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔtɔgeheba bana ba yi' wa daa ba kpa' la timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba za'a nyɔya.

ref: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
text: Gee dabeha atã la girego la en tole la, vo'ohum n tari vom en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na kɛ̃ ba, ti ba ihege, ti sɛba woo en nyɛ ba la, dabeem kãtɛ en kɛ̃ ba.

ref: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
text: Ti ba wum ti kua kɛ'ɛŋa ze'ele saazuo na yele ba yeti, “ Zom ya ka'lam na!” Ti ba zɔna saazuo sagbanɛ puan, ba dinduma la ninin.

ref: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
text: Sankaŋa puan, ti tinmiiŋo kãtɛ damɛ, ti tiŋa la yiya puregerɛ pia lu, ti nɛreba tuha ayopɔi ki, tinmiiŋo la puan, ti dabeem kɛ̃ sɛba en yee la, ti ba dikɛ pɛka bo' Yinɛ en sɔi saazuo ha la.

ref: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
text: Yi'i yele la tole ya. Bihɛ ta'a yele la me kɔ'ɔm yɛ'a la nananawa na. Tintanɛ buyopɔi

ref: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
text: Ti suŋera buyopɔi daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti kuuhi kekeko ze'ele saazuo ha vɔɔla baha na yeti, “Tiŋa zuo sɔ'ɔlum la nyaa dela tumam Daana la dinɛ la a Kiresta la dinɛ, eŋa en wan dɛna ka naba, wuuha wuuha.”

ref: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
text: yuuna yeti, “Tu bo' fum fara fara, Gani za'a Yinɛ wa, Se'em en de, gee daa pun dɛna, Se'ere en sɔi la, fum dikɛ la fum pankãtɛ la, pɔhɛ bihera tiŋa wa

ref: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
text: Tihi se'ehi en ka dole fum ihege la suure, gee fum leregere saŋa en ŋwana. Saŋa la paaɛ ya ti fum gakɛ sɛba woo en ki la, la fum yɔ fum yamehi la en de fum nɔtɔgeheba la, la fum nɛresunduma la, la sɛba woo en daa pu'uheri fum yu'urɛ la, ba tarenduma, la kpɛ'ɛnduma za'a, gee me sagum sɛba woo en daa sagum timpaberɛ wa la.”

ref: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
text: Ti Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la en boi saazuo ha la yu'ɛ, ti nɔpu'ulegɔ daka la zɛ'a pu'uhegɔ deo la puan, ti bugum nyagera, ti dameŋa damena, ti saa tahera, ti tiŋa miina, ti sakuga kãra ka'ara na.

ref: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
text: Ti pɔka wa tara puurɛ, virege kaaha la too, kɔ'ɔm wula ti ka dɔgɛ.

ref: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
text: Ti daalehum kãtɛ bu yima me niiɛ saazuo ha, ti bunsɛla kãtɛ mɔlegɔ kuyima niiɛ na, tara zuto tu yopɔi, la iila pia, vuge muhi siyopɔi zuo kuyina kuyina woo.

ref: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
text: Ti a zuurɛ ve'e ŋmarebibehi buyina puregerɛ butã puan saazuo ha bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na. Ti bunsɛla wa nyaa zɛ'a pɔka wa en boi bini ti ka dɔgɛ wa nɛŋan, ti a obe bia la, ka han kɔ'ɔm lu na.

ref: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
text: Ti pɔka la dɔgɛ bi budibela en yeti ka dɛna tihi wa za'a naba, la ka tara kuto doore biha ka nɛreba. Gee bia wa en ta luta na la, ba zɛ ka mɛ tɔ', tari ka kiŋɛ Yinɛ zi'an, Yinɛ na'am kuka la zuo.

ref: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
text: Gee ti pɔka la zɔ kɛ̃ muuen ha, ta nyɛ zi'an ti Yinɛ pun maahum biŋe ka, ti ka bɔna bilam ti ba biha ka suŋa suŋa ta paaɛ dabeha tuhere, la dabeha kɔbehi siyi, la dabeha pihiyoobe. Maikɛl nyaŋɛ bunsɛla la

ref: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
text: Ti zaberɛ kɔ' Yinɛ yire ha, ti Maikɛl, la Yinɛ suŋereba la zabera la bunsɛla la. Ti bunsɛla la, la a suŋereba la me zabera lɛbeha na,

ref: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
text: gee ba daa nyaŋɛ ba mɛ, ti zi'an ka le bɔna tinsuŋan la ha bo' ba.

ref: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
text: Ti ba lobe bunsɛla kãtɛ wa bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na. Se'em en de diimen diimen zugɔ la ti ba yi'ira Sagena la Sitaani, se'em en pa'aheri tiŋa wa zuo za'a nɛreba la. Ba lobe en bahɛ la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, lagum lobe a suŋereba la, la en bahɛ na.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
text: Ti mam wum kɔkɛ'ɛŋa saazuo la ha en muuli yeti, “Nananawa ti Faarɛ, la paŋa, la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la, la Kiresta, kpe'eŋo la saŋa nyaa paaɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba lobe se'em en zuuheri tu suurɔ la, bahɛ mɛ. Se'em en daa zuuheri ba bulika la yu'uŋɔ tu Yinɛ wa nɛŋan la.

ref: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
text: Gee ba nyaŋɛ en mɛ la Pebila la ziim la, la ba nuurɛ yetɔgum, se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa ka fabele ba vom yele, la kum nɛŋan me.

ref: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
text: Bala zuo, nyɛɛna, tinsuŋan wa, la yamam sɛba en boi bini la. Gee yele kãtɛ boi timpaberɛ wa, la ko'kãtɛ wa zuo, la Sagena la en sige ya puan na, la sunsunyiim kãtɛ la, Se'ere en sɔi la, a mi' ti a saŋa ka wugɛ.” Bunsɛla la zabɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo

ref: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
text: Bunsɛla wa en ta baŋɛ ti ba lobe en bahɛ la timpaberɛ wa zuo na la, ti a pɔhɛ pɔka la en dɔgɛ bibudibela la dika.

ref: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
text: Gee ba bo' pɔka wa la tugeho kãtɛ kelumpagehi siyi, ti ka ɛkɛ piiɛ bunsɛla la, kɛ̃ muuen ha, ta bɔna zi'an ti ka wan bɔna ti ba biha en suŋa suŋa ta paaɛ saŋa, la sahi la saŋa girego.

ref: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
text: Ti bunsɛla wa ɔkɛ ko'om a nuuren na, ti bu zɔta wuu kulega la, digera pɔka wa, ti bu di pɔka wa.

ref: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
text: Gee timpaberɛ en suŋɛ pɔka la, doohe la tiŋa la ya'ɛ mɛ vole kulaa la ti bunsɛla la ɔkɛ a nuuren bahɛ na la.

ref: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
text: Ti bunsɛla la suure nyiie la pɔka la, ti a lebe, ta kɔ' zaberɛ la pɔka la kɔma basɛba la, sɛba en dole Yinɛ nuurɛ, gee tari Yezu yɛla la.

ref: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
text: Ti bunsɛla la ba' a mɛŋa ze'ele ko'kãtɛ tintombi'ihego la puan.

ref: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti bunsilemasio ihege ko'kãtɛ wa puan, tara iila pia, la zuto tuyopɔi, ti muŋa vuge iilɛ woo, gee ti ba gulehɛ tuurɛ yu'ura labelɛ a zuo woo.

ref: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
text: Bunsilemasio sɛka ti mam nyɛ la ani mɛ wuu yeeobaa la, ti a nama ana wuu saheŋa nama, ti a nuurɛ ana wuu gbigenɛ nuurɛ la. Ti bunsɛla la bo' en a paŋa, la a na'am la, la a kpe'eŋo kãtɛ la.

ref: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
text: Ti bunsilemasio wa zuo la kuyima ana wuu ba pɔgelum mɛ yɛ'a kum, gee ti pɔgeleŋɔ kɛka la wa'am na bɛ'e. Ti timpaberɛ wa nɛreba za'a dɔla bunsilemasio wa la pakerɛ.

ref: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
text: Ba na'aheri bunsɛla la mɛ, la eŋa en dikɛ a kpe'eŋo kãtɛ la bo' bunsilemasio la, gee na'aha bunsilemasio la me yeti, “Ani en boi ana wuu bunsilemasio wa, ani en boi wan ta'am zabɛ la ka?”

ref: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
text: Ti bunsɛla wa ya'ɛ a nuurɛ tuura Yinɛ, tuura a yu'urɛ la, la a tanɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la, la sɛba woo en boi tinsuŋan ha la.

ref: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
text: Ti ba me le bo' en sore ti a zabɛ la nɛresunduma la nyaŋɛ ba. Ba bo' en la kpe'eŋo ti a sɔna buuri woo, la nɛreba woo, la tugum woo la tihi woo.

ref: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
text: Ti nɛreba woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo wan na'aha en. Se'em woo yu'urɛ en ka gulehɛ iŋɛ vom gɔŋɔ la gee ti ba baŋɛ naam timpaberɛ wa la, gɔŋɔ wa dela pebila wa gɔŋɔ, pebila sɛka ti ba kɔregɛ la.

ref: If you have ears, then listen!
text: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha.

ref: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
text: La han dɛna la ba nyɔgɛ ya, ba nyɔgere ya yɛ'a mɛ, ya han ku mɛ la su'a, su'a en kuuri ya. Ŋwana dela sɛba en de nɛresunduma la, ya ŋmibera, gee sɛ zɛ'a ya sakerɛ la puan. Bunsilemasio buyi' daana

ref: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
text: Ti mam le nyɛ ti bunsilemasio kayima me tahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo yehe na, tara iila ayi' wuu pebila iila la, ti a kua ana wuu bunsɛla la.

ref: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
text: Ti a tum yia bunsilemasio la pahi la za'a bo' yia bunsilemasio la, bahɛ ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba za'a, na'aha yia bunsilemasio la, se'em nɔre-kɛka en be'ɛ la.

ref: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
text: Ti yi'i bunsilemasio wa tum kirehi kãra, ta pun bahɛ ti bugum ze'ele saazuo ha lu timpaberɛ wa zuo na, timpaberɛ wa nɛreba za'a ninin.

ref: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
text: Ti doohe la kirehi wa ti ba bo' sore ti a ze'ele yia bunsilemasio la na'arɛ tum bo' en la, ti a nyaŋɛ pã'ahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba za'a, yɛta ba yeti, ba maalɛ yia bunsilemasio la ti su'a pɔgelum en gee ti a sina gee vɔna la naanɛ,

ref: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
text: ti bunsilemasio wa bo' naanɛ la vo'ohum, ti naanɛ wa pun tana tɔgera, gee kuura sɛba en ka sakɛ ba na'aha bunsilemasio wa naanɛ la.

ref: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
text: Gee ti naanɛ wa doohe nɛra woo, bumbila la bunkutɛ, buntata la naha, se'em en boi fai, la da'aberɛ za'a daalɛ ba zuɔ nu'uhi, bii ba gbɛ'ɛra,

ref: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
text: ti se'em ka bɔna wan ta'am da' bii koohe sɛla gee ka tara bunsilemasio la daalehum la . Daalehum la en de bunsilemasio la yu'urɛ, bii a yu'urɛ la kãlɛ.

ref: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
text: Yɛm en nari ka'lam. Bahɛ ti se'em en tari yɛm lagum bunsilemasio wa kãlɛ la, se'ere en sɔi la, de dela nɛresaala kãlɛ. Kãlɛ la dela kɔbehi siyoobe, la pihiyoobe la buyoobe.

ref: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
text: Ti mam wum ti kua ze'ele tinsuŋan na, sũna wuu kulaahi ko'om en sũni la, gee tahera wuu saa en taheri la. Kua la ti mam wum la ani mɛ wuu kɔ̃len-ŋmɛ'ɛreba en ŋmɛ'ɛri ba kɔlɔ la.

ref: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
text: Ti ba yuuna yuum paalɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la bunvɔɔhi banaahi la kiinduma la nɛŋan. Ti nɛra ka bɔna mina yuunɛ la, han dagena tuha kɔbega la tuha pinaahi la anaahi la ma'a ti ba yɔ to'e ba timpaberɛ wa zuo la.

ref: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
text: Nɛrebana wa ka galum ba mɛhi la pɔgehi, se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa dela burakaha. Bana en dole Pebila la zi'an woo ti ka yɛ'a. Ba yɔ to'e ba mɛ saaleba woo puan ti ba dɛna wala diina bo' Yinɛ, la Pebila la,

ref: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
text: ti pumpɔreŋɔ ka tabelɛ ze'ele ba nɔyan na, ba ka tari gbelegere zi'an. Suŋereba batã yelehum

ref: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera ayima ɛgeri sagbama wa zuo, tara faarɛ yelehum la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la, ti a muulɛ bo' sɛba en vɔi timpaberɛ wa zuo, tiŋa woo, buuri woo, la tugum woo, la nɛreba woo.

ref: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
text: Ti a yele la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “Zoti ya Yinɛ, gee bo' ya en pɛka, se'ere en sɔi la, a gakerɛ saŋa la paaɛ ya. Me na'ahɛ ya en, eŋa se'em en naam saazuo la tiŋa, ko'kãtɛ, la kulaahi, ko'om zi'ihi woo la.”

ref: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
text: Ti suŋera ayima, en pa'ahɛ buyi' la, me dɔla yeti, “A lu ya, Babilɔn paŋadaana la lu ya! A bahɛ ti tihi woo nyu a yaalegɔ sunsunyiim daam la mɛ.”

ref: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
text: Ti suŋera ayima, butã daana me dɔla na, kaaha la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “Sɛba woo en na'ahɛ bunsilemasio la, la ka naanɛ la, gee to'e ka daalehum ba gbɛ'ɛran bii ba nu'uhen la,

ref: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
text: ba me wan nyu Yinɛ sunsunyiim daam la en ka'ɛ iŋɛ Yinɛ sunsunyiim wula puan, ti sɛla ka gɛregɛ bɔ la. Ba wan nyɛ namehegɔ bugum puan, la tahi bugum puan, Fa'a suŋereba la nɛŋan, la Pebila la nɛŋan.

ref: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
text: Ti ba namehegɔ zu'uhi kɔ'ɔm gulegera zɔna saazuo ka tara ba'ahegɔ. Vo'ohego ka boi, beyiine, bii yu'uŋɔ bɔ'ɔra sɛba en na'ahɛ bunsilemasio la, la a naanɛ la, la se'em woo en to'e bunsilemasio la daalehum la en de a yu'urɛ la.”

ref: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
text: Ŋwana yi'iri la nɛresunduma la ti ba ŋmibe, la sɛba woo en tari Yinɛ nuurɛ la, gee gurɛ Yezu sakerɛ la kankaŋi la.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
text: Ti mam wum kua tinsuŋan la, ti ka yeti, “Gulehɛ dina wa biŋe yeti, Yehe zina yɛ'a ha, Sum wan bɔna la sɛba woo en ki Daana la sore dɔlega puan.” Sia la yeti, “Ɛ̃ɛ, Ba wan vo'ohe ba namehegɔ la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ba tuuma la wan dɔla ba.” Timpaberɛ wa buŋa

ref: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
text: Ti mam bihɛ nyɛ ti sagbanɛ peele niiɛ na, ti se'em en zi' de zuo la wuna la Asaala Dayua la, ti salema muŋa vuge a zuo, ti kɛɛrega en diti bɔna a nu'en!

ref: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
text: Ti suŋera ayima ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la puan yehe na, yi' se'em daana en yage sagbanɛ la zuo la, la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “Dikɛ fum kɛɛrega la kɛ̃, se'ere en sɔi la kɛ'a saŋa la paaɛ ya. Beni zuo, timpaberɛ wa zuo dia la bi'ɛ mɛ.”

ref: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
text: Bala ti se'em en zi sagbanɛ la zuo la, lobege a kɛɛrega la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, kɛ̃ timpaberɛ wa zuo filam.

ref: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
text: Ti suŋera ayima le ze'ele tinsuŋan pu'uhegɔ yire la puan yehe na, me zãla kɛɛrega n diti.

ref: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
text: Ti suŋera ayima me ze'ele nyɔ'a daka la bɔba yehe na, ti suŋera sɛka en tari bugum paŋa la, yi' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa bo' se'em en zãli kɛɛrega la en diti la yeti, “Dikɛ fum kɛɛrega la en diti la kɛ̃ tiihi wala timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, se'ere en sɔi la, si wala la bi'ɛ mɛ.”

ref: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
text: Ti suŋera la lobege a kɛɛrega la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, kɛ̃ tiihi wala za'a, ka'ɛ ba iŋɛ daam ŋmɛkerɛ boko kãtɛ en de Yinɛ sunsunyiim daam boko la puan .

ref: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
text: Daam boko wa me boi la tiŋa la yiŋa ha, ti ziim ze'ele daam ŋmɛkerɛ boko wa puan ɔkera, paara yeeho gareŋenɛ, zɔta yɛ'a wuu miila kɔbehi siyi.

ref: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
text: Ti mam nyɛ yele tinsuŋan ha, yele n karegɛ gee yalegɛ nuurɛ. Suŋereba bayopɔi en tari namehegɔ tuyopɔi, en de ba'ahegɔ namehegɔ. Bama en wan tum ba'ahɛ Yinɛ sunsunyiim la.

ref: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti sɛla niiɛ na, ana wuu ateko la, gee dɛna salema nyagera, ti bugum gɛregɛ ko'om la puan. Ti sɛba en nyaŋɛ bunsilemasio la, ka pu'uhɛ a naanɛ la, ka to'e a yu'urɛ la, la a kãlɛ la, nyaa zɛ'a salema ateko wa nuuren, pɛga kɔlɔ ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' ba.

ref: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
text: Ti ba nyaa yuuna Moose en de Yinɛ yameŋa yuunɛ la, la Pebila la yuunɛ la yeti, “Fum tuuma karegɛ mɛ gee dɛna pakerɛ, Daana Yinɛ kãtɛ wa. Fum sadɔtɔ za'a dela tuuntɔɔ la yelemɛŋerɛ, tihi wa za'a naba wa.

ref: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
text: Daana, ani en kan zɔta fum, gee na'aha fum yu'urɛ wa? Se'ere en sɔi la, fum ma'a en de Fa'a. Tihi za'a wan wa'am fum nɛŋan na, na'aha hɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, fum tuuntɔɔ tuuma la za'a niiɛ mɛ na.”

ref: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
text: Dina pooren ti mam bihɛ, ti pu'uhegɔ yire wa tanɛ deo la en de aze'nyɛ deo bɔna tinsuŋan la yu'ɛ,

ref: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
text: ti tanɛ deo wa puan, ti suŋereba bayopɔi la ze'ele bini yehe na la bã'ahi siyopɔi, yɛ fupeelo n nyagere, ti salema tama paga ba nyu'urɔ zuto.

ref: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
text: Ti bunvɔɔhi banaahi la ayima, bo' suŋereba bayopɔi wa, salema laahi siyopɔi en pirɛ la Yinɛ sunsunyiim, Yinɛ se'ere en vɔi wuuha wuuha la.

ref: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
text: Ti pu'uhegɔ yire la pirɛ la zu'uhi en ze'eti Yinɛ suŋa la puan na, la a paŋa la puan na, ti se'em ka bɔna wan kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, gura ti bã'ahi siyopɔi la ti suŋereba bayopɔi la tara la ta tum ba'ahɛ.

ref: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
text: Ti mam wum kɔkɛ'ɛŋa pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ti ka tɔgera bɔ'ɔra suŋereba bayopɔi la yeti, “Kiŋɛ ya ta ka'ahɛ Yinɛ sunsunyiim laahi siyopɔi la bahɛ timpaberɛ la zuo.”

ref: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
text: Ti yia suŋera la kiŋɛ ta ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ timpaberɛ la zuo, ti nɔtankɛ'ɛhi la nɔre-kãra en dune paaɛ paaɛ sige sɛba woo en tari bunsɛla la daalehum la, la sɛba woo en pu'uheri bunsilemasio la naanɛ la zuo.

ref: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
text: Ti yi'i suŋera la me kiŋɛ ta ka'ɛ a laa la, bahɛ ateko puan, ti ateko la lebege ana wuu kum ziim la, ti bunvua woo en boi ateko la puan ki.

ref: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
text: Ti ta'a suŋera la wa'am na ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ mɔga, la kulaahi puan, ti ba ko'om la lebege ziim.

ref: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
text: Ti mam wum ti suŋera sɛka en biheri ko'om la yeti, “Fum dela sira Daana, Fa'a Daana wa, Se'em en de, gee daa pun Dɛna, Se'ere en sɔi la fum gakɛ yele ana wa mɛ.

ref: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, bamam en daa kɔrum nɛresunduma la, nɔtɔgeheba la. Fum bo' ba la ziim ti ba nyu. Sɛla en nari ba en bala.”

ref: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
text: Ti mam wum ti kaabegɔ bimbine la kɔkɛ yeti, “Ɛ̃ɛ, Daana Yinɛ, Gani za'a Daana, Fum gakerɛ wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ la tuuntɔɔ!”

ref: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
text: Ti suŋera bunaahi daana la ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ yintule zuo, ti ba bo' yintule la sore ti a taaɛ nɛreba la bugum.

ref: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
text: Ti wuntɛɛntuulega sirum si'ira ba, ti ba tugum ŋmɛregɛ ka'ara be'em baha Yinɛ yu'urɛ la, se'em en tari namehegɔ yele ana wa paŋa la, gee tugum ka tee ba suya, dikɛ pɛka bo' en.

ref: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
text: Ti suŋera bunuu daana la ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ bunsilemasio la sɔ'ɔlum la puan, ti a sɔ'ɔlum la lebege lika ma'a, ti nɛreba duna ba zɛlehi la wuŋerɛ,

ref: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
text: gee ka'ara be'em baha Yinɛ se'ere en sɔi saazuo wa yu'urɛ la, ba wuŋerɛ la, la ba nɔra kãra la zuo, gee la bala za'a, ba ka tee ba tuumbe'ero la puan.

ref: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
text: Ti suŋera buyoobe daana la ka'ɛ a laa bahɛ Yufaratesi mɔgekãtɛ la puan, ti de ko'om la kiihɛ za'a, ti la ta'am maalɛ sore bo' dapɔya bɔba naduma la.

ref: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti sɛfi'iha sitã, en ani wuu lono la, ti kayima ze'ele bunsɛla la nuuren yehe na, ti kayima ze'ele bunsilemasio la nuuren yehe na, ti kayima ze'ele nɔtɔgeha pumparema la nuuren yehe na.

ref: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
text: Bana wa dela kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi se'ehi en tuna kirehi, kina timpaberɛ wa zuo na'kãreba nɛŋan, ti ba lagehɛ ba gura zaberɛ, dabeherɛ kãtɛ la en de Gani Za'a Yinɛ dabeherɛ la puan.

ref: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
text: Ba wan lagehɛ la zi'an ti ba yi'ira Hiburu puan ti Hamagedɔn la.

ref: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
text: Ti suŋera buyopɔi daana la ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ sabeeo puan, ti kɔkɛ'ɛŋa ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo na yeti, “La tum ya.”

ref: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
text: Ti tinkãtɛ la bɔgɛ butã, ti tihi woo tinkãra wɔ̃rɛ. Ti Yinɛ tiɛ tinkãtɛ Babilɔn yele, bo' en daam wanɛ en de a sunsunyiim kãtɛ daam .

ref: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
text: Ti tihi se'ehi en boi ko'kãra tiŋasuka la mɛlum, ti zoore la deyina me ka bɔna,

ref: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
text: Ti suŋereba bayopɔi la puan ayima, sɛba en zãli laahi siyopɔi la, wa'am na yele mam yeti, “Wa'ana ti n pa'alɛ hɔ, yiyɛkɛka la en ki'ire lɛm kulaahi wa ti Yinɛ wan ta sagum en se'em.

ref: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
text: Se'em ti timpaberɛ wa zuo na'duma tum yaluntuuma la en, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba tum yaalum tuuma la en ta buge wuu ba nyu la daam la.”

ref: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
text: Lilegere wa puan, ti suŋera wa tari mam sia puan, ta kɛ̃ muuen ha, ti mam ta nyɛ ti pɔka yage bunsilemasio mɔlegɔ zuo, ti tuurɛ yu'ura ma'a gulehɛ lama duŋa la iŋa za'a. Ti duŋa la tara zuto tuyopɔi, la iila pia.

ref: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
text: Ti pɔka wa bagɛ ka mɛŋa la fumɔlɔ en gulɛ la salema, bãhi, la peranini, ti a nu'en a zãli la salema wula, ti ka pirɛ la tuunfi'iha, la a yaalegɔ la dɛgerɔ ma'a.

ref: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
text: Ti yu'urɛ en de sugelum yu'urɛ, gulehɛ lama pɔka la gbi'iri yeti, “Babilɔn kãtɛ la en de yiyɛkɛka la, Yaalegɔ za'a ma la ti timpaberɛ wa za'a galeŋɔ lɔkɔ.”

ref: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
text: Ti mam nyɛ ti pɔka wa buge mɛ la en nyu Yinɛ nɛresunduma ziim, la sɛba en muuli Yezu yele la sukpe'ene la ziim la. Mam en nyɛ ka la, la ba' iŋɛ mam la pakerɛ.

ref: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
text: Gee suŋera la yele mam yeti,“Beni ti la ba' iŋɛ hɔ pakerɛ bala?” N wan yele fum pɔka wa sugelum yele, la duŋa la en tara zuto tuyopɔi, la iila pia ti ka yaga ka zuo la.

ref: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
text: Bunsilemasio la ti fum nyɛ ni la, daa vɔi mɛ, gee ki, gee nyaa bɔna bini ti ku ze'ele boko kãtɛ la en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha la dooe na, ti ba sagum en. Ti sɛba woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo ti ba yu'ura ka gulehɛ bɔna vom gɔŋɔ la puan tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan na la, la wan ta iŋɛ ba pakerɛ ba han le ta nyɛ bunsilemasio la, beni zuo, ku daa boi mɛ, gee ki, gee le yeti ku vo'e na.

ref: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
text: “Putɛ'se'ere en tari bɔkerɛ n wan baŋɛ dina wa. Zuto tuyopɔi la dela zɔya ayopɔi ti pɔge yiyɛa wa yaga a zuo, gee me ze'ele bo' naduma bayopɔi,

ref: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
text: ti ba puan banuu ki, ti ayima vɔna, gee ti ayima la nan ka wa'am na. Gee a han ta wa'am na, a wan bɔna la dabeha fii wa.

ref: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
text: Gee bunsilemasio seko en daa boi gee ki la, eŋa pa'aheri la banii, lagum pa'ahɛ bayopɔi la ti ba yeti ba sagum ba la puan.

ref: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
text: Gee iila pia la me ti fum nyɛ la dela, naduma pia en nan ka to'e sɔ'ɔlum biha, gee ba wan to'e na'am kpe'eŋo, gogo kaareŋa buyina ma'a, tum la bunsilemasio la.

ref: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
text: Bana za'a iŋɛ la puti'irɛ deyina, ti ba dikɛ ba paŋa, la ba kpe'eŋo bo' bunsilemasio la.

ref: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
text: Ba wan kɔ' zaberɛ la Pebila la, gee Pebila la wan nyaŋɛ ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, Pebila la en de Daanduma Daana, la Naduma naba. Ti sɛba en boi la en la, a yi' ba mɛ, gee loohe ba ti ba dɛna sakereba.”

ref: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
text: Ti suŋera la yele mam yeti, “Ko'om la ti fum nyɛ ni sebo ti yiyɛkɛka la zi' ni bu zuo la, ze'ele bo' la timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba, nɛrebɔbegɔ, tihi, la tugum.

ref: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
text: Ti iila pia la ti fum nyɛ ni la, bamam la bunsilemasio la wan ta yaaɛ yiyɛkɛka wa, mugehe en ta bahɛ en beele, ba wan aahum a iŋa obe, gee nyɔ' en bugumen.

ref: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en lu' ba suya puan, ti ba tum eŋa en boti se'em la, doohe ba dikɛ ba sɔ'ɔlum bo' bunsilemasio la, gura ti Yinɛ yelehum la ta wa'am na tum.

ref: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
text: Pɔka la ti fum nyɛ la ze'ele bo' la tinkãtɛ sɛka en sɔi naduma woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo biha la.”

ref: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
text: Lilegere dina en tole la, ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera ayima ze'ele saazuo sigera na tara kpe'eŋo en ka tari makerɛ. Ti timpaberɛ wa za'a niiɛ piim la a suŋa.

ref: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
text: Ti suŋera wa yi' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, “Wai wai, a lu ya, a lu ya, Babilɔn en de tinkãtɛ la lu ya! Ka nyaa lebege la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi bɔŋa zi'an, Ka dela kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi se'ehi en fi'ɛ bɔŋa zi'an. Ka dela niihe se'ehi en fi'ɛ bɔŋa zi'an, la bunsɛlebɛ'ɛhi bɔŋa zi'an.

ref: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, a bahɛ ti tihi woo en nyu a yaalegɔ sunsunyiim daam la, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma tum yaluntuuma la en. Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo leebeduma pãrɛ la a tarega la paŋa.”

ref: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
text: Ti mam wum ti kua ka yima ze'ele saazuo na yeti, “Yehe ya a puan na n nɛreba wa, la ya da lagum tum a be'em la, ta lagum to'e a namehegɔ la.

ref: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
text: A be'em la duurum mɛ wuu zoore la, paara saazuo ha. Yinɛ tiɛ a tuure kãra la mɛ.

ref: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
text: Bo' en wuu eŋa en bo' se'em la, lebehe yɔ a be'em la sugum nuuren buyi'. Pirɛ a wanɛ sugum nuuren buyi' la daam kpe'eŋo bo' en wuu eŋa en pirɛ se'em bo' la.

ref: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
text: Wuu eŋa en daa pa'ali a mɛŋa se'em, dɛla se'em fai a na'am puan la, lebehe namehegɔ la susageŋɔ bala mɛa bo' en. Wuu en diŋɛ yele a suuren ti, ‘N dela pɔge naba, n dagi pokuurɛ, susageŋɔ kan malum paaɛ mam la.’

ref: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
text: Bala zuo, la kan yuue la a namehegɔ en sigeri a zuo na; nɔyalegerɛ, la kaahega la kom pɛɛlega. Bugum wan taaɛ en di ta bugehe bugehe. Se'ere en sɔi la, Kpe'eŋo daana la, Daana Yinɛ la en gakeri en la.”

ref: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
text: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma sɛba en tuni yaluntuuma la en, gee dɛla fai a puan la, wan kɛla gee si'ina a zuo, ba han ta nyɛ en diti bugum ti a zu'uhi gulegera la.

ref: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
text: Ba wan zɛ'a zaaɛ zaarɛ ha ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba, la namehegɔ seko en sigeri a zuo la. Ba wan yɛta yeti, “Wai, wai, tinkãtɛ la, Babilɔn tinkãtɛ la. Nananawa ma'a ti gakerɛ dina sige fum zuo ŋwana na.”

ref: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
text: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo leebeduma wan kɛla gee si'ina a zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra ka le da'ara ba lɔgerɔ la.

ref: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
text: Ba lɔgerɔ la en de salema, salempɛɛlega, nunɔm zɔtɔ la peranini, tanpɛɛla, tanmɔla, tansaala, tansaalehi mɔlɔ, la dɔsunɔ yima yima, la wɔbenyɛna lɔgerɔ yima yima, la dɔsunɔ lɔgerɔ yima yima, nyɔgum, kuto, la bɔrumbɔlehi kuga,

ref: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
text: sinamɔn tiihi kaam, puuro seto en tari nyɛlum, digelum, la Mɛɛ, nyuuŋo suŋɔ puuro, daam, olive kaam, kisuma zom la mui, nii, piihi, yiiri la yiiri toto, la nɛresaaleba vom ti ba kɔɔha ba da'aba.

ref: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
text: “Fum lɔgesuma seto ti fum sia bɔta la woo tole ya gee bahɛ hɔ, ti fum dikãresuma la za'a tole, fum lɔgeseto woo en nyagere la za'a bɔregɛ mɛ gee bahɛ hɔ, kan le malum lebe na.”

ref: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
text: Leebeduma sɛba en sɔi lɔge tuna woo la, sɛba en doohe fum puan pãrɛ la za'a wan zɛ'a zaaɛ zaarɛ ha la dabeem, wuŋerɛ se'ere en sigeri fum zuo la, kɛla gee si'ina baha na.

ref: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
text: “Wai, wai, tinkãtɛ la, en daa ni bagɛ ka mɛŋa la fusunɔ la, bagɛ ka mɛŋa la fumɔlɔ suma, en gulɛ la salema, bãhi, la peranini,

ref: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
text: ti nananawa ma'a ti lɔge tuna woo mɛlum.” Ti uurenɔ kãra kpɛ'ɛnduma, sɛba en tari uurenɔ tuna, la sɛba woo en dole ko'kãtɛ la zuo kɔɔha la za'a ze' la zaaɛ zaarɛ ha,

ref: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
text: gee kaaha baha na, ban nyɛ ti a taaɛ bugum dita, ti a zu'uhi gulegera na la. “Tinkani en boi wuna la tinkãtɛ kana wa?”

ref: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
text: Gee nyaa vaara tintɔnɔ yɛɛla ba zuto, gee kɛla gee fabela, kaaha vɔɔla yeti, “Aba, aba, tinkãtɛ wa, Fum zi'an ti sɛba woo en tari uurenɔ fum ko'kãra wa zuo, pãrɛ la lɔgerɔ. Gee nananawa ma'a en bala ti tiŋa wa gã'a voore wa.

ref: The heavens should be happy with God's people and apostles and prophets. God has punished her for them.”
text: Nyɛɛna ya baha a zuo na saazuo duma wa, yamam nɛresunduma, tuntuneba, la nɔtɔgeheba wa, nyɛɛni ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ gakɛ en mɛ ya zuo.”

ref: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
text: Ti suŋera ayima en de bunkpe'eŋo ɔ̃begɛ kugere en ani wuu nɛɛrɛ kugekãtɛ en ka tari makerɛ la lobe bahɛ ko'kãtɛ puan, gee yeti, “Gbiregere dina taaba en wan lubɛ Babilɔn tinkãtɛ wa ti ka, kan le malum bɔna.

ref: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
text: Kɔlɔ ŋmɛ'a kan le bɔna, ti baam baanduma, la nyiihe, la benkuna peebego, kan le wuna fum puan le yɛ'ɛha ti se'em daana en mi' koohego kan le bɔna fum puan yɛ'ɛha Nɛɛŋɔ kuga dameŋa, kan le bɔna fum puan yɛ'ɛha.

ref: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
text: Butolela bugum niihum, kan le niiɛ fum puan, ti pɔge dita kua, la sire-ɛla kua kan le bɔna fum puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, fum leebeduma la en daa de tiŋa wa zuo nɛrekãreba, ti fum dikɛ fum nini la pã'ahɛ tihi wa za'a.

ref: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
text: Fum en ka'ahɛ nɔtɔgeheba la, la nɛresunduma la za'a ziim, la sɛba woo ti ba kɔrum ba la su'a timpaberɛ wa zuo la.”
