text: “Bihɛ, n kini mɛ kalam kalam na! Sum wan bɔna la fum se'em daana en gurɛ nɔtɔgeheba yelehum la en boi gɔnkuna wa puan la suŋa suŋa.” Ba'ahegɔ la maalegɔ

ref: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
text: Mam Yɔn, mam de se'em en wum gee nyɛ yele ana wa. Mam wum gee nyɛ a la, mam lu la tiŋa suŋera sɛka en pa'alɛ mam yele ana wa la nama tiŋa ti n na'ahɛ en.

ref: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
text: Gee a yele mam yeti, “Fum dagi fum na'ahɛ mam, mam me dela fum tuntuna tadaana, la fum taaba la, la nɔtɔgeheba la, la sɛba woo en dole gɔnkuna wa yelehum la. Bala na'ahɛ Yinɛ ma'a.”

ref: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
text: Ti a yele mam yeti, “Da pi' gɔŋɔ wa purega yelehum wa, se'ere en sɔi la, saŋa la pɔri mɛ.

ref: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
text: Bahɛ ti se'em en tuni be'em, kelum tuna a be'em, ti se'em en tuni a dɛgerɔ tuuma, kelum tuna a dɛgerɔ tuuma, ti se'em en de tuuntɔɔ kelum vɔna tuuntɔɔ, ti se'em en de tuulum nɛra, kelum vɔna a tuulum vom.”

ref: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
text: “ Bihɛ, n kini mɛ kalam kalam na, n tari n yɔɔrɛ mɛ, ti n yɔ nɛra woo en tum se'em.

ref: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
text: Mam n de Alifa, la Omega, yia la pooren, Pɔhega la ba'ahegɔ.”

ref: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
text: Sum wan bɔna la sɛba en pee ba futo, bala ti ba wan tara sore ti ba paaɛ vom tia la, gee me ta'am doohe tiŋa la nayahi la puan kɛ̃.

ref: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
text: Yiŋa la ha, baahi, sɔ̃niniduma, yaluntuuma duma, nɛrekuureba, la bage kaabereba, la sɛba woo en nɔŋɛ gee tuna pumpɔreŋɔ tuuma en boi bilam.

ref: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
text: “La dela mam Yezu en tum n suŋera fum zi'an na, ti a yele fum, lageŋɔ zi'ihi la yɛla. Mam en de nyɛgerɛ la, gee dɛna Deviti yaaŋa la. Mam en de bulika beŋma'arɛ la.”

ref: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
text: Sia la, la pɔgeditiga la yeti, “wa'ana.” Bahɛ ti se'em woo en wum yelehum wa yeti, “Wa'am na.” Gee bahɛ ti se'em woo ti ko'nyuuro tari en wa'am na. Bahɛ ti se'em woo en boti, wa'am na to'e vom ko'om wa ti bu dɛna bo'olum bo' en.

ref: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
text: N kageri se'em woo en wum gɔŋɔ kuna wa purega wa. Ti se'em han dikɛ sɛla pa'ahɛ bini, Yinɛ me wan dikɛ too seko woo ti a tɔgɛ ku yele gɔŋɔ wa puan la, pa'ahɛ a daana zuo.

ref: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
text: Se'em me han yehe sɛla gɔŋɔ wa purega wa puan bahɛ, Yinɛ me wan yehe nɛra eŋa daana tɔtɛ en de se'em vom tia la puan, la tuulum tiŋa la ti ba tɔgɛ ba yele gɔŋɔ wa puan la bahɛ.

ref: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
text: Se'em daana en nyɛ yele ana wa la yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ, n kini la kalam kalam na.” Taari, wa'am na Daana Yezu.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
text: Daana Yezu zu'uhegɔ, bɔna la nɛresunduma la woo.

ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: «Bɩsɛ-ya, pugla n nãn ka mi bʋraaga wʋn yõkɛ pʋʋrɛ dɔgɛ budibla, tɩ ba wi'ira a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ A'emanuwɛlɩ.» A võorɛ de la Nawẽnnɛ boe la tɩ lɛ.

ref: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
text: Tɩ Azakɔbɩ dɔgɛ Azozɛfʋ n de Amaari sɩra la. Amaari ẽŋa n dɔgɛ Azezi tɩ ba wi'iri Krisi la.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Tɩ Azɔɔrɩ dɔgɛ Asadɔkɩ, tɩ Asadɔkɩ dɔgɛ Akim, tɩ Akim dɔgɛ A'eliyuudɩ.

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Azezi-krisi dɔka daan ẽŋɛ la wãna: A ma Amaari daan yetɩ a ele la Azozɛfʋ, la ba daan nãn ka gã'arɛ la taaba, dee tɩ a tara pʋʋrɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ a tara pʋʋrɛ la.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: La Azozɛfʋ n daan yetɩ a di ẽ na de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, a ka boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽ yãnnɛ nẽrba nifum, a daan tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ a basɛ ẽ sĩm.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: La ẽn daan tẽ'esrɩ bɛla la, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ malɛka wa'am wa puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ ẽ zãasŋɔ pʋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azozɛfʋ, Adavidi yɩɩŋa, da zoe dabeem, di Amaari tɩ a dɛna fʋ pɔga, pʋʋrɛ la tɩ a tarɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ ẽ.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: A wʋn dɔgɛ la budibla, tɩ fʋ sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi, la de ẽŋa n wʋn fãagɛ a nẽrba la ba tõon-be'ero pʋam basɛ.»

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Bõn-bãma wuu ẽŋɛ mɛ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsa la yele se'em na n ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ:

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ nẽegɛ, ẽŋɛ wʋ Zuudãana malɛka la n yele ẽ se'em na, di a pɔga la.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: La a daan ka gã'arɛ la ẽ, halɩ tɩ a wa dɔgɛ budibla la, tɩ a sɩ bia la yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Ba daan dɔgɛ Azezi la Bɛtelɛm n boe Zude so'olʋm, se'emwẽnnɛ tɩ A'erɔdɩ de naba la. Tɩ bãŋrɩba ze'ele dapɔa bɔba wa'am Zerizalɛm,

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: wa soke yetɩ: «Bia la tɩ ba dɔgɛ la boe la bɛ, sɛka n wʋn dɛna Zifdõma naba la? Tõma yẽ la a wõrbiire dapɔa bɔba, bɛla ĩyã tɩ tõma wa'am tɩ tɩ nã'asɛ ẽ.»

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: La naba A'erɔdɩ n daan wõm bɛla la, ẽŋa la Zerizalɛm dõma wuu yɛm n yɔɔrɩ.

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: Tɩ A'erɔdɩ daan lagsɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la wuu, soke ba yetɩ: «Tẽn-kãna tɩ ba wʋn dɔgɛ Krisi la?»

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛtelɛm n boe Zude so'olʋm na, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la yʋʋn gʋlsɛ yele la wãna:

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: ‹Bɛtelɛm n de tẽn-pɩka, fʋ ka pɔ'ɔgɛ Zude so'olʋm tẽn-kãra la pʋam, se'ere n soe la, nẽra n wʋn yese fõn pʋam dɛna nẽŋadãana bɩsra Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n de mam nẽrba la yelle.› »

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: La A'erɔdɩ sʋgɛ mɛ wi bãŋrɩba la soke ba bãŋɛ wẽnse'ere tɩ wõrbiire la yese,

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: dee tõm ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya Bɛtelɛm ka ɛ soke bãŋɛ bia la yelle sõŋa sõŋa, la ya sãn yẽ ẽ, ya leme wa'am wa yele mam tɩ mam mẽ sẽŋɛ ka nã'asɛ ẽ.»

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: La ban wõm naba la yetɔga la, ba sẽnnɩ mɛ wɛ'ɛsa, darɛ le yẽ wõrbiire la tɩ ba daan yẽ dapɔa bɔba la n tole ba nẽŋa, tarɩ ba halɩ ka paagɛ bia la n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la ze'ele.

ref: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
text: La ban daan yẽ wõrbiire la, ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ halɩ zozo'e.

ref: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
text: Tɩ ba kẽ yire la pʋam yẽ bia la, la a ma Amaari, ka dũma tẽŋa bia la nẽŋam nã'asɛ ẽ, dee lorge ba lɔgrɔ loose ban tarɩ sɛla la bɔ ẽ. Ba daan de la sãlma, la tɩdarɛ, la miiri n tarɩ yũuŋ-yẽlga.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan kã'an ba zãasŋɔ pʋam yetɩ, ba da le doose naba A'erɔdɩ zẽ'am, bɛla tɩ ba daan doose sore ayẽma lebe kule ba tẽŋa. A'erɔdɩ boorɩ tɩ a kʋ Azezi

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Bãŋrɩba la n fõrgɛ la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Azozɛfʋ zãasŋɔ pʋam, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge tarɩ bia la, la a ma la zoe sẽŋɛ Ezipti ka bɔna bilam halɩ tɩ m le wa yele fʋ se'ere. Se'ere n soe la, naba A'erɔdɩ wʋn ɛɛra bia la mɛ tɩ a kʋ.»

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ isge yʋ'ʋŋɔ tarɩ bia la, la a ma la sẽŋɛ Ezipti,

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: ka bɔna bilam halɩ tɩ A'erɔdɩ wa ki. La ẽŋɛ la bɛla, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsa yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Mam wi m Dayʋa la n boe Ezipti la tɩ a wa'am.»

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: La A'erɔdɩ n daan bãŋɛ tɩ bãŋrɩba la pã'asɛ ẽŋa mɛ na, a sũure n yĩige halɩ, tɩ a tõm nẽrba yetɩ ba sẽŋɛ Bɛtelɛm, la tẽn-sɛba n boe a kẽnkɛrsɩ la wuu ka kʋ budim-sɛba wuu n masɛ wʋ yʋʋma ayi, la sɛba n ka paagɛ bɛla la, wʋ ẽŋa n daan soke bãŋrɩba la bãŋɛ se'emwẽnnɛ tɩ wõrbiire la yese la.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azeremi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ:

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: «Ba wõm tagursi Rama tẽŋa, kʋʋnɔ la fablɩgɔ zozo'e. La de la Arasɛlɩ n kellɩ a kɔma yelle, tɩ ba pã'asra ẽ tɩ a ka sakra tɩ a go'oge. Se'ere n soe la, a kɔma la ka le bɔna.»

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: La naba A'erɔdɩ daan wa ki mɛ, tɩ Zuudãana malɛka puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Azozɛfʋ zãasŋɔ pʋam Ezipti, yele ẽ yetɩ:

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: «Isge tarɩ bia la, la a ma la lebe Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, sɛka n daan eerɩ bia la tɩ a kʋ la ki mɛ.»

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ isge tarɩ bia la, la a ma la kule Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋa.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: La a daan wõm mɛ tɩ Arkelawusi n sɔɔgɛ a sɔ A'erɔdɩ zẽ'am di Zude na'am. Tɩ a daan zoe dabeem ka sẽŋɛ Zude so'olʋm. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã'an ẽ zãasŋɔ pʋam, tɩ a sẽŋɛ Galile so'olʋm.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: A daan sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era la tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Nazarɛtɩ. Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n daan yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. Ba daan yetɩ: «Ba wʋn wi'ira Krisi la tɩ Nazarɛtɩ nẽra.»

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Tõm-ya tõon-sɛba n pa'alɩ tɩ ya teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: yetɩ: «Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm lɛm mɛ.»

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Azã daan yɛ la fuugo n de yʋgnɛ kõbrɔ, dɩkɛ sagãnnɛ lʋ a sɩa. A dia daan de la saniiro la seero.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Tɩ nẽrba daan ze'ele Zerizalɛm, la Zude so'olʋm wuu, la tẽn-sɛba n lɛm kʋlaaga yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Zʋrdẽ la wuu sẽnna a zẽ'am,

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: tɔgra ba tõon-be'ero pa'ala ẽ, tɩ a misra ba Zʋrdẽ ko'om na pʋam.

ref: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: La Azã yẽ la Farisi dõma, la Saduse dõma zozo'e n sẽm a zẽ'am tɩ a mise ba ko'om pʋam, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ãn wʋ kãmpẽesɩ buuri, ãne n pa'alɛ ya tɩ ya tã wʋn zoe põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ n sẽm na?

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Nẽra n põn tara lɩa a nu'usum tɩ a lʋbɛ tɩɩsɩ. Tɩ-sɛka woo n ka wɔnnɩ biɛ sõma, a wʋn lʋbɛ ẽ mɛ, lobge bugum pʋam, tɩ bugum di ẽ.

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ẽŋa wʋn welge nẽrba wʋ nẽra n zãlɩ pɩ'ɩgɔ a nu'usum tɩ a vaagɛ a si zɛɛŋa yeele, ẽŋɛ a bãarɛ pʋam dee yõ urgo la, la bugum n ka kĩŋri.» Azezi ko'om misga (Amarkɩ

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Azezi daan ze'ele la Galile wa'am Zʋrdẽ Azã zẽ'am tɩ a mise ẽ ko'om pʋam.

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Tɩ Azã daan zagsɛ, dee yetɩ: «La de nɩ na mam n wʋn wa'am fõn zẽ'am tɩ fõn mise mam, tɩ fõn sẽna mam zẽ'am?»

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ la ẽŋɛ bɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, tɩ la de la bɛla tɩ tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ lan masɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na wuu.» Azezi n yele bɛla la, tɩ Azã sakɛ

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: mise ẽ, tɩ a dɔɔra. Haya, tɩ saazuum yo'oge, tɩ a yẽ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n signi a zuo wʋ na'adawenne.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Tɩ kõa ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu, a paagɛ la mam yɛm.»

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Tɩ Azezi wõm tɩ ba yõkɛ Azã yʋ yʋ'a deego pʋam, tɩ a lebe Galile so'olʋm.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: La a daan basɛ Nazarɛtɩ tẽŋa, dee sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era la Kapɛrnaʋm n lɛm Galile mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, Zabulõ la Nɛftali so'olʋm pʋam,

ref: After Jesus had gone without eating for
text: Azezi daan lʋ la nõorɛ dabsa pinaasɩ, wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ. Tɩ a kɔm dõnna.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Tɩ Asũtãana lɛm ẽ, yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fʋ botɩ kuga wã lebge dia.»

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Da makɛ fʋ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ bɩsɛ.› »

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Tɩ Asũtãana le tarɩ Azezi zom tãŋa n ba wɔgɛ zuo, ka pa'alɛ ẽ dũnia so'olʋm wuu la a na'am pãŋa,

ref: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
text: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asɛ mam, mam wʋn bɔ fʋ bãna wã wuu.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ fõrgɛ, Asũtãana! La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nã'asra Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, dee sakra ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a.› »

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ:

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: «Zabulõ la Nɛftali tẽnsɩ n tɔglɛ ko-kãtɛ bɔba, Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba, Galile tẽnsɩ tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ boe a pʋam na,

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Tɩ ba basɛ ba yʋgsɩ la bilam mẽŋa, dee dɔla ẽ.

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Azezi daan eerɩ la Galile mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, yẽ bʋraasɩ bayi, Asimõ tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la a yɩbga A'ãndre, tɩ ba lobrɩ yʋka mõgrɛ la pʋam, ba daan de la zũn-yõgrɩba.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽŋa le yẽ bʋraasɩ bayi n de Azebede kɔma, Azakɩ la a yɩbga Azã. Tɩ bãma, la ba sɔ Azebede bɔna õorŋɔ pʋam malgra ba yʋgsɩ. Tɩ Azezi wi ba,

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: tɩ ba basɛ õorŋɔ la, la ba sɔ la tɔtɔ, dee dɔla ẽ. Azezi zãsnɩ nẽrba, dee bɔ'ɔra ba ĩmã'asʋm (Aluki

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Azezi daan kaarɩ la Galile so'olʋm wuu zãsna nẽrba Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, mõola Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga dee botɩ bã'adõma, la kobro wuu n boe nẽrba la pʋam yẽta ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ daan yese Siiri so'olʋm wuu, tɩ ba tara bã'adõma wuu tɩ bã'asɩ buuri to'oto'ore tarɩ ba, nẽrba ĩisi n dõnnɩ tɩ ba nãmsra, la kʋlkã'arsɩ n dolɩ sɛba, la nẽrba n luti kinki'ire, la kɔɔrnɔ wa'am a zẽ'am, tɩ a botɩ ba wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e ze'ele Galile, la Dekapɔlɩ, la Zerizalɛm, la Zude, la tẽnsɩ n boe Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba doose ẽ.

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Tɩ Azezi yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, zom tãŋa zuo zĩ'ire. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lagsɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya sãn ka ɩta Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na gãnna lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, ya kãn tã'agɛ kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.» Azezi zãsʋm sũ-isgre yelle (Aluki

ref: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
text: Bɛla, fʋ sãn tarɩ fʋ bɔ'a wa'am kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zẽ'a tɩ fʋ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, sãn tẽegɛ tɩ fõn tarɩ la yelle la fʋ sɔbia,

ref: and he taught them:
text: Tɩ a yãŋa zãsʋm ba yetɩ:

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: «Nẽr-sɛba n mi tɩ ba ka masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la ba bõnɔ.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Sũ-sã'aŋɔ dõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ ba sũure mã'agɛ.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Sũ-bʋgsʋm dõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ba dũnia tɩ ba sɔna.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Ninbãal-zɔtba tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zoe ba ninbãalga.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Pʋpeelem dõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ba wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: Yãma de la dũnia wã peelem. Tẽŋa n boe tãŋa zuo ka tã wʋn sʋgɛ.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ, ya põn da pɔɩ. Da pɔɩ saazuum, bẽm ĩyã, a de la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Da pɔɩ tẽŋa, bẽm ĩyã, a de la a nãma dɔglgɔ zẽ'a. Da pɔɩ Zerizalɛm, bẽm ĩyã, a de la Nawẽnnɛ n de na-kãtɛ la tẽŋa.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: Azezi daan le yetɩ: «Wʋ yaarʋm n botɩ dia zã'asʋm na, yãma mẽ masɛ tɩ ya ãna wʋ yaarʋm dũnia wã pʋam. La yaarʋm sãn sabgɛ, bẽm n wʋn basɛ tɩ a le zã'asʋm? A lebge la zãŋa, a ka le tara yõorɔ, a de la ba lobe ẽ basɛ yẽŋa, tɩ nẽrba nẽ ẽ la nãma.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: La ba ka yõ'orɩ fɩtla vugra la pɩ'ɩgɔ, ba wʋn zoole ẽ na fɩtla zoolgo zẽ'a, tɩ a nẽegɛ peelem bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe yire la pʋam na.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: «Da tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ mam wa'am tɩ m sã'am na Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ, bɩɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la. Mam ka wa'am tɩ m sã'am ba, mam wa'am tɩ m pa'alɛ la ba võorɛ ba'asɛ, dee ẽŋɛ ban yele se'em na.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: «Yãma wõm tɩ ba daan yele kʋrʋm dõma yetɩ: ‹Da kʋ nẽra, la nẽra sãn kʋ nẽra, ba di a sarɩya.›

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo sũure n isge la a sɔbia, la masɛ tɩ ba di a sarɩya. La sɛka n yele a sɔbia yetɩ: ‹Yalma›, la masɛ tɩ sarɩyaditba nẽŋadõma di a sarɩya. La sɛka n yele a sɔbia yetɩ: ‹Fʋ ka tarɩ võorɛ›, ẽŋa dãana masɛ mɛ tɩ a kẽ bug-tẽŋa.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: fʋ basɛ fʋ bɔ'a la kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne la nẽŋam, dee dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ ka malgɛ fʋ yelle la, la fʋ sɔbia la, dee yãŋa leme wa'am wa dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ'a la bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: Fʋ sãn tara yelle la nẽra, tɩ a tara fʋ wɛ'ɛsa sarɩyadia zẽ'am, malgɛ fʋ yelle la tɔtɔ, yãma n nãn boe sore la, tɩ a da dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ sarɩyadita, tɩ a tɔgɛ fʋ yetɔga la dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ gu'ura, tɩ a kẽ'esɛ fɔ yʋ'a deem.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Mam yetɩ fʋ la asɩra, tɩ fʋ kãn yẽ sore yese yʋ'a deem na, halɩ tɩ fʋ wʋn wa yɔ fʋ sãnnɛ na wuu ba'asɛ.» Azezi zãsʋm yalsɩ yelle

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: «Ba yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-sɛka n lebse a pɔga, a bɔ ẽ gõŋɔ n pa'alɛ tɩ a lebse ẽ mɛ.›

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: «Ya ken wõm tɩ ba yʋʋn yele kʋrʋm dõma yetɩ: ‹Da viige ya pɔrɛ. Fʋ sãn bĩŋe nõorɛ pɔɩ tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ bɛla.›

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya ‹ẽe› dɛna ‹ẽe›, tɩ ya ‹ayɛɩ› dɛna ‹ayɛɩ›. La sãn gãŋɛ bɛla, sɛla n pa'asɛ la de la be'em dãana bõnɔ.» Da lebse be'em be'em ĩyã (Aluki

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: «Ya wõm tɩ ba daan yetɩ: ‹Nẽra sãn pusge a tadãana nifo, ba mẽ pusge a nifo, nẽra sãn vike a tadãana yẽnnɛ, ba mẽ vike a yẽnnɛ.›

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ya be'em, ya da ẽŋɛ be'em lebse. La nẽra sãn wẽ fʋ zʋʋgɔ pɛka, fʋ basɛ tɩ a wẽ fʋ gɔbga pɛka pa'asɛ.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: La nẽra sãn sãm fɔ tɩ a to'oge fʋ fu-pɩka, fʋ basɛ tɩ a to'oge fʋ fu-kãtɛ pa'asɛ.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: La nẽra sãn pẽrgɛ fɔ tɩ fʋ kɔlgɛ ẽ kilomẽtrɩ ayɩla, fʋ kɔlgɛ ẽ kilomẽtrɩ bayi.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Nẽra sãn sose fʋ sɛla, fʋ bɔ ẽ. La nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a peŋe sɛla fʋ zẽ'am, da mõŋɛ ẽ.» Nõŋɛ-ya ya bɛ'ɛba (Aluki

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: «Ya wõm tɩ ba daan yetɩ: ‹Fʋ nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana, dee sise fʋ bɛ.›

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, ya nõŋɛ ya bɛ'ɛba, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n nãmsrɩ ya la.

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya wʋn dɛna ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum na kɔma, se'ere n soe la, ẽŋa n botɩ wʋntɛɛŋa yẽkra bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sõma la nẽr-be'ero, dee botɩ saaga niira bɔ'ɔra sɛba ɩrgɔ n masɛ, la sɛba ɩrgɔ n ka masɛ.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Yãma sãn nõŋɛ sɛba n nõŋɛ ya la mã'a, bẽm yɔɔrɔ tɩ ya wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am? Lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba mẽ ɩtɩ la bɛla.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: La yãma sãn pʋ'ʋsra la ya sɔbiisi mã'a, bẽm tɩ yãma ɩtɩ gãnna ya taaba la? Nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ na mẽ ɩtɩ la bɛla.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: «Guuse-ya, da ɩta-ya ɩr-sõma nẽrba nẽŋam tɩ ba yẽta ya. Ya sãn ɩta bɛla, ya kãn to'oge yɔɔrɔ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum zẽ'am.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ sõŋɛ nasdõma, da basɛ tɩ fʋ gɔbga nu'ugo bãŋɛ fʋ zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo n ɩtɩ sɛla.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: Ya sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, da ɩta-ya wʋ pʋlibsum dõma n ɩtɩ se'em na, bãma nõŋɛ la ba zɛ ba wẽndeto pʋam, la sorɔ-puursi zuto pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ tɩ nẽrba yẽta ba. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ba põn to'oge ba yɔɔrɔ mɛ.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, fʋ kẽ fʋ deem yʋ kʋlɩŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ fʋ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe zẽ'a n sʋgɛ la. La fʋ Sɔ la n yẽ tɩ fʋ sʋgɛ mɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ la, a wʋn yɔ fɔ.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Ya sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, ya da tɔgra yetɔga n ka tarɩ võorɛ kaana wʋ nẽr-sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em na, ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma n tɔgrɩ zɔ'ɔra la ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn lerge bãma pʋ'ʋsgɔ mɛ.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Bɛla, pʋ'ʋsra-ya wãna: ‹Tõma Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum, botɩ fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ yẽta pẽka,

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: wa'am wa di na'am nẽrba tẽŋasʋka, botɩ fõn boorɩ se'em ẽŋɛ tẽŋa zuo, wʋ lan ɩtɩ fõn yire se'em na.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Ya sãn basɛ nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ ya be'em na taalɛ, ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: La ya sãn ka basɛ nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ ya be'em na taalɛ, ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ kãn basɛ ya taalɛ.

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Fʋ sãn lʋ nõorɛ, wire fʋ nẽŋa dee sãasɛ fʋ zuugo,

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: tɩ nẽrba da bãŋɛ tɩ fʋ lʋ la nõorɛ. La fʋ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe zẽ'a n sʋgɛ la wʋn bãŋɛ. La fʋ Sɔ la n yẽ fõn sʋgɛ lʋ nõorɛ la, a wʋn yɔ fɔ.» Azezi zãsʋm Nawẽnnɛ yire bõntarsõm yelle (Aluki

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Dẽŋɛ-ya ɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm, la sɛla n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ya bõn-bãma wuu pa'asɛ.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Da fabla-ya beere yelle, tɩ beere ya wʋn bãŋɛ ya wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, daarɛ woo n bɩsrɩ a mẽŋa yelle.»

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: «Da lagsrɩ-ya bõntarsõm bĩŋra dũnia wã zuo, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ bõn-mõgla la tẽŋanzẽem wʋn sã'am ba, tɩ nayigba mẽ kẽ'era zũura.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Lagsra-ya bõntarsõm bĩŋra Nawẽnnɛ yire, bilam tɩ mõka la tẽŋanzẽem kãn sã'am ba, tɩ nayigba mẽ kãn kẽ zũ.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Se'ere n soe la, ya bõntarsõm n boe zẽ'a na, ya pʋtẽ'era mẽ boe la bilam.

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: La fʋ nini sãn ka ãna sõŋa, fʋ ĩyã wuu boe la lika pʋam. Peelem sɛka n wʋn nẽegɛ fʋ pʋam na sãn dɛna lika, la de la lika mẽŋa mẽŋa.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: Nẽra ka boe n tã wʋn sakɛ zuudãandõma bayi, a wʋn sise ayɩla mɛ, dee nõŋɛ ayɩla, bɩɩ a wʋn nanna ayɩla la mɛ, dee pɔ'ɔra ayɩla la. Bɛla, yãma ka tã wʋn sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de ya Zuudãana, dee botɩ bõntarsõm mẽ dɛna ya zuudãana.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Da fabla-ya yɛta tɩ: ‹Bẽm tɩ wʋn di?› bɩɩ ‹tɩ wʋn yũ la bẽm?› bɩɩ ‹tɩ wʋn yɛ la bẽm?›

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam yele ya yetɩ, ya da fabla ya vɔm yelle, yãma n wʋn di sɛla, bɩɩ ya wʋn yũ sɛla, la ya da fabla ya ĩyã yelle, ya wʋn yɛ sɛla. Vɔm ka gãnnɩ dia? Bɩɩ ĩyã ka gãnnɩ futo?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Bɩsɛ-ya bõn-ẽgla, ba ka bʋtɩ, ba mẽ ka lagsrɩ bõnkɔɔla ɩta bã pʋam. La ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔrɩ ba dia mɛ. Yãma ka tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna bõn-ẽgla halɩ zozo'e?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: La bẽm tɩ ya fabla futo yelle? Bɩsɛ-ya mõ-puuro n ãn sõŋa se'em, ba ka tõnnɩ, ba mẽ ka wʋgrɩ futo.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, baa naba Asalomõ na'am n yʋʋn tarɩ pãŋa la, a yʋʋn ka tã'agɛ yɛ fuugo n ãn sõŋa wʋ mõ-puugo ayɩla.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: La Nawẽnnɛ sãn botɩ mõorɔ n boe zĩna dee tɩ ba wʋn yõ ba bugum beere ãna sõŋa bɛla, a kãn bɩsɛ yãma yelle gãnna bɛla? Bẽm tɩ yãma ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra zo'oge?

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: La de la nẽr-sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n eerɩ bõn-bãma wuu. La yãma Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mi tɩ yãma boorɩ bõn-bãma wuu mɛ.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: «Da zɛrgra-ya taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ da zɛrgɛ ya.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Se'ere n soe la, yan wʋn zɛrgɛ nẽrba se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn zɛrgɛ ya la bɛla. La sɛla tɩ ya dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ nẽrba la, ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ ya.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: Bẽm tɩ fʋ bɩsra sɔkɔ n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum, dee ka bɩsra dɔɔgɔ n boe fʋ mẽŋa nifum na?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne yɛta fʋ sɔbia yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ fʋ yese sɔkɔ a nifum, dee tɩ dɔɔgɔ bɔna fõn mẽŋa nifum?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Pʋlibsum dãana, dẽŋɛ yese dɔɔgɔ la fʋ mẽŋa nifum, dee yãŋa tã'agɛ yẽ sõŋa yese sɔkɔ la n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum na.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Yãma nẽrsaalba n de tõon-be'ero dõma la sãn mina tɩ ya bɔ ya kɔma bõn-sõma, ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum kãn bɔ yãma sɛba n sosrɩ ẽ na bõn-sõma n gãnnɩ bɛla?

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Ãne n boe yãma pʋam tɩ a bia wʋn sose ẽ borborɩ, tɩ a bɔ ẽ kugre?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: «Sose-ya Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ya. Ɛ-ya, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ. Wẽ-ya kʋlɩŋa, tɩ a wʋn yo'oge bɔ ya.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, a wʋn bɔ ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka woo n eerɩ, wʋn yẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n wẽ'erɩ kʋlɩŋa, ba wʋn yo'oge bɔ ẽ.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Bɩɩ a sãn sose zũfo, tɩ a bɔ ẽ bõnsɛla?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Yãma n boorɩ tɩ nẽrba ɩta se'em bɔ'ɔra ya la, yãma mẽ ɩta bɛla bɔ'ɔra ba. Ẽna de la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ, la a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n gʋlsɛ se'em n bɛla.» Sorɔɔrɔ bayi yelle (Aluki

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n wõm mam yetɔga wã, dee ẽŋɛ wʋ yetɔga la n yele se'em na, a dãana ãn wʋ yɛm dãana n mẽ a yire tãmpĩa zuo.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: La zanõ-mika, la so-bãalga we'esɩ la vɔm zẽ'am. La de la nẽrba fẽe n dolɩ ẽ.» Tɩa woo la a biɛ (Aluki

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ba tõoma pʋam tɩ ya wʋn bãŋɛ ba. Bẽm ĩyã, zãaŋa ka tã wʋn wɔm tã'ama, la kẽkɔ ka tã wʋn wɔm kẽnkãma.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Tɩ-sõŋɔ woo wɔnnɩ na biɛ sõma, la tɩ-be'ego woo wɔnnɩ na biɛ be'ero.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tɩ-sõŋɔ ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ be'ero, la tɩ-be'ego mẽ ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ sõma.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tɩ-sɛka woo n ka wɔnnɩ biɛ sõma, ba wʋn lʋbɛ ẽ mɛ yõ la bugum.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Bɛla, la de la nẽrba la tõoma pʋam tɩ yãma wʋn bãŋɛ ban de nẽr-sɛba.» Nẽr-sɛba n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ asɩra sɩra (Aluki

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n wõm mam yetɔga wã, dee ka sakɛ ɩta yetɔga la n yele se'em na, a ãn wʋ yalma n mẽ a yire tẽntõnbĩ'isgo zuo.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wẽ ẽ, tɩ kʋsebgo vuge wẽ ẽ, tɩ a wuu lui saam saam.»

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: Azezi daan le yele yetɩ: «Dagɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n wi'iri mam tɩ ‹Zuudãana, Zuudãana› la n wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam. La de la nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum n boorɩ se'em na n wʋn kẽ.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Sarɩyadia daarɛ, nẽrba zozo'e wʋn yele mam yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, Zuudãana, tõma daan dɩkɛ la fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba. La fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ tõma daan dɩkɛ dige kʋlkã'arsɩ, dee daan tõm tõon-yãlma zozo'e la fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ.›

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: La mam wʋn yele ba yetɩ: ‹Mam põn ka mina ya, sẽŋɛ-ya fõrgɛ, tõon-be'ero dõma wã.› » Deto bayi mẽa makrɛ (Aluki

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wẽ ẽ, tɩ kʋsebgo vuge wẽ ẽ, tɩ a ka lui, se'ere n soe la, a mẽ la tãmpĩa zuo.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: La Azezi n tɔgɛ yetɔga la kɩ'ɩlɩm na, zãsŋɔ la di'ige nẽrba la zozo'e mɛ.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Se'ere n soe la, a zãsŋɔ la tarɩ la pãŋa, la ka ãn wʋ ba lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n zãsnɩ se'em na.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: La ba wʋn lobge Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n wʋn soe nɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na basɛ la yẽŋa lika pʋam, bilam ba wʋn kaasra, dee dɩkɛ ba nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.»

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Tɩ zaanõorɛ paagɛ, tɩ ba tarɩ nẽrba zozo'e tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ tarɩ ba wa'am Azezi zẽ'am. Tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔga dige kʋlkã'arsɩ la, dee botɩ sɛba wuu n de bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Tɩ kõnkonne wa'am a zẽ'am wa ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fʋ sãn bɔɔra, fʋ tã wʋn basɛ tɩ m yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ le ãna sõŋa.»

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Tɩ Azezi tẽegɛ a nu'ugo kalʋm ẽ, dee yetɩ: «Mam bɔɔra, yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa.» Bilam mẽŋa tɩ kõnkɔm na basɛ ẽ, tɩ a le ãna sõŋa.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn wa'am wa botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.»

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Azezi n wõm bɛla la, la di'ige ẽ mɛ. Tɩ a yele nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ mam nãn ka yẽ nẽra n bɔ mam sɩra wʋ bʋra-ẽna wã, baa Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la pʋam mam ka yẽ.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam tõntõnna n bẽ'erɩ kɔɔrʋm gã yire, a ba nãmsra mɛ zozo'e.»

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ, nẽrba zozo'e wʋn ze'ele zanõ-bɔba, la dapɔ-bɔba wa'am wa zĩ'ire la Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ di Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: A yele bɛla, dee yele sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la yetɩ: «Kule, tɩ la wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ fõn bɔ mam sɩra se'em na.» La wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ a tõntõnna la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Azezi botɩ nẽrba zozo'e yẽ ĩmã'asʋm (Amarkɩ

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Tɩ ba kaasra tagursi yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, bẽm boe tõma la fõn tẽŋasʋka? Fõn wa'am tɩ fʋ nãmsɛ tõma mɛ dee tɩ wakatɛ la nãn ka paagɛ?»

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Tɩ tẽŋa la dõma wuu yese tʋ'ʋsra Azezi, la ban yẽ ẽ na, ba sose ẽ yetɩ, a fõrgɛ dee basɛ ba tẽŋa.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi sẽŋɛ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ yire ka yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ dɛɛm-pɔka n bẽ'erɩ gã, tɩ a ĩyã tʋlgɛ.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Tɩ Azezi kalʋm pɔka la nu'ugo, tɩ bã'aga la basɛ ẽ. Tɩ a isge ẽŋɛ Azezi sãanɛ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: La de la bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Ẽŋa n to'oge tõma tooro, la tɩ bã'asɩ basɛ.» Azezi doosgo yelle (Aluki

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãama ayɩla wa'am a zẽ'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, mam wʋn dɔla fʋ sẽnna zẽ'e-sɛba wuu tɩ fʋ wʋn sẽnna.»

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Tɩ a karẽnbia ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, basɛ tɩ m dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ m sɔ dee wa'am wa dɔla fɔ.»

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la paagɛ nẽegɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fãagɛ tɔ, tõma yetɩ ki mɛ.»

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Azezi daan kẽ la õorŋɔ, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la doose ẽ.

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ wa'am wa vugra mõgrɛ la zuo. Tɩ ko-mĩ'isi botɩ õorŋɔ la zɩgra la a mise. La Azezi daan gĩsri mɛ.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Tɩ ba wuu palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Bẽm nẽra n de ẽna tɩ kʋsebgo, la ko'om sakra a nõorɛ?» Kʋlkã'arsɩ n dolɩ bʋraasɩ bayi, tɩ Azezi dige ba (Amarkɩ

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Ba daan dooge mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba paagɛ la Gadara dõma tẽŋa pʋam, tɩ bʋraasɩ bayi tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dolɩ ba ze'ele yɔɔrɔ zẽ'a sẽnna tʋ'ʋsra ẽ. Ba daan de la zɔl-wẽensɩ, tɩ nẽrba zɔta ka sakra dɔla bilam tɔlla.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: La kurkurdõma n daan zo'oge lɛm bilam ɔbra.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la sose Azezi yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ dige to tɩ tɩ yese, fʋ basɛ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kurkurdõma la.»

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya.» Tɩ ba yese sẽŋɛ ka kẽ kurkurdõma la. Tɩ kurkurdõma la ze'ele tãŋa zuo zoe sige ka lui mõgrɛ la pʋam ki.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Tɩ sɛba n gu'uri kurkurdõma la zoe sẽŋɛ tẽŋa pʋam ka yele lan ẽŋɛ se'em na wuu, la bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la daan dolɩ la.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Azezi daan kẽ õorŋɔ lebe dooge la mõgrɛ la ke bɔba kule Kapɛrnaʋm, ẽn kẽ'erɩ zẽ'a la.

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Tɩ Azezi wõm bɛla, yele ba yetɩ: «Sɛba n tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm ka eerɩ tɩba, la de bã'adõma n eerɩ tɩba.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
text: La nẽra kãn dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm wɔ-kɛgsɩ pʋam. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, dã-paalga la sãn kɛlla, ba wʋn botɩ wɔɔsɩ la tãsɛ mɛ, tɩ dãam na ka'agɛ, tɩ wɔɔsɩ la mẽ sã'am. La masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm na wɔ-paalsɩ pʋam, bɛla tɩ ba wuu kãn sã'am.» Pɔka n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, la pugla vo'ore yelle (Amarkɩ

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Tɩ yel-ẽna saagɛ tẽŋa la wuu. Azezi botɩ fɔɔsɩ bayi yẽta

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba tẽ'esra ba sũure pʋam yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna pɔ'ɔrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: La Azezi mi ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na mɛ, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma tẽ'esra pʋtẽ'e-be'ero ya sũure pʋam?

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: Mam wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa tarɩ nõorɛ dũnia zuo wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ.» Azezi yele bɛla, dee yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «Isge, dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala la kule.»

ref: The man got up and went home.
text: Tɩ a isge kule.

ref: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
text: Sẽŋɛ-ya ka zãsʋm Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na võorɛ, Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam boorɩ tɩ nẽrsaalba zɔta la taaba ninbãalga, dee dagɩ tɩ ba dɩkɛ ba bõnɔ kãabra bɔ'ɔra mam.› Bɛla, mam ka wa'am tɩ m wi sɛba dõma n yetɩ bãma masɛ mɛ na, mam wa'am na tõon-be'ero dõma ĩyã.» Ba soke Azezi nõorlʋa yelle (Amarkɩ

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Azezi daan yese zẽ'e-ẽŋa mɛ fõrgra, tɩ fɔɔsɩ bayi dɔla ẽ tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe tɩ ninbãalga!»

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
text: Ban daan yesrɩ la, tɩ ba tarɩ nẽra tɩ kʋlkã'arga dolɩ ẽ, tɩ a dɛna muko, wa'am Azezi zẽ'am.

ref: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
text: Azezi daan ze'ele la bilam sẽnna tɔlla, yẽ bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Amatie n zĩ tɔ'ɔsra lampo. Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge dɔla mam.» Tɩ Amatie isge dɔla ẽ.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Amatie yire dita, tɩ lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma zozo'e wa'am wa lagɩm na Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la zẽa dita.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma yẽ bɛla, soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma karẽnsãama la lagɩm na lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma dita.»

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: Azezi n ken tɔgra la ba bɛla la, tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadãana ayɩla wa'am a zẽ'am, wa ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam yele yetɩ: «Mam pɔyʋa n ki lɛɛlɛ wã, wa'am wa dɩkɛ fʋ nu'ugo paglɛ a zuo, tɩ a vo'oge.»

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: Tɩ Azezi gelge yẽ ẽ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «M bia, keŋe fʋ sũure! Fõn bɔ mam sɩra la n basɛ tɩ fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.» Tɩ pɔka la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm bilam mẽŋa.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Tɩ Azezi dige kʋlkã'arga la, tɩ a yese. Tɩ muko la tɔgra. Tɩ la di'ige nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Nẽra nẽra nãn ka yẽ wãna buuri Ɩsrayɛlɩ pʋam.»

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ tõma la Farisi dõma lʋta nõorɛ, dee tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi la ka lʋta?»

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn di pɔka ẽŋɛ kibsa wi a tadãandõma tɩ ba wa'am naagɛ la ẽ dita, ba tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-sã'aŋɔ wakat-sɛka tɩ pɔgdita la ken bɔna la ba lɛ mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ. La dabsa sẽm mɛ, tɩ ba wʋn yese pɔgdita la ba zẽ'am. Bɛla tɩ ba yãŋa wʋn lʋ nõorɛ.

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Nẽra kãn dɩkɛ tãn-paalga lige fu-kɛka. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, tãn-paalga la wʋn botɩ fu-kɛka la ãagɛ mɛ gãŋɛ bɛla.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, a daan tẽ'esrɩ a sũurum yetɩ: «Mam sãn kalʋm a fuugo mã'a, mam wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.»

ref: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
text: Tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan isge dɔla ẽ wɛ'ɛsa.

ref: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
text: Ban daan we'esɩ la, tɩ pɔka ayɩla n luti sɩkẽŋa yʋʋma pia la ayi tɩ la ka gɔ'ɔra, wa'am Azezi poorʋm wa kalʋm a fuugo nõorɛ.

ref: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
text: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ Zifdõma nẽŋadãana la yire yẽ wɩ-pɛɛbrɩba la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n bɔgrɩ.

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yese-ya, tɩ pugla la ka ki, a gĩsri mɛ.» Tɩ nẽrba la la'ara ẽ.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: La ẽn botɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yese la, a kẽ la deem gʋrɛ pugla la nu'ugo, tɩ a isge.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ kẽ yire, tɩ fɔɔsɩ la paagɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma bɔ sɩra tɩ mam wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ yan boorɩ se'em na?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe! Zuudãana.»

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
text: Tɩ a kalʋm ba nini dee yetɩ: «La wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ yan bɔ sɩra se'em na.»

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Tɩ ba nini na pike yẽta. Tɩ a kã'an ba yetɩ: «Da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽra bãŋɛ.»

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: La ban fõrgɛ la, ba mõolɛ bõn-ẽŋa mɛ tẽŋa la zẽ'a woo. Azezi botɩ muko tɔgra

ref: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
text: La Farisi dõma daan yetɩ: «La de la kʋlkã'arsɩ naba n bɔ ẽ pãŋa, tɩ a tara digra kʋlkã'arsɩ la.» Azezi zoe nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ ninbãalga

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Azezi daan kaarɩ la tẽn-kãra la tẽn-pɩgsɩ wuu, zãsna nẽrba Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, mõola Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga, dee botɩ bã'adõma, la kobro buuri wuu yẽta ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: La Azezi n yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, ba ninbãalga n yõkɛ ẽ. Se'ere n soe la, ba targɛ mɛ bãlɛ, ãna wʋ piisi n ka tarɩ dũnsĩina.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Va'am na bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ zo'oge mɛ, la tõntõnɩba n wʋn lagsɛ bõnkɔɔla la pɔ'ɔrɩ mɛ.

ref: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã, sose-ya va'am na dãana tɩ a tõm tõntõnɩba pa'asɛ tɩ ba lagsɛ bõnkɔɔla la.»

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: la Afilipi, la Abatelemi, la Atoma, la Amatie n de lampo-tɔ'ɔsa la, la Azakɩ n de Alfe dayʋa, la Atade,

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: La ba sãn tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ, da fabla-ya yãma n wʋn tɔgɛ se'em, bɩɩ yãma n wʋn tɔgɛ sɛla yelle. Se'ere n soe la, wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋn bɔ ya bõn-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn tɔgɛ.

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Sẽŋɛ-ya Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n ãn wʋ piisi n bɔɩ la zẽ'am,

ref: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
text: Azezi le yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam tõm ya wʋ piisi saasaadõma tẽŋasʋka. Bɛla ĩyã, dɛna-ya yɛm dõma wʋ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma, la ya ãna wʋ na'adawɛma n ka tarɩ yelle se'em na.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: la Asimõ n daan de Zelɔtɩ nẽra ayɩla la, la Azudaasɩ Iskariyo, sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi la.

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: La de la bãna pia la ayi tɩ Azezi daan tõm, yele ba yetɩ: «Da sẽŋɛ-ya bu-zãŋsɩ la zẽ'am, la da kẽ-ya Samaari dõma tẽnsɩ pʋam.

ref: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
text: La ya da dɩkɛ tãmpɔkɔ sore la sẽnnɛ ĩyã, da dɩkɛ-ya futo bayi, bɩɩ tagra, bɩɩ doore. Ba wʋn bɔ tõntõnna ẽn boorɩ sɛla.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: Ya sãn paagɛ tẽn-kãtɛ bɩɩ tẽn-pɩka pʋam, soke-ya bãŋɛ nẽr-sõŋɔ sãn bɔna mĩ wʋn to'oge ya tɩ ya kẽ'era a yire, bɔna-ya bilam ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn fõrgɛ tẽŋa la.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ sarɩyadia daarɛ tẽn-ẽŋa dõma wʋn yẽ toogo gãnna Sodom la Gomõorɩ tẽnsɩ nẽrba.» Azezi yetɩ nãmsgɔ n wʋn wa'am (Amarkɩ

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Sɔbia wʋn botɩ ba yõkɛ a sɔbia kʋ. La sɔdõma mẽ wʋn ẽŋɛ ba kɔma bɛla. La kɔma mẽ wʋn zɛbɛ la ba dɔgrɩba, botɩ ba kʋ ba.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa la nẽrba la. Se'ere n soe la, ba wʋn wa yõgɛ ya mɛ tarɩ sẽŋɛ sarɩyadia zẽ'esʋm, dee wẽ ya ba wẽndeto pʋam.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: La ba wʋn yõgɛ ya sẽŋɛ gɔmnadõma, la na'adõma nẽŋam, yãma n dolɩ mam na ĩyã, tɩ ya wʋn dɛna kaset-dõma tɔgɛ bɔ bãma la bu-zãŋsɩ.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: La dagɩ yãma mẽŋa n wʋn tɔgɛ, la de la ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la n boe ya pʋam na n wʋn tɔgɛ.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Nẽrba wuu wʋn sise ya mam yʋ'ʋrɛ la ĩyã. La sɛka n ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Ba sãn nãmsɛ ya tẽŋa ayɩla pʋam, ya zoe sẽŋɛ tẽŋa ayẽma. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ya kãn yãŋɛ kaagɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽnsɩ ba'asɛ dee tɩ mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la leme wa'am.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: Karẽnbia ka gãnnɩ a karẽnsãama, la tõntõnna mẽ ka gãnnɩ a zuudãana.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: La karẽnbia sãn wa ãna wʋ a karẽnsãama la, la tõntõnna sãn wa ãna wʋ a zuudãana la, la seke mɛ. Ban wi'iri yidãana la tɩ Abelzebuli n de Asũtãana la, ba wʋn tʋʋra a yire la dõma gãnna la bɛla.» La masɛ tɩ ya zɔta la Nawẽnnɛ (Aluki

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Da zɔta-ya sɛba n kʋʋrɩ ĩyã, dee ka tãna wʋn kʋ sɩa la. Zɔta-ya Nawẽnnɛ n tã wʋn botɩ ya ĩyã, la ya sɩa sã'am bug-tẽŋa la.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Ba ka koosrɩ mõom niinto bayi la lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla mã'a? La baa niila ayɩla kãn ki, tɩ la sãn dagna ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n sakɛ.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: La baa yãma zuugo zomto, Nawẽnnɛ mi ba wuu kãllɛ mɛ.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Bɛla ĩyã, da zɔta-ya dabeem, yãma tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna niinto zozo'e mɛ.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n wʋn ze'ele nẽrba nẽŋam yetɩ ẽŋa de la mam nẽra, mam mẽ wʋn ze'ele mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nẽŋam yetɩ a de la mam nẽra.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n sɩ'ɩsɛ nẽrba nẽŋam yetɩ ẽŋa ka mi mam, mam mẽ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nẽŋam tɩ mam ka mi ẽ.» Tɩ nõŋɛ Azezi gãnna (Aluki

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: «Da tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ mam tarɩ la nõ-yẽnnɛ wa'am dũnia zuo. Mam ka wa'am tɩ m botɩ nẽrba dɛna nõorɛ ayɩla dũnia zuo, la de la welgre tɩ mam tarɩ wa'am.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam wa'am tɩ m welge la dayɔɔsɩ la ba sɔdõma, la pugunto la ba madõma, la dayɔɔsɩ pɔgba la ba sɩrba madõma.

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Tɩ nẽra mẽŋa yir-dõma wʋn dɛna a bɛ'ɛba.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma gãnna mam, a ka masɛ tɩ a dɛna mam nẽra. La nẽr-sɛka woo n nõŋɛ a dayʋa bɩɩ a pɔyʋa gãnna mam, a ka masɛ tɩ a dɛna mam nẽra.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n ka sakɛ nãmsgɔ wʋ nẽra n zɛɛrɩ a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam, a ka masɛ tɩ a dɛna mam nẽra.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a gu a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n basɛ a vɔm mam ĩyã, a wʋn yẽ ẽ.» Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ yɔɔrɔ (Amarkɩ

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ẽn de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la ĩyã, a wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa yɔɔrɔ. La nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge sɩra dãana ẽn de sɩra dãana la ĩyã, a wʋn to'oge sɩra dãana yɔɔrɔ.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge ya, a to'oge la mam. La nẽr-sɛka woo n to'oge mam, a to'oge la sɛka n tõm mam na.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ mam nẽrba wã ayɩla ko-mã'asga tɩ a yũ, ẽn de mam karẽnbia la ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ a kãn koŋe yɔɔrɔ.»

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Azã karẽnbiisi la n fõrgɛ la, tɩ Azezi yãŋa tɔgra Azã yelle bɔ'ɔra nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma daan sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ weem? Yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la mõorɔ tɩ kʋsebgo mĩini bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Se'ere n soe la, Azã wa'am mɛ wa lʋta nõorɛ dee ka yũura dãam, tɩ ba yetɩ kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ.

ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Azezi n daan pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a yese bilam sẽŋɛ la so'olʋm na tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam ka zãsna nẽrba, dee mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Tɩ Azã daan bɔna yʋ'a deem, wõm Krisi tõoma la yelle, tõm a karẽnbiisi a zẽ'am,

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na bɩɩ, tɩ gu'ura nẽra ayẽma?»

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele Azã yãma n wõnnɩ se'em, la yãma n yẽtɩ se'em na:

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Fɔɔsɩ nini yẽtɩ mɛ, tɩ kɔɔrnɔ sẽnna, tɩ kõnkɔma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa, tɩ wansɩ wõnna, tɩ kũm vɔ'ɔra, tɩ nasdõma wõnna Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: La nẽr-sɛka n ka lebrɩ poorʋm mam ĩyã la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.»

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: La bẽm tɩ yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ? Nẽra n yɛ fu-sõma? Ayɛɩ. Nẽr-sɛba n yɛ fu-sõma la boe na'adõma yiɛ.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: La yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la bẽm? Ya sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa bɩɩ? Ẽe. La mam yetɩ ya tɩ a de la nẽra n gãnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽrsaalba tɩ ba dɔgɛ la wuu pʋam, nẽra ka boe n gãnnɩ Azã n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na. La nẽr-sɛka n de pɩka Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam gãnnɩ Azã mɛ.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: Lan pɔsɛ Azã n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm sẽnnɩ nẽŋa la pãŋa, la pãnsɩ dõma n mõrgrɩ tɩ ba fãagɛ ẽ.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: La ya sãn bɔɔra tɩ ya bãŋɛ a võorɛ, Azã n de A'eli tɩ ba yʋʋn yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: Mam wʋn dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba? Ba ãn wʋ kɔma n zĩ da'am wi'ira taaba yetɩ:

text: Tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa'am wa dita dee yũura, tɩ ba yetɩ, a de la dita, la dã-yũura, la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma sɩrɛ. Yɛm dãana tõoma n pa'alɩ tɩ a tarɩ yɛm.» Azezi zɛrgɛ tẽn-sɛba nẽrba n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la (Aluki

text: Mam sɔgrɩga la fakɛ mɛ, la mam zeero la ka tɩbgɛ.»

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.

text: Tɩ basɛba lui tãmpĩa zuo, zẽ'a tɩ tẽntõnnɔ ka zo'oge. Tẽntõnnɔ n ka zo'oge la ĩyã, ba yese la tɔtɔ.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Sɛka n tarɩ wʋn to'oge pa'asɛ, tɩ la zo'oge. La sɛka n ka tarɩ, baa sɛla tɩ a tarɩ fẽe la, ba wʋn to'oge ẽ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ɩtɩ la bãma lɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Ya wʋn wõm, la ya kãn bãŋɛ a võorɛ, ya wʋn bɩsɛ, la ya kãn yẽ.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la nẽr-sõma zozo'e yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ yãma n yẽ sɛla la mɛ, la ba ka yẽ, ba yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ ba wõm sɛla tɩ yãma wõm na mɛ, la ba ka wõm.» Azezi pa'alɛ bõnbuuri makrɛ la võorɛ (Amarkɩ

ref: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it at once.
text: Tãmpĩa zuo tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm kõ-yẽlga la, to'oge ẽ bilam mẽŋa la sũ-yẽlga.

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message about the kingdom, but don't understand it. Then the evil one comes and snatches the message from their hearts.
text: Sore la nõorʋm tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga la, dee ka bãŋɛ a võorɛ. Tɩ Asũtãana n de tõon-be'ero dãana la wa'am wa yese yetɔga la a sũure pʋam.

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: La gõ'osɩ la zẽ'a tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga la, tɩ a yɛm yɔɔra la dũnia wã yɛla, tɩ bõntarsõm yɛla pã'asra ẽ, botɩ yetɔga la ka tãna wʋn wɔm biɛ a sũure pʋam.

ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and understand the message. They produce as much as
text: La zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka n wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, wõm a võorɛ, wɔnna biɛ. Tɩ basɛba wɔm kɔbga kɔbga, tɩ basɛba pisyoob-yoobɩ, tɩ basɛba pitã-tã.» Kanarsɩ makrɛ

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Azezi le makɛ la makrɛ ayɩla bɔ ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ bʋraa n bʋrɛ si sõma a va'am.

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: Tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ, tɩ nẽrba gã gĩsra, tɩ a bɛ tarɩ kanarsɩ sẽŋɛ ka bʋrɛ lagɩm mĩ.

ref: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
text: Tɩ si la yese bɩ waagɛ. Tɩ kanarsɩ la mẽ waagɛ, tɩ ba yẽ tɩ ba dagɩ si.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Tɩ tõntõnɩba la sẽŋɛ va'am na dãana zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, dagɩ si sõma tɩ fõn daan bʋrɛ fʋ va'am na? La bẽm tɩ kanarsɩ bɔna mĩ zozo'e?›
