ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: La sɛka n to'oge bi-pɩka n ãn wʋ ẽna, mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, a to'oge la mam mẽŋa.

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: La a sãn zagsɛ ka selse ba, fʋ yele wẽndeego la nẽrba. La a sãn zagsɛ ka selse wẽndeego la nẽrba, ya bɩsra ẽ wʋ nẽra n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ, bɩɩ tõon-be'ero dãana.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: La nẽra sãn botɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ bãna wã n bɔ mam sɩra la ayɩla basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ a wʋn yẽ la, ba sãn dɩkɛ nẽerɛ kug-kãtɛ lʋ yulse a yugla lobe ẽ basɛ ko-kãtɛ pʋam n sõɩ.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: Fʋ nu'ugo bɩɩ fʋ nã'arɛ sãn yetɩ a botɩ fʋ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, wige ba basɛ. Fʋ sãn tara nu'u-yẽŋɔ bɩɩ nã'a-yẽŋɔ dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ yire vɔa, n sõɩ fõn tarɩ nu'usi bayi, la nãma bayi, dee tɩ ba lobe fʋ basɛ bugum n ka kĩŋri pʋam.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: Tɩ Azezi le yetɩ: «Fʋ sɔbia sãn ẽŋɛ be'em, sẽŋɛ ka yẽ ẽ fõn na ẽŋa mã'a, pa'alɛ ẽ a tuure la. A sãn sakɛ fʋ nõorɛ, fʋ to'oge ẽ basɛ.

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa'am tɩ a fãagɛ la nẽr-sɛba n bɔɩ la.]

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ ãn na bɛla, a ka boorɩ tɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ bãna ayɩla bɔɩ.» Nẽrba n wʋn malgɛ ba yelle la taaba se'em

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: La a sãn zagsɛ fʋ nõorɛ la, fʋ doose nẽra ayɩla bɩɩ bayi sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am. Tɩ nẽrba bayi bɩɩ batã dɛna kaset-dõma yelle la mãalgɔ ĩyã, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na.

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: La mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ bõn-sɛka woo tɩ ya wʋn lʋ dũnia wã zuo, wʋn lʋ Nawẽnnɛ yire. La sɛla tɩ ya wʋn lorge dũnia wã zuo, wʋn lorge Nawẽnnɛ yire.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: Mam ken yɛta ya tɩ nẽrba bayi sãn naagɛ nõorɛ dũnia wã zuo sose mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɛla, a wʋn ẽŋɛ bɔ ba.

ref: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
text: La nẽrba bayi bɩɩ batã sãn lagsɛ taaba mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, mam boe la ba lɛ mɛ.» Tõntõnna n ka bɔ'ɔrɩ sugri makrɛ

ref: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lɛm Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, mam sɔbia sãn ẽŋɛ mam be'em, nõorɛ wãne tɩ mam wʋn basɛ ẽ taalɛ? Nõorɛ ayopɔɩ bɩɩ?»

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: La de la bɛla, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãna wʋ naba n boorɩ tɩ a tõntõnɩba geele a tõoma pa'alɛ ẽ.

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: La ẽn pɔsɛ bɔna mĩ gɛɛla la, tɩ ba tarɩ nẽra ayɩla n di a sãnnɛ lig-kõba yʋ'ʋrɛ n de talã tʋspia wa'am.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: La a daan ka tarɩ sɛla wʋn yɔ, tɩ a zuudãana la bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba koose ẽ, la a pɔga la a kɔma, la a lɔgrɔ wuu yɔ sãnnɛ na.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: Tɩ tõntõnna la ka dũma tẽŋa a zuudãana la nẽŋam sose ẽ yetɩ, a ẽŋɛ sugri tɩ ẽŋa wʋn yɔ sãnnɛ na wuu.

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Tɩ a zuudãana la zoe a ninbãalga, basɛ sãnnɛ na, dee basɛ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: Tɩ a tõntõn-tadãana la ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam sose ẽ yetɩ, a ẽŋɛ sugri tɩ ẽŋa wʋn yɔ a sãnnɛ na.

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: Tɩ a zagsɛ, dee tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem yetɩ, a sãn ka yɔ ẽŋa a sãnnɛ na, a kãn yese.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: La a tõntõn-taaba la n yẽ bɛla la, tɩ ba sũure sã'am zozo'e. Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ba zuudãana la zẽ'am ka yele ẽ sɛla n ẽŋɛ la wuu.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Tɩ zuudãana la botɩ ba wi ẽ, tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Tõntõn-be'ego wã, sãn-sɛka wuu tɩ fõn di la, mam basɛ mɛ tɩ la ba'asɛ, fõn sose mam na ĩyã.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: La a zuudãana la sũure yĩige mɛ, tɩ a basɛ tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem tɩ ba nãmsɛ ẽ halɩ tɩ a yɔ sãnnɛ na wuu.

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn ẽŋɛ ya la bɛla, ya sãn ka basɛ ya sɔbiisi tõon-be'ero taalɛ la ya sũure wuu.»

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Azezi n daan tɔgɛ bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a yese Galile sẽŋɛ la Zude so'olʋm Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Nẽrba basɛba n boe tɩ ba dɔgɛ ba tɩ ba ãna wʋ ba fɔ ba mɛ na, ka dita pɔgsɩ. Tɩ nẽrba mẽ fɔ bʋraasɩ basɛba. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ĩyã, tɩ sɛba mẽ ka dita pɔgsɩ. Sɛka n tã wʋn yãŋɛ to'oge yetɔg-ẽna, a to'oge.» Azezi pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ kɔm-pɩgsɩ (Amarkɩ

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e doose ẽ, tɩ a botɩ ba bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba wa'am a zẽ'am tɩ ba di a yɛm bɩsɛ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ bʋraa lebse a pɔga n wen ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛka woo mɛ bɩɩ?»

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Yãma ka karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la? Pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ, Nawẽnnɛ n nãam nẽrsaalba la, a nãam na bʋraa la pɔka.

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ a yetɩ, bʋraaga basɛ a sɔ, la a ma, dee lagɩm na a pɔga, tɩ bãma bayi lebge ayɩla.

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Bɛla, ba ka le dɛna nẽrba bayi, ba yãŋa lebge la ayɩla. Bɛla, sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lagɩm na taaba, nẽrsaala da welge ba.»

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma la soke ẽ yetɩ: «La bẽm ĩyã tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, bʋraa sãn yetɩ a lebse a pɔga, a bɔ ẽ pɔgdire lebsgo gõŋɔ, dee tarɩ ẽ ka lebse?»

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La de la ya sũure n kɛ tɩ ya ka sakɛ pa'algɔ la ĩyã, tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn sakɛ tɩ ya lebse ya pɔgba. Pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ, la yʋʋn ka ãn wãna.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: La mam yetɩ ya tɩ, bʋraaga sãn lebse a pɔga tɩ la sãn dagna tɩ a gã la bʋraasɩ, a sãn le di pɔka ayẽma, a ẽŋɛ la be'em wʋ nẽra n gã'arɛ la a tadãana pɔga.»

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «La dagɩ nẽrba wuu n wʋn yãŋɛ to'oge yetɔg-ẽna, sãn dagna sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba to'oge.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Tɩ nẽrba tarɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ wa'am Azezi zẽ'am tɩ a dɩkɛ nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la zɛbɛ nẽrba la.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Tɩ bʋraaga wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, mam wʋn tõm na tõon-sõŋɔ kãna yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ?»

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Tɩ a soke yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ yetɔg-bãna?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Da kʋ nẽra, da gã'arɛ la fʋ tadãana pɔga, da zũ, da tɔgɛ põmpɔrŋɔ kaseto,

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: La a dɩkɛ la a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba, dee fõrgɛ. Budib-polle n de bõntarsa yelle (Amarkɩ

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fʋ sɔkra ma sɛla n ãn sõŋa yelle? Nawẽnnɛ mã'a n ãn sõŋa. Fʋ sãn bɔɔra tɩ fʋ yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, fʋ sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ a nõorɛ se'em na.»

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya kɔm-pɩgsɩ la tɩ ba wa'am mam zẽ'am, da gu'ura-ya ba. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la nẽr-sɛba n ãn wʋ kɔma wã bõnɔ.»

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: La nẽŋam dõma zozo'e wʋn lebge poorʋm dõma, tɩ poorʋm dõma zozo'e lebge nẽŋam dõma.»

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: nanna fʋ sɔ, la fʋ ma, la fʋ nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa.»

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Tɩ budib-polle la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam põn sakra bõn-bãna wuu mɛ, bẽm n deege?»

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn bɔɔra tɩ fʋ masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, sẽŋɛ ka koose fõn tarɩ sɛla la wuu dɩkɛ bɔ nasdõma, tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ bõntarsõm Nawẽnnɛ yire, dee wa'am wa dɔla mam.»

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Budib-polle la n wõm bɛla la, a fõrgɛ mɛ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, se'ere n soe la, a tarɩ la bõntarsõm zo'oge.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ la tɔ mɛ bɔ bõntarsa tɩ a kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: La mam le yɛta ya tɩ, la de la nãana tɩ yʋgnɛ doose fupĩim yoko pʋam yese, gãnna bõntarsa n wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.»

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: La a karẽnbiisi la n wõm bɛla la, tɩ la di'ige ba halɩ zozo'e, tɩ ba yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna bɛla, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ?»

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Tɩ Azezi bɩsra ba, dee yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽna, la Nawẽnnɛ tã sɛla woo ɩta mɛ.»

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «Tõma basɛ sɛla woo mɛ, dee dɔla fõn. Bẽm tɩ tõma wʋn yẽ?»

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ wakat-sɛka tɩ sɛla woo wʋn lebge paalsɩ la, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n wʋn zĩ'ire a na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la, yãma sɛba n dolɩ mam na mẽ wʋn zĩ'ire na'am bĩmbĩna pia la ayi zuto, di Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri pia la ayi la sarɩya.

ref: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
text: La sɛba wuu n basɛ ba yiɛ, bɩɩ ba kẽendõma, la ba yɩbsɩ, bɩɩ ba sɔdõma, bɩɩ ba madõma, bɩɩ ba kɔma, bɩɩ ba vatɔ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, ba wʋn to'oge bãma n basɛ sɛla la nõorɛ kɔbga, dee yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: Azezi le yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm ãn wʋ yidãana n yese bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ, sẽŋɛ ka dɩkɛ tõntõnɩba tɩ ba tõm a va'am.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: La a tɔgɛ kʋ la tõntõnɩba la tɩ ẽŋa wʋn yɔ ba lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla daarɛ pʋam, dee tõm ba a va'am na tɩ ba ka tõm.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: Tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa bɩ fẽe, tɩ a le yese yẽ nẽrba basɛba n ze da'am ka tõnna,

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: ‹Ya mẽ sẽŋɛ mam va'am na ka tõm, tɩ mam wʋn yɔ ya lan masɛ se'em.›

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Tɩ a le yese wẽnnɛ n we'esɩ lua, le yẽ nẽrba basɛba n ze, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: ‹Bẽm ĩyã tɩ yãma zɛ kalam daarɛ la wuu pʋam ka ɩta sɛla sɛla?›

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: ‹Nẽra nẽra ka dɩkɛ tõma tõoma.› Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: ‹Ya mẽ sẽŋɛ m va'am na ka tõm.›

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: Tɩ wẽnnɛ kẽ, tɩ va'am na dãana yele sɛka n bɩsrɩ a tõoma la yelle yetɩ: ‹Wi tõntõnɩba la tɩ ba wa'am tɩ fʋ yɔ ba. Dẽŋɛ yɔ sɛba n sẽŋɛ poorʋm na ka paagɛ sɛba n dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ la.›

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Tɩ sɛba n sẽŋɛ wẽnnɛ n we'esɩ lua la wuu to'ose lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla ayɩla.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: La sɛba n dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ la n to'ose lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla ayɩla la, ba yõnnɩ yidãana la mɛ,

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: yetɩ: ‹Sɛba n wa'am poorʋm na tõm na karf-ayɩla mã'a, la fõn basɛ tɩ ba to'ose zõna la tõma sɛba n tõm halɩ daarɛ la wuu pʋam nãmsɛ la wʋntɛɛŋa la.›

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: Tɩ a lebe yele ba nẽra ayɩla yetɩ: ‹M sɩrɛ, mam ka ẽŋɛ fʋ sɛla n ka masɛ. Tɩ tɔgɛ kʋ, tɩ fʋ sakɛ tɩ fõn wʋn to'oge la lag-kõbrɛ ayɩla.

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: To'oge fʋ yɔɔrɔ sẽŋɛ fõrgɛ. La de la mam n boorɩ tɩ m yɔ sɛba n wa'am poorʋm na zõna la fõn.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Mam ka tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ m dɩkɛ m bõnɔ ẽŋɛ sɛla tɩ mam boorɩ? Bɩɩ mam n ẽŋɛ sõŋa la ĩyã tɩ fõn ɩta sũure?›

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark

text: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la bẽm bɔ fɔ?» Tɩ pɔka la lerge yetɩ: «Bɔ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ sãn wa dita fʋ na'am na, tɩ m dayɔɔsɩ bayi wã ayɩla zĩ'ire fʋ zʋʋgɔ, tɩ ayɩla zĩ'ire fʋ gɔbga.»

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: Tɩ naba la sẽŋɛ tɩ a bɩsɛ nẽrba la, yẽ tɩ nẽra n boe mĩ ka yɛ pɔgdire kibsa la fuugo.

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n daan wõm a zãsŋɔ la, la di'ige ba mɛ. Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba ɩta se'em na, bõn-kãna n gãnnɩ? (Amarkɩ

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Ẽna n de Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la pɔspɔsɩ dãana, ẽŋa n gãnnɩ wuu.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: La bʋyi dãana n ãn wʋ ẽŋa n wãna n yetɩ: ‹Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fõn nõŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa se'em na.›

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Farisi dõma n lagsɛ taaba la, tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ:

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele mam Zuudãana yetɩ, zĩ'ire m zʋʋgɔ bɔba halɩ tɩ m wa ẽŋɛ fʋ bɛ'ɛba tɩ ba bɔna fʋ nãma tẽŋa.›

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: «Yãma tẽ'esrɩ la bẽm la Krisi yelle: Ãne yɩɩŋa n de ẽ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «A de la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa.»

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa daan botɩ Adavidi wi ẽ tɩ Zuudãana yetɩ:

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Adavidi sãn wi ẽ yetɩ, Zuudãana, la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ a dɛna Adavidi yɩɩŋa?»

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka yãŋɛ lerge ẽ se'ere. Lan pɔsɛ daar-ẽŋa, nẽra ka le saagɛ soke ẽ sokre.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Sɛka n zẽkɛ a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sike ẽ. La sɛka n sike a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zẽkɛ ẽ.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ ya tẽŋa la tɩ a lebge la daboto.

ref: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
text: Sɛka n de nẽr-keko ya pʋam, a dɛna ya tõntõnna.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: Azezi daan yetɩ: «Zerizalɛm nẽrba, Zerizalɛm nẽrba, yãma kʋʋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, dee lɔbra sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm ba ya zẽ'am na kuga kʋʋra. Nõorɛ faa tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m lagsɛ ya wʋ nõ-yã'aŋa n lagsrɩ a kɔma ũna a kempagsɩ pʋam se'em na, tɩ ya ka bɔɔra.

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Bɛla, sɛka n pɔɩ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na, a po pɔɩ la sɛla n boe a zuo la wuu.

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Tɩ Azezi yele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ, la a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ:

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: «Lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma n sɔɔgɛ Amoyisi zẽ'a.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Bɛla, ban zãsnɩ ya se'em na, ya ɩta bɛla, dee da ɩta bãma n ɩtɩ se'em na. Se'ere n soe la, bãma n zãsnɩ se'em na, ba ka ɩtɩ bɛla.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Ba zãsŋɔ la ãn wʋ ze-tɩbsa tɩ ba lʋ zelge nẽrba, la ba mẽŋa ka boorɩ tɩ ba kalʋm ba la ba nu'ubiire.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Ba tõnnɩ ba tõoma la wuu tɩ nẽrba n bɩsra ba, bɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ gãna gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga ba pʋam dɩkɛ lʋ ba dĩa bɩɩ ba kẽnkãnsɩ. Dee pɛ fu-wogro ẽŋɛ bisa tɩ ba wɔgɛ tɩ nẽrba nã'asra ba.

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Ba nõŋɛ la nã'asgɔ zẽ'esɩ dia zẽ'esʋm, la ba wẽndeto pʋam.

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: Ba nõŋɛ tɩ nẽrba n pʋ'ʋsra ba da'asɩ pʋam, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba wi'ira ba yetɩ: ‹Karẽnsãama.›

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: La yãma da basɛ tɩ ba wi'ira ya tɩ karẽnsãama. Se'ere n soe la, yãma tarɩ la karẽnsãama ayɩla mã'a, la ya wuu de la sɔbiisi la taaba.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Da wi'ira-ya nẽra tɩ sɔ dũnia wã zuo Nawẽnnɛ sore la doosgo ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, ya tarɩ la Sɔ ayɩla mã'a n boe saazuum.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: La ya da basɛ tɩ nẽra wi'ira ya tɩ nẽŋadãana. Se'ere n soe la, ya tarɩ la nẽŋadãana ayɩla mã'a n de Krisi.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Yãma Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ya to'osrɩ la pɔkõpa n tarɩ sɛla la wuu, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋs-wogro tɩ nẽrba bɩsra ya. Ya wʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ gãnna wuu].

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ya kaarɩ la dũnia zẽ'esɩ wuu ɛɛra tɩ ya yẽ nẽra ayɩla tɩ a sakɛ dɔla ya sore la. La ya sãn yẽ nẽra ayɩla, ya basɛ tɩ a lebge la tõon-be'ero dãana gãnna ya mẽŋa nõorɛ ayi, wʋn sẽŋɛ bug-tẽŋa.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: Yãma sɛba n de nẽŋadõma de la fɔɔsɩ, ya wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, yãma yetɩ, nẽra sãn pɔɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, la de la zãŋa. La sɛka sãn pɔɩ sãlma la n boe Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam na, dẽnɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽn pɔɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na.

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Yãma ka wõnnɩ sɛla võorɛ, dee ãna wʋ fɔɔsɩ la. Bẽm n gãnnɩ? Sãlma la bɩɩ, Wẽnde-kãtɛ la n botɩ sãlma la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ na n gãnnɩ?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Ya ken yetɩ, nẽra sãn pɔɩ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na, la de la zãŋa. La nẽra sãn pɔɩ sɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ bĩŋe bĩmbĩnne na zuo la, dẽnɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽn pɔɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na.

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Fɔɔsɩ, bẽm n gãnnɩ? Sɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ la bɩɩ, kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na n botɩ sɛla tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ na n gãnnɩ?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: La sɛka n pɔɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, a po pɔɩ la Nawẽnnɛ n boe mĩ na.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: La sɛka n pɔɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire, a pɔɩ la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne na, la sɛka n zĩ a zuo la.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma bɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽvõorɔ buuri wuu pia pʋrɛ mɛ, baa ba sãn ãna pɩka pɩka, dee basra yel-sɛba n de kãŋkãŋɛ lɔɔ la pʋam na, ka ɩta. La de la sɛla n masɛ, la ninbãal-zʋa, la sakrɛ. La de la bõn-bãna tɩ ya wʋn dẽŋɛ ɩta, dee yãŋa ɩta sɛla tɩ ya ɩtɩ la pa'asɛ.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Yãma nẽŋadõma de la fɔɔsɩ, yãma yoorɩ ya bõn-yũula mɛ yɛsra lislima basra, dee vɔlla yʋgma.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Yãma peerɩ ya laasɩ, la ya wama pɔa mɛ tɩ ba ãna sõŋa, dee tɩ ba pʋam pɩrɛ la nayigum, la be'ero bõnɔ.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Yãma Farisi dõma, ya de la fɔɔsɩ! Peege-ya ya laasɩ la pʋam pɔspɔsɩ, tɩ ba poore la mẽ wʋn ãna sõŋa.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, yãma ãn wʋ yɔɔrɔ tɩ ba takɛ tɩ ba zuum pelge ãna sõŋa sõŋa, dee tɩ ba pʋam pɩrɛ la kõba, la põ'osgɔ wuu.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Yãma mẽ ãn na bɛla. Yãma botɩ nẽrba bɩsra ya tɩ ya ãna wʋ nẽrba n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, dee tɩ ya pʋam pɩrɛ la pʋlibsum, la tõon-be'ero.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma, pʋlibsum dõma wã, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Ya mẽtɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yɔɔrɔ, dee malgra nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ yɔɔrɔ tɩ ba ãna sõŋa.

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: Dee yetɩ, yãma daan sãn boe nɩ yãma yaabdõma wakatɛ la, yãma daan kãn po lagɩm na ba kʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la.

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Ya mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ ya mẽŋa kaseto tɩ yãma de la sɛba n yʋʋn kʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la yɩɩsɩ.

ref: So keep on doing everything they did.
text: Tɔ. Ken sẽŋɛ-ya nẽŋa ẽŋɛ ya yaabdõma la n daan ẽŋɛ sɛla la ba'asɛ.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: Bõnsɛ'ɛdõma wã! Kãmpẽesɩ buuri! Yãma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne põsɛ sarɩyadia tɩ ba kãn kẽ'esɛ ya bug-tẽŋa?

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ bõn-bãna taalɛ wʋn bɔna zamãan-ẽna nẽrba wuu zuto.» Azezi sũure sã'am Zerizalɛm dõma ĩyã (Aluki

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya kãn le malɩn yẽ mam, halɩ tɩ ya wʋn wa yetɩ: ‹Pẽgɛ-ya sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la.› »

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Azezi daan ze'ele la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam yese fõrgra. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la lɛm ẽ pa'alɛ ẽ tɩ a bɩsɛ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la n mẽ sõŋa se'em.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma yẽ bõn-bãna wã wuu? Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ba wʋn lʋbɛ ba wuu mɛ saam saam, tɩ kugre baa ayɩla kãn weege a tadãana zuo.»

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Nẽrba zozo'e n wʋn dɩkɛ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ wa'am wa pã'asra ya, tɩ ba nẽra woo yetɩ: ‹Mam n de Krisi la.› La ba wʋn botɩ nẽrba zozo'e tuuge mɛ.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Ya wʋn wa wõm zɛbrɛ yelle, la zɛbrɛ dãmnɛ. La da basɛ-ya tɩ dabeem tarɩ ya. Dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-bãma ẽŋɛ, la, la nãn dagɩ dũnia ba'asgɔ.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”

text: La pɔsɛ la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a ɛɛra sore tɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi. Azezi diti Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la a karẽnbiisi (Amarkɩ

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Ban daan dita la, tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya nẽra ayɩla n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.»

ref: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
text: Borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la pɔspɔsɩ daarɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ la bɛ ka mãasʋm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la tɩ fʋ di?»

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ban daan boe mĩ dita la, tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ borborɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «To'oge-ya di. Ẽna de la mam ĩyã.»

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
text: Bãna wã de la mam zɩɩm n wʋn yese saagɛ nẽrba zozo'e ĩyã, pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la ba tɩ a basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ, mam kãn le yũ viinye biɛ bõn-yũula wã, halɩ tɩ la ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn le naagɛ la ya yũ ba mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.»

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
text: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Yãma wuu wʋn lebe la poorʋm dee basɛ mam zĩna yʋ'ʋŋɔ, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn kʋ dũnsĩina, dee tɩ piisi la wɩrgɛ.› »

ref: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra, tɩ zĩna yʋ'ʋŋɔ wã, lan dẽŋɛ nõ-rɔɔgɔ n wʋn kaasɛ, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam.»

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Getsemane, tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Zĩ'ire-ya kalam, dee tɩ m sẽŋɛ nẽŋa ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ mam zozo'e tɩ la ãna wʋ mam yetɩ m ki mɛ na. Weege-ya kalam gu'ura mam, da gĩse-ya.»

ref: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele yetɩ: «Baa bãna wuu sãn lebe poorʋm dee basɛ fõn, mam kãn malɩn basɛ fõn.»

ref: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele yetɩ: «Baa la sãn dɛna tɩ mam lagɩm na fõn ki, mam kãn malɩn sɩ'ɩsɛ fõn.» Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la wuu lagɩm yele bɛla. Azezi pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ Getsemane (Amarkɩ

text: La a sẽŋɛ la nẽŋa fẽe ka võglɛ a nẽŋa tẽŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «M Sɔ, la sãn tãna, fʋ basɛ tɩ nãmsgɔ ẽna tole dee basɛ ma. La, la da kãn dɛna mam n boorɩ se'em, la dɛna fõn boorɩ se'em.»

ref: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
text: Isge-ya tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ. Sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam na n wãna n sẽm.» Ba yõkɛ Azezi (Amarkɩ

ref: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.

ref: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «M sɩrɛ, ẽŋɛ fõn wa'am tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ se'em na.» Tɩ nẽrba la paagɛ yõkɛ Azezi.

text: Tɩ sɛba yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ gu bɩsɛ A'eli la sãn wʋn wa'am wa fãagɛ ẽ.»

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: La daan pɔsɛ la wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka, tɩ tẽŋa la wuu lebge lika tarɩ ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ karf-atã.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Karf-atã la wakatɛ tɩ Azezi tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani.» La võorɛ de la: «Mam Wẽnnaam, mam Wẽnnaam, bẽm tɩ fõn basɛ mam?»

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla zoe sẽŋɛ ka dɩkɛ nẽtɩŋa lʋsɛ dã-mĩ'isgo pʋam zẽkɛ la dɔɔgɔ bɔ ẽ tɩ a yũ.

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Tɩ fuugo la n yuli Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam beŋe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la ãagɛ pʋsʋka saazuum halɩ ka paagɛ tẽŋa. Tɩ tẽŋa mĩim, tɩ tãmpĩisi wõrɛ.

ref: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
text: Tɩ yɔɔrɔ be'ese, tɩ pʋpeelem dõma zozo'e n yʋʋn ki vo'oge.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Tɩ Azezi n wa vo'oge la, tɩ ba ze'ele yɔɔrɔ pʋam yese kẽ Zerizalɛm, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yẽ ba.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: La Rom sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛma, la sʋdaasɩ la n ze gu'ura Azezi la n yẽ tẽŋa la n mĩim, la bõn-sɛba n ẽŋɛ la, dabeem n yõkɛ ba zozo'e, tɩ ba yetɩ: «A sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.»

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: La pɔgsɩ zozo'e n daan ze zãazãarɛ dee bɩsra, ba daan ze'ele la Galile doose Azezi tɩ ba sõŋra ẽ.

ref: That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea
text: Bʋraaga ayɩla n daan de bõntarsa, n ze'ele Arimate, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azozɛfʋ. A de la Azezi karẽnbia.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: Wẽnnɛ n daan mã'agɛ, tɩ a sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am ka sose ẽ tɩ a basɛ tɩ a dɩkɛ Azezi kũm na sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ. Tɩ Apilatɩ bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba bɔ ẽ.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ dɩkɛ kũm na, dɩkɛ tãn-paalɛ n ãn sõŋa vile ẽ,

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: tarɩ ẽ ka laagɛ yɔ-paalɛ pʋam tɩ a botɩ ba tũ tãmpĩa ĩyã, a mẽŋa ĩyã. Tɩ ba bilge kug-kãtɛ lige yɔɔgɔ la nõorɛ dee fõrgɛ.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Amaari n ze'ele Magdala, la Amaari ayẽma la daan zĩ mɛ tɩ ba nẽŋa tɔ yɔɔgɔ la.

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: Daar-sɛka tɩ Zifdõma mãasnɩ ba vo'osgo daarɛ la n tole tɩ beere wiige, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma lagsɛ taaba sẽŋɛ Apilatɩ zẽ'am,

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma tẽrɩ tɩ põmpã'asʋm dãana wã, ẽn daan nãn vɔa la, a daan yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn vo'oge dabsa atã daarɛ.›

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Bɛla, bɔ nõorɛ tɩ ba gu'ura yɔɔgɔ la sõŋa sõŋa ka paagɛ dabsa atã daarɛ, la sãn dagna bɛla, a karẽnbiisi la wʋn wa zũ kũm na mɛ dee yele nẽrba tɩ a vo'oge mɛ. Pã'asgɔ ẽna wã yãŋa wʋn dɛna be'em gãnna pɔspɔsɩ la.»

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Tɩ Apilatɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Tarɩ-ya gu'urba sẽŋɛ ka basɛ tɩ ba gu'ura yɔɔgɔ la wʋ yãma n boorɩ se'em na.»

ref: So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.
text: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka bɩ yɔɔgɔ la nõorɛ dee basɛ tɩ gu'urba gu'ura ẽ.

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Karẽnbiisi pia la ayɩla la daan sẽŋɛ la Galile, ka zom tãn-sɛka tɩ Azezi bɔ ba nõorɛ yetɩ ba sẽŋɛ la zuo.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la tẽŋa la kẽma la lagsɛ kʋ nõorɛ ayɩla, bɔ sʋdaasɩ la ligri zozo'e,

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: La ya zãsʋm ba tɩ ba sakɛ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ mam bɔ ya nõorɛ tɩ ya ɩta la. La bãŋɛ-ya tɩ mam boe la ya mɛ wakatɛ wuu, halɩ tɩ la ka paagɛ dũnia ba'asgɔ.»

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Da zoe-ya dabeem, sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele m sɔbiisi la yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ Galile, tɩ la de la bilam tɩ ba wʋn yẽ mam.»

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
text: Tɩ tẽŋa daan mĩim zozo'e, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ malɛka ze'ele saazuum sigum wa bilge kugre la bĩŋe kẽnkɛrŋa zẽa a zuo.

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
text: A nẽŋa daan yẽgrɩ wʋ sa-yãka. Tɩ a futo pelge ãna yɩlɩ yɩlɩ.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ sɛba n gu'uri yɔɔgɔ la. Tɩ ba gõgra dee ãna wʋ ba ki mɛ na.

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
text: Tɩ malɛka la yele pɔgsɩ la yetɩ: «Da zoe-ya dabeem, mam mi tɩ ya eerɩ la Azezi tɩ ba ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ la.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: dee sẽŋɛ-ya tɔtɔ ka yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, a vo'oge mɛ. La a wʋn dẽŋɛ ya nẽŋa sẽŋɛ Galile. Bilam tɩ ya wʋn ka yẽ ẽ. Sɛla tɩ mam tarɩ tɩ m yele ya n bɛla.»

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Tɩ Azezi darɛ tʋ'ʋsɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ ba. Tɩ ba lɛm ẽ ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam gʋrɛ a nãma nã'asɛ ẽ.

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Pɔgsɩ la n daan boe sore wɛ'ɛsa la, tɩ sɛba n gu'uri yɔɔgɔ la nẽrba basɛba sẽŋɛ tẽŋa pʋam ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma bõn-sɛba wuu n ẽŋɛ la.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: dee yele ba yetɩ: «Yele-ya nẽrba la yetɩ, yʋ'ʋŋɔ tɩ yãma gã gĩsra dee tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la wa'am wa zũ kũm na.

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la to'oge ligri la, dee ẽŋɛ wʋ ban yele ba se'em na. Tɩ yetɔg-ẽŋa saagɛ Zifdõma la tẽŋasʋka halɩ wa paam zĩna. Azezi puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi (Amarkɩ

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Tɩ Azezi lɛm ba, yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ bɔ mam pãŋa wuu n boe saazuum, la tẽŋa wã zuo mɛ.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Bɛla, sẽŋɛ-ya dũnia wuu ka botɩ nẽrba buuri wuu lebge mam karẽnbiisi. Mise-ya ba ko'om pʋam, la Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa, la a Sɩa yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: La ba yẽ ẽ mɛ, ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam nã'asɛ ẽ, la basɛba daan sẽ'enɩ mɛ.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Tɩ ba basɛ ba yʋgsɩ la bilam mẽŋa, dee dɔla ẽ.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ fẽe yẽ Azebede dayɔɔsɩ Azakɩ la a yɩbga Azã n boe õorŋɔ pʋam malgra ba yʋgsɩ.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Tɩ Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ darɛ saagɛ Galile so'olʋm zẽ'a woo. Azezi botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm (Amatie

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: La ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn põn gʋlsɛ se'em, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, mam wʋn tõm m tõntõnna tɩ a dẽŋɛ fʋ nẽŋa ka malgɛ sore bɔ fɔ.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Azã daan yɛ la fuugo n de yʋgma kõbrɔ, dee dɩkɛ sagãnnɛ lʋ a sɩa. A daan diti la seero la saniiro.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: A daan mõolɩ yetɩ: «Nẽra n boe mam poorʋm sẽna, ẽŋa tarɩ pãŋa gãnna mam mɛ. Mam ka masɛ tɩ m dõbrɛ a nẽŋam pirge a tagra.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi sẽŋɛ Kapɛrnaʋm. Tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ paagɛ, tɩ Azezi kẽ ba wẽndeem zãsna nẽrba.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: La zãsŋɔ la di'ige nẽrba la mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a zãsŋɔ la daan tarɩ la pãŋa, la ka ãn wʋ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n zãsnɩ se'em na.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa Azezi ze'ele la Nazarɛtɩ n boe Galile so'olʋm wa'am Azã zẽ'am, tɩ a mise ẽ Zʋrdẽ ko'om na pʋam.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Tɩ Azezi ze'ele ko'om na pʋam dɔɔra, yẽ tɩ saazuum darɛ yo'oge, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ãna wʋ na'adawenne na sige a zuo,

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Bʋraa ayɩla n de kõnkonne n wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam sose ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn bɔɔra, fʋ tã wʋn basɛ tɩ m yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ le ãna sõŋa.»

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: «Wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm lɛm mɛ. Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero dee sakɛ kõ-yẽlga la.»

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Tɩ a darɛ wi ba, tɩ ba basɛ ba sɔ Azebede la a tõntõnɩba õorŋɔ la pʋam, dee dɔla ẽ. Kʋlkã'arga n tarɩ bʋraa tɩ Azezi botɩ a yese (Aluki

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ lɛm ẽ, gʋrɛ a nu'ugo botɩ a isge. Tɩ bã'a la basɛ ẽ, tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba sãanɔ.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: La ba daan yẽ ẽ mɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽrba wuu eerɩ fʋ mɛ.»

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Tɩ bʋraa daan bɔna wẽndeem na tɩ kʋlkã'arga tara ẽ, tɩ bʋraa la kaasɛ yetɩ:

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: «Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra, fõn bẽm yelle n boe la tõma? Fõn wa'am tɩ fʋ sã'am na tõma? Mam mi fõn de sɛka. Fõn de la pʋpeelem dãana n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.»

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Tɩ Azezi tãsɛ kʋlkã'arga la yetɩ: «Go'oge, yese dee basɛ ẽ.»

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la bigse bʋraa la tɩ a gõgra, dee kaasɛ tagurga yese dee basɛ ẽ.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Tɩ ba wuu zɛ yɛrɩ, sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Wãna de la bẽm? La de la zãsŋɔ paalga n tarɩ pãŋa. A tɔgrɩ la kʋlkã'arsɩ, tɩ ba sakra a nõorɛ.»

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛba n lɛm na tɩ m ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bilam mẽ, tɩ la de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam wa'am.»

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Tɩ a ninbãalga yõkɛ Azezi, tɩ a tẽegɛ a nu'ugo kalʋm ẽ yetɩ: «Mam bɔɔra, yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa.»

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Tɩ Azezi la a nẽrba daan yese Zifdõma wẽndeem na, tɩ Azakɩ la Azã doose ba, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Asimõ la A'ãndre dõma yire.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Tɩ Asimõ dɛɛm-pɔka ĩyã ba tʋlgɛ, tɩ a gã. Tɩ Azezi paagɛ, tɩ ba darɛ tɔgɛ pɔka la yelle bɔ ẽ.

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Tɩ wẽnnɛ wa lui sobe, tɩ ba tarɩ bã'adõma wuu, la sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ tarɩ ba wa'am Azezi zẽ'am.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba wuu lagsɛ taaba yire la zanõrʋm.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Tɩ Azezi botɩ nẽrba zozo'e tɩ bã'asɩ to'oto'ore tarɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee dige kʋlkã'arsɩ zozo'e, tɩ ba yese. La a ka sakɛ tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la tɔgɛ, se'ere n soe la, ba põn mina ẽ mɛ. Azezi mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga Galile (Aluki

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Azezi daan isge la bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ zẽ'a tɩ nẽrba ka boe, tɩ a ka pʋ'ʋsɛ.

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Tɩ Asimõ la nẽr-sɛba n daan boe la ẽ na yese ɛɛra ẽ.

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Tɩ a kaagɛ Galile so'olʋm wuu mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, dee dige kʋlkã'arsɩ tɩ ba yese dee basɛ nẽrba. Azezi botɩ kõnkonne yẽ ĩmã'asʋm (Amatie

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Tɩ bã'a la basɛ ẽ bilam mẽŋa, tɩ a le ãna sõŋa.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ a fõrgɛ, dee kã'an ẽ yetɩ:

ref: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
text: «Da kãn yele nẽra nẽra, la sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽema zẽ'am ka pa'alɛ ẽ fɔ mẽŋa tɩ a bɩsɛ, dee bɔ ẽ bõn-sɛba tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ yetɩ ba bɔ la, tɩ la pa'alɛ ba tɩ fʋ sɩrɩ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.»

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: La bʋraa la fõrgɛ mɛ ka dɩkɛ yelle la mõolɛ pa'alɛ la ẽŋɛ se'em. Bɛla tɩ Azezi daan ka le tãna wʋn kẽ tẽŋa pʋam, sãn dagna tɩ a li'ise mɛ kẽ. A daan boe la zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ nẽrba ka boe mĩ na, tɩ nẽrba daan zɛ'ɛta zẽ'a woo sẽnna a zẽ'am.

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: wakat-sɛka tɩ Abiata de Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la? A yʋʋn kẽ la Nawẽnnɛ deego la pʋam dɩkɛ borborɩ sɛba tɩ ba bĩŋe bɔ Nawẽnnɛ na obe. La sisri mɛ, nẽra nẽra ka obrɩ ba, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma mã'a. La Adavidi yʋʋn obe ba mɛ, dee bɔ nẽr-sɛba n boe a zẽ'am na.»

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: lagsɛ halɩ zozo'e tɩ zẽ'a ka bɔna, baa deego la nõorʋm. Tɩ a tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ'ɔra ba.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Tɩ nẽrba banaasɩ zɛ kɔɔrŋɔ wa'am Azezi zẽ'am.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: «Bẽm tɩ ẽna tɔgra bɛla? A tɔgrɩ pɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ. Ãn tã wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ mã'a?»

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: Bɛla m wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la tarɩ pãŋa dũnia wã zuo mɛ, wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ.» Azezi yele bɛla, dee yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ:

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.

text: Azezi yele bɛla dee le yele ba yetɩ: «La de la nẽrsaala ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ vo'osgo daarɛ, a ka ẽŋɛ nẽrsaala vo'osgo daarɛ ĩyã.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Alevi yire dita, tɩ lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba la tõon-be'ero dõma zozo'e lagɩm na Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la dita, se'ere n soe la, nẽr-bãma buuri n daan zo'oge dɔla ẽ.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n de Farisi dõma la yẽ Azezi n diti la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma la, tɩ ba soke a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ a lagɩm na lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma dita?»

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Azã karẽnbiisi, la Farisi dõma daan lʋtɩ la nõorɛ. Tɩ ba wa'am wa soke Azezi yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi, la Farisi dõma karẽnbiisi la lʋta nõorɛ dee tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi la ka lʋta nõorɛ?»

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ ba wʋn yese pɔgdita la ba zẽ'am, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yãŋa wʋn lʋ nõorɛ.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Nẽra kãn dɩkɛ tãn-paalɛ lige fu-kɛka. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, tãn-paalga la wʋn botɩ fu-kɛka la ãagɛ mɛ gãnna kʋrʋm na.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: La nẽra mẽ kãn dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm wɔ-kɛgsɩ pʋam. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, dã-paalga la sãn kɛlla, ba wʋn botɩ wɔɔsɩ la tãsɛ mɛ, tɩ dãam na, la wɔɔsɩ la wuu sã'am. La masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm na wɔ-paalsɩ pʋam.» Vo'osgo daarɛ yelle (Amatie

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma nãn ka karẽŋɛ Adavidi la a nẽrba la kɔm n yʋʋn dõnnɩ tɩ ba bɔɔra dia tɩ ba di, tɩ Adavidi yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em,

text: Bɛla, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n soe sɛla woo, baa la vo'osgo daarɛ.»

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, ba vuta õorŋɔ lɛm na tɩ nẽrba la da wa mike ẽ.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Tɩ ba bɩsra Azezi wʋn botɩ bʋraa la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a kãn botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Ba daan eerɩ la ba doose bilam dõrɛ ẽ.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.

ref: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,

text: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n ze'ele Zerizalɛm wa'am wa yetɩ: «Abelzebuli n dolɩ ẽ, la de la ẽŋa n de kʋlkã'arsɩ naba la pãŋa tɩ a tarɩ digra kʋlkã'arsɩ tɩ ba yɛsra.»

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n daan zĩ kaagɛ ẽ, tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ ma, la fʋ yɩbsɩ n wãna n boe yẽŋa ɛɛra fɔ.»

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Tɩ Azezi wi ba, makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Asũtãana wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne dige Asũtãana?

text: La Azezi daan kule la yire, tɩ nẽrba le lagsɛ zozo'e. Tɩ Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la ka yẽ folle wʋn di.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.

text: La yire ayɩla dõma sãn welge zɛbra taaba, yi-ẽŋa wʋn wõrgɛ mɛ.

text: La Asũtãana sãn isge zɛbɛ la a mẽŋa welge, a pãŋa la ba'asɛ mɛ, a kãn tã'agɛ ze'ele.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: La nẽra ka tã wʋn kẽ pãŋa dãana yire ka vaagɛ a lɔgrɔ, sãn dagna tɩ a dẽŋɛ lʋ pãŋa dãana la mɛ, dee vaagɛ a lɔgrɔ la.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: La nẽra sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, Nawẽnnɛ kãn malɩn basɛ ẽ taalɛ. Tõon-be'e-ẽna taalɛ boe la a dãana zuo wakatɛ wuu.»

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew

text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ãne n de mam ma, la ãndõma n de mam yɩbsɩ?»

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm, bɩɩ tɩ wʋn yetɩ a wõn na bẽm?

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.

text: La zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõnnɩ kõ-yẽlga la sakra, wɔm biɛ. Tɩ basɛba wɔm pitã-tã, tɩ basɛba pisyoob-yoobɩ, tɩ basɛba kɔbga kɔbga.» Fɩtla makrɛ (Aluki

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?

text: A le yele ba yetɩ: «Mõrgɛ-ya wõm yãma n selsrɩ se'em na võorɛ. Bõn-sɛka tɩ ya dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ nẽrba la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn makɛ la bɛla lebse ya, le makɛ pa'asɛ.

text: La biɛ la sãn bɩ, kaara la sɛ'ɛrɩ ba mɛ, se'ere n soe la, bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ paagɛ mɛ.» Sɔ'ɔrɔ biire makrɛ (Amatie

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: La ba sãn bʋrɛ ẽ, a bɩtɩ mɛ puse wil-kãra gãnna zẽvõorɔ buuri wuu. Tɩ niinto tãna wʋn tɛ ba tʋgrɔ a zuo a mã'asʋm pʋam.»

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: A ka tɔgɛ bɔ ba tɩ la sãn dagna magsa. La a sãn yese la a karẽnbiisi la mã'a kẽnkɛrŋa, tɩ a pa'alɩ ba yɛla la wuu võorɛ. Azezi botɩ kʋsebgo yõkɛ (Amatie

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya zɔta dabeem bɛla? Bẽm tɩ ya ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra?»

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”

text: A daan nãmsɛ mɛ zozo'e tɩbdõma zẽ'am, dɩkɛ ẽn tarɩ sɛla wuu sã'am, dee tɩ la ka sõna, la malɩn gãnna la bɛla.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Tɩ a kẽ yire la yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya vɔɔla dee kɛlla? Bia la ka ki, a gĩsri mɛ.»

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.

text: Tɩ Azezi wõm ban yele se'em na, dee yele Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana la yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem, wen bɔ mam sɩra.»

text: ba ka bãŋɛ borborɩ bõn-yãlma la võorɛ, ba pʋtẽ'era ka like. Azezi botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm Zenezarɛtɩ tẽŋam (Amatie

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: La ban daan ze'ele õorŋɔ la pʋam dooge la, tɩ nẽrba la bãŋɛ Azezi tɔtɔ.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Tɩ ba zoe kẽ so'olʋm na wuu, tɩ ba zɛ bã'adõma ba bõn-gã'ala pʋam wa'am, bãma n wõm tɩ Azezi boe zẽ'e-sɛka la.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: La Azezi daan sãn ẽn sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka woo, tẽn-kãra, la tẽn-pɩgsɩ, la yiɛ n boe kẽnkɛrsɩ la, ba tarɩ la bã'adõma sẽnna gã'ata da'asɩ pʋam, dee bɛlna ẽ yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ bã'adõma la kalʋm a fuugo nõorɛ mã'a. La sɛba wuu n daan kalʋm a fuugo la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba n ze'ele Zerizalɛm daan wa'am wa lagsɛ Azezi zẽ'am,

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sɩrɩ zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ mɛ, dee tɩ ya dɔla ya mẽŋa malŋɔ.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Farisi dõma, la Zifdõma wuu sãn ka wobse nu'usi wʋ ba yaabdõma malŋɔ la, ba ka diti.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Ba sãn ze'ele da'am wa'am, ba wʋn peege ba mẽŋa mɛ dee di. La malŋɔ basɛba zozo'e tɩ ba ɩtɩ, la de la wama, la yɔa, la kuto laasɩ peere.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la soke Azezi yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi la ka sakra tɩ yaabdõma malŋɔ la, dita dia la nu'usi n ka ãn sõŋa?»

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «A'ezayi n yʋʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la tɔgɛ ya yelle la, a tarɩ bʋʋrɔ, yãma de la pʋlibsum dõma wʋ lan gʋlsɛ se'em na. La gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-bãna pẽgrɩ mam na nõorɛ yetɔga, la ba sũure la zãagɛ la mam mɛ.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: Ban nã'asrɩ mam na, la de la zãŋa. Se'ere n soe la, ba zãsnɩ la zãsŋɔ n de nẽrsaalba nõorɛ.›

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Ya basɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ mɛ dee dɔla nẽrsaalba malŋɔ.»

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: La yãma yetɩ: Nẽra sãn yele a sɔ, bɩɩ a ma yetɩ: Mam n tarɩ bõn-sɛba tɩ m sõŋɛ fʋ la de la ‹Kɔrbã›, ,

ref: You won't let those people help their parents.
text: la ka le masɛ tɩ a ɩta sɛla bɔ'ɔra a sɔ, la a ma.

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Yãma mẽŋa malŋɔ la tɩ ya zãsnɩ nẽrba la, botɩ ya basɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la dɛna la zãŋa. La bõnɔ zozo'e n wõm na bɛla tɩ ya ɩtɩ.»

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: Dee daan le yetɩ: «La de la bõn-sɛka n ze'etɩ nẽrsaala pʋam yɛsra n botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: La Azezi le wi la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ, tɩ ba wa'am a zẽ'am, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma wuu, selse-ya mam sõŋa wõm a võorɛ.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: La a daan ze'ele nẽrba la zẽ'am kẽ la deem. Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la soke ẽ makrɛ la võorɛ.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma yɛm mẽ ka like? Yãma ka bãŋɛ tɩ sɛla n ze'etɩ yẽŋa kẽ'era nẽrsaala pʋʋrʋm ka tã wʋn botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ?

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: Se'ere n soe la, bõn-bãma ka kẽ'erɩ sũure pʋam, ba kẽ'erɩ la pʋʋrɛ pʋam tɩ ba yẽ'era ba basra.»

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Bõn-be'ero bãna wuu ze'etɩ la nẽrsaala sũure pʋam botɩ a dɛna dẽgrɔ.» Pɔka n bɔ Azezi sɩra (Amatie

ref: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
text: Tɩ pɔka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, la de la sɩra, la baasɩ n boe ba dãana taablɩ tilum diti la kɔma di-zõra n luti la.»

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Azezi daan ze'ele zẽ'e-ẽŋa sẽŋɛ la Tiiri so'olʋm ka kẽ yire pʋam, dee ka bɔɔra tɩ nẽra bãŋɛ tɩ ẽŋa boe bilam. La a daan ka tã'agɛ sʋgɛ.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Tɩ pɔka ayɩla tɩ kʋlkã'arga daan dolɩ a pɔyʋa, wõm Azezi yelle, sẽŋɛ ka ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam,

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: sose ẽ yetɩ, a dige kʋlkã'arga la tɩ a yese dee basɛ a pɔyʋa la. Pɔka la daan de la Grɛkɩ, ba dɔgɛ ẽ na Fenisi tẽŋa n boe Siiri so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ tɩ kɔma dẽŋɛ di tɩgɛ, se'ere n soe la, la ka masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ kɔma dia bɔ baasɩ.»

ref: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn lerge se'em wã ĩyã, kule, tɩ kʋlkã'arga la basɛ fʋ pɔyʋa la.»

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Tɩ pɔka la daan lebe kule, la ẽn paagɛ yire la, a yẽ tɩ a pɔyʋa la gã la bõn-gã'ala pʋam, la kʋlkã'arga la sɩrɩ yese dee basɛ ẽ mɛ. Azezi botɩ bʋraa n de waŋa, dee ka tãna tɔgra sõŋa, tã'agɛ tɔgra dee wõnna

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Azezi le ze'ele Tiiri so'olʋm doose la Sidõ, doose Dekapɔlɩ tẽnsɩ pʋam lebe Galile mõgrɛ la bɔba.

ref: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
text: Tɩ ba tarɩ bʋraa n de waŋa, dee ka tãna tɔgra sõŋa wa'am a zẽ'am, wa belem ẽ yetɩ, a dɩkɛ a nu'ugo paglɛ waŋa la zuo.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Tɩ Azezi tarɩ ẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam yese kẽnkɛrŋa dɩkɛ a nu'ubiɛ tũm a tʋba pʋam, dee tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm kalʋm bʋraa la zɩlɩŋa.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Dee gorge saazuo sĩ'im vo'ose fõo, dee yele bʋraa la yetɩ: «Efata», a võorɛ de la «yo'oge!»

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ bʋraa la tʋba yo'oge, tɩ a zɩlɩŋa lakɛ, tɩ a tɔgra sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da tɔgɛ bɔ nẽra nẽra. La ẽn daan kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da tɔgɛ bɔ nẽra nẽra la, ba daan tõn tɔgra mɛ zozo'e.

ref: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
text: Tɩ la di'ige ba zozo'e halɩ tɩ ba yɛta tɩ: «A ẽŋɛ sɛla woo la sõŋa, botɩ wansɩ wõnna, tɩ mugro tɔgra.»

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ: «Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa la Farisi dõma, la naba A'erɔdɩ dãbɩllɛ la.»

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: A daan tɔgɛ ba la vẽelga vẽelga. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ wi ẽ yese kẽnkɛrŋa ka yele ẽ yetɩ, a da le tɔgɛ bɛla.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: «Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ wã ninbãalga n tarɩ mam, se'ere n soe la, dabsa atã tɩ ba boe mam zẽ'am dee ka tara dia wʋn di.

ref: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
text: Mam sãn basɛ ba tɩ ba kule ba yiɛ la kɔm, ba wʋn ka vibe mɛ sore, se'ere n soe la, basɛba ze'ele la zẽ'a n zãagɛ wa'am.»

ref: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
text: Tɩ a botɩ nẽrba la zĩ'ire tẽŋa. Dee dɩkɛ borborɩ bayopɔɩ la pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee fiise bɔ a karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba pʋɩ nẽrba la. Tɩ ba pʋɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la.

ref: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
text: La ba daan tarɩ la zũn-pɩgsɩ mẽ fẽe. Tɩ Azezi pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ zũma la ĩyã, dee botɩ a karẽnbiisi la dɩkɛ pʋɩ nẽrba la.

ref: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
text: dee tɩ ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi la kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Dalmanuta so'olʋm. Farisi dõma pʋlibsum yelle (Amatie

ref: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma daan wa'am wa wẽ'era nõke'ene la Azezi, yetɩ ba makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ, a botɩ bõn-yãlma ze'ele saazuo ẽŋɛ n pa'alɩ tɩ ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ yire.

ref: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la daan yĩm ka dɩkɛ borborɩ, sãn dagna borborɩ ayɩla mã'a tɩ ba tarɩ õorŋɔ la pʋam.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
text: Tɩ Azezi daan bãŋɛ, soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya tɔgra la taaba tɩ la de la yãma n ka tarɩ borborɩ la ĩyã? Yãma ka wõm a võorɛ? Yãma yɛm ka like?

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: Tɩ Azezi gʋrɛ fʋa la nu'ugo ve'ege ẽ tẽŋa la pʋam yese kẽnkɛrŋa, ka tʋbsɛ nõtõorʋm ẽŋɛ a nini, dee dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ a zuo, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽtɩ sɛla mɛ bɩɩ?»

ref: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
text: Tɩ bʋraa la dɔ'ɔgɛ zuugo bɩsɛ, yetɩ: «Mam yẽtɩ nẽrba tɩ ba ãna wʋ tɩɩsɩ la, la ba sẽnnɩ mɛ.»

ref: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
text: Tɩ Azezi le dɩkɛ a nu'usi kalʋm fʋa la nini. Tɩ a nini pike yɩlɩŋɛ, tɩ a yẽta sɛla woo sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
text: Tɩ Azezi basɛ ẽ yetɩ a kule, a da le kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam. Apɩyɛɛrɩ pa'alɛ Azezi n de nẽr-sɛka (Amatie

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
text: Tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «La yãma yetɩ mam de la ãne?» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge yetɩ: «Fõn de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na.»

ref: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
text: Tɩ Azezi kã'an ba yetɩ, ba da tɔgɛ ẽŋa yelle bɔ nẽra nẽra. Azezi dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ a nãmsgɔ, la a kũm yelle (Amatie

ref: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yãŋa zãsna a karẽnbiisi la, yele ba yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la nãmsɛ zozo'e. Tẽŋa wã kẽma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma nifo wʋn kẽ mam mɛ, tɩ ba kʋ mam, la dabsa atã daarɛ mam wʋn vo'oge mɛ.»

ref: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Tɩ Azezi gelge bɩsɛ a karẽnbiisi la, dee tãsɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asũtãana, sẽŋɛ lebe mam poorʋm, se'ere n soe la, fʋ tẽ'esgɔ la dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ tẽ'esgɔ, la de la nẽrsaala tẽ'esgɔ.»

ref: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Dee wi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, la a karẽnbiisi la a zẽ'am, yele ba yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a doose mam, dẽnɩ tɩ a basɛ a mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, dee sakɛ nãmsgɔ daarɛ woo wʋ nẽra n buke a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a gu a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka woo n sakɛ tɩ a koŋe a vɔm mam ĩyã, la kõ-yẽlga la ĩyã, a wʋn yẽ vɔm.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself?
text: Nẽra sãn yẽ dũnia sɛla woo dee koŋe vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a yõorɔ de la bẽm?

ref: What could you give to get back your soul?
text: Bẽm tɩ nẽrsaala wʋn dɩkɛ teege vɔm? Sɛla kaɩ.

ref: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n zoe yãnnɛ mam ĩyã, la mam yetɔga la ĩyã, zamãana ẽna wã n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ la sɩra, dee dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma wã tẽŋasʋka, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn zoe yãnnɛ na ẽ, wakat-sɛka tɩ a wʋn leme wa'am la a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa, la a malɛkadõma la.»

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ A'eli põn wa'am mɛ, tɩ nẽrba ẽŋɛ ẽ ban boorɩ se'em, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ a yelle Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.» Kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ bia, tɩ Azezi dige ẽ tɩ a yese (Amatie

ref: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
text: La Azezi daan kẽ la deem, ẽŋa la a karẽnbiisi la mã'a, tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ tõma ka tã'agɛ dige kʋlkã'ar-ẽna?»

ref: Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said,
text: Azezi yele bɛla dee tarɩ la bi-pɩka wa ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka, dɩkɛ a nu'usi kɔkɛ bia la, dee yele ba yetɩ:

ref: And his clothes became much whiter than any bleach on earth could make them.
text: Tɩ a futo pelge yɩlɩ yɩlɩ yẽgra halɩ, tɩ nẽra ka bɔna dũnia wã zuo tãna wʋn peege tɩ ba pelge bɛla.

ref: Then Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
text: Tɩ A'eli, la Amoyisi puke ba zẽ'am tɔgra la Azezi.

ref: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Tɩ sawatɛ wa'am wa lile ba la a mã'asʋm. Tɩ kõa ze'ele sawatɛ la pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ. Selse-ya a yetɔga.»

ref: At once the disciples looked around, but they saw only Jesus.
text: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ ba bɩsɛ kaagɛ ka le yẽ nẽra, sãn dagna Azezi mã'a n boe ba zẽ'am.

ref: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
text: Tɩ ba to'oge ẽn yele se'em na, dee sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Kũm vo'ore võorɛ de la bẽm?»

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Tɩ ba soke Azezi yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ A'eli dẽŋɛ wa'am, tɩ Krisi yãŋa wa'am poorʋm?»

ref: Jesus answered: Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected?
text: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Asɩra, A'eli n wʋn dẽŋɛ wa'am wa malgɛ sɛla woo. La bẽm tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la yelle yetɩ, a wʋn nãmsɛ mɛ zozo'e dee yẽ pɔ'ɔrɛ?

ref: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
text: Ban lebe ka lɛm karẽnbiisi basɛba la zẽ'am na, ba yẽ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e n kaagɛ ba, tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba wẽ'era nõkɛ'ɛma la ba.

ref: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
text: Tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ wa'am a zẽ'am. La kʋlkã'arga la n yẽ Azezi la, bilam mẽŋa a dãan bia la mɛ, tɩ a lui tẽŋa bilna, tɩ nõtõn-fulunfuka yɛsra a nõorʋm.

ref: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
text: «Nẽra sãn to'oge bi-pɩka ẽna tadãana mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, a to'oge la mam mẽŋa, la nẽra sãn to'oge mam mẽŋa, la dagɩ mam tɩ a to'oge, a to'oge la sɛka n tõm mam na.»

ref: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
