ref: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”

ref: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
text: Tɩ Azezi soke bia la sɔ yetɩ: «Wakat-kãna tɩ bõn-ẽna ẽŋɛ ẽ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «A bõnbɩ'ɩlʋm.

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: La Azezi daan yẽ tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n zotɩ sẽna a zẽ'am, tɩ a tãsɛ kʋlkã'arga la yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga n botɩ nẽra dɛna muko la waŋa, mam yetɩ fʋ yese dee basɛ ẽ, da le doose ẽ.»

ref: The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.

text: La nẽra sãn botɩ kɔm-pɩgsɩ bãna wã n bɔ mam sɩra la ayɩla basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ a wʋn yẽ la, ba sãn dɩkɛ nẽerɛ kug-kãtɛ lʋ yulse a yugla lobe ẽ basɛ ko-kãtɛ pʋam n sõɩ.

ref: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.

ref: But in the beginning God made a man and a woman.

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married.

text: Bɛla, sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lagɩm na taaba, nẽrsaala da welge ba.»

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.

ref: I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.”
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n ka sakɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɔna ẽ wʋ bia la, a kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.»

ref: When Jesus saw this, he became angry and said, “Let the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these little children belong to the kingdom of God.
text: La Azezi n yẽ bɛla la, la ka ẽŋɛ a sũure yẽlʋm, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya kɔm-pɩgsɩ la tɩ ba wa'am mam zẽ'am, da gu'ura-ya ba, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm de la nẽr-sɛba n ãn wʋ kɔma wã bõnɔ.

ref: Jesus looked closely at the man. He liked him and said, “There's one thing you still need to do. Go sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come with me.”

ref: When the man heard Jesus say this, he went away gloomy and sad because he was very rich.

ref: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
text: Tɩ Azezi bɩsɛ a karẽnbiisi la kaagɛ, dee yele ba yetɩ: «La tɔ mɛ bɔ bõntatba tɩ ba kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.»

ref: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
text: Tɩ a yetɔga la di'ige a karẽnbiisi la. Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «M kɔma, la tɔ mɛ tɩ nẽra kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”

ref: Jesus' disciples were even more amazed. They asked each other, “How can anyone ever be saved?”

ref: Jesus looked at them and said, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Tɩ Azezi bɩsra ba dee yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽna, la Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn ẽŋɛ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ tã sɛla woo ɩta mɛ.»

ref: The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:

ref: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,

ref: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya ka mi yãma n sosrɩ sɛla. Ya wʋn tã'agɛ nãmsɛ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam nãmsrɩ wã, bɩɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ nãmsɛ wʋ mam n sakɛ tɩ m nãmsɛ se'em na halɩ ka ki?»

ref: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
text: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla.» Tɩ Azezi lebe yele ba yetɩ: «Asɩra, ya wʋn nãmsɛ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam nãmsrɩ wã halɩ ka ki.

ref: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
text: La, la dagɩ mam n wʋn looge sɛka n wʋn zĩ'ire mam zʋʋgɔ bɩɩ mam gɔbga. Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm zẽ'e-bãma tɩ a bɔ la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ la.»

ref: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e tãsɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Go'oge fʋ yetɔga la.» Tɩ a malɩn tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Adavidi yɩɩŋa, zoe m ninbãalga.»

ref: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”

text: Tɩ a lobe a fugɔbga basɛ, dee vaa isge sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am.

ref: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
text: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka yẽ bõn-biire la n lʋ zanõrʋm sore nõorʋm, tɩ ba lorge ẽ.

text: Guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ nẽrba wʋn tarɩ ya mɛ sẽŋɛ ka sãm ba sarɩyadia zẽ'esʋm, tɩ ba wẽ ya ba wẽndeto pʋam. La ba wʋn tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ gɔmnadõma, la na'adõma nẽŋam mam ĩyã. La ya wʋn tɔgɛ mam yɛla pa'alɛ ba.

ref: But before the end comes, the good news must be preached to all nations.
text: La dẽnɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la dẽŋɛ mõolɛ bɔ buuri wuu, dee tɩ dũnia ba'asgɔ paagɛ.

ref: When you are arrested, don't worry about what you will say. You will be given the right words when the time comes. But you will not really be the ones speaking. Your words will come from the Holy Spirit.
text: La ba sãn wa yõgɛ ya tarɩ sẽŋɛ ka sãm sarɩyadia zẽ'am, ya da fabla yãma n wʋn tɔgɛ se'em, wakatɛ sãn wa paagɛ, tɔgɛ-ya yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya la, se'ere n soe la, la dagɩ ya mẽŋa n wʋn tɔgɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wʋn sõŋɛ ya.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray each other and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Sɔbia wʋn botɩ ba yõkɛ sɔbia kʋ, tɩ sɔ mẽ wʋn ẽŋɛ a bia bɛla, la kɔma wʋn zɛbra la ba sɔdõma, la ba madõma botɩ ba kʋ ba.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you keep on being faithful right to the end, you will be saved. The Horrible Thing (Matthew
text: Nẽrba zozo'e wʋn sise ya mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã. La nẽr-sɛka n wʋn mõrgɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: If you are on the roof of your house, don't go inside to get anything.
text: La nẽr-sɛka n boe gɔsgɔ da sige bɔɔra tɩ a kẽ deem dɩkɛ sɛla.

ref: If the Lord doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of his chosen and special ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Nawẽnnɛ sãn ka bo'oge nɩ dabs-bãma sõorɛ, nẽra ka boe nɩ n wʋn põsɛ. La Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa nẽrba tɩ a looge la ĩyã, tɩ a bo'oge dabs-bãma sõorɛ.

text: La nẽr-sɛka n boe va'am da kãn lebe kule yire tɩ a dɩkɛ a fuugo.

ref: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Dabs-bãma, la wʋn dɛna la toogo bɔ pɔg-sɛba n tarɩ pʋa la, la sɛba n tarɩ kɔma tɩ ba mõgra la.

ref: Pray that it won't happen in winter.
text: Sose-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ yel-bãma da wa dɛna ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ.

text: Guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ mam dẽŋɛ yele ya sɛla woo mɛ.» Azezi n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la lemŋo (Amatie

text: Saazuum la tẽŋa wʋn tole mɛ, la mam yetɔga kãn malɩn tole.

ref: He will send his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the earth. A Lesson from a Fig Tree (Matthew
text: La a wʋn tõm a malɛkadõma mɛ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ dũnia gɔbga la zʋʋgɔ, la dapoore la zanõrɛ, ka lagsɛ nẽr-sɛba tɩ a looge la halɩ ka paagɛ dũnia tɛka wuu.»

ref: No one knows the day or the time. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: Nẽra nẽra ka mi daar-sɛka la wakat-sɛka tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn leme wa'am. Baa malɛkadõma n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, bɩɩ Dayʋa la mẽŋa ka mi, sãn dagna Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mã'a.

ref: So be alert! You don't know when the master of the house will come back. It could be in the evening or at midnight or before dawn or in the morning.

ref: So watch out and be ready! You don't know when the time will come.

ref: But if he comes suddenly, don't let him find you asleep.
text: Guuse-ya tɩ yidãana la da wa'am wa di'ige ya, tɩ ya gĩsri mɛ.

ref: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
text: La wʋn ãna wʋ bʋraa n we'esɩ sore dee dɩkɛ a yire gu'ule a tõntõnɩba, bɔ tõntõnna woo a tõonɛ, dee yele zanõr-gu'ura tɩ a gu'ura.

ref: I tell everyone just what I have told you. Be alert!
text: Mam n yetɩ ya se'em na, mam yetɩ nẽrba wuu la bɛla, gu'ura-ya.»

text: Dee yetɩ: «M Sɔ, fõn tã sɛla woo ɩta, basɛ tɩ nãmsgɔ ẽna fõrgɛ dee basɛ ma. La, la da kãn dɛna mam n boorɩ se'em, la dɛna fõn boorɩ se'em.»

ref: Jesus took along Peter, James, and John. He was sad and troubled and

text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka darɛ wa'am Azakari zẽ'am wa zɛ tɩdarɛ yõ'a bĩmbĩnne na zʋʋgɔ bɔba.

ref: Zechariah was confused and afraid when he saw the angel.
text: La Azakari n yẽ ẽ na, a palẽŋa n ẽkɛ halɩ, tɩ dabeem kẽ ẽ.

ref: But the angel told him: Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayers. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you must name him John.
text: Tɩ malɛka la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azakari, da zoe dabeem, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wõm fʋ pʋ'ʋsgɔ mɛ. Fʋ pɔga A'elizabɛtɩ wʋn dɔgɛ la budibla, tɩ fʋ sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azã.

ref: His birth will make you very happy, and many people will be glad.
text: La fʋ wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm. La nẽrba zozo'e wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga bia la dɔka ĩyã.

ref: Your son will be a great servant of the Lord. He must never drink wine or beer, and the power of the Holy Spirit will be with him from the time he is born.
text: A wʋn dɛna la nẽr-keko Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ nifum, la a kãn yũ dãam bɩɩ bõn-yũula n bugri. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋn bɔna la ẽ halɩ lan pɔsɛ ẽn boe a ma pʋʋrʋm, la wakatɛ wuu.

ref: John will lead many people in Israel to turn back to the Lord their God.
text: A wʋn botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba zozo'e lebe sakɛ ba Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: He will go ahead of the Lord with the same power and spirit that Elijah had. And because of John, parents will be more thoughtful of their children. And people who now disobey God will begin to think as they ought to. This is how John will get people ready for the Lord.
text: La a wʋn dẽŋɛ Zuudãana nẽŋam tara Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'eli n daan tarɩ se'em na. La a wʋn naagɛ sɔdõma la ba kɔma yɛla, dee wʋn botɩ nẽr-sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la tara yɛm ɩta wʋ sɛba n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ la. A wʋn botɩ nẽrba mãasʋm sõŋa gu'ura tɩ Zuudãana.»

ref: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know this is going to happen? My wife and I are both very old.”
text: Tɩ Azakari soke malɛka la yetɩ: «Bẽm n wʋn botɩ mam bãŋɛ tɩ bõn-ẽna de la sɩra? Se'ere n soe la, mam de la bʋra-yã'aŋa dee tɩ mam pɔga mẽ kʋrgɛ.»

ref: The angel answered, “I am Gabriel, God's servant, and I was sent to tell you this good news.
text: Tɩ malɛka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Agabrɩyɛlɩ, mam ze la Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam. Ẽŋa n tõm mam yetɩ, m wa'am yele fʋ yetɔg-yẽlga wã.

ref: You have not believed what I have said. So you will not be able to say a thing until all this happens. But everything will take place when it is supposed to.”
text: La fõn ka sakɛ mam yetɔga la ĩyã, fʋ nõorɛ n wʋn mãam. La wakatɛ sãn paagɛ, bõn-sɛba tɩ mam yele fʋ la wʋn ẽŋɛ. La fʋ kãn le tãna tɔgra, halɩ tɩ la paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ bõn-bãna wʋn ẽŋɛ la.»

ref: The crowd was waiting for Zechariah and kept wondering why he was staying in the temple so long.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa, nẽrba la daan gu'uri mɛ tɩ Azakari yese. La ẽn daan boe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la pʋam yuura la, ba palẽŋa n ẽkɛ.

ref: When he did come out, he could not speak, and they knew he had seen a vision. He motioned to them with his hands, but did not say a thing.
text: La ẽn yãŋa wa yese la, a ka tã'agɛ tɔgɛ la nẽrba la. A nõorʋm n mãam tɩ a tara nu'usi magra pa'ala ba. Bɛla tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ a yẽ la bõn-yãlma zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la pʋam.

ref: When Zechariah's time of service in the temple was over, he went home.
text: Dabs-sɛba tɩ Azakari daan wʋn tõm Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam n ba'asɛ la, a daan lebe kule la a yire.

ref: “What the Lord has done for me will keep people from looking down on me.” An Angel Tells about the Birth of Jesus
text: Dee yetɩ: «Wãna de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ tõoma. Wakat-ẽna ẽŋa n yãŋa bɩsɛ mam yelle, dee yese bõn-ẽna n de nɩ yãnnɛ bɔ mam nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.» Malɛka n tɔgɛ Azezi dɔka yelle

ref: The angel greeted Mary and said, “You are truly blessed! The Lord is with you.”
text: Tɩ malɛka la sẽŋɛ Amaari zẽ'am ka pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ tõoma. Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ boe la fʋ mɛ, a ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.»

ref: Then the angel told Mary, “Don't be afraid! God is pleased with you,
text: Tɩ malɛka la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Amaari, da zoe dabeem, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

ref: and you will have a son. His name will be Jesus.
text: Fʋ wʋn yõkɛ la pʋʋrɛ dɔgɛ budibla, sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi.

ref: He will be great and will be called the Son of God Most High. The Lord God will make him king, as his ancestor David was.
text: A wʋn dɛna la nẽr-keko. La ba wʋn wi'ira ẽ tɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu Bia. La Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ẽ a yaaba Adavidi na'am,

ref: He will rule the people of Israel forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
text: tɩ a dɛna Azakɔbɩ buuri naba wakatɛ wuu, a na'am na kãn malɩn ba'asɛ.»

ref: Mary asked the angel, “How can this happen? I am not even married!”
text: Tɩ Amaari soke malɛka la yetɩ: «Mam ka mi bʋraa, la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ mam yõkɛ pʋʋrɛ?»

ref: The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come down to you, and God's power will come over you. So your child will be called the holy Son of God.
text: Tɩ malɛka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla wuu Sɩa wʋn sige fʋ zuo, la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa wʋn bɔna la fɔ, bɛla bia la tɩ fʋ wʋn dɔgɛ la wʋn welge la to'ore, ba wʋn wi'ira ẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.

ref: Your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a son, even though she is old. No one thought she could ever have a baby, but in three months she will have a son.
text: Bãŋɛ tɩ fʋ sõogɔ A'elizabɛtɩ tɩ ba yetɩ a de la kõndɔgrɛ la, a tarɩ la pʋʋrɛ wõr-sɩyoobɩ n wãna. Baa la ẽn kʋrgɛ la, a wʋn dɔgɛ la budibla.

ref: Mary said, “I am the Lord's servant! Let it happen as you have said.” And the angel left her. Mary Visits Elizabeth
text: Tɩ Amaari lerge yetɩ: «Mam de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ tõntõn-pɔka. Nawẽnnɛ botɩ la ẽŋɛ wʋ fõn yele se'em na.» Tɩ malɛka la fõrgɛ dee basɛ ẽ. Amaari sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ A'elizabɛtɩ

ref: A short time later Mary hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea.
text: La dabsa bãma pʋam, tɩ Amaari isge sẽŋɛ tɔtɔ paagɛ Zude so'olʋm tẽŋa ayɩla tɩ tãnsɩ boe mĩ.

ref: She went into Zechariah's home, where she greeted Elizabeth.
text: La ẽn paagɛ la, a kẽ la Azakari yire pʋ'ʋsɛ A'elizabɛtɩ.

ref: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, her baby moved within her. The Holy Spirit came upon Elizabeth.
text: La A'elizabɛtɩ n wõm a pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, bia la wẽrgɛ mɛ a pʋʋrʋm. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wa'am A'elizabɛtɩ zẽ'am la pãŋa,

ref: Then in a loud voice she said to Mary: God has blessed you more than any other woman! He has also blessed the child you will have.
text: tɩ a tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la fõn yel-sõnnɛ gãnna pɔgsɩ la wuu. La bia la tɩ fʋ wʋn dɔgɛ la, tarɩ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

ref: Why should the mother of my Lord come to me?
text: Mam de la ãne tɩ mam Zuudãana ma sẽna mam zẽ'am?

ref: As soon as I heard your greeting, my baby became happy and moved within me.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam wõm fõn pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, bia la n boe mam pʋʋrʋm na wẽrgɛ mɛ la sũ-yẽlga.

ref: The Lord has blessed you because you believed that he will keep his promise. Mary's Song of Praise
text: Fõn sakɛ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ a tɔgɛ bɔ fʋ la wʋn sɩrɩ ẽŋɛ la, fʋ yẽ zu-yẽlga.» Amaari n yõom yõoma pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ la yelle

ref: Mary said: With all my heart I praise the Lord,
text: Tɩ Amaari yele yetɩ: «Mam pẽgrɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ la m sũure wuu.

ref: and I am glad because of God my Savior.
text: Mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ n de mam Fãagra la ĩyã,

ref: God cares for me, his humble servant. From now on, all people will say God has blessed me.
text: se'ere n soe la, a bɩsɛ mam n de a tõntõn-pɔg-tarma la yelle mɛ. Bɛla lan pɔsɛ lɛɛlɛ wã wɛ'ɛsa, zamãana wuu nẽrba wʋn wi'ira mam tɩ zu-yẽlga dãana.

ref: God All-Powerful has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n tã wuu la n ẽŋɛ bõn-kãra bɔ mam. A de la pʋpeelem dãana.

ref: He always shows mercy to everyone who worships him.
text: La nẽr-sɛba n zotɩ ẽ na, a zotɩ ba ninbãalga mɛ zamãana wuu.

ref: The Lord has used his powerful arm to scatter those who are proud.
text: A dɩkɛ la a nu'ugo tõm tõon-kãra, dee botɩ zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma la zoe wɩrgɛ la ba zẽk-m-mẽŋa pʋtẽ'era la.

ref: God drags strong rulers from their thrones and puts humble people in places of power.
text: La sãn dɛna pãnsɩ dõma, a sikri ba mɛ ba na'am pʋam, dee zẽkra sik-m-mẽŋa dõma.

ref: God gives the hungry good things to eat, and sends the rich away with nothing.
text: La sɛba n ka tarɩ sɛla la, a bɔ'ɔrɩ ba bõntarsõm zɔ'ɔra mɛ, dee botɩ bõntatba lebe fõrgɛ dee ka to'oge sɛla sɛla.

ref: God helps his servant Israel and is always merciful to his people.
text: A tẽerɩ zɔta tõma Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba n de a tõntõnɩba la ninbãalga mɛ, dee sõŋra tõma,

ref: The Lord made this promise to our ancestors, to Abraham and his family forever!
text: wʋ ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tõma yaaba Abraham, la tõma sɔ-yaabdõma, la ba yɩɩsɩ la wuu tɩ a wʋn sõŋra tõma wakatɛ wuu se'em na.»

ref: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months. Then she went back home. The Birth of John the Baptist
text: La Amaari daan weege A'elizabɛtɩ zẽ'am ẽŋɛ wʋ wõr-sɩtã, dee yãŋa lebe kule. Azã dɔka yelle

ref: her neighbors and relatives heard how kind the Lord had been to her, and they too were glad.
text: Tɩ a sawiiba, la a yir-dõma wõm tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ zoe a ninbãalga zo'oge, tɩ ba wuu lagɩm ɩta sũ-yẽlga la ẽ.

ref: Eight days later they did for the child what the Law of Moses commands. They were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
text: Bia la dɔka dabsa anii daarɛ tɩ ba wa'am tɩ ba wã ẽ, daan bɔɔra tɩ ba sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ a sɔ Azakari yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John.”
text: La a ma ka sakɛ, yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, a yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn dɛna la Azã.»

ref: The people argued, “No one in your family has ever been named John.”
text: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Ya yir-dõma la pʋam nẽra ka boe mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azã.»

ref: So they motioned to Zechariah to find out what he wanted to name his son.
text: Tɩ ba yɩbsɛ la nu'usi soke bia la sɔ tɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽŋa n boorɩ tɩ ba sɩ bia la yʋ'ʋrɛ se'em.

ref: Zechariah asked for a writing tablet. Then he wrote, “His name is John.” Everyone was amazed.
text: Tɩ a botɩ ba bɔ ẽ dɔ-palaka, tɩ a to'oge gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «A yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Azã.» Tɩ la di'ige nẽrba la waa wuu.

ref: All the neighbors were frightened because of what had happened, and everywhere in the hill country people kept talking about these things.
text: Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ sɛba n lɛm bilam na wuu. Tɩ kõ-ẽŋa saagɛ Zude tẽn-sɛba tɩ tãnsɩ boe mĩ na wuu.

ref: Everyone who heard about this wondered what this child would grow up to be. They knew the Lord was with him. Zechariah Praises the Lord
text: Bãma wuu n daan wõm yɛla la, ba daan tẽ'esrɩ mɛ dee sɔkra yetɩ: «Bi-ẽna yetɩ a wa ãna la wãne?» Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa boe la ẽ mɛ. Azakari n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ nẽrba la yelle

ref: The Holy Spirit came upon Zechariah, and he began to speak:
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wa'am bia la sɔ Azakari zẽ'am la pãŋa. Tɩ a to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ yetɩ:

ref: Praise the Lord, the God of Israel! He has come to save his people.
text: «Basɛ-ya tɩ pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ Zuudãana la, se'ere n soe la, a wa'am mɛ wa bɩsɛ a nẽrba yelle, fãagɛ ba basɛ.

ref: Our God has given us a mighty Savior from the family of David his servant.
text: A bɔ tõma la Fãagra n tarɩ pãŋa n wʋn yese a tõntõnna Adavidi buuri la pʋam,

ref: Long ago the Lord promised by the words of his holy prophets
text: wʋ ẽŋa n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔgra kʋrʋm se'em na.

ref: to save us from our enemies and from everyone who hates us.
text: A yetɩ a wʋn fãagɛ tɔ tɩ bɛ'ɛba nu'usum, la nẽr-sɛba n sisri tõma la nu'usum.

ref: God said he would be kind to our people and keep his sacred promise.
text: Bɛla, a zoe tɩ yaabdõma ninbãalga mɛ, dee tẽegɛ ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la ba la yelle.

ref: that he would rescue us from our enemies. Then we could serve him without fear,
text: tɩ a wʋn fãagɛ tɔ tɩ bɛ'ɛba nu'usum, dee wʋn botɩ tɩ tõm a tõoma, tɩ dabeem kana.

ref: by being holy and good as long as we live.
text: Dee dɔla ẽ la pʋpeelem, dee ɩta tɩ la masra ẽn boorɩ se'em ka paagɛ tɩ vɔm tɛka.

ref: You, my son, will be called the prophet of God Most High. You will go ahead of the Lord to get everything ready for him.
text: La fõn, m bia, ba wʋn wi'ira fʋ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu nõtɔ'ɔsa. Bɛla ĩyã, fõn wʋn dẽŋɛ tɩ Zuudãana la nẽŋam malgɛ sore bɔ ẽ,

ref: You will tell his people that they can be saved when their sins are forgiven.
text: dee yele Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ a wʋn fãagɛ ba basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

ref: God's love and kindness will shine upon us like the sun that rises in the sky.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n zotɩ tõma ninbãalga la ĩyã tɩ a wʋn botɩ a peelem n ãn wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa n yẽkɛ bulika la,

ref: On us who live in the dark shadow of death this light will shine to guide us into a life of peace.
text: nẽegɛ bɔ nẽr-sɛba n boe lika pʋam, la nẽr-sɛba n zotɩ kũm dabeem na, dee pa'alɛ tɩ so-sɛka tɩ tɩ wʋn doose yẽ sũ-mã'asʋm.»

ref: As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel.
text: Tɩ bia la daan bɩta, tɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ pa'asra. A daan boe la weem ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ a wa'am Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la zẽ'am tɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽ.

ref: About that time Emperor Augustus gave orders for the names of all the people to be listed in record books.
text: Ban daan dɔgɛ Azã la, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ na-keko A'ogisti daan bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba kãalɛ a tẽŋa la wuu nẽrba.

ref: At this time a man named Simeon was living in Jerusalem. Simeon was a good man. He loved God and was waiting for him to save the people of Israel. God's Spirit came to him
text: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ bʋraa daan boe Zerizalɛm tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Asimeyõ. A de la nẽr-sõŋɔ dee sakra Nawẽnnɛ sõŋa, dee gu'ura Ɩsrayɛlɩ Fãagra la n sẽm na. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ẽ.

ref: These first records were made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
text: La daan de la pɔspɔsɩ n bɛla tɩ ba kãalɛ nẽrba la, wakat-sɛka tɩ Akirinusi daan de Siiri tẽnsɩ gɔmna la.

ref: Everyone had to go to their own hometown to be listed.
text: Tɩ nẽrba wuu kule ba mẽnsɩ tẽnsʋm tɩ ba kãalɛ ba.

ref: So Joseph had to leave Nazareth in Galilee and go to Bethlehem in Judea. Long ago Bethlehem had been King David's hometown, and Joseph went there because he was from David's family.
text: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ mẽ yese Nazarɛtɩ n boe Galile so'olʋm sẽŋɛ Bɛtelɛm n boe Zude so'olʋm pʋam, zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ ba yʋʋn dɔgɛ naba Adavidi. Se'ere n soe tɩ a sẽŋɛ bilam na, a de la naba Adavidi buuri.

ref: Mary was engaged to Joseph and traveled with him to Bethlehem. She was soon going to have a baby,
text: Ẽŋa la Amaari n de pɔg-sɛka n yetɩ a ele ẽ na sẽŋɛ mɛ tɩ ba kãalɛ ba, wakat-ẽŋa Amaari tarɩ la pʋʋrɛ.

ref: she gave birth to her first-born son. She dressed him in baby clothes and laid him on a bed of hay, because there was no room for them in the inn. The Shepherds
text: Tɩ a dɔgɛ a budib-kẽema, vile ẽ na fuugo gã'arɛ dũnsi yɔnnɔ daka pʋam. Se'ere n soe la, zẽ'a n ka boe sãama yire la pʋam tɩ ba wʋn gã'arɛ. Dũnsĩima la malɛkadõma yelle

ref: That night in the fields near Bethlehem some shepherds were guarding their sheep.
text: La tẽn-ẽŋa dũnsĩima daan boe la sẽkam gu'ura ba dũnsi yʋ'ʋŋɔ.

ref: All at once an angel came down to them from the Lord, and the brightness of the Lord's glory flashed around them. The shepherds were frightened.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka sige ba zẽ'am. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ gilge ba. Tɩ dabeem ba kẽ ba halɩ.

ref: But the angel said, “Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, which will make everyone happy.
text: Tɩ malɛka la yele ba yetɩ: «Da zoe-ya dabeem, tɩ mam tarɩ la kõ-yẽlga tɩ m yele ya, n wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba wuu sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm.

ref: This very day in King David's hometown a Savior was born for you. He is Christ the Lord.
text: La de la yʋ'ʋŋ-ẽna tɩ ba dɔgɛ ya Fãagra la Adavidi tẽŋa pʋam, ẽŋa n de Zuudãana Krisi la.

ref: You will know who he is, because you will find him dressed in baby clothes and lying on a bed of hay.”
text: La bõn-ẽna n wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ la de la sɩra, ya wʋn yẽ bilɩa tɩ ba vile ẽ na fuugo gã'arɛ dũnsi yɔnnɔ daka pʋam.»

ref: Suddenly many other angels came down from heaven and joined in praising God. They said:
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma kʋʋŋ-kʋʋŋɔ darɛ wa'am wa naagɛ la ayɩla la. Tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:

ref: “Praise God in heaven! Peace on earth to everyone who pleases God.”
text: «Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum n soe pẽka, la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a nõŋɛ la tarɩ sũ-mã'asʋm dũnia zuo.»

ref: After the angels had left and gone back to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.”
text: La malɛkadõma la lebe la Nawẽnnɛ yire, dee tɩ dũnsĩima la yele taaba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ Bɛtelɛm ka bɩsɛ sɛla n ẽŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele tɩ la.»

ref: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and they saw the baby lying on a bed of hay.
text: La ba isge mɛ tɔtɔ sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ yẽ Amaari, la Azozɛfʋ, la bilɩa la tɩ a gã dũnsi yɔnnɔ daka pʋam.

ref: When the shepherds saw Jesus, they told his parents what the angel had said about him.
text: La dũnsĩima la n yẽ ẽ na, ba tɔgɛ bia la yelle wʋ malɛka la n yele ba se'em na.

ref: But Mary kept thinking about all this and wondering what it meant.
text: La Amaari tẽ'esrɩ yɛla wã wuu mɛ a sũure pʋam.

ref: As the shepherds returned to their sheep, they were praising God and saying wonderful things about him. Everything they had seen and heard was just as the angel had said.
text: Dee tɩ dũnsĩima la daan lɛbra dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ zozo'e, se'ere n soe la, ba wõm dee yẽ malɛka la n yele ba se'em na. Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ sɩa yelle

ref: Eight days later Jesus' parents did for him what the Law of Moses commands. And they named him Jesus, just as the angel had told Mary when he promised she would have a baby. Simeon Praises the Lord
text: La dabsa anii la daarɛ n daan paagɛ, tɩ ba botɩ ba wã bia la wʋ Amoyisi gõŋɔ n pa'alɛ tɩ ba wã ba budimto se'em na. Tɩ ba sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Azezi, yʋ'ʋr-sɛka tɩ malɛka daan yele ba tɩ ba sɩ ẽ na, wakat-sɛka tɩ Amaari daan nãn ka yõkɛ a pʋʋrɛ la. Ba tarɩ Azezi ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ

ref: The time came for Mary and Joseph to do what the Law of Moses says a mother is supposed to do after her baby is born. They took Jesus to the temple in Jerusalem and presented him to the Lord,
text: La wakatɛ la n paagɛ tɩ Amaari malgɛ a mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wʋ Amoyisi lɔɔ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na. Tɩ Azozɛfʋ la Amaari tarɩ bia la sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm Wẽnde-kãtʋm tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: just as the Law of the Lord says, “Each first-born baby boy belongs to the Lord.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam tɩ budib-kẽema woo wʋn dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ.

ref: The Law of the Lord also says parents have to offer a sacrifice, giving at least a pair of doves or two young pigeons. So that is what Mary and Joseph did.
text: Ba daan sẽŋɛ tɩ ba kãabɛ la sɛla bɔ Nawẽnnɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na. La yetɩ, ba bɔ mõom dawɛma bayi bɩɩ na'adawen-biɛ bayi.

ref: and told him that he would not die until he had seen Christ the Lord.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la daan põn yele ẽ tɩ a kãn ki, sãn dagna tɩ a yẽ la Krisi tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn tõm tɩ a wa'am na.

ref: When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple to do what the Law of Moses says should be done for a new baby, the Spirit told Simeon to go into the temple.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la botɩ a sẽŋɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi sɔ, la a ma daan tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ tɩ ba ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ n yele se'em na.

ref: Simeon took the baby Jesus in his arms and praised God,
text: Tɩ Asimeyõ to'oge bia la a nu'usum, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ:

ref: “Lord, I am your servant, and now I can die in peace, because you have kept your promise to me.
text: «Zuudãana, lɛɛlɛ wã, fõn bĩŋe nõorɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ mɛ. Fʋ yãŋa tã basɛ mam n de fõn tõntõnna sore tɩ m basɛ dũnia dee wa'am fõn yire la sũ-mã'asʋm.

ref: With my own eyes I have seen what you have done to save your people,
text: Se'ere n soe la, m nini yẽ Fãagra la tɩ fʋ tõm na,

ref: and foreign nations will also see this.
text: ẽŋa tɩ fʋ mãasʋm bɔ tɩ dũnia nẽrba wuu yẽ.

ref: Your mighty power is a light for all nations, and it will bring honor to your people Israel.”
text: Ẽŋa n de peelem n wʋn botɩ la nẽegɛ bɔ buuri wuu tɩ ba bãŋɛ fõn, dee wʋn botɩ tõma Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n de fõn nẽrba yẽ pẽka.»

ref: Jesus' parents were surprised at what Simeon had said.
text: La Asimeyõ n tɔgɛ bia la yɛla la, la daan di'ige bia la sɔ, la a ma mɛ.

ref: Then he blessed them and told Mary, “This child of yours will cause many people in Israel to fall and others to stand. The child will be like a warning sign. Many people will reject him,
text: Tɩ Asimeyõ kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ba, dee yele bia la ma Amaari tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge bi-ẽna tɩ a botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma zozo'e lui mɛ, dee tɩ zozo'e mẽ isge. Ẽŋa n wʋn pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, dee tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn zagsɛ ẽ.

ref: and you, Mary, will suffer as though you had been stabbed by a dagger. But all this will show what people are really thinking.” Anna Speaks about the Child Jesus
text: Ẽŋa n wʋn nẽegɛ pa'alɛ sɛla n sʋgɛ ba pʋtẽ'era pʋam na. Dee yetɩ, Amaari mẽŋa wʋn yẽ sũ-sã'aŋɔ wʋ sʋ'a tɩ ba sɩɩsɛ lʋ nẽra sũure la.

ref: The prophet Anna was also there in the temple. She was the daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher, and she was very old. In her youth she had been married for seven years, but her husband died.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔs-pɔka n mẽ daan boe tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna A'annɩ. Tɩ a sɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Afanuyɛlɩ, a de la Azɛɛrɩ buuri nẽra. Ẽŋa daan de la pug-sa'arɛ dee ele sɩra paagɛ yʋʋma ayopɔɩ tɩ a sɩra la ki.

ref: At this time Anna came in and praised God. She spoke about the child Jesus to everyone who hoped for Jerusalem to be set free. The Return to Nazareth
text: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a paagɛ ba zẽ'am, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, dee tɔgra bia la yelle bɔ'ɔra sɛba wuu n gu'uri wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn le fãagɛ Zerizalɛm nẽrba la basɛ.

ref: After Joseph and Mary had done everything that the Law of the Lord commands, they returned home to Nazareth in Galilee.
text: La ban ẽŋɛ sɛla woo ba'asɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, tɩ Azozɛfʋ la Amaari lebe kule Nazarɛtɩ n boe Galile so'olʋm na.

ref: The child Jesus grew. He became strong and wise, and God blessed him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple
text: Tɩ bia la bɩta tara pãŋa la yɛm, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔna la ẽ. Azezi daan de la bia, dee weege Zerizalɛm

ref: Every year Jesus' parents went to Jerusalem for Passover.
text: Yʋʋnɛ woo Azezi sɔ, la a ma sẽnnɩ la Zerizalɛm dita Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa.

ref: And when Jesus was twelve years old, they all went there as usual for the celebration.
text: La Azezi daan tarɩ la yʋʋma pia la ayi, dee tɩ ba sẽŋɛ kibsa la wʋ ban ẽn ɩta se'em na.

ref: After Passover his parents left, but they did not know that Jesus had stayed on in the city.
text: La kibsa la dabsa n ba'asɛ la, tɩ ba lebe kulla. Dee tɩ Azezi weege Zerizalɛm, tɩ a sɔ la a ma daan ka bãŋɛ tɩ a weege ya.

ref: They thought he was traveling with some other people, and they went a whole day before they started looking for him.
text: Ba daan tẽ'esɛ tɩ a dolɩ la ba tẽŋa dõma, dee sẽŋɛ dabsrɛ mõonɛ sẽnnɛ. La ban ɛ ẽ ba nẽrba la, la ba sɩrdõma zẽ'am dee ka yẽ ẽ na,

ref: Three days later they found Jesus sitting in the temple, listening to the teachers and asking them questions.
text: La dabsa atã daarɛ tɩ ba yẽ ẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ a zĩ la karẽnsãandõma sɛlsra, dee sɔkra ba sokrɩsɩ.

ref: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
text: Azezi n daan tarɩ yɛm la ẽn lergrɩ se'em na, di'ige sɛba wuu n daan wõm na mɛ.

ref: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
text: La a dɔgrɩba la n yẽ ẽ na, la ba di'ige ba mɛ. Tɩ a ma soke ẽ yetɩ: «M bia, bẽm tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ tɩ wãna? Mam la fʋ sɔ ɛ fʋ mɛ halɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Why did you have to look for me? Didn't you know that I would be in my Father's house?”
text: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya ɛɛra ma? Yãma ka mi tɩ la masɛ mɛ tɩ mam bɔna m Sɔ yire wã bɩɩ?»

ref: But they did not understand what he meant.
text: Tɩ ba ka bãŋɛ Azezi yetɔga la võorɛ.

ref: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.
text: Tɩ Azezi doose ba kule Nazarɛtɩ, dee sakɛ ba nõorɛ. Tɩ a ma tẽ'esra yel-bãma wuu a sũurum.

ref: Jesus became wise, and he grew strong. God was pleased with him and so were the people.
text: Tɩ Azezi bɩta, tɩ a yɛm pa'asra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ la nẽrba nõŋɛ ẽ.

ref: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”
text: Tɩ lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba mẽ wa'am tɩ a mise ba ko'om pʋam, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?»

ref: Do something to show that you really have given up your sins. Don't start saying you belong to Abraham's family. God can turn these stones into children for Abraham.
text: Tõm-ya tõon-sɛba n pa'alɩ tɩ ya teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Da kãn tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ Abraham n de yãma yaaba la, ya wʋn põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ mɛ. Mam yetɩ yãma mɛ tɩ, Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn dɩkɛ kuga wã botɩ ba lebge Abraham yɩɩsɩ.

ref: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.
text: La Azã daan zɛrgɛ naba A'erɔdɩ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a daan fãagɛ la a kẽema pɔga yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'erodɩadɩ di, dee tõm tõon-be'ero basɛba zozo'e pa'asɛ, tɩ Azã yele ẽ yetɩ la ka ãn sõŋa.

ref: Annas and Caiaphas were the Jewish high priests. At that time God spoke to Zechariah's son John, who was living in the desert.
text: La A'annɩ, la Akayifu de la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma. Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azakari bia Azã daan boe mõom tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tɔgɛ bɔ ẽ.

ref: Isaiah the prophet wrote about John when he said, “In the desert someone is shouting, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord! Make a straight path for him.
text: Azã n mõolɩ se'em na ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Nẽra n mõolɩ weem tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: Malgɛ-ya sore sõŋa gu'ura Zuudãana, malgɛ-ya sɔa la tɩ ba tɔ tẽntẽesɩ bɔ ẽ.

ref: Fill up every valley and level every mountain and hill. Straighten the crooked paths and smooth out the rough roads.
text: Palɛ-ya bɔgsɩ la wuu, sirge-ya tãnsɩ la kũnkũma la wuu, mãalɛ-ya so-gɔlma tɩ ba tɔ tẽntẽesɩ, palɛ-ya sorɔɔrɔ bogro tɩ ba naagɛ taaba.

ref: Then everyone will see the saving power of God.’ ”
text: Bɛla nẽrsaalba wuu wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em fãagra nẽrba basra.»

ref: Crowds of people came out to be baptized, but John said to them, “You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: La nẽrba zozo'e n sẽŋɛ ka lagsɛ Azã zẽ'am tɩ a mise ba ko'om pʋam. Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ãn wʋ kãmpẽesɩ buuri, ãne n yele yãma yetɩ, ya tã wʋn zoe põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ n sẽm na?

ref: The crowds asked John, “What should we do?”
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?»

ref: Some soldiers asked him, “And what about us? What do we have to do?” John told them, “Don't force people to pay money to make you leave them alone. Be satisfied with your pay.”
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ mẽ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Dee tõma, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?» Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Da to'oge-ya nẽra bõnɔ la pãŋa bɩɩ dõrɛ nẽrba to'oge sɛla. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya yɔɔrɔ la seke ya.»

ref: John said, “I am just baptizing with water. But someone more powerful is going to come, and I am not good enough even to untie his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
text: La ban tẽ'esrɩ bɛla la, tɩ Azã yele ba yetɩ: «Mam misri ya la ko'om pʋam, la nẽra n boe tara pãŋa gãnna mam sẽna, la mam põn ka masɛ tɩ m pirge a tagra bɔ ẽ. Ẽŋa n wʋn mise ya Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la bugum pʋam.

ref: In many different ways John preached the good news to the people.
text: Yetɔg-bãna, la yetɔga basɛba zozo'e tɩ Azã daan dɩkɛ zãsʋm nẽrba la pa'alɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la.

ref: Finally, Herod put John in jail, and this was the worst thing he had done. The Baptism of Jesus (Matthew
text: La A'erɔdɩ daan yõkɛ Azã ka pagɛ mɛ, bɛla A'erɔdɩ malɩn tõm la tõon-be'ero pa'asɛ. Azezi ko'om misga (Amatie

ref: While everyone else was being baptized, Jesus himself was baptized. Then as he prayed, the sky opened up,
text: Azã n daan misri nẽrba wuu ko'om pʋam na, a daan mẽ mise Azezi mɛ. La Azezi daan pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ saazuum yo'oge,

ref: and the Holy Spirit came down upon him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, “You are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.” The Ancestors of Jesus (Matthew
text: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sige a zuo, tɩ ba yẽ ẽ tɩ a wõn na na'adawenne. Tɩ kõa ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire yetɩ: «Fõn de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu. Fʋ paagɛ la mam yɛm.» Azezi yaabdõma (Amatie

ref: Enosh, and Seth. The family of Jesus went all the way back to Adam and then to God.
text: tɩ Akayinam sɔ dɛna A'enõsɩ, tɩ A'enõsɩ sɔ dɛna Asetɩ, tɩ Asetɩ sɔ dɛna Adam, la Nawẽnnɛ n nãam Adam.

ref: The Scriptures say: ‘God will tell his angels to take care of you. They will catch you in their arms, and you will not even hurt your feet on the stones.’ ”
text: La ba wʋn kɔkɛ fɔ la ba nu'usi tɩ fʋ kãn wẽ fʋ nã'arɛ la kugre.› »

ref: After the sun had set, people with all kinds of diseases were brought to Jesus. He put his hands on each one of them and healed them.
text: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan bɔna mĩ kẽ'era, tɩ ba tarɩ bã'adõma tɩ bã'asɩ buuri to'oto'ore tarɩ ba, sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am. Tɩ Azezi dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba nẽra woo zuo, tɩ ba wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: The devil said to Jesus, “If you are God's Son, tell this stone to turn into bread.”
text: Bɛla tɩ Asũtãana yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fʋ yele kugre wã tɩ a lebge dia.»

ref: Then the devil led Jesus up to a high place and quickly showed him all the nations on earth.
text: Tɩ Asũtãana tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka zom tãŋa zuo, pa'alɛ ẽ dũnia so'olʋm wuu nif-kabsɩgɔ pʋam.

ref: Just worship me, and you can have it all.”
text: Fʋ sãn ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asɛ mam, ba wuu wʋn dɛna la fõn dẽnnɛ.»

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus to Jerusalem and had him stand on top of the temple. The devil said, “If you are God's Son, jump off.
text: Tɩ Asũtãana le tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm ka zom Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zuo yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn sɩrɩ dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, fʋ ze'ele kalam ẽkɛ bisge tẽŋa,

ref: He taught in the Jewish synagogues, and everyone praised him. The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Matthew
text: La a ẽn zãsna la nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, tɩ ba wuu pẽgra ẽ. Nazarɛtɩ nẽrba ka bɔ Azezi sɩra (Amatie

ref: Jesus went back to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and as usual he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath. When he stood up to read from the Scriptures,
text: La Azezi daan sẽŋɛ la Nazarɛtɩ, ban daan uge ẽ tẽn-sɛka la. Tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ a kẽ ba wẽndeem wʋ ẽŋa ẽn kẽ'era se'em na, yãŋa isge ze'ele tɩ a karẽŋɛ.

ref: “The Lord's Spirit has come to me, because he has chosen me to tell the good news to the poor. The Lord has sent me to announce freedom for prisoners, to give sight to the blind, to free everyone who suffers,
text: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe la mam, ẽŋa n looge mam bɔ mam pãŋa, tɩ m bʋglɛ sũ-sã'aŋɔ dõma sũure, tɩ m mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ nasdõma, dee tõm mam tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ yamsɩ tɩ ba wʋn yẽ yolsgo, dee tɔgɛ bɔ fɔɔsɩ tɩ ba nini wʋn pike, dee yese sɛba n boe nãmsgɔ pʋam la basɛ,

ref: and to say, ‘This is the year the Lord has chosen.’ ”
text: dee mõolɛ tɩ wakatɛ paagɛ ya, tɩ Zuudãana wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽrba yel-sõnnɛ.»

ref: Jesus closed the book, then handed it back to the man in charge and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue looked straight at Jesus.
text: La ẽn karẽŋɛ ba'asɛ la, a lebe laglɛ gõŋɔ la mɛ bɔ sɛka n gu'uri gõnnɔ la, dee zĩ'ire. Tɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe wẽndeem na bɩsra ẽ zĩi.

ref: All the people started talking about Jesus and were amazed at the wonderful things he said. They kept on asking, “Isn't he Joseph's son?”
text: Tɩ ba tɔgɛ pẽgɛ ẽ, la a yetɔg-sõma la tɩ a tɔgɛ la di'ige ba mɛ. Dee tɩ ba sɔkra yetɩ, la ka de Azozɛfʋ dayʋa la n wãna?

ref: Jesus answered: You will certainly want to tell me this saying, “Doctor, first make yourself well.” You will tell me to do the same things here in my own hometown that you heard I did in Capernaum.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam põn mina tɩ ya wʋn makɛ makrɛ ẽna bɔ mam yetɩ: ‹Tɩɩm dãana, tɩbɛ fʋ mẽŋa.› La ya wʋn ken yele mam yetɩ: ‹Bõn-sɛba tɩ fʋ daan ẽŋɛ Kapɛrnaʋm tẽŋam tɩ tõma wõm na, ẽŋɛ bɛla nõo fʋ mẽŋa tẽŋam kalam mẽ.› »

ref: But you can be sure that no prophets are liked by the people of their own hometown.
text: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ba ka nannɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa baa ayɩla a mẽŋa tẽŋam.

ref: Once during the time of Elijah there was no rain for three and a half years, and people everywhere were starving. There were many widows in Israel,
text: La de la sɩra tɩ mam yetɩ ya tɩ: Pɔkõpa zozo'e yʋʋn boe Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋam A'eli wakatʋm na mɛ, la saaga ka ni yʋʋma atã la wõr-sɩyoobɩ, tɩ kɔm yʋʋn ba bɔna tẽŋa la wuu pʋam.

ref: but Elijah was sent only to a widow in the town of Zarephath near the city of Sidon.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ ka tõm A'eli yetɩ a sẽŋɛ pɔkõ-bãma zẽ'am. A tõm ẽ na pɔkõorɛ n boe Sarɛpɩta n boe Sidõ so'olʋm pʋam na zẽ'am.

ref: During the time of the prophet Elisha, many men in Israel had leprosy. But no one was healed, except Naaman who lived in Syria.
text: La kõnkɔma zozo'e yʋʋn boe Ɩsrayɛlɩ tẽŋam A'elize wakatʋm na mɛ, la baa ayɩla ka yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, la Anãamã sɛka n yese Siiri so'olʋm na mã'a n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.»

ref: But Jesus slipped through the crowd and got away. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Mark
text: La Azezi kẽ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tẽŋasʋka fõrgɛ. Bʋraaga tɩ kʋlkã'arga tarɩ ẽ (Amarkɩ

ref: Jesus went to the town of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people on the Sabbath.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi sẽŋɛ la Galile tẽŋa ayẽma yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Kapɛrnaʋm, tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ daan paagɛ, tɩ a sẽŋɛ ba wẽndeem ka zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

ref: His teaching amazed them because he spoke with power.
text: Tɩ a zãsŋɔ la daan di'ige ba, a yetɔga la n tarɩ pãŋa la ĩyã.

ref: There in the synagogue was a man with an evil spirit. He yelled out,
text: Tɩ bʋraa daan bɔna wẽndeem na tɩ kʋlkã'arga tara ẽ, tɩ a tĩi kaasɛ yetɩ:

ref: They all were amazed and kept saying to each other, “What kind of teaching is this? He has power to order evil spirits out of people!”
text: Tɩ ba wuu palẽŋa ẽkɛ tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Yetɔga ẽna de la bẽm? A tarɩ la pãŋa la ke'eŋo, dee tãsra kʋlkã'arsɩ, tɩ ba yɛsra.»

ref: News about Jesus spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Tɩ Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ saagɛ zẽ'e-bãma wuu. Azezi botɩ nẽrba zozo'e yẽ ĩmã'asʋm (Amatie

ref: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's home. When Jesus got there, he was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever.
text: Tɩ Azezi isge ba wẽndeego la pʋam yese ka kẽ Asimõ yire. Tɩ Asimõ dɛɛm-pɔka bẽ'era, tɩ a ĩyã ba tʋlgɛ. Tɩ ba tɔgɛ a yelle bɔ Azezi,

ref: Demons went out of many people and shouted, “You are the Son of God!” But Jesus ordered the demons not to speak because they knew he was the Messiah.
text: La kʋlkã'arsɩ daan yesrɩ mɛ dee basra nẽrba zozo'e, dee kaasra yetɩ: «Fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.» Tɩ Azezi tãsɛ ba, ka basɛ tɩ ba tɔgɛ sɛla sɛla, se'ere n soe la, ba daan bãŋɛ tɩ a de la Krisi n de Fãagra la. Azezi mõolɩ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam (Amarkɩ

ref: The next morning Jesus went out to a place where he could be alone, and crowds came looking for him. When they found him, they tried to stop him from leaving.
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Azezi yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽra ka boe mĩ. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e ɛɛra ẽ. La ba daan yẽ ẽ mɛ, bɔɔra tɩ ba gu ẽ tɩ a da le yese ba zẽ'am.

ref: But Jesus said, “People in other towns must hear the good news about God's kingdom. This is why I was sent.”
text: La Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ mam mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm n ãn se'em tẽnsɩ basɛba mẽ pʋam, la de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm mam.»

ref: So he kept on preaching in the synagogues in Judea.
text: La a ẽn mõola kõ-yẽlga la Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam mɛ.

ref: But Jesus would often go to some place where he could be alone and pray. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk (Matthew
text: La Azezi ẽn sẽnna la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽrba ka boe la pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ. Azezi botɩ kɔɔrŋɔ isge sẽnna (Amatie

ref: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, “My friend, your sins are forgiven.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yẽ ban bɔ ẽŋa sɩra se'em na, yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «M sɩrɛ, fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna fʋ zuo.»

ref: Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to row it out a little way from the shore. Then Jesus sat down in the boat to teach the crowd.
text: La õorŋɔ ayɩla daan de la Asimõ bõnɔ. Tɩ Azezi kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam, dee yele Asimõ yetɩ: «Mõm õorŋɔ la tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽŋa fẽe.» La Azezi n yele bɛla la, Asimõ mõm õorŋɔ la mɛ, tɩ a lebe ko'om pʋam fẽe. Tɩ Azezi zĩ'ire õorŋɔ la pʋam pa'alɛ nẽrba la.

ref: “Master,” Simon answered, “we have worked hard all night long and have not caught a thing. But if you tell me to, I will let the nets down.”
text: Tɩ Asimõ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma nãmsɛ mɛ yʋ'ʋŋɔ wã wuu dee ka yõkɛ sɛla sɛla. La fõn yele la ĩyã, tɩ wʋn lobe tɩ yʋgsɩ la.»

ref: They did this and caught so many fish that their nets began ripping apart.
text: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ kʋlko'om ka lobe ba yʋgsɩ la, yãŋa yõgɛ zũma ba zo'oge, tɩ ba yʋgsɩ la zɩgra la ba ãasʋm.

ref: Then they signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. The men came, and together they filled the two boats so full that they both began to sink.
text: Tɩ ba yɩbsɛ ba taaba õorŋɔ ayɩla la pʋam yetɩ, ba wa'am sõŋɛ ba. La ba taaba la sẽŋɛ mɛ ka sõŋɛ ba, tɩ ba pɩrɛ õornɔ bayi la wuu la zũma, halɩ tɩ ba zɩgra la ba mise.

ref: His partners James and John, the sons of Zebedee, were surprised too. Jesus told Simon, “Don't be afraid! From now on you will bring in people instead of fish.”
text: La dabeem mẽ n daan kẽ Azebede dayɔɔsɩ Azakɩ la Azã n daan ẽn dɔla la Asimõ tõnna la. Tɩ Azezi yele Asimõ yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem, tɩ lan sẽŋɛ zĩna wɛ'ɛsa wuu, fʋ wʋn ɛɛra la nẽrba tɩ ba dɔla mam wʋ yãma n yõgrɩ zũma se'em na.»

ref: The men pulled their boats up on the shore. Then they left everything and went with Jesus. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: La Azezi n yele bɛla la, ba tarɩ õornɔ la dooge mɛ yãŋa basɛ sɛla woo dee dɔla Azezi. Azezi botɩ kõnkonne yẽ ĩmã'asʋm (Amatie

ref: News about Jesus kept spreading. Large crowds came to listen to him teach and to be healed of their diseases.
text: La bɛla wuu, Azezi yelle daan malɩn saagɛ la zẽ'esɩ zozo'e, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e lagsɛ tɩ ba selse a yetɔga la, dee tɩ a botɩ ba bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they went up on the roof, where they removed some tiles and let the mat down in the middle of the room.
text: La nẽrba la mike mɛ, tɩ ba ka yẽ folle tarɩ ẽ kẽ. Bɛla ĩyã tɩ ba zɛ kɔɔrŋɔ la zom gɔsgɔ, ka lakɛ gɔsgɔ la bɔbrɛ ayɩla, doose bilam sike ẽ la a bõn-gã'ala la nẽrba la sʋkam bĩŋe ẽ Azezi nẽŋam.

ref: The Pharisees and the experts began arguing, “Jesus must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma la tẽ'esra yetɩ: «A tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la ãne tɔgra pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ. Ãne n tã wʋn basɛ nẽra tõon-be'ero taalɛ, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ mã'a?»

ref: Is it easier for me to tell this man that his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and walk?
text: Bẽm n de nãana gãnna, mam sãn yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ, a tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna a zuo n de nãana, bɩɩ mam sãn yele ẽ yetɩ, a isge sẽnna n de nãana?

ref: But now you will see that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” Jesus then said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk home.”
text: Bɛla m wʋn botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la tarɩ pãŋa dũnia zuo wʋn basɛ nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.» A yele bɛla dee yele kɔɔrŋɔ la yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ fʋ isge dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala la kule.»

ref: At once the man stood up in front of everyone. He picked up his mat and went home, giving thanks to God.
text: Bilam mẽŋa tɩ kɔɔrŋɔ la isge ba wuu nẽŋam dɩkɛ a bõn-gã'ala la kulla, dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Everyone was amazed and praised God. What they saw surprised them, and they said, “We have seen a great miracle today!” Jesus Chooses Levi (Matthew
text: Tɩ nẽrba la wuu palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ dabeem kẽ ba, tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Tɩ zĩna yẽ bõn-yãlma.» Azezi wi Alevi tɩ a dɔla ẽ (Amatie

ref: Later, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi yese sẽŋɛ ka yẽ lampo-tɔ'ɔsa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Alevi, tɩ a zĩ lampo to'osgo deem. Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge dɔla mam.»

ref: Levi left everything and went with Jesus.
text: Tɩ a isge basɛ sɛla woo dee dɔla ẽ.

ref: In his home Levi gave a big dinner for Jesus. Many tax collectors and other guests were also there.
text: Tɩ Alevi dʋgɛ dia zo'oge ẽŋɛ kibsa a yire bɔ Azezi, tɩ ẽŋa la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la nẽrba zozo'e zẽa dita.

ref: The Pharisees and some of their teachers of the Law of Moses grumbled to Jesus' disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with these tax collectors and other sinners?”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba, la ba lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma yõnna Azezi karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bẽm ĩyã tɩ yãma lagɩm na lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma dita?»

ref: Jesus answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm ka eerɩ tɩba. Sɛba n bẽ'erɩ n eerɩ tɩba.

ref: I didn't come to invite good people to turn to God. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Matthew
text: Mam ka wa'am dũnia zuo sɛba dõma n yetɩ bãma masɛ ya la ĩyã, mam wa'am tɩ m yele la tõon-be'ero dõma tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.» Ba soke Azezi nõorlʋa yelle (Amatie

ref: Some people said to Jesus, “John's followers often pray and go without eating, and so do the followers of the Pharisees. But your disciples never go without eating or drinking.”
text: Tɩ nẽrba la yele Azezi yetɩ: «Azã karẽnbiisi, la Farisi dõma karẽnbiisi ẽn lʋta nõorɛ mɛ dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, dee tɩ fõn karẽnbiisi dita dee yũura.»

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.”
text: Ayɛɩ. La wakatɛ wʋn wa'am tɩ ba wʋn yese pɔgdita la ba zẽ'am, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yãŋa wʋn lʋ nõorɛ.»

ref: Jesus then told them these sayings: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and make the hole even bigger.
text: Tɩ Azezi le makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Nẽra kãn ãagɛ fu-paalɛ dɩkɛ lige fu-kɛka. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, a wʋn sã'am fu-paalɛ la mɛ, la tãn-paalɛ la kãn masɛ la fu-kɛka la.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The new wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined.
text: La nẽra mẽ kãn dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm wɔ-kɛgsɩ pʋam. A sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, dã-paalga la sãn kɛlla, ba wʋn botɩ wɔɔsɩ la tãsɛ mɛ. Bɛla dãam na wʋn ka'agɛ mɛ sã'am, tɩ wɔɔsɩ la mẽ sã'am.

ref: New wine must be put only into new wineskins.
text: La masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dã-paalga sũm na wɔ-paalsɩ pʋam.

ref: No one wants new wine after drinking old wine. They say, “The old wine is better.”
text: La nẽra sãn yũ dã-kɛka n kele la, a ka le bɔɔra dã-paalga. A wʋn yetɩ, dã-kɛka la n ãn sõŋa.»

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. And the man stood up.
text: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ba tẽ'esgɔ la, yele bʋraa la nu'ugo n ki la yetɩ: «Isge ze'ele nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka.» Tɩ a isge ze'ele.

ref: Some Pharisees said, “Why are you picking grain on the Sabbath? You're not supposed to do that!”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma nẽrba basɛba yẽ ban ẽŋɛ bɛla la, yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ yãma ẽŋɛ sɛla n sisri tõma vo'osgo daarɛ?»

ref: Long ago your own people did these same things to the prophets. So when this happens to you, be happy and jump for joy! You will have a great reward in heaven.
text: Wakat-sɛka tɩ ba ɩtɩ yãma bɛla la, ẽŋɛ-ya sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn yẽ yɔɔrɔ zo'oge mɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire. Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ ba yaabdõma yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la bɛla.

ref: Ask God to bless anyone who curses you, and pray for everyone who is cruel to you.
text: La ya kã'ara kã'a-sõma bɔ'ɔra sɛba n kã be'em bɔ'ɔra ya la, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ɩtɩ ya be'em na.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “On the Sabbath should we do good deeds or evil deeds? Should we save someone's life or destroy it?”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ m soke ya mɛ bɩsɛ, sore boe tɩ nẽra ẽŋɛ sõŋa vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a ẽŋɛ be'em? Sore boe tɩ a fãagɛ nẽra vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, a kʋ ẽ?»

ref: One was Simon, and Jesus named him Peter. Another was Andrew, Peter's brother. There were also James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
text: Ba daan de la Asimõ tɩ Azezi sɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la A'ãndre n de Apɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbga, la Azakɩ, la Azã, la Afilipi, la Abatelemi,

ref: On another Sabbath Jesus was teaching in a synagogue, and a man with a paralyzed right hand was there.
text: Vo'osgo daarɛ ayẽma tɩ Azezi kẽ Zifdõma wẽndeem zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga. Tɩ bʋraa daan bɔna mĩ, tɩ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo ki.

ref: The teachers and the Pharisees were furious and started saying to one another, “What can we do about Jesus?” Jesus Chooses His Twelve Apostles (Matthew
text: Tɩ ba sũnsõa yĩige halɩ tɩ ba bãnna ban wʋn ẽŋɛ Azezi se'em. Azezi looge a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi (Amatie

ref: The next morning he called his disciples together and chose twelve of them to be his apostles.
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a wi a karẽnbiisi la, tɩ ba wa'am a zẽ'am. Tɩ a looge karẽnbiisi pia la ayi bãma pʋam. Bãma n de nẽr-sɛba tɩ a wʋn tõm na.

ref: Everyone was trying to touch Jesus, because power was going out from him and healing them all. Blessings and Troubles (Matthew
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu ɛɛra tɩ ba kalʋm ẽ, se'ere n soe la, pãŋa n yesrɩ a pʋam bɔ'ɔra ba wuu ĩmã'asʋm. Zu-yẽlga la toogo yelle (Amatie

ref: Matthew, Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus. The rest of the apostles were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: la Amatie, la Atoma, la Azakɩ sɛka n de Alfe bia la, la Asimõ sɛka n daan de Zelɔtɩ nẽra ayɩla la,

ref: You well-fed people are in for trouble. You will go hungry! You people who are laughing now are in for trouble. You are going to cry and weep!
text: La yãma sɛba n tarɩ sɛla woo lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ la seke la, wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn ka sẽnna la kɔm. La yãma sɛba n la'arɩ lɛɛlɛ wã wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ya sũure wʋn ka sã'am tɩ ya kaasɛ.

ref: These people had come to listen to Jesus and to be healed of their diseases. All who were troubled by evil spirits were also healed.
text: Ba wa'am tɩ ba selse la Azezi yetɔga, dee tɩ a bɔ ba bã'adõma ĩmã'asʋm. La nẽrba basɛba n daan boe mĩ tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dɔla ba, tɩ ba daan yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: God will bless you hungry people. You will have plenty to eat! God will bless you people who are now crying. You will laugh!
text: Yãma sɛba kɔm n dõnnɩ lɛɛlɛ wã tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn ka di seke. Yãma sɛba n kaasrɩ lɛɛlɛ wã tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ya wʋn ka la'ara.

ref: God will bless you when others hate you and won't have anything to do with you. God will bless you when people insult you and say cruel things about you, all because you are a follower of the Son of Man.
text: Yãma sɛba tɩ nẽrba sisri ya, dee bɔkra ya, tʋʋra ya, dee ka nanna ya wʋ ya de la tõon-be'ero dõma, mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ĩyã la, tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

ref: You are in for trouble when everyone says good things about you. That is what your own people said about those prophets who told lies. Love for Enemies (Matthew
text: La yãma sɛba tɩ nẽrba wuu pẽgrɩ ya la, wʋn yẽ toogo. Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ ba yaabdõma mẽ yʋʋn pẽgrɩ nõtɔ'ɔs-sɛba n de põmpɔrndõma la bɛla.» Nõŋɛ-ya ya bɛ'ɛba (Amatie

ref: If you give to others, you will be given a full amount in return. It will be packed down, shaken together, and spilling over into your lap. The way you treat others is the way you will be treated.
text: Bɔ-ya ya taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn bɔ ya. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya zo'oge wʋ ban dɩkɛ wan-kãtɛ makɛ pɩrɛ sõŋa tɩ la saara. Bõn-sɛka tɩ yãma dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ ya taaba la, bõn-ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ bɔ ya.»

ref: is like someone who dug down deep and built a house on solid rock. When a flood came and the river rushed against the house, it was built so well that it didn't even shake.
text: A ãn wʋ nẽra n boorɩ tɩ a mẽ a yire, tũ paagɛ tãmpĩa, ẽebɛ a yire tãmpĩa la zuo. Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wẽ yire la, tɩ a ka lui, ẽn mẽ sõŋa la ĩyã.

ref: If someone slaps you on one cheek, don't stop that person from slapping you on the other cheek. If someone wants to take your coat, don't try to keep back your shirt.
text: Nẽra sãn wẽ fʋ pɛka ayɩla, fʋ basɛ tɩ a wẽ ayẽma la pa'asɛ. La nẽra sãn fãagɛ fʋ fu-kãtɛ, fʋ basɛ tɩ a to'oge fʋ fu-pɩka pa'asɛ.

ref: Give to everyone who asks and don't ask people to return what they have taken from you.
text: La nẽra sãn sose fʋ sɛla, fʋ bɔ ẽ. La nẽra sãn dɩkɛ sɛla n de fʋ bõnɔ, fʋ da yetɩ a bɔ fɔ fʋ bõnɔ na.

ref: Treat others just as you want to be treated.
text: La yãma n boorɩ tɩ nẽrba ɩta bõn-sɛba bɔ'ɔra ya la, ya mẽ ɩta bɛla bɔ'ɔra ba.

ref: If you love only someone who loves you, will God praise you for that? Even sinners love people who love them.
text: La ya sãn nõŋɛ sɛba n nõŋɛ ya la mã'a, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya sɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Baa la tõon-be'ero dõma nõŋɛ sɛba n nõŋɛ ba mɛ.

ref: Have pity on others, just as your Father has pity on you. Judging Others (Matthew
text: Zɔta-ya nẽrba ninbãalga, wʋ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n zotɩ nẽrba ninbãalga se'em na.» Da zɛrgra-ya taaba (Amatie

ref: Jesus said: Don't judge others, and God won't judge you. Don't be hard on others, and God won't be hard on you. Forgive others, and God will forgive you.
text: «Da tɔgra-ya nẽra yelle yetɩ a ẽŋɛ be'em, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ kãn tɔgɛ ya yelle yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ be'em. Da zɛrgra-ya nẽrba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ kãn zɛrgɛ ya. Basɛ-ya taaba taalɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn basɛ ya taalɛ.

ref: Jesus also used some sayings as he spoke to the people. He said: Can one blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a ditch?
text: Tɩ Azezi daan makɛ makrɛ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Fʋa tã wʋn ve'ege fʋa mɛ tɩ ba wuu kãn lui boko pʋam?

ref: Are students better than their teacher? But when they are fully trained, they will be like their teacher.
text: Karẽnbia ka gãnnɩ a karẽnsãama, la karẽnbia sãn zãsʋm sɛla woo ba'asɛ, a ãn wʋ a karẽnsãama la.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: Bẽm tɩ fʋ bɩsra sɔkɔ n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum, dee ka bɩsra dɔɔgɔ n boe fʋ mẽŋa nifum na?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye? You show-offs! First, get the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye. A Tree and Its Fruit (Matthew
text: Fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne yɛta fʋ sɔbia yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ fʋ yese sɔkɔ a nifum, dee ka bɩsra dɔɔgɔ n boe fʋ mẽŋa nifum? Pʋlibsum dãana, dẽŋɛ yese dɔɔgɔ la fʋ mẽŋa nifum, dee yãŋa tã'agɛ yẽ sõŋa yese sɔkɔ la n boe fʋ sɔbia nifum na.» Tɩa woo la a biɛ (Amatie

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: «Tɩ-sõŋɔ ka wɔnnɩ biɛ be'ero. Tɩ-be'ego mẽ ka wɔnnɩ biɛ sõma.

ref: You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces. You cannot pick figs or grapes from thornbushes.
text: Ba bãŋrɩ tɩɩsɩ wuu la ba biɛ pʋam. Gõ'osɩ kãn wɔm kẽnkãma, la kẽkɔ mẽ kãn wɔm tã'ama.

ref: Good people do good things because of the good in their hearts, but bad people do bad things because of the evil in their hearts. Your words show what is in your heart. Two Builders (Matthew
text: Nẽr-sõŋɔ sũure n ãn sõŋa botɩ a tɔgra la bõn-sõma, la nẽr-be'ego sũure n ka ãn sõŋa botɩ a tɔgra la bõn-be'ero. Se'ere n soe la, bõn-sɛba n boe nẽrsaala sũurum n yesrɩ a nõorʋm.» Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga sakrɛ yelle (Amatie

ref: Why do you keep on saying that I am your Lord, when you refuse to do what I say?
text: «Bẽm ĩyã tɩ yãma wi'ira mam tɩ ‹Zuudãana, Zuudãana›, dee ka ɩta mam n yetɩ ya ɩta se'em na?

ref: But anyone who hears what I say and doesn't obey me is like someone whose house wasn't built on solid rock. As soon as the river rushed against that house, it was smashed to pieces!
text: La nẽr-sɛka n wõnnɩ mam yetɔga, dee ka ɩta mam n yele se'em na, ãn wʋ nẽra n mẽ a yire tẽntõnnɔ zuo, dee ka tũ ẽebɛ. Tɩ saaga ni, tɩ ko'om pɩrɛ kʋlsɩ zoe wa wẽ yire la, tɩ a kɔ'ɔn lui saam saam.»

ref: I have officers who give orders to me, and I have soldiers who take orders from me. I can say to one of them, ‘Go!’ and he goes. I can say to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes. I can say to my servant, ‘Do this!’ and he will do it.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrba n soe mam, la mam mẽ soe la sʋdaasɩ basɛba. Mam sãn yele sʋda-sɛka yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ›, a we'esɩ mɛ. La mam sãn yele ayẽma yetɩ: ‹Wa'am›, a sẽm mɛ. Mam sãn yele m tõntõnna yetɩ: ‹Ẽŋɛ wãna›, a ɩtɩ mɛ.»

ref: He loves our nation and even built us a synagogue.”
text: A nõŋɛ tõma buuri mɛ, la ẽŋa n mẽ wẽndeego bɔ tõma. Bɛla ĩyã, mõrgɛ sõŋɛ ẽ.»

ref: When the Lord saw the woman, he felt sorry for her and said, “Don't cry!”
text: La Zuudãana Azezi n yẽ pɔkõorɛ la, tɩ a ninbãalga yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Da kɛlla.»

ref: The leaders went to Jesus and begged him to do something. They said, “This man deserves your help!
text: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am ka tĩi belem ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraa la de la nẽr-sõŋɔ.

ref: Simon answered, “I suppose it would be the one who had owed more and didn't have to pay it back.” “You are right,” Jesus said.
text: Tɩ Asimõ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ sɛka sãnnɛ n zo'oge la.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn lerge se'em na de la sɩra.»

ref: And when he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish leaders to ask him to come and heal the servant.
text: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la wõm Azezi yelle, tõm Zifdõma kẽma basɛba a zẽ'am tɩ ba sose ẽ yetɩ, a wa'am wa botɩ a tõntõnna la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: So Jesus went with them. When Jesus wasn't far from the house, the officer sent some friends to tell him, “Lord, don't go to any trouble for me! I am not good enough for you to come into my house.
text: Tɩ Azezi dɔla ba wɛ'ɛsa ka lɛm yire la, tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la le tõm a sɩrdõma tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, da nãmsɛ fʋ mẽŋa. Mam ka narɩ tɩ fʋ wa'am mam yire,

ref: And I am certainly not worthy to come to you. Just say the word, and my servant will get well.
text: bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam tẽ'esɛ yetɩ mam mẽŋa ka narɩ tɩ m wa'am fõn zẽ'am. Wen tɔgɛ yetɔga mã'a, tɩ m tõntõnna la wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “In all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!”
text: Azezi n wõm bɛla la, la daan di'ige ẽ mɛ, tɩ a wẽrgɛ yele nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ yãma mɛ tɩ mam nãn ka yẽ nẽra n bɔ mam sɩra wʋ bʋra-ẽna wã, baa Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la pʋam mam ka yẽ.»

ref: The officer's friends returned and found the servant well. A Widow's Son
text: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n tõm sɛba la lebe a yire ka yẽ tɩ tõntõnna la tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm. Azezi botɩ pɔkõorɛ bia vo'oge

ref: As they came near the gate of the town, they saw people carrying out the body of a widow's only son. Many people from the town were walking along with her.
text: La Azezi paagɛ tẽŋa la nõorʋm, tʋ'ʋsɛ la nẽrba n zɛɛrɩ budibla n ki wɛ'ɛsa tɩ ba ka laagɛ. Budibla la ma de la pɔkõorɛ. La a bia de la ẽŋa mã'a. Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba zozo'e daan kɔlgɛ ẽ dɔla wɛ'ɛsa.

ref: Jesus went over and touched the stretcher on which the people were carrying the dead boy. They stopped, and Jesus said, “Young man, get up!”
text: A yele bɛla dee sẽŋɛ paagɛ kalʋm na sãŋandɛ la, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n zɛɛrɩ kũm na ze'ele, tɩ a yele kũm na yetɩ: «Budib-polle, mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ fʋ isge.»

ref: The boy sat up and began to speak. Jesus then gave him back to his mother.
text: Tɩ sɛka n ki la vo'oge isge zĩ'ire, yãŋa tɔgra. Tɩ Azezi yele a ma la yetɩ: «Fʋ bia n bɛla.»

ref: Everyone was frightened and praised God. They said, “A great prophet is here with us! God has come to his people.”
text: Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba wuu, tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔs-kãtɛ n wa'am tõma zẽ'am.» Dee le yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ n wa'am tɩ a sõŋɛ a nẽrba.»

ref: News about Jesus spread all over Judea and everywhere else in that part of the country. John the Baptist (Matthew
text: Tɩ yetɔga la saagɛ Zude so'olʋm, la tẽn-sɛba n lɛm na wuu, tɩ ba wõm Azezi n ẽŋɛ se'em. Azã n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam tõm a karẽnbiisi Azezi zẽ'am (Amatie

ref: John's followers told John everything that was being said about Jesus. So he sent two of them to ask the Lord, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: tõm ba Zuudãana Azezi zẽ'am, tɩ ba ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na bɩɩ, tɩ gu'ura nẽra ayẽma?»

ref: When these messengers came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the one we should be looking for? Or are we supposed to wait for someone else?’ ”
text: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Azezi zẽ'am yele ẽ yetɩ: «Azã sɛka n mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na n tõm tõma yetɩ, tɩ soke fʋ bɩsɛ, fõn de sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ a wʋn wa'am na bɩɩ, tɩ gu'ura nẽra ayẽma?»

ref: At that time Jesus was healing many people who were sick or in pain or were troubled by evil spirits, and he was giving sight to a lot of blind people.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa Azezi daan botɩ bã'adõma zozo'e tɩ bã'asɩ to'oto'ore tarɩ ba, la sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dolɩ ba n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee botɩ fɔɔsɩ zozo'e nini yẽta.

ref: Jesus said to the messengers sent by John, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard. Blind people are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People who have leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can now hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge sɛba tɩ Azã tõm na yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele Azã yãma n yẽ bõn-sɛba, dee wõm se'em na wuu: Fɔɔsɩ nini yẽtɩ mɛ, tɩ kɔɔrnɔ sẽnna, tɩ kõnkɔma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm le ãna sõŋa, tɩ wansɩ wõnna, tɩ kũm vɔ'ɔra, tɩ nasdõma wõnna Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: La nẽr-sɛka n ka lebrɩ poorʋm mam ĩyã tarɩ zu-yẽlga.»

ref: After John's messengers had gone, Jesus began speaking to the crowds about John: What kind of person did you go out to the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Sɛba tɩ Azã tõm na lebe kule mɛ. Tɩ Azezi yãŋa tɔgɛ nẽrba la Azã yelle yetɩ: «Yãma daan sẽŋɛ mõom tɩ ya ka bɩsɛ la bẽm? Yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la mõogɔ tɩ kʋsebgo mĩini bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

ref: What kind of man did you really go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothes and live in luxury are in the king's palace.
text: La bẽm tɩ yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ? Yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la nẽra n yɛ fu-sõma bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ. Nẽr-sɛba n yɛɛrɩ fu-sõma dee tarɩ bõn-sõma la, kẽ'erɩ la na'adõma yiɛ pʋam.

ref: What then did you go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was! I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: La yãma sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la bẽm? Ya sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa bɩɩ? Ẽe. La mam yetɩ ya tɩ a de la nẽra n gãnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

ref: In the Scriptures, God calls John his messenger and says, “I am sending my messenger ahead of you to get things ready for you.”
text: La de la ẽŋa yelle tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam tõntõnna n wãna tɩ mam tõm tɩ a dẽŋɛ fʋ nẽŋa malgɛ sore bɔ fɔ.›

ref: No one ever born on this earth is greater than John. But whoever is least important in God's kingdom is greater than John.
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ nẽrsaalba tɩ ba dɔgɛ la wuu pʋam, nẽra ka boe n gãnnɩ Azã, la nẽr-sɛka n de pɩka Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam gãnnɩ Azã mɛ.

ref: Everyone had been listening to John. Even the tax collectors had obeyed God and had done what was right by letting John baptize them.
text: La nẽrba la wuu, baa la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba la n wõm Azã yetɔga la, ba basɛ tɩ Azã mise ba ko'om pʋam mɛ. Bɛla pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ ba sakɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ sɩra.

ref: But the Pharisees and the experts in the Law of Moses refused to obey God and be baptized by John.
text: La Farisi dõma, la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma n zagsɛ tɩ Azã ka mise ba ko'om pʋam na, ba zagsɛ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ ba la.»

ref: Jesus went on to say: What are you people like? What kind of people are you?
text: Tɩ Azezi le yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ mam wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ la zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba n ɩtɩ se'em na. Ba ɩrgɔ wõn na bẽm?

ref: You are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other, “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not cry!”
text: Ba ãn wʋ kɔma n zĩ da'am wi'ira taaba yetɩ: ‹Tõma peebe wɩɩsɩ mɛ, tɩ yãma zagsɛ ka wa. Tɩ tõma yõom kʋʋrɛ yõoma, tɩ yãma ka kɛlla.›

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “John has a demon in him!”
text: Se'ere n soe la, Azã n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na wa'am mɛ wa lʋta nõorɛ, dee ka yũura dãam, tɩ ba yetɩ kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ.

ref: But because the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, you say, “Jesus eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.”
text: Tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wa'am wa dita dee yũura, tɩ ba yetɩ, a de la dita, la dã-yũura, la lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba la tõon-be'ero dõma sɩrɛ.

ref: Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what its followers do. Simon the Pharisee
text: Nẽr-sɛba n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n boorɩ se'em na n mi tɩ a tarɩ yɛm.» Azezi boe Asimõ yire

ref: A Pharisee invited Jesus to have dinner with him. So Jesus went to the Pharisee's home and got ready to eat.
text: Farisi nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asimõ n daan wi Azezi tɩ a wa'am ẽŋa yire tɩ ba di. Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ yire la ka zĩ'ire bɔna mĩ tɩ a di.

ref: Then she came and stood behind Jesus. She cried and started washing his feet with her tears and drying them with her hair. The woman kissed his feet and poured the perfume on them.
text: Yãŋa ze'ele lɛm Azezi nãma bɔba kɛlla. Tɩ a nintãm yɛsra tɔgra Azezi nãma zuo. Tɩ pɔka la suure duuse Azezi nãma la, la a zomto, dee kɔkɛ a nãma la, sõogɛ ba la tɩdarɛ kaam na.

ref: The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and said to himself, “If this man really were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him! He would know that she is a sinner.”
text: Tɩ Farisi nẽra la n wi Azezi la yẽ bɛla, tẽ'esɛ yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, a wʋn bãŋɛ nɩ pɔg-ẽna n kalʋm ẽ wã n de nẽr-sɛka, a wʋn bãŋɛ nɩ tɩ a tõnnɩ la yalsɩ tõoma.»

ref: Jesus said to the Pharisee, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher, what is it?” Simon replied.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Asimõ, mam tarɩ la sɛla tɩ m yele fo.» Tɩ Asimõ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, yele!»

ref: Jesus told him, “Two people were in debt to a moneylender. One of them owed him
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra n daan boe tɩ nẽrba bayi tara a sãnnɛ. Nẽra ayɩla sãnnɛ de la lig-kõba kɔbsnuu. Tɩ ayẽma la mẽ sãnnɛ dɛna lig-kõba pinuu.

ref: But Jesus told the woman, “Because of your faith, you are now saved. May God give you peace!”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele pɔka la yetɩ: «Fõn bɔ mam sɩra la ĩyã n fãagɛ fɔ basɛ. Sẽŋɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm.»

ref: Since neither of them could pay him back, the moneylender said that they didn't have to pay him anything. Which one of them will like him more?”
text: Tɩ ba daan ka tãna wʋn yɔ ẽ a ligri la. Tɩ a basɛ ligri la bɔ ba. Bãma bayi la, ãne n wʋn nõŋɛ ligri la dãana gãnna?»

ref: He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Have you noticed this woman? When I came into your home, you didn't give me any water so I could wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
text: Dee gelge bɩsɛ pɔka la, yele Asimõ yetɩ: «Fõn yẽ pɔka wã? Mam n kẽ fõn yire la, fõn ka bɔ mam ko'om tɩ mam peege m nãma. La pɔka la peege mam nãma mɛ la a nintãm, dee duuse ba la a zomto.

ref: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the time I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
text: Fõn ka kɔkɛ mam tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ fʋ to'oge mam sõŋa. La mam n kẽ'em na, pɔka la kɔkɛ mam nãma mɛ ka basɛ.

ref: You didn't even pour olive oil on my head, but she has poured expensive perfume on my feet.
text: La fõn ka nã'asɛ mam ẽŋɛ kaam mam zuugo, la a sõogɛ mam nãma mɛ la tɩdarɛ kaam.

ref: So I tell you that all her sins are forgiven, and that is why she has shown great love. But anyone who has been forgiven for only a little will show only a little love.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam yele fʋ yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ a tõon-be'ero n zo'oge la taalɛ mɛ. Bɛla n soe tɩ a nõŋrɛ la zo'oge. La nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ a tõon-be'ero taalɛ fẽfẽe la, a nõŋrɛ mẽ de la fẽe.»

ref: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
text: Azezi yele bɛla, dee yele pɔka la yetɩ: «Fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka le bɔna fʋ zuo.»

ref: Some other guests started saying to one another, “Who is this who dares to forgive sins?”
text: Tɩ sɛba n daan diti la ẽ na tɔgra la taaba sɔkra yetɩ: «A tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la ãne basɛ nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ?»

ref: and so were some women who had been healed of evil spirits and all sorts of diseases. One of the women was Mary Magdalene, who once had seven demons in her.
text: La pɔgsɩ basɛba n mẽ daan dolɩ ẽ. La kʋlkã'arsɩ, la bã'asɩ n daan nãmsrɩ pɔg-bãma, tɩ Azezi botɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Pɔgsɩ la ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Amaari n yese Magdala, ẽŋa tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ bayopɔɩ daan yese dee basɛ ẽ na.

ref: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it. But they don't have deep roots, and they believe only for a little while. As soon as life gets hard, they give up.
text: La tãmpĩa la zuo tɩ bõnbuuri sɛba lui la, ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛba n wõm yetɔga la, dee sakɛ la sũ-yẽlga, la yetɔga la ka kẽ ba sũure pʋam sõŋa. Ba sakɛ la wakatɛ fẽe, tɩ yelle sãn paagɛ ba, tɩ ba basɛ.

ref: Other seeds fell on rocky ground and started growing. But the plants did not have enough water and soon dried up.
text: Tɩ basɛba lui tãmpĩa zuo bule, la mã'asrɛ n ka boe mĩ na ĩyã tɩ ba zẽkɛ ki.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
