ref: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
text: Basɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ, tɩ tõma mẽ basrɩ sɛba n ẽŋɛ tõma be'em taalɛ mɛ. Da basɛ tɩ yelle paagɛ tɔ tɩ tɩ lui.› »

ref: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, a wʋn bɔ ẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n eerɩ, wʋn yẽ. La nẽr-sɛka n wẽ'erɩ kʋlɩŋa, ba wʋn yo'oge bɔ ẽ.

ref: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
text: Bõn-sɛka n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Azonaasɩ la, pa'alɛ Niniivi dõma la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa, bɛla Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ de la sɛka n pa'alɩ zamãan-ẽna dõma Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa.

ref: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
text: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ pɔka ze'ele nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Pɔg-sɛka n dɔgɛ fɔ, tɩ fʋ mõgɛ a bĩ'isum na, tarɩ zu-yẽlga.»

ref: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
text: La Azezi daan dige la kʋlkã'arga n tarɩ bʋraa tɩ a dɛna muko, tɩ kʋlkã'arga la daan yese, dee tɩ bʋraa la n de muko la yãŋa tã'agɛ tɔgra, tɩ la di'ige nẽrba la.

ref: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
text: Tɩ nẽrba basɛba bɔɔra tɩ ba makɛ ẽ bɩsɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ, a tõm tõon-yãlma n pa'alɛ tɩ a pãŋa la ze'etɩ la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
text: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, yele ba yetɩ: «So'olʋm sɛka woo nẽrba n welge wẽrgɛ zɛbra la taaba, so'olʋm na wʋn sã'am mɛ. La so'olʋm ẽŋa yiɛ mẽ wʋn lui mɛ.

ref: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
text: La Asũtãana sãn welge zɛbra la a mẽŋa, a so'olʋm na kãn tã'agɛ ze'ele. Yãma n yetɩ mam digri kʋlkã'arsɩ la Abelzebuli pãŋa la ĩyã tɩ mam yele bɛla.

ref: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
text: Yãma n yetɩ mam digri kʋlkã'arsɩ la Abelzebuli pãŋa la, yãma nẽrba tarɩ la ãn pãŋa digri kʋlkã'arsɩ? Bɛla, ya nẽrba la mẽŋa n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ.

ref: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
text: Pãŋa dãana sãn mãasʋm sõŋa la a bʋdaan-lɔgrɔ gu'ura a yire, sɛla kãn ẽŋɛ a bõntarsõm.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
text: Farisi dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma eerɩ la zẽ'esɩ nẽŋa tɩ ya zĩ'ire wẽndeto pʋam, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba nã'asra ya da'asɩ pʋam.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
text: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãama ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fʋ sãn tɔgra bɛla fʋ po pɔ'ɔra la tõma wuu.»

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
text: Azezi le yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga sãn yese dee basɛ nẽra, a sẽŋɛ la pɔgẽntɩɩsɩ pʋam ka ɛɛra zẽ'a tɩ a vo'ose, la a sãn ɛ zẽ'a koŋe, a yetɩ mɛ tɩ: ‹Mam wʋn lebe la m yi-sɛka tɩ m yese dee basɛ la pʋam.›

ref: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
text: La a sãn lebe ka yẽ tɩ a pɩɩsɛ mɛ malgɛ ãna sõŋa,

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
text: a wʋn lebe mɛ ka tarɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ bayopɔɩ n de wẽensɩ gãnna ẽŋa mẽŋa wa'am, tɩ ba kẽ lagɩm bɔna mĩ. Bɛla, nẽr-ẽŋa toogo yãŋa wʋn gãnna a kʋrʋm toogo la mɛ.» Nẽr-sɛba n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tarɩ zu-yẽlga

ref: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n wõnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, dee sakra ɩta lan yele se'em na, n tõn tarɩ zu-yẽlga.» Nẽrba boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ la bõn-yãlma (Amatie

ref: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
text: Tɩ Farisi nẽra la yẽ tɩ Azezi ka peege nu'usi wʋ ban ɩtɩ ba sisgo se'em na, dee yetɩ a di, tɩ la di'ige ẽ.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
text: Farisi dõma, ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma bɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽvõorɔ buuri wuu pia pʋrɛ mɛ, baa ba sãn ãna pɩka pɩka, la ya ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, la ya ka nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la bõn-bãna tɩ ya wʋn dẽŋɛ ɩta, dee yãŋa ɩta sɛla tɩ ya ɩtɩ la pa'asɛ.

ref: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
text: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n lagsɛ Azezi zẽ'am zo'oge, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Zamãan-ẽna wã nẽrba de la nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ. Ba eerɩ la bõn-yãlma n wʋn pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa, la ba kãn yẽ bõn-yãlma ayẽma, sãn dagna bõn-yãlma sɛka n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azonaasɩ la.

ref: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
text: Sarɩyadia daarɛ, na-pɔka la n yʋʋn yese wẽntulle gɔbga bɔba la wʋn isge mɛ zɛrgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, a yʋʋn ze'ele la zẽ'a n zãagɛ wa'am wa selse naba Asalomõ yɛm yetɔga la, la sɛka n gãnnɩ Asalomõ n boe kalam, dee tɩ yãma ka sɛlsra ẽ.

ref: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
text: Sarɩyadia daarɛ, Niniivi dõma mẽ wʋn isge zɛrgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma mɛ ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, Azonaasɩ n yʋʋn mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ ba la, ba yʋʋn teege yɛm mɛ basɛ tõon-be'ero. La sɛka n gãnnɩ Azonaasɩ n boe kalam, dee tɩ yãma ka teege yɛm.» Peelem yelle (Amatie

ref: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
text: «Nẽra kãn yõ fɩtla sʋgɛ, bɩɩ dɩkɛ pɩ'ɩgɔ vuge ẽ, a wʋn dɩkɛ ẽ zoole la sɛla zuo tɩ a nẽegɛ bɔ sɛba n kẽ'erɩ la.

ref: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
text: Fʋ nini n nẽerɩ peelem bɔ'ɔra fʋ ĩyã wʋ fɩtla la, bɛla fʋ nini sãn ãna sõŋa, fʋ ĩyã wuu tarɩ peelem mɛ. La fʋ nini sãn ka ãna sõŋa, fʋ ĩyã wuu boe la lika pʋam.

ref: So be sure your light isn't darkness.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, guuse tɩ bõn-sɛka n de peelem fʋ pʋam na, da dɛna lika.

ref: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
text: Ya de la geto! La dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ poorʋm na, n ken ẽŋɛ pʋam na?

ref: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
text: Ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma mẽtɩ la yɔɔrɔ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la. La yãma yaabdõma n yʋʋn kʋ ba.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
text: Azezi n daan tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ Farisi nẽra ayɩla wi ẽ tɩ a wa'am ẽŋa yire tɩ ba di. Tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka kẽ zĩ'ire bɔna mĩ tɩ a di.

ref: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Yãma Farisi dõma peerɩ ya wama la ya laasɩ pɔa mɛ, la yãma mẽŋa pʋam pɩrɛ la dẽgrɔ n de fãaga la pʋtʋʋlgɔ.

ref: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
text: Dɩkɛ-ya yan tarɩ sɛla la bɔ nasdõma la ya sũure wuu, tɩ sɛla woo wʋn ãna sõŋa bɔ ya.

ref: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
text: Ya wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, ya ãn wʋ yɔɔrɔ n naagɛ la tẽŋa tɩ nẽrba nẽera ba zuto tɔlla, dee ka mina tɩ yɔɔrɔ n boe mĩ.»

ref: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma mẽ wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma n botɩ nẽrba la ɩta se'em na, ãn wʋ ze-tɩbsa tɩ ya zelgrɩ nẽrba la, la yãma mẽŋa ka boorɩ tɩ ya kalʋm ba la ya nu'ubiire.

ref: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
text: La yãma n mẽtɩ sɛba tɩ ya yaabdõma kʋ la yɔɔrɔ la, la pa'alɛ tɩ ya sakɛ tɩ ban yʋʋn tõm se'em na ãn sõŋa mɛ.

ref: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ yɛm na yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn tõm nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la Tõntõnɩba bɔ ba, la ba wʋn kʋ basɛba, dee nãmsɛ basɛba.›

ref: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
text: Bɛla Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sɩbgɛ zamãan-ẽna wã dõma wʋ la de la bãma n kʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo, wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã,

ref: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
text: lan pɔsɛ la Abɛlɩ kʋa, halɩ wa paagɛ Azakari tɩ ba kʋ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne, la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ tẽŋasʋka la. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ tõon-be'ero bãma wuu taalɛ boe la zamãan-ẽna nẽrba zuo.

ref: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
text: Yãma lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la wʋn yẽ toogo. Se'ere n soe la, yãma n gu'uri nẽrba tɩ ba ka bãŋra Nawẽnnɛ sɩra. Yãma mẽŋa ka sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la, dee mẽ gu'ura nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ tɩ ba sakɛ la.»

ref: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
text: La Azezi n ze'ele bilam fõrgɛ la, tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la Farisi dõma sũure ba isge la ẽ. Tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba bẽrɛ ẽ, sɔkra ẽ sokrɩsɩ zozo'e

ref: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
text: bɔɔra tɩ a tɔgɛ tuuge tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.

ref: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n lagsɛ zo'oge mikra taaba, nẽera taaba nãma, tɩ Azezi dẽŋɛ yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Guuse ya mẽŋa la Farisi dõma pʋlibsum na n ãn wʋ dãbɩllɛ n botɩ dãam wuu kɛlla la.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
text: La Nawẽnnɛ sãn botɩ mõorɔ n boe zĩna dee tɩ ba wʋn yõ ba bugum beere ãna sõŋa bɛla, a kãn bɩsɛ ya yelle sõŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Bẽm tɩ yãma ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra zo'oge?

ref: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
text: Ɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na gãnna wuu, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ya bõn-bãma pa'asɛ.» Bõntarsõm n ka sã'anɩ (Amatie

ref: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
text: «M sɩrdõma, mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya da zɔta dabeem la sɛba n kʋʋrɩ ĩyã mã'a, dee ka tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla pa'asɛ la.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
text: La baa yãma zuugo zomto, Nawẽnnɛ mi ba wuu kãllɛ mɛ. Bɛla ĩyã, da zɔta-ya dabeem, tɩ yãma tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna niinto zozo'e mɛ.» Sɛka n sakrɩ Azezi, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋn sõŋɛ ẽ mɛ (Amatie

ref: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
text: «M nẽrba, ya ka zo'oge, la da zɔta-ya dabeem, se'ere n soe la, la paagɛ ya Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm mɛ tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ ya a so'olʋm.

ref: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
text: Koose-ya yãma n tarɩ sɛla la dɩkɛ ligri la bɔ nasdõma. Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya eerɩ la bõntarsõm n ka sã'anɩ. Ɛ-ya bõntarsõm tɩ ba bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire wakatɛ wuu, bilam tɩ nayigba kãn tã'agɛ zũ, tɩ bõn-mõgla mẽ kãn tã'agɛ sã'am ba.

ref: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wʋn pa'alɛ ya wakat-ẽŋa yan wʋn tɔgɛ se'em.» Bõntarsa n ka tarɩ yɛm makrɛ

ref: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Gergo wã, yʋ'ʋŋ-ẽna wã m wʋn to'oge fʋ vɔm na. La ãne n wʋn sɔna bõn-sɛba tɩ fʋ bĩŋe la?› »

ref: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
text: «La mam yetɩ ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n ze'ele nẽrba nẽŋam yetɩ ẽŋa de la mam nẽra, mam n de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma nẽŋam yetɩ a de la mam nẽra.

ref: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n sɩ'ɩsɛ nẽrba nẽŋam tɩ ẽŋa dagɩ mam nẽra, mam mẽ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma nẽŋam tɩ a dagɩ mam nẽra.

ref: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
text: Ba sãn yõgɛ ya sẽŋɛ ba wẽndeto pʋam, bɩɩ na'adõma nẽŋam, bɩɩ kɛ'ɛndõma nẽŋam tɩ ba di ya sarɩya, da fabla-ya yan wʋn lerge se'em, bɩɩ yan wʋn tɔgɛ se'em to'oge ya mẽŋa.

ref: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
text: Azezi n tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ nẽra ayɩla n boe nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, yele m kẽema tɩ a dɩkɛ tõma sɔ n ki dee bõn-sɛba la, pʋɩ bɔ ma m bõnɔ.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
text: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Ãne n bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m dɛna ya sarɩyadita, bɩɩ m pʋta ya bõntarsõm bɔ'ɔra ya?»

ref: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
text: Dee yele nẽrba la wuu yetɩ: «Guusra-ya ya mẽŋa, da ɛɛra bõntarsõm zozo'e, se'ere n soe la, la dagɩ nẽra n tarɩ bõntarsõm zo'oge ĩyã tɩ a tarɩ yõvore.»

ref: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
text: tɩ a tẽ'esɛ a pʋtẽ'erʋm yetɩ: ‹Zẽ'a ka boe tɩ m wʋn sũm m bõnkɔɔla la. Bɛla ĩyã, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?›

ref: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
text: Yãŋa yele a mẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Mam mi mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em. Mam wʋn wõrɛ m bã la mɛ, malgɛ mẽ bã-kãra, sũm m bõnkɔɔla, la m sɛla woo.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bɛla ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya da fabla ya vɔm yelle, ya wʋn di sɛla, bɩɩ ya wʋn yɛ fu-sɛba la yelle.

ref: Life is more than food or clothing.
text: Vɔm yelle gãnnɩ dia mɛ, tɩ ĩyã yelle mẽ gãnna futo.

ref: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
text: Bɩsɛ-ya mõ-puuro n ãn sõŋa se'em, ba ka tõnnɩ, ba mẽ ka wʋgrɩ futo. La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ, baa naba Asalomõ na'am n yʋʋn tarɩ pãŋa la, a yʋʋn ka tã'agɛ yɛ fuugo n ãn sõŋa wʋ mõ-puugo ayɩla.

ref: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ yan wʋn di sɛla, bɩɩ yan wʋn yũ sɛla yelle pakɛ ya.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
text: La de la nẽr-sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n eerɩ bõn-bãma wuu. La yãma Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mi tɩ yãma boorɩ bõn-bãma wuu mɛ.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
text: La tõntõnna la sãn tẽ'esɛ yetɩ, ẽŋa zuudãana la yuuri mɛ ka kulna, yãŋa wẽ'era tõntõn-bʋraasɩ, la tõntõn-pɔgsɩ, dita, dee yũura bugra,

ref: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
text: Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam wa'am tɩ m botɩ nẽrba nõorɛ n dɛna ayɩla dũnia zuo bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, mam yetɩ ya tɩ mam tarɩ la welgre wa'am.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
text: Tõntõn-sɛba tɩ ba zuudãana wʋn paam tɩ ba ze gu'ura ẽ, tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra tɩ, a wʋn basɛ tɩ ba zĩ'ire, tɩ zuudãana la mẽŋa mãasʋm wa pʋɩ ba dia.

ref: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
text: A sãn wa'am sõnsʋa, bɩɩ bewiine, wa yẽ tɩ ba ken gu'ura ẽ, ba tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

ref: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
text: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ yidãana sãn mina wakat-sɛka tɩ nayiga wʋn wa zũ ẽ, a wʋn gu'ura a yire mɛ kãn basɛ tɩ nayiga la kẽ zũ ẽ.

ref: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn makɛ makrɛ ẽna wã la tõma mã'a bɩɩ, nẽrba wuu?»

ref: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ãne n de tõntõn-sõŋɔ n tarɩ yɛm? La de la ẽŋa tɩ a zuudãana wʋn yetɩ a bɩsra a yire yelle, bɔ'ɔra a tõntõnɩba dia wʋ lan masɛ, dee sẽŋɛ sore.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Zuudãana la sãn leme wa yẽ tɩ tõntõnna la tõm wʋ ẽŋa n yele se'em na, tõntõnna la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

ref: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra tɩ, a zuudãana la wʋn dɩkɛ ẽŋa n tarɩ sɛla la wuu bɔ ẽ mɛ tɩ a bɩsra.

ref: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
text: a zuudãana la wʋn kulum na daar-sɛka tɩ a ka tẽ'esrɩ tɩ a wʋn wa'am, la wakat-sɛka tɩ a ka mi, wa di'ige ẽ. La a wʋn dige ẽ mɛ botɩ a lagɩm na sɛba n ka bɔ ẽŋa sɩra la yẽ sɩbgrɛ.

ref: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
text: Tõntõn-sɛka n mi a zuudãana n boorɩ se'em, dee ka mãasʋm bɩɩ ka tõm ẽn boorɩ se'em na, ba wʋn wẽ ẽ mɛ zozo'e.

ref: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
text: La tõntõn-sɛka n ka mi a zuudãana n boorɩ se'em, dee ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ ba wẽ ẽ, ba wʋn wẽ ẽ na fẽe. Sɛka tɩ ba bɔ ẽ zo'oge, ba mẽ wʋn soke ẽ yɛla zozo'e yelle mɛ, la sɛka tɩ ba gu'ule ẽ zo'oge, ba mẽ wʋn soke ẽ halɩ yɛla zozo'e yelle gãnna mɛ.» Nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Azezi wʋn welge mɛ la sɛba n dolɩ Azezi (Amatie

ref: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
text: «Mam wa'am tɩ m tʋʋrɛ la bugum dũnia wã zuo, la mam boorɩ nɩ mɛ zozo'e tɩ bugum na põn darɛ dita.

ref: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
text: La dẽnɩ tɩ mam yẽ nãmsgɔ zozo'e wʋ mam mise la ko'om pʋam na, la a yelle la wʋn nãmsɛ mam mɛ halɩ tɩ la wa ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ.

ref: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
text: La sɔ wʋn sise a dayʋa, tɩ dayʋa mẽ sise a sɔ. La ma wʋn sise a pɔyʋa, tɩ pɔyʋa mẽ sise a ma. La dayʋa pɔga wʋn sise a sɩra ma, tɩ sɩra ma mẽ sise a dayʋa pɔga.» Bãŋɛ-ya bõn-sɛba n ɩtɩ lɛɛlɛ wã võorɛ bɔkɛ (Amatie

ref: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Ya sãn yẽ tɩ wara sobge bɔbrɛ ayẽma, ya yetɩ mɛ tɔtɔ tɩ saaga wʋn ni mɛ, tɩ a sɩrɩ ni.

ref: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
text: La ya sãn le yẽ tɩ kʋsebgo vugri bɔbrɛ ayẽma, ya yetɩ mɛ tɩ wɔlgɔ lɛm mɛ, tɩ wɔlgɔ wʋn sɩrɩ paagɛ.

ref: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
text: Pʋlibsum dõma wã! Yãma bɩsrɩ mɛ mina tẽŋa, la saazuum yɛla n ãn se'em. La bẽm tɩ yãma ka mina bõn-sɛba n ɩtɩ lɛɛlɛ wã võorɛ bɔkɛ?» Malgɛ fʋ yɛla la Nawẽnnɛ (Amatie

ref: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
text: «Bẽm tɩ ya mẽŋa ka tẽ'esɛ bãŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ ya wʋn ẽŋɛ bɔkɛ?

ref: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
text: Fʋ sãn tara yelle la nẽra, tɩ a tarɩ fʋ wɛ'ɛsa sarɩyadia zẽ'am, mõrgɛ tɩ ya mãalɛ yelle la sore, tɩ a da dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ sarɩyadita, tɩ a tɔgɛ fʋ yetɔga la dɩkɛ fʋ bɔ gu'ura, tɩ a kẽ'esɛ fɔ yʋ'a deem.

ref: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
text: Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra, tɩ fʋ kãn yẽ sore yese yʋ'a deem na, halɩ tɩ fʋ wʋn wa yɔ fʋ sãnnɛ na wuu ba'asɛ.»

ref: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
text: La wakat-ẽŋa nẽrba basɛba n wa'am wa tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Azezi, gɔmna Apilatɩ n daan botɩ ba kʋ Galile nẽrba basɛba wakat-sɛka tɩ ba boe mĩ kãabra bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ ba zɩɩm yese gaam na kãabgɔ la zɩɩm.

ref: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Galile nẽr-bãma n daan yẽ nãmsgɔ ẽŋa la, bãma n de tõon-be'ero dõma gãnna Galile dõma basɛba la bɩɩ?

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ayɛɩ, yãma sãn ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, yãma wuu mẽ wʋn sã'am na bɛla.

ref: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm wõn na bẽm? La mam wʋn dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la ẽ?

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ayɛɩ, yãma sãn ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, yãma wuu mẽ wʋn sã'am na bɛla.» Kẽnkãŋa n ka wɔnnɩ makrɛ

ref: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
text: Azezi le makɛ la makrɛ ẽna wã yetɩ: «Bʋraaga n tarɩ kẽnkãŋa n sele a va'am pʋam, yãŋa wa'am tɩ a bɩsɛ a sãn tara biɛ, ka yẽ sɛla sɛla.

ref: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
text: Tɩ gu'ura la lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, basɛ ẽ yʋʋn-ẽna mã'a, tɩ mam wʋn kɔ gilge a tille, ẽŋɛ bugulgo basɛ ẽ,

ref: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
text: la yʋʋnɛ sãn kaagɛ tɩ a wɔm, la ãn sõŋa, la a sãn ken ka wɔm, fʋ yãŋa sɛ ẽ basɛ.› » Azezi botɩ pɔka yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ

ref: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
text: Azezi daan boe la Zifdõma wẽndeem vo'osgo daarɛ zãsna nẽrba,

ref: and a woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for
text: tɩ pɔka ayɩla bɔna mĩ tɩ kʋlkã'arga tara ẽ botɩ a dɛna kobgo paagɛ yʋʋma pia la anii, yũre ka tãna wʋn dɔ'ɔgɛ.

ref: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yẽ pɔka la, wi ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, fʋ yẽ yolsgo, fʋ bã'aga la basɛ fɔ.»

ref: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Pʋlibsum dõma wã! Yãma nẽra woo ka lorgrɩ a lɔlga, bɩɩ a bõŋa sẽnna yũula ko'om vo'osgo daarɛ mɛ bɩɩ?

ref: This woman belongs to the family of Abraham, but Satan has kept her bound for
text: Tɩ pɔg-ẽna wã n de Abraham yɩɩŋa tɩ Asũtãana lʋ ẽ yʋʋma pia la anii wã, la ka narɩ tɩ mam lorge ẽ vo'osgo daarɛ?»

ref: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
text: La ẽŋa n yele bɛla la, tɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ a bɛ'ɛba la wuu, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm la a tõon-yãlma sɛba wuu tɩ a ẽŋɛ la. Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm wõn na bẽm? (Amatie

ref: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
text: La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba sẽŋɛ ka yele Azezi yetɩ: «Basɛ kalam dee sẽŋɛ zẽ'a ayẽma, tɩ Naba A'erɔdɩ eerɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ a kʋ.»

ref: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
text: La dẽnɩ tɩ mam sẽŋɛ m sẽnnɛ na zĩna, la beesã, la dabsa atã daarɛ, se'ere n soe la, la ka masɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ki zẽ'a ayẽma, sãn dagna Zerizalɛm pʋam.

ref: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
text: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽrba basɛba n de poorʋm dõma wʋn lebge nẽŋam dõma, tɩ basɛba n mẽ de nẽŋam dõma wʋn wa lebge poorʋm dõma.» Azezi sũure sã'am mɛ Zerizalɛm dõma ĩyã (Amatie

ref: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
text: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka yele yɛm be'ero dãana la yetɩ: ‹Zĩna la beesã, mam wʋn dige kʋlkã'arsɩ mɛ tɩ ba yese, dee botɩ bã'adõma yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, la dabsa atã daarɛ tɩ mam wʋn ba'asɛ m tõoma la wuu.›

ref: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
text: A ãn wʋ sɔ'ɔrɔ biire tɩ nẽra bʋrɛ a va'am pʋam, tɩ a bɩ lebge tɩ-kãtɛ. Tɩ niinto sẽnna tɛ'ɛra ba tʋgrɔ a wila zuo.»

ref: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
text: La a le yetɩ: «Mam wʋn le dɩkɛ la bẽm makɛ la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm?

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
text: Azezi daan we'esɩ la Zerizalɛm dɔla tẽn-kãra la tẽn-pɩgsɩ pʋam zãsna nẽrba.

ref: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
text: «Nargɛ-ya pãŋa doose zanõrɛ n ãn mika kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam, tɩ mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn wa ɛɛra mɛ tɩ ba kẽ, la ba kãn yãŋɛ.

ref: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
text: La wakatɛ sãn paagɛ, yidãana wʋn isge yʋ yaŋa la mɛ, la ya sãn weege yẽŋa, ya wʋn wẽ'era mɛ kɔkɔ yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, yo'oge tɩ tɩ kẽ.› La a wʋn lerge ya yetɩ: ‹Mam ka mi yãma n ze'ele zẽ'a.›

ref: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
text: La ya wʋn yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Dagɩ tõma la fõn n daan lagɩm dita, dee yũura, tɩ fʋ sẽnna tõma tẽŋam zãsna nẽrba?›

ref: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
text: La a wʋn le lerge ya yetɩ: ‹Mam ka mi yãma n ze'ele zẽ'a, fõrgɛ ya mam zẽ'am, yãma de la tõon-be'ero dõma.›

ref: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
text: La ya wʋn ka yẽ Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba wuu, tɩ ba boe Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam, la yãma wʋn weege la yẽŋa kɛlla dee dɩkɛ ya nu'usi gɩ nõorʋm.

ref: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
text: La nẽrba wʋn yese dapɔ-bɔba, la zanõ-bɔba, la zʋʋgɔ bɔba, la gɔbga bɔba wa'am wa zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam di.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: Zerizalɛm nẽrba, Zerizalɛm nẽrba, yãma kʋʋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, dee lɔbra sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm ba ya zẽ'am la kuga kʋʋra. Nõorɛ faa tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m lagsɛ ya wʋ nõ-yã'aŋa n lagsrɩ a kɔma ũna a kempagsɩ pʋam se'em na, tɩ ya ka bɔɔra.

ref: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ ya tẽŋa bɔ ya, tɩ a lebge daboto. Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya kãn le yẽ mam, halɩ tɩ ya wʋn wa yetɩ: ‹Pẽgɛ-ya sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la.› »

ref: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
text: La bʋraaga ayɩla n boe mĩ tɩ ĩ-fʋʋsŋɔ bã'aga tara ẽ, tɩ a zɛ Azezi nẽŋam.

ref: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
text: Tɩ Azezi soke lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma la yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ nẽra botɩ nẽrba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ bɩɩ, sore ka boe?»

ref: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
text: Tɩ ba sĩna. Tɩ Azezi botɩ bʋraaga la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, basɛ ẽ tɩ a kule.

ref: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
text: Tɩ ba makɛ tɩ ba lerge ẽ koŋe. Dɛna-ya sik-m-mẽŋa dõma

ref: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
text: Tɩ Azezi yẽ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba wi tɩ ba wa'am wa di la n loosrɩ zẽ'e-sõma zĩ'ita, tɩ a makɛ makrɛ yele ba yetɩ:

ref: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
text: «Nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ pɔgdire kibsa wi fo, da kãn sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire zẽ'e-sõŋɔ, tã tɩ a mẽ wi la nẽra n gãnnɩ fõn.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n zẽkrɩ a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sike ẽ, la nẽr-sɛka n sikri a mẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn zẽkɛ ẽ.»

ref: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
text: La Azezi yele sɛka n wi ẽ na yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ dia wʋntɛɛŋa, bɩɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ wi nẽrba tɩ ba wa'am wa di, da wi'ira fʋ sɩrdõma, bɩɩ fʋ sɔbiisi, bɩɩ fʋ nẽrba, bɩɩ fʋ sawiiba n de bõntatba, se'ere n soe la, ba mẽ wʋn wa ẽŋɛ bɛla daar-sɛka mɛ wi fo lebse bɛla sãnnɛ.

ref: They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death. The Great Banquet (Matthew
text: Fʋ sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, fʋ wʋn tara zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, ba kãn yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ lebse fo. Bɛla, wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ nẽr-sɛba n ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ a yɛm na vo'oge la, fʋ wʋn yẽ yɔɔrɔ.» Kibs-kãtɛ makrɛ (Amatie

ref: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
text: Tɩ tõntõnna la sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ a zuudãana la n yele se'em na, dee yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M zuudãana, fõn yetɩ mam ẽŋɛ se'em na, mam ẽŋɛ mɛ, la zẽ'a ken dee mɛ.›

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
text: La nẽrba la n lagsɛ zẽa dia zẽ'a la Azezi la, ba wõm ẽn yele se'em na mɛ, tɩ nẽra ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n wʋn po di dia Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam na tarɩ zu-yẽlga.»

ref: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
text: Tɩ Azezi makɛ makrɛ bɔ ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe, ẽŋɛ dia zozo'e, wi nẽrba zozo'e tɩ ba wa'am wa di.

ref: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
text: Tɩ dia la wakatɛ paagɛ, tɩ a tõm a tõntõnna yetɩ, a sẽŋɛ ka yele nẽrba la tɩ ẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Wa'am-ya tɩ mam mãasʋm ba'asɛ mɛ gu'ura ya.›

ref: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
text: Tɩ ba nẽra woo yɛta lan pakɛ ẽŋa se'em, tɩ ẽŋa sɔsra sugri. Pɔspɔsɩ dãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Mam da la va'am, tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ lan ãn se'em, bɛla tɩ mam sɔsra ẽ sugri.›

ref: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
text: Tɩ ayɩla yetɩ: ‹Mam da la nii-kɔɔrsɩ pia bɔɔra tɩ m makɛ ba bayi bayi kɔ bɩsɛ, bɛla tɩ mam sɔsra ẽ sugri.›

ref: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
text: Tɩ tõntõnna la lebe ka tɔgɛ bɛla bɔ a zuudãana la. Tɩ a zuudãana la sũure yĩige, tɩ a yele a tõntõnna la yetɩ: ‹Yese tɔtɔ sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la sorɔɔrɔ zuto ka tarɩ nasdõma, la kɔɔrnɔ, la fɔɔsɩ, la wɔbsɩ wa'am tɩ ba di.›

ref: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
text: Tɩ zuudãana la le yele a tõntõnna la yetɩ: ‹Yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ sorɔ-pɩgsɩ la wuu zuo, ka mõrgɛ tarɩ nẽrba la wa'am tɩ ba kẽ tɩ mam yire pɩrɛ.›

ref: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
text: La mam yetɩ yãma mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba tɩ mam dẽŋɛ yele ba tɩ ba wa'am, tɩ ba zagsɛ la, ba nẽra nẽra kãn le malɩn di mam dia la.» Azezi karẽnbia n masɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em (Amatie

ref: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
text: Azezi daan sẽnnɩ mɛ, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ dɔla ẽ, tɩ a gelge yele ba yetɩ:

ref: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
text: «Nẽra sãn yetɩ a doose mam dee ka nõŋɛ mam gãnna a sɔ, la a ma, la a pɔga, la a kɔma, la a sɔbiisi, la a tãpa, bɩɩ a sãn ka nõŋɛ mam gãnna a mẽŋa vɔm, a kãn tã'agɛ dɛna mam karẽnbia.

ref: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
text: La sɛka woo n ka sakɛ nãmsgɔ wʋ nẽra n zɛɛrɩ a kũm dɔ-puurŋa dɔla mam, a kãn tã'agɛ dɛna mam karẽnbia.

ref: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
text: Ya mi tɩ nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam bɔɔra tɩ a mẽ de-tʋgma, a wʋn dẽŋɛ zĩ'ire mɛ geele bɩsɛ, ẽn tarɩ se'em na wʋn seke tɩ a yãŋɛ mẽ ba'asɛ, bɩɩ la kãn seke?

ref: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
text: La sãn dagna bɛla, a sãn ẽebɛ ẽbrɛ dee ka yãŋɛ mẽ ba'asɛ, sɛba wuu n yẽ bɛla la wʋn la ẽ mɛ

ref: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
text: yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-ẽna ẽebɛ la deego, dee ka yãŋɛ mẽ ba'asɛ.›

ref: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
text: La a sãn bãŋɛ tɩ a kãn tã'agɛ, naba ayẽma la n ken zãagɛ la, a wʋn dẽŋɛ tõm na a tõntõnɩba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ naba la zẽ'am ka soke ẽ bɩsɛ, bãma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ zɛbrɛ la ba'asɛ.

ref: So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own. Salt and Light (Matthew
text: Bɛla mẽ, yãma nẽr-sɛka woo n ka basrɩ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a tarɩ la, ka tã wʋn dɛna mam karẽnbia.

ref: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
text: Yaarʋm ãn sõŋa mɛ, la a sãn sabgɛ, ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne botɩ a le ãna yẽlʋm?

ref: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
text: A ka le tara yõorɔ la tẽŋa, la a ka tã wʋn dɛna bugulgo mẽ, ba lobrɩ ẽ basra mɛ. Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.»

ref: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
text: Yãŋa yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn lebe kule la m sɔ yire ka yele ẽ yetɩ, mam tõm na Nawẽnnɛ la fõn be'em,

ref: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
text: Tɩ yɩbga la yele a sɔ yetɩ: ‹M sɔ, pʋɩ fʋ bõntarsõm na bɔ mam m bõnɔ.› Tɩ a sɔ pʋɩ a bõntarsõm na bɔ ba.

ref: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
text: La ẽn sã'am a bõntarsõm na wuu ba'asɛ la, kɔm kãtɛ n wa'am tẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ a ka le tara sɛla wʋn di.

ref: Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus.
text: Lampo-tɔ'ɔsrɩba, la tõon-be'ero dõma zozo'e n daan sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am tɩ ba selse a yetɔga.

ref: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la yõnna yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã to'orɩ la tõon-be'ero dõma, dee dita la ba.»

ref: Then Jesus told them this story:
text: Tɩ Azezi makɛ makrɛ wã soke ba yetɩ:

ref: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
text: La a sãn yẽ ẽ, a wʋn yõkɛ ẽ mɛ buke la sũ-yẽlga.

ref: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
text: La a sãn kule yire, a wʋn wi a sɩrdõma, la a sawiiba mɛ yele ba yetɩ: ‹Naagɛ-ya la mam tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, mam yẽ m pesgo la n bɔɩ la mɛ.›

ref: Jesus said, “In the same way there is more happiness in heaven because of one sinner who turns to God than over
text: Bɛla mam yetɩ ya tɩ tõon-be'ero dãana ayɩla sãn teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, sũ-yẽlga boe Nawẽnnɛ yire pʋam la bɛla gãnna nẽr-sõma piswɛɩ la awɛɩ n ka eerɩ tɩ ba teege yɛm.

ref: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
text: Bɩɩ, pɔg-kãna n boe wʋn tara lig-kõba pia, tɩ ayɩla sãn bɔɩ, tɩ a kãn yõ fɩtla pɩɩsɛ deego la ɛ lagfɔ la sõŋa halɩ wa yẽ ẽ?

ref: Then she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found the coin I lost.”
text: La a sãn yẽ ẽ, a wʋn wi a sɩrdõma, la a sawiiba mɛ yele ba yetɩ: ‹Naagɛ-ya la mam tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, mam yẽ m lagfɔ la n bɔɩ la mɛ.›

ref: Jesus said, “In the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.” Two Sons
text: Bɛla mam yetɩ ya tɩ tõon-be'ero dãana ayɩla sãn teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, sũ-yẽlga boe Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma zẽ'am na bɛla.» Budibla n basɛ a sɔ yire, la a kẽema makrɛ

ref: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
text: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Bʋraa n daan boe tara a dayɔɔsɩ bayi.

ref: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
text: Tɩ la ẽŋɛ dabsa fẽe, tɩ yɩbga la lagsɛ a bõntarsõm na wuu yese sẽŋɛ tẽŋa n zãagɛ, ka tõm tõon-yooro sã'am ẽn tarɩ sɛla la wuu.

ref: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
text: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la nẽra ayɩla zẽ'am tɩ a dɩkɛ ẽ tɩ a tõnna. Tɩ nẽra la dɩkɛ ẽ botɩ a sẽŋɛ mõom ka gu'ura a kurkurdõma.

ref: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
text: Tɩ a kɔm dõnna tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a di kurkuri dia. Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka bɔ ẽ.

ref: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
text: Tɩ a tẽ'esɛ a sũurum yetɩ: ‹Mam sɔ tarɩ tõntõnɩba zo'oge mɛ tɩ ba dita tɩgra dee basra, dee tɩ mam bɔna kalam kiira la kɔm.›

ref: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
text: mam ka le masɛ tɩ ba wi mam tɩ fʋ bia. Ɩta mam wʋ fʋ tõntõnɩba la ayɩla.›

ref: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
text: Dee isge kulla a sɔ yire. La ẽn nãn boe zãazãarɛ la, tɩ a sɔ la yẽ ẽ, tɩ a ninbãalga yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a zoe tʋ'ʋsɛ kɔkɛ ẽ, mõbgɛ ẽ.

ref: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
text: Tɩ bia la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹M sɔ, mam tõm na Nawẽnnɛ la fõn be'em. Mam ka masɛ tɩ ba le wi'ira mam tɩ fʋ bia.›

ref: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
text: Tɩ a sɔ la yele a tõntõnɩba yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ-ya tɔtɔ ka tarɩ fu-sɛka n ãn sõŋa gãnna la wa'am wa yɛ ẽ, dee dɩkɛ nutʋa pire a nu'ubiire, dee dɩkɛ tagra pire ẽ.

ref: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
text: La ya yõkɛ lɔl-polle n nõbɛ wa'am wa kʋ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ dia di, dee ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.

ref: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam bia wã daan wõn na a ki mɛ, dee le vo'oge. A daan bɔɩ mɛ, tɩ tɩ le yẽ ẽ.› Tɩ ba yãŋa ɩta sũ-yẽlga.

ref: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a dayɔ-kẽema la boe la va'am. La ẽn lebe kulna wa lɛm yire la, a wõm na duursi tɩ ba wẽ'erɩ wa'ara.

ref: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
text: Tɩ a wi a sɔ tõntõnna ayɩla soke ẽ yetɩ: ‹Bẽm n ẽŋɛ?›

ref: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
text: Tɩ tõntõnna la lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Fʋ yɩbga la n wa'am, tɩ fʋ sɔ kʋ lɔl-polle n nõbɛ, ẽŋa n yẽ fʋ yɩbga la tɩ a tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm na ĩyã.›

ref: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
text: Tɩ kẽema la sũure yĩige halɩ. Tɩ a zagsɛ tɩ ẽŋa kãn kẽ yire. Tɩ a sɔ yese belem ẽ yetɩ, a wa'am wa kẽ.

ref: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
text: Tɩ a lerge a sɔ la yetɩ: ‹Bɩsɛ, yʋʋma zozo'e tɩ mam tõnnɩ fʋ tõoma, dee ka zagsɛ fʋ nõorɛ baa daarɛ ayɩla, la fõn ka bɔ mam bulla baa daarɛ ayɩla tɩ m kʋ tɩ mam la m sɩrdõma ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga.

ref: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
text: La fʋ bi-ẽna wã n dɩkɛ fʋ bõntarsõm sẽŋɛ ka tõm yalsɩ tõoma sã'am ba wuu dee kulum wã, fʋ kʋ la lɔl-polle bɔ ẽ.›

ref: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
text: Tɩ a sɔ lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹M bia, fõn boe la mam zẽ'am daarɛ woo. La mam n tarɩ sɛla wuu la de la fõn bõnɔ.

ref: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
text: La, la masɛ mɛ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm, se'ere n soe la, fʋ yɩbga wã daan wõn na a ki mɛ, dee le vo'oge. A daan bɔɩ mɛ. Tɩ tɩ le yẽ ẽ.› »

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
text: Tɩ Azezi le yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Bʋraaga n daan boe dɛna bõntarsa, tara tõntõnna tɩ a bɩsra a yire yelle. Tɩ ba wa'am wa yele bõntarsa la yetɩ, tõntõnna la sã'anɩ a bõntarsõm na mɛ.

ref: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
text: Tɩ zuudãana la wi a tõntõnna la yetɩ: ‹Bẽm tɩ ba tɔgra fõn yelle tɩ mam wõnna wãna? Geele pa'alɛ ma fõn ẽŋɛ mam bõntarsõm na se'em. Fʋ kãn le bɩsra mam yire yelle.›

ref: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
text: Tɩ tõntõnna la yele a mẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Mam zuudãana la yetɩ a dige mam a tõoma la pʋam mɛ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Kʋa? Mam ka tarɩ pãŋa wʋn kɔ. La yãnnɛ n wʋn tara mam la sosgo.

ref: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
text: Mam mi mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ a sãn wa dige ma, m tara sɩrdõma n wʋn to'oge mam ba yiɛ sõŋa.›

ref: “A hundred barrels of olive oil,” the man answered. So the manager said, “Take your bill and sit down and quickly write ‘
text: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: ‹Mam tarɩ a sãnnɛ na kaam dugl-kãra kɔbga.› Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹To'oge fʋ sãnnɛ gõŋɔ tɔtɔ zĩ'ire gʋlsɛ yetɩ, kaam dugl-kãra pinuu.›

ref: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
text: Dee soke ayẽma yetɩ: ‹Fõn tarɩ mam zuudãana sãnnɛ na wãne?› Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: ‹Mam tarɩ a sãnnɛ na mui bɔra kɔbga.› Tɩ a yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹To'oge fʋ sãnnɛ gõŋɔ gʋlsɛ yetɩ pinii.›

ref: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
text: Baa la tõntõnna wã ɩrgɔ n ka doose sore la, a zuudãana pẽgɛ ẽ mɛ, ẽn tõm na yɛm na ĩyã. Dũni-ẽna wã dõma tarɩ la yɛm tõnna la taaba gãnna nẽr-sɛba n bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ peelem.

ref: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya dɩkɛ dũnia bõntarsõm yõgɛ sɩrdõma, tɩ dũnia bõntarsõm na sãn wa ba'asɛ, ba wʋn to'oge ya tɩ ya bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.» Bõntarsõm sɩra sɩra

ref: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
text: Tɩ Abraham lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹Ba sãn kãn sakɛ Amoyisi, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la nõorɛ, baa nẽra n ki sãn vo'oge lebe, ba kãn sakɛ.› »

ref: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
text: Farisi dõma n nõŋɛ ligri la n wõm bɛla la, ba la'arɩ Azezi mɛ.

ref: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma pa'alɩ ya mẽŋa nẽrba nẽŋam wʋ ya de la nẽr-sõma. La Nawẽnnɛ mi ya pʋtẽ'era n ãn se'em mɛ. Bõn-sɛka tɩ nẽrsaalba pẽgrɩ la, de la bõn-dẽgrɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
text: Amoyisi lɔɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yʋʋn pa'alɩ nẽrba ban wʋn ãna se'em, ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Azã wa'am na. Lan pɔsɛ Azã n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, ba mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm kõ-yẽlga mɛ, tɩ nẽra woo nargra pãŋa tɩ a kẽ.

ref: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
text: La, la de la nãana tɩ saazuum la dũnia tole gãnna tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la bõn-pɩka ayɩla bɔɩ.

ref: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n basɛ a pɔga, dee le di pɔka ayẽma, a ẽŋɛ la be'em wʋ nẽra n gã'arɩ la a tadãana pɔga, la sɛka woo n di pɔka tɩ a sɩra basɛ mẽ ẽŋɛ la be'em.» Bõntarsa, la nasa Alazaarɩ makrɛ

ref: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
text: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Bõntarsa n daan boe, yɛɛra futo n ba ãna sõŋa, dee tara sɛla woo daarɛ woo tɩ la seke.

ref: But a poor beggar named Lazarus was brought to the gate of the rich man's house.
text: Tɩ nasa ayɩla mẽ daan bɔna, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Alazaarɩ, tɩ a ĩyã wuu tara nõtɔ. A daan ẽn gã la bõntarsa la zanõrʋm,

ref: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
text: bɔɔra tɩ a di bõntarsa la n diti tɩ di-zõra luta la. Tɩ baasɩ sẽnna lɛɛla a nõtɔ la.

ref: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
text: Tɩ Alazaarɩ wa ki. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire Abraham zẽ'am. Tɩ bõntarsa la mẽ wa ki, tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ.

ref: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
text: Tɩ bõntarsa la daan bɔna bug-tẽŋa nãmsra, yãŋa gorge yẽ Abraham halɩ zẽ'a n zãagɛ, tɩ Alazaarɩ zẽa a sakʋkɔ.

text: Ban boe mĩ tɔgra bɛla la, tɩ Azezi darɛ wa'am wa zɛ ba tẽŋasʋka, yele ba yetɩ: «Sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna la ya.»

ref: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
text: Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ba, tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma yẽ la ki-isgne.

ref: But Jesus said, “Why are you so frightened? Why do you doubt?
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ya tẽ'esra pʋtẽ'era bʋyi bʋyi?

ref: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
text: Bɩsɛ-ya mam nu'usi, la mam nãma, la de la mam. Sa'abɛ-ya mam bɩsɛ, ki-isgne ka tarɩ nẽnnɔ, la kõba, wʋ yãma n yẽ mam n tarɩ se'em wã.»

ref: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
text: A tɔgɛ bɛla dee pa'alɛ ba la a nu'usi, la a nãma.

ref: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
text: Ba sũure n ãn yẽlʋm dee tɩ la di'ige ba, tɩ ba ken ka bɔ sɩra, tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma tarɩ sɛla wʋn di kalam mɛ bɩɩ?»

ref: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
text: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ zũn-sẽ'enɛ bɔ ẽ.

ref: He took it and ate it as they watched.
text: Tɩ a to'oge obe ba nifum bilam.

ref: Jesus said to them, “While I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.”
text: Bɛla poorʋm, a yele ba yetɩ: «Mam n daan ken bɔna la yãma la, mam n yele ya bõn-sɛba la n wãna. Mam daan yele ya yetɩ: Dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-sɛba wuu n gʋlsɛ mam yelle Amoyisi lɔɔ gõnnɔ pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam, la yõoma gõnnɔ pʋam na ẽŋɛ.»

ref: He told them: The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death.
text: yele ba yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ yetɩ, Krisi la wʋn nãmsɛ mɛ ki, la dabsa atã daarɛ a wʋn vo'oge mɛ.

ref: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
text: Ẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã tɩ ba wʋn pɔsɛ Zerizalɛm mõolɛ bɔ buuri wuu yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

ref: you must tell everything that has happened.
text: Yãma n de bõn-bãma wuu kaset-dõma wʋn pa'alɛ nẽrba wuu.

ref: I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Mark
text: La mam wʋn tõm sɛka tɩ mam Sɔ daan bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ ya la, tɩ a wa'am wa bɔna la ya. Bɔna-ya Zerizalɛm gu'ura halɩ tɩ a ze'ele saazuum wa'am wa bɔ ya pãŋa.» Azezi zom Nawẽnnɛ yire (Amarkɩ

ref: Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi tarɩ ba mɛ tẽŋa la pʋam yese sẽŋɛ ka lɛm Betani, zẽkɛ a nu'usi saazuum kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ba.

ref: As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven.
text: Ẽŋa n boe mĩ kã'ara kã'a-sõma bɔ'ɔra ba la, a basɛ ba mɛ dee zom Nawẽnnɛ yire.

ref: After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy.
text: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asɛ ẽ, dee lebe Zerizalɛm la sũ-yẽlga zozo'e.

ref: They spent their time in the temple, praising God.
text: Ba daan boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na daarɛ woo pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: From the very beginning the Word was with God.
text: A põn bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ.

ref: received its life from him, and his life gave light to everyone.
text: Ẽŋa n de vɔm dãana. La de la ẽŋa n botɩ nẽrsaalba yẽta peelem.

ref: The light keeps shining in the dark, and darkness has never put it out.
text: Peelem na nẽegɛ lika pʋam mɛ, la lika ka yãŋɛ kĩŋe ẽ.

ref: God sent a man named John,
text: Nawẽnnɛ daan tõm na nẽra ayɩla, a yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Azã.

ref: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
text: A wa'am tɩ a tɔgɛ la peelem na yelle pa'alɛ nẽrba tɩ ba wuu wʋn tã'agɛ bɔ ẽ sɩra.

ref: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
text: Sɛka n de peelem sɩra sɩra la, ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am dũnia zuo. Ẽŋa n nẽegɛ tɩ nẽrsaalba wuu yẽta peelem.

ref: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
text: Sɛka n de peelem na daan wa bɔna la dũnia zuo. Nawẽnnɛ la ẽŋa n yʋʋn nãam dũnia. Baa la bɛla, nẽrsaalba ka boorɩ tɩ ba bãŋɛ ẽ.

ref: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
text: A daan wa'am na a mẽŋa tẽŋa pʋam, la a nẽrba la ka to'oge ẽ.

ref: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
text: Ba ka lebge Nawẽnnɛ kɔma wʋ nẽrsaalba n dɔgrɩ se'em na, la ka de ĩyã n boorɩ se'em, bɩɩ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em. La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ ba lebge a kɔma.

ref: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
text: Sɛka n de Yetɔga la daan lebge la nẽrsaala, kẽ'era tõma tẽŋasʋka. Tõma yẽ a na'am pãŋa mɛ, na'am pãŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Bi-yẽŋa n ze'ele a Sɔ zẽ'am na'am pãŋa. La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, la a sɩra wuu boe la a zẽ'am.

ref: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
text: Ẽŋa n botɩ tõma wuu yẽta yel-sõnnɛ woo yel-sõnnɛ, ẽn ba tara Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã.

ref: No one has ever seen God. The only Son, who is truly God and is closest to the Father, has shown us what God is like. John the Baptist Tells about Jesus (Matthew
text: Nẽra nẽra nãn ka yẽ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la Nawẽnnɛ Bi-yẽŋa la n boe la ẽ lɛ n botɩ tõma bãŋɛ ẽ, ẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ de la bʋyɩla. Azã bɔ Azezi kaseto (Amatie

ref: The religious authorities in Jerusalem sent priests and temple helpers to ask John who he was. He told them plainly, “I am not the Messiah.”
text: Tɩ Azã tɔgɛ ba vẽelga yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na.»

ref: Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, “No, I am not!” And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said “No!”
text: Tɩ ba le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la ãne? Fõn de la A'eli bɩɩ?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ A'eli.» Tɩ ba le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la n yetɩ a wa'am na bɩɩ?» Tɩ a yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ.»

ref: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
text: Tɩ ba le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛla fõn de la ãne? Tɩ tɩ tarɩ fõn lerge se'em na, sẽŋɛ ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ sɛba n tõm tõma la. Fõn mẽŋa yetɩ fõn de la ãne?»

ref: John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, “I am only someone shouting in the desert, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord!’ ”
text: Ban soke ẽ bɛla la, tɩ Azã lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam de la sɛka n mõolɛ weem tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: ‹Malgɛ-ya sore tɩ a dɛna tẽntẽa bɔ Zuudãana la› », wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn põn yele se'em na.

ref: Some Pharisees had also been sent to John.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n daan sẽŋɛ Azã zẽ'am na, Farisi dõma n daan tõm ba.

ref: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
text: Tɩ ba daan soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn sãn dagna Krisi la, bɩɩ A'eli, bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la, bẽm tɩ fõn misra nẽrba ko'om pʋam?»

ref: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
text: Tɩ Azã lerge ba yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna mam, mam misri ya la ko'om pʋam, la nẽra ayɩla n boe ya tẽŋasʋka tɩ ya ka mina ẽ.

ref: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: Ẽŋa wa bɔna la mam poorʋm, la mam ka masɛ tɩ m pirge a tagra.»

ref: John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany. The Lamb of God
text: Bõn-bãna wuu daan ẽŋɛ la Betani, Zʋrdẽ kʋlaa la doone, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Azã daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na. Azã pa'alɛ tɩ Azezi n de Nawẽnnɛ Pebila

ref: The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said: Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Azã yẽ Azezi n sẽm a zẽ'am. Tɩ a yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, ẽna n de Nawẽnnɛ Pebila n yesrɩ nẽrsaalba tõon-be'ero basra.

ref: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
text: La de la ẽŋa yelle tɩ mam daan yele yetɩ: ‹Nẽra n wa bɔna mam poorʋm dɛna nẽr-keko gãnna mam na, se'ere n soe la, a dẽŋɛ mam mɛ bɔna.›

ref: I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
text: Mam daan ka mi ẽn de sɛka, la mam wa'am wa misra la nẽrba ko'om pʋam, tɩ m botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma bãŋɛ ẽ.»

ref: I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him.
text: Azã le tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Mam yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigra ãna wʋ na'adawenne, wa zoole a zuo.

ref: Before this I didn't know who he was. But the one who sent me to baptize with water had told me, “You will see the Spirit come down and stay on someone. Then you will know that he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.”
text: La mam daan ka mi ẽ, la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm mam tɩ m mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, yele mam tɩ: ‹Fʋ sãn wa yẽ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n sige zoole sɛka zuo la, la de la ẽŋa n misri nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la pʋam.›

ref: I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God. The First Disciples of Jesus
text: La mam yẽ ẽ mɛ, bɔ kaseto yetɩ, ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la.» Azezi pɔspɔsɩ karẽnbiisi

ref: The next day, John was there again, and two of his followers were with him.
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Azã daan le zɛ bilam la a karẽnbiisi bayi,

ref: When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, “Here is the Lamb of God!”
text: yẽ Azezi n sẽnnɩ, tɩ a yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Pebila la n wãna.»

ref: John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi bayi la wõm ẽn yele se'em na, isge dɔla Azezi.

ref: When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, “What do you want?” They answered, “Rabbi, where do you live?” The Hebrew word “Rabbi” means “Teacher.”
text: Tɩ Azezi gelge yẽ ban dolɩ ẽ, soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma eerɩ la bẽm?» Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Rabi, fõn kẽ'erɩ la bɛ?»

ref: Jesus replied, “Come and see!” It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Wa'am-ya tɩ ya wʋn yẽ.» Tɩ ba doose sẽŋɛ ka yẽ ẽn kẽ'erɩ zẽ'a na, daan weege bɔna a zẽ'am daar-ẽŋa, la daan de wʋ wẽngɛlŋa karf-anaasɩ.

ref: One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
text: Nẽrba bayi la n wõm Azã n tɔgɛ Azezi yelle se'em doose ẽ na, ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ de la A'ãndre, a de la Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbga.

ref: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, “We have found the Messiah!” The Hebrew word “Messiah” means the same as the Greek word “Christ.”
text: Tɩ A'ãndre dẽŋɛ sẽŋɛ ka yẽ a kẽema Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma yẽ Mesi la. La boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ Krisi.»

ref: Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, “Simon son of John, you will be called Cephas.” This name can be translated as “Peter.” Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
text: Tɩ a daan tarɩ Asimõ sẽŋɛ Azezi zẽ'am, tɩ Azezi bɩsɛ ẽ, dee yetɩ: «Fõn de la Azã bia Asimõ. Ba wʋn wi'ira fʋ tɩ Asefasɩ, bɩɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ.» Azezi wi Afilipi, la Anatanayɛlɩ

ref: Philip then found Nathanael and said, “We have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
text: Tɩ Afilipi sẽŋɛ ka yẽ Anatanayɛlɩ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma yẽ sɛka tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ a yelle lɔɔ gõnnɔ pʋam, tɩ a yelle ken gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na. A de la Azezi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ tẽŋa, Azozɛfʋ dayʋa.»

ref: Nathanael asked, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip answered, “Come and see.”
text: Tɩ Anatanayɛlɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bõn-sõŋɔ tã wʋn ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ?» Tɩ Afilipi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Yi wa'am wa bɩsɛ.»

ref: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yẽ Anatanayɛlɩ n sẽm a zẽ'am, tɩ a tɔgɛ a yelle yetɩ: «Ẽna de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽra sɩra sɩra n ka tarɩ yelle.»

ref: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
text: Tɩ Anatanayɛlɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne mina mam?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boe kẽnkãŋa la tẽŋa, lan dẽŋɛ Afilipi n wʋn wi fʋ la, mam yẽ fʋ mɛ.»

ref: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
text: Tɩ Anatanayɛlɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma naba.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n yele fõn tɩ mam yẽ fõn boe kẽnkãŋa tẽŋa la ĩyã, tɩ fʋ bɔ mam sɩra? Fʋ wʋn yẽ bõn-kãra n gãnnɩ bõn-bãna.»

ref: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
text: Dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ yire n yo'oge, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛkadõma zɔnna dee sigra Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la zuo.»

ref: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
text: La dabsa atã daarɛ, pɔgdire kibsa n daan boe Kana, Galile so'olʋm pʋam. Tɩ Azezi ma daan bɔna mĩ.

ref: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
text: Tɩ ba mẽ wi Azezi la a karẽnbiisi, tɩ ba wa'am kibsa la.

ref: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
text: Tɩ dãam daan pɔ'ɔgɛ, tɩ Azezi ma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Dãam na pɔ'ɔgɛ mɛ.»

ref: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M ma, dagɩ fõn wʋn yele mam, mam n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em. Mam wakatɛ nãn ka paagɛ.»

ref: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
text: Tɩ Azezi ma la yele tõntõnɩba la yetɩ: «Ẽŋa n yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ se'em wuu, ya ẽŋɛ.»

ref: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
text: La kuga dʋgrɔ bayoobɩ n daan boe mĩ tɩ Zifdõma daan tara pɛɛra ba mẽŋa wʋ ba sisgo n yetɩ ba ɩta se'em na. Ko'om lɩtrɩ pinii bɩɩ kɔbga n daan pɩtɩ dʋkɔ ayɩla.

ref: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ãgɛ-ya ko'om pɩrɛ dʋgrɔ la.» Tɩ ba ãgɛ pɩrɛ ba.

ref: he said, “Now take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.” The servants did as Jesus told them,
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Kãbgɛ-ya tarɩ ka bɔ sɛka n bɩsrɩ dia yelle la.» Tɩ ba kãbgɛ tarɩ ka bɔ ẽ.

ref: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
text: La sɛka n bɩsrɩ dia la yelle la n leme ko'om na, ba lebge la dãam, tɩ a ka mina ban ze'ele zẽ'a. La tõntõnɩba la n ãgɛ ko'om na mi mɛ. Tɩ sɛka n bɩsrɩ dia la yelle la wi pɔgdita la,

ref: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
text: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra woo ẽn dẽŋɛ bɔ la dã-sõma, tɩ ba sãn wa yũ tɩgɛ, tɩ ba yãŋa bɔ'ɔra dã-sɛba n ka ba ãna sõŋa la. La fõn dɩkɛ la dã-sõma ze'ele bɔ'ɔra poorʋm.»

ref: But Jesus was talking about his body as a temple.
text: La Azezi n tɔgrɩ Wẽndeego yelle la, la de la a mẽŋa ĩyã yelle tɩ a tɔgrɩ.

ref: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
text: La Zifdõma Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa daarɛ n daan lɛm, tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm,

ref: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
text: Tɩ a dɩkɛ mĩ'isi ẽŋɛ ka'asrɛ dige ba, la niigi, la piisi la wuu Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam tɩ ba yese, dee zã'agɛ lig-tɛɛrba la taablɩdõma lʋbɛ, tɩ ba ligri la ka'asɛ.

ref: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la tẽegɛ tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ, fõn deego la nõŋlʋm tɩ mam tarɩ la, wʋn di mam wʋ bugum na.»

ref: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Sã'am-ya Wẽnde-kãtɛ wã, tɩ mam wʋn le mẽ ẽ dabsa atã pʋam.»

ref: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
text: ka kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la zẽnzakam yẽ nẽrba n koosrɩ niigi, la piisi, la na'adawɛma, la lig-tɛɛrba n zĩ mĩ.

ref: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
text: Dee yele sɛba n koosrɩ na'adawɛma la yetɩ: «Tarɩ-ya ba yese. Da dɩkɛ-ya mam Sɔ deego la ẽŋɛ leebgo zẽ'a.»

ref: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tõon-yãlma kãna tɩ fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ pa'alɛ tõma tɩ fõn tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ wãna?»

ref: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
text: La Azezi n daan wa ki dee vo'oge la, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la tẽegɛ tɩ a daan yele la wãna, tɩ ba bɔ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ yetɔga, la Azezi n daan yele se'em na sɩra. Azezi mi nẽrsaala woo tagsgɔ

ref: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
text: La Azezi n daan boe Zerizalɛm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la, nẽrba zozo'e n daan yẽ tɩ a tõnnɩ tõon-yãlma, tɩ ba bɔ ẽ sɩra.

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
text: La Azezi daan ka dɩkɛ a sũure wuu pa'alɛ ba, se'ere n soe la, a mi ba wuu mɛ.

ref: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
text: A ka eerɩ tɩ nẽra tɔgɛ nẽrsaalba yelle bɔ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a mi bõn-sɛka n boe nẽrsaalba sũure pʋam mɛ.

ref: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
text: Bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe Farisi dõma pʋam tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Anikodɛm. A daan de la Zifdõma nẽŋadãana.

ref: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
text: Bʋra-ẽŋa daan wa'am na Azezi zẽ'am yʋ'ʋŋɔ wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tõm fõn tɩ fʋ dɛna karẽnsãama, se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma wʋ fõn ɩtɩ se'em wã wuu, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ n boe la a dãana.»

ref: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn ka yẽ dɔg-paalga, a ka tã wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm.»

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn ka dɔgɛ la ko'om, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, a ka tã wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
text: Nẽrsaala n dɔgɛ sɛka la de la nẽrsaala, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n dɔgɛ sɛka la tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa vɔm.

ref: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
text: Da basɛ tɩ la di'ige fo, mam n yetɩ: Dẽnɩ tɩ ba le dɔgɛ ya la ĩyã.

ref: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
text: Kʋsebgo vugri la ẽn boorɩ zẽ'a, tɩ fʋ wõnna a dãmnɛ, dee ka mina ẽn ze'ele zẽ'a sẽna, la ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'a. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n dɔgɛ sɛka la ãna bɛla.»

ref: “How can this be?” Nicodemus asked.
text: Tɩ Anikodɛm le soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bõn-ẽŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne ẽŋɛ?»

ref: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de Ɩsrayɛlɩ karẽnsãam-kɛ'ɛma dee ken ka mina bõn-ẽna?

ref: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
text: Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ tõma tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ tõma mi, dee yɛta sɛla tɩ tõma yẽ. La ya ka sakɛ tõma yetɔga la.

ref: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
text: Mam sãn tɔgɛ dũnia wã yɛla pa'alɛ ya, tɩ ya ka sakɛ, mam sãn tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire yɛla pa'alɛ ya, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne sakɛ?

ref: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
text: Nẽra nẽra nãn ka zom Nawẽnnɛ yire, sãn dagna Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sige.

ref: And the Son of Man must be lifted up, just as the metal snake was lifted up by Moses in the desert.
text: Wʋ Amoyisi n yʋʋn zẽkɛ bõnsɛla dɔɔgɔ zuo weego pʋam se'em na, dẽnɩ tɩ ba zẽkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la mẽ bɛla.

ref: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
text: Tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra wʋn tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.»

ref: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
text: Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ nẽrsaalba mɛ halɩ, tɩ a tõm a Bi-yẽŋa dũnia zuo, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra kãn sã'am, la a wʋn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
text: Nawẽnnɛ ka tõm a Dayʋa la dũnia tɩ a di nẽrba sarɩya sɩbgɛ ba, a tõm ẽ tɩ a fãagɛ ba mɛ basɛ.

ref: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
text: La sɛka n bɔ ẽ sɩra, ba kãn di a sarɩya. La sɛka n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la, a põn koŋe bʋʋrɔ mɛ, ẽn ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Bi-yẽŋa la sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
text: Bõn-ẽna n de sarɩyadia la: Peelem wa'am dũnia pʋam mɛ, la nẽrsaalba nõŋɛ la lika gãnna peelem na, ba tõoma n de tõon-be'ero la ĩyã.

ref: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero sisri peelem mɛ, a ka sẽm peelem pʋam, a zotɩ mɛ tɩ a tõoma la wʋn puke.

ref: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ sɩra sẽm na peelem zẽ'am, tɩ a tõoma la pa'alɛ tɩ a sakrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ. Azã tɔgɛ Azezi yelle

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi la a karẽnbiisi sẽŋɛ la Zude so'olʋm ka bɔna bilam misra nẽrba ko'om pʋam.

ref: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
text: Tɩ Azã karẽnbiisi basɛba, la Zifdõma nẽra daan wẽ'era nõke'ene, Zifdõma sisgo n yetɩ ba pɛɛra ba mẽŋa malgra la yelle.

ref: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
text: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azã zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, sɛka n daan boe la fõn Zʋrdẽ doone ke bɔba la, sɛka tɩ fõn daan tɔgɛ a yelle la, ẽn wãna n misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam, tɩ nẽrba wuu sẽnna a zẽ'am.»

ref: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
text: Tɩ Azã lerge ba yetɩ: «Nẽra ka tã wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ la sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ.

ref: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
text: Yãma mẽŋa tã wʋn bɔ kaseto mɛ tɩ mam yetɩ: ‹Dagɩ mam n de Krisi la. La, ba tõm mam mɛ tɩ m dẽŋɛ a nẽŋa wa'am.›

ref: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
text: La ãn wʋ nẽra n di pɔga: Pɔgdita la n soe pɔka la, la pɔgdita la sɩrɛ n ze dee sɛlsra a yetɔga la, a tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ zozo'e. Bɛla mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ zozo'e.

ref: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ ẽŋa lebge kãtɛ dee tɩ mam lebge pɩka.

ref: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
text: Sɛka n yese saazuum wa'am n gãnnɩ wuu, la sɛka n yese dũnia de la dũnia nẽra, la a tɔgrɩ la dũnia yelle. Sɛka n yese Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am na n gãnnɩ wuu,

ref: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
text: la a tɔgrɩ la ẽn wõm dee yẽ sɛla la, la nẽra nẽra ka to'oge a yetɔga la.

ref: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n to'oge a yetɔga la sakɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana.

ref: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
text: Se'ere n soe la, sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na tɔgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ a Sɩa pãŋa zozo'e la ĩyã.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
text: Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ a Dayʋa la mɛ, tɩ a dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ a nu'usum.

ref: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
text: Sɛka n bɔ a Dayʋa la sɩra tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. La sɛka n ka sakɛ a Dayʋa la kãn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre boe a dãana zuo.»

ref: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
text: La, la daan dagɩ Azezi mẽŋa n misri ba ko'om pʋam, la de la a karẽnbiisi la.

ref: Jesus left Judea and started for Galilee again.
text: La Azezi n daan bãŋɛ ban tɔgrɩ se'em na, tɩ a ze'ele Zude so'olʋm lɛbra Galile.

ref: This time he had to go through Samaria,
text: La daan de la a doose Samaari so'olʋm pʋam tole.

ref: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
text: Tɩ pɔka la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fõn dɛna Zifu sɔsra mam n de Samaari pɔka yetɩ, m bɔ fʋ ko'om tɩ fʋ yũ?» Se'ere n soe la, Zifdõma la Samaari dõma daan nẽ'esrɩ la taaba.

ref: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
text: Tɩ pɔka la lerge yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, fõn ka tarɩ lʋwannɛ, dee tɩ lʋa la kɔ, fõn wʋn yẽ vɔm ko-bãma la bɛ?

ref: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
text: Tõma yaaba Azakɔbɩ n bɔ tõma lʋa la, ẽŋa la a kɔma, la a dũnsi yũ lʋa la ko'om mɛ. Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn gãnnɩ tõma yaaba la bɩɩ?»

ref: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
text: La sɛka n wʋn yũ ko-sɛba tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ẽ na, koyũuro kãn le malɩn tarɩ ẽ. La ko-sɛba tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ẽ na wʋn lebge wʋ bulga, bulla a pʋam bɔ'ɔra ẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.»

ref: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
text: Tɩ pɔka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, bɔ ma ko-sɛba tɩ m wʋn yũ tɩ koyũuro kãn le tara mam na, tɩ m da le wa'am kalam wa ãkɛ ko'om.»

ref: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ ka wi fʋ sɩra tɩ ya wa'am.»
