ref: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
text: Fʋ ele la sɩrba banuu, la fõn boe la sɛka lɛɛlɛ wã dagɩ fʋ sɩra, bɛla fʋ tɔgɛ la asɩra.»

ref: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
text: Tɩ pɔka la yele Azezi yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, lɛɛlɛ wã mam bãŋɛ tɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.

ref: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
text: Tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na tãn-ẽna zuo. Tɩ yãma Zifdõma yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrba pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ Zerizalɛm.»

ref: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, bɔ mam yetɔga wã sɩra tɩ wakatɛ n sẽm tɩ ya kãn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Sɔ la tãn-ẽna zuo, bɩɩ Zerizalɛm.

ref: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
text: Yãma Samaari dõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la sɛka tɩ ya ka mi. La tõma Zifdõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la sɛka tɩ tõma mi, se'ere n soe la, fãarɛ ze'ele la Zifdõma zẽ'am.

ref: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
text: La wakatɛ sẽna, la a paam mɛ, tɩ sɛba n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra sɩra la, wʋn pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ la a Sɩa pãŋa, la sɩra. Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsra ẽ se'em n bɛla.

ref: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
text: Nawẽnnɛ de la Sɩa, dẽnɩ tɩ sɛba n pʋ'ʋsrɩ ẽ na, pʋ'ʋsra ẽ la a Sɩa pãŋa, la sɩra.»

ref: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
text: Tɩ pɔka la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam mi tɩ Mesi la sẽm mɛ, sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge, tɩ ba wi'ira tɩ Krisi la. Ẽŋa sãn wa'am, a wʋn pa'alɛ tɔ sɛla woo.»

ref: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n de ẽŋa n tɔgrɩ la fõn wã.»

ref: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
text: Bɛla mã'a tɩ a karẽnbiisi la wa'am, tɩ la di'ige ba, ẽn tɔgrɩ la pɔka la. La baa bɛla, nẽra nẽra ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ la bẽm?» bɩɩ: «Fõn tɔgrɩ la pɔka la tɩ bẽm?»

ref: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
text: Tɩ pɔka la basɛ a yoore la, dee sẽŋɛ tẽŋa pʋam ka yele nẽrba yetɩ:

ref: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
text: «Wa'am-ya wa bɩsɛ bʋraa n yele mam sɛla wuu tɩ mam ẽŋɛ. A nõo kãn dɛna la Krisi la?»

ref: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
text: Tɩ ba yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
text: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ a karẽnbiisi la mõrgra ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, di wɛ!»

ref: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam tarɩ la dia dita tɩ yãma ka mina.»

ref: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la daan sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Nẽra n tarɩ dia wa'am wa bɔ ẽ bɩɩ?»

ref: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam dia de la m ẽŋɛ sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em, dee tõm a tõonɛ na ba'asɛ.

ref: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
text: Yãma yetɩ: ‹La deege la wõr-sɩnaasɩ tɩ bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ paagɛ.› La mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya gorge bɩsɛ vatɔ la, ba bɩ mɛ paagɛ lagsgɔ.

ref: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
text: Sɛka n lagsrɩ la põn tara la yɔɔrɔ. Lagsgɔ la de la nẽrba tɩ a lagsrɩ tɩ ba tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. Bɛla tɩ sɛka n bʋrɛ, la sɛka n lagsɛ la lagɩm ɩta sũ-yẽlga.

ref: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
text: Yetɔg-ẽna wã de la sɩra: ‹Ayẽma n bʋtɩ, tɩ ayẽma lagsra›.

ref: I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
text: Mam tõm ya tɩ ya sẽŋɛ ka lagsɛ la nẽrba, wʋ va'am tɩ yãma ka tõm dee lagsra a bõnkɔɔla. La de la nẽrba basɛba n tõm tɩ yãma po dita tõonɛ na yõorɔ.»

ref: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
text: Samaari tẽn-ẽŋa nẽrba zozo'e bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ, pɔka la n yele ba yetɩ: «A yele mam na sɛla wuu tɩ mam ẽŋɛ» la ĩyã.

ref: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
text: Bɛla tɩ Samaari dõma la wa'am Azezi zẽ'am wa sose ẽ yetɩ, a bɔna bãma zẽ'am. Tɩ a bɔna ba zẽ'am dabsa ayi.

ref: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
text: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan le bɔ ẽ sɩra pa'asɛ, a yetɔga la ĩyã.

ref: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
text: La ba daan yele pɔka la yetɩ: «La ka le dɛna fõn yele la ĩyã tɩ tõma bɔ ẽ sɩra. Tõma mẽŋa wõm a yetɔga mɛ, la tõma mi tɩ ẽna sɩrɩ de la dũnia Fãagra la.» Azezi botɩ naba tõntõn-kɛ'ɛma bia yẽ ĩmã'asʋm

ref: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
text: Azezi mẽŋa daan yetɩ: «Ba ka nannɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa a mẽŋa tẽŋa.»

ref: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
text: La ẽn paagɛ Galile la, ba to'oge ẽ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, se'ere n soe la, ba mẽŋa daan sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la wakatɛ ka yẽ ẽn ẽŋɛ se'em na wuu mɛ.

ref: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
text: La Azezi daan le sẽŋɛ la Kana n boe Galile so'olʋm na, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a daan botɩ ko'om lebge dãam na. La naba tõntõn-kɛ'ɛma n daan boe Kapɛrnaʋm tɩ a dayʋa bẽ'era.

ref: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
text: Tɩ a wõm tɩ Azezi ze'ele la Zude wa'am Galile, tɩ a sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am ka sose ẽ yetɩ, ẽŋa bia n boe mĩ tɩ a ki, a wa'am wa botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ya sãn ka yẽ bõn-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ, ya ka bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra.»

ref: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
text: Tɩ naba tõntõn-kɛ'ɛma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, wa'am tɔtɔ tɩ m bia la nãn ka ki.»

ref: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ, fʋ bia la yeege mɛ.» Tɩ bʋraa la daan bɔ Azezi yetɔga la sɩra, kulla.

ref: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
text: La ẽn boe sore kulla la, tɩ a tõntõnɩba tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ bia la yeege mɛ.»

ref: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
text: Tɩ a soke ba tɩ: «Wakat-kãna tɩ la sõɩ?» Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zaam wʋntɛɛŋa karf-ayɩla tɩ a ɔɔrɔ la basɛ ẽ.»

ref: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
text: Tɩ a sɔ la bãŋɛ tɩ la de la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi yele ẽŋa tɩ a bia la yeege mɛ na. Tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma wuu bɔ Azezi sɩra.

ref: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
text: Ẽna de la bõn-yãlma bʋyi dãana tɩ Azezi ẽŋɛ Galile, ẽn daan ze'ele Zude lebe Galile la.

ref: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm tɩ a ka di Zifdõma kibsa.

ref: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
text: La kʋlga n daan boe Zerizalɛm tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ la Ebre yetɔga dɛna Betɛsda, a lɛm na piisi zanõrɛ, tɩ põnsɩ banuu bɔna mĩ.

ref: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
text: La bʋraa ayɩla n daan boe mĩ tɩ bã'a tara ẽ yʋʋma pitã la anii.

ref: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
text: La a yetɔga la ka boe ya sũure pʋam, bɛla n soe tɩ ya ka bɔ sɛka tɩ a tõm na sɩra.

ref: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
text: Tɩ Azezi yẽ ẽ gãɩ, bãŋɛ tɩ a bẽ yuuge mɛ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ boorɩ tɩ fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm?»

ref: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
text: Tɩ bã'ara la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam ka tara nẽra tɩ ko'om na sãn wa dãmɛ tɩ a wʋn yõkɛ mam basɛ kʋlga la pʋam. Mam sãn yetɩ m sige, ayẽma n dẽŋɛ mam sige.»

ref: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge, dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala sẽŋɛ.»

ref: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
text: Bilam zẽ'am tɩ bʋraa la daan yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dɩkɛ a bõn-gã'ala sẽnna. La daar-ẽŋa daan de la Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ.

ref: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
text: Bɛla tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma yele bʋraa la n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na yetɩ: «La de la vo'osgo daarɛ, la sisri mɛ tɩ fʋ tʋ bõn-gã'ala.»

ref: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
text: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Sɛka n botɩ mam yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na, yele mam tɩ m dɩkɛ m bõn-gã'ala sẽŋɛ.»

ref: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
text: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽr-kãna n yele fõn yetɩ, fʋ dɩkɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala sẽŋɛ?»

ref: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
text: Tɩ sɛka n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na ka mina ẽn de sɛka, se'ere n soe la, Azezi daan kẽ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n boe bilam na pʋam fõrgɛ.

ref: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Azezi yẽ bʋraa la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, fʋ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Da le tõm tõon-be'ero, tɩ sɛla n de be'em gãnna bɛla da paagɛ fɔ.»

ref: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
text: Tɩ bʋraa la sẽŋɛ ka yele Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n botɩ ẽŋa yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na de la Azezi.

ref: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
text: Tɩ ba daan ɛɛra tɩ ba nãmsɛ Azezi, ẽn botɩ kɔɔrŋɔ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ la ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa Azezi tarɩ a Sɔ nõorɛ mɛ

ref: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ tõnnɩ mɛ wa paam nãnannɛ wã, tɩ mam mẽ tõnna.»

ref: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
text: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma daan malɩn ɛɛra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, la dagɩ ẽn sã'am vo'osgo daarɛ sisgo la mã'a ĩyã, la de la ẽn ken wi'ira Nawẽnnɛ tɩ ẽŋa Sɔ, ɩta a mẽŋa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

ref: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ Dayʋa la ka tã ɩta sɛla sɛla la a mẽŋa nõorɛ. A ɩtɩ la ẽn yẽ tɩ a Sɔ ɩtɩ se'em na. Sɔ la n ɩtɩ se'em na, a Dayʋa la mẽ ɩtɩ la bɛla mẽŋa.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Sɔ la nõŋɛ a Dayʋa la mɛ, pa'alɛ ẽ sɛla wuu tɩ ẽŋa mẽŋa ɩtɩ, la a wʋn pa'alɛ ẽ na tõon-kãra n gãnnɩ bãna wã tɩ la di'ige ya.

ref: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
text: La wʋ Sɔ la n vo'orɩ kũm bɔ'ɔra ba vɔm na, Dayʋa la mẽ bɔ'ɔra sɛba tɩ a boorɩ vɔm na bɛla.

ref: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
text: La Sɔ la ka diti nẽra nẽra sarɩya, a basɛ sarɩyadia la wuu bɔ la a Dayʋa la,

ref: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
text: tɩ nẽrba wuu wʋn nã'asra Dayʋa la, wʋ ba nã'asrɩ a Sɔ la se'em na. La sɛka n ka nã'asrɩ Dayʋa la, a ka nã'asrɩ Sɔ la n tõm ẽ na.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ sɛka n selse mam yetɔga dee bɔ sɛka n tõm mam na sɩra, a tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. La ba kãn di a sarɩya. La a yese la kũm pʋam tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ wakatɛ sẽm mɛ, la a paam mɛ, tɩ sɛba n ka tarɩ vɔm na wʋn wõm Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la kõa, la sɛba n wõm na wʋn vɔa.

ref: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ vɔm a mẽŋa bɔ'ɔra se'em na, a bɔ a Dayʋa la mẽ vɔm na bɛla, tɩ a tara bɔ'ɔra.

ref: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
text: La a bɔ ẽ nõorɛ mɛ tɩ a dita sarɩya, ẽn de Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ĩyã.

ref: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ mam n tɔgɛ se'em na di'ige ya, se'ere n soe la, wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ sɛba wuu n boe yɔɔm wʋn wõm a kõa,

ref: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
text: vo'oge ze'ele yɔɔrɔ pʋam yese. Tɩ sɛba n ẽŋɛ sõŋa yẽ vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la sɛba n ẽŋɛ be'em, a wʋn di ba sarɩya.

ref: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
text: Mam ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla la m mẽŋa nõorɛ. Mam diti sarɩya wʋ mam n wõm se'em Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na. La mam sarɩyadia masɛ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka eerɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ mam mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, la de la sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em.

ref: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.
text: Mam sãn bɔ m mẽŋa kaseto, a kãn dɛna kaseto asɩra.

ref: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
text: La nẽra ayẽma n boe bɔ'ɔra mam kaseto, la mam mi tɩ a kaseto la tɩ a bɔ'ɔrɩ mam yelle la de la sɩra.

ref: You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth.
text: La yãma tõm nẽra mɛ Azã zẽ'am, tɩ a tɔgɛ sɩra kaseto bɔ ya.

ref: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
text: Sãn dɛna mam, mam kaseto ka ze'etɩ nẽrsaala zẽ'am, la mam yele bɛla tɩ ya tã'agɛ yẽ la fãarɛ.

ref: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
text: Azã daan ãn wʋ fɩtla n laŋɛ nẽegɛ, la yãma daan sakɛ mɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga wakatɛ fẽe mã'a la a peelem na.

ref: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
text: La mam tarɩ kaseto mɛ gãnna Azã dẽnnɛ na. La de la tõon-sɛba tɩ mam Sɔ bɔ mam tɩ m tõm na, tõon-bãma tɩ mam tõnnɩ na n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam kaseto pa'ala tɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n tõm mam.

ref: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
text: La mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na bɔ'ɔrɩ mam kaseto mɛ. Yãma nãn ka wõm a kõa, la ya nãn ka yẽ ẽ.

ref: You search the Scriptures, because you think you will find eternal life in them. The Scriptures tell about me,
text: Yan eerɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ yetɔga la võorɛ la, ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya wʋn yẽ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ a pʋam. La de la gõn-ẽŋa n tɔgrɩ mam yelle.

ref: but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
text: La ya ka boorɩ tɩ ya wa'am mam zẽ'am wa yẽ vɔm na.

ref: I don't care about human praise,
text: Mam ka eerɩ nã'asgɔ nẽrsaalba zẽ'am.

ref: but I do know that none of you love God.
text: La mam mi ya, mam mi tɩ ya ka nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
text: Mam wa'am na m Sɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ, tɩ ya ka to'oge mam. La ayẽma sãn wa'am na a mẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ, ẽŋa tɩ ya to'orɩ.

ref: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
text: Yãma to'osrɩ la pẽka taaba zẽ'am, dee ka ɛɛra pẽka n ze'etɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a zẽ'am. Yãma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ mam sɩra?

ref: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
text: Da tẽ'esɛ-ya tɩ mam n wʋn yetɩ ya ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ mam Sɔ la zẽ'am, la de la Amoyisi n wʋn yetɩ ya ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ, ẽŋa tɩ ya te'ege.

ref: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
text: Ya sãn bɔ nɩ Amoyisi sɩra, ya wʋn bɔ mam mẽ sɩra mɛ, se'ere n soe la, a gʋlsɛ mam yelle mɛ.

ref: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
text: La ya sãn ka bɔ ẽŋa n gʋlsɛ bõn-sɛba la sɩra, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ mam yetɔga la sɩra?»

ref: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
text: Tɩ ba lagsɛ borborɩ banuu la tɩ ba di deege la gito, tɩ ba pɩrɛ pɩtɔ pia la ayi.

ref: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
text: Tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ a kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam. Tɩ õorŋɔ la darɛ paagɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba we'esɩ la. Azezi de la dia n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm

ref: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
text: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõon-yãlma kãna tɩ fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ tõma yẽ, bɔ fʋ sɩra? Tõon-kãna tɩ fõn wʋn tõm?

ref: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Nẽra ka tã wʋn wa'am mam zẽ'am tɩ la sãn dagna mam Sɔ n tõm mam na n tarɩ ẽ wa'am. La mam n wʋn vo'oge ẽ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

ref: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
text: «Budibla n boe kalam tara borborɩ banuu, la zũn-pɩgsɩ bayi. La bãna de la bẽm, tɩ tɩ wʋn dɩkɛ bɔ nẽrba n zo'oge wãna?»

ref: The ground was covered with grass, and Jesus told his disciples to tell everyone to sit down. About
text: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ nẽrba la zĩ'ire.» La mõogɔ n daan boe zẽ'e-ẽŋa zo'oge. Tɩ ba zĩ'ire, la bʋraasɩ kãllɛ daan de wʋ tʋsanuu.

ref: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
text: ka kẽ õorŋɔ pãrgra ko'om na wɛ'ɛsa Kapɛrnaʋm, mõgrɛ la ke bɔba. Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan sobe, dee tɩ Azezi nãn ka sẽŋɛ ba zẽ'am.

ref: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi daan sẽŋɛ la Galile mõgrɛ tɩ ba ken wi'ira tɩ Tiberiadɩ mõgrɛ la doone.

ref: A large crowd had seen him work miracles to heal the sick, and those people went with him.
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ doose ẽ, ban yẽ tɩ a tõnnɩ tõon-yãlma botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm na ĩyã.

ref: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
text: Tɩ Afilipi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Baa tɩ sãn da dia lig-kõba kɔbsyi, la ka tã wʋn seke tɩ nẽra woo to'oge fẽfẽe.»

ref: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
text: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbia ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'ãndre n de Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbga yele ẽ yetɩ:

ref: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
text: Tɩ Azezi to'oge borborɩ la pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee pʋɩ bɔ nẽrba la n zĩ la, la a daan ken ẽŋɛ zũma la mẽ na bɛla. Tɩ nẽra woo to'oge ẽn boorɩ se'em di tɩgɛ.

ref: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
text: La ban daan di tɩgɛ la, tɩ Azezi yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Lagsɛ-ya borbor-gito la n deege la, tɩ sɛla da sã'am.»

ref: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
text: La Azezi daan bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ ba boorɩ tɩ ba pẽrgɛ ẽŋa mɛ tɩ a di na'am, tɩ a lebe sẽŋɛ tãŋa la zuo ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a. Azezi sẽnnɩ ko'om zuo (Amatie

ref: The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day
text: Tɩ beere daan wiige, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n weege mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba la bãŋɛ tɩ õorŋɔ ayɩla mã'a n zaam boe. La ba yẽ tɩ Azezi ka kẽ õorŋɔ la pʋam la a karẽnbiisi la, a karẽnbiisi la mã'a n fõrgɛ.

ref: Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.”
text: Da tõnna-ya dia n wʋn sã'am ĩyã, tõnna-ya di-sɛba n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa n wʋn bɔ ya vɔm ẽŋa. Se'ere n soe la, la de la ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la bɔ ẽ a nõorɛ.»

ref: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn pa'alɛ ba wuu.› La sɛka woo n selse mam Sɔ la, dee to'oge a pa'algɔ la, sẽm mam zẽ'am mɛ.

ref: They started arguing with each other and asked, “How can he give us his flesh to eat?”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma la wẽ'era nõke'ene la taaba yetɩ: «Nẽr-ẽna wã wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ tõma a ĩyã tɩ tõma di?»

ref: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
text: Tɩ zaanõorɛ daan paagɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la sige mõgrʋm,

ref: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
text: Tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ vugra tɩ ko'om na wẽ'era ke'enke'em.

ref: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
text: Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ masɛ wʋ kilomẽtrɩ anuu bɩɩ kilomẽtrɩ ayoobɩ, yẽ tɩ Azezi sẽnnɩ ko'om na zuo lɛnna õorŋɔ la, tɩ dabeem yõkɛ ba.

ref: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La de la mam. Da zoe-ya dabeem.»

ref: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
text: Tɩ õornɔ basɛba ze'ele Tiberiadɩ wa'am wa lɛm zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Zuudãana Azezi daan pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ dia la ĩyã, dee tɩ ba di la.

ref: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
text: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n yẽ tɩ Azezi, la a karẽnbiisi la ka boe zẽ'e-ẽŋa la, tɩ ba kẽ õornɔ sẽŋɛ Kapɛrnaʋm tɩ ba ka ɛ ẽ.

ref: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
text: La ba daan sẽŋɛ ka yẽ Azezi la mõgrɛ la doone ke bɔba, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, wãne mã'a tɩ fõn wa'am kalam?»

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
text: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ yan eerɩ mam na, dagɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la mam bõn-yãlma la võorɛ, la de la yan di tɩgɛ la ĩyã.

ref: “What exactly does God want us to do?” the people asked.
text: Tɩ ba soke Azezi yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tõm tõon-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ?»

ref: Jesus answered, “God wants you to have faith in the one he sent.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Tõon-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ ya tõm, de la ya sakɛ sɛka tɩ a tõm na.»

ref: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
text: Tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn di la dia tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ mannɩ weem, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ se'em na, a yʋʋn bɔ ba la dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ ba di.»

ref: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ la dagɩ Amoyisi n yʋʋn bɔ ya dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire la, la de la mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ ya dia sɩra sɩra n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire.

ref: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
text: Dia sɩra sɩra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ya la, de la sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sige dũnia bɔ'ɔra nẽrba vɔm na.»

ref: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
text: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, bɔ'ɔra tɔ di-ẽŋa daarɛ woo.»

ref: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam n de dia n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm na. Nẽr-sɛka woo n sẽm mam zẽ'am, kɔm kãn malɩn yõkɛ ẽ, la sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra, koyũuro kãn malɩn tara ẽ.

ref: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
text: La mam yele yãma mɛ, tɩ ya yẽ mam mɛ, la ya ka bɔ mam sɩra.

ref: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
text: Sɛba wuu tɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔrɩ mam, sẽm mam zẽ'am mɛ. La sɛka n sẽm mam zẽ'am, mam kãn dige nẽra baa ayɩla basɛ.

ref: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum tɩ m ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em, mam sigum tɩ m tõm na sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em.

ref: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
text: La sɛka n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em de la m da botɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ a bɔ mam na baa ayɩla sã'am, la de la m vo'oge ba wuu dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

ref: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na n boorɩ se'em de la sɛka woo n yẽ a Dayʋa la, dee bɔ ẽ sɩra wʋn yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. Mam wʋn vo'oge ẽ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.»

ref: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma yõnna Azezi, ẽn yetɩ, ẽŋa n de dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum na ĩyã.

ref: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
text: Tɩ ba daan yɛta taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna dagɩ Azozɛfʋ bia Azezi, sɛka tɩ tõma mi a sɔ, la a ma la? La bẽm tɩ a yetɩ, ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ yire?»

ref: Jesus told them: Stop grumbling!
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Da yõnna-ya.

ref: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
text: Nẽra nãn ka yẽ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, sãn dagna sɛka n ze'ele a zẽ'am na mã'a n yẽ ẽ.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la asɩra, tɩ sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: I am the bread that gives life!
text: Mam n de dia n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm na.

ref: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
text: Yãma yaabdõma yʋʋn di mannɩ mɛ weego pʋam, la ba ki mɛ.

ref: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
text: La di-sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum na, de la dia tɩ nẽr-sɛka n di kãn ki.

ref: I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
text: Mam n de di-sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum, tɩ nẽra sãn di di-ẽna, a wʋn vɔa wakatɛ wuu. La di-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn bɔ dũnia dõma tɩ ba yẽ vɔm na, de la mam ĩyã.»

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya sãn ka di Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la ĩyã, la ya sãn ka yũ a zɩɩm, ya ka tarɩ vɔm na ya pʋam.

ref: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
text: Sɛka n diti mam ĩyã dee yũura mam zɩɩm tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, la mam wʋn vo'oge ẽ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

ref: My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam ĩyã de la dia mẽŋa mẽŋa, la mam zɩɩm de la bõn-yũula mẽŋa mẽŋa.

ref: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Sɛka n diti mam ĩyã, dee yũura mam zɩɩm boe la mam mɛ, tɩ mam bɔna la ẽ.

ref: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
text: Mam Sɔ n tõm mam na, de la vɔm dãana, ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ mam tara vɔm, bɛla, sɛka n diti mam ĩyã mẽ wʋn vɔa mam ĩyã.

ref: The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
text: Ẽna de la dia n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigum, a ka ãn wʋ di-sɛka tɩ ya yaabdõma yʋʋn di ki la. Sɛka n di di-ẽna wʋn vɔa wakatɛ wuu.»

ref: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
text: Azezi n daan zãsnɩ nẽrba Kapɛrnaʋm wẽndeego la pʋam na, tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna.

ref: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
text: La a karẽnbiisi basɛba zozo'e n daan wõm bɛla la, tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Yetɔg-ẽna to'ore tɔ mɛ, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ sakɛ yetɔg-ẽna wã?»

ref: Jesus knew that his disciples were grumbling. So he asked, “Does this bother you?
text: Tɩ Azezi bãŋɛ tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yõnnɩ mɛ bõn-ẽna yelle, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bõn-ẽna botɩ yãma lɛbra la poorʋm kãn le doose mam?

ref: What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from?
text: La ya sãn yẽ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la zonnɩ ẽn daan boe zẽ'a na, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?

ref: The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm na, nẽrsaala ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla. Yetɔg-sɛba tɩ mam tɔgɛ bɔ ya la, ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am bɔ'ɔra vɔm.

ref: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
text: La basɛba n boe ya tẽŋasʋka ka bɔ mam sɩra.» Se'ere n soe tɩ Azezi yele bɛla la, a daan dẽŋɛ bãŋɛ mɛ halɩ pɔspɔsɩ sɛba n kãn bɔ ẽŋa sɩra, la sɛka n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽŋa.

ref: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”
text: Tɩ a le yele ba yetɩ: «Bɛla n soe tɩ mam yetɩ, nẽra ka tã wʋn wa'am mam zẽ'am, tɩ la sãn dagna mam Sɔ la n bɔ ẽ sore.»

ref: Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him.
text: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla la poorʋm, a karẽnbiisi la zozo'e basɛ ẽ mɛ dee fõrgɛ ka le doose ẽ.

ref: Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him.
text: Tɩ Azezi soke a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la yetɩ: «Yãma mẽ boorɩ tɩ ya basɛ mam dee fõrgɛ mɛ bɩɩ?»

ref: Simon Peter answered, “Lord, there is no one else that we can go to! Your words give eternal life.
text: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma wʋn basɛ fõn dee sẽŋɛ la ãn zẽ'am? Fõn tarɩ la yetɔga n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
text: La tõma bɔ sɩra mɛ dee bãŋɛ tɩ fõn de la Pʋpeelem Dãana tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.»

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam n looge yãma pia la ayi la. La ya nẽra ayɩla de la Asũtãana nẽra.»

ref: Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
text: A daan tɔgrɩ la Asimõ bia Azudaasɩ Iskariyo yelle. Se'ere n soe la, ẽŋa n daan de a karẽnbiisi pia la ayi la nẽra ayɩla n wʋn wa pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.

ref: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Azezi daan gilgri la Galile, a ka boorɩ tɩ a gilge Zude, Zifdõma nẽŋadõma n eerɩ ẽ tɩ ba kʋ la ĩyã.

ref: It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters,
text: Tɩ Zifdõma põnsɩ kibsa daan lɛm,

ref: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
text: tɩ Azezi yɩbsɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Basɛ kalam dee sẽŋɛ Zude tɩ fʋ karẽnbiisi mẽ yẽ fʋ tõoma la tɩ fʋ tõnnɩ na.

ref: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ ẽ, a ka sʋgrɩ a tõoma. Fõn tõnnɩ tõon-bãna wã, botɩ nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ fɔ.»

ref: Even Jesus' own brothers had not yet become his followers.
text: La a yɩbsɩ la daan ka bɔ ẽ sɩra.

ref: Jesus answered, “My time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam wakatɛ nãn ka paagɛ, la wakatɛ woo ãn sõŋa mɛ bɔ yãma.

ref: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
text: Dũnia dõma kãn tã'agɛ sise yãma, la ba sisri mam mɛ, mam n tɔgrɩ pa'ala tɩ ba tõoma de la be'em na ĩyã.

ref: Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.”
text: Yãma sẽŋɛ kibsa la, tɩ mam nãn kãn sẽŋɛ, se'ere n soe la, mam wakatɛ nãn ka paagɛ.»

ref: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
text: A yele ba bɛla dee weege la Galile. Azezi boe la põnsɩ kibsa

ref: After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
text: La a yɩbsɩ la n daan sẽŋɛ kibsa la, a mẽ sẽŋɛ mɛ, la a ka botɩ nẽrba yẽ ẽ.

ref: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma ɛɛra ẽ kibsa la pʋam, sɔkra yetɩ: «A boe la bɛ?»

ref: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
text: Tɩ nẽrba waasna tɔgra a yelle nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam zozo'e, tɩ sɛba yetɩ: «A de la nẽr-sõŋɔ», tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, a pã'asrɩ la nẽrba.»

ref: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
text: La nẽra nẽra daan ka saagɛ tɔgɛ a yelle nẽrba la zẽ'am, ban zotɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la ĩyã.

ref: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
text: La kibsa la daan paagɛ la pʋsʋka, tɩ Azezi kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na zãsna nẽrba.

ref: The leaders were surprised and said, “How does this man know so much? He has never been taught!”
text: Tɩ la di'ige Zifdõma, tɩ ba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna ẽŋɛ la wãne mina Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ sõŋa, dee ka yẽ zãsŋɔ?»

ref: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam zãsŋɔ la ka ze'ele mam mẽŋa zẽ'am, la ze'ele la sɛka n tõm mam na zẽ'am.

ref: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
text: Nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, a wʋn bãŋɛ zãsŋɔ la sãn ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, bɩɩ mam sãn tɔgra la mam mẽŋa pãŋa.

ref: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n tɔgrɩ la a mẽŋa pãŋa, eerɩ la a mẽŋa pẽka. La nẽr-sɛka n eerɩ sɛka n tõm ẽ na pẽka, ẽŋa de la sɩra dãana, la sɛla n ka masɛ ka boe a pʋam.

ref: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
text: Amoyisi n bɔ ya lɔɔ la, dagɩ bɛla? La ya nẽra nẽra ka ɩtɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na. Bẽm tɩ ya ɛɛra mam tɩ ya kʋ?»

ref: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ fo! Ãn eerɩ fʋ tɩ a kʋ?»

ref: Jesus answered: I worked one miracle, and it amazed you.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam daan tõm tõon-yãlma ayɩla mɛ, tɩ la di'ige ya.

ref: Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons
text: Amoyisi n yʋʋn bɔ ya nõorɛ yetɩ, ya wãta ya budimto. La, la dagɩ Amoyisi n pɔsɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla, la de la tɩ sɔ-yaabdõma. La yãma wãtɩ mɛ baa vo'osgo daarɛ.

ref: in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath?
text: Yãma sãn wãta ya budimto vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ la ka sã'am Amoyisi lɔɔ la, bẽm tɩ yãma sũnsõa yĩige la mam, mam n daan botɩ bʋraa la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm vo'osgo daarɛ la ĩyã.

ref: Don't judge by appearances. Judge by what is right.
text: Da kãn dɩkɛ-ya nini n yẽ se'em mõŋɛ nẽra bʋʋrɔ, bɔ-ya bʋʋrɔ wʋ lan masɛ se'em na.»

ref: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
text: Tɩ Zerizalɛm nẽrba basɛba soke yetɩ: «Dagɩ bʋra-ẽna tɩ ba eerɩ tɩ ba kʋ la?

ref: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
text: Bɩsɛ-ya, a tɔgrɩ la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ pʋam, dee tɩ ba ka yele ẽ se'ere. Nẽŋadõma la na'an bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ a de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na bɩɩ?

ref: But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.”
text: Tõma mi bʋra-ẽna n ze'ele zẽ'a, la Krisi la sãn wa sẽna, nẽra kãn bãŋɛ ẽn ze'ele zẽ'a.»

ref: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, “Do you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him.
text: Bɛla tɩ a Azezi daan tɔgɛ ke'enke'em ẽn zãsnɩ nẽrba Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yetɩ: «Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya mi mam mɛ dee bãŋɛ mam n ze'ele zẽ'a, la mam ka wa'am na m mẽŋa nõorɛ. La sɛka n tõm mam na de la sɩra dãana, yãma ka mi ẽ.

ref: But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.”
text: La mam nõo mi ẽ, se'ere n soe la, mam ze'ele la ẽŋa zẽ'am, ẽŋa n tõm mam.»

ref: Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
text: Tɩ ba ɛɛra sore tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, la nẽra nẽra ka kalʋm ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a wakatɛ daan nãn ka paagɛ.

ref: A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!” Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
text: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la nẽrba zozo'e daan bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ, dee yɛta tɩ: «Krisi la sãn wa wa'am, a kãn yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma gãnna bʋra-ẽna n ẽŋɛ wã.» Farisi dõma tõm nẽrba tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi

ref: When the Pharisees heard the crowd arguing about Jesus, they got together with the chief priests and sent some temple police to arrest him.
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma wõm nẽrba la n waasnɩ Azezi yelle, tɩ bãma la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma tõm gu'urba tɩ ba ka yõkɛ Azezi.

ref: But Jesus told them, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «Mam wʋn ken bɔna la ya la wakatɛ fẽe, dee lebe sɛka n tõm mam na zẽ'am.

ref: You will look for me, but you won't find me. You cannot go where I am going.”
text: Ya wʋn ɛɛra mam, la ya kãn yẽ mam, la mam n boe zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam.»

ref: The people asked each other, “Where can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks?
text: Bɛla tɩ Zifdõma yele taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wʋn wa sẽŋɛ la bɛ tɩ tõma kãn yẽ ẽ? A yetɩ a sẽŋɛ la Zifdõma n wɩrgɛ bɔna Grɛkɩdõma tẽŋasʋka la zẽ'am, ka zãsʋm Grɛkɩdõma bɩɩ?

ref: What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?” Streams of Life-Giving Water
text: Yetɔg-ẽna wã võorɛ de la bẽm tɩ a yetɩ: ‹Ya wʋn ɛɛra mam, la ya kãn yẽ mam, la mam n boe zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam?› » Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm

ref: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, “If you are thirsty, come to me and drink!
text: La kibsa la ba'asgɔ daarɛ n de a dabs-kãtɛ daarɛ, tɩ Azezi daan isge ze'ele tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Koyũuro sãn tara nẽr-sɛka, a wa'am mam zẽ'am wa yũ.

ref: Have faith in me, and you will have life-giving water flowing from deep inside you, just as the Scriptures say.”
text: Nẽr-sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra, ko'om n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm wʋn bulla a pʋam, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na.»

ref: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
text: Azezi n yele bɛla la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa tɩ sɛba n bɔ'ɔrɩ ẽ sɩra la wʋn wa to'oge. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ daan nãn ka bɔ a Sɩa la, Azezi n daan nãn ka yẽ a na'am pãŋa la ĩyã. Nẽrba la welse mɛ Azezi ĩyã

ref: When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, “He must be the Prophet!”
text: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n wõm yetɔg-bãna la, tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ẽna sɩrɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa la.»

ref: Others said, “He is the Messiah!” Others even said, “Can the Messiah come from Galilee?
text: Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la Krisi la.» Tɩ basɛba daan yetɩ: «Krisi la wʋn ze'ele la Galile bɩɩ?

ref: The Scriptures say that the Messiah will come from the family of King David. Doesn't this mean that he will be born in David's hometown of Bethlehem?”
text: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yele tɩ Krisi la wʋn dɛna la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa, a wʋn ze'ele la Bɛtelɛm, tẽn-sɛka tɩ Adavidi yʋʋn boe la.»

ref: The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus.
text: Bɛla tɩ welgre wa'am nẽrba la pʋam Azezi ĩyã.

ref: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him. The Leaders Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
text: Tɩ ba nẽrba basɛba daan bɔɔra tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, la nẽra nẽra ka kalʋm ẽ.

ref: When the temple police returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, they were asked, “Why didn't you bring Jesus here?”
text: La gu'urba la tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma la tõm na lebe ba zẽ'am mɛ, tɩ ba soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya ka yõkɛ ẽ wa'am?»

ref: They answered, “No one has ever spoken like this man!”
text: Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Nẽra nãn ka tɔgɛ wʋ bʋra-ẽna wã.»

ref: The Pharisees said to them, “Have you also been fooled?
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma la soke ba yetɩ: «A pã'asɛ yãma mẽ yãŋɛ mɛ?

ref: Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him.
text: Tõma nẽŋadõma nẽra ayɩla, bɩɩ Farisi nẽra bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ bɩɩ?

ref: And these people who don't know the Law are under God's curse anyway.”
text: La de la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n ka mi lɔɔ la, ba wʋn yẽ la kã'a-be'ero.»

ref: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,
text: Tɩ Anikodɛm n de ba nẽra ayɩla n daan sẽŋɛ ka yẽ Azezi la, yele ba yetɩ:

ref: “Our Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.”
text: «Tõma lɔɔ la bɔ sore tɩ tɩ mõŋɛ nẽra bʋʋrɔ, dee ka selse ẽ bãŋɛ ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ sɛla?»

ref: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
text: Tɩ ba lebse yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽ de la Galile nẽra bɩɩ? Ɛ bɩsɛ, fʋ wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa ka ze'etɩ Galile.» [

ref: Everyone else went home,
text: Tɩ nẽra woo daan kule a yire.

ref: but Jesus walked out to the Mount of Olives.
text: La Azezi sẽŋɛ la Oliivi tãŋa zuo.

ref: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
text: Tɩ beere wiige bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ a le lebe Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ nẽrba wuu daan sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ a zẽa zãsna ba.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd.
text: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma, la Farisi dõma tarɩ pɔka tɩ ba kuke tɩ a boe mĩ ɩta yalsɩ wa'am wa botɩ a ze'ele nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka,

ref: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
text: dee yele Azezi yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, tõma kuke la pɔka wã tɩ a boe mĩ ɩta yalsɩ.

ref: The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?”
text: Amoyisi bɔ tõma nõorɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam yetɩ, pɔka sãn ẽŋɛ wãna, tɩ lobe ẽ na kuga kʋ. La fõn yetɩ bo?»

ref: They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
text: Ba yele bɛla tɩ ba di la a yɛm bɩsɛ, tã'agɛ dõrɛ ẽ. Tɩ Azezi suure tara a nu'ubiire gʋlsra tẽŋa.

ref: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
text: Tɩ ba ken sɔkra ẽ, tɩ a dɔ'ɔgɛ yele ba yetɩ: «Sɛka n boe yãma tẽŋasʋka ka tõm tõon-be'ego, a dẽŋɛ dɩkɛ kugre lobe ẽ.»

ref: The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
text: La ban wõm bɛla la, bõnkɛgsɩ n dẽŋɛ yese ayɩla ayɩla, tɩ ba wuu yese, dee basɛ Azezi mã'a la pɔka la, tɩ a zɛ a nẽŋam.

ref: Jesus stood up and asked her, “Where is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?”
text: Tɩ Azezi dɔ'ɔgɛ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Pɔka, sɛba n yõkɛ fʋ wa'am na bɛɛ? Nẽra nẽra ka di fʋ sarɩya sɩbgɛ fɔ?»

ref: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
text: Tɩ pɔka la lerge Azezi yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, Zuudãana.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam mẽ kãn di fʋ sarɩya. Sẽŋɛ, la da le tõm tõon-be'ero.»] Azezi de la dũnia peelem

ref: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
text: Tɩ Azezi le tɔgɛ la nẽrba la yetɩ: «Mam de la dũnia peelem. Nẽr-sɛka n dolɩ mam, a kãn malɩn sẽnna lika pʋam, a wʋn yẽ peelem n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm.»

ref: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ fʋ mẽŋa kaseto, la dagɩ kaseto asɩra.»

ref: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Baa mam sãn bɔ'ɔra m mẽŋa kaseto, mam kaseto de la sɩra. Se'ere n soe la, mam mi mam n ze'ele zẽ'a wa'am, la mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a. La yãma ka mi mam n ze'ele zẽ'a, la mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a.

ref: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
text: Yãma diti sarɩya la nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em. La mam ka diti nẽra nẽra sarɩya.

ref: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
text: La baa mam sãn dita sarɩya, mam sarɩyadia de la sɩra, se'ere n soe la, mam ka de m yɩla. La de la mam, la mam Sɔ n tõm mam na.

ref: Your Law requires two witnesses to prove that something is true.
text: La yãma lɔɔ la pʋam la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Nẽrba bayi kaseto de la sɩra.›

ref: I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
text: Mam mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ m mẽŋa kaseto, la mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na mẽ bɔ'ɔrɩ mam kaseto mɛ.»

ref: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
text: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn Sɔ la boe la bɛ?» Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Yãma ka mi mam, la ya ka mi mam Sɔ la. Ya sãn mi ni mam, ya wʋn mi ni mam Sɔ la mẽ.»

ref: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
text: Azezi n daan zãsnɩ nẽrba Wẽnde-kãtʋm na ligri lagsgɔ dagsɩ zẽ'am na, tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna. La nẽra nẽra ka yõkɛ ẽ, a wakatɛ n nãn ka paagɛ la ĩyã. Azezi pa'alɛ ẽŋa n de sɛka

ref: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
text: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam wʋn fõrgɛ mɛ, la ya wʋn ɛ mam koŋe, la ya wʋn ki la ya tõon-be'ero la pʋam. Mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam.»

ref: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Ẽn yetɩ: ‹Mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a na, ya kãn tã'agɛ wa'am bilam› na, a yetɩ a kʋ la a mẽŋa bɩɩ?»

ref: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ze'ele la tẽŋa, la mam ze'ele la saazuum wa'am. Yãma de la dũnia wã nẽrba, la mam dagɩ dũnia wã nẽra.

ref: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam yetɩ, ya wʋn ki la ya tõon-be'ero la pʋam. Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn ka bɔ sɩra tɩ mam de la sɛka tɩ mam de la, ya wʋn ki la ya tõon-be'ero la pʋam.»

ref: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
text: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la ãne?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam de la sɛka tɩ mam yetɩ ya halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la.

ref: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
text: Mam tarɩ nɩ yɛla zozo'e mɛ n wʋn tɔgɛ yãma yelle, dee di ya sarɩya. La sɛka n tõm mam na de la sɩra dãana, la sɛla tɩ mam wõm a zẽ'am n bɛla tɩ mam tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra dũnia dõma.»

ref: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
text: La ba ka bãŋɛ tɩ a tɔgrɩ ba la a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yelle.

ref: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
text: Bɛla tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn wa zẽkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la, ya wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam de la sɛka tɩ mam de la. La ya wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam ka ɩtɩ sɛla sɛla la m mẽŋa nõorɛ, mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n pa'alɛ mam se'em na mã'a tɩ mam tɔgrɩ.

ref: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
text: La sɛka n tõm mam na boe la mam mɛ, a ka basɛ mam m yɩla, se'ere n soe la, mam ɩtɩ la sɛla n ãn a sũure yẽlʋm daarɛ woo.»

ref: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
text: Ẽn tɔgɛ bɛla la, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra. Sɩra wʋn to'oge ya basɛ

ref: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele Zifdõma sɛba n daan bɔ ẽ sɩra la yetɩ: «Ya sãn sakra mam yetɔga la ka basɛ, ya sɩrɩ de la mam karẽnbiisi.

ref: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
text: La ya wʋn bãŋɛ sɩra, la sɩra la wʋn to'oge ya basɛ.»

ref: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
text: Tɩ nẽrba la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ, tõma ka de nẽra nẽra yamsɩ. La wãne tɩ fõn yetɩ, tɩ wʋn to'oge tɩ mẽŋa?»

ref: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero de la tõon-be'ero yamŋa.

ref: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
text: Yamŋa dagɩ yire la nẽra wakatɛ wuu, la yire la dayʋa de la yire la nẽra wakatɛ wuu.

ref: If the Son gives you freedom, you are free!
text: Bɛla ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la sãn to'oge ya basɛ, ya sɩrɩ to'oge ya mẽŋa.

ref: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
text: Mam mi tɩ ya de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ. La yãma eerɩ mam mɛ tɩ ya kʋ, yãma n ka to'orɩ mam yetɔga la ĩyã.

ref: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
text: Mam tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ mam yẽ m Sɔ zẽ'am na. La yãma mẽ ɩtɩ la sɛla tɩ ya wõm ya sɔ zẽ'am na.»

ref: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
text: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Abraham n de tõma sɔ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn de nɩ na Abraham kɔma, ya wʋn tõm nɩ wʋ Abraham n tõm se'em na.

ref: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
text: La yãma eerɩ mam mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ ya kʋ, mam n tɔgɛ yãma sɩra la tɩ mam wõm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na ĩyã. Abraham ka ẽŋɛ bɛla.

ref: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
text: Ya tõnnɩ na ya sɔ n tõnnɩ se'em na.» Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma dagɩ tãmpĩiba. Tõma tarɩ la sɔ ayɩla, n de Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sãn de nɩ ya sɔ, ya wʋn nõŋɛ nɩ mam, se'ere n soe la, mam ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am. Mam ka wa'am m mẽŋa, ẽŋa n tõm mam.

ref: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
text: Bẽm tɩ yãma ka wõnna mam yetɔga la võorɛ? La de la yãma n ka boorɩ tɩ ya sakɛ mam yetɔga la ĩyã.

ref: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
text: Ya sɔ de la Asũtãana, la ya boorɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ la ya sɔ la n boorɩ se'em na. A de la nẽr-kʋʋra halɩ pĩ'iluŋo. A ka boe sɩra pʋam, sɩra n ka boe a pʋam na ĩyã. A sãn parna, ẽn ãn se'em n bɛla, se'ere n soe la, a de la põmpɔrma, la põmpɔrŋɔ wuu sɔ.

ref: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
text: La, mam n tɔgrɩ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ ya ka bɔ'ɔra mam sɩra.

ref: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
text: Yãma nẽr-kãna n tã wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ mam tõm tõon-be'ego? La mam n tɔgrɩ sɩra la, bẽm tɩ ya ka bɔ'ɔra mam sɩra?

ref: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
text: Sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽra selsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga. La yãma n dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la ĩyã, bɛla tɩ ya ka sɛlsra.»

ref: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma tarɩ bʋʋrɔ tɩ tõma yetɩ, fʋ de la Samaari nẽra, kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ fo.»

ref: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga ka dolɩ mam, mam nã'asrɩ la m Sɔ, la yãma ka sakɛ tɩ ya nã'asra mam.

ref: years old!” they said. “How could you have seen Abraham?”
text: Mam ka eerɩ m mẽŋa nã'asgɔ. Nẽra ayɩla n boe n eerɩ mam nã'asgɔ, dee dita sarɩya.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽra sãn sakɛ mam yetɔga, a kãn malɩn ki.»

ref: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
text: Tɩ Zifdõma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nãnannɛ wã tõma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ kʋlkã'arga n sɩrɩ dolɩ fo. Abraham la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la ki mɛ. Tɩ fõn yetɩ, nẽra sãn sakɛ fõn yetɔga, a kãn malɩn ki.

ref: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
text: Fõn gãnnɩ tõma yaaba Abraham n ki la mɛ bɩɩ? Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la mẽ ki mɛ. Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn de la ãne?»

ref: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam sãn nã'asra m mẽŋa, mam nã'asgɔ la de la zãŋa. La de la mam Sɔ la n nã'asrɩ mam, sɛka tɩ ya wi'iri ya Nawẽnnɛ na.

ref: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
text: La yãma ka mi ẽ. La mam nõo mi ẽ. Mam sãn yetɩ, mam ka mi ẽ, mam wʋn dɛna la põmpɔrma wʋ yãma la. La mam mi ẽ mɛ, dee sakra a yetɔga.

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ Abraham n yʋʋn nãn ka boe la, mam boe mɛ.»

ref: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
text: Tɩ ba pĩ'ise kuga tɩ ba lobe ẽ. Tɩ Azezi li'ise Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yese.

ref: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
text: La Azezi daan sẽnnɩ tɔlla mɛ, yẽ bʋraa tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna fʋa.

ref: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, nẽr-kãna n tõm tõon-be'ero? Bʋraa wã bɩɩ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma n tõm tõon-be'ero, tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna fʋa?»

ref: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La dagɩ bʋraa wã bɩɩ a sɔ bɩɩ a ma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, tɩ a dɛna fʋa. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ a tõm na ẽ pa'alɛ a tõoma.

ref: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ tõma tõm sɛka n tõm mam na tõoma, la nãn ken dɛna wʋntɛɛŋa wã. Yʋ'ʋŋɔ sẽm mɛ tɩ nẽra kãn le tã'agɛ tõm.

ref: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
text: Mam n nãn boe dũnia pʋam wã, mam de la dũnia peelem.»

ref: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
text: A yele bɛla dee tʋbsɛ la nõtõorʋm tẽŋa bũn tẽntõnnɔ bɩ fʋa la nini,

ref: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
text: dee yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ ka wire fʋ nẽŋa Silowe kʋlgam.» Tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka wire a nẽŋa, dee lɛbra, tɩ a nini na yẽta.

ref: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
text: Tɩ a sawiiba, la sɛba n mi ẽ kʋrʋm tɩ a sosrɩ mɛ na yetɩ: «Dagɩ ẽna n daan zĩ sɔsra la?»

ref: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
text: Tɩ sɛba yetɩ: «La de la ẽŋa.» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, a wõn na ẽŋa dee la dagɩ ẽ.» Tɩ a mẽŋa yetɩ: «La de la mam.»

ref: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
text: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Bʋraa la yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azezi la n bũn tẽntõnnɔ bɩ mam nini, dee yele mam tɩ m sẽŋɛ Silowe kʋlgam ka wire. Tɩ mam sẽŋɛ ka wire, yãŋa yẽta.»

ref: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
text: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «A boe la bɛ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Mam ka mi.» Farisi dõma soke fʋa lan ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ ẽŋa yẽta

ref: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
text: La Azezi n daan bũn tẽntõnnɔ bɩ fʋa la nini daar-sɛka tɩ a yẽta la, daan de la Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ.

ref: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma la mẽ le soke ẽ yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn yẽta?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «A bũn na tẽntõnnɔ bɩ mam nini, tɩ mam wire, yãŋa yẽta.»

ref: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma basɛba yetɩ: «Nẽr-ẽna wã ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am, se'ere n soe la, a ka sakrɩ vo'osgo daarɛ sisgo.» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Nẽra n de tõon-be'ero dãana wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tõm tõon-yãlma wãna?» Tɩ welgre kẽ Farisi dõma la tẽŋasʋka.

ref: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
text: Tɩ ba le soke bʋraa la yetɩ: «Fõn wʋn yele a yelle tɩ bẽm, ẽn basɛ tɩ fõn nini yẽta la?» Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «A de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa.»

ref: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
text: Tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la ka bɔɔra tɩ ba sakɛ tɩ a daan de la fʋa dee yãŋa yẽta, tɩ ba wi a sɔ la a ma,

ref: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
text: soke ba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la yãma dayʋa la tɩ yãma yetɩ yãma dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna la fʋa la? La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ a yãŋa yẽta?»

ref: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
text: Tɩ a sɔ la a ma lerge yetɩ: «Tõma mi tɩ a de la tõma dayʋa, la tõma daan dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna la fʋa.

ref: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
text: La tõma ka mi lan ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ a yãŋa yẽta, bɩɩ sɛka n botɩ a nini na yẽta. Soke-ya ẽ, tɩ a ka le dɛna bia, a mẽŋa wʋn tɔgɛ.»

ref: The man's parents said this because they were afraid of their leaders. The leaders had already agreed that no one was to have anything to do with anyone who said Jesus was the Messiah.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ a sɔ la a ma la yetɩ: «Soke-ya ẽ tɩ a ka de bia.»

ref: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
text: Tɩ ba le wi bʋra-sɛka n daan de fʋa la nõorɛ ayi pʋam, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tɔgɛ sɩra Nawẽnnɛ ĩyã. Tõma mi tɩ bʋra-ẽna de la tõon-be'ero dãana.»

ref: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
text: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «A sãn dɛna tõon-be'ero dãana, bɩɩ a dagɩ tõon-be'ero dãana, mam ka mi. Mam n mi sɛla de la, mam daan de la fʋa, la lɛɛlɛ wã mam yẽtɩ mɛ.»

ref: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
text: Tɩ ba soke ẽ yetɩ: «A ẽŋɛ fõn na bẽm? A ẽŋɛ la wãne botɩ fõn yẽta?»

ref: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text: Tɩ a lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam põn dẽŋɛ yele ya mɛ, tɩ ya ka selse, bẽm tɩ ya le bɔɔra tɩ ya wõm? Yãma mẽ boorɩ tɩ ya lebge la a karẽnbiisi bɩɩ?»

ref: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
text: Tɩ ba tʋ ẽ, dee yetɩ: «Fõn de a karẽnbia, tõma de la Amoyisi karẽnbiisi.

ref: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
text: Tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn tɔgɛ bɔ la Amoyisi, la ẽna, tõma ka mi ẽn ze'ele zẽ'e-sɛka.»

ref: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
text: Tɩ bʋraa la yele ba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la yel-di'iŋa, yãma ka mi ẽn ze'ele zẽ'a, la ẽŋa n botɩ mam nini yẽta.

ref: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
text: Tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka selsrɩ tõon-be'ero dõma sosgo, la nẽra sãn pʋ'ʋsra ẽ, dee ɩta ẽn boorɩ se'em, a selsrɩ la ẽŋa dãana sosgo.

ref: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
text: Halɩ pĩ'iluŋo, ba nãn ka wõm tɩ nẽra botɩ nẽra tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna fʋa nini yẽta.

ref: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
text: Bʋra-ẽna sãn ka ze'ele nɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a kãn yãŋɛ nɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla.»

ref: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
text: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ba dɔgɛ fõn na tõon-be'ero pʋam, tɩ fõn yetɩ fʋ pa'alɛ tõma?» Tɩ ba dige ẽ ba wẽndeem na. Sɛba n mi dee ka bɔɔra tɩ ba sakɛ ãn wʋ fɔɔsɩ la

ref: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
text: Tɩ Azezi wõm tɩ ba dige ẽ mɛ, yãŋa wa yẽ ẽ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn bɔ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la sɩra mɛ?»

ref: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
text: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Zuudãana, Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la de la ãne? Yele ma tɩ m bɔ ẽ sɩra.»

ref: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ yẽ ẽ mɛ, ẽŋa n tɔgrɩ la fʋ lɛ wã.»

ref: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
text: Tɩ bʋraa la yetɩ: «Zuudãana, mam bɔ fʋ sɩra mɛ», dee ka dũma Azezi nẽŋam.

ref: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «Mam wa'am dũnia wã zuo tɩ welgre n bɔna, tɩ fɔɔsɩ yẽta, dee tɩ sɛba n yẽtɩ la lebge fɔɔsɩ.»

ref: When the Pharisees heard Jesus say this, they asked, “Are we blind?”
text: Tɩ Farisi dõma sɛba n daan boe mĩ na n wõm bɛla la, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma mẽ de la fɔɔsɩ bɩɩ?»

ref: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn de nɩ fɔɔsɩ, ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka boe ya zuo. La lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma yetɩ yãma yẽtɩ mɛ, bɛla tɩ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ bɔna ya zuo.»

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
text: La Azezi daan yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n ka dolɩ zanõrɛ kẽ'era piisi denne pʋam, dee dɔla lalga kẽ'era, ẽŋa de la nayiga la fãara.

ref: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
text: La zanõrɛ la gu'ura yo'orɩ mɛ bɔ'ɔra ẽŋa. Tɩ piisi la mina a kõa, tɩ a wi'ira ẽn soe sɛba la yʋ'ʋra, botɩ ba dɔla ẽ yɛsra.

ref: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
text: La a sãn tarɩ a piisi la wuu yese, a tollɩ la ba nẽŋa sẽnna, tɩ ba dɔla ẽ, ban mi a kõa la ĩyã.

ref: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
text: La ba kãn malɩn doose sãana, ba wʋn zoe mɛ fõrgɛ dee basɛ ẽ, ban ka mi a kõa la ĩyã.»

ref: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
text: Azezi makɛ la makrɛ ẽna wã bɔ ba, la ba ka bãŋɛ ẽn tɔgɛ se'em bɔ ba la võorɛ. Azezi n de zanõrɛ

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
text: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ mam n de piisi denne na zanõrɛ.

ref: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
text: Sɛba wuu n dẽŋɛ mam wa'am na de la nayigba la fãarba, la piisi la ka selse ba.

ref: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
text: Mam n de zanõrɛ la. La nẽr-sɛka n dolɩ mam zẽ'am kẽ'era, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ. La a wʋn kẽ'era dee yɛsra, yẽta a dia.

ref: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
text: Nayiga sẽm tɩ a zũ mɛ, la a kʋ, la a sã'am mã'a, la mam wa'am tɩ nẽrba n yẽ vɔm sõŋa sõŋa.» Azezi n de dũnsĩin-sõŋɔ

ref: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
text: Tɩ Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Mam n de dũnsĩin-sõŋɔ. Dũnsĩin-sõŋɔ sakɛ kũm mɛ a piisi ĩyã.

ref: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
text: La nẽra n tõnnɩ tɩ ba yɔɔra ẽ, tɩ a ka sɔna piisi la, a ka bɩsrɩ ba sõŋa, la a sãn yẽ saasaa n sẽm, a basrɩ piisi la mɛ dee zɔta. La saasaa la wʋn yõgɛ piisi la mɛ, botɩ ba wɩrgɛ.

ref: Hired workers run away because they don't care about the sheep.
text: Se'ere n soe la, a tõnnɩ tɔ'ɔra la yɔɔrɔ, a ka fablɩ piisi la yelle.

ref: I am the good shepherd. I know my sheep, and they know me.
text: Mam n de dũnsĩin-sõŋɔ la, la mam mi m piisi la mɛ, tɩ ba mẽ mina mam,

ref: Just as the Father knows me, I know the Father, and I give up my life for my sheep.
text: wʋ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n mi mam, tɩ mam mẽ mina ẽ se'em na. Mam sakɛ kũm mɛ m piisi la ĩyã.

ref: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
text: La mam tarɩ piisi basɛba mɛ n ka boe denne wã pʋam. Dẽnɩ tɩ mam tarɩ ba wa'am, tɩ ba wõm mam kõa, lagɩm na bãna tɩ ba dɛna ayɩla, tɩ dũnsĩina ayɩla bɩsra ba.

ref: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
text: Mam Sɔ nõŋɛ mam mɛ, mam n dɩkɛ m vɔm bɔ'ɔra nẽrsaalba, dee wʋn le to'oge ẽ na ĩyã.

ref: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
text: Nẽra nẽra ka to'orɩ mam vɔm. Mam mẽŋa n wʋn dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ. Mam tarɩ pãŋa mɛ wʋn dɩkɛ m vɔm bɔ, dee tara pãŋa wʋn le to'oge ẽ. Mam Sɔ n bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em n bɛla.»

ref: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
text: Yetɔg-bãna ĩyã tɩ welgre daan le wa'am Zifdõma la tẽŋasʋka.

ref: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
text: Tɩ basɛba zozo'e yetɩ: «Kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ ẽ, a de la zɔlgɔ, bẽm tɩ ya sɛlsra ẽ?»

ref: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
text: Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Yetɔg-bãna wã dagɩ kʋlkã'arga n dolɩ nẽra yetɔga. Kʋlkã'arga tã wʋn botɩ fʋa nini yẽta?» Azezi la Nawẽnnɛ de la ayɩla

ref: That winter, Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Temple Festival.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa Zifdõma Wẽnde-kãtɛ la malgrɛ tẽerɛ kibsa n daan boe Zerizalɛm. La daan de la ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ.

ref: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
text: Tɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na ka gilgra zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Asalomõ võnana la.

ref: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma lagsɛ gilge ẽ, yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn wʋn basɛ tɩ tõma sẽ'ena ka paagɛ la dabʋrɛ? Fʋ sãn dɛna la Krisi la, sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na, fʋ yele to sɩra sɩra.»

ref: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam põn yele ya mɛ, tɩ ya ka bɔ mam sɩra. Tõon-sɛba tɩ mam tõnnɩ na m Sɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ la, pa'alɩ mam n de sɛka mɛ.

ref: But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me.
text: La yãma ka bɔ mam sɩra, yãma n dagɩ mam piisi la ĩyã.

ref: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
text: Mam piisi la wõnnɩ mam kõa mɛ, mam mi ba mɛ, la ba dolɩ mam mɛ.

ref: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
text: Mam bɔ'ɔrɩ ba la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, tɩ ba kãn malɩn sã'am, la nẽra ka tã wʋn fãagɛ ba mam nu'usum.

ref: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
text: Mam Sɔ la n bɔ mam bãma la, a gãnnɩ sɛla woo mɛ, nẽra ka tã wʋn fãagɛ ba mam Sɔ la nu'usum.

ref: and I am one with the Father.
text: Mam, la mam Sɔ la de la ayɩla.»

ref: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma le pĩ'ise kuga tɩ ba lobe ẽ kʋ.

ref: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ botɩ mam tõm tõon-sõma zozo'e mɛ pa'alɛ ya. Tõon-sõma bãma pʋam, tõon-kãna ĩyã tɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya lobe mam na kuga?»

ref: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La dagɩ tõon-sõma yelle tɩ tõma bɔɔra tɩ tɩ lobe fʋ la kuga kʋ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋr-sã'aŋɔ la ĩyã, fõn de nẽrsaala dee ẽŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa tɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.»

ref: Jesus replied: In your Scriptures doesn't God say, “You are gods”?
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ ya lɔɔ gõŋɔ la pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: ‹Ya de la wẽna.›

ref: You can't argue with the Scriptures, and God spoke to those people and called them gods.
text: Tɩ mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ yetɔga la de la sɩra, la a ka tã wʋn sã'am. A wi'iri sɛba tɩ a tɔgɛ yetɔga bɔ ba la tɩ wẽna.

ref: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
text: La mam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tõm dũnia zuo wã, yãma yele mam tɩ m sã'anɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam n yele tɩ mam de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la ĩyã?

ref: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
text: Mam sãn ka tõnna m Sɔ tõoma, ya da bɔ mam sɩra.

ref: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
text: La mam sãn tõnna a tõoma, baa ya sãn ka bɔ mam sɩra, ya bɔ m tõoma la sɩra, tɩ ya tã'agɛ bãŋɛ, la ya sakɛ sɩra tɩ mam Sɔ la boe la mam lɛ mɛ, tɩ mam mẽ bɔna la ẽ.»

ref: Again they wanted to arrest Jesus. But he escaped
text: Tɩ ba le zɩgra tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a põsɛ.

ref: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
text: La ẽn põsɛ la, a le dooge la Zʋrdẽ kʋlaa la doone, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Azã daan pɔsɛ bɔna mĩ misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, ka bɔna bilam.

ref: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
text: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am. Ba daan tɔgrɩ yetɩ: «Azã ka tõm tõon-yãlma baa ayɩla, la Azã n daan tɔgɛ bʋra-ẽna yelle se'em wuu la de la sɩra.»

ref: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
text: La nẽrba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ bilam.

ref: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
text: Tɩ a tãpa la daan tõm tɩ ba ka yele Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fʋ sɩrɛ la bẽ'erɩ mɛ.»

ref: When Jesus heard this, he said, “His sickness won't end in death. It will bring glory to God and his Son.”
text: La Azezi n wõm bɛla la, tɩ a yetɩ: «Bã'a-ẽna ba'asgɔ kãn dɛna kũm. La de la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ yẽ zẽkrɛ, la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn zẽkɛ bõn-ẽŋa pʋam.»

ref: But he stayed where he was for two more days.
text: La ẽn wõm tɩ Alazaarɩ bẽ'erɩ mɛ na, a ken weege ẽn daan boe zẽ'e-sɛka la dabsa ayi.

ref: Then he said to his disciples, “Now we will go back to Judea.”
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ a yele a karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Tɩ lebe-ya Zude.»

ref: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama, lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ Zifdõma daan eerɩ fõn tɩ ba lobe la kuga kʋ. Dee tɩ fõn le yetɩ fʋ lebe bilam?»

ref: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Bulika tɩ la ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ, la de la karf-pia la ayi tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa boe. Nẽra sãn sẽnna wʋntɛɛŋa, a ka wẽ'erɩ a nãma, se'ere n soe la, dũnia peelem boe mɛ, tɩ a yẽta.
