ref: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
text: La nẽra sãn sẽnna yʋ'ʋŋɔ, a wẽ'erɩ a nãma mɛ, se'ere n soe la, peelem ka boe tɩ a yẽta.»

ref: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
text: Azezi yele bɛla dee yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ sɩrɛ Alazaarɩ gĩsri mɛ, la mam wʋn sẽŋɛ ka nẽegɛ ẽ.»

ref: They replied, “Lord, if he is asleep, he will get better.”
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, a sãn gĩsra, a wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.»

ref: Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
text: Azezi tɔgɛ ba la Alazaarɩ kũm yelle. La ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ a tɔgrɩ la gẽem yelle.

ref: I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.”
text: La mam n daan ka boe mĩ na, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ yãma ĩyã, tɩ ya wʋn bɔ mam sɩra. Tɩ sẽŋɛ-ya a zẽ'am.»

ref: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
text: Tɩ Atoma yele a karẽnbi-taaba yetɩ: «Tɩ mẽ sẽŋɛ-ya tɩ tɩ ka lagɩm ki.»

ref: When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
text: Azezi n daan paagɛ la, tɩ ban laagɛ Alazaarɩ de la dabsa anaasɩ.

ref: Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem,
text: La Betani daan lɛm Zerizalɛm masɛ wʋ kilomẽtrɩ atã.

ref: and many people had come from the city to comfort Martha and Mary because their brother had died.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma zozo'e wa'am tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsɛ Amartɩ, la Amaari ba tã la kũm.

ref: When Martha heard that Jesus had arrived, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed in the house.
text: La Amartɩ n wõm tɩ Azezi sẽm mɛ na, a daan yese mɛ tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ. Dee tɩ Amaari weege zẽa yire.

ref: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
text: Tɩ Amartɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn daan sãn boe nɩ kalam, mam tã wã kãn ki.

ref: Jesus told her, “Your brother will live again!”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ tã la wʋn vo'oge basɛ kũm.»

ref: Martha answered, “I know he will be raised to life on the last day, when all the dead are raised.”
text: Tɩ Amartɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam mi tɩ a wʋn vo'oge dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ, tɩ kũm wuu wʋn vo'oge la.»

ref: Jesus then said, “I am the one who raises the dead to life! Everyone who has faith in me will live, even if they die.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n de kũm vo'ore dãana, la vɔm dãana. Sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra, baa a sãn ki, a wʋn vo'oge.

ref: “Yes, Lord!” she replied. “I believe you are the Christ, the Son of God. You are the one we hoped would come into the world.”
text: Tɩ Amartɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽe, Zuudãana, mam bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn de la Krisi, Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la n sẽm dũnia zuo la.»

ref: After Martha said this, she went and privately said to her sister Mary, “The Teacher is here, and he wants to see you.”
text: La ẽn yele bɛla la, a fõrgɛ mɛ, sẽŋɛ ka wi a yɩbga Amaari, sʋgɛ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Karẽnsãama wa'am mɛ, dee wi'ira fõn.»

ref: As soon as Mary heard this, she got up and went out to Jesus.
text: La Amaari n wõm bɛla la, a isge mɛ tɔtɔ wɛ'ɛsa a zẽ'am.

ref: He was still outside the village where Martha had gone to meet him.
text: La Azezi daan nãn ka paagɛ tẽŋa la pʋam, a ken bɔna la Amartɩ n daan tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ zẽ'e-sɛka la.

ref: Many people had come to comfort Mary, and when they saw her quickly leave the house, they thought she was going out to the tomb to cry. So they followed her.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n wa'am wa pʋ'ʋsɛ kũm na, dee bɔna la Amaari lɛ yire la pʋam na, yẽ tɩ a isge tɔtɔ yɛsra, tɩ ba tẽ'esra tɩ a we'esɩ la yɔɔm tɩ a ka kaasɛ, tɩ ba dɔla ẽ.

ref: Mary went to where Jesus was. Then as soon as she saw him, she knelt at his feet and said, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
text: La Amaari n paagɛ Azezi n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la, yẽ ẽ na, a yigle mɛ a nẽŋam, yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, fõn daan sãn boe nɩ kalam, mam tã wã kãn ki.»

ref: When Jesus saw that Mary and the people with her were crying, he was terribly upset
text: La Azezi n daan yẽ tɩ a kellɩ mɛ, la Zifdõma la n dolɩ ẽ na mẽ kellɩ mɛ na, tɩ a sũure sã'am halɩ, tɩ la dãam ẽ zozo'e,

ref: and asked, “Where have you put his body?” They replied, “Lord, come and you will see.”
text: tɩ a soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma laagɛ ẽ na bɛ?» Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, wa'am wa bɩsɛ.»

ref: Jesus started crying,
text: Tɩ Azezi kɛlla.

ref: and the people said, “See how much he loved Lazarus.”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma la n yẽ bɛla la, yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya ẽn nõŋɛ ẽ se'em.»

ref: Some of them said, “He gives sight to the blind. Why couldn't he have kept Lazarus from dying?”
text: Tɩ basɛba yele yetɩ: «Dagɩ bʋra-ẽna n daan botɩ fʋa la nini yẽta la, a ka tã wʋn basɛ nɩ tɩ ẽna mẽ da ki?»

ref: Jesus was still terribly upset. So he went to the tomb, which was a cave with a stone rolled against the entrance.
text: Tɩ Azezi sũure le sã'am halɩ, tɩ a wɛ'ɛsa yɔɔm na. Yɔɔgɔ la daan de la tãmpĩa yoko, tɩ kugre lige a nõorɛ.

ref: Then he told the people to roll the stone away. But Martha said, “Lord, you know that Lazarus has been dead four days, and there will be a bad smell.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Lakɛ-ya kugre la.» Tɩ sɛka n ki la tã Amartɩ yele Azezi yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, a põn põ'ogɛ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, la de la dabsa anaasɩ n wãna tɩ a boe yɔɔm.»

ref: Jesus replied, “Didn't I tell you that if you had faith, you would see the glory of God?”
text: Tɩ Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Mam ka yele fʋ yetɩ, fʋ sãn bɔ mam sɩra, fʋ wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa?»

ref: After the stone had been rolled aside, Jesus looked up toward heaven and prayed, “Father, I thank you for answering my prayer.
text: Tɩ ba lakɛ kugre la. Tɩ Azezi gorge saazuum yetɩ: «M Sɔ, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ, fõn selse mam pʋ'ʋsgɔ la.

ref: I know that you always answer my prayers. But I said this, so the people here would believe you sent me.”
text: Mam mi tɩ daarɛ woo fʋ selsrɩ mam pʋ'ʋsgɔ mɛ. La mam yele bɛla la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ wã ĩyã, tɩ ba wʋn bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn tõm mam.»

ref: When Jesus had finished praying, he shouted, “Lazarus, come out!”
text: A yele bɛla dee tɔgɛ la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Alazaarɩ, yese!»

ref: The man who had been dead came out. His hands and feet were wrapped with strips of burial cloth, and a cloth covered his face. Jesus then told the people, “Untie him and let him go.” The Plot To Kill Jesus (Matthew
text: Tɩ sɛka n daan ki la isge yese. Tɩ tãna vile a nãma la a nu'usi, la tãnnɛ ayẽma n vile a nẽŋa. Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Lorge-ya tãna la, dee basɛ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ.» Zifdõma nẽŋadõma eerɩ sore tɩ ba kʋ Azezi (Amatie

ref: Many of the people who had come to visit Mary saw the things Jesus did, and they put their faith in him.
text: La Zifdõma sɛba n daan wa'am Amaari zẽ'am wa yẽ Azezi n ẽŋɛ se'em na, ba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ.

ref: Others went to the Pharisees and told what Jesus had done.
text: Tɩ basɛba sẽŋɛ Farisi dõma zẽ'am ka yele ba Azezi n ẽŋɛ se'em na.

ref: Then the chief priests and the Pharisees called the council together and said, “What should we do? This man is working a lot of miracles.
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma lagsɛ ba sarɩyaditba kẽma yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Bʋra-ẽna wã tõnnɩ la tõon-yãlma zozo'e.

ref: If we don't stop him now, everyone will put their faith in him. Then the Romans will come and destroy our temple and our nation.”
text: Tɩ sãn basɛ ẽ tɩ a ɩta bɛla, nẽrba wuu wʋn bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ, la Rom dõma wʋn wa'am wa sã'am tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ wã, la tɩ so'olʋm wã wuu mɛ.»

ref: One of the council members was Caiaphas, who was also high priest that year. He spoke up and said, “You people don't have any sense at all!
text: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Akayifu n daan de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yʋʋn-ẽŋa, yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma ka mi sɛla sɛla.

ref: Don't you know it is better for one person to die for the people than for the whole nation to be destroyed?”
text: Ya ka mi tɩ bʋraa ayɩla sãn ki nẽrba la ĩyã, n sõɩ tɩ buuri la wuu n wʋn sã'am?»

ref: Caiaphas did not say this on his own. As high priest that year, he was prophesying that Jesus would die for the nation.
text: La, la dagɩ a mẽŋa yɛm tɩ a yele bɛla, la de la ẽn daan de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yʋʋn-ẽŋa la tɩ a to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ Azezi wa ki bɔ bãma buuri la.

ref: Yet Jesus would not die just for the Jewish nation. He would die to bring together all of God's scattered people.
text: La, la dagɩ bãma buuri la mã'a ĩyã tɩ la masɛ tɩ a ki, la de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma n wɩrgɛ zẽ'esɩ wuu la ĩyã, tɩ a yetɩ a lagsɛ ba tɩ ba lebge buuri ayɩla.

ref: From that day on, the council started making plans to put Jesus to death.
text: La sẽŋɛ la daar-ẽŋa, tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

ref: Because of this plot against him, Jesus stopped going around in public. He went to the town of Ephraim, which was near the desert, and he stayed there with his disciples.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Azezi ka le ɛɛra Zifdõma tẽŋasʋka tɩ ba yẽta ẽ. A fõrgɛ bilam sẽŋɛ la tẽnsɩ n lɛm mõom, ka kẽ'era tẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Efrayim la a karẽnbiisi la.

ref: It was almost time for Passover. Many of the Jewish people who lived out in the country had come to Jerusalem to get themselves ready for the festival.
text: La Zifdõma Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa n daan lɛm, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e ze'ele tẽn-pɩgsɩ pʋam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm lan dẽŋɛ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la, tɩ ba ka malgɛ ba mẽŋa wʋ ba sisgo n yetɩ ba ɩta se'em na.

ref: They looked around for Jesus. Then when they were in the temple, they asked each other, “You don't think he will come here for Passover, do you?”
text: Tɩ ba ɛɛra Azezi, sẽŋɛ ka zɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Yãma tẽ'esɛ la dẽna? A sɩrɩ kãn wa'am kibsa wã?»

ref: The chief priests and the Pharisees told the people to let them know if any of them saw Jesus. This is how they hoped to arrest him.
text: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Farisi dõma la daan bɔ la nõorɛ tɩ nẽra sãn mina ẽn boe zẽ'a, a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.

ref: If you serve me, you must go with me. My servants will be with me wherever I am. If you serve me, my Father will honor you.
text: La nẽra sãn bɔɔra tɩ a tõm bɔ mam, dẽnɩ tɩ a dɔla mam. La mam n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la, mam tõntõnna mẽ wʋn bɔna la bilam. La nẽra sãn tõnna bɔ'ɔra mam, mam Sɔ wʋn pẽgɛ ẽ.

ref: Judas did not really care about the poor. He asked this because he carried the moneybag and sometimes would steal from it.
text: Ẽn yele bɛla la, dagɩ tɩ a fablɩ la nasdõma yelle, la de la ẽn de nayiga, la ẽŋa n tarɩ ba ligri tãmpɔkɔ, zũura sɛla tɩ ba sũm a pʋam na.

ref: they took palm branches and went out to greet him. They shouted, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord! God bless the King of Israel!”
text: Tɩ ba kɔ karkɔnsɩ võorɔ tʋ'ʋsra ẽ, dee tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ozaana! Pẽgɛ-ya nẽr-sɛka n sẽm na Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ la, ẽŋa n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma naba la.»

ref: Jesus found a donkey and rode on it, just as the Scriptures say,
text: Tɩ Azezi yẽ bõn-biire bã ẽ wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ:

ref: Mary took a very expensive bottle of perfume and poured it on Jesus' feet. She wiped them with her hair, and the sweet smell of the perfume filled the house.
text: Tɩ Amaari dɩkɛ tɩdarɛ ka-sõma ligri n zo'oge, tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ «naarɩ» n paagɛ lɩtrɩ pʋsʋka wa'am wa ka'agɛ ẽŋɛ Azezi nãma, dɩkɛ a zomto duuse ba. Tɩ yire la wuu lebge tɩdarɛ kaam na yũuŋo.

ref: A lot of people came when they heard that Jesus was there. They also wanted to see Lazarus, because Jesus had raised him from death.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma zozo'e wõm tɩ Azezi boe la Betani. Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ mĩ, la dagɩ Azezi mã'a ĩyã tɩ ba sẽŋɛ, ba sẽŋɛ tɩ ba mẽ ka yẽ la Alazaarɩ tɩ Azezi daan vo'oge la.

ref: A meal had been prepared for Jesus. Martha was doing the serving, and Lazarus himself was there.
text: Tɩ ba daan ẽŋɛ dia bilam bɔ ẽ, tɩ Amartɩ daan pʋta dia la. La Alazaarɩ daan lagɩm na nẽrba la zẽa la Azezi dita.

ref: A disciple named Judas Iscariot was there. He was the one who was going to betray Jesus, and he asked,
text: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbia ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azudaasɩ Iskariyo, sɛka n wʋn wa pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na, yetɩ:

ref: Jesus replied, “Leave her alone! She has kept this perfume for the day of my burial.
text: Tɩ Azezi yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya pɔka la bãalam, tɩ a dẽŋɛ ẽŋɛ la ba wʋn ẽŋɛ mam se'em mam laarɛ daarɛ.

ref: You will always have the poor with you, but you won't always have me.” A Plot To Kill Lazarus
text: Nasdõma boe la ya lɛ mɛ daarɛ woo, la mam kãn bɔna la ya daarɛ woo.»

ref: So the chief priests made plans to kill Lazarus.
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la bãm yetɩ, bãma mẽ wʋn kʋ Alazaarɩ mɛ,

ref: Philip from Bethsaida in Galilee was there too. So they went to him and said, “Sir, we would like to meet Jesus.”
text: Bãma n sẽŋɛ Afilipi n ze'ele Betisayida n boe Galile so'olʋm pʋam na zẽ'am, ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Kɛ'ɛma, tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ yẽ la Azezi.»

ref: But the Pharisees said to each other, “There is nothing we can do! Everyone in the world is following Jesus.” Some Greeks Want To Meet Jesus
text: Bɛla tɩ Farisi dõma yɛta taaba yetɩ: «Ya yẽ ya, ya ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla. Bɩsɛ-ya, nẽrba wuu dolɩ ẽ mɛ.» Grɛkɩdõma eerɩ tɩ ba yẽ Azezi, tɩ Azezi tɔgra a kũm yelle

ref: So Father, bring glory to yourself. A voice from heaven then said, “I have already brought glory to myself, and I will do it again!”
text: A yele bɛla dee yetɩ: «Mam Sɔ, nã'asɛ fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ.» Tɩ kõa ze'ele saazuum yetɩ: «Mam nã'asɛ m yʋ'ʋrɛ mɛ, la mam wʋn le nã'asɛ ẽ.»

ref: “The Lord has blinded the eyes of the people, and he has made the people stubborn. He did this so that they could not see or understand, and so that they would not turn to the Lord and be healed.”
text: «Nawẽnnɛ pĩ ba nini mɛ, tɩ ba da yẽta la ba nini na, dee lige ba pʋtẽ'era, tɩ ba da wõm a yetɔga la võorɛ. Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ba wʋn teege nɩ yɛm tɩ a bɔ ba laafɛ.»

ref: The next day a large crowd was in Jerusalem for Passover. When they heard that Jesus was coming for the festival,
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n wa'am kibsa la wõm tɩ Azezi sẽm Zerizalɛm.

ref: “People of Jerusalem, don't be afraid! Your King is now coming, and he is riding on a donkey.”
text: «Siyõ nẽrba, da zɔta-ya dabeem. Bɩsɛ-ya, ya naba n bã bõn-biire sẽna la.»

ref: At first, Jesus' disciples did not understand. But after he had been given his glory, they remembered all this. Everything had happened exactly as the Scriptures said it would.
text: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la daan nãn ka bãŋɛ yel-ẽŋa võorɛ. La Azezi n yãŋa wa yẽ na'am pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, tɩ ba tẽegɛ tɩ bõn-ẽŋa n gʋlsɛ a yelle, la ba daan ẽŋɛ la bɛla bɔ ẽ.

ref: A crowd had come to meet Jesus because they had seen him call Lazarus out of the tomb. They kept talking about him and this miracle.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ, ban wõm tɩ a tõm na tõon-yãlm-ẽŋa la ĩyã.

ref: Some Greeks had gone to Jerusalem to worship during Passover.
text: La Grɛkɩdõma n daan boe nẽr-sɛba n sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm kibsa tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ na pʋam.

ref: Philip told Andrew. Then the two of them went to Jesus and told him. The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up
text: Tɩ Afilipi sẽŋɛ ka yele A'ãndre, tɩ A'ãndre la Afilipi doose taaba sẽŋɛ ka yele Azezi.

ref: Jesus said: The time has come for the Son of Man to be given his glory.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Wakatɛ la paagɛ mɛ tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la wʋn yẽ a na'am pãŋa.

ref: I tell you for certain that a grain of wheat that falls on the ground will never be more than one grain unless it dies. But if it dies, it will produce lots of wheat.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ba sãn ka bʋrɛ ke-biire tɩ a yɛm bule yese dee tɩ a mẽŋa põ'ogɛ, ke-bi-ẽŋa ayɩla mã'a n wʋn bɔna, la ba sãn bʋrɛ ẽ tɩ a yɛm na bule yese dee tɩ a mẽŋa ki, a wʋn wɔm biɛ zozo'e.

ref: If you love your life, you will lose it. If you give it up in this world, you will be given eternal life.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ a vɔm, a wʋn koŋe vɔm, la nẽr-sɛka n ka nõŋɛ a vɔm dũnia wã zuo, a wʋn tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Now I am deeply troubled, and I don't know what to say. But I must not ask my Father to keep me from this time of suffering. In fact, I came into the world to suffer.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã mam sũure sã'am mɛ, la m wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Mam wʋn yetɩ: M Sɔ, sõŋɛ ma tɩ m põsɛ nãmsgɔ ẽna wã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la de la yel-ẽna ĩyã tɩ mam wa'am tɩ m nãmsɛ.»

ref: When the crowd heard the voice, some of them thought it was thunder. Others thought an angel had spoken to Jesus.
text: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ze bilam na wõm, yetɩ: «Saaga n dãmɛ.» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Malɛka n tɔgɛ la ẽ.»

ref: Then Jesus told the crowd, “That voice spoke to help you, not me.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele yetɩ: «La dagɩ mam ĩyã tɩ kõ-ẽŋa tɔgɛ, la de la yãma ĩyã.

ref: This world's people are now being judged, and the ruler of this world is already being thrown out!
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã de la dũnia wã sarɩyadia wakatɛ. Lɛɛlɛ wã ba wʋn dige dũnia wã naba basɛ yẽŋa.

ref: If I am lifted up above the earth, I will make everyone want to come to me.”
text: La ba sãn wa zẽkɛ mam saazuum, mam wʋn tarɩ nẽrba wuu wa'am mam zẽ'am.»

ref: Jesus was talking about the way he would be put to death.
text: Azezi yele bɛla pa'alɛ la ẽŋa wʋn ki se'em.

ref: The crowd said to Jesus, “The Scriptures teach that the Messiah will live forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ pʋam pa'alɛ tõma yetɩ, Krisi wʋn bɔna la wakatɛ wuu, la bẽm tɩ fõn yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ ba zẽkɛ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la saazuum? Ãne n de Nẽrsaala Dayɔ-ẽŋa?»

ref: Jesus answered, “The light will be with you for only a little longer. Walk in the light while you can. Then you won't be caught walking blindly in the dark.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Peelem ken bɔna la ya lɛ mɛ wakatɛ fẽe. Peelem wã n ken bɔna la ya wakat-sɛka la, sẽnna-ya tɩ lika da wa liŋe ya. Sɛka n sẽnnɩ lika pʋam ka mi ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'a.

ref: Have faith in the light while it is with you, and you will be children of the light.” The People Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus After Jesus had said these things, he left and went into hiding.
text: Wakat-sɛka tɩ peelem ken bɔna la yãma la, bɔ-ya sɛka n de peelem na sɩra, tɩ ya lebge peelem na kɔma.» Azezi yele la bɛla dee fõrgɛ ba zẽ'am, sẽŋɛ ka sʋgɛ.

ref: He had worked a lot of miracles among the people, but they were still not willing to have faith in him.
text: Baa la Azezi n ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma zozo'e nẽrba la nẽŋam na, ba ka bɔ ẽ sɩra.

ref: This happened so that what the prophet Isaiah had said would come true, “Lord, who has believed our message? And who has seen your mighty strength?”
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ. A yʋʋn yetɩ: «Zuudãana, ãne n bɔ tõma kõa la sɩra? La Zuudãana la pãŋa puke pa'alɛ la ãne?»

ref: The people could not have faith in Jesus, because Isaiah had also said,
text: Ba ka tã wʋn bɔ sɩra, se'ere n soe la, A'ezayi yʋʋn le yetɩ:

ref: Isaiah said this, because he saw the glory of Jesus and spoke about him.
text: A'ezayi yʋʋn yele la bɛla, se'ere n soe la, a yʋʋn yẽ la Azezi na'am pãŋa, tɩ a yʋʋn tɔgɛ a yelle.

ref: Even then, many of the leaders put their faith in Jesus, but they did not tell anyone about it. The Pharisees had already given orders for the people not to have anything to do with anyone who had faith in Jesus.
text: Baa la bɛla wuu, Zifdõma nẽŋadõma la pʋam nẽrba zozo'e bɔ ẽ sɩra mɛ, la Farisi dõma la ĩyã ba ka sakɛ yele, ba zotɩ mɛ tɩ ba wʋn dige ba ba wẽndeego la pʋam.

ref: And besides, the leaders liked praise from others more than they liked praise from God. Jesus Came To Save the World
text: Ba daan nõŋɛ la nẽrsaalba pẽka gãnna Nawẽnnɛ pẽka. Azezi yetɔga n diti nẽrba sarɩya

ref: In a loud voice Jesus said: Everyone who has faith in me also has faith in the one who sent me.
text: Azezi tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra, la dagɩ mam mã'a tɩ a bɔ'ɔrɩ sɩra, a bɔ'ɔrɩ la sɛka n tõm mam na sɩra.

ref: And everyone who has seen me has seen the one who sent me.
text: La sɛka n yẽ mam, yẽ la sɛka n tõm mam na.

ref: I am the light that has come into the world. No one who has faith in me will stay in the dark.
text: Mam de la peelem n wa'am dũnia zuo, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra kãn bɔna lika pʋam.

ref: I am not the one who will judge those who refuse to obey my teachings. I came to save the people of this world, not to be their judge.
text: La nẽra sãn wõm mam yetɔga, dee ka sakɛ yetɔga la, la dagɩ mam n wʋn di a sarɩya, se'ere n soe la, mam ka wa'am tɩ m di dũnia dõma sarɩya, mam wa'am tɩ m fãagɛ ba mɛ.

ref: But everyone who rejects me and my teachings will be judged on the last day by what I have said.
text: Sɛka n zagsɛ mam, ka sakɛ mam yetɔga la, a tarɩ sɛka n wʋn di a sarɩya. La de la yetɔga la tɩ mam tɔgɛ la n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ a ka tarɩ bʋʋrɔ dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ.

ref: I don't speak on my own. I say only what the Father who sent me has told me to say.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam ka tɔgrɩ la m mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ. La de la mam Sɔ n tõm mam na mẽŋa n bɔ mam nõorɛ mam wʋn yele se'em, la mam wʋn tɔgɛ sɛla yelle.

ref: I know that his commands will bring eternal life. This is why I tell you exactly what the Father has told me.
text: La mam mi tɩ sɛka n sakɛ a nõorɛ la tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. La bõn-sɛba tɩ mam yetɩ la, la de la mam Sɔ n yele mam se'em na n bɛla tɩ mam yele.»

ref: It was before Passover, and Jesus knew that the time had come for him to leave this world and to return to the Father. He had always loved his followers in this world, and he loved them to the very end.
text: Lan dẽŋɛ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la, Azezi mi mɛ tɩ ẽŋa wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ tɩ ẽŋa wʋn fõrgɛ dũnia sẽŋɛ a Sɔ zẽ'am. La a daan nõŋɛ a nẽrba la n boe dũnia mɛ, a nõŋɛ ba la halɩ nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ.

ref: Even before the evening meal started, the devil had made Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, decide to betray Jesus.
text: Azezi la a karẽnbiisi la daan boe mĩ dita la zaanõorɛ dia. La Asũtãana daan põn botɩ Asimõ dayʋa Azudaasɩ Iskariyo tẽ'esɛ a sũurum tɩ ẽŋa wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ Azezi.

ref: Jesus knew he had come from God and would go back to God. He also knew that the Father had given him complete power.
text: La Azezi daan mi tɩ ẽŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am, la ẽŋa lebrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la a mi tɩ ẽŋa Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ la ẽŋa nu'usum.

ref: So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
text: Tɩ a isge dia la zẽ'am yɛɛgɛ a fu-sɛka n boe zuum na, dee dɩkɛ tãnnɛ lʋ a sɩa.

ref: He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
text: Yãŋa pʋrgɛ ko'om ẽŋɛ laaga pʋam, pɛɛra a karẽnbiisi la nãma, dɩkɛ tãnnɛ la n lʋ a sɩa la duusra ba.

ref: But when he came to Simon Peter, this disciple asked, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
text: Tɩ a daan paagɛ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ zẽ'am, tɩ a soke ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, fõn yetɩ fʋ peege la mam nãma?»

ref: Jesus answered, “You don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn ka mi mam ɩtɩ se'em wã võorɛ lɛɛlɛ, la fʋ wʋn wa bãŋɛ.»

ref: “You will never wash my feet!” Peter replied. “If I don't wash you,” Jesus told him, “you don't really belong to me.”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ! Fõn kãn peege mam nãma.» Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam sãn ka peege fo, fʋ ka le tara pʋrɛ la mam.»

ref: Peter said, “Lord, don't wash just my feet. Wash my hands and my head.”
text: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «M Zuudãana, da peege m nãma la mã'a, po peege m nu'usi la m zuugo.»

ref: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛka n sɔ ĩyã ka le bɔɔra tɩ a sɔ, sãn dagna a nãma mã'a tɩ a wʋn peege, se'ere n soe la, a ĩyã wuu dẽkɛ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa. Ya dẽkɛ mɛ ãna sõŋa sõŋa, la, la dagɩ ya wuu.»

ref: Jesus knew who would betray him. That is why he said, “except for one of you.”
text: Azezi daan mi sɛka n yetɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ na mɛ, bɛla ĩyã tɩ a yetɩ, dagɩ ya wuu n ãn sõŋa.

ref: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
text: La Azezi n peege ba nãma kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a le yɛ la a fuugo la, lebe ka zĩ'ire dia la zẽ'am, yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma bãŋɛ mam n ẽŋɛ se'em na võorɛ?

ref: You call me your teacher and Lord, and you should, because that is who I am.
text: Yãma wi'iri mam tɩ Karẽnsãama, la Zuudãana, ya yele sõŋa. Mam de la bɛla.

ref: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
text: Se'ere n soe la, la de la mãmsgɔ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ bɔ ya, tɩ ya mẽ ɩta wʋ mam n ẽŋɛ ya se'em na.

ref: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ tõntõnna ka gãnnɩ a zuudãana, la sɛka tɩ ba tõm na ka gãnnɩ sɛka n tõm ẽ na.

ref: I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, “The man who ate with me has turned against me!”
text: La dagɩ ya wuu yelle tɩ mam tɔgrɩ, mam mi sɛba tɩ mam looge la mɛ, la dẽnɩ tɩ bõn-sɛba n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na ẽŋɛ. La yetɩ: ‹Nẽr-sɛka n diti la mam na, wẽrgɛ mɛ zɛbra la mam.›

ref: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
text: Mam dẽŋɛ yɛta ya mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, tɩ la nãn ka ẽŋɛ. Tɩ la sãn wa ẽŋɛ, ya wʋn bɔ sɩra tɩ mam de la sɛka tɩ mam de la.

ref: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
text: Tɩ karẽnbi-ẽŋa dente Azezi, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, ãn de ẽ?»

ref: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
text: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla la, sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ ẽ zozo'e, tɩ a yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya nẽra ayɩla n wʋn wa pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.»

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
text: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la ayɩla, sɛka tɩ a nõŋɛ la daan zĩ la Azezi sakʋkɔ.

ref: and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant.
text: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ kãbsɛ ẽ tɩ a soke Azezi bãŋɛ la de la ãn yelle tɩ a tɔgrɩ la.

ref: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
text: La Azudaasɩ n daan zɛɛrɩ ligri tãmpɔkɔ la, nẽrba basɛba tẽ'esɛ tɩ Azezi yele ẽ tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka da la bõn-sɛba tɩ ba boorɩ tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ kibsa la, bɩɩ a bɔ nasdõma sɛla.

ref: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
text: Azudaasɩ n daan to'oge borborɩ la, dee kɔ'ɔn yese. La daan de la yʋ'ʋŋɔ. Azezi bɔ a karẽnbiisi nõor-paalga

ref: Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
text: Nawẽnnɛ sãn yẽ nã'asgɔ a ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa wʋn nã'asɛ ẽ. Lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ a wʋn nã'asɛ ẽ.

ref: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
text: M kɔma, mam ken bɔna la ya la wakatɛ fẽe mã'a. La ya wʋn ɛ mam, la mam yetɩ ya lɛɛlɛ wã wʋ mam n daan yele Zifdõma se'em na: Ya ka tã wʋn wa'am zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam we'esɩ la.

ref: Simon Peter asked, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “You can't go with me now, but later on you will.”
text: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ soke Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn we'esɩ la bɛ?» Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n we'esɩ zẽ'a na, fʋ ka tã wʋn doose mam sẽŋɛ lɛɛlɛ wã. La fʋ wʋn wa doose mam poore wa'am.»

ref: Peter asked, “Lord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, bẽm ẽŋɛ tɩ mam kãn tã'agɛ doose fõn sẽŋɛ lɛɛlɛ wã? Mam sakɛ tɩ m ki mɛ fõn ĩyã.»

ref: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn wʋn sakɛ tɩ fʋ ki mam ĩyã? Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ lan dẽŋɛ nõ-rɔɔgɔ n wʋn kaasɛ, fʋ wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ nõorɛ atã tɩ fõn ka mi mam.»

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
text: Azezi le yele ba yetɩ: «Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya sũure sã'am. Bɔ-ya Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, la ya bɔ mam mẽ sɩra.

ref: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
text: Kẽ'a zẽ'esɩ boe mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yire zo'oge mɛ. Mam we'esɩ tɩ m ka malgɛ la zẽ'a ya ĩyã. La sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, mam kãn yele nɩ ya.

ref: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
text: La mam sãn sẽŋɛ ka malgɛ zẽ'a la ya ĩyã, mam wʋn leme wa'am wa tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ mam zẽ'am, tɩ mam n boe zẽ'a na, yãma mẽ bɔna bilam.

ref: You know the way to where I am going.”
text: La mam n we'esɩ zẽ'e-sɛka la, ya mi a sore la.»

ref: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
text: Tɩ Atoma yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, tõma ka mi fõn we'esɩ zẽ'a na, tõma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne tã'agɛ bãŋɛ a sore?»

ref: “I am the way, the truth, and the life!” Jesus answered. “Without me, no one can go to the Father.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam n de sore, la sɩra, la vɔm dãana. Nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn wa'am mam Sɔ zẽ'am, tɩ la sãn ka doose la mam.

ref: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
text: Ya sãn mi ni mam, ya wʋn mi ni mam Sɔ la mẽ. La lɛɛlɛ wã, ya mi ẽ, la ya yẽ ẽ.»

ref: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.”
text: Tɩ Afilipi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, pa'alɛ tɔ fʋ Sɔ la, tɩ bɛla seke tõma mɛ.»

ref: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Afilipi, mam boe la ya yuuge mɛ, tɩ fõn nãn ken ka mina mam? Sɛka n yẽ mam, yẽ mam Sɔ mɛ. La ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn yetɩ, m pa'alɛ ya m Sɔ la?

ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
text: Fõn ka bɔ sɩra tɩ mam boe la mam Sɔ la lɛ, tɩ mam Sɔ la bɔna la mam lɛ? Yetɔg-sɛba tɩ mam tɔgrɩ la yãma la, ka ze'etɩ mam mẽŋa zẽ'am, la de la mam Sɔ la n boe la mam lɛ la n tõnnɩ a tõoma.

ref: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
text: Bɔ-ya mam sɩra, mam n yetɩ mam boe la m Sɔ la lɛ, tɩ mam Sɔ la bɔna la mam lɛ la. La ya sãn ka sakɛ bɛla, ya bɔ mam sɩra m tõoma la ĩyã.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sɩra la, a mẽ wʋn tõm tõon-sɛba tɩ mam tõnnɩ na, la a wʋn tõm tõon-kãra n gãnnɩ bɛla, se'ere n soe la, mam we'esɩ la m Sɔ zẽ'am.

ref: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
text: La bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ ya wʋn sose la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ bɔ ya tɩ m Sɔ la yẽ nã'asgɔ a Dayʋa la ĩyã.

ref: I will do whatever you ask me to do. The Holy Spirit Is Promised
text: Ya sãn sose sɛla la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ.» Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wa'aŋɔ

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
text: «Ya sãn nõŋɛ mam, ya wʋn sakɛ mam nõorɛ la mɛ.

ref: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
text: La mam wʋn sose m Sɔ la, tɩ a bɔ ya Sõŋra ayẽma tɩ a bɔna la ya lɛ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
text: A de la sɩra Sɩa tɩ dũnia dõma ka tã wʋn to'oge ẽ, se'ere n soe la, ba ka tã wʋn yẽ ẽ, la ba ka mi ẽ. La yãma mi ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a boe la ya zẽ'am, la a wʋn bɔna la ya lɛ.

ref: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
text: Mam kãn basɛ ya kɩɩbsɩ, mam wʋn leme wa'am ya zẽ'am.

ref: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
text: La sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, dũnia nẽrba kãn le yẽ mam. La yãma nõo wʋn yẽ mam, se'ere n soe la, mam n vɔa la, yãma mẽ wʋn vɔa mɛ.

ref: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: La daar-ẽŋa daarɛ, ya wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam boe la m Sɔ la lɛ, tɩ yãma bɔna la mam, tɩ mam bɔna la yãma.

ref: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
text: Sɛka n bãŋɛ mam nõorɛ la tɩ mam bɔ la, dee sakra ɩta bɛla, la de la ẽŋa n nõŋɛ mam. Mam Sɔ la wʋn nõŋɛ sɛka n nõŋɛ mam na mɛ. La mam wʋn nõŋɛ ẽŋa dãana, dee dɩkɛ m mẽŋa pa'alɛ ẽ.»

ref: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
text: Tɩ Azudaasɩ ayẽma n dagɩ Azudaasɩ Iskariyo la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ fõn wʋn dɩkɛ fʋ mẽŋa pa'alɛ tõma, dee kãn pa'alɛ dũnia nẽrba?»

ref: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn nõŋɛ mam, a wʋn sakɛ ɩta mam n yele se'em na, la mam Sɔ wʋn nõŋɛ a dãana. La tõma wʋn wa'am a zẽ'am wa bɔna la ẽ.

ref: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
text: Sɛka n ka nõŋɛ mam, a ka sakɛ ɩta mam yetɔga n yele se'em na. La yetɔga la tɩ ya wõnnɩ wã dagɩ mam mẽŋa yetɔga, la de la mam Sɔ la n tõm mam na yetɔga.

ref: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
text: Mam ken bɔna la ya zẽ'am dee tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna bɔ ya.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
text: La Sõŋra la, mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a wʋn tõm na mam yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ a wa'am ya zẽ'am na, ẽŋa n wʋn zãsʋm ya sɛla woo, dee tẽegɛ ya bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ mam yele ya la.

ref: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
text: Mam basrɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔ ya, mam mẽŋa sũ-mã'asʋm tɩ mam bɔ'ɔrɩ ya. Mam ka bɔ'ɔrɩ ya wʋ dũnia n bɔ'ɔrɩ se'em na. Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya sũure sã'am, da zɔta-ya dabeem.

ref: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
text: Ya wõm mam n yele ya tɩ mam we'esɩ mɛ, la mam wʋn leme wa'am ya zẽ'am. Ya sãn nõŋɛ nɩ mam, ya wʋn ẽŋɛ nɩ sũ-yẽlʋm mam n we'esɩ m Sɔ zẽ'am na ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, mam Sɔ la n gãnnɩ mam.

ref: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã, mam tɔgɛ ya mɛ tɩ la nãn ka paagɛ, tɩ la sãn wa paagɛ, ya wʋn bɔ mam sɩra.

ref: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
text: Mam kãn le tɔgɛ la ya lɛ zo'oge, se'ere n soe la, dũnia naba sẽm mɛ. La a ka tarɩ pãŋa la mam.

ref: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
text: La dẽnɩ tɩ dũnia dõma bãŋɛ tɩ mam nõŋɛ m Sɔ la mɛ, la mam ɩtɩ wʋ mam Sɔ la n bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na. Isge-ya, tɩ fõrgɛ kalam.»

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
text: Azezi zãsʋm ba yetɩ: «Mam de la viinye tɩa mẽŋa mẽŋa. La mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n de viinye tɩa la dãana.

ref: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
text: Wil-sɛka n puse mam zuo dee ka wɔnna biɛ, a wãarɩ ẽ mɛ basra. La wil-sɛka woo n wɔnnɩ biɛ, a yorsrɩ ẽ mɛ tɩ a ãna sõŋa malɩn wɔnna biɛ zɔ'ɔra.

ref: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
text: Yãma põn ãna sõŋa mɛ, yetɔg-sɛba tɩ mam tɔgɛ bɔ ya la ĩyã.

ref: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
text: Bɔna-ya la mam lɛ, tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la ya. Wʋ tɩa wille n ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ a to'ore tɩ a sãn ka bɔna la tɩa la, bɛla, ya mẽ sãn ka bɔna la mam, ya ka tã wʋn wɔm biɛ.

ref: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
text: Mam n de tɩa la, tɩ yãma dɛna wila la. Sɛka n boe la mam, tɩ mam bɔna la ẽ, ẽŋa dãana tõnnɩ tõon-sõma zɔ'ɔra mɛ, se'ere n soe la, ya ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla, tɩ ya sãn ka bɔna la mam.

ref: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
text: Nẽra sãn ka bɔna la mam lɛ, ba wʋn lobe ẽ basɛ wʋ tɩa wille la, tɩ a kʋɩ. La ba vaarɩ wil-sɛba n kʋɩ la ɩta la bugum pʋam, tɩ bugum dita ba.

ref: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
text: Ya sãn bɔna la mam, tɩ mam yetɔga la bɔna ya pʋam, ya sose yan boorɩ sɛla wuu, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ.

ref: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
text: Ya sãn tõm tõon-sõma zo'oge pa'alɛ tɩ yãma de la mam karẽnbiisi, bɛla ya wʋn nã'asɛ mam Sɔ.

ref: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
text: Mam nõŋɛ ya wʋ mam Sɔ n nõŋɛ mam se'em na. Tara-ya mam nõŋlʋm na ya sũure pʋam.

ref: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
text: Ya sãn sakɛ mam nõorɛ la, ya wʋn tara mam nõŋlʋm na ya sũure pʋam, wʋ mam sakɛ m Sɔ nõorɛ tara a nõŋlʋm se'em na.

ref: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
text: Mam tɔgɛ ya wãna tɩ ya tara la mam sũ-yẽlga, tɩ ya sũ-yẽlga wʋn zo'oge pãɩ.

ref: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
text: Mam nõorɛ n wãna: Nõŋɛ-ya taaba, wʋ mam n nõŋɛ ya se'em na.

ref: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
text: Nẽra ka boe n tarɩ nõŋlʋm gãnna nẽra n sakɛ tɩ a ki a sɩrdõma ĩyã.

ref: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
text: Ya sãn ẽŋɛ mam n yele ya se'em na, ya de la mam sɩrdõma.

ref: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
text: Mam ka le wi'ira ya tɩ m tõntõnɩba, se'ere n soe la, tõntõnna ka mi a zuudãana n ɩtɩ sɛla. Mam wi'iri ya tɩ m sɩrdõma, se'ere n soe la, mam botɩ yãma bãŋɛ bõn-sɛba tɩ mam Sɔ yele mam na wuu mɛ.

ref: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Dagɩ yãma n looge mam, mam n looge ya, dɩkɛ m tõonɛ na ẽŋɛ ya nu'usum tɩ ya sẽŋɛ ka tõm tõon-sõma wʋ tɩa n wɔnnɩ biɛ la, tɩ ya tõon-sõma la wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu. Bɛla, bõn-sɛka woo tɩ ya wʋn sose mam Sɔ la, la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, a wʋn bɔ ya.

ref: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
text: Mam n bɔ ya nõorɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ se'em de la ya nõŋɛ taaba.» Dũnia nẽrba sise Azezi la a karẽnbiisi

ref: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
text: Tɩ Azezi le yele yetɩ: «Dũnia nẽrba sãn sise ya, ya bãŋɛ tɩ ba dẽŋɛ sise la mam.

ref: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
text: Ya sãn de nɩ na dũnia n soe ya, dũnia nẽrba wʋn nõŋɛ nɩ ya, se'ere n soe la, ya de la dũnia bõnɔ. La mam loose ya la dũnia pʋam yese, la dagɩ dũnia n soe ya, bɛla tɩ dũnia nẽrba sisra ya.

ref: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
text: Tẽegɛ-ya yetɔg-sɛka tɩ mam yele ya tɩ: ‹Tõntõnna ka gãnna a zuudãana la.› Ba sãn nãmsɛ mam, ba mẽ wʋn nãmsɛ yãma mɛ. La ba sãn sakɛ mam yetɔga, ba wʋn sakɛ yãma mẽ yetɔga.

ref: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
text: La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ ya bõn-bãma wuu mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, ba ka mi sɛka n tõm mam na.

ref: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
text: Mam sãn ka wa'am nɩ, la mam sãn ka tɔgɛ nɩ la ba lɛ, ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka boe ba zuo. La lɛɛlɛ wã, ba ka tã wʋn yetɩ bãma tarɩ bʋʋrɔ.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
text: Sɛka n sisri mam na, a mẽ sisri mam Sɔ la mɛ.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
text: Mam sãn ka tõm nɩ tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla nãn ka tõm ba tẽŋasʋka, ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ ka boe ba zuo. La lɛɛlɛ wã, ba yẽ mam tõoma mɛ, dee sise mam na m Sɔ la.

ref: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
text: Bɛla pʋam tɩ bõn-sɛka n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na ẽŋɛ. La yetɩ: ‹Ba sise mam na zãŋa.›

ref: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
text: Sõŋra la wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am, a de la sɩra Sɩa tɩ mam wʋn tõm, tɩ a ze'ele mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am ya zẽ'am. Ẽŋa wʋn tɔgɛ mam yelle bɔ ya.

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text: La yãma mẽ wʋn tɔgɛ mam yelle, se'ere n soe la, halɩ pĩ'iluŋo tɩ ya boe la mam lɛ.

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: Mam tɔgɛ bɛla bɔ ya tɩ sɛla da wa botɩ ya basɛ ka le dɔla mam.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
text: Ba wʋn wa dige ya ba wẽndeto pʋam, la wakatɛ sẽna tɩ nẽrba wʋn kʋ ya, dee tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma tõm na Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ.

ref: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
text: Ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la bɛla, se'ere n soe la, ba ka mi mam Sɔ la, la mam mẽŋa.

ref: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
text: La mam yele ya bɛla tɩ wakat-ẽŋa sãn wa paagɛ, ya tẽegɛ tɩ mam põn yele ya.» Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa tõoma «Mam ka yele ya bɛla pɔspɔsɩ, se'ere n soe la, mam daan boe la ya lɛ mɛ.

ref: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
text: La nãnannɛ wã mam we'esɩ la sɛka n tõm mam na zẽ'am, la ya nẽra baa ayɩla ka sokrɩ mam yetɩ, mam we'esɩ la bɛ?

ref: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
text: La mam n yele ya bõn-ẽna wã ĩyã, sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ ya zozo'e.

ref: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
text: La mam yetɩ ya la sɩra, tɩ la ãn sõŋa bɔ ya tɩ mam sẽŋɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam sãn ka sẽŋɛ, Sõŋra la kãn wa'am ya zẽ'am, la mam sãn sẽŋɛ, mam wʋn tõm ẽ bɔ ya.

ref: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
text: La a sãn wa'am, a wʋn botɩ dũnia nẽrba bãŋɛ tɩ ba tuuge mɛ tõon-be'ero yelle, la sɛla n masɛ yelle, la sarɩyadia yelle.

ref: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
text: A wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ ba tõon-be'ero la de la ban ka bɔ mam sɩra la.

ref: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
text: A wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ sɛla n masɛ, se'ere n soe la, mam we'esɩ la m Sɔ zẽ'am, la ya kãn le yẽ mam.

ref: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
text: A wʋn botɩ ba bãŋɛ sarɩyadia yelle, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ di dũnia wã naba sarɩya mɛ, tɩ a koŋe bʋʋrɔ.

ref: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
text: Mam ken tara la yɛla zozo'e tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ ya, la ya kãn tã'agɛ to'oge ba nãnannɛ wã.

ref: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n de sɩra dãana la sãn wa'am, a wʋn pa'alɛ ya sɩra wuu, se'ere n soe la, a yetɔga la ka ze'etɩ a mẽŋa zẽ'am, a wʋn tɔgɛ la sɛla tɩ a wõm na wuu bɔ ya, dee yele ya sɛla n boe nẽŋa sẽna.

ref: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
text: A wʋn nã'asɛ mam, se'ere n soe la, a wʋn dɩkɛ la sɛla n boe mam zẽ'am na tɔgɛ bɔ ya.

ref: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
text: Mam Sɔ la n tarɩ sɛla wuu la de la mam bõnɔ. Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam yetɩ, a wʋn dɩkɛ la sɛla n boe mam zẽ'am na tɔgɛ bɔ ya.» Sũ-sã'aŋɔ wʋn lebge sũ-yẽlga

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
text: «La sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya kãn le yẽ mam, la, la sãn le ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya wʋn le yẽ mam.»

ref: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
text: Tɩ karẽnbiisi basɛba sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Ẽŋa n yetɩ, la sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ kãn le yẽ ẽŋa, la, la sãn le ẽŋɛ fẽe, tɩ wʋn le yẽ ẽŋa, dee le yetɩ, ẽŋa we'esɩ la a Sɔ zẽ'am na, a võorɛ de la bẽm?»

ref: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
text: Bɛla tɩ ba daan yɛta tɩ: «Ẽn yetɩ la sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe la, a võorɛ de la bẽm? Tõma ka mi bõn-sɛka yelle tɩ a tɔgrɩ la.»

ref: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
text: La Azezi n daan mi tɩ ba boorɩ tɩ ba soke ẽ mɛ na, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Mam n yetɩ, la sãn ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya kãn le yẽ mam, la, la sãn le ẽŋɛ fẽe, ya wʋn le yẽ mam na yelle tɩ yãma sɔkra taaba?

ref: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
text: Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ ya wʋn kaasɛ fablɛ, la dũnia dõma sũure wʋn ãna yẽlʋm. Ya sũure wʋn sã'am, la ya sũ-sã'aŋɔ la wʋn wa lebge sũ-yẽlga.

ref: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
text: Pɔka sãn bɔna mĩ virgra, sũ-sã'aŋɔ n tarɩ ẽ, a nãmsgɔ wakatɛ n paagɛ la ĩyã. La a sãn dɔgɛ bia la kɩ'ɩlɩm, a ka le tẽra a nãmsgɔ la yelle, a sũure ãn yẽlʋm mɛ, ẽn dɔgɛ bia la ĩyã.

ref: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
text: Bɛla nãnannɛ wã yãma mẽ tarɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la mam wʋn le wa yẽ ya, la ya sũure wʋn ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm. La nẽra baa ayɩla kãn tã'agɛ sã'am ya sũ-yẽlga la.

ref: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: La daar-ẽŋa daarɛ, ya kãn le soke mam sɛla baa ayɩla yelle. Mam yetɩ ya la sɩra sɩra, tɩ bõn-sɛka tɩ ya wʋn sose mam Sɔ la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, a wʋn bɔ ya.

ref: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
text: Ya nãn ka sose sɛla la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ. Sose-ya tɩ ya wʋn to'oge tɩ ya sũ-yẽlga zo'oge pãɩ.

ref: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
text: Mam makɛ la makrɛ tɔgɛ bɔ ya. La wakatɛ sẽm mɛ tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ m Sɔ la yelle vẽelga vẽelga bɔ ya, kãn le makɛ makrɛ tɔgɛ.

ref: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
text: Daar-ẽŋa daarɛ ya wʋn sose mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ, mam ka yetɩ mam wʋn sose ẽ bɔ ya.

ref: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
text: Mam Sɔ la mẽŋa nõŋɛ yãma mɛ, se'ere n soe la, yãma nõŋɛ mam mɛ, dee bɔ mam sɩra tɩ mam ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am.

ref: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
text: Mam ze'ele la m Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am dũnia zuo, la mam fõrgrɩ dũnia zuo wɛ'ɛsa la m Sɔ la zẽ'am.»

ref: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
text: Tɩ a karẽnbiisi la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nãnannɛ wã, fõn yãŋa tɔgrɩ la vẽelga vẽelga, dee ka le makra makrɛ.

ref: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
text: Tõma yãŋa mi tɩ fõn mi sɛla woo mɛ, fõn ka le ɛɛra tɩ nẽra soke fo. Bɛla tõma sakɛ tɩ fõn ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am.»

ref: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Ya yãŋa sakɛ ya?

ref: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
text: Tɔ. Wakatɛ sẽna, la a põn paam mɛ, tɩ ya wuu wʋn wɩrgɛ tɩ ya nẽra woo bɔna a yire dee basɛ mam m yɩla. La mam ka de m yɩla, se'ere n soe la, mam Sɔ la boe la mam mɛ.

ref: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
text: Mam yele ya bɛla tɩ ya tara la sũ-mã'asʋm mam yetɔga la ĩyã. Dũnia wã pʋam ya tarɩ la nãmsgɔ, la keŋe ya sũure, tɩ mam yãŋɛ dũnia mɛ.»

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
text: Azezi n tɔgɛ bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a gorge bɩsɛ la saazuum yetɩ: «M Sɔ, mam daarɛ paagɛ ya, nã'asɛ mam n de fʋ Dayʋa la, tɩ mam mẽ nã'asɛ fɔ.

ref: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
text: La de la fõn bɔ mam nõorɛ tɩ m sɔna nẽrsaalba wuu, dee bɔ sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam na wuu vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
text: La vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ la de la ba bãŋɛ fõn, fʋ yɩla mã'a n de Sɩra Dãana Nawẽnnɛ na, la ba bãŋɛ mam Azezi-krisi tɩ fʋ tõm na.

ref: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
text: Mam basɛ tɩ fõn yẽ nã'asgɔ tẽŋa wã zuo mɛ. Mam tõm tõon-sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam tɩ m tõm na ba'asɛ mɛ.

ref: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã, m Sɔ, botɩ m yẽ nã'asgɔ fʋ zẽ'am, wʋ mam n daan tarɩ na'am pãŋa fʋ zẽ'am se'em, lan dẽŋɛ dũnia n daan nãn ka nãam na.

ref: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
text: Mam botɩ sɛba tɩ fõn looge dũnia nẽrba pʋam bɔ mam na bãŋɛ fõn mɛ. Ba daan de la fõn nẽrba, tɩ fõn dɩkɛ ba bɔ mam, la ba sakɛ fʋ yetɔga la mɛ.

ref: They know that you gave me everything I have.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã ba bãŋɛ tɩ fõn bɔ mam sɛla la wuu ze'ele la fõn zẽ'am.

ref: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam tɔgɛ ba la yetɔg-sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam na, tɩ ba to'oge, dee bãŋɛ sɩra sɩra tɩ mam ze'ele la fõn zẽ'am. La ba bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn tõm mam.

ref: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ la bãma ĩyã, mam ka pʋ'ʋsrɩ dũnia nẽrba ĩyã, la de la nẽr-sɛba tɩ fʋ bɔ mam na ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, bãma de la fõn nẽrba.

ref: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
text: La mam bõnɔ de la fõn bõnɔ, tɩ fõn bõnɔ dɛna mam bõnɔ, la mam yẽ nã'asgɔ mɛ ba zẽ'am.

ref: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
text: Mam ka le bɔna dũnia zuo, la bãma boe la dũnia zuo dee tɩ mam sẽna fõn zẽ'am. M Sɔ n de pʋpeelem dãana, gu'ura ba la fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ la tɩ fʋ bɔ mam na pãŋa, tɩ ba wʋn dɛna ayɩla wʋ mam la fõn n de ayɩla se'em na.

ref: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
text: Mam n daan boe la ba lɛ la, mam daan gu'uri ba la yʋ'ʋr-sɛka tɩ fõn bɔ mam na pãŋa. Mam bɩsɛ ba yelle mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla ka bɔɩ, sãn dagna sɛka n de a sã'am, tɩ fõn gõŋɔ n yele se'em na ẽŋɛ la.

ref: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã mam sẽm na fõn zẽ'am, la mam tɔgrɩ la wãna mam nãn ken bɔna dũnia zuo, tɩ ba wʋn tara mam sũ-yẽlga, tɩ ba sũ-yẽlga zo'oge pãɩ.

ref: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
text: Mam tɔgɛ fõn yetɔga la bɔ ba mɛ, la dũnia nẽrba sisri ba mɛ, se'ere n soe la, ba dagɩ dũnia bõnɔ, wʋ mam mẽ dagɩ dũnia nẽra se'em na.

ref: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
text: Mam ka sosrɩ tɩ fʋ yese ba dũnia wã zuo, la de la tɩ fʋ gu ba la be'em dãana.

ref: They don't belong to this world, and neither do I.
text: La bãma dagɩ dũnia nẽrba, wʋ mam mẽ n dagɩ dũnia nẽra se'em na.

ref: Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours.
text: Botɩ fõn sɩra la wʋn basɛ tɩ ba welge to'ore tara pʋpeelem dɛna fʋ nẽrba. Fõn yetɔga la n de sɩra.

ref: I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me.
text: Mam mẽ tõm ba dũnia pʋam wʋ fõn tõm mam dũnia zuo se'em na.

ref: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
text: La bãma ĩyã tɩ mam welge m mẽŋa to'ore dɩkɛ m mẽŋa bɔ fõn, tɩ fõn sɩra la wʋn botɩ ba mẽ welge ba mẽŋa to'ore tara pʋpeelem dɛna fʋ nẽrba.

ref: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
text: La dagɩ bãma mã'a ĩyã tɩ mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ, la de la sɛba n wʋn wa bɔ mam sɩra ba yetɔga la ĩyã la mẽ yelle.

ref: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ fõn tɩ fʋ botɩ ba lagɩm dɛna bʋyɩla, wʋ fõn de mam Sɔ n boe la mam lɛ, tɩ mam mẽ bɔna la fõn se'em na, tɩ ba mẽ wʋn lagɩm dɛna bʋyɩla la tõma, tɩ dũnia nẽrba la wʋn bɔ sɩra tɩ fõn tõm mam.

ref: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
text: La mam bɔ ba na'am pãn-sɛka tɩ fõn bɔ mam na mɛ tɩ ba dɛna ayɩla, wʋ tõma n de ayɩla se'em na.

ref: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
text: Tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la ba, tɩ fõn bɔna la mam, tɩ ba wʋn sɩrɩ dɛna ayɩla, tɩ dũnia nẽrba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ la de la fõn tõm mam, la fõn nõŋɛ ba wʋ fõn nõŋɛ mam se'em na.

ref: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
text: M Sɔ, mam boorɩ tɩ nẽr-sɛba tɩ fʋ bɔ mam na, mẽ bɔna la mam lɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la, tɩ ba wʋn yẽ mam na'am pãŋa, na'am pãn-sɛka tɩ fõn bɔ mam na, se'ere n soe la, fõn nõŋɛ mam mɛ, tɩ dũnia yʋʋn nãn ken ka bɔna.

ref: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
text: M Sɔ, fõn de sɛka n masɛ. Dũnia nẽrba ka bãŋɛ fõn, la mam nõo bãŋɛ fõn mɛ, la nẽr-bãma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ la de la fõn tõm mam.

ref: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
text: Mam botɩ ba bãŋɛ fõn mɛ, la mam wʋn ken botɩ ba bãŋɛ fɔ, tɩ nõŋlʋm sɛka tɩ fõn nõŋɛ mam na, wʋn bɔna la ba, tɩ mam mẽ bɔna la ba.»

ref: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõnna ayɩla n de nẽr-sɛka tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan sɛ a tʋbrɛ fiige la sɔbia, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Dagɩ fõn tɩ mam yẽ la bʋraa la gaarɛ la pʋam na?»

ref: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
text: Tɩ Azezi le soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne tɩ yãma eerɩ?» Tɩ ba yetɩ: «Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la.»

ref: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam yele ya tɩ la de la mam. La sãn dɛna la mam tɩ ya eerɩ, basɛ-ya nẽr-bãna tɩ ba fõrgɛ.»

ref: Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‘I did not lose anyone you gave me.’ ”
text: A yele bɛla tɩ a yetɔga tɩ a tɔgɛ la n ẽŋɛ, a yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba tɩ fõn bɔ mam na, nẽra baa ayɩla ka bɔɩ.»

ref: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
text: La Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ daan tarɩ la sʋkɔbgɔ, fooge sʋkɔbgɔ la sɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tõntõnna zʋʋgɔ tʋbrɛ fiige. Tõntõnna la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Amalkusi.

ref: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la, la ba kɛ'ɛma, la Zifdõma gu'urba la yõkɛ Azezi lʋ ẽ,

ref: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ weege zɛ la yẽŋa, yaŋa nõorʋm, tɩ karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana mi la, yese ka tɔgɛ la pɔka la n gu'uri zanõrɛ la, dee botɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ kẽ.

ref: but asked him, “Aren't you one of that man's followers?” “No, I am not!” Peter answered.
text: Tɩ tõntõn-pɔka la n gu'uri zanõrɛ la, soke Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Fõn dagɩ la bʋra-ẽna karẽnbia ayɩla?» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ a karẽnbia.»

ref: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
text: La ɔɔrɔ n daan boe, tɩ tõntõnɩba, la gu'urba la tʋʋrɛ bugum kaagɛ ɔɔsra. Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan po bɔna mĩ ɔɔsra bugum na. Kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana soke Azezi a zãsŋɔ la yelle (Amatie

ref: The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching.
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la soke Azezi a karẽnbiisi la, la a zãsŋɔ la yelle.

ref: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
text: Bẽm tɩ fʋ sɔkra mam? Soke sɛba n wõm mam yetɔga la, bãma mi mam n yele se'em na mɛ.»

ref: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
text: Tɩ A'annɩ botɩ ba tarɩ ẽ ban lʋ ẽ se'em na, sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Akayifu zẽ'am. Apɩyɛɛrɩ le sɩ'ɩsɛ tɩ ẽŋa ka mi Azezi (Amatie

ref: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
text: Tɩ ba daan tarɩ Azezi Akayifu yire sẽŋɛ gɔmna yire. La daan de la bulika yʋ'ʋŋɔ yʋ'ʋŋɔ. La Zifdõma la mẽŋa ka kẽ gɔmna la yire, ba ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ ba da dẽgɛ la ba mẽŋa, tã'agɛ di Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la.

ref: Pilate came out and asked, “What charges are you bringing against this man?”
text: Tɩ Apilatɩ yese sẽŋɛ ba zẽ'am ka soke ba yetɩ: «Bẽm yelle tɩ yãma tarɩ bʋra-ẽna wã wa'am?»

ref: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
text: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «A sãn ka ẽŋɛ nɩ be'em, tõma kãn yõkɛ ẽ wa'am wa bɔ fõn.»

ref: And so what Jesus said about his death would soon come true.
text: La de la bɛla tɩ Azezi n daan tɔgɛ pa'alɛ kũm sɛka buuri tɩ ẽŋa wʋn ki la ẽŋɛ.

ref: Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
text: Tɩ Apilatɩ lebe kẽ deem ka wi Azezi soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de Zifdõma naba la?»

ref: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ fõn sɔkra bɛla bɩɩ, la de la nẽrba basɛba n tɔgɛ mam yelle bɛla bɔ fõn?»

ref: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
text: Tɩ Apilatɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam dagɩ Zifu. Fʋ mẽŋa buuri, la ba kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma n yõkɛ fʋ tarɩ wa bɔ mam. Bẽm tɩ fõn ẽŋɛ?»

ref: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam so'olʋm dagɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ. Mam so'olʋm sãn de nɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ, mam tõntõnɩba la wʋn zɛbɛ nɩ mɛ tɩ ba da yõkɛ mam bɔ Zifdõma la. La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam so'olʋm dagɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ.»

ref: “So you are a king,” Pilate replied. “You are saying that I am a king,” Jesus told him. “I was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.”
text: Tɩ Apilatɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Bɛla fõn de la naba?» Tɩ Azezi lerge yetɩ: «La de la fõn yele se'em na, mam de la naba. Mam dɔgɛ wa'am dũnia wã tɩ m tɔgɛ la sɩra kaseto. La sɛka woo n dolɩ sɩra selsrɩ la mam kõa.»

ref: And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?”
text: La yãma malʋm mɛ tɩ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa wakatɛ woo m yese yʋ'a deem nẽra ayɩla basɛ. Yãma boorɩ tɩ m yese la Zifdõma naba la basɛ bɩɩ?»

ref: They shouted, “No, not him! We want Barabbas.” Now Barabbas was a terrorist.
text: Tɩ ba tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Da kãn basɛ ẽŋa. Tõma boorɩ tɩ fʋ basɛ la Abarabasɩ.» La Abarabasɩ daan de la fãara.

ref: Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip.
text: Bɛla tɩ Apilatɩ botɩ ba tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka wẽ ẽ na ka'asa.

ref: The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ dɩkɛ gõ'osɩ wʋgɛ mũŋa vuge ẽ, dee yɛ ẽ fuugo n de gomusga,

ref: They came up to him and said, “Hey, you king of the Jews!” They also hit him with their fists.
text: sẽŋɛ lɛm ẽ yɛta tɩ: «Zifdõma naba, naa», dee wẽ'era a pɛgsɩ.

text: Tɩ Apilatɩ le yese nẽrba la zẽ'am ka yele ba yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam wʋn tarɩ ẽ yesem na ya zẽ'am tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ mam ka yẽ sɛla n de be'em bʋra-ẽna zẽ'am.»

ref: Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, “Here is the man!”

ref: When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, “Nail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!” Pilate told them, “You take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.”
text: La kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la, la ba tõntõnɩba la n yẽ ẽ na, ba kaasrɩ la tagurga yetɩ: «Ka ẽ, ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.» Tɩ Apilatɩ yele ba yetɩ: «To'oge-ya ẽ ka ka ya mẽŋa, se'ere n soe la, mam ka yẽ sɛla n de be'em a zẽ'am.»

ref: Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, “If you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.”

text: Bɛla tɩ Apilatɩ bɔ ba Azezi tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ ka ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo. Ba tarɩ Azezi sẽŋɛ ka ka dɔ-puurŋa zuo (Amatie
