text: La bɛla poorʋm Azezi le dɩkɛ a mẽŋa mɛ pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi la Tiberiadɩ mõgrɛ nõorʋm. Ẽn pa'alɛ ba a mẽŋa se'em n wãna:

ref: Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the brothers James and John, were there, together with two other disciples.
text: Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ, la Atoma tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ lɩɩba la, la Anatanayɛlɩ n ze'ele Kana n boe Galile so'olʋm, la Azebede kɔma, la Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba bayi n daan lagɩm bɔna la taaba.

ref: Simon Peter said, “I'm going fishing!” The others said, “We will go with you.” They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
text: Tɩ Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Mam we'esɩ tɩ m ka yõgɛ la zũma.» Tɩ basɛba la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma mẽ wʋn doose la fʋ sẽŋɛ.» Tɩ ba yese ka kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ. La yʋ'ʋŋ-ẽŋa ba ka yõkɛ sɛla sɛla.

ref: Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was.
text: Tɩ beere bɔna mĩ yɩlɩŋra, tɩ Azezi zɛ mõgrɛ la nõorʋm, tɩ a karẽnbiisi la ka bãŋɛ tɩ la de la ẽŋa.

ref: Jesus shouted, “Friends, have you caught anything?” “No!” they answered.
text: Tɩ Azezi soke ba yetɩ: «M kɔma, yãma tarɩ zũma?» Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ.»

ref: So he told them, “Let your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.” They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Lobe-ya yʋka la õorŋɔ la zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ ya wʋn yõgɛ zũma.» Tɩ ba lobe ba yʋka la, yõgɛ zũma zo'oge ka tãna wʋn ve'ege ẽŋɛ õorŋɔ la pʋam.

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, “It's the Lord!” When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water.
text: Tɩ karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ Azezi nõŋɛ la yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «La de la Zuudãana la.» La Apɩyɛɛrɩ n wõm tɩ a de la Zuudãana la, tɩ a dɩkɛ a fuugo yɛ, ẽkɛ lui ko'om pʋam dɔɔra.

ref: When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it.
text: La ban dooge doone na, ba yẽ la bugsãana n diti, tɩ zũma dɔga ba zuo, la borborɩ.

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Looge-ya zũn-sɛba tɩ ya yõgɛ lɛɛlɛ la basɛba wa'am.»

ref: Jesus said, “Come and eat!” But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord.
text: Tɩ Azezi yele ba yetɩ: «Wa'am-ya wa di.» La karẽnbiisi la nẽra baa ayɩla daan ka saagɛ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn de la ãne?» Se'ere n soe la, ba daan bãŋɛ tɩ a de la Zuudãana la.

ref: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish.
text: Tɩ Azezi paagɛ dɩkɛ borborɩ la pʋɩ ba, dee dɩkɛ zũma la bɔ ba, tɩ ba di.

ref: This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death. Jesus and Peter
text: Azezi n daan vo'oge la, nõorɛ atã n bɛla tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi la. Azezi tɔgrɩ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ

ref: When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?” Simon Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I do!” “Then feed my lambs,” Jesus said.
text: Ban daan di kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ Azezi soke Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Asimõ, Azã bia, fõn nõŋɛ mam gãnna bãna n nõŋɛ mam se'em na?» Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽe! Zuudãana, fõn mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ fõn mɛ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Gu'ura m pebilsi.»

ref: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I love you!” “Then take care of my sheep,” Jesus told him.
text: Tɩ Azezi le soke ẽ nõorɛ bʋyi yetɩ: «Asimõ, Azã bia, fõn nõŋɛ mam?» Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ẽe! Zuudãana, fõn mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ fõn mɛ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsra m piisi.»

ref: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, “Lord, you know everything. You know I love you.” Jesus replied, “Feed my sheep.
text: Tɩ Azezi le soke ẽ nõorɛ atã yetɩ: «Asimõ, Azã bia, fõn nõŋɛ mam?» Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sũure daan sã'am, ẽn soke ẽ nõorɛ atã yetɩ: «Fõn nõŋɛ mam mɛ bɩɩ» la. Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn mi sɛla wuu mɛ, fõn mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ fõn mɛ.» Tɩ Azezi yele ẽ yetɩ: «Gu'ura m piisi la.

ref: I tell you for certain that when you were a young man, you dressed yourself and went wherever you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands. Then others will wrap your belt around you and lead you where you don't want to go.”
text: Mam yetɩ fʋ la sɩra sɩra, tɩ fõn daan de budib-paalga la, fʋ daan lʋtɩ fʋ sagãnnɛ mɛ sẽnna fõn boorɩ zẽ'a, la fʋ sãn wa kʋrgɛ, fʋ wʋn zẽkɛ fʋ nu'usi tɩ nẽra lʋ fʋ sagãnnɛ, dee tarɩ fʋ sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ fʋ ka boorɩ.»

ref: Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, “Follow me!” Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
text: Azezi daan yele bɛla pa'alɛ la kũm sɛka buuri tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ wʋn ki tɩ la nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ. La ẽn yele bɛla la, a yele ẽ yetɩ: «Dɔla mam!» Karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ Azezi nõŋɛ la yelle

ref: Peter turned and saw Jesus' favorite disciple following them. He was the same one who had sat next to Jesus at the meal and had asked, “Lord, who is going to betray you?”
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ gelge mɛ yẽ karẽnbi-sɛka tɩ Azezi nõŋɛ la n dolɩ ba poore. La de la karẽnbi-ẽŋa n daan dente Azezi dia la wakatɛ, soke ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la de la ãne n yetɩ a pa'alɛ tɩ ba yõkɛ fõn?»

ref: When Peter saw this disciple, he asked Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ n yẽ ẽ na, tɩ a soke Azezi yetɩ: «Zuudãana, dee ẽna wã, bẽm n wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽ?»

ref: Jesus answered, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return? You must follow me.”
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Mam sãn bɔɔra tɩ a vɔa, halɩ tɩ mam wa leme wa'am, bẽm n pakɛ fõn? Fõn wen dɔla mam.»

ref: So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?”
text: Tɩ yetɔg-ẽna wã saagɛ sɔbiisi la zẽ'am tɩ karẽnbi-ẽŋa kãn ki. La Azezi ka yele tɩ a kãn ki, a yetɩ: «Mam sãn bɔɔra tɩ a vɔa halɩ tɩ mam wa leme wa'am, bẽm n pakɛ fõn?»

ref: This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
text: La de la karẽnbi-ẽŋa n bɔ bõn-bãna kaseto, la ẽŋa n gʋlsɛ ba. La tõma mi tɩ a kaseto la de la sɩra.

ref: Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
text: La Azezi daan ẽŋɛ yɛla basɛba zozo'e mɛ. Ba sãn yetɩ ba gʋlsɛ nɩ ba wuu ayɩla-yɩla, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ zẽ'a kãn bɔna dũnia zuo n wʋn to'oge gõnnɔ na tɩ ba wʋn gʋlsɛ nɩ na.

ref: Theophilus, I first wrote to you about all that Jesus did and taught from the very first
text: Ateofili, mam pɔspɔsɩ gõŋɔ la pʋam mam tɔgɛ pa'alɛ fõn Azezi n daan pɔsɛ tõm se'em, dee pa'alɛ nẽrba se'em wuu halɩ

ref: until he was taken up to heaven. But before he was taken up, he gave orders to the apostles he had chosen with the help of the Holy Spirit.
text: ka paagɛ daar-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tarɩ ẽ zom a yire la mɛ. La ẽn daan nãn ka zom Nawẽnnɛ yire la, a daan tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la pãŋa pa'ala Tõntõnɩba tɩ a looge la, bãma n wʋn tõnna se'em.

ref: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
text: La ban lagsɛ bɔna la ẽ na, tɩ a bɔ ba nõorɛ yetɩ: «Da yese-ya Zerizalɛm tẽŋa pʋam, gu'ura-ya bõn-sɛka tɩ mam Sɔ daan bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ya, tɩ mam yele ya la.

ref: John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit. Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
text: Se'ere n soe la, Azã daan misri ya la ko'om pʋam, la, la kãn yuuge dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mise ya a Sɩa pʋam.» Azezi zom Nawẽnnɛ yire

ref: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
text: La Tõntõnɩba la n lagsɛ taaba la, soke Azezi mɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana, la de la lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ fõn wʋn to'oge Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma na'am le bɔ ba bɩɩ?»

ref: Jesus said to them, “You don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls.
text: Tɩ Azezi lerge ba yetɩ: «La ka de ya bãŋɛ wakatɛ la dabs-sɛba tɩ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ looge la a mẽŋa pãŋa.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋn wa'am ya zuo, wa bɔ ya pãŋa. La ya wʋn dɛna mam kaset-dõma pa'alɛ mam yɛla Zerizalɛm, la Zude, la Samaari tẽnsʋm, halɩ ka paagɛ dũnia tɛka wuu.»

ref: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
text: Ẽŋa n tɔgɛ bɛla ba'asɛ la, dee zɔnna Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ ba bɩsra ẽ, tɩ wara lile ẽ.

ref: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
text: La ẽn zonnɩ na, ba ken bɩsra la saazuum zĩi, tɩ bʋraasɩ bayi yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ darɛ wa'am wa zɛ ba zẽ'am,

ref: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
text: dee soke ba yetɩ: «Galile nẽrba, bẽm tɩ yãma zɛ bɩsra saazuum? Azezi n yese ya zẽ'am wɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire wã, a wʋn le leme wʋ yãma n yẽ ẽ tɩ a wɛ'ɛsa se'em wã.» Nẽr-sɛka n wʋn sɔɔgɛ Azudaasɩ zẽ'a

ref: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
text: La ban paagɛ Zerizalɛm tẽŋam na, ba zom na saazuum deego tɩ ba ẽn bɔna mĩ na pʋam. La sɛba n daan boe mĩ na de la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azã, la Azakɩ, la A'ãndre, la Afilipi, la Atoma, la Abatelemi, la Amatie, la Azakɩ n de Alfe bia, la Asimõ sɛka n daan de Zelɔtɩ nẽra, la Azakɩ bia Azudaasɩ.

ref: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
text: Bãma wuu la pɔgsɩ, la Azezi ma Amaari, la Azezi yɩbsɩ n daan ẽn lagsɛ taaba pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla wakatɛ wuu.

ref: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
text: (La ẽn daan tõm be'em na tɩ ba yɔ ẽ, tɩ a da va'am la a tõon-be'ero la yɔɔrɔ, lui aliko, tɩ a pʋʋrɛ pusge, tɩ a yõorɔ wuu yurge.

ref: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
text: Zerizalɛm tẽŋa nẽrba wuu wõm bɛla mɛ, tɩ ba wi'ira va'am na yʋ'ʋrɛ la ba tẽŋa yetɔga yetɩ «Akeldama». A võorɛ de la zɩɩm va'am.)

ref: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ ken tɔgɛ yetɩ: «La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yõoma gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹A yire wʋn lebge daboogo, tɩ nẽra nẽra da le kẽ'era mĩ.› Dee ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Nẽra wʋn sɔɔgɛ a zẽ'am tõm.›

ref: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
text: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ nẽra ayɩla pa'asɛ tõma Tõntõnɩba la pʋam pa'alɛ Azezi vo'ore la sɩra. La masɛ tɩ nẽr-ẽŋa dɛna la nẽr-sɛka n daan põn bɔna tɩ pʋam wakat-sɛka wuu tɩ Zuudãana Azezi daan boe la tõma la, lan pĩ'ilum la Azã n daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam na, wa paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Azezi daan yese tõma sʋkam zom Nawẽnnɛ yire la.»

ref: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
text: Tɩ ba bãm yetɩ nẽrba bayi. Ayɩla de la Azozɛfʋ, tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Abarsabasɩ, dee tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ ken dɛna Azustusi. Tɩ ayẽma la mẽ yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Amatɩyasɩ.

ref: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
text: Tɩ ba yãŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Tɩ Zuudãana, fõn mi nẽrsaalba wuu sũure. Pa'alɛ tɔ nẽrba bayi wã pʋam, fõn looge nẽr-sɛka

ref: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
text: tɩ a wʋn sɔɔgɛ Azudaasɩ zẽ'am lagɩm na Tõntõnɩba la, tõm tõon-sɛka tɩ Azudaasɩ basɛ dee sẽŋɛ lan masɛ tɩ a sẽŋɛ zẽ'am na.»

ref: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
text: Ba pʋ'ʋsɛ bɛla dee pɔsɛ mɛ, tɩ la yõkɛ Amatɩyasɩ, tɩ ba botɩ a naagɛ la Tõntõnɩba pia la ayɩla la.

ref: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
text: La Zifdõma kibsa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Pãntekɔtɩ daarɛ n daan paagɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi wuu daan bɔna zẽ'a ayɩla.

ref: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
text: Tɩ voole darɛ ze'ele saazuum wa'am wʋ kʋseb-kãtɛ wa pɩrɛ de-sɛka tɩ ba zĩ mĩ na.

ref: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa daan wa'am ba wuu zẽ'am la pãŋa, tɩ ba tɔgra bu-zãŋsɩ yetɔga, wʋ Sɩa la n bɔ ba tɩ ba tɔgɛ se'em na.

ref: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
text: La Zifdõma n daan dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore sõŋa n yese dũnia so'olʋm wuu wa'am wa bɔna Zerizalɛm.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
text: La nẽrba la n daan wõm voole la, ba daan lagsɛ zo'oge mɛ, tɩ la daan di'ige ba, se'ere n soe la, nẽra woo daan wõm tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la a mẽŋa buuri yetɔga.

ref: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
text: Tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba zɛ yɛrɩ, sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, nẽr-bãna wã wuu n tɔgrɩ wã de la Galile dõma.

ref: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
text: La, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ tõma nẽra woo wõm tɩ ba tɔgra a buuri yetɔga?

ref: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
text: Tõma nẽrba basɛba yese la Partɩ, la Medi, la Elamɩ, la Mezopotami tẽnsʋm, tɩ basɛba mẽ yese Zude, la Kapadɔsɩ, la Põ, la Azi tẽnsʋm,

ref: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
text: Nẽrba la basɛba de la Zifdõma, la basɛba dagɩ Zifdõma dee sakɛ dɔla lɔɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ Zifdõma. Basɛba mẽ de la Krɛtɩ dõma, la Araabɩ dõma. La baa bɛla wuu tõma wõm mɛ tɩ ba tɔgrɩ tõma buuri yetɔga pa'ala tõon-kãra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ.»

ref: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
text: Tɩ ba palẽŋa ẽkɛ, tɩ ba ka mina ba wʋn tẽ'esɛ se'em, yãŋa sɔkra taaba yetɩ: «Yel-ẽna võorɛ de la bẽm?»

ref: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
text: Tɩ basɛba la'ara ba yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã yũ la dãam buge.» Apɩyɛɛrɩ mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga

ref: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la a Tõntõn-taaba pia la ayɩla la ze'ele saazuo, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la yetɩ: «Yãma Zifdõma, la yãma sɛba n boe Zerizalɛm wã wuu, selse-ya mam n yetɩ m yele ya se'em wã, tɩ ya tã'agɛ bãŋɛ yel-ẽna wã võorɛ.

ref: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
text: Yãma n tẽ'esrɩ tɩ nẽrba wã yũ la dãam buge la, la dagɩ bɛla, la nãn de la bulika.

ref: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
text: La bõn-ẽna n ɩtɩ wã de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa Azʋwɛlɩ n yʋʋn yele se'em na n ɩtɩ.»

ref: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
text: A yʋʋn yele tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Dũnia ba'asgɔ sãn lɛm, mam wʋn basɛ tɩ m Sɩa sige nẽrsaalba buuri wuu zuto. A wʋn botɩ ya dayɔɔsɩ la ya pɔyɔɔsɩ to'oge mam yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba. Dee botɩ ya kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ bɩsɛ yẽ yɛla wʋ zãasŋɔ la, dee botɩ ya bõnkɛgsɩ zãasʋm.

ref: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
text: Asɩra, wakat-ẽŋa mam wʋn basɛ tɩ m Sɩa la sige m tõntõn-bʋraasɩ, la m tõntõn-pɔgsɩ zuto, tɩ ba to'oge mam yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

ref: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
text: Mam wʋn botɩ bõn-yãlma ẽŋɛ saazuum, dee botɩ yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ mam pãŋa ẽŋɛ tẽŋa zuo: Zɩɩm, la bugum, la bug-zũ'usi zozo'e n wʋn bɔna.

ref: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
text: Wẽntulle wʋn wa lebge la lika, tɩ wõrga mõ'ogɛ ãna wʋ zɩɩm na, dee tɩ Zuudãana daarɛ n de na'am kãtɛ daarɛ paagɛ.

ref: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
text: La nẽr-sɛka woo n wʋn wi Zuudãana yʋ'ʋrɛ sose tɩ a fãagɛ ẽ, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.»

ref: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
text: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, selse-ya mam n yetɩ m yele ya se'em wã. La de la Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la yelle. Nawẽnnɛ n looge ẽ bɔ ya, botɩ a tõm tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ tɩ tẽŋasʋka, wʋ yãma n põn mina se'em na, pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa n tõm ẽ tɩ zẽ'am.

ref: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn põn tẽ'esɛ bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na ĩyã, tɩ a dɩkɛ Azezi ẽŋɛ ya nu'usum, tɩ ya botɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la yõkɛ ẽ tarɩ ka ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.

ref: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ, fãagɛ ẽ kũm nãmsgɔ pʋam basɛ, kũm n ka tã'agɛ tara ẽ na ĩyã.

ref: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
text: Adavidi yʋʋn põn tɔgɛ a yelle yetɩ: ‹Mam yẽtɩ mam Zuudãana la mam nẽŋam mɛ daarɛ woo. A boe la mam zʋʋgɔ bɔba sõŋra mam tɩ sɛla kãn botɩ mam bike.

ref: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
text: Bɛla ĩyã mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ, dee tɔgra sũ-yẽlga yetɔga. La baa mam wʋn ki, mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ mɛ,

ref: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
text: tɩ fõn Nawẽnnɛ kãn basɛ mam kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋam, fõn kãn basɛ tɩ fʋ pʋpeelem dãana põ'ogɛ.

ref: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
text: Fõn botɩ mam bãŋɛ yõvore sore mɛ. Fõn wʋn wa botɩ mam yẽ sũ-yẽlga mɛ zozo'e fõn nẽŋam.› »

ref: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
text: «M sɔbiisi, basɛ-ya tɩ m tɔgɛ ya vẽelga vẽelga tɩ yaaba naba Adavidi yelle. A yʋʋn ki mɛ, tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ. Tɩ a yɔɔgɔ bɔna tɩ tẽŋasʋka kalam halɩ wa paam zĩna.

ref: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
text: Adavidi yʋʋn de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, la a bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe la nõorɛ pɔɩ yetɩ, a wʋn wa botɩ a yɩɩsɩ la nẽra ayɩla le di na'am wʋ ẽn yʋʋn diti na'am se'em na.

ref: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
text: Adavidi dẽŋɛ yẽ Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ a tõm se'em na mɛ. Tɩ a tɔgɛ Krisi kũm vo'ore yelle yetɩ: Nawẽnnɛ kãn basɛ ẽ kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋam, a kãn botɩ a ĩyã põ'ogɛ.»

ref: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
text: Azezi ẽŋa n wãna tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge. Tɩ tõma waa wuu dɛna kaset-dõma tɔgra a yelle bɔ'ɔra ya.

ref: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yãŋa zẽkɛ ẽ mɛ ka zĩ'ire a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ a to'oge a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a wʋn bɔ tõma wuu la. Bõn-ẽŋa n wãna tɩ ya yẽtɩ dee wõnna lɛɛlɛ wã.

ref: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
text: La dagɩ Adavidi n yʋʋn zom Nawẽnnɛ yire. La a mẽŋa yʋʋn yetɩ: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele mam Zuudãana yetɩ: Zĩ'ire m zʋʋgɔ bɔba,

ref: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
text: halɩ tɩ m botɩ fʋ bɛ'ɛba bɔna fʋ nãma tẽŋa.»

ref: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
text: «Bɛla, Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri wuu masɛ mɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ sɩra sɩra tɩ Azezi tɩ ya daan ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ la, Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ ya tɩ ẽŋa n de Krisi, dee dɛna tɩ Zuudãana.»

ref: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
text: Nẽrba la n daan wõm yetɔga wã la, la daan ba kẽ ba sũure mɛ halɩ tɩ ba soke Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Tõntõnɩba basɛba la yetɩ: «Tɩ sɔbiisi, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?»

ref: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ba yetɩ: «Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, tɩ ya nẽra woo botɩ ba mise ẽ ko'om pʋam la Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ dee bɔ ya a Sɩa la.

ref: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
text: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la, de la yãma la ya kɔma, la sɛba wuu n boe zẽ'a n zãagɛ tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wa wi ba a zẽ'am na ĩyã.»

ref: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan tɔgɛ la yetɔga basɛba zozo'e pa'alɛ ba sɩra, dee sɩgɛ ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ fãagɛ ya zamãan-ẽna nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ la pʋam.»

ref: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
text: La ba daan ba mõrgɛ botɩ Tõntõnɩba la zãsna ba mɛ, dee naagɛ taaba la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla, lagsra dita la taaba, fiisra borborɩ dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ. Sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la vɔm n ãn se'em

ref: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
text: La dabeem daan kẽ nẽrba la wuu mɛ, se'ere n soe la, Azezi Tõntõnɩba la tõm na tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ zozo'e.

ref: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
text: La nẽr-sɛba n daan bɔ Azezi sɩra la wuu daan lagɩm taaba la nõorɛ ayɩla, tɩ ba bõntarsõm lebge ba wuu bõnɔ,

ref: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
text: dee daan kɔɔsra ba lɔgrɔ la ba bõntarsõm, pʋta bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba wuu n bɔɔra sõŋrɛ se'em na.

ref: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
text: La daarɛ woo ba ẽn lagsra Wẽnde-kãtʋm na mɛ, la taaba yiɛ, dita dee fiisra borborɩ, tara sũ-yẽlga la yɛm ayɩla,

ref: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
text: pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ, dee yẽta yel-sõnnɛ nẽrba wuu zẽ'am. La daarɛ woo Zuudãana botɩ nẽrba n yẽta fãarɛ pa'asra sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la pʋam.

ref: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
text: Daarɛ ayɩla zaanõorɛ karf-atã, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã dɔla taaba wɛ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, wakat-sɛka tɩ nẽrba ẽn sẽnna pʋ'ʋsra la.

ref: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
text: La bʋraa n boe tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna kɔɔrŋɔ. La daarɛ woo ba ẽn tara ẽ sẽnna zĩ'ita la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la nõ-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ nõ-sõŋɔ la, tɩ a sɔsra nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam na.

ref: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
text: La kɔɔrŋɔ la n yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã n boe mĩ tɩ ba kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ a sose ba yetɩ, ba bɔ ẽ sɛla.

ref: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
text: Tɩ ba bɩsra ẽ zĩi, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ tõma.»

ref: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
text: Tɩ a malɩn bɩsra ba, tẽ'esra tɩ ba wʋn bɔ ẽ sɛla.

ref: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Mam ka tarɩ ligri wʋn bɔ fɔ. La sɛla tɩ mam tarɩ la, mam wʋn bɔ fɔ. La Azezi-krisi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, isge sẽnna.»

ref: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ gʋrɛ la a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo zẽkɛ ẽ tɩ a isge. Tɩ a nãma la zĩila keŋe bilam mẽŋa,

ref: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
text: tɩ a vaagɛ isge ze'ele saazuo, sẽnna dɔla ba kẽ'era Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, ẽgra sisra, dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
text: Tɩ nẽrba la wuu yẽ ẽ tɩ a sẽnnɩ dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
text: Tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ la de la ẽŋa n ẽn zẽa Wẽnde-kãtɛ la nõ-sõŋɔm na sɔsra. La ban yẽ sɛla n ẽŋɛ ẽ na, la di'ige ba mɛ zozo'e. Apɩyɛɛrɩ mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Wẽnde-kãtʋm

ref: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
text: La nẽrba la n yẽ bʋra-sɛka n daan de kɔɔrŋɔ la n dolɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã la, la di'ige ba wuu mɛ, tɩ ba zoe ka lagsɛ taaba ba zẽ'am zẽ'e-sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de «Asalomõ võnana» na.

ref: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ n yẽ bɛla la, tɩ a soke nẽrba la yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, bẽm tɩ bõn-ẽna wã di'ige ya? La bẽm tɩ ya bɩsra tõma, wʋ la de la tõma mẽŋa pãŋa, bɩɩ tõma n ba dɔla Nawẽnnɛ sõŋa la ĩyã tɩ tõma botɩ bʋra-ẽna isge sẽnna?

ref: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tɩ yaabdõma Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la, n zẽkɛ a tõntõnna Azezi la yʋ'ʋrɛ. Ẽŋa tɩ ya daan yõkɛ sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ sɛba n soe pãŋa la nu'usum, tɩ baa gɔmna Apilatɩ n daan boorɩ tɩ a basɛ ẽ na, ya daan zagsɛ mɛ a nẽŋam.

ref: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
text: La yan daan zagsɛ Azezi la, ya zagsɛ la pʋpeelem dãana, la sɩra dãana, dee daan bɔɔra tɩ Apilatɩ basɛ nẽr-kʋʋra la bɔ ya.

ref: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
text: Bɛla yãma botɩ ba kʋ la yõvore dãana, ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge. Tõma daan yẽ bõn-ẽna mɛ, tɔgra a yelle bɔ'ɔra ya.

ref: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
text: La de la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ n tarɩ pãŋa la ĩyã, la tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la n botɩ bʋra-ẽna tɩ yãma mi dee yẽ wã keŋe. La de la ẽn bɔ Azezi sɩra la n botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm sõŋa ya wuu nẽŋam wã.

ref: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
text: Tɔ. M sɔbiisi, mam bãŋɛ tɩ yan ka mi la ĩyã, tɩ yãma la ya nẽŋadõma daan ẽŋɛ bɛla.

ref: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
text: La bɛla pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn põn dẽŋɛ botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba wuu tɔgɛ yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ Krisi wa nãmsɛ la, ẽŋɛ.

ref: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
text: Bɛla, teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, dee wa doose Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a wʋn basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ,

ref: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
text: dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm, dee tõm Krisi tɩ a daan põn dẽŋɛ looge la bɔ ya. Ẽŋa n de Azezi.

ref: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ sɛla wuu wʋn teege lebge paalsɩ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn põn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔgɛ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm se'em na.

ref: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
text: Bɛla tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Ya Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ nẽra ayɩla ze'ele ya buuri pʋam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa bɔ yãma, wʋ ẽŋa n botɩ mam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa se'em na. Dẽnɩ tɩ ya selse bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a wʋn tɔgɛ bɔ ya la,

ref: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
text: la nẽr-sɛka woo n ka selse Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔs-ẽŋa, ba wʋn yese ẽ mɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka kʋ.›

ref: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
text: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, lan pĩ'ilum la Asãmwɛlɩ la sɛba n tɔgɛ ẽŋa poorʋm na wuu, yʋʋn põn tɔgɛ dabs-bãna yelle mɛ.

ref: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
text: Yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yʋʋn tɔgɛ la de la yãma ĩyã, la ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma la ken dɛna la yãma ĩyã. A yele Abraham yetɩ: ‹Fʋ yɩɩsɩ la ĩyã tɩ mam wʋn ẽŋɛ dũnia buuri wuu yel-sõnnɛ.›

ref: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
text: Nawẽnnɛ looge a tõntõnna la, dẽŋɛ tõm ẽ na ya zẽ'am tɩ a ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ botɩ ya nẽra woo basɛ a tõon-be'ero.»

ref: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã ken tɔgra la nẽrba la lɛ, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma, la Wẽnde-kãtɛ gu'urba nẽŋadãana, la Saduse dõma wa'am ba zẽ'am.

ref: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
text: Tɩ ba sũure yĩige la Tõntõnɩba la n zãsnɩ nẽrba la, dee mõola Azezi kũm vo'ore yelle pa'ala tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge mɛ na.

ref: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
text: Tɩ ba yõgɛ ba ka yʋ, gu'ura tɩ beere wiige, se'ere n soe la, wẽnnɛ daan sobe mɛ.

ref: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
text: La nẽr-sɛba n daan wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, ba zozo'e daan bɔ yetɔga la sɩra mɛ. Ba kãllɛ daan de wʋ tʋsanuu.

ref: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
text: La beere n wiige, tɩ Zifdõma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma, la lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma lagsɛ taaba Zerizalɛm pʋam.

ref: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
text: La A'annɩ n de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la Akayifu, la Azã, la Alɛgsãndrɩ, la sɛba wuu n de kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana yir-dõma boe mĩ mɛ.

ref: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
text: Tɩ ba botɩ ba tarɩ ba wa'am wa ze'ele ba nẽŋam, tɩ ba soke ba yetɩ: «Ãne n bɔ yãma pãŋa la nõorɛ tɩ yãma ɩta bõn-bãna?»

ref: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ Apɩyɛɛrɩ pãŋa, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽŋadõma, la kẽma,

ref: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
text: yãma n sokrɩ tõma zĩna daarɛ wã tõon-sõŋ-sɛka tɩ tõma ẽŋɛ bɔ kɔɔrŋɔ la yelle, la bõn-sɛka n botɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na,

ref: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
text: dẽnɩ tɩ ya wuu, la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma wuu bãŋɛ tɩ la de la Azezi-krisi n ze'ele Nazarɛtɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã tɩ nẽra wã n ze ya nẽŋam wã tarɩ ĩmã'asʋm. Ẽŋa tɩ ya daan ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ.

ref: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
text: Ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɔgɛ yetɩ: ‹Kug-sɛka tɩ yãma mẽtba zagsɛ la, lebge la kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa.›

ref: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
text: Fãarɛ ka boe nẽra ayẽma zẽ'am, sãn dagna Azezi-krisi mã'a n tã wʋn fãagɛ nẽrba dũnia zuo basɛ.»

ref: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã n yẽ bʋraanɛ tɔgra la, la di'ige nẽrba la mɛ, ban mi tɩ ba nini ka yo'oge, dee tɩ ba dagna gõn-mitba la, tɩ ba bãŋɛ tɩ ba daan boe la Azezi lɛ.

ref: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
text: La ban yẽ bʋraa la n yẽ ĩmã'asʋm zɛ ba zẽ'am na, ba ka mi ban wʋn lerge se'em bɔ ba.

ref: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
text: Tɩ ba yele ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am yetɩ ba yese. Tɩ ba yese. Dee tɩ ba bãm yele taaba yetɩ:

ref: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
text: «Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽr-bãna la wãne? Nẽrba wuu n boe Zerizalɛm wã mi mɛ tɩ bãma n ẽŋɛ yel-kɩrga wã, tõma kãn tã'agɛ sɩ'ɩsɛ bõn-ẽna.

ref: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
text: La tõma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ ba tõoma la da le saagɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa, la de la tɩ zɛbɛ ba yetɩ, ba da le tɔgɛ Azezi yelle bɔ nẽra nẽra.»

ref: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
text: Bɛla tɩ ba wi ba yetɩ, ba da le sakɛ tɩ ba tɔgɛ, dee zãsʋm nẽrba Azezi yɛla.

ref: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã lerge ba mɛ yetɩ: «Tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ, la masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tɩ tõma sakɛ yãma nõorɛ gãnna Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ mɛ bɩɩ?

ref: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
text: Tõma ka tã wʋn basɛ ka tɔgɛ sɛla tɩ tõma yẽ dee wõm na.»

ref: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
text: Bʋraa la tɩ yel-kɩrga la ẽŋɛ tɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm na, gãŋɛ yʋʋma pinaasɩ mɛ. Sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra sose mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba bʋraanɛ

ref: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
text: La ban basɛ ba la, Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã sẽŋɛ la ba taaba zẽ'am ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma la n yele bãma se'em na wuu.

ref: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: La ban wõm bɛla la, tɩ ba naagɛ taaba pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ la yɛm ayɩla yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn nãam saazuum la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la bõn-sɛba wuu n boe ba pʋam na.

ref: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
text: Fõn yʋʋn botɩ tõma yaaba Adavidi n de fʋ tõntõnna la tɔgɛ la fʋ Sɩa pãŋa yetɩ: ‹Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ dũnia buuri wuu sũure yĩige, tɩ nẽrba tẽ'esra tẽ'es-yaalsɩ?

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
text: Tɩ dũnia na'adõma mãasʋm zɛ, tɩ tẽŋa la nẽŋadõma lagsɛ taaba tɩ ba zɛbɛ la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, la Krisi tɩ a looge la?›

ref: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
text: La de la sɩra tɩ A'erɔdɩ, la Apõnsɩ-pilatɩ, la bu-zãŋsɩ, la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba lagsɛ taaba tẽn-ẽna wã pʋam zɛbɛ la fʋ tõntõn-sõŋɔ Azezi tɩ fʋ looge la.

ref: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
text: Bɛla ba ẽŋɛ la bõn-sɛka tɩ fõn yʋʋn põn dẽŋɛ looge yɛm la fʋ pãŋa bɔɔra tɩ la ẽŋɛ se'em na.

ref: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana, lɛɛlɛ wã bɩsɛ ban zɛbrɩ tõma n de fʋ tõntõnɩba la se'em, bɛla ĩyã, bɔ tɩ pãŋa tɩ tɩ mõolɛ fʋ kõ-yẽlga la bʋraanɛ.

ref: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
text: La fʋ pãŋa, botɩ sɛba n bẽ'erɩ yẽ ĩmã'asʋm, dee tɩ tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ ẽŋɛ, Azezi n de fʋ tõntõn-sõŋɔ la yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.»

ref: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
text: Ban pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ ba'asɛ la, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba lagsɛ la mĩim mɛ. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ba wuu pãŋa, tɩ ba tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bʋraanɛ. Sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la, pʋtɩ ban tarɩ sɛla wuu la taaba

ref: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
text: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n daan bɔ Azezi sɩra la lagɩm tara la yɛm ayɩla, la sũure ayɩla. Tɩ ba nẽra baa ayɩla ka yetɩ, ẽŋa n tarɩ sɛla la de la ẽŋa mẽŋa mã'a bõnɔ, la de la ba wuu bõnɔ.

ref: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
text: La Tõntõnɩba la tɔgrɩ Zuudãana Azezi kũm vo'ore yelle la pãŋa zozo'e, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔna ba wuu zuo.

ref: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
text: Bʋraa n daan boe tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Ananiyasɩ, tɩ a pɔga yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Asafɩɩra. Tɩ ba koose ba va'am,

ref: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
text: tɩ a looge ligri la basɛba sʋgɛ, dee tarɩ lig-sɛba n weege la tarɩ ka bɔ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la. La ẽn ẽŋɛ bɛla la, a pɔga la mi mɛ.

ref: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Ananiyasɩ, bẽm tɩ Asũtãana kẽ fʋ sũurum, tɩ fʋ parɩm bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, looge fʋ va'am na tɩ fʋ koose la ligri basɛba sʋgɛ?

ref: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
text: Fõn nãn ka koose nɩ ẽ na, a de la fʋ bõnɔ. Fõn koose la mẽ, fʋ tã wʋn ẽŋɛ fʋ yɛm n boorɩ se'em mɛ. Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ fõn tẽ'esɛ fʋ sũurum ẽŋɛ yel-ẽna wã? Fʋ ka parɩm bɔ nẽrsaala, fʋ parɩm bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
text: Ananiyasɩ n wõm yetɔg-ẽna la, a lui la tẽŋa ki. La sɛba wuu n wõm bõn-ẽna la, dabeem n kẽ ba.

ref: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
text: Tɩ budib-paalsɩ isge dɩkɛ ẽ põbɛ, zɛ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ.

ref: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
text: Tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe masɛ wʋ karf-atã, tɩ a pɔga mẽ wa'am, la a ka bãŋɛ bõn-sɛka n ẽŋɛ la.

ref: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ soke ẽ yetɩ: «Yele ma, yãma n koose ya va'am na, ligri la wuu n wãna bɩɩ?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe! Ba wuu n bɛla.»

ref: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla tɩ ya makɛ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɩsɛ? Bãŋɛ tɩ sɛba n laagɛ fʋ sɩra la boe la zanõrʋm. Ba wʋn zɛ fʋ mẽ yese.»

ref: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text: Tɩ pɔka la kɔ'ɔn lui tẽŋa ki Apɩyɛɛrɩ nẽŋam. Tɩ budib-paalsɩ la kẽ'em wa yẽ tɩ a ki mɛ, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka laagɛ yɛglɛ a sɩra la.

ref: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
text: Tɩ dabeem daan kẽ Azezi nẽrba la wuu, la sɛba wuu n wõm bõn-ẽna la. Azezi Tõntõnɩba la tõnnɩ la tõon-yãlma

ref: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Tɩ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la tõm tõon-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ zozo'e nẽrba la wuu sʋkam. Tɩ sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la lagsɛ taaba la yɛm ayɩla, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ «Asalomõ võnana» la.

ref: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
text: Tɩ sɛba n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la ka sakɛ lagɩm na ba, la nẽrba pẽgrɩ Azezi nẽrba la mɛ zozo'e.

ref: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
text: La bʋraasɩ la pɔgsɩ zozo'e n daan bɔ'ɔrɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra pa'asra a nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam.

ref: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
text: Halɩ tɩ nẽrba tara bã'adõma sẽnna gã'ata sorɔɔrɔ zuto bõn-gã'ala pʋam, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sãn wa dɔla bilam zẽ'a tɔlla, a yinyisŋa lile ba tɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
text: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e yese tẽn-sɛba n gilge Zerizalɛm na tarɩ bã'adõma, la sɛba tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ dolɩ ba wa'am. La ba wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ. Azezi Tõntõnɩba nãmsgɔ yelle

ref: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la sɛba n de Saduse dõma n boe a zẽ'am na, daan isge ɩta sũure,

ref: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
text: yõgɛ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deego pʋam.

ref: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka sẽŋɛ yʋ'ʋŋɔ ka yo'oge yʋ'a deego la nõorɛ, tarɩ ba yese yẽŋa ka yele ba yetɩ:

ref: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
text: «Sẽŋɛ-ya Wẽnde-kãtʋm na ka tɔgɛ vɔm paalga wã yelle wuu bɔ nẽrba la.»

ref: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
text: Ban wõm bɛla la, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na bewiine zãsna nẽrba. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la, la sɛba n boe a zẽ'am na, daan wi Zifdõma sarɩyaditba, la ba kẽma wuu tɩ ba lagsɛ taaba. Tɩ ba tõm nẽrba yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ yʋ'a deem na ka tarɩ Tõntõnɩba la wa'am.

ref: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
text: La ban tõm sɛba la n paagɛ yʋ'a deem na, ba ka yẽ ba, leme wa yele ba yetɩ:

ref: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
text: «Tõma n paagɛ la, tõma yẽ tɩ yʋ'a deego la kʋlɩnsɩ la pagɛ mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ sɛba n gu'uri la zɛ deego la nõam, tɩ tõma yo'oge ka yẽ nẽra nẽra.»

ref: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
text: La Wẽnde-kãtɛ gu'urba nẽŋadãana, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma la n wõm yel-ẽna la, ba ze la yɛrɩ, tẽ'esra yel-ẽna n de yel-sɛka.

ref: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
text: Tɩ nẽra wa'am wa yele ba yetɩ: «Selse-ya, bʋra-sɛba tɩ ya kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deego pʋam na ze la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na zãsna nẽrba.»

ref: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
text: Tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la gu'urba nẽŋadãana, la a nẽrba sẽŋɛ ka yõgɛ ba wa'am, dee ka le dãam ba, ba zotɩ mɛ tɩ nẽrba la wʋn lobe ba la kuga.

ref: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
text: Ban tarɩ ba wa'am wa ze'ele ba nẽŋam na, kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la soke ba yetɩ:

ref: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
text: «Tõma daan ka yele ya yetɩ, ya da le zãsʋm nẽrba la nẽr-ẽna wã yʋ'ʋrɛ? La bɩsɛ-ya yãma n ẽŋɛ se'em na, yãma zãsŋɔ saagɛ Zerizalɛm wuu mɛ, dee bɔɔra tɩ ya dɩkɛ bʋra-ẽna kũm taalɛ dɔglɛ tõma zuto.»

ref: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ la a Tõntõn-taaba la lerge ba yetɩ: «La masɛ mɛ tɩ tõma sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ gãnna nẽrsaalba nõorɛ.

ref: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
text: Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la n vo'oge Azezi tɩ ya daan ka lablɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ la.

ref: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
text: Nawẽnnɛ zẽkɛ ẽ mɛ zĩ'ire a zʋʋgɔ bɔba, botɩ a dɛna nẽŋam dãana la Fãagra bɔ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma sore tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

ref: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
text: Tõma de la bõn-bãna kaset-dõma, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a tarɩ bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba n sakrɩ ẽ na, mẽ de la kaset-dãana.»

ref: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
text: Ban wõm yetɔg-ẽna wã la, ba sũure yĩige mɛ halɩ tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ba.

ref: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
text: Tɩ bʋraa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agamalɩyɛlɩ, a de la Farisi nẽra, a zãsnɩ la nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ yɛla, a de la nẽra tɩ ba wuu nannɩ. Tɩ a isge ze'ele nẽrba la n lagsɛ la tẽŋasʋka, bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba yese Tõntõnɩba la yẽŋa tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe. Tɩ ba yese ba.

ref: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, guuse ya mẽŋa la yãma n yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ nẽr-bãna wã se'em na.

ref: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
text: La bɛla poorʋm, Azudaasɩ n yese Galile mẽ daan isge mɛ nẽrba kãalgɔ wakatɛ, tɩ nẽrba basɛba dɔla ẽ. Tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan dolɩ ẽ na mẽ wɩrgɛ.

ref: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, lɛɛlɛ wã mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya basɛ nẽr-bãna, da kãn ẽŋɛ ba sɛla sɛla. La sãn dɛna nẽrsaalba pʋtẽ'era, la ba tõoma, la wʋn wa lebge zãŋa.

ref: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
text: La, la sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ, ya kãn tã'agɛ sã'am ba. Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa, tɩ ya da wa yẽ tɩ ya zɛbrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ.» Tɩ ba sakɛ a yetɔga la,

ref: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
text: dee wi Tõntõnɩba la, tɩ ba wa'am. Tɩ ba botɩ ba wẽ ba, dee yele ba yetɩ: «Da le tɔgɛ-ya Azezi yɛla bɔ nẽrba.» Dee yãŋa basɛ ba yetɩ, ba fõrgɛ.

ref: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
text: Tɩ Tõntõnɩba la yese sarɩyadia zẽ'am na sũ-yẽlga, ban masɛ tɩ ba yẽ nãmsgɔ Azezi ĩyã la.

ref: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: La daarɛ woo ba boe la Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, la ba yiɛ pʋam, ken zãsna nẽrba, dee mõola Azezi kõ-yẽlga yetɩ, ẽŋa n de Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.

ref: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
text: La dabs-bãma wakatɛ karẽnbiisi la daan pa'asrɩ mɛ zɔ'ɔra, tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n tɔgrɩ Grɛkɩ yetɔga la daan yõnna la Zifdõma nẽr-sɛba n tɔgrɩ Ebre yetɔga la yetɩ, ban sõŋrɩ nẽrba la dia daarɛ woo la, ba ka bɔ'ɔrɩ bãma pɔkõpa la wʋ lan masɛ.

ref: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Tõntõnɩba pia la ayi la wi karẽnbiisi la wuu, yele ba yetɩ: «La ka masɛ tɩ tõma basɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga mõolgɔ dee pʋta dia.

ref: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
text: Bɛla tɩ sɔbiisi, bɩsɛ ya pʋam looge bʋraasɩ bayopɔɩ n de nẽr-sõma, dee dɛna yɛm dõma, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ba lɛ zozo'e, tɩ tɩ dɩkɛ tõon-ẽna wã ẽŋɛ ba nu'usum.

ref: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
text: Dee tɩ tõma wɩsgɛ tɩ mẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ, la a yetɔga la mõolgɔ.»

ref: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
text: Tɩ ban yele se'em na daan ẽŋɛ nẽrba la wuu sũure yẽlʋm. Tɩ ba looge A'etɩɛnɩ n bɔ Azezi sɩra zozo'e tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ẽ lɛ, la Afilipi, la Aprokɔɔrɩ, la Anikanɔɔrɩ, la Atimõ, la Aparmɛnasɩ, la Anikolasɩ n yese Antɩyɔsɩ dee dɔla Zifdõma pʋ'ʋsgɔ sore.

ref: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
text: Tɩ ba tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ Tõntõnɩba la zẽ'am, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ba nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba.

ref: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
text: La Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la daan saarɩ zẽ'esɩ mɛ pa'asra, tɩ karẽnbiisi la kãllɛ mẽ pa'asra Zerizalɛm pʋam zozo'e. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma zozo'e daan bɔ'ɔra Azezi sɩra. A'etɩɛnɩ yõkrɛ

ref: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
text: La A'etɩɛnɩ n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ la a pãŋa zozo'e la, daan tõnnɩ la tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.

ref: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma basɛba n daan de yamsɩ dee yãŋa to'oge ba mẽŋa, tɩ ba wi'ira ba wẽndeego tɩ soe m mẽŋa dõma wẽndeego, n yese Sirɛnɩ, la Alɛgsãndri, la Zifdõma basɛba n yese Silisi, la Azi tẽnsɩ n wẽ'erɩ nõke'ene la A'etɩɛnɩ.

ref: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ A'etɩɛnɩ yɛm tɩ a tɔgra, tɩ ba ka tã'agɛ lerge ẽ.

ref: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
text: Tɩ ba ɛ nẽrba botɩ ba dõrɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma wõm tɩ a tɔgɛ la Amoyisi la Nawẽnnɛ tɔg-be'ero.»

ref: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
text: Ban ẽŋɛ bɛla la, ba botɩ nẽrba la, la ba kẽma, la ba lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma la wuu sũure isge mɛ, tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ tarɩ sẽŋɛ sarɩyaditba zẽ'am,

ref: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
text: dee botɩ nẽrba parɩm bɔ ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã tɔgrɩ la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ tɩ a bɔ Amoyisi la tɔg-be'ero ka gɔ'ɔra.

ref: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
text: Tõma wõm tɩ a yetɩ, Azezi n yese Nazarɛtɩ la wʋn sã'am Wẽnde-kãtɛ la mɛ, dee teege ɩr-sɛba tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn pa'alɛ tɩ yaabdõma yetɩ tɩ ɩta la.»

ref: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
text: Tɩ sarɩyaditba la wuu n zĩ la bɩsra A'etɩɛnɩ zĩi, yẽ tɩ a nẽŋa ãn wʋ malɛka nẽŋa la.

ref: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana soke A'etɩɛnɩ yetɩ: «La de la bɛla bɩɩ?»

ref: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
text: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi la m sɔdõma, selse-ya. Nawẽnnɛ n de na'am kãtɛ dãana la yʋʋn puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ tɩ yaaba Abraham, ẽn yʋʋn nãn boe Mezopotami nãn ka sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire Haran tẽŋam na,

ref: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
text: a yʋʋn yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Yese dee basɛ fʋ tẽŋa la fʋ yir-dõma, dee sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fʋ la.›

ref: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
text: Tɩ Abraham yese Kalde dõma tẽŋam sẽŋɛ ka zĩ'ire Haran. La a sɔ kũm poorʋm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ le botɩ a yese sẽŋɛ tẽn-sɛka tɩ ya boe mĩ zĩna wã pʋam.

ref: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka bɔ ẽ zẽ'a tẽŋa la pʋam baa fẽe, dee bĩŋe nõorɛ yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn bɔ ẽ tẽn-ẽŋa, la a kũm poorʋm tɩ a yɩɩsɩ wʋn wa sɔna tẽŋa la. La wakat-ẽŋa a yʋʋn nãn ka dɔgɛ bia.

ref: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele ẽ na wãna: ‹Fʋ yɩɩsɩ la wʋn wa sẽŋɛ tẽŋa ayẽma ka kẽ'era mĩ wa lebge yamsɩ bilam, la ba wʋn nãmsɛ ba mɛ yʋʋma kɔbsnaasɩ.

ref: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
text: La mam wʋn wa sɩbgɛ so'olʋm sɛka n botɩ ba dɛna yamsɩ la mɛ. La bɛla poorʋm tɩ ba wʋn yese bilam leme wa pʋ'ʋsɛ mam kalam.›

ref: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la Abraham yetɩ, ẽŋa la a yɩɩsɩ wuu wãta ba budimto tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ ba de la ẽŋa nẽrba. Tɩ Abraham yʋʋn wa dɔgɛ A'izakɩ, tɩ dabsa anii daarɛ tɩ a wã ẽ. Tɩ A'izakɩ mẽ wa dɔgɛ Azakɔbɩ ẽŋɛ bɛla. Tɩ Azakɔbɩ mẽ wa dɔgɛ tɩ yaabdõma pia la ayi la mẽ ẽŋɛ bɛla.

ref: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
text: Tɩ tɩ yaabdõma la yʋʋn yũuna la ba yɩbga Azozɛfʋ, koose ẽ bɔ Ezipti dõma. La Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn boe la ẽ lɛ mɛ,

ref: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
text: yʋʋn yese ẽ a nãmsgɔ wuu pʋam, dee bɔ ẽ yel-sõnnɛ, la yɛm Afaarõ n de Ezipti naba la nẽŋam. Tɩ naba la botɩ Azozɛfʋ dɛna gɔmna sɔna Ezipti tẽŋa, la a yire wuu bɩsra.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
text: Tɩ kɔm kãtɛ wa'am Ezipti, la Kanaan so'olʋm halɩ tɩ la dɛna toogo. Tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn ka tara dia.

ref: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
text: Tɩ Azakɔbɩ yʋʋn wõm tɩ dia boe Ezipti, tɩ a tõm tɩ yaabdõma tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka da.

ref: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
text: La a le tõm ba mɛ nõor-ayi pʋam, tɩ Azozɛfʋ botɩ a kẽendõma la bãŋɛ ẽ. Tɩ Afaarõ mẽ bãŋɛ Azozɛfʋ sɔ yir-dõma yelle.

ref: Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were
text: Tɩ Azozɛfʋ tõm tɩ ba wi a sɔ Azakɔbɩ, la a yir-dõma wuu tɩ ba wa'am. Ba kãllɛ yʋʋn paagɛ nẽrba pisyopɔɩ la anuu.

ref: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
text: Tɩ Azakɔbɩ isge sẽŋɛ Ezipti. Bilam tɩ ẽŋa la tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn ki.

ref: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
text: Tɩ ba zɛ ba sẽŋɛ Sisɛm ka laagɛ ba yɔ-sɛka tɩ Abraham yʋʋn da la ligri A'emõorɩ kɔma zẽ'am na.

ref: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la Abraham wakatɛ n yʋʋn lɛm na, nẽrba la yʋʋn yɩrgɛ mɛ zo'oge Ezipti.

ref: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
text: Halɩ tɩ nẽra ayɩla wa di Ezipti na'am ka mina Azozɛfʋ yelle.

ref: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
text: Tɩ naba la yʋʋn ẽŋɛ yɛm wẽeŋa la tõma yaabdõma nãmsɛ tɩ buuri la, pẽrgɛ ba botɩ ba basɛ ba bilɛɛsɩ tɩ ba ki.

ref: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
text: Wakat-ẽŋa tɩ ba yʋʋn dɔgɛ Amoyisi tɩ a dɛna bi-sõŋɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam. Tɩ ba uge ẽ a sɔ yire wõr-sɩtã,

ref: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
text: dee tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka basɛ, tɩ Afaarõ bipugla yʋʋn dɩkɛ ẽ uge, tɩ a lebge wʋ a mẽŋa bia.

ref: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
text: Tɩ Amoyisi zãsʋm Ezipti dõma yɛm wuu, yʋʋn dɛna nẽr-kãtɛ a yetɔga la a tõoma wuu pʋam.

ref: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
text: yãŋa yẽ tɩ Ezipti nẽra nãmsrɩ ba nẽra ayɩla. Tɩ a po'oge tɩ nẽra la, wẽ Ezipti nẽra la kʋ lerge sãnnɛ.

ref: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
text: A tẽ'esrɩ tɩ a buuri la wʋn bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ a botɩ ẽŋa fãagɛ ba Ezipti dõma nu'usum, la ba ka bãŋɛ.

ref: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a le yẽ a buuri nẽrba bayi tɩ ba zɛbrɩ la taaba. Tɩ a pãsɛ ba, bɔɔra tɩ ba sũure mã'agɛ, soke ba yetɩ: ‹M sɩrdõma, ya de la sɔbiisi, la bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ ya zɛbra la taaba?›

ref: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
text: Tɩ sɛka n ɩtɩ a tadãana be'em na yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Ãne n bɔ fʋ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ sɔna tɩ bɩsra, dee dɛna tɩ sarɩyadita?

ref: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
text: Fʋ boorɩ tɩ fʋ kʋ mam wʋ fõn zaam kʋ Ezipti nẽra se'em na?›

ref: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
text: Yetɔg-ẽna ĩyã tɩ Amoyisi zoe sẽŋɛ Madiyã ka kẽ'era. Bilam tɩ a dɔgɛ dayɔɔsɩ bayi.

ref: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
text: La yʋʋma pinaasɩ poorʋm, daarɛ ayɩla a boe la weem lɛm Sinayi tãŋa, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka ze'ele tũntuugo tɩ bugum boe mĩ dita pʋam puke a mẽŋa bilam, tɩ a yẽ ẽ.

ref: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
text: Amoyisi n yẽ ẽ na, la di'ige ẽ mɛ, tɩ a lɛm tɩ a bɩsɛ. Tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele ẽ yetɩ:

ref: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
text: ‹Mam de la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ fʋ yaabdõma Abraham, la A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la.› Tɩ Amoyisi gõgra dee ka tã'agɛ bɩsɛ.

ref: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana la yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Pirge fʋ tagra la, tɩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ fʋ ze la de la mam zẽ'e-sõŋɔ.

ref: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
text: Mam yẽ m nẽrba la n boe Ezipti nãmsgɔ la mɛ, dee wõm ba fablɩgɔ la, tɩ mam sigum tɩ m fãagɛ ba. Bɛla ĩyã mam yetɩ m tõm fʋ la Ezipti.› »

ref: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
text: Tɩ A'etɩɛnɩ ken yetɩ: «Amoyisi ẽna wã tɩ ba yʋʋn zagsɛ ẽ yele ẽ yetɩ: ‹Ãne n bɔ fɔ nõorɛ tɩ fʋ sɔna tɔ, dee dɛna tɩ sarɩyadita?› Ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn botɩ a malɛka tɔgɛ bɔ ẽ tũntuum na. Ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm tɩ a sɔna a nẽrba la dee yese ba nãmsgɔ la pʋam.

ref: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
text: Ẽŋa n tõm tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa Ezipti tẽŋam yese ba bilam, dee tõm tõon-yãlma tarɩ ba pãrgɛ mõg-mõlga la, dee bɔna la ba weego la pʋam yʋʋma pinaasɩ.

ref: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
text: Amoyisi ẽŋa n yʋʋn yele Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ nẽra ayɩla ze'ele ya buuri pʋam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa bɔ yãma wʋ ẽŋa n botɩ mam dɛna a nõtɔ'ɔsa se'em na.›

ref: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
text: La tɩ yaabdõma n yʋʋn lagsɛ weem na, la de la ẽŋa n ze'ele tɩ yaabdõma, la malɛka n tɔgɛ la ẽ Sinayi tãŋa zuo tẽŋasʋka, to'oge Nawẽnnɛ vɔm yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ tɔ.

ref: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
text: Tɩ tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn ka bɔɔra tɩ ba sakɛ a nõorɛ, yʋʋn zagsɛ ẽ, dee tẽ'esɛ ba sũurum bɔɔra tɩ ba lebe Ezipti.

ref: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
text: Bɛla tɩ ba yãŋa yagbɛ na-biire kãabra ẽ tɩ a dɛna ba bagrɛ, ɩta sũ-yẽlga la ba mẽŋa nu'usi n ẽŋɛ sɛla la.

ref: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bure a poore bɔ ba, dee basɛ ba tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsra wõrbiɛ n boe saazuum, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam yetɩ: ‹Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba, yãma tẽ'esrɩ tɩ la de la mam tɩ ya yʋʋn kʋʋrɩ dũnsi, la kãabgɔ basɛba weem yʋʋma pinaasɩ bɔ'ɔra la bɩɩ?

ref: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
text: Ayɛɩ, ya yʋʋn zɛɛrɩ la ya bagrɛ Amolɔkɩ fuu-deego, la ya bagrɛ Arefã wõrbiire, bõn-bãma tɩ ya yʋʋn ẽŋɛ pʋ'ʋsra. Bɛla ĩyã mam wʋn botɩ ba pe'ege ya sẽŋɛ zãagɛ halɩ gãŋɛ Babilɔnɩ tẽŋam.›

ref: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
text: Tɩ sɔ-yaabdõma yʋʋn tarɩ la fuu-deego weem n pa'alɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boe ba zẽ'am. Ba yʋʋn ẽŋɛ ẽ tɩ a ãna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n tɔgɛ bɔ Amoyisi yetɩ, ba ẽŋɛ ẽ tɩ a ãna wʋ ẽn yẽ a wõnnɛ se'em na.

ref: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
text: Tɩ yaabdõma yʋʋn to'oge fuu-deego la ba mẽ sɔdõma zẽ'am, zɛ ẽ doose la Azoze wa'am wa zɛbɛ to'oge so'olʋm sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn dige nẽrba tẽŋa la pʋam tɩ ba yese ba nẽŋam na. Tɩ fuu-deego la weege bɔna wa paagɛ naba Adavidi wakatʋm.

ref: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
text: Adavidi yʋʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, dee sose ẽ yetɩ, a bɔ ẽ sore tɩ a mẽ pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego bɔ ẽ tɩ a bɔna la Azakɔbɩ yɩɩsɩ lɛ.

ref: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
text: La, la de la a bia Asalomõ n yãŋa n mẽ deego la bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
text: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ, saazuum de la mam na'am bĩmbĩnne, dee tɩ dũnia dɛna mam nãma dɔglgɔ zẽ'a. Bẽm deego tɩ ya wʋn mẽ bɔ mam? La bẽm vo'osgo zẽ'a tɩ ya yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ bɔ mam?

ref: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
text: Yãma yʋʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ mɛ malɛkadõma zẽ'am. La ya ka sakɛ lɔɔ la.» Ba lobe A'etɩɛnɩ la kuga kʋ

text: Ban wõm bɛla la, ba sũnsõa yĩige mɛ, tɩ ba ɔbra yẽna.

ref: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.

ref: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire yo'oge mɛ, tɩ Nẽrsaala Dayʋa la zɛ Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ bɔba.»

ref: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
text: Tɩ ba lɩ ba tʋba kaasɛ tagurga, dee lagɩm zoe paagɛ yõkɛ ẽ,

text: ve'ege ẽ tẽŋa la pʋam yese ka lɔbra ẽ la kuga tɩ ba kʋ. Sɛba n lobrɩ ẽ na kuga la yɛɛsɛ ba futo gu'ule la budib-polle yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Asolɩ.

ref: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
text: La Asolɩ daan kɔ'ɔn nãmsra Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la mɛ bɔɔra tɩ a sã'am ba wuu, kẽ'era yiɛ pʋam yõgra bʋraasɩ la pɔgsɩ botɩ ba tara ba sẽnna yʋ'ʋra. Afilipi mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga Samaari

ref: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.

ref: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.

ref: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.

ref: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ la Azã daan tɔgɛ Zuudãana Azezi yetɔga la pa'alɛ nẽrba mɛ, dee daan lɛbra Zerizalɛm. La sore zuo, ba mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga Samaari tẽnsɩ zozo'e pʋam mɛ. Afilipi la Etiopi bʋraa yelle

text: Tɩ a isge sẽŋɛ. La Etiopi bʋraa n daan dolɩ sore la, a de la nẽr-kãtɛ, a gu'uri la Etiopi pɔg-naba Akãndase bõntarsõm. A daan sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ,

text: La isge kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ ba wʋn yele fo fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em.»

text: Mam wʋn pa'alɛ ẽ ẽn wʋn nãmsɛ se'em mam yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.»

ref: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.

ref: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”

ref: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.

text: Tɩ Asolɩ ken nãmsra Azezi karẽnbiisi la, yetɩ a kʋ ba, sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana zẽ'am

ref: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.

ref: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Tɩ Asolɩ lerge yetɩ: «Zuudãana, fõn de la ãne?» Tɩ kõa la lerge yetɩ: «Mam de la Azezi, sɛka tɩ fʋ nãmsrɩ la.

text: Tɩ bʋra-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na zɛ yɛrɩ, ka yele se'ere. Ba wõm kõa la mɛ, dee ka yẽ nẽra.

ref: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.

ref: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.

ref: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Tɩ Ananiyasɩ sẽŋɛ ka kẽ yire la pʋam dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ Asolɩ zuo yele ẽ yetɩ: «M sɔbia Asolɩ, fõn daan boe sore zuo sẽnna tɩ Zuudãana Azezi puke a mẽŋa pa'alɛ fʋ la, ẽŋa n tõm mam fʋ zẽ'am tɩ m botɩ fʋ nini le yẽta, dee tɩ fʋ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa.»

ref: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
text: dee tɩ a di dia, tɩ a pãŋa le wa'am. Asolɩ mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Damaasɩ tẽŋa pʋam Asolɩ daan weege Damaasɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la zẽ'am na dabsa fẽe,

ref: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
text: darɛ kẽ Zifdõma wẽndetʋm mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga yetɩ, Azezi de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa.

ref: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
text: Tɩ la di'ige sɛba wuu n wõm na. Tɩ ba yetɩ: «Dagɩ bʋra-ẽna wã n daan boe Zerizalɛm kɔ'ɔn nãmsra nẽr-sɛba n pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ la? A nõo ka wa'am kalam tɩ a yõgɛ ba lʋ mɛ tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am?»

ref: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
text: La dabsa faa n pa'asɛ tɩ Zifdõma lagɩm ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla yetɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

ref: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
text: Tɩ Asolɩ daan bãŋɛ ban bãm yetɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na. Ba daan gu'uri tẽŋa la yansɩ la wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ, bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

ref: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
text: Tɩ daarɛ ayɩla yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ a karẽnbiisi daan botɩ a sige pɩ'ɩgɔ pʋam, tɩ ba dɩkɛ ẽ sike lalga poorʋm, tɩ a fõrgɛ. Asolɩ boe Zerizalɛm

ref: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
text: Tɩ Abarnabasɩ daan tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Azezi Tõntõnɩba la zẽ'am ka pa'alɛ ba Asolɩ n daan yẽ Zuudãana Azezi Damaasɩ sore zuo, la Zuudãana la n tɔgɛ ẽ se'em, la ẽn daan kãbɛ sũure mõolɛ Azezi yelle Damaasɩ se'em.

ref: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
text: Tɩ Asolɩ yãŋa daan bɔna la ba Zerizalɛm, ɛɛra tẽŋa la pʋam wuu, kãbɛ sũure mõola Zuudãana Azezi yelle.

ref: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
text: La a daan tɔgrɩ mɛ wẽ'era nõke'ene la Zifdõma sɛba n tɔgrɩ Grɛkɩ yetɔga la. Tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ.

ref: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
text: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la n bãŋɛ bɛla la, ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ la Sezaare, tɩ ba botɩ a sẽŋɛ Tarsɩ.

ref: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
text: Tɩ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n boe Zude, la Galile, la Samaari tẽnsʋm na, daan ka le yẽ nãmsgɔ. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sõŋra ba, tɩ ba kãllɛ pa'asra, tɩ ba zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ nã'asra Zuudãana la ba sũure wuu. A'ene yẽ ĩmã'asʋm

ref: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan eerɩ gilgra la zẽ'esɩ la wuu, tɩ daarɛ ayɩla tɩ a sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la n boe Lida la zẽ'am.

ref: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
text: La bilam a yẽ la bʋraa ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'ene, a daan bẽ mɛ kɔɔrʋm gã yʋʋma anii.

ref: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «A'ene, Azezi-krisi bɔ fʋ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ. Isge vaagɛ fʋ bõn-gã'ala la bĩŋe.» Tɩ a isge bilam zẽ'am.

ref: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
text: Tɩ Lida, la Saarõ tẽnsɩ nẽrba wuu yẽ tɩ bʋraa la yẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ, tɩ ba teege yɛm dɩkɛ ba mẽŋa bɔ Zuudãana. Atabita vo'ore

ref: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
text: La pɔka ayɩla n daan boe Zope dɛna Azezi karẽnbia tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Atabita. A daan tõnnɩ la tõon-sõma sõŋra nasdõma.

ref: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
text: La wakat-ẽŋa bã'a n yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a ki. Tɩ ba sɔ ẽ, tarɩ ẽ zom deego n boe deego zuum ka gã'arɛ ẽ.

ref: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
text: Zope ka zãagɛ la Lida. La karẽnbiisi la n wõm tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ boe bilam na, tɩ ba tõm bʋraasɩ bayi yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ ka wi ẽ yetɩ, a wa'am, a da yuuge.

ref: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ isge doose ba sẽŋɛ. La ban paagɛ la, ba tarɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ zom na deego la n boe deego zuo la. Tɩ pɔkõpa wuu lɛm Apɩyɛɛrɩ kɛlla, dee pa'ala fu-sɛba tɩ Adɔrkasɩ daan pɛ bɔ ba, ẽn daan vɔa la.

ref: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ botɩ ba wuu yese yẽŋa, dee ka dũma tẽŋa pʋ'ʋsɛ, dee wẽrgɛ kũm na bɔba yetɩ: «Atabita, isge.» Tɩ a pike nini yẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, isge zĩ'ire.

ref: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ gʋrɛ a nu'ugo, tɩ a isge ze'ele, tɩ a wi Azezi nẽrba la, la pɔkõpa la pa'alɛ ba tɩ a le vɔa mɛ.

ref: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
text: La Zope tẽŋa dõma wuu n daan wõm yel-ẽna wã la, ba nẽrba zozo'e n bɔ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra.

ref: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan yuuge Zope paagɛ la dabsa se'em. A daan boe la Asimõ n de gãnzapa la yire.

ref: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
text: Bʋraa n daan boe Sezaare, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Akɔrnɛlɩ. A daan de la sʋda-kɛ'ɛma, ba wi'iri sʋda-sɛba tɩ a de kɛ'ɛma ba pʋam na tɩ Itali dõma sʋdaasɩ.

ref: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
text: A daan de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma wuu daan nanna Nawẽnnɛ. La a daan sõŋrɩ la Zifdõma nasdõma zozo'e, dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ daarɛ woo.
