ref: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
text: Tɩ wẽnnɛ daan gelge, tɩ a yẽ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka wʋ a zãasʋm mɛ na, tɩ a wa'am wa kẽ a zẽ'am yele ẽ yetɩ: «Akɔrnɛlɩ!»

ref: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Tɩ a daan bɩsra malɛka la zĩi, tɩ dabeem tara ẽ, tɩ a yetɩ: «M dãana, fʋ yetɩ bo?» Tɩ a lerge yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, la fõn sõŋrɩ nasdõma la paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm mɛ, a tẽrɩ fʋ yelle mɛ.

ref: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
text: La nãnannɛ wã, tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope ka yele Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩ a wa'am.

ref: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
text: A de la sãana bɔna Asimõ n de gãnzapa la yire. A yire la lɛm na ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm.»

ref: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
text: La malɛka la n tɔgɛ la Akɔrnɛlɩ dee fõrgɛ la, tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ wi a tõntõnɩba bayi, la a sʋda-sõŋɔ ayɩla n tõnnɩ a tõoma, dee mẽ nanna Nawẽnnɛ,

ref: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
text: yãŋa tɔgɛ sɛla n ẽŋɛ la wuu pa'alɛ ba, dee tõm ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope.

ref: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
text: La beere n wiige, tɩ ba bɔna sore wɛ ka lɛm Zope, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ zom gɔsgɔ tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ, la masɛ wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka.

ref: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
text: Tɩ a kɔm daan dõnna, tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a di. La ban nãn boe mĩ ɩta dia tɩ ba bɔ ẽ na, tɩ a bɩsɛ yẽ wʋ a zãasʋm mɛ na,

ref: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
text: a daan yẽ tɩ saazuum n yo'oge, tɩ sɛla n ãn wʋ fu-kãtɛ n lʋ zẽ'esɩ sɩnaasɩ signa tẽŋa.

ref: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
text: La dũnsi buuri wuu, la tẽŋa bõn-vuula, la bõn-ẽgla buuri wuu n boe a pʋam.

ref: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Tɩ a wõm kõa n yele ẽ yetɩ: «Apɩyɛɛrɩ, isge kʋ di.»

ref: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, Zuudãana, mam ken ka di sɛla n de sisgo bõnɔ bɩɩ bõn-dẽgrɔ baa fẽe.»

ref: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Tɩ kõa la le yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ, a ãn sõŋa, da kãn yetɩ, a de la bõn-dẽgrɔ.»

ref: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
text: Bõnɔ la ẽŋɛ la nõor-atã, dee yãŋa lebe saazuum.

ref: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan zɛ yɛrɩ, tẽ'esra a pʋtẽ'erʋm bõn-ẽŋa tɩ a yẽ la võorɛ. La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ sɛba tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ daan tõm na soke Asimõ yire n boe zẽ'a, wa'am wa zɛ yaŋa nõorʋm,

ref: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
text: yãŋa wi soke yetɩ, Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ boe la kalam bɩɩ?

ref: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ ken tẽ'esra la bõnɔ la tɩ a yẽ la yelle, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bʋraasɩ batã n eerɩ fo, ban wãna.

ref: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
text: Isge sige doose ba, da sẽ'ena, tɩ mam n tõm ba.»

ref: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ sige bʋraasɩ la zẽ'a ka yele ba yetɩ: «Mam tɩ ya eerɩ la. Bẽm yelle ĩyã tɩ yãma wa'am kalam?»

ref: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
text: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Akɔrnɛlɩ, a de la sʋda-kɛ'ɛma, tɩ a ɩrgɔ masɛ, tɩ a nanna Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ Zifdõma wuu tɔgra a yelle sõŋa. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ, a botɩ fõn wa'am a yire wa yele ẽ fõn tarɩ yetɔg-sɛka la.»

ref: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ botɩ ba kẽ yire la gã'arɛ. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a isge dɔla ba wɛ, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba n boe Zope la kɔlgɛ ẽ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ.

ref: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
text: La dabsa ayi daarɛ tɩ ba paagɛ Sezaare. Tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ daan wi a yir-dõma la a sɩrdõma lagsɛ gu'ura ba.

ref: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ n paagɛ kẽ'era la, tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam nã'asɛ ẽ.

ref: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ gʋrɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Isge, mam mẽ de la nẽrsaala wʋ fõn na.»

ref: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
text: Tɩ ba sõsra la taaba kẽ'era, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yẽ nẽrba n lagsɛ zo'oge.

ref: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
text: Tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Ya mi tɩ Zifdõma sisri mɛ, ba ka lagnɩ la nẽra n de sãana, la ba ka kẽ'erɩ a yire. La Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ mam mɛ tɩ m da yetɩ, nẽrsaala de dẽgrɔ dãana, bɩɩ a ka narɩ m lagɩm na ẽ.

ref: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ fõn wi mam, tɩ mam wa'am ka zagsɛ. La mam boorɩ tɩ m bãŋɛ la bẽm yelle ĩyã tɩ yãma botɩ mam wa'am?»

ref: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
text: Tɩ Akɔrnɛlɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «La de la dabsa anaasɩ n wãna wẽngɛlŋa wʋ wakat-ẽna wã, tɩ mam daan boe m yire pʋ'ʋsra, tɩ bʋraa kɔ'ɔn yɛ fuugo n yẽgrɩ zɛ mam nẽŋam,

ref: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: dee yetɩ: ‹Akɔrnɛlɩ, Nawẽnnɛ to'oge fʋ pʋ'ʋsgɔ la, la a tẽrɩ fõn sõŋrɩ nasdõma se'em na mɛ.

ref: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
text: Bɛla, tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope ka wi Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩ a wa'am. A boe la Asimõ n de gãnzapa la yire. A yire la lɛm na ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm.›

ref: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam tõm nẽrba tɔtɔ tɩ ba wa'am wa wi fʋ tɩ fʋ wa'am. La fõn wa'am wã, fʋ ẽŋɛ sõŋa. Tɩ wuu yãŋa boe la Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam kalam tɩ tɩ selse bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yele fõn yetɩ, fʋ yele tõma la.» Apɩyɛɛrɩ mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ Akɔrnɛlɩ

ref: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yãŋa pɔsɛ yele yetɩ: «Sɩra sɩra, mam yãŋa bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka tũ'usri nẽrba.

ref: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
text: La buuri wuu pʋam nẽr-sɛka n nannɩ Nawẽnnɛ dee ɩta sɛla woo tɩ la masra, ẽŋa dãana n paagɛ a yɛm.

ref: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
text: Tɔ. Ya mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm a yetɔga la bɔ la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, a de la kõ-yẽlga n pa'alɩ tɩ tɩ wʋn tara sũ-mã'asʋm la Nawẽnnɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã, ẽŋa n de nẽrsaalba wuu Zuudãana.

ref: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
text: Yãma mi yel-sɛba wuu n daan ẽŋɛ Zude la, la daan pĩ'ilum na Galile Azã n daan mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga dee mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam na poorʋm.

ref: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
text: Ya ken mina Azezi n yese Nazarɛtɩ la, la Nawẽnnɛ n daan bɔ ẽ a Sɩa pãŋa se'em, tɩ a daan sẽŋɛ zẽ'esɩ wuu ẽŋɛ ɩr-sõma, botɩ Asũtãana n daan nãmsrɩ sɛba la wuu yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ daan boe la ẽ mɛ.

ref: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
text: Tõma yẽ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a daan tõm Zerizalɛm la Zifdõma so'olʋm na wuu mɛ, dee tɩ ba daan ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.

ref: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan vo'oge ẽ dabsa atã daarɛ, botɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ.

ref: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
text: La a ka dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ nẽrba la wuu, a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ la tõma sɛba n de kaset-dõma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan põn dẽŋɛ looge la. A vo'ore la poorʋm, tõma daan lagɩm na ẽ mɛ di dee yũ.

ref: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
text: A daan bɔ tõma nõorɛ yetɩ, tɩ mõolɛ ẽŋa yetɔga, dee yele nẽrba, tɩ la de la ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tɩ a di nẽr-vɔɔsɩ la kɩ'ɩndõma sarɩya.

ref: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
text: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la wuu yʋʋn tɔgɛ a yelle mɛ yetɩ, sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ a dãana tõon-be'ero taalɛ a yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.» Sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ n daan ken tɔgra yetɔg-bãna la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sige sɛba wuu n wõm yetɔga la zuto.

ref: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n daan botɩ a Sɩa la mẽ sige bu-zãŋsɩ la zuto la, la daan di'ige Zifdõma sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra n doose Apɩyɛɛrɩ wa'am na mɛ.

ref: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
text: Se'ere n soe la, ba daan wõm tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la bu-zãŋsɩ yetɔga pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã to'oge la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wʋ tõma n daan to'oge se'em na. Bɛla, ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ gu ba yetɩ, ba da to'oge ko'om misga?»

ref: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
text: Dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba mise ba ko'om pʋam la Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ. Tɩ ba sose Apɩyɛɛrɩ yetɩ, a weege bãma zẽ'am ẽŋɛ dabsa fẽe pa'asɛ.

ref: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
text: La Azezi Tõntõnɩba, la a karẽnbi-sɛba n boe Zude so'olʋm na wõm mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la mẽ sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga mɛ.

ref: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
text: La Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan lebe la Zerizalɛm, tɩ Zifdõma basɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la wẽ'era nõke'ene la ẽ,

ref: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
text: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bẽm n ẽŋɛ tɩ fõn kẽ lagɩm na bu-zãŋsɩ n ka wãtɩ la dita?»

ref: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yãŋa pa'alɛ ba yɛla la wuu n daan doose se'em na yetɩ:

ref: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
text: «Mam daan boe la Zope pʋ'ʋsra, yẽ sɛla wʋ zãasŋɔ la, tɩ sɛla la ze'ele saazuum sigra wʋ fu-kãtɛ n lʋ zẽ'esɩ sɩnaasɩ sige wa paam mam boe zẽ'a la.

ref: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
text: Tɩ mam bɩsra bõnɔ na zĩi, yẽ yire dũnsi, la we-dũnsi, la bõn-vuula, la bõn-ẽgla.

ref: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n yetɩ: ‹Apɩyɛɛrɩ, isge kʋ di.›

ref: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Tɩ mam yetɩ: ‹Ayɛɩ, Zuudãana, sɛla n de sisgo bõnɔ bɩɩ bõn-dẽgrɔ ken ka kẽ mam nõorʋm.›

ref: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Tɩ kõa la le ze'ele saazuum tɔgɛ bɔ mam yetɩ: ‹Bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ, a ãn sõŋa, da kãn yetɩ, a de la bõn-dẽgrɔ.›

ref: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
text: La daan ẽŋɛ la nõorɛ atã, dee viise lebe saazuum.

ref: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
text: Tɩ bʋraasɩ batã kɔ'ɔn paam yi-sɛka tɩ mam boe mĩ na. Ba daan tõm ba la Sezaare tẽŋam yetɩ, ba wa'am mam zẽ'am.

ref: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele mam yetɩ, m doose ba sẽŋɛ, m da sẽ'ena. Tɩ tɩ sɔbiisi bãna bayoobɩ wã kɔlgɛ mam, tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ ka kẽ Akɔrnɛlɩ yire.

ref: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
text: Tɩ a yele tõma tɩ ẽŋa daan yẽ la malɛka n ze ẽŋa yire, yele ẽŋa yetɩ: ‹Tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zope ka wi Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɩ a wa'am.

ref: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
text: Ẽŋa n wʋn tɔgɛ yetɔg-sɛba n wʋn botɩ fõn na fʋ yir-dõma wuu yẽ fãarɛ.›

ref: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
text: Mam n boe mĩ tɩ m tɔgɛ la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sige ba zuto wʋ ẽn daan sige tõma zuo se'em pɔspɔsɩ la.

ref: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Bɛla tɩ mam tẽegɛ Zuudãana n daan yetɩ: ‹Azã daan misri nẽrba la ko'om pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ wʋn mise ya a Sɩa pʋam.›

ref: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
text: La sãn dɛna tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba a Sɩa, wʋ ẽn daan bɔ tõma n bɔ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi sɩra se'em na, mam de la ãne wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ?»

ref: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
text: Ban wõm bɛla la, ba sĩm mɛ dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ mẽ yẽ sore mɛ tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero yẽ yõvore.» Antɩyɔsɩ nẽrba bɔ Azezi sɩra

ref: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
text: La A'etɩɛnɩ kũm na poorʋm, nẽr-sɛba n yẽ nãmsgɔ la wɩrgɛ mɛ, sẽŋɛ halɩ Fenisi, la Sipri, la Antɩyɔsɩ ka mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bɔ'ɔra Zifdõma mã'a.

ref: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
text: La ba bʋraasɩ basɛba daan ze'ele la Sipri, la Sirɛnɩ so'olʋm sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ mẽ ka mõolɛ bu-zãŋsɩ, tɔgɛ Zuudãana Azezi kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ba.

ref: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
text: La Zuudãana pãŋa daan boe la ba mɛ. Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e daan bɔ Zuudãana sɩra dɔla ẽ.

ref: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
text: La Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n boe Zerizalɛm na n wõm bɛla la, ba tõm na Abarnabasɩ yetɩ a sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ.

ref: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
text: La ẽn paagɛ yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ nẽrba la yel-sõnnɛ se'em na, a sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ. Tɩ a pa'alɛ ba yetɩ, ba keŋe ba mẽŋa dɔla Zuudãana la ba sũure wuu da basɛ.

ref: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
text: Se'ere n soe la, a daan de la nẽr-sõŋɔ, dee bɔ Azezi sɩra zo'oge, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ẽ. La nẽrba zozo'e n daan pa'asɛ dɔla Zuudãana.

ref: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
text: La Abarnabasɩ babsɛ mɛ sẽŋɛ Tarsɩ tɩ a ka ɛ Asolɩ.

ref: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
text: La ẽn yẽ ẽ na, a tarɩ ẽ mɛ wa'am Antɩyɔsɩ. La yʋʋnɛ tõrɛ tɩ ba lagsɛ la Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, dee zãsʋm nẽrba zozo'e. La de la Antɩyɔsɩ tẽŋam tɩ ba daan pɔsɛ wi karẽnbiisi la tɩ Krisi nẽrba.

ref: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
text: La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba daan ze'ele Zerizalɛm sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ.

ref: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
text: Tɩ ba nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agabisi isge ze'ele tɔgɛ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa yetɩ, kɔm kãtɛ n wʋn wa'am dũnia wuu zuo. La kɔm ẽŋa sɩrɩ wa'am mɛ na-keko Akloorɩ wakatʋm.

ref: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
text: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la yetɩ, nẽra woo looge ẽn tarɩ sɛla bɔ tɩ ba to'oge sõŋɛ Azezi nẽr-sɛba n boe Zude la.

ref: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
text: Tɩ ba daan ẽŋɛ bɛla dɩkɛ ligri la ẽŋɛ Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ nu'usum, tõm ba yetɩ, ba tarɩ sẽŋɛ ka bɔ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la kẽma n boe Zerizalɛm na.

ref: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
text: La masɛ la wakat-ẽŋa tɩ naba A'erɔdɩ daan nãmsra Azezi nẽrba basɛba,

ref: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
text: dee botɩ ba kʋ Azakɩ n de Azã sɔbia la sʋkɔbgɔ.

ref: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
text: La ẽn yẽ tɩ la ẽŋɛ Zifdõma sũure yẽlʋm mɛ na, a le botɩ ba yõkɛ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, wakat-sɛka tɩ Zifdõma diti borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la.

ref: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
text: La ẽn yõkɛ ẽ kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem na, a botɩ sʋdaasɩ pia la ayoobɩ n pʋɩ bana-banaasɩ gu'ura ẽ sɔɔsna taaba. A boorɩ tɩ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa n tole, dee tɩ a tarɩ ẽ yese nẽrba la nẽŋam ka di a sarɩya.

ref: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
text: Bɛla tɩ ba daan pagɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ basɛ yʋ'a deego la pʋam. Tɩ Azezi nẽrba la daan pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a ĩyã halɩ ka basɛ. Malɛka yese Apɩyɛɛrɩ yʋ'a deem basɛ

ref: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
text: Be-sɛka n wʋn wiige tɩ A'erɔdɩ yese Apɩyɛɛrɩ la, daar-ẽŋa yʋ'ʋŋɔ Apɩyɛɛrɩ daan gã la sʋdaasɩ bayi tẽŋasʋka gĩsra. Ba daan ba ẽ mɛ la bãnsɩ bayi, tɩ gu'urba la bɔna zanõrʋm gu'ura yʋ'a deego la.

ref: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
text: La haya! Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka kɔ'ɔn wa'am. Tɩ peelem kɔ'ɔn nẽegɛ deego la pʋam. Tɩ malɛka la wẽ Apɩyɛɛrɩ lʋgrɛ nẽegɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Isge tɔtɔ.» Tɩ bãnsɩ la ba'agɛ lui tẽŋa dee basɛ a nu'usi la.

ref: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
text: Tɩ malɛka la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Ga fʋ sagãnnɛ, dee pire fʋ tagra.» Tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɛla. Tɩ malɛka la le yele ẽ yetɩ: «Yɛ fʋ fuugo dee doose mam.»

ref: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
text: La ban gãŋɛ pɔspɔsɩ gu'urba la, la gu'urba bʋyi dõma la, ba yãŋa paagɛ la kut-yan-sɛka n yesrɩ kẽ'era tẽŋa la pʋam na, tɩ yaŋa la yo'oge a mẽŋa, tɩ ba yese yẽŋa yãŋa dɔla sore wɛ. Bilam zẽ'am tɩ malɛka la fõrgɛ dee basɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ.

ref: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yɛm yãŋa wa'am, tɩ a yetɩ: «Lɛɛlɛ wã mam bãŋɛ sɩra sɩra, tɩ Zuudãana n tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa fãagɛ mam A'erɔdɩ nu'usum, la bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ Zifdõma eerɩ tɩ ba ẽŋɛ mam na.»

ref: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
text: La ẽn bãŋɛ bõn-ẽna võorɛ la, a sẽŋɛ la Azã-markɩ ma Amaari yire. La nẽrba n lagsɛ bilam zo'oge bɔna mĩ pʋ'ʋsra.

ref: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
text: La ẽn paagɛ mĩ na, a wẽ la yaŋa nõorɛ, tɩ pɔsɔɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Arode wa'am tɩ a bɩsɛ.

ref: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
text: La ẽn bãŋɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ kõa la, tɩ a sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm tɩ a ka yo'oge yaŋa la, dee zoe kẽ ka yetɩ: «Apɩyɛɛrɩ ze yaŋa nõorʋm.»

ref: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
text: Tɩ ba yele pɔsɔɔ la yetɩ: «Fõn de la zɔlgɔ.» Tɩ a le yetɩ: «La de la ẽŋa.» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «La de la a sɩa».

ref: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ ken wẽ'era yaŋa la. Tɩ ba yo'oge yẽ tɩ la de la ẽŋa. Tɩ la di'ige ba.

ref: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
text: Tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ yɩbsɛ nu'usi pa'alɛ ba yetɩ, ba ãna sĩm. Dee tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba Zuudãana n ẽŋɛ se'em tarɩ ẽŋa yʋ'a deego la pʋam yese yẽŋa. Dee yele ba yetɩ: «Yele-ya Azakɩ, la tɩ sɔbiisi la, sɛla n ẽŋɛ la.» Dee yese sẽŋɛ zẽ'a ayẽma.

ref: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
text: La beere n wiige la, dãmŋɔ zozo'e n wa'am sʋdaasɩ la tẽŋasʋka, tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ ka bɔna.

ref: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
text: Tɩ A'erɔdɩ botɩ ba ɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, ka yẽ ẽ. Tɩ a di gu'urba la sarɩya, bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba kʋ ba. La bɛla poorʋm a yese Zude sẽŋɛ la Sezaare ka bɔna bilam. A'erɔdɩ kũm yelle

ref: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
text: La naba A'erɔdɩ sũure daan yĩige mɛ halɩ la Tiiri la Sidõ tẽnsɩ nẽrba. Tɩ ba ẽŋɛ nõorɛ ayɩla tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka yẽ ẽ, yãŋa lagsɛ taaba dẽŋɛ yõkɛ Ablastisi n bɩsrɩ naba A'erɔdɩ yire yelle la sɩa. Tɩ bãma la ẽŋa yãŋa doose taaba sẽŋɛ ka sose naba A'erɔdɩ yetɩ, a basɛ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm wa'am bãma la ẽŋa tẽŋasʋka. Se'ere n soe la, ba dia daan ze'etɩ la naba la tẽŋam sẽnna.

ref: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
text: La A'erɔdɩ looge la daarɛ yetɩ, ba tɔgɛ yetɔga la. La daarɛ la n paagɛ la, a yɛ la a na'am futo zĩ'ire a na'am kʋka zuo tɔgra yetɔg-kãra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la.

ref: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
text: Tɩ ba kaasɛ yetɩ: «Kõa wã de la Nawẽnnɛ kõa, la dagɩ nẽrsaala kõa.»

ref: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɛka botɩ bã'a yõkɛ A'erɔdɩ bilam zẽ'am, tɩ zũnzũa di ẽ, tɩ a ki, ẽn ka pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ dee pẽgɛ a mẽŋa la ĩyã.

ref: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
text: La Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ n daan tõm ba tõoma Zerizalɛm ba'asɛ la, ba daan tarɩ Azã-markɩ mɛ lebe Antɩyɔsɩ.

ref: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
text: Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ n boe Antɩyɔsɩ la pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la karẽnsãandõma n daan boe mĩ. Ba de la Abarnabasɩ, la Asimeyõ tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ nẽr-sabga, la Alusiyusi n ze'ele Sirɛnɩ, la Amanayɛnɩ tɩ ba daan uge ẽ la naba A'erɔdɩ, la Asolɩ.

ref: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
text: La daarɛ ayɩla ba daan lʋ la nõorɛ pʋ'ʋsra dee pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele ba yetɩ: «Looge-ya Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ tɩ ba tõm tõon-sɛka tɩ mam looge ba tɩ ba tõm na.»

ref: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
text: Tɩ ba lʋ nõorɛ dɩkɛ nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto pʋ'ʋsɛ, dee basɛ ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ. Asolɩ la Abarnabasɩ boe Sipri so'olʋm mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga

ref: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n tõm ba la, ba sẽŋɛ la Selesi tẽŋam ka kẽ õorŋɔ bilam sẽŋɛ Sipri so'olʋm tɩ ko'om gilge la.

ref: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
text: La ban paagɛ Salamini tẽŋam na, ba daan mõolɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga mɛ Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam. Ba daan tarɩ la Azã-markɩ tɩ a sõŋra ba.

ref: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
text: La ba daan doose la Sipri so'olʋm la pʋam ka paagɛ Pafɔsɩ. Bilam ba yẽ la tɩɩm dãana n parɩm tɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa. A de la Zifu, a yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Abar-zezi.

ref: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
text: A daan boe la tẽŋa la gɔmna Asɛryusi-polusi n de yɛm dãana la zẽ'am. Tɩ gɔmna la daan botɩ ba wi Abarnabasɩ la Asolɩ, a daan boorɩ tɩ a wõm na Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

ref: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
text: La tɩɩm dãana la yʋ'ʋrɛ la Grɛkɩ de la A'elimasɩ. A daan sɩ'ɩsrɩ Asolɩ la Abarnabasɩ yetɔga la mɛ, dee daan ɛɛra tɩ a gu gɔmna la tɩ a da sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la.

ref: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la Asolɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ ayẽma n de Apolɩ la pãŋa, tɩ a bɩsra tɩɩm dãana la zĩi,

ref: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
text: yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ tarɩ la yɛmkẽgsɩga la pã'asgɔ, fʋ de la Asũtãana bia, fʋ de la tõon-sõma wuu bɛ. Fõn dɩkrɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la mɛ tɩ a lɛbgra põmpɔrŋɔ.

ref: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã, bãŋɛ tɩ Zuudãana pãŋa wʋn botɩ fʋ fɔ mɛ. Fʋ kãn le yẽ peelem halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka.» La bilam zẽ'am tɩ tɩɩm dãana la kɔ'ɔn fɔ, ba'abra ɛɛra nẽra tɩ a ve'ege ẽ.

ref: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
text: La gɔmna la n yẽ lan ẽŋɛ se'em na, a bɔ yetɔga la sɩra mɛ, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana zãsŋɔ la daan kẽ a sũure mɛ. Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ boe Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Pisidi so'olʋm

ref: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
text: Apolɩ la a taaba la ze'ele la Pafɔsɩ kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Pɛrzɩ n boe Pãnfili so'olʋm pʋam na. Tɩ Azã-markɩ basɛ ba bilam dee lebe Zerizalɛm.

ref: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
text: Tɩ ba daan ze'ele Pɛrzɩ sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Pisidi so'olʋm pʋam na. Tɩ Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ paagɛ, tɩ ba kẽ ba wẽndeem zĩ'ire.

ref: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
text: La ban karẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ wẽndeego la nẽŋadõma tõm nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ sɔbiisi, ya sãn tara yetɔga n wʋn bɔ nẽrba la bʋraanɛ, ya tɔgɛ.»

ref: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
text: Tɩ Apolɩ isge yɩbsɛ la nu'ugo yele ba yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba, la yãma sɛba n po nanna Nawẽnnɛ, selse-ya.

ref: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
text: Nawẽnnɛ tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ wã, ẽŋa n yʋʋn looge tɩ yaabdõma, ban yʋʋn boe Ezipti na, ẽŋa n botɩ ba yɩrgɛ zo'oge. La ẽŋa n yʋʋn tõm pãŋa tõoma tarɩ ba yese Ezipti.

ref: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
text: Bɛla, guuse-ya tɩ lan gʋlsɛ se'em Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na da paagɛ ya.

ref: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
text: Dee yʋʋn sã'am buuri bayopɔɩ Kanaan so'olʋm pʋam, dee bɔ ba tẽŋa la tɩ ba sɔna.

ref: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn to'oge Asayuli na'am na, dee botɩ Adavidi dɛna naba. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn tɔgɛ Adavidi yelle yetɩ: ‹Mam yẽ la Azese bia Adavidi n ãn mam sũure yẽlʋm. Ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em na wuu.›

ref: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
text: La de la ẽŋa buuri nẽra ayɩla n de Azezi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a dɛna Fãagra bɔ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, wʋ ẽn yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

ref: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
text: Ẽŋa n daan nãn ka wa'am na, Azã n daan mõolɩ a wa'aŋɔ yelle bɔ Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba wuu yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero tɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam.

ref: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: La Azã n daan boe mĩ tɩ a tõm a tõoma ba'asɛ la, a soke ba yetɩ: ‹Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam de la ãne? Yãma n tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam de nẽr-sɛka la, mam dagɩ ẽŋa. La selse-ya, ẽŋa boe la mam poorʋm sẽna, mam ka masɛ tɩ m pirge a tagra.›

ref: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
text: M sɔbiisi, yãma n de Abraham yɩɩsɩ, la yãma sɛba n po nanna Nawẽnnɛ, la de la tõma ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm fãarɛ kõa wã.

ref: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
text: La Zerizalɛm nẽrba, la ba nẽŋadõma daan ka bãŋɛ Azezi n de nẽr-sɛka. La ba ka bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la tɩ ba karẽŋrɩ vo'osgo daarɛ wuu la võorɛ. La ban di Azezi sarɩya la, nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n yʋʋn yele se'em na ẽŋɛ mɛ.

ref: But God raised him from death!
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan botɩ a vo'oge.

ref: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
text: La a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa mɛ pa'alɛ sɛba n daan doose ẽ Galile sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm na dabsa faa, bãma n de a kaset-dõma Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba la tẽŋasʋka.

ref: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
text: La tõma mõolɩ la kõ-yẽlga la bɔ yãma: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma la,

ref: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
text: a ẽŋɛ bõn-ẽŋa bɔ la tõma n de ba yɩɩsɩ la. La de la a vo'oge Azezi mɛ. La ãn wʋ lan gʋlsɛ yõoma gõŋɔ yõoma bʋyi dãana la pʋam yetɩ: ‹Fʋ de la mam bia. Zĩna mam n lebge fʋ sɔ.›

ref: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn vo'oge ẽ tɩ a kãn põ'ogɛ. Bɛla tɩ a yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Mam wʋn bɔ ya bõn-sõma sɩra sɩra tɩ mam põn bĩŋe nõorɛ la Adavidi la.›

ref: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ la yele yõoma gõŋɔ pʋam zẽ'a ayẽma yetɩ: ‹Fʋ kãn basɛ fʋ pʋpeelem dãana tɩ a põ'ogɛ.›

ref: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
text: Wakat-sɛka tɩ Adavidi yʋʋn vɔa la, a tõm Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n boorɩ se'em mɛ, dee ki tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ a yaabdõma yɔɔm, tɩ a ĩyã põ'ogɛ.

ref: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge sɛka la ka põ'ogɛ.

ref: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
text: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, yãma masɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ la de la Azezi ĩyã tɩ tõma mõolɛ pa'alɛ yãma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Amoyisi lɔɔ yʋʋn ka tã wʋn basɛ tɩ ya yelle masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am,

ref: But everyone who has faith in Jesus is set free.
text: la nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ Azezi sɩra, a yelle masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am mɛ.

ref: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
text: La yetɩ: ‹Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ yãma sɛba n de sɩ'ɩsrɩba la, ya palẽŋa wʋn ẽkɛ mɛ tɩ ya ki. Se'ere n soe la, tõon-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn tõm ya zamãana wã pʋam na, ya kãn nan tɩ la wʋn sɩrɩ ẽŋɛ, baa nẽra sãn tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ya.› »

ref: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
text: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ n daan isge tɩ ba yese la, tɩ nẽrba la sose ba yetɩ, ba le wa'am vo'osgo daarɛ wa tɔgɛ yetɔg-sɛba tɩ ba tɔgɛ la.

ref: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
text: La ban yese tɩ ba fõrgɛ la, tɩ Zifdõma zozo'e, la nẽrba basɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma dee sakɛ dɔla Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ tɩ a bɔ Zifdõma, daan dɔla Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ. Tɩ Apolɩ dõma pa'alɛ ba yetɩ, ba mõrgɛ bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na pʋam.

ref: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
text: Tɩ vo'osgo daarɛ le paagɛ, tɩ nẽrba lagsɛ zozo'e wʋ la de la tẽŋa la nẽrba wuu la, tɩ ba selse Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

ref: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
text: La Zifdõma n yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n zo'oge la, tɩ ba ẽŋɛ sũure sɩ'ɩsɛ Apolɩ n tɔgɛ se'em na, dee tɔgra tɔg-be'ero.

ref: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan kãbɛ sũure tɔgɛ ba yetɩ: «Tõma wʋn dẽŋɛ nɩ tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga wã bɔ la yãma. La yãma n zagsɛ la, yãma pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka masɛ tɩ ya yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ. Bɛla ĩyã tõma wʋn basɛ yãma, dee sẽŋɛ la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am.

ref: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ bɔ tõma nõorɛ yetɩ: ‹Mam looge fo tɩ fʋ ãna wʋ peelem bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ, botɩ nẽrba yẽ fãarɛ dũnia zẽ'esɩ wuu.› »

ref: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
text: La bu-zãŋsɩ la n wõm bɛla la, ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ halɩ, tɩ ba pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la. La nẽr-sɛba n masɛ tɩ ba yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ la daan sakɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ.

ref: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana yetɔga la saagɛ tẽŋa la wuu.

ref: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma daan sa'asɛ pɔgsɩ n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, dee dɛna yʋ'ʋrɛ dõma, la tẽŋa la nẽrzuto, botɩ ba nãmsɛ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ dige ba ba tẽŋa la pʋam tɩ ba yese.

ref: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ pɩɩsɛ ba nãma tẽntõnnɔ basɛ, pa'alɛ tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, dee wɛ'ɛsa Ikonim.

ref: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
text: La Azezi karẽnbiisi la n boe Antɩyɔsɩ la daan tarɩ sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la ba.

ref: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
text: Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ n paagɛ Ikonim la, ba le kẽ la Zifdõma wẽndeego pʋam tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba. Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e, Zifdõma la nẽrba basɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra.

ref: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
text: La Zifdõma nẽr-sɛba n ka sakɛ yetɔga la, bãma n sa'asɛ bu-zãŋsɩ botɩ ba sũure sã'am la Azezi karẽnbiisi la.

ref: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
text: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan yuuge Ikonim mɛ, te'ege Zuudãana Azezi tɔgɛ a yetɔga la bʋraanɛ. Tɩ Zuudãana daan bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba tõm tõon-yãlma la yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ tɩ ẽŋa yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la de la sɩra.

ref: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
text: La tẽŋa la nẽrba daan welse mɛ, tɩ basɛba bɔna Zifdõma poorʋm, tɩ basɛba bɔna Azezi Tõntõnɩba la poorʋm.

ref: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
text: Tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ, la Zifdõma, la ba nẽŋadõma bɔɔra tɩ ba nãmsɛ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ lobe ba la kuga.

ref: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
text: La bʋraa n daan boe Listri ka tãna sẽnna. A ma dɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a dɛna la kɔɔrŋɔ.

ref: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
text: Tɩ a daan zẽa sɛlsra Apolɩ n tɔgrɩ la. Tɩ Apolɩ bɩsra ẽ zĩi, bãŋɛ tɩ a bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ tãna wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele ẽ yetɩ: «Isge ze'ele saazuo.» Tɩ kɔɔrŋɔ la vaagɛ isge sẽnna.

ref: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
text: La nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n yẽ Apolɩ n ẽŋɛ se'em na, tɩ ba tĩi tɔgɛ la Likoni tẽŋa yetɔga yetɩ: «Wẽna n lebge nẽrsaalba sigum tõma zẽ'am.»

ref: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
text: Tɩ ba daan wi'ira Abarnabasɩ tɩ Azeyusi, dee wi'ira Apolɩ tɩ A'ɛrmẽsɩ, ẽŋa n tarɩ yetɔga la wa'am na ĩyã.

ref: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
text: Ba daan tarɩ la deego tẽŋa la zanõ-bɔba kãabra Azeyusi. Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽema la tarɩ lɔlsɩ, la tɩɩsɩ puuro wa'am zanõrʋm na, tɩ ẽŋa la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la bɔɔra tɩ ba kãabɛ Abarnabasɩ la Apolɩ.

ref: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
text: La Tõntõnɩba Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ n wõm ban yetɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na, ba sũure sã'am mɛ, tɩ ba ãasʋm ba futo, dee zoe kẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ:

ref: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: «Nẽrba wã, bẽm tɩ ya yetɩ ya ẽŋɛ wãna? Tõma mẽ de la nẽrsaalba wʋ yãma n ãn se'em na, la tõma mõolɩ la kõ-yẽlga pa'ala yãma tɩ ya basɛ bõn-bãna wã n ka tarɩ yõorɔ, dee dɔla Nawẽnnɛ n de vɔm dãana. Ẽŋa n nãam saazuum la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la bõn-sɛba wuu n boe ba pʋam na.

ref: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
text: Kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn basɛ buuri wuu tɩ ba ɩta la ba mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em.

ref: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
text: La baa bɛla wuu, Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ sõŋa mɛ pa'alɛ ẽŋa n de sɛka. Ẽŋa n botɩ saaga niira bɔ'ɔra ya, tɩ ya bõnkɔɔla bɩta, tɩ dia bɔna zo'oge, tɩ ya sũure ãna yẽlʋm.»

ref: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
text: Baa la Apolɩ n tɔgɛ bɛla la, la ẽŋɛ la toogo bɔ ba, dee tɩ ba yãŋɛ gu nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tɩ ba ka kãabɛ ba.

ref: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
text: La Zifdõma n ze'ele Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Pisidi so'olʋm, la Ikonim wa'am wa sa'asɛ nẽrba la, botɩ ba lobe Apolɩ la kuga, vuuge ẽ tẽŋa la pʋam yese basɛ, dee tẽ'esɛ tɩ a ki mɛ.

ref: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
text: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la lagsɛ gilge ẽ, tɩ a isge kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ ẽŋa la Abarnabasɩ sẽŋɛ Dɛrbɩ. Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ lebrɩ Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Siiri so'olʋm

ref: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
text: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga Dɛrbɩ mɛ, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e lebge Azezi karẽnbiisi, dee tɩ ba yãŋa lebe Listri, la Ikonim, la Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Pisidi so'olʋm.

ref: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
text: La ba daan tɔgɛ pa'alɛ karẽnbiisi la mɛ, bɔ ba bʋraanɛ yetɩ, ba mõrgɛ bɔna Azezi sɩra la pʋam da basɛ. Dee yele ba yetɩ: «Tɩ wʋn yẽ la nãmsgɔ zozo'e dee kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.»

ref: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
text: La tẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ ba sẽŋɛ la, ba looge la kẽma Azezi nẽrba la pʋam, lʋ nõorɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee dɩkɛ ba basɛ Zuudãana tɩ ba bɔ sɩra la nu'usum.

ref: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
text: La ban doose Pisidi la, ba paagɛ Pãnfili so'olʋm.

ref: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
text: La ba mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Pɛrzɩ mɛ, dee sẽŋɛ Atali.

ref: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
text: La ba kẽ la õorŋɔ bilam sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ, tẽn-sɛka tɩ ba daan dɩkɛ ba ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum tɩ a sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba tõm tõon-sɛka tɩ ba tõm ba'asɛ wã.

ref: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
text: La ban paagɛ la, ba botɩ Azezi nẽrba la lagsɛ mɛ, tɩ ba tɔgɛ pa'alɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ n tõm la bãma se'em, la a yo'oge sore mɛ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ tɩ ba tãna wʋn bɔ ẽ sɩra.

ref: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
text: La ba boe la karẽnbiisi la lɛ bilam yuuge mɛ.

ref: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
text: Nẽrba basɛba n ze'ele Zude wa'am Antɩyɔsɩ wa zãsna Azezi karẽnbiisi la yetɩ: «Ya sãn ka wãta ya budimto wʋ Amoyisi lɔɔ la n zãsnɩ se'em na, ya kãn tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ.»

ref: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
text: Tɩ Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan ka sakɛ ban yele se'em na, daan tĩi wẽ'era nõke'ene la ba. Tɩ karẽnbiisi la kʋ nõ-yẽnnɛ yetɩ, Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ, la bãma nẽrba basɛba sẽŋɛ Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma n boe Zerizalɛm na zẽ'am nõke'ene ẽŋa yelle.

ref: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
text: La Azezi nẽrba la n tõm ba yetɩ ba sẽŋɛ la, ba doose la Fenisi la Samaari tẽnsʋm tɔgɛ pa'alɛ bu-zãŋsɩ n teege yɛm sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ se'em. Bɛla botɩ Fenisi la Samaari tẽnsɩ karẽnbiisi la sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ zozo'e.

ref: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
text: La ban paagɛ Zerizalɛm na, Azezi Tõntõnɩba la, la Azezi nẽrba, la ba kẽma la wuu to'oge ba mɛ sõŋa sõŋa. Tɩ ba tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ se'em wuu la bãma.

ref: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
text: Tɩ Farisi nẽrba basɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la isge yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ ba wãta bu-zãŋsɩ la, dee zãsʋm ba tɩ ba sakɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ la wuu.»

ref: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
text: Tɩ Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma la lagsɛ tɩ ba bɩsɛ ba wʋn ẽŋɛ yelle la se'em.

ref: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
text: La ban wẽ'erɩ nõke'ene zɔ'ɔra la, tɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ isge yele ba yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, yãma mi mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la mam kʋrʋm kʋrʋm yãma pʋam, tɩ m tɔgɛ a kõ-yẽlga la bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ tɩ ba sakɛ.

ref: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n mi nẽrsaala pʋtẽ'era la, bɔ ba a Sɩa mɛ, wʋ ẽŋa n daan bɔ tõma se'em na, pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa fãagɛ ba mɛ.

ref: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
text: La a ka welge tõma la bãma. La ban bɔ Azezi sɩra la ĩyã tɩ a yese be'em ba sũure pʋam basɛ.

ref: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
text: Bɛla, bẽm tɩ ya makra Nawẽnnɛ bɔɔra tɩ ya zelge karẽnbiisi la zeero tɩ tõma yaabdõma, la tõma ka yãŋɛ tʋ?

ref: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
text: La ka ãn sõŋa, tõma mi tɩ la de la Zuudãana Azezi yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã tɩ tõma yẽ fãarɛ, wʋ bãma n mẽ yẽ fãarɛ se'em na.»

ref: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la wuu ãna sĩm sɛlsra Abarnabasɩ la Apolɩ n tɔgrɩ pa'ala tõon-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la bãma bu-zãŋsɩ tẽŋasʋka la.

ref: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
text: La ban tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ Azakɩ yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, selse-ya.

ref: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
text: Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ tɔgɛ pa'alɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n dẽŋɛ bɩsɛ bu-zãŋsɩ yelle se'em, looge ba nẽrba basɛba tɩ ba dɛna a mẽŋa nẽrba.

ref: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
text: ‹Bɛla poorʋm, mam wʋn leme wa'am, wa le mẽ naba Adavidi deego la n lui la mɛ. Mam wʋn le malgɛ ẽ mɛ, mẽ a daboogo la zuo.

ref: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
text: Bɛla tɩ nẽr-sɛba n weege la wuu, la bu-zãŋsɩ wuu tɩ mam looge tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba la, wʋn ɛ mam n de ba Zuudãana.›

ref: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
text: Tɩ Azakɩ ken yetɩ: «Bɛla tɩ mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ tɩ da nãmsɛ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma dee teege yɛm dɔla Nawẽnnɛ na.

ref: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Tɩ gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ bɔ ba yetɩ, ba da di di-sɛba tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ baga la, tɩ ba wʋn dẽgɛ ba, ba da tõnna yalsɩ tõoma, ba da di sɛla tɩ ba tẽgɛ kʋ, la ba da di zɩɩm.

ref: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
text: Se'ere n soe la, halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm ba karẽŋrɩ Amoyisi gõnnɔ Zifdõma wẽndeto la pʋam ba vo'osgo dabsa wuu mɛ, dee mõola ba tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam.» Ba gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ

ref: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
text: Bɛla tɩ Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma, la Azezi nẽrba la wuu tẽ'esɛ yetɩ, la ãn sõŋa tɩ ba looge nẽrba bãma pʋam tõm ba tɩ ba doose la Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ. Tɩ ba looge Azudi sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n ken dɛna Abarsabasɩ la, la Asɩlaasɩ. Bãma tɩ ba daan nannɩ.

ref: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
text: La ba daan gʋlsɛ la gõŋɔ bɔ ba yetɩ: «Tõma n de Azezi Tõntõnɩba, la Azezi nẽrba la kẽma n pʋ'ʋsrɩ yãma bu-zãŋsɩ n de tɩ sɔbiisi n boe Antɩyɔsɩ, la Siiri, la Silisi so'olʋm.

ref: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
text: Tõma wõm tɩ tõma nẽrba basɛba n wa'am wa dãana yãma la yetɔga botɩ yãma yɛm yɔɔra, tõma ka bɔ ba nõorɛ.

ref: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ tõma lagsɛ taaba kʋ nõorɛ ayɩla, tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ tõma looge nẽrba basɛba tõm ba tɩ ba doose la tɩ sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ Abarnabasɩ la Apolɩ wa'am yãma zẽ'am.

ref: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Bãma n põsɛ ba yõorɛ tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi tõoma la ĩyã.

ref: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
text: Bɛla tɩ tõma tõm Azudi la Asɩlaasɩ tɩ ba wa'am wa tɔgɛ bõn-bãma pa'alɛ ya.

ref: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la tõma tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ tɩ da zelge ya zeero basɛba pa'asɛ, sãn dagna yel-sɛba n de kãŋkãŋɛ wã.

ref: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
text: La de la ya da di di-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ bɔ baga, ya da di zɩɩm, la ya da di sɛla tɩ ba tẽgɛ kʋ, la ya da tõnna yalsɩ tõoma. Ya sãn guuse ya mẽŋa ka ẽŋɛ bõn-bãna, ya ẽŋɛ sõŋa. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ya ĩmã'asʋm.»

ref: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
text: Ba daan keese sɛba tɩ ba tõm na mɛ, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ. La ban paagɛ bilam na, ba lagsɛ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la mɛ, dɩkɛ gõŋɔ la bɔ ba.

ref: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
text: La ban daan karẽŋɛ gõŋɔ la, la bɔ ba bʋraanɛ mɛ, botɩ ba sũure ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm zozo'e.

ref: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
text: Tɩ Azudi la Asɩlaasɩ n daan de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la tɔgɛ zozo'e keŋe ba sɔbiisi la sũure, pa'asɛ ba pãŋa.

ref: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
text: La ba daan yuuge mĩ na fẽe, dee tɩ ba sɔbiisi la basɛ ba tɩ ba lebe sɛba n tõm ba la zẽ'am la sũ-mã'asʋm. [

ref: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
text: La Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ daan weege la Antɩyɔsɩ bilam, tɩ bãma la nẽrba basɛba zozo'e zãsna nẽrba Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga dee mõola a kõ-yẽlga la. Apolɩ la Abarnabasɩ welge taaba

ref: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
text: La daarɛ ayɩla tɩ Apolɩ yele Abarnabasɩ yetɩ: «Tɩ lebe ka kaagɛ tɩ sɔbiisi la n boe tẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ tɩ daan mõolɛ Zuudãana yetɔga bɔ ba la, bɩsɛ ba ãn se'em.»

ref: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Tɩ Abarnabasɩ daan bɔɔra tɩ Azã sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n ken dɛna Amarkɩ la po doose ba sẽŋɛ.

ref: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
text: La Apolɩ daan tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ka ãn sõŋa tɩ a doose bãma, se'ere n soe la, a daan basɛ ba mɛ Pãnfili, ka le doose la ba tõm.

ref: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
text: Tɩ ba daan wẽ nõke'ene welge taaba. Tɩ Abarnabasɩ daan botɩ Amarkɩ doose ẽ, tɩ ba kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Sipri.

ref: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
text: La Apolɩ daan looge la Asɩlaasɩ tɩ a doose ẽ. Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la dɩkɛ ba ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
text: Tɩ ba yãŋa sẽŋɛ Siiri la Silisi tẽnsʋm ka pa'asɛ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pãŋa.

ref: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
text: La Apolɩ dõma sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ la Dɛrbɩ la Listri tẽnsʋm. Tɩ Azezi karẽnbia daan bɔna mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Atimõtɩ, a ma de la Zifu n sakɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra. Dee tɩ a sɔ dɛna Grɛkɩ.

ref: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
text: Tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi n boe Listri la Ikonim tẽnsʋm na daan tɔgra a yelle sõŋa.

ref: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ bɔɔra tɩ a dɔla la ba lɛ tõnna, daan wã ẽ Zifdõma sɛba n boe tẽn-bãma la ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, ba wuu daan mi tɩ a sɔ de la Grɛkɩ.

ref: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
text: La ba daan dolɩ la tẽnsɩ pʋam bɩsra Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la, dee tɔgra pa'ala ba Azezi Tõntõnɩba la, la kẽma la n boe Zerizalɛm na n looge yɛm se'em, dee yɛta ba tɩ ba sakɛ bõn-sɛba tɩ ba looge la.

ref: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
text: Bɛla tɩ Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la daan yẽ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo la pʋam, tɩ ba kãllɛ pa'asra daarɛ woo. Apolɩ boe la Trʋasɩ zãasʋm tɩ ba wi ẽ Masedʋanɩ

ref: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ dõma daan bɔɔra tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azi tẽnsʋm ka mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa gu ba, tɩ ba doose Frizi la Galatɩ so'olʋm.

ref: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
text: La ban sẽŋɛ ka lɛm Mizi so'olʋm na, ba boorɩ tɩ ba sẽŋɛ la Bitini, tɩ Azezi Sɩa la ka bɔ ba sore.

ref: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
text: Tɩ ba doose Mizi tole sẽŋɛ Trʋasɩ.

ref: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
text: La yʋ'ʋŋɔ tɩ Apolɩ bɩsɛ yẽ Masedʋanɩ nẽra wʋ zãasŋɔ la, tɩ bʋraa la zɛ a zẽ'am bɛlna ẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am Masedʋanɩ wa sõŋɛ tɔ.»

ref: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
text: La Apolɩ n daan yẽ bɛla la, tõma mãasʋm mɛ tɔtɔ tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ Masedʋanɩ, bẽm ĩyã, tõma tẽ'esɛ bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ n wi tõma yetɩ, tɩ sẽŋɛ ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ba. Alidi bɔ Azezi sɩra Filipi tẽŋam

ref: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
text: Tɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ bilam kɔ'ɔn sẽŋɛ Samotrasɩ. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Neapolisi.

ref: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
text: La tõma yãŋa ze'ele la bilam sẽŋɛ Filipi n de Masedʋanɩ tẽn-kãra la tẽŋa ayɩla. Filipi tẽŋa de la Rom dõma n soe ẽ. Tõma ẽŋɛ bilam na dabsa faa.

ref: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
text: Tɩ vo'osgo daarɛ tɩ tõma yese tẽŋa la pʋam sẽŋɛ kʋlaa nõorʋm, tõma tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ zẽ'e-ẽŋa de la Zifdõma pʋ'ʋsgɔ zẽ'a. Tɩ tõma zĩ'ire bilam tɔgra bɔ'ɔra pɔgsɩ n lagsɛ.

ref: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
text: La pɔka ayɩla n daan boe mĩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Alidi, a ze'ele la Tiyatiiri. A daan koosrɩ la fu-sõma n de gomusga. Tɩ a sɛlsra tõma yetɔga la, tɩ Zuudãana botɩ a bãŋɛ Apolɩ yetɔga la võorɛ dee sakɛ.

ref: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
text: Tɩ tõma daan mise ẽŋa la a yir-dõma ko'om pʋam. Tɩ a sose tõma yetɩ: «Ya sãn sakɛ tɩ mam sɩrɩ bɔ Zuudãana sɩra mɛ, ya wa'am kẽ'era mam yire.» La a pẽrgɛ tõma mɛ, tɩ tõma sakɛ. Apolɩ dõma boe la yʋ'a deem Filipi tẽŋam

ref: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
text: Tɩ daarɛ ayɩla tɩ tõma wɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ zẽ'am, tɩ pugla n de yamŋa tɩ kʋlkã'arga dolɩ ẽ daan tʋ'ʋsɛ tõma. Kʋlkã'arga la daan botɩ a yẽta la yel-sɛba n boe nẽŋam sẽnna tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, la sɛba n soe ẽ na daan yẽtɩ ligri zozo'e mɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
text: Tɩ a dɔla Apolɩ la tõma tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã de la Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla wuu la tõntõnɩba, ba mõolɩ la fãarɛ sore pa'ala ya.»

ref: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
text: La a ẽŋɛ bɛla la dabsa faa. Tɩ Apolɩ sũure sã'am la ẽ, tɩ a wẽrgɛ yele kʋlkã'arga la yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ fʋ la Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ, tɩ fʋ yese dee basɛ pugla wã.» Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la yese bilam zẽ'am dee basɛ ẽ.

ref: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
text: Tɩ sɛba n soe ẽ na n bãŋɛ tɩ bãma kãn le yẽ ligri a zẽ'am na, tɩ ba yõkɛ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ ve'ege ba kẽ da'am ka sãm ba tẽŋa la nẽŋadõma zẽ'am.

ref: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
text: Ba pa'alɩ la bõn-sɛba n sisri, tɩ tõma n de Rom nẽrba kãn sakɛ ẽŋɛ.»

ref: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lagɩm zɛbra Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ, tɩ sɛba n diti sarɩya la botɩ ba vɛ ba futo la yɛɛsɛ, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba wẽ ba la ka'asa.

ref: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
text: Tɩ ba wẽ ba zozo'e dee kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deem, dee yele gu'ura la tɩ a gu'ura ba sõŋa.

ref: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
text: Ban yele bɛla la, tɩ a kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a de-sɛka n boe pʋam pʋam na, lʋ ba nãma lablɛ la dɔɔgɔ.

ref: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
text: Tɩ sõnsʋa tɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ pʋ'ʋsra dee yõona yõoma pẽgra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ sɛba n po bɔna yʋ'a deem na sɛlsra.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
text: Tɩ tẽŋa darɛ mĩim zozo'e, tɩ yʋ'a deego la mĩim la a ẽbrɛ waa wuu, tɩ kʋlɩnsɩ la wuu yo'ose, tɩ bãnsɩ la wuu ba'asɛ.

ref: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
text: Tɩ yʋ'a deego gu'ura la nẽegɛ isge yẽ tɩ kʋlɩnsɩ la yo'ose mɛ, tɩ a tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba yese mɛ, tɩ a fooge sʋ'a tɩ a kʋ a mẽŋa.

ref: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Da kãn ẽŋɛ fʋ mẽŋa sɛla, tõma wuu boe la kalam.»

ref: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
text: Tɩ yʋ'a deego gu'ura la wi yetɩ, ba tarɩ bugum wa'am. Dee ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ kẽ deego la pʋam ka lui Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ nẽŋam gõgra la dabeem,

ref: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
text: isge tarɩ ba yese, soke ba yetɩ: «Bʋraasɩ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne yẽ fãarɛ?»

ref: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
text: Tɩ ba lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Bɔ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra, tɩ fõn na fʋ yir-dõma wuu wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.»

ref: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
text: Tɩ ba tɔgɛ Zuudãana yetɔga bɔ ẽŋa la sɛba wuu n boe a yire la.

ref: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
text: La wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ a tarɩ ba ka pɛ ba nõtɔ la. La bilam zẽ'am tɩ ba mise ẽŋa la a yir-dõma wuu ko'om pʋam la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
text: Tɩ a tarɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ sẽŋɛ a yire ka bɔ ba dia. Tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma la wuu sũure daan ãna yẽlʋm zozo'e, ban bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
text: La beere n wiige, tɩ sɛba n diti sarɩya la tõm ba tõntõnɩba yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ ka yele sɛka n gu'uri yʋ'a deego la yetɩ, a basɛ bʋraasɩ la.

ref: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
text: Tɩ yʋ'a deego gu'ura la tɔgɛ bɔ Apolɩ yetɩ: «Sarɩyaditba la bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, m basɛ tɩ ya yese. Bɛla yese-ya fõrgɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm.»

ref: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ yele tõntõnɩba la yetɩ: «Tõma n de Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽrba, tɩ ba ka di tõma sarɩya dee wẽ tõma, kẽ'esɛ tõma yʋ'a deem, yãŋa yetɩ ba li'ise basɛ tõma? Ayɛɩ, ba mẽŋa wa'am wa yese to.»

ref: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
text: Tɩ tõntõnɩba la sẽŋɛ ka yele sarɩyaditba la bɛla. Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba, ban wõm tɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽrba la ĩyã.

ref: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
text: Tɩ ba mẽŋa wa'am wa belem ba, botɩ ba yese, dee sose ba yetɩ, ba fõrgɛ dee basɛ ba tẽŋa.

ref: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
text: La ba yese yʋ'a deem na sẽŋɛ la Alidi yire ka tɔgɛ bɔ Azezi karẽnbiisi la bʋraanɛ dee fõrgɛ.

ref: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
text: Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ doose la Amfipoli, la Apoloni tẽnsʋm tole sẽŋɛ Tesaloniki, zẽ'e-ẽŋa Zifdõma wẽndeego n daan boe mĩ.

ref: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
text: Tɩ Apolɩ daan kẽ mĩ wʋ ẽ ẽn ɩta se'em na, tɔgɛ bõn-sɛba n boe Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na pa'alɛ ba vo'osgo dabsa atã.

ref: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
text: A tɔgɛ pa'ala ba yetɩ, la masɛ mɛ tɩ Krisi nãmsɛ ki dee vo'oge. Dee yetɩ: «Azezi tɩ mam mõolɩ a yelle bɔ'ɔra ya wã de la Krisi la.»

ref: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
text: Tɩ ba nẽrba basɛba, la Grɛkɩdõma nẽrba zozo'e n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, la pɔgsɩ zozo'e n de nẽrzuto daan sakɛ po dɔla Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ.

ref: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma daan ẽŋɛ sũure, sẽŋɛ da'am ka ɛ nẽrba n ka tarɩ võorɛ lagsɛ tɩ ba dãm tẽŋa la. Tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Azasõ yire ka ɛɛra Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ tɩ ba tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la nẽrba zẽ'am.

ref: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
text: La ba sẽŋɛ ka ɛ ka yẽ ba, yõkɛ Azasõ la Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba sẽŋɛ tẽŋa la kɛ'ɛndõma zẽ'am ka tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã eerɩ dãmna la dũnia wuu. La ban wãna n wa'am kalam,

ref: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
text: tɩ Azasõ to'oge ba. Nẽr-bãna wã wuu sɩ'ɩsrɩ la Rom na-keko nõorɛ yetɩ, naba ayẽma n boe tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Azezi.»

ref: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
text: La kɛ'ɛndõma la, la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n wõm bɛla la, ba sũure isge mɛ.

ref: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
text: Tɩ ba botɩ Azasõ la sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na bĩŋe ligri, dee tɩ ba basɛ ba. Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ sẽŋɛ Beere

ref: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
text: La wẽnnɛ n daan sobe, tɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la botɩ Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ sẽŋɛ Beere. La ban paagɛ la, ba sẽŋɛ mɛ ka kẽ Zifdõma wẽndeem.

ref: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
text: La bãma tarɩ pʋtẽ'e-sõnnɛ mɛ gãnna Zifdõma sɛba n boe Tesaloniki la, sakɛ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la ba sũure wuu, dee daan karẽŋra Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la daarɛ woo tɩ ba bɩsɛ Apolɩ n yele se'em na de la sɩra bɩɩ la dagɩ sɩra.

ref: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
text: La ba nẽrba zozo'e, la Grɛkɩdõma pɔgsɩ n de yʋ'ʋrɛ dõma, la bʋraasɩ zozo'e sakɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra mɛ.

ref: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n boe Tesaloniki la wõm tɩ Apolɩ mõolɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga Beere, tɩ ba sẽŋɛ mĩ ka dãm nẽrba la, sa'asɛ ba la Apolɩ la Asɩlaasɩ.

ref: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
text: Tɩ karẽnbiisi la ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ botɩ nẽrba basɛba kɔlgɛ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm. Dee tɩ Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ weege Beere.

ref: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
text: La sɛba n kɔlgɛ Apolɩ la tarɩ ẽ paagɛ la Atɛnɩ, tɩ Apolɩ soolʋm ba yetɩ, ba sãn paagɛ, ba yele Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ yetɩ, ba wa'am tɔtɔ. Apolɩ boe Atɛnɩ mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga

ref: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
text: La Apolɩ daan boe la Atɛnɩ gu'ura Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ. La a daan yẽ la baga n boe tẽŋa la pʋam zozo'e, tɩ a sũure sã'am.

ref: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
text: Tɩ a sẽŋɛ Zifdõma wẽndeem ka tɔgra la Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, dee tɔgra daarɛ woo la nẽrba n boe da'am.

ref: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
text: Tɩ bãŋrɩba daan bɔna mĩ tɔgra la Apolɩ, basɛba dolɩ la A'epikiri zãsŋɔ, dee tɩ basɛba dɔla Astoyisi zãsŋɔ. Tɩ nẽrba basɛba soke yetɩ: «Yetɔg-tɔgsa wã boorɩ tɩ a yetɩ bẽm?» Tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «La ãn wʋ a mõolɩ la wẽn-paalsɩ yelle la.» Se'ere n soe la, Apolɩ mõolɩ la Azezi kõ-yẽlga, la kũm vo'ore yelle.

ref: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
text: Tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ Arewopaazɩ zẽ'am ka yetɩ: «Tõma boorɩ la tɩ bãŋɛ zãsŋɔ paalga sɛka tɩ fõn tɔgrɩ la võorɛ.

ref: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
text: Fõn tɔgɛ yel-sɛba tɩ tõma wõm wã de la yel-sãasɩ bɔ tõma, bɛla tɩ tõma bɔɔra tɩ bãŋɛ a võorɛ.»

ref: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
text: Wakatɛ wuu Atɛnɩ dõma, la nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ bilam na, tɔgrɩ la yel-paala dee sɛlsra yel-paala.

ref: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ isge ze'ele Arewopaazɩ la tẽŋasʋka yetɩ: «Atɛnɩ tẽŋa nẽrba, mam yẽ tɩ ya de la nẽrba n nã'asrɩ baga zozo'e sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
text: Mam n gilge ya tẽŋa wã pʋam na, mam yẽ bõn-sɛba tɩ ya ẽŋɛ nã'asra mɛ, la mam yẽ la kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zuo tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Wẽn-sɛka tɩ tõma ka mi.› Wẽn-sɛka tɩ yãma nã'asrɩ dee ka mina ẽ na, tɩ mam mõolɩ bɔ'ɔra ya.

ref: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
text: Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋa n nãam dũnia la sɛla wuu n boe ba pʋam, ẽŋa n soe saazuum la tẽŋa. A ka kẽ'erɩ deego tɩ nẽrsaalba mẽ pʋam.

ref: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
text: Sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ nẽrsaalba tõm bɔ ẽ. Ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ sɛla woo yõvore, la vo'osʋm, la sɛla woo.

ref: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
text: A nãam la nẽra ayɩla botɩ nẽrba buuri wuu n boe dũnia zuo ze'ele la ẽŋa zẽ'am. Ẽŋa n makɛ wakat-sɛba la zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ ba wʋn bɔna.

ref: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
text: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ ba ɛ ẽ mɛ, ba sãn ɛɛra babla, tã tɩ ba wʋn yẽ ẽ. La a ka zãagɛ la tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla.

ref: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
text: Ẽŋa n botɩ tɩ bɔna, sẽnna dee tara vɔm, wʋ yãma Atɛnɩ so'olʋm nẽrba basɛba n daan yetɩ: ‹Tõma mẽ de la a kɔma.›

ref: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
text: Tõma n de a kɔma la, tɩ da tẽ'esɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wõn na bõn-nãanɩsɩ tɩ nẽrsaalba dɩkɛ sãlma bɩɩ zũuro bɩɩ kuga tẽ'esɛ mãalɛ la nu'usi.

ref: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
text: Nawẽnnɛ ka geele wakat-sɛka tɩ tõma daan ka mi la. La lɛɛlɛ wã, a bɔ nõorɛ zẽ'a woo yetɩ, nẽrba wuu teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

ref: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
text: Se'ere n soe la, a looge la daarɛ tɩ a di dũnia nẽrba sarɩya, a dẽŋɛ looge la nẽra, ẽŋa n wʋn di nẽrba la wuu sarɩya la sɩra. A vo'oge nẽr-ẽŋa pa'alɛ tɩ la de la sɩra.»

ref: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
text: Ban wõm tɩ Apolɩ tɔgrɩ kũm vo'ore yelle la, tɩ sɛba la'ara ẽ, dee tɩ basɛba yetɩ: «Tõma wʋn le selse fʋ yetɔg-bãna la da-se'ere.»

ref: When Paul left the council meeting,
text: Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele ba zẽ'am fõrgɛ.

ref: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
text: La nẽrba basɛba daan doose ẽ mɛ bɔ Azezi sɩra. Nẽr-sɛba n daan bɔ sɩra la de la Adenisi, ẽŋa de la Arewopaazɩ sarɩyaditba nẽra ayɩla, la pɔka ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Adamarisi, la nẽrba basɛba.

ref: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Apolɩ isge Atɛnɩ sẽŋɛ la Korẽntɩ.

ref: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
text: Ẽn paagɛ bilam na, a yẽ la Zifdõma bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Akila tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ Põ, tɩ ẽŋa la a pɔga Aprisili ze'ele Rom n boe Itali so'olʋm sẽŋɛ paam, se'ere n soe la, na-keko Akloorɩ n bɔ nõorɛ tɩ Zifdõma wuu n boe Rom tẽŋam fõrgɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ ba zẽ'am.

ref: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
text: La bãma la Apolɩ tõoma daan de la bʋyɩla, ba pɛ'ɛrɩ la futo tɩ ba da'ara tĩ'ira fuu-deto, bɛla ĩyã tɩ Apolɩ kẽ'era bilam, tɩ ba lagɩm tõnna.

ref: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
text: La Zifdõma vo'osgo daarɛ woo Apolɩ sẽnnɩ ba wẽndeem mɛ tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ'ɔra ba, mõrgra tɩ a botɩ Zifdõma, la Grɛkɩdõma sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la.

ref: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
