text: La Asɩlaasɩ la Atimõtɩ n daan ze'ele Masedʋanɩ paagɛ la, Apolɩ daan basɛ a tõoma la mɛ, dee mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la pa'ala Zifdõma tɩ Azezi n de Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.

ref: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
text: Tɩ ba sɩ'ɩsɛ dee tɔgra ẽ tɔg-be'ero. Tɩ a pĩsũm a futo pa'alɛ tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, dee yele ba yetɩ: «Ya sãn yẽ nãmsgɔ, ya yelle boe la ya mẽŋa zuo, la dagɩ mam yelle. La zĩna tɛka mam wʋn sẽŋɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la zẽ'am.»

ref: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
text: Apolɩ daan yele bɛla, dee yese mɛ, sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era nẽra ayɩla yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Atitiyusi-zustusi yire. A de la bu-zãŋka dee pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a yire lɛm Zifdõma wẽndeego la.

ref: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
text: La Akrispusi n de wẽndeego la nẽŋadãana, la a yir-dõma wuu daan bɔ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra mɛ. La Korẽntɩ nẽrba zozo'e n daan selsrɩ kõ-yẽlga la, mẽ bɔ sɩra mɛ, tɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam.

ref: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
text: Tɩ daarɛ ayɩla yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ Apolɩ yẽ wʋ zãasŋɔ la, tɩ Zuudãana yele ẽ yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem sĩna, ken tɔgra,

ref: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
text: tɩ mam boe la fʋ mɛ, nẽra nẽra kãn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ fʋ be'em. Se'ere n soe la, tẽŋa wã pʋam nẽrba zozo'e de la mam nẽrba.»

ref: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
text: Apolɩ zĩ'ire bilam na yʋʋnɛ la wõr-sɩyoobɩ zãsna nẽrba Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

ref: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
text: La wakat-sɛka tɩ Agaliyõ daan de Akayi so'olʋm gɔmna la, Zifdõma daan isge naagɛ la taaba dõrɛ Apolɩ tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ sarɩyadia zẽ'am,

ref: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
text: ka yetɩ: «Ẽna wã n mõrgrɩ nẽrba tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ se'em na sɩ'ɩsrɩ la tõma lɔɔ.»

ref: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ daan bɔna mĩ tɩ a tɔgɛ, tɩ Agaliyõ yele Zifdõma la yetɩ: «La sãn de nɩ na sɛla n ka masɛ bɩɩ tõon-be'ero tɩ a ẽŋɛ, mam wʋn mõrgɛ selse yãma Zifdõma.

ref: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
text: La, la sãn dɛna ya wẽ'erɩ la nõke'ene la yetɔga, la yʋ'ʋra, la ya mẽŋa lɔɔ yɛla, ya mẽŋa bɩsɛ bõn-bãma yelle. Tɩ mam kãn di bõn-ẽŋa sarɩya.»

ref: Then he sent them out of the court.
text: A yele bɛla, dee dige ba sarɩya zẽ'am na mɛ tɩ ba yese.

ref: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
text: Tɩ ba wuu yõkɛ Asostɛnɩ n de Zifdõma wẽndeego nẽŋadãana wẽ ẽ sarɩyadia la zẽ'am. La Agaliyõ toogo ka boe la ban ẽŋɛ se'em na. Apolɩ lebe Antɩyɔsɩ n boe Siiri

ref: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
text: La Apolɩ ken zĩ'ire Korẽntɩ la dabsa zozo'e la karẽnbiisi la lɛ, dee keese ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Siiri. Tɩ Aprisili la Akila doose ẽ. La ban daan paagɛ Sãnkre la, Apolɩ dẽŋɛ siige la a zuugo, se'ere n soe la, a bĩŋe la nõorɛ la Nawẽnnɛ. Dee tɩ ba yãŋa kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ

ref: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
text: ka paagɛ Efɛɛzɩ, tɩ Apolɩ basɛ Aprisili la Akila bilam. A sẽŋɛ ka kẽ la Zifdõma wẽndeem tɔgɛ la ba.

ref: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
text: Tɩ ba sose ẽ yetɩ, a weege ba zẽ'am yuuge. Tɩ a zagsɛ,

ref: He told them goodbye and said, “If God lets me, I will come back.”
text: dee keese ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sãn sakɛ, m wʋn leme ya zẽ'am.» La a daan kẽ la õorŋɔ Efɛɛzɩ,

ref: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
text: sẽŋɛ Sezaare ka sige bilam, sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Azezi nẽr-sɛba n boe bilam na, dee sẽŋɛ Antɩyɔsɩ.

ref: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
text: La a yuuge bilam mɛ fẽe, dee yese ɛ Galatɩ la Frizi pa'asɛ karẽnbiisi la wuu pãŋa. Apolɔsɩ boe Efɛɛzɩ la Korẽntɩ tẽnsʋm

ref: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
text: La Zifu bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Apolɔsɩ tɩ ba dɔgɛ ẽ Alɛgsãndri n wa'am Efɛɛzɩ, a de la tɔg-mita dee mina Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ sõŋa.

ref: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
text: Ba daan pa'alɛ ẽ Zuudãana Azezi sore yɛla mɛ, tɩ a daan zãsna nẽrba so-ẽŋa yɛla sõŋa la a sũure wuu. La a daan mi la Azã ko'om misga la mã'a.

ref: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
text: Tɩ a daan tɔgra Zifdõma wẽndeem la bʋraanɛ. La Aprisili la Akila n wõm ẽn tɔgrɩ la, tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka pa'alɛ ẽ Nawẽnnɛ sore la yɛla sõŋa pa'asɛ.

ref: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a sẽŋɛ Akayi, tɩ Azezi karẽnbi-sɛba n boe Efɛɛzɩ la gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ bɔ Azezi karẽnbiisi la n boe Akayi la yetɩ, ba to'oge ẽ sõŋa. La ẽn paagɛ bilam na, a sõŋɛ sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ daan bɔ ba a yel-sõnnɛ tɩ ba bɔ ẽ sɩra la mɛ zozo'e.

ref: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: La a daan dɩkɛ la Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ tɔgɛ yãŋɛ Zifdõma nẽrba wuu nẽŋam, pa'alɛ ba tɩ Azezi de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm.

ref: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
text: La Apolɔsɩ n daan nãn boe Korẽntɩ la, Apolɩ doose la tãnsɩ zuo tẽnsɩ la wuu pʋam tole, sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Efɛɛzɩ yẽ karẽnbiisi basɛba bilam,

ref: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
text: soke ba yetɩ: «Yãma n bɔ Azezi sɩra la, yãma to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mɛ bɩɩ?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, tõma nãn ka wõm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe.»

ref: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ soke ba yetɩ: «Ko'om misga kãna tɩ yãma to'oge?» Tɩ ba lerge yetɩ: «Tõma to'oge la Azã ko'om misga.»

ref: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ yele ba yetɩ: «Azã daan misri nẽrba ko'om pʋam pa'ala tɩ ba teege la yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. A daan yetɩ nẽrba la, tɩ nẽra n weege ẽŋa poorʋm sẽna, ba doose ẽŋa. Nẽr-ẽŋa de la Azezi.»

ref: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: La ban wõm bɛla la, ba botɩ ba mise ba ko'om pʋam mɛ Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

ref: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ dɩkɛ a nu'usi paglɛ ba zuto, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa wa'am ba zuo, tɩ ba tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋsɩ dee to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

ref: There were about twelve men in this group.
text: Ba daan de wʋ nẽrba pia la ayi.

ref: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
text: Apolɩ daan kẽ Efɛɛzɩ la wõr-sɩtã, daan kẽ'era Zifdõma wẽndeem mõola Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bʋraanɛ bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee mõrgɛ pa'alɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yɛla tɩ ba sakɛ.

ref: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
text: Tɩ basɛba daan kɛ'ɛm ba sũure ka sakɛ, dee tɔgra Zuudãana sore la tɔg-be'ero nẽrba nẽŋam. Tɩ Apolɩ basɛ ba dee welge karẽnbiisi la fõrgɛ. Tɩ daarɛ woo tɩ a zãsna ba Atiranusi karẽndeego pʋam.

ref: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
text: A ẽŋɛ bɛla la yʋʋma ayi. La nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe Azi tẽnsʋm na wõm Zuudãana yetɔga mɛ, Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ wuu. Aseeva dayɔɔsɩ yelle

ref: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ daan basɛ tɩ Apolɩ tõm na tõon-yãlm-kãra.

ref: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
text: La ba daan sãn ẽn dɩkɛ ãnkɩra bɩɩ futo n kalʋm a ĩyã tarɩ sẽŋɛ ka kalʋm bã'adõma, ba yẽtɩ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ, tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ yɛsra dee basra ba.

ref: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
text: Tɩ Zifdõma basɛba n daan gilgri digra kʋlkã'arsɩ nẽrba zẽ'am, daan makɛ tɩ ba dige kʋlkã'arsɩ la Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ, yele ba yetɩ: «Mam yetɩ ya la Azezi sɛka tɩ Apolɩ mõolɩ la yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ ya yese.»

ref: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
text: Ba daan de la Aseeva n de Zifdõma kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana dayɔɔsɩ bayopɔɩ n ɩtɩ bɛla.

ref: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
text: Tɩ kʋlkã'arga lerge ba yetɩ: «Mam mi Azezi mɛ, dee mina Apolɩ n de sɛka, la yãma de la ãndõma?»

ref: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
text: Tɩ kʋlkã'arga la n tarɩ sɛka la isge zɛbɛ ba la pãŋa yãŋɛ ba wuu, tɩ ba ze'ele yire la zoe yese bɛɛla la pɔglʋŋɔ.

ref: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ la wuu n boe Efɛɛzɩ la wõm yel-ẽna wã. Tɩ dabeem kẽ ba wuu, tɩ ba pẽgra Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
text: Tɩ nẽrba zozo'e bɔ Azezi sɩra wa'am wa pelge ba pʋa tɔgɛ ba tõon-be'ero nẽrba nẽŋam.

ref: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
text: Tɩ ba zozo'e n tarɩ ba gõnnɔ tõnna tɩɩm tõoma la, lagsɛ ba yõ la bugum nẽrba la wuu nifum. Tɩ ba geele gõnnɔ na wuu ligri, tɩ ba de la lig-kõba tʋspinuu.

ref: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
text: La Zuudãana n pa'alɛ a pãŋa bɛla la, tɩ a yetɔga la saara zẽ'a wuu dee yẽta pãŋa. Efɛɛzɩ dõma dãmŋɔ yelle

ref: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Apolɩ tẽ'esɛ a sũurum yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn sẽŋɛ la Masedʋanɩ, la Akayi ka ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm, dee yetɩ: «Mam sãn paagɛ bɔna Zerizalɛm, dẽnɩ tɩ mam paagɛ Rom.»

ref: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
text: La a tõm na Atimõtɩ, la A'erastɩ n de a sõŋrɩba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ Masedʋanɩ, dee weege a mẽŋa Azi yuuge fẽe.

ref: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
text: La wakat-ẽŋa dãmŋɔ zozo'e n isge Efɛɛzɩ Zuudãana Azezi sore la doosgo yelle.

ref: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
text: La bʋraa n daan de kuta, tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Ademetrisi. A daan tarɩ la zũuro kuta bõndõma tɩ ba wõna la ba wẽnnɛ Artemisi deego la. Tɩ zũuro tõntõnɩba la daan yẽ yõorɔ zozo'e tõon-ẽŋa pʋam.

ref: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
text: Tɩ Ademetrisi lagsɛ ba, la sɛba wuu n tõnnɩ tõon-ẽŋa buuri, yele ba yetɩ: «Bʋraasɩ wã, ya mi tɩ tõon-ẽna wã pʋam tɩ tɩ yẽtɩ bõntarsõm.

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: Ya mẽŋa wõm dee yẽ Apolɩ n ɩtɩ se'em. A pa'alɩ nẽrba tɩ wẽn-sɛba tɩ nẽrsaalba ẽŋɛ la ba mẽŋa nu'usi la dagɩ wẽna sɩra sɩra. A ẽŋɛ bɛla mõrgɛ teege nẽrba zozo'e pʋtẽ'era mɛ Efɛɛzɩ kalam, la, la dee la fẽe tɩ a paagɛ Azi tẽnsɩ wã wuu pʋam.

ref: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
text: Bõn-ẽna wʋn botɩ tõma tõonɛ na sã'am mɛ. La sɛla n pa'asɛ, wẽn-kãtɛ Artemisi deego la wʋn lebge la zãŋa, la Artemisi mẽŋa tɩ Azi nẽrba, la dũnia nẽrba wuu nã'asrɩ la, pãŋa wʋn sã'am mɛ.»

ref: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: La ban wõm yetɔg-bãna la, ba sũa yĩige mɛ tɩ ba tĩi tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Efɛɛzɩ dõma wẽnnɛ Artemisi n tarɩ pãŋa.»

ref: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
text: Tɩ tẽŋa la wuu dãmɛ. Tɩ nẽrba la yõkɛ Agayusi la Aristarkɩ tarɩ ba zoe sẽŋɛ lagsgɔ zẽ'am, bãma bayi de la Masedʋanɩ nẽrba n dolɩ Apolɩ.

ref: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ bɔɔra tɩ a kẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la zẽ'am, tɩ karẽnbiisi la ka sakɛ.

ref: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
text: Tɩ Azi tẽŋa nẽŋadõma basɛba n de a sɩrdõma tõm nẽrba yetɩ, ba wa'am a zẽ'am wa belem ẽ yetɩ, a da sẽŋɛ lagsgɔ zẽ'am na.

ref: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la lagɩm vɔɔla zozo'e, tɩ nẽra woo tɔgra ẽŋa n tẽ'esɛ se'em, tɩ ba zozo'e ka mina bõn-sɛka n botɩ ba lagsɛ.

ref: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma looge Alɛgsãndrɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽrba la nẽŋam ka tɔgɛ, tɩ nẽrba la basɛba pa'alɛ ẽ yelle la võorɛ. Tɩ Alɛgsãndrɩ yɩbsɛ la nu'usi tɩ a tɔgɛ yelle la pa'alɛ nẽrba la.

ref: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: La ban bãŋɛ tɩ a de la Zifu la, tɩ ba wuu lagɩm taaba tĩi kaasɛ yetɩ: «Efɛɛzɩ dõma wẽnnɛ Artemisi n tarɩ pãŋa.» Ba kaasɛ bɛla paagɛ karf-ayi.

ref: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
text: Tɩ tẽŋa la gõn-gʋlsa botɩ ba go'oge, tɩ a yele ba yetɩ: «Efɛɛzɩ nẽrba, dũnia nẽrba wuu mi mɛ tɩ wẽn-kãtɛ Artemisi pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego la boe la Efɛɛzɩ kalam, la tõma n de a pʋ'ʋsgɔ deego la gu'urba, la kugre la n wõn na Artemisi ze'ele saazuum lui la gu'urba.

ref: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
text: Nẽra nẽra kãn yãŋɛ sɩ'ɩsɛ yel-ẽna. Bɛla ĩyã, go'oge-ya voole, da lɔgɛ-ya ẽŋɛ sɛla n ka masɛ.

ref: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
text: Yan tarɩ nẽr-bãna wa'am kalam wã, ba ka yele tɩ wẽnnɛ na yel-be'ego, la ba ka kẽ a pʋ'ʋsgɔ deem na ẽŋɛ sɛla n de be'em.

ref: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
text: La Ademetrisi la a tõntõnɩba la sãn tara yelle la nẽr-sɛka, sarɩyadia daarɛ boe mɛ, la sarɩyaditba mẽ boe mɛ. A sãn bɔɔra, a tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ bilam ka sãm.

ref: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
text: La ya sãn yẽ la yel-sɛka tɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya malgɛ, tẽŋa la nẽrba lagsgɔ daarɛ, tɩ wʋn malgɛ.

ref: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
text: Yãma n ẽŋɛ sɛla zĩna wã wʋn wa'am na yelle, tɩ ba wʋn dõrɛ tɩ yetɩ, tɩ gire la tẽŋa. La tɩ kãn yãŋɛ pa'alɛ sɛla n botɩ tɩ lagsɛ gire girga la. La tɩ kãn yãŋɛ tɔgɛ la võorɛ sõŋa.»

ref: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
text: Ẽŋa n yele bɛla kɩ'ɩlɩm na, dee yele ba tɩ ba wɩrgɛ.

ref: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
text: Lan daan gire mã'agɛ la poorʋm, tɩ Apolɩ botɩ Azezi karẽnbiisi la lagsɛ, tɩ a tɔgɛ bɔ ba bʋraanɛ, keese ba dee wɛ'ɛsa Masedʋanɩ.

ref: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
text: La ẽn daan dolɩ so'olʋm ẽŋa tẽn-sɛba pʋam tɔlla la, a tɔgɛ yetɔga zozo'e mɛ bɔ karẽnbiisi la bʋraanɛ. Dee yãŋa tole ka paagɛ Grɛsɩ.

ref: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
text: La ẽn paagɛ bilam na, a zĩ'ire mĩ na wõr-sɩtã, mãasʋm tɩ a kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Siiri, wõm tɩ Zifdõma dolɩ la a nãma. Tɩ a tẽ'esɛ yetɩ, ẽŋa wʋn lebe doose la Masedʋanɩ.

ref: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text: Sɛba n daan dolɩ ẽ na de la: Apirusi bia Asopatɛɛrɩ n ze'ele Beere, la Aristarkɩ la Asekõndusi n ze'ele Tesaloniki, la Agayusi n ze'ele Dɛrbɩ, la Atisiki la Atrofim n ze'ele Azi, pa'asɛ la Atimõtɩ.

ref: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
text: Nẽr-bãna wã daan dẽŋɛ tole la nẽŋa ka gu'ura tõma Trʋasɩ. Dee tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ Filipi tẽŋam.

ref: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
text: La borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ kibsa la poorʋm, tɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ Filipi, tɩ dabsa anuu daarɛ tɩ tõma paagɛ sɛba n boe Trʋasɩ gu'ura tõma la zẽ'am, zĩ'ire bilam dabsa ayopɔɩ. Apolɩ boe Trʋasɩ

ref: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
text: La vo'osgo daarɛ ayɩla yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ tõma lagsɛ tɩ tɩ di, dee fiise borborɩ. Tɩ Apolɩ daan isge tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la nẽrba la halɩ ka paagɛ sõnsʋa, se'ere n soe la, a boorɩ tɩ beere sãn wiige, a fõrgɛ mɛ.

ref: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
text: La fɩtladõma zozo'e n daan boe saazuum deto batã dãana la tɩ tõma lagsɛ a pʋam na.

ref: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
text: Tɩ budib-polle yʋ'ʋrɛ n de A'etikusi zẽa deego la takolle zuo. La Apolɩ n tɔgɛ yuuge la, tɩ gẽem yõkɛ ẽ, tɩ a gĩse. La a gĩse la gẽ-bergo ze'ele saazuum deego la tɩ tõma boe mĩ na lui tẽŋa. Tɩ ba zẽkɛ ẽ, tɩ a ki mɛ.

ref: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ sige yigle õbgɛ ẽ zãla a nu'usum, dee yetɩ: «Da fabla-ya, a vɔa mɛ.»

ref: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
text: A yele bɛla dee lebe zom ka fiise la borborɩ obe, dee tɔgɛ la ba yuuge halɩ ka paagɛ bewiine, dee yãŋa fõrgɛ.

ref: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
text: La budibla la vɔa mɛ, tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ kule. Tɩ la ẽŋɛ nẽrba la sũure yẽlʋm halɩ zozo'e. Apolɩ sẽŋɛ Milɛtɩ

ref: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
text: La tõma kẽ la õorŋɔ dẽŋɛ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ Asɔsɩ, a daan yetɩ a sẽŋɛ la nãma wa yakɛ tõma bilam.

ref: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
text: La ẽn yakɛ tõma Asɔsɩ la, a kẽ la õorŋɔ, tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ Mitilɛnɩ.

ref: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
text: La tõma ze'ele la bilam doose ko'om na pʋam, tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Kiyo zanõ-bɔba. Tɩ beere le wiige, tɩ tõma doose Samõsɩ. Tɩ beere le wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Milɛtɩ.

ref: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
text: La Apolɩ daan looge yɛm tɩ a ka gõlgɛ sẽŋɛ la Efɛɛzɩ, se'ere n soe la, a daan ka boorɩ tɩ a gʋ'ʋm a mẽŋa Azi so'olʋm na pʋam, a daan kãblɩ mɛ tɩ a paagɛ Zerizalɛm dee tɩ Pãntekɔtɩ daarɛ la paagɛ. Apolɩ tɔgɛ bɔ Efɛɛzɩ kẽma

ref: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele Milɛtɩ soolʋm Efɛɛzɩ yetɩ, ba wi Azezi nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la kẽma tɩ ba wa'am.

ref: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
text: La ban paagɛ a zẽ'am na, a yele ba yetɩ: «Yãma mi mam daan ãn se'em, wakat-sɛka wuu tɩ mam daan boe la yãma la mɛ, lan pĩ'ilum na daar-sɛka tɩ mam wa'am Azi so'olʋm pʋam na.

ref: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
text: Mam sike m mẽŋa mɛ tõm Nawẽnnɛ tõoma la nintãm, baa nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ mam yẽ Zifdõma n dolɩ mam nãma la pʋam.

ref: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
text: Mam mõolɛ bõn-sɛba n wʋn sõŋɛ ya mɛ pa'alɛ ya nẽrba sʋkam la ya yiɛ pʋam, ka sʋgɛ sɛla sɛla.

ref: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
text: Mam tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ mɛ yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, sakɛ doose Nawẽnnɛ, dee bɔ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra.

ref: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa nõorɛ mɛ yetɩ m sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm, la mam ka mi bõn-sɛka n wʋn ka ẽŋɛ mam mĩ.

ref: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
text: La mam n mi se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ mam mɛ tẽn-sɛba wuu pʋam tɩ mam sẽŋɛ la, tɩ ba wʋn nãmsɛ mam mɛ dee kẽ'esɛ mam yʋ'a deem.

ref: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: La mam ka geele m mẽŋa yõvore la tɩ a tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ mam, sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ mam de la mam yãŋɛ tõm m tõonɛ na tɩ Zuudãana Azezi bɔ mam na ba'asɛ. Tõon-ẽŋa de la mam mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na kõ-yẽlga.

ref: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam mi tɩ ya kãn le yẽ mam, yãma sɛba wuu tɩ mam mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yɛla ya zẽ'am na.

ref: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
text: Bɛla ĩyã mam yetɩ ya mɛ zĩna, tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka sãn koŋe vɔm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a taalɛ ka boe mam zuo.

ref: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na wuu bɔ ya mɛ, ka sʋgɛ sɛla sɛla.

ref: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
text: Tara-ya ya mẽŋa, la nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa gu'ule ya la sõŋa. Bɩsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la tɩ a fãagɛ la a Bia zɩɩm tɩ ba dɛna a mẽŋa nẽrba la sõŋa.

ref: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
text: Mam mi tɩ mam sãn fõrgɛ deege, nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ n wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa sã'am ya, wʋ saasaadõma n yõgrɩ dũnsi se'em na.

ref: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
text: La nẽrba basɛba wʋn wa isge ya pʋam tɔgra põmpɔrŋɔ yetɔga, pã'asɛ karẽnbiisi la tɩ ba doose ba.

ref: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, guuse-ya ya mẽŋa, dee tẽra tɩ yʋʋma atã, mam kã'an ya nẽra woo wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ mɛ, la nintãm ka basɛ.

ref: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam dɩkɛ ya ẽŋɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum, la a yel-sõnnɛ yetɔga la pʋam. Ẽŋa n tã wʋn sõŋɛ ya tɩ ya yẽ pãŋa pa'asɛ, dee wʋn botɩ ya po lagɩm to'oge bõn-sõma tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la.

ref: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
text: Mam daan ka tarɩ yɛmleego la nẽra nẽra ligri, la a sãlma, bɩɩ a futo.

ref: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
text: Ya mẽŋa mi tɩ mam dɩkɛ la m nu'usi tõm bɩsɛ m mẽŋa la sɛba n dolɩ mam na.

ref: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
text: Mam ẽŋɛ bɛla wuu pa'alɛ ya tɩ dẽnɩ ya tõm sõŋɛ tarɩmdõma. La ya tẽra Zuudãana Azezi n yele se'em na, a daan yetɩ: ‹Sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ tarɩ sũ-yẽlga zozo'e gãnna sɛka n to'orɩ.› »

ref: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
text: La Apolɩ n daan tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, ẽŋa la ba wuu ka la dũma tẽŋa, tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
text: Tɩ ba wuu tĩi kɛlla, dee kɔkɛ Apolɩ mõbgɛ ẽ.

ref: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
text: Ba sũure n daan sã'am zozo'e, ẽn yetɩ ba kãn le yẽ ẽ na ĩyã. La ba kɔlgɛ ẽ mɛ sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ õorŋɔ la zẽ'am.

ref: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
text: La bilam tɩ tõma yẽ õorŋɔ ayẽma n we Fenisi. Tɩ tõma kẽ sẽŋɛ.

ref: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
text: La tõma daan sẽŋɛ mɛ ka tũule yẽta Sipri, la tõma daan basɛ Sipri tẽŋa la gɔbga bɔba, dee tole ka paagɛ Siiri so'olʋm sige Tiiri. Bilam tɩ ba yetɩ ba sike õorŋɔ la lɔgrɔ.

ref: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
text: La dabsa ayopɔɩ la daarɛ, tɩ tõma isge tɩ tɩ tole, tɩ bãma la ba pɔgba, la ba kɔma wuu kɔlgɛ tõma yese tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa. Tɩ tõma wuu ka dũma ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ,

ref: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
text: dee keese taaba. Tɩ tõma yãŋa kẽ õorŋɔ, dee tɩ ba lebe kulla.

ref: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
text: La tõma ze'ele Tiiri sẽŋɛ la Tolemasɩ ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Azezi karẽnbi-sɛba n boe bilam na, dee ẽŋɛ daarɛ ayɩla ba zẽ'am.

ref: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ Sezaare ka sige Afilipi yire kẽ'era bilam. Afilipi daan de la nẽrba bayopɔɩ la tɩ ba daan looge Zerizalɛm na nẽra ayɩla, a daan mõolɩ la Azezi kõ-yẽlga.

ref: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
text: Tõma daan ẽŋɛ bilam na dabsa faa, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agabisi ze'ele Zude wa'am,

ref: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
text: wa paam tõma zẽ'am wa fooge Apolɩ sagãnnɛ lʋ a mẽŋa nãma la a nu'usi, dee yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yele la wãna yetɩ: ‹Zifdõma wʋn lʋ sagãn-ẽna wã dãana la wãna Zerizalɛm, dɩkɛ ẽ ẽŋɛ bu-zãŋsɩ nu'usum.› »

ref: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
text: La tõma n wõm bɛla la, tɩ tõma la nẽr-sɛba n boe bilam na, daan bɛlna Apolɩ yetɩ, a da sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

ref: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
text: Tɩ a lerge tõma yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ ya kaasrɩ sã'ana m sũure? Mam sakɛ mɛ tɩ ba lʋ ma, la dagɩ bɛla mã'a, baa mam sãn wʋn ki Zerizalɛm Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã, mam ken sakɛ mɛ.»

ref: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
text: La tõma n kãn yãŋɛ botɩ a sakɛ bɔ tõma la ĩyã, tɩ tõma koŋe yele ẽ yetɩ: «Zuudãana n boorɩ se'em ẽŋɛ.»

ref: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ tõma daan yãŋa mãasʋm wɛ'ɛsa Zerizalɛm.

ref: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
text: Tɩ Sezaare tẽŋa karẽnbiisi basɛba kɔlgɛ tõma sẽŋɛ Aminasõ yire, bilam tɩ tõma wʋn ka gã'arɛ. Aminasõ de la Sipri nẽra, a de la Azezi karẽnbia halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm. Apolɩ sõsɛ la kẽma Azakɩ yire

ref: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
text: La tõma n paagɛ Zerizalɛm na, tɩ sɔbiisi la to'oge tõma la sũ-yẽlga.

ref: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
text: La ban wõm bɛla la, ba pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, dee yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Tɩ sɔbia, fõn yẽ Zifdõma tʋsa tʋsa n bɔ Azezi sɩra se'em dee mõrgɛ sakra Amoyisi lɔɔ sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
text: Ba wõm tɩ ba yetɩ, fõn zãsnɩ Zifdõma sɛba wuu n boe bu-zãŋsɩ tẽnsʋm na, yɛta tɩ ba basɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ, la ba da wãta ba kɔma, la ba da dɔla Zifdõma dɔgmike.

ref: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
text: Ba wʋn wõm tɩ fõn wa'am mɛ, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne?

ref: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
text: Ẽŋɛ tõma n wʋn yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ se'em na. Nẽrba banaasɩ n boe tõma zẽ'am kalam bĩŋe nõorɛ la Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
text: Tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ ka lagɩm na ba malgɛ fʋ mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dee yɔ, ba wʋn yɔ se'em na bɔ ba tɩ ba siige ba zuto. Bɛla wʋn botɩ nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ tɩ fõn mẽŋa dolɩ la Amoyisi lɔɔ la, la ban tɔgɛ fʋ yelle se'em na wuu dagɩ sɩra.

ref: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: La sãn dɛna bu-zãŋsɩ la n bɔ Azezi sɩra la yelle, tõma daan looge yɛm gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ yele ba yetɩ: ‹Ba da dita di-sɛba tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ baga, la ba da di zɩɩm, la ba da di sɛla tɩ ba tẽgɛ kʋ. La ba da tõnna yalsɩ tõoma.› »

ref: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
text: La dabsa ayopɔɩ la n dee fẽe la a ba'asɛ la, tɩ Zifdõma ze'ele Azi so'olʋm wa'am wa yẽ Apolɩ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, botɩ nẽrba la sũnsõa yĩige la Apolɩ, tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ,

ref: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
text: dee tĩi tɔgra yetɩ: «Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, sõŋɛ-ya tɔ! Bʋra-ẽna wã gilgri la tẽnsɩ wuu zãsna nẽrba tɩ ba sɩ'ɩsra Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma ka dɔla Amoyisi lɔɔ, dee tɔgra Wẽnde-kãtɛ la tɔg-be'ero. A tarɩ la nẽrba n dagɩ Zifdõma wa'am wa kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam dẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la.»

ref: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
text: Se'ere n soe la, ba daan yẽ la Efɛɛzɩ nẽra Atrofim n dolɩ Apolɩ tẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ a doose ẽ kẽ la Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam.

ref: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
text: Tɩ tẽŋa la daan gire, tɩ nẽrba wuu zoe lagsɛ taaba yõkɛ Apolɩ ve'ege ẽ Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yese. Dee yʋ zanõa la tɔtɔ.

ref: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
text: Tɩ ba daan zɩgra tɩ ba kʋ Apolɩ. Tɩ Rom sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma wõm tɩ Zerizalɛm tẽŋa wuu gire mɛ.

ref: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text: Tɩ a kɔ'ɔn doose sʋdaasɩ, la ba kɛ'ɛndõma tɩ ba zoe sẽŋɛ nẽrba la zẽ'am. La ban yẽ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma, la sʋdaasɩ la, tɩ ba basɛ ka le wẽ'era Apolɩ.

ref: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la paagɛ yõkɛ Apolɩ, dee bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba ba ẽ na bãnsɩ bayi. Dee tɩ a soke bãŋɛ ẽn de nẽr-sɛka, la ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ sɛla.

ref: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
text: Tɩ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la tãsra yẽma yẽma, halɩ tĩi vɔɔla tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la ka tã'agɛ wõm sɛla n ẽŋɛ, tɩ a bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ bãma yi-kãtɛ la pʋam.

ref: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
text: La ban tarɩ ẽ paagɛ bĩmbĩna la n zonnɩ kẽ'era deego la pʋam na, ba buke ẽ mɛ, nẽrba la n ba gita la ĩyã.

ref: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
text: Tɩ ba wuu dɔla tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Kʋ-ya ẽ.» Apolɩ tɔgɛ tɩ a to'oge a mẽŋa (Tõoma

ref: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
text: La ban yetɩ ba kẽ'esɛ Apolɩ sʋdaasɩ la yire la, tɩ Apolɩ soke sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la yetɩ: «Mam tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ m yele fʋ sɛla mɛ bɩɩ?» Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn wõnnɩ Grɛkɩ yetɔga mɛ?

ref: “Aren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led
text: Bɛla fõn dagɩ Ezipti nẽr-sɛka n daan sa'asɛ nẽrba la gɔmna, dee daan doose nẽr-kʋʋrba tʋsanaasɩ sẽŋɛ weem na?»

ref: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Ayɛɩ, mam de la Zifu, mam ze'ele la Tarsɩ n boe Silisi so'olʋm. Mam de la tẽn-ẽŋa n tarɩ yõorɔ la nẽra. Mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ, bɔ mam sore tɩ m tɔgɛ la nẽrba la.»

ref: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
text: Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la bɔ ẽ nõorɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele bĩmbĩna la zuo zẽkɛ a nu'ugo. Tɩ nẽrba la ãna sĩm. Tɩ a tɔgɛ Ebre n de ba mẽŋa yetɔga bɔ ba.

ref: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
text: A yele ba yetɩ: «M sɔdõma la m sɔbiisi, selse-ya mam n yetɩ m tɔgɛ se'em to'oge m mẽŋa ya nẽŋam.»

ref: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
text: Ban wõm tɩ a tɔgrɩ la Ebre yetɔga bɔ'ɔra ba la, tɩ ba go'oge malɩn sɛlsra ẽ. Tɩ a yetɩ:

ref: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
text: «Mam de la Zifu, ba dɔgɛ mam na Tarsɩ n boe Silisi so'olʋm pʋam na, uge mam Zerizalɛm kalam. Tɩ Agamalɩyɛlɩ zãsʋm mam tɩ yaabdõma lɔɔ la sõŋa sõŋa. Mam daan wɩsgɛ m mẽŋa mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ, wʋ yãma n wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa se'em zĩna wã.

ref: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
text: La mam daan nãmsɛ sɛba n dolɩ Azezi sore la mɛ zozo'e, yõgɛ bʋraasɩ la pɔgsɩ botɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deem, dee kʋ basɛba.

ref: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
text: Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la kẽma la wuu n tã wʋn yetɩ, mam n tɔgɛ se'em na de la sɩra. Mam daan to'oge gõnnɔ mɛ ba zẽ'am wɛ'ɛsa tɩ m ka bɔ Zifdõma sɛba n boe Damaasɩ la, dee yõgɛ so-ẽŋa karẽnbiisi la n boe mĩ na, tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm ka nãmsɛ ba.

ref: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
text: La mam daan boe la sore wɛ'ɛsa ka lɛm Damaasɩ, la daan masɛ wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa wẽnnɛ zuu-tẽŋasʋka, tɩ peelem daan ze'ele saazuum darɛ wa nẽegɛ zozo'e gilge mam.

ref: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Tɩ mam lui tẽŋa, wõm kõa n yele mam yetɩ: ‹Asolɩ, Asolɩ, bẽm tɩ fʋ nãmsra mam?›

ref: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
text: Tɩ mam lerge yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, fõn de la ãne?› Tɩ a lerge mam yetɩ: ‹Mam de la Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la tɩ fʋ nãmsrɩ.›

ref: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
text: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n daan dolɩ mam na yẽ peelem na, dee ka wõm kõa la n tɔgrɩ la mam na.

ref: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
text: Tɩ mam soke yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, fõn yetɩ m ẽŋɛ la wãne?› Tɩ Zuudãana la yele mam yetɩ: ‹Isge sẽŋɛ Damaasɩ, tɩ ba wʋn ka yele fo sɛla wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ fʋ ẽŋɛ.›

ref: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
text: La peelem na yãlgɛ mam nini mɛ tɩ mam ka yẽta. Tɩ sɛba n daan dolɩ mam na ve'ege mam tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ Damaasɩ.

ref: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
text: La bʋraa n daan boe mĩ tɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Ananiyasɩ. A daan sakrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n boe Damaasɩ la wuu tɔgra a yelle sõŋa.

ref: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
text: Tɩ a wa'am wa ze'ele lɛm mam zẽ'a yele mam yetɩ: ‹M sɔbia Asolɩ, le yẽta.› Tɩ mam nini le yẽta wakat-ẽŋa mẽŋa, tɩ mam yẽ ẽ.

ref: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
text: Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: ‹Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la n looge fo tɩ fʋ bãŋɛ ẽn boorɩ se'em, la fʋ yẽ sɩra dãana la, dee tɩ a tɔgɛ la a mẽŋa nõorɛ bɔ fɔ,

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
text: tɩ fʋ wʋn dɛna a kaset-dãana yele nẽrba wuu, fõn yẽ dee wõm se'em na.

ref: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, bẽm tɩ fõn gʋ'ʋna? Isge pʋ'ʋsɛ la Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ, botɩ ba mise fʋ ko'om pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ fʋ tõon-be'ero taalɛ.›

ref: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
text: La bɛla poorʋm mam daan lebe la Zerizalɛm ka bɔna Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam pʋ'ʋsra, yẽ Zuudãana wʋ zãasŋɔ la,

ref: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
text: tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: ‹Ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ yese Zerizalɛm, se'ere n soe la, tẽŋa la nẽrba kãn sakɛ fõn wʋn tɔgɛ mam yelle bɔ ba la.›

ref: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
text: Tɩ mam lerge ẽ yetɩ: ‹M Zuudãana, nẽrba wã mi mɛ tɩ mam n daan sẽnnɩ ba wẽndeto pʋam yõgra nẽr-sɛba n bɔ fõn sɩra la, botɩ ba wẽ'era ba, dee kẽ'esra ba yʋ'a deto pʋam.

ref: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
text: La wakat-sɛka tɩ ba daan kʋ A'etɩɛnɩ n tɔgɛ fõn yɛla bɔ nẽrba la, mam daan boe mĩ mɛ sakɛ tɩ a kũm na masɛ mɛ, dee daan gu'ura sɛba n kʋ ẽ na futo.›

ref: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana la yele mam yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ, mam yetɩ m tõm fʋ la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am zãazãarɛ.› »

ref: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
text: La nẽrba la daan selsrɩ Apolɩ yetɔga la mɛ, la ẽn wa tɔgɛ bu-zãŋsɩ yelle la, tɩ ba tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Kʋ-ya ẽ, nẽr-ẽna ka narɩ a bɔna dũnia zuo.»

ref: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
text: Tɩ ba kaasra tagursi yɛɛsra ba futo lɔbra, dee vaara tẽntõnnɔ lɔbra saazuum.

ref: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba botɩ Apolɩ kẽ bãma yi-kãtɛ la pʋam tɩ ba wẽ ẽ na ka'asa, dee soke ẽ bãŋɛ, bẽm n soe tɩ ba tĩi kaasra ẽŋa ĩyã bɛla.

ref: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
text: Tɩ ba lʋ ẽ yetɩ ba wẽ ẽ na ka'asa, tɩ a soke sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n ze mĩ na yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ ba wẽ Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra dee nãn ka di a sarɩya?»

ref: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
text: La sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n wõm bɛla la, tɩ a sẽŋɛ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fʋ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Bʋraa wã de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra.»

ref: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la sẽŋɛ Apolɩ zẽ'am ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Yele ma: Fõn sɩrɩ de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra?» Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «Ẽe!»

ref: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la yetɩ: «Mam mẽŋa yɔ la ligri zozo'e dee lebge Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra.» Tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ: «Ba kɔ'ɔn dɔgɛ mam tɩ mam dɛna la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra.»

ref: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
text: Tɩ sɛba n yetɩ ba wẽ ẽ na ka'asa la vuta lɛbra poorʋm. Tɩ dabeem yõkɛ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la, ẽŋa n bãŋɛ tɩ Apolɩ de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra dee tɩ ẽŋa botɩ ba ba ẽ na bãnsɩ la ĩyã. Apolɩ ze Zifdõma kẽma nẽŋam

ref: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
text: La sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la daan boorɩ tɩ a bãŋɛ la sɩra sɩra yel-sɛka n botɩ Zifdõma la dõrɛ ẽ. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a ba'agɛ Apolɩ basɛ. Dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma n po dita sarɩya la wuu lagsɛ. Tɩ ba lagsɛ. Tɩ a tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ka ze'ele ba nẽŋam.

ref: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ bɩsra sarɩyaditba la zĩi, dee yele yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam n tõm m tõoma la wuu Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam wa paam zĩna, mam bãŋɛ m sũure pʋam mɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa.»

ref: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Ananiyasɩ bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n ze lɛm Apolɩ la wẽ a nõorɛ.

ref: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ yele ẽ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wẽ fɔ. Fʋ ãn wʋ dagoone tɩ ba saasɛ, tɩ a zuum mã'a ãna sõŋa la, fõn zĩ tɩ fʋ di mam sarɩya wʋ lɔɔ n yele se'em na, la fõn sã'am lɔɔ la mɛ, fõn bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba wẽ mam na.»

ref: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
text: Tɩ sɛba n ze lɛm Apolɩ la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn tʋʋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana?»

ref: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
text: Tɩ a lerge yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam ka mi tɩ a de la Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Da tɔgɛ fʋ buuri nẽŋadãana be'em.› »

ref: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ bãŋɛ tɩ sarɩyaditba la basɛba de la Saduse dõma, dee tɩ basɛba mẽ dɛna Farisi dõma, tɩ a tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele ba wuu yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam de la Farisi nẽra, mam sɔ de la Farisi mẽŋa mẽŋa. Mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ sɛba n kiiri la wʋn le vo'oge mɛ na, bɛla ĩyã tɩ ba sãm mam.»

ref: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
text: Ẽn yele bɛla la, tɩ Farisi dõma la Saduse dõma wẽ'era nõkɛ'ɛma la taaba, tɩ nẽrba la welse.

ref: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
text: Se'ere n soe la, Saduse dõma la ka sakɛ tɩ kũm wʋn vo'oge, la ba ka sakɛ tɩ malɛkadõma la sɩɩsɩ bɔna, la Farisi dõma sakɛ bɛla wuu mɛ.

ref: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
text: Tɩ lɔɔ karẽnsãandõma basɛba n de Farisi dõma la isge yãbra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Tõma ka yẽ bʋra-ẽna wã tõon-be'ego. Tã tɩ la dɛna la sɩa, bɩɩ malɛka n tɔgɛ bɔ ẽ.»

ref: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
text: Tɩ nõke'ene na wẽ'a daan zo'oge gãŋɛ. Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la zɔta dabeem yetɩ, ba na'an wʋn ãasʋm Apolɩ. Bɛla tɩ a bɔ sʋdaasɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, ba sẽŋɛ ka to'oge ẽ nẽrba la zẽ'am, tarɩ ẽ wa'am wa yʋ sʋdaasɩ la yire.

ref: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
text: Tɩ daar-ẽŋa yʋ'ʋŋɔ, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi wa'am Apolɩ zẽ'am wa yele ẽ yetɩ: «Keŋe fʋ sũure, tɩ fõn tɔgɛ mam yɛla Zerizalɛm kalam se'em na, dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ ken tɔgɛ mam yɛla Rom tẽŋam bɛla.» Ba eerɩ sore tɩ ba kʋ Apolɩ

ref: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
text: La ba daan sẽŋɛ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la kẽma la zẽ'am ka yetɩ: «Tõma lagsɛ mɛ pɔɩ yetɩ, tõma kãn di sɛla, sãn dagna tɩ tõma kʋ la Apolɩ.

ref: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã, yãma sarɩyaditba la wuu, tõm-ya tɩ ba ka yele sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la tɩ a tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am ya zẽ'am, dee ẽŋɛ-ya wʋ ya boorɩ tɩ ya soke bãŋɛ la a yelle la pa'asɛ, tɩ tõma mãasʋm mɛ tɩ kʋ ẽ tɩ a nãn ka paam.»

ref: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ tã dayʋa bãŋɛ ban bãm yele se'em na, sẽŋɛ sʋdaasɩ la yire ka yele Apolɩ.

ref: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ wi sʋda-kɛ'ɛndõma la ayɩla yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tarɩ budib-ẽna wã sẽŋɛ ya kɛ'ɛma la zẽ'am, tɩ a tarɩ la sɛla tɩ a yele ẽ.»

ref: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
text: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ba kɛ'ɛma la zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Apolɩ tɩ ba yʋ la n wi mam yetɩ, m tarɩ budib-ẽna wã wa'am fʋ zẽ'am, tɩ a tarɩ la sɛla tɩ a yele fo.»

ref: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
text: Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la gʋrɛ a nu'ugo tarɩ ẽ yese kẽnkɛrŋa ka soke ẽ yetɩ: «Fõn tarɩ la bẽm yelle tɩ fʋ yele mam?»

ref: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
text: Tɩ a lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Zifdõma n bãm yetɩ, bãma wʋn sose fõn beesã tɩ fʋ tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am, wʋ bãma boorɩ tɩ ba bɩsɛ la a yelle la pa'asɛ.

ref: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
text: Tɩ kɛ'ɛma la yele budibla la yetɩ: «Da yele nẽra tɩ fõn yele mam sɛla.» Dee botɩ a lebe. Ba tarɩ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ ka ẽŋɛ gɔmna Afeligsi nu'usum

ref: The commander called in two of his officers and told them, “By nine o'clock tonight have
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma kɛ'ɛma la wi a sʋda-kɛ'ɛndõma bayi yele ba yetɩ: «Basɛ-ya tɩ sʋdaasɩ kɔbsyi, la wi-bã'arba pisyopɔɩ, la kãn-lɔbrɩba kɔbsyi mãasʋm. Tɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ ẽna wã karf-awɛɩ ya sẽŋɛ Sezaare.

ref: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
text: Mãasʋm-ya wiiri bɔ Apolɩ tɩ a bã tɩ ya tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ gɔmna Afeligsi zẽ'am la ĩmã'asʋm.»

ref: The commander wrote a letter that said:
text: Dee gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ yetɩ:

ref: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
text: «Mam Akloorɩ-lisiasɩ n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra fõn gɔmna sõŋɔ Afeligsi. Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ.

ref: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
text: Zifdõma n daan yõkɛ bʋra-ẽna wã bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ ẽ. Tɩ mam wõm tɩ a de la Rom tẽŋa nu'ugo nẽra, tɩ mam sẽŋɛ la sʋdaasɩ ka fãagɛ ẽ,

ref: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
text: dee bɔɔra tɩ m bãŋɛ bõn-sɛka n soe tɩ ba dõrɛ ẽ tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am.

ref: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
text: La mam daan yẽ tɩ la de la Zifdõma lɔɔ yɛla ĩyã, dee a ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n narɩ ba kʋ ẽ, bɩɩ ba yʋ ẽ yʋ'a deem.

ref: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
text: Tɩ ba yele mam yetɩ, Zifdõma bãm mɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ, bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam ẽŋɛ tɔtɔ tõm tɩ ba tarɩ ẽ wa'am fõn zẽ'am, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n sãm ẽ na wa'am fõn zẽ'am wa tɔgɛ ẽn ẽŋɛ se'em.»

ref: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la ẽŋɛ ẽn yele ba se'em na, tarɩ Apolɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ ẽŋa sẽŋɛ Antipatrisi.

ref: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ sʋda-sɛba n sẽnnɩ nãma la lebe ba yire la, dee tɩ sɛba n bã wiiri la tarɩ Apolɩ sẽŋɛ.

ref: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
text: La ban paagɛ Sezaare la, ba dɩkɛ gõŋɔ la mɛ bɔ gɔmna la dee dɩkɛ Apolɩ ẽŋɛ a nu'usum.

ref: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
text: Tɩ a karẽŋɛ gõŋɔ la, dee soke yetɩ, ẽŋa yese la so'olʋm kãna? Tɩ Apolɩ pa'alɛ ẽ tɩ ẽŋa yese la Silisi so'olʋm.

ref: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
text: Tɩ gɔmna la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sɛba n tarɩ fʋ wa'am sãm na sãn paam, mam wʋn soke bãŋɛ lan ẽŋɛ se'em.» A yele bɛla dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba kẽ'esɛ ẽ naba A'erɔdɩ yire tɩ ba gu'ura ẽ.

ref: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
text: La dabsa anuu daarɛ, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana Ananiyasɩ doose la kẽma basɛba, la yetɔg-mita yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Atɛrtuli wa'am Sezaare. Tɩ ba dɩkɛ Apolɩ yelle la tɩ ba tɔgɛ bɔ gɔmna la.

ref: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
text: Tɩ ba wi Apolɩ tɩ a wa'am. Tɩ Atɛrtuli yãŋa tɔgra dõta Apolɩ yetɩ: «Gɔmna sõŋɔ Afeligsi. La de la fõn bɩsrɩ tõma sõŋa la ĩyã tɩ tẽŋa la tara laafɛ, tɩ yɛla zozo'e teege tɩ tẽŋa la malgɛ.

ref: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
text: Fõn ẽŋɛ sɛla wuu zẽ'esɩ wuu bɔ tõma la, tõma to'oge mɛ pʋ'ʋsra fʋ zozo'e.

ref: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
text: La mam ka boorɩ tɩ m gʋ'ʋm fɔ, bɛla mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ fɔ ẽŋɛ sugri dee selse m yetɔga wã fẽfẽe.

ref: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
text: Tõma yẽ nẽr-ẽna tɩ a de la tẽn-wã'ara, ẽŋa n sa'asrɩ Zifdõma dũnia wuu zuo tɩ ba gita tẽnsɩ. A de la Nazarɛtɩ dõma põmpɔrŋɔ sore la nẽŋadãana.

ref: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
text: dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n sãm ẽ na wa'am fõn zẽ'am.] Fʋ sãn soke ẽ fʋ mẽŋa, fʋ wʋn wõm bõn-sɛba wuu n botɩ tõma sãm ẽ.»

ref: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
text: Atɛrtuli n tɔgɛ ba'asɛ la, tɩ Zifdõma la lagɩm sakɛ yetɩ, la de la bɛla. Apolɩ tɔgɛ po'oge a mẽŋa Afeligsi nẽŋam

ref: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
text: Zifdõma la n tɔgɛ kɩ'ɩlɩm na, tɩ gɔmna la bɔ Apolɩ nõorɛ tɩ a tɔgɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ: «Mam mi tɩ fõn de la sarɩyadita so'olʋm ẽna wã pʋam halɩ yʋʋma n zo'oge. Bɛla mam sũure ãn yẽlʋm tɩ m tɔgɛ to'oge m mẽŋa.

ref: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
text: Fõn tã wʋn bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ la ka gãŋɛ dabsa pia la ayi tɩ mam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm tɩ m ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
text: La Zifdõma la ka yẽ mam tɩ mam wẽ'erɩ nõke'ene la nẽra Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, la ba ken ka yẽ tɩ mam sa'asrɩ nẽrba ba wẽndeto la pʋam bɩɩ tẽŋa la zẽ'e-sɛka.

ref: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
text: La ba kãn yãŋɛ yẽ sɛla pa'alɛ tɩ bãma n yetɩ mam ẽŋɛ se'em na de sɩra.

ref: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
text: La mam yetɩ fʋ tɩ mam sɩrɩ dolɩ la so-sɛka tɩ ba yetɩ põmpɔrŋɔ sore la. La mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ tõma yaabdõma yʋʋn pʋ'ʋsrɩ la, dee sakra sɛla wuu n gʋlsɛ bɔna Amoyisi lɔɔ pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na.

ref: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
text: Mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la Nawẽnnɛ wʋ bãma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ se'em na, tɩ nẽrba wuu, nẽr-sõma la nẽr-bɛ'ɛsɩ wʋn vo'oge mɛ.

ref: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
text: Bɛla tɩ mam mõrgra daarɛ woo tɩ m bãŋɛ m sũure pʋam tɩ mam ka ẽŋɛ be'em Nawẽnnɛ la nẽrba nifum.

ref: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
text: La de la yʋʋma zozo'e tɩ mam daan ka boe Zerizalɛm, dee yãŋa tarɩ ligri leme tɩ m sõŋɛ m tẽŋa nẽrba, dee bɔ bɔ'a tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
text: Mam daan boe mĩ ɩta la bɛla, tɩ ba yẽ mam Wẽnde-kãtʋm na, tɩ mam daan põn malgɛ m mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am kɩ'ɩlɩm mɛ, la nẽrba daan ka boe la mam mĩ zo'oge, la ba mẽ daan ka gire mĩ.

ref: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
text: La Zifdõma basɛba n ze'ele Azi so'olʋm n daan boe mĩ, ba sãn tara nɩ na yelle la mam, ba mẽŋa n wʋn wa'am nɩ wa yele fʋ mam n ẽŋɛ se'em.

ref: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
text: La nẽr-bãna wã sãn yẽ mam tõon-be'ego, wakat-sɛka tɩ mam daan boe ba sarɩyadia zẽ'am na, ba yele.

ref: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
text: La ba kãn yẽ sɛla, sãn dagna mam daan ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka tɔgɛ ba ke'enke'em yetɩ: ‹La de la mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge mɛ na ĩyã, tɩ mam zɛ ya nẽŋam tɩ ya dita mam sarɩya zĩna wã.› »

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
text: La Afeligsi n daan põn mina kõ-yẽlga la sore yelle la, tɩ a viige yetɔga la, dee yele ba yetɩ: «Sʋda-kɛ'ɛma Alisiasɩ sãn paam, mam wʋn kʋ'ʋgɛ yetɔga la bɔ ya.»

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: Dee bɔ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la n gu'uri Apolɩ la nõorɛ yetɩ, a bɩsra ẽ, la a bɔ ẽ yolsgo, la a sɩrdõma sãn tara sɛla bɔɔra tɩ ba bɔ ẽ, a basɛ tɩ ba bɔ ẽ. Apolɩ ze Afeligsi la Adrisili nẽŋam

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: La dabsa fẽe n tole, tɩ Afeligsi lagɩm na a pɔga Adrisili n de Zifu botɩ ba wi Apolɩ, tɩ a wa'am wa tɔgra nẽra n wʋn bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra se'em yelle, tɩ ba sɛlsra.

ref: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
text: La Apolɩ n yãŋa wa tɔgra ɩrgɔ n masɛ, la yõk-m-mẽŋa, la sarɩyadia n sẽm yelle, tɩ dabeem yõkɛ Afeligsi, tɩ a yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Basɛ bɛla, mam sãn wa yẽ nẽŋa, mam wʋn le wi fo.»

ref: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
text: A daan tẽ'esrɩ tɩ Apolɩ na'an wʋn bɔ ẽ na ligri, bɛla ĩyã tɩ a ẽn wi'ira ẽ sõsra la ẽ lɛ.

ref: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
text: Tɩ yʋʋma ayi tole, tɩ Apɔrsusi-fɛstusi yãŋa sɔɔgɛ Afeligsi zẽ'am. La Afeligsi daan boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ãn Zifdõma sũure yẽlʋm, bɛla ĩyã tɩ a basɛ tɩ Apolɩ ken bɔna yʋ'a deem.

ref: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
text: Afɛstusi n paagɛ to'oge na'am a so'olʋm na pʋam na, a ẽŋɛ la dabsa atã Sezaare, dee ze'ele bilam sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

ref: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
text: Tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma, la Zifdõma nẽŋadõma wa'am a zẽ'am wa dõrɛ Apolɩ. Dee sose ẽ mõrgra

ref: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
text: yetɩ, a sõŋɛ ba botɩ ba tarɩ Apolɩ leme Zerizalɛm, se'ere n soe la, ba daan bãm bɔɔra tɩ ba sʋgɛ kʋ ẽ na sore.

ref: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
text: Tɩ Afɛstusi lerge yetɩ: «Apolɩ boe la yʋ'a deem Sezaare, la mam mẽŋa wʋn lebe la bilam lɛɛlɛ wã.

ref: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
text: La basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽŋadõma doose mam sẽŋɛ Sezaare, tɩ a sãn tõm na tõon-be'ego, ya tɔgɛ bilam.»

ref: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
text: Afɛstusi daan ka ẽŋɛ gãŋɛ dabsa anii bɩɩ dabsa pia ba zẽ'am dee lebe Sezaare. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ a kẽ zĩ'ire sarɩyadia zẽ'am, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am.

ref: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ kẽ, tɩ Zifdõma sɛba n ze'ele Zerizalɛm wa'am na isge gilge ẽ, dõta ẽ yetɩ, a tõm na tõon-be'ero zozo'e, la ba ka tã'agɛ pa'alɛ tɩ bãma n yele se'em na de la sɩra.

ref: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ tɩ a to'oge a mẽŋa yetɩ: «Mam ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Zifdõma lɔɔ, bɩɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la, la mam mẽ ka ẽŋɛ Rom na-keko la be'em.»

ref: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
text: Tɩ Afɛstusi bɔɔra tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn Zifdõma sũure yẽlʋm, soke Apolɩ yetɩ: «Fõn boorɩ tɩ fʋ sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm tɩ m ka di fʋ yelle wã sarɩya bilam mɛ bɩɩ?»

ref: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ lerge yetɩ: «Mam põn bɔna la nẽr-sɛka tɩ Rom na-keko bɔ ẽ nõorɛ la sarɩyadia zẽ'am, bɛla la masɛ tɩ ba di mam sarɩya la Rom. Fõn mẽŋa mi tɩ mam ka ẽŋɛ Zifdõma be'em.

ref: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
text: Mam sãn ẽŋɛ nɩ na be'em bɩɩ sɛla n masɛ tɩ ba kʋ mam, mam kãn zagsɛ. La ban dõrɛ mam se'em na dagɩ sɩra, nẽra ka tã wʋn dɩkɛ mam ẽŋɛ ba nu'usum. Mam boorɩ tɩ Rom na-keko la n di m sarɩya.»

ref: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
text: Tɩ Afɛstusi la a poorʋm dõma yẽ taaba, dee tɩ a lerge Apolɩ yetɩ: «Fõn yetɩ, fõn boorɩ tɩ Rom na-keko la n di fʋ sarɩya. Bɛla, fʋ wʋn sẽŋɛ la na-keko la zẽ'am.» Apolɩ boe Agripa la Abɛrnisi nẽŋam

ref: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
text: La dabsa fẽe n tole, tɩ naba Agripa la a tã Abɛrnisi sẽŋɛ Sezaare tɩ ba ka pʋ'ʋsɛ Afɛstusi.

ref: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
text: La ba yuuge mĩ na fẽe, tɩ Afɛstusi tɔgɛ Apolɩ yelle la bɔ naba la yetɩ: «Bʋraa n boe kalam tɩ Afeligsi basɛ tɩ a bɔna yʋ'a deem.

ref: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
text: La mam daan sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm, tɩ Zifdõma kẽma, la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma tarɩ a yelle wa'am wa tɔgɛ bɔ mam, dee sose mam yetɩ, m di a sarɩya botɩ ba sɩbgɛ ẽ.

ref: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
text: Tɩ mam lerge ba yetɩ, tõma Rom dõma ka dɩkrɩ nẽra ɩta nẽrba nu'usum tɩ ba sɩbgɛ ẽ, dee tɩ a nãn ka ze'ele sɛba n sãm ẽ na nẽŋam tɔgɛ a nõorɛ.

ref: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
text: La ba doose mam mɛ wa'am kalam, la mam ka gʋ'ʋm, beere n wiige, tɩ mam kẽ zĩ'ire sarɩyadia zẽ'am, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ bʋraa la wa'am.

ref: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
text: La sɛba n sãm ẽ na ka pa'alɛ tõon-be'ero sɛba tɩ a ẽŋɛ, wʋ mam n tẽ'esɛ se'em na.

ref: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
text: La de la nõke'ene tɩ ba wẽ'erɩ la ẽ, ba pʋ'ʋsgɔ sore yɛla ĩyã, la bʋraa yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azezi n ki, tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ a vo'oge mɛ ĩyã.

ref: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
text: Tɩ mam ka tã'agɛ bãŋɛ mam wʋn ẽŋɛ yel-ẽna wã se'em, tɩ mam soke Apolɩ yetɩ, ẽŋa boorɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ la Zerizalɛm tɩ m sẽŋɛ ka di a sarɩya la bilam?

ref: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ zagsɛ dee yetɩ, ba basɛ tɩ Rom na-keko la tɔgɛ a yetɔga la. Tɩ mam bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba gu'ura ẽ tɩ m wa botɩ ba tarɩ ẽ sẽŋɛ na-keko la zẽ'am.»

ref: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
text: Tɩ naba Agripa yele Afɛstusi yetɩ: «Mam mẽ boorɩ tɩ m wõm bʋra-ẽna wã yetɔga.» Tɩ Afɛstusi lerge yetɩ: «Beere sãn wiige, fʋ wʋn wõm a yetɔga.»

ref: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ Agripa la Abɛrnisi yɛ fõŋɔ lɔgrɔ wa'am wa kẽ sarɩyadia deem na, la sʋda-kɛ'ɛndõma, la tẽŋa la nẽŋadõma. Tɩ Afɛstusi bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, ba tarɩ Apolɩ wa'am.

ref: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
text: Dee yetɩ: «Naba Agripa, la sɛba wuu n lagsɛ wã, bɩsɛ-ya bʋra-ẽna wã tɩ Zifdõma wuu wa'am wa tɔgɛ a yelle la mam, Zerizalɛm la kalam, tĩi tɔgra yetɩ, la ka masɛ tɩ a ken vɔa.

ref: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
text: La mam yẽ tɩ a ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ a ki. La a mẽŋa yetɩ, ẽŋa boorɩ tɩ ba tarɩ a yetɔga la sẽŋɛ la na-keko zẽ'am. Tɩ mam tɔgɛ kʋ yetɩ, mam wʋn botɩ a sẽŋɛ.

ref: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
text: La mam ka tarɩ a yelle la zuo wʋn gʋlsɛ bɔ na-keko la. Bɛla tɩ mam botɩ ba tarɩ ẽ wa'am ya zẽ'am, la fõn naba Agripa mẽŋa nẽŋam, tɩ ya sãn sogse ẽ kɩ'ɩlɩm, mam wʋn yẽ yelle gʋlsɛ.

ref: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ka ãn sõŋa tɩ mam tarɩ nẽra ka bɔ na-keko la tɩ a di a sarɩya, dee ka pa'alɛ yel-sɛka ĩyã tɩ ba yõkɛ ẽ.»

ref: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
text: Tɩ Agripa yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Sore boe tɩ fʋ tɔgɛ.» Tɩ Apolɩ zẽkɛ a nu'ugo tɩ a tɔgɛ to'oge a mẽŋa, yetɩ:

ref: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
text: «Naba Agripa, mam sũure ãn yẽlʋm, mam n boe fʋ nẽŋam zĩna wã tɩ m tɔgɛ to'oge m mẽŋa la bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ Zifdõma dõrɛ mam na.

ref: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
text: Lan gãnnɩ wuu, mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga, fõn mi Zifdõma dɔgmike, la ba nõkɛ'ɛma la yelle sõŋa sõŋa la ĩyã. Bɛla mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ fʋ mã'agɛ fʋ mẽŋa selse m yetɔga wã.

ref: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
text: Ban mi mam la yuuge mɛ, ba sãn bɔɔra, ba tã wʋn ze'ele bɔ mam kaseto mɛ yetɩ, mam de la Farisi nẽra, bãma n sakrɩ tɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ yɛla sõŋa gãnna.

ref: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ mam ze sarɩyadia zẽ'am kalam wã, la de la mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma la ĩyã.

ref: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
text: Tõma buuri pia la ayi la tõnnɩ mɛ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ, dee tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ ẽn bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na wʋn ẽŋɛ mɛ. Naba Agripa, la de la pʋtẽ'e-ẽna wã ĩyã tɩ Zifdõma dõrɛ mam.

ref: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
text: Bẽm tɩ la dɛna toogo bɔ ya, tɩ ya sakɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'orɩ kũm?

ref: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
text: Mam mẽŋa daan tẽ'esɛ mɛ, tɩ dẽnɩ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em wuu sã'am Azezi n de Nazarɛtɩ nẽra la yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
text: Mam tõm na bɛla Zerizalɛm, la mam daan to'oge nõorɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am mɛ, yõgɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zozo'e botɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ba yʋ'a deem, di ba sarɩya yetɩ ba kʋ ba, tɩ mam daan sakɛ.

ref: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
text: La wakatɛ zozo'e tɩ mam daan nãmsɛ ba Zifdõma wẽndeto pʋam, mõrgra tɩ ba basɛ ba sore la. Tɩ mam sũure daan yĩige la ba halɩ tɩ mam doose tẽnsɩ basɛba nãmsɛ ba.

ref: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
text: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam daan to'oge nõorɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma zẽ'am tɩ m sẽŋɛ Damaasɩ.

ref: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
text: Naa, mam daan boe la sore wʋntɛɛŋa zuu-tẽŋasʋka wɛ'ɛsa, yẽ peelem n ze'ele saazuum yẽkɛ nẽegɛ gãnna wʋntɛɛŋa gilge mam, la sɛba n dolɩ mam na.

ref: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
text: Tɩ tõma wuu lui tẽŋa, tɩ mam wõm kõa n yele mam la Ebre yetɔga yetɩ: ‹Asolɩ, Asolɩ, bẽm tɩ fʋ nãmsra mam? Fʋ sãn ka sakɛ mam, la wʋn ãna toogo bɔ fʋ, wʋ dũŋa n ka nannɩ a dãana ka'asrɛ.›

ref: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Tɩ mam soke yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana, fõn de la ãne?› Tɩ Zuudãana la yele mam yetɩ: ‹Mam de la Azezi, sɛka tɩ fʋ nãmsrɩ la.

ref: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
text: La isge ze'ele, mam dɩkɛ m mẽŋa pa'alɛ fɔ tɩ m botɩ fʋ lebge la mam tõntõnna. Fʋ wʋn dɛna la mam kaset-dãana yele nẽrba fõn yẽ mam se'em, dee ken pa'alɛ ba bõn-sɛba tɩ mam wʋn botɩ fʋ yẽ la.

ref: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
text: La mam wʋn ken tõm fʋ la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am, tɩ fʋ ka pike ba nini yese ba lika pʋam tɩ ba vɔa peelem pʋam, dee yese ba Asũtãana nu'usum lebse ba Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum, tɩ ba bɔ mam sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba tõon-be'ero taalɛ, tɩ ba lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sõma yẽ pʋrɛ. La mam wʋn fãagɛ fʋ Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ nu'usum basɛ.› »

ref: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
text: Bɛla tɩ mam pɔsɛ Damaasɩ, la Zerizalɛm, la Zude so'olʋm wuu, la bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am mõolɛ yetɩ, ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, dee lebe sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee ẽŋɛ sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ ba teege yɛm.

ref: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
text: Bɛla tɩ Zifdõma daan yõkɛ mam Wẽnde-kãtʋm na yetɩ ba kʋ mam.

ref: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ sõŋɛ mam mɛ wa paam zĩna, bɛla tɩ mam zɛ kalam tɔgra mam n yẽ sɛla bɔ'ɔra yãma, kɛ'ɛndõma la tarɩmdõma wuu. Mam n yele sɛla wuu la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la Amoyisi n yʋʋn yetɩ sɛla wʋn ẽŋɛ la.

ref: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
text: Ba yʋʋn yetɩ, dẽnɩ tɩ Krisi nãmsɛ ki, la ẽŋa n wʋn dɛna pɔspɔsɩ nẽra n wʋn vo'oge, dee wʋn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga n de peelem bɔ Zifdõma la bu-zãŋsɩ.»

ref: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
text: La Apolɩ n tɔgrɩ bɛla tɩ a to'oge a mẽŋa la, tɩ Afɛstusi tãsɛ ẽ yetɩ: «Apolɩ, fõn zallɩ mɛ, fõn zãsʋm mɛ zo'oge tɩ la botɩ fʋ yirsra.»

ref: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ lerge ẽ yetɩ: «Afɛstusi sõŋɔ, mam ka zallɩ. Mam tɔgrɩ la yel-sɩra, la yɛm yetɔga.

ref: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
text: Naba Agripa mi yel-bãna mɛ, mam tã wʋn tɔgɛ mɛ la bʋraanɛ a nẽŋam. Mam mi tɩ a mi bõn-bãna wuu mɛ, se'ere n soe la, ba ka ẽŋɛ lika pʋam.»

ref: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
text: A yele bɛla dee yetɩ: «Naba Agripa, fõn sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n yele se'em na mɛ? Mam mi tɩ fõn sakɛ mɛ.»

ref: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
text: Tɩ Agripa yele Apolɩ yetɩ: «Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fʋ botɩ mam lebge la Krisi nẽra wakatɛ fẽe wãna pʋam?»

ref: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ yetɩ: «La sãn yuuge, bɩɩ la sãn ka yuuge, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ tɩ fõn la sɛba wuu n selsrɩ mam zĩna wã wʋn lebge ãna wʋ mam ãn se'em na, sãn dagna bãnsɩ la mã'a tɩ mam ka boorɩ tɩ ba ba ya.»

ref: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
text: Tɩ naba la, la gɔmna Afɛstusi, la Abɛrnisi, la nẽrba la wuu isge yese,

ref: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
text: ka yele taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ tɩ a ki, bɩɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ẽ yʋ'a deem.»
