ref: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
text: Tɩ Agripa yele Afɛstusi yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã sãn ka yele nɩ tɩ ẽŋa boorɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ la na-keko la zẽ'am, tɩ tã wʋn basɛ nɩ ẽ.»

ref: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
text: Tɩ ba daan looge yɛm tɩ ba botɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ Itali, tɩ ba dɩkɛ Apolɩ la nẽrba basɛba tɩ ba yʋ la, ẽŋɛ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Azuliyusi nu'usum, a daan de la sʋda-sɛba tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ na-keko sʋdaasɩ la kɛ'ɛma ayɩla.

ref: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
text: La õorŋɔ n ze'ele Adramiti tẽŋam daan bɔna bilam yetɩ a sẽŋɛ tẽnsɩ basɛba n boe Azi ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm. Tɩ tõma kẽ wɛ'ɛsa, tɩ Aristarkɩ n ze'ele Tesaloniki n boe Masedʋanɩ so'olʋm, wa'am wa doose tõma.

ref: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ tõma paagɛ Sidõ tẽŋam, tɩ Azuliyusi ẽŋɛ Apolɩ sõŋa bɔ ẽ nõorɛ tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka bɩsɛ a sɩrdõma, dee tɩ ba sõŋɛ ẽ.

ref: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
text: Tɩ tõma le kẽ õorŋɔ bilam wɛ'ɛsa. Tɩ kʋsebgo vugra tʋ'ʋsra tõma, tɩ tõma wẽrgɛ bẽglɛ Sipri sẽŋɛ.

ref: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
text: La tõma doose la ko-kãtɛ la n lɛm Silisi la Pãnfili la, sẽŋɛ ka paagɛ Miira n boe Lisi so'olʋm sige.

ref: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
text: Bilam tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la yẽ õorŋɔ n ze'ele Alɛgsãndri tɩ a sẽŋɛ Itali. Tɩ a botɩ tõma kẽ.

ref: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
text: La dabsa faa tɩ tõma nãmsɛ la õorŋɔ la sẽnna fẽfẽ halɩ ka lɛm Kinidi tẽŋam. La kʋsebgo la ka botɩ tõma yãŋɛ sẽŋɛ bilam bɔba, tɩ tõma lɔkɛ sẽŋɛ lɛm Salmõnɩ bẽglɛ Krɛtɩ sẽŋɛ.

ref: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
text: La tõma daan bẽglɛ la ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm, daan nãmsɛ halɩ, dee yãŋa paagɛ zẽ'a yʋ'ʋrɛ n de õorŋɔ ze'elgo zẽ'e-sõŋɔ n lɛm Laze.

ref: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
text: La dabsa zozo'e daan tole mɛ, tɩ Zifdõma nõorɛ lʋa daarɛ tole. La wakat-ẽŋa la daan de la toogo bɔ nẽrba tɩ ba dɩkɛ õorŋɔ doose ko'om na pʋam. Bɛla tɩ Apolɩ yele ba yetɩ:

ref: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
text: «Bʋraasɩ wã, mam mi tɩ tõma sẽnnɛ wã wʋn wa dɛna la toogo. La dagɩ lɔgrɔ, la õorŋɔ la mã'a n wʋn sã'am, la de la tõma mẽŋa yõorɛ põsga.»

ref: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
text: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la ka selse Apolɩ n yele se'em na, a selse la sɛka n bɩsrɩ õorŋɔ la sẽnnɛ yelle, la õorŋɔ la dãana yetɔga.

ref: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
text: La õorŋɔ ze'elgo zẽ'a na ka ãn sõŋa tɩ ba wʋn gã'arɛ tɩ kʋsebgo, la ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ la tole. Bɛla tɩ nẽrba la zozo'e yetɩ, tɩ basɛ bilam dee wĩbe sẽŋɛ paagɛ Fenigsi n mẽ boe Krɛtɩ so'olʋm. Zẽ'e-ẽŋa õorŋɔ ze'elgo zẽ'am la tɔglɛ la zanõrɛ gɔbga, la zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo bɔba. Bilam tɩ ba tã wʋn zĩ'ire tɩ kʋsebgo, la ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ la tole. Kʋseb-kãtɛ vugri ko'om zuo

ref: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
text: Tɩ kʋsebgo ze'ele gɔbga bɔba wa'am wa vugra fẽfẽ. Tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ban boorɩ se'em, bɛla tɩ ba mãasʋm õorŋɔ la basɛ zẽ'e-ẽŋa, dee bẽgla Krɛtɩ ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm wɛ'ɛsa.

ref: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
text: Tɩ la ka yuuge, tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ «Erakilõ» ze'ele Krɛtɩ tẽŋa bɔba, wa'am wa vugra ke'enke'em wẽ'era õorŋɔ la.

ref: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
text: Tɩ tõma ka yãŋɛ botɩ õorŋɔ la sẽŋɛ nẽŋa bɔba, tɩ tõma basɛ õorŋɔ la tɩ kʋsebgo la tarɩ ẽ wɛ'ɛsa.

ref: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
text: Tɩ tõma sẽŋɛ ka lɛm tẽn-pɩka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Koda tɩ ko'om gilge ẽ, bẽgla ẽ tɩ kʋsebgo la ka le vugra ke'enke'em tʋ'ʋsra tõma. Tɩ tõma keŋe tɩ mẽŋa õbgɛ õorŋ-pɩka la zoole õorŋ-kãtɛ la zuo, la, la ka ẽŋɛ nãana.

ref: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
text: Tɩ ba dɩkɛ mĩ'isi lʋ õorŋ-kãtɛ la kãŋkãŋɛ tɩ a tara pãŋa. Ba zotɩ mɛ tɩ, tɩ sãn paagɛ zẽ'e-sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Sirti n dagɩ kʋlko'om tɩ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo bɔna mĩ na, a wʋn fɩrɛ. Bɛla tɩ ba sike õorŋɔ la fu-kãtɛ, dee tɩ kʋsebgo la zã'ara ẽ sẽnna fẽfẽ.

ref: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
text: La kʋsebgo la ken vugra la ke'enke'em nãmsra tõma, tɩ beere wiige, tɩ ba yese lɔg-sɛba n boe õorŋɔ la pʋam na, lobe basɛ ko'om na pʋam.

ref: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
text: Tɩ beere le wiige, tɩ ba mẽŋa yese õorŋɔ la mẽŋa lɔgrɔ lobe basɛ ko'om na pʋam.

ref: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
text: Tɩ la ẽŋɛ dabsa se'em, tɩ tõma ka yẽ wẽntulle la wõrbiɛ, tɩ kʋsebgo la ken vugra ke'enke'em nãmsra tõma. Tɩ tõma kɔ'ɔgɛ vɔm pʋtẽ'erɛ.

ref: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
text: Tɩ la daan yuuge tɩ nẽrba la ka di. Tɩ Apolɩ ze'ele ba tẽŋasʋka yetɩ: «Ya daan sãn sakɛ nɩ mam nõorɛ la, daan ka fõrgɛ Krɛtɩ, tɩ kãn yẽ nɩ nãmsgɔ la yel-bãna wã wuu.

ref: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam sosrɩ ya tɩ ya keŋe ya sũure. Tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla kãn ki ya pʋam, sãn dagna õorŋɔ la mã'a n wʋn sã'am.

ref: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
text: Zaam yʋ'ʋŋɔ Nawẽnnɛ n soe mam tɩ mam dɛna a tõntõnna la, tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa ze'ele mam zẽ'am

ref: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
text: yetɩ: ‹Apolɩ, da zoe dabeem. Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ ka ze'ele na-keko la nẽŋam. La Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ botɩ sɛba n boe õorŋɔ pʋam na wuu vɔa.›

ref: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
text: Bɛla, bʋraasɩ wã, keŋe-ya ya sũure, tɩ mam bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra mɛ tɩ la wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ malɛka la n yele mam se'em na.

ref: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
text: La kʋsebgo la wʋn pe'ege tɩ ka basɛ la tẽŋa tɩ ko'om gilge.»

ref: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
text: Tɩ ba lorge kuto la n botɩ õorŋɔ zɛ'ɛta la basɛ ko-kãtɛ la pʋam, dee lorge mĩ'isi la n lʋ dɔ-sɛba n wʋn botɩ õorŋɔ la sẽŋɛ ban boorɩ zẽ'e-sɛka la basɛ, dee zẽkɛ fuugo la n boe õorŋɔ la nẽŋam na, tɩ kʋsebgo wẽ'era fuugo la, tɩ õorŋɔ la wɛ'ɛsa doone.

ref: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
text: Tɩ nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ õorŋɔ la tõoma la daan bɔɔra tɩ ba zoe dee basɛ õorŋɔ la, bɛla tɩ ba sike õorŋ-pɩka la ko'om pʋam, ẽŋɛ wʋ ba boorɩ tɩ ba dɩkɛ la kuto n botɩ õorŋɔ zɛ'ɛta basɛba basɛ õorŋɔ la nẽŋam.

ref: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
text: Tɩ ba zɔta dabeem tɩ õorŋɔ la wʋn wẽ tãmpĩisi, tɩ ba dɩkɛ kuto n botɩ õorŋɔ zɛ'ɛta banaasɩ basɛ ko'om pʋam õorŋɔ la poorʋm, dee kã'ara Nawẽnnɛ tɩ beere wiige.

ref: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
text: Tɩ Apolɩ yele sʋda-kɛ'ɛma, la sʋdaasɩ la yetɩ: «Nẽr-bãna wã sãn ka bɔna õorŋɔ wã pʋam, yãma wuu kãn yãŋɛ põsɛ.»

ref: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la wã mĩ'i-sɛba n lʋ õorŋ-pɩka la, dee basɛ ẽ tɩ ko'om tarɩ ẽ fõrgɛ.

ref: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
text: Bɛla mam belnɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya di dia yẽ pãŋa vɔa, tɩ ya zomto baa ayɩla kãn bɔɩ.»

ref: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
text: Ẽn yele bɛla la, dee dɩkɛ borborɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ ba wuu nẽŋam, dee fiise borborɩ la obe.

ref: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
text: Tɩ ba wuu yẽ bʋraanɛ mẽ di dia la.

ref: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
text: Tɩ ba di tɩgɛ, dee dɩkɛ bõnkɔɔla n boe õorŋɔ la pʋam na, lobe basɛ ko'om na pʋam, ba boorɩ tɩ õorŋɔ la n fakɛ. Õorŋɔ sã'aŋɔ yelle

ref: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
text: Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ ba ka bãŋɛ tẽŋa la. La ba yẽ la zẽ'a n tã wʋn dooge, tɩ ba yetɩ, ba sãn tãna, ba botɩ õorŋɔ la tɔglɛ bilam.

ref: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
text: Tɩ õorŋɔ la sẽŋɛ zẽ'a tɩ ko'om ka zo'oge mĩ ka zom tẽntõnbĩ'isgo zuo, tɩ a nẽŋam fɩrɛ, tɩ a koŋe sẽnnɛ. Tɩ ko-mĩ'isi wẽ'era a poorʋm la pãŋa, tɩ a sã'am.

ref: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
text: Tɩ sʋdaasɩ la bɔɔra tɩ ba kʋ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba yõgɛ la, tɩ sɛka da lui ko'om pʋam pãm dooge zoe fõrgɛ.

ref: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
text: Tɩ sʋda-kɛ'ɛma la bɔɔra tɩ a fãagɛ Apolɩ basɛ, ka sakɛ tɩ ba kʋ nẽrba la, dee bɔ nõorɛ yetɩ, sɛba n mi ko'om bũŋa la ẽgɛ lui ko'om pʋam yiɛn pãm dooge doone.

ref: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
text: Dee tɩ sɛba n ka mi bũŋa la dɩkɛ dɔ-palagsɩ, la õorŋɔ la bõn-sɛka n sã'am woo bũn dooge. Bɛla tɩ tõma wuu daan põsɛ dooge doone.

ref: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
text: La tõma n daan põsɛ paagɛ doone na, tɩ tõma bãŋɛ tɩ tẽŋa la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Maltɩ.

ref: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
text: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba to'oge tõma sõŋa sõŋa. Tɩ saaga daan niira tɩ ɔɔrɔ bɔna, tɩ ba tʋʋrɛ bugum bɔ tõma, tɩ tõma ɔɔsra.

ref: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ vaagɛ dɔɔrɔ wa'am wa ẽŋɛ bugum pʋam, tɩ tʋʋlgɔ wõŋɛ bõnsɛla, tɩ a yese vile Apolɩ nu'ugo.

ref: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
text: Tɩ tẽŋa la nẽrba yẽ bõnsɛla n vile a nu'ugo la, tɩ ba yele taaba yetɩ: «Bʋra-ẽna wã sɩrɩ de la nẽr-kʋʋra. Baa la ẽn yãŋɛ põsɛ ko'om pʋam na, wẽnnɛ ka sakɛ tɩ a vɔa.»

ref: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
text: Tɩ Apolɩ mẽsɛ a nu'ugo, tɩ bõnsɛla la lui bugum pʋam, dee tɩ sɛla ka ẽŋɛ ẽ.

ref: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
text: Tɩ nẽrba la gu'ura tɩ ba wʋn yẽ tɩ a ĩyã fʋʋsʋm ya, bɩɩ a wʋn kɔ'ɔn lui tẽŋa ki. Tɩ la yuuge, tɩ ba ka yẽ tɩ sɛla ẽŋɛ ẽ, tɩ ba teege pʋtẽ'era yetɩ, a de la wẽnnɛ.

ref: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
text: La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ tõma daan boe mĩ na lɛm na tẽŋa la nẽŋadãana va'am. A yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Apubulsi, a daan to'oge tõma mɛ la sũ-yẽlga. Tɩ tõma bɔna a yire dabsa atã.

ref: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
text: Tɩ a sɔ bẽ'era gã yẽ'era-waafɔ, dee tɩ ɔɔrɔ tara ẽ. Tɩ Apolɩ kẽ a deem dɩkɛ nu'usi paglɛ a zuo, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ, tɩ a yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
text: Lan ẽŋɛ bɛla la, tɩ tẽŋa la nẽr-sɛba n de bã'adõma wuu sẽnna, tɩ Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba, tɩ ba yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
text: Tɩ ba daan gõŋɛ tõma sõŋa sõŋa, la tõma n daan fõrgrɩ la, ba bɔ tõma la bɔ'a n seke tõma sore la sẽnnɛ. Apolɩ dõma tole wɛ'ɛsa Rom

ref: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
text: La wõr-sɩtã n tole, tɩ tõma yẽ õorŋɔ n ze'ele Alɛgsãndri wa'am bɔna Maltɩ tẽŋam, tɩ ɔɔrɔ la kʋseb-kãtɛ la wakatɛ tole. Õorŋɔ la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la «Diyɔskuri». Tɩ tõma kẽ õorŋɔ la wɛ'ɛsa.

ref: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
text: La tõma sẽŋɛ paagɛ la Sɩrakusi zĩ'ire bilam dabsa atã,

ref: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
text: le ze'ele bilam kẽ õorŋɔ sẽŋɛ paagɛ Regio. Tɩ beere wiige, tɩ kʋsebgo ze'ele tõma poorʋm wa vugra zã'ara tõma. Tɩ tõma paagɛ Puzɔllɩ dabsa ayi daarɛ sige.

ref: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
text: Bilam tɩ tõma yẽ Azezi karẽnbiisi basɛba. Tɩ ba sose tõma, tɩ tõma zĩ'ire ba zẽ'am dabsa ayopɔɩ, dee yãŋa wɛ'ɛsa Rom.

ref: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
text: Tɩ karẽnbi-sɛba n boe Rom na wõm tõma yelle. Tɩ basɛba tʋ'ʋsɛ tõma halɩ «Apiyusi da'am», dee tɩ basɛba mẽ tʋ'ʋsɛ tõma zẽ'e-sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n de «sãama deto batã» la. La Apolɩ n yẽ ba la, tɩ a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, le yẽ bʋraanɛ.

ref: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
text: Tɩ tõma paagɛ Rom, tɩ ba bɔ Apolɩ nõorɛ tɩ a kẽ'era ẽn boorɩ zẽ'e-sɛka, dee botɩ sʋdaaga gu'ura ẽ. Apolɩ boe Rom mõola Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga

ref: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
text: Tɩ dabsa atã poorʋm, tɩ Apolɩ wi Zifdõma nẽŋadõma sɛba n boe tẽŋa la pʋam lagsɛ, yele ba yetɩ: «M sɔbiisi, mam daan ka ẽŋɛ tɩ nẽrba la be'em, la mam ka sã'am lɔɔ sɛba tɩ tɩ to'oge tɩ yaabdõma zẽ'am na, la baa bɛla ba daan yõkɛ mam mɛ Zerizalɛm, tarɩ mam ka bɔ Rom sʋdaasɩ tɩ ba yʋ mam.

ref: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
text: Tɩ ba sogse mam, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba basɛ mam, ban daan ka yẽ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ sɛla n masɛ kũm na ĩyã.

ref: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
text: Tɩ Zifdõma zagsɛ tɩ ba da basɛ mam, bɛla n pẽrgɛ mam tɩ mam sose nõorɛ yetɩ, mam boorɩ tɩ m sẽŋɛ la Rom na-keko la zẽ'am tɩ a di m sarɩya, la dagɩ mam n tarɩ yelle la m nẽrba la.

ref: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
text: Bɛla tɩ mam wi ya tɩ ya wa'am tɩ sõsɛ la taaba. Ban lʋ mam na bãnsɩ wã, la de la sɛka tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma tarɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura ẽ na ĩyã.»

ref: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
text: Tɩ ba yele ẽ yetɩ: «Tõma ka to'oge gõnnɔ Zude tẽŋam n gʋlsɛ fʋ yelle. Zifdõma sɛba n sẽnnɩ kalam na, ba nẽra nẽra mẽ ka tɔgɛ fʋ tõon-be'ero yelle.

ref: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
text: La tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ wõm na fõn tẽ'esrɩ se'em, la sãn dɛna sore la tɩ fʋ dolɩ la, tõma mi tɩ zẽ'a woo ba sɩ'ɩsrɩ ẽ mɛ.»

ref: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
text: Tɩ ba bɔ Apolɩ daarɛ. Tɩ daarɛ la paagɛ, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e lagsɛ Apolɩ zẽ'am. Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle, la Azezi yelle n gʋlsɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ gõnnɔ pʋam, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ pʋam na, pa'alɛ ba bulika halɩ ka paagɛ zaanõorɛ, mõrgra tɩ a botɩ ba sakɛ.

ref: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
text: Tɩ basɛba sakɛ, dee tɩ basɛba ka sakɛ.

ref: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
text: Tɩ nẽrba la ka wõm taaba bɔɔra tɩ ba fõrgɛ. Tɩ Apolɩ tɔgɛ ba yetɔga ayɩla yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yʋʋn botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi tɔgɛ la ya yaabdõma la de la sɩra.

ref: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
text: A yʋʋn yetɩ: ‹Sẽŋɛ bu-bãna wã zẽ'am ka yele ba yetɩ: Ya wʋn selse, la ya kãn wõm a võorɛ. Ya wʋn bɩsɛ, la ya kãn yẽ sɛla sɛla.

ref: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
text: Se'ere n soe la, bu-bãna wã pʋtẽ'era ka like, la ba tʋba lɩ mɛ, dee tɩ ba mũm ba nini. Tɩ ba nini da yẽ, tɩ ba tʋba da wõm, tɩ ba pʋtẽ'era da like. Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ba wʋn teege nɩ yɛm, tɩ mam bɔ ba laafɛ.› »

ref: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
text: Apolɩ n tɔgɛ yetɔg-ẽŋa la, tɩ Zifdõma wẽ'era nõke'ene la taaba fõrgra.]

ref: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
text: Apolɩ daan kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a boe mĩ na yʋʋma ayi, daan yɔɔra deego la ligri, dee tɔ'ɔsra sɛba wuu n sẽnnɩ a zẽ'am na,

ref: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
text: mõola Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm yelle, dee zãsna ba Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yɛla. A daan tɔgrɩ la bʋraanɛ, tɩ nẽra ka gu'ura ẽ tɩ a da tɔgɛ.

ref: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
text: Mam Apolɩ n de Azezi-krisi yamŋa n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã. Nawẽnnɛ n wi mam tɩ m dɛna Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna, welge mam to'ore tɩ m mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga.

ref: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
text: Nawẽnnɛ daan bĩŋe la nõorɛ halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ a bɔ tɔ a kõ-yẽlga la, botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gʋlsɛ a gõŋɔ pʋam.

ref: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la boe a zẽ'am mɛ la pãŋa, pa'alɛ tɩ a de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, ẽŋa n ki dee vo'oge la.

ref: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Azezi-krisi ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ, botɩ tõma dɛna a Tõntõnɩba tɩ tɩ botɩ so'olʋm wuu nẽrba bɔ ẽ sɩra, sakɛ a nõorɛ a yʋ'ʋrɛ nã'asgɔ ĩyã.

ref: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
text: Yãma mẽ po bɔna la nẽr-bãma pʋam, yãma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi tɩ ya dɛna Azezi-krisi nẽrba la.

ref: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: Mam gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra la yãma sɛba wuu n boe Rom, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ ya, wi ya tɩ ya welge to'ore dɛna a nẽrba la. Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm. Apolɩ boorɩ tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka kaagɛ Krisi nẽrba n boe Rom

ref: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
text: Pɔspɔsɩ, Azezi-krisi ĩyã mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ mam Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ yãma wuu ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, ba tɔgrɩ yãma n bɔ Azezi sɩra se'em mɛ dũnia wã wuu.

ref: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
text: Mam tẽrɩ ya yelle m pʋ'ʋsgɔ pʋam mɛ wakatɛ wuu. Nawẽnnɛ tɩ mam tõnnɩ la m sũure wuu bɔ'ɔra ẽ, mõola a Dayʋa kõ-yẽlga la, mi tɩ sɛla tɩ mam yele la de la sɩra.

ref: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
text: Wakat-sɛka woo tɩ mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ, mam sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ tɩ la sãn dɛna ẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em, a botɩ m yãŋɛ wa'am ya zẽ'am wa kaagɛ ya yãŋa.

ref: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam boorɩ la m sũure wuu tɩ m yẽ ya, tɩ m wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a bɔ ya tɩ ya yẽ pãŋa.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ nõorɛ faa tɩ mam makɛ tɩ m wa'am ya zẽ'am. La gu'a n gu mam halɩ wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã. Mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ m wa'am tɩ m tõonɛ na tara yõorɔ yãma zẽ'am, wʋ ẽn tarɩ yõorɔ so'olʋm basɛba pʋam se'em na.

ref: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam bɔɔra tɩ m mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la mẽ bɔ yãma sɛba n boe Rom la. Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga pãŋa

ref: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Yãnnɛ ka tarɩ mam la kõ-yẽlga la mõolgɔ. A de la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa n botɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n bɔ ẽ sɩra la yẽta fãarɛ, pɔspɔsɩ de la Zifdõma, pa'asɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma.

ref: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
text: Kõ-yẽlga la n pukri pa'ala tõma Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masɛ a zẽ'am. La de la bɔ sɩra, tarɩ ka ba'asɛ la bɔ sɩra, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Nẽra n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra masɛ a zẽ'am wʋn vɔa, ẽn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.» Nẽrsaalba koŋe bʋʋrɔ mɛ

ref: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum pa'alɛ tɩ ẽŋa sũure yĩige mɛ la nẽrsaalba n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, la sɛla wuu tɩ ba ɩtɩ tɩ la ka dɔla ẽŋa sore, tɩ ba tõon-be'ero la gu'ura nẽrba tɩ ba ka mina sɩra la, tɩ ẽŋa wʋn sɩbgɛ ba.

ref: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
text: Se'ere n soe la, sore tɩ nẽrsaalba wʋn doose bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yelle de la vẽelga bɔ ba, Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n puke ẽ pa'alɛ ba.

ref: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
text: Halɩ lan pɔsɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ, Nawẽnnɛ yɛla tɩ nẽrsaalba ka yẽtɩ n de a ke'eŋo n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ, la ẽn de Nawẽnnɛ na, nẽrba yẽ ba mɛ a tõoma la pʋam. Bɛla tɩ nẽrsaalba ka tãna wʋn yetɩ bãma ka mi Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
text: Ba mi Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, la ba ka nã'asrɩ ẽ, la ba ka pʋ'ʋsrɩ ẽ a yel-sõnnɛ wʋ lan narɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na. Ba tẽ'esgɔ la lebge la tẽ'es-yooro, tɩ ba pʋtẽ'e-yooro la kẽ'esɛ ba lika pʋam.

ref: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
text: Ba tẽ'esɛ yetɩ bãma de la yɛm dõma, la ba de la gɔɔnɔ.

ref: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
text: Lan de ba nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ n ka kiiri n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, ba tõn nã'asra la bõna tɩ nẽrba mãalɛ n ãn wʋ nẽrsaalba n kiiri, la bõn-ẽgla, la sɛla n tarɩ nãma anaasɩ, la bõn-vuula.

ref: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba, tɩ ba tõnna dẽgrɔ tõoma, wʋ ba yɛm n boorɩ se'em, tõnna yãnnɛ tõoma la taaba.

ref: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
text: Ba dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ yelle n de sɩra la basɛ mɛ, dee dɔla põmpɔrŋɔ. Ba nã'asrɩ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nãam na, tõnna bɔ'ɔra ba, dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n de sɛka n nãam sɛla woo la. Ẽŋa n masɛ pẽka wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

ref: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba, tɩ ba ɩta ba mẽŋa yɛmleego n de yãnnɛ. Tɩ ba pɔgsɩ basɛ lan de ba gã se'em na, dee gã la taaba.

ref: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
text: Tɩ bʋraasɩ mẽ basɛ lan de ba gã la pɔgsɩ la. Tɩ ba bʋra-taaba yɛmleego tara ba. Tɩ bʋraasɩ ɩta sɛla n de yãnnɛ gã la taaba. Bɛla tɩ ba yẽta sɩbgrɛ ba mẽŋa, n masɛ la ba tuure la.

ref: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
text: Ban zagsɛ ka bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ na, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ba ba tẽ'es-yooro la pʋam, tɩ ba ɩta sɛla n ka masɛ tɩ ba ɩta.

ref: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
text: Sɛla n ka masɛ wuu n boe ba sũa pʋam. La de la be'em, la nõŋɛ bõnɔ zozo'e, la pʋtʋʋlgɔ, la ɩta sũure la tadãana bõnɔ, la nẽr-kʋa, la zɛbrɛ, la yɛm be'ero, la sũ-toogo. Ba wã'arɩ la taaba,

ref: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
text: tɔgra be'em zɛrgra taaba, ba de la wẽn-sɩ'ɩsrɩba, ka nanna taaba, zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma, wẽ'er-m-yõ'ogɔ dõma, nẽrba n mi sɔa n wʋn doose tõm be'em, nẽrba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ ba madõma, la ba sɔdõma nõorɛ.

ref: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
text: Ba yɛm ka like, ba de la nõgõla dõma, ba ka tarɩ nõŋlʋm, ba ka zotɩ ninbãalga.

ref: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
text: Ba mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla la masɛ la kũm, la baa bɛla ba ken ɩta la bõn-bãma, dee yetɩ nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla la ɩtɩ sõŋa mɛ.

ref: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
text: Fõn nẽr-sɛka n zɛrgrɩ nẽrba basɛba la, fʋ kãn yẽ bʋʋrɔ, se'ere n soe la, fõn zɛrgrɩ nẽrba basɛba dee ɩta bãma n ɩtɩ se'em na, fʋ mõŋrɩ la fʋ mẽŋa bʋʋrɔ.

ref: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
text: Tɩ mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ diti nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero bãma sarɩya dɔla la sɩra.

ref: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
text: Fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn zɛrgrɩ fʋ taaba, dee ɩta fʋ mẽŋa bãma n ɩtɩ se'em na, fõn wʋn yãŋɛ põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n diti tõon-be'ero sarɩya la mɛ bɩɩ?

ref: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
text: Bɩɩ fõn ka nannɩ la Nawẽnnɛ ɩr-sõŋɔ, la a sũ-bʋgsʋm, la a sũ-mã'arɛ n zo'oge la? Fʋ mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ɩr-sõŋɔ la boorɩ tɩ fʋ teege la yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

ref: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
text: La fõn sũure kɛ'ɛm mɛ tɩ fʋ ka bɔɔra tɩ fʋ teege yɛm, bɛla tɩ fʋ lagsra sɩbgrɛ bĩŋra fʋ mẽŋa zuo gu'ura daar-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn pa'alɛ a sũ-isgre di nẽrba sarɩya la sɩra la.

ref: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
text: Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ nẽra woo yɔɔrɔ masɛ wʋ ẽn tõm se'em.

ref: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n mõrgɛ ɩta sõŋa ɛɛra nã'asgɔ la pẽka, la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, a wʋn bɔ ba la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
text: La nẽr-sɛba n ka sakɛ bɔ, sɩ'ɩsra sɩra, dee ɩta sɛla n ka masɛ la, wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre, la sɩbgrɛ.

ref: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: A wʋn botɩ toogo la sũ-sã'aŋɔ n paagɛ nẽr-sɛba wuu n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero la. Pɔspɔsɩ de la Zifdõma, pa'asɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma.

ref: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: La nẽr-sɛba wuu n ɩtɩ sõŋa wʋn yẽ la na'am pãŋa, la nã'asgɔ, la sũ-mã'asʋm. Pɔspɔsɩ de la Zifdõma, pa'asɛ la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma.

ref: God doesn't have any favorites!
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ ka bɔkrɩ nẽrba.

ref: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n ka bãŋɛ Amoyisi lɔɔ la dee tõnna tõon-be'ero, ba wʋn sã'am ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã tɩ la ka doose la lɔɔ la. La Zifdõma n tarɩ Amoyisi lɔɔ la dee tõnna tõon-be'ero la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ la lɔɔ la di ba sarɩya.

ref: God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
text: La dagɩ sɛba n selsrɩ lɔɔ la n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la de la sɛba n sakɛ ɩtɩ wʋ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na n masɛ.

ref: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
text: Bɛla ba pa'alɩ tɩ sɛla tɩ lɔɔ la yetɩ ba ɩta la boe ba sũurum mɛ, tɩ ba pʋtẽ'erɛ n mi se'em na mẽ pa'ala tɩ bɛla de la sɩra. Bɛla tɩ ba pʋtẽ'era ẽn yɛta ba tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, bɩɩ ba ẽŋɛ sõŋa.

ref: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
text: Bõn-bãna wʋn puke la daar-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ Azezi-krisi di nẽrba yɛla n sʋgɛ sarɩya, masɛ wʋ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam mõolɛ n yetɩ se'em na. Zifdõma, la lɔɔ

ref: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
text: Fõn yetɩ fõn de la Zifu la, fõn te'ege la Amoyisi lɔɔ la yẽsna tɩ fõn de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽra,

ref: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
text: fõn mi Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em mɛ. Lɔɔ la zãsʋm fõn mɛ tɩ fõn mina sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la sɛla n ka ãn sõŋa bɔkɛ.

ref: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
text: Fʋ tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn mi mɛ tãna wʋn pa'alɛ fɔɔsɩ sore. Fʋ tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn de la peelem bɔ nẽr-sɛba n boe lika pʋam na.

ref: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
text: Fʋ tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn zãsnɩ na sɛba n ka mi, dee dɛna kɔma karẽnsãama, se'ere n soe la, fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ lɔɔ la pʋam, fõn tarɩ bãŋrɛ zõnzõnnɔ, la sɩra mɛ.

ref: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
text: Fõn zãsnɩ fʋ taaba la, bẽm tɩ fʋ ka zãsna fʋ mẽŋa? Fõn mõolɩ yɛta nẽrba tɩ ba da zũura, dee tɩ fõn mẽŋa zũura.

ref: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
text: Fõn yetɩ nẽrba tɩ ba da ɩta yalsɩ, dee ɩta yalsɩ fʋ mẽŋa. Fõn nẽ'esrɩ baga tɩ ba mãalɛ nã'asra la tɩ ba de la bõn-põ'osa, dee kẽ'era ba deto pʋam zũura ba yõorɔ dita.

ref: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
text: Fõn yẽsnɩ mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la, dee sɩ'ɩsra lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, ɩta Nawẽnnɛ yãnnɛ.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Yãma Zifdõma ĩyã tɩ buuri basɛba nẽrba tɔgra be'em pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ.»

ref: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
text: Fʋ sãn sakra lɔɔ la, wãaga la tarɩ yõorɔ mɛ. La fʋ sãn sɩ'ɩsɛ lɔɔ la, la de la zãŋa, la ãn wʋ fʋ ka wã mɛ na.

ref: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
text: La buuri sɛka n ka wãtɩ, nẽra sãn sakɛ ɩta lɔɔ la n yetɩ ba ɩta se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn geele ẽ wʋ nẽra n wã.

ref: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n ka wã, dee sakɛ ɩta lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, wʋn zɛrgɛ fʋ mɛ fõn tarɩ lɔɔ gõŋɔ la, wã dee sɩ'ɩsra lɔɔ la.

ref: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
text: La dagɩ sɛka n ɩtɩ Zifdõma ɩrgɔ tɩ nẽrba yẽta n de Zifu. La dagɩ wãaga tɩ nẽrba yẽtɩ la n botɩ fʋ dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽra.

ref: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
text: Zifu sɩra sɩra de la sɛka n de Zifu a sũure pʋam. Wãaga sɩra sɩra de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n ɩtɩ sũure pʋam, la dagɩ wãaga wʋ lɔɔ n gʋlsɛ se'em na. Nawẽnnɛ n pẽgrɩ nẽr-ẽŋa, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba.

ref: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
text: La sãn dɛna bɛla, Zifdõma tarɩ zu-yẽlga la bẽm pʋam gãnna sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, la wãaga la yõorɔ de la bẽm?

ref: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
text: Yõorɔ la zo'oge mɛ. Pɔspɔsɩ, Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ a yetɔga ẽŋɛ la Zifdõma nu'usum.

ref: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
text: La ba nẽrba basɛba sãn dagna sɩra dõma, bɛla wʋn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ kãn dɛna sɩra dãana mɛ bɩɩ?

ref: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
text: Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. Baa nẽrba wuu sãn dɛna põmpɔrndõma, Nawẽnnɛ ken dɛna la sɩra dãana wʋ lan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Fʋ yetɔga pʋam, nẽrba mi tɩ fõn masɛ mɛ dɛna sɩra dãana. Nẽrba sãn zɛrgɛ fɔ, fõn tarɩ bʋʋrɔ mɛ.»

ref: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
text: La tõma n ɩtɩ sɛla n ka masɛ la sãn botɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Tɩ wʋn yetɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma ka masɛ, ẽn sɩbgrɩ tɩ la ĩyã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. Mam tɔgrɩ wʋ nẽrsaalba n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na.

ref: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
text: Nawẽnnɛ sãn dagna sɩra dãana, a kãn yãŋɛ nɩ dita dũnia nẽrba sarɩya.

ref: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
text: La mam põmpɔrŋɔ sãn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra pukra pa'asra, tɩ la dɛna nã'asgɔ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, bẽm tɩ ba wʋn di mam sarɩya tɩ mam de la tõon-be'ero dãana?

ref: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
text: La sãn dɛna bɛla, bẽm tɩ tɩ kãn yetɩ: «Tɩ ɩta be'em tɩ la botɩ sɛla n de sõŋa wa'am?» Nẽrba basɛba dõtɩ mam tɩ mam n yele bɛla. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di nẽr-bãma sarɩya wʋ lan masɛ. Nẽra ka boe n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
text: Bɛla, tõma Zifdõma gãnnɩ nẽr-sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ! La dagɩ bɛla. Mam dẽŋɛ põn yele tɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, tõma wuu boe la tõon-be'ero yamnɛ pʋam,

ref: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
text: wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽra ka boe n masɛ, baa ayɩla.

ref: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
text: Nẽra ka boe n tarɩ yɛm ɛɛra tɩ a bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, baa ayɩla.

ref: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
text: Nẽrba wuu bure la poore bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, ba wuu tuuge mɛ. Nẽra ka boe n ɩtɩ sõŋa, baa ayɩla.

ref: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
text: Ba nõa ãn wʋ yɔɔgɔ n vuke. Ba tarɩ ba zɩlɩsɩ la pã'asra la nẽrba. Ba nõorɛ yetɔga ãn wʋ bõnsɛla zẽlõm na.

ref: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
text: Ba nõa pɩrɛ la kã'a-be'ero, la yetɔg-be'ero.

ref: These people quickly become violent.
text: Ba ka yagsnɩ la nẽrba kʋa.

ref: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
text: Ban doose zẽ'e-sɛka woo tole, la de la sã'aŋɔ, la toogo tɩ ba basrɩ mĩ.

ref: They don't know how to live in peace.
text: Ba ka mi sũ-mã'asʋm sore.

ref: They don't even fear God.”
text: Ba nẽnsɩ ka boe tɩ ba zoe Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
text: Tõma mi tɩ sɛla wuu tɩ lɔɔ la yetɩ la, a yetɩ la sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na. Bɛla tɩ nẽrba wuu nõa wʋn mũm, tɩ ba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ ba koŋe Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka boe n tã wʋn sakɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Lɔɔ la wen botɩ nẽra bãŋra mɛ tɩ a de la tõon-be'ero dãana. Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ nẽrba masɛ a zẽ'am se'em

ref: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã Nawẽnnɛ puke pa'alɛ ẽŋa n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ lɔɔ la nẽŋa ka bɔna mĩ. Lɔɔ gõnnɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba dẽŋɛ põn tɔgɛ a yelle mɛ.

ref: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrsaalba wuu tõm na tõon-be'ero koŋe Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

ref: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã, a botɩ ba masɛ a zẽ'am na zãŋa, Azezi-krisi n fãarɩ ba tõon-be'ero pʋam na ĩyã.

ref: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
text: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ a pa'alɛ lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ ẽŋa de la sɩra dãana, ẽŋa mẽŋa masɛ mɛ, dee botɩ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra la masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.

ref: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
text: Bɛla, bẽm pʋam tɩ nẽra tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ? Sɛla ka boe. Bẽm ĩyã? La dagɩ nẽra n sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã tɩ a masɛ, la de la ẽn bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra ĩyã.

ref: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
text: Tõma yẽ tɩ la de la nẽra n bɔ Krisi sɩra ĩyã tɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la dagɩ ẽn sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã.

ref: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
text: Bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ de la Zifdõma mã'a Nawẽnnɛ? A ka po dɛna nẽr-sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la Nawẽnnɛ? A po dɛna la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
text: Nawẽnnɛ de la ayɩla, a wʋn botɩ Zifdõma masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ sɩra ĩyã, dee botɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la mẽ masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ sɩra ĩyã.

ref: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
text: Tɩ wʋn yele Abraham n de tɩ yaaba la yelle yetɩ bo? A yʋʋn yẽ la bẽm?

ref: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
text: La sãn de nɩ Abraham tõoma ĩyã n botɩ a dɛna nẽra n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a yʋʋn tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ mɛ. La a ka tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Abraham bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a de la nẽra n masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.»

ref: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
text: Nẽra sãn tõnna tõoma, a to'orɩ la yɔɔrɔ. Yɔɔrɔ la dagɩ bɔ'a tɩ ba bɔ ẽ, la de la a bõnɔ.

ref: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
text: La nẽra sãn ka tõm tõonɛ, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ tõon-be'ero dõma masra a zẽ'am na sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ a lebge la nẽra n masɛ a zẽ'am ẽn bɔ sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
text: Bɛla tɩ naba Adavidi mẽ tɔgɛ yetɩ, nẽra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ a zẽ'am, tɩ la dagna a tõoma ĩyã, tarɩ sũ-yẽlga.

ref: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
text: Zu-yẽlga ẽŋa tɩ ba tɔgɛ a yelle la, de la nẽr-sɛba n wã la mã'a bõnɔ? Ayɛɩ, la po dɛna la nẽr-sɛba n ka wã la bõnɔ. Tõma dẽŋɛ yetɩ: «Abraham n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.»

ref: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
text: Wakat-kãna tɩ bõn-ẽŋa ẽŋɛ? La de la Abraham n yʋʋn wã la poorʋm bɩɩ, lan dẽŋɛ a wãaga la? La de la ẽn yʋʋn nãn ka wã la.

ref: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ Abraham yãŋa yʋʋn wã, tɩ la dɛna dãalgɔ pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a de la nẽra n masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am, ẽn bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, wakatɛ sɛka tɩ a nãn ka wã la. Bɛla tɩ Abraham dɛna sɛba wuu n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra dee ka wã la sɔ-yaaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ ba de la nẽrba n masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.

ref: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
text: La Abraham ken dɛna la nẽr-sɛba n wã la sɔ-yaaba, la dagɩ nẽr-sɛba n wen wã la mã'a, la de la sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra wʋ Abraham n yʋʋn bɔ sɩra se'em, dee yʋʋn nãn ka wã la. Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ la sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la

ref: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ la Abraham, la a yɩɩsɩ yetɩ ba wʋn wa sɔna dũnia. La dagɩ Abraham n sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ la, la de la ẽn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am na ĩyã.

ref: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
text: La sãn de nɩ nẽrba n sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã tɩ ba tɔ'ɔra bõn-sõma sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la, bɛla bɔ sɩra la ka tarɩ yõorɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõor-bĩŋre la ka tarɩ võorɛ.

ref: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
text: Lɔɔ la sɩ'ɩsgɔ botɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũure n isgra. La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ lɔɔ la ka boe, sɩ'ɩsgɔ mẽ ka boe.

ref: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
text: Bɛla, la de la bɔ sɩra ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõor-bĩŋre la wʋn dɛna yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a n tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ Abraham yɩɩsɩ la wuu. La, la ka de a yɩɩsɩ sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la mã'a ĩyã, la de la nẽr-sɛba wuu n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra wʋ Abraham n yʋʋn bɔ sɩra se'em na. Abraham n de tɩ wuu sɔ-yaaba.

ref: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam wʋn botɩ fʋ lebge so'olʋm buuri zozo'e sɔ-yaaba.» A de la tõma sɔ-yaaba Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ sɩra la zẽ'am. Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ kũm vɔ'ɔra, dee botɩ sɛla n ka boe bɔna.

ref: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
text: Abraham yʋʋn bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ a ka tarɩ nɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ la tã wʋn ẽŋɛ. Bɛla tɩ a lebge so'olʋm buuri zozo'e sɔ-yaaba, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn yetɩ a yɩɩsɩ wʋn zo'oge se'em na.

ref: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla bɔ ẽ na, a yɛm yʋʋn ka yɔɔrɩ, a bɔ sɩra mɛ. La ẽn yʋʋn bɔ sɩra la basɛ mɛ tɩ a yẽ pãŋa nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
text: La dagɩ ẽŋa mã'a ĩyã tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.»

ref: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
text: La po dɛna la tõma mẽ ĩyã. Tõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ tɩ masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.

ref: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
text: Nawẽnnɛ daan basɛ tɩ ba kʋ Azezi la tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, dee vo'oge ẽ tɩ a botɩ tõma dɛna nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ tõma dɛna sɛba n masɛ a zẽ'am na, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã. La tõma tarɩ sũ-mã'asʋm mɛ naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

ref: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
text: Azezi-krisi n botɩ tɩ tãna wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am yẽ a yel-sõnnɛ, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã. Yel-sõnnɛ ẽŋa tɩ tõma te'ege zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ, dee ɩta sũ-yẽlga tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ na'am pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
text: La tõma ken ɩta la sũ-yẽlga nãmsgɔ pʋam, tõma mi tɩ nãmsgɔ botɩ tɩ mõrgra sẽnna la nẽŋa.

ref: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
text: Pʋtẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ tɩ tarɩ la kãn ẽŋɛ tɩ yãnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bɔ tõma la ẽŋɛ la a nõŋlʋm tɩ sũa pʋam.

ref: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
text: La de la toogo tɩ nẽra ki nẽra n masɛ ĩyã. La dase'ere, tã tɩ nẽr-sɛka wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ sakɛ ki nẽr-sõŋɔ ĩyã.

ref: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ ẽŋa n nõŋɛ tõma zozo'e se'em mɛ, tɩ tõma n daan ken dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma la, tɩ Krisi ki tõma ĩyã.

ref: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
text: Krisi n ki tɩ a zɩɩm yese la ĩyã n botɩ tõma masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, bɛla, la de la sɩra tɩ Krisi n wʋn fãagɛ tõma, tɩ tɩ põsɛ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre.

ref: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
text: Tõma daan de la Nawẽnnɛ bɛ'ɛba, la a Dayʋa la kũm ĩyã tɩ tõma naagɛ la ẽ. Bɛla, tõma n naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ na, la de la sɩra tɩ Krisi n vɔa la ĩyã, tɩ wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
text: Sɛla n pa'asɛ, tõma ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ, tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã, ẽŋa n botɩ tõma naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ na. Adam la Azezi-krisi yelle

ref: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
text: La de la nẽra ayɩla n yʋʋn tõm tõon-be'ero ĩyã n botɩ tõon-be'ero wa'am dũnia. Tɩ tõon-be'ero la tarɩ kũm wa'am. Bɛla tɩ kũm kʋʋra nẽrsaalba wuu, ban tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero la ĩyã.

ref: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
text: Halɩ lan dẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ Amoyisi a lɔɔ la, tõon-be'ero yʋʋn boe dũnia zuo mɛ. La lɔɔ la n yʋʋn nãn ka boe la, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka geelɩ tõon-be'ero.

ref: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
text: Halɩ lan pɔsɛ Adam wakatɛ tarɩ ka paagɛ Amoyisi wakatɛ la, kũm põn bɔna mɛ tãna nẽrba. Baa la nẽr-sɛba tõon-be'ero n ka ãn wʋ Adam n yʋʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ se'em na wuu ki mɛ. Adam yʋʋn de la Azezi n yetɩ a wa'am na wõnnɛ.

ref: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
text: La ba dagɩ bʋyɩla. Adam tuure la, la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a la ka tã wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ taaba. Nẽra ayɩla tuure ĩyã n botɩ nẽrba zozo'e kiira. La bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n ãn sõŋa gãnna, la de la nẽra ayɩla n de Azezi-krisi ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ nẽrba zozo'e yel-sõnnɛ zo'oge gãŋɛ.

ref: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
text: Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a la n ɩtɩ sɛla la, de la to'ore la nẽra ayɩla tõon-be'ero n ẽŋɛ sɛla la. Nẽra ayɩla tõon-be'ero la tarɩ la sarɩyadia wa'am, tɩ la sẽna la sɩbgrɛ. La nẽrba n tõm tõon-be'ero zozo'e la poorʋm, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ nẽrba la botɩ ba masɛ mɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
text: Lan de nẽra ayɩla tuure ĩyã tɩ kũm tãna nẽrba wuu la, bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽra ayɩla n de Azezi-krisi mã'a ĩyã, nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ ba yel-sõnnɛ zo'oge, botɩ ba masɛ a zẽ'am na, wʋn vɔa mɛ, dita na'am Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

ref: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
text: Bɛla, nẽra ayɩla tuure n botɩ Nawẽnnɛ dita nẽrba wuu sarɩya sɩbgra ba la, bɛla mẽ, nẽra ayɩla mã'a tõonɛ n masɛ la ĩyã n botɩ nẽrba wuu masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tara vɔm.

ref: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
text: Nẽra ayɩla n sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ ĩyã n botɩ nẽrba zozo'e dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma la, bɛla mẽ, nẽra ayɩla mã'a n sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ nẽrba zozo'e dɛna nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
text: La sãn dɛna lɔɔ la, a wa'am mɛ tɩ tuure wʋn zo'oge pãɩ. La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ tõon-be'ero zo'orɩ pa'asra, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ zo'orɩ mɛ pa'asra gãnna bɛla.

ref: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
text: Bɛla, tõon-be'ero n tarɩ pãŋa botɩ nẽrba kiira la, bɛla mẽ, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n tarɩ pãŋa botɩ nẽrba masɛ a zẽ'am tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

ref: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
text: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Tɩ wʋn ken tõnna la tõon-be'ero tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ pa'asɛ zo'oge bɩɩ?

ref: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
text: Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La sãn dɛna tõon-be'ero ɩrgɔ yelle, tõma de la kũm, tõon-be'ero ka le sɔna tõma. Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne le tõnna tõon-be'ero?

ref: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
text: Yãma ka mi tɩ tõma wuu n mise ko'om lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, tõma lagɩm na ẽ mɛ dɛna ayɩla a kũm na pʋam?

ref: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
text: Bɛla, ko'om misga la pʋam tõma po lagɩm na ẽ mɛ ki, tɩ ba laagɛ tõma la ẽ lɛ. Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Sɔ na'am pãŋa n botɩ Krisi vo'oge la, tõma mẽ vɔa la vɔm paalga.

ref: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
text: La de la sɩra tɩ tõma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla a kũm pʋam na, bɛla, tõma mẽ wʋn lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla, vo'oge wʋ ẽŋa n vo'oge se'em na.

ref: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
text: Tɩ mina-ya tɩ tõma n de se'em kʋrʋm na lagɩm na Krisi mɛ ki dɔ-puurŋa la zuo, tɩ tõma tõon-be'ero yɛmleego pãŋa wʋn sã'am ba'asɛ, tɩ tõma kãn le dɛna tõon-be'ero yamsɩ.

ref: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra sãn ki, tõon-be'ero ka le tara pãŋa la ẽ.

ref: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
text: La tõma sãn lagɩm na Krisi ki, tõma mi sɩra sɩra tɩ tõma mẽ wʋn lagɩm na ẽ vɔa.

ref: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
text: Tõma mi tɩ Krisi ki mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ, tɩ a kãn le ki. Kũm ka le tara pãŋa la ẽ.

ref: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
text: Krisi n ki la, a ki la nõorɛ ayɩla sã'am tõon-be'ero pãŋa wakatɛ wuu ĩyã. La lɛɛlɛ tɩ a vɔa wã, a vɔa bɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

ref: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
text: Bɛla mẽ, geele-ya ya mẽŋa wʋ yãma ki mɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero ka le sɔna ya, dee vɔa bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ, yãma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã.

ref: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
text: Bɛla, da basɛ-ya tɩ tõon-be'ero le sɔna ya vɔm wã n ba'asrɩ la, botɩ ya sakɛ a yɛmleego n de be'em na.

ref: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
text: Da dɩkɛ-ya ya ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore tɩ ba dɛna tõon-be'ero tõoma lɔgrɔ tɩ ya tara ba tõnna be'em. Dɩkɛ-ya ya mẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ wʋ nẽrba n ki dee vo'oge. Dɩkɛ-ya ya ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la wuu bɔ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a tara ba tõnna sɛla n masɛ.

ref: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
text: Tõon-be'ero kãn le sɔna ya, se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ la ka le sɔna ya, la de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n soe ya. Dɛna-ya sɛla n masɛ yamsɩ

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
text: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bo? Tɩ wʋn tõnna la tõon-be'ero, lɔɔ la n ka soe tõma, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ sɔna tõma la ĩyã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla.

ref: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
text: Ya mi tɩ ya sãn dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa bɔ nẽra dɛna a yamŋa sakɛ a nõorɛ, ya sɩrɩ de la sɛka tɩ ya sakrɩ la yamsɩ. Bɛla, ya sãn sakɛ tõon-be'ero, ya de la tõon-be'ero yamsɩ, tɩ la sẽna la kũm. La ya sãn sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, ya de la a yamsɩ, tɩ a botɩ ya dɛna nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
text: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ: Kʋrʋm, yãma daan de la tõon-be'ero yamsɩ, la yãma to'oge sɩra zãsŋɔ la mɛ wʋ ban zãsʋm ya se'em na, sakɛ ẽ la ya sũure wuu.

ref: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yese ya tõon-be'ero yamnɛ pʋam mɛ, botɩ ya dɛna sɛla n masɛ yamsɩ.

ref: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
text: Mam dɩkɛ la nẽrsaalba yelle makɛ bɔ ya, yãma n ka wõnnɩ sɛla võorɛ tɔtɔ la ĩyã. Yãma n daan dɩkɛ ya ĩyã zẽ'esɩ wuu bɔ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la tõon-be'ero tɩ ba tara ba tõnna tõon-be'ero la, bɛla mẽ, lɛɛlɛ wã, dɩkɛ-ya ya mẽŋa wuu bɔ, tõnna sɛla n masɛ, welge to'ore dɛna pʋpeelem dõma.

ref: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
text: Yãma n daan de tõon-be'ero yamsɩ la, sɛla n masɛ daan ka soe ya.

ref: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
text: Tõon-sɛba tɩ ya daan tõm na, ya yẽ la bẽm yõorɔ ba pʋam? Lɛɛlɛ wã, la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya, tõon-bãma ba'asgɔ de la kũm.

ref: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ yese ya tõon-be'ero pʋam mɛ, tɩ ya lebge Nawẽnnɛ yamsɩ. Ya yõorɔ de la ya welge to'ore malgra ya mẽŋa dɛna pʋpeelem dõma. Bõn-ẽŋa ba'asgɔ de la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
text: Asɩra, tõon-be'ero yɔɔrɔ de la kũm. La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a de la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, tɩn lagɩm na tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã.

ref: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam tɔgrɩ la yãma n mi lɔɔ la. Ya mi tɩ lɔɔ la soe nẽra la wakat-sɛka tɩ a vɔa la.

ref: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
text: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ: Pɔka sãn ele, lɔɔ la lʋ ẽ mɛ lagɩm na a sɩra halɩ ka paagɛ a sɩra la vɔm tɛka. La a sɩra la sãn ki, pɔka la yese elle lɔɔ la n lʋ ẽ na a sɩra la pʋam mɛ.

ref: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
text: Bɛla, a sãn ele bʋraaga ayẽma, dee tɩ a sɩra la ken vɔa, a ẽŋɛ la yalsɩ. La a sɩra la sãn ki, ɛlga lɔɔ la ka le sɔna ẽ. Bɛla, a sãn ele bʋraaga ayẽma, a ka ẽŋɛ yalsɩ.

ref: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
text: M sɔbiisi, yãma zẽ'am la mẽ de la bɛla. La sãn dɛna la Amoyisi lɔɔ la yelle, yãma de la kũm, tɩ lɔɔ la ka le sɔna ya, yãma n lagɩm na Krisi ki la ĩyã. Bɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma yãŋa de la nẽra ayẽma n soe ya, la de la Krisi n ki tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ na n soe ya, tɩ tɩ wʋn tõnna tõon-sõma bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
text: Kʋrʋm, tõma n daan vɔa wʋ tõma mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na, lɔɔ la botɩ tõma yɛmleego bɔɔra la be'em, basɛ tɩ tõma ĩyã zẽ'esɩ wuu ɩta sɛla n sẽm na kũm.

ref: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, tõma to'oge tɩ mẽŋa lɔɔ la zẽ'am mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, tõma lebge la kũm lɔɔ la zẽ'am, a daan tarɩ tõma wʋ tõma boe la yʋ'a deego pʋam na. Bɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, tõma dolɩ la so-paalɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ tɔ, tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, la ka le dɛna lɔɔ kɛka n gʋlsɛ la tɩ tõma dolɩ. Lɔɔ n pa'alɛ tõon-be'ero n de bõn-sɛka

ref: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
text: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Tɩ wʋn yetɩ lɔɔ la de la tõon-be'ero bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La lɔɔ la daan sãn ka boe nɩ, mam kãn bãŋɛ tõon-be'ero n de sɛla. Lɔɔ la daan sãn ka yele yetɩ: «Da ɩta sũure», mam kãn bãŋɛ nɩ sũure n de sɛla.

ref: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
text: Lɔɔ la n bɔ tõon-be'ero sore tɩ a botɩ mam ɩta sɛla n de sũure buuri wuu. Se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ la sãn ka boe nɩ, tõon-be'ero de la bõn-kɩ'ɩŋa.

ref: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
text: Kʋrʋm, mam n daan ka bãŋɛ lɔɔ la, mam daan vɔa mɛ, tɩ yelle kana. La mam n yãŋa bãŋɛ lɔɔ la, tɩ tõon-be'ero tara pãŋa tõnna, dee tɩ mam ki.

ref: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
text: Lɔɔ la n de nɩ a tarɩ nẽrba sẽŋɛ vɔm zẽ'am na, a tarɩ mam sẽŋɛ la kũm zẽ'am.

ref: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
text: Tõon-be'ero la n yẽ folle la, a dɩkɛ la lɔɔ la pã'asɛ mam yãŋɛ, botɩ mam ki.

ref: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
text: Bɛla, lɔɔ la de la bõn-sõŋɔ. Nõorɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ la mẽ de la bõn-sõŋɔ, masɛ ãna sõŋa.

ref: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
text: Bɛla, sɛla n ãn sõŋa la n botɩ mam lebge kũm bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La de la tõon-be'ero la n soe bɛla, a dɩkɛ la lɔɔ la n ãn sõŋa la botɩ mam ki, tɩ la puke pa'alɛ tɩ a sɩrɩ de la tõon-be'ero. Bɛla tɩ lɔɔ la botɩ tõon-be'ero yãŋa lebge sɛla n de tõon-be'e-kãtɛ halɩ tɩ la yõsgɛ.

ref: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
text: Tɩ mi tɩ lɔɔ la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yelle. La mam de la nẽrsaala tɩ tõon-be'ero tara mam ɩta ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em.

ref: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
text: Mam ka wõnnɩ mam n ɩtɩ sɛla la võorɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka ɩtɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla la, mam ɩtɩ la sɛla tɩ mam ka boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ la.

ref: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
text: Mam sãn ɩta sɛla tɩ mam ka boorɩ la, la pa'alɛ tɩ mam sakɛ tɩ lɔɔ la ãn sõŋa mɛ.

ref: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Bɛla, la dagɩ mam n ɩtɩ bɛla, la de la tõon-be'ero n boe mam pʋam tõnna.

ref: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Mam sãn ɩta sɛla tɩ mam ka boorɩ la, la dagɩ mam n ɩtɩ bɛla, la de la tõon-be'ero la n boe mam pʋam tõnna.

ref: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
text: Bɛla, mam bãŋɛ bõn-ẽŋa mɛ: Mam n boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa la, mam tã ɩta la sɛla n de be'em mã'a.

ref: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
text: Mam sũure pʋam, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la.

ref: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
text: La mam yẽ la bõnɔ ayẽma n boe mam pʋam zɛbra la mam pʋtẽ'e-sõŋɔ la, botɩ mam dɛna tõon-be'ero la n boe mam pʋam tõnna la yamŋa.

ref: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
text: La de la ninbãalga bɔ mam. Ãne n wʋn fãagɛ mam be'em n boe mam pʋam tara mam wɛ'ɛsa kũm zẽ'am wã pʋam?

ref: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
text: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, ẽŋa n fãagɛ tɔ, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Bɛla, mam pʋtẽ'erɛ pʋam mam sakrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la. La mam mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na n botɩ mam tõnna tõon-be'ero.

ref: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
text: Bɛla, sɩbgrɛ ka le bɔna nẽr-sɛba n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la zuto.

ref: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm Azezi-krisi ĩyã la n fãagɛ tɔ tõon-be'ero, la kũm pãŋa pʋam.

ref: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
text: Lɔɔ la ka tã wʋn fãagɛ nẽrsaalba basɛ, se'ere n soe la, nẽrsaalba ɩtɩ la ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em botɩ a ka tarɩ pãŋa. La sɛla tɩ lɔɔ la ka tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ la, Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ mɛ. A tõm na a mẽŋa Dayʋa, tɩ a lebge nẽrsaala ãna wʋ nẽrsaalba n de tõon-be'ero dõma, tɩ a ki yese tõon-be'ero. A kũm na pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ di tõon-be'ero sarɩya sã'am a pãŋa nẽrsaalba pʋam.

ref: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
text: Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ tɩ masɛ ɩta wʋ lɔɔ la n yetɩ se'em na, tõma n vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em, dee ka vɔa wʋ nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na.

ref: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n vɔa wʋ nẽrsaalba n boorɩ se'em na, nẽrsaalba mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em na n pakɛ ba. La nẽr-sɛba n vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na n pakɛ ba.

ref: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
text: Nẽrsaala mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em, sẽm na kũm. La a sãn dɔla Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, la bɔ'ɔrɩ la vɔm, la sũ-mã'asʋm.

ref: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na ka tã wʋn paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm.

ref: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
text: La yãma ka le vɔa wʋ nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na, yãma vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe la yãma la ĩyã. Nẽr-sɛka woo n ka tarɩ Krisi Sɩa la, a ka de Krisi nẽra.

ref: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
text: La Krisi sãn bɔna la ya, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ ya la vɔm, Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ ya masɛ a zẽ'am na ĩyã. La ya ĩyã wʋn ki mɛ tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

ref: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge Azezi la, a Sɩa la sãn bɔna la ya lɛ, ẽŋa n vo'oge Azezi-krisi n ki la, wʋn botɩ a Sɩa n boe la ya lɛ la, mẽ bɔ ya ĩyã n kiiri la vɔm paalga.

ref: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
text: Ya sãn ɩta nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na, ya wʋn ki mɛ. La ya sãn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa kʋ tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrsaala mẽŋa boorɩ la, ya wʋn vɔa.

ref: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
text: Nẽr-sɛba wuu n dolɩ sore tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɩ ba la, de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

ref: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bɔ ya la ka botɩ ya dɛna yamsɩ, le zɔta dabeem. A botɩ ya dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma, tɩ a bɔ sore tɩ ya wi'ira Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Abba», n boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ «tɩ Sɔ».

ref: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la mẽŋa tɔgrɩ la tõma sɩa pa'ala yetɩ tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

ref: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
text: Tõma n de a kɔma la, tɩ wʋn po to'oge bõn-sõma sɛba tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ a kɔma la, tɩ wʋn lagɩm na Krisi po to'oge bõn-sɛba tɩ a wʋn to'oge la. Tõma n nãmsrɩ la Krisi lɛ la, tɩ mẽ wʋn po lagɩm na ẽ yẽ nã'asgɔ. Nã'asgɔ sɛka n boe nẽŋa

ref: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
text: Mam mi mɛ tɩ nãmsgɔ sɛba tɩ tõma tarɩ zĩna wã de la fẽe, ka tã wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ la nã'asgɔ sɛka tɩ tõma wʋn wa yẽ la.

ref: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ sɛla wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nãam na gu'ura bɔɔra tɩ ba yẽ Nawẽnnɛ kɔma nã'asgɔ n wʋn puke la tɔtɔ.

ref: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
text: Nawẽnnɛ n nãam bõn-sɛba la wuu lebge la sɛla n ka le tara võorɛ. La dagɩ ba mẽŋa n boorɩ bɛla, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ ba lebge bɛla, dee botɩ ba ken tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura

ref: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
text: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn fãagɛ ba sã'aŋɔ tɩ ba boe a pʋam na mɛ, tɩ ba to'oge ba mẽŋa, lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ kɔma po yẽ nã'asgɔ.

ref: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
text: Tɩ mi tɩ halɩ wa paam zĩna, bõn-nãanɩsɩ la nãmsrɩ mɛ õosra la toogo, wʋ pɔka n virgri la.

ref: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
text: La, la dagɩ bõn-nãanɩsɩ la mã'a, tõma mẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dẽŋɛ bɔ tɩ a Sɩa pa'alɛ tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ nã'asgɔ la, nãmsrɩ mɛ tɩ sũure pʋam gu'ura wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn fãagɛ tɩ ba'asɛ tɩ tɩ dɛna a kɔma.

ref: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
text: Se'ere n soe la, tõma yẽ fãarɛ mɛ, la tõma ken tara la pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura. La fʋ sãn yẽ sɛla tɩ fʋ tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura la, fʋ ka le tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura. Ãne n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ a yẽ?

ref: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
text: La tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ tõma nãn ka yẽ la, tõma mã'agɛ la tɩ mẽŋa gu'ura ẽ.

ref: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
text: Bɛla mẽ, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sẽnnɩ sõŋra tõma mɛ tɩ tɔrgɔ pʋam. Se'ere n soe la, tõma ka mi tõma n wʋn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ se'em tɩ la masɛ. La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mẽŋa sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra tõma mɛ, tɔgra yetɔga tɩ nẽra ka tã wʋn tɔgɛ.

ref: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n mi nẽrba sũure la, mi sɛla tɩ Sɩa la boorɩ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Sɩa la sosrɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na.

ref: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
text: Tɩ mi tɩ sɛla woo pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ sõŋa bɔ'ɔra la nẽr-sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ, sɛba tɩ a looge ba, masɛ wʋ ẽn looge yɛm se'em na.

ref: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
text: Nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ põn dẽŋɛ looge la, a põn dẽŋɛ looge la yɛm tɩ ẽŋa wʋn botɩ ba ãna wʋ a Dayʋa la, tɩ a Dayʋa la wʋn dɛna kẽema a sɔbiisi zozo'e zẽ'am.

ref: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
text: Bɛla tɩ wʋn yetɩ bẽm? Nawẽnnɛ sãn bɔna la tõma, ãne n tã wʋn zɛbɛ la tõma?

ref: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
text: Nawẽnnɛ ka basɛ a mẽŋa Dayʋa la tɩ a põsɛ kũm. A basɛ mɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ tõma wuu ĩyã. A wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne kãn bɔ tõma sɛla wuu la a Dayʋa la?

ref: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
text: Ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ dõrɛ nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la? La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ ba masɛ.

ref: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
text: Ãne n wʋn di ba sarɩya sɩbgɛ ba? Nẽra ka boe. Azezi-krisi ki mɛ, dee vo'oge, sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ, sɔsra tõma ĩyã.

ref: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
text: Ãne n wʋn welge tõma la Krisi nõŋlʋm na? Toogo bɩɩ, sũ-sã'aŋɔ bɩɩ, nãmsgɔ bɩɩ, kɔm bɩɩ, nõŋɔ bɩɩ, yel-pakrɛ bɩɩ, bɩɩ kũm n wʋn yãŋɛ welge tõma? Sɛla ka boe.

ref: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
text: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ, fõn doosgo ĩyã tɩ tõma põsra tɩ yõorɛ daarɛ woo. Ba ɩtɩ tõma wʋ piisi tɩ ba tarɩ sẽnna kɔɔrna.»

ref: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
text: La yel-bãma wuu pʋam, sɛka n nõŋɛ tõma la n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma pãŋa tɩ tõma yãŋra.

ref: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
text: Mam mi sɩra sɩra, tɩ sɛla sɛla ka boe n wʋn yãŋɛ welge tõma la Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm. La sãn dɛna kũm, bɩɩ vɔm, bɩɩ malɛkadõma, bɩɩ pãnsɩ dõma, bɩɩ ke'eŋo dõma, bɩɩ sɛla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, bɩɩ sɛla n sẽm,

ref: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
text: bɩɩ bõn-sɛba n tarɩ pãŋa saazuum, bɩɩ sɛla n tarɩ pãŋa tẽŋa, bõn-nãanɩŋa baa ayɩla ka tã wʋn welge tõma la Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm tɩ a nõŋɛ tõma tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã la.

ref: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
