text: Mam yetɩ la sɩra, mam de la Krisi nẽra, mam ka parnɩ. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ mam mina m sũure pʋam tɩ mam n yetɩ m yele se'em na, de la sɩra.

ref: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
text: Mam tarɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ zozo'e, la sũ-dõoma daarɛ woo m buuri la ĩyã.

ref: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
text: La sãn tã nɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã be'em bɔ mam, tɩ mam welge la Krisi, tɩ la sõŋɛ mam sɔbiisi la n de mam buuri la, mam boorɩ mɛ.

ref: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
text: Ba de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n looge ba, botɩ ba lebge a kɔma, bɔ ba nã'asgɔ, bĩŋe nõa la ba lɛ, bɔ ba lɔɔ, la sore tɩ ba nã'asra ẽ, dee yele ba ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛba bɔ ba.

ref: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
text: Ba buuri la ze'ele la tɩ sɔ-yaaba Abraham, la a yɩɩsɩ la pʋam. La Krisi n lebge nẽrsaala la, de la bãma buuri nẽra. Ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ sɛla woo la n soe pẽka wakatɛ n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ. Amina.

ref: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
text: Mam n yele se'em na, la ka boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka ẽŋɛ ẽn yetɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na. La, la dagɩ sɛba wuu n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la n de Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma mẽŋa mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
text: La boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ, la dagɩ kɔm-sɛba tɩ Abraham dɔgɛ la wuu n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma. La de la kɔm-sɛba n dɔgɛ tɩ la doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na, n de Abraham yɩɩsɩ mẽŋa mẽŋa, bãma n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

ref: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
text: Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la wãna yetɩ: «Wakat-ẽna sãn wa paagɛ, mam wʋn leme wa'am, la Asaara wʋn tara dayʋa.»

ref: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
text: La dagɩ bɛla mã'a, tõma yaaba A'izakɩ pɔga Arabɛka yʋʋn dɔgɛ la dayɔɔsɩ bayi, tɩ ba dɛna lɩɩba.

ref: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
text: Wakat-sɛka tɩ a yʋʋn nãn ka dɔgɛ ba la, ba nãn ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n de sõŋa bɩɩ be'em, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele yetɩ: «Kẽema la n wʋn lebge a yɩbga la tõntõnna, tõnna bɔ'ɔra ẽ.» La a yele bɛla tɩ ẽn looge yɛm se'em na n sẽŋɛ nẽŋa. Nawẽnnɛ n looge nẽr-sɛka la, la dagɩ a tõoma ĩyã, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n looge yɛm wi ẽ.

ref: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam nõŋɛ la Azakɔbɩ, dee basɛ A'eso.»

ref: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele Amoyisi yetɩ: «Mam wʋn zoe la nẽr-sɛba tɩ mam boorɩ la ninbãalga. La mam wʋn tara la nẽr-sɛka tɩ mam boorɩ la zĩile.»

ref: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
text: Bɛla, la dagɩ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em, bɩɩ ẽn mõrgrɩ se'em, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n zotɩ ninbãalga.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
text: La Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam la gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele naba Afaarõ yetɩ: «Mam n bɔ fʋ na'am tɩ m dɩkɛ fʋ pa'alɛ m pãŋa, tɩ m yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn yese dũnia wuu.»

ref: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ zotɩ la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a boorɩ la ninbãalga, la ẽn boorɩ tɩ sɛka sũure kɛ'ɛm na, a botɩ a sũure kɛ'ɛm mɛ.

ref: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
text: Dase'ere fʋ wʋn soke mam yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna bɛla, bẽm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zɛrgra nẽrba tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa? Ãne n tã wʋn zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na?»

ref: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
text: Nẽrsaala, fõn de la ãne wẽ'era nõke'ene la Nawẽnnɛ? Yɔgrɔ lɔkɔ wʋn soke yagba n yagbɛ ẽ na yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ fʋ yagbɛ mam wãna?»

ref: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
text: Yagba la tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em la yɔgrɔ la mɛ. A tã wʋn dɩkɛ yɔgrɔ bʋyɩla mɛ, dɩkɛ ba pʋsʋka yagbɛ lɔkɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ dɛna tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ, dee dɩkɛ pʋsʋka yagbɛ lɔkɔ n dagɩ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ.

ref: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
text: La de la bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ. Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ a pa'alɛ la a sũ-isgre, dee botɩ nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ a pãŋa. La a yõkɛ a mẽŋa mɛ, mã'agɛ a sũure la sɛba n masɛ tɩ a sũ-isgre sige ba zuto, dee nara tɩ a sã'am ba la.

ref: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
text: La a ken bɔɔra tɩ a pa'alɛ la a nã'asgɔ n zo'oge se'em nẽr-sɛba tɩ a zotɩ ba ninbãalga la zẽ'am. Bãma de la sɛba tɩ a põn mãasʋm tɩ ba po yẽ a nã'asgɔ.

ref: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
text: La de wʋ A'oze gõŋɔ la pʋam tɩ la põn gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n yʋʋn dagɩ mam nẽrba la, mam wʋn wi ba tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba. La buuri sɛka tɩ mam yʋʋn ka nõŋɛ ba la, mam wʋn yetɩ mam nõŋɛ ba mɛ.

ref: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
text: La zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba yʋʋn yetɩ ba tɩ ba dagɩ mam nẽrba la, zẽ'e-ẽŋa ba wʋn wi'ira ba tɩ mam n de Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa la kɔma.»

ref: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa A'ezayi yʋʋn tɔgɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle yetɩ: «Baa Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma sõorɛ sãn zo'oge wʋ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo n boe ko-kãtɛ nõorʋm na, ba nẽrba fẽfẽe mã'a n wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em na, a wʋn ẽŋɛ a wuu mɛ tɔtɔ tẽŋa zuo.»

ref: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
text: La ken dɛna wʋ A'ezayi n yʋʋn põn yele se'em yetɩ: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la yʋʋn sãn ka deege nɩ yɩɩsɩ fẽe bɔ tõma, tõma wʋn lebge nɩ wʋ Sodom dõma, dee ãna wʋ Gomõorɩ dõma.» Nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am

ref: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
text: Bɛla, tɩ wʋn yetɩ bo? Bu-zãŋsɩ n daan ka eerɩ tɩ ba dɛna nẽrba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ ba dɛna la nẽrba n masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
text: Bẽm ĩyã? Ba ka eerɩ tɩ ba masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am bɔ sɩra ĩyã, ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma tõoma mã'a ĩyã tɩ bãma wʋn masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Ba wẽ ba nãma la kugre tɩ nẽrba wẽ'erɩ nãma luta,

ref: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
text: wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ ya! Mam bĩŋri la kugre n wẽ'erɩ nãma Siyõ, a de la kugre n botɩ nẽrba wẽ'era nãma luta. La nẽr-sɛka n bɔ ẽ sɩra, yãnnɛ kãn di ẽ.»

ref: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam n boorɩ se'em na m sũure wuu, dee sɔsra Nawẽnnɛ se'em de la mam buuri la yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
text: Ba ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masra a zẽ'am, dee bɔɔra tɩ ba ẽŋɛ sɔa ba mẽŋa masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Bɛla, ba ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em botɩ nẽrba masra a zẽ'am na.

ref: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
text: Krisi n de lɔɔ la kʋkɩ'ɩ, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra wʋn masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
text: Amoyisi yʋʋn gʋlsɛ tɔgɛ nẽra n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ lɔɔ la botɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am yelle, yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n sakɛ lɔɔ la n yetɩ se'em na, wʋn vɔa, ẽn sakɛ lɔɔ la ĩyã.»

ref: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
text: «Dee da yetɩ, ãne n wʋn sige kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa?» La boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ, ãne n wʋn sige botɩ Krisi vo'oge.

ref: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
text: Bɛla, la ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ bẽm? La yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la lɛm fʋ mɛ, a boe la fʋ nõorʋm, la fʋ sũurum.» La de la yetɔg-ẽŋa n bɛla tɩ tõma mõolɩ yɛta nẽrba tɩ ba bɔ sɩra.

ref: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
text: Fʋ sãn tɔgɛ la fʋ nõorɛ nẽrba zẽ'am tɩ Azezi de la Zuudãana, la fʋ sãn bɔ sɩra fʋ sũurum tɩ Nawẽnnɛ vo'oge ẽ mɛ, fʋ wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
text: La de la sũure pʋam tɩ nẽra bɔ'ɔrɩ Azezi sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a masɛ a zẽ'am, nẽra tɔgrɩ la a nõorɛ yetɔga yẽta fãarɛ.

ref: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ ẽ sɩra, yãnnɛ kãn di ẽ.»

ref: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
text: Bɛla, welgre ka boe Zifdõma, la bu-zãŋsɩ tẽŋasʋka. Ba lagɩm tara la Zuudãana ayɩla n ɩtɩ bõn-sõma bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba wuu n sosrɩ ẽ na.

ref: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
text: Se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka woo n sosrɩ Zuudãana sõŋrɛ, a wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.»

ref: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
text: La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne sose ẽ sõŋrɛ, ba sãn ka bɔ ẽ sɩra? La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bɔ ẽ sɩra, ba sãn ka wõm a kõ-yẽlga la? La ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne wõm kõ-yẽlga la, tɩ nẽra sãn ka mõolɛ?

ref: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
text: La kõ-yẽlga la wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne mõolɛ, Nawẽnnɛ sãn ka tõm nẽrba? Wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na: «La de la sũ-yẽlga, fʋ sãn yẽ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ kõ-yẽlga sẽna la.»

ref: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
text: La, la dagɩ nẽrba wuu n sakɛ kõ-yẽlga la. Bɛla tɩ A'ezayi gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Zuudãana, ãne n bɔ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ tõma mõolɛ la sɩra?»

ref: No one can have faith without hearing the message about Christ.
text: Bɛla, la de la kõ-yẽlga la selsgo n sẽm na bɔ sɩra, la kõ-yẽlga la de la Azezi-krisi yelle tɩ ba tɔgrɩ.

ref: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
text: Bɛla, mam sokrɩ ya yetɩ, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba ka wõm kõ-yẽlga la mɛ bɩɩ? Ba wõm mɛ. La de wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na: «Ba kõa la paagɛ tẽŋa wã wuu mɛ, ba yetɔga la saagɛ dũnia tɛka wuu mɛ.»

ref: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
text: Mam wʋn ken soke ya yetɩ, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma ka wõm a võorɛ mɛ bɩɩ? Ba wõm mɛ. Amoyisi n yʋʋn de pɔspɔsɩ dãana n yele tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn botɩ ya tara sũure la so'olʋm nẽrba n dagɩ mam nẽrba, mam wʋn botɩ ya sũure isge la so'olʋm sɛka n ka mi sɛla võorɛ.»

ref: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
text: La A'ezayi yẽ bʋraanɛ mɛ yele tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba n daan ka eerɩ mam na, ba yẽ mam mɛ, mam puke m mẽŋa mɛ pa'alɛ nẽr-sɛba n daan ka sokrɩ mam yelle la.»

ref: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
text: Dee yele Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle yetɩ: «Mam tẽegɛ m nu'usi mɛ daarɛ la wuu teese nẽrba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ, dee ka sakra.»

ref: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
text: Bɛla mam sokrɩ mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ zagsɛ a nẽrba la mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla, mam mẽ de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽra, mam de la Abraham yɩɩŋa, Abẽnzamẽ buuri.

ref: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
text: Nawẽnnɛ ka zagsɛ a nẽrba la tɩ a põn dẽŋɛ looge la. Ya mi Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la pʋam zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ A'eli yʋʋn yõnnɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle, yetɩ:

ref: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
text: «Zuudãana, ba kʋ fʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la mɛ, ba sirge fʋ kãabgɔ zẽ'esɩ la mɛ. Mam m yɩla mã'a n weege, tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba mẽ kʋ mam.»

ref: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
text: Bɛla, bẽm ẽŋɛ? Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba ka yẽ bãma n eerɩ sɛla la. La de la sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge fẽe ba pʋam na mã'a n yẽ. La nẽrba basɛba la lebge la nẽrba n ka wõnnɩ sɛla võorɛ.

ref: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
text: Bɛla mẽ, lɛɛlɛ wã, nẽrba boe mɛ fẽe, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge ba a yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã.

ref: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: La de la a yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã tɩ a looge ba, la dagɩ ba tõoma ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ loore la sãn de nɩ na nẽrba tõoma ĩyã, a yel-sõnnɛ na ka le dɛna yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
text: La ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em yetɩ: «Halɩ wa paam zĩna, Nawẽnnɛ n bʋrsɛ ba yɛm tɩ ba ka wõnna sɛla võorɛ, botɩ ba nini ka yẽta, tɩ ba tʋba ka wõnna.»

ref: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
text: Adavidi mẽ yele Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Botɩ ba dia la dɛna bẽrgɔ bɔ ba, yõkɛ ba, tɩ ba wẽ nãma lui yẽ nãmsgɔ n de ba yɔɔrɔ.

ref: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
text: Botɩ ba nini ãna lim, tɩ ba da yẽta, botɩ ba nãmsra wakatɛ wuu wʋ nẽra n tʋ zeero gõ'osra.»

ref: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
text: Bɛla mam sokrɩ ya yetɩ: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n wẽ nãma lui la, la ba'asɛ mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla. La ban tuuge la ĩyã, tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ tã'agɛ yẽ fãarɛ. Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ la botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n ɩta sũure.

ref: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
text: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma tuure la n tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ dũnia, botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ tara Nawẽnnɛ bõntarsõm na. Ba sãn yẽ fãarɛ ba zõnzõnnɔ, la wʋn tara yõorɔ zo'oge gãnna. Bu-zãŋsɩ, la Zifdõma fãarɛ yelle

ref: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
text: Mam yãŋa yetɩ m tɔgɛ la yãma sɛba n de bu-zãŋsɩ la: Mam n de Krisi Tõntõnna tõnna bɔ'ɔra yãma n de bu-zãŋsɩ la, mam pẽgrɩ m tõonɛ na mɛ.

ref: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
text: Mam ɩtɩ bɛla tɩ m botɩ m buuri la n ɩta sũure, tɩ la botɩ ba nẽrba basɛba yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n daan basɛ ba kẽnkɛrŋa la, a botɩ dũnia nẽrba wuu n naagɛ la ẽŋa. La Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn wa le to'oge ba la, la wʋn ãna la wãne? La wʋn ãna wʋ nẽra n ki dee vo'oge.

ref: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
text: Fʋ sãn dɩkɛ pɔspɔsɩ borborɩ la bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, borbor-sɛba n weege la wuu mẽ de la a bõnɔ. Tɩa yẽga sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ, tɩa la wila mẽ de la a bõnɔ.

ref: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
text: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la ãn wʋ Oliivi tɩa tɩ ba sele, tɩ ba kʋ'ʋsɛ a wila basɛ. La fõn de bu-zãŋka la ãn wʋ Oliivi tɩa n boe weem wille tɩ ba kʋ'ʋgɛ, dɩkɛ toŋe la tɩa tɩ ba sele la wila. Fõn ãn wʋ wille la n to'orɩ sõŋrɛ tɩa tɩ ba sele la yẽga zẽ'am na.

ref: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
text: Bɛla, da pɔ'ɔra wila tɩ ba kʋ'ʋsɛ basɛ la. Da wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ, tɩ la dagɩ fõn tarɩ yẽga la, yẽga la n tarɩ fõn.

ref: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
text: Tã tɩ fʋ wʋn yele yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ kʋ'ʋsɛ wila la basɛ, tɩ a dɩkɛ la mam toŋe.»

ref: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
text: La de la sɩra, la de la ban ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ a kʋ'ʋsɛ ba basɛ. La fõn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ fʋ toŋe mĩ. Bɛla, da ɩta tɩta'alʋm, zɔta dabeem.

ref: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n ka basɛ tɩa mẽŋa wila la, bãŋɛ tɩ a ken kãn basɛ fõn mẽ, fʋ sãn ka bɔ ẽ sɩra.

ref: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
text: Bɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ sõŋa se'em, dee tɔɩ se'em. A tɔɩ mɛ la sɛba n ka sakrɩ ẽ na, dee ɩta fʋ sõŋa, fʋ sãn ken bɔna a sõŋa la pʋam. Sãn dagna bɛla, a mẽ wʋn kʋ'ʋgɛ fʋ basɛ mɛ.

ref: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
text: La Zifdõma la sãn basɛ ba sɩ'ɩsgɔ la, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ ba mɛ lebe toŋe la tɩa la. Nawẽnnɛ tarɩ pãŋa mɛ wʋn dɩkɛ ba lebe toŋe.

ref: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
text: Fõn ãn wʋ Oliivi tɩa n boe weem wille, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kʋ'ʋgɛ toŋe Oliivi tɩa tɩ ba sele n dagɩ fʋ zẽ'am. La bãma n de tɩa tɩ ba sele la mẽŋa wila la, la de la nãana tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ ba lebe toŋe la ba mẽŋa tɩa la lɛ.

ref: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ yelle n sʋgɛ la, tɩ ya da bɩsɛ ya mẽŋa tɩ yãma de la yɛm dõma: Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽrba basɛba n kɛ'ɛm ba sũure ka bɔ sɩra la, la wʋn yuuge mɛ halɩ tarɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
text: Bɛla tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri wuu wʋn yẽ fãarɛ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam la gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Fãagra la wʋn ze'ele la Siyõ wa'am. A wʋn botɩ Azakɔbɩ yɩɩsɩ da le sɩ'ɩsra.

ref: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
text: Mam nõorɛ n bɛla tɩ mam wʋn bĩŋe la ba, wakat-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn yese ba tõon-be'ero basɛ la.»

ref: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
text: Bãma n zagsɛ kõ-yẽlga la, ba lebge la Nawẽnnɛ bɛ'ɛba, tɩ la dɛna yãma sõŋrɛ. La, la sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ n looge ba la, ba de la nẽr-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ ba, ba yaabdõma ĩyã.

ref: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
text: Nawẽnnɛ ka teerɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a looge, ẽŋɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ ba la.

ref: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
text: Kʋrʋm, yãma bu-zãŋsɩ la zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, a zoe yãma ninbãalga mɛ, Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n zagsɛ ẽ na ĩyã.

ref: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
text: Bɛla, bãma n zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, tɩ a zoe yãma ninbãalga la, la de la tɩ a mẽ wʋn zoe ba ninbãalga.

ref: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ nẽrba wuu tɩ ba sɩ'ɩsɛ ẽ, tɩ a lagɩm zoe la ba wuu ninbãalga.

ref: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
text: Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, la a yɛm, la a bãŋrɛ la zo'oge mɛ. Nẽra ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ a pʋtẽ'erɛ. La nẽra ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ a sorɔɔrɔ tɩ a dolɩ la võorɛ.

ref: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
text: La de wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Ãne n mi Zuudãana pʋtẽ'erɛ? Bɩɩ ãne n tã wʋn kã'an ẽ?

ref: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
text: Bɩɩ ãne n bɔ ẽ sɛla, dee tɩ a wa lebse ẽ?»

ref: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
text: Sɛla woo ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ sɛla woo bɔna, dee dɛna a bõnɔ. Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ wakatɛ wuu. Amina.

ref: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
text: M sɔbiisi, Nawẽnnɛ n zoe tõma ninbãalga bɛla la, mam kã'anɩ ya tɩ ya dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa bɔ ẽ tɩ la ãna wʋ kãabgɔ n vɔɩ, tɩ ya welge to'ore dɛna a nẽrba n paarɩ a yɛm. Bɛla n de pʋ'ʋsgɔ sore n masɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
text: Da ãna-ya wʋ dũnia wã dõma n ãn se'em na. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya yɛm like dɛna paalga, tɩ ya teege, tã'agɛ bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em: Sɛla n ãn sõŋa, sɛla n paarɩ a yɛm, sɛla n masɛ sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ mam na ĩyã, mam yetɩ ya wuu mɛ, tɩ ya da tẽ'esɛ tẽ'esgɔ n gãnnɩ ya mẽŋa. La tara ya tẽ'esgɔ n masɛ, tɩ la doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a tɩ a bɔ ya nẽra woo, yãma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la.

ref: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
text: Tõma ĩyã de la ayɩla, dee tara zẽ'esɩ zozo'e, tɩ zẽ'a woo tõoma dɛna to'oto'ore.

ref: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
text: Bɛla mẽ, baa la tõma n zo'oge la, tõma lebge la ĩyã ayɩla, tõma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la. La tõma lagɩm na taaba ãna wʋ ĩyã ayɩla zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la.

ref: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
text: Tɩ to'oge la bɔ'a to'oto'ore, masɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ nẽra woo se'em na. Bɛla, sɛka n tarɩ bɔ'a n de a tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, a tɔ'ɔsra tɔgra tɩ la masɛ wʋ bɔ sɩra n de se'em na.

ref: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
text: Nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de sõŋrɛ tõonɛ, a sõŋra. Nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de a zãsna nẽrba, a zãsna.

ref: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
text: Nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de a kã'ana nẽrba, a kã'ana. La sɛka bɔ'a n de a bɔ'ɔra, a bɔ'ɔra la a sũure wuu. La sɛka bɔ'a n de a dɛna nẽŋadãana, a wɩsgɛ a mẽŋa tõnna sõŋa. Tɩ nẽr-sɛka bɔ'a n de a zɔta nẽrba ninbãalga, a ɩta la sũ-yẽlga.

ref: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya nõŋlʋm dɛna sɩra nõŋlʋm, sise-ya be'em, la ya gʋrɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa.

ref: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
text: Nõŋɛ-ya taaba sɔbiisi nõŋlʋm. La ya nanna taaba, tẽ'esra taaba yelle.

ref: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
text: Wɩsgɛ-ya ya mẽŋa, da zɔta-ya zẽyãgrɛ. Tõnna-ya Nawẽnnɛ tõoma la ya sũure wuu.

ref: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
text: Ɩta-ya sũ-yẽlga yãma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la ĩyã. Mã'agɛ-ya ya sũure toogo pʋam, mõrgɛ-ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ wakatɛ wuu.

ref: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
text: Sõŋɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ sõŋrɛ la, la ya ɩta sãama sãanɔ.

ref: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
text: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n nãmsrɩ ya la. Pʋ'ʋsra-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ ba, la ya da kã'ara kã'a-be'ero bɔ'ɔra ba.

ref: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
text: Ɩta-ya sũ-yẽlga la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ sũ-yẽlga la. La ya kɛlla la sɛba n kellɩ.

ref: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
text: Tara-ya pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla la taaba. Da dɛna-ya pa'al-m-mẽŋa dõma, sakra-ya lagna la tarɩmdõma. Da bɩsra ya mẽŋa tɩ yãma de la yɛm dõma.

ref: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
text: Da lebse nẽra be'em, be'em ĩyã. Nargɛ-ya pãŋa ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa nẽrba wuu zẽ'am.

ref: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
text: La sãn dɛna la yãma, ya sãn tãna, ya botɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna yãma la nẽrba wuu tẽŋasʋka.

ref: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
text: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, da lebse-ya lerge be'em ya mẽŋa. Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũure isge. Tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam n de m lebse be'em, la de la mam n wʋn yɔ.»

ref: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
text: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Fʋ bɛ kɔm sãn dõnna, bɔ ẽ dia tɩ a di, koyũuro sãn tarɩ ẽ, bɔ ẽ ko'om tɩ a yũ. Fʋ sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, la ãn wʋ bugsãana tɩ fʋ dɩkɛ dɔglɛ a zuugo la.»

ref: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
text: Da basɛ tɩ be'em yãŋɛ fɔ, ẽŋɛ sõŋa yãŋɛ be'em.

ref: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
text: Nẽra woo sakɛ tẽŋa la sɔ'ɔnɩba n soe ke'eŋo la. Se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ la ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ tẽŋa la sɔ'ɔnɩba ke'eŋo.

ref: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
text: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka sãn sɩ'ɩsɛ tẽŋa la sɔ'ɔnɩba nõorɛ, a sɩ'ɩsɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ tɩ la dɛna se'em na. Nẽr-sɛba wuu n sɩ'ɩsrɩ la, eerɩ la sɩbgrɛ bɔ'ɔra ba mẽŋa.

ref: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ sõŋa la ka zotɩ dabeem la sɛba n bɩsrɩ tẽŋa la, la de la nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ be'em na n zotɩ dabeem. Fõn boorɩ tɩ fʋ da zɔta nẽr-sɛba n soe ke'eŋo la mɛ bɩɩ? Ɩta sõŋa tɩ ba pẽgɛ fɔ.

ref: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
text: Se'ere n soe la, sɛka n soe ke'eŋo la tõnnɩ la Nawẽnnɛ tõoma fʋ sõŋrɛ ĩyã. La fʋ sãn ẽŋɛ be'em, zoe a dabeem, tɩ la dagɩ zãŋa tɩ a tara ke'eŋo wʋn sɩbgɛ. A tõnnɩ la Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ sɩbgra nẽr-sɛka n ẽŋɛ be'em pa'ala Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre.

ref: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ la nara tɩ nẽrba sakra nõorɛ, la dagɩ ban wʋn sɩbgɛ ya la mã'a ĩyã, la de la yan mi ya sũure pʋam tɩ la ãn sõŋa la ĩyã.

ref: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
text: La ken dɛna la bõn-ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ ya yɔɔra lampo. Sɛba n to'osrɩ lampo la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ba tõon-ẽŋa tɩ ba tõnna sõŋa.

ref: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
text: Da tara-ya nẽra sãnnɛ. Sãnnɛ sɛka tɩ ya wʋn tara de la ya nõŋɛ taaba. Nẽr-sɛka woo n nõŋɛ a tadãana sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la wuu mɛ.

ref: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Da ɩta yalsɩ, da kʋ nẽra, da zũura, da ɩta sũure la nẽra bõnɔ.» Nawẽnnɛ nõa bãna, la basɛba la wuu lagsɛ taaba la yetɔg-ẽna ayɩla pʋam n yetɩ: «Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa la.»

ref: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
text: Nõŋlʋm basɛ mɛ tɩ nẽra ka ɩta a tadãana be'em. Bɛla, nẽra n nõŋɛ a taaba sakɛ la lɔɔ la wuu.

ref: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
text: Ẽŋɛ-ya bɛla, yãma n mi wakat-sɛka tɩ tɩ boe wã la ĩyã, wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ dɛna ya nẽegɛ dee basɛ gẽem. Fãarɛ la lɛm tõma mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã gãnna wakat-sɛka tɩ tõma daan pɔsɛ bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la.

ref: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
text: Tẽŋa sʋtʋm mɛ tɩ beere deege wiire. Bɛla, basɛ-ya lika tõoma, dee dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ tõnna peelem tõoma.

ref: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ tõnna la pʋpeelem wʋ nẽrba n sẽnnɩ wʋntɛɛŋa. Tɩ basɛ-ya pʋyã'an-yooro, la dãam buka, la tũulgo, la yalsɩ, la zɛbrɛ, dee da ɩta sũure la taaba.

ref: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
text: Ɩta-ya tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n ẽŋɛ se'em na, da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em yelle pakɛ ya, tɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya ɩta yãma mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em.

ref: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
text: To'oge-ya nẽr-sɛka n ka tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam na sõŋa, la ya da wẽ'era nõke'ene la ẽ lɛ a tẽ'esgɔ pʋam.

ref: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
text: Nẽr-sɛka tẽ'esɛ mɛ tɩ ẽŋa tã wʋn di sɛla woo mɛ, tɩ sɛka pãŋa n pɔ'ɔrɩ la dita bõnkɔɔla la zẽerɔ mã'a.

ref: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
text: Fõn de la ãne zɛrgra nẽra ayẽma tõntõnna? A sãn zɛ saazuo, bɩɩ a sãn lui mɛ, bɛla pakɛ la a zuudãana. La a wʋn zɛ la saazuo, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana la tarɩ pãŋa mɛ wʋn botɩ a zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ.

ref: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
text: Nẽr-sɛka tẽ'esrɩ mɛ tɩ dabsa basɛba ãn sõŋa gãnna dabsa basɛba mɛ. Tɩ ayẽma yetɩ ba wuu de la ayɩla. Dẽnɩ tɩ nẽra woo bãŋɛ tɩ ẽŋa n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na masɛ mɛ.

ref: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n tẽ'esrɩ dabsa n ãn sõŋa yelle la, ɩtɩ bɛla nã'asra la Zuudãana. La nẽr-sɛka n diti sɛla woo la, mẽ bɔ'ɔrɩ la Zuudãana nã'asgɔ, ẽn pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ dia la ĩyã la. La sɛka n ka diti dia basɛba la, ɩtɩ bɛla mẽ bɔ'ɔra la Zuudãana nã'asgɔ, ẽŋa mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
text: La de se'em na, tõma nẽr-sɛka ka vɔa a mẽŋa ĩyã, la nẽr-sɛka ka kiiri a mẽŋa ĩyã.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
text: Tõma sãn vɔa, tɩ vɔa la Zuudãana ĩyã. La tɩ sãn ki, tɩ ki la Zuudãana ĩyã. Bɛla, tɩ sãn vɔa mɛ, bɩɩ tɩ sãn ki mɛ, tɩ de la Zuudãana la nẽrba.

ref: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Krisi ki mɛ dee vo'oge, tɩ a dɛna sɛba n ki, la sɛba n vɔa Zuudãana.

ref: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
text: La fõn, bẽm tɩ fõn zɛrgra fʋ sɔbia? La fõn mẽ, bẽm tɩ fõn pɔ'ɔra fʋ sɔbia? Tɩ wuu wʋn ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne nẽŋam tɩ a di tɩ sarɩya.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam n de Zuudãana la vɔa mɛ, nẽrsaalba wuu wʋn ka dũma mam nẽŋam, tɩ nẽra woo wʋn nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
text: Bɛla, tõma nẽra woo wʋn tɔgɛ a mẽŋa yelle bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
text: Bɛla, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ da le zɛrgra taaba. La de la tɩ looge yɛm, da ẽŋɛ sɛla n wʋn botɩ tɩ sɔbia tuuge, bɩɩ n wʋn botɩ a basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore.

ref: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
text: Mam n lagɩm na Zuudãana Azezi dɛna ayɩla la, mam mi sɩra sɩra tɩ sɛla ka boe dɛna dẽgrɔ a mẽŋa. La nẽra sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ sɛla de la dẽgrɔ dee di, la de la dẽgrɔ bɔ ẽ.

ref: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
text: Bõn-sɛka tɩ fõn diti la sãn botɩ fʋ sɔbia sũure sã'am, fʋ ka le ɩta wʋ nõŋlʋm n de se'em na. Da sakɛ tɩ dia tɩ fʋ diti la botɩ fʋ sɔbia tɩ Krisi ki a ĩyã la sã'am.

ref: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
text: Da basɛ tɩ sɛla n ãn sõŋa bɔ fõn na, lebge sɛla n wʋn dɛna yʋ'ʋrɛ sã'aŋɔ.

ref: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
text: Bẽm ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na dagɩ dia, la bõn-yũula yelle, la de la sɛla n masɛ, la sũ-mã'asʋm, la sũ-yẽlga tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ.

ref: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n dolɩ Krisi wãna, ẽŋa dãana n paarɩ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm, la nẽrba mẽ yetɩ mɛ tɩ ẽn ɩtɩ se'em na ãn sõŋa.

ref: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
text: Bɛla, tɩ ɩta-ya bõn-sɛba wuu n sẽm na sũ-mã'asʋm, dee sõŋra nẽrba basɛba tɩ tɩ wuu lagɩm sẽnna nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

ref: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
text: Da sã'am-ya Nawẽnnɛ tõonɛ dia ĩyã. Dia wuu ãn sõŋa mɛ tɩ ba di. La nẽra sãn di sɛla n wʋn botɩ a tadãana tuuge, la ka ãn sõŋa.

ref: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
text: La ãn sõŋa tɩ fʋ da ẽŋɛ bõn-sɛba wuu n wʋn botɩ fʋ sɔbia tuuge Nawẽnnɛ sore la zuo, la sãn dɛna nẽnnɔ ɔbga, bɩɩ dãam yũa, bɩɩ sɛla woo n wʋn botɩ a tuuge.

ref: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
text: Fõn mi sɛla la bõn-ẽŋa yelle la, tara ẽ fõn mẽŋa la Nawẽnnɛ mã'a zẽ'am. Nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ a bɩsɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa, dee ka zɛrgra a mẽŋa, ẽŋa dãana n tarɩ zu-yẽlga.

ref: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
text: La nẽr-sɛka n zɛrgrɩ a mẽŋa la sɛla tɩ a diti yelle, a koŋe bʋʋrɔ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, a ka ɩtɩ ẽŋa n bɔ sɩra mina se'em na. La bõn-sɛka woo n ka dolɩ la bɔ sɩra de la tõon-be'ero.

ref: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ tõma sɛba n tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam na, sõŋɛ nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ pãŋa la, ba tɔrgɔ pʋam. La tɩ da ɛɛra sɛla n ãn tõma yẽlʋm.

ref: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ tõma nẽra woo ɛɛra sɛla n wʋn ẽŋɛ a tadãana yẽlʋm, tɩ la sõŋɛ ẽ tɩ a sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

ref: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
text: Azezi-krisi ka ɛ sɛla n ãn a mẽŋa sũure yẽlʋm, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Tʋʋrɛ sɛba tɩ ba tʋʋrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na, sige la mam zuo.»

ref: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ sũ-mã'arɛ, la bʋraanɛ la wʋn botɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla la taaba wʋ Azezi-krisi n boorɩ se'em na.

ref: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
text: Tɩ ya wuu wʋn tara nõorɛ ayɩla, la yɛm ayɩla nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la.

ref: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
text: Bɛla, tɔ'ɔsra-ya taaba wʋ Krisi n to'oge yãma se'em na, tɩ la wʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

ref: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
text: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ Krisi lebge la Zifdõma tõntõnna, tɩ a ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe a nõorɛ la tɩ yaabdõma se'em na, pa'alɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana.

ref: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
text: A ken wa'am mɛ tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ la wʋn nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ, ẽn zotɩ ba ninbãalga la ĩyã, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bɛla tɩ mam wʋn nã'asra fõn so'olʋm buuri wuu tẽŋasʋka, la mam wʋn ken yõom nã'asɛ fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ.»

ref: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
text: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «So'olʋm wuu nẽrba, lagɩm-ya la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba ɩta sũ-yẽlga.»

ref: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
text: La le gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «So'olʋm wuu nẽrba, nã'asɛ-ya Zuudãana, buuri wuu nẽrba, pẽgɛ-ya ẽ.»

ref: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
text: A'ezayi mẽ gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Azese yɩɩŋa n wʋn wa'am, a wʋn sɔna la so'olʋm buuri wuu. La so'olʋm buuri wuu wʋn tara pʋtẽ'erɛ la ẽ lɛ.»

ref: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
text: Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ tõma tara pʋtẽ'erɛ la ẽ na wʋn bɔ ya sũ-yẽlga, la sũ-mã'asʋm zozo'e, yãma n bɔ Krisi sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a Sɩa pãŋa wʋn botɩ yan tara pʋtẽ'erɛ a zẽ'am na zo'oge gãŋɛ. Apolɩ tõoma, la ẽn tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em

ref: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam mi mɛ sɩra sɩra tɩ yãma ɩtɩ sõŋa mɛ zozo'e, la ya tarɩ bãŋrɛ wuu zo'oge mɛ tãna wʋn kã'an taaba.

ref: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
text: La baa bɛla, mam gʋlsɛ yãma mɛ zẽ'esɩ basɛba la sũ-ke'eŋo tɩ m tẽegɛ ya yãma n põn zãsʋm mina sɛla la. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ mam yel-sõnnɛ,

ref: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
text: botɩ mam dɛna Azezi-krisi tõntõnna tɩ m tõnna a tõoma bɔ'ɔra bu-zãŋsɩ la ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ tõoma tɩ mam tõnnɩ na de la m mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ ba welge to'ore dɛna bɔ'a n paarɩ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm.

ref: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
text: Mam n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ mam n tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

ref: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
text: Mam kãn saagɛ tɔgɛ sɛla yelle, sãn dagna Krisi n botɩ mam ẽŋɛ sɛla botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ na mã'a. La de la mam yetɔga, la mam tõoma pʋam

ref: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
text: tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pãŋa botɩ mam ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ n tarɩ pãŋa. Bɛla tɩ mam ze'ele Zerizalɛm mõolɛ Azezi-krisi kõ-yẽlga la wuu zẽ'esɩ wuu halɩ ka paagɛ Iliri so'olʋm.

ref: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
text: La mam n boorɩ se'em de la m mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ ba nãn ka wõm a yelle la, mam ka boorɩ tɩ m mõolɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽra ayẽma dẽŋɛ mõolɛ la, tɩ m da mẽ nẽra ayẽma ẽbrɛ zuo.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
text: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba ka mõolɛ ba a yelle la, wʋn bãŋɛ. La nẽr-sɛba n nãn ka wõm a yelle la, wʋn wõm a võorɛ.»

ref: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
text: Bɛla n gu mam nõorɛ faa, tɩ mam ka yãŋɛ wa'am ya zẽ'am.

ref: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam tõm m tõoma zẽ'e-bãma ba'asɛ mɛ. La de la yʋʋma zozo'e tɩ mam boorɩ la m sũure wuu tɩ m wa'am wa bɩsɛ ya.

ref: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
text: La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ mam sãn wa wɛ'ɛsa Ɛspayẽ, mam wʋn doose yẽ ya, dee bɔna ya zẽ'am wakatɛ fẽe tɩ m pʋtẽ'erɛ kɔ'ɔgɛ, dee tɩ ya yãŋa sõŋɛ mam tɩ m tole.

ref: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Masedʋanɩ, la Akayi so'olʋm na looge la yɛm lagsɛ ligri tɩ ba sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Zerizalɛm na tarɩmdõma.

ref: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
text: La de la ba mẽŋa n looge yɛm, la, la de la ba ẽŋɛ bɛla. Zifdõma la n botɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma po lagɩm to'oge bõn-sõma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ba la. Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ ba mẽ sõŋɛ Zifdõma, la ban tarɩ sɛla.

ref: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
text: Mam sãn tarɩ ligri la tɩ ba lagsɛ la sẽŋɛ ka bɔ ba kɩ'ɩlɩm, mam wʋn wa'am wa kaagɛ ya, dee tole Ɛspayẽ.

ref: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
text: Mam mi tɩ mam sãn wa wa'am ya zẽ'am, tɩ wʋn yẽ Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ zo'oge.

ref: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
text: M sɔbiisi, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nõŋlʋm na ĩyã, mam sosrɩ ya mɛ, tɩ ya lagɩm na mam mõrgɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ mam ĩyã.

ref: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ m yãŋɛ põsɛ la sɛba n boe Zude so'olʋm ka bɔ Azezi sɩra la zẽ'am, la tɩ mam n tarɩ sõŋrɛ la wɛ'ɛsa Zerizalɛm na, Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la wʋn to'oge ba la sũ-yẽlga.

ref: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ sãn bɔɔra, mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am la sũ-yẽlga, la mam wʋn yẽ vo'osgo yãma zẽ'am.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n de sũ-mã'asʋm dãana wʋn bɔna la ya wuu lɛ. Amina.

ref: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ya to'oge tɩ sɔbi-pɔka Afeebe ya zẽ'am. A de la Sãnkre Wẽndeego yel-bɩsra.

ref: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
text: To'oge-ya ẽ sõŋa Zuudãana doosgo la pʋam, wʋ lan masɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba ɩta se'em na. La sɛla sãn wa bɔna tɩ a bɔɔra ya sõŋrɛ, ya sõŋɛ ẽ. Ẽŋa mẽŋa sõŋɛ nẽrba zo'oge mɛ, la mam mẽŋa mẽ.

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Apriska, la Akila n de mam tõntõn-taaba Azezi-krisi doosgo pʋam na.

ref: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
text: Ba daan põsɛ ba yõorɛ mɛ mam ĩyã fãagɛ mam. La dagɩ mam m yɩla mã'a n pʋ'ʋsrɩ ba ba yel-sõnnɛ, bu-zãŋsɩ Wẽndeto la wuu nẽrba mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ba.

ref: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Azezi nẽrba la wuu n lagsrɩ ba yire la. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya m sɩr-nõŋrɛ A'epayinɛtɩ bɔ mam. Ẽŋa n de pɔspɔsɩ nẽra n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra Azi so'olʋm.

ref: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Amaari n daan tõm nãmsɛ zo'oge bɔ ya la.

ref: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ A'ãndronikusi, la Azuniasɩ n de mam buuri n daan po lagɩm na mam bɔna yʋ'a deem na. Ba de la nẽrba tɩ ba nannɩ Krisi Tõntõnɩba la pʋam, la ba põn dẽŋɛ mam mɛ lebge Krisi nẽrba.

ref: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Ampiliyatusi n de mam sɩrɛ tɩ mam nõŋɛ Zuudãana doosgo pʋam na.

ref: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya A'urbẽ n de tɩ tõntõn-tadãana Krisi doosgo pʋam na, la m sɩr-nõŋrɛ A'ɛstakisi.

ref: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Apelɛsɩ n mõrgɛ Krisi doosgo pʋam na. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Aristobuli yir-dõma.

ref: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya A'erodiyõ n de m buuri nẽra la. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya nẽr-sɛba n boe Anarsisi yire dɔla Zuudãana la.

ref: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Atirfɛnɩ, la Atirfozɩ tɩ ba de la pɔgsɩ n mõrgrɩ tõnna Zuudãana doosgo pʋam. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya m sõma Apɛrsidi n mõrgɛ tõm zozo'e bɔ Zuudãana la.

ref: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Arufusi tɩ nẽrba zozo'e bɩsrɩ Zuudãana doosgo pʋam na, la a ma n lebge wʋ mam ma la.

ref: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Asẽnkriti, la Afelgõ, la A'ɛrmẽsɩ, la Apatrobasɩ, la A'ɛrmasɩ, la sɔbiisi sɛba wuu n boe la ba lɛ la.

ref: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Afilolɔgɩ, la Azuli, la Aneere la a tã, la A'olẽmpasɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sɛba n boe la ba lɛ la.

ref: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm. Krisi Wẽndeto nẽrba la wuu pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

ref: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
text: M sɔbiisi, mam kã'anɩ ya tɩ ya guusra ya mẽŋa la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ welgre sẽnna ya zẽ'am, dee sɩ'ɩsra zãsŋɔ sɛka tɩ ya to'oge, botɩ nẽrba basra Nawẽnnɛ sore la. Zãagɛ ya mẽŋa la ba lɛ.

ref: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-bãma buuri ka tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi, ba tõnnɩ na ba mẽŋa sõŋrɛ ĩyã. Ba tɔgrɩ la nõ-yẽlʋm yetɔga, la yetɔg-yẽlsɩ pã'asra nẽr-sɛba yɛm n ka zo'oge la.

ref: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
text: Nẽrba wuu bãŋɛ yãma n sakrɩ Zuudãana se'em mɛ. Bɛla, mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ yãma ĩyã. La mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ ya tara yɛm mina sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la ya dɛna pʋpeelem dõma, da ɩta sɛla n de be'em.

ref: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n de sũ-mã'asʋm dãana la kãn yuuge dee sã'am Asũtãana pãŋa botɩ a bɔna ya nãma tẽŋa. Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya lɛ.

ref: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
text: Mam tõntõn-tadãana Atimõtɩ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya. Alusiyusi, la Azasõ, la Asozipatɛɛrɩ n de mam buuri la mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

ref: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
text: Mam Atɛrtiyusi n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã la, mam mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya la Zuudãana yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
text: Pẽgɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ, ẽŋa n tã wʋn botɩ ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, wʋ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam mõolɛ pa'alɛ ya Azezi-krisi yelle, botɩ ya bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yelle n sʋgɛ la. Yel-ẽŋa sʋgɛ mɛ halɩ yʋʋma yʋʋma.

ref: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba gõnnɔ na yãŋa botɩ a puke mɛ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ n bɔ nõorɛ se'em na. A botɩ so'olʋm buuri wuu nẽrba bãŋɛ ẽ mɛ, tɩ ba wʋn bɔ ẽ sɩra, sakɛ ẽ.

ref: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
text: Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n de yɛm dãana la sɔna nã'asgɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ, Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã. Amina.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
text: Mam Apolɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi, botɩ mam dɛna Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna wʋ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, la tɩ sɔbia Asostɛnɩ n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã

ref: To God's church in Corinth. Christ Jesus chose you to be his very own people, and you worship in his name, as we and all others do who call him Lord.
text: bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Korẽntɩ la. Nawẽnnɛ wi yãma tɩ ya welge la to'ore dɛna a nẽrba Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Gõŋɔ la ken dɛna la sɛba wuu n boe zẽ'a woo pʋ'ʋsra Azezi-krisi n de bãma la tõma Zuudãana la ĩyã.

ref: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.

ref: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ wakatɛ wuu ya ĩyã, ẽn bɔ ya a yel-sõnnɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã la.

ref: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
text: Krisi kõ-yẽlga la tɩ ba tɔgɛ bɔ ya la, yãma gʋrɛ ẽ mɛ kãŋkãŋɛ.

ref: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
text: Bɛla, yãma ka koŋe bɔ'a baa ayɩla, yãma n gu'uri tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wa'am wa puke a mẽŋa la.

ref: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
text: Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya tɩ ya lagɩm na a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana la dɛna ayɩla la, de la sɩra dãana. Welgre Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka

ref: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam sosrɩ ya la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ tɩ ya wuu sakra taaba nõorɛ, tɩ welgre da bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka. Ẽŋɛ-ya nõorɛ ayɩla, tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla la taaba.

ref: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
text: M sɔbiisi, se'ere n soe tɩ mam yele bɛla la, Akolowe yir-dõma basɛba n wa'am wa tɔgɛ pa'alɛ mam tɩ ka-wõm-taaba n boe ya tẽŋasʋka.

ref: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
text: Mam wõm tɩ yãma nẽrba basɛba yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Apolɩ, tɩ basɛba yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Apolɔsɩ, tɩ basɛba yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, tɩ basɛba mẽ yetɩ, bãma dolɩ la Krisi.

ref: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
text: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Krisi welse mɛ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Apolɩ n daan ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo yãma ĩyã, bɩɩ ba mise yãma ko'om pʋam la Apolɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ?

ref: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, mam n ka mise ya nẽra baa ayɩla ko'om pʋam sãn dagna Akrispusi la Agayusi la.

ref: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
text: Bɛla, nẽra kãn tã'agɛ yele yetɩ, ba daan mise ẽŋa ko'om pʋam la mam yʋ'ʋrɛ.

ref: I did baptize the family of Stephanas, but I don't remember if I baptized anyone else.
text: Ẽhẽe! Mam daan ken mise la Astefanasɩ la a yir-dõma ko'om pʋam. La mam ka le mina mam sãn ken mise nẽr-sɛka.

ref: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
text: Krisi ka tõm mam tɩ m mise nẽrba ko'om pʋam, a tõm mam tɩ m mõolɛ la Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga bɔ nẽrba, la, la dagɩ la nẽrsaalba yɛm, tɩ la da basɛ tɩ Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo la pãŋa la lebge zãŋa. Nawẽnnɛ yɛm gãnnɩ dũnia yɛm

ref: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
text: Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo yetɔga la de la yalnɛ bɔ nẽr-sɛba n ka yẽ fãarɛ la. La tõma sɛba n yẽ fãarɛ la mi tɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa.

ref: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn sã'am yɛm dõma yɛm, dee yese bãŋrɩba bãŋrɛ.»

ref: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
text: Yɛm dõma yɛm võorɛ de la bẽm? Gõn-mitba mi'ilum võorɛ de la bẽm? Dũnia wã nõke'en-dõma n wẽ'erɩ nõkɛ'ɛma tɩ bãma mi yɛla zozo'e la võorɛ de la bẽm? Nawẽnnɛ pa'alɛ tɩ dũnia wã yɛm de la yalnɛ.

ref: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ sɛla la a yɛm na, nẽrsaalba ka tã'agɛ bãŋɛ ẽ la ba mẽŋa yɛm. Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ kõ-yẽlga la mõolgɔ tɩ nẽrsaalba yetɩ la de la yalnɛ la, fãagɛ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la.

ref: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
text: Zifdõma boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ la bõn-yãlma n pa'alɛ tɩ la de la sɩra, la Grɛkɩdõma eerɩ la yɛm.

ref: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
text: Tõma nõo mõolɩ la Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo la kõ-yẽlga, kõ-yẽlga ẽŋa tɩ Zifdõma dɩkɛ tɩ a dɛna pɔ'ɔrɛ yetɔga, tɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la dɩkɛ tɩ la dɛna yalnɛ.

ref: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n looge sɛba la, sɛba n de Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma la, mi tɩ Krisi de la pãŋa dãana la yɛm dãana n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
text: Bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ, tɩ nẽrsaalba dɩkɛ tɩ a dɛna yalnɛ na, tarɩ yɛm mɛ gãnna nẽrsaalba yɛm. La sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ tɩ nẽrsaalba tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ka tarɩ pãŋa la, tarɩ pãŋa mɛ gãnna nẽrsaalba pãŋa.

ref: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
text: M sɔbiisi, lɛɛlɛ wã, tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ yãma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi la n ãn se'em. Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ dũnia yɛm, la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ pãŋa, la nẽrzuto ka zo'oge ya pʋam.

ref: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ looge sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ la de la yalnɛ, tɩ a ẽŋɛ la yɛm dõma yãnnɛ, la a ken looge la sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ a ka tarɩ pãŋa la, tɩ a botɩ pãnsɩ dõma n yẽ yãnnɛ.

ref: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
text: La a looge la sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma ka bɩsrɩ, la sɛla tɩ ba nẽ'esrɩ, la sɛla tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ tɩ a de la zãŋa la, tɩ a sã'am sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ a de la ninmõ'orɛ bõnɔ.

ref: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
text: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ nẽra n da yãŋɛ zẽkɛ a mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam.

ref: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tɩ ya lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ tɩ tara Nawẽnnɛ yɛm, dee masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, welge to'ore lebge a nẽrba n yese tõon-be'ero pʋam.

ref: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
text: Bɛla tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a wẽ yõ'ogɔ, a wẽ yõ'ogɔ la sɛla tɩ Zuudãana ẽŋɛ la.»

ref: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam n daan wa'am ya zẽ'am wa mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga n sʋgɛ bɔ ya la, la dagɩ la yetɔg-mi'ilum, bɩɩ nẽrsaalba yɛm tɩ mam mõolɛ bɔ ya.

ref: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
text: Se'ere n soe la, mam daan looge yɛm tɩ mam kãn mõolɛ sɛla sɛla yelle, sãn dagna Azezi-krisi yelle, ẽŋa n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la yelle.

ref: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
text: Mam n daan mõolɛ dee zãsʋm kõ-yẽlga la, la daan dagɩ la nẽrsaalba yɛm yetɔga tɩ mam daan tɔgɛ bɔ ya tɩ ya sakɛ, mam daan tɔgɛ mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ a pãŋa,

ref: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
text: tɩ yãma n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba yɛm ĩyã, la de la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n ãn se'em

ref: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
text: La baa bɛla, tõma tɔgrɩ la yɛm yetɔga bɔ'ɔra sɛba n bɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la doosgo pʋam. La dagɩ dũnia wã yɛm, la mẽ ka de sɛba n soe dũnia wã yɛm. Bãma wʋn lebge la zãŋa.

ref: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
text: Tõma mõolɩ la Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n daan sʋgɛ tɩ nẽrba ka mina. Nawẽnnɛ daan põn looge yel-bãma mɛ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo tɩ a botɩ tɩ yẽ nã'asgɔ a zẽ'am.

ref: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
text: Sɛba n soe dũnia wã baa ayɩla ka mi yɛm ẽna wã. Ba daan sãn mi nɩ ẽ, ba kãn ka nɩ Zuudãana n tarɩ na'am pãŋa wuu la dɔ-puurŋa zuo kʋ.

ref: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
text: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm tɩ a bɔ nẽr-sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ na, gãnnɩ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽra nãn ka yẽ, bɩɩ wõm, bɩɩ tẽ'esɛ a pʋtẽ'erʋm.»

ref: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
text: Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a Sɩa la puke bõn-bãma pa'alɛ la tõma. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la mi sɛla woo mɛ, baa la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sɛba n sʋgɛ.

ref: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
text: Ãne n mi nẽrsaala pʋtẽ'erɛ? La de la nẽrsaala mẽŋa sɩa mã'a. Bɛla, nẽra mẽ ka mi Nawẽnnɛ pʋtẽ'erɛ, sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la mã'a.

ref: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
text: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma ka to'oge dũnia wã sɩa, tõma to'oge la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n ze'ele a zẽ'am, tɩ tɩ yãŋɛ bãŋɛ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a bɔ tɔ.

ref: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
text: La tõma n tɔgrɩ yel-bãna wã, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba yɛm yetɔga, tõma tɔgrɩ la yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɛ tõma, tõma tɔgrɩ la yel-sɛba n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am bɔ'ɔra nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.

ref: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
text: La nẽr-sɛka n ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la kãn tã'agɛ to'oge sɛla n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am, la de la yalnɛ bɔ ẽ, a kãn tã'agɛ bãŋɛ a võorɛ. Nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ẽ lɛ mã'a n mi Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la yelle.

ref: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
text: Nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ẽ lɛ n tã wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla woo n ãn se'em bɔkɛ. La nẽra ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ ẽŋa dãana yelle bɔkɛ.

ref: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
text: La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Ãne n mi Zuudãana pʋtẽ'erɛ? Ãne n wʋn yãŋɛ kã'an ẽ?» La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma tarɩ la Krisi pʋtẽ'erɛ.

ref: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam daan ka tã'agɛ tɔgɛ ya wʋ nẽrba n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa. Mam daan tɔgɛ ya wʋ nẽrba n vɔa wʋ ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, wʋ ya de la bilɛɛsɩ Krisi doosgo la pʋam.

ref: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
text: Yãma n daan ka tã wʋn to'oge zãsŋɔ ke'eŋo n ãn wʋ di-ke'ene la ĩyã, mam daan tɔgɛ ya wʋ bilɛɛsɩ n mõgrɩ bĩ'isum dee ka paagɛ di-ke'ene dia. La baa lɛɛlɛ wã, ya ken ka paagɛ ya to'oge zãsŋɔ ke'eŋo,

ref: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
text: yãma n ken vɔa wʋ ya mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na ĩyã. Ya sãn ɩta sũure zɛbra la taaba, bɛla ka pa'alɛ tɩ ya vɔa wʋ ya mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, dee ɩta wʋ dũnia nẽrba n ɩtɩ se'em na sɩra?

ref: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
text: Ya nẽra ayɩla sãn yetɩ: «Mam dolɩ la Apolɩ», tɩ ayɩla yetɩ: «Mam dolɩ la Apolɔsɩ», ya ka ɩtɩ wʋ dũnia nẽrba la?

ref: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
text: Apolɔsɩ de la ãne? Apolɩ de la ãne? Tõma wen dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba n daan botɩ ya bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra. Tõma nẽra woo tõnnɩ la tõon-sɛka tɩ Zuudãana la bɔ tɔ tɩ tɩ tõm.

ref: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
text: Tõma ãn wʋ kaarba la, mam sele mɛ, tɩ Apolɔsɩ basɛ ko'om. La Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ ba bɩta.

ref: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
text: Sɛka n sellɩ, la sɛka n basrɩ ko'om na de la zãŋa. Nawẽnnɛ mã'a n botɩ ba bɩta.

ref: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
text: Sɛka n sellɩ, la sɛka n basrɩ ko'om na de la ayɩla. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn yɔ nẽra woo wʋ ẽn tõm se'em na.

ref: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
text: Tõma lagɩm taaba tõnna la Nawẽnnɛ tõoma. La yãma ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ va'am, la ya ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ deego.

ref: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
text: Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ mam yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã, mam tõm wʋ tõnmẽta n mi mẽa sõŋa n ẽebɛ ẽbrɛ, tɩ nẽra ayẽma mẽ dɔglɛ a zuo. La nẽra woo guuse a mẽŋa la ẽn mẽtɩ se'em na.

ref: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
text: La Azezi-krisi lemŋo daarɛ, nẽra woo n tõm se'em wʋn puke mɛ peelem pʋam. La de la bugum n wʋn makɛ tõoma la, pa'alɛ nẽra woo tõoma n ãn se'em.

ref: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
text: Bugum na sãn ka di nẽra tõoma, a dãana wʋn to'oge yɔɔrɔ.

ref: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
text: La bugum na sãn di nẽra tõoma, a dãana wʋn koŋe yɔɔrɔ. La nẽra la mẽŋa wʋn yẽ fãarɛ, la a wʋn ãna wʋ a doose la bugum pʋam põsɛ.

ref: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
text: Ya ka mi tɩ ya de la Nawẽnnɛ deego tɩ a Sɩa bɔna ya pʋam?

ref: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
text: Nẽra sãn sã'am Nawẽnnɛ deego la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sã'am a dãana. Nawẽnnɛ deego de la a mẽŋa mã'a bõnɔ, la yãma n de a deego la.

ref: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
text: Nẽra da pã'asɛ a mẽŋa. Nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa tarɩ dũnia wã yɛm, la masɛ mɛ tɩ a lebge gɔɔŋɔ dũnia wã pʋam, bɛla n wʋn basɛ tɩ a lebge yɛm dãana.

ref: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
text: Sɛla tɩ dũnia dõma tẽ'esɛ tɩ la de la yɛm na, de la yalnɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. La de wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ bẽtɩ yɛm dõma mɛ yõgra ba ba yɛmkẽgsɩga la pʋam.»

ref: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
text: La ken gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana mi yɛm dõma tẽ'esgɔ mɛ, a mi tɩ ba de la zãŋa.»

ref: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
text: Mam Apolɩ, bɩɩ Apolɔsɩ, bɩɩ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, bɩɩ dũnia wã, bɩɩ yõvore, bɩɩ kũm, bɩɩ sɛla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, bɩɩ sɛla n wʋn wa wa'am, yãma n soe ba wuu.

ref: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text: La yãma, Krisi n soe ya, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɔna Krisi.

ref: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
text: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ tõma de la Krisi tõntõnɩba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ a yɛla n daan sʋgɛ ẽŋɛ tõma nu'usum tɩ tɩ tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

ref: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
text: Ba sãn dɩkɛ tõonɛ ẽŋɛ nẽra nu'usum tɩ a bɩsra, ba boorɩ tɩ a dɛna la sɩra dãana.

ref: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
text: La sãn dɛna mam, yãma bɩɩ dũnia wã sarɩyaditba sãn tɔgra mam yelle se'em, la ka ɩtɩ mam se'ere. Mam mẽ ka diti m mẽŋa sarɩya.

ref: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
text: Mam mi m sũure pʋam tɩ mam ka ẽŋɛ sɛla n ka ãn sõŋa, la, la dagɩ bɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ mam masɛ ya. La de la Zuudãana n diti mam sarɩya.

ref: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
text: Bɛla, da dẽŋɛ-ya di nẽra sarɩya dee tɩ Zuudãana la nãn ka leme. A sãn wa leme, a wʋn dɩkɛ sɛla n sʋgɛ lika pʋam yese peelem pʋam, dee botɩ bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽrsaalba tẽ'esrɩ ba sũure pʋam puke. Wakat-ẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn pẽgɛ nẽra woo wʋ lan masɛ tɩ a pẽgɛ ẽ se'em.

ref: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam n dɩkɛ m mẽŋa la Apolɔsɩ makɛ bɛla wuu la, la de la yãma ĩyã, tɩ tõma n ẽŋɛ se'em na wʋn dɛna zãsŋɔ bɔ ya. Tɩ ya «da ẽŋɛ yõsɛ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.» Bɛla, ya nẽra nẽra da zẽkɛ a mẽŋa wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ la nẽra ayɩla dee pɔ'ɔra ayẽma.

ref: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
text: Bẽm soe tɩ fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn gãnnɩ fʋ taaba? Bẽm tɩ fõn tarɩ tɩ la dagna Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ fɔ? La fʋ sãn to'oge, wãne tɩ fʋ pa'ala fʋ mẽŋa wʋ fʋ ka to'oge mɛ na?

ref: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
text: Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ yãma põn tara Nawẽnnɛ bõntarsõm na wuu tɩ la seke mɛ. La yãma lebge la na'adõma dee tɩ tõma ka pa'asɛ. Mam boorɩ nɩ tɩ ya sɩrɩ dɛna la na'adõma, tɩ tõma mẽ po di na'am na.

ref: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
text: Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tõma sɛba n de a Tõntõnɩba la, tɩ tɩ bɔna la nẽrba la wuu poorʋm, ãna wʋ nẽrba tɩ ba di ba sarɩya yetɩ ba kʋ ba, tɩ dũnia dõma wuu bɩsra dee la'ara, nẽrsaalba, la malɛkadõma wuu.

ref: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
text: Tõma ãn wʋ zɔllɔ Krisi ĩyã, la yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ yãma de la yɛm dõma Krisi doosgo pʋam. Ya tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma ka tarɩ pãŋa, la yãma tarɩ pãŋa. Nẽrba pɔ'ɔrɩ tõma mɛ, la ba nã'asrɩ yãma mɛ.

ref: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
text: Kɔm la koyũuro tarɩ tõma mɛ, tɩ tõma ka tara fu-sõma, tɩ ba nãmsra tõma, tɩ tõma ɛɛra yɛ'ɛna halɩ wa paam wakat-ẽna.

ref: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
text: La tõma tõnnɩ la tɩ yõ'ogɔ pãŋa nãmsra yẽta tɩ nõorɛ dia. Ba sãn tʋ tõma, tõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra ba. Ba sãn nãmsra tõma, tõma wĩbri mɛ.

ref: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
text: Ba sãn tɔgɛ tõma yetɔg-be'ero, tõma lebsrɩ la yetɔg-sõma. Dũnia dõma bɩsrɩ tõma, tɩ tõma ãna wʋ sɔgrɔ la, ka tara võorɛ ba zẽ'am, halɩ wa paam zĩna.

ref: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
text: Mam ka gʋlsɛ wãna bɔ ya tɩ m botɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ ya, mam yetɩ m kã'an ya mɛ wʋ m kɔma tɩ m nõŋɛ la.

ref: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
text: Baa ya sãn tara nẽrba tʋspia tɩ ba bɩsra ya Krisi doosgo la pʋam, ya tarɩ la sɔ ayɩla mã'a. Mam n de ya sɔ Azezi-krisi doosgo la pʋam, mam n wa'am wa mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ya, tɩ ya yẽ vɔm na ĩyã.

ref: and I want you to be like me.
text: Bɛla, mam belnɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya ɩta mam n ɩtɩ se'em na.

ref: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
text: Bɛla tɩ mam tõm Atimõtɩ ya zẽ'am. A de la mam bi-nõŋrɛ n de sɩra dãana Zuudãana doosgo la pʋam. Ẽŋa n wʋn tẽegɛ ya mam n vɔa se'em Krisi doosgo la pʋam, la de la bõn-bãma tɩ mam zãsnɩ Azezi nẽrba n lagsrɩ zẽ'a woo la.

ref: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
text: Ya nẽrba basɛba tẽ'esrɩ tɩ mam kãn leme wa'am ya zẽ'am, zẽkra ba mẽnsɩ.

ref: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
text: La Zuudãana la sãn bɔɔra, la kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am ya zẽ'am, la mam wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla tɩ zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma la wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ, tɩ la sãn dagna ba yetɔga la mã'a.

ref: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm dagɩ nõorɛ yetɔga, la de la pãŋa.

ref: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
text: Yãma boorɩ la bẽm? Yãma boorɩ tɩ m wa'am na kafõbga bɩɩ? Bɩɩ m wa'am na nõŋlʋm la sũ-bʋgsʋm?

ref: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
text: Ba tɔgrɩ tɩ ya ɩtɩ la yalsɩ, halɩ yalsɩ sɛka buuri tɩ baa sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka sakɛ ɩta. Mam wõm tɩ ba yetɩ nẽra ayɩla n boe ya pʋam gã la a sɔ pɔga.

ref: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
text: Dee tɩ yãma zẽkra ya mẽŋa. La de nɩ na yãma sũure sã'am, tɩ ya yese bʋra-sɛka n ẽŋɛ yel-be'e-ẽŋa la ya tẽŋasʋka.

ref: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
text: yele la Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ yetɩ: Yãma n lagsɛ la taaba, tɩ mam pʋtẽ'erɛ bɔna la yãma, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi pãŋa bɔna la ya lɛ la,

ref: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
text: ya yese nẽr-ẽŋa ya tẽŋasʋka basɛ Asũtãana nu'usum tɩ a sã'am a ĩyã, dee tɩ a sɩa wʋn yẽ fãarɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi lemŋo daarɛ.

ref: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
