text: Yãma n zẽkrɩ ya mẽnsɩ la, la ka ãn sõŋa. Yãma ka mi tɩ dãbɩllɛ fẽe n botɩ borborɩ zom wuu ukra?

ref: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
text: Yese-ya tõon-be'e-ẽŋa n ãn wʋ dãbɩllɛ la ya tẽŋasʋka, bɛla tɩ ya wʋn ãna sõŋa wʋ zom paalga tɩ ba ka ẽŋɛ dãbɩllɛ ba pʋam. Ya põn ãna sõŋa wʋ borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ la. Se'ere n soe la, Krisi daan sakɛ mɛ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ tõma ĩyã wʋ Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa pebila la.

ref: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
text: Bɛla, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ yese pʋtẽ'e-be'ero, la tõon-be'ero wuu tɩ vɔm pʋam, dee dɛna pʋpeelem dõma, la sɩra dõma wʋ ba diti Zɔɔg-basɛ kibsa la borborɩ n ka tarɩ dãbɩllɛ la.

ref: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
text: Mam n daan gʋlsɛ gõn-sɛka la, mam daan yele ya tɩ ya da lagɩm na sɛba n ɩtɩ yalsɩ.

ref: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
text: Mam ka tɔgrɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe dũnia pʋam ɩta yalsɩ, bɩɩ nõŋɛ bõnɔ, bɩɩ dɛna nayigba, bɩɩ bag-kãabrɩba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ na yelle. Ya sãn yetɩ ya welge ya mẽŋa la bãma, dẽnɩ tɩ ya yese dũnia wã pʋam.

ref: I was talking about your own people who are immoral or greedy or worship idols or curse others or get drunk or cheat. Don't even eat with them!
text: Mam daan gʋlsɛ bɔ ya yetɩ, ya da dɔla la nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ a mẽŋa tɩ ẽŋa dolɩ la Nawẽnnɛ dee ɩta yalsɩ, bɩɩ nõŋɛ bõnɔ, bɩɩ kãabra baga, bɩɩ tʋʋra nẽrba, bɩɩ yũura dãam bugra, bɩɩ zũura. Da põn lagɩm-ya la nẽr-ẽŋa dãana di.

ref: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
text: Ya nẽra ayɩla sãn tara yelle la a tadãana, bẽm tɩ a tarɩ ẽ wɛ'ɛsa sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɩ ba ka mãalɛ yelle la, dee ka tarɩ ẽ wɛ'ɛsa Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zẽ'am?

ref: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
text: Yãma ka mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n wʋn wa di dũnia dõma sarɩya? La yãma sãn wʋn wa di dũnia dõma sarɩya, ya ka tã wʋn di yel-pɩg-bãna wã sarɩya?

ref: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
text: Yãma ka mi tɩ tõma n wʋn wa di malɛkadõma sarɩya? Lan de bɛla la, bẽm tɩ yãma ka tãna wʋn di dũnia wã yɛla sarɩya?

ref: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
text: La yãma sãn tara yel-bãna buuri la taaba, yãma tarɩ sẽnna la sɛba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla la zẽ'am.

ref: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya. Bɛla, nẽra baa ayɩla ka boe yãma pʋam n tarɩ yɛm, wʋn tã'agɛ mãalɛ yel-sɛba tɩ yãma tarɩ la taaba la?

ref: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
text: La ka masɛ tɩ yãma n de sɔbiisi sãn tara yelle la taaba, ya tara taaba sẽnna sarɩyaditba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: When one of you takes another to court, all of you lose. It would be better to let yourselves be cheated and robbed.
text: Yãma n tarɩ taaba sẽnna sarɩyadia zẽ'am na, la põn pa'alɛ tɩ ya tuuge mɛ zozo'e. Nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ya be'em, bɩɩ zũ ya, bẽm tɩ yãma ka ɩta sugri, dee tara taaba sẽnna sarɩyadia zẽ'am?

ref: But instead, you cheat and rob other followers.
text: Yãma malɩn ɩta taaba la be'em, zũura taaba, yãma n de sɔbiisi Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam na.

ref: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
text: Yãma ka mi tɩ tõon-be'ero dõma kãn yẽ zẽ'a Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam? Da pã'asɛ ya mẽŋa. Nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, bɩɩ sɛba n kãabrɩ baga, bɩɩ sɛba n gã la taaba pɔgba la taaba sɩrba, bɩɩ bʋra-sɛba n gã la taaba,

ref: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
text: bɩɩ nayigba, bɩɩ sɛba n nõŋɛ bõnɔ, bɩɩ sɛba n yũuri dãam bugra, bɩɩ sɛba n tʋʋrɩ nẽrba, bɩɩ fãarba, bãma kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
text: Yãma nẽrba basɛba daan ãn na bɛla. La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa peege yãma tõon-be'ero mɛ botɩ ya lebge Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, masɛ a zẽ'am Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã. Dɩkɛ ya ĩyã nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ

ref: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
text: Yãma nẽrba basɛba yetɩ: «Mam tarɩ sore wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla woo mɛ.» La mam yetɩ ya tɩ dagɩ sɛla woo n tarɩ yõorɔ. Mam mẽ tarɩ sore wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla woo mɛ. La mam kãn sakɛ lebge sɛla baa ayɩla yamŋa.

ref: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
text: Yãma yetɩ, dia boe la pʋʋrɛ ĩyã, tɩ pʋʋrɛ mẽ bɔna dia ĩyã. La de la sɩra, la Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sã'am bãma bayi la wuu mɛ. La nẽrsaala ĩyã ka boe yalsɩ ĩyã, a boe la Zuudãana ĩyã. Tɩ Zuudãana mẽ sɔna ĩyã la.

ref: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, ẽŋa n wʋn ken vo'oge tõma la a pãŋa.

ref: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
text: Yãma ka mi tɩ ya ĩyã la de la Azezi-krisi ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla? Bɛla, la ãn sõŋa tɩ nẽra dɩkɛ Krisi ĩyã zẽ'a ayɩla la sẽŋɛ ka lagɩm na pɔg-yalsɩ dõma mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la põn ka masɛ.

ref: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
text: Ya ka mi tɩ sɛka n lagɩm a mẽŋa la pɔg-sɛka n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, ẽŋa la pɔka la lebge la ĩyã ayɩla? La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ, bʋraaga la pɔka sãn lagɩm taaba, bãma bayi lebge la ayɩla.

ref: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
text: La sɛka n lagɩm a mẽŋa la Zuudãana la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ a lebge la ayɩla la Zuudãana.

ref: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
text: Zoe-ya yalsɩ. Tõon-be'e-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽrsaala ɩtɩ la ka boe a ĩyã pʋam. La tõon-be'e-sɛka n de yalsɩ la boe la nẽrsaala ĩyã pʋam.

ref: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
text: Ya ka mi tɩ ya ĩyã la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa deego? Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bɔ ya la boe ya pʋam mɛ, ya ka soe ya mẽŋa.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
text: Nawẽnnɛ da ya la da'aga n de toogo, bɛla, dɩkɛ-ya ya ĩyã, nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
text: Mam yetɩ m lerge la yãma n daan gʋlsɛ soke mam se'em na: «La ãn sõŋa tɩ bʋraaga da di pɔga.»

ref: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
text: La, lan wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ ya da ẽŋɛ yalsɩ, bʋraaga woo di a pɔga, tɩ pɔka woo mẽ ele a sɩra.

ref: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
text: Se'ere n soe la, pɔka ka soe a mẽŋa ĩyã. La de la a sɩra n soe ẽ. La bʋraaga mẽ ka soe a mẽŋa ĩyã. La de la a pɔga n soe ẽ.

ref: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text: Pɔka la a sɩra da zagsɛ tɩ bãma kãn gã la taaba, sãn dagna tɩ ya ẽŋɛ la nõorɛ ayɩla looge wakatɛ fẽe tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, bɛla poorʋm ya le lagɩm na taaba tɩ Asũtãana da botɩ ya ẽŋɛ be'em, ya sãn ka tã'agɛ yõkɛ ya mẽŋa.

ref: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
text: Mam n yele ya se'em wã, la dagɩ pẽrgrɛ, la de la sore tɩ mam pa'alɛ ya.

ref: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
text: Mam yetɩ sɛba n ka tara pɔgba bɩɩ n ka tara sɩrba, la pɔkõpa yetɩ, ba sãn ken bɔna bãma bayɩla mã'a wʋ mam n ãn se'em na, la ãn sõŋa.

ref: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
text: La ba sãn ka tãna wʋn yõkɛ ba mẽŋa, ba di pɔgba, bɩɩ ba ele sɩrba. La ãn sõŋa tɩ ya di pɔgba, bɩɩ ya ele sɩrba, sõna yãma n zĩ tɩ a yɛmleego tara ya nãmsra.

ref: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
text: La sãn dɛna sɛba n tarɩ pɔgba bɩɩ n tarɩ sɩrba la, mam yetɩ ya la wãna: La, la dagɩ mam mẽŋa nõorɛ, la de la Zuudãana Azezi nõorɛ: Pɔka da basɛ a sɩra.

ref: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
text: La a sãn basɛ a sɩra, a zẽa, a da le bɔta bʋraasɩ, bɩɩ ẽŋa la a sɩra la mãalɛ ba yelle la le naagɛ taaba. Bʋraaga mẽ da lebse a pɔga.

ref: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
text: La sãn dɛna nẽrba basɛba la, mam yetɩ la wãna: La de la mam mẽŋa n yele, la dagɩ Zuudãana nõorɛ: Bʋraaga n dolɩ Azezi sore sãn tara pɔga n ka dolɩ Azezi sore, dee sakɛ tɩ a bɔna la ẽ, a da lebse ẽ.

ref: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
text: La pɔka n dolɩ Azezi sore sãn tara sɩra n ka dolɩ Azezi sore, dee sakɛ tɩ a bɔna la ẽ, a da basɛ ẽ.

ref: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
text: Sɩra n ka dolɩ Azezi sore ãn sõŋa mɛ, a pɔga n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽra la ĩyã. La pɔka n ka dolɩ Azezi sore ãn sõŋa mɛ, a sɩra n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽra la ĩyã. Bɛla ba kɔma wʋn ãna sõŋa mɛ, sãn dagna bɛla, ba kɔma wʋn ãna wʋ nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

ref: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
text: La sɛka n ka dolɩ Azezi sore la, sãn bɔɔra tɩ a basɛ sɛka n dolɩ Azezi sore la, a basɛ ẽ tɩ a fõrgɛ. La sãn dɛna bɛla, la ka de pẽrgrɛ tɩ pɔka bɩɩ bʋraaga n dolɩ Azezi sore bɔna la sɛka n ka dolɩ Azezi sore. Nawẽnnɛ wi to tɩ tɩ vɔa la sũ-mã'asʋm la taaba.

ref: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
text: Pɔka, fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ fõn sãn wʋn tã'agɛ botɩ fʋ sɩra la yẽ fãarɛ? Bɩɩ bʋraaga, fõn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ fõn sãn wʋn tã'agɛ botɩ fʋ pɔga la yẽ fãarɛ? Sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ de la Nawẽnnɛ sakrɛ

ref: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
text: Sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la nẽra woo vɔa wʋ ẽn daan ãn se'em tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ na, wʋ Zuudãana n bɔ ẽ tɩ a vɔa se'em na. La de la bɛla tɩ mam pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu tɩ ba ɩta.

ref: If you are already circumcised, don't try to change it. If you are not circumcised, don't get circumcised.
text: Nẽr-sɛka sãn wã, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ, a da ɛɛra tɩ a sʋgɛ wãaga la. Nẽr-sɛka n mẽ ka wã, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ, a da wã.

ref: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
text: Sɛka n wã, bɩɩ sɛka n ka wã, ba wuu ka tarɩ yõorɔ. Sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ de la Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ sakrɛ.

ref: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
text: Nẽra woo ken ãna wʋ ẽn daan ãn se'em tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ na.

ref: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
text: Fʋ daan sãn dɛna la yamŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi fo, da basɛ tɩ fʋ yɛm yɔɔra. La sore sãn wa bɔna tɩ fʋ wʋn to'oge fʋ mẽŋa, fʋ doose bilam to'oge fʋ mẽŋa.

ref: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
text: La nẽr-sɛka n de yamŋa tɩ Zuudãana la wi ẽ, a de la nẽra tɩ Zuudãana botɩ a to'oge a mẽŋa. Bɛla mẽ, nẽr-sɛka n dagɩ yamŋa tɩ Zuudãana Krisi wi ẽ, a de la Krisi yamŋa.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
text: Nawẽnnɛ da ya la toogo, bɛla da lebge-ya nẽrba yamsɩ.

ref: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
text: Mam yetɩ m tɔgɛ la sɛba n ka tarɩ sɩrba bɩɩ pɔgba la yelle, mam ka to'oge nõorɛ Zuudãana Azezi zẽ'am. La de la mam mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ, la ya wʋn bɔ mam sɩra Zuudãana la n zoe mam ninbãalga la ĩyã.

ref: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
text: Yel-sɛba n boe lɛɛlɛ wã ĩyã, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ nẽra woo ken ãna wʋ ẽn ãn se'em na.

ref: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
text: Fʋ sãn tara pɔga, da ɛɛra tɩ fʋ lebse ẽ. La fʋ sãn ka tara pɔga, da ɛɛra tɩ fʋ di pɔga.

ref: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
text: La fʋ sãn di pɔga, la dagɩ be'em. La pug-sɛka n mẽ ele sɩra, la dagɩ be'em. Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ pɔgba, bɩɩ sɩrba la wʋn yẽ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ dũnia wã pʋam, bɛla tɩ mam ka bɔɔra tɩ yel-bãma paagɛ ya.

ref: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam n yetɩ ya se'em n wãna: Wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ tɔ la ka le zo'oge. Lan pɔsɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, la ãn sõŋa tɩ sɛba n tarɩ pɔgba la, ãna wʋ ba ka tarɩ pɔgba,

ref: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
text: tɩ sɛba n kellɩ, ãna wʋ ba ka kellɩ, tɩ sɛba n ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga, ãna wʋ ba ka ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga, tɩ sɛba n da'arɩ, ãna wʋ ba ka tarɩ sɛla tɩ ba da'arɩ la.

ref: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
text: Tɩ sɛba n yẽtɩ dũnia bõntarsõm, ãna wʋ ba ka yẽtɩ bõntarsõm. Se'ere n soe la, dũnia wã n ãn se'em wã tollɩ mɛ.

ref: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
text: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ ya yɛm yɔɔra la sɛla sɛla. Sɛka n ka tarɩ pɔga dɩkrɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ wuu tõnna la Zuudãana la tõoma, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ la ãna Zuudãana la sũure yẽlʋm.

ref: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
text: La sɛka n di pɔga, dũnia yɛla n pakɛ ẽ, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ la ãna a pɔga sũure yẽlʋm.

ref: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
text: Bɛla, a pʋtẽ'erɛ n boorɩ se'em na pʋɩ mɛ. La pɔka bɩɩ pugla n mẽ ka tarɩ sɩra dɩkrɩ a pʋtẽ'erɛ wuu tõnna la Zuudãana la tõoma, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ a ĩyã, la a sɩa wʋn wa dɛna Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ. La sɛka n tarɩ sɩra, dũnia yɛla n pakɛ ẽ, a eerɩ la ẽŋa n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ la ãna a sɩra sũure yẽlʋm.

ref: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
text: La de la yãma sõŋrɛ ĩyã tɩ mam yele bɛla. Mam ka boorɩ tɩ m mõrgɛ ya dɩkɛ yelle dɔglɛ ya zuto. Mam boorɩ tɩ ya tõm na sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la ya dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa wuu bɔ Zuudãana la, tõnna a tõoma.

ref: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
text: La sãn dɛna sɛba n lɛgrɩ pugunto tɩ ba di la, sɛka sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa sãn basɛ a zɛba la ka di ẽ, ẽŋa ẽŋɛ ẽ na be'em, dee tɩ a yɛmleego tara ẽ, a di ẽ wʋ ẽn tẽ'esɛ se'em na, tɩ la dagɩ be'em.

ref: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
text: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n di pɔga, a ẽŋɛ sõŋa. La nẽr-sɛka n mẽ ka di pɔga, a ẽŋɛ sõŋa gãnna.

ref: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
text: Pɔg-sɛka n ele, dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔna la a sɩra lɛ, ẽn ken vɔa wakatɛ la. La a sɩra la sãn ki, sore boe mɛ tɩ a le ele nẽr-sɛka tɩ a boorɩ, la dẽnɩ tɩ a dɛna nẽra n dolɩ Zuudãana Azezi sore.

ref: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
text: La mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ a sãn ka le ele sɩra, a sũure wʋn ãna yẽlʋm gãnna ẽn ele. Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'ala ya.

ref: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
text: La sãn dɛna bõn-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ bɔ baga la nẽnnɔ yelle, tɩ mi tɩ tɩ wuu tarɩ bãŋrɛ mɛ, wʋ yãma n yele se'em na. La bãŋrɛ botɩ nẽra zẽkra la a mẽŋa. La nõŋlʋm sõŋrɩ nẽrba mɛ.

ref: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
text: Nẽr-sɛka sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa mi sɛla, a ken ka mina wʋ lan masɛ tɩ a mina se'em.

ref: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
text: La nẽra sãn nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, Nawẽnnɛ mi ẽ mɛ.

ref: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
text: Bɛla, la sãn dɛna baga nẽnnɔ na yelle, tɩ mi tɩ baga de la sɛla n de zãŋa dũnia wã zuo, dee ken mina tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a n boe.

ref: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
text: Bõndõma n boe saazuum bɩɩ tẽŋa zuo, tɩ nẽrsaalba wi'iri tɩ wẽna. Asɩra, bõndõma zozo'e n boe tɩ nẽrsaalba wi'iri tɩ wẽna, bɩɩ zuudãandõma.

ref: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
text: La tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n boe dɛna tɩ Sɔ n nãam sɛla woo, la ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ tɩ vɔa. La tõma ken mina tɩ Zuudãana ayɩla mã'a n boe, a de la Azezi-krisi, la doose la ẽŋa tɩ sɛla woo bɔna, la ken doose la ẽŋa tɩ tɩ tara vɔm.

ref: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
text: La, la dagɩ nẽra woo n mi wãna. Nẽrba basɛba malʋm baga mɛ, tɩ halɩ la zĩna ba sãn obe baga nẽnnɔ, ba ken tẽ'esra a yelle mɛ tɩ la sɩrɩ de la baga dia. La ban ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla sõŋa la, ba tẽ'esrɩ ba sũure pʋam tɩ ba ẽŋɛ la be'em.

ref: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
text: La dagɩ dia n wʋn botɩ tɩ lɛm Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ sãn ka di, sɛla ka pɔ'ɔrɩ tɔ, la tɩ sãn di, a ka pa'asrɩ tɩ sɛla.

ref: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
text: La guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ yan tarɩ nõorɛ dita sɛla woo la, da basɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo pʋam na tõm tõon-be'ero.

ref: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla sõŋa la, sãn yẽ fõn tarɩ bãŋrɛ la n zĩ baga kãabgɔ zẽ'am ɔbra nẽnnɔ, la wʋn pã'asɛ ẽ tɩ a mẽ obe la baga nẽnnɔ.

ref: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
text: Fʋ sãn tõm tõon-be'e-ẽna sã'am fʋ sɔbia n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ sore yɛla sõŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ, fʋ ẽŋɛ la Krisi be'em.

ref: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
text: Bɛla, la sãn dɛna dia n wʋn basɛ tɩ mam sɔbia tõm tõon-be'ero, mam kãn le malɩn di ẽ, tɩ m da basɛ tɩ m sɔbia tõm tõon-be'ero.

ref: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
text: Baa nẽrba basɛba sãn ka sakɛ tɩ mam de Krisi Tõntõnna, yãma masɛ mɛ tɩ ya sakɛ. Yãma n dolɩ Krisi sore la, n de sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ mam de Krisi Tõntõnna.

ref: When people question me, I tell them
text: Sɛba n zɛrgrɩ mam na, mam tɔgrɩ la wãna lɛrgra ba.

ref: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
text: Tõma ka tarɩ sore tɩ ba bɔ tɩ dia la ko'om tɩ tõoma la ĩyã?

ref: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
text: Tõma ka tarɩ sore wʋn di pɔgba n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore tɩ ba dɔla tɩ ɛɛra wʋ tɩ Tõntõn-taaba, la Zuudãana yɩbsɩ, la Apɩyɛɛrɩ n ɩtɩ se'em na bɩɩ?

ref: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
text: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam na Abarnabasɩ mã'a n de tɩ tõnna tɩ mẽŋa dita bɩɩ?

ref: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
text: Ãne n de sʋdaaga dee bɩsra a mẽŋa dia yelle? Ãne n sele tɩɩsɩ dee ka dita ba biɛ? La ãne n gu'uri dũnsi dee ka dita ba ɩɩlʋm?

ref: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
text: La gʋlsɛ lɔɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bɔ Amoyisi gõnnɔ la pʋam yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ-ya naafɔ n kɔɔrɩ nõorɛ yʋka.» La de la niigi yelle mã'a tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn tɔgɛ bɩɩ?

ref: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
text: Ayɛɩ, la de la tõma yelle tɩ la gʋlsɛ bɛla. Bɛla nẽr-sɛka n kɔɔrɩ tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ a wʋn yẽ mɛ, tɩ sɛka n wẽ'erɩ bõnkɔɔla mẽ tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ a wʋn yẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ ba wuu yẽ ba pʋrɛ.

ref: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
text: Tõma n mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la bɔ ya la, la ãn wʋ bõnbuuri tɩ tõma bʋrɛ. Bɛla, la masɛ mɛ tɩ ya looge ya bõntarsõm na sõŋɛ tõma wʋ bõnkɔɔla pʋrɛ tɩ ya bɔ tõma.

ref: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
text: Nẽrba basɛba sãn tara nõorɛ tɩ ba to'oge yãma bõntarsõm, tõma n tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ tɩ to'oge yãma bõntarsõm gãnna. La tõma ka to'oge ya sɛla wʋ tõma n tarɩ nõorɛ se'em na, tõma sakɛ nãmsgɔ mɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tõma ka boorɩ tɩ tɩ gu Krisi kõ-yẽlga la mõolgɔ.

ref: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
text: Yãma ka mi tɩ sɛba n tõnnɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam, diti la bilam? La tɩ sɛba n kãabrɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ na, to'orɩ ba pʋrɛ la kãabgɔ zẽ'a na?

ref: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
text: Bɛla, Zuudãana la bɔ nõorɛ mɛ tɩ sɛba n mõolɩ kõ-yẽlga la, mẽ tɔ'ɔra ba dia bilam.

ref: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
text: La mam ka to'oge ya sɛla baa ayɩla wʋ mam n tarɩ nõorɛ se'em na. La mam ka gʋlsɛ bɛla mẽ yetɩ ya bɔ mam sɛla. Baa mam sãn wʋn ki sõɩ, tɩ nẽra da wa basɛ tɩ mam ka tara yẽsõm.

ref: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
text: Mam n mõolɩ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la, mam ka tã wʋn wẽ m yõ'ogɔ a ĩyã. La de la pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam mõolɛ. Mam sãn ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la, mam wʋn yẽ la toogo.

ref: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
text: La sãn de nɩ la mam mẽŋa n looge yɛm mõola kõ-yẽlga la, mam wʋn to'oge nɩ la yɔɔrɔ. La, lan de pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam mõolɛ la, la de la tõonɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ dɔglɛ mam zuo.

ref: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
text: Bɛla, bẽm n de mam yɔɔrɔ? La de la mam n mõolɩ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ'ɔra nẽrba zãŋa, dee ka tɔ'ɔra ba sɛla wʋ mam tarɩ nõorɛ se'em na.

ref: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
text: Mam soe m mẽŋa mɛ, mam dagɩ nẽra yamŋa. La baa bɛla, mam dɩkɛ m mẽŋa lebge la nẽrba wuu yamŋa, tɩ m tã'agɛ botɩ ba nẽrba zozo'e dɔla Krisi.

ref: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
text: Mam sãn bɔna Zifdõma zẽ'am, mam vɔa wʋ Zifdõma la, tɩ m tã'agɛ botɩ ba doose Azezi. Amoyisi lɔɔ la ka le tara nõorɛ la mam, la mam sãn bɔna la nẽr-bãma n dolɩ lɔɔ la, mam sakrɩ lɔɔ wʋ bãma la, tɩ m botɩ sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la doose Azezi.

ref: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
text: La mam sãn bɔna nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ lɔɔ la zẽ'am, mam vɔa wʋ nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ lɔɔ la, tɩ m botɩ ba doose Azezi. La bɛla ka pa'alɛ tɩ mam ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la. Mam dolɩ la Krisi lɔɔ.

ref: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
text: Mam sãn bɔna la nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ pãŋa Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo pʋam, mam vɔa wʋ bãma, tɩ m sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba da basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore. Mam ɩtɩ sɛla woo la bɛla bɔ'ɔra nẽrba buuri wuu, ɩta mam wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ m tã'agɛ fãagɛ ba nẽrba basɛba.

ref: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
text: Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la ĩyã tɩ mam ɩta bɛla wuu tɩ m wa po to'oge a pʋrɛ.

ref: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
text: Yãma mi tɩ zʋa pʋam, zɔtba la wuu lagɩm zɔta mɛ, la ba nẽra ayɩla mã'a n wʋn zoe yãŋɛ to'oge bɔ'a. Bɛla, mõrgɛ-ya wʋ zɔtba n zotɩ se'em na, tɩ ya yãŋɛ to'oge bɔ'a Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
text: Zɔtba wuu yõkrɩ ba mẽŋa mɛ sɛla woo pʋam tɩ ba to'oge bɔ'a n kãn yuuge dee sã'am. La tõma yõkrɩ tɩ mẽŋa tɩ tɩ wa to'oge la bɔ'a n kãn malɩn sã'am.

ref: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam ka zɔta yoo yoo, la mam ka zɛbrɩ yoo yoo wʋ nẽra n wẽ'erɩ kʋsebgo.

ref: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
text: Mam mõrgrɩ m ĩyã mɛ tara m mẽŋa sõŋa sõŋa. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka boorɩ tɩ mam n mõolɩ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la, mam mẽŋa wa koŋe yɔɔrɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ tɩ ya tẽ'esɛ la tɩ yaabdõma n yʋʋn dolɩ Amoyisi la yelle. Ba wuu yʋʋn boe la sawatɛ la tẽŋa, la ba wuu yʋʋn doose la ko-kãtɛ la pʋam tole.

ref: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
text: Ba wuu yʋʋn di la dia ayɩla, dee yũ ko'om ayɩla n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
text: Ba yʋʋn yũ la ko'om n yesrɩ tãmpĩa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba pʋam. Tãmpĩ-ẽŋa yʋʋn de la Krisi n dolɩ ba.

ref: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
text: La baa bɛla, ba nẽrba zozo'e yʋʋn ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm, tɩ a basɛ tɩ ba ki weego la pʋam.

ref: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
text: Bõn-bãna wuu ẽŋɛ tɩ la pa'alɛ la tõma, tɩ tɩ da bɔɔra tɩ ẽŋɛ be'em wʋ bãma la.

ref: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
text: Da dɔla-ya baga wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em na. La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽrba la zĩ'ire mɛ di dee yũ, dee isge dɛ'ɛna.»

ref: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
text: Tɩ da ɩta yalsɩ wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ ba nẽrba tʋspisyi la atã ki daarɛ ayɩla la.

ref: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
text: Yãma nẽrba basɛba yetɩ: «Sore boe mɛ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla woo.» Asɩra, la, la dagɩ sɛla woo n tarɩ yõorɔ. Ba yetɩ: «Sore boe mɛ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla woo.» La, la dagɩ sɛla woo n sõŋrɩ.

ref: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
text: Tɩ da makɛ-ya tɩ Zuudãana la bɩsɛ wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, tɩ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma kʋ ba la.

ref: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
text: Tɩ da yõnna-ya la Nawẽnnɛ wʋ bãma basɛba n yʋʋn yõm se'em, tɩ kũm malɛka kʋ ba la.

ref: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
text: Bõn-bãma wuu yʋʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ yaabdõma tɩ la dɛna la mãmsgɔ bɔ nẽrba basɛba. La ba gʋlsɛ bɛla, tɩ la dɛna la zãsŋɔ bɔ tõma sɛba n boe wakat-ẽna tɩ dũnia lɛm ba'asgɔ wã.

ref: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
text: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa ɩtɩ sõŋa dee zɛ sõŋa, a guuse da lui.

ref: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
text: Yel-sɛba n paarɩ ya la, la de la yel-bãma n mẽ paarɩ nẽrba basɛba. La Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana, a kãn basɛ tɩ yel-sɛka n gãnnɩ ya pãŋa paagɛ ya. La yelle pʋam, a wʋn bɔ ya pãŋa tɩ ya wĩbe, dee ẽŋɛ sore tɩ ya yese. Baga dia, la Zuudãana kũm tẽerɛ dia yelle

ref: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
text: Bɛla m sɔbiisi, yese-ya ya mẽŋa baga kãabgɔ pʋam.

ref: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
text: Mam tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra ya wʋ ya de la nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ yɛm. Ya mẽŋa tẽ'esɛ mam n yetɩ ya sɛla la võorɛ.

ref: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
text: Bõn-yũula la tɩ tõma ẽn dɩkɛ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ dee yũ la, pa'alɛ tɩ tõma lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla, a zɩɩm n yese bɔ tõma la ĩyã. La borborɩ la tɩ tõma ẽn fiise pʋɩ taaba obe la, pa'alɛ tɩ tõma lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ĩyã ayɩla.

ref: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
text: Borborɩ la n de ayɩla la, tõma mẽ de la ĩyã ayɩla, baa tõma n zo'oge la. Se'ere n soe la, tɩ wuu lagɩm ɔbra la borbor-ẽŋa ayɩla.

ref: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
text: Tẽ'esɛ-ya Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yelle. Sɛba n diti di-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ kãabɛ Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zuo la, ba lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
text: Bẽm tɩ mam yɛta wãna? Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam yetɩ bagrɛ, bɩɩ di-sɛba tɩ ba kãabɛ bɔ bagrɛ la, de la sɛla bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

ref: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
text: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ sɛla tɩ bag-kãabrɩba kãabrɩ la, ba kãabrɩ bɔ'ɔra la kʋlkã'arsɩ, dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ. Mam ka boorɩ tɩ ya lagɩm na kʋlkã'arsɩ.

ref: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
text: Ya ka tã wʋn yũ Zuudãana Azezi bõn-yũula, dee le yũ kʋlkã'arsɩ bõn-yũula, ya ken ka tãna wʋn di Zuudãana kũm tẽerɛ dia, dee le di kʋlkã'arsɩ dia.

ref: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
text: Tɩ sãn ɩta bɛla, tɩ wʋn botɩ Zuudãana sũure n isge. Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma tarɩ pãŋa gãnna ẽ mɛ bɩɩ? Nã'asra-ya Nawẽnnɛ sɛla woo pʋam

ref: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
text: Nẽra nẽra da ɛɛra a mẽŋa sõŋrɛ mã'a, ɛɛra-ya taaba sõŋrɛ.

ref: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
text: Ya tarɩ sore tɩ ya da nẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ ba koosrɩ da'asɩ pʋam obe mɛ. Da basɛ-ya tɩ la nãmsra ya pʋtẽ'era tɩ ya soke nẽnnɔ na n ze'ele zẽ'a.

ref: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Dũnia, la bõn-sɛba wuu n boe a pʋam de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ.»

ref: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
text: Nẽra n ka bɔ Azezi sɩra sãn wi ya tɩ ya wa'am a yire wa di, tɩ ya sakɛ sẽŋɛ, di-ya di-sɛba wuu tɩ a tarɩ wa bɔ ya la, da basɛ-ya tɩ la nãmsra ya pʋtẽ'era, tɩ ya soke nẽnnɔ na n ze'ele zẽ'a.

ref: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
text: La dagɩ yãma mẽŋa pʋtẽ'era yelle tɩ mam yele, la de la nẽr-sɛka n yele ya la pʋtẽ'erɛ. La yãma mẽ tã soke mam yetɩ: «Bẽm soe tɩ nẽra ayẽma pʋtẽ'erɛ wʋn gu mam, tɩ mam kãn di sɛla tɩ mam tarɩ nõorɛ tɩ m di?

ref: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
text: Mam sãn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ dia ĩyã dee di, bẽm tɩ nẽra wʋn zɛrgɛ mam la bõn-sɛka tɩ mam pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a ĩyã?»

ref: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
text: Lan de se'em na, ya sãn dita mɛ, bɩɩ ya yũuri mɛ, bɩɩ sɛla woo tɩ ya ɩtɩ, ɩta-ya ba wuu nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
text: Da ẽŋɛ-ya sɛla Zifdõma, bɩɩ sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zẽ'am tɩ la gu ba Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo.

ref: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
text: Ɩta-ya mam n ɩtɩ se'em na, wʋ mam mẽ n ɩtɩ wʋ Krisi n ɩtɩ se'em na. Bʋraaga la pɔka n wʋn ãna se'em Wẽndeto pʋam

ref: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
text: Mam pẽgrɩ ya, yãma n tẽrɩ mam yelle sɛla woo pʋam, dee dɔla bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ mam pa'alɛ ya la.

ref: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
text: La mam boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Krisi de la nẽra woo zuudãana, tɩ sɩra mẽ dɛna a pɔga zuudãana, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɛna Krisi zuudãana.

ref: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
text: Bɛla, bʋra-sɛka n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, bɩɩ n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee vuge a zuugo, a pɔ'ɔrɩ la a zuudãana.

ref: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
text: La pɔg-sɛka n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, bɩɩ n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee ka vuge a zuugo, a pɔ'ɔrɩ la a zuudãana. La ãn wʋ ẽn põn a zuugo la.

ref: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
text: Pɔka sãn ka bɔɔra tɩ a vuge a zuugo, a yi sɛ a zomto. La tɩ mi tɩ la de la yãnnɛ bɔ pɔka tɩ a põn a zuugo, bɩɩ a sɛ a zomto. Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ a vuge a zuugo.

ref: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
text: Bʋraaga ka de a vuge a zuugo, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nãam ẽ tɩ a wõna la a mẽŋa, dɛna nã'asgɔ bɔ ẽ. La pɔka de la nã'asgɔ bɔ bʋraaga.

ref: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
text: Nawẽnnɛ ka dɩkɛ pɔka kõbrɛ nãam bʋraaga, a daan dɩkɛ la bʋraaga kõbrɛ nãam pɔka.

ref: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ ka nãam bʋraaga pɔka ĩyã, la a nãam pɔka la bʋraaga ĩyã.

ref: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, la malɛkadõma ĩyã, la masɛ tɩ pɔka vugra a zuugo tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ a tarɩ nõorɛ.

ref: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
text: La Zuudãana doosgo la pʋam, pɔka boe la bʋraaga nu'usum, tɩ bʋraaga mẽ bɔna pɔka nu'usum.

ref: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
text: Nawẽnnɛ daan dɩkɛ la bʋraaga nãam pɔka. Tɩ pɔgsɩ mẽ dɔgra bʋraasɩ. Bɛla wuu ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
text: Tẽ'esɛ-ya ya mẽŋa bɩsɛ. La masɛ tɩ pɔka pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ pʋam dee ka vuge a zuugo?

ref: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
text: La nẽr-sɛka sãn bɔɔra tɩ a wẽ nõke'ene bõn-ẽna wã yelle, dẽnɩ tɩ a bãŋɛ tɩ tõma, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu la, ka tarɩ ɩrgɔ ayẽma. Zuudãana kũm tẽerɛ dia (Amatie

ref: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
text: Mam n yetɩ m yele ya se'em wã, mam ka yetɩ m pẽgɛ ya, bẽm ĩyã, ya ẽn lagsɛ taaba la, ya ka ɩtɩ sõŋa, ya ɩtɩ la be'em.

ref: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
text: Pɔspɔsɩ, mam wõm tɩ ya sãn ẽn lagsɛ taaba tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, welgre n boe ya tẽŋasʋka. La mam sakɛ tɩ yel-sɛba tɩ ba yele la, basɛba de la sɩra.

ref: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ welgre bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka, tɩ ba bãŋɛ sɛba n sɩrɩ dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔkɛ.

ref: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
text: Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn lagsɛ, nẽra woo dẽŋɛ ka dita la a mẽŋa dia. Tɩ sɛba kɔm dõnna, dee tɩ basɛba yũ buge.

ref: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
text: Wakat-sɛka woo tɩ ya obrɩ borborɩ wã, dee yũura bõn-yũula wã, ya pa'alɩ la Zuudãana la kũm yelle, halɩ tɩ a wʋn wa leme wa'am.

ref: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n ka nannɩ Zuudãana la ĩyã, dee obe borborɩ la, dee yũ bõn-yũula la, a eerɩ la sɩbgrɛ bɔ'ɔra a mẽŋa.

ref: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
text: Ban daan di dia kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a dɩkɛ la bõn-yũula yetɩ: «Bõn-yũul-bãna pa'alɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe la nõor-paalga la nẽrsaalba mam zɩɩm n wʋn yese la ĩyã. Wakat-sɛka tɩ ya ẽn yetɩ ya yũ ba, ɩta-ya wãna tẽra mam yelle.»

ref: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
text: Zãsŋɔ sɛka tɩ mam daan zãsʋm ya la, mam to'oge ẽ na Zuudãana la zẽ'am. La de la wãna: Yʋ'ʋŋ-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn yõkɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, a dɩkɛ la borborɩ,

ref: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
text: pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ dee fiise bɔ ba yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam ĩyã, tɩ mam bɔ'ɔrɩ ya. Ɩta-ya wãna tẽra mam yelle.»

ref: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
text: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka woo n obrɩ borborɩ wã, dee yũura bõn-yũula wã, dee ka masɛ tɩ a obe dee yũ, a tõnnɩ la tõon-be'ero pɔ'ɔra Zuudãana ĩyã, la a zɩɩm na.

ref: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
text: Bɛla, nẽra woo tẽ'esɛ bɩsɛ a sãn masɛ, deege obe borborɩ la, dee yũ bõn-yũula la.

ref: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ ya pʋam, nẽrba zozo'e dɛna bã'adõma, tɩ sɛba ka tara pãŋa, tɩ basɛba ki.

ref: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
text: Tɩ sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ mẽŋa bɩsɛ tɩ sãn masɛ, Nawẽnnɛ kãn sɩbgɛ tɔ.

ref: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
text: La Zuudãana la sãn sɩbgra tɔ, a pa'alɩ tɩ mɛ tɩ tɩ ɩta sõŋa, tɩ a da wa lagɩm tɩ la dũnia nẽrba di tɩ sarɩya sɩbgɛ tɔ.

ref: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
text: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, ya sãn lagsɛ taaba tɩ ya di Zuudãana kũm tẽerɛ dia la, ya gu'ura taaba.

ref: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
text: Nẽra kɔm sãn dõnna, a di a yire dee wa'am, tɩ ya da kãn lagsɛ taaba ɛ sɩbgrɛ bɔ ya mẽŋa. La yel-sɛba n weege la, mam sãn wa wa'am, mam wʋn mãalɛ.

ref: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
text: Ya mi tɩ yan daan nãn ka bɔ Azezi sɩra la, ya daan taagɛ la baga n de mugro la poore dɔla, ka tãna wʋn basɛ.

ref: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
text: Bɛla tɩ mam bɔɔra tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sãn bɔna la nẽra, a ka tã wʋn tɔgɛ Azezi yelle be'em. La nẽra mẽ ka tã wʋn yetɩ, Azezi de la Zuudãana, tɩ la sãn dagna Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ a yele.

ref: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a la de la to'oto'ore, la, la de la Sɩa ayɩla mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ.

ref: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
text: Tõoma la de la to'oto'ore, la, la de la Zuudãana ayɩla tɩ tɩ tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra.

ref: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
text: Tõoma la n tõnnɩ se'em na de la to'oto'ore, la, la de la Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n tõnnɩ ba wuu nẽrba wuu pʋam.

ref: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra woo la bɔ'a nẽrba la wuu sõŋrɛ ĩyã.

ref: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
text: Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla la bɔ'a tɩ a tɔgra yɛm yetɔga, la Sɩa ẽŋa ayɩla n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayẽma bɔ'a tɩ a tɔgra bãŋrɛ yetɔga.

ref: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
text: La Sɩa ẽŋa mẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla bɔ'a tɩ a bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ sɩra zozo'e, dee bɔ'ɔra ayẽma pãŋa tɩ a botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
text: Sɩa la n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla pãŋa tɩ a tãna tõnna tõon-yãlma, dee bɔ'ɔra ayẽma bɔ'a tɩ a tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla yɛm tɩ a tãna wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'a, la sɛla n ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'a bɔkɛ. Sɩa ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra ayɩla bɔ'a tɩ a tãna tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, dee bɔ'ɔra ayẽma bɔ'a tɩ a tãna pa'ala yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la võorɛ.

ref: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
text: La de la Sɩa ẽŋa ayɩla n ɩtɩ bɛla wuu. Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽra woo la bɔ'a to'oto'ore wʋ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em. Azezi-krisi nẽrba ãn wʋ nẽra ayɩla ĩyã la

ref: The body of Christ has many different parts, just as any other body does.
text: Azezi-krisi nẽrba ãn wʋ ĩyã ayɩla n tarɩ zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore. La baa la zẽ'esɩ la n de to'oto'ore la, ba wuu de la ĩyã ayɩla.

ref: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
text: Tõma Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n de yamsɩ, la sɛba n dagɩ yamsɩ, tõma wuu n daan mise ko'om pʋam na, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ tɩ lebge la ĩyã ayɩla. Nawẽnnɛ tõm na Sɩa ayɩla bɔ tɩ wuu.

ref: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
text: Nẽra ĩyã ka tarɩ zẽ'a ayɩla mã'a. A tarɩ la zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore.

ref: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
text: Nã'arɛ sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa n dagɩ nu'ugo la, ẽŋa ka po dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla, bɛla kãn tã'agɛ basɛ tɩ a kãn dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla.

ref: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
text: La tʋbrɛ mẽ sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa n ka de nifo la, ẽŋa ka po dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla, bɛla kãn tã'agɛ basɛ tɩ a kãn dɛna ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla.

ref: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
text: Tɩ ĩyã la wuu sãn de nɩ nifo, a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ la wãne wõm yetɔga? La a wuu sãn de nɩ tʋbrɛ, a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ la wãne wõm yũuŋo?

ref: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ ĩyã zẽ'a woo wʋ ẽn boorɩ se'em, lagɩm ba taaba.

ref: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
text: Ba wuu sãn de nɩ la zẽ'a ayɩla mã'a, ĩyã kãn boe nɩ.

ref: It takes many parts to make a single body.
text: Bɛla tɩ zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore bɔna, la ba de la ĩyã ayɩla.

ref: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
text: Bɛla nifo ka tã wʋn yele nu'ugo yetɩ, ẽŋa ka boorɩ a sõŋrɛ. La zuugo mẽ ka tã wʋn yele nãma yetɩ, ẽŋa ka boorɩ ba sõŋrɛ.

ref: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
text: Lan pa'asɛ, ĩyã la zẽ'e-sɛba n ãn wʋ ba ka tarɩ pãŋa la, tarɩ yõorɔ mɛ.

ref: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
text: Zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ tõma bɩsɛ tɩ ba ka tarɩ yõorɔ zo'oge la, tɩ bɩsrɩ la bãma yelle sõŋa sõŋa. La zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ tɩ tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba ka masɛ tɩ nẽrba yẽ ba la, bãma tɩ tɩ malgrɩ sõŋa gãnna.

ref: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
text: La ka de tɩ dɩkɛ sɛla pĩ zẽ'e-sõma la. Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n ẽŋɛ tɩ ĩyã la, basɛ tɩ tɩ bɩsra zẽ'e-sɛba tɩ tɩ tẽ'esɛ tɩ ba ka tarɩ yõorɔ zo'oge la yelle sõŋa sõŋa,

ref: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
text: tɩ welgre da bɔna ĩyã la pʋam. La tɩ ĩyã la zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la wʋn sõŋra taaba.

ref: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
text: Bɛla, zẽ'a ayɩla sãn nãmsra, zẽ'esɩ la wuu mẽ nãmsrɩ mɛ. Zẽ'a ayɩla sãn yẽ nã'asgɔ, zẽ'esɩ la wuu tarɩ la sũ-yẽlga.

ref: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
text: Ya wuu de la Krisi ĩyã la. La nẽra woo de la ĩyã la zẽ'a ayɩla.

ref: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ nẽra woo a zẽ'am a nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la pʋam. Pɔspɔsɩ de la sɛba tɩ Azezi mẽŋa daan looge tõm ba la. Bʋyi dãana de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba. Bʋtã de la karẽnsãandõma, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-yãlma, la sɛba tɩ a bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm, la sɛba tɩ a looge tɩ ba sõŋra nẽrba, la sɛba n wʋn bɔna nẽrba nẽŋa, la sɛba n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ.

ref: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
text: La dagɩ tɩ wuu n de sɛba tɩ Azezi mẽŋa daan looge tõm na, dagɩ tɩ wuu n de nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, dagɩ tɩ wuu n de karẽnsãandõma. La dagɩ tɩ wuu n tarɩ pãŋa tõnna tõon-yãlma,

ref: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
text: la dagɩ tɩ wuu n botɩ bã'adõma yẽta ĩmã'asʋm, dagɩ tɩ wuu n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, dagɩ tɩ wuu n tã wʋn pa'alɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la võorɛ.

ref: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
text: Ɛɛra-ya bɔ'a sɛba n gãnnɩ wuu. La mam wʋn pa'alɛ ya so-sɛka n gãnnɩ ba wuu.

ref: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text: Mam sãn tɔgra nẽrsaalba buuri wuu yetɔga, la malɛkadõma yetɔga, dee ka tara nõŋlʋm, mam ãn wʋ dẽnlɔŋɔ bɩɩ pɩrga n wẽ'erɩ zãŋa.

ref: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
text: La mam sãn tara bɔ'a tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee tãna wʋn bãŋɛ yɛla n sʋgɛ wuu võorɛ, dee tara mi'ilum wuu, la mam sãn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra tãna wʋn botɩ tãŋa vurge, dee ka tara nõŋlʋm, mam de la zãŋa.

ref: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
text: La mam sãn dɩkɛ mam n tarɩ sɛla wuu pʋɩ nasdõma, la baa mam sãn sakɛ tɩ ba yõ mam ĩyã bugum, dee ka tara nõŋlʋm, bɛla ka tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ mam.

ref: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
text: Sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka ɩtɩ sɛla n ka masɛ, a ka eerɩ a mẽŋa sõŋrɛ mã'a, a sũure ka isgri, nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ẽ be'em, a ka tẽrɩ ẽ a sũure pʋam.

ref: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
text: Sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga la tõon-be'ero, a ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga la sɩra.

ref: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
text: Nõŋlʋm ka malɩn ba'asra. Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ tɩ to'osrɩ tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, la yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, la mi'ilum wʋn tole.

ref: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
text: Tõma n mi se'em, la tõma n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la, de la fẽe.

ref: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
text: La wakatɛ sãn wa paagɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malgɛ sɛla woo tɩ la masɛ sõŋa sõŋa, sɛla fẽe tɩ tõma mi la wʋn tole.

ref: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
text: Mam n daan de bia la, mam daan tɔgrɩ wʋ bia la, mam daan tẽ'esra wʋ bia la, la mam daan wõnnɩ yɛla võorɛ wʋ bia la. La mam yãŋa n bɩ la, mam basɛ biyã'atɛ mɛ.

ref: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
text: Tõma n mi sɛla lɛɛlɛ wã, ãn wʋ tõma bɩsrɩ la bɩsga n ka yẽtɩ sõŋa pʋam. La wakat-ẽŋa tɩ wʋn yẽ sɛla woo sõŋa. Mam n mi sɛla la, nãn de la fẽe. La mam wʋn wa bãŋɛ ẽ sõŋa sõŋa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n mi mam sõŋa se'em na.

ref: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
text: Ɛ-ya nõŋlʋm, la ya ken ɛɛra Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a la, lan gãnnɩ, bɔ'a sɛka tɩ ya wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba.

ref: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
text: Sɛka n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ ka tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, a tɔgrɩ bɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ. Se'ere n soe la, nẽra ka wõnnɩ a yetɔga la võorɛ, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ a tɔgra la yɛla n sʋgɛ.

ref: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
text: La sɛka n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, ẽŋa tɔgrɩ sõŋra la nẽrba, botɩ ba sẽnna nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo la pʋam, dee kã'ana ba, bɔ'ɔra ba bʋraanɛ.

ref: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
text: Sɛka n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ sõŋrɩ la a mẽŋa. La sɛka n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba sõŋrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba.

ref: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
text: Mam boorɩ nɩ tɩ ya wuu tɔgra la yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, la mam boorɩ tɩ ya tɔ'ɔsra la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba gãnna. Nẽra sãn tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ tɩ nẽra ka bɔna wʋn to'oge yetɔga la tɔgɛ a võorɛ pa'alɛ nẽrba la tɩ la sõŋɛ ba, sɛka n to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la n gãnnɩ.

ref: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text: M sɔbiisi, mam sãn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne sõŋɛ ya? A ka tarɩ võorɛ bɔ ya. Sɛla n wʋn sõŋɛ ya, de la mam sãn pa'alɛ ya sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa puke pa'alɛ mam, bɩɩ m botɩ ya bãŋɛ sɛla, bɩɩ m to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ ya, bɩɩ m zãsʋm ya.

ref: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
text: La ãn wʋ de'eŋo lɔgrɔ la: Wɩa sãn pɛɛbra bʋra bʋra, bɩɩ kõlŋɔ sãn wẽ'era bʋra bʋra, fʋ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ wɩa la peebgo võorɛ, bɩɩ kõlŋɔ la wẽ'a võorɛ?

ref: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
text: Benkonne sãn peebe bʋra bʋra, ka peebe zɛbrɛ peebgo, ãne n wʋn mãasʋm yese zɛbrɛ?

ref: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
text: Bɛla mẽ, ya sãn tɔgra yetɔg-zãŋka tɩ nẽra ka wõnnɩ a võorɛ, nẽra wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne bãŋɛ yãma n boorɩ tɩ ya yele se'em? Ya yetɔga la de la zãŋa.

ref: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
text: La mam sãn ka wõnna yetɔga, mam na sɛka n tɔgrɩ la de la sãama la taaba.

ref: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
text: Bɛla, yãma n mõrgɛ ɛɛra Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'a la, ɛɛra-ya bɔ'a sɛba n wʋn sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

ref: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, sɛka n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋka, a sose Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a botɩ a yãŋɛ tɔgɛ a võorɛ.

ref: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
text: Mam sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ la yetɔg-zãŋka, mam sɩa n pʋ'ʋsrɩ, la mam ka wõnnɩ a võorɛ la m mẽŋa yɛm.

ref: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
text: Bɛla, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Mam wʋn pʋ'ʋsra la m sɩa, dee ken pʋ'ʋsra la m yɛm, la m yõona la m sɩa, dee ken yõona la m yɛm.

ref: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
text: Fʋ sãn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ la yetɔg-zãŋka, nẽra wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne ze'ele nẽrba la n lagsɛ la pʋam yetɩ: «Amina», dee ka wõm fõn yele se'em na võorɛ?

ref: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
text: Baa fõn pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ se'em na sãn ãna sõŋa, la ka tarɩ sõŋrɛ bɔ nẽr-ẽŋa.

ref: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, mam n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ gãnna yãma wuu la.

ref: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
text: La Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am, mam boorɩ tɩ m tɔgɛ la yetɔga banuu la m yɛm tɩ nẽrba wõm ba võorɛ tɩ la zãsʋm ba, sõna mam wʋn tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ tʋspia.

ref: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
text: M sɔbiisi, da ãna-ya wʋ kɔma n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na. La, la sãn dɛna be'em ɩrgɔ yelle, ãna-ya wʋ kɔm-pɩgsɩ n ka ɩtɩ be'em na. La ya tẽ'esgɔ pʋam, ãna-ya wʋ bõnkɛgsɩ la.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn basɛ tɩ sãama n tɔgrɩ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ mõolɛ m kõ-yẽlga bɔ m nẽrba. Baa la wãna wuu, ba kãn selse mam.»

ref: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
text: Sɛba n bɔ sɩra la sãn lagsɛ taaba zẽ'a ayɩla, tɩ nẽra woo tɔgra la yetɔg-zãŋsɩ, tɩ sɛba n ka mi, dee ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra kẽ, ba kãn yetɩ ya zallɩ mɛ?

ref: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
text: La sɛba n bɔ sɩra la sãn lagsɛ taaba, tɩ nẽra woo tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, tɩ nẽra n ka mi, dee ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra kẽ, ẽn wõm sɛla woo la, wʋn basɛ tɩ a bãŋɛ a tõon-be'ero, dee pa'alɛ ẽ a tuure.

ref: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
text: Yel-sɛba n sʋgɛ a sũure pʋam wʋn puke mɛ peelem pʋam, tɩ a wʋn ka dũma dɩkɛ a nẽŋa võglɛ tẽŋa nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sɩrɩ boe la ya lɛ.» Ɩta-ya sɛla woo tɩ ba dɔla sore

ref: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
text: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Ya sãn lagsɛ taaba tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ, ya nẽra ayɩla tã wʋn pʋrɛ yõonɛ, tɩ ayẽma zãsʋm, tɩ ayẽma pa'alɛ bõn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ puke pa'alɛ ẽ, tɩ ayẽma tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋka, tɩ ayẽma tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋka la võorɛ pa'alɛ nẽrba. Ɩta-ya bɛla wuu tɩ la sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

ref: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
text: La sãn dɛna yetɔg-zãŋsɩ tɩ ya yetɩ ya tɔgɛ, nẽrba bayi bɩɩ nẽrba batã mã'a tɔgɛ ayɩla ayɩla, tɩ nẽra ayɩla tɔgɛ ba võorɛ pa'alɛ.

ref: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
text: La nẽra sãn ka bɔna wʋn tã'agɛ tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la võorɛ pa'alɛ, sɛba n yetɩ ba tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ la sĩna da tɔgɛ. Ba tɔgɛ ba sũure pʋam bɔ ba mẽŋa, la Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
text: Sɛba n de Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la, bayi bɩɩ batã tɔgɛ. Tɩ sɛba n weege la bɩsɛ ba yetɔga la sãn masɛ bɩɩ ka masɛ.

ref: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
text: La nẽra ayɩla sãn tɔgra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa puke sɛla pa'alɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ la zẽ'am, nẽr-sɛka n dẽŋɛ tɔgra la go'oge.

ref: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
text: Ya wuu tã wʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ bɔ nẽrba mɛ, la ya tɔgra ayɩla ayɩla, tɩ la wʋn zãsʋm nẽrba wuu, dee bɔ ba bʋraanɛ.

ref: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa tɔ'ɔsra yetɔga bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la tã wʋn yõkɛ ba mẽŋa mɛ.

ref: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ boorɩ tɩ sɛla woo dɔla la sore la sũ-mã'asʋm, a ka boorɩ tɩ tɩ tõnna yooro yooro.

ref: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
text: Pɔgsɩ sĩna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am, wʋ ba ɩtɩ se'em zẽ'esɩ wuu la. Sore ka boe tɩ ba darɛ tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am. La ba sakra nõorɛ wʋ Zifdõma lɔɔ n yele se'em na.

ref: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
text: Sɛla sãn bɔna tɩ ba bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ, ba soke ba sɩrba ba yiɛ. La ka narɩ tɩ pɔgsɩ tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ zẽ'am.

ref: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
text: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ la de la yãma zẽ'am tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la pɔsɛ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ yãma mã'a n to'oge yetɔga la?

ref: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
text: Sɛka n tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, bɩɩ ẽŋa de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ẽ lɛ, a sakɛ tɩ sɛla tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra ya wã de la Zuudãana la nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ.

ref: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
text: La nẽra sãn ka selse yel-ẽna, yãma mẽ da selse ẽ.

ref: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
text: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, mõrgɛ-ya tɔ'ɔsra Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba, dee da gu-ya nẽrba tɩ ba da tɔgɛ yetɔg-zãŋsɩ.

ref: But do everything properly and in order.
text: La ɩta-ya sɛla woo tɩ la ãna sõŋa, dee dɔla sore.

ref: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
text: M sɔbiisi, lɛɛlɛ wã mam boorɩ tɩ m tẽegɛ ya la kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam daan mõolɛ bɔ ya tɩ ya bɔ sɩra zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ la yelle.

ref: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
text: Ya sãn gʋrɛ ẽ kãŋkãŋɛ wʋ mam n mõolɛ ya se'em na, ya wʋn yẽ fãarɛ. La sãn dagna bɛla, yan bɔ sɩra la de la zãŋa.

ref: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
text: Zãsŋɔ sɛka n gãnnɩ tɩ mam daan to'oge n bɛla tɩ mam dẽŋɛ zãsʋm ya: Krisi ki la tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.

ref: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
text: Tɩ ba daan laagɛ ẽ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ a vo'oge dabsa atã daarɛ, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.

ref: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ a karẽnbiisi n gãnnɩ kɔbsnuu wakatɛ ayɩla. Ba nẽrba basɛba zozo'e ken vɔa mɛ, la basɛba ki mɛ.

ref: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
text: Bɛla poorʋm tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Azakɩ, dee yãŋa dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ Tõntõnɩba la wuu.

ref: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
text: Bɛla wuu poorʋm, a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa pa'alɛ la mam n ãn wʋ bia tɩ ba dɔgɛ tɩ a wakatɛ ka paagɛ la.

ref: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
text: Mam de la pɩka Tõntõnɩba la wuu zẽ'am, dee ka nara tɩ ba wi'ira mam tɩ Tõntõnna, mam n daan nãmsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la ĩyã.

ref: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã tɩ mam dɛna mam n de se'em lɛɛlɛ wã. Yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ mam na dagɩ zãŋa. Mam tõm gãnna Tõntõnɩba basɛba la wuu mɛ. La, la dagɩ mam mẽŋa n tõnnɩ bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na ĩyã tɩ mam yãŋɛ tõnna bɛla.

ref: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
text: Bɛla, la sãn dɛna la mam n mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la, bɩɩ bãma, tɩ wuu mõolɛ la kõ-yẽlga sɛka tɩ mam tẽegɛ ya a yelle la, sɛka tɩ ya daan sakɛ la. Kũm vo'ore

ref: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
text: Tõma n mõolɩ tɩ Krisi ki mɛ dee vo'oge la, wãne tɩ ya nẽrba basɛba yetɩ, sɛba n ki la kãn vo'oge?

ref: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
text: La sãn dɛna sɩra tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ki la kãn vo'oge, bɛla Krisi yi ka vo'oge.

ref: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
text: La Krisi sãn ka vo'oge, kõ-yẽlga la tɩ tõma mõolɩ la de la zãŋa, tɩ yãma bɔ sɩra la mẽ dɛna zãŋa.

ref: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
text: La sãn dɛna bɛla, tõma yi parnɩ mɛ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, tõma n yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ botɩ Krisi vo'oge mɛ, dee tɩ kũm kãn vo'oge la ĩyã.

ref: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
text: Sɛba n ki la sãn kãn vo'oge, bɛla, Krisi yi ka vo'oge.

ref: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
text: La Krisi sãn ka vo'oge, yan bɔ sɩra la de la zãŋa, la ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ ken bɔna la ya zuto.

ref: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
text: La sɛba n bɔ Krisi sɩra dee ki la mẽ yi bɔɩ mɛ.

ref: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
text: Tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la Krisi la sãn dɛna la tõma n vɔa dũnia wã zuo mã'a ĩyã, tõma n yi de ninbãal-dõma gãnna nẽrsaalba wuu.

ref: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
text: La, la dagɩ bɛla, Krisi ki dee vo'oge mɛ, ẽŋa n de pɔspɔsɩ dãana n vo'oge, pa'alɛ tɩ nẽrba basɛba n ki la mẽ wʋn vo'oge.

ref: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
text: La de la nẽra ayɩla ĩyã tɩ nẽrba kiira, bɛla, la mẽ de la nẽra ayɩla ĩyã tɩ kũm wʋn vo'oge.

ref: Adam brought death to all of us, and Christ will bring life to all of us.
text: Nẽrsaalba wuu de la Adam yɩɩsɩ, bɛla tɩ ba kiira. Bɛla tɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n mẽ de Krisi nẽrba wʋn vo'oge.

ref: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
text: La nẽra woo tarɩ la a wakatɛ, pɔspɔsɩ de la Krisi n vo'oge, la a lemŋo daarɛ, sɛba n de a nẽrba la wʋn vo'oge mɛ.

ref: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, Krisi wʋn sã'am sɛba wuu n soe ke'eŋo, la na'am, la pãŋa wuu, dee yãŋa dɩkɛ so'olʋm na lebse bɔ a Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, dee tɩ sɛla woo ba'asɛ.

ref: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
text: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ Krisi di na'am halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ a bɛ'ɛba wuu bɔna a nãma tẽŋa.

ref: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
text: Kũm n de bɛ-sɛka tɩ a wʋn wa sã'am poorʋm.

ref: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ la a nãma tẽŋa.» Lan yetɩ Krisi n soe sɛla woo la, la ka tã tɩ a sɔna Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ a sɔna sɛla woo la.

ref: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ sãn dɩkɛ sɛla woo ẽŋɛ Krisi nu'usum, ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la mẽ wʋn dɩkɛ a mẽŋa ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nu'usum. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɛna nẽŋadãana sɔna sɛla woo.

ref: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
text: Sɛba n ki la sãn kãn malɩn vo'oge, nẽr-sɛba n botɩ ba misra ba ko'om pʋam sɛba n ki ĩyã la, wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne? Kũm sãn kãn malɩn vo'oge, bẽm tɩ ba ɩta bɛla?

ref: And why do we always risk our lives
text: La bẽm tɩ tõma põsra tɩ yõorɛ wakatɛ wuu?

ref: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam põsrɩ m yõorɛ mɛ daarɛ woo, mam n yetɩ ya se'em na de la sɩra. La de la sɩra wʋ mam n ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga ya yelle yãma n dolɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã se'em na.

ref: Don't fool yourselves. Bad friends will destroy you.
text: Da pã'asɛ-ya ya mẽŋa. Fʋ sãn dɔla nẽr-be'ego, fʋ wʋn lebge la nẽr-be'ego.

ref: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
text: Basɛ-ya tẽ'esɛ yooro, dee da le tõnna tõon-be'ero. Mam yetɩ ya tɩ la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya: Ya nẽrba basɛba ka mi Nawẽnnɛ. Sɛba n wʋn vo'oge la ĩyã n wʋn ãna se'em

ref: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
text: La nẽr-sɛka wʋn soke yetɩ: «Sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge la wãne wãne? Ba wʋn wa tara la ĩyã bẽm buuri?»

ref: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
text: Sokre ẽna de la gɔɔn-sokre! Fõn ka mi tɩ fʋ sãn bʋrɛ sɛla biire, a yɛm n wʋn bule yese vɔa, dee tɩ a pɔkɔ põ'ogɛ?

ref: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
text: Nawẽnnɛ botɩ bõnbuuri la yɛsra ãna wʋ ẽŋa n boorɩ tɩ ba ãna se'em na. Bõnbuuri woo n yesrɩ ãna se'em de la to'oto'ore.

ref: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
text: Bõn-vɔɔsɩ mẽ ka tarɩ ĩyã ayɩla. Nẽrsaalba ĩyã de la to'ore, dũnsi ĩyã de la to'ore, niinto ĩyã de la to'ore, tɩ zũma ĩyã mẽ dɛna to'ore.

ref: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
text: Bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum tarɩ la ba ĩyã, tɩ sɛba n boe tẽŋa zuo mẽ tara ba ĩyã, la ba sõŋa dagɩ ayɩla.

ref: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
text: Wẽntulle peelem boe la a to'ore, tɩ wõrga peelem bɔna a to'ore, tɩ wõrbiɛ peelem mẽ bɔna ba to'ore. La wõrbiɛ la peelem mẽ le bɔna la to'oto'ore.

ref: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
text: Kũm vo'ore la mẽ wʋn wa ãna la bɛla. Nẽra sãn ki tɩ ba laagɛ ẽ, a ĩyã põ'orɩ mɛ. La Nawẽnnɛ sãn wa vo'oge ẽ, a kãn le põ'ogɛ.

ref: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
text: Ban laagɛ ẽ na, a tarɩ la dũnia zuo ĩyã. La Nawẽnnɛ sãn wa vo'oge ẽ, a tarɩ la ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ẽ. Dũnia zuo ĩyã boe mɛ, la ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ mẽ boe mɛ.

ref: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Adam n de pɔspɔsɩ nẽra la, Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ vɔm.» La Krisi tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ poorʋm Adam na, tarɩ la Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm.

ref: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
text: La dagɩ ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ la tɩ tɩ to'orɩ yiɛn. Tɩ to'orɩ la dũnia wã ĩyã yiɛn, dee yãŋa wʋn to'oge ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ.

ref: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn dɩkɛ la tẽŋa tẽntõnnɔ nãam pɔspɔsɩ nẽra la. La poorʋm nẽra la ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am.

ref: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
text: Dũnia nẽrba wuu ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn dɩkɛ tẽŋa tẽntõnnɔ nãam na. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire nẽrba mẽ ãna wʋ nẽr-sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am na.

ref: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
text: Bɛla, tõma n ãn wʋ nẽr-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ tẽntõnnɔ nãam na, tɩ mẽ wʋn wa ãna wʋ nẽr-sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire wa'am na.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
text: M sɔbiisi, bɛla tɩ mam gʋlsɛ yele ya tɩ, nẽrsaala ĩyã wã kãn tã'agɛ kẽ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam. Ĩyã wã n põ'orɩ la kãn tã'agɛ yẽ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: I will explain a mystery to you. Not every one of us will die, but we will all be changed.
text: Selse-ya tɩ m yele ya yelle n sʋgɛ. Tõma nẽrsaalba wuu kãn ki dee tɩ dũnia ba'asɛ, la tɩ wuu ĩisi wʋn teege mɛ.

ref: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
text: Dũnia ba'asgɔ daarɛ, benkonne n wʋn peebe, tɩ tɩ wuu teege tɔtɔ nini kãbsɩgɔ pʋam. Benkonne na sãn peebe, sɛba n ki la wʋn vo'oge la ĩyã n kãn malɩn ki, la Nawẽnnɛ wʋn teege tõma ĩyã mɛ.

ref: Our dead and decaying bodies will be changed into bodies that won't die or decay.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrsaala ĩyã wã n põ'orɩ la, lebge ĩyã n ka põ'orɩ, tɩ ĩyã n kiiri la, lebge ĩyã n ka kiiri.

ref: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
text: La ĩyã n põ'orɩ la sãn lebge ĩyã n ka põ'orɩ, tɩ ĩyã n kiiri la lebge ĩyã n ka kiiri, wakat-ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ se'em na wʋn ẽŋɛ. La gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ yãŋɛ kũm, tɩ kũm ka le bɔna.

ref: Where is its victory? Where is its sting?”
text: Kũm, fʋ pãŋa boe la bɛ? Fʋ toogo boe la bɛ?»

ref: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
text: La de la tõon-be'ero n botɩ kũm tara pãŋa kʋʋra nẽrba. Tɩ lɔɔ botɩ tõon-be'ero yẽta pãŋa.

ref: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
text: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, ẽŋa n botɩ tõma yãŋɛ tõon-be'ero, la kũm, Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã la.

ref: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
text: Bɛla, m sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, mõrgɛ-ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ. Tõnna-ya Zuudãana tõoma la wakatɛ wuu da basɛ, yãma n mi tɩ nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ ya nãmsrɩ Zuudãana la ĩyã dagɩ zãŋa la.

ref: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã, mam boorɩ tɩ m tɔgɛ la ligri la tɩ ya lagsrɩ tɩ ya sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la yelle. Doose-ya pa'alg-sɛka tɩ mam pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la n boe Galatɩ so'olʋm na.

ref: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
text: Lasrɩ daarɛ woo, nẽra woo looge lig-sɛba tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ la bĩŋe, da zɛ-ya gu'ura tɩ mam sãn wa paam, tɩ ya yãŋa lagsɛ.

ref: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
text: Tɩ mam sãn wa paam, mam wʋn gʋlsɛ la gõŋɔ tõm nẽr-sɛba tɩ ya mẽŋa wʋn looge la, tɩ ba tarɩ bɔ'a la sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm.

ref: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
text: La, la sãn le nara tɩ mam mẽŋa sẽŋɛ, bãma wʋn doose la mam tɩ tɩ sẽŋɛ. Apolɩ tɔgrɩ a sore sẽnnɛ yelle

ref: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
text: Mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am, la mam wʋn doose la Masedʋanɩ so'olʋm wa'am.

ref: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
text: La mam sãn wa'am, mam wʋn yuuge ya zẽ'am fẽe, bɩɩ tã tɩ mam wʋn bɔna la ya tɩ ɔɔrɔ wakatɛ wa tole, tɩ ya sõŋɛ ma tɩ m sẽŋɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn sẽŋɛ la.

ref: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
text: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ m wen doose bɩsɛ ya dee tole, mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ mam wʋn bɔna ya zẽ'am yuuge, Zuudãana la sãn bɔ mam sore.
