ref: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ yo'oge la sore bɔ mam tɩ m tõm a tõoma zozo'e, baa bɛ'ɛba n zo'oge la.

ref: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
text: Atimõtɩ sãn wa'am ya zẽ'am, ya to'oge ẽ sõŋa, dee ẽŋɛ-ya yan wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ a bʋraanɛ da bo'oge. Se'ere n soe la, a tõnnɩ Zuudãana tõoma la wʋ mam n tõnnɩ se'em na.

ref: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
text: Nẽra nẽra da pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ, sõŋɛ-ya ẽ tɩ a leme wa'am mam zẽ'am la sũ-mã'asʋm. Mam, la sɔbiisi la gu'uri ẽ mɛ.

ref: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
text: La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔbia Apolɔsɩ, mam yele ẽ mɛ nõorɛ faa tɩ a doose la sɔbiisi basɛba la wa'am wa bɩsɛ ya. La a ka boorɩ tɩ a wa'am lɛɛlɛ wã. A sãn wa yẽ nẽŋa, a wʋn wa'am. Ba'asgɔ yetɔga, la pʋ'ʋsgɔ

ref: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
text: Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo la pʋam. Keŋe ya mẽŋa, tara-ya bʋraanɛ.

ref: Show love in everything you do.
text: Ɩta-ya sɛla woo la nõŋlʋm.

ref: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
text: M sɔbiisi, ya mi Astefanasɩ la a yir-dõma yelle, bãma n dẽŋɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra Akayi so'olʋm pʋam. Ba tõm mɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Mam belnɩ ya mɛ

ref: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
text: tɩ ya nanna nẽr-bãma, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n tõnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma wʋ bãma la.

ref: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
text: Mam tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ Astefanasɩ la Afɔrtunatusi la Akayikusi n wa'am na. Se'ere n soe la, ba sõŋɛ mam wʋ yãma boe nɩ la mam zẽ'am na.

ref: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
text: Ba keŋe mam, la yãma sũure mɛ. Pẽgra-ya nẽr-bãma buuri.

ref: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
text: Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Azi so'olʋm pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya. Akila, la Aprisili, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-sɛba n lagsrɩ ba yire la pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya sõŋa Zuudãana Azezi doosgo pʋam.

ref: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
text: Sɔbiisi la wuu pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya. Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm.

ref: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
text: Mam Apolɩ n gʋlsɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ wã la m mẽŋa nu'ugo, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

ref: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n ka nõŋɛ Zuudãana la, kã'a-be'ego wʋn bɔna a dãana zuo. Maranata.

ref: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya.

ref: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
text: Mam nõŋɛ ya wuu mɛ Azezi-krisi doosgo pʋam.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
text: Mam Apolɩ n de Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, la tɩ sɔbia Atimõtɩ n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu n boe Korẽntɩ, la Akayi so'olʋm wuu pʋam na.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
text: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm. Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
text: Tɩ pẽgɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la. Ẽŋa n de Sɔ n zotɩ ninbãalga, la ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ n sõŋrɩ nẽrba bɔ'ɔra ba bʋraanɛ sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
text: Ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma bʋraanɛ tɩ tooro wuu pʋam, tɩ tõma n yẽtɩ bʋraanɛ na, tõma mẽ wʋn yãŋɛ sõŋra nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe tooro buuri wuu pʋam bɔ ba bʋraanɛ.

ref: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
text: Tõma n yẽtɩ nãmsgɔ zo'oge wʋ Krisi la, bɛla, tõma mẽ wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ zo'oge Krisi ĩyã.

ref: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
text: Tõma sãn bɔna toogo pʋam, la de la tɩ yãma wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ, la fãarɛ. La tõma sãn yẽta bʋraanɛ, la de la tɩ yãma wʋn yẽ bʋraanɛ tɩ la sõŋɛ ya tɩ yãma mẽ wʋn yãŋɛ wĩbra nãmsgɔ pʋam wʋ tõma n mẽ wĩbri nãmsgɔ pʋam na.

ref: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
text: Tõma tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la yãma mɛ, mina sõŋa sõŋa tɩ yãma n po nãmsra la tõma la, yãma mẽ wʋn po yẽ bʋraanɛ la tõma.

ref: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
text: Tɩ sɔbiisi, tõma boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la tooro sɛba n paagɛ tõma Azi so'olʋm pʋam na. Tooro la daan nãmsɛ tõma mɛ zo'oge gãŋɛ tõma pãŋa, tɩ tõma daan põn ka le tara pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ tõma wʋn vɔa.

ref: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
text: Tõma daan kɔ'ɔgɛ tɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ yetɩ tõma vɔm tɛka n bɛla. La bɛla paagɛ tõma tɩ tɩ da te'ege la tɩ mẽŋa, la tɩ te'ege Nawẽnnɛ n vo'orɩ kũm na.

ref: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
text: La de la ẽŋa n fãagɛ tõma kũm ẽŋa pʋam, la a wʋn ken fãagɛ tõma mɛ. Asɩra, tõma tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ la ẽ lɛ mɛ tɩ a wʋn le fãagɛ tõma mɛ.

ref: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
text: Yãma n sõŋrɩ tõma pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra tõma la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn lerge nẽrba pãɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ sõŋɛ tõma, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ tõma ĩyã. Sɛla n gu Apolɩ tɩ a viige a sore sẽnnɛ

ref: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
text: Sɛla n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma sũ-yẽlga de la tõma mi tɩ sũa pʋam tɩ tõma yel-ɩrɛ n ãn se'em dũnia wã pʋam, la, lan gãnnɩ wuu yãma zẽ'am, de la pʋpeelem, la sɩra n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. La dagɩ la nẽrsaala yɛm tɩ tõma ɩtɩ bɛla, la de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã.

ref: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
text: Tõma n gʋlsrɩ yãma sɛla wã, la dagɩ sɛla ayẽma, la de la yãma n karẽŋrɩ sɛla wõnna a võorɛ la. La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ ya wʋn wõm a wuu võorɛ mɛ,

ref: just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
text: wʋ yãma n dẽŋɛ bãŋɛ a võorɛ fẽe, tɩ Zuudãana lemŋo daarɛ tõma n de yãma zu-zẽkrɛ, tɩ yãma mẽ dɛna tõma zu-zẽkrɛ la.

ref: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
text: Mam n tarɩ pʋtẽ'e-ẽŋa la, mam daan boorɩ mɛ tɩ m dẽŋɛ wa'am yãma zẽ'am, tɩ ya wʋn to'oge yel-sõnnɛ nõorɛ bʋyi.

ref: once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea.
text: Mam daan boorɩ la m dẽŋɛ wa'am yãma zẽ'am, yãŋa ze'ele yãma zẽ'am tole Masedʋanɩ so'olʋm, le ze'ele bilam leme wa'am yãma zẽ'am, tɩ ya sõŋɛ mam tɩ m sẽŋɛ Zude so'olʋm.

ref: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
text: Mam n looge yɛm tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam de la nõgõla dãana bɩɩ? Bɩɩ yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam n looge yɛm se'em na de la nẽrsaala yɛm loore tɩ mam tãna wʋn yetɩ «ẽe», le yetɩ «ayɛɩ»?

ref: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
text: Nawẽnnɛ n de mam sɩra dãana, tɩ mam n tɔgɛ se'em bɔ yãma la dagɩ «ẽe», la «ayɛɩ».

ref: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Azezi-krisi n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa tɩ mam, la Atimõtɩ, la Asilvẽ daan mõolɛ a kõa yãma zẽ'am na, dagɩ «ẽe», la «ayɛɩ», ẽŋa zẽ'am la de la «ẽe» wakatɛ wuu.

ref: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla wuu la, Azezi-krisi n yetɩ «ẽe», ẽŋa wʋn ẽŋɛ. Bɛla, la de la Azezi-krisi ĩyã tɩ tõma yɛta tɩ: «Amina!» tɩ la dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nã'asgɔ.

ref: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ tõma la yãma lagɩm zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ Krisi doosgo pʋam, la ẽŋa n looge to, welge tɩ to'ore.

ref: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n de mam kaset-dãana, mam sãn parna, a botɩ m ki. La de la, la wʋn sõŋɛ ya tɩ ya sũure da sã'am ĩyã n basɛ tɩ mam ka le wa'am Korẽntɩ.

ref: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
text: La dagɩ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ mõrgɛ ya mɛ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam. Tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na ya lɛ mɛ tõm tɩ ya yẽ sũ-yẽlga. Se'ere n soe la, yãma ze mɛ kãŋkãŋɛ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam.

ref: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
text: Bɛla, mam looge la yɛm yetɩ mam kãn le wa'am ya zẽ'am, tɩ m da botɩ ya sũure le sã'am.

ref: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
text: Mam sãn botɩ yãma sũure sã'am, ãne n wʋn bɔ mam sũ-yẽlga, sãn dagna yãma sɛba tɩ mam wʋn botɩ nɩ ya sũure sã'am na?

ref: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
text: Bɛla tɩ mam daan gʋlsɛ bɔ ya, mam ka boorɩ tɩ m sãn wa wa'am ya zẽ'am, m tara sũ-sã'aŋɔ la yãma n de sɛba n wʋn bɔ mam sũ-yẽlga la. Mam mi sɩra sɩra tɩ mam sãn tara sũ-yẽlga, yãma mẽ tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ.

ref: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
text: La daan de la toogo, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la nintãm pʋam, tɩ mam daan gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ la bɔ ya. La dagɩ tɩ mam daan boorɩ tɩ m botɩ ya sũure n sã'am, la de la tɩ ya bãŋɛ mam n nõŋɛ ya zo'oge paagɛ se'em. Bɔ-ya nẽr-sɛka n koŋe la sugri

ref: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
text: Nẽra sãn botɩ sũ-sã'aŋɔ wa'am, la dagɩ mam mã'a sũure tɩ a sã'am, la de la yãma wuu sũure tɩ a sã'am. La tɩ m da tɔgɛ tole, m yetɩ a sã'am na ya nẽrba zozo'e sũure.

ref: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
text: Sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ yãma nẽrba zozo'e bɔ nẽr-ẽŋa dãana la, la seke mɛ.

ref: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã, sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la ya bɔ ẽ sugri, keŋe a sũure, tɩ la da kẽ'esɛ ẽ sũ-sã'aŋɔ zozo'e pʋam tɩ a sã'am.

ref: You should make them sure of your love for them.
text: Bɛla, mam kã'anɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ sɛla n wʋn botɩ a bãŋɛ tɩ yãma nõŋɛ ẽ mɛ.

ref: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
text: Mam n daan gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ la bɔ yãma la, mam daan boorɩ tɩ m bɩsɛ la ya sãn sakra mam sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
text: Ya sãn bɔ nẽra sugri, mam mẽ bɔ'ɔrɩ a dãana sugri mɛ. La sãn dɛna mam, mam bɔ sugri mɛ, la bõn-sɛka sãn bɔna dɛna mam bɔ sugri, mam ɩtɩ bɛla la yãma ĩyã Krisi nifum,

ref: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
text: tɩ la da botɩ Asũtãana yãŋɛ tɔ. Se'ere n soe la, tõma mi ẽn boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em mɛ. Apolɩ sũ-sã'aŋɔ Trʋasɩ

ref: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
text: Mam n daan paagɛ Trʋasɩ tɩ m mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la, mam daan yẽ tɩ Zuudãana la yo'oge sore bɔ mam mɛ,

ref: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
text: la mam pʋtẽ'erɛ daan ka gã zẽ'a ayɩla, mam n ka yẽ m sɔbia Atiti bilam na ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ mam daan keese ba, dee tole Masedʋanɩ. Krisi tã'arɛ

ref: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ tõma dɔla Krisi poore daarɛ woo a tã'arɛ la pʋam, dee botɩ tõma ĩyã nẽrba bãŋra Krisi zẽ'a woo, wʋ yũuŋ-yẽlga n taagɛ zẽ'a wuu la.

ref: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
text: La sãn dɛna la nẽrba basɛba, la de la kũm yũuŋo n sẽm na kũm, la nẽrba basɛba zẽ'am, la de la vɔm yũuŋo n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm. Ãne n wʋn tã'agɛ tõm tõon-ẽŋa?

ref: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
text: Tõma ka ãn wʋ nẽrba basɛba zozo'e n dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la lɛɛbra ɛɛra yõorɔ la. Ayɛɩ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm tõma, tɩ tõma pelge tɩ pʋʋrɛ tɔgra a nẽŋam Krisi doosgo pʋam.

ref: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
text: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ pẽgɛ la tɩ mẽŋa bɩɩ? Bɩɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ bɔ ya la gõnnɔ n pa'alɩ tõma n ãn sõŋa se'em, bɩɩ tõma boorɩ la ya gʋlsɛ gõnnɔ n pa'alɩ tõma n ãn se'em bɔ tɔ, wʋ nẽrba basɛba n ɩtɩ se'em na?

ref: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
text: Yãma mẽŋa n de tõma gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ tõma sũure pʋam, tɩ nẽrba wuu mina dee karẽŋra ẽ.

ref: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
text: La de la vẽelga tɩ yãma de la Krisi gõŋɔ tɩ a doose tõma tõoma pʋam gʋlsɛ. La dagɩ gʋlsgɔ ko'om n gʋlsɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa la Sɩa n gʋlsɛ. La ka gʋlsɛ kug-palagsɩ zuto, la gʋlsɛ la nẽrsaalba sũa pʋam.

ref: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
text: Tõma n tɔgrɩ bɛla la, tõma mi sõŋa sõŋa tɩ bõn-ẽŋa de la sɩra Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

ref: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
text: La ka boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ tõma tã wʋn tõm tõon-ẽŋa la tõma mẽŋa pãŋa, tõma pãŋa ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
text: La de la ẽŋa n botɩ tõma tã'agɛ dɛna a nõor-bĩŋre paalga la tõntõnɩba. La dagɩ lɔɔ n gʋlsɛ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa. Lan de se'em na, lɔɔ n gʋlsɛ la sẽm na kũm, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la bɔ'ɔrɩ la vɔm.

ref: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
text: Lɔɔ la yʋʋn gʋlsɛ la kug-palagsɩ zuto, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ wakatɛ ẽŋa, tɩ Amoyisi nẽŋa nẽegɛ yẽgra, tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma ka tãna wʋn bɩsɛ ẽ. La na'am pãŋa la yʋʋn tollɩ mɛ. Lɔɔ la n sẽm na kũm na n yʋʋn puke la na'am pãŋa bɛla la,

ref: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n tõnnɩ tõon-sɛka la, wʋn tara na'am pãŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ zozo'e.

ref: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
text: Tõon-sɛka n sẽm na sɩbgrɛ la n yʋʋn tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, bɛla, tõon-sɛka n botɩ nẽrba masra Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na tarɩ na'am pãŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ zozo'e.

ref: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
text: Tõma tã wʋn põn yetɩ na'am pãn-sɛka n yʋʋn boe la lebge la zãŋa, se'ere n soe la, sɛka n boe lɛɛlɛ wã gãnnɩ ẽ mɛ zozo'e.

ref: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
text: Bɛla, sɛla n yʋʋn tollɩ la n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, sɛla n boe wakatɛ wuu la n tarɩ na'am pãŋa zo'oge gãnna bɛla.

ref: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
text: Tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'e-ẽŋa gu'ura la ĩyã, tõma tarɩ bʋraanɛ zo'oge mɛ.

ref: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
text: Tõma ka ɩtɩ wʋ Amoyisi n yʋʋn dɩkɛ tãnnɛ pĩ a nẽŋa tɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma da yẽ na'am pãŋa n nẽegɛ a nẽŋa n tollɩ la.

ref: The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
text: La Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la yɛm yʋʋn ka like. Halɩ wakat-ẽŋa wa paam lɛɛlɛ wã, ba sãn karẽŋra nõor-bĩŋre kɛka gõŋɔ la, tãnnɛ na ken pĩ ba nẽnsɩ mɛ, tɩ ba ka bɔkra a võorɛ. Tãn-ẽŋa ka pilgri, sãn dagna Krisi peelem pʋam mã'a.

ref: When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over
text: Asɩra, wakat-ẽŋa tarɩ wa paam zĩna, ba sãn karẽŋra Amoyisi gõnnɔ la, tãnnɛ na ken pĩ ba yɛm mɛ.

ref: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
text: La nẽra sãn teege yɛm lebe Zuudãana la zẽ'am, tãnnɛ na pilgri mɛ.

ref: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
text: Ban yetɩ Zuudãana la, la de la Sɩa la. La Zuudãana Sɩa la n boe zẽ'a la, to'og-m-mẽŋa boe la bilam.

ref: So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
text: Tõma wuu n dolɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, tãnnɛ na pilge mɛ, tɩ tõma nẽnsɩ yẽgra la Zuudãana na'am pãŋa. Tɩ Zuudãana Sɩa tɛɛra tõma, tɩ tõma ãna wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na, tɩ na'am pãŋa la pa'asra zɔ'ɔra.

ref: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n zoe tõma ninbãalga bɔ tõma tõon-ẽŋa la ĩyã n soe tɩ tõma bʋraanɛ ka bɔ'ɔra.

ref: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
text: Tõma zagsɛ mɛ ka tõnna tõon-sɛba wuu n sʋgɛ n de yãnnɛ tõoma la, tõma ka ɩtɩ yɛm pã'asra nẽrba. La tõma ka teerɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga. Tõma tɔgrɩ pa'ala la sɩra vẽelga vẽelga, tɩ nẽrba sũure n mi se'em na wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ tõma ɩtɩ sɛla n ãn sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ nifum.

ref: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
text: La kõ-yẽlga la tɩ tõma mõolɩ la sãn ken sʋgɛ mɛ, a sʋgɛ la nẽr-sɛba n wʋn sã'am na.

ref: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
text: La de la sɛba n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la. Asũtãana n de dũnia wã naba la n pĩ ba yɛm tɩ ba da bãŋɛ Krisi n de Nawẽnnɛ wõnnɛ na kõ-yẽlga na'am pãŋa.

ref: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
text: La dagɩ tõma mẽŋa yelle tɩ tõma mõolɩ, la de la Azezi-krisi n de Zuudãana la kõa tɩ tõma mõolɩ. La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma de la yãma tõntõnɩba Azezi ĩyã.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn yetɩ: «Peelem nẽegɛ lika pʋam» na, la de la ẽŋa n botɩ peelem nẽegɛ tõma sũa pʋam, botɩ tõma bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa n nẽegɛ Krisi nẽŋa zuo.

ref: We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us.
text: La tõma n tarɩ bõntarsõm ẽŋa la, tõma ãn wʋ yɔgrɔ lɔgrɔ tɩ ba dɩkɛ tʋʋlʋm bõnɔ ẽŋɛ mĩ. Tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ pãn-kãtɛ ẽŋa ka ze'ele tõma zẽ'am, a ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
text: Tõma yẽtɩ nãmsgɔ buuri to'oto'ore mɛ sɛla woo pʋam, la a ka yãŋɛ tõma. Tõma fablɩ mɛ ka mina tõma n wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em, la tõma bʋraanɛ ka bo'oge.

ref: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
text: Nẽrba nãmsrɩ tõma mɛ, la Nawẽnnɛ ka basɛ tõma, ba wẽ'erɩ tõma lʋbra mɛ, la tõma ka ki.

ref: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
text: Tõma n vɔa wã, tõma põsrɩ tɩ yõorɛ mɛ wakatɛ wuu Azezi ĩyã, tɩ nẽrba wʋn yẽ a vɔm tõma ĩyã n kiiri la pʋam.

ref: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
text: Bɛla, kũm zɩgrɩ la a kʋ tõma, tɩ yãma yẽta vɔm.

ref: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam bɔ sɩra mɛ n soe tɩ mam tɔgɛ.» Tõma mẽ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra mɛ, bɛla n soe tɩ tõma tɔgra.

ref: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
text: Tõma mi mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, a mẽ wʋn po vo'oge tõma mɛ la Azezi, la a wʋn lagɩm tarɩ tõma la yãma sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
text: Bõn-bãma wuu n ɩtɩ la de la yãma ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ wʋn paara nẽrba zozo'e, tɩ nẽrba wʋn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ zɔ'ɔra pa'asra, tɩ la dɛna a nã'asgɔ.

ref: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
text: Bɛla tɩ tõma bʋraanɛ ka bɔ'ɔra. La baa tõma ĩyã n sã'anɩ na, tõma sɩa lebgrɩ la paalga daarɛ woo.

ref: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
text: Nãmsgɔ sɛka tɩ tõma yẽtɩ wakatɛ fẽfẽe wã de la pɩka ka tãna wʋn dɩkɛ makɛ la nã'asgɔ zozo'e n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ tɩ a wʋn botɩ tõma yẽ la.

ref: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
text: Tõma pʋtẽ'erɛ n boe la sɛla la, dagɩ sɛla tɩ nini yẽtɩ. A de la sɛla tɩ nini ka yẽtɩ. Bõn-sɛka tɩ nini yẽtɩ tollɩ mɛ. La sɛla tɩ nini ka yẽtɩ la boe la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
text: Tõma ĩyã tɩ tɩ tarɩ dũnia wã zuo ãn wʋ fuu-deego. Tõma mi tɩ fuu-deego ẽna sãn wa sã'am, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ kẽ'a zẽ'a saazuum bɔ tɔ. A de la kẽ'a zẽ'a n boe wakatɛ wuu, a dagɩ nẽrba n ẽŋɛ ẽ.

ref: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
text: La tõma nãmsrɩ mɛ bɔɔra zozo'e tɩ tɩ kẽ saazuum kẽ'a zẽ'a na.

ref: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
text: Se'ere n soe la, tɩ sãn kẽ kẽ'a zẽ'e-ẽŋa, la ãn wʋ tɩ yɛ la fuugo la, tɩ kãn dɛna bɛɛla.

ref: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
text: Tõma n ken bɔna dũnia zuo ĩyã wã pʋam na, tõma nãmsrɩ mɛ tara toogo. La dagɩ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ basɛ la dũnia wã zuo ĩyã la, tõma boorɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n dɩkɛ saazuum ĩyã la yɛ tɔ, tɩ sɛla n de vɔm wʋn pĩ sɛla n kiiri la.

ref: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n ẽŋɛ tõma tɩ tõma ãna bɛla, dee bɔ tõma a Sɩa n de sɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ tõma wʋn yẽ sɛla wuu tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ tɩ la.

ref: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
text: Bɛla tɩ tõma tara bʋraanɛ zozo'e daarɛ woo, dee mina tɩ tõma n ken bɔna dũnia ĩyã wã pʋam na, tõma zãagɛ la Zuudãana kẽ'a zẽ'a na mɛ.

ref: But we live by faith, not by what we see.
text: La tõma dolɩ la sɛla tɩ tõma bɔ sɩra dee nãn ka yẽ.

ref: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
text: Tõma tarɩ bʋraanɛ mɛ zo'oge, dee boorɩ nɩ tɩ tɩ basɛ dũnia ĩyã wã, dee sẽŋɛ ka bɔna Zuudãana la zẽ'am.

ref: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
text: Bɛla tɩ tõma mõrgra bɔɔra tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ tɩ la paagɛ a yɛm, tõma sãn bɔna dũnia ĩyã wã pʋam, bɩɩ tɩ sãn sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies. Bringing People to God
text: Se'ere n soe la, tõma wuu wʋn ze'ele la Krisi nẽŋam tɩ a di tõma sarɩya, dee bɔ nẽra woo yɔɔrɔ tɩ la masɛ wʋ ẽn tõm se'em dũnia wã, a sãn tõm sõŋa, bɩɩ a sãn tõm be'em. Krisi n naagɛ tõma la Nawẽnnɛ

ref: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
text: Bɛla tõma n mi tɩ zɔta Zuudãana n de se'em na, tɩ tõma mõrgra tɩ tɩ tɔgɛ yãŋɛ nẽrba tɩ ba sakɛ ẽ. Wẽnnaam mi tõma sõŋa mɛ. La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ yãma sũure pʋam ya mẽ mi mam mɛ.

ref: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
text: Tõma ken ka bɔɔra tɩ tɩ pa'alɛ ya tõma n ãn sõŋa se'em, tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ bɔ ya la folle tɩ ya tara yẽsõm tõma ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ tɔgɛ lerge nẽr-sɛba n wẽ'erɩ yõ'ogɔ la sɛla n yẽtɩ la nini, dee ka bɩsra ba sũure pʋam na.

ref: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
text: Tõma sãn dɛna zɔllɔ, la de la Nawẽnnɛ ĩyã. La tõma sãn ka zalla, la de la yãma sõŋrɛ ĩyã.

ref: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
text: La de la Krisi nõŋlʋm n soe tõma la ĩyã. Tõma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ nẽra ayɩla n ki nẽrba wuu ĩyã, tɩ la pa'alɛ tɩ nẽrba wuu po lagɩm na ẽ ki mɛ.

ref: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
text: A ki nẽrba wuu ĩyã tɩ nẽr-sɛba n vɔa la, da le vɔa ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, ba vɔa wʋ sɛka n ki ba ĩyã dee vo'oge la n boorɩ se'em.

ref: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ lɛɛlɛ wã, tõma ka le bɩsra nẽra wʋ nẽrsaala n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na. La baa tõma daan sãn bɩsra Krisi wʋ nẽrsaalba n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na, lɛɛlɛ wã tõma ka le bɩsra ẽ bɛla.

ref: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
text: Nẽra sãn lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla, a lebge la nẽr-paalga, yel-kɛgsɩ la tole mɛ, tɩ sɛla woo lebge paalsɩ.

ref: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ bɛla wuu, ẽŋa n botɩ Krisi naagɛ tõma la ẽŋa, dee bɔ tõma tõon-ẽna tɩ tɩ botɩ nẽrba naagɛ la ẽŋa.

ref: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
text: Tõma n yetɩ se'em na, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n botɩ Krisi botɩ dũnia dõma la ẽŋa naagɛ taaba, dee ka geele ba tõon-be'ero. Dee bɔ tõma yetɔga tɩ tɩ tɔgɛ naarɛ yelle bɔ nẽrba.

ref: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
text: Bɛla, Krisi n tõm tõma tɩ tõma sɔɔgɛ a zẽ'am tɔgra la yãma wʋ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n tɔgrɩ kã'ana ya tõma yetɔga la pʋam. Krisi ĩyã tõma sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
text: Krisi ka tõm tõon-be'ero. La Nawẽnnɛ basɛ mɛ tɩ a lebge wʋ tõon-be'ero dãana tõma ĩyã, tɩ tõma wʋn lebge nẽrba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am ẽŋa ĩyã.

ref: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Tõma n lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ tõnna la ĩyã, tõma sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya da basɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ya to'oge Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na dɛna zãŋa.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ yele a gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Mam lerge ya pʋ'ʋsgɔ la wakat-sɛka tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ na. Fãarɛ la daarɛ, mam sõŋɛ ya mɛ.» La selse-ya, lɛɛlɛ wã de la wakatɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ yel-sõnnɛ, lɛɛlɛ wã de la fãarɛ daarɛ la.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
text: Tõma ka boorɩ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sɛla n wʋn botɩ nẽra tuuge, tɩ nẽra da zɛrgɛ tɔ tɩ tõoma la pʋam.

ref: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
text: La sɛla woo pʋam tõma pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ tõma de la nẽrba n tõnnɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõoma. Tõma mã'arɩ tɩ sũure mɛ, wĩbra nãmsgɔ pʋam, la toogo, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ pʋam.

ref: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
text: Ba wẽ tõma mɛ, dee yʋ tõma yʋ'a deto pʋam. Ba botɩ nẽrba zozo'e gire mɛ zɛbɛ la tõma, tõma tõnnɩ tõon-ke'eno mɛ targɛ ka gĩsra, ka dita.

ref: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
text: La tõma tõnnɩ na pʋpeelem, la bãŋrɛ, la sũ-mã'arɛ, la ɩr-sõŋɔ, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra.

ref: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
text: Tõma tɔgrɩ la sɩra yetɔga la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa. Tõma n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ la n de tõma zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ tɩ tõma tarɩ zɛbra dee gu'ura tɩ mẽŋa.

ref: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
text: Nẽrba basɛba nã'asrɩ tõma mɛ, tɩ basɛba pɔ'ɔra tõma, basɛba sã'anɩ tõma yʋ'ʋrɛ mɛ, tɩ basɛba pẽgra tõma. Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ tõma de la põmpɔrndõma, baa la tõma n tɔgrɩ sɩra la.

ref: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
text: Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ tõma de la sãama, baa la ban mi tõma sõŋa la, ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ nẽrba n kiiri, dee tɩ tõma vɔa. Ba sɩbgrɩ tõma mɛ, dee tɩ tõma ka kiira.

ref: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
text: Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ tõma tarɩ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la tõma tarɩ la sũ-yẽlga daarɛ woo. Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ nasdõma, dee tɩ tõma botɩ nẽrba zozo'e tara bõntarsõm. Ba bɩsrɩ tõma wʋ nẽrba n ka tarɩ sɛla, dee tɩ tõma tara sɛla woo.

ref: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
text: Korẽntɩ dõma, tõma tɔgɛ ya la vẽelga, tõma pelge la tɩ pʋʋrɛ tɔgɛ la ya.

ref: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
text: La dagɩ tõma n ka nõŋɛ yãma, la de la yãma n ka pa'alɛ ya nõŋlʋm.

ref: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
text: Mam tɔgrɩ la ya wʋ m kɔma la, ya mẽ nõŋɛ tõma, pelge-ya ya pʋʋrɛ la tõma. Lika la peelem ka tã wʋn lagɩm taaba

ref: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
text: Da dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa lagɩm na nẽr-sɛba n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la. Se'ere n soe la, sõŋa la be'em kãn yãŋɛ lagɩm taaba. Bẽm lagŋɔ n boe peelem, la lika tẽŋasʋka?

ref: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
text: Bẽm naa-nõorɛ n boe Krisi, la Asũtãana tẽŋasʋka? Bɩɩ sɛka n bɔ Krisi sɩra, la sɛka n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra tarɩ la bẽm pʋrɛ la taaba?

ref: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
text: Bẽm lagŋɔ n boe Wẽnnaam deego, la baga tɩ ba mãalɛ tẽŋasʋka? La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa la deego wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yele yetɩ: «Mam wʋn bɔna la ba, kẽ'era la ba. Mam wʋn dɛna ba Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba.»

ref: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Yese-ya ba pʋam, welge-ya ya mẽŋa dee basɛ ba. La ya da kalʋm sɛla n de dẽgrɔ, tɩ mam wʋn to'oge ya.

ref: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
text: Mam wʋn dɛna ya Sɔ, tɩ ya dɛna mam dayɔɔsɩ, la m pɔyɔɔsɩ.» Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la n yele.

ref: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
text: Bɛla m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, nõor-bĩŋre bãma wuu n de tõma bõnɔ na, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ malgɛ tɩ mẽŋa la dẽgrɔ wuu n dẽgrɩ ĩyã, la sɩa, dee ãna sõŋa, vɔa pʋpeelem pʋam masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, zɔta Nawẽnnɛ. Apolɩ tarɩ sũ-yẽlga la Korẽntɩ dõma

ref: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ tõma nõŋlʋm bɔna ya sũure pʋam. Tõma ka ẽŋɛ nẽra baa ayɩla be'em, tõma ka sã'am nẽra yelle, la tõma ka zãm nẽra to'oge a sɛla.

ref: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
text: Mam n tɔgrɩ bɛla la, dagɩ tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m mõŋɛ ya la bʋʋrɔ. Mam daan põn dẽŋɛ yele ya tõma n nõŋɛ ya zo'oge se'em mɛ, tõma sãn ki, bɩɩ tõma sãn vɔa mɛ.

ref: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
text: Mam bɔ ya sɩra mɛ, la mam yẽsnɩ mɛ yãma ĩyã. Baa tõma toogo la wuu pʋam, mam tarɩ bʋraanɛ, la sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ.

ref: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
text: Asɩra, tõma n daan paagɛ Masedʋanɩ na, tõma daan ka yẽ vo'osgo baa fẽfẽe. Tõma yẽ toogo buuri wuu mɛ, nẽrba n zɛbɛ la tõma, dee tɩ dabeem tara tõma.

ref: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ sũ-sã'aŋɔ dõma bʋraanɛ na, daan botɩ Atiti n wa'am tɩ tõma yẽ bʋraanɛ.

ref: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
text: La dagɩ a wa'aŋɔ la mã'a ĩyã, la de la ẽn wa'am wa yele tõma yãma n bɔ ẽŋa bʋraanɛ se'em na ĩyã. A yele tõma la yãma n boorɩ tɩ ya le yẽ mam zo'oge se'em, dee yele mam yãma sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la yãma n wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa mam ĩyã se'em. Bɛla n soe tɩ mam sũ-yẽlga le zo'oge pa'asɛ.

ref: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
text: Mam gõŋɔ la tɩ mam daan gʋlsɛ la sãn sã'am ya sũure, mam ka ɩtɩ m sãn bãŋɛ nɩ. Mam sãn wʋn yetɩ m sãn bãŋɛ nɩ, la de la mam n daan bãŋɛ tɩ gõŋɔ la sã'am yãma sũure la. La bɛla de la wakatɛ fẽfẽe pʋam.

ref: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
text: La mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, la dagɩ mam n botɩ ya sũure sã'am na ĩyã, la de la ya sũ-sã'aŋɔ la n botɩ ya teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Yãma sũ-sã'aŋɔ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em. Bɛla tõma ka ẽŋɛ ya sɛla n de be'em.

ref: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
text: Asɩra, sũ-sã'aŋɔ sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na botɩ nẽra tɛɛra la yɛm basra tõon-be'ero, yẽta fãarɛ. Nẽra nẽra ka ɩtɩ a sãn bãŋɛ nɩ bɛla pʋam. La dũnia sũ-sã'aŋɔ sẽm na kũm.

ref: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
text: Bɛla, mam n daan gʋlsɛ ya la, la dagɩ sɛka n tõm tõon-be'ero la ĩyã, la dagɩ nẽr-sɛka tɩ a ẽŋɛ ẽ be'em na mẽ ĩyã. La de la, la botɩ ya bãŋɛ sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam yãma n nõŋɛ tõma zo'oge se'em.

ref: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
text: La de la bɛla n soe tɩ tõma yẽ bʋraanɛ, la dagɩ bʋraanɛ na mã'a tɩ tõma yẽ, tõma ken yẽ sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ, tõma n yẽ tɩ Atiti tarɩ sũ-yẽlga, yãma n keŋe a sũure se'em na.

ref: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
text: La sãn dɛna mam n pẽgɛ yãma Atiti zẽ'am na, yãma ka ẽŋɛ mam yãnnɛ. Tõma n ẽn tɔgra sɩra bɔ'ɔra ya daarɛ woo la, bɛla tõma n pẽgɛ yãma Atiti zẽ'am na mẽ de la sɩra.

ref: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
text: Atiti sãn tẽegɛ yãma sakrɛ, la yãma n to'oge ẽ na dabeem na, a nõŋɛ yãma zo'oge pa'asɛ mɛ.

ref: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
text: Mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ, mam n tã wʋn bɔ yãma sɩra sɛla woo pʋam na ĩyã.

ref: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: M sɔbiisi, tõma boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ Masedʋanɩ Wẽndeto la nẽrba yel-sõnnɛ se'em.

ref: Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously.
text: Ba yẽ toogo mɛ nãmsɛ zozo'e, la ba tarɩ sũ-yẽlga zo'oge mɛ, tɩ baa la ba nõŋɔ la n zo'oge la, ba bɔ sõŋrɛ zo'oge mɛ la ba sũure wuu.

ref: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
text: Mam yetɩ ya tɩ ba bɔ masɛ wʋ ban tã se'em, la ba bɔ halɩ gãŋɛ la ba pãŋa.

ref: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
text: Ba mẽŋa n sose tõma tɩ tɩ bɔ ba sore tɩ ba po sõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Zerizalɛm na.

ref: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
text: Ba ẽŋɛ gãŋɛ tõma n daan tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ se'em na mɛ. Ba dẽŋɛ dɩkɛ la ba mẽŋa bɔ Zuudãana la, dee dɩkɛ ba mẽŋa bɔ tõma doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em.

ref: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
text: Bɛla tɩ tõma sose Atiti n pɔsɛ sõŋrɛ tõon-ẽŋa yãma zẽ'am na, yetɩ a tõm ẽ ba'asɛ.

ref: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
text: Yãma mõrgɛ sɛla woo pʋam mɛ: Yãma bɔ Krisi sɩra zo'oge mɛ, tãna tɔgra Nawẽnnɛ yelle sõŋa, tara Nawẽnnɛ mi'ilum zo'oge, wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa sɛla woo pʋam, la yãma tarɩ tõma nõŋlʋm zo'oge mɛ. Bɛla, mõrgɛ-ya la sõŋrɛ tõon-ẽŋa mẽ tõonɛ.

ref: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
text: Mam n yetɩ bɛla la, la dagɩ pãŋa tɩ mam ɩtɩ ya. Mam dɩkɛ la nẽrba basɛba n ẽŋɛ se'em makɛ bɔ ya, tɩ ya pa'alɛ yãma nõŋlʋm n de nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra se'em.

ref: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
text: Yãma mi tõma Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ na mɛ: Baa la ẽŋa n daan de bõntarsõm dãana la, a daan ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa la nasa yãma ĩyã. Tɩ ẽn ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa nasa la, wʋn botɩ yãma lebge bõntatba Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
text: Bɛla, lɛɛlɛ wã, tõm-ya ẽ la yɛmleego ba'asɛ, wʋ yãma n yetɩ yãma boorɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ se'em na. La ya bɔ masɛ wʋ yãma n tarɩ se'em.

ref: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra sãn bɔ la yɛmleego masɛ wʋ ẽn tarɩ se'em, Nawẽnnɛ to'orɩ a bɔ'a la mɛ, a ka bɩsrɩ sɛla tɩ fʋ ka tarɩ la.

ref: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
text: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ yãma n sõŋrɩ nẽrba basɛba la kẽ'esɛ ya nõŋɔ pʋam. La de la tɩ ya zõna taaba.

ref: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
text: La de la yãma sɛba n tarɩ zo'oge lɛɛlɛ wã la, sõŋɛ sɛba n de nasdõma la. Tɩ bãma mẽ sãn wa tara zo'oge, tɩ ya mẽ sãn bɔɔra sõŋrɛ, tɩ ba sõŋɛ ya, bɛla tɩ ya wuu wʋn zõna taaba,

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
text: tɩ la doose wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ la yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n vaagɛ zo'oge la, ka tarɩ sɛla tɩ la zo'oge gãŋɛ. La sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ sɛka n vaagɛ fẽe la.»

ref: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
text: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ yɛmloore ẽŋa Atiti sũure pʋam tɩ a bɔɔra tɩ a sõŋɛ ya wʋ tõma n boorɩ tɩ sõŋɛ yãma se'em na.

ref: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
text: Atiti sakɛ tõma sosgo mɛ, ken pa'asɛ, a looge la yɛm a mẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ a wa'am yãma zẽ'am wa sõŋɛ ya.

ref: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
text: Tõma tõm na tɩ sɔbia ayɩla tɩ Wẽndeto nẽrba la wuu pẽgrɩ ẽ, ẽn tõnnɩ kõ-yẽlga la tõoma ĩyã, tɩ ẽŋa la Atiti wa'am.

ref: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
text: Sɛla n pa'asɛ, Wẽndeto nẽrba la n looge ẽ tɩ a lagɩm la tõma ɛɛra tõnna sõŋrɛ tõon-ẽna wã. Tõma tõnnɩ tõon-ẽna nã'asra la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa, dee pa'ala tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ sõŋɛ nẽrba.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
text: Tõma guusri la tɩ mẽŋa tɩ nẽr-sɛka da wa tã'agɛ zɛrgɛ tõma, tõma n bɩsrɩ ligri la n zo'oge yelle se'em na.

ref: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
text: Tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ãn sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la nẽrsaalba mẽ zẽ'am.

ref: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
text: Tõma tõm na tɩ sɔbia ayẽma la bãma. Tõma makɛ ẽ mɛ nõorɛ faa bɩsɛ, la a wɩsgɛ a mẽŋa mɛ tõnna. Lɛɛlɛ wã a wɩsgɛ a mẽŋa gãnna kʋrʋm mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, a bɔ yãma sɩra zo'oge mɛ.

ref: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
text: La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔbia Atiti yelle, a de la mam tõntõn-tadãana n lagɩm na mam tõnna yãma ĩyã. La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔbi-sɛba n dolɩ ẽ na, ba de la nẽrba tɩ Wẽndeto nẽrba la tõm, tɩ la dɛna Krisi nã'asgɔ.

ref: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
text: Bɛla, ẽŋɛ-ya sɛla n pa'alɩ ba, la Wẽndeto nẽrba la tɩ ya nõŋɛ ba, tɩ ba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ tõma n pẽgɛ yãma ba zẽ'am na, la de la sɩra.

ref: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
text: La ka le dɛna tɩ mam gʋlsɛ yele ya sõŋrɛ tɩ ya yetɩ ya bɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Zerizalɛm na yelle.

ref: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
text: Mam mi tɩ ya boorɩ tɩ ya sõŋɛ mɛ, bɛla tɩ mam pẽgɛ ya Masedʋanɩ dõma zẽ'am, yele ba yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Akayi la põn mãasʋm na yʋʋnɛ n tole tɩ ba sõŋɛ.» La yãma n wɩsgɛ ya mẽŋa la keŋe nẽrba zozo'e mɛ, tɩ ba mẽ bɔɔra tɩ ba ẽŋɛ.

ref: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
text: La mam tõm na tɩ sɔbiisi la tɩ ba wa'am ya zẽ'am, tɩ tõma n pẽgrɩ yãma se'em bõn-ẽŋa pʋam na, da kãn dɛna zãŋa. Mam boorɩ la ya mãasʋm ba'asɛ wʋ mam n yele se'em na.

ref: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
text: La sãn dagna bɛla, Masedʋanɩ dõma la sãn doose mam wa'am wa yẽ tɩ ya ka mãasʋm, tõma n daan bɔ yãma sɩra zo'oge se'em na, la wʋn dɛna la yãnnɛ zozo'e bɔ tõma, la, la mẽ wʋn dɛna la yãnnɛ bɔ yãma.

ref: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
text: Bɛla tɩ mam bɩsɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa tɩ mam sose tɩ sɔbiisi la tɩ ba dẽŋɛ nẽŋa wa'am yãma zẽ'am wa bɩsɛ yɛmleego bɔ'a la tɩ ya yetɩ ya bɔ la yelle. Tɩ mam sãn wa paam, tɩ ya põn mãasʋm bɔ'a la ba'asɛ mɛ, tɩ la wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ya bɔ la ya yɛmleego, la dagɩ mõrgrɛ.

ref: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
text: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka n bʋrɛ fẽe, wʋn lagsɛ la fẽe. La nẽr-sɛka n bʋrɛ zo'oge, wʋn lagsɛ zo'oge.

ref: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
text: Bɛla, nẽra woo bɔ wʋ ẽn looge yɛm se'em na, tɩ la da dɛna nẽnzũŋre, bɩɩ mõrgrɛ. La de la nẽr-sɛka n bɔ'ɔrɩ la sũ-yẽlga tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ.

ref: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ tarɩ ke'eŋo mɛ tãna wʋn bɔ ya bõn-sõma buuri wuu, tɩ ya wʋn tara yãma n boorɩ sɛla la wuu daarɛ woo tɩ la seke, dee tara sɛla wʋn tõm tõon-sõma wuu.

ref: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ ka-kɔɔra bõnbuuri, dee bɔ'ɔra ẽ dia tɩ a dita la, wʋn bɔ ya sɛla tɩ ya tãna wʋn tõm tõon-sõma zozo'e wʋ bõnbuuri n wɔm zo'oge se'em, tɩ ya tõoma n masɛ wʋn zɔ'ɔra pa'asra.

ref: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
text: A wʋn bɔ ya bõntarsõm tɩ la seke sɛla woo pʋam, tɩ ya wʋn bɔ'ɔra la ya sũure wuu, tɩ nẽrba zozo'e wʋn pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, yãma bɔ'a la tɩ tõma dɩkɛ bɔ ba la ĩyã.

ref: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
text: Se'ere n soe la, sõŋrɛ tõonɛ na tɩ ya tõnnɩ na, la dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la sõŋrɛ mã'a ĩyã, la ken botɩ nẽrba zozo'e n pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
text: Ba sãn to'oge sõŋrɛ ẽŋa, ba wʋn pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ yãma n pa'alɛ tɩ yãma sakrɩ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la, la yãma n tarɩ yɛmleego, tara ya bõntarsõm pʋta la bãma, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu la ĩyã.

ref: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
text: Ba wʋn nõŋɛ ya mɛ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ ya ĩyã, ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ zozo'e la ĩyã.

ref: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
text: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, a bɔ'a n ka tarɩ makrɛ la ĩyã.

ref: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
text: Mam Apolɩ tɩ ya yetɩ, mam sãn bɔna yãma zẽ'am, mam de la nẽr-bʋgsgɔ, la mam sãn ka bɔna yãma zẽ'am, mam tarɩ la bʋraanɛ tɔgra la, mam belnɩ ya mɛ la Krisi sũ-bʋgsʋm, la a sõŋa.

ref: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
text: Mam sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya da ɩta, tɩ mam sãn wa'am wa bɔna ya zẽ'am, la dɛna pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam tara bʋraanɛ tɔgra, se'ere n soe la, mam mi tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ la bʋraanɛ la nẽr-sɛba n yetɩ tõma ɩtɩ la tõma nẽrsaalba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na.

ref: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
text: Tõma de la nẽrsaalba, la tõma ka zɛbrɩ wʋ nẽrsaalba n ɩtɩ se'em na.

ref: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
text: Zɛbrɛ lɔg-sɛba tɩ tõma tarɩ zɛbra la dagɩ nẽrsaala zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ, ba de la pãŋa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, n tã sã'ana sɛla wuu n tarɩ ke'eŋo. Tõma tarɩ ba sã'ana la tẽ'esgɔ sɛba wuu n de põmpɔrŋɔ,

ref: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
text: la bõn-sɛba wuu n zẽkrɩ ba mẽŋa botɩ nẽrba ka bãŋra Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa. Dee yãŋra tẽ'esgɔ buuri wuu botɩ ba sakra Krisi.

ref: And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
text: Yãma sãn wa ẽŋɛ sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ ya sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ sõŋa, tõma mãasʋm mɛ tɩ tɩ sɩbgɛ sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ la wuu.

ref: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
text: Yãma bɩsrɩ la sɛla tɩ yãma nini yẽtɩ la. Nẽra sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la Krisi nẽra, a dãana mẽ tẽ'esɛ wãna wã: Ẽŋa n de Krisi nẽra la, tõma mẽ de la Krisi nẽrba.

ref: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
text: La mam sãn wẽ m yõ'ogɔ zo'oge fẽe la ke'eŋo sɛka tɩ Zuudãana bɔ tõma la, yãnnɛ ka tarɩ mam, la de la Zuudãana n bɔ tõma ke'eŋo tɩ tɩ sõŋɛ ya tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam, la dagɩ tɩ sã'am ya.

ref: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
text: Mam ka boorɩ tɩ la ãna wʋ mam yetɩ m kẽ'esɛ ya la dabeem la m gõnnɔ na.

ref: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrba n yetɩ se'em n wãna: «Apolɩ gõnnɔ na yetɔga kɛ'ɛm mɛ, dee dɛna toogo. La a mẽŋa sãn bɔna tõma zẽ'am, a ka tarɩ pãŋa, tɩ a yetɔga la dɛna ninbãalga.»

ref: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
text: Nẽr-sɛka dãana n tɔgrɩ bɛla la, a bãŋɛ sõŋa tɩ tõma n ka boe yãma zẽ'am dee tɔgra se'em gõnnɔ na pʋam na, tõma sãn wa'am wa bɔna ya zẽ'am, tõma ken ãna la bɛla.

ref: We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves.
text: Tõma kãn saagɛ zõsõm tɩ mẽŋa, bɩɩ dɩkɛ tɩ mẽŋa makɛ la sɛba n pa'alɛ ba mẽŋa tẽ'esɛ tɩ bãma ãn sõŋa la. Ba dɩkrɩ la ba mẽŋa mãmsra, makra ba mẽŋa la taaba. Ban ɩtɩ se'em na, ba ka tarɩ yɛm.

ref: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
text: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma kãn wẽ yõ'ogɔ tɩ la yõsgɛ, tõma wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ masɛ la tõon-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ tõma la beene. Ẽŋa n bɔ tõma sore tɩ tõma paagɛ yãma zẽ'am.

ref: We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
text: Tõma ka yõsgɛ tɩ beene wʋ tõma ka wa'am na yãma zẽ'am na, tõma n de pɔspɔsɩ nẽrba n wa'am yãma zẽ'am wa mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la.

ref: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
text: Tõma ka wẽ'erɩ yõ'ogɔ la tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrba basɛba tõm, bɛla tõma ka yõsgɛ. La tõma tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ yãma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la wʋn pa'asra mɛ, tɩ tõma tõonɛ na zɔ'ɔra pa'asra yãma zẽ'am, tɩ la ken masɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ tõma se'em na beene.

ref: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
text: Tɩ tõma yãŋa wʋn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la so'olʋm sɛba n boe ya nẽŋa la pʋam, tɩ la kãn dɛna tɩ tõma wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ la tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrba basɛba dẽŋɛ tõm ba tõoma zẽ'am.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a wẽ yõ'ogɔ, a wẽ yõ'ogɔ la sɛla tɩ Zuudãana ẽŋɛ la.»

ref: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
text: Se'ere n soe la, la dagɩ nẽr-sɛka n pa'alɩ a mẽŋa tɩ ẽŋa ãn sõŋa la n masɛ, la de la nẽr-sɛka tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a ãn sõŋa la.

ref: Please put up with a little of my foolishness.
text: Mam sosrɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya sakɛ m yalnɛ wã fẽe, asɩra, sakɛ-ya m yetɔga wã.

ref: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
text: La mam zotɩ la dabeem tɩ nẽr-sɛka wʋn pã'asɛ ya, tɩ ya wẽrgɛ ka le dɔla Krisi la sũure ayɩla, la pʋpeelem, wʋ Awa tɩ bõnsɛla la yʋʋn dɩkɛ yɛmkẽgsɩga pã'asɛ ẽ tɩ a tuuge la.

ref: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽra woo sãn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa mõola Azezi ayẽma yelle, tɩ la ka dɛna Azezi sɛka tɩ tõma mõolɛ bɔ ya la, yãma to'orɩ mɛ tɔtɔ, la yãma to'orɩ la sɩa ayẽma n dagɩ sɛka tɩ ya põn to'oge la, la yãma to'orɩ la kõ-yẽlga ayẽma n dagɩ sɛka tɩ yãma põn to'oge la, sakra ba sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
text: Mam ka tẽ'esɛ tɩ nẽr-bãma n pa'alɩ tɩ bãma de la Tõntõnɩba n gãnnɩ na, mam de poorʋm dãana ba zẽ'am.

ref: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
text: Baa la mam sãn ka mina yetɔga wʋ yetɔg-mita, la dagɩ tɩ mam ka tarɩ bãŋrɛ. La tõma pa'alɛ ya bõn-ẽŋa mɛ vẽelga vẽelga sɛla woo pʋam, wakatɛ wuu.

ref: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
text: La mam n daan boe ya zẽ'am bɔɔra sõŋrɛ sɛba wuu la, mam ka te'ege ya nẽra nẽra tɩ a sõŋɛ mam. La daan de la tɩ sɔbi-sɛba n ze'ele Masedʋanɩ wa'am na n daan bɔ mam sɛla wuu n pɔ'ɔgɛ mam na. Mam daan guusri m mẽŋa mɛ tɩ m da dɛna zeero bɔ ya, la mam wʋn ken ãna la bɛla.

ref: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
text: Krisi sɩra n boe mam sũure pʋam na, mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ nẽra ka tã wʋn botɩ mam ka wẽ yõ'ogɔ la bõn-ẽŋa yelle Akayi so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
text: Bẽm ĩyã tɩ mam yele wãna? La de la mam ka nõŋɛ ya mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa mi tɩ mam nõŋɛ ya mɛ.

ref: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
text: Mam n ɩtɩ se'em na, mam wʋn ken ɩta la bɛla, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ tɩ ba wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ yetɩ bãma n tõnnɩ se'em, la tõma n tõnnɩ se'em na de la bʋyɩla la, da yẽ folle.

ref: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
text: Nẽr-bãma de la põmpɔrŋɔ Tõntõnɩba, ba pã'asrɩ la nẽrba, ɩta ba mẽŋa ãna wʋ ba de la Krisi Tõntõnɩba.

ref: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
text: La ẽna wã dagɩ bõn-di'iŋa. Se'ere n soe la, Asũtãana mẽŋa teerɩ mɛ ãna wʋ peelem malɛka.

ref: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
text: Bɛla, la dagɩ di'ire tɩ a tõntõnɩba ɩta ba mẽŋa ãna wʋ sɛla n masɛ tõntõnɩba. La ba ba'asgɔ wʋn ãna wʋ ba tõoma la n de se'em na. Apolɩ pa'alɛ ẽŋa n nãmsɛ a tõoma pʋam se'em

ref: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
text: Mam le yɛta mɛ tɩ nẽra da bɩsɛ mam wʋ mam de la yalma la. La ya sãn bɩsra mam wʋ yalma, ya yi sakɛ tɩ m wẽ m yõ'ogɔ fẽe, wʋ mam de la yalma la.

ref: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
text: Mam n tɔgrɩ wẽ'era m yõ'ogɔ wã, la dagɩ Zuudãana n yetɩ mam yele se'em na. Mam keŋe la m sũure tɔgra wʋ yalma la, wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ.

ref: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
text: Nẽrba zozo'e n wẽ'erɩ yõ'ogɔ la dũnia yɛla la, mam mẽ wʋn wẽ m yõ'ogɔ mɛ.

ref: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
text: Yãma de la yɛm dõma, dee sakɛ tɔ'ɔra yalmdõma la yɛmleego!

ref: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
text: Yãma sakrɩ mɛ tɩ ba kẽ'esra ya yamnɛ pʋam, sakɛ tɩ ba dita ya, fãara ya, ka nanna ya, sakɛ tɩ ba wẽ'era ya pɛgsɩ.

ref: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
text: Tõma n daan ẽŋɛ tɩ mẽŋa tarɩmdõma ka ẽŋɛ ya wʋ bãma n ẽŋɛ ya se'em na, la de la yãnnɛ bɔ mam bɩɩ? Nẽra sãn tãna wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ sɛla yelle, mam mẽ tã wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ mɛ a yelle. Wãna de la yalnɛ tɩ mam tɔgrɩ.

ref: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
text: Ba de la Ebre dõma, mam mẽ de la Ebre nẽra. Ba de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, mam mẽ de la Ɩsrayɛlɩ nẽra. Ba de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ, mam mẽ de la Abraham yɩɩŋa.

ref: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
text: Ba yetɩ bãma de la Krisi tõntõnɩba, mam wʋn tɔgɛ wʋ mam yɛm n bɔɩ la, mam n de Krisi tõntõnna gãnna ba. Mam tõm nãmsɛ gãnna bãma mɛ. Ba kẽ'esɛ mam yʋ'a deem gãnna bãma mɛ, ba wẽ mam zo'oge gãnna bãma mɛ, nõorɛ zozo'e mam deege la m ki.

ref: Five times my own people gave me
text: Nõorɛ bʋnuu tɩ Zifdõma wẽ mam la ka'asa, ka'asrɛ ayɩla kaɩ pinaasɩ.

ref: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
text: Rom dõma wẽ mam mɛ nõorɛ atã. Daarɛ ayɩla nẽrba lobe mam mɛ la kuga deege la ba kʋ. Nõorɛ bʋtã tɩ mam boe õorŋɔ pʋam, tɩ a sã'am ko-kãtɛ pʋam mise. Mam ẽŋɛ daarɛ ayɩla mɛ ko'om pʋam, wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ.

ref: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
text: Mam sɔa la sẽnnɛ pʋam, mam yẽ yel-pakrɩsɩ mɛ zozo'e la kʋlsɩ ko'om n pɩrɛ, la fãarba. Mam yẽ yɛla mɛ la Zifdõma n de mam buuri la, la bu-zãŋsɩ. Mam yẽ yel-pakrɩsɩ mɛ tẽnsɩ pʋam, la weto pʋam, la ko-kãtɛ pʋam. Mam daan yẽ yɛla mɛ la sɛba n parɩm tɩ bãma de la tɩ sɔbiisi la.

ref: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
text: Mam daan tõm tõon-ke'eno nãmsɛ mɛ. La nõorɛ faa mam ka gĩse. Kɔm, la koyũuro yõkɛ mam mɛ. Mam gã'arɛ kɔm mɛ wakatɛ basɛba. Wakatɛ basɛba ɔɔrɔ yõkɛ mam mɛ, tɩ mam ka tara fuugo wʋn pile.

ref: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
text: Tɩ mam ka tɔgra yel-sɛba n weege la. La sɛla n de pakrɛ bɔ mam daarɛ woo, de la Azezi nẽrba n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu la yelle.

ref: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
text: Nẽra sãn bɔna tɔrgɔ pʋam, la ãn wʋ mam mẽ boe la tɔrgɔ pʋam na. La nẽra sãn basɛ Nawẽnnɛ doosgo, mam sũure n sã'anɩ zozo'e.

ref: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
text: La sãn dɛna yõ'ogɔ wẽ'a, mam wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ la m tɔrgɔ la.

ref: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
text: Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi Sɔ n soe pẽka wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ la, mi mɛ tɩ mam ka parnɩ.

ref: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
text: Mam n daan boe Damaasɩ la, gɔmna tɩ naba Aretasɩ botɩ a sɔna Damaasɩ bɩsra la, daan botɩ ba gu'ura la tẽŋa la nõa tɩ ba yõkɛ mam.

ref: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
text: Tɩ ba daan botɩ mam sige pɩ'ɩgɔ pʋam, tɩ ba dɩkɛ mam doose sawure sike lalga poorʋm, la de la bɛla tɩ mam yãŋɛ põsɛ.

ref: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ mam wẽ yõ'ogɔ, la yõ'ogɔ wẽ'a la ka tarɩ yõorɔ. La mam wʋn ba'asõm tɔgɛ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Zuudãana la puke pa'alɛ mam, tɩ mam yẽ wʋ zãasŋɔ la.

ref: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
text: Nõorɛ atã dãana n wãna tɩ mam mãasʋm tɩ m wa'am ya zẽ'am wa bɩsɛ ya. La, la dagɩ yãma n wʋn bɩsɛ mam yelle. Se'ere n soe la, mam ka eerɩ ya sɛla, la de la yãma mẽŋa tɩ mam eerɩ. Yãma ãn wʋ mam kɔma la. Asɩra, la dagɩ kɔma n wʋn lagsɛ sɛla bĩŋe ba sɔdõma, la de la sɔdõma n wʋn lagsɛ sɛla bĩŋe ba kɔma.

ref: As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time.
text: Mam mi tɩ bʋra-ẽŋa zẽkɛ mɛ zom Nawẽnnɛ yire, la mam n yele se'em na, mam ka mi a sãn sɩrɩ zẽkɛ zom, bɩɩ la sãn ãna wʋ zãasŋɔ la,

ref: But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell.
text: la mam n mi sɛla de la a wõm na yetɔga mĩ tɩ nẽra ka tã wʋn tɔgɛ, la sɛla tɩ ba ka bɔ nẽrsaala nõorɛ tɩ a yele.

ref: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
text: Mam wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ la bʋra-ẽŋa n yẽ sɛla la yelle, la mam kãn wẽ yõ'ogɔ m yɛla basɛba yelle, tɩ la sãn dagna mam tɔrgɔ la yelle mã'a.

ref: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
text: Mam sãn bɔɔra tɩ m wẽ yõ'ogɔ, mam kãn dɛna yalma, se'ere n soe la, mam wʋn tɔgɛ la sɩra. La mam kãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, mam ka boorɩ tɩ ba tẽ'esɛ mam yelle gãŋɛ ban yẽ mam n ɩtɩ se'em, bɩɩ ban wõnnɩ mam n yele se'em na.

ref: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
text: La, lan wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ mam da tã'agɛ zẽkɛ m mẽŋa la bõn-yãlma tɩ Nawẽnnɛ puke pa'alɛ mam na, a basɛ tɩ Asũtãana ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn wʋ gõ'a mam ĩyã pʋam nãmsra mam tɩ mam ka tãna wʋn zẽkɛ m mẽŋa.

ref: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
text: Mam sose Zuudãana la nõorɛ bʋtã yetɩ a botɩ bõn-ẽŋa fõrgɛ dee basɛ mam.

ref: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
text: La a lerge mam yetɩ: «Mam yel-sõnnɛ seke fʋ mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, la de la tɔrgɔ pʋam tɩ mam pãŋa tõnnɩ zozo'e.» Bɛla, mam wʋn wẽ yõ'ogɔ m tɔrgɔ la pʋam, tɩ Krisi pãŋa la wʋn bɔna la mam lɛ.

ref: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam ɩta sũ-yẽlga m tɔrgɔ la pʋam, la tʋʋrɛ pʋam, la tooro pʋam, la nãmsgɔ pʋam, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ pʋam, mam n sakɛ Krisi la ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, mam tɔrgɔ la pʋam tɩ mam tarɩ pãŋa. Apolɩ fablɩ Korẽntɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba yelle

ref: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
text: Yãma n mõrgɛ mam tɩ mam tɔgra wʋ yalma la. La de la yãma n de nɩ ya tɔgɛ po'oge mam, pa'alɛ mam n ãn se'em. Baa mam sãn ka dɛna sɛla, nẽr-sɛba n pa'alɩ tɩ bãma n de Tõntõnɩba n gãnnɩ la, mam ka de poorʋm dãana ba zẽ'am.

ref: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
text: Yel-sɛba wuu tɩ mam daan wĩbri tõnna ya zẽ'am na pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ mam de la Krisi Tõntõnna. Bõn-yãlma, la yel-kɩrsɩ n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa daan ẽŋɛ mɛ.

ref: You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
text: Bẽm yel-sõnnɛ tɩ mam ẽŋɛ bɔ Azezi nẽrba zẽ'esɩ basɛba, tɩ la pɔ'ɔgɛ yãma? Sãn dagna mam n ka to'oge sõŋrɛ ya zẽ'am na mã'a. Bɔ-ya mam sugri yel-ẽŋa pʋam.

ref: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
text: La sãn dɛna mam, mam wʋn dɩkɛ sɛla wuu tɩ mam tarɩ, la m mẽŋa wuu bɔ la yɛmleego yãma sõŋrɛ ĩyã. Mam n nõŋɛ yãma zo'oge la ĩyã n basɛ tɩ yãma ka nõŋɛ mam bɩɩ?

ref: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
text: Ya mi mɛ tɩ mam ka to'oge sõŋrɛ ya zẽ'am, la ya nẽrba basɛba yetɩ mam ẽŋɛ la yɛmkẽgsɩga pã'asɛ ya.

ref: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
text: Mam daan sose la Atiti tɩ a wa'am ya zẽ'am, dee tõm tɩ sɔbia la, tɩ a doose ẽ. Bɛla, Atiti pã'asɛ yãma to'oge sɛla mɛ bɩɩ? Mam la Atiti tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla, la tõma doose la sore ayɩla.

ref: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
text: Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ tõma n tɔgrɩ bɛla wuu la, tõma eerɩ la bʋʋrɔ yãma zẽ'am bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ. Tõma tɔgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, Krisi doosgo pʋam. Tɩ sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, tõma tɔgrɩ bɛla wuu tɩ la sõŋɛ la yãma, tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam.

ref: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
text: Mam zotɩ mɛ tɩ m wʋn wa paam ya zẽ'am yẽ tɩ ya ka ãn wʋ mam n boorɩ se'em na, tɩ yãma mẽ yẽ tɩ mam ka ãn wʋ yãma n boorɩ se'em na. Mam zotɩ mɛ tɩ zɛbrɛ, la ɩta sũure la taaba, la sũ-isgre, la gẽelgɔ, la pɔ'ɔg-taaba, la ya'anzũm, la zẽk-m-mẽŋa, la ɩr-yooro wʋn bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka.

ref: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
text: Mam zotɩ mɛ tɩ mam sãn wa wa'am yãma zẽ'am, mam dãana Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ mam yãma ĩyã, tɩ m sũure sã'am la ya nẽr-sɛba n daan tõm tõon-be'ero, dee ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la yalsɩ, la tõon-yooro sɛba tɩ ba ẽŋɛ la.

ref: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
text: Nõorɛ bʋtã dãana n wãna tɩ mam sẽm ya zẽ'am. La mam wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n yele se'em na yetɩ: «Yɛla wuu malgrɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrba bayi bɩɩ batã dɛna kaset-dõma.»

ref: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
text: Mam n daan wa'am ya zẽ'am nõorɛ bʋyi dãana la, mam daan kã'an sɛba n tõm tõon-be'ero, la nẽrba basɛba la wuu mɛ. La lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ mam ka boe ya zẽ'am na, mam le yɛta ya mɛ tɩ mam sãn wa'am, mam kãn zoe nẽra ninbãalga.

ref: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
text: Yãma boorɩ tɩ ya yẽ la sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ Krisi n botɩ mam tɔgra, ya wʋn yẽ. Krisi sãn ɩta sɛla ya zẽ'am, tɔrgɔ ka tarɩ ẽ, a tarɩ pãŋa zo'oge mɛ ya zẽ'am.

ref: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
text: Asɩra, a ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa la tarma, tɩ ba ka ẽ dɔ-puurŋa zuo. La a vɔa mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa. Tõma n de ayɩla la Krisi la, tõma mẽ de la tarɩmdõma, la tõma vɔa la Krisi lɛ, wʋn tõnna yãma zẽ'am la Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa.

ref: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
text: Vẽesɛ-ya ya mẽŋa bɩsɛ ya sãn bɔna Krisi sore la zuo. Ya mẽŋa geele bɩsɛ, yãma ka mi tɩ Krisi boe la yãma mɛ? Sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, ya koŋe mɛ.

ref: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
text: La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ yãma mi mɛ tɩ tõma mõrgɛ mɛ ka koŋe.

ref: We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
text: Tõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ ya ĩyã tɩ ya da ẽŋɛ sɛla n de be'em. La dagɩ tɩ tõma boorɩ tɩ tɩ pa'alɛ tɩ tõma yãŋɛ mɛ, la de la tɩ yãma wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa, baa la tõma sãn ãna wʋ tõma koŋe mɛ.

ref: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
text: Se'ere n soe la, tõma ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ sɛla n sɩ'ɩsrɩ sɩra, tõma dolɩ la sɩra.

ref: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
text: Tõma sãn bɔna tɔrgɔ pʋam dee tɩ yãma tara pãŋa, tõma tarɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ. La tõma n sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɛla tɩ pʋ'ʋsgɔ pʋam, de la ya vɔm wuu wʋn masɛ ãna sõŋa.

ref: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam gʋlsra ya bõn-bãma, mam n ka boe ya zẽ'am na, tɩ m sãn wa wa'am ya zẽ'am, m da dɩkɛ Zuudãana n bɔ mam nõor-sɛka la sɩbgɛ ya. A bɔ mam nõorɛ la tɩ m sõŋɛ ya mɛ tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam, la dagɩ tɩ m sã'am ya.

ref: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
text: Sɛla n deege, m sɔbiisi, tara-ya sũ-yẽlga, mõrgɛ-ya tõm masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, bɔ'ɔra-ya taaba bʋraanɛ, tara-ya nõorɛ ayɩla la taaba, vɔa-ya la sũ-mã'asʋm la taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n de nõŋlʋm, la sũ-mã'asʋm dãana la wʋn bɔna la ya lɛ.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm. Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will bless you and be kind to you! May God bless you with his love, and may the Holy Spirit join all your hearts together.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm, la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa lagɩmtaarɛ wʋn bɔna la ya wuu.

ref: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm.

ref: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
text: Azezi-krisi n dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ tɩ ba kʋ ẽ tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, tɩ a fãagɛ tɔ dũnia wã be'em pʋam basɛ, wʋ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na.

ref: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
text: Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina. Azezi kõ-yẽlga la de la ayɩla mã'a

ref: I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message,
text: Mam ze la yɛrɩ la yãma n darɛ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya Krisi yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã, dee to'oge kõa ayẽma wʋ kõ-yẽlga la.

ref: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
text: Kõ-yẽlga ayẽma ka boe. La de la nẽrba n bʋrsrɩ ya pʋtẽ'era bɔɔra tɩ ba teege Krisi kõ-yẽlga la.

ref: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
text: Nẽra sãn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga ayẽma bɔ ya, tɩ la dagna sɛka tɩ tõma mõolɛ bɔ ya la, baa la sãn dɛna tõma mẽŋa, bɩɩ malɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire, Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ego bɔna a dãana zuo.

ref: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
text: Tõma põn yele ya mɛ, la mam le yɛta ya mɛ, tɩ nẽra sãn mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga ayẽma n de to'ore la sɛka tɩ ya to'oge la, Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ego bɔna a dãana zuo.

ref: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
text: Bɛla, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ nẽrba n pẽgɛ mam bɩɩ, bɩɩ Nawẽnnɛ n pẽgɛ ma? Yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ mam boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ tɩ la paagɛ la nẽrsaalba yɛm bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, mam sãn boorɩ nɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ tɩ la paagɛ nẽrsaalba yɛm, mam ka de nɩ Krisi tõntõnna. Apolɩ n lebge Azezi tõntõnna se'em

ref: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ tɩ ya bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ kõ-yẽlga la tɩ mam mõolɩ la ka ze'ele nẽrsaalba zẽ'am.

ref: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
text: La mam mẽ ka to'oge ẽ nẽra zẽ'am, la mẽ dagɩ nẽra n zãsʋm mam. La de la Azezi-krisi mẽŋa n puke ẽ pa'alɛ mam.

ref: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
text: Ya wõm mam daan ãn se'em Zifdõma sore la pʋam, daan nãmsra Azezi nẽrba la zozo'e, mõora tɩ m sã'am ba sore la.

ref: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
text: Tõma Zifdõma sore la pʋam, mam daan sẽŋɛ nẽŋa mɛ wɩsgɛ m mẽŋa la tɩ yaabdõma dɔgmike la yelle, ɩta lan yele se'em na gãnna m zuutaaba zozo'e.

ref: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
text: Ẽŋa n daan wa bɩsɛ tɩ la ãn sõŋa, tɩ a puke a Dayʋa la pa'alɛ mam tɩ m mõolɛ a Dayʋa la kõ-yẽlga bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ la, mam daan ka sẽŋɛ nẽr-sɛka zẽ'am tɩ a zãsʋm mam.

ref: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
