text: La mam mẽ daan ka sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm sɛba n dẽŋɛ mam dɛna Azezi Tõntõnɩba la zẽ'am. Mam daan sẽŋɛ la Araabɩ so'olʋm, bɛla poorʋm tɩ mam lebe Damaasɩ.

ref: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
text: Bɛla poorʋm yʋʋma atã n tole, tɩ mam yãŋa sẽŋɛ Zerizalɛm tɩ m ka bãŋɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ, daan ẽŋɛ dabsa pia la anuu a zẽ'am.

ref: The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother.
text: La mam ka yẽ Azezi Tõntõnna ayẽma pa'asɛ, sãn dagna Azakɩ n de Zuudãana yɩbga la mã'a.

ref: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
text: Mam n gʋlsɛ ya sɛla wã, mam yetɩ ya Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tɩ la dagɩ põmpɔrŋɔ.

ref: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
text: Bɛla poorʋm mam ze'ele Zerizalɛm sẽŋɛ la Siiri, la Silisi so'olʋm.

ref: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
text: La Azezi-krisi nẽr-sɛba n boe Zude tẽnsɩ la daan nãn ka yẽ mam.

ref: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
text: Ba daan wen wõm mɛ tɩ ba tɔgrɩ mam yelle yetɩ: «Sɛka n daan nãmsrɩ tõma mõora tɩ a sã'am kõ-yẽlga tɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra la, yãŋa mõolɩ la kõ-yẽlga la.»

ref: And because of me, they praised God.
text: Tɩ ba nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ mam ĩyã.

ref: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
text: Bɛla poorʋm yʋʋma pia la anaasɩ n tole, tɩ mam la Abarnabasɩ lebe Zerizalɛm, la mam tarɩ la Atiti lagɩm mĩ.

ref: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n daan puke pa'alɛ mam, tɩ mam sẽŋɛ. Mam daan pa'alɛ ba la kõ-yẽlga tɩ mam mõolɛ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ la. La de la Azezi nẽrba nẽŋadõma la mã'a tɩ mam tɔgɛ bɔ ba. Mam daan ka boorɩ tɩ tõon-sɛka tɩ mam daan tõm, dee ken tõnna wã, wa dɛna zãŋa.

ref: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
text: La Atiti n de Grɛkɩ n dolɩ la mam na, ba põn ka pẽrgɛ yetɩ ba wã ẽ.

ref: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
text: Nẽrba basɛba n daan pã'asɛ tɩ bãma de la Azezi karẽnbiisi, li'ise kẽ tõma tẽŋasʋka bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ tõma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, tõma n to'oge tɩ mẽŋa se'em, bãma daan boorɩ tɩ ba basɛ tɩ tõma le dɛna la Zifdõma lɔɔ yamsɩ.

ref: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
text: Tõma daan ka sakɛ ban boorɩ se'em na baa fẽfẽe, tɩ kõ-yẽlga n de sɩra la wʋn ken bɔna yãma ĩyã.

ref: Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message.
text: Nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba bɩsrɩ wʋ nẽŋadõma la, ba sãn dɛna nẽŋadõma bɩɩ ba dagɩ nẽŋadõma, la ka pakɛ mam, Nawẽnnɛ ka tũ'usri nẽrba. Nẽŋadõma bãma ka mõrgɛ mam tɩ m ẽŋɛ sɛla sɛla pa'asɛ mam zãsŋɔ la pʋam.

ref: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
text: Ba tõn yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ kõ-yẽlga la ẽŋɛ la mam nu'usum tɩ m mõolɛ bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ, wʋ ẽn dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ nu'usum tɩ a mõolɛ bɔ Zifdõma la.

ref: God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ Apɩyɛɛrɩ pãŋa, tõm ẽ Zifdõma zẽ'am na, ẽŋa n ken bɔ mam pãŋa tɩ mam tõnna bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am.

ref: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
text: La Azakɩ, la Apɩyɛɛrɩ, la Azã tɩ ba bɩsrɩ wʋ ba wuu kɛ'ɛndõma la, n bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ mam yel-sõnnɛ dɩkɛ tõonɛ na ẽŋɛ mam nu'usum na, ba yõgɛ mam, la Abarnabasɩ nu'usi mɛ, pa'alɛ tɩ tɩ de la ayɩla lagɩm tõnna Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, tɩ tõma tõnna bu-zãŋsɩ zẽ'am, tɩ bãma mẽ tõnna Zifdõma zẽ'am.

ref: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
text: Sɛla tɩ ba daan sose tõma de la tɩ sõŋra ba Wẽndeego la nasdõma mã'a. La de la bɛla tɩ mam daan ɩtɩ zozo'e. Apolɩ pa'alɛ Apɩyɛɛrɩ a tuure

ref: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
text: Apɩyɛɛrɩ n daan wa'am Antɩyɔsɩ la, mam daan tɔgɛ ẽ mɛ vẽelga vẽelga ka sakɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, a daan ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ka masɛ.

ref: He used to eat with Gentile followers of the Lord, until James sent some Jewish followers. Peter was afraid of the Jews and soon stopped eating with Gentiles.
text: Ẽn daan boe Antɩyɔsɩ la, a daan lagnɩ na bu-zãŋsɩ la dita mɛ. La nẽrba n daan ze'ele Azakɩ zẽ'am wa'am, tɩ a welge ka le dita la bu-zãŋsɩ la, se'ere n soe la, a daan zotɩ la sɛba n yetɩ dẽnɩ tɩ nẽr-sɛba wuu n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore wãta la.

ref: He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled.
text: Tɩ tɩ sɔbiisi basɛba n de Zifdõma daan po ɩta wʋ Apɩyɛɛrɩ n ɩtɩ se'em na, halɩ tɩ Abarnabasɩ mẽ po kẽ ba pʋlibsum ɩrgɔ la pʋam.

ref: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
text: La mam n daan yẽ tɩ ban ɩtɩ se'em na ka masɛ wʋ kõ-yẽlga la sɩra n de se'em na ĩyã, mam daan yele Apɩyɛɛrɩ ba wuu nẽŋam yetɩ: «Fõn de Zifu dee daan vɔa wʋ bu-zãŋsɩ n vɔa se'em na, fõn wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãne mõrgra bu-zãŋsɩ la tɩ ba vɔa wʋ Zifdõma?»

ref: We are Jews by birth and are not sinners like Gentiles.
text: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma dɔgɛ dɛna la Zifdõma, tõma dagɩ bu-zãŋsɩ n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la.

ref: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
text: Tõma n te'ege Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a basɛ tɩ tɩ masɛ a zẽ'am tõma n bɔ Krisi sɩra ĩyã la, bõn-ẽŋa sãn basɛ tɩ tõma mẽ dɛna tõon-be'ero dõma wʋ bu-zãŋsɩ la, yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ Krisi botɩ tɩ tõnna la tõon-be'ero bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ.

ref: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
text: Nẽra sãn basɛ Zifdõma lɔɔ tɩ a daan dolɩ la, dee le yetɩ ba dɔla ẽ, la de la a mẽŋa n sã'anɩ lɔɔ la.

ref: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
text: La sãn dɛna lɔɔ la, a botɩ mam lebge la kũm a ĩyã, mam ka le dɔla ẽ. Mam lagɩm na Krisi ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo, tɩ m vɔa Nawẽnnɛ ĩyã,

ref: I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me.
text: tɩ mam n vɔa wã, la ka le dɛna mam mẽŋa n vɔɩ, la de la Krisi n botɩ mam vɔa. La vɔm sɛka tɩ mam vɔa lɛɛlɛ wã, de la mam n bɔ ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa n nõŋɛ mam ki mam yelle sɩra la, n basɛ tɩ mam vɔa.

ref: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
text: Mam ka sakɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ na de la zãŋa. La sãn dɛna la nẽra n dolɩ lɔɔ ĩyã n basɛ tɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, Krisi kũm na de nɩ na zãŋa.

ref: You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you?
text: Yãma Galatɩ dõma, ãne n botɩ yãma zuto lebge tɩ ya ka tara yɛm? Mam daan pa'alɛ ya Krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la yelle mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, tɩ la dɛna vẽelga bɔ ya.

ref: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
text: Mam boorɩ tɩ m soke ya la sokre ayɩla mã'a tɩ ya lerge mam: Yãma n to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la, la de la yãma n sakrɩ ɩta lɔɔ la n yele se'em na ĩyã bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la de la yãma n wõm kõ-yẽlga la, bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
text: Yãma yɛm na pɔ'ɔgɛ paagɛ la wãna? Yãma n pɔsɛ lebge Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la a Sɩa pãŋa la, yãma yãŋa boorɩ tɩ ya ba'asɛ la ya mẽŋa pãŋa?

ref: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
text: Yãma n yẽ se'em wuu la lebge la zãŋa bɩɩ? Dẽnɩ tɩ la da kãn lebge zãŋa.

ref: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
text: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ya a Sɩa, dee ɩta bõn-yãlma ya tẽŋasʋka la, la dagɩ yãma n ɩtɩ lɔɔ n yele se'em na ĩyã, la de la yãma n wõm kõ-yẽlga la, bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
text: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la n sɩrɩ de Abraham yɩɩsɩ.

ref: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
text: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la põn yele mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tɩ bu-zãŋsɩ masɛ a zẽ'am, ban bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn põn tɔgɛ kõ-yẽlga ẽŋa bɔ Abraham mɛ yetɩ: «Fõn ĩyã tɩ dũnia buuri wuu wʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ.»

ref: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
text: La de la bɛla, Abraham n yʋʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra yẽ yel-sõnnɛ na, nẽr-sɛba n mẽ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra wʋn lagɩm na Abraham yẽ yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.”
text: Nẽr-sɛba n te'ege lɔɔ la, kã'a-be'ero boe ba zuto, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Kã'a-be'ego boe nẽr-sɛka woo n ka ɩtɩ wʋ lan gʋlsɛ se'em wuu Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ gõŋɔ pʋam na zuugo.»

ref: No one can please God by obeying the Law. The Scriptures also say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.”
text: La de la vẽelga tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla ka tã wʋn yẽ bʋʋrɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am ẽn dolɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nẽra n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra masɛ a zẽ'am wʋn vɔa, ẽn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.»

ref: The Law isn't based on faith. It promises life only to people who obey its commands.
text: Nẽra n dolɩ lɔɔ la, la dagɩ tɩ a bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, bẽm ĩyã, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, a wʋn vɔa bɛla ĩyã.»

ref: But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse.
text: La tõma n ka ẽŋɛ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na wuu ĩyã, Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ego n boe tõma zuto. La Krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la, a dɔkɛ kã'a-be'ego la tõma zuto mɛ, tɩ ẽŋa mẽŋa sɔɔgɛ tõma zẽ'am, tɩ kã'a-be'ego la bɔna a zuugo, se'ere n soe la, la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Kã'a-be'ego boe nẽr-sɛka tɩ ba yulse ẽ dɔɔgɔ zuo la zuugo.»

ref: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
text: Krisi ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ Abraham na, mẽ wʋn dɛna bu-zãŋsɩ bõnɔ, bãma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã, dee tɩ tõma mẽ to'oge Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ tɔ, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã. Lɔɔ la ka sã'am nõor-bĩŋre

ref: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam yetɩ m dɩkɛ la nẽrsaala yelle makɛ pa'alɛ ya. Nẽra sãn looge yɛm dee gʋlsɛ bĩŋe, tɩ la doose la tẽŋa la lɔɔ, nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn sã'am ẽ, bɩɩ pa'asɛ sɛla mĩ.

ref: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ la Abraham, la a yɩɩŋa. La ka gʋlsɛ yetɩ, a bĩŋe a nõorɛ bɔ la a yɩɩsɩ, wʋ la de la nẽrba zozo'e. La yetɩ, a yɩɩŋa, la de la nẽra ayɩla mã'a. Nẽr-ẽŋa de la Krisi.

ref: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
text: Se'ere n soe la, la sãn de nɩ na lɔɔ la dɔlga ĩyã tɩ nẽra wʋn to'oge sɛla sɔna, la ka le dɛna nõor-bĩŋre ĩyã. La, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ la ĩyã, tɩ a ẽŋɛ Abraham yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
text: La sãn dɛna bɛla, bẽm ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bɔ lɔɔ la? A yʋʋn bɔ ẽ tɩ a botɩ nẽrba n bãŋɛ ba tõon-be'ero, halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Abraham yɩɩŋa la wʋn wa wa'am, sɛka ĩyã tɩ a yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ la. Malɛkadõma n yʋʋn to'oge lɔɔ la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am bɔ la, la doose la nẽra ayɩla n boe Nawẽnnɛ, la nẽrba tẽŋasʋka wẽ'era nu'ugo.

ref: There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him. Slaves and Children
text: Nẽra ka wẽ'erɩ nu'ugo la nẽra ayɩla mã'a. La Nawẽnnɛ de la a ayɩla mã'a, dee yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ, tɩ nu'u-wẽ'era ka bɔna.

ref: Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law.
text: Bɛla, lɔɔ la sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõor-bĩŋre la mɛ bɩɩ? Ayɛɩ, la dagɩ bɛla baa fẽfẽe. Se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ sãn boe nɩ n tã wʋn botɩ nẽrba tara vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, bɛla a ken tã nɩ wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am mɛ, ban sakrɩ lɔɔ la ĩyã.

ref: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yetɩ, dũnia dõma wuu boe la tõon-be'ero nu'usum. La de la bɛla tɩ sɛba n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ba, ban bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
text: Bɛla lɔɔ la n yʋʋn de tõma gu'ura pa'ala tõma sore halɩ wa paam wakat-sɛka tɩ Krisi wa'am na, tɩ tõma wʋn dɛna nẽrba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ Azezi-krisi wa'am tɩ tɩ bɔ ẽ sɩra la, lɔɔ la ka le dɛna tõma gu'ura.

ref: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Yãma n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la, ya wuu de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
text: Yãma wuu tɩ ba mise ya ko'om pʋam na, ya lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla, ãna wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na.

ref: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
text: Yãma n lagɩm na Krisi la, bɔkrɛ ka le bɔna tɩ fʋ de la Zifu, bɩɩ bu-zãŋka, yamŋa bɩɩ nẽra n soe a mẽŋa, pɔka bɩɩ bʋraaga, ya wuu de la ayɩla.

ref: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
text: Yãma n lagɩm na Krisi la, ya de la Abraham yɩɩsɩ, wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ la.

ref: Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs.
text: Mam n yetɩ m yele se'em n wãna: Bia n yetɩ a wa sɔɔgɛ sɔna a sɔ bõnɔ dee ken dɛna bõn-pɩka, a ãn wʋ a de la yamŋa la, baa la ẽŋa n wʋn wa sɔna sɛla woo la.

ref: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
text: Ẽn nãn de bia la, a boe la nẽrba nu'usum tɩ ba gu'ura ẽ, dee bɩsra a sɛla woo yelle bɔ'ɔra ẽ halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ a sɔ looge la, tɩ a yãŋa sɔna sɛla woo.

ref: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
text: Tõma n mẽ daan ka bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, ãna wʋ kɔma la, tõma daan de la dũnia wã yɛla yamsɩ.

ref: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
text: La wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la n paagɛ la, a tõm na a mẽŋa Dayʋa, tɩ pɔka dɔgɛ ẽ, tɩ a dɔla Zifdõma lɔɔ la,

ref: so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children.
text: tɩ a fãagɛ sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la, tɩ tɩ wʋn lebge Nawẽnnɛ kɔma.

ref: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
text: La yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma la, a tõm na a Dayʋa Sɩa tɩ a bɔna tɩ sũure pʋam, tɩ Sɩa la wi'ira Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Abba, m Sɔ.»

ref: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
text: Bɛla tɩ ka le dɛna yamsɩ, tɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma. Tõma n de a kɔma la ĩyã, a wʋn bɔ tɔ sɛla wuu tɩ a bĩŋe tɩ a bɔ a kɔma la. Apolɩ yɛm yɔɔrɩ Galatɩ dõma yelle

ref: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
text: Kʋrʋm, yan daan ka mi Nawẽnnɛ na, ya daan dolɩ la dũnia wã bõn-yooro n dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ poore, dɛna ba yamsɩ.

ref: But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers?
text: La nãnannɛ wã tɩ ya mi Nawẽnnɛ na, la, lan gãnnɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n mi ya la, la ẽŋɛ la wãne tɩ yãma le dɔla bõn-yooro n ka tarɩ pãŋa dee ka tara võorɛ poore, le bɔɔra tɩ ya dɛna ba yamsɩ?

ref: You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years.
text: Yãma looge la dabsa, la wõrsɩ, la wakatɛ basɛba yʋʋnɛ pʋam, la yʋʋma dɔla tɩ la dɛna sisgo.

ref: I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
text: Yãma n ɩtɩ se'em na botɩ dabeem n tara mam, tɩ mam n tõm se'em yãma ĩyã la, tã wʋn dɛna zãŋa.

ref: My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me
text: M sɔbiisi, mam belnɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya ãna wʋ mam na, tɩ mam lebge ãna wʋ yãma la. Yãma daan ka ẽŋɛ mam tuuge.

ref: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
text: Ya mi tɩ la daan de la mam bã'aga la ĩyã tɩ mam yẽ folle mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la bɔ ya pɔspɔsɩ.

ref: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
text: Mam bã'aga la daan de la nãmsgɔ bɔ ya, la yãma daan ka nẽ'esɛ mam, la ya mẽ daan ka zagsɛ mam, ya daan to'oge mam wʋ mam de la Nawẽnnɛ malɛka, bɩɩ Azezi-krisi la.

ref: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
text: Yãma sũure daan ãn yẽlʋm mɛ, tɩ mam tãna wʋn yetɩ, yãma daan sãn tã nɩ, ya wʋn dɩkɛ ya nini bɔ mam. Bẽm ẽŋɛ tɩ yãma ka le tara sũ-yẽlga?

ref: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
text: Mam n tɔgɛ ya sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ mam yãŋa lebge ya bɛ bɩɩ?

ref: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
text: Nẽr-sɛba tɩ mam yetɩ la, ban mõrgrɩ se'em yãma ĩyã la, la dagɩ ya sõŋrɛ ĩyã. Ba boorɩ tɩ ba welge ya la mam, tɩ ya lagɩm na bãma.

ref: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
text: Nẽra sãn mõrgra tɩ la sãn dɛna sõŋa ĩyã, la ãn sõŋa. La ãn sõŋa tɩ ya ɩta bɛla wakatɛ wuu, la, la da dɛna mam n boe ya zẽ'am wakatɛ sɛka la mã'a.

ref: My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you.
text: M kɔm-nõŋrɩsɩ, mam le tara la toogo ya ĩyã wʋ pɔka n virgri la, halɩ tɩ ya wʋn wa ãna wʋ Krisi n ãn se'em na.

ref: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
text: Mam boorɩ nɩ na m bɔna ya zẽ'am lɛɛlɛ wã bãŋɛ mam wʋn tɔgɛ se'em pa'alɛ ya. La mam n boe zẽ'e-sɛka wã, mam ka mi mam wʋn yele ya se'em. Asaara la Agaarɩ makrɛ

ref: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
text: Yele-ya ma, yãma sɛba n boorɩ tɩ lɔɔ la sɔna ya la, yãma ka wõnnɩ lɔɔ la n yetɩ se'em na?

ref: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ, Abraham tarɩ la dayɔɔsɩ bayi. Ayɩla de la yam-pɔka bia, tɩ ayẽma dɛna a pɔga bia.

ref: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
text: Yam-pɔka bia la dɔgɛ wʋ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em na. La a pɔga bia la dɔgɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

ref: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
text: Bõn-ẽna de la makrɛ bɔ tõma. Pɔgsɩ bayi la makrɩ la yɛla bayi. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nẽrba a lɔɔ, dee bĩŋe a nõorɛ la. Pɔspɔsɩ pɔka la yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Agaarɩ la mãmsrɩ la lɔɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ Sinayi tãŋa zuo la. Nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ lɔɔ la de la yamsɩ, wʋ Agaarɩ la a kɔma n de yamsɩ se'em na.

ref: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
text: Agaarɩ ãn wʋ Sinayi tãŋa n boe Araabɩ tẽŋa la, dee ken ãna wʋ Zerizalɛm sɛka n boe lɛɛlɛ wã la, se'ere n soe la, Zerizalɛm la a nẽrba la boe la lɔɔ yamnɛ pʋam.

ref: But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave.
text: La Zerizalɛm sɛka n boe saazuum na ka boe yamnɛ pʋam, tõma de la tẽn-ẽŋa nẽrba, wʋ Asaara kɔma n dagɩ yamsɩ la.

ref: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, kõndɔgrɛ, fõn ka dɔgɛ la. Ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga wa'ara kĩnkĩma sɩrna, fõn ka virge la. Se'ere n soe la, pɔg-sɛka tɩ a sɩra basɛ la kɔma n wʋn zo'oge gãnna pɔg-sɛka n tarɩ sɩra la.»

ref: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
text: La, la mẽ ãn wʋ bi-sɛka tɩ ba daan dɔgɛ ẽ wʋ nẽrsaala n boorɩ se'em na n nãmsɛ bi-sɛka tɩ ba dɔgɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em na, la ken dɛna la bɛla mẽ lɛɛlɛ wã.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
text: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yetɩ bẽm? A yetɩ: «Dige yam-pɔka, la a bia la, se'ere n soe la, yam-pɔka la bia kãn lagɩm na pɔg-sɛka n dagɩ yamŋa la bia po to'oge ba sɔ bõnɔ.»

ref: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
text: Bɛla, m sɔbiisi, tõma dagɩ yam-pɔka kɔma, tõma de la pɔg-sɛka n dagɩ yamŋa la kɔma.

ref: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
text: Krisi fãagɛ tɩ basɛ tɩ tɩ sɔna la tɩ mẽŋa. Bɛla, zɛ-ya kãŋkãŋɛ, da sakɛ-ya le kẽ'esɛ ya mẽŋa yamnɛ pʋam.

ref: I, Paul, promise you that Christ won't do you any good if you get circumcised.
text: Selse-ya, mam Apolɩ yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya sãn basɛ tɩ ba wã ya, Krisi ka le tara yõorɔ bɔ ya.

ref: And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Yãma sɛba n te'ege lɔɔ la ɛɛra tɩ ya masɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam na, ya welge ya mẽŋa mɛ la Krisi, ya koŋe Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

ref: But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ.
text: La sãn dɛna tõma, la de la tõma n bɔ Krisi sɩra la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa basɛ tɩ tõma tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ a wa basɛ tɩ tɩ masɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
text: Azezi-krisi doosgo la pʋam, fʋ sãn wã, bɩɩ fʋ sãn ka wã, la ka tarɩ võorɛ, sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ de la fʋ bɔ Azezi sɩra tõnna la nõŋlʋm.

ref: You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth.
text: Yãma daan dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore mɛ sõŋa sõŋa, ãne n gu ya tɩ ya basɛ da le sakɛ sɩra la?

ref: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
text: Tẽ'esgɔ bãma n botɩ ya basɛ sɩra la ka ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya la zẽ'am.

ref: A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough,
text: Ba makɛ yetɩ: «Dãbɩllɛ fẽe n botɩ borborɩ wuu ukra.»

ref: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
text: La sãn dɛna mam, Zuudãana la ĩyã mam bɔ ya sɩra mɛ tɩ yãma tẽ'esgɔ kãn bɔna ayẽma la mam n pa'alɛ ya se'em na. Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ ya pʋtẽ'era yuulna la, baa ẽn de nẽr-sɛka, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn sɩbgɛ ẽ.

ref: My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised.
text: M sɔbiisi, la sãn dɛna sɩra tɩ mam mẽŋa ken mõola mɛ yɛta tɩ ba wãta, bẽm tɩ Zifdõma nãmsra mam? La sãn de nɩ na bɛla, mam n mõolɩ Krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo tɩ ba nãmsra mam na kãn le bɔna.

ref: I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
text: Mam boorɩ nɩ mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ ya pʋtẽ'era yuulna tɩ ya wãta la, sãn põn bɔɔra, ba fɔ ba mẽŋa pa'asɛ.

ref: My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love.
text: La sãn dɛna yãma, m sɔbiisi, Nawẽnnɛ wi ya tɩ ya sɔna la ya mẽŋa. La da basɛ-ya tɩ yan soe ya mẽŋa la basɛ tɩ ya ɩta ya mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em. Vɔa-ya la taaba la nõŋlʋm tõnna sõŋra taaba.

ref: All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself.
text: Se'ere n soe la, lɔɔ la wuu boe la yetɔg-ẽna ayɩla pʋam n yetɩ: «Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa.»

ref: But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves. God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
text: La ya sãn zɛbra la taaba, yĩ'ira dee dõnna taaba wʋ dũnsi la, guuse-ya, tɩ ya wʋn sã'am taaba mɛ ba'asɛ. Vɔa-ya wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em

ref: If you are guided by the Spirit, you won't obey your selfish desires.
text: Selse-ya, mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya dɔla Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em, tɩ ya kãn ɩta nẽrsaalba yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em.

ref: But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sãn sɔna ya, ya ka le bɔna lɔɔ la nu'usum.

ref: People's desires make them give in to immoral ways, filthy thoughts, and shameful deeds.
text: Nẽrsaala mẽŋa yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em na de la vẽelga vẽelga. La de la yalsɩ, la tõon-dẽgrɔ, la tõon-yooro,

ref: They worship idols, practice witchcraft, hate others, and are hard to get along with. People become jealous, angry, and selfish. They not only argue and cause trouble, but they are
text: la baga kãabgɔ, la tɩɩm tõoma, la taaba sisga, la zɛbrɛ, la ɩta sũure la tadãana bõnɔ, la sũ-isgre, la gãŋɛ taaba, la ka-wõm-taaba, la welge taaba.

ref: envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
text: La nõŋɛ bõnɔ, la dãam buka, la pʋyã'an-yooro, la bõnɔ basɛba n wõn na bõn-bãma. Mam dẽŋɛ kã'ana ya mɛ, wʋ mam n daan põn yele ya se'em na, tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ bõn-bãma kãn yẽ zẽ'am Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm pʋam.

ref: God's Spirit makes us loving, happy, peaceful, patient, kind, good, faithful,
text: La Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa tõoma de la nõŋlʋm, la sũ-yẽlga, la sũ-mã'asʋm, la sũ-mã'arɛ, la sõŋa, la ɩr-sõŋɔ, la ẽŋɛ sɩra,

ref: gentle, and self-controlled. There is no law against behaving in any of these ways.
text: la sũ-bʋgsʋm, la yõk-m-mẽŋa. Lɔɔ ka boe n sɩ'ɩsrɩ bõn-bãma.

ref: And because we belong to Christ Jesus, we have killed our selfish feelings and desires.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n de Azezi-krisi nẽrba la ka ba mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em n de be'em, la ba yɛmleego la mɛ dɔ-puurŋa zuo, tɩ ba ka le sɔna ba.

ref: God's Spirit has given us life, and so we should follow the Spirit.
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ tɩ vɔm na, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ dɔla ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em na.

ref: But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
text: Tɩ da dɛna-ya uk-m-mẽŋa dõma, tɩ da ɛɛra-ya taaba nõorɛ, bɩɩ ɩta sũure la taaba.

ref: My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself.
text: M sɔbiisi, ya sãn yẽ nẽra tɩ a tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, yãma sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boe la ya lɛ la, pa'alɛ-ya ẽ la sũ-bʋgsʋm tarɩ ẽ leme so-sõŋɔ la zuo. La guuse fʋ mẽŋa, tɩ fʋ mẽ da po tõm a tõon-be'ero la.

ref: You obey the law of Christ when you offer each other a helping hand.
text: Sõŋra-ya taaba malgra taaba yɛla, tɩ bɛla tɩ ya wʋn ẽŋɛ Krisi nõorɛ n yele se'em na.

ref: If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong.
text: Nẽra sãn tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa de la sɛla dee ka dɛna sɛla sɛla, a pã'asrɩ la a mẽŋa.

ref: Do your own work well, and then you will have something to be proud of. But don't compare yourself with others.
text: Nẽra woo geele bɩsɛ a mẽŋa tõoma n ãn se'em, a sãn yẽ a tõoma la pʋam sɛla n de a ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, tɩ a ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa ĩyã. La a da magra a mẽŋa la nẽrba basɛba.

ref: We each must carry our own load.
text: Nẽra woo n wʋn tara a mẽŋa zuugo yelle.

ref: Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
text: Nẽr-sɛka tɩ nẽra zãsnɩ ẽ Krisi yetɔga la, a basɛ tɩ sɛka n zãsnɩ ẽ na yẽta pʋrɛ ẽn tarɩ sɛla woo la pʋam.

ref: You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant.
text: Da pã'asɛ-ya ya mẽŋa, ba ka pɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ. Nẽra n bʋrɛ bõn-sɛba la, bõn-bãma tɩ a wʋn lagsɛ.

ref: If you follow your selfish desires, you will harvest destruction, but if you follow the Spirit, you will harvest eternal life.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ a mẽŋa yɛmleego boorɩ, a wʋn yẽ a yɛmleego yõorɔ n de kũm. La sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa boorɩ la, a wʋn yẽ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ sɛla n de vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up.
text: Bɛla, tɩ da targɛ-ya la tõon-sõma tõoma. Tɩ sãn ɩta dee ka basɛ, wakatɛ la sãn wa paagɛ, tɩ wʋn yẽ a yõorɔ.

ref: We should help people whenever we can, especially if they are followers of the Lord. Final Warnings
text: Bɛla, tɩn yẽ folle sɛka woo, tɩ tõm ẽŋɛ nẽrba wuu sõŋa, lan gãnnɩ wuu, tɩ ɩta sɛba n lagɩm na tõma bɔ Krisi sɩra la sõŋa. Ba'asgɔ yetɔga

ref: You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
text: Bɩsɛ-ya gʋlsgɔ biɛ kãr-bãna wã de la mam Apolɩ n gʋlsɛ la m mẽŋa nu'ugo.

ref: Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ.
text: Sɛba n mõrgrɩ ya bɔɔra tɩ ya wã la, de la nẽrba n boorɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ nẽrsaalba zẽ'am. Ban ka boorɩ tɩ ba yẽ nãmsgɔ Krisi kũm dɔ-puurŋa la ĩyã mã'a yelle tɩ ba ɩta bɛla.

ref: They are circumcised, but they don't obey the Law of Moses. All they want is to brag about having you circumcised.
text: Bãma sɛba n wãtɩ la mẽŋa ka ɩtɩ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na, ba boorɩ la ya wã tɩ ba tã'agɛ wẽ yõ'ogɔ, bãma n botɩ ya wã la.

ref: But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
text: La sãn dɛna mam, mam ka wẽ'erɩ m yõ'ogɔ la sɛla yelle, sãn dagna tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo la mã'a yelle. Krisi kũm na ĩyã dũnia yɛla wuu ãn wʋ ba ki mɛ ka le sɔna mam, tɩ mam mẽ ãna wʋ mam ki mɛ, ka le bɔna dũnia yɛla pʋam.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are circumcised or not. All that matters is that you are a new person.
text: La dagɩ nẽra n wã, bɩɩ nẽra n ka wã n tarɩ yõorɔ, sɛla n tarɩ yõorɔ de la tɩ tarɩ la vɔm paalga.

ref: If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
text: Sɛba wuu n dolɩ nõor-ẽna wã, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ba sũ-mã'asʋm, dee zoe ba ninbãalga, bãma la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la wuu.

ref: On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
text: Nẽra nẽra da le basɛ tɩ toogo paagɛ mam, se'ere n soe la, yisa sɛba tɩ mam tarɩ m ĩyã la pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ mam de la Azezi nẽra.

ref: My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
text: M sɔbiisi, tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya. Amina.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. To God's people who live in Ephesus and are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
text: Mam Apolɩ n de Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na, n gʋlsɛ gõn-ẽna wã bɔ'ɔra yãma Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Efɛɛzɩ dɛna sɩra dõma Azezi-krisi doosgo pʋam na.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
text: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm. Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ tõma yel-sõnnɛ se'em Krisi ĩyã

ref: Praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ for the spiritual blessings that Christ has brought us from heaven!
text: Tɩ pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la, ẽŋa n bɔ tɔ a Sɩa yel-sõnnɛ wuu n ze'ele saazuum, tõma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã.

ref: Before the world was created, God had Christ choose us to live with him and to be his holy and innocent and loving people.
text: Nawẽnnɛ looge tõma la ẽŋa ĩyã, lan dẽŋɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ, tɩ tɩ welge to'ore dɛna a nẽrba, ka tara yelle a nẽŋam.

ref: God was kind and decided that Christ would choose us to be God's own adopted children.
text: A nõŋlʋm na ĩyã tɩ a dẽŋɛ looge tõma tɩ tɩ lebge a kɔma Azezi-krisi ĩyã, wʋ a mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em na.

ref: God treated us with undeserved grace because of the Son he dearly loves, and so we should praise God.
text: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ tɩ pẽgɛ ẽ mɛ, a yel-sõnnɛ n zo'oge gãŋɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ tɔ zãŋa, tõma n de ayɩla la a Dayɔ-nõŋrɛ la ĩyã.

ref: and by what Christ has done, God has shown us his own mysterious ways.
text: Nawẽnnɛ basɛ tɩ tõma bãŋɛ ẽn boorɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em n daan sʋgɛ tɩ a looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ Krisi ĩyã la mɛ.

ref: Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
text: Wakatɛ la sãn wa paagɛ, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em na mɛ, a wʋn lagsɛ sɛla wuu n boe saazuum, la dũnia zuo, tɩ ba sakɛ Zuudãana ayɩla n de Krisi.

ref: God always does what he plans, and this is why he appointed Christ to choose us.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tɩ sɛla woo ɩta wʋ ẽn boorɩ se'em na, n dẽŋɛ looge tõma tɩ tɩ dɛna a nẽrba Krisi ĩyã.

ref: He did this so we Jews would bring honor to him and be the first ones to have hope because of him.
text: Tɩ tõma n dẽŋɛ tara pʋtẽ'erɛ la Krisi la, wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba pẽgɛ ẽ a na'am pãŋa la ĩyã.

ref: Christ also brought you the truth, which is the good news about how you can be saved. You put your faith in Christ and were given the promised Holy Spirit to show you belong to God.
text: Yãma n mẽ wõm sɩra kõ-yẽlga la, n basɛ tɩ ya bɔ Krisi sɩra yẽ fãarɛ, ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ya a Sɩa wʋ ẽn bĩŋe nõorɛ se'em na, Sɩa ẽŋa de la dãalgɔ pa'alɛ tɩ ya de la a nẽrba.

ref: The Spirit also makes us sure we will be given what God has stored up for his people. Then we will be set free, and God will be honored and praised. Paul's Prayer
text: A Sɩa la n de sɛla n pa'alɛ tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ sɛla woo tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ tɔ la, halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ a wʋn fãagɛ tõma wuu n de a nẽrba la ba'asɛ la, bõn-ẽŋa n wʋn basɛ tɩ tɩ pẽgɛ ẽ, a na'am pãŋa la ĩyã. Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ Efɛɛzɩ dõma ĩyã

ref: I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ mam n wõm yãma n bɔ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi sɩra, la yãma n tarɩ nõŋlʋm la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu se'em na,

ref: So I never stop being grateful for you, as I mention you in my prayers.
text: tɩ mam tẽra ya yelle m pʋ'ʋsgɔ pʋam, pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ ya ĩyã ka gɔ'ɔra.

ref: I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God.
text: Mam sosrɩ la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ n de Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, tɩ a bɔ ya a yɛm Sɩa tɩ a vẽlgɛ ya yɛm, tɩ ya bãŋɛ ẽn ãn se'em.

ref: My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
text: Mam ken sɔsra Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a botɩ ya pʋtẽ'era like tɩ ya bãŋɛ ẽn wi ya tɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla, dee bãŋɛ bõn-sõma sɛba tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la n ba ãna sõŋa, dee zo'oge se'em.

ref: I want you to know about the great and mighty power that God has for us followers. It is the same wonderful power he used
text: Mam ken sɔsra ẽ tɩ a botɩ ya bãŋɛ a pãŋa la n ãn kãtɛ gãŋɛ se'em tɩ a tarɩ tõnna la tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra la lɛ, wʋ a na'am pãn-kãtɛ n tõnnɩ tõon-kãra se'em na.

ref: when he raised Christ from death and let him sit at his right side in heaven.
text: Pãn-kãtɛ ẽŋa n bɛla tɩ a dɩkɛ vo'oge Krisi botɩ a zĩ'ire a zʋʋgɔ bɔba saazuum Nawẽnnɛ yire.

ref: There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well.
text: A botɩ Krisi n gãnna na'adõma wuu, la pãnsɩ dõma wuu, la kɛ'ɛndõma wuu, la ke'eŋo buuri wuu, ẽŋa yʋ'ʋrɛ n gãnnɩ yʋ'ʋra wuu, la dagɩ dũnia ẽna pʋam mã'a, la ken dɛna la dũnia sɛka n wʋn wa'am na pʋam.

ref: God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything.
text: Nawẽnnɛ basɛ mɛ tɩ Krisi sɔna sɛla woo, dee botɩ a dɛna a nẽrba wuu Zuudãana sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: The church is the body of Christ and is filled with Christ who completely fills everything.
text: Krisi nẽrba la ãn wʋ a mẽŋa ĩyã la, ẽŋa n vɔa ba pʋam, la ẽŋa n boe zẽ'a woo, botɩ sɛla woo bɔna.

ref: In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God.
text: Ya daan ãn wʋ ya ki mɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, ya tõon-be'ero, la ya tuure la ĩyã.

ref: You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God.
text: Yãma n daan ɩtɩ se'em na ãn wʋ dũnia nẽrba n ka mi Nawẽnnɛ n ɩtɩ se'em na, dɔla Asũtãana n de pãnsɩ dõma n boe saazuum naba la, ẽŋa n tõnnɩ na sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la.

ref: Once we were also ruled by the selfish desires of our bodies and minds. We had made God angry, and we were going to be punished like everyone else.
text: Tõma wuu daan lagɩm ãna wʋ bãma n ãn se'em na, tõma daan vɔa wʋ nẽrsaala yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em, ɩta tɩ ĩyã, la tɩ yɛm n boorɩ se'em n de be'em na. La tõma n daan ãn se'em na basɛ tɩ tɩ daan masɛ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɛ wʋ nẽrba basɛba la.

ref: But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you.
text: Tɩ baa tõma n daan ãn wʋ tɩ ki mɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero ĩyã la, a basɛ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na Krisi yẽ vɔm. La de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ ĩyã tɩ a fãagɛ ya.

ref: God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven.
text: La de la tõma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lagɩm tõma la Krisi vo'oge, basɛ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na ẽŋa zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ yire.

ref: God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: A ẽŋɛ tõma sõŋa Krisi ĩyã, tɩ a pa'alɛ la nẽrba wakatɛ n sẽm a yel-sõnnɛ n zo'oge gãŋɛ se'em.

ref: You were saved by faith in God, who treats us much better than we deserve. This is God's gift to you, and not anything you have done on your own.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ la ĩyã tɩ ya yẽ fãarɛ, yan bɔ Krisi sɩra la ĩyã, la dagɩ ya mẽŋa pãŋa, la de la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a.

ref: It isn't something you have earned, so there is nothing you can brag about.
text: La dagɩ ya tõoma ĩyã, tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka da pẽgɛ a mẽŋa.

ref: God planned for us to do good things and to live as he has always wanted us to live. This is why he sent Christ to make us what we are. United by Christ
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n nãam tɔ, botɩ tɩ lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla, tõnna tõon-sõma tɩ a põn dẽŋɛ mãasʋm tɩ tɩ wa tõm na. Krisi ĩyã tɩ lebge la buuri ayɩla

ref: At that time you did not know about Christ. You were foreigners to the people of Israel, and you had no part in the promises God had made to them. You were living in this world without hope and without God,
text: Tẽegɛ-ya tɩ wakat-sɛka tɩ ya ka boe la Krisi la, ya daan de la sãama la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, ka tara nõorɛ la bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma la. Ya daan vɔa la dũnia wã zuo ka mina Nawẽnnɛ, dee ka tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla.

ref: and you were far from God. But Christ offered his life's blood as a sacrifice and brought you near God.
text: Ya daan zãagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma n lagɩm na Azezi-krisi dɛna ayɩla la, a zɩɩm na n yese la basɛ tɩ ya yãŋa lɛm ẽ mɛ.

ref: Christ has made peace between Jews and Gentiles, and he has united us by breaking down the wall of hatred that separated us. Christ gave his own body
text: La de la Krisi n botɩ tɩ tara sũ-mã'asʋm, ẽŋa n basɛ tɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma lebge buuri ayɩla. Ẽŋa n ki yese be'em n ãn wʋ lalga

ref: to destroy the Law of Moses with all its rules and commands. He even brought Jews and Gentiles together as though we were only one person, when he united us in peace.
text: bɔna tɩ tẽŋasʋka welge to tɩ tɩ sisra taaba la, dee basɛ tɩ Amoyisi lɔɔ, la yel-sɛba tɩ a yetɩ ba ɩta la ka le tõnna, botɩ Zifdõma, la sɛba n dagɩ Zifdõma naagɛ taaba lagɩm na ẽŋa lebge buur-paalga ayɩla tara sũ-mã'asʋm la taaba.

ref: On the cross Christ did away with our hatred for each other. He also made peace between us and God by uniting Jews and Gentiles in one body.
text: Krisi n ki kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo la, a lagɩm ba tɩ ba lebge la buuri ayɩla naagɛ ba la Nawẽnnɛ botɩ ba ka le sisra taaba.

ref: Christ came and preached peace to you Gentiles, who were far from God, and peace to us Jews, who were near God.
text: Krisi wa'am wa mõolɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm kõ-yẽlga bɔ yãma sɛba n daan zãagɛ, la tõma sɛba n lɛm na.

ref: And because of Christ, all of us can come to the Father by the same Spirit.
text: Krisi ĩyã tɩ tõma wuu tara sore wʋn lɛm tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ayɩla n boe la tɩ wuu lɛ la ĩyã.

ref: You Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens with everyone else who belongs to the family of God.
text: Bɛla, ya ka le dɛna sãama, bɩɩ nẽrba n ze'ele zẽ'a ayẽma, ya de la buuri ayɩla la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, ya de la a yire nẽrba.

ref: You are like a building with the apostles and prophets as the foundation and with Christ as the most important stone.
text: Yãma po bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ la n ãn wʋ deego la mẽeŋa pʋam. Azezi Tõntõnɩba, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n de deego la ẽbrɛ. Tɩ Azezi-krisi mẽŋa dɛna kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa.

ref: Christ is the one who holds the building together and makes it grow into a holy temple for the Lord.
text: Ẽŋa n basɛ tɩ deego la wuu zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ lɛbgra Wẽnde-kãtɛ ãna sõŋa bɔ Zuudãana.

ref: And you are part of that building Christ has built as a place for God's own Spirit to live.
text: Yãma n lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla la, ya mẽ po bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ deego la tɩ a boe mĩ mẽta la mẽeŋa pʋam, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa Sɩa kẽ'era mĩ.

ref: Christ Jesus made me his prisoner, so I could help you Gentiles.
text: Bɛla tɩ mam Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ. Mam boe la yʋ'a deem Azezi-krisi yelle, mam n mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga bɔ yãma bu-zãŋsɩ la ĩyã.

ref: You have surely heard about God's gift of undeserved grace in choosing me to help you.
text: Mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ ya wõm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ a yel-sõnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la ẽŋɛ la mam nu'usum tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ yãma.

ref: In fact, this letter tells you a little about how God has shown me his mysterious ways.
text: Nawẽnnɛ puke a yelle n sʋgɛ la pa'alɛ mam mɛ, wʋ mam gʋlsɛ se'em fẽe bɔ ya la.

ref: As you read the letter, you will also find out how well I really do understand the mystery about Christ.
text: Ya sãn karẽŋɛ mam n gʋlsɛ se'em na, ya wʋn yẽ mam n bãŋɛ Krisi yelle n daan sʋgɛ la võorɛ n ãn se'em.

ref: No one knew about this mystery until God's Spirit told it to his holy apostles and prophets.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka puke ẽ pa'alɛ kʋrʋm dõma, la lɛɛlɛ wã a botɩ a Sɩa la puke ẽ pa'alɛ la sɛba tɩ a tõm, la a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba.

ref: And the mystery is this: Because of Christ Jesus, the good news has given the Gentiles a share in the promises God gave to the Jews. God has also let the Gentiles be part of the same body.
text: Yel-ẽŋa n sʋgɛ la de la kõ-yẽlga mõolgɔ n botɩ bu-zãŋsɩ wuu lagɩm na Zifdõma tara pʋrɛ, lagɩm taaba dɛna ayɩla, dee lagɩm tɔ'ɔra sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

ref: God treated me with kindness. His power worked in me, and it became my job to spread the good news.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a tɩ a botɩ mam dɛna a tõntõnna mõola a kõ-yẽlga la, la a pãŋa.

ref: I am the least important of all God's people. But God was kind and chose me to tell the Gentiles that because of Christ there are blessings that cannot be measured.
text: Mam n de nẽra n pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la wuu pʋam, la bɛla wuu Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la mam yel-sõn-ẽŋa tɩ m mõolɛ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la bɔ bu-zãŋsɩ tɩ ba bãŋɛ bõn-sõma n zo'oge gãŋɛ tɩ nẽrba ka tãna wʋn bãŋɛ ba'asɛ la,

ref: God, who created everything, wanted me to help everyone understand the mysterious plan that had always been hidden in his mind.
text: dee botɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em n daan sʋgɛ la. Ẽŋa n nãam sɛla woo la, sʋgɛ bõn-ẽŋa mɛ halɩ yʋʋma yʋʋma wa paam zĩna.

ref: Then God would use the church to show the powers and authorities in the spiritual world that he has many different kinds of wisdom.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Azezi nẽrba la wʋn basɛ tɩ kɛ'ɛndõma, la pãnsɩ dõma n boe saazuum bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm n de to'oto'ore se'em wuu.

ref: God did this according to his eternal plan. And he was able to do what he had planned because of all Christ Jesus our Lord had done.
text: La doose la Zuudãana Azezi-krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ bɛla masɛ wʋ ẽn looge a yɛm se'em halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo la.

ref: Christ now gives us courage and confidence, so we can come to God by faith.
text: Tõma n bɔ Krisi sɩra lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla la, sore boe mɛ tɩ tõma tãna wʋn paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɩ dabeem ka tara tõma.

ref: This is why you should not be discouraged when I suffer for you. After all, it will bring honor to you. Christ's Love for Us
text: Bɛla tɩ mam sɔsra ya tɩ ya da basɛ tɩ ya bʋraanɛ bo'oge mam nãmsrɩ ya yelle la ĩyã, mam nãmsgɔ la de la yãma zu-zẽkrɛ. Krisi nõŋlʋm

ref: I kneel in prayer to the Father.
text: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam ka dũma tẽŋa tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam,

ref: God is wonderful and glorious. I pray that his Spirit will make you become strong followers
text: Mam sosrɩ ẽ tɩ a Sɩa bɔ ya pãŋa ya sũure pʋam zozo'e wʋ a na'am pãn-kãtɛ la n zo'oge se'em na,

ref: and that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. Stand firm and be deeply rooted in his love.
text: tɩ Krisi wʋn bɔna ya sũure pʋam, yan bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ya wʋn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ tara nõŋlʋm zozo'e.

ref: I pray that you and all God's people will understand what is called wide or long or high or deep.
text: Tɩ yãma wʋn naagɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu tã'agɛ bãŋɛ Krisi nõŋlʋm n zo'oge se'em, ẽn yalgɛ, la ẽn wɔgɛ, la ẽn ãn ko'ogo se'em,

ref: I want you to know all about Christ's love, although it is too wonderful to be measured. Then your lives will be filled with all that God is.
text: la ya bãŋɛ a nõŋlʋm na n gãŋɛ tɩ nẽrba ka tãna wʋn bãŋɛ ba'asɛ la. Tɩ bõn-sɛba wuu n ze'etɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wʋn pɩrɛ ya.

ref: I pray that Christ Jesus and the church will forever bring praise to God. His power at work in us can do far more than we dare ask or imagine. Amen.
text: ẽŋa n masɛ tɩ a yẽta nã'asgɔ Krisi nẽrba la zẽ'am Azezi-krisi ĩyã zamãana woo zamãana wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

ref: As a prisoner of the Lord, I beg you to live in a way that is worthy of the people God has chosen to be his own.
text: Mam n boe yʋ'a deem Zuudãana doosgo yelle la, mam sosrɩ ya tɩ ya dɔla sorɔɔgɔ la sõŋa, wʋ lan masɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya tɩ ya ãna se'em na.

ref: Always be humble and gentle. Patiently put up with each other and love each other.
text: La masɛ mɛ tɩ ya sikra ya mẽŋa sɛla woo pʋam, tara sũ-bʋgsʋm, la sũ-mã'arɛ, la ya tara nõŋlʋm da isgra sũure la taaba.

ref: Try your best to let God's Spirit keep your hearts united. Do this by living at peace.
text: Mõrgɛ-ya tara nõ-yẽnnɛ n ze'etɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa zẽ'am, naagɛ-ya taaba tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna ya tẽŋasʋka.

ref: All of you are part of the same body. There is only one Spirit of God, just as you were given one hope when you were chosen to be God's people.
text: Azezi nẽrba de la ĩyã ayɩla, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ayɩla mã'a bɔna, la Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya tɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura bõn-sɛba la de la bõn-yẽnnɛ.

ref: There is one God who is the Father of all people. Not only is God above all others, but he works by using all of us, and he lives in all of us.
text: la Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla n de tɩ wuu Sɔ n soe sɛla woo, tɩ a pãŋa tara sɛla woo tõnna, dee bɔna sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: Christ has generously divided out his gifts to us.
text: La tõma nẽra woo to'oge yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a masɛ wʋ Krisi n bɔ tɩ se'em.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “When he went up to the highest place, he led away many prisoners and gave gifts to people.”
text: Bɛla tɩ la gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Ẽn daan zom saazuum na, a daan yãŋɛ a bɛ'ɛba mɛ yõgɛ ba, dee bɔ nẽrba bɔ'a.»

ref: When it says, “he went up,” it means that Christ had been deep in the earth.
text: Lan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «A zom saazuum na», a võorɛ de la bẽm? La võorɛ de tɩ a daan dẽŋɛ sige mɛ halɩ ka paagɛ tẽŋa wã pʋam pʋam.

ref: This also means that the one who went deep into the earth is the same one who went into the highest heaven, so he would fill the whole universe.
text: Sɛka n daan sige la, la de la ẽŋa n ken zom saazuum-zuum tɩ a sɔna sɛla woo.

ref: so his people would learn to serve and his body would grow strong.
text: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ ba mãasʋm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tɩ ba tõnna Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, tɩ Krisi nẽrba n de a ĩyã la bɩta dee pa'asra,

ref: This will continue until we are united by our faith and by our understanding of the Son of God. Then we will be mature, just as Christ is, and we will be completely like him.
text: halɩ tɩ tɩ wʋn wa lagɩm taaba bɔ sɩra dɛna ayɩla, bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, lebge nẽrba n bɩ ba'asɛ paagɛ wʋ Krisi n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam se'em na.

ref: We must stop acting like children. We must not let deceitful people trick us by their false teachings, which are like winds that toss us around from place to place.
text: Bɛla tɩ kãn le ãna wʋ kɔma tɩ nẽrba zãsnɩ zãsŋɔ buuri to'oto'ore pã'asra ba, ɩta yɛm tɩ ba botɩ ba tuuge, wʋ kʋsebgo, la ko-mĩ'isi n wẽ'erɩ õorŋɔ tɩ a yɛgna se'em na.

ref: Love should always make us tell the truth. Then we will grow in every way and be more like Christ, the head
text: La de la tɩn tɔgrɩ sɩra, la nõŋlʋm na ĩyã, tɩ tɩ bɩta sɛla woo pʋam wʋ ka paagɛ Krisi n de Zuudãana la.

ref: of the body. Christ holds it together and makes all of its parts work perfectly, as it grows and becomes strong because of love. The Old Life and the New Life
text: La de la ẽŋa n de zuugo la n botɩ a nẽrba n ãn wʋ ĩyã la lagɩm taaba tõnna, wʋ kõb-suglsi n botɩ ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore toŋe taaba, tɩ zẽ'a woo tõnna wʋ lan masɛ se'em, tɩ ĩyã la wuu bɩta sõŋa sõŋa la nõŋlʋm. Vɔm paalga Krisi ĩyã

ref: Their minds are in the dark, and they are stubborn and ignorant and have missed out on the life that comes from God. They no longer have any feelings about what is right,
text: Ba yɛm na boe la lika pʋam, la ba ka vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, ban kɛ'ɛm ba sũure ka bɔɔra tɩ ba bãŋɛ la ĩyã.

ref: and they are so greedy they do all kinds of indecent things.
text: Ba zuugo ka zɛɛrɩ la tõon-be'ero ɩrgɔ, ba tõnnɩ la yalsɩ, la be'em buuri wuu, ba boorɩ la tõon-dẽgrɔ mã'a.

ref: But this isn't what you were taught about Jesus Christ. He is the truth, and you heard about him and learned about him.
text: Yãma n wõm Azezi-krisi yelle, dee tɩ ba zãsʋm yãma n de a nẽrba sɩra n ze'ele a zẽ'am na,

ref: You were told that your foolish desires will destroy you and that you must give up your old way of life with all its bad habits.
text: dẽnɩ tɩ ya basɛ yãma n daan vɔa se'em kʋrʋm na. Basɛ-ya vɔm kɛka, la a yɛmleego la n boorɩ sɛla n de be'em n wʋn pã'asɛ ya botɩ ya sã'am na.

ref: Let the Spirit change your way of thinking
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa teege ya pʋtẽ'era tɩ ya lebge paalsɩ.

ref: and make you into a new person. You were created to be like God, and so you must please him and be truly holy. Rules for the New Life
text: Vɔa-ya vɔm paalga wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam ya tɩ ya ãna wʋ a mẽŋa n ãn se'em na. Vɔm ẽŋa de la sɛla n masɛ, la pʋpeelem n ze'ele sɩra pʋam.

ref: We are part of the same body. Stop lying and start telling each other the truth.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, basɛ-ya põmpɔrŋɔ, tɩ ya nẽra woo tɔgra sɩra bɔ'ɔra a tadãana, tɩ tɩ wuu lagɩm dɛna la ĩyã ayɩla la taaba.

ref: Don't get so angry that you sin. Don't go to bed angry
text: Ya sũure sãn isge, da tõm-ya tõon-be'ero, la ya da basɛ tɩ wẽnnɛ lui dee tɩ ya sũure la ka sige.

ref: and don't give the devil a chance.
text: Da bɔ-ya Asũtãana folle.

ref: If you are a thief, quit stealing. Be honest and work hard, so you will have something to give to people in need.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n daan zũuri, a da le zũura, a mõrgɛ a mẽŋa tõnna sɛla n masɛ, tɩ a wʋn tara sɛla sõŋɛ sɛba n boorɩ sõŋrɛ.

ref: Stop all your dirty talk. Say the right thing at the right time and help others by what you say.
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ yetɔg-be'ero yese ya nõorʋm, tɔgra-ya yetɔg-sõma n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽrba bʋraanɛ, tɩ la sõŋra nẽr-sɛba n selsrɩ la, dee ɩta ba sõŋa.

ref: Don't make God's Spirit sad. The Spirit makes you sure that someday you will be free from your sins.
text: Da ẽŋɛ-ya sɛla sã'am Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sũure, tɩ la de la ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ bɔ ya tɩ la dɛna dãalgɔ pa'alɛ tɩ daarɛ n sẽm tɩ ẽŋa wʋn fãagɛ ya fasɩ fasɩ.

ref: Stop being bitter and angry and mad at others. Don't yell at one another or curse each other or ever be rude.
text: Basɛ-ya sũ-toogo, la sũ-yĩire, la sũ-isgre, la nõke'ene, la taaba tʋʋrɛ, la pʋtʋʋlgɔ wuu.

ref: Instead, be kind and merciful, and forgive others, just as God forgave you because of Christ.
text: Ɩta-ya taaba sõŋa, la ya tara taaba zĩile, basra-ya taaba tõon-be'ero taalɛ, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ Krisi ĩyã la.

ref: Do as God does. After all, you are his dear children.
text: Yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ kɔma tɩ a nõŋɛ la, ãna-ya wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na.

ref: Let love be your guide. Christ loved us and offered his life for us as a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Ɩta-ya sɛla woo la nõŋlʋm wʋ Krisi n nõŋɛ tɩ se'em dɩkɛ a vɔm bɔ tõma ĩyã, tɩ la ãna wʋ kãabgɔ n tarɩ yũuŋ-sõŋɔ paara Nawẽnnɛ yɛm na.

ref: You are God's people, so don't let it be said that any of you are immoral or indecent or greedy.
text: Yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la, la ka masɛ tɩ yalsɩ, la tõon-dẽgrɔ buuri wuu, la bõnɔ zozo'e yɛmleego yelle pʋrɛ ya tẽŋasʋka.

ref: Don't use dirty or foolish or filthy words. Instead, say how thankful you are.
text: La ken ka masɛ tɩ ya tɔgra yetɔg-yooro, la yetɔg-be'ero, la yetɔg-gãlsɩ, la de la ya pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: Being greedy, indecent, or immoral is just another way of worshiping idols. You can be sure people who behave in this way will never be part of the kingdom that belongs to Christ and to God. Living as People of Light
text: Bãŋɛ-ya sõŋa, tɩ nẽra n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, bɩɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ dãana, bɩɩ nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ bõnɔ zozo'e kãn yẽ sɛla sɛla Krisi, la Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam. La sɛka n nõŋɛ bõnɔ zozo'e ãn wʋ nẽra n kãabrɩ baga dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ la.

ref: Don't let anyone trick you with foolish talk. God punishes everyone who disobeys him and says foolish things.
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽra pã'asɛ ya la yetɔg-yooro, tɩ la de la bõn-bãma n isgri Nawẽnnɛ sũure tɩ a sɩbgra sɛba n zagsɛ a nõorɛ la.

ref: You used to be like people living in the dark, but now you are people of the light because you belong to the Lord. So act like people of the light
text: Kʋrʋm, ya daan boe la lika pʋam, la lɛɛlɛ wã, yan lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã, ya boe la peelem pʋam. Bɛla, ɩta-ya wʋ nẽrba n boe peelem pʋam n ɩtɩ se'em na.

ref: and make your light shine. Be good and honest and truthful,
text: Peelem tõoma de la sɛla woo n ãn sõŋa, la sɛla n masɛ, la sɩra.

ref: as you try to please the Lord.
text: Bãŋɛ-ya sɛla n paarɩ Zuudãana yɛm bɔkɛ.

ref: Don't take part in doing those worthless things that are done in the dark. Instead, show how wrong they are.
text: Da po tõnna-ya tõon-sɛba tɩ nẽrba tõnnɩ lika pʋam n ka tarɩ yõorɔ la, tõn pa'alɛ-ya tɩ ba ka ãn sõŋa.

ref: It is disgusting even to talk about what is done in the dark.
text: Nẽr-bãma n sʋgɛ ɩta sɛla la de la yãnnɛ halɩ, tɩ nẽra kãn saagɛ tɔgɛ a yelle.

ref: But the light will show what these things are really like.
text: Ya sãn pa'alɛ tɩ ba ka ãn sõŋa, nẽrba wʋn yẽ tɩ sɛla woo de la vẽelga.

ref: Light shows up everything, just as the Scriptures say, “Wake up from your sleep and rise from death. Then Christ will shine on you.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, bõn-sɛka woo n de vẽelga boe la peelem pʋam. Bɛla tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Fõn sɛka n gĩsri la, nẽegɛ, isge kũm na tẽŋasʋka tɩ Krisi peelem nẽegɛ fɔ, bɔ fʋ vɔm.»

ref: These are evil times, so make every minute count.
text: Dɩkɛ-ya folle tɩ ya tarɩ la wuu tõm tõon-sõma, tɩ wakatɛ la de la tõon-be'ero wakatɛ.

ref: Don't be stupid. Instead, find out what the Lord wants you to do.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, da dɛna-ya geto, bãŋɛ-ya Zuudãana la n boorɩ se'em.

ref: Don't destroy yourself by getting drunk, but let the Spirit fill your life.
text: Da yũura-ya dãam bugra, tɩ a sã'anɩ la nẽra. Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa sɔna ya, tõnna la ya lɛ sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: When you meet together, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, as you praise the Lord with all your heart.
text: Naagɛ-ya taaba yõona Nawẽnnɛ yõoma, yõona-ya yõom-sõma buuri wuu tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ya, nã'asra Zuudãana la ya sũure wuu.

ref: Always use the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to thank God the Father for everything. Wives and Husbands
text: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ sɛla woo pʋam wakatɛ wuu, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ. Pɔgsɩ la ba sɩrba sakra taaba

ref: Honor Christ and put others first.
text: Sakra-ya taaba, yãma n nã'asrɩ Krisi la ĩyã.

ref: A wife should put her husband first, as she does the Lord.
text: Pɔgsɩ, sakra-ya ya sɩrba wʋ yãma n sakrɩ Zuudãana se'em na.

ref: A husband is the head of his wife, as Christ is the head and the Savior of the church, which is his own body.
text: Se'ere n soe la, sɩra n de pɔka la zuudãana, wʋ Krisi n de a nẽrba la Zuudãana la. Krisi nẽrba la de la a ĩyã, tɩ Krisi dɛna ba Fãagra.

ref: Wives should always put their husbands first, as the church puts Christ first.
text: Krisi nẽrba n sakrɩ ẽ se'em na, pɔgsɩ mẽ sakra ba sɩrba bɛla sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: A husband should love his wife as much as Christ loved the church and gave his life for it.
text: Sɩrba, nõŋɛ-ya ya pɔgba wʋ Krisi n nõŋɛ a nẽrba dɩkɛ a mẽŋa vɔm bɔ ba ĩyã la.

ref: He made the church holy by the power of his word, and he made it pure by washing it with water.
text: A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ a malgɛ ba la a yetɔga, tɩ ba ãna sõŋa wʋ ban sɔɔrɩ ĩyã la ko'om se'em na, botɩ ba welge to'ore dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba.

ref: Christ did this, so he would have a glorious and holy church, without faults or spots or wrinkles or any other flaws.
text: Tɩ ba da tara dẽgrɔ, la be'em, la sɛla n ka ãn sõŋa, ba tara pʋpeelem ka tara dõrga, tɩ a wʋn tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am la nã'asgɔ.

ref: In the same way, a husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself. A husband who loves his wife shows he loves himself.
text: Bɛla mẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ sɩrba nõŋɛ ba pɔgba wʋ ba mẽŋa ĩyã la. Sɛka n nõŋɛ a pɔga, nõŋɛ la a mẽŋa.

ref: None of us hate our own bodies. We provide for them and take good care of them, just as Christ does for the church,
text: Nẽra ka boe n sisri a mẽŋa ĩyã, a diisri ẽ mɛ, bɩsra a yelle sõŋa wʋ Krisi n ɩtɩ se'em bɔ'ɔra a nẽrba la,

ref: because we are each part of his body.
text: tõma n de a ĩyã zẽ'esɩ to'oto'ore la ĩyã.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother to get married, and he becomes like one person with his wife.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bʋraaga wʋn basɛ a sɔ, la a ma, dee lagɩm na a pɔga, tɩ bãma bayi lebge ĩyã ayɩla.»

ref: This is a great mystery, but I understand it to mean Christ and his church.
text: Ẽna de la yel-kãtɛ n sʋgɛ yetɔg-bãna pʋam, mam n tɔgrɩ la de la Krisi, la a nẽrba la yelle.

ref: So each husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself, and each wife should respect her husband.
text: La, la sãn dɛna yãma, nẽra woo nõŋɛ a pɔga wʋ a mẽŋa. Tɩ pɔka woo mẽ nanna a sɩra.

ref: Children, you belong to the Lord, and you do the right thing when you obey your parents. The first commandment with a promise says,
text: Kɔma, sakra-ya ya dɔgrɩba nõorɛ Zuudãana la ĩyã, tɩ bɛla n masɛ.

ref: and you will have a long and happy life.”
text: a yetɩ: «Fʋ sãn ɩta bɛla, fʋ wʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ, la yõ-woko dũnia zuo.»

ref: Parents, don't be hard on your children. Raise them properly. Teach them and instruct them about the Lord. Slaves and Masters
text: La yãma n de sɔdõma la, da ɩta ya kɔma sɛla n wʋn botɩ ba sũure yĩige, uge-ya ba, kã'an ba, dee zãsʋm ba wʋ Zuudãana n boorɩ se'em. Yamsɩ, la ba zuudãandõma yelle

ref: Slaves, you must obey your earthly masters. Show them great respect and be as loyal to them as you are to Christ.
text: Yamsɩ, sakra-ya sɛba n soe ya dũnia zuo wã la ya sũure wuu, nanna-ya ba dee zɔta ba, wʋ yan sakrɩ Krisi la.

ref: Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. You are slaves of Christ, so with your whole heart you must do what God wants you to do.
text: Da ɩta-ya bɛla ba nifum mã'a tɩ ba pẽgɛ ya. Yãma n de Krisi yamsɩ la, ɩta-ya Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em la ya sũure wuu.

ref: Gladly serve your masters, as though they were the Lord himself, and not simply people.
text: Tõnna-ya ba tõoma la bɔ'ɔra ba la yɛmleego, wʋ ya tõnnɩ bɔ'ɔra la Zuudãana la, la dagɩ nẽrsaalba ĩyã.

ref: You know that you will be rewarded for any good things you do, whether you are slaves or free.
text: La ya mina tɩ nẽra woo wʋn yẽ a tõonɛ yõorɔ Zuudãana Krisi zẽ'am masɛ wʋ tõon-sõŋɔ sɛka tɩ a tõm na, a sãn dɛna la nẽra yamŋa bɩɩ a sãn dagna nẽra yamŋa.

ref: Slave owners, you must treat your slaves with this same respect. Don't threaten them. They have the same Master in heaven that you do, and he doesn't have favorites. The Fight against Evil
text: La sãn dɛna yãma n de zuudãandõma la, yãma mẽ de la ya ɩta sõŋa bɔ'ɔra ya yamsɩ la, da gi'ira-ya ba, mina-ya tɩ yãma, la bãma wuu lagɩm tarɩ la Zuudãana ayɩla n boe saazuum n ka bɔkrɩ nẽrba. Zɛbrɛ lɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔrɩ

ref: Finally, let the mighty strength of the Lord make you strong.
text: Sɛla n deege, keŋe ya mẽŋa yẽ Zuudãana pãŋa ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ a doosgo la pʋam, a pãn-kãtɛ la ĩyã.

ref: Put on all the armor that God gives, so you can defend yourself against the devil's tricks.
text: Yɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ wuu, tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, tɩ Asũtãana da yãŋɛ pã'asɛ ya.

ref: We are not fighting against humans. We are fighting against forces and authorities and against rulers of darkness and powers in the spiritual world.
text: Tõma zɛbrɛ ka boe la nẽrsaalba, tõma zɛbrɩ la Asũtãana kɛ'ɛndõma, la a pãnsɩ dõma, la dũnia lika tõon-be'ero na'adõma, la kʋlkã'arsɩ wuu n boe saazuum.

ref: So put on all the armor that God gives. Then when that evil day comes, you will be able to defend yourself. And when the battle is over, you will still be standing firm.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, dɩkɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ zɛbrɛ lɔgrɔ la wuu yɛ, tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ ze'ele zɛbɛ be'em wakatɛ. La zɛbrɛ la sãn wa ba'asɛ, ya wʋn ken zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ.

ref: Be ready! Let the truth be like a belt around your waist, and let God's justice protect you like armor.
text: Bɛla, mãasʋm-ya zɛ, tara-ya sɩra tɩ a ãna wʋ sagãnnɛ tɩ ya lʋ ya sɩa la, la ya ɩta sɛla n masɛ tɩ la ãna wʋ kuto fuugo tɩ ya yɛ pĩ ya yõ'ogɔ,

ref: Your desire to tell the good news about peace should be like shoes on your feet.
text: tara-ya yɛmleego mõola sũ-mã'asʋm kõ-yẽlga la wʋ tagra tɩ ya piti sẽnna la.

ref: Let your faith be like a shield, and you will be able to stop all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
text: La ya bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra sɛla woo pʋam tɩ la gu'ura ya la Asũtãana pɛɛma n ãn wʋ bugum na, tɩ ya tã'agɛ kĩŋe ba.

ref: Let God's saving power be like a helmet, and for a sword use God's message that comes from the Spirit.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ fãarɛ tɩ ya tarɩ la ãna wʋ kuto zuu-wannɛ gu'ura ya, dee dɩkɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga n ãn wʋ sʋkɔbgɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ ya la zɛbɛ.

ref: Never stop praying, especially for others. Always pray by the power of the Spirit. Stay alert and keep praying for God's people.
text: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ sɛla woo pʋam, pʋ'ʋsra-ya dee sɔsra ẽ wakatɛ wuu la a Sɩa pãŋa. Nargɛ-ya pãŋa pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu ĩyã da basɛ.

ref: Pray that I will be given the message to speak and that I may fearlessly explain the mystery about the good news.
text: La ya mẽ pʋ'ʋsra mam ĩyã tɩ m sãn yetɩ m tɔgɛ, a wʋn ẽŋɛ yetɔga m nõorʋm tɩ m tã'agɛ mõolɛ a kõ-yẽlga n sʋgɛ la, la bʋraanɛ tɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ a võorɛ.

ref: I was sent to do this work, and this is the reason I am in jail. So pray that I will be brave and will speak as I should. Final Greetings
text: La de la kõ-yẽlga ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm mam tɩ m mõolɛ, bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam bɔna yʋ'a deem. Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ tɩ m wʋn tã'agɛ mõolɛ la bʋraanɛ wʋ lan masɛ se'em. Ba'asgɔ yetɔga

ref: I pray that God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will give peace, love, and faith to every follower!
text: Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Sɔ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn bɔ tɩ sɔbiisi la wuu sũ-mã'asʋm, la nõŋlʋm, dee botɩ ya bɔ ẽ sɩra.

ref: May God be kind to everyone who keeps on loving our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽr-sɛba wuu n nõŋɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi la nõŋlʋm n ka ba'asrɩ la yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: From Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus. To all God's people who belong to Christ Jesus at Philippi and to all your bishops and deacons.
text: Mam Apolɩ la Atimõtɩ n de Azezi-krisi tõntõnɩba n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba wuu n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, la ya nẽŋadõma, la ya yel-bɩsrɩba n boe Filipi.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul's Prayer for the Church in Philippi
text: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm. Apolɩ pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ Krisi nẽrba n boe Filipi ĩyã

ref: Every time I think of you, I thank my God.
text: Mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ a yel-sõnnɛ ya ĩyã wakatɛ sɛka woo tɩ mam tẽegɛ ya yelle.

ref: And whenever I mention you in my prayers, it makes me happy.
text: Wakatɛ wuu tɩ mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ ya wuu ĩyã la, mam pʋ'ʋsrɩ la sũ-yẽlga,

ref: This is because you have taken part with me in spreading the good news from the first day you heard about it.
text: yãma n sõŋɛ mam la kõ-yẽlga la tõonɛ halɩ pɔspɔsɩ daarɛ wa paam zĩna la ĩyã.

ref: God is the one who began this good work in you, and I am certain that he won't stop before it is complete on the day that Christ Jesus returns.
text: Mam mi sɩra sɩra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n pɔsɛ tõon-sõŋɔ ẽŋa ya sũure pʋam na, wʋn ken tõm ẽ bɛla halɩ ka ba'asɛ Azezi-krisi lemŋo daarɛ.

ref: You have a special place in my heart. So it is only natural for me to feel the way I do. All of you have helped in the work God has given me, as I defend the good news and tell about it here in jail.
text: La masɛ mɛ tɩ mam tẽ'esɛ bɛla ya wuu ĩyã, se'ere n soe la, mam yɛm boe la ya lɛ mɛ. Bẽm ĩyã, la sãn dɛna mam n boe yʋ'a deem wã, bɩɩ mam n tɔgrɩ pa'ala nẽrba tɩ kõ-yẽlga la de la sɩra la, ya wuu lagɩm na mam mɛ yẽ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: God himself knows how much I want to see you. He knows I care for you in the same way Christ Jesus does.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n de mam kaset-dãana tɩ mam yɛm boe la ya wuu mɛ, mam nõŋɛ ya la Azezi-krisi nõŋlʋm.

ref: I pray that your love will keep on growing and you will fully know and understand
text: La mam n sosrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bõn-sɛka de la ya nõŋlʋm wʋn pa'asra daarɛ woo, la bãŋrɛ, la yɛm vẽlgrɛ,

ref: how to make the right choices. Then you will still be pure and innocent when Christ returns. And until that day,
text: tɩ ya wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla n ãn sõŋa, tɩ Krisi lemŋo daarɛ ya wʋn dɛna pʋpeelem dõma ka tara dõrga,

ref: Jesus Christ will keep you busy doing good deeds that bring glory and praise to God. What Life Means to Paul
text: tɩ ya tõoma wʋn dɛna tõon-sõma n masɛ n ze'ele Azezi-krisi zẽ'am, tɩ la wʋn botɩ Nawẽnnɛ yẽ pẽka la nã'asgɔ. Krisi ĩyã tɩ mam vɔa

ref: My dear friends, I want you to know that what has happened to me has helped to spread the good news.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam boorɩ mɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ sɛla n ẽŋɛ mam na basɛ mɛ tɩ kõ-yẽlga la mõolgɔ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa.

ref: The Roman guards and all the others know I am here in jail because I serve Christ.
text: La basɛ tɩ nayire sʋdaasɩ, la nẽrba basɛba la wuu bãŋɛ tɩ mam n boe yʋ'a deem na de la Krisi tõoma ĩyã.

ref: Some are preaching about Christ because they are jealous and envious of us. Others are preaching because they want to help.
text: La de la sɩra tɩ nẽrba basɛba mõolɩ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la sũure ĩyã, la gẽelgɔ, la basɛba mõolɩ la pʋtẽ'e-sõŋɔ.

ref: They love Christ and know I am here to defend the good news about him.
text: Pʋtẽ'e-sõŋɔ dõma la mõolɩ ẽ na nõŋlʋm, ba mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n looge mam tɩ m tɔgɛ pa'alɛ tɩ kõ-yẽlga la de la sɩra.

ref: But the ones who are jealous of us are not sincere. They just want to cause trouble for me while I am in jail.
text: La basɛba la mõolɩ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la gẽelgɔ, la dagɩ la pʋpeelem. Ba tẽ'esrɩ tɩ bõn-ẽŋa wʋn pa'asɛ mam na nãmsgɔ, mam n boe yʋ'a deem na.

ref: But that doesn't matter. All that matters is that people are telling about Christ, whether they are sincere or not. This is what makes me glad. I will keep on being glad,
text: La bɛla dagɩ yelle, la sãn dɛna la pʋtẽ'e-sõŋɔ bɩɩ pʋtẽ'e-be'ego, lan de se'em wuu, Krisi kõ-yẽlga la mõolɛ mɛ, bɛla mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ. La mam wʋn ken ɩta sũ-yẽlga mɛ,

ref: because I know that your prayers and the help that comes from the Spirit of Christ Jesus will keep me safe.
text: se'ere n soe la, mam mi tɩ yãma pʋ'ʋsgɔ ĩyã, la Azezi-krisi Sɩa sõŋrɛ ĩyã, bõn-ẽŋa wʋn ba'asõm bɔ mam na fãarɛ.

ref: I honestly expect and hope I will never do anything to be ashamed of. Whether I live or die, I always want to be as brave as I am now and bring honor to Christ.
text: Mam n boorɩ sɛla, dee tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la, de la mam kãn ẽŋɛ sɛla baa ayɩla tɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ mam. La de la mam tara bʋraanɛ lɛɛlɛ wã la wakatɛ wuu, basɛ tɩ Krisi yẽ nã'asgɔ mam ĩyã, la sãn dɛna mam vɔa mɛ, bɩɩ mam sãn ki mɛ.

ref: If I live, it will be for Christ, and if I die, I will gain even more.
text: La sãn dɛna la mam, mam sãn vɔa, la de la Krisi ĩyã, la kũm de la yõorɔ bɔ mam.

ref: I don't know what to choose. I could keep on living and doing something useful.
text: La mam sãn ken vɔa, mam wʋn tõm na tõon-sõma sõŋɛ nẽrba. Bɛla, mam ka mi mam n wʋn looge sɛka.

ref: It is a hard choice to make. I want to die and be with Christ, because this would be much better.
text: Mam boe la yɛla bayi tẽŋasʋka, mam boorɩ tɩ m basɛ vɔm ẽna mɛ dee sẽŋɛ ka bɔna Krisi zẽ'am, bɛla n ãn sõŋa gãnna.

ref: But I know that all of you still need me. This is why I am sure I will stay on to help you grow and be happy in your faith.
text: Mam mi tɩ bõn-ẽŋa de la sɩra. Bɛla, mam mi tɩ mam wʋn ken vɔa mɛ bɔna la ya wuu, sõŋɛ ya tɩ ya sẽŋɛ nẽŋa Azezi-krisi doosgo pʋam, tara sũ-yẽlga.

ref: Then, when I visit you again, you will have good reason to take great pride in Christ Jesus because of me.
text: La mam sãn leme wa bɔna la ya lɛ, ya zuugo wʋn zẽkɛ pa'asɛ mam ĩyã Azezi-krisi doosgo la pʋam. Mõrgra-ya Krisi kõ-yẽlga la ĩyã

ref: Above all else, you must live in a way that brings honor to the good news about Christ. Then, whether I visit you or not, I will hear that all of you think alike. I will know that you are working together and are struggling side by side to get others to believe the good news.
text: La bõn-sɛka n gãnnɩ wuu, de la ya basɛ tɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ ãna wʋ Krisi kõ-yẽlga la n pa'alɩ se'em na. Tɩ mam sãn wa'am ya zẽ'am bɩɩ m sãn ka wa'am, m wʋn wõm tɩ ya ze la kãŋkãŋɛ, tara yɛm ayɩla, lagɩm taaba la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla mõrgra tõnna tɩ nẽrba bɔ'ɔra kõ-yẽlga la sɩra.

ref: Be brave when you face your enemies. Your courage will show them that they are going to be destroyed, and it will show you that you will be saved. God will make all of this happen,
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ dabeem tara ya la bɛ'ɛba baa fẽfẽe, tɩ bɛla n wʋn pa'alɛ ba tɩ ba wʋn sã'am mɛ, la yãma wʋn yẽ la fãarɛ, bɛla ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: and he has blessed you. Not only do you have faith in Christ, but you suffer for him.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ na, la dagɩ ya bɔ Krisi sɩra mã'a, la de la ya ken nãmsɛ a ĩyã.

ref: You saw me suffer, and you still hear about my troubles. Now you must suffer in the same way.
text: Ya nãmsɛ wʋ yãma n yẽ mam n nãmsɛ se'em, dee wõnna tɩ mam ken nãmsra lɛɛlɛ wã.

ref: Christ encourages you, and his love comforts you. God's Spirit unites you, and you are concerned for others.
text: Yãma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la basɛ tɩ ya tara bʋraanɛ mɛ, tɩ a nõŋlʋm na bɛlsra ya. La yãma lagɩm na taaba la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, dee tara nõŋlʋm, la taaba zĩile.

ref: Now make me completely happy! Live in harmony by showing love for each other. Be united in what you think, as if you were only one person.
text: Lan de bɛla la, lagɩm-ya taaba la nõorɛ ayɩla tara nõŋlʋm, la yɛm ayɩla, la pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla, bɛla tɩ mam sũ-yẽlga wʋn zo'oge gãŋɛ.

ref: Don't be jealous or proud, but be humble and consider others more important than yourselves.
text: Da ɩta-ya sɛla sɛla gẽela taaba la pa'al-m-mẽŋa, sikra ya mẽŋa tẽ'esra tɩ nẽrba basɛba la gãnnɩ ya.

ref: Care about them as much as you care about yourselves
text: Nẽra nẽra da tẽ'esra a mẽŋa mã'a sõŋrɛ yelle. Ɛɛra-ya ya taaba sõŋrɛ.

ref: and think the same way that Christ Jesus thought:
text: Tara-ya pʋtẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Azezi-krisi daan tarɩ la.

ref: Christ was truly God. But he did not try to remain equal with God.
text: Azezi-krisi la Nawẽnnɛ n de ayɩla la, a ka tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa n zõn na Nawẽnnɛ na, de la sɛla tɩ ẽŋa wʋn gʋrɛ kãŋkãŋɛ.

ref: Instead he gave up everything and became a slave, when he became like one of us.
text: A sike a mẽŋa mɛ, basɛ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na, dee lebge nẽrsaala, ẽŋɛ a mẽŋa tõntõnna, wa bɔna la nẽrsaalba lɛ.

ref: Christ was humble. He obeyed God and even died on a cross.
text: Ẽŋa n daan de nẽrsaala la, a sike a mẽŋa mɛ, sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ halɩ ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo.

ref: Then God gave Christ the highest place and honored his name above all others.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽkɛ ẽ halɩ, bɔ ẽ yʋ'ʋrɛ n gãnnɩ yʋ'ʋra wuu.

ref: So at the name of Jesus everyone will bow down, those in heaven, on earth, and under the earth.
text: Tɩ Azezi ĩyã, bõn-sɛba wuu n boe saazuum, la tẽŋa zuo, la tẽŋa pʋam, wʋn ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam pẽgɛ ẽ, a yʋ'ʋrɛ la ĩyã.

ref: And to the glory of God the Father everyone will openly agree, “Jesus Christ is Lord!” Lights in the World
text: Tɩ nẽra woo wʋn yetɩ, Azezi-krisi n de Zuudãana, tɩ la wʋn dɛna nã'asgɔ bɔ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ. Ãna-ya wʋ peelem dũnia wã pʋam

ref: My dear friends, you always obeyed when I was with you. Now that I am away, you should obey even more. So work with fear and trembling to discover what it really means to be saved.
text: Bɛla, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, mam n daan boe la ya lɛ la, ya daan sakrɩ mɛ wakatɛ wuu, la mam n ka boe ya zẽ'am wã, ken ɩta-ya gãnna bɛla. Tõnna-ya fãarɛ la tõoma sõŋa sõŋa la Nawẽnnɛ zʋa.

ref: God is working in you to make you willing and able to obey him.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n boe la ya lɛ tõnna, botɩ ya bɔɔra ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, dee tãna ɩta ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em.

ref: Do everything without grumbling or arguing.
text: Ɩta-ya sɛla woo tɩ yõŋa, la nõkɛ'ɛma wẽ'a da bɔna.

ref: Then you will be the pure and innocent children of God. You live among people who are crooked and evil, but you must not do anything they can say is wrong. Try to shine as lights among the people of this world,
