ref: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
text: La bɔ'ɔra-ya taaba bʋraanɛ daarɛ woo, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n tɔgrɩ la tõma zĩna se'em na, tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka da kɛ'ɛm a sũure tɩ tõon-be'ero basɛ tɩ a tuuge.

ref: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
text: Tõma sãn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ wʋ tõma n bɔ sɩra se'em pɔspɔsɩ la ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, tɩ wʋn lagɩm na Krisi to'oge pʋrɛ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn like those who rebelled.”
text: Bɛla tɩ la yele Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Zĩna, ya sãn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõa, ya da kɛ'ɛm ya sũure, wʋ ya yaabdõma n yʋʋn kɛ'ɛm ba sũure sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ se'em na.»

ref: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
text: Sɛba n yʋʋn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõa dee sɩ'ɩsɛ a nõorɛ la de la bãna? Ba de la nẽr-sɛba wuu tɩ Amoyisi yʋʋn tarɩ Ezipti tẽŋa yese la.

ref: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
text: Sɛba tɩ a yʋʋn pɔɩ yetɩ ba kãn kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a yetɩ a bɔ ba tɩ ba yẽ vo'osgo la de la bãna? Ba de la sɛba n sɩ'ɩsɛ la.

ref: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
text: La tõma yẽ tɩ ba yʋʋn ka yãŋɛ kẽ, ban ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ tɩ tɩ kẽ a vo'osgo zẽ'am na ken bɔna mɛ, bɛla tɩ guuse, tɩ ba'asgɔ ya nẽr-sɛka da wa yẽ tɩ a koŋe ka kẽ.

ref: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
text: Se'ere n soe la, tõma mẽ wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la wʋ nẽr-bãma n wõm se'em na. La bãma zẽ'am, yetɔga la tɩ ba yʋʋn wõm na ka tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ ba, bẽm ĩyã, sɛba n wõm na ka bɔ sɩra.

ref: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
text: La tõma sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la, tõma wʋn kẽ vo'osgo la zẽ'am tɩ a yʋʋn yetɩ: «Mam sũ-yĩire la pʋam, mam pɔɩ mɛ yetɩ, ba kãn malɩn kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam yetɩ m bɔ ba tɩ ba yẽ vo'osgo la.» Nawẽnnɛ n yele bɛla la, tɩ a dẽŋɛ põn tõm a tõoma wuu ba'asɛ mɛ halɩ lan pɔsɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ wakatɛ.

ref: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla tɔgɛ dabsa ayopɔɩ daarɛ la yelle yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ nãam sɛla woo ba'asɛ, dee vo'ose la dabsa ayopɔɩ daarɛ.»

ref: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
text: La zẽ'e-ẽna wã la ken tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Ba kãn malɩn kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ mam yetɩ m bɔ ba tɩ ba yẽ vo'osgo la.»

ref: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n yʋʋn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la pɔspɔsɩ la ka kẽ, ban ka sakɛ la ĩyã. La dẽnɩ tɩ nẽrba kẽ.

ref: Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn!”
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ le looge daarɛ ayẽma tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ zĩna. Tɩ yaabdõma n sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ na, yʋʋma zozo'e n tole tɩ a botɩ Adavidi tɔgɛ wʋ lan põn gʋlsɛ se'em na yetɩ: «Zĩna, ya sãn wõm Nawẽnnɛ kõa, ya da kɛ'ɛm ya sũure.»

ref: If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest.
text: Azoze yʋʋn sãn tarɩ nɩ ba paagɛ la vo'osgo asɩra sɩra zẽ'am, Nawẽnnɛ kãn le tɔgra vo'osgo daarɛ ayẽma yelle.

ref: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
text: Bɛla, vo'osgo n ken bɔna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la ĩyã, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n vo'ose dabsa ayopɔɩ daarɛ la.

ref: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka n kẽ Nawẽnnɛ vo'osgo la zẽ'am, mẽ basrɩ a tõoma mɛ dee vɔ'ɔsra, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ba'asɛ a tõoma dee vo'ose se'em na.

ref: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
text: Bɛla, tɩ wɩsgɛ tɩ mẽŋa yẽ vo'osgo ẽna, tɩ guuse tɩ nẽra baa ayɩla da doose sɩ'ɩsgɔ la sore koŋe ka kẽ.

ref: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la vɔa mɛ, tara pãŋa, dee tara nõorɛ gãnna sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõa bayi. A kẽ'erɩ la nẽra pʋam mina a sɩa, la a sũure yɛla wuu bɔkra, dee mina a pʋtẽ'erɛ, la a yɛm wuu n ãn se'em, wʋ sʋ'a n welgrɩ kõb-suglsi paara kõb-sõom na.

ref: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
text: Sɛla baa ayɩla ka boe n sʋgɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka yẽta. Bõn-sɛba tɩ a nãam na wuu de la vẽelsɩ a zẽ'am. La de la ẽŋa n de sɛka tɩ tɩ wʋn ze'ele a nẽŋam tɔgɛ tɩ yɛla wuu bɔ ẽ. Azezi-krisi n gãnnɩ kãabgɔ kẽma wuu

ref: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
text: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa Azezi n zom saazuum Nawẽnnɛ yire. Bɛla, tɩ ze'ele-ya kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore la doosgo pʋam.

ref: Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin!
text: Tɩ tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n tarɩ tɩ zĩile tɩ tɔrgɔ pʋam, se'ere n soe la, a yẽ nãmsgɔ mɛ sɛla woo pʋam wʋ tõma la, la a ka tõm tõon-be'ero.

ref: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
text: Bɛla, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ lɛm Nawẽnnɛ n de yel-sõnnɛ dãana la na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am la bʋraanɛ, tɩ a zoe tɩ ninbãalga ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ, dee sõŋɛ tɔ wakat-sɛka tɩ tɩ boorɩ.

ref: Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins.
text: Nawẽnnɛ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana woo, ba loorɩ ẽ na nẽrsaalba pʋam tɩ a tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, nẽrba ĩyã. Ẽŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a, dee kãabra bɔ'ɔra ẽ ba tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

ref: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
text: A tã wʋn wõm sɛba n ka mi, la sɛba n tuuri la võorɛ mɛ zoe ba ninbãalga, ẽŋa mẽŋa n boe tɔrgɔ pʋam na ĩyã.

ref: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
text: La de la bɛla ĩyã, tɩ la dɛna a kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ a mẽŋa tõon-be'ero, la nẽrba la tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

ref: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
text: Nẽra ka tã wʋn nã'asɛ a mẽŋa dɩkɛ tõon-ẽna bɔ a mẽŋa dɛna kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n loorɩ, wʋ ẽn yʋʋn looge Aarõ se'em na.

ref: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
text: Bɛla mẽ, la dagɩ Krisi mẽŋa n ɛ nã'asgɔ lebge kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, la de la Nawẽnnɛ n looge ẽ yele yetɩ: «Fõn de mam Dayʋa, zĩna mam lebge la fʋ Sɔ.»

ref: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
text: Dee le yele zẽ'a ayẽma yetɩ: «Fʋ de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na.»

ref: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
text: Azezi-krisi n daan boe dũnia wã wakat-sɛka la, a pʋ'ʋsɛ mɛ sose Nawẽnnɛ n de sɛka n tã wʋn fãagɛ ẽ kũm pʋam basɛ la, belem ẽ na nintãm. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lerge a sosgo, a sik-m-mẽŋa la pʋam.

ref: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
text: Baa la ẽn de Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, nãmsgɔ la pʋam a bãŋɛ sakrɛ n de sɛla mɛ.

ref: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yele yetɩ, a de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na. Sẽŋɛ-ya nẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo la pʋam da basɛ

ref: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
text: Yɛla zozo'e tɩ tõma tarɩ wʋn tɔgɛ yel-ẽŋa yelle, la, la tɔ mɛ tɩ mam pa'alɛ ya a võorɛ tɩ ya wõm, se'ere n soe la, yãma lebge la mã'asga ka wõnna sɛla võorɛ tɔtɔ.

ref: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
text: Lan yuuge paagɛ se'em wã, la seke nɩ mɛ tɩ yãma lebge karẽnsãandõma pa'ala nẽrba basɛba. La baa bɛla, yãma ken bɔɔra tɩ ba zãsna ya la Nawẽnnɛ pɔspɔsɩ yɛla n de nãana, wʋ kɔma n boorɩ bĩ'isum dee ka bɔɔra di-ke'ene na.

ref: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n ken ãna wʋ bia n mõgrɩ bĩ'isum na, a nãn ka tã wʋn bãŋɛ sɛla n masɛ, la sɛla n ka masɛ yelle bɔkɛ.

ref: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
text: La sɛba n de bõnkɛgsɩ la diti la di-ke'ene, bãma n de sɛba n zãsʋm mina sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la be'em bɔkra.

ref: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
text: Bɛla, tɩ sẽŋɛ-ya nẽŋa zãsʋm bõnkɛgsɩ zãsŋɔ, dee basɛ Krisi pɔspɔsɩ yɛla la zãsŋɔ tɩ tɩ dẽŋɛ mina la. Tɩ kãn le zãsna pĩ'iluŋo yɛla la n de tɩ teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero n sẽm na kũm, la tɩ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra,

ref: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
text: la ko'om misga zãsŋɔ, la ban paglɩ nu'usi nẽrba zuto pʋ'ʋsra yelle, la kũm vo'ore, la dũnia ba'asgɔ sarɩyadia yelle.

ref: Let's grow up, if God is willing.
text: Tɩ wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em n bɛla, tɩ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa, Nawẽnnɛ sãn sakɛ.

ref: But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
text: bãma sãn lebe poorʋm dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore, la ka tã tɩ ba le tarɩ ba leme tɩ ba teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-bãma ãn wʋ ba le ka'ara Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la kũm dɔ-puurŋa zuo, dee yaala ẽ nẽrba tẽŋasʋka.

ref: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
text: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ: Saaga sãn niira zozo'e tɩ va'am yũura ko'om sõŋa, a bõnkɔɔla wʋn mãalɛ mɛ bɔ sɛba n kɔɔrɩ ẽ na, bɛla a yẽtɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ mɛ.

ref: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
text: La va'am na sãn yɛsra gõ'osɩ, la mõ-yooro mã'a, a ka tarɩ yõorɔ, ba wʋn kã la be'em bɔ ẽ, la ba'asgɔ ba wʋn yõ ẽ na bugum.

ref: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, baa tõma n tɔgrɩ wãna la, tõma mi tɩ sũure pʋam sɩra sɩra tɩ yãma boe la so-sõŋɔ n wʋn botɩ ya yẽ fãarɛ la zuo.

ref: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
text: Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana, a kãn yĩm ya tõoma, la yãma n pa'alɛ tɩ yãma nõŋɛ ẽ mɛ, tõm bɔ a nẽrba, dee ken tõnna bɔ'ɔra ba lɛɛlɛ wã.

ref: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
text: Bɛla tõma n boorɩ se'em de la ya nẽra woo keŋe a mẽŋa bɛla, sẽnna nẽŋa ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, yẽ bõn-sɛba tɩ a tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura la.

ref: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
text: Tõma ka boorɩ tɩ ya tara zẽyãgrɛ, ɩta-ya wʋ nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, mõrgɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa yẽ sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la. Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ tɩ a bĩŋe la ka viiri

ref: No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham,
text: Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ la Abraham na, a pɔɩ mɛ, la nẽra n ka boe gãnna a mẽŋa la ĩyã, a pɔɩ la a mẽŋa

ref: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
text: yetɩ: «Asɩra, mam wʋn ẽŋɛ fʋ yel-sõnnɛ zozo'e, dee botɩ fʋ yɩɩsɩ yɩrgɛ zozo'e.»

ref: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
text: Tɩ Abraham mõrgɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa, yẽ sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ la.

ref: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
text: Nẽrsaala sãn yetɩ a pɔɩ, a pɔtɩ la sɛla n gãnnɩ a mẽŋa, tɩ pɔtɛ la dɛna sɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ ẽn yele se'em na de la sɩra, tɩ nõke'ene ba'asɛ.

ref: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn boorɩ tɩ a pa'alɛ la sɛba tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ la ba la, tɩ ẽŋa n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na ka viiri, bɛla tɩ a bĩŋe a nõorɛ dee pɔɩ pa'asɛ.

ref: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n ka parnɩ na, n ẽŋɛ bõn-bãma bayi n ka viiri. Bɛla n bɔ'ɔrɩ tõma bʋraanɛ zozo'e, tõma sɛba n basɛ sɛla woo, dee tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla tɩ a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ tɔ la.

ref: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
text: Tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la botɩ tõma zɛ la kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, wʋ ka'aga n ka tẽŋa kẽ paagɛ pʋam pʋam yõkɛ kãŋkãŋɛ la. Bɛla tɩ tõma wʋn kẽ gãŋɛ fu-sɛka n beŋe Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la, paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
text: La de la zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ Azezi dẽŋɛ tõma kẽ, bɔna mĩ tõma ĩyã, dɛna kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na.

ref: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
text: Amelkisedɛkɩ ẽŋa yʋʋn de la Salɛm naba, la Nawẽnnɛ n gãnnɩ wuu kãabgɔ kẽema. Abraham n yʋʋn sẽŋɛ ka zɛbɛ yãŋɛ na'adõma dee lebe kulla la, Amelkisedɛkɩ n tʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ẽ.

ref: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
text: Tɩ Abraham looge lɔg-sɛba wuu tɩ a to'oge la pia pʋam ayɩla bɔ ẽ. Amelkisedɛkɩ yʋ'ʋrɛ la võorɛ de la: «Naba n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ», sɛla n pa'asɛ, a de la Salɛm naba, n boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ: «Sũ-mã'asʋm naba».

ref: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
text: Ba ka mi a sɔ, la a ma, la a yaabdõma. Sɛla ka boe n pa'alɩ a vɔm pĩ'iluŋo, la a vɔm ba'asgɔ, a ãn wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, a de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
text: Bɩsɛ-ya Amelkisedɛkɩ n tarɩ ke'eŋo se'em. Ẽŋa tɩ tɩ yaaba Abraham yʋʋn looge lɔg-sɛba tɩ a zɛbɛ to'oge la pia pʋam ayɩla bɔ ẽ.

ref: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
text: Alevi yɩɩsɩ sɛba n de kãabgɔ kẽma la, Amoyisi lɔɔ la bɔ ba nõorɛ tɩ ba tɔ'ɔsra pia pʋam ayɩla la Abraham yɩɩsɩ n de ba mẽŋa nẽrba la zẽ'am.

ref: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
text: La Amelkisedɛkɩ n dagɩ ba buuri la to'oge pia pʋam wuu ayɩla mɛ Abraham zẽ'am, dee kã kã'a-sõma bɔ Abraham n de sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ ẽ na.

ref: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
text: La de la sɩra, sẽ'eŋɔ ka boe, sɛka n kã kã'a-sõma bɔ nẽra n gãnnɩ sɛka tɩ a kã bɔ ẽ na.

ref: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
text: La sãn dɛna Alevi yɩɩsɩ la n to'osrɩ pia pʋam ayɩla la, ba de la nẽrsaalba n kiiri, la Amelkisedɛkɩ de la sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yetɩ, a vɔa mɛ.

ref: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
text: Tɩ tã wʋn yetɩ, Abraham n yʋʋn looge sɛla tɩ a to'oge la pia pʋam ayɩla bɔ Amelkisedɛkɩ la, Alevi n de sɛka tɩ a buuri to'osrɩ pia pʋrɛ la mẽ bɔ ẽ mɛ.

ref: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Amelkisedɛkɩ n yʋʋn tʋ'ʋsɛ Abraham wakat-sɛka la, tɩ Alevi nãn boe la Abraham zɩɩm pʋam. Azezi n de kãabgɔ kẽema wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ la wakatɛ wuu ĩyã

ref: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
text: Lɔɔ la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bɔ a nẽrba la ẽebɛ la Leviti dõma kãabgɔ tõoma la zuo. Leviti dõma kãabgɔ tõoma la yʋʋn ka tã wʋn basɛ tɩ sɛla woo masɛ ãna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na. A sãn tã nɩ, la ka le de nɩ tɩ kãabgɔ kẽema buuri ayẽma wa'am wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ la dagna Aarõ buuri.

ref: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
text: Bɛla, kãabgɔ kẽma tõoma la sãn teege, dẽnɩ tɩ lɔɔ la mẽ teege.

ref: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yelle tɩ ba tɔgrɩ la, de la buuri ayẽma nẽra, la buur-ẽŋa nẽra nẽra nãn ka tõm kãabgɔ kẽema tõoma.

ref: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
text: La de la vẽelga tɩ tɩ Zuudãana la de la Azuda buuri nẽra, la Amoyisi n yʋʋn tɔgɛ kãabgɔ kẽma tõoma yelle la, a ka po tɔgɛ buur-ẽŋa yelle.

ref: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
text: Kãabgɔ kẽema ayẽma n ãn wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n wa'am na n basɛ tɩ yetɔga la tɩ tõma tɔgrɩ la dɛna vẽelga.

ref: That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end.
text: A ka lebge kãabgɔ kẽema wʋ nẽrsaala dɔgʋm lɔɔ n dolɩ se'em na, la de la vɔm n ka sã'anɩ pãŋa n botɩ a lebge kãabgɔ kẽema.

ref: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
text: Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ la yele yetɩ: «Fʋ de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã, wʋ Amelkisedɛkɩ n ãn se'em na.»

ref: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
text: Bɛla tɩ kʋrʋm nõorɛ la n yele se'em na ka le tõnna, ẽn ka tarɩ pãŋa, dee ka tara yõorɔ la ĩyã.

ref: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Amoyisi lɔɔ la ka tã'agɛ basɛ tɩ sɛla sɛla masɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na. La lɛɛlɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ ẽŋɛ la so-paalɛ, tɩ tõma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla n ãn sõŋɔ gãnna la basɛ tɩ tõma lɛnna Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
text: La Azezi n lebge kãabgɔ kẽema la, Nawẽnnɛ pɔɩ mɛ yetɩ: «Mam n de Zuudãana la pɔɩ mɛ, mam kãn viige m nõorɛ. Fʋ de la kãabgɔ kẽema wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.»

ref: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
text: La Azezi vɔa la wakatɛ wuu, nẽra ka sɔɔrɩ a kãabgɔ kẽema tõoma la tõnna.

ref: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
text: La de la bɛla tɩ a tãna wʋn fãagɛ sɛba wuu n doose la ẽŋa lɛnna Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, wakatɛ wuu ĩyã. Se'ere n soe la, a vɔa mɛ wakatɛ wuu yõkra Nawẽnnɛ sɩa bɔ'ɔra ba.

ref: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
text: La de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n ãn wãna n masɛ tɩ tɩ tara: A de la pʋpeelem dãana, ka tara yelle, ka tara dõrga, a welge a mẽŋa mɛ la tõon-be'ero dõma, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ saazuum-zuum.

ref: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
text: Ẽŋa ka ãn wʋ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma basɛba la, tɩ daarɛ woo la dɛna ba dẽŋɛ kãabɛ ba mẽŋa tõon-be'ero ĩyã, dee yãŋa kãabɛ nẽrba tõon-be'ero ĩyã la. Ẽŋa dɩkɛ la a mẽŋa kãabɛ nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a bɔ nẽrba tõon-be'ero ĩyã.

ref: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
text: Amoyisi lɔɔ la yʋʋn loorɩ la nẽrsaalba pãŋa n pɔ'ɔgɛ tɩ ba dɛna kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadõma. La Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe a nõorɛ dee pɔɩ bɛla poorʋm na, a looge la a Dayʋa n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.

ref: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
text: Tõma yetɔga la tɩ tõma tɔgrɩ la zuo n wãna: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, ẽŋa n zĩ saazuum Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo dãana la na'am bĩmbĩnne zʋʋgɔ.

ref: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
text: Ẽŋa n boe zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la pʋam tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, zẽ'e-ẽŋa de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa yire, la dagɩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ nẽrsaalba tĩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na.

ref: Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer.
text: Ba loorɩ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana woo tɩ a bɔ'ɔra la Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a, la a kãabra bɔ'ɔra ẽ. Bɛla tɩ la mẽ masɛ tɩ ẽŋa tara sɛla bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
text: Azezi sãn boe nɩ na tẽŋa wã zuo, a ka de nɩ kãabgɔ kẽema. Se'ere n soe la, kãabgɔ kẽma põn bɔna mɛ bɔ'ɔra bɔ'a wʋ Amoyisi lɔɔ la n de se'em na.

ref: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
text: Bãma n tõnnɩ tõon-sɛka la de la sɛla n ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire la wõnnɛ, la a mãmsgɔ. La ãn wʋ Amoyisi n yʋʋn yetɩ a tĩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ sõŋa sõŋa tɩ fʋ wʋn tĩ ẽ tɩ a ãna wʋ mam n pa'alɛ fʋ tãŋa zuo se'em na.»

ref: Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, Azezi tarɩ la tõon-keko gãnna bãma. Nõorɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe la tõma Azezi ĩyã la n ãn sõŋa gãnna, se'ere n soe la, sɛla tɩ a yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ tɔ nõor-bĩŋre ẽna pʋam na n ãn sõŋa gãnna.

ref: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
text: Pɔspɔsɩ nõor-bĩŋre la yʋʋn sãn ka gelge nɩ, la ka de nɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ bʋyi dãana.

ref: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ zɛrgɛ ba mɛ, bɛla tɩ a yele yetɩ: «Dabsa n sẽm tɩ mam wʋn bĩŋe nõor-paalga la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma, la Zuda dõma lɛ.

ref: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
text: La kãn ãna wʋ nõor-bĩŋre sɛka tɩ mam yʋʋn bĩŋe la ba yaabdõma wakat-sɛka tɩ mam gʋrɛ ba nu'usi tarɩ ba Ezipti yese la. Ba yʋʋn ka doose mam nõorɛ la tɩ mam bĩŋe yetɩ ba ẽŋɛ se'em na, bɛla n soe tɩ mam basɛ ba. Mam n de Zuudãana la n yele.

ref: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Mam n yetɩ m bĩŋe m nõorɛ se'em la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma dabsa bãma poorʋm n wãna: Mam wʋn dɩkɛ m lɔɔ la ẽŋɛ la ba pʋtẽ'era pʋam, mam wʋn gʋlsɛ ba la ba sũure pʋam. Mam wʋn dɛna ba Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ ba dɛna mam nẽrba.

ref: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
text: Nẽra nẽra kãn le zãsʋm a tadãana bɩɩ a sɔbia yele ẽ tɩ a bãŋɛ Zuudãana la, se'ere n soe la, ba nẽrba wuu wʋn bãŋɛ mam mɛ, halɩ lan pɔsɛ la kɔm-pɩgsɩ ka paagɛ bõnkɛgsɩ.

ref: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
text: Lan de se'em na, mam wʋn basɛ ba koŋre la taalɛ mɛ, mam kãn le tẽra ba tõon-be'ero la yɛla.»

ref: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n tɔgrɩ nõor-bĩŋre paalga yelle la, a botɩ pɔspɔsɩ dãana la dɛna la kɛka. La sɛla n lebge kɛka kʋrgɛ deege la a bɔɩ.

ref: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
text: Pɔspɔsɩ nõor-bĩŋre la yʋʋn tarɩ la zẽ'e-sõŋɔ tẽŋa zuo, la sɔa tɩ ba dɔla pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
text: Ba yʋʋn tĩ la fuu-deego beŋe zẽ'esɩ bayi, wi'ira pɔspɔsɩ zẽ'a la tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ. Bilam, fɩtladõma dɔglgɔ zẽ'a, la taablɩ tɩ ba dɔglɩ borborɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ n boe mĩ.

ref: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
text: Kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne n de sãlma tɩ ba yõ'orɩ tɩdarɛ a zuo n boe mĩ, pa'asɛ la nõor-bĩŋre daka tɩ ba dɩkɛ sãlma yũlge a wuu. Sãlma lɔkɔ tɩ mannɩ boe a pʋam, la Aarõ dasaarɛ n puse võorɔ la, la kug-palagsɩ sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ nõorɛ se'em na gʋlsɛ ba zuto la n boe daka la pʋam.

ref: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
text: Daka la zuo, ba ẽŋɛ la malɛkadõma tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ seribẽ n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa ẽŋɛ mĩ, tɩ ba kempagsɩ yɛrgɛ tɩ ba mã'asʋm pĩ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba ɩtɩ zɩɩm mĩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basra nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ la. La lɛɛlɛ wã, mam kãn wɛgsɛ bõn-bãma yelle pa'alɛ ya.

ref: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
text: Sɛla woo mãasʋm na bɛla bɔna a zẽ'a, tɩ kãabgɔ kẽma la kẽ'era pɔspɔsɩ deego la pʋam daarɛ woo tõnna ba tõoma.

ref: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
text: La deego bʋyi dãana la pʋam, kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la mã'a n kẽ'erɩ mĩ nõorɛ ayɩla yʋʋnɛ pʋam. A sãn kẽ'era mĩ, dẽnɩ tɩ a tara dũnsi zɩɩm ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, a mẽŋa tuure, la nẽrba tuure ĩyã.

ref: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa pa'alɩ tɩ pɔspɔsɩ deego la n daan ken bɔna la, sore ka pakɛ tɩ nẽra tãna wʋn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la pʋam.

ref: This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices.
text: Bɛla de la mãmsgɔ pa'ala tõma bõn-sɛba n boe lɛɛlɛ wã võorɛ. Bɔ'a, la dũnsi tɩ ba kãabrɩ bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ na, ka tã wʋn basɛ tɩ sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na sũure masɛ a zẽ'am.

ref: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
text: Ba de la dia, la bõn-yũula, la ko'om peere buuri to'oto'ore yelle mã'a. Ɩrgɔ bãma de la ĩyã yɛla n de dũnia wã bõnɔ n yʋʋn boe tɩ nẽrba dɔla, halɩ wa paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ malɩn malgɛ sɛla woo sõŋa. Azezi-krisi ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõor-paalga

ref: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
text: La Krisi n wa'am na, a de la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana, ẽŋa n bɔ tõma bõn-sõma tɩ tɩ tarɩ wã, ẽŋa n kẽ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam n gãnnɩ na, la dagɩ fuugo deego tɩ nẽrsaalba tĩ, a dagɩ dũnia wã bõnɔ.

ref: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
text: A daan kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la pʋam na nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a wakatɛ wuu ĩyã. La dagɩ bʋʋsɩ zɩɩm, la lɔl-pɔla zɩɩm tɩ a dɩkɛ kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, a dɩkɛ la a mẽŋa zɩɩm kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ fãagɛ tõma wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.

ref: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
text: Ba yʋʋn tarɩ la bʋʋsɩ, la lɔlsɩ zɩɩm, la na-sa'arɛ tɩ ba yõ tãmpɛglʋm mĩisra nẽr-sɛba n sã'am lɔɔ la, tɩ ba le malgɛ ba tɩ ba ãna sõŋa masɛ la lɔɔ la.

ref: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
text: Krisi zɩɩm na tarɩ pãŋa mɛ gãnna bɛla zozo'e. Ẽŋa ka tarɩ tuure, la doose la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe wakatɛ wuu tɩ a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, peege tɩ tõon-be'ero n sẽm na kũm na, tɩ tɩ sũure ãna sõŋa, tɩ tɩ tõnna bɔ'ɔra vɔm dãana Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings!
text: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõor-paalga ẽŋa ĩyã. Ẽn ki la ĩyã, a yɔ fãagɛ nẽr-sɛba n tuuge pɔspɔsɩ nõorɛ bĩŋre la pʋam mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ba la, ba wʋn to'oge bõn-sõma tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ a nẽrba la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ ĩyã.

ref: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
text: Nẽra sãn pa'alɛ ba wʋn pʋɩ ẽŋa bõntarsõm se'em ẽŋa kũm poorʋm, dẽnɩ tɩ a ki dee tɩ ba yãŋa pʋɩ ba.

ref: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
text: Nẽra la sãn ken vɔa, a nõorɛ la tɩ a bɔ la de la yoogo. La de la a kũm poorʋm tɩ a tarɩ yõorɔ.

ref: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
text: La mẽ de la bɛla baa la Nawẽnnɛ pɔspɔsɩ nõor-bĩŋre la, la de la dũnsi zɩɩm n yese tɩ a pɔsɛ tõnna.

ref: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
text: Amoyisi n yʋʋn tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ wuu pa'alɛ nẽrba wuu wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em kɩ'ɩlɩm na, a yãŋa dɩkɛ la lɔl-pɔla, la bʋʋsɩ zɩɩm, la ko'om, dɩkɛ pesgo kõbrɔ n ãn mõlga, vile izɔpɩ võorɔ lʋsɛ ba pʋam, mĩise nõor-bĩŋre gõŋɔ la, la nẽrba la wuu,

ref: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
text: dee yele yetɩ: «Zɩɩ-bãna wã n pa'alɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la ya lɛ tɩ ya sakɛ ẽ.»

ref: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
text: Amoyisi yʋʋn ken dɩkɛ zɩɩm na mĩise fuu-deego la, la pʋ'ʋsgɔ lɔgrɔ la wuu mɛ.

ref: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
text: La sãn dɛna lɔɔ la sore, la ãn wʋ la de la sɛla woo tɩ zɩɩm malgrɩ tɩ ba le ãna sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Tɩ zɩɩm sãn ka yese, Nawẽnnɛ ka basrɩ nẽrba tõon-be'ero taalɛ. Krisi kũm n yese nẽrba tõon-be'ero basɛ

ref: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
text: Lan yʋʋn masɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ dũnsi zɩɩm malgɛ bõn-sɛba n mãmsrɩ sɛla n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la, tɩ ba le ãna sõŋa bɛla la, la sãn dɛna bõnɔ la mẽŋa n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la, dẽnɩ tɩ la dɛna kãabgɔ n ãn sõŋa gãnna bɛla.

ref: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
text: Krisi n kẽ zẽ'e-sɛka la, dagɩ deego tɩ nẽrsaala ẽŋɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n mãmsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la, a kẽ la Nawẽnnɛ yire mẽŋa, bɔna Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam tõma sõŋrɛ ĩyã.

ref: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
text: Krisi ka kẽ nõorɛ faa wʋ kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana la n kẽ'erɩ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la yʋʋnɛ woo la zɩɩm n dagɩ a mẽŋa zɩɩm na, a kẽ la nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
text: La sãn dagɩ nɩ bɛla, a daan wʋn nãmsɛ nɩ mɛ nõorɛ faa, halɩ lan pɔsɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo wa paam zĩna. La sɛla n de sɩra, a wa'am na nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ wã, tɩ a yese tõon-be'ero, ẽn dɩkɛ a mẽŋa kãabɛ la ĩyã.

ref: We die only once, and then we are judged.
text: Nẽra woo kiiri la bʋyɩla, dee tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dita a sarɩya.

ref: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
text: Bɛla mẽ, Krisi dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ la nõorɛ ayɩla yese nẽrba zozo'e tõon-be'ero basɛ. A wʋn le leme wa'am mɛ nõorɛ bʋyi pʋam, la, la kãn dɛna tõon-be'ero yɛsga ĩyã, la de la nẽr-sɛba n gu'uri ẽ tɩ ba yẽ fãarɛ la ĩyã.

ref: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
text: Amoyisi lɔɔ la yɛla yʋʋn mãmsrɩ la bõn-sõma n sẽm na mã'a, ba ka de sɛla n de sɩra la mẽŋa mẽŋa. A botɩ nẽrba kãabra la kãabgɔ bãma mẽŋa yʋʋnɛ woo bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ ka ba'asra. La a ka tã wʋn botɩ nẽr-sɛba n kãabrɩ bɛla la lɛm Nawẽnnɛ masɛ sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
text: Sɛba n ɩtɩ bɛla pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ na, sãn malgɛ nɩ mɛ ãna sõŋa wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, ba kãn le tẽ'esra tɩ tõon-be'ero boe bãma zuto, la ba wʋn basɛ mɛ kãn le kãabra.

ref: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
text: Se'ere n soe la, lɔlsɩ, la bʋʋsɩ zɩɩm ka tã wʋn yese tõon-be'ero basɛ.

ref: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ Krisi n daan sẽm dũnia zuo la, a daan yele Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Fõn ka boorɩ kãabgɔ, la bɔ'a, la fõn ẽŋɛ la ĩyã bɔ mam.

ref: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
text: Kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba yõ'orɩ bĩmbĩnne zuo, la kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba kãabrɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã la, ka ɩtɩ fõn sũure yẽlʋm.

ref: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
text: Bɛla tɩ mam yetɩ: ‹Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, mam n wãna n yetɩ m ẽŋɛ fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ mam yelle fʋ gõŋɔ pʋam se'em na.› »

ref: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
text: A dẽŋɛ yetɩ: «Fõn ka boorɩ kãabgɔ, la bɔ'a, la kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba yõ'orɩ bĩmbĩnne zuo, la kãabgɔ sɛka tɩ ba kãabrɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã, la ka ɩtɩ fõn sũure yẽlʋm.» La kãabgɔ bãma, ba kãabrɩ ba wʋ lɔɔ la n yele se'em na.

ref: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
text: Dee le yetɩ: «Mam n wãna n yetɩ m ẽŋɛ fõn boorɩ tɩ m ẽŋɛ se'em na.» A saam pɔspɔsɩ kãabgɔ la mɛ dee vigle bʋyi dãana.

ref: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
text: Azezi-krisi ẽŋɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, a dɩkɛ la a mẽŋa kãabɛ bɔ ẽ nõorɛ ayɩla wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, tɩ tõma welge to'ore dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba.

ref: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
text: Kãabgɔ kẽema woo ze la a tõoma zẽ'am daarɛ woo tõnna, la a kãabrɩ la kãabgɔ bãma mẽŋa nõorɛ faa, dee tɩ la ka tãna tõon-be'ero yɛsra.

ref: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
text: La Krisi dɩkɛ a mẽŋa kãabɛ la nõorɛ ayɩla bɔ yese nẽrba tõon-be'ero wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, dee sẽŋɛ ka zẽa Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ,

ref: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
text: gu'ura tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wa ẽŋɛ a bɛ'ɛba tɩ ba bɔna a nãma tẽŋa.

ref: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
text: Bɛla, kãabgɔ bʋyɩla la mã'a tɩ a welge nẽrba to'ore tɩ ba dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba masɛ sɛla woo pʋam wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mẽ pa'alɛ tõma tɩ bõn-ẽŋa de la sɩra, se'ere n soe la, a yele yetɩ:

ref: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
text: «Mam n yetɩ m bĩŋe m nõorɛ se'em la ba dabsa bãma poorʋm n wãna: Mam wʋn dɩkɛ m lɔɔ la ẽŋɛ la ba sũure pʋam, mam wʋn gʋlsɛ ba ba pʋtẽ'era pʋam. Mam n de Zuudãana n yele.»

ref: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
text: Dee ken yetɩ: «Mam kãn le tẽra ba tõon-be'ero, la ba tuure la yelle.»

ref: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tõon-be'ero taalɛ la, la ka le nara tɩ ba kãabra tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã. Tɩ zɛ-ya kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ doosgo pʋam

ref: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
text: M sɔbiisi, tõma mi tɩ Azezi zɩɩm na ĩyã, tõma tarɩ nõorɛ mɛ tɩ tɩ kẽ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ mẽŋa mẽŋa la pʋam.

ref: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
text: Azezi n dɩkɛ a mẽŋa ĩyã kãabɛ bɔ la, a ãagɛ fuugo la n beŋe tõma la Nawẽnnɛ na mɛ, yo'oge so-paalɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm bɔ tõma.

ref: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
text: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana n bɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba yelle.

ref: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ lɛm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am la pʋpeelem, la sũure n bɔ sɩra zõnzõnnɔ, la sũure n peege tɩ tɩ mina tɩ tõon-be'ero ka le bɔna, la ĩyã n peege la ko-sõma.

ref: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
text: Tɩ pa'alɛ-ya tɩn tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la da basɛ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ de la sɩra dãana, a wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

ref: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
text: Tɩ bɩsra taaba yelle wʋn kɛŋra taaba, tɩ tɩ tara nõŋlʋm, dee tõnna tõon-sõma.

ref: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
text: Tɩ da basɛ taaba lagsgɔ wʋ nẽrba basɛba n ɩtɩ se'em na, tɩ bɔ'ɔra-ya taaba bʋraanɛ, yãma n yẽ tɩ Azezi lemŋo daarɛ la lɛm mɛ na.

ref: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la, a sãn mina dee ken tõnna tõon-be'ero la yɛmleego, kãabgɔ ka le bɔna tõon-be'ero yɛsga ĩyã.

ref: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
text: Sɛla n yãŋa deege de la nẽr-ẽŋa gu'ura Nawẽnnɛ sarɩya, la bugum n wʋn di sɛba n sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ na, la dabeem.

ref: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
text: La sãn dɛna la Amoyisi lɔɔ la, nẽra sãn sã'am lɔɔ la, tɩ nẽrba bayi, bɩɩ batã bɔna mĩ dɛna kaset-dõma, ninbãalga ka boe, dẽnɩ tɩ a dãana ki.

ref: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
text: La sãn dɛna bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n kɛsɛ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la, kãn yẽ sɩbgrɛ n de be'em halɩ gãnna bɛla bɩɩ? Nẽr-ẽŋa bɩsɛ Azezi zɩɩm na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe a nõorɛ a ĩyã la zãŋa. La, la de la zɩɩm ẽŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ botɩ nẽra la welge to'ore dɛna Nawẽnnɛ nẽra. Ẽŋa dãana ken pɔ'ɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n de yel-sõnnɛ dãana la mɛ.

ref: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
text: Tõma mi Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ: «Mam n de m lebse be'em, la de la mam n wʋn yɔ.» La ken yetɩ: «Zuudãana la n wʋn di a nẽrba sarɩya.»

ref: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
text: La de la yelle mẽŋa mẽŋa bɔ sɛka n lui Nawẽnnɛ n de vɔm dãana la nu'usum.

ref: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
text: Ba daan tʋʋrɩ dee nãmsra ya nẽrba basɛba mɛ nẽrba zẽ'am, la yãma naagɛ la sɛba tɩ ba nãmsrɩ la mɛ sõŋɛ ba.

ref: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
text: La yãma zoe sɛba n boe yʋ'a deem na ninbãalga mɛ. La ban fã yãma lɔgrɔ la, yãma sakɛ mɛ koŋe ba la sũ-yẽlga, yãma n mi tɩ yãma tarɩ la bõntarsõm sõma n gãnnɩ bɛla n kãn sã'am na ĩyã.

ref: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
text: Bɛla, da basɛ-ya sũ-ke'eŋo sɛka tɩ ya tarɩ la, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ yɔɔrɔ zozo'e.

ref: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
text: Yãma de la ya nargɛ pãŋa sẽŋɛ nẽŋa ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, tɩ ya wʋn to'oge sɛla tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ya la.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
text: La gʋlsɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «La deege la fẽe, fẽfẽe mã'a, tɩ sɛka n sẽm na wʋn wa'am, a kãn yuuge.

ref: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
text: Nẽr-sɛka n bɔ mam sɩra masɛ mam zẽ'am wʋn vɔa, ẽn bɔ mam sɩra la ĩyã. La sɛka n lebe poorʋm, mam sũure kãn ãna yẽlʋm la a dãana.»

ref: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
text: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma dagɩ sɛba n lebrɩ poorʋm wʋn sã'am na, tõma de la sɛba n bɔ sɩra wʋn yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
text: Bɔ sɩra de la fõn mi sɩra sɩra tɩ fõn tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la, fʋ wʋn yẽ, a botɩ fʋ mina la sɛla n ka yẽtɩ la nini.

ref: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
text: La de la kʋrʋm kʋrʋm dõma n yʋʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a tɔgɛ ba yelle sõŋa.

ref: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
text: La de la tõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ tõma bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ la a yetɔga nãam tẽŋa la saazuum, bɛla tɩ tõma mina tɩ sɛla n yẽtɩ wã ze'ele la sɛla n ka yẽtɩ pʋam.

ref: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
text: Abɛlɩ n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a yʋʋn kãabɛ kãabgɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ gãnna Akãɩ kãabgɔ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la bɔ sɩra la ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ to'oge a bɔ'a la, dee yetɩ a masɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am. Abɛlɩ n bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, baa ẽn ki la, a yelle ken tɔgra mɛ.

ref: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
text: La de la A'enõkɩ n bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn zẽkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ a yire, tɩ a ka ki, tɩ ba yʋʋn ka le yẽ ẽ, Nawẽnnɛ n zẽkɛ ẽ na ĩyã. Lan dẽŋɛ a zẽkrɛ la, la gʋlsɛ a yelle yetɩ, a paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm mɛ.

ref: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
text: Nẽra sãn ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, a ka tã wʋn paagɛ a yɛm. Nẽra sãn lɛnna Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔ sɩra tɩ a boe mɛ, la a yɔɔrɩ sɛba n eerɩ ẽ mɛ.

ref: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
text: Anoe n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a zoe Nawẽnnɛ sakɛ a nõorɛ. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn pa'alɛ ẽ na bõn-sɛba n wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ nẽrba nãn ka yẽtɩ, tɩ a sakɛ mãalɛ õorŋɔ kãtɛ, tɩ ẽŋa la a yir-dõma kẽ mĩ yẽ fãarɛ. Bɛla a pa'alɛ tɩ dũnia dõma n ka sakɛ la koŋe bʋʋrɔ mɛ, dee tɩ ẽŋa masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, ẽn bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã.

ref: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
text: Abraham n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a yʋʋn sakɛ a wi'a, yese wɛ'ɛsa tẽn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ na bɔbrɛ, dee ka mina ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'e-sɛka la.

ref: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
text: Abraham n bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, a yʋʋn sẽŋɛ ka kẽ'era tẽn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ẽ na pʋam wʋ sãana la, yʋʋn tĩ fuu-deego bɔna mĩ. A'izakɩ, la Azakɔbɩ mẽ yʋʋn kẽ'erɩ la fuu-deto pʋam. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn bĩŋe nõorɛ bɔ la bãma wuu.

ref: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Abraham yʋʋn tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tẽn-sɛka ẽbrɛ n ẽebɛ sõŋa, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa dɛna sɛka n makɛ dee mẽ.

ref: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
text: La de la Asaara n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ baa ẽn yʋʋn de kõndɔgrɛ dee kʋrgɛ zo'oge la, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ mɛ tɩ a dɔgɛ bia, se'ere n soe la, a bɔ la sɩra tɩ sɛka n bĩŋe a nõorɛ la, wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽn yele se'em na mɛ.

ref: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Bɛla n soe, tɩ bʋraaga ayɩla n yʋʋn kʋrgɛ ka zãagɛ la kũm, dɔgɛ yɩɩsɩ tɩ ba zo'oge ãna wʋ wõrbiɛ n boe saazuum, bɩɩ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo n boe ko-kãtɛ nõorʋm tɩ nẽrba ka tã wʋn kãalɛ ba.

ref: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
text: Nẽr-bãma wuu ki la ba bɔ sɩra la pʋam. Ba yʋʋn ka to'oge bõn-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ba la, la ba yʋʋn tũule yẽ ba la halɩ zãazãarɛ, tɩ ba sũure ãna yẽlʋm. La ba bãŋɛ mɛ yele tɩ bãma de la sãama ka tara zẽ'a dũnia zuo.

ref: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
text: Sɛba n tɔgrɩ wãna la pa'alɩ tɩ ba eerɩ la tẽn-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn wi'ira ba mẽŋa tẽŋa.

ref: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
text: Ba yʋʋn sãn tẽ'esra tẽn-sɛka tɩ ba yese dee basɛ la yelle tɩ a sɩa tara ba, ba tarɩ nɩ sore wʋn lebe bilam mɛ.

ref: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
text: Lan de se'em na, ba yʋʋn eerɩ la tẽn-sɛka n ãn sõŋa gãnna. Tẽn-ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ yire. Bɛla n soe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ka zoe yãnnɛ tɩ ba wi'ira ẽ tɩ ba Nawẽnnɛ, la de la ẽŋa n mãasʋm tẽŋa bɔ ba.

ref: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ: «La wʋn doose la A'izakɩ tɩ fʋ yẽ yɩɩsɩ tɩ mam bĩŋe nõorɛ la.»

ref: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
text: Abraham yʋʋn tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tã wʋn vo'oge A'izakɩ mɛ. Bɛla n soe tɩ Nawẽnnɛ lebse bɔ ẽ a dayʋa. La ãn wʋ a vo'oge ẽ mɛ na, yel-ẽna pʋam mãmsgɔ n boe mĩ.

ref: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
text: Azakɔbɩ n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ẽn yʋʋn wa yɔna kũm na, tɩ a kã kã'a-sõma bɔ Azozɛfʋ dayɔɔsɩ bayi, tike a dasaarɛ ka dũma nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
text: Azozɛfʋ n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ẽn yʋʋn wa dee kũm na, tɩ a yele Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma yetɩ, ba wʋn wa yese Ezipti tẽŋa mɛ, dee pa'alɛ ba wʋn wa ẽŋɛ ẽŋa kõba se'em.

ref: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
text: Amoyisi dɔgrɩba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ba yʋʋn dɔgɛ ẽ, dɩkɛ ẽ sʋgɛ wõr-sɩtã. Ba yẽ mɛ tɩ a de la bia n ãn sõŋa, tɩ ba ka zoe dabeem la naba la n bɔ nõorɛ se'em na.

ref: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
text: Amoyisi n yʋʋn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ẽn wa lebge bõnkɛka la, tɩ a zagsɛ tɩ ba da wi'ira ẽŋa tɩ naba Afaarõ pɔyʋa la dayʋa.

ref: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
text: A yʋʋn boorɩ mɛ tɩ a lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la po nãmsra, gãnna ẽŋa n ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga la sɛla n de be'em wakatɛ fẽfẽe.

ref: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
text: A bɩsɛ mɛ tɩ ba sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽŋa wʋ ba wʋn pɔ'ɔgɛ Krisi la, n tarɩ yõorɔ zo'oge gãnna Ezipti tẽŋa bõntarsõm wuu. Se'ere n soe la, a yʋʋn tarɩ la pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura yɔɔrɔ n boe nẽŋa.

ref: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
text: Ẽn bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a ze'ele Ezipti fõrgɛ dee ka zoe naba la sũ-isgre, a yʋʋn mõrgɛ mɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, se'ere n soe la, a yẽ la Nawẽnnɛ tɩ ba ka yẽtɩ la nini.

ref: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
text: La ẽn bɔ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ a botɩ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma kʋ Zɔɔg-basɛ pebila ẽŋɛ zɩɩm mĩise deto nõa, tɩ malɛka sɛka n wʋn kʋ kɔm-kẽendõma la, da kʋ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma kɔm-kẽendõma.

ref: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
text: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ mõg-mõlga la ko'om yʋʋn welge dee basɛ tẽn-kɛ'ɛŋa tɩ ba doose tole, tɩ Ezipti dõma mẽ paagɛ doose tɩ ba tole, tɩ ko'om di ba.

ref: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
text: Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ba yʋʋn sẽŋɛ kaagɛ Zeriko tẽŋa dabsa ayopɔɩ, tɩ a lalsɩ lui.

ref: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
text: Yɛla zozo'e boe mɛ tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ nɩ, la nẽŋa ka boe tɩ mam wʋn tɔgɛ Azedeyõ, la Abarakɩ, la Asãmsõ, la Azefte, la Adavidi, la Asãmwɛlɩ, la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la yelle.

ref: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
text: La de la ban bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la ĩyã, tɩ ba yãŋɛ na'adõma to'ose ba so'olʋm, ba tõm sɛla n masɛ mɛ, ba yẽ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe nõorɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ mɛ, ba mũm gɩgma nõa mɛ.

ref: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
text: La ba kĩŋe bugum n diti la pãŋa mɛ, ba põsɛ sʋkɔbgɔ kũm mɛ, ba yẽ pãŋa mɛ ba tɔrgɔ pʋam, dɛna gãndɔɔrɔ zɛbrɛ zẽ'a, zɛbɛ yãŋɛ satẽma zɛbzɛbrɩba dige ba.

ref: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
text: Pɔgsɩ yẽ ba nẽrba n ki mɛ dee vo'oge. Sɛba yẽ nãmsgɔ mɛ zozo'e dee zagsɛ yolsgo, se'ere n soe la, ba boorɩ tɩ ba wa vo'oge mɛ yẽ vɔm n ãn sõŋa gãnna.

ref: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
text: Ba la'arɩ sɛba mɛ, wẽ ba la kafõbsɩ, ba ba sɛba mɛ la bãnsɩ kẽ'esɛ yʋ'a deem.

ref: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
text: Ba lobe sɛba la kuga kʋ, wã sɛba la sɔya kʋ, sɛ basɛba la sʋkɔbrɔ kʋ. Tɩ sɛba ɛɛra yɛ'ɛna, yɛ piisi gãna bɩɩ bʋʋsɩ gãna, sakɛ ka tara sɛla sɛla, tɩ ba fɔ'ɔna ba, nãmsra ba.

ref: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
text: Ba yʋʋn eerɩ la weem, la tãnsɩ zuto, gã tãnsɩ yogro, la tẽŋa yogro pʋam. Dũnia ka narɩ tɩ a tara nẽr-bãma.

ref: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
text: Nẽr-bãma wuu, Nawẽnnɛ tɔgɛ ba yelle sõŋa mɛ ban bɔ ẽ sɩra la ĩyã, la baa bɛla ba ka to'oge Nawẽnnɛ n bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ba sɛla la.

ref: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn põn dẽŋɛ looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ la sɛla n ãn sõŋa gãnna bɔ tõma, bɛla tɩ a ka botɩ ba masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tɩ tõma ka naagɛ la ba.

ref: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
text: Bɛla, tõma n tarɩ nẽrba zozo'e n gilge tõma pa'ala tɩ sore la, tɩ mẽ basɛ bõn-sɛba n gu'uri tɩ sẽnnɛ, la tõon-be'ero tɩ tɩ ɩtɩ nãana nãana la, dee mõrgɛ zɔta zʋa n boe tɩ nẽŋa na.

ref: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
text: Tɩ bɩsra-ya Azezi, ẽŋa n botɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra, dee wʋn tarɩ tɩ paagɛ a ba'asgɔ zẽ'am. A wĩbe mɛ ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo ka zoe yãnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, a yẽ sũ-yẽlga sɛka n gu'uri ẽ na mɛ, la a yãŋa zĩ la Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne zʋʋgɔ.

ref: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
text: Tẽ'esɛ-ya bɩsɛ, tõon-be'ero dõma n sɩ'ɩsɛ ẽ, nãmsɛ ẽ se'em, tɩ a wĩbe, tɩ ya da basɛ tɩ ya ĩyã ki tɩ ya bʋraanɛ bo'oge.

ref: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
text: Yãma n zɛbrɩ la tõon-be'ero la, ya ken ka zɛbɛ ki.

ref: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
text: Da yĩm-ya kã'aŋɔ sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã'an ya wʋ nẽra n kã'anɩ a kɔma se'em yetɩ: «M bia, Zuudãana sãn sɩbgɛ fɔ, da dɩkɛ tɩ la dɛna zãŋa, a sãn pa'alɛ fɔ fʋ tuure, da basɛ tɩ fʋ bʋraanɛ bo'oge.

ref: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana demsrɩ la sɛba tɩ a nõŋɛ, dee sɩbgra sɛka woo tɩ a dɩkɛ tɩ a dɛna a bia.»

ref: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
text: Wĩbra-ya, tɩ la de la ya demsgo, Nawẽnnɛ ɩtɩ ya la a kɔma ɩrgɔ. Bia ka boe tɩ a sɔ ka sɩbgra ẽ.

ref: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
text: Nawẽnnɛ sãn ka demse ya wʋ ẽn ɩtɩ a kɔma wuu se'em na, bɛla ya yi de la tãmpĩiba, ya dagɩ a kɔma.

ref: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
text: La sãn dɛna tɩ sɔdõma n dɔgɛ tɩ la, ba daan sɩbgrɩ tɩ mɛ, dee tɩ tɩ nanna ba. Bɛla, tɩ sakɛ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nõorɛ gãnna, tɩ tɩ wʋn yẽ vɔm a zẽ'am.

ref: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
text: Tɩ sɔdõma la daan sɩbgrɩ tɩ la wakatɛ fẽfẽe ĩyã wʋ bãma mẽŋa n tẽ'esɛ se'em na. La Nawẽnnɛ sɩbgrɩ tɩ la tõma mẽŋa sõŋrɛ ĩyã, tɩ a botɩ tɩ welge to'ore dɛna pʋpeelem dõma wʋ ẽŋa la.

ref: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
text: Wakatɛ sɛka tɩ ba sɩbgrɩ tõma la, la de la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la ka de sũ-yẽlga. La bɛla poorʋm nẽr-sɛba n yẽ sɩbgrɛ ẽŋa la yẽtɩ a yõorɔ mɛ, n de sũ-mã'asʋm, la vɔm n masɛ ãna sõŋa. Tɩ mõrgɛ da zagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ

ref: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
text: Bɛla, keŋe-ya ya nu'usi n targɛ, la ya dũma n ki la.

ref: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya nãma la sẽnna sorɔɔrɔ n de tẽntẽesɩ zuto, tɩ nẽr-sɛka n dũ'usri la da lui, a wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
text: Mõrgɛ-ya ɛ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna yãma la nẽrba wuu tẽŋasʋka, la ya ɛ pʋpeelem. Tɩ pʋpeelem sãn ka bɔna, nẽra ka tã wʋn yẽ Zuudãana la.

ref: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
text: Guuse-ya tɩ ya nẽra nẽra da wa mõŋɛ a mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ, ken guuse-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka da ãna wʋ yẽg-toogo n puse ya tẽŋasʋka, wʋn basɛ tɩ toogo la loŋe nẽrba zozo'e sã'am ba.

ref: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽra nẽra ɩta yalsɩ, bɩɩ nẽra n pɔ'ɔrɩ sɛla n de Nawẽnnɛ bõnɔ, wʋ A'eso n yʋʋn dɩkɛ a kẽnnɔ bõnɔ teege dia laaga ayɩla la.

ref: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
text: La yãma mi tɩ bɛla poorʋm, ẽn wa bɔɔra tɩ a sɔ kã kã'a-sõma bɔ ẽ, ẽŋa n de kẽema ĩyã la, a ka tã'agɛ. A ka yẽ sore yãŋɛ teege yelle la tɩ a ẽŋɛ la, baa ẽn kellɩ la.

ref: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
text: Yãma n lɛm Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am se'em na, de la to'ore la Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma n yʋʋn lɛm sɛla tɩ ba tã wʋn kalʋm dee yẽ zẽ'am na. Bãma yʋʋn lɛm na tãŋa yẽ bugum n diti, la lika n ãn lim, la kʋsebgo kãtɛ.

ref: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
text: La ba wõm na benkonne peebgo, la kõa n tɔgɛ, tɩ nẽrba la n yʋʋn wõm na belem yetɩ, la da le tɔgɛ pa'asɛ.

ref: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
text: Se'ere n soe la, ba ka tã wʋn to'oge ẽn yele se'em yetɩ: «Sɛka sãn kalʋm tãŋa la, baa a sãn dɛna dũŋa, ba lobe ẽ na kuga kʋ» la.

ref: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
text: Bõn-sɛba n yʋʋn ẽŋɛ la bɔ'ɔrɩ la dabeem, halɩ tɩ Amoyisi yele yetɩ, ẽŋa zotɩ la dabeem, halɩ tɩ ẽŋa gõgra.

ref: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
text: La yãma lɛm na tãŋa tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Siyõ tãŋa n de vɔm dãana Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa, ẽŋa n de Zerizalɛm mẽŋa mẽŋa n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ malɛkadõma tʋsa tʋsa bɔna mĩ.

ref: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
text: La ya ken lɛm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba lagsgɔ n de a kɔm-kẽendõma tɩ ba yʋ'ʋra gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire la. La ya lɛm Nawẽnnɛ n de nẽrba wuu sarɩyadita. Ya lɛm na nẽr-sɛba n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, dee masɛ sɛla woo pʋam na sɩɩsɩ zẽ'am.

ref: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
text: Yãma lɛm na Azezi n de sɛka n boe Nawẽnnɛ la tõma tẽŋasʋka botɩ Nawẽnnɛ bĩŋe nõor-paalga la tõma lɛ. A zɩɩm na n yese bɔ tõma la pa'alɛ la sɛla n ãn sõŋa gãnna Abɛlɩ zɩɩm na.

ref: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
text: Ɩta-ya guusgo, da zagsɛ ka selse sɛka n tɔgrɩ la ya lɛ la. Sɛba n yʋʋn zagsɛ ka selse nẽr-sɛka n kã'anɩ ba tẽŋa zuo la ka põsɛ nãmsgɔ. Bɛla, tõma sãn zagsɛ ka selse sɛka n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la yetɔga, tõma kãn yãŋɛ põsɛ.

ref: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
text: A kõa la yʋʋn botɩ tẽŋa la mĩim mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, a bĩŋe la nõorɛ yetɩ: «Mam wʋn le mĩim dũnia mɛ nõorɛ ayɩla, la dagɩ dũnia mã'a, saazuum mẽ wʋn mĩim mɛ.»

ref: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
text: Ẽn yetɩ a wʋn le mĩim nõor-ayɩla la, pa'alɛ mɛ tɩ sɛla tɩ a nãam na wʋn mĩim mɛ sã'am bɔɩ, dee tɩ sɛla n kãn tã'agɛ mĩim sã'am na mã'a n wʋn weege.

ref: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
text: Bɛla, tõma n wʋn to'oge so'olʋm n kãn sã'am na ĩyã, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ a yel-sõnnɛ, tõnna se'em n wʋn ãna a sũure yẽlʋm bɔ'ɔra ẽ, la tɩ nã'asra ẽ, dee zɔta ẽ.

ref: Our God is like a destructive fire!
text: Lan de se'em na, tõma Nawẽnnɛ ãn wʋ bugum n diti.

ref: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
text: Ken nõŋɛ-ya taaba wʋ sɔbiisi la.

ref: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
text: Da basɛ-ya sãama sãanɔ ɩrgɔ, tɩ nẽrba basɛba ẽŋɛ la bɛla, ẽŋɛ malɛkadõma sãanɛ dee ka bãŋɛ.

ref: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
text: Tẽra-ya sɛba n boe yʋ'a deem na yelle, wʋ yãma mẽ po bɔna la yʋ'a deem na. Tẽra-ya sɛba tɩ ba nãmsrɩ ba la yelle, wʋ yãma mẽŋa n nãmsrɩ la.

ref: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ pɔgdire tara nã'asgɔ, dee da basɛ tɩ dẽgrɔ tõoma bɔna mĩ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di nẽr-sɛba n ɩtɩ yalsɩ, la nẽr-sɛba n tũuli la sarɩya mɛ.

ref: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ ligri nõŋlʋm sɔna ya. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya sũure ãna yẽlʋm la yãma n tarɩ sɛla la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Mam kãn basɛ fɔ, mam kãn malɩn basɛ fɔ.»

ref: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ tõma nẽra woo tãna wʋn yele la bʋraanɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana de la mam sõŋra, mam kãn zoe dabeem. Nẽrsaala tã wʋn ẽŋɛ mam na bẽm?»

ref: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
text: Tẽra-ya ya nẽŋadõma n mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga bɔ ya la yelle. Bɩsɛ-ya ba vɔm n daan ãn se'em ka paagɛ ba kũm, tɩ ya mẽ ɩta bãma n bɔ sɩra ẽŋɛ se'em na.

ref: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
text: Azezi-krisi de la ẽn ãn se'em zaam, la zĩna, la wakatɛ wuu.

ref: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ zãsŋɔ sãasɩ buuri to'oto'ore botɩ ya tuuge. Sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la tɩ basɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a la keŋe tɩ sũure, la, la da dɛna ɩrgɔ sɔa n de dia yelle, sɛba n dolɩ ɩrgɔ bãma la ka yẽ yõorɔ.

ref: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
text: Tõma tarɩ la kãabgɔ, tɩ Zifdõma n tõnnɩ ba kãabgɔ tõoma la ka tara sore tɩ ba naagɛ tõma kãabgɔ la pʋam.

ref: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
text: Zifdõma kãabgɔ kẽma nẽŋadãana tarɩ la dũnsi zɩɩm kẽ'era Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'e-sõŋɔ la kãabra tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã, dee tara ba nẽnnɔ na yɛsra tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa yõ'ora.

ref: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
text: La de la bɛla tɩ Azezi mẽ yese tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa ka ki, tɩ a mẽŋa zɩɩm malgɛ nẽrba tɩ ba ãna sõŋa Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
text: Bɛla, tɩ yese-ya sẽŋɛ a zẽ'am tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa ka po sakɛ pɔ'ɔrɛ la.

ref: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
text: Tõma ka tarɩ tẽŋa dũnia wã zuo n boe wakatɛ wuu ĩyã, tõma eerɩ la tẽn-sɛka n sẽm na.

ref: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
text: Azezi ĩyã, tɩ nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ wakatɛ wuu tɩ la ãna wʋ kãabgɔ la, la boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ tɩ nõorɛ yetɔga zẽkɛ a yʋ'ʋrɛ pa'alɛ ẽn de sɛka.

ref: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Da basɛ-ya ɩr-sõma ɩrgɔ, la taaba sõŋrɛ, tɩ tõon-bãma n ãn wʋ kãabgɔ n ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũure yẽlʋm.

ref: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
text: Sakra-ya ya nẽŋadõma nõorɛ, dee nanna ba yetɔga. La de la bãma n bɩsrɩ yãma n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la yelle, ba wʋn ka tɔgɛ pa'alɛ Nawẽnnɛ bãma n bɩsɛ ya yelle se'em mɛ. Ẽŋɛ-ya ya wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em tɩ ba tã'agɛ tõm ba tõoma la sũ-yẽlga da yõnna, tɩ bɛla ka tarɩ yõorɔ bɔ ya.

ref: Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right.
text: Pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'ɔra tɔ. Tõma mi tɩ sũure pʋam mɛ tɩ tõma ka ẽŋɛ be'em, tõma boorɩ mɛ tɩ tɩ yel-ɩrɛ ãna sõŋa sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
text: Mam sosrɩ ya mɛ zozo'e tɩ ya pʋ'ʋsra m ĩyã tɩ m yẽ sore leme wa'am ya zẽ'am tɔtɔ. Pʋ'ʋsgɔ, la ba'asgɔ yetɔga

ref: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n de sũ-mã'asʋm dãana n vo'oge tɩ Zuudãana Azezi, tɩ a bɩsra tõma yelle wʋ dũnsĩin-kɛ'ɛma, a zɩɩm na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ bĩŋe nõorɛ la tõma wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ la ĩyã.

ref: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
text: Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tɩ ya tã'agɛ ɩta sɛla woo n ãn sõŋa, dɔla a yɛm n boorɩ se'em. A wʋn basɛ tɩ sɛla n ãn a sũure yẽlʋm ẽŋɛ tɩ pʋam, Azezi-krisi ĩyã, ẽŋa n de sɛka n masɛ nã'asgɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

ref: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
text: M sɔbiisi, mam sosrɩ ya tɩ ya sakɛ to'oge kã'aŋɔ yetɔg-bãna wã, tɩ mam n gʋlsɛ ya gõn-sɛka wã ka zo'oge.

ref: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
text: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ ba yo'oge tɩ sɔbia Atimõtɩ basɛ mɛ, a sãn wa'am tɔtɔ, mam na ẽŋa wʋn doose taaba wa'am wa kaagɛ ya.

ref: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya ya nẽŋadõma, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Itali dõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
text: Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ wʋn bɔna la ya wuu.

ref: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
text: Mam Azakɩ n de Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi tõntõnna n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã pʋ'ʋsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu n wɩrgɛ bɔna zẽ'a woo la. Yelle sãn paagɛ nẽra

ref: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
text: M sɔbiisi, nãmsgɔ to'oto'ore sãn paagɛ ya Azezi sore la pʋam, ya dɩkɛ ba tɩ ba dɛna ya sũ-yẽlga zõnzõnnɔ,

ref: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
text: La mõrgɛ-ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ ka paagɛ a tɛka, tɩ ya wʋn masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, dɛna nẽrba n bɩ, tɩ sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ ya.

ref: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
text: Yɛm sãn pɔ'ɔgɛ ya nẽr-sɛka, a sose Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ'ɔrɩ nẽrba wuu la yɛmleego dee ka mõŋra ba la, tɩ a wʋn bɔ ẽ.

ref: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
text: La a sãn sose ẽ, dẽnɩ tɩ a bɔ ẽ sɩra, da sẽ'ena baa fẽfẽe. Se'ere n soe la, nẽr-sɛka woo n sẽ'enɩ, a ãn wʋ ko-kãtɛ ko-mĩ'isi tɩ kʋsebgo vugri wẽ'era ba tɩ ba yɛgna se'em na.

ref: If you are that kind of person, you can't make up your mind, and you surely can't be trusted. So don't expect the Lord to give you anything at all. Poor People and Rich People
text: A de la nẽra n de pʋtẽ'era bayi dãana, tɩ a yɛm yɔɔra ẽn ɩtɩ bõn-sɛba la wuu pʋam.

ref: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
text: La masɛ tɩ sɔbi-sɛka n de nasa ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽkrɩ ẽ mɛ.

ref: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
text: La bõntarsa mẽ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga Nawẽnnɛ n sikri ẽ na, se'ere n soe la, a bõntarsõm na wʋn tole wʋ mõ-puuro la.

ref: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
text: Wʋntɛɛŋa sãn wõŋɛ mõogɔ, a puuro la zẽkrɩ mɛ sʋʋra kʋta, tɩ ba dasãnnɛ na bɔta. Bõntarsa mẽ ãn na bɛla, a wʋn ki mɛ a bõntarsõm na tõoma pʋam.

ref: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n wĩbri nãmsgɔ pʋam tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Se'ere n soe la, ẽn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ẽ bɔ'a n de vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ na.

ref: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
text: La de la nẽra woo yɛmleego n de be'em n pã'asrɩ ẽ, botɩ a kẽ'era yɛla pʋam.

ref: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
text: La be'em yɛmleego sãn yõkɛ pʋʋrɛ, a dɔgrɩ la tõon-be'ero. La tõon-be'ero la sãn wa bɩ, a sẽm na kũm.

ref: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
text: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, da pã'asɛ-ya ya mẽŋa.

ref: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
text: Bɔ'a wuu n ãn sõŋa, la bɔ'a n masɛ wuu ze'etɩ la saazuum, Nawẽnnɛ n de peelem dãana la zẽ'am. Ẽŋa n nãam bõn-sɛba wuu n boe saazuum nẽera peelem, tɩ ba tɛɛra, la ẽŋa ka teerɩ, a ka tarɩ lika.

ref: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
text: Ẽŋa mẽŋa n boorɩ tɩ a basɛ tɩ a sɩra yetɔga la bɔ tõma vɔm. A ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ tõma dɛna la pɔspɔsɩ dõma bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ a nãam na pʋam. Sɛlsra-ya, dee ɩta Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em na

ref: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
text: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, bãŋɛ-ya bõn-ẽna wã sõŋa, nẽra woo mõrgɛ sɛlsra sõŋa, la a mã'agɛ a mẽŋa tɔgra, la a da isgra sũure tɔtɔ.

ref: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
text: Nẽrsaala sũure sãn isge, a ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
text: Bɛla, basɛ-ya sɛla n de tõon-dẽgrɔ buuri wuu, la sɛla n de pʋtʋʋlgɔ wuu, dee sike ya mẽŋa to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la tɩ a dɩkɛ ẽŋɛ ya sũure pʋam, n tã wʋn fãagɛ ya la.

ref: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
text: Da pã'asɛ ya mẽŋa, dɛna-ya nẽrba n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, da dɛna-ya nẽrba n selsrɩ mã'a.

ref: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n selsrɩ yetɔga la, dee ka sakra ɩta lan yele se'em na, a dãana ãn wʋ nẽra n bɩsɛ bɩsga pʋam yẽ a mẽŋa n ãn se'em,

ref: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
text: la ẽn bɩsɛ a mẽŋa kɩ'ɩlɩm dee fõrgɛ la, a darɛ yĩm ẽŋa n ãn se'em na mɛ.

ref: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
text: La nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ n masɛ sõŋa n yolsrɩ nẽrba la, dee sakɛ ẽ sõŋa ɩta lan yele se'em na, ẽŋa dagɩ nẽra n selsrɩ dee yĩnna, ẽŋa dãana tarɩ la sũ-yẽlga ẽŋa n ɩtɩ sɛla la pʋam.

ref: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n tẽ'esrɩ tɩ ẽŋa de la nẽra n dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ, dee ka tãna a nõorɛ yõkra, a pã'asrɩ la a mẽŋa, nẽr-ẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ doosgo la de la zãŋa.

ref: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
text: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ tɩ tɩ dɔla ẽ se'em tɩ la ãna sõŋa ka tara dẽgrɔ n wãna: La de la fʋ bɩsɛ kɩɩbsɩ la pɔkõpa yelle ba nãmsgɔ pʋam, la fʋ guuse fʋ mẽŋa la dũnia wã tõon-dẽgrɔ.

ref: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
text: M sɔbiisi, yãma n sakɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n tarɩ na'am pãŋa la, da tũ'usra-ya nẽrba.

ref: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
text: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ, wʋ yãma sãn lagsɛ taaba tɩ nẽra n pire sãlma nutʋa dee yɛ fu-sõma wa'am wa kẽ ya lagsgɔ la zẽ'a, tɩ nasa n mẽ yɛ fu-zõra kẽ,

ref: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
