text: tɩ yãma sãn bɩsɛ nẽr-sɛka n yɛ fu-sõma la yele ẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am wa zĩ'ire kalam n de zẽ'e-sõŋɔ», dee yele nasa la yetɩ: «Zɛ saazuo, bɩɩ zĩ'ire tẽŋa mam nãma zẽ'am wã.»

ref: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
text: Yãma sãn ẽŋɛ wãna, yãma ka tũ'usri nẽrba sɩra? Bɛla, ya tarɩ la pʋtẽ'e-be'ero bɔkra nẽrba, yetɩ ayẽma n gãnnɩ ayẽma.

ref: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
text: M sɔbi-nõŋrɩsɩ, selse-ya. Nawẽnnɛ looge la nẽr-sɛba tɩ dũnia dõma bɩsrɩ tɩ ba de la nasdõma la, botɩ ba bɔ ẽ sɩra dɛna bõntatba, tɩ ba wʋn wa bɔna a so'olʋm tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ sɛba n nõŋɛ ẽ na pʋam.

ref: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
text: La yãma pɔ'ɔrɩ la nasdõma. La dagɩ bõntatba n nãmsrɩ ya, dee yõgra ya sẽnna sarɩyaditba zẽ'am?

ref: Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
text: La de la bãma n pɔ'ɔrɩ Krisi yʋ'ʋr-sõŋɔ sɛka tɩ ba bɔ ya la.

ref: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
text: Ya sãn sakɛ ɩta Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm lɔɔ la n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nõŋɛ fʋ tadãana wʋ fʋ mẽŋa la», ya ẽŋɛ sõŋa.

ref: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
text: La ya sãn tũ'usra nẽrba, ya tõm na tõon-be'ego, la lɔɔ la pa'alɛ ya tɩ ya tõm na tõon-be'ego.

ref: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
text: Nẽr-sɛka sãn sakɛ lɔɔ la wuu, dee sɩ'ɩsɛ ayɩla tõm tõon-be'ego, a sɩ'ɩsɛ la ba wuu.

ref: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ yalsɩ» la, ẽŋa n ken yetɩ: «Da kʋ nẽra». La fʋ sãn ka ẽŋɛ yalsɩ, dee kʋ nẽra, fʋ de la nẽra n sɩ'ɩsɛ lɔɔ la.

ref: Speak and act like people who will be judged by the law that sets us free.
text: Tɔgra-ya dee tõnna tɩ la masɛ la lɔɔ n yolsrɩ nẽrba la, mina tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn dɩkɛ la ẽŋa di ya sarɩya.

ref: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
text: Nawẽnnɛ sãn wa dita sarɩya, a kãn zoe ninbãalga la nẽr-sɛka n ka zotɩ a taaba ninbãalga. Fʋ sãn zoe nẽrba ninbãalga, Nawẽnnɛ mẽ wʋn zoe fʋ ninbãalga. Bɔ sɩra, la tõoma

ref: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
text: M sɔbiisi, nẽra sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, dee ka tõnna tõoma n pa'alɩ bɛla, a yõorɔ de la bẽm? Bɔ sɩra ẽŋa tã wʋn fãagɛ ẽ mɛ bɩɩ?

ref: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
text: Tɩ dɩkɛ makrɛ, wʋ sɔbia n ka tarɩ fuugo yɛɛra, dee ka tara dita,

ref: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
text: tɩ yãma nẽra ayɩla sãn yele ẽ yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ la sũ-mã'asʋm ka di tɩgɛ, dee yɛ fuugo tɩ ɔɔrɔ da tara fɔ» dee ka bɔ ẽ sõŋrɛ sɛka tɩ a boorɩ la, a yõorɔ de la bẽm?

ref: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
text: La mẽ de la bɛla la bɔ sɩra, ya sãn bɔ sɩra tɩ la ka lagɩm na tõoma, a de la zãŋa.

ref: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
text: La nẽr-sɛka tã mɛ yetɩ, ayẽma bɔ la sɩra, la ayẽma tõnnɩ la tõon-sõma. Mam wʋn yele ẽ yetɩ, pa'alɛ mam fʋ bɔ sɩra la n ka lagɩm na tõoma la, tɩ mam wʋn dɩkɛ m tõon-sõma la pa'alɛ tɩ mam bɔ sɩra.

ref: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
text: Fõn sakɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a n boe. La ãn sõŋa. Kʋlkã'arsɩ mẽ sakɛ la bɛla, dee gõgra la dabeem.

ref: Well, our ancestor Abraham pleased God by putting his son Isaac on the altar to sacrifice him.
text: Tɩ yaaba Abraham tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn yetɩ a masɛ ya la, la de la a tõoma ĩyã, ẽn yʋʋn dɩkɛ a dayʋa A'izakɩ tɩ a kãabɛ bɔ Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

ref: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
text: Fʋ yẽ mɛ tɩ a bɔ sɩra, la a tõoma la lagɩm na taaba. A tõoma la ĩyã n botɩ a bɔ sɩra la masɛ sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
text: Bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ n gʋlsɛ se'em na ẽŋɛ. La yetɩ: «Abraham bɔ la Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɩsɛ a bɔ sɩra la, yetɩ a masɛ mɛ ẽŋa zẽ'am.» Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ẽ yetɩ a sɩrɛ.

ref: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
text: Ya yẽ tɩ la de la nẽra tõoma n botɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a masɛ ya, la dagɩ a bɔ sɩra mã'a ĩyã.

ref: For example, Rahab had been a prostitute. But she pleased God when she welcomed the spies and sent them home by another way.
text: La yʋʋn mẽ de la bɛla la Arahabɩ n de pɔka n ɩtɩ yalsɩ la. Zifdõma n yʋʋn tõm nẽrba la, tɩ a to'oge ba sʋgɛ a yire, botɩ ba doose sore ayẽma kule la, tõon-ẽŋa ĩyã tɩ a masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
text: Nẽra sɩa sãn fõrgɛ deege ẽ, a ĩyã de la kũm. La mẽ de la bɛla, nẽra sãn bɔ sɩra tɩ la ka lagɩm na tõoma, la de la zãŋa.

ref: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
text: M sɔbiisi, da kãn bɔɔra-ya tɩ ya wuu dɛna karẽnsãandõma. Se'ere n soe la, ya mi tɩ tõma sɛba n de karẽnsãandõma la sarɩyadia n wʋn dɛna toogo gãnna.

ref: All of us do many wrong things. But if you can control your tongue, you are mature and able to control your whole body.
text: Tõma wuu tuuri mɛ zozo'e. Nẽra sãn ka tuura a yetɔga pʋam, a de la nẽra n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, tãna wʋn yõkra a mẽŋa sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
text: Tõma sãn ẽŋɛ wiiri nõorɛ salba tɩ ba sakɛ ɩta tɩn boorɩ se'em, ba ĩyã la wuu ɩtɩ la tõma n boorɩ se'em.

ref: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
text: Ken bɩsɛ-ya õornɔ n ãn se'em na, baa ban kãrgɛ se'em, dee tɩ kʋseb-kãra vugra zã'ara ba la, ba tarɩ la dɔ-pɩka wẽrgra ba, tɩ ba sẽnna wɛ'ɛsa zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ sɛka n tarɩ ba la boorɩ.

ref: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
text: Nẽrsaala zɩlɩŋa mẽ ãn na bɛla, a de la ĩyã zẽ'a ayɩla n ãn pɩka pɩka, dee tɔgra yel-kãra. La de la bug-pɩka n tã wʋn di we-kãtɛ wuu.

ref: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
text: Zɩlɩŋa mẽ ãn wʋ bugum na. A boe la tɩ ĩyã la pʋam tõnna tõon-be'ero tɩ la taara tɩ ĩyã la wuu. A bugum na ze'etɩ la bug-tẽŋa sã'ana tɩ vɔm wuu.

ref: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
text: Nẽrsaala tã dũnsi buuri wuu, la bõn-ẽgla buuri wuu, la bõn-vuula buuri wuu, la sɛba n boe ko'om pʋam wuu mɛ, botɩ ba sakra ẽ.

ref: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
text: La nẽra ka boe n tã wʋn yãŋɛ a zɩlɩŋa. A pɩrɛ la yetɔg-be'ero tɔgra ka gɔ'ɔra, a tarɩ la yabga n kʋʋrɩ.

ref: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
text: Kã'a-be'ero, la kã'a-sõma ze'etɩ la nõ-ẽŋa ayɩla pʋam yɛsra. M sɔbiisi, la ka masɛ tɩ la dɛna bɛla.

ref: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
text: Ko-sõma, la ko-be'ero tã wʋn bulla bulnifo ayɩla pʋam? M sɔbiisi, kẽnkãŋa tã wʋn wɔnna tã'ama?

ref: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
text: Bɩɩ sẽnsãabga tã wʋn wɔnna kẽnkãma? Bɛla, bulga n bulli ko-be'ero ka tã wʋn bulla ko-sõma. Yɛm n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am

ref: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
text: Ãne n boe ya pʋam tẽ'esɛ tɩ ẽŋa tarɩ yɛm, la bãŋrɛ? A ẽŋɛ yel-ɩr-sõŋɔ, dee sike a mẽŋa pa'alɛ tɩ a tarɩ yɛm.

ref: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
text: La, la sãn dɛna tɩ ya ɩtɩ la sũure, dee gẽela taaba, da wẽ-ya yõ'ogɔ tɩ ya de la yɛm dõma parɩm sɩ'ɩsɛ sɩra la.

ref: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
text: Yɛm ẽŋa dagɩ yɛm sɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. A de la tẽŋa wã zuo bõnɔ, a de la nẽrsaala bõnɔ, a ze'ele la Asũtãana zẽ'am.

ref: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
text: Sũure la gẽelgɔ n boe zẽ'e-sɛka la, tõon-yooro, la tõon-be'ero buuri wuu boe la bilam.

ref: But the wisdom that comes from above leads us to be pure, friendly, gentle, sensible, kind, helpful, genuine, and sincere.
text: La yɛm n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am ãn na sõŋa ka tara dẽgrɔ, dee botɩ nẽra tara sũ-mã'asʋm la a taaba, la sũ-bʋgsʋm, ɩta sugri, zɔta nẽrba ninbãalga zozo'e, tõnna tõon-sõma zozo'e, ka bɔkra nẽrba, dee ka dɛna pʋlibsum dãana.

ref: When peacemakers plant seeds of peace, they will harvest justice.
text: Sũ-mã'asʋm dõma n botɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna nẽrba tẽŋasʋka la tõoma de la tõon-sõma n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: Why do you fight and argue with each other? Isn't it because you are full of selfish desires that fight to control your body?
text: Zɛbrɛ la nõkɛ'ɛma n boe ya tẽŋasʋka la ze'ele la bɛ? La de la yan boorɩ se'em n de be'em n boe ya pʋam botɩ ya ɩta bɛla.

ref: You want something you don't have, and you will do anything to get it. You will even kill! But you still cannot get what you want, and you won't get it by fighting and arguing. You should pray for it.
text: Ya boorɩ la sɛla dee ka yẽta, bɔɔra tɩ ya kʋʋra nẽrba. Ya ɩtɩ la sũure la ya taaba bõnɔ dee ken ka yẽta, bɛla tɩ ya zɛbra dee wẽ'era nõkɛ'ɛma la taaba. Yan ka tarɩ la, la de la yãma n ka sose Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

ref: Yet even when you do pray, your prayers are not answered, because you pray just for selfish reasons.
text: La ya sãn sɔsra mɛ dee ka yẽta, yãma n sosrɩ se'em na ka ãn sõŋa. Ya sosrɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ la ya yɛm n boorɩ se'em.

ref: You people aren't faithful to God! Don't you know if you love the world, you are God's enemies? And if you decide to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
text: Yãma ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ la sɩra. Yãma ka mi tɩ sɛka n nõŋɛ dũnia wã lɔgrɔ, la de la be'em la Nawẽnnɛ? Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka woo n boorɩ tɩ a yõgɛ sɩtɔ la dũnia wã lɔgrɔ, a wʋn dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ bɛ.

ref: Do you doubt the Scriptures that say, “God truly cares about the Spirit he has put in us”?
text: Bɩɩ yãma tẽ'esɛ tɩ lan gʋlsɛ se'em Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na de la zãŋa? La yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ a ẽŋɛ tɩ pʋam na, boorɩ tɩ ẽŋa mã'a n sɔna tɔ.»

ref: In fact, God treats us with even greater kindness, just as the Scriptures say, “God opposes everyone who is proud, but he blesses all who are humble with undeserved grace.”
text: La a ɩtɩ la yel-sõnnɛ n ãn sõŋa gãnna bɔ'ɔra tɔ, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ zagsrɩ zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma mɛ, dee ɩta sik-m-mẽŋa dõma yel-sõnnɛ.»

ref: Surrender to God! Resist the devil, and he will run from you.
text: Bɛla, dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, dee sɩ'ɩsɛ Asũtãana, tɩ a wʋn zoe dee basɛ ya.

ref: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Clean up your lives, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you people who can't make up your mind.
text: Lɛm-ya Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ a mẽ wʋn lɛm ya. Tõon-be'ero dõma, peege ya nu'usi, pʋtẽ'era ayi dõma, peege ya sũure.

ref: Be sad and sorry and weep. Stop laughing and start crying. Be gloomy instead of glad.
text: Bãŋɛ-ya ya ninbãalga, kaasɛ-ya fablɛ la sũ-sã'aŋɔ. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya lɔ'ɔrɔ lebge kʋʋnɔ, tɩ ya sũ-yẽlga lebge sũ-sã'aŋɔ.

ref: Be humble in the Lord's presence, and he will honor you. Saying Cruel Things about Others
text: Sike-ya ya mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, tɩ a wʋn zẽkɛ ya.

ref: My friends, don't say cruel things about others! If you do, or if you condemn others, you are condemning God's Law. And if you condemn the Law, you put yourself above the Law and refuse to obey either it
text: M sɔbiisi, da tɔgra-ya taaba yelle be'em. Nẽr-sɛka n tɔgɛ a tadãana yelle be'em, bɩɩ zɛrgra ẽ, a tɔgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ lɔɔ la be'em dee zɛrgra ẽ. La fʋ sãn zɛrgra lɔɔ la, fʋ ka sakrɩ lɔɔ la, fʋ lebge la lɔɔ la sarɩyadita.

ref: or God who gave it. God is our judge, and he can save or destroy us. What right do you have to condemn anyone? Warning against Bragging
text: Nawẽnnɛ mã'a n bɔ'ɔrɩ lɔɔ, la ẽŋa mã'a n diti sarɩya, la ẽŋa mã'a n tã wʋn fãagɛ, bɩɩ sã'am. La fõn tẽ'esɛ tɩ fõn de la ãne, dita fʋ tadãana sarɩya? Da wẽ'era-ya yõ'ogɔ

ref: You should know better than to say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to the city. We will do business there for a year and make a lot of money!”
text: Selse-ya! Yãma sɛba n yetɩ: «Zĩna bɩɩ beere, tõma wʋn sẽŋɛ la tẽn-ẽna ka ẽŋɛ yʋʋnɛ, leebe yẽ yõorɔ» la,

ref: What do you know about tomorrow? How can you be so sure about your life? It is nothing more than mist that appears for only a little while before it disappears.
text: ya ka mi ya vɔm n wʋn ãna se'em beere. Ya ãn wʋ pika n yʋ, tɩ la ẽŋɛ fẽe tɩ a pilge.

ref: You should say, “If the Lord lets us live, we will do these things.”
text: La de nɩ na ya ẽn yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sãn bɔɔra, tɩ tõma vɔa, tõma wʋn ẽŋɛ la wãna la wãna.»

ref: Yet you are stupid enough to brag, and it is wrong to be so proud.
text: La yãma pa'alɩ la ya mẽŋa dee wẽ'era yõ'ogɔ yãma n boorɩ tɩ ya ẽŋɛ sɛla la pʋam. Pa'al-m-mẽŋa wãna buuri wuu de la be'em.

ref: If you don't do what you know is right, you have sinned.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n mi sɛla n ãn sõŋa, dee ka ɩta, a tõm na tõon-be'ero.

ref: You rich people should cry and weep! Terrible things are going to happen to you.
text: Bõntatba, selse-ya! Kaasra-ya dee fabla, se'ere n soe la, toogo n sẽm ya zuto.

ref: Your treasures have already rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
text: Ya bõntarsõm na wʋn põ'ogɛ mɛ. Bõn-mõgla n wʋn mõgɛ ya futo la.

ref: Your money has rusted, and the rust will be evidence against you, as it burns your body like fire. Yet you keep on storing up wealth in these last days.
text: Tẽŋanzẽem n wʋn kẽ ya sãlma, la ya zũuro la. Tẽŋanzẽem na n wʋn pa'alɛ tɩ ya ka ẽŋɛ sõŋa, la a wʋn di ya wʋ bugum na. Yãma lagsrɩ la bõntarsõm bĩŋra dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ.

ref: You refused to pay the people who worked in your fields, and now their unpaid wages are shouting out against you. The Lord All-Powerful has surely heard the cries of the workers who harvested your crops.
text: Tõntõnɩba n tõnnɩ ya vatɔ pʋam tɩ ya mõŋɛ ba yɔɔrɔ la kellɩ mɛ. Nẽr-sɛba n lagsɛ ya bõnkɔɔla la kellɩ mɛ, tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la wõm.

ref: While here on earth, you have thought only of filling your own stomachs and having a good time. But now you are like fat cattle on their way to be butchered.
text: Dũnia zuo ya vɔa la na'am pʋam, ɩta ya yɛm n boorɩ se'em, dita tɩgra nõbra, ãna wʋ dũnsi la gu'ura kʋa daarɛ.

ref: You have condemned and murdered innocent people, who couldn't even fight back. Be Patient and Kind
text: Ya tɔgɛ la sɩra dãana yelle kʋ, yetɩ ya kʋ ẽ, tɩ a ka tãna wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ ya tɩ ya kʋ ẽ. Kã'aŋɔ

ref: My friends, be patient until the Lord returns. Think of farmers who wait patiently for the autumn and spring rains to make their valuable crops grow.
text: M sɔbiisi, mã'ara ya sũure gu'ura Zuudãana la lemŋo. Bɩsɛ-ya kaara n mã'arɩ a sũure se'em gu'ura tɩ a va'am bõnkɔɔla biɛ wʋn wa bɩ sõŋa. A ẽn gu'ura mɛ tɩ sig-saaga wa ni, tɩ poorʋm saasɩ ni, tɩ bõnkɔɔla la biɛ bɩ sõŋa.

ref: Be patient like those farmers and don't give up. The Lord will soon be here!
text: Bɛla, yãma mẽ mõrgɛ mã'ara ya sũure, tara bʋraanɛ, tɩ Zuudãana la lemŋo lɛm mɛ.

ref: Don't grumble about each other or you will be judged, and the judge is right outside the door.
text: M sɔbiisi, da yõnna-ya taaba, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ da wa di ya sarɩya. Bɩsɛ-ya, sarɩyadita la ze la zanõrʋm.

ref: My friends, follow the example of the prophets who spoke for the Lord. They were patient, even when they had to suffer.
text: M sɔbiisi, Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n yʋʋn tɔgɛ a yetɔga, tɩ ba nãmsɛ ba, tɩ ba mã'agɛ ba sũure la, ya mẽ ẽŋɛ wʋ bãma n ẽŋɛ se'em na.

ref: In fact, we praise the ones who endured the most. You remember how patient Job was and how the Lord finally helped him. The Lord did this because he is so merciful and kind.
text: Tõma yetɩ mɛ tɩ sɛba n mõrgɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore pʋam na, de la zu-yẽlga dõma. Ya wõm ban tɔgɛ Azɔɔbɩ n ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ se'em yelle, la ya yẽ ba'asgɔ Nawẽnnɛ n ẽŋɛ se'em bɔ ẽ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ de la ninbãal-zɔta, dee tara nẽrba zĩile.

ref: My friends, above all else, don't take an oath. You must not swear by heaven or by earth or by anything else. “Yes” or “No” is all you need to say. If you say anything more, you will be condemned.
text: M sɔbiisi, sɛla n gãnnɩ wuu de la ya da pɔɩ. Da pɔɩ saazuum, bɩɩ tẽŋa, bɩɩ sɛla ayẽma. Basɛ-ya tɩ ya «ẽe» dɛna «ẽe», tɩ ya «ayɛɩ» dɛna «ayɛɩ», tɩ Nawẽnnɛ da di ya sarɩya.

ref: If you are having trouble, you should pray. And if you are feeling good, you should sing praises.
text: Nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam nãmsra, a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ. Nẽr-sɛka sũure n ãn yẽlʋm, a yõom yõom-sõma pẽgɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: If you are sick, ask the church leaders to come and pray for you. Ask them to put olive oil on you in the name of the Lord.
text: Nẽra sãn bɔna ya pʋam bẽ'era, a wi Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba kẽma, tɩ ba ẽŋɛ kaam ẽŋɛ a zuugo la Zuudãana Azezi yʋ'ʋrɛ, pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ.

ref: If you have faith when you pray for sick people, they will get well. The Lord will heal them, and if they have sinned, he will forgive them.
text: Ban bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra pʋ'ʋsɛ la, la wʋn fãagɛ bã'ara la mɛ, Zuudãana la wʋn bɔ ẽ ĩmã'asʋm mɛ tɩ a isge. La a sãn tõm tõon-be'ero, a wʋn basɛ ẽ taalɛ.

ref: If you have sinned, you should tell each other what you have done. Then you can pray for one another and be healed. The prayer of an innocent person is powerful, and it can help a lot.
text: Bɛla, pelge ya pʋa tɔgɛ ya tõon-be'ero pa'alɛ taaba, pʋ'ʋsɛ bɔ taaba, tɩ ya wʋn yẽ ĩmã'asʋm. Nẽra n masɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am pʋ'ʋsgɔ tõnnɩ mɛ la pãŋa zozo'e.

ref: Elijah was just as human as we are, and for three and a half years his prayers kept the rain from falling.
text: A'eli yʋʋn de la nẽrsaala wʋ tõma la, la a pʋ'ʋsɛ mɛ sose Nawẽnnɛ la a sũure wuu tɩ saaga da ni, tɩ saaga ka tɔkɛ tẽŋa yʋʋma atã la wõr-sɩyoobɩ.

ref: But when he did pray for rain, it fell from the skies and made the crops grow.
text: Tɩ a le pʋ'ʋsɛ, tɩ saaga ni, tɩ bõnkɔɔla wɔm biɛ.

ref: My friends, if any followers have wandered away from the truth, you should try to lead them back.
text: M sɔbiisi, ya nẽra ayɩla sãn bʋrgɛ dee basɛ sɩra sore, tɩ nẽra tarɩ ẽ leme wa'am,

ref: If you turn sinners from the wrong way, you will save them from death, and many of their sins will be forgiven.
text: ya bãŋɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n tarɩ tõon-be'ero dãana a tõon-be'ero sorɔɔgɔ zuo leme wa'am, a fãagɛ la tõon-be'ero dãana kũm pʋam, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ a tõon-be'ero zozo'e taalɛ.

ref: From Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. To God's people who are scattered like foreigners in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
text: Mam Apɩyɛɛrɩ n de Azezi-krisi Tõntõnna n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge tɩ ya dɛna a nẽrba wɩrgɛ bɔna Põ, la Galatɩ, la Kapadɔsɩ, la Azi, la Bitini so'olʋm pʋam dɛna sãama la.

ref: God the Father decided to choose you as his people, and his Spirit has made you holy. You have obeyed Jesus Christ and are sprinkled with his blood. I pray that God will be kind to you and will keep on giving you peace! A Real Reason for Hope
text: La de la tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n looge ya wʋ ẽŋa n dẽŋɛ looge a yɛm se'em na, tɩ a Sɩa welge ya to'ore, tɩ Azezi-krisi zɩɩm peege ya tɩ ya ãna sõŋa, sakra a nõorɛ. Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm zozo'e. Tɩn tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is so good, and by raising Jesus from death, he has given us new life and a hope that lives on.
text: Tɩ pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Sɔ la a yel-sõnnɛ. A ninbãal-zʋa n zo'oge la ĩyã tɩ a botɩ tɩ yẽ vɔm paalga, Azezi-krisi n ki dee vo'oge la ĩyã. Bɛla tɩ tɩ tara pʋtẽ'erɛ wakatɛ wuu gu'ura sɛla tɩ tɩ bɔ sɩra la,

ref: You have faith in God, whose power will protect you until the last day. Then he will save you, just as he has always planned to do.
text: Yãma n bɔ sɩra la, Nawẽnnɛ gu'uri ya mɛ la a pãŋa halɩ wʋn ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ wakatɛ fãagɛ ya ba'asɛ.

ref: On that day you will be glad, even if you have to go through many hard trials for a while.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ ya tara sũ-yẽlga, baa la de ya sãn yẽta yɛla to'oto'ore n nãmsrɩ ya wakatɛ fẽe lɛɛlɛ wã.

ref: Your faith will be like gold that has been tested in a fire. And these trials will prove that your faith is worth much more than gold that can be destroyed. They will show that you will be given praise and honor and glory when Jesus Christ returns.
text: Nãmsgɔ la de la sɛla n makrɩ bɩsra ya bɔ sɩra la n ãn se'em. Sãlma de la sɛla n sã'anɩ, la ba ẽn dɩkɛ ẽ makɛ la bugum bɩsɛ ẽn ãn se'em. La yãma bɔ sɩra la tarɩ yõorɔ mɛ zozo'e gãnna sãlma. Bɛla tɩ la makra ya bɔ sɩra la bɩsra, tɩ ya sãn ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, ya wʋn yẽ pẽka, la nã'asgɔ Azezi-krisi lemŋo daarɛ.

ref: You have never seen Jesus, and you don't see him now. But still you love him and have faith in him, and no words can tell how glad and happy
text: Yãma nãn ka yẽ ẽ, dee nõŋɛ ẽ, ya nãn ka yẽtɩ ẽ, dee bɔ ẽ sɩra, bɛla tɩ ya ɩta sũ-yẽlga zozo'e n ka tã wʋn pa'alɛ.

ref: you are to be saved. This is why you have faith.
text: Se'ere n soe la, ya yẽ fãarɛ mɛ n de ya bɔ sɩra la yõorɔ.

ref: Some prophets told how God would treat you with undeserved grace, and they searched hard to find out more about the way you would be saved.
text: Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la n yʋʋn tɔgɛ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn wa ẽŋɛ bɔ ya la, yʋʋn ɛ mɛ vẽesɛ tɩ ba bãŋɛ fãarɛ wã yelle.

ref: The Spirit of Christ was in them and was telling them how Christ would suffer and would then be given great honor. So they searched to find out exactly who Christ would be and when this would happen.
text: Azezi-krisi Sɩa yʋʋn boe la ba mɛ, dẽŋɛ pa'alɛ ba yetɩ Krisi wʋn wa yẽ la nãmsgɔ dee yãŋa yẽ na'am pãŋa. Tɩ ba yʋʋn ɛɛra tɩ ba bãŋɛ wakatɛ sɛka tɩ bõn-bãma wʋn ẽŋɛ, la lan wʋn wa doose se'em tɩ la ẽŋɛ.

ref: But they were told that they were serving you and not themselves. They preached to you by the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven. And their message was only for you, even though angels would like to know more about it. Chosen To Live a Holy Life
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn pa'alɛ ba tɩ yel-sɛba tɩ ba mõolɩ la dagɩ bãma mẽŋa ĩyã, la de la yãma ĩyã. Yel-bãma n bɛla tɩ sɛba n mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la tɔgɛ bɔ ya. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n tõm a Sɩa tɩ a wa'am wa bɔ ba pãŋa tɩ ba tɔgɛ. Tɩ baa malɛkadõma la mẽŋa bɔɔra tɩ ba wõm a võorɛ. Tɩ dɛna pʋpeelem dõma

ref: Be alert and think straight. Put all your hope in how God will treat you with undeserved grace when Jesus Christ appears.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, yõkɛ ya tagsgɔ tara ya mẽŋa sõŋa, dee gu'ura la ya sũure wuu yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ya tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ a ẽŋɛ bɔ ya Azezi-krisi lemŋo wakatɛ la.

ref: Behave like obedient children. Don't let your lives be controlled by your desires, as they used to be.
text: Sakra-ya Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, da le vɔa-ya wʋ yãma n daan nãn ka bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, tɩ ya yɛmleego botɩ ya bɔɔra sɛla n de be'em na.

ref: Always live as God's holy people should, because God is the one who chose you, and he is holy.
text: Dɛna-ya pʋpeelem dõma ya yel-ɩrɛ wuu pʋam, wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya la n de pʋpeelem dãana se'em na.

ref: That's why the Scriptures say, “I am the holy God, and you must be holy too.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Dɛna-ya pʋpeelem dõma, tɩ mam de la pʋpeelem dãana.»

ref: You say that God is your Father, but God doesn't have favorites! He judges all people by what they do. So you must honor God while you live as strangers here on earth.
text: Yãma n pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na, ya wi'iri ẽ tɩ ya Sɔ. Nawẽnnɛ ka tũ'usri nẽrba. A diti nẽrsaalba sarɩya la sɩra, wʋ nẽra woo tõoma n de se'em na. Bɛla, pa'alɛ-ya tɩ yãma nannɩ ẽ ya yel-ɩrɛ wuu pʋam, yãma n ken bɔna dũnia zuo wã.

ref: You were rescued from the useless way of life you learned from your ancestors. But you know you were not rescued by such things as silver or gold that don't last forever.
text: Nawẽnnɛ fãagɛ ya mɛ vɔm sɛka n ka tarɩ yõorɔ tɩ ya daan zãsʋm ya yaabdõma zẽ'am na pʋam. Ya mi tɩ la dagɩ bõn-sɛba n sã'anɩ wʋ sãlma, bɩɩ zũuro tɩ a dɩkɛ yɔ fãagɛ ya.

ref: You were rescued by the precious blood of Christ, that spotless and innocent lamb.
text: La de la Krisi zɩɩm n tarɩ pãŋa la. Ba kʋ Krisi la tõma ĩyã wʋ pebila n ãn sõŋa dee ka tara zãnnɛ.

ref: Christ was chosen even before the world was created, but because of you, he did not come until these last days.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn põn looge ẽ mɛ halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia nãaŋɔ, yãŋa botɩ a wa'am dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ wã yãma ĩyã.

ref: And when he did come, it was to lead you to have faith in God, who raised him from death and honored him in a glorious way. This is why you have put your faith and hope in God.
text: Krisi ĩyã tɩ ya bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, Nawẽnnɛ n vo'oge Krisi, bɔ ẽ na'am pãŋa, bɛla tɩ ya dɩkɛ ya bɔ sɩra, la yan tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la te'ege Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: You obeyed the truth, and your souls were made pure. Now you sincerely love each other. But you must keep on loving with all your heart.
text: Yãma n sakɛ sɩra la, ya lebge la pʋpeelem dõma, tɩ ya nõŋɛ ya sɔbiisi la nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra. Bɛla, nõŋɛ-ya taaba zozo'e la ya sũure wuu.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Humans wither like grass, and their glory fades like wild flowers. Grass dries up, and flowers fall to the ground.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽrsaala woo ãn wʋ mõorɔ, la a sõŋa la wuu ãn wʋ mõ-puuro la. Mõorɔ zẽkrɩ mɛ, tɩ ba puuro la sʋʋra.

ref: But what the Lord has said will stand forever.” Our good news to you is what the Lord has said.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga boe la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.» Yetɔg-ẽŋa n de kõ-yẽlga la tɩ ba mõolɛ bɔ ya la.

ref: Stop being hateful! Quit trying to fool people, and start being sincere. Don't be jealous or say cruel things about others.
text: Bɛla, basɛ-ya pʋtʋʋlgɔ wuu, la põmpɔrŋɔ, la pʋlibsum, la sũure, la taaba pɔ'ɔrɛ.

ref: Be like newborn babies who are thirsty for the pure spiritual milk that will help you grow and be saved.
text: Nõŋɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga wʋ bilɛɛsɩ n nõŋɛ ba ma bĩ'isum mã'a la. Tɩ a wʋn basɛ tɩ ya bɩ Azezi-krisi sore doosgo la pʋam, yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: You have already found out how good the Lord really is.
text: Tɩ yãma bãŋɛ Zuudãana la n ãn sõŋa se'em mɛ. Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa nẽrba

ref: Come to Jesus Christ. He is the living stone people have rejected, but which God has chosen and highly honored.
text: Lɛm-ya Zuudãana n de vɔm dãana la zẽ'am, ẽŋa ãn wʋ kug-sɛka tɩ nẽrba zagsɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge ẽ tɩ a tara yõorɔ zozo'e.

ref: And now you are living stones being used to build a spiritual house. You are also a group of holy priests, and with the help of Jesus Christ you will offer sacrifices that please God.
text: Yãma mẽ vɔa mɛ. Basɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ ya tɩ ya ãna wʋ kuga mẽ deego tɩ a Sɩa bɔna mĩ, tɩ ya ãna wʋ kãabgɔ kẽma bɔna mĩ tõnna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boorɩ se'em, tɩ la paara a yɛm, Azezi-krisi ĩyã.

ref: It is just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a choice and precious cornerstone. No one who has faith in this one will be disappointed.”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, mam looge la kugre n tarɩ yõorɔ zozo'e ẽebɛ m deego Siyõ, ẽŋa n de kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa. Nẽr-sɛka n bɔ ẽ sɩra, yãnnɛ kãn yõkɛ ẽ.»

ref: You are followers of the Lord, and this stone is precious to you. But it isn't precious to those who refuse to follow him. They are the builders who tossed aside the stone that turned out to be the most important one of all.
text: Yãma sɛba n bɔ ẽ sɩra la, a tarɩ la yõorɔ zozo'e bɔ ya. La sɛba n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la, la gʋlsɛ ba yelle yetɩ: «Kug-sɛka tɩ tõnmẽtba zagsɛ la n lebge kugre n botɩ deego la tara pãŋa.»

ref: But you are God's chosen and special people. You are a group of royal priests and a holy nation. God has brought you out of darkness into his marvelous light. Now you must tell all the wonderful things he has done. The Scriptures say,
text: La sãn dɛna yãma, yãma de la buuri tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge, ya de la ẽŋa n de naba la kãabgɔ kẽma, dee ken dɛna a nẽrba n welge to'ore tara pʋpeelem. A wi ya la lika pʋam, tarɩ yese a peelem n ãn sõŋa zozo'e pʋam, tɩ ya botɩ nẽrba bãŋɛ a tõon-kãra la.

ref: “Once you were nobody. Now you are God's people. At one time no one had mercy on you. Now God has treated you with kindness.” Live as God's Servants Should
text: Kʋrʋm, ya daan dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, la lɛɛlɛ wã ya de la a nẽrba. A daan ka zotɩ ya ninbãalga, la a yãŋa zotɩ ya ninbãalga mɛ. Krisi nẽrba yel-ɩrɛ

ref: Dear friends, you are foreigners and strangers on this earth. So I beg you not to surrender to those desires that fight against you.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, yãma de la sãama la kɔɔs-weto dũnia zuo, bɛla mam kã'anɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya basɛ nẽrsaalba n boorɩ se'em n de be'em n sã'anɩ ya la.

ref: Always let others see you behaving properly, even though they may still accuse you of doing wrong. Then on the day of judgment, they will honor God by telling the good things they saw you do.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ ãna sõŋa nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ na tẽŋasʋka, tɩ baa ba sãn dõta ya yetɩ ya de la tõon-be'ero dõma, ba wʋn yẽ ya tõon-sõma la, nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ a wa'aŋɔ daarɛ.

ref: The Lord wants you to obey all human authorities, especially the Emperor, who rules over everyone.
text: Sakra-ya dũnia pãnsɩ dõma wuu Zuudãana Azezi ĩyã. Sakra-ya na-keko n soe ke'eŋo wuu,

ref: You must also obey governors, because they are sent by the Emperor to punish criminals and to praise good citizens.
text: la ya sakra gɔmnadõma tɩ a tõm tɩ ba sɩbgrɩ sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, dee nã'asra nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-sõma la.

ref: You are free, but still you are God's servants, and you must not use your freedom as an excuse for doing wrong.
text: Ɩta-ya wʋ nẽrba n soe ba mẽŋa, la da dɩkɛ-ya yan soe ya mẽŋa la tɩ la dɛna sɛla n botɩ ya tõnna be'em, tõnna-ya wʋ Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba n masɛ tɩ ba tõnna se'em na.

ref: Respect everyone and show special love for God's people. Honor God and respect the Emperor. The Example of Christ's Suffering
text: Nanna-ya nẽrba wuu, nõŋɛ ya sɔbiisi, zɔta-ya Nawẽnnɛ, nanna-ya na-keko.

ref: Servants, you must obey your masters and always show respect to them. Do this, not only to those who are kind and thoughtful, but also to those who are cruel.
text: Tõntõnɩba, sakra-ya ya zuudãandõma dee zɔta ba. La, la dagɩ sɛba n de nẽr-sõma, la sũ-bʋgsʋm dõma la mã'a, sakra-ya baa sɛba n de nẽr-tɔɔsɩ la.

ref: God will bless you, even if others treat you unfairly for being loyal to him.
text: Nẽra sãn sakra nãmsgɔ n ka masɛ nɩ tɩ a nãmsɛ, ẽn sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã, la de la yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: You don't gain anything by being punished for some wrong you have done. But God will bless you, if you have to suffer for doing something good.
text: Fʋ sãn tõm na tõon-be'ero tɩ ba nãmsra fɔ tɩ fʋ sakɛ wĩbra, a ka tarɩ yõorɔ. La fʋ sãn tõm na tõon-sõma tɩ ba nãmsra fɔ, tɩ fʋ sakɛ wĩbra, la de la yel-sõnnɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am.

ref: After all, God chose you to suffer as you follow in the footsteps of Christ, who set an example by suffering for you.
text: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wi ya. Krisi mẽ daan nãmsɛ yãma ĩyã mɛ, basɛ mãmsgɔ tɩ ya dɔla a nabara ɩta wʋ ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ se'em na.

ref: Christ did not sin or ever tell a lie.
text: A daan ka tõm tõon-be'ego, la põmpɔrŋɔ ka yese a nõorʋm.

ref: Although he was abused, he never tried to get even. And when he suffered, he made no threats. Instead, he had faith in God, who judges fairly.
text: Ban daan tʋ ẽ na, a ka lebse tʋ, la ban daan nãmsɛ ẽ na, a daan ka zɛbɛ la ba, a dɩkɛ a mẽŋa basɛ la sɛka n diti sarɩya la sɩra la nu'usum.

ref: Christ carried the burden of our sins. He was nailed to the cross, so we would stop sinning and start living right. By his cuts and bruises you are healed.
text: Ẽŋa n to'oge tõma zẽ'am ki dɔ-puurŋa zuo tõma tõon-be'ero ĩyã, tɩ la sãn dɛna la tõon-be'ero tõoma, tõma ãn wʋ tɩ ki mɛ, dee vɔa ɩta sɛla n masɛ. A pɔglʋŋɔ la ĩyã tɩ ya yẽ laafɛ.

ref: You had wandered away like sheep. Now you have returned to the one who is your shepherd and protector.
text: Ya daan ãn wʋ piisi n bɔɩ, la lɛɛlɛ wã, ya leme na ya dũnsĩina la zẽ'am, ẽŋa n bɩsrɩ ya.

ref: If you are a wife, you must put your husband first. Even if he opposes our message, you will win him over by what you do. No one else will have to say anything to him,
text: Bɛla mẽ, pɔgsɩ, sakra-ya ya sɩrba, tɩ baa sɛba n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga sɩra la sãn yẽ ya yel-ɩrɛ, ba wʋn sakɛ tɩ la ka doose la yetɔga,

ref: because he will see how you honor God and live a pure life.
text: ban yẽtɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ n ãn sõŋa dɛna nã'asgɔ la.

ref: Don't depend on things like fancy hairdos or gold jewelry or expensive clothes to make you look beautiful.
text: Da basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽnnõm dɛna yẽŋa sõŋa, n de zuto pã'aŋɔ, la sãlma lɔgrɔ, la fu-sõma yɛa.

ref: Be beautiful in your heart by being gentle and quiet. This kind of beauty will last, and God considers it very special.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ ya sõŋa la dɛna sũure pʋam sõŋa n de sũ-bʋgsʋm la sũ-mã'asʋm. Sõŋa ẽna n ka sã'anɩ dee tara yõorɔ zozo'e Nawẽnnɛ nifum.

ref: Long ago those women who worshiped God and put their hope in him made themselves beautiful by putting their husbands first.
text: Kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, pɔg-sɛba n yʋʋn de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tara pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na, sõŋa mẽ yʋʋn ãn na bɛla, sakra ba sɩrba.

ref: For example, Sarah obeyed Abraham and called him her master. You are her true children, if you do right and don't let anything frighten you.
text: Asaara yʋʋn ãn na bɛla sakra a sɩra Abraham nõorɛ, wi'ira ẽ tɩ a zuudãana. Ya sãn tõnna tõon-sõma, dee ka basɛ tɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ya dabeem, ya de la a pɔyɔɔsɩ.

ref: If you are a husband, you should be thoughtful of your wife. Treat her with honor, because she isn't as strong as you are, and she shares with you in the gift of life. Then nothing will stand in the way of your prayers. Suffering for Doing Right
text: Bɛla mẽ, yãma sɛba n de sɩrba la, bɔna-ya la ya pɔgba la lɛ, la wõm taaba, la ya bãŋɛ tɩ ba ka tarɩ pãŋa wʋ yãma la, la ya nanna ba, tɩ bãma la yãma n wʋn lagɩm to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ n de vɔm. Ɩta-ya bɛla tɩ sɛla da gu ya pʋ'ʋsgɔ. Ɩta-ya sõŋa baa ya sãn nãmsra

ref: Finally, all of you should agree and have concern and love for each other. You should also be kind and humble.
text: Ba'asgɔ pʋam, mam yetɩ ya wuu tɩ ya tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ayɩla, la taaba zĩile, la sɔbiisi nõŋrɛ, la ninbãal-zʋa, la sik-m-mẽŋa.

ref: Don't be hateful and insult people just because they are hateful and insult you. Instead, treat everyone with kindness. You are God's chosen ones, and he will bless you. The Scriptures say,
text: Nẽra sãn ẽŋɛ ya be'em, ya da lebse ẽ be'em. Nẽra sãn tʋ ya, ya da tʋ lebse, tõn kã-ya kã'a-sõma bɔ ẽ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya la, la de la kã'a-sõma tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ ya.

ref: “Do you really love life? Do you want to be happy? Then stop saying cruel things and quit telling lies.
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «Nẽr-sɛka n boorɩ tɩ a vɔm ãna yẽlʋm, dee tɩ a yẽ sũ-yẽlga, dẽnɩ tɩ a yõkɛ a nõorɛ la yetɔg-be'ero, dee basɛ põmpɔrŋɔ.

ref: Give up your evil ways and do right, as you find and follow the road to peace.
text: A zãagɛ a mẽŋa la tõon-be'ero, dee ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa, la a mõrgɛ ɛ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm bɔna nẽrba tẽŋasʋka da basɛ.

ref: The Lord watches over everyone who obeys him, and he listens to their prayers. But he opposes everyone who does evil.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana bɩsrɩ la sɛba n de sɩra dõma, dee sɛlsra ba pʋ'ʋsgɔ, la a wẽrgrɩ a nẽŋa mɛ, dee basra nẽr-sɛba n de tõon-be'ero dõma.»

ref: Can anyone really harm you for being eager to do good deeds?
text: Yãma sãn mõrgɛ tõnna tõon-sõma, ãne n wʋn ẽŋɛ ya be'em?

ref: Even if you have to suffer for doing good things, God will bless you. So stop being afraid and don't worry about what people might do.
text: La baa nẽrba sãn nãmsra ya yan ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ ĩyã, ya wʋn yẽ yel-sõnnɛ. Bɛla, da zɔta-ya ba dabeem, la ya da basɛ tɩ la dãana ya.

ref: Honor Christ and let him be the Lord of your life. Always be ready to give an answer when someone asks you about your hope.
text: Nã'asra-ya Krisi ya sũure pʋam, basɛ-ya tɩ a dɛna ya Zuudãana. Mãasʋm-ya wakatɛ wuu lɛrgra nẽr-sɛka woo n sokrɩ ya, bɔɔra tɩ a bãŋɛ yãma n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ gu'ura sɛla la yelle n ãn se'em na.

ref: Give a kind and respectful answer and keep your conscience clear. This way you will make people ashamed for saying bad things about your good conduct as a follower of Christ.
text: La ya ɩta bɛla la sũ-bʋgsʋm, la wɔɔrɛ. Ɩta-ya sɛla tɩ ya mi ya sũure pʋam tɩ la ãn sõŋa, tɩ yãnnɛ yõkɛ sɛba n tɔgrɩ ya be'em, yãma n tõnnɩ sõŋa Krisi ĩyã la.

ref: You are better off to obey God and suffer for doing right than to suffer for doing wrong.
text: Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa nãmsra tɩ la sãn dɛna la Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ bɛla, bɛla n ãn sõŋa gãnna yãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ be'em nãmsra.

ref: Christ died once for our sins. An innocent person died for those who are guilty. Christ did this to bring you to God, when his body was put to death and his spirit was made alive.
text: Krisi nãmsɛ mɛ ki bʋyɩla tɩ a yese tõon-be'ero, ẽŋa n de sɛka n masɛ la, ki la sɛba n ka masɛ ĩyã, tɩ a tarɩ ya sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Ba kʋ ẽ mɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa basɛ tɩ a vo'oge

ref: Christ then preached to the spirits that were being kept in prison.
text: sẽŋɛ ka mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga bɔ sɩɩsɩ sɛba tɩ ba yʋ la.

ref: They had disobeyed God while Noah was building the boat, but God had been patient with them. Eight people went into that boat and were brought safely through the flood.
text: Sɩɩsɩ bãma de la nẽr-sɛba n yʋʋn ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ Anoe wakatɛ la. Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn mã'agɛ la a sũure gu'ura, wakat-sɛka tɩ Anoe yʋʋn mãalɩ õorŋɔ la, õorŋɔ ẽŋa pʋam tɩ nẽrba fẽe, banii mã'a kẽ mĩ põsɛ ko'om na pʋam yẽ fãarɛ.

ref: Those flood waters were like baptism that now saves you. But baptism is more than just washing your body. It means turning to God with a clear conscience, because Jesus Christ was raised from death.
text: Bɛla de la makrɛ pa'ala ko'om misga n fãarɩ ya lɛɛlɛ wã. Ko'om misga dagɩ ĩyã dẽgrɔ yɛsga yelle, la de la sɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ nẽra looge yɛm tɩ a doose Nawẽnnɛ la pʋpeelem. Ko'om misga fãarɩ ya mɛ Azezi-krisi n ki dee vo'oge la ĩyã.

ref: Christ is now in heaven, where he sits at the right side of God. All angels, authorities, and powers are under his control.
text: Ẽŋa n zẽkɛ sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ yire ka zĩ'ire Nawẽnnɛ zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ malɛkadõma, la kɛ'ɛndõma, la pãnsɩ dõma wuu sakra ẽ.

ref: Christ suffered here on earth. Now you must be ready to suffer as he did, because suffering shows that you have stopped sinning.
text: Bɛla, Krisi n nãmsɛ a ĩyã pʋam na, ya mẽ keŋe ya mẽŋa tara pʋtẽ'erɛ ẽŋa mẽŋa. Nẽr-sɛka woo n nãmsɛ a ĩyã pʋam basɛ tõon-be'ero mɛ.

ref: It means you have turned from your own desires and want to obey God for the rest of your life.
text: Tɩ ẽn ken vɔa dũnia zuo wakat-sɛka la, a kãn le vɔa wʋ nẽrsaala yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em, a wʋn vɔa wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em.

ref: You have already lived long enough like people who don't know God. You were immoral and followed your evil desires. You went around drinking and partying and carrying on. In fact, you even worshiped disgusting idols.
text: Kʋrʋm, yãma n daan lagɩm na sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ ɩta bãma n boorɩ se'em na, da le ɩta-ya bɛla. Yãma daan lagɩm na ba mɛ, ba yalsɩ, la ba be'em yɛmleego, la ba dã-yũure, la ba pʋyã'an-yooro, la ba dã-buka, la ba baga kãabgɔ n de be'em na pʋam.

ref: Now your former friends wonder why you have stopped running around with them, and they curse you for it.
text: La lɛɛlɛ wã, yãma n ka le lagɩm na ba lɛ tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ bãma la, ba ka bãŋɛ a võorɛ, tɩ ba tʋʋra ya.

ref: But they will have to answer to God, who judges the living and the dead.
text: La ba wʋn wa tɔgɛ bãma n ẽŋɛ se'em bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n mãasʋm gu'ura tɩ a di sɛba n vɔa, la sɛba n ki sarɩya la.

ref: The good news has even been preached to the dead, so that after they have been judged for what they have done in this life, their spirits will live with God.
text: Bɛla ĩyã tɩ ba daan mõolɛ kõ-yẽlga la mẽ bɔ sɛba n ki la, tɩ ban ki wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n di nẽrsaalba wuu sarɩya tɩ ba kiira la, ba tã'agɛ yẽ vɔm tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔ'ɔrɩ.

ref: Everything will soon come to an end. So be serious and be sensible enough to pray.
text: Sɛla woo ba'asgɔ lɛm mɛ. Bɛla ĩyã, tara-ya ya mẽŋa sõŋa, dee mõrgɛ la Nawẽnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ.

ref: Most important of all, you must sincerely love each other, because love wipes away many sins.
text: Sɛla n gãnnɩ wuu de la ya nõŋɛ taaba zozo'e, se'ere n soe la, nõŋlʋm n botɩ tɩ basra taaba tõon-be'ero zozo'e taalɛ.

ref: Welcome people into your home and don't grumble about it.
text: Ɩta-ya taaba sãanɔ, da yõnna.

ref: Each of you has been blessed with one of God's many wonderful gifts to be used in the service of others. So use your gift well.
text: Yãma nẽra woo to'oge la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ bɔ'a, dɩkɛ-ya ba sõŋɛ nẽrba basɛba. Bɛla tɩ ya wʋn dɛna nẽrba n bɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ'a to'oto'ore la yelle sõŋa.

ref: If you have the gift of speaking, preach God's message. If you have the gift of helping others, do it with the strength that God supplies. Everything should be done in a way that will bring honor to God because of Jesus Christ, who is glorious and powerful forever. Amen. Suffering for Being a Christian
text: Nẽr-sɛka n tɔgrɩ, a tɔgɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n yele se'em, nẽr-sɛka n tõnnɩ sõŋrɛ tõoma, a sõŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ẽ pãŋa se'em. Tɩ tõon-bãma wuu wʋn nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ, la pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina. Sɛka n nãmsrɩ Krisi doosgo ĩyã tarɩ zu-yẽlga

ref: Dear friends, don't be surprised or shocked that you are going through testing that is like walking through fire.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, da basɛ-ya tɩ nãmsgɔ kãtɛ sɛka n paarɩ ya la ãna wʋ yel-di'iŋa bɔ ya.

ref: Be glad for the chance to suffer as Christ suffered. It will prepare you for even greater happiness when he makes his glorious return.
text: Tõn tara-ya sũ-yẽlga, tɩ yãma po nãmsra la Krisi nãmsgɔ, tɩ ya wʋn tã'agɛ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm wakatɛ sɛka tɩ a na'am pãŋa wʋn puke la.

ref: Count it a blessing when you suffer for being a Christian. This shows that God's glorious Spirit is with you.
text: Ba sãn tʋʋra ya yãma n de Krisi nẽrba la ĩyã, ya tarɩ zu-yẽlga, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la n tarɩ nã'asgɔ zozo'e la boe la ya lɛ mɛ.

ref: But you deserve to suffer if you are a murderer, a thief, a crook, or a busybody.
text: La da basɛ-ya tɩ ya nẽr-sɛka nãmsra ẽŋa n de nẽr-kʋʋra, bɩɩ nayiga, bɩɩ tõon-be'ero dãana, bɩɩ nẽra n kẽ'erɩ nẽrba yɛla n ka pakɛ ẽ pʋam.

ref: Don't be ashamed to suffer for being a Christian. Praise God that you belong to him.
text: La nẽra sãn nãmsra ẽŋa n de Krisi nẽra ĩyã, a da zɔta yãnnɛ. A nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ, ban wi'iri ẽ Krisi nẽra la ĩyã.

ref: God has already begun judging his own people. And if his judgment begins with us, imagine how terrible it will be for those who refuse to obey his message. The Scriptures say,
text: Wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn di sarɩya, la a wʋn pɔsɛ la a mẽŋa nẽrba. La sãn dɛna tɩ a wʋn dẽŋɛ di la tõma sarɩya pɔspɔsɩ, sɛba n ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga la ba'asgɔ wʋn ãna la wãne?

ref: “If good people barely escape, what will happen to sinners and to others who don't respect God?”
text: La gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam yetɩ: «La sãn dɛna toogo bɔ tõon-sõma dãana dee tɩ a yẽ fãarɛ, tõon-be'ero dãana n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore yelle wʋn ãna la wãne?»

ref: If you suffer for obeying God, you must have complete faith in your faithful Creator and keep on doing right.
text: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛba n nãmsrɩ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na, ba ɩta sɛla n ãn sõŋa, dee dɩkɛ ba mẽŋa ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam ba dee dɛna sɩra dãana la nu'usum.

ref: Church leaders, I am writing to encourage you. I too am a leader, as well as a witness to Christ's suffering, and I will share in his glory when it is shown to us.
text: Mam yetɩ m kã'an na sɛba n de Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba kẽma bɔna ya pʋam na. Mam mẽ de la kẽema, dee dɛna Krisi nãmsgɔ la kaset-dãana, la mam wʋn po yẽ a na'am pãŋa n wʋn wa puke la mɛ.

ref: Just as shepherds watch over their sheep, you must watch over everyone God has placed in your care. Do it willingly in order to please God, and not simply because you think you must. Let it be something you want to do, instead of something you do merely to make money.
text: Mam yetɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya bɩsra Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la tɩ a gu'ule ya wʋ dũnsĩima la. La, la da kãn dɛna dɔk-m-zuugo yelle, la dɛna la yɛmleego wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em na. Da ɩta-ya yan boorɩ bõntarsõm ĩyã, ɩta-ya ya yɛm n boe a pʋam ĩyã.

ref: Then when Christ the Chief Shepherd returns, you will be given a crown that will never lose its glory.
text: Tɩ dũnsĩin-kɛ'ɛma la sãn wa puke, ya wʋn to'oge yɔɔrɔ n ka sã'anɩ n tarɩ pẽka.

ref: All of you young people should obey your elders. In fact, everyone should be humble toward everyone else. The Scriptures say, “God opposes proud people, but he helps everyone who is humble.”
text: Bɛla mẽ, kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ, sakra-ya kẽma la. La ya wuu tara sik-m-mẽŋa la taaba, tɩ la gʋlsɛ yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ sisri zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma mɛ, dee ɩta sik-m-mẽsɩ dõma yel-sõnnɛ.»

ref: Be humble in the presence of God's mighty power, and he will honor you when the time comes.
text: Bɛla, sike ya mẽŋa Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo dãana la nẽŋam, tɩ wakatɛ sãn paagɛ, a wʋn zẽkɛ ya.

ref: God cares for you, so turn all your worries over to him.
text: Basɛ-ya ya fablɩgɔ la wuu bɔ ẽ, tɩ a bɩsrɩ ya yelle mɛ.

ref: Be on your guard and stay awake. Your enemy, the devil, is like a roaring lion, sneaking around to find someone to attack.
text: Dɛna-ya nẽrba n yõkrɩ ya mẽŋa, dee guusra ya mẽŋa sõŋa, tɩ ya bɛ Asũtãana eerɩ ya mɛ, wʋ gɩgnɛ n kaarɩ ɛɛra sɛla tɩ a yõkɛ obe.

ref: But you must resist the devil and stay strong in your faith. You know that all over the world the Lord's followers are suffering just as you are.
text: Bɔ-ya Azezi-krisi sɩra ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, dee sɩ'ɩsɛ Asũtãana, la ya bãŋɛ tɩ ya sɔbiisi n boe dũnia zẽ'a woo mẽ nãmsrɩ la nãmsgɔ bãma mẽŋa buuri.

ref: But God shows undeserved kindness to everyone. That's why he appointed Christ Jesus to choose you to share in his eternal glory. You will suffer for a while, but God will make you complete, steady, strong, and firm.
text: Ya wʋn ken nãmsɛ mɛ wakatɛ fẽe. La Nawẽnnɛ n soe yel-sõnnɛ wuu la wi ya mɛ tɩ ya po yẽ a na'am pãŋa n ka tarɩ ba'asgɔ, yãma n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la ĩyã. Ẽŋa wʋn le malgɛ ya tɩ ya ãna sõŋa, bɔ ya pãŋa tɩ ya ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ, tɩ sɛla kãn tã'agɛ botɩ ya bike.

ref: God will be in control forever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: Ẽŋa n soe pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina. Pʋ'ʋsgɔ

ref: Silvanus helped me write this short letter, and I consider him a faithful follower of the Lord. I wanted to encourage you and tell you how kind God really is, so you will keep on having faith in him.
text: Mam botɩ Asilvẽ n gʋlsɛ gõn-pɩka wã, ẽŋa n de sɔbia tɩ mam bɩsɛ tɩ a de sɩra dãana. Mam gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã tɩ m bɔ ya la bʋraanɛ, dee botɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ na tɩ ya tarɩ la, de la Nawẽnnɛ yel-sõnnɛ mẽŋa mẽŋa pʋam tɩ ya boe.

ref: Greetings from the Lord's followers in Babylon. They are God's chosen ones. Mark, who is like a son to me, sends his greetings too.
text: Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe Babilɔnɩ tɩ a tũ'use ba wʋ yãma la pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya, Amarkɩ n lebge mam dayʋa la mẽ pʋ'ʋsrɩ ya.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. I pray that God will give peace to everyone who belongs to Christ.
text: Pʋ'ʋsɛ-ya taaba la nõŋlʋm. Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la yãma sɛba wuu n lagɩm na Krisi dɛna ayɩla la.

ref: From Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ. To everyone who shares with us in the privilege of believing that our God and Savior Jesus Christ will do what is just and fair.
text: Mam Asimõ-pɩyɛɛrɩ n de Azezi-krisi yamŋa la a Tõntõnna, n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra yãma sɛba tɩ Azezi-krisi n de Nawẽnnɛ la tɩ Fãagra n masɛ sɛla woo pʋam, botɩ ya bɔ ẽ sɩra sõŋa wʋ tõma n bɔ ẽ sɩra se'em na.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to you and will let you live in perfect peace! May you keep learning more and more about God and our Lord Jesus. Living as the Lord's Followers
text: Yãma n bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi la, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm zozo'e. Nawẽnnɛ n looge tɩ la, tɩ yel-ɩrɛ n masɛ tɩ la ãna se'em

ref: We have everything we need to live a life that pleases God. It was all given to us by God's own power, when we learned he had invited us to share in his wonderful goodness.
text: Azezi n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa la n botɩ tɩ bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi tõma tɩ tɩ po yẽ a na'am pãŋa, la a sõŋa. Bɛla tɩ a bɔ tɔ bõn-sɛba wuu n wʋn basɛ tɩ tɩ vɔa vɔm sɛka n dolɩ a sore.

ref: God made great and marvelous promises, so his nature would become part of us. Then we could escape our evil desires and the corrupt influences of this world.
text: La de la bɛla tɩ a bɔ tɔ bɔ'a sɛba n ãn sõŋa gãnna wuu tɩ a bĩŋe nõorɛ tɩ a bɔ tɔ la. Tɩ bõn-bãma ĩyã ya wʋn tã'agɛ lɔkɛ be'em yɛmleego n boe dũnia zuo sã'ana nẽrba la, dee lebge ãna wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ãn se'em na.

ref: Do your best to improve your faith by adding goodness, understanding,
text: Bɛla, nargɛ-ya pãŋa, tɩ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam, ɩr-sõŋɔ pa'asɛ, tɩ ɩr-sõŋɔ la pʋam, bãŋrɛ pa'asɛ,

ref: If you keep growing in this way, it will show that what you know about our Lord Jesus Christ has made your lives useful and meaningful.
text: Se'ere n soe la, ya sãn ɩta bõn-bãna wã zozo'e, ba wʋn basɛ tɩ ya tõm Nawẽnnɛ tõoma, dee bãŋɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi pa'asɛ.

ref: But if you don't grow, you are like someone who is nearsighted or blind, and you have forgotten that your past sins are forgiven.
text: La sɛka n ka ɩtɩ bõn-bãna wã ãn wʋ fʋa la, a ka yẽtɩ, la a yĩm mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ a tõon-be'ero tɩ a ẽŋɛ kʋrʋm na taalɛ mɛ.

ref: My friends, you must do all you can to show God has really chosen and selected you. If you keep on doing this, you won't stumble and fall.
text: Bɛla m sɔbiisi, mõrgɛ ya mẽŋa ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n wi ya, la ẽn looge ya la pʋam. Ya sãn ẽŋɛ bɛla, ya kãn bʋrgɛ dee basɛ Nawẽnnɛ sore.

ref: Then our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will give you a glorious welcome into his kingdom that will last forever.
text: La de la bɛla tɩ ya wʋn yẽ sore sõŋa kẽ Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana, la tɩ Fãagra la so'olʋm n boe wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ la pʋam.

ref: You are holding firmly to the truth you were given. But I am still going to remind you of these things.
text: La de la bɛla ĩyã tɩ mam tẽera ya bõn-bãna wakatɛ wuu, baa la yãma n põn mina ba, la yãma n ze kãŋkãŋɛ sɩra la tɩ ya to'oge la pʋam na.

ref: In fact, I think I should keep on reminding you until I leave this body.
text: Mam n nãn vɔa wã, mam tẽ'esɛ tɩ la masɛ mɛ tɩ mam tẽegɛ ya, tɩ ya da gĩse.

ref: And our Lord Jesus Christ has already told me that I will soon leave it behind.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n puke pa'alɛ mam, tɩ mam mina tɩ la deege la fẽe tɩ mam basɛ vɔm ẽna wã dee fõrgɛ.

ref: This is why I am doing my best to make sure that each of you remembers all of this after I am gone. The Message about the Glory of Christ
text: Bɛla mam wʋn mõrgɛ mɛ, la mam fõrgrɛ poorʋm, ya wʋn tẽra bõn-bãna yelle sɛla woo pʋam. Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n tɔgɛ Azezi-krisi yelle se'em na de la sɩra

ref: When we told you about the power and the return of our Lord Jesus Christ, we were not telling clever stories someone had made up. But with our own eyes we saw his true greatness.
text: Tõma n tɔgɛ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi pãŋa, la a wa'aŋɔ la yelle la, la dagɩ sɔlma tɩ nẽrsaalba tẽ'esɛ nãam la ba mẽŋa yɛm. Tõma sɩrɩ yẽ a na'am pãŋa n zo'oge se'em mɛ la tɩ nini.

ref: God, our great and wonderful Father, truly honored him by saying, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la, n bɔ ẽ nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa. Ẽŋa n de na'am pãŋa wuu dãana la yetɩ: «Ẽna de la mam Dayʋa tɩ mam nõŋɛ la m sũure wuu, a paagɛ la mam yɛm.»

ref: We were there with Jesus on the holy mountain and heard this voice speak from heaven.
text: Tõma n daan boe la ẽ lɛ tãŋa la zuo la, tɩ tõma wõm kõa la n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ.

ref: All of this makes us even more certain that what the prophets said is true. So you should pay close attention to their message, as you would to a lamp shining in some dark place. You must keep on paying attention until daylight comes and the morning star rises in your hearts.
text: Bɛla n basɛ tɩ tõma malɩn bãŋɛ sõŋa sõŋa tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la de la sɩra. La ãn sõŋa tɩ ya tara yetɔg-bãma sõŋa, ba ãn wʋ fɩtla n nẽegɛ lika pʋam. Tara-ya ba halɩ ka paagɛ beere yɩlɩŋrɛ, tɩ Krisi nẽegɛ ya sũure pʋam wʋ bewã'arɛ la.

ref: But you need to realize that no one alone can understand any of the prophecies in the Scriptures.
text: Sɛla n gãnnɩ de la ya bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba yetɔga la n gʋlsɛ Nawẽnnɛ gõŋɔ pʋam na, nẽra ka tã wʋn pa'alɛ ba võorɛ la a mẽŋa pʋtẽ'erɛ.

ref: The prophets did not think these things up on their own, but they were guided by the Spirit of God.
text: Se'ere n soe la, ba ka de nẽrsaalba yɛm n boorɩ se'em, la de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n botɩ nẽrsaalba tɔgɛ yetɔga n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa zẽ'am.

ref: Sometimes false prophets spoke to the people of Israel. False teachers will also sneak in and speak harmful lies to you. But these teachers don't really belong to the Master who paid a great price for them, and they will quickly destroy themselves.
text: Kʋrʋm kʋrʋm, nẽrba n yʋʋn boe Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la pʋam parna tɩ bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba. Bɛla, põmpɔrŋɔ karẽnsãandõma n mẽ wʋn bɔna ya pʋam. Ba wʋn ẽŋɛ la yɛm tarɩ zãsŋɔ n sẽm na sã'aŋɔ wa'am, dee wʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Zuudãana n fãagɛ ba la, bɛla n wʋn basɛ tɩ sã'aŋɔ wa'am ba zuto tɔtɔ.

ref: Many people will follow their evil ways and cause others to tell lies about the true way.
text: La nẽrba zozo'e n wʋn doose ba, ba tõon-yooro la poorʋm, tɩ la wʋn basɛ tɩ nẽrba tɔgɛ sɩra sore la yetɔg-be'ero.

ref: They will be greedy and cheat you with smooth talk. But long ago God decided to punish them, and God doesn't sleep.
text: Ban boorɩ bõntarsõm zo'oge la ĩyã tɩ ba wʋn tɔgɛ nõ-yẽlʋm yetɔga pã'asɛ ya, yẽ yõorɔ ya zẽ'am. La halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ ba sɩbgrɛ põn mãasʋm gu'ura ba, ba sã'aŋɔ kãn viige.

ref: God did not have pity on the angels that sinned. He had them tied up and thrown into the dark pits of hell until the time of judgment.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn ka basɛ malɛkadõma sɛba n tõm tõon-be'ero la tɩ ba põsɛ, a lobe ba basɛ la bug-tẽŋa bog-ko'ogo n de lika pʋam, ba ba la bãnsɩ gu'ura sarɩyadia daarɛ.

ref: And during Noah's time, God did not have pity on the ungodly people of the world. He destroyed them with a flood, though he did save eight people, including Noah, who preached the truth.
text: La a yʋʋn ka basɛ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm dõma la tɩ ba põsɛ, sãn dagna nẽrba banii mã'a. La de la Anoe n mõolɛ sɛla n masɛ yelle, la a yir-dõma mã'a tɩ a yʋʋn fãagɛ, dee basɛ tɩ sa-kãtɛ ni, tɩ ko'om libe tẽŋa sã'am dũnia nẽrba wuu n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: God punished the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah by burning them to ashes, and this is a warning to anyone else who wants to sin.
text: La a ken di Sodom la Gomõorɩ tẽnsɩ sarɩya mɛ sɩbgɛ ba, basɛ tɩ bugum di ba, tɩ ba lebge tãmpɛglʋm, tɩ la dɛna makrɛ bɔ sɛba n ka dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sore la.

ref: Lot lived right and was greatly troubled by the terrible way those wicked people were living. He was a good man, and day after day he suffered because of the evil things he saw and heard. So the Lord rescued him.
text: Alotɩ n yʋʋn de tõon-sõma dãana vɔa la ba lɛ, wõnna dee yẽta ba tõon-be'ero la, a sũure n sã'anɩ daarɛ woo, tɩ la nãmsra ẽ.

ref: This shows that the Lord knows how to rescue godly people from their sufferings and to punish evil people while they wait for the day of judgment.
text: Bɛla pa'alɩ tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mi ẽŋa n ɩtɩ se'em fãara nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ a sore la nãmsgɔ pʋam mɛ, dee mina ẽŋa n ɩtɩ tõon-be'ero dõma se'em gu'ura sarɩyadia daarɛ tɩ ba wʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ.

ref: The Lord is especially hard on people who disobey him and don't think of anything except their own filthy desires. They are reckless and proud and are not afraid of cursing the glorious beings in heaven.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ ba yɛmleego n boorɩ se'em n de tõon-dẽgrɔ, dee pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ nõorɛ la ba lɛ la, a wʋn sɩbgɛ la bãma gãnna. Põmpɔrŋɔ karẽnsãandõma la de la zẽk-m-mẽŋa dõma, la tɩta'alʋm dõma, ba ka zotɩ dabeem tɩ ba tʋ saazuum pãnsɩ dõma.

ref: Although angels are more powerful than these evil beings, even the angels don't dare to accuse them to the Lord.
text: La baa malɛkadõma n tarɩ pãŋa, la ke'eŋo gãnna ba la, ka tɔgrɩ ba yelle pɔ'ɔra ba Zuudãana la nẽŋam.

ref: These people are no better than senseless animals that live by their feelings and are born to be caught and killed. They speak evil of things they don't know anything about. But their own corrupt deeds will destroy them.
text: Nẽr-bãma ɩtɩ la yooryooro, ba ãn wʋ dũnsi n ka tarɩ yɛm tɩ ba yõgrɩ kʋʋra la. Ba tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ ba ka mi be'em, ba mẽ wʋn sã'am wʋ dũnsi la.

ref: They have done evil, and they will be rewarded with evil. They think it is fun to have wild parties during the day. They are immoral, and the meals they eat with you are spoiled by the shameful and selfish way they carry on.
text: Ba wʋn yẽ tõon-be'ero yɔɔrɔ ba tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. Ba tõnnɩ na ba yɛm n boorɩ se'em n de tõon-dẽgrɔ peelem pʋam, tɩ ba sũure ãna yẽlʋm. Ba sãn lagɩm na ya dita, ɩta sũ-yẽlga la ba põmpã'asʋm na, la de la dẽgrɔ, la yãnnɛ.

ref: All they think about is having sex with someone else's husband or wife. There is no end to their wicked deeds. They trick people who are easily fooled, and their minds are filled with greedy thoughts. But they are headed for trouble!
text: Ba yɛm boorɩ la yalsɩ tõoma mã'a, ba ka tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero tɩgra. Ba pã'asrɩ la nẽr-sɛba pʋtẽ'era n ka zo'oge Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo pʋam na tɩ ba tõnna be'em. Ba nõŋɛ la bõnɔ zozo'e. Nawẽnnɛ kã'a-be'ero boe ba zuto.

ref: They have left the true road and have gone down the wrong path by following the example of the prophet Balaam. He was the son of Beor and loved what he got from being a crook.
text: Ba basɛ so-sõŋɔ la mɛ dee bʋrgɛ dɔla Abalaam n de Abozɔɔrɩ dayʋa la sore, ẽŋa nõŋɛ la ligri bɔɔra tɩ a tõm tõon-be'ero to'oge yɔɔrɔ.

ref: But a donkey corrected him for this evil deed. It spoke to him with a human voice and made him stop his foolishness.
text: La Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn gi Abalaam n de a nõtɔ'ɔsa la mɛ a tõon-be'ero la ĩyã. A yʋʋn botɩ Abalaam mẽŋa bõŋa n tɔgɛ la ẽ wʋ nẽrsaala la, gu ẽ tɩ a da tõm a yalnɛ tõoma la.

ref: These people are like dried up water holes and clouds blown by a windstorm. The darkest part of hell is waiting for them.
text: Nẽr-bãma ãn wʋ bulsi n ka tarɩ ko'om, dee ãna wʋ sawara tɩ kʋsebgo pe'ege la. Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm na zẽ'a n de lika tam gu'ura ba.

ref: They brag out loud about their stupid nonsense. And by being vulgar and crude, they trap people who have barely escaped from living the wrong kind of life.
text: Ba pa'alɩ la ba mẽŋa tɔgra yetɔg-kãra n ka tarɩ võorɛ, dee tara ba yɛm n boorɩ se'em n de tõon-dẽgrɔ la pã'asra nẽr-sɛba n nãn welge ba mẽŋa la nẽr-sɛba n vɔa tuure pʋam na.

ref: They promise freedom to everyone. But they are merely slaves of filthy living, because people are slaves of whatever controls them.
text: Ba yetɩ nẽrba la tɩ ba wʋn yẽ yolsgo, dee tɩ ba mẽŋa dɛna tõon-dẽgrɔ n wʋn sã'am ba la yamsɩ. Se'ere n soe la, nẽra woo de la sɛla n soe ẽ na yamŋa.

ref: When they learned about our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they escaped from the filthy things of this world. But they are again caught up and controlled by these filthy things, and now they are in worse shape than they were at first.
text: Nẽr-sɛba n bãŋɛ Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana, la tɩ Fãagra la, ba basɛ dũnia tõon-dẽgrɔ mɛ. Ba sãn le kẽ ba pʋam basɛ tɩ ba sɔna ba, ba poorʋm toogo la wʋn gãnna pɔspɔsɩ la.

ref: They would have been better off if they had never known about the right way. Even after they knew what was right, they turned their backs on the holy commandments they were given.
text: Bɛla, ba sãn ka bãŋɛ nɩ sɛla n masɛ sore la sõɩ ban bãŋɛ ẽ dee wẽrgɛ basɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ tɩ ba to'oge tɔgɛ bɔ ba la.

ref: What happened to them is just like the true saying, “A dog will come back to lick up its own vomit. A pig that has been washed will roll in the mud.”
text: La ẽŋɛ ba bɛla pa'alɛ tɩ magsa wã de la sɩra, ba yetɩ: «Baaga lebe ka di la sɛla tɩ a tĩ la», dee le yetɩ: «Kurkuri tɩ ba peege ẽ sõŋa sõŋa la, lebe ka bilim na bɛglʋm pʋam.»

ref: what God's prophets said would happen. You must never forget what the holy prophets taught in the past. And you must remember what the apostles told you our Lord and Savior has commanded us to do.
text: Mam boorɩ tɩ ya tẽra la yetɔg-sɛba tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ halɩ yʋʋma yʋʋma, la tɩ Zuudãana Azezi n de tɩ Fãagra la nõorɛ tɩ Tõntõnɩba la tɩ a tõm ya zẽ'am na tɔgɛ bɔ ya la.

ref: But first you must realize that in the last days some people won't think about anything except their own selfish desires. They will make fun of you
text: Sɛla n gãnnɩ de la ya bãŋɛ tɩ dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ, nẽrba n la'arɩ nẽrba n wʋn wa wa'am, wa vɔa vɔm sɛka n de be'em tɩ ba mẽŋa boorɩ. Ba wʋn la'ara ya,

ref: and say, “Didn't your Lord promise to come back? Yet the first leaders have already died, and the world hasn't changed a bit.”
text: dee yetɩ: «Azezi n daan bĩŋe nõorɛ yetɩ a wʋn leme wa'am na, a bɛɛ? Halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ tɩ yaabdõma ki wa paam wakat-ẽna wã, sɛla woo ken ãna la bɛla wʋ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo n yʋʋn ãn se'em na.»

ref: They will say this because they want to forget that long ago the heavens and the earth were made at God's command. The earth came out of water and was made from water.
text: Ban tɔgrɩ bɛla la, ba boorɩ tɩ ba yetɩ bãma yĩm mɛ tɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm Nawẽnnɛ tɔgɛ la yetɔga tɩ saazuum la tẽŋa nãam, tɩ zẽ'a woo daan dɛna ko'om, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ botɩ tẽŋa ze'ele ko'om pʋam bɔna.

ref: Later it was destroyed by the waters of a mighty flood.
text: La ken dɛna la ko'om, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋʋn botɩ ba libe dũnia ẽŋa sã'am ẽ.

ref: But God has commanded the present heavens and earth to remain until the day of judgment. Then they will be set on fire, and ungodly people will be destroyed.
text: Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga mẽŋa n ken botɩ dũni-ẽna wã, la saazuum bɔna gu'ura tɩ bugum wa sã'am ba. A basɛ tɩ ba bɔna gu'ura la daar-sɛka tɩ a wʋn di nẽrsaalba sarɩya, sã'am tõon-be'ero dõma la.

ref: The Lord isn't slow about keeping his promises, as some people think he is. In fact, God is patient, because he wants everyone to turn from sin and no one to be lost.
text: Zuudãana la n bĩŋe a nõorɛ tɩ a ẽŋɛ sɛla la, la ka yuuri tɩ a kãn ẽŋɛ wʋ nẽrba basɛba n tẽ'esrɩ se'em na. A mã'agɛ la a sũure la ya, a ka boorɩ tɩ nẽra nẽra sã'am, a boorɩ tɩ nẽra woo n teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

ref: The day of the Lord's return will surprise us like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a loud noise, and the heat will melt the whole universe. Then the earth and everything on it will be seen for what they are.
text: Zuudãana la lemŋo daarɛ wʋn ãna wʋ nayiga n liŋri se'em na. Daar-ẽŋa, vool-kãtɛ n wʋn ẽŋɛ, tɩ bugum di bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum, tɩ saazuum bɔɩ. Tɩ tẽŋa, la bõn-sɛba n boe a pʋam na wuu mẽ ka le bɔna.

ref: Everything will be destroyed. So you should serve and honor God by the way you live.
text: Bõn-bãma wuu n wʋn sã'am bɛla la, dẽnɩ tɩ ya basɛ tɩ ya yel-ɩrɛ, la ya Nawẽnnɛ sore doosgo ãna sõŋa, tɩ ya sakra ẽ sɛla woo pʋam.

ref: You should look forward to the day when God judges everyone, and you should try to make it come soon. On that day the heavens will be destroyed by fire, and everything else will melt in the heat.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ ya gu'ura Nawẽnnɛ daarɛ, tɩ Azezi wʋn wa'am na, la ya nargɛ pãŋa tɩ a wa'am tɔtɔ. Daar-ẽŋa, bugum n wʋn di saazuum, tɩ bõn-sɛba n boe saazuum wʋn yẽlgɛ sã'am.

ref: But God has promised us a new heaven and a new earth, where justice will rule. We are really looking forward to this!
text: Tõma gu'uri la saazuum paalga, la dũnia paalga tɩ sɛla woo masɛ ãna sõŋa, wʋ ẽn bĩŋe a nõorɛ se'em na.

ref: My friends, while you are waiting, you should make certain the Lord finds you pure, spotless, and living at peace.
text: Bɛla, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, yãma n gu'uri bɛla la, nargɛ-ya pãŋa ãna sõŋa, tɩ ya da tara dõrga Nawẽnnɛ nifum, tɩ ya wʋn bɔna la ẽ la sũ-mã'asʋm.

ref: Don't forget that the Lord is patient because he wants people to be saved. This is also what our dear friend Paul said when he wrote you with the wisdom God had given him.
text: Bãŋɛ-ya tɩ Zuudãana la n mã'agɛ a sũure gu'ura la, a boorɩ tɩ ya yẽ la fãarɛ. La de la bɛla mẽ tɩ tɩ sɔbi-nõŋrɛ Apolɩ daan dɩkɛ yɛm sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ na gʋlsɛ bɔ ya.

ref: Paul talks about these same things in all his letters, but part of what he says is hard to understand. Some ignorant and unsteady people even destroy themselves by twisting what he said. They do the same thing with other Scriptures too.
text: A gõn-sɛba wuu pʋam tɩ a tɔgrɩ bɛla yelle la, a gʋlsɛ la bɛla. A gõnnɔ na pʋam, zẽ'esɩ basɛba n boe mĩ tɩ ba wõŋa tɔ, tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka mi, dee ka vigle ban bɔ sɩra la pʋam na, tɛɛra ba võorɛ n de se'em, wʋ ban ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ gõnnɔ basɛba se'em na. Ban ɩtɩ bɛla la sẽm na ba mẽŋa sã'aŋɔ.

ref: My dear friends, you have been warned ahead of time! So don't let the errors of evil people lead you down the wrong path and make you lose your balance.
text: La sãn dɛna yãma, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, yãma n dẽŋɛ tara tiire la, guuse-ya, da basɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero dõma n tuuge la, tuuge ya. Da basɛ-ya tɩ ba botɩ ya basɛ yãma n bɔ sɩra zɛ kãŋkãŋɛ wã.

ref: Let the gift of undeserved grace and the understanding that come from our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ help you keep on growing. Praise Jesus now and forever! Amen.
text: Sẽŋɛ-ya nẽŋa vɔa Azezi-krisi n de tɩ Zuudãana la tɩ Fãagra la yel-sõnnɛ pʋam, bãŋra ẽ pa'asra. Ẽŋa n soe na'am pãŋa lɛɛlɛ wã, la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

ref: The Word that gives life was from the beginning, and this is the one our message is about. Our ears have heard, our eyes have seen, and our hands have touched this Word.
text: Tõma gʋlsɛ la sɛka n boe halɩ pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ yelle bɔ'ɔra ya. A de la sɛka tɩ tõma wõm a yetɔga, yẽ ẽ la tɩ nini, bɩsɛ ẽ sõŋa sõŋa, kalʋm ẽ na tɩ nu'usi. Ẽŋa n de yetɔga n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm.

ref: The one who gives life appeared! We saw it happen, and we are witnesses to what we have seen. Now we are telling you about this eternal life that was with the Father and appeared to us.
text: Sɛka n de vɔm na puke a mẽŋa mɛ, tɩ tõma yẽ ẽ. Tɩ tõma tɔgra a yelle, mõola vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ la yelle bɔ'ɔra ya. A daan boe la tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, tɩ a puke ẽ pa'alɛ tõma.

ref: We are telling you what we have seen and heard, so you may share in this life with us. And we share in it with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text: Sɛla tɩ tõma yẽ dee wõm na, tõma tɔgrɩ la ẽŋa bɔ'ɔra yãma, tɩ yãma wʋn lagɩm na tõma, mẽ naagɛ la tõma tara lagɩmtaarɛ la tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi.

ref: We are writing to tell you these things, because this makes us truly happy. God Is Light
text: Tõma gʋlsɛ ya la wãna tɩ tɩ wʋn tara sũ-yẽlga zozo'e. Tɩ vɔa pʋpeelem pʋam wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n ãn se'em na

ref: Jesus told us God is light and doesn't have any darkness in him. Now we are telling you.
text: Kõa n wãna tɩ tõma wõm Azezi-krisi zẽ'am, tɔgra ẽ bɔ'ɔra ya: Nawẽnnɛ de la peelem, lika ka boe a zẽ'am baa fẽfẽe.

ref: If we say we share in life with God and keep on living in the dark, we are lying and are not living by the truth.
text: Tɩ sãn yetɩ tõma tarɩ lagɩmtaarɛ la Nawẽnnɛ, dee ken vɔa lika pʋam tõnna be'em, tɩ parnɩ mɛ, tɩ ka ɩtɩ sɩra.

ref: But if we live in the light, as God does, we share in life with each other. And the blood of his Son Jesus washes all our sins away.
text: La tɩ sãn vɔa peelem pʋam wʋ Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n boe peelem pʋam na, tɩ tarɩ lagɩmtaarɛ la taaba mɛ, la a Dayʋa Azezi zɩɩm peerɩ tɩ mɛ yɛsra tɩ tõon-be'ero wuu.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we are fooling ourselves, and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: La tɩ sãn yetɩ, tõma ka tarɩ tõon-be'ero, tɩ pã'asɛ la tɩ mẽŋa, la sɩra ka boe tɩ zẽ'am.

ref: But if we confess our sins to God, he can always be trusted to forgive us and take our sins away.
text: Tɩ sãn pelge tɩ pʋʋrɛ tɔgɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn basɛ tɩ tõon-be'ero taalɛ mɛ, dee peege to, yese sɛla wuu n ka masɛ, se'ere n soe la, a de la sɩra dãana dee ɩta sɛla n masɛ.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we make God a liar, and his message isn't in our hearts.
text: Tɩ sãn yetɩ, tõma ka tõm tõon-be'ero, tɩ yetɩ a de la põmpɔrma, la a yetɔga la ka boe la tɩ lɛ.

ref: My children, I am writing this so you won't sin. But if you do sin, Jesus Christ always does the right thing, and he will speak to the Father for us.
text: M kɔma, mam gʋlsrɩ ya wãna tɩ ya da tõm na tõon-be'ero. La nẽra sãn tõm be'em, tõma tarɩ la sõŋra n boe tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, la de la Azezi-krisi n de pʋpeelem dãana.

ref: Christ is the sacrifice that takes away our sins and the sins of all the world's people.
text: La de la ẽŋa n dɩkɛ a mẽŋa bɔ tɩ la dɛna kãabgɔ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ basɛ tõma tõon-be'ero taalɛ. La, la dagɩ tõma mã'a tõon-be'ero taalɛ, la de la dũnia nẽrba wuu tõon-be'ero taalɛ.

ref: When we obey God, we are sure we know him.
text: Tɩ sãn sakra Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, bɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ tɩ sɩrɩ mi ẽ.

ref: But if we claim to know him and don't obey him, we are lying and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa mi ẽ, dee ka sakra a nõorɛ, a de la põmpɔrma, la a ka tarɩ sɩra.

ref: We truly love God only when we obey him as we should, and then we know we belong to him.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la, nẽr-ẽŋa n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra. Bɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ tɩ lagɩm na ẽ dɛna ayɩla.

ref: If we say we are his, we must follow the example of Christ. The New Commandment
text: Sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ a dãana vɔa wʋ Azezi-krisi n daan vɔa se'em na. Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ de la tɩ nõŋɛ taaba

ref: My dear friends, I am not writing to give you a new commandment. It is the same one you were first given, and it is the message you heard.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, la dagɩ nõor-paalga tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, a de la nõor-kɛka tɩ ya tarɩ halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la. Nõor-kɛka ẽŋa de la kõ-sɛka tɩ ya daan wõm na.

ref: But it really is a new commandment, and you know its true meaning, just as Christ does. You can see the darkness fading away and the true light already shining.
text: La a yɛrɩ dɛna la nõor-paalga tɩ mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra ya. La de la Azezi-krisi zẽ'am, la yãma zẽ'am tɩ yel-ẽŋa de sɩra, se'ere n soe la, lika la tollɩ mɛ, dee tɩ peelem sɩra sɩra darɛ nẽera.

ref: If we claim to be in the light and hate someone, we are still in the dark.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa boe la peelem pʋam, dee sisra a sɔbia, ken bɔna la lika pʋam.

ref: But if we love others, we are in the light, and we don't cause problems for them.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ a sɔbia, vɔa la peelem pʋam, bɛla ĩyã sɛla ka boe a zẽ'am n wʋn basɛ tɩ a tuuge.

ref: If we hate others, we are living and walking in the dark. We don't know where we are going, because we can't see in the dark.
text: La sɛka n sisri a sɔbia, boe la lika pʋam, la a sẽnnɩ na lika pʋam, ka mina ẽn we'esɩ zẽ'a, se'ere n soe la, lika la fɔ a nini mɛ.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because your sins have been forgiven in the name of Christ.
text: Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔma, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ basɛ ya tõon-be'ero taalɛ mɛ Azezi-krisi yʋ'ʋrɛ ĩyã.

ref: Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you have defeated the evil one.
text: Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m sɔdõma, se'ere n soe la, yãma mi sɛka n boe halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo la mɛ. Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ, se'ere n soe la, yãma yãŋɛ be'em dãana Asũtãana la mɛ.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because you have known the Father. Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you are strong. God's message is firm in your hearts, and you have defeated the evil one.
text: Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔma, se'ere n soe la, yãma mi tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ mɛ. Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m sɔdõma, se'ere n soe la, yãma mi sɛka n boe halɩ lan dẽŋɛ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo la mɛ. Mam gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra la yãma, m kɔmbɩ'ɩsɩ, se'ere n soe la, yãma tarɩ pãŋa mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la boe ya pʋam mɛ, la yãma yãŋɛ be'em dãana Asũtãana la mɛ. Da nõŋɛ-ya dũnia

ref: Don't love the world or anything that belongs to the world. If you love the world, you cannot love the Father.
text: Da nõŋɛ-ya dũnia, la bõn-sɛba n boe a pʋam na. Nẽra sãn nõŋɛ dũnia wã, Nawẽnnɛ n de tɩ Sɔ la nõŋlʋm ka boe la a dãana lɛ.

ref: Our foolish pride comes from this world, and so do our selfish desires and our desire to have everything we see. None of this comes from the Father.
text: Se'ere n soe la, bõn-sɛba n de dũnia wã bõnɔ de la nẽrsaala mẽŋa n boorɩ se'em, la nini n yẽ sɛla bɔɔra n de be'em, la nẽra n pa'alɩ a mẽŋa a bõntarsõm ĩyã. Bãma wuu ka ze'ele tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, ba de la dũnia bõnɔ.

ref: The world and the desires it causes are disappearing. But if we obey God, we will live forever. The Enemy of Christ
text: Dũnia, la bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ nẽrba yɛmleego boorɩ la tollɩ mɛ. La nẽr-sɛka n ɩtɩ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em n wʋn bɔna wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Krisi sɩ'ɩsrɩba yelle

ref: Children, this is the last hour. You heard that the enemy of Christ would appear at this time, and many of Christ's enemies have already appeared. So we know the last hour is here.
text: M kɔma, lɛɛlɛ wã la de la dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ. Yãma wõm tɩ ba yetɩ Krisi sɩ'ɩsra n sẽm. La lɛɛlɛ wã, Krisi sɩ'ɩsrɩba zo'oge mɛ bɔna, bɛla tõma mi mɛ tɩ la de la dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ.

ref: These people came from our own group, yet they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But they left, which proves they did not belong to our group.
text: Nẽr-bãma ze'ele la tõma zẽ'am yese, la ba daan dagɩ tõma nẽrba sɩra sɩra. Ba daan sãn de nɩ tõma nẽrba, ba wʋn weege nɩ bɔna la tõma zẽ'am. La ban basɛ tõma la, dẽnɩ tɩ la ẽŋɛ bɛla tɩ la pa'alɛ vẽelga tɩ ba nẽra baa ayɩla dagɩ tõma nẽra.

ref: Christ, the Holy One, has blessed you, and now all of you understand.
text: La sãn dɛna yãma, Krisi bɔ ya Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa mɛ, bɛla yãma wuu mi sɩra mɛ.

ref: I did not need to write you about the truth, since you already know it. You also know that liars do not belong to the truth.
text: Mam n gʋlsɛ bɔ ya la, mam ka yetɩ ya ka mi sɩra la, la tõn dɛna la yan mi ẽ na ĩyã, la yãma mi tɩ sɛla n de põmpɔrŋɔ ka ze'etɩ sɩra pʋam.

ref: And a liar is anyone who says Jesus isn't truly Christ. Anyone who says this is an enemy of Christ and rejects both the Father and the Son.
text: Ãne n de põmpɔrma la? La de la sɛka n sɩ'ɩsrɩ yetɩ Azezi dagɩ Krisi la. Ẽŋa n de Krisi sɩ'ɩsra la. A sɩ'ɩsrɩ la Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa la.

ref: If we reject the Son, we reject the Father. But if we say we accept the Son, we have the Father.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Dayʋa la, mẽ ka boe la Sɔ la. La nẽr-sɛka n yetɩ ẽŋa mi Dayʋa la, mẽ boe la Sɔ la mɛ.

ref: Keep thinking about the message you first heard, and you will always be one in your heart with the Son and with the Father.
text: La sãn dɛna yãma, mõrgɛ-ya tara yetɔg-sɛba tɩ ya daan wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la ya sũure pʋam. Ba sãn ken bɔna ya sũure pʋam, yãma mẽ wʋn ken bɔna la Sɔ, la Dayʋa la lɛ.

ref: I am writing to warn you about those people who are misleading you.
text: La de la nẽr-sɛba n zɩgrɩ tɩ ba pã'asɛ ya tɩ ya tuuge la yelle tɩ mam gʋlsɛ wãna bɔ'ɔra ya.

ref: But Christ has blessed you with the Holy Spirit. Now the Spirit stays in you, and you don't need any teachers. The Spirit is truthful and teaches you everything. So stay one in your heart with Christ, just as the Spirit has taught you to do. Children of God
text: La sãn dɛna yãma, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la tɩ ya to'oge Krisi zẽ'am na, boe la ya lɛ mɛ, yãma ka le ɛɛra tɩ ba zãsʋm ya. Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la n boe la ya lɛ la, zãsnɩ ya sɛla woo mɛ, la de la sɩra, a ka parnɩ. Bɔna-ya la Krisi wʋ ẽn zãsnɩ ya se'em na.

ref: Children, stay one in your hearts with Christ. Then when he returns, we will have confidence and won't have to hide in shame.
text: Ẽe, m kɔma, bɔna-ya la ẽ lɛ, tɩ a sãn wa puke daarɛ tɩ tɩ tara sũ-ke'eŋo, tɩ yãnnɛ da yõkɛ tɔ a nẽŋam a lemŋo la daarɛ.

ref: You know Christ always does right and everyone who does right is a child of God.
text: Yãma mi tɩ Krisi de la sɛka n masɛ, bɛla, bãŋɛ-ya tɩ nẽr-sɛka n mẽ ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ de la Nawẽnnɛ bia.
