ref: Think how much the Father loves us. He loves us so much that he lets us be called his children, as we truly are. But since the people of this world did not know who Christ is, they don't know who we are.
text: Bɩsɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la n nõŋɛ tɩ se'em. A nõŋlʋm na zo'oge gãŋɛ mɛ, tɩ a wi'ira tõma tɩ a kɔma. La tõma sɩrɩ de la a kɔma. Wãna n soe tɩ dũnia dõma ka mina tõma: La de la ban ka mi Nawẽnnɛ na ĩyã.

ref: My dear friends, we are already God's children, though what we will be hasn't yet been seen. But we do know when Christ returns, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, lɛɛlɛ wã tɩ de la Nawẽnnɛ kɔma. La tɩn wʋn wa ãna se'em na nãn ka puke. La tɩ mi tɩ Krisi sãn wa puke, tɩ wʋn ãna wʋ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na, tɩn wʋn wa yẽ ẽŋa n ãn se'em na ĩyã.

ref: Everyone who sins breaks God's law, because sin is the same as breaking God's law.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero, a sɩ'ɩsrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ. Se'ere n soe la, tõon-be'ero de la Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ sɩ'ɩsgɔ.

ref: You know Christ came to take away our sins. He isn't sinful,
text: La yãma mi tɩ Azezi-krisi wa'am tɩ a yese la nẽrba tõon-be'ero basɛ, la a mẽŋa ka tarɩ tõon-be'ero.

ref: and people who stay one in their hearts with him won't keep on sinning. If they do keep on sinning, they don't know Christ, and they have never seen him.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n lagɩm na Krisi bɔna ka tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero. La sɛka n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero ka yẽ ẽ, la a ka bãŋɛ ẽ.

ref: Children, don't be fooled. Anyone who does right is good, just like Christ himself.
text: M kɔma, da basɛ-ya tɩ nẽra pã'asɛ ya. Sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, de la nẽra n masɛ, wʋ Azezi mẽŋa n masɛ se'em na.

ref: Anyone who keeps on sinning belongs to the devil. He has sinned from the beginning, but the Son of God came to destroy all that he has done.
text: Sɛka n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero de la Asũtãana nẽra, se'ere n soe la, Asũtãana tõnnɩ tõon-be'ero la halɩ pĩ'iluŋo. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la wa'am tɩ a sã'am Asũtãana tõoma.

ref: God's children cannot keep on being sinful. His life-giving power lives in them and makes them his children, so they cannot keep on sinning.
text: Sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ bia ka le tõnna tõon-be'ero, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ pãŋa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n boe a pʋam, a ka le tãna wʋn tõnna tõon-be'ero, Nawẽnnɛ n de a Sɔ la ĩyã.

ref: You can tell God's children from the devil's children, because those who belong to the devil refuse to do right or to love each other. Love Each Other
text: Bɛla n pa'alɩ tɩ ba bãŋra Nawẽnnɛ kɔma, la sɛba n de Asũtãana kɔma bɔkra. Sɛka n ka ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, bɩɩ sɛka n ka nõŋɛ a sɔbia dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ bia. Tɩ nõŋɛ taaba

ref: From the beginning you were told we must love each other.
text: Kõ-yẽlga tɩ ya wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la de la tɩ nõŋɛ-ya taaba.

ref: Don't be like Cain, who belonged to the devil and murdered his own brother. Why did he murder him? He did it because his brother was good, and he was evil.
text: La ka de tɩ ãna wʋ Akãɩ la, ẽŋa de la Asũtãana nẽra, yʋʋn kʋ a yɩbga. La bẽm n soe tɩ a yʋʋn kʋ ẽ? La de la a tõoma n yʋʋn de tõon-be'ero, dee tɩ a yɩbga tõoma masɛ la ĩyã.

ref: My friends, don't be surprised if the people of this world hate you.
text: Bɛla m sɔbiisi, dũnia nẽrba sãn sisra ya, da kãn basɛ-ya tɩ la dɛna sɛla n di'ige ya.

ref: Our love for each other proves we have gone from death to life. But if you don't love each other, you are still under the power of death.
text: Tõma mi mɛ tɩ tõma ze'ele la kũm pʋam yese bɔna vɔm pʋam, tõma n nõŋɛ tɩ sɔbiisi la ĩyã. Nẽr-sɛka n ka nõŋɛ a sɔbia ken bɔna la kũm pʋam.

ref: If you hate each other, you are murderers, and we know murderers do not have eternal life.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n sisri a sɔbia de la nẽr-kʋʋra. La ya mi tɩ nẽr-kʋʋra ka tarɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: We know what love is because Jesus gave his life for us. This is why we must give our lives for each other.
text: La de la bõn-ẽna wã n botɩ tõma bãŋɛ nõŋlʋm n ãn se'em: Azezi ki mɛ bɔ tõma, bɛla, tõma mẽ de la tɩ dɩkɛ tɩ vɔm bɔ tɩ sɔbiisi ĩyã.

ref: If we have all we need and see one of our own people in need, we must have pity on that person, or else we cannot say we love God.
text: Nẽra sãn tara dũnia wã bõntarsõm, dee yẽ a sɔbia n boorɩ sõŋrɛ, ka zoe a ninbãalga sõŋɛ ẽ, ẽŋa dãana ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm.

ref: Children, you show love for others by truly helping them, and not merely by talking about it.
text: M kɔma, da basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ nõŋlʋm dɛna yetɔga, la nõ-yẽlʋm mã'a, tɩ basɛ tɩ la dɛna nõŋlʋm sɩra sɩra n lagɩm na tõoma.

ref: When we love others, we know we belong to the truth, and we feel at ease in the presence of God.
text: Bɛla n wʋn basɛ tɩ tɩ bãŋɛ tɩ tõma de la sɩra dõma, la tɩ sũure wʋn ãna ke'eŋo Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam.

ref: But even if we don't feel at ease, God is greater than our feelings, and he knows everything.
text: Baa tɩ sũure sãn ka bɔ tɩ bʋʋrɔ, tɩ bãŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ gãnnɩ tɩ sũure mɛ, la a mi sɛla woo bɔkɛ mɛ.

ref: Dear friends, if we feel at ease in the presence of God, we will have the courage to come near him.
text: M kɔm-nõŋrɩsɩ, tɩ sũure sãn bɔ tɩ bʋʋrɔ, tɩ tarɩ sũ-ke'eŋo Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam mɛ,

ref: He will give us whatever we ask, because we obey him and do what pleases him.
text: la bõn-sɛka wuu tɩ tɩ sosrɩ ẽ, a bɔ'ɔrɩ tɩ mɛ, tõma n sakrɩ a nõorɛ, dee ɩta sɛla n paarɩ a yɛm na ĩyã.

ref: God wants us to have faith in his Son Jesus Christ and to love each other. This is also what Jesus taught us to do.
text: Ẽŋa n bɔ a nõorɛ se'em n wãna: La de la tɩ bɔ a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi sɩra, dee nõŋɛ taaba wʋ ẽŋa n bɔ tɩ a nõorɛ se'em na.

ref: If we obey God's commandments, we will stay one in our hearts with him, and he will stay one with us. The Spirit he has given us is proof that we are one with him.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n sakrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ la, a boe la Nawẽnnɛ lɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ bɔna la ẽ. Bõn-sɛka n basɛ tɩ tõma mina tɩ Nawẽnnɛ boe la tõma lɛ n wãna: La de la a Sɩa tɩ a bɔ tõma la.

ref: Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world,
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, da bɔ-ya nẽr-sɛba wuu n yetɩ bãma tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa la sɩra, magɛ-ya ba bɩsɛ la sãn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa. Se'ere n soe la, põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba zozo'e n boe dũnia wã zuo.

ref: and you can know which ones come from God. His Spirit says that Jesus Christ had a truly human body.
text: Bõn-sɛka n wʋn basɛ tɩ ya bãŋɛ Sɩa sɛka n de Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n wãna: Nẽr-sɛka woo n yetɩ Azezi-krisi n wa'am dũnia zuo, la a tarɩ la nẽrsaala ĩyã, ẽŋa n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa.

ref: But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
text: La sɛka n ka tɔgrɩ Azezi yelle bɛla, ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, a tarɩ la Krisi sɩ'ɩsra la sɩa. Sɩ'ɩsra ẽŋa tɩ ya daan wõm tɩ a sẽm na, la lɛɛlɛ wã, a darɛ bɔna dũnia zuo mɛ.

ref: Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world.
text: La yãma, m kɔma, yãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, la yãma yãŋɛ põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la mɛ. Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n boe la yãma lɛ la, tarɩ pãŋa gãnna sɩ'ɩsra la sɩa n boe la dũnia dõma la mɛ.

ref: These enemies belong to this world, and the world listens to them, because they speak its language.
text: Põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba la de la dũnia wã bõnɔ, bɛla n soe tɩ ba tɔgra dũnia yɛla, tɩ dũnia dõma sɛlsra ba.

ref: We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
text: La sãn dɛna tõma, tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n mi Nawẽnnɛ selsrɩ tõma mɛ. La sɛka n dagɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽra ka selsrɩ tõma. La de la bɛla tɩ tõma wʋn bãŋɛ sɩra Sɩa, la põmpɔrŋɔ sɩa la bɔkɛ. Nawẽnnɛ de la nõŋlʋm dãana

ref: My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, tɩ nõŋɛ-ya taaba, se'ere n soe la, nõŋlʋm ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am. Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm de la Nawẽnnɛ bia, la a mi Nawẽnnɛ mɛ.

ref: God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him.
text: La nẽr-sɛka n ka tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka mi Nawẽnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ de la nõŋlʋm dãana.

ref: God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n pa'alɛ ẽŋa n nõŋɛ tõma se'em n wãna: A tõm na a Dayɔ-yẽŋa dũnia zuo, tɩ tɩ tara vɔm ẽŋa ĩyã.

ref: Real love isn't our love for God, but his love for us. God sent his Son to be the sacrifice by which our sins are forgiven.
text: Nõŋlʋm na n doose se'em n wãna: La dagɩ tõma n dẽŋɛ nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n nõŋɛ tõma, tõm a Dayʋa, tɩ a dɛna kãabgɔ tõma tõon-be'ero taalɛ basga ĩyã.

ref: Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n nõŋɛ tɩ bɛla la, dẽnɩ tɩ tõma mẽ nõŋɛ taaba.

ref: No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
text: Nẽra nẽra nãn ka yẽ Nawẽnnɛ. Tɩ sãn nõŋɛ taaba, Nawẽnnɛ boe la tɩ lɛ mɛ, la a nõŋlʋm n boe la tɩ lɛ la, de la nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ.

ref: God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us.
text: Bõn-sɛka n basɛ tɩ tõma bãŋɛ tɩ tõma boe la Nawẽnnɛ lɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ bɔna la tõma lɛ n wãna: La de la a Sɩa tɩ a bɔ tõma la.

ref: God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him.
text: La tõma yẽ mɛ, tɩ tõma tɔgra yetɩ, Nawẽnnɛ n de Sɔ la tõm na a Dayʋa, tɩ a dɛna dũnia Fãagra.

ref: God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God
text: Nẽr-sɛka n sakɛ yetɩ Azezi de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa, Nawẽnnɛ boe la ẽ lɛ mɛ, tɩ a mẽ bɔna la Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: and are sure God loves us. God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us.
text: La tõma mi mɛ, dee bɔ sɩra tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ tõma mɛ. Nawẽnnɛ de la nõŋlʋm dãana. Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm boe la Nawẽnnɛ lɛ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mẽ bɔna la ẽ.

ref: If we truly love others and live as Christ did in this world, we won't be worried about the day of judgment.
text: Tõma sãn tara Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ, tɩ wʋn ãna wʋ Azezi n ãn se'em na dũnia wã zuo. Bɛla tɩ wʋn tara sũ-ke'eŋo sarɩyadia daarɛ.

ref: A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
text: Sɛka n tarɩ nõŋlʋm ka zotɩ dabeem, nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ yesrɩ la dabeem, se'ere n soe la, dabeem zʋa pa'alɩ mɛ tɩ sɩbgrɛ n boe. Sɛka n zotɩ dabeem na ka tarɩ nõŋlʋm zõnzõnnɔ.

ref: But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see?
text: Nẽr-sɛka sãn yetɩ, ẽŋa nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee sise a sɔbia, a de la põmpɔrma. Se'ere n soe la, a sãn ka nõŋɛ a sɔbia tɩ a yẽtɩ la, a ka tã wʋn nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ a ka yẽtɩ la.

ref: The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
text: Nõorɛ sɛka tɩ Krisi bɔ tõma la n wãna: Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dẽnɩ tɩ a mẽ nõŋɛ a sɔbia.

ref: If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n bɔ sɩra tɩ Azezi de la Krisi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõm na, de la Nawẽnnɛ bia. Nẽr-sɛka n nõŋɛ sɔ, mẽ nõŋɛ a kɔma mɛ.

ref: If we love and obey God, we know we will love his children.
text: Sɛla n basɛ tɩ tõma mina tɩ tõma nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ kɔma n wãna: La de la tõma n nõŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ, dee sakra a nõorɛ wuu la.

ref: We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm de la tɩ sakra a nõorɛ. A nõorɛ la tɩ a bɔ tɩ la ka tɔɩ.

ref: Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n de Nawẽnnɛ bia n yãŋrɩ dũnia be'em. Tõma n yãŋɛ dũnia be'em na, de la tõma n bɔ Azezi-krisi sɩra la.

ref: No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God. Who Jesus Is
text: Ãne n tã wʋn yãŋɛ dũnia? La de la nẽr-sɛka n bɔ sɩra tɩ Azezi de la Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la mã'a.

ref: Water and blood came out from the side of Jesus Christ. It wasn't just water, but water and blood. The Spirit tells about this, because the Spirit is truthful.
text: Azezi-krisi n wa'am dũnia zuo la, la doose la ko'om, la zɩɩm. La dagɩ ko'om misga la mã'a, la ken dɛna la ẽn ki tɩ a zɩɩm yese la. La de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ a kaseto, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n de sɩra dãana la ĩyã.

ref: In fact, there are three who tell about it.
text: La de la bõn-bãna batã n bɔ'ɔrɩ kaseto.

ref: They are the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and they all agree.
text: La de la Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la ko'om, la zɩɩm. La bãma batã kaseto de la bʋyɩla.

ref: We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son.
text: Tõma n sakrɩ nẽrsaalba kaseto tɩ ba bɔ'ɔrɩ la, Nawẽnnɛ kaseto tarɩ pãŋa gãnna bɛla mɛ. La de la Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n bɔ a Dayʋa la kaseto.

ref: If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar.
text: Bɛla, nẽr-sɛka n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la sɩra, tarɩ kaseto ẽŋa mɛ a sũure pʋam. La nẽr-sɛka n ka bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra, basɛ tɩ a ãna wʋ põmpɔrma la, ẽŋa n ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ a Dayʋa la kaseto la ĩyã.

ref: God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son.
text: Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ kaseto la se'em n wãna: A bɔ tɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ, la vɔm ẽŋa de la a Dayʋa la ĩyã.

ref: And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life. Knowing about Eternal Life
text: Nẽr-sɛka tɩ a Dayʋa boe la ẽ na, tarɩ vɔm na mɛ. La nẽr-sɛka tɩ a Dayʋa ka boe la ẽ na, ka tarɩ vɔm. Vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ

ref: All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life.
text: Mam gʋlsɛ wãna bɔ'ɔra ya tɩ ya bãŋɛ tɩ yãma sɛba n bɔ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa sɩra la, tarɩ la vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him.
text: Tõma sũure ãn ke'eŋo mɛ la Nawẽnnɛ, tɩ tõma mina tɩ tõma sãn sose sɛla tɩ la masɛ wʋ ẽn boorɩ se'em, a selsrɩ tõma mɛ.

ref: And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
text: La tõma n mi tɩ a selsrɩ tõma n sosrɩ ẽ sɛla la, tõma mi tɩ a bɔ'ɔrɩ tɩ sɛla tɩ tɩ sosrɩ la mɛ.

ref: Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly.
text: Nẽr-sɛka sãn yẽ a sɔbia n tõnnɩ tõon-be'ego n ka sẽm na kũm, a pʋ'ʋsɛ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ẽ, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ wʋn bɔ ẽ vɔm. La de la tõon-be'ego sɛka n ka sẽm na kũm tɩ mam yetɩ. Tõon-be'ego n boe n sẽm na nẽra kũm, la dagɩ tõon-be'ego ẽŋa yelle tɩ mam yetɩ ya pʋ'ʋsɛ a ĩyã.

ref: Everything that is wrong is sin, but not all sins are deadly.
text: Sɛla n ka masɛ wuu de la tõon-be'ero. La, la dagɩ tõon-be'ego woo n sẽm na kũm.

ref: We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
text: Tõma mi tɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n de Nawẽnnɛ bia ka le tõnna tõon-be'ero. La Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la gu'uri ẽ mɛ, tɩ be'em dãana Asũtãana ka tãna wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽ sɛla.

ref: We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
text: Tõma mi tɩ tõma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. La dũnia dõma wuu boe la be'em dãana Asũtãana nu'usum.

ref: We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
text: Tõma mi tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa n wa'am wa bɔ tɩ yɛm, tɩ tɩ bãŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ n de sɩra dãana la. Tõma lagɩm na sɛka n de sɩra dãana la dɛna ayɩla, a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi ĩyã. Ẽŋa n de Nawẽnnɛ sɩra sɩra, n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Children, you must stay away from idols.
text: M kɔma, guuse-ya ya mẽŋa la baga.

ref: From the church leader. To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth.
text: Mam n de kẽema la n gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra zuudãan-pɔka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la, la a kɔma tɩ mam nõŋɛ sɩra sɩra la. La, la dagɩ mam mã'a, nẽr-sɛba wuu n bãŋɛ sɩra la nõŋɛ ba mɛ.

ref: We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
text: Se'ere n soe la, sɩra la n boe la tɩ lɛ, dee wʋn ken bɔna la tɩ lɛ wakatɛ wuu.

ref: I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love. Truth and Love
text: Tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa Azezi-krisi wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ yel-sõnnɛ, zoe tɩ ninbãalga, dee bɔ tɩ sũ-mã'asʋm, tɩ tɩ wʋn vɔa la sɩra, dee tara nõŋlʋm. Sɩra, la nõŋlʋm

ref: I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do.
text: Mam ɩtɩ sũ-yẽlga mɛ halɩ zozo'e, mam n yẽ tɩ fʋ kɔma la basɛba dolɩ la sɩra sore wʋ tɩ Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ n yele tɩ se'em na.

ref: Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do.
text: Lɛɛlɛ wã, zuudãan-pɔka, mam n gʋlsɛ fʋ wã dagɩ nõor-paalga, la de la nõor-sɛka tɩ tɩ wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la, mam sosrɩ fʋ mɛ tɩ tɩ nõŋɛ taaba.

ref: Love means we do what God tells us. And from the beginning, he told you to love him.
text: Nõŋlʋm n de sɛla n wãna: La de la tɩ ɩta wʋ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ n yele se'em na. Nõor-sɛka tɩ ya wõm halɩ pɔspɔsɩ la de la bɛla, la de la ya nõŋɛ taaba.

ref: Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ.
text: Nẽrba zozo'e n saagɛ dũnia pã'asrɩ nẽrba tɩ ba tuura. Nẽr-bãma ka sakɛ tɩ Azezi lebge la nẽrsaala wa'am dũnia zuo. Nẽr-ẽŋa dãana n de põmpã'asʋm dãana, la Krisi sɩ'ɩsra.

ref: So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward.
text: Guuse-ya ya mẽŋa, da kãn basɛ-ya tɩ tõma n tõm sɛla bɔ ya la bɔɩ, tɩ ya wʋn to'oge yɔɔrɔ wuu.

ref: Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yõsgɛ tole nẽŋa ka bɔna Krisi zãsŋɔ la pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ ka boe la ẽ. La sɛka woo n boe a zãsŋɔ la pʋam, Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la a Dayʋa boe la ẽ mɛ.

ref: If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them.
text: Nẽra sãn tarɩ zãsŋɔ ayẽma wa'am ya zẽ'am, ya da to'oge ẽ ya yire, la ya da pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ.

ref: Greeting them is the same as taking part in their evil deeds. Final Greetings
text: Nẽr-sɛka n pʋ'ʋsɛ ẽ, lagɩm na ẽ mɛ a tõon-be'ero pʋam. Ba'asgɔ yetɔga

ref: I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
text: Mam tarɩ yɛla zozo'e mɛ tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ ya, la mam ka boorɩ tɩ m gʋlsɛ ba gõŋɔ pʋam bɔ ya. La mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ mɛ tɩ mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am, dee tɔgɛ m mẽŋa bɔ ya, tɩ tɩ sũ-yẽlga wʋn dɛna sũ-yẽlga zõnzõnnɔ.

ref: Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
text: Fʋ sɔbi-pɔka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ looge la kɔma pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ.

ref: From the church leader. To my dear friend Gaius. I love you because we follow the truth,
text: Mam n de kẽema la, n gʋlsɛ bɔ'ɔra m sɩrɛ Agayusi tɩ mam nõŋɛ sɩra sɩra.

ref: dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɛ, mam kã'arɩ Nawẽnnɛ tɩ sɛla woo ãna sõŋa bɔ fɔ, tɩ fʋ wʋn tara ĩmã'asʋm wʋ fõn dolɩ Nawẽnnɛ sõŋa se'em na.

ref: It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth.
text: Tɩ sɔbiisi n wa'am kalam wa yele fõn dolɩ sɩra sore se'em, la fõn vɔa la sɩra se'em. Tɩ mam ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga zozo'e.

ref: Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth. Working Together
text: Sɛla ka bɔ'ɔrɩ mam sũ-yẽlga gãnna mam n wõm tɩ ba yetɩ mam kɔma dolɩ sɩra la.

ref: Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɛ, fõn ɩtɩ sɛla bɔ'ɔra sɔbiisi la, baa la sɛba n de sãama la, fʋ ɩtɩ la sɩra.

ref: They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve.
text: Ba tɔgɛ fʋ nõŋlʋm na yelle mɛ Azezi nẽrba zẽ'am. Fʋ sãn sõŋɛ ba tɩ ba sẽŋɛ ba sore la wʋ Nawẽnnɛ n boorɩ se'em, fʋ ẽŋɛ sõŋa.

ref: When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Azezi-krisi tõoma yelle tɩ ba wɛ'ɛsa sore la, dee ka to'oge sɛla nẽr-sɛba n ka dolɩ Azezi la zẽ'am.

ref: We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
text: Tõma de la tɩ sõŋɛ nẽr-bãma buuri pa'alɛ tɩ tõma naagɛ la ba mɛ dɛna sɩra kõ-yẽlga la tõntõnɩba.

ref: I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us.
text: Mam daan gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ mɛ bɔ Azezi nẽrba, la Adiyotrɛfɩ n boorɩ tɩ a dɛna nẽŋadãana la ka to'oge mam n yele se'em na.

ref: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
text: Bɛla, mam sãn wa wa'am, mam wʋn pa'alɛ sɛla tɩ a ẽŋɛ, la ẽn parnɩ tɔgra tõma yetɔg-be'ero la. La, la dagɩ bɛla mã'a, a ken zagsra mɛ ka tɔ'ɔsra sɔbiisi, dee mẽ gu'ura nẽr-sɛba n boorɩ tɩ ba tɔ'ɔsra ba la, digra ba Azezi nẽrba la pʋam.

ref: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɛ, da pagsɛ sɛla n de be'em, pagsra sɛla n ãn sõŋa. Nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ sɛla n ãn sõŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽra. La nẽr-sɛka woo n ɩtɩ sɛla n de be'em ka mi Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
text: La sãn dɛna Ademetrisi, nẽrba wuu tɔgrɩ a yelle sõŋa mɛ, tɩ ẽn dolɩ sɩra se'em na mẽ pa'ala a yelle sõŋa. Tõma mẽ tɔgrɩ a yelle sõŋa mɛ. La fʋ mi tɩ tõma kaseto de la sɩra.

ref: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
text: Mam tarɩ yɛla zozo'e mɛ tɩ m tɔgɛ bɔ fɔ, la mam ka boorɩ tɩ m gʋlsɛ ba gõŋɔ pʋam.

ref: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
text: Mam tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ mɛ tɩ mam wʋn wa yẽ fɔ tɩ tɩ sõsɛ la taaba.

ref: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
text: Sũ-mã'asʋm wʋn bɔna la fɔ. Fʋ sɩrdõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ fɔ. Pʋ'ʋsɛ tɩ sɩrdõma la wuu ayɩla ayɩla bɔ tɔ.

ref: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
text: M sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, mam daan boorɩ mɛ tɩ m gʋlsɛ ya la m sũure wuu tɔgɛ fãarɛ la tɩ tɩ wuu lagɩm yẽ la yelle. Mam bɩsɛ mɛ tɩ la de la pẽrgrɛ tɩ mam gʋlsɛ kã'an ya, tɩ ya mõrgɛ zɛbɛ ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ yetɔga la tɩ ya bɔ sɩra la pʋam. Nawẽnnɛ dɩkɛ yetɔg-bãma bɔ a nẽrba la nõorɛ ayɩla mã'a wakatɛ wuu ĩyã.

ref: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
text: Se'ere n soe la, nẽrba basɛba n li'ise kẽ ya pʋam. Nẽr-bãma de la sɛba n sɩ'ɩsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ, tɛɛra a yel-sõnnɛ yetɔga la, dɩkɛ tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ, dee sɩ'ɩsra Azezi-krisi n de tɩ kɛ'ɛma la tɩ Zuudãana ayɩla mã'a la. Halɩ kʋrʋm kʋrʋm tɩ la põn gʋlsɛ sɩbgrɛ sɛka tɩ ba wʋn yẽ.

ref: Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them.
text: Baa la yãma n mi bõn-bãma wuu sõŋa sõŋa la, mam boorɩ tɩ m tẽegɛ ya mɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n yʋʋn fãagɛ a nẽrba Ezipti tẽŋa la, a yʋʋn yãŋa basɛ mɛ tɩ nẽr-sɛba n ka bɔ ẽ sɩra la ki.

ref: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
text: La malɛkadõma sɛba n yʋʋn ka sakɛ Nawẽnnɛ n bɔ ba ke'eŋo se'em basɛ zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba la, Nawẽnnɛ ba ba mɛ la bãnsɩ, basɛ tɩ ba bɔna lika pʋam wakatɛ wuu gu'ura dabs-kãtɛ la n de sarɩyadia daarɛ la.

ref: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
text: La sãn dɛna Sodom la Gomõorɩ dõma, la tẽnsɩ sɛba n lɛm ba la, n mẽ yʋʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ bɛla, tõm yalsɩ tõoma, la, la dagɩ la nẽrsaalba mã'a. Ba yʋʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ mɛ n de bugum n ka kĩŋri, tɩ la dɛna kã'aŋɔ bɔ nẽrba basɛba.

ref: The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels.
text: La de la bõn-bãma buuri n bɛla tɩ nẽr-bãma mẽ ɩtɩ. Ban tẽ'esrɩ se'em na botɩ ba dẽgra la ba mẽŋa, ba pɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ n tarɩ nõorɛ la ba lɛ la mɛ, dee tʋʋra saazuum pãnsɩ dõma.

ref: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
text: La baa malɛkadõma kɛ'ɛma Amisɛlɩ n yʋʋn tɔgrɩ la Asũtãana wẽ'era nõke'ene Amoyisi kũm ĩyã la, a ka saagɛ tɔgɛ zɛrgɛ ẽ tʋ ẽ, a yʋʋn yetɩ: «Zuudãana la wʋn sɩbgɛ fɔ.»

ref: But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings.
text: Nẽr-bãma tɔgrɩ la sɛla tɩ ba ka mi be'em, ba ɩtɩ la yooryooro, ba ãn wʋ dũnsi n ka tarɩ yɛm n ɩtɩ se'em na, bõn-ẽŋa n sã'anɩ ba.

ref: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
text: Ba wʋn yẽ toogo, se'ere n soe la, ba dolɩ la so-sɛka tɩ Akãɩ yʋʋn doose la, la ba kẽ'esɛ ba mẽŋa mɛ tuure pʋam yɔɔrɔ ĩyã, wʋ Abalaam n ẽŋɛ se'em ligri ĩyã la. Ban sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ wʋ Akoore n yʋʋn sɩ'ɩsɛ Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ se'em na, ba ki mɛ.

ref: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
text: Ba sãn lagɩm na ya dita nõŋlʋm dia, la de la yãnnɛ bɔ ya. Ba ka zotɩ yãnnɛ, ba kɔ'ɔn dita mɛ ɩta sũ-yẽlga ba mẽŋa ĩyã. Ba ãn wʋ sawara n ka tarɩ ko'om tɩ kʋsebgo pe'erɩ, ba ãn wʋ tɩɩsɩ n ka tarɩ biɛ ba wɔŋa wakatɛ, tɩ ba võ, basɛ tɩ ba võorɔ, la ba yẽga wuu kʋɩ.

ref: Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
text: Ba ken ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ ko-mĩ'isi n wẽ'erɩ la pãŋa, ba yãnnɛ tõoma la ãn wʋ ko-kãtɛ fulfuka la, ba ãn wʋ wõrbiɛ sɛba n eerɩ. Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm na zẽ'a n de lika tam bĩŋe wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ gu'ura ba.

ref: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
text: La po dɛna la nẽr-bãma yelle tɩ A'enõkɩ n de tɩ yaaba bʋyopɔɩ dãana lan pɔsɛ la Adam na, yʋʋn to'oge Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgɛ yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ sẽm mɛ la a malɛkadõma tʋsa tʋsa

ref: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
text: tɩ a di nẽrsaalba wuu sarɩya, sɩbgɛ tõon-be'ero dõma wuu, ban sɩ'ɩsɛ ẽ tõnna tõon-be'ero la wuu ĩyã, la yetɔg-be'ero wuu tɩ ba tɔgɛ pɔ'ɔgɛ ẽ na ĩyã.»

ref: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
text: Nẽr-bãma de la nẽrba n yõnnɩ wakatɛ wuu, ba sũure ka ãn yẽlʋm la ban ãn se'em na. Ba ɩtɩ la ba mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em, tɔgra pa'al-m-mẽŋa yetɔga, dee tɔgra nẽrba nõ-yẽlʋm yetɔga tɩ ba yẽ yõorɔ. Kã'aŋɔ

ref: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: La sãn dɛna yãma, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, tẽra-ya yetɔg-sɛba tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi Tõntõnɩba la daan dẽŋɛ tɔgɛ bɔ ya la.

ref: They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God.
text: Ba daan yele ya yetɩ: «Dũnia ba'asgɔ wakatɛ, nẽrba n la'arɩ nẽrba n wʋn wa bɔna dɔla ba mẽŋa yɛm n boorɩ se'em, sɩ'ɩsra Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
text: La de la bãma n tarɩ welgre sẽna. Ba dolɩ la nẽrsaalba pʋtẽ'era n boorɩ se'em, Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa ka boe la ba lɛ.

ref: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
text: La yãma, m sɩr-nõŋrɩsɩ, sõŋra-ya taaba sẽnna nẽŋa yan bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra la pʋam, pʋ'ʋsra-ya Nawẽnnɛ la a Sɩa pãŋa.

ref: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
text: Bɔna-ya Nawẽnnɛ nõŋlʋm na pʋam gu'ura tɩ tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi n de ninbãal-zɔta la wʋn bɔ ya vɔm n ka ba'asrɩ.

ref: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
text: Zɔta-ya sɛba yɛm n yɔɔrɩ la ninbãalga.

ref: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
text: Fãagɛ-ya sɛba n boe bugum pʋam na. La, la sãn dɛna basɛba la, zɔta-ya ba ninbãalga, la ya guuse ya mẽŋa da lagɩm na ba tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ tɩ ba mẽŋa boorɩ la baa fẽfẽe.

ref: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
text: Nawẽnnɛ ayɩla mã'a n de tɩ Fãagra tɩ Zuudãana Azezi-krisi ĩyã, n soe pẽka, la nã'asgɔ, la pãŋa, la ke'eŋo, lan dẽŋɛ pĩ'iluŋo, la lɛɛlɛ wã, la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

ref: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
text: Gõŋɔ wã tarɩ la sɛla tɩ Azezi-krisi puke pa'alɛ. Nawẽnnɛ n daan bɔ ẽ bõn-bãma tɩ a pa'alɛ a tõntõnɩba sɛla n wʋn ẽŋɛ wakatɛ fẽfẽe pʋam. Azezi-krisi daan tõm na a malɛka a tõntõnna Azã zẽ'am tɩ a pa'alɛ ẽ bõn-bãma.

ref: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
text: Azã n gʋlsɛ sɛla wuu tɩ a yẽ la pa'alɛ tɩ la de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ Azezi-krisi puke pa'alɛ ẽŋa.

ref: God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n karẽŋrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɩ a to'oge tɔgɛ wã, la sɛba n selsrɩ bõn-sɛba n gʋlsɛ gõn-ẽna wã pʋam dee sakra ba tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Wakatɛ la lɛm mɛ, tɩ bɛla wuu wʋn ẽŋɛ. Azã pʋ'ʋsrɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n lagsrɩ tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ

ref: May kindness and peace be yours from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness. Jesus was the first to conquer death, and he is the ruler of all earthly kings. Christ loves us, and by his blood he set us free from our sins.
text: Mam Azã n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã bɔ'ɔra tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ n boe Azi so'olʋm pʋam na Wẽndeto nẽrba. Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n daan boe, n ken bɔna, dee wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu la, la sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ n boe a na'am bĩmbĩnne nẽŋam, la Azezi-krisi n tɔgrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yelle sɩra, ẽŋa n ki dee dɛna pɔspɔsɩ dãana n vo'oge, dɛna dũnia na'adõma naba la, wʋn ẽŋɛ ya yel-sõnnɛ, dee bɔ ya sũ-mã'asʋm. Azezi-krisi nõŋɛ tõma mɛ, ki tɩ a zɩɩm fãagɛ tõma tõon-be'ero pʋam,

ref: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
text: botɩ tõma dɛna a so'olʋm kãabgɔ kẽma tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ n de a Sɔ la. Ẽŋa n soe nã'asgɔ, la pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.

ref: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
text: Bɩsɛ-ya, a boe la sawara pʋam sẽna. Nẽrsaalba wuu wʋn yẽ ẽ, baa la nẽr-sɛba n daan lʋ ẽ na. Buuri wuu nẽrba n boe dũnia wʋn kɛlla mɛ fabla a yelle. Ẽe, la de la bɛla. Amina.

ref: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
text: Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana la yetɩ: «Mam de la pĩ'iluŋo la ba'asgɔ, mam n daan boe, n ken bɔna, dee wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu.» Azã yẽ Azezi-krisi wʋ zãasŋɔ la

ref: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
text: Mam de la Azã n de ya sɔbia n po nãmsra, lagɩm na yãma po tara pʋrɛ Nawẽnnɛ so'olʋm na pʋam, ken po lagɩm na yãma mõrgra sẽnna nẽŋa Azezi doosgo la pʋam. Mam daan tɔgɛ la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga, la Azezi-krisi yelle, tɩ ba tarɩ mam sẽŋɛ tẽŋa tɩ ko'om gilge yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Patmõsɩ.

ref: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana daarɛ n de Lasrɩ daarɛ la, tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la mam, botɩ mam wõm kõa n tɔgɛ ke'enke'em mam poorʋm wʋ benkonne n peebe la,

ref: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
text: yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ sɛla tɩ fʋ wʋn yẽ la ẽŋɛ gõŋɔ pʋam, soolʋm bɔ tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ la Wẽndeto nẽrba: Efɛɛzɩ, la Simirni, la Pɛrgamɩ, la Tiyatiiri, la Sardɩ, la Filadɛlfi, la Laodise.»

ref: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
text: Tɩ mam wẽrgɛ tɩ m bɩsɛ sɛka n tɔgrɩ la mam na, la mam n wẽrgɛ la, mam yẽ la sãlma fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ.

ref: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
text: La nẽra n boe fɩtladõma la tẽŋasʋka ãna wʋ nẽrsaala dayʋa la. A yɛ la fuugo n wɔgɛ sige paagɛ a nãma, dee dɩkɛ sãlma sẽbrɩga bobe a yõ'ogɔ.

ref: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
text: A zomto daan pelge mɛ ãna yɩlɩ yɩlɩ wʋ lɔnnɔ na, tɩ a nini ãna wʋ bugwitum na.

ref: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
text: A nãma daan yẽgrɩ wʋ zũuro tɩ ba zuge ba bugum pʋam na, dee tɩ a kõa la ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ dãmŋɔ la.

ref: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
text: A daan zãlɩ la wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ la a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo. Tɩ sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõa ayi ze'ele a nõorʋm yɛsra. A nẽŋa daan yẽgrɩ wʋ wʋntɛɛŋa n ãn se'em na.

ref: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
text: Mam n daan yẽ ẽ na, mam lui la tẽŋa a nẽŋam ãna wʋ kũm na. Tɩ a dɩkɛ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo paglɛ mam zuo yetɩ: «Da zoe dabeem, tɩ mam n de pĩ'iluŋo, la ba'asgɔ.

ref: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
text: Mam de la sɛka n vɔɩ. Mam daan ki mɛ, la mam n wãna, mam vɔa mɛ wakatɛ wuu. Mam n tarɩ ke'eŋo sɔna kũm, la kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa.

ref: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
text: Bɛla, gʋlsɛ bõn-sɛba tɩ fʋ yẽ, sɛla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, la sɛla n wʋn wa ẽŋɛ poorʋm na.

ref: I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
text: Wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ tɩ fʋ yẽ mam zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo pʋam, la sãlma fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ la võorɛ n wãna: Wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ la de la tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ la Wẽndeto la nẽrba gu'urba, la fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ la de la tẽnsɩ bayopɔɩ la Wẽndeto nẽrba.»

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
text: Sɛka tɩ mam yẽ la, a ken yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Efɛɛzɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n zãlɩ wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo pʋam, dee sẽnna sãlma fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ tẽŋasʋka la n yele se'em n wãna:

ref: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
text: ‹Mam mi yãma n tõnnɩ se'em, yãma n nãmsrɩ dee mõrgra sẽnna nẽŋa se'em mɛ. Mam mi tɩ ya ka sakɛ basɛ tɩ nẽr-wẽensɩ tõnna la ya. Yãma makɛ nẽr-sɛba n yetɩ bãma de la Krisi Tõntõnɩba dee dagna bɛla la bɩsɛ mɛ, la yãma bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ ba de la põmpɔrndõma.

ref: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
text: Yãma mõrgɛ sẽnna nẽŋa mɛ, ya nãmsrɩ mam yʋ'ʋrɛ la ĩyã mɛ, dee ka basɛ tɩ ya bʋraanɛ bo'oge.

ref: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
text: La yel-sɛka tɩ mam tarɩ la yãma n wãna: Ya ka le nõŋɛ mam wʋ yãma n daan nõŋɛ mam se'em pɔspɔsɩ la.

ref: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
text: Tẽegɛ-ya yãma n daan ɩtɩ se'em dee basɛ la. Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, dee ɩta yãma n daan ɩtɩ se'em pɔspɔsɩ la. Sãn dagna bɛla, mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am wa dɩkɛ ya fɩtla la a dɔglgɔ zẽ'am mɛ, ya sãn ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

ref: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
text: La baa bɛla, ya ɩtɩ bõn-sõŋɔ mɛ. Yãma sise sɛla tɩ Nikolayiti nẽrba ɩtɩ la mɛ, wʋ mam n mẽ sise ba tõoma se'em na.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden. The Letter to Smyrna
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa. Sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn bɔ ẽ nõorɛ tɩ a di vɔm tɩa n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la biɛ.› » Krisi kõa tɩ a bɔ Simirni Wẽndeego nẽrba

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
text: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Simirni Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n de pĩ'iluŋo la ba'asgɔ, sɛka n daan ki dee vo'oge la n yele se'em n wãna:

ref: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
text: ‹Mam mi ya nãmsgɔ la ya nõŋɔ la yelle mɛ, la baa bɛla, ya de la bõntatba. Mam mi be'em yetɔg-sɛba tɩ nẽr-sɛba n yetɩ bãma de la Zifdõma dee ka dɛna la tɔgɛ ya. Ba de la Asũtãana nẽrba.

ref: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
text: Da zoe-ya dabeem yãma n wʋn nãmsɛ la. Bɩsɛ-ya, Asũtãana wʋn makɛ ya bɩsɛ mɛ, botɩ ba kẽ'esɛ ya nẽrba basɛba yʋ'a deem, la ya wʋn yẽ nãmsgɔ mɛ dabsa pia. Bɔ-ya mam sɩra halɩ ka paagɛ ya kũm, tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ya bɔ'a n de vɔm.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death. The Letter to Pergamum
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa. Sɛka n yãŋɛ, kũm bʋyi dãana kãn ẽŋɛ ẽ sɛla.› » Krisi kõa tɩ a bɔ Pɛrgamɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
text: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Pɛrgamɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n tarɩ sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõa ayi la n yele se'em n wãna:

ref: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
text: ‹Mam mi zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ yãma boe la mɛ, zẽ'e-ẽŋa tɩ Asũtãana na'am bĩmbĩnne boe. La ya dɩkɛ ya mẽŋa lablɛ la mam mɛ, ze'ele kãŋkãŋɛ bɔ mam sɩra ka basɛ, baa la wakatɛ sɛka tɩ ba daan kʋ A'ãntipasɩ n de sɩra dãana n tɔgɛ mam yelle ya zẽ'am, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Asũtãana boe la.

ref: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
text: La baa bɛla, mam tarɩ la yelle fẽe la ya. Yãma tarɩ la nẽrba n boe ya pʋam dɔla Abalaam zãsŋɔ. Abalaam yʋʋn zãsʋm na Abalakɩ ẽn wʋn ẽŋɛ se'em bẽrɛ Ɩsrayɛlɩ dõma botɩ ba obe baga nẽnnɔ, dee tõm yalsɩ tõoma.

ref: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
text: Yãma mẽ tarɩ la nẽrba basɛba n boe ya zẽ'am n dolɩ Nikolayiti dõma zãsŋɔ.

ref: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
text: Teege-ya yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Sãn dagna bɛla, mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am lɛɛlɛ wã, la mam wʋn zɛbɛ la nẽr-bãma la sʋkɔbgɔ n ze'ele m nõorʋm yɛsra la.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa. Sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn bɔ ẽ dia n sʋgɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de mannɩ n ze'ele saazuum tɩ a di, la mam wʋn bɔ ẽ kug-pɛɛlga. Kug-ẽŋa zuo yʋ'ʋr-paalɛ n gʋlsɛ mĩ, tɩ nẽra nẽra ka mina ẽ, sãn dagna sɛka n to'oge ẽ na mã'a.› » Krisi kõa tɩ a bɔ Tiyatiiri Wẽndeego nẽrba

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
text: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Tiyatiiri Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Nawẽnnɛ Dayʋa la nini n ãn wʋ bugwitum, tɩ a nãma yẽgra wʋ zũuro tɩ ba zuge bugum pʋam na n yele se'em n wãna:

ref: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
text: ‹Mam bãŋɛ ya tõoma, la ya nõŋlʋm, la yan bɔ sɩra, la yan tõm bɔ nẽrba, la yan mõrgɛ sẽnna nẽŋa se'em mɛ. La mam mi tɩ ya tõnnɩ lɛɛlɛ wã zɔ'ɔra gãnna yan tõm se'em pɔspɔsɩ la mɛ.

ref: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
text: La yel-sɛka tɩ mam tarɩ la yãma n wãna: La de la yan sakɛ basɛ Azezabɛlɩ n de pɔka n yetɩ ẽŋa de la Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsa, tɩ a zãsna nẽrba, pã'asra mam tõntõnɩba tɩ ba ɩta yalsɩ, dee ɔbra baga nẽnnɔ na.

ref: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
text: Mam bɔ ẽ na wakatɛ tɩ a teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero, la a ka boorɩ tɩ a teege yɛm basɛ a yalsɩ tõoma la.

ref: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
text: Bɛla, mam wʋn basɛ tɩ a gã'arɛ yẽ nãmsgɔ zozo'e. La sɛba n lagɩm na ẽ tõnna yalsɩ tõoma la wʋn yẽ toogo zozo'e, sãn dagna tɩ ba basɛ la tõon-be'e-sɛba tɩ a botɩ ba tõnna la.

ref: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
text: Mam wʋn ken basɛ tɩ a kɔma ki, tɩ Wẽndeto nẽrba la wuu wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam de la sɛka n mi nẽrba tẽ'esgɔ, la ban boorɩ se'em. La mam wʋn yɔ ya nẽra woo masɛ wʋ ẽn tõnnɩ se'em.

ref: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
text: La yãma Tiyatiiri nẽr-sɛba n ka sakrɩ zãsŋɔ bãma, dee ka mina sɛla tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Asũtãana yɛla n sʋgɛ la, mam ka tarɩ sɛla wʋn yele ya pa'asɛ.

ref: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
text: La gʋrɛ-ya sɛla tɩ ya tarɩ la kãŋkãŋɛ halɩ tɩ mam wa wa'am.

ref: I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ ẽŋɛ mam n boorɩ se'em ka paagɛ ba'asgɔ, mam wʋn botɩ a sɔna so'olʋm buuri wuu.

ref: I will give each of them the same power my Father has given me. They will rule the nations with an iron rod and smash those nations to pieces like clay pots. I will also give them the morning star.
text: La de la pãn-sɛka tɩ mam to'oge m Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am na tɩ mam wʋn bɔ ẽ. A wʋn tara la kuto dasaarɛ sɔna ba wʋ ban wõtɩ lɔg-kɛgsɩ la. La mam wʋn ken bɔ ẽ bewã'arɛ.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› »

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
text: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Sardɩ Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ, la wõrbiɛ bayopɔɩ la n yele se'em n wãna: ‹Mam mi ya tõoma mɛ, fʋ yʋ'ʋrɛ yese mɛ tɩ ba yetɩ ya vɔa mɛ, la, ya ki mɛ.

ref: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
text: Bɛla, guuse ya mẽŋa, tɩ sɛla n deege la a ki la, yãagɛ da ki ba'asɛ. Se'ere n soe la, mam bãŋɛ mɛ tɩ ya tõoma la ka paagɛ Nawẽnnɛ yɛm.

ref: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
text: Tẽegɛ-ya zãsŋɔ la tɩ ya daan wõm dee to'oge ẽ se'em na, sakɛ-ya ẽ teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero. Ya sãn ka guuse, mam wʋn wa'am wa di'ige ya wʋ nayiga la, la ya kãn bãŋɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ mam wʋn wa'am ya zẽ'am.

ref: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
text: La nẽrba basɛba n boe yãma pʋam Sardɩ ka dẽgɛ ba futo. Bãma n wʋn yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ doose mam, bãma n masɛ la ĩyã.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ wʋn wa yɛ la fu-pɛɛlsɩ bɛla, la mam kãn saam a yʋ'ʋrɛ vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam. La mam wʋn wi a yʋ'ʋrɛ mam Sɔ, la a malɛkadõma la nẽŋam yetɩ a de la mam nẽra.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Philadelphia
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› » Krisi kõa tɩ a bɔ Filadɛlfi Wẽndeego nẽrba

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
text: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Filadɛlfi Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n de pʋpeelem dãana, la sɩra dãana n tarɩ naba Adavidi kʋlɩnbiɛ, sɛka n yʋ'ʋrɩ tɩ nẽra ka tãna yɔ'ɔra, dee yɔ'ɔra tɩ nẽra ka tãna yʋ'ʋra la n yele se'em n wãna:

ref: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
text: ‹Mam mi ya tõoma mɛ, mam mi tɩ ya pãŋa ka zo'oge, la baa la bɛla, ya sakɛ mam yetɔga la mɛ, la ya ka sɩ'ɩsɛ tɩ yãma ka mi mam. Mam yo'oge la sore bɔ ya, la nẽra nẽra kãn yãŋɛ yʋ ẽ.

ref: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
text: Mam n wʋn ẽŋɛ nẽr-sɛba n dolɩ Asũtãana la se'em n wãna: Nẽr-bãma yetɩ bãma de la Zifdõma, la dagɩ bɛla, ba de la põmpɔrndõma. Mam wʋn basɛ mɛ tɩ ba wa'am wa ka dũma ya nẽŋam, la ba wʋn bãŋɛ tɩ mam nõŋɛ ya.

ref: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
text: Yãma n sakɛ mam yetɔga la mõrgɛ sẽŋɛ nẽŋa na ĩyã, mam mẽ wʋn gu'ura ya sõŋa tɩ toogo sɛka n sẽm dũnia wuu zuo, tɩ a makɛ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe dũnia zuo la, da paagɛ ya.

ref: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
text: Mam sẽm na lɛɛlɛ, gʋrɛ-ya sɛla tɩ ya tarɩ la kãŋkãŋɛ, tɩ nẽra da to'oge ya yɔɔrɔ.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn botɩ a ãna wʋ zẽerɛ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yire pʋam, la ba kãn malɩn yese ẽ mĩ. La mam wʋn gʋlsɛ mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ, la a tẽŋa n de Zerizalɛm paalga n ze'ele saazuum Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am signa la yʋ'ʋrɛ a zuo, la mam wʋn ken gʋlsɛ mam mẽŋa yʋ'ʋr-paalga la a zuo.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Laodicea
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› » Krisi kõa tɩ a bɔ Laodise Wẽndeego nẽrba

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
text: «Gʋlsɛ bɔ Laodise Wẽndeego nẽrba la gu'ura yetɩ: Sɛka n de Amina la, sɛka n de sɩra dãana, dee sɩrɩ tɔgra sɛla tɩ a nini yẽ, dee ken dɛna sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam sɛla wuu ze'ele a zẽ'am n yele se'em n wãna:

ref: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
text: ‹Mam mi ya tõoma mɛ, mam mi tɩ ya dagɩ mã'asga, ya mẽ dagɩ tʋʋlga. Mam boorɩ nɩ tɩ ya dɛna la mã'asga bɩɩ tʋʋlga.

ref: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
text: La yãma n ka de mã'asga bɩɩ tʋʋlga mam tõoma la pʋam, dee ãna wam na, mam wʋn ʋkɛ ya mɛ m nõorʋm basɛ.

ref: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
text: Ya yetɩ yãma de la bõntatba, yãma tarɩ sɛla woo mɛ tɩ sɛla ka pɔ'ɔgɛ yãma. La, ya ka mi tɩ ya de la ninbãal-dõma, la farɛ dõma. Ya de la nasdõma, la bɛɛla, la fɔɔsɩ.

ref: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
text: Bɛla ĩyã, mam kã'anɩ ya mɛ tɩ ya da sãlma mẽŋa mẽŋa tɩ ba zuge bugum pʋam yese ba mam zẽ'am, tɩ ya wʋn sɩrɩ dɛna bõntatba. La ya da fu-pɛɛlsɩ yɛ pĩ ya yãnnɛ na, dee da tɩɩm dõ ya nini yẽta.

ref: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
text: Mam sɩbgrɩ la sɛba wuu tɩ mam nõŋɛ, dee dɛmsra ba. Bɛla, nargɛ-ya pãŋa teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero.

ref: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
text: Bɩsɛ-ya, mam ze la kʋlɩŋa nõorʋm wẽ'era, nẽr-sɛka n wõm mam kõa yo'oge kʋlɩŋa bɔ mam, mam wʋn kẽ a zẽ'am lagɩm na ẽ di, tɩ a mẽ lagɩm na mam di.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ, mam wʋn botɩ a lagɩm na mam po zĩ'ire mam na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo, wʋ mam yãŋɛ po lagɩm na mam Sɔ Nawẽnnɛ zĩ'ire a na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa n yetɩ a nẽrba se'em na sõŋa sõŋa.› »

ref: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
text: Bɛla poorʋm, mam bɩsɛ mɛ yẽ tɩ kʋlɩŋa n yo'oge Nawẽnnɛ yire. Tɩ kõa la tɩ mam wõm pɔspɔsɩ n tɔgɛ la mam wʋ benkonne n peebe la, yele mam yetɩ: «Zom wa'am kalam, tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fʋ sɛla n wʋn wa ẽŋɛ.»

ref: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa darɛ bɔna la mam botɩ mam yẽ na'am bĩmbĩnne n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ nẽra zẽa a zuo.

ref: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
text: Sɛka n zĩ bĩmbĩnne na zuo la nẽegɛ yẽgra wʋ kuga n tarɩ yõorɔ yʋ'ʋrɛ n de zaspɩ, la sardʋanɩ. La gõmatɩ-tãpɔ n yẽgrɩ ãna wʋ kugre yʋ'ʋrɛ n de emerɔdɩ n kaagɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne na.

ref: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
text: La na'am bĩmbĩna pisyi la anaasɩ n daan gilge na'am bĩmbĩn-ẽŋa, tɩ kẽma pisyi la anaasɩ n yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ vuge sãlma mũnsi zẽa ba zuto.

ref: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
text: Tɩ saaga ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnnum na yẽgra, tãsra dee sũnna, la fɩtladõma bayopɔɩ n lasʋm dita na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam. Bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ la.

ref: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
text: Na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam, bõnɔ n gãɩ ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ yẽgra wʋ bɩsga. La bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ n gilge na'am bĩmbĩnne na n boe tẽŋasʋka la, tɩ nini pɩrɛ ba nẽŋa, la poore.

ref: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
text: Pɔspɔsɩ bõn-vɔa la wõn na gɩgnɛ, tɩ bʋyi dãana la wõna lʋlla, tɩ bʋtã dãana la tara nẽŋa n wõn na nẽrsaala nẽŋa. La bõn-vɔa bʋnaasɩ dãana la wõn na tugfo n ẽgrɩ.

ref: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
text: Bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ayɩla woo tarɩ la kempagsɩ bayoobɩ tɩ nini pɩrɛ ba zẽ'a woo, tilum na zuum. Yʋ'ʋŋɔ la wʋntɛɛŋa, ba yõonɩ mɛ ka gɔ'ɔra yetɩ: «Sõŋa, sõŋa, sõŋa, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana n ãn sõŋa. Ẽŋa n daan boe, n ken bɔna, dee wʋn bɔna wakatɛ wuu.»

ref: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
text: Bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ẽn nã'asra sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo n vɔa wakatɛ wuu la mɛ, pẽgra ẽ dee pʋ'ʋsra ẽ a yel-sõnnɛ.

ref: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
text: «Tɩ Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ, fõn masɛ fʋ to'oge na'am pãŋa, la nã'asgɔ, la ke'eŋo, la de la fõn nãam sɛla woo, la de la fõn boorɩ ĩyã, tɩ fõn nãam ba tɩ ba bɔna.»

ref: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
text: La mam yẽ la gõŋɔ n boe sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo pʋam, tɩ a nẽŋa la a poore wuu gʋlsɛ, tɩ ba dɩkɛ bõnɔ n tabrɩ tabɛ ẽ zẽ'esɩ bayopɔɩ.

ref: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
text: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛka n tarɩ pãŋa n tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Ãne n masɛ wʋn lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ la, dee lakɛ gõŋɔ la?»

ref: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: La nẽra nẽra daan ka boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, bɩɩ dũnia zuo, bɩɩ tẽŋa wã pʋam pʋam n tã wʋn lakɛ gõŋɔ la, dee bɩsɛ a pʋam.

ref: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Tɩ mam daan kɛlla zozo'e, ban ka yẽ nẽra n masɛ n wʋn lakɛ gõŋɔ la, dee bɩsɛ a pʋam na ĩyã.

ref: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
text: Tɩ kẽma la nẽra ayɩla yele mam yetɩ: «Da kɛlla. Bɩsɛ, sɛka n de Azuda buuri gɩgnɛ n de naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa la yãŋɛ mɛ, ẽŋa n tã wʋn lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ zẽ'esɩ bayopɔɩ la, dee lakɛ gõŋɔ la.»

ref: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ Pebila n ze na'am bĩmbĩnne na tẽŋasʋka, tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ, la kẽma la gilge ẽ basɛ tẽŋasʋka. Pebila la ãn wʋ ba daan kʋ ẽ mɛ na. A tarɩ la ɩɩla bayopɔɩ, la nini bayopɔɩ n de Nawẽnnɛ sɩɩsɩ bayopɔɩ la tɩ a tõm dũnia wuu pʋam na.

ref: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
text: Tɩ Pebila la paagɛ to'oge gõŋɔ la sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la zʋʋgɔ pʋam.

ref: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
text: Ba daan yõonɩ la yõom-paalɛ yetɩ: «Fõn masɛ tɩ fʋ to'oge gõŋɔ la lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ la. Se'ere n soe la, ba daan kʋ fõn mɛ, tɩ fõn dɩkɛ fʋ zɩɩm na yɔ to'oge nẽrsaalba buuri wuu, la yetɔga buuri wuu, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
text: Fõn botɩ ba dɛna la fʋ so'olʋm kãabgɔ kẽma tõnna bɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ, dee wʋn di na'am dũnia zuo.»

ref: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
text: Tɩ mam le bɩsɛ, wõm malɛkadõma kʋʋŋɔ kõa, ba daan de la tʋsa nõorɛ tʋsa tʋsa. Ba daan gilge la na'am bĩmbĩnne na, la bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ, la kẽma la.

ref: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
text: Ba daan yõonɩ mɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Pebila tɩ ba daan kʋ la n masɛ tɩ a to'oge ke'eŋo, la bõntarsõm, la yɛm, la pãŋa, la nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa, la pẽka.»

ref: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
text: Tɩ mam wõm bõn-nãanɩsɩ wuu n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire, la tẽŋa zuo, la tẽŋa pʋam pʋam, la ko-kãtɛ pʋam, la bõn-nãanɩsɩ n boe zẽ'esɩ wuu n yetɩ: «Sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo, la Pebila la n soe pẽka, la nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa, la ke'eŋo wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.»

ref: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
text: Tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la yetɩ: «Amina.» Tɩ kẽma la ka dũma tẽŋa nã'asra ẽ.

ref: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
text: La mam yẽ mɛ tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ na n tabɛ zẽ'esɩ bayopɔɩ la ayɩla. Tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ayɩla n tɔgrɩ tɩ a kõa ãna wʋ saaga n tãsrɩ la yetɩ: «Wa'am!»

ref: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
text: Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ wemõa n de pɛɛlga. Tɩ nẽra bã ẽ zãla tãpɔ, tɩ ba bɔ ẽ mũŋa. Tɩ a dɛna nẽra n yãŋrɩ, sẽŋɛ tɩ a yãŋɛ.

ref: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋyi dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔa bʋyi dãana n yetɩ: «Wa'am!»

ref: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
text: Tɩ wemõa n de mõlga yese. Tɩ ba bɔ sɛka n bã ẽ na ke'eŋo tɩ a yese dũnia sũ-mã'asʋm tɩ nẽrba kʋʋra taaba, dee bɔ ẽ sʋkɔbgɔ n ãn kãtɛ.

ref: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
text: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋtã dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔa bʋtã dãana la n yetɩ: «Wa'am!» Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ wemõ-sabga. Tɩ sɛka n bã ẽ na zãla bõn-makrɩga a nu'ugo pʋam.

ref: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
text: Tɩ mam wõm dãmnɛ n ãn wʋ kõa n ze'ele bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la tẽŋasʋka yetɩ: «Si ligri wʋn zo'oge mɛ, tɩ nẽra daarɛ ayɩla yɔɔrɔ wʋn da si wʋlla ayɩla, dee tɩ kamãana wʋlsɩ batã dɛna nẽra daarɛ ayɩla yɔɔrɔ. La, la sãn dɛna kaam la dãam, da pa'asɛ sɛla.»

ref: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋnaasɩ dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam wõm bõn-vɔa bʋnaasɩ dãana n yetɩ: «Wa'am!»

ref: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
text: Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ wemõa n de puka. Sɛka n bã ẽ na yʋ'ʋrɛ n de Akũm, tɩ kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa dɔla a poore. Tɩ ba bɔ ba ke'eŋo tɩ ba kʋ dũnia nẽrba banaasɩ woo pʋam nẽra ayɩla. Sʋkɔbgɔ, la kɔm, la bã'asɩ, la we-dũnsi n wʋn kʋ ba.

ref: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
text: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋnuu dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ mam yẽ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba daan kʋ ba, ban mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la, dee vɔa wʋ yetɔga la n yele se'em na ĩyã, sɩɩsɩ n boe kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na tilum.

ref: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
text: Tɩ ba tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Zuudãana n gãnnɩ wuu n ãn sõŋa, n de sɩra dãana, fõn wʋn yuuge paagɛ la wãne dee di dũnia nẽrba sarɩya sɩbgɛ ba, ban kʋ tõma la ĩyã?»

ref: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
text: Tɩ ba bɔ ba nẽra woo fu-pɛɛlga, dee yele ba yetɩ ba gu fẽe, halɩ tɩ ba kʋ ba sɔbiisi n de ba tõntõn-taaba, n de ba kʋ ba wʋ bãma la, tɩ ba sõorɛ paagɛ.

ref: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
text: Tɩ Pebila la lakɛ bõnɔ bʋyoobɩ dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim zozo'e, tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa lebge lika wʋ tãn-sabga, dee tɩ wõrga lebge mõlga wʋ zɩɩm na.

ref: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
text: Tɩ wõrbiɛ ze'ele saazuum sʋʋgɛ tẽŋa, wʋ kẽnkãŋa tɩ kʋseb-kãtɛ mĩim tɩ kẽnkãma sʋʋgɛ la.

ref: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
text: Tɩ sagõŋɔ kilim wʋ sõŋɔ la bɔɩ. Tɩ tãnsɩ wuu, la tẽn-sɛba tɩ ko'om gilge la wuu ka le bɔna ba zẽ'esɩ.

ref: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
text: Tɩ dũnia na'adõma, la nẽr-kegro, la sʋdaasɩ kɛ'ɛndõma, la bõntatba, la pãnsɩ dõma, la yamsɩ, la burkĩndõma wuu sʋgra tãnsɩ bogro pʋam, la tãmpĩisi lɛma.

ref: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
text: Dee yɛta tãnsɩ, la tãmpĩisi la yetɩ: «Lui-ya tɩ zuto sʋgɛ tɔ, tɩ sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la da yẽta tɔ, tɩ Pebila la sũ-isgre da paagɛ tɔ.

ref: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
text: Se'ere n soe la, ba sũ-isgre daarɛ paagɛ mɛ, nẽra ka boe n wʋn yãŋɛ põsɛ.»

ref: from Benjamin. People from Every Nation
text: ). Ba ze'ele la Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri la wuu pʋam.

ref: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
text: «Nãn da sã'am-ya tẽŋa, bɩɩ ko-kãtɛ, bɩɩ tɩɩsɩ, basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba la dĩa dãalgɔ, dee tɩ ya sã'am.»

ref: Then I heard how many people had been marked on the forehead. There were
text: Tɩ mam wõm sɛba tɩ ba ẽŋɛ ba Nawẽnnɛ dãalgɔ la sõorɛ. Ba de la nẽrba tʋskɔbga la tʋspinaasɩ la anaasɩ (

ref: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
text: yetɩ: «Asɩra, Nawẽnnɛ n soe nã'asgɔ, la na'am pãŋa, la yɛm, la yel-sõnnɛ pʋ'ʋsgɔ, la pẽka, la ke'eŋo, la pãŋa wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Amina.»

ref: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
text: Bɛla poorʋm mam bɩsrɩ mɛ, yẽ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e tɩ nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn kãalɛ. Ba de la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba, la buuri wuu nẽrba, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba. Ba daan yɛ la fu-pɛɛlsɩ zãla karkɔŋa võorɔ ba nu'usum zɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne, la Pebila la nẽŋam.

ref: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
text: Ba tɔgrɩ la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Fãarɛ ze'etɩ la tõma Nawẽnnɛ n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo, la Pebila la zẽ'am.»

ref: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
text: Malɛkadõma la wuu daan ze gilge la na'am bĩmbĩnne, la kẽma, la bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la. Ba daan ẽn ka la dũma dɩkɛ ba nẽnsɩ võglɛ tẽŋa na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam nã'asra Nawẽnnɛ

ref: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
text: Tɩ kẽma la ayɩla soke mam yetɩ: «Ãndõma n de nẽr-bãma n yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ la, ba ze'ele la bɛ?»

ref: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
text: Tɩ mam lerge ẽ yetɩ: «M zuudãana, fõn mi.» Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «Ba de la nẽr-sɛba n ze'ele toog-kãtɛ pʋam na. Ba peege ba futo mɛ Pebila la zɩɩm pʋam tɩ ba pelge.

ref: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
text: Bɛla n soe tɩ ba zɛ Nawẽnnɛ na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam, tõnna bɔ'ɔra ẽ wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ a yire la pʋam. Sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la n wʋn gu'ura ba.

ref: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
text: Kɔm la koyũuro kãn le yõkɛ ba, wʋntɛɛŋa la a tʋʋlgɔ kãn le wõŋɛ ba.

ref: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
text: Se'ere n soe la, Pebila la n boe na'am bĩmbĩnne tẽŋasʋka la n wʋn bɩsra ba yelle, ẽŋa n wʋn tarɩ ba sẽŋɛ ko'om n bulli n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm zẽ'am. La Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn duuse ba nintãm wuu.»

ref: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
text: Pebila la lakɛ la bõnɔ bayopɔɩ dãana n tabɛ la, tɩ la ãna yɩrɩrɩr Nawẽnnɛ yire wakatɛ fẽe paagɛ karf-ayɩla pʋsʋka.

ref: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ n ze Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam, tɩ ba bɔ ba benkɔma bayopɔɩ.

ref: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
text: La malɛka ayẽma n wa'am wa ze'ele kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am. A daan tarɩ la sãlma laaga tɩ ba tarɩ yõ'ora tɩdarɛ. Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ tɩdarɛ pãɩ, tɩ a dɩkɛ ba lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba wuu pʋ'ʋsgɔ yõ sãlma kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne n boe na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam na zuo bɔ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
text: Tɩ tɩdarɛ la tɩ a yõ la bug-zũ'usi lagɩm na Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba la pʋ'ʋsgɔ, ze'ele malɛka la n ze Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam na nu'usum zɔnna saazuum.

ref: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
text: Tɩ malɛka la dɩkɛ laaga la tɩ ba tarɩ yõ'ora tɩdarɛ la, pɩrɛ ẽ na tɩdarɛ bugum na n boe kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne zuo la lobge ba tẽŋa zuo. Tɩ saaga tãsra, yãgra dee sũnna, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim. Benkɔma peebgo

ref: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
text: Malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n zãlɩ benkɔma bayopɔɩ la, mãasʋm mɛ tɩ ba peebe ba.

ref: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
text: Tɩ pɔspɔsɩ malɛka la peebe benkonne. Tɩ sakuga la bugum n gaam na zɩɩm ka'agɛ dũnia zuo. Tɩ dũnia zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, bugum di zẽ'a ayɩla, tɩ tɩɩsɩ zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, bugum di zẽ'a ayɩla, tɩ mõ-mã'asa wuu di bugum.

ref: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋyi dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ ba lobge sɛla ko-kãtɛ pʋam n ãn wʋ tãn-kãtɛ tɩ bugum diti. Tɩ ko'om na zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla lebge zɩɩm.

ref: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
text: Tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ sɛba n boe ko'om pʋam na batã pʋam woo ayɩla ki. Tɩ õornɔ batã pʋam woo ayɩla sã'am.

ref: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋtã dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ wõrbi-kãtɛ n diti bug-zɩlɩŋa ze'ele saazuum lui mõga, la kʋlsɩ ko'om pʋam, a lui la ko'om na zẽ'esɩ batã ayɩla pʋam.

ref: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
text: Wõrbiire la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Azẽlõm. Tɩ ko'om na zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla lebge zẽlõm. La nẽrba zozo'e n yũ ko'om na ki, ko'om na n de zẽlõm na ĩyã.

ref: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋnaasɩ dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ wẽntulle zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla, la wõrga zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla, la wõrbiɛ zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam zẽ'a ayɩla pãŋa ba'asɛ, tɩ ba wuu zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla lebge lika. Tɩ wʋntɛɛŋa zẽ'esɩ batã pʋam ayɩla ka le nẽera peelem, tɩ yʋ'ʋŋɔ mẽ bɛla.

ref: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
text: Tɩ mam yẽ sɩl-kãtɛ n ẽgrɩ saazuum-zuum, wõm ẽn tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Nẽr-sɛba n boe tẽŋa wã zuo wʋn yẽ la toogo, malɛkadõma batã n weege la n wʋn peebe ba benkɔma, tɩ ba yẽ toogo.»

ref: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋnuu dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ mam yẽ wõrbiire n ze'ele saazuum lui dũnia zuo. Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ bog-ko'ogo saafɩbiire.

ref: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
text: Tɩ wõrbiire la pakɛ bog-ko'ogo la, tɩ bug-zũ'usi ze'ele bog-ko'ogo la pʋam yɛsra wʋ bug-kãtɛ zũ'usi la, botɩ wʋntɛɛŋa, la sapebsʋm sobe.

ref: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
text: Tɩ tẽnta'aba ze'ele zũ'usi la pʋam yese saagɛ dũnia zuo. Tɩ ba bɔ ba toogo wʋ nãnsɩ toogo la.

ref: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
text: La ba yele ba yetɩ ba da ẽŋɛ mõorɔ, la bõn-sɛba wuu n yesrɩ tẽŋa, la tɩɩsɩ sɛla, ba pɔglʋm nẽr-sɛba n ka tarɩ Nawẽnnɛ dãalgɔ ba dĩa zuto la mã'a.

ref: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
text: Ba daan ka bɔ tẽnta'aba la nõorɛ tɩ ba kʋ nẽrba la, la de la ba nãmsɛ ba wõr-sɩnuu. Ban botɩ toog-sɛka nãmsra nẽrba la ãn wʋ nãŋa n ẽn dõn nẽra tɩ la dõnna se'em na.

ref: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
text: La dabsa bãma, nẽrsaalba wʋn ɛɛra kũm, la ba kãn yẽ ẽ. Ba wʋn bɔɔra tɩ ba ki, la kũm wʋn zoe dee basɛ ba.

ref: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
text: Tẽnta'aba la ãn wʋ wiiri n mãasʋm gu'ura zɛbrɛ, la sɛla n boe ba zuto ãna wʋ sãlma mũnsi, dee tɩ ba nẽnsɩ ãna wʋ nẽrba nẽnsɩ la.

ref: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
text: Ba tarɩ la zomto n ãn wʋ pɔgsɩ zomto la. Dee tɩ ba yẽna ãna wʋ gɩgma yẽna la.

ref: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
text: Ba dɩkɛ la bõnɔ n ãn wʋ kuto futo pĩ ba yõ'orɔ. La ba kempagsɩ la dãmnɛ ãn wʋ wiiri toto tɩ wiiri zozo'e ve'erɩ zɔta wɛ'ɛsa zɛbrɛ zẽ'a.

ref: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
text: Ba tarɩ la zɔa wʋ nãnsɩ zɔa la dõnna. Ba zɔa bãma n bɔ ba ke'eŋo tɩ ba nãmsɛ nẽrba wõr-sɩnuu.

ref: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
text: Ba daan tarɩ la naba, a de la malɛk-sɛka n gu'uri bog-ko'ogo la. A yʋ'ʋrɛ la Ebre yetɔga de la «Abadõ», la Grɛkɩ yetɔga de la «Apoliyõ», n boorɩ tɩ la yetɩ «Sã'ama».

ref: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
text: Pɔspɔsɩ toogo la tole mɛ. La toogo bʋyi n wʋn ken wa'am.

ref: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋyoobɩ dãana peebe benkonne. Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele sãlma kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne n boe Nawẽnnɛ nẽŋam kʋgra banaasɩ la zẽ'am.

ref: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
text: Kõa la yele malɛka bʋyoobɩ dãana n zãlɩ benkonne na yetɩ: «Lorge malɛkadõma banaasɩ tɩ ba lʋ ba, tɩ ba bɔna Efratɩ kʋl-kãtɛ zẽ'am na basɛ.»

ref: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
