text: Tɩ a lorge malɛkadõma banaasɩ la basɛ. Ba daan mãasʋm malɛg-bãma gu'ura la wakatɛ ẽŋa, la dabsrɛ ẽŋa, la wõr-ẽŋa, la yʋʋn-ẽŋa, tɩ ba kʋ nẽrsaalba batã woo pʋam ayɩla.

ref: By listening, I could tell there were more than
text: Tɩ ba pa'alɛ mam ba sʋdaasɩ n bã'arɩ wiiri kãllɛ, ba de la tʋsa kɔbsyi nõorɛ tʋsrɛ.

ref: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ wiiri la, la ba wi-bã'arba n ãn se'em: Ba tarɩ la kuto futo n pĩ'iri yõ'orɔ, tɩ ba mõ'ogɛ ãna wʋ bugum, dee dɛna yõosnɛ, la dõbʋʋlʋm. Wiiri la zuto ãn wʋ gɩgma zuto la. Bugum, la bug-zũ'usi, la kidibre n ze'ele ba nõa pʋam yɛsra.

ref: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
text: Tɩ tooro bãma batã n de bugum, la bug-zũ'usi, la kidibre n ze'ele wiiri la nõa pʋam yɛsra la, kʋ nẽrsaalba batã pʋam woo ayɩla.

ref: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
text: Wiiri la ke'eŋo boe la ba nõa, la ba zɔa pʋam. Ba zɔa la ãn wʋ bõnsɛ'ɛdõma la, ba tarɩ la zuto bãma tɩ ba tarɩ ɩta nẽrba be'em sã'ana ba.

ref: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
text: La nẽr-sɛba n weege tɩ tooro bãma ka kʋ ba la, ka teege yɛm basɛ ban ɩtɩ sɛla tɩ ba mẽŋa boorɩ la. Ba ken dɔla la kʋlkã'arsɩ, la baga tɩ ba dɩkɛ sãlma, la zũuro, la yõgõm, la kuga, la dɔɔrɔ mãalɛ, tɩ ba ka yẽta, ka wõnna, ka tãna sẽnna.

ref: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
text: Ba ken ka teege yɛm basɛ ba nẽr-kʋa, la ba tɩɩm tõoma, la ba yalsɩ tõoma, la ba nayigum na.

ref: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
text: Tɩ mam daan yẽ malɛka ayẽma n tarɩ pãŋa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigra. Tɩ sawara pile ẽ wʋ fuugo tɩ a yɛ, dee tɩ gõmatɩ-tãpɔ kɔkɛ a zuugo. A nẽŋa ãn wʋ wẽntulle, dee tɩ a nãma ãna wʋ bug-zɩlɩsɩ la.

ref: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
text: A zãlɩ la gõn-pɩka n lakɛ a nu'usum. A dɩkɛ a zʋʋgɔ nã'arɛ taglɛ la ko-kãtɛ zuo, dee dɩkɛ a gɔbga nã'arɛ taglɛ tẽŋa zuo.

ref: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
text: A daan tɔgɛ mɛ ke'enke'em wʋ gɩgnɛ n kaasɛ la. Ẽn daan tɔgɛ la, saaga n tãsɛ nõorɛ bʋyopɔɩ.

ref: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
text: Saaga la n tãsɛ tɔgɛ la, mam yetɩ m gʋlsɛ la ban yele se'em na. La mam wõm na kõa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ mam yetɩ: «Sʋgɛ sɛla tɩ saaga tãsɛ bʋyopɔɩ tɔgɛ la, da gʋlsɛ ba.»

ref: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
text: Tɩ malɛk-sɛka tɩ mam daan yẽ tɩ a ze ko-kãtɛ la tẽŋa zuo la, n zẽkɛ a zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo saazuum,

ref: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
text: pɔɩ la Nawẽnnɛ n boe wakatɛ wuu, n nãam saazuum, la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la sɛla wuu n boe ba pʋam na, yetɩ: «La kãn le yuuge.

ref: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
text: Malɛka bʋyopɔɩ dãana la sãn peebe benkonne, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn ẽŋɛ ẽn looge yɛm tɩ a ẽŋɛ se'em n sʋgɛ la mɛ, wʋ ẽn yʋʋn tɔgɛ la a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba n de a tõntõnɩba se'em na.»

ref: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
text: Tɩ kõa la n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ tɩ mam wõm na, le yele mam yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ ka to'oge gõn-pɩka la n lakɛ bɔna malɛka la n ze ko-kãtɛ la tẽŋa zuo la nu'um.»

ref: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
text: Tɩ mam sẽŋɛ malɛka la zẽ'am ka yele ẽ yetɩ: «Bɔ mam gõn-pɩka la.» Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «To'oge ẽ obe vole. A wʋn lebge la toogo fʋ pʋʋrʋm. La fʋ nõorʋm, a wʋn ãna yẽlʋm wʋ seero la.»

ref: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
text: Tɩ mam to'oge gõn-pɩka la a nu'um obe vole. Tɩ a ãna yẽlʋm wʋ seero mam nõorʋm. La mam n vole ẽ na, a lebge la toogo mam pʋʋrʋm.

ref: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
text: Tɩ ba yele mam yetɩ: «Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ le tɔgɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga pa'alɛ la wʋn ẽŋɛ tẽnsɩ zozo'e nẽrba, la so'olʋm zozo'e nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri zozo'e nẽrba, la na'adõma zozo'e se'em.»

ref: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
text: La basɛ a zẽnzaka la, da makɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, ba dɩkɛ ẽ bɔ la bu-zãŋsɩ la, ba wʋn nẽ Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa la mɛ wõrsɩ pinaasɩ la ayi sã'am.»

ref: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
text: Nẽr-bãma bayi de la Oliivi tɩɩsɩ bayi, dee ken dɛna fɩtladõma bayi n ze dũnia wuu Zuudãana nẽŋam na.

ref: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
text: Nẽra sãn ɛɛra tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba be'em, bugum n ze'etɩ ba nõa pʋam dita ba bɛ'ɛba la. Asɩra, nẽr-sɛka n eerɩ tɩ a ẽŋɛ ba be'em, dẽnɩ tɩ a ki bɛla.

ref: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
text: Ba tarɩ la ke'eŋo wʋn yõkɛ saaga tɩ a ka niira wakat-sɛba wuu tɩ ba to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga tɔgra bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la. La ba ken tara ke'eŋo mɛ wʋn botɩ ko'om lebge zɩɩm, dee ken tara ke'eŋo wʋn botɩ tooro buuri wuu paagɛ dũnia nẽrba wakat-sɛka wuu tɩ ba boorɩ.

ref: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
text: Ba sãn wa tɔgɛ yetɔga la ba'asɛ, dũŋa n wʋn ze'ele bog-ko'ogo la pʋam yese zɛbɛ la ba, yãŋɛ kʋ ba.

ref: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
text: Tɩ ba kũm na wʋn gã peelem nẽŋam tẽn-kãtɛ la pʋam. Bilam tɩ ba daan ka ba Zuudãana dɔ-puurŋa zuo. Tẽŋa la yʋ'ʋrɛ n pa'alɛ ẽn ãn se'em de la Sodom, la Ezipti.

ref: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
text: Tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la buuri wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba wʋn bɩsra ba kũm na la dabsa atã la pʋsʋka, dee kãn sakɛ tɩ ba dɩkɛ ba kũm na laagɛ.

ref: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
text: Dũnia nẽrba sũure wʋn ẽŋɛ yẽlʋm mɛ, ban yẽ tɩ nẽrba bayi la ki la ĩyã. Tɩ ba wʋn ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga de'em, bɔ taaba bɔ'a, se'ere n soe la, nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba bayi la nãmsɛ nẽrba dũnia mɛ.

ref: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
text: La dabsa atã la pʋsʋka la poorʋm, vo'osʋm n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n wʋn ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wa'am wa kẽ ba pʋam, tɩ ba vo'oge isge. Tɩ dabeem zozo'e wʋn yõkɛ sɛba n yẽ ba la.

ref: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
text: La nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba bayi la wʋn wõm na kõa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire yele ba yetɩ: «Zom-ya wa'am.» Tɩ ba kẽ sawara pʋam zɔnna wɛ'ɛsa Nawẽnnɛ yire, tɩ bɛ'ɛba la bɩsra ba.

ref: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
text: Wakatɛ ẽŋa mẽŋa, tẽŋa n wʋn mĩim halɩ zozo'e. Tɩ Zerizalɛm tẽŋa la zẽ'esɩ pia pʋam, zẽ'a ayɩla wʋn lui saam. Tɩ nẽrba tʋsa ayopɔɩ ki, tẽŋa la n mĩim na ĩyã. Tɩ dabeem wʋn yõkɛ nẽr-sɛba n weege la, tɩ ba bɔ Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum nã'asgɔ.

ref: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
text: Toogo bʋyi dãana tole mɛ. La toogo bʋtã dãana la dee la fẽe la a wa'am. Benkonne bʋyopɔɩ dãana

ref: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋyopɔɩ dãana peebe benkonne, tɩ kõosɩ ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Dũnia so'olʋm lebge la tõma Zuudãana, la Krisi tɩ a tõm na n soe, la a na'am na boe la wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ.»

ref: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
text: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, n daan boe, n ken bɔna la, tõma pʋ'ʋsrɩ la fõn fʋ yel-sõnnɛ, fõn dɩkɛ fʋ ke'eŋo n zo'oge vigle fʋ na'am na ĩyã.

ref: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
text: Bu-zãŋsɩ wuu nẽrba sũure isge mɛ, la lɛɛlɛ wã fõn sũ-yĩire wakatɛ n paagɛ, tɩ fõn wʋn di sɛba n ki la sarɩya, la fõn wʋn yɔ fʋ tõntõnɩba la n de fʋ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la fʋ nẽrba la n nã'asrɩ fõn, kɛ'ɛndõma la tarɩmdõma wuu mɛ. La de la wakatɛ tɩ fõn wʋn sã'am nẽr-sɛba n sã'anɩ dũnia la.»

ref: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
text: Tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la yo'oge, tɩ nõor-bĩŋre daka la yẽta. Tɩ saaga yãgra, sũnna, tãsra, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim, tɩ sakuga zozo'e lui.

ref: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
text: Pɔka la tarɩ la pʋʋrɛ virgra tɩ a dɔgɛ, wʋlsra kaasra, dɔka toogo n tarɩ ẽ na ĩyã.

ref: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
text: Tɩ bõn-bãnnɛ ayẽma puke saazuum: A de la pʋryõŋ-yãlma n ãn kãtɛ dɛna mõlga, a tarɩ la zuto bayopɔɩ, la ɩɩla pia, tɩ mũnsi vuge a zuto la wuu.

ref: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
text: Tɩ a zʋʋrɛ kã'asɛ wõrbiɛ batã pʋam ayɩla saazuum lobe basɛ tẽŋa zuo. Pʋryõŋ-yãlma la ze la pɔka la n yetɩ a dɔgɛ la nẽŋam gu'ura tɩ pɔka la dɔgɛ tɩ a yõkɛ a bia la obe.

ref: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
text: La pɔka la dɔgɛ la budibla n wʋn tara kuto dasaarɛ sɔna so'olʋm buuri wuu. Tɩ ba zɛ bia la tarɩ sẽŋɛ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am, a na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am.

ref: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
text: Tɩ pɔka la zoe sẽŋɛ weem, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ mãasʋm a ĩyã la, bilam tɩ ba diise ẽ dabsa tʋsrɛ la kɔbsyi la pisyoobɩ.

ref: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
text: Tɩ zɛbrɛ isge Nawẽnnɛ yire. Tɩ malɛkadõma kɛ'ɛma Amisɛlɩ, la a malɛkadõma zɛbra la pʋryõŋ-yãlma la, tɩ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la, la a malɛgsɩ mẽ zɛbra la ba.

ref: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
text: La ba koŋe pãŋa mɛ, tɩ sore ka le bɔna tɩ ba bɔna Nawẽnnɛ yire.

ref: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
text: Tɩ ba daan lobe pʋryõŋ-yãlma kãtɛ la basɛ. Ẽŋa n de bõnsɛla n yʋʋn boe, tɩ ba wi'ira ẽ tɩ «Dõta» bɩɩ «Asũtãana» n pã'asrɩ dũnia nẽrba wuu la. Ba lobe ẽ, la a malɛgsɩ basɛ la tẽŋa zuo.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em Nawẽnnɛ yire yetɩ: «Nãnannɛ wã, Nawẽnnɛ fãarɛ daarɛ paagɛ mɛ, Nawẽnnɛ yãŋa pa'alɛ a pãŋa, la a na'am mɛ, dee pa'alɛ Krisi tɩ a tõm na ke'eŋo, se'ere n soe la, ba dige sɛka n wã'arɩ tɩ sɔbiisi Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ la mɛ.

ref: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
text: La de la Pebila la zɩɩm ĩyã, la ban mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la ĩyã, tɩ tɩ sɔbiisi la yãŋɛ ẽ. Ba ka zoe tɩ ba wʋn kʋ ba.

ref: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
text: Bɛla, Nawẽnnɛ yire, la yãma sɛba wuu n boe mĩ na, ẽŋɛ-ya sũ-yẽlga. La toogo n wʋn yẽ tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, se'ere n soe la, Asũtãana sige ya zẽ'am la sũ-yĩire, ẽn mi tɩ a wakatɛ deege la fẽe la ĩyã.»

ref: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
text: Pʋryõŋ-yãlma la n yẽ tɩ ba lobe ẽ basɛ la tẽŋa zuo la, a babsɛ la pɔka la n dɔgɛ bia la.

ref: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
text: Tɩ ba bɔ pɔka la sɩl-kãtɛ kempagsɩ bayi tɩ a ẽkɛ sẽŋɛ weem, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wʋn diisra ẽ yʋʋma atã la pʋsʋka la, zãagɛ la bõnsɛla la.

ref: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
text: Tɩ bõnsɛla la ʋkɛ ko'om a nõorʋm wʋ mõgrɛ la pɔka la poorʋm tɩ ba pe'ege ẽ fõrgɛ.

ref: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
text: La tẽŋa sõŋɛ pɔka la mɛ, ya'agɛ nõorɛ fõogɛ ko'om na n ze'ele pʋryõŋ-yãlma la nõorʋm na.

ref: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
text: Tɩ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la sũure yĩige la pɔka la, tɩ a sẽŋɛ ka zɛbra la pɔka la yɩɩsɩ sɛba n weege ken sakra Nawẽnnɛ nõorɛ, dee tɔgra Azezi yelle bɔ'ɔra nẽrba la.

ref: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
text: Pʋryõŋ-yãlma la sẽŋɛ ka zɛ la ko-kãtɛ la nõorʋm.

ref: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, mam yẽ la dũŋa n ze'ele ko-kãtɛ pʋam dooge. A tarɩ la ɩɩla pia, la zuto bayopɔɩ, tɩ mũnsi pia vuge a ɩɩla la wuu. La yʋ'ʋra n pɔ'ɔrɩ Nawẽnnɛ yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ a zuto la zuo.

ref: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
text: Dũŋa la tɩ mam yẽ la wõn na webaaga. Tɩ a nã'a-tala ãna kãra, tara nã'a-ẽesɩ wʋ tẽŋambõŋa la. Dee tɩ a nõorɛ ãna wʋ gɩgnɛ nõorɛ la. Tɩ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la dɩkɛ a pãŋa, la a na'am bĩmbĩnne, la a ke'eŋo bɔ dũŋa la.

ref: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
text: Dũŋa la zuugo ayɩla daan pɔglʋm mɛ, ãna wʋ a ki mɛ na, la a pɔglʋŋɔ la daan bɔɩ mɛ. Tɩ la di'ige dũnia nẽrba wuu, tɩ ba dɔla dũŋa la.

ref: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
text: Tɩ nẽrba wuu nã'asra pʋryõŋ-yãlma la, ẽn bɔ dũŋa la a ke'eŋo la ĩyã. Dee nã'asra dũŋa la yetɩ: «Ãne n tarɩ ke'eŋo wʋ dũŋa la? La ãne n tã wʋn zɛbɛ la ẽ lɛ?»

ref: The beast was allowed to brag and claim to be God, and for
text: Tɩ ba bɔ dũŋa la nõorɛ tɩ a tɔgɛ tɩta'alʋm yetɔga, dee tʋ Nawẽnnɛ. A to'oge ke'eŋo tɩ a tõm na wõrsɩ pinaasɩ la ayi.

ref: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
text: Tɩ a pɔsɛ tɔgra pɔ'ɔra Nawẽnnɛ dee pɔ'ɔra a yʋ'ʋrɛ, la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ a boe, la sɛba n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la wuu.

ref: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
text: Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ nõorɛ tɩ a zɛbɛ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba yãŋɛ ba. Ba bɔ ẽ ke'eŋo tɩ a yãŋɛ la buuri wuu nẽrba, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm wuu nẽrba.

ref: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
text: Nẽrba wuu n boe dũnia zuo wʋn nã'asɛ dũŋa la mɛ, sãn dagna sɛba yʋ'ʋra n gʋlsɛ Pebila tɩ ba daan kʋ la vɔm gõŋɔ pʋam na. Nẽr-bãma yʋ'ʋra daan põn gʋlsɛ vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam na halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ.

ref: If you have ears, then listen!
text: Sɛka n wõm yetɔga wã, a selse sõŋa sõŋa.

ref: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
text: Nẽr-sɛka n de ba yõkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ, ba wʋn yõkɛ ẽ sẽŋɛ. Nẽr-sɛka n de ba kʋ ẽ na sʋ'a, ba wʋn kʋ ẽ na sʋ'a. Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tara sũ-ke'eŋo, dee bɔ Nawẽnnɛ sɩra.

ref: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, mam yẽ la dũŋa ayẽma n ze'ele tẽŋa pʋam yese. A tarɩ la ɩɩla bayi n wõn na pebila ɩɩla, dee tɔgra wʋ pʋryõŋ-yãlma la.

ref: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
text: A tarɩ la pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la ke'eŋo tõnna a nifum. A pẽrgɛ dũnia nẽrba wuu mɛ tɩ ba nã'asra pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la n daan pɔglʋm ãna wʋ a ki mɛ, dee yeege la.

ref: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
text: Dũŋa bʋyi dãana la ẽŋɛ la bõn-yãlm-kãra, halɩ botɩ bugum zɛ'ɛta saazuum sigra dũnia, nẽrba nifum.

ref: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
text: Bõn-yãlma la tɩ a ẽŋɛ pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la nifum na, botɩ a pã'asɛ dũnia nẽrba mɛ. A yele ba tɩ ba mãalɛ bõnɔ tɩ a ãna wʋ dũŋa la tɩ ba pɔglʋm na sʋ'a tɩ a vɔa la.

ref: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
text: Tɩ ba bɔ dũŋa bʋyi dãana la ke'eŋo tɩ a bɔ bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ tɩ a ãna wʋ pɔspɔsɩ dũŋa la vo'osʋm, tɩ a tãna tɔgra, dee kʋ sɛba wuu n ka nã'asrɩ ẽ na.

ref: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
text: La dũŋa la pẽrgɛ nẽrba wuu mɛ, tarɩmdõma la kɛ'ɛndõma, bõntatba la nasdõma, yamsɩ la burkĩndõma tɩ ba dãalɛ ba zʋʋgɔ nu'ugo bɩɩ ba dĩire.

ref: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
text: Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka tãna wʋn da bɩɩ koose sɛla, a sãn ka tara dũŋa la dãalgɔ, a yʋ'ʋrɛ, bɩɩ a yʋ'ʋrɛ la kãllɛ.

ref: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ tara yɛm: Nẽr-sɛka n tarɩ pʋtẽ'erɛ, ẽŋa n wʋn tã'agɛ bãŋɛ dũŋa la kãllɛ võorɛ. Se'ere n soe la, kãllɛ la võorɛ de la nẽrsaala kãllɛ. Kãllɛ la biɛ de la

ref: I looked and saw the Lamb standing on Mount Zion! With him were
text: Tɩ mam le bɩsɛ, yẽ Pebila la n ze Siyõ tãŋa zuo. Tɩ nẽrba tʋskɔbga la tʋspinaasɩ la anaasɩ (

ref: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire tɩ a dãmnɛ ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ dãmnɛ, dee ãna wʋ saaga n tãsrɩ sũnna, dee ãna wʋ kõl-wẽ'erba n wẽ'erɩ ba kõnnɔ.

ref: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
text: Nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ bãma ze la na'am bĩmbĩnne, la bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ, la kẽma la nẽŋam yõona yõom-paalɛ. Nẽra nẽra ka tã wʋn zãsʋm yõonɛ na, sãn dagna nẽrba tʋskɔbga la tʋspinaasɩ la anaasɩ la n yẽ fãarɛ dũnia zuo la mã'a.

ref: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
text: Nẽr-bãma ka dẽgɛ ba mẽŋa la pɔgsɩ, ba tarɩ ba mẽŋa sõŋa mɛ. Ba dolɩ la Pebila la zẽ'e-sɛka woo tɩ a we'esɩ. Ba yẽ fãarɛ nẽrsaalba pʋam tɩ ba dɛna la pɔspɔsɩ dõma bɔ Nawẽnnɛ, la Pebila la.

ref: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
text: Põmpɔrŋɔ yetɔga ka tole ze'ele ba nõorʋm, ba ka tarɩ tuure. Malɛkadõma batã kõosɩ

ref: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛka ayẽma n ẽgrɩ saazuum-zuum, a tarɩ la Nawẽnnɛ kõ-yẽlga n boe wakatɛ wuu tɩ a mõolɛ bɔ nẽr-sɛba wuu n boe dũnia zuo, so'olʋm wuu nẽrba, la buuri wuu nẽrba, la yetɔga buuri wuu nẽrba, la tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba.

ref: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
text: A daan tɔgrɩ mɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Zoe-ya Nawẽnnɛ, la ya nã'asɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ tɩ a wʋn di nẽrba sarɩya. Ka-ya dũma nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ n nãam saazuum, la tẽŋa, la ko-kãtɛ, la kʋlsɩ wuu n bulli la.»

ref: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋyi dãana dɔla pɔspɔsɩ dãana la poore yɛta tɩ: «A lui mɛ, a lui mɛ, Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la lui sã'am mɛ. Ẽŋa n botɩ so'olʋm wuu nẽrba tõm a yalsɩ tõoma zozo'e wʋ dãam tɩ a yũuse ba la.»

ref: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋtã dãana dɔla malɛkadõma bayi la tɔgra ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Nẽra sãn nã'asɛ dũŋa la, la a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la, la a sãn to'oge a dãalgɔ la a dĩire zuo bɩɩ a nu'ugo zuo,

ref: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
text: Nawẽnnɛ sũure wʋn yĩige la ẽ, tɩ a sɩbgɛ ẽ wʋ dã-ke'eŋo n ka pulum tɩ a pɩrɛ wannɛ bɔ ẽ tɩ a yũ. A wʋn nãmsɛ la kidibre n diti bugum pʋam, malɛg-sõma, la Pebila la nẽŋam.

ref: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
text: Nẽr-bãma nãmsgɔ bug-zũ'usi zonnɩ la saazuum wakatɛ wuu. Nẽr-sɛba wuu n nã'asrɩ dũŋa la, la a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la, dee to'oge a yʋ'ʋrɛ dãalgɔ la, kãn malɩn yẽ vo'osgo wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ.»

ref: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
text: Bɛla, dẽnɩ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n sakra a nõorɛ dee bɔ Azezi sɩra la, tara sũ-ke'eŋo.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele saazuum yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ yetɩ: ‹Lan pɔsɛ lɛɛlɛ wã, nẽr-sɛba n kiiri Zuudãana doosgo pʋam na tarɩ zu-yẽlga.› Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa yetɩ: ‹La de la sɩra, ba wʋn vo'ose la ba tõon-tooro, ba tõon-sõma la dolɩ ba mɛ.› » Dũnia bõnkɔɔla lagsgɔ

ref: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
text: Tɩ mam bɩsra, yẽ sawatɛ n de pɛɛlga, tɩ nẽra n wõn na nẽrsaala dayʋa zɔa a zuo. A vuge la sãlma mũŋa, dee tarɩ geego n saasɛ tara nõorɛ a nu'um.

ref: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
text: Tɩ malɛka ayẽma ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam yese tɔgɛ la sɛka n zo sawatɛ zuo la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Dɩkɛ geego la sɛɛra, tɩ lagsgɔ wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, se'ere n soe la, tẽŋa zuo bõnkɔɔla bɩ mɛ seke lagsgɔ.»

ref: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
text: Tɩ sɛka n zĩ sawatɛ zuo la dɩkɛ a geego la doose tẽŋa zuo, sɛ tẽŋa bõnkɔɔla lagsɛ.

ref: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
text: Tɩ malɛka ayẽma ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ n boe saazuum na pʋam yese, ẽŋa mẽ tarɩ la geego n saasɛ tara nõorɛ.

ref: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
text: Tɩ malɛka ayẽma n tarɩ ke'eŋo sɔna bugum ze'ele kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'a yese tɔgɛ ke'enke'em la malɛk-sɛka n tarɩ geego n tarɩ nõorɛ la yetɩ: «Dɩkɛ fʋ geego la sɛ viinye tɩɩsɩ la biɛ n boe tẽŋa zuo la. Se'ere n soe la, ba biɛ la bɩ mɛ.»

ref: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
text: Tɩ malɛka la dɩkɛ a geego doose tẽŋa zuo sɛ viinye tɩɩsɩ la biɛ, dɩkɛ ba lobge bõn-kãtɛ tɩ ba wẽkrɩ viinye biɛ a pʋam n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-yĩire la pʋam.

ref: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
text: Tɩ ba wẽkɛ biɛ la wẽkrɛ bõnɔ na pʋam tẽŋa la kẽnkɛrŋa. Tɩ zɩɩm ze'ele wẽkrɛ bõnɔ na pʋam yese pɩrɛ tẽŋa zoe paagɛ kilomẽtrɩ kɔbstã. Tɩ wemõa sãn sige ba pʋam, la de ko'ogo paara la a garẽŋrɛ.

ref: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
text: Tɩ mam le yẽ bõn-bãnnɛ ayẽma saazuum n de yel-kãtɛ n ẽgrɩ palẽŋa. La de la malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ n zãlɩ tooro bayopɔɩ n de ba'asgɔ tooro. Se'ere n soe la, bãma n wʋn ẽŋɛ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-yĩire la ba'asɛ.

ref: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
text: Tɩ mam yẽ sɛla n ãn wʋ ko-kãtɛ n yẽgrɩ wʋ bɩsga n gaam na bugum. La mam yẽ la nẽr-sɛba n zɛbɛ yãŋɛ dũŋa, la a bõnɔ tɩ ba mãalɛ, dee ka sakɛ to'oge a yʋ'ʋrɛ la kãllɛ dãalgɔ la, n ze ko-kãtɛ n yẽgrɩ wʋ bɩsga la nõorʋm. Ba zãlɩ la kõnnɔ tɩ Nawẽnnɛ bɔ ba.

ref: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
text: Ba yõonɩ na Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnna Amoyisi yõonɛ, la Pebila la yõonɛ yetɩ: «Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, fõn tõoma de la tõon-kãra, la tõon-yãlma. So'olʋm buuri wuu Naba, fõn yɛla la masɛ mɛ, la ba de la sɩra.

ref: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
text: Zuudãana, ãne n kãn zoe fõn, nã'asɛ fõn yʋ'ʋrɛ la? Se'ere n soe la, fõn mã'a n de pʋpeelem dãana. So'olʋm wuu nẽrba wʋn wa'am wa ka dũma nã'asɛ fɔ, se'ere n soe la, fõn tõoma n masɛ la puke mɛ tɩ ba yẽ.»

ref: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
text: Bɛla poorʋm mam bɩsɛ mɛ, yẽ tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ n boe Nawẽnnɛ yire la pʋam deego la yo'oge mɛ.

ref: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
text: Tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n zãlɩ tooro bayopɔɩ la ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam yese, ba yɛ la fu-pɛɛlsɩ n yẽgrɩ, dee dɩkɛ sãlma sẽbrɩsɩ bobe ba yõ'orɔ.

ref: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
text: Tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ banaasɩ la ayɩla bɔ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la sãlma laasɩ bayopɔɩ n pɩrɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n vɔa wakatɛ wuu la sũ-isgre.

ref: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
text: Tɩ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pɩrɛ la bug-zũ'usi n pa'alɩ Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa, la a ke'eŋo. Tɩ nẽra nẽra ka tãna wʋn kẽ Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam, sãn dagna tɩ tooro bayopɔɩ la tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la zãlɩ la n ba'asɛ.

ref: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam tɔgra ke'enke'em la malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la yetɩ: «Sẽŋɛ-ya ka ka'agɛ Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre la n pɩrɛ laasɩ bayopɔɩ la basɛ dũnia zuo.»

ref: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
text: Tɩ pɔspɔsɩ malɛka sẽŋɛ ka ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ dũnia zuo. Tɩ nõrtãnkɛ'ɛ-tɔɔsɩ n dõnnɩ ĩyã yõgɛ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ dũŋa la dãalgɔ, dee nã'asra a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la.

ref: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋyi dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ ko-kãtɛ pʋam. Tɩ ko-kãtɛ la lebge wʋ nẽra n ki zɩɩm, tɩ bõn-vɔɔsɩ sɛba wuu n boe ko-kãtɛ la pʋam ki.

ref: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋtã dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ mõga, la kʋlsɩ ko'om pʋam, tɩ ko'om na lebge zɩɩm.

ref: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
text: Tɩ mam wõm malɛka n bɩsrɩ ko'om yelle la n yele Nawẽnnɛ yetɩ: «Fõn de la pʋpeelem dãana, fõn daan boe mɛ, dee ken bɔna. Fõn di sarɩya se'em na masɛ mɛ.

ref: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
text: Se'ere n soe la, ba kʋ la sɛba n welge to'ore dɛna fõn nẽrba, la fõn nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɩ ba zɩɩm yese, bɛla tɩ fõn bɔ ba zɩɩm tɩ ba yũ. Bɛla n masɛ la ba.»

ref: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele kãabgɔ bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am yetɩ: «Asɩra, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, fõn sarɩyadia la masɛ mɛ, dɛna sɩra.»

ref: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋnaasɩ dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ wẽntulle zuo tɩ a yõ'ogɛ nẽrba la a bugum.

ref: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
text: Tɩ tʋʋlg-kãtɛ yõ'ogɛ nẽrba, tɩ ba tʋʋra Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana n tara nõorɛ la tooro bãma la. La ba ka teege yɛm basɛ tõon-be'ero nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ.

ref: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋnuu dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ dũŋa la na'am bĩmbĩnne na zuo. Tɩ dũŋa la so'olʋm wuu lebge lika. Tɩ toogo basɛ tɩ nẽrba ɔbra ba zɩlɩsɩ, ba ĩyã n dõnnɩ ĩyã.

ref: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
text: Nõrtãnkɛ'ɛsɩ la tooro n tarɩ nẽrba la ĩyã, tɩ ba tʋʋra Nawẽnnɛ n boe saazuum na. La ba ka teege yɛm basɛ ba tõon-be'ero la.

ref: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋyoobɩ dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ basɛ mõg-kãtɛ sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ Efratɩ la pʋam. Tɩ a ko'om kẽesɛ, lebge sore bɔ na'adõma sɛba n ze'ele wẽntulle doore bɔba la.

ref: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ kʋlkã'arsɩ batã n ãn wʋ lonno n ze'ele pʋryõŋ-yãlma la nõorʋm, la dũŋa la nõorʋm, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la nõorʋm yɛsra.

ref: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
text: Ba de la Asũtãana kʋlkã'arsɩ n ɩtɩ bõn-yãlma. Ba we'esɩ la dũnia na'adõma wuu zẽ'am tɩ ba lagsɛ ba, tɩ ba zɛbɛ la Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, a dabs-kãtɛ la daarɛ.

ref: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
text: Tɩ Zuudãana yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ-ya, mam sẽm wʋ nayiga la. Nẽr-sɛka n ka gĩsri dee yɛ a futo tarɩ zu-yẽlga, bɛla tɩ a kãn sẽnna beele tɩ nẽrba yẽta a yãnnɛ.»

ref: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
text: Tɩ kʋlkã'arsɩ la lagsɛ na'adõma la zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba wi'iri la Ebre yetɔga tɩ «Armagedõ».

ref: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
text: Tɩ malɛka bʋyopɔɩ dãana ka'agɛ a laaga bõnɔ sapebsʋm pʋam. Tɩ kõa ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnne n boe Wẽnde-kãtɛ la pʋam zẽ'am yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ.»

ref: There were flashes of lightning, roars of thunder, and the worst earthquake in all history.
text: Tɩ saaga yãgra, sũnna, tãsra, tɩ tẽŋa mĩim halɩ zozo'e. Nẽrsaalba n boe tẽŋa zuo tɛka, tẽŋa nãn ka mĩim be'em be'em wʋ bɛla.

ref: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
text: Tẽŋa la n mĩim na, tẽn-kãtɛ la pãrsɛ la zẽ'esɩ batã. Tɩ dũnia so'olʋm wuu tẽnsɩ lui. Nawẽnnɛ tẽegɛ Babilɔnɩ tẽn-kãtɛ la yelle mɛ, botɩ a yũ a sũ-isgre kãtɛ n ãn wʋ dãam n boe a wannɛ pʋam na.

ref: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
text: Tɩ tẽn-sɛba wuu tɩ ko'om gilge, la tãnsɩ wuu bɔɩ, tɩ nẽra ka le yẽta ba.

ref: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
text: Tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n daan zãlɩ laasɩ bayopɔɩ la, ayɩla wa'am mam zẽ'am wa yele mam yetɩ: «Wa'am, tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fɔ pɔg-yalsɩ dãana n de tẽn-kãtɛ n mẽ lɛm kʋlsɩ zozo'e nõorʋm na n wʋn yẽ sɩbgrɛ se'em.

ref: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
text: La de la ẽŋa tɩ dũnia na'adõma tõm yalsɩ tõoma la ẽ zozo'e. La dũnia nẽrba mẽ tõm na a yalsɩ tõoma wʋ dãam tɩ ba yũ buge la.»

ref: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la mam, tɩ malɛka la tarɩ mam sẽŋɛ mõom. Tɩ mam yẽ pɔka n bã dũŋa n de mõlga tɩ yʋ'ʋra n tʋʋrɩ Nawẽnnɛ gʋlsɛ a ĩyã wuu. Dũŋa la daan tarɩ la zuto bayopɔɩ, la ɩɩla pia.

ref: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
text: Pɔka la daan yɛ la fu-mõlga n de gomusga, dee yɛ nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ n de sãlma, la kug-sõma buuri ligri n zo'oge. La a zãlɩ la sãlma laaga n pɩrɛ la bõn-be'ero n de a yalsɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ la.

ref: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
text: Yʋ'ʋrɛ n daan gʋlsɛ a dĩire zuo, tɩ ba ka mina a võorɛ. Yʋ'ʋrɛ la de la «Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la, a de la dũnia pɔg-yalsɩ dõma, la tõon-dẽgrɔ wuu ma».

ref: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
text: La mam yẽ tɩ pɔg-ẽŋa yũ la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba zɩɩm buge, nẽr-sɛba n bɔ Azezi sɩra tɩ ba kʋ ba la zɩɩm. Mam n yẽ ẽ na, la di'ige mam mɛ zozo'e.

ref: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
text: Tɩ malɛka la soke mam yetɩ: «Bẽm tɩ la dɛna di'ire bɔ fõn? Mam wʋn tɔgɛ pɔka la, la dũŋa la tɩ a bã n tarɩ zuto bayopɔɩ, la ɩɩla pia la võorɛ pa'alɛ fɔ.

ref: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
text: Dũŋa la tɩ fʋ yẽ la, kʋrʋm a vɔa mɛ, la a ka le bɔna, la a wʋn ze'ele la bog-ko'ogo la pʋam dooge, sẽŋɛ sã'aŋɔ zẽ'am. La dũnia nẽr-sɛba yʋ'ʋra n ka gʋlsɛ bɔna vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam halɩ dũnia pĩ'iluŋo wakatɛ la sãn yẽ dũŋa la, la wʋn di'ige ba mɛ, dũŋa la n daan vɔa, ka le wa bɔna, dee le wa bɔna la ĩyã.

ref: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
text: Dẽnɩ tɩ fʋ tara yɛm, la pʋtẽ'erɛ. Zuto bayopɔɩ la de la tãnsɩ bayopɔɩ tɩ pɔka la zo ba zuto.

ref: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
text: La ba ken dɛna la na'adõma bayopɔɩ. Banuu bayopɔɩ la pʋam na'am ba'asɛ mɛ, la ayɩla n boe lɛɛlɛ wã, dee tɩ ayẽma nãn ka wa'am. La a sãn wa wa'am, a wʋn bɔna la wakatɛ fẽe.

ref: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
text: La dũŋa la n daan vɔɩ dee ka le bɔna la, ẽŋa de la naba bʋnii dãana, la a ken dɛna la bayopɔɩ dõma la pʋam nẽra ayɩla. La a we'esɩ la sã'aŋɔ zẽ'a.

ref: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
text: Ɩɩla pia la tɩ fʋ yẽ la, de la na'adõma pia, la ba nãn ka to'oge na'am. La ba wʋn wa to'oge na'am lagɩm na dũŋa la di karf-ayɩla.

ref: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
text: Bãma pia la pʋtẽ'erɛ de la ayɩla, ba dɩkɛ ba pãŋa, la ba ke'eŋo la wuu bɔ la dũŋa la.

ref: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
text: Ba wʋn zɛbɛ la Pebila la mɛ, la Pebila la wʋn zɛbɛ yãŋɛ ba mɛ, ẽŋa n de zuudãandõma Zuudãana, la na'adõma Naba la ĩyã. Pebila la, la nẽr-sɛba tɩ a wi, looge ba tɩ ba dɛna sɩra dõma la, n wʋn zɛbɛ yãŋɛ ba.»

ref: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
text: Malɛka la le yele mam yetɩ: «Kʋlsɩ la tɩ fʋ yẽ, tɩ pɔg-yalsɩ dãana la zĩ ba nõorʋm na, de la nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ, tẽnsɩ wuu nẽrba, la so'olʋm to'oto'ore, la yetɔga buuri to'oto'ore nẽrba.

ref: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
text: Ɩɩla pia la tɩ fʋ yẽ la, la dũŋa la wʋn sise pɔg-yalsɩ dãana la mɛ, wʋn to'ose sɛla wuu tɩ a tarɩ la, dee basɛ ẽ beele. La ba wʋn obe a nẽnnɔ mɛ, dee yõ ẽ na bugum.

ref: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
text: Se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ n basɛ tɩ ba pʋtẽ'era tẽ'esra tɩ ba ẽŋɛ ẽŋa n boorɩ se'em. Tɩ ba wʋn lagɩm taaba la nõorɛ ayɩla dɩkɛ ba na'am ke'eŋo bɔ dũŋa la, halɩ ka paagɛ wakat-sɛka tɩ la wʋn ẽŋɛ wʋ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga n yele se'em na ba'asɛ.

ref: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
text: Pɔka la tɩ fʋ yẽ la de la tẽn-kãtɛ n soe dũnia na'adõma wuu.»

ref: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
text: Bɛla poorʋm, mam le yẽ la malɛka n ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sigra. A daan tarɩ la ke'eŋo zozo'e, tɩ a na'am pãŋa peelem nẽegɛ dũnia wuu.

ref: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
text: A tɔgɛ la ke'enke'em yetɩ: «A lui mɛ, a lui mɛ! Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la lui mɛ. A lebge la kʋlkã'arsɩ kẽ'a zẽ'a, a de la kʋlkã'ar-bɛ'ɛsɩ, la bõn-ẽgla n de bõn-dẽgrɔ tɩ nẽrba sisri sʋka zẽ'a.

ref: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
text: So'olʋm buuri wuu tõm na a yalsɩ tõoma wʋ dãam tɩ ba yũ la. Tɩ dũnia na'adõma lagɩm na ẽ tõm yalsɩ tõoma. Tɩ dũnia leebdõma doose a nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ n zo'oge la poorʋm yẽ bõntarsõm, bɛla tɩ a sã'am.»

ref: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa ayẽma n ze'ele saazuum yetɩ: «M nẽrba, yese-ya a pʋam fõrgɛ, tɩ ya da kãn lagɩm na ẽ tõm a tõon-be'ero la, tɩ ya da lagɩm na ẽ yẽ tooro sɛba tɩ a wʋn yẽ la.

ref: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
text: Se'ere n soe la, a tõon-be'ero la zo'oge mɛ halɩ paagɛ saazuum. Nawẽnnɛ tẽrɩ a tõon-be'ero la wuu yelle mɛ.

ref: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
text: Ẽŋɛ-ya ẽ wʋ ẽŋa n ẽŋɛ basɛba se'em na, yɔ-ya ẽ ẽn ẽŋɛ se'em na nõorɛ ayi. Pɩrɛ-ya a wannɛ na la dã-ke'eŋo nõorɛ ayi bɔ ẽ, wʋ ẽn botɩ nẽrba basɛba yũ se'em na.

ref: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
text: Nãmsɛ-ya ẽ tɩ a yẽ toogo la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, wʋ ẽŋa n nã'asrɩ a mẽŋa la a nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ se'em na: A yetɩ a mẽŋa tɩ: ‹Mam zĩ dɛna la pɔg-naba, mam dagɩ pɔkõorɛ, la mam kãn yẽ nãmsgɔ, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ.›

ref: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
text: Bɛla ĩyã tooro wʋn wa'am a zuo la daarɛ ayɩla pʋam: La de la bã'asɩ n kʋʋrɩ, la sũ-sã'aŋɔ, la kɔm, la bugum n wʋn di ẽ, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n di a sarɩya la de la ke'eŋo dãana.»

ref: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
text: Dũnia na'adõma n tõnnɩ yalsɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ la tẽn-ẽŋa lɛ, dee yẽta nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ a zẽ'am na, sãn wa yẽ bugum n laŋɛ tẽŋa la dita tɩ zũ'usi gulla, ba wʋn kɛlla mɛ fabla a yelle.

ref: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
text: Ban zotɩ dabeem la a nãmsgɔ la, ba wʋn zɛ la zãazãarɛ dee yɛta yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Tẽn-kãtɛ la, Babilɔnɩ tẽŋa n tarɩ pãŋa la, karf-ayɩla pʋam tɩ fõn yẽ sɩbgrɛ wãna!»

ref: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
text: Tɩ dũnia leebdõma mẽ wʋn kɛlla fabla a yelle, nẽra nẽra n ka le da'ara ba bõn-kɔɔsla la ĩyã.

ref: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
text: Ba bõn-kɔɔsla la de la sãlma, la zũuro, la kug-sõma buuri ligri n zo'oge, la tãn-sõma, la tãn-mõlsɩ n de gomusga, la tãn-saalsɩ n yẽgrɩ, la dɔɔrɔ buuri wuu n tarɩ yũuŋ-yẽlga, la lɔg-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ wɔbgɔ yẽna mãalɛ, la lɔg-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ dɔɔrɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ mãalɛ, la lɔg-sɛba tɩ ba dɩkɛ yõgõm, la kuto, la sãlm-kuga mãalɛ.

ref: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
text: La tɩɩsɩ pɔgrɔ n tarɩ yũuŋo, la bõnɔ n ãn wʋ kakadule, la tɩdarɛ kaam, la miiri, la tɩdarɛ tɩ ba yõ'orɩ, la dãam, la kaam, la zom, la bõnkɔɔla, la niigi, la piisi, la wiiri, la toto, la halɩ baa nẽrsaalba.

ref: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
text: Ba wʋn yele tẽŋa la yetɩ: «Bõn-sɛba wuu tɩ fʋ yɛm boorɩ la fõrgɛ dee basɛ fʋ mɛ. Fʋ bõntarsõm, la fʋ nẽnnõm lɔgrɔ la tole mɛ dee basɛ fɔ, tɩ nẽra nẽra kãn le malɩn yẽ ba.»

ref: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
text: Leebdõma n leebe lɔg-bãma yẽ bõntarsõm tẽn-ẽŋa pʋam na, wʋn zoe la dabeem la a nãmsgɔ la. Ba wʋn zɛ la zãazãarɛ kaasra dee fabla

ref: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
text: yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Tẽn-kãtɛ la n daan ãn wʋ pɔka n yɛɛrɩ fu-sõma, la fu-mõlsɩ n de gomusga, dee ɩta nẽnnõm la sãlma lɔgrɔ, la kug-sõma buuri ligri n zo'oge la.

ref: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
text: Karf-ayɩla pʋam tɩ a bõntarsõm na wuu darɛ sã'am.» Õornɔ kɛ'ɛndõma, la nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ õornɔ sẽnna sore, la sɛba n tõnnɩ õornɔ na pʋam, la nẽr-sɛba n kẽ'erɩ õornɔ na ɛɛra lɛɛbra la ze la zãazãarɛ,

ref: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
text: la ban yẽtɩ tɩ bugum diti tẽŋa la tɩ zũ'usi gulla la, ba kaasrɩ mɛ yɛta tɩ: «Tẽŋa nãn ka boe n de tẽn-kãtɛ wʋ ẽna.»

ref: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
text: La ba vaarɩ la tẽntõnnɔ pukra ba zuto, kaasra, dee fabla yetɩ: «Waɩ, waɩ! Tẽn-kãtɛ ẽna bõntarsõm ĩyã tɩ nẽr-sɛba n tarɩ õornɔ ɛɛra ko-kãtɛ la pʋam lɛɛbra la lebge bõntatba. La karf-ayɩla pʋam tɩ a bõntarsõm na wuu darɛ sã'am.»

ref: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
text: Tɩ malɛka n tarɩ pãŋa õbgɛ kug-kãtɛ n ãn wʋ nẽerɛ la lobge ko-kãtɛ la pʋam dee yetɩ: «Ba wʋn lobge Babilɔnɩ n de tẽn-kãtɛ la basɛ la bɛla la pãŋa, tɩ ba kãn le malɩn yẽ ẽ.

ref: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
text: Ba kãn le wõm kõl-wẽ'erba wẽ'a, la yõonyõonɩba yõoma, la wɩɩsɩ, la benkɔma peebgo, la ba kãn le yẽ nẽra n tarɩ nu'ugo tõonɛ a pʋam, la ba kãn wõm nẽerɛ dãmnɛ mĩ.

ref: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
text: Fɩtla bugum peelem kãn le nẽegɛ mĩ. Ba kãn le wõm pɔgdire kõa mĩ. Tẽŋa la leebdõma daan de la dũnia nẽrzuto. La tẽŋa la tɩɩm tõoma pã'asɛ so'olʋm buuri wuu nẽrba mɛ.»

ref: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
text: La de la Babilɔnɩ tẽŋa la pʋam tɩ ba yẽ Nawẽnnɛ nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba, la nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba daan kʋ dũnia wuu la zɩɩm.

ref: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
text: Bɛla poorʋm, mam wõm na kõa n tɔgrɩ ke'enke'em Nawẽnnɛ yire wʋ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ kõosɩ la yetɩ: «Aleluya! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ! Fãarɛ, la nã'asgɔ, la ke'eŋo de la tõma Wẽnnaam n soe.

ref: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
text: Ẽŋa sarɩyadia la masɛ mɛ, dɛna sɩra. Se'ere n soe la, a di pɔg-tutulga la n daan tõnnɩ yalsɩ tõoma zozo'e sã'ana dũnia la sarɩya mɛ sɩbgɛ ẽ, ẽn botɩ a tõntõnɩba ki la ĩyã.»

ref: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
text: Tɩ ba le yetɩ: «Aleluya! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ. Tẽn-kãtɛ la tɩ bugum laŋɛ dita la bug-zũ'usi zonnɩ na saazuum wakatɛ wuu.»

ref: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
text: Tɩ kõa ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am yetɩ: «Yãma n de Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba n zotɩ ẽ na, tarɩmdõma, la kɛ'ɛndõma, nã'asɛ-ya tɩ Nawẽnnɛ.»

ref: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
text: Tɩ mam le wõm kõa n ãn wʋ nẽr-kʋʋŋɔ zozo'e n tɔgrɩ, dee ãna wʋ ko-kãtɛ dãmnɛ, dee ãna wʋ saaga n tãsrɩ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Aleluya! Nã'asɛ-ya Nawẽnnɛ. Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo dãana la n soe na'am.

ref: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
text: Basɛ-ya tɩ tɩ ẽŋɛ sũ-yẽlga sɩrʋm nã'asɛ Nawẽnnɛ, se'ere n soe la, Pebila la pɔgdire wakatɛ paagɛ mɛ, tɩ pɔka la mẽ mãasʋm a mẽŋa ba'asɛ.

ref: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
text: Tɩ ba bɔ ẽ fu-sõŋɔ n yẽgrɩ ka tara dẽgrɔ, tɩ a yɛ.» Fu-sõŋɔ la de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba tõon-sõma.

ref: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
text: Tɩ malɛka la yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ yetɩ: Sɛba tɩ ba wi ba tɩ ba wa'am wa di Pebila la pɔgdire kibsa la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.» Dee le yetɩ: «Yetɔg-bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga sɩra sɩra.»

ref: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
text: Tɩ mam ka dũma tẽŋa a nẽŋam tɩ m nã'asɛ ẽ, tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ bɛla! Mam mẽ de la Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnna wʋ fõn, la fʋ sɔbiisi la n sakɛ sɩra tɩ Azezi pa'alɛ la. La de la Nawẽnnɛ n de fʋ nã'asɛ.» Sɩra la tɩ Azezi puke pa'alɛ la n bɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔ'ɔsra tɔgra. We-bã'ara n bã wemõ-pɛɛlga

ref: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
text: La mam yẽ la Nawẽnnɛ yire n yo'oge, tɩ wemõ-pɛɛlga bɔna mĩ, tɩ sɛka n bã ẽ na yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna Atẽntẽa, la Sɩra Dãana. A diti la sarɩya tɩ la masɛ, dee zɛbra zɛbrɛ tɩ la masɛ.

ref: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
text: A nini ãn wʋ bugwitum na, dee vuge mũnsi zozo'e a zuum. A tarɩ la yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ tɩ nẽra ka mina ẽ, sãn dagna a mẽŋa mã'a.

ref: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
text: La a yɛ la fu-woko n lʋsɛ zɩɩm pʋam. A yʋ'ʋrɛ de la Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga.

ref: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ yire zɛbzɛbrɩba yɛ fu-pɛɛlsɩ n ãn sõŋa ka tara dẽgrɔ, bã wemõ-pɛɛlsɩ dɔla ẽ.

ref: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
text: Tɩ sʋkɔbgɔ n saasɛ tara nõorɛ ze'ele a nõorʋm yɛsra. A wʋn dɩkɛ la sʋ'a la zɛbɛ yãŋɛ so'olʋm buuri wuu. A wʋn tara la kuto dasaarɛ sɔna ba. A wʋn mẽrɛ ba la viinye biɛ wẽkrɛ bõnɔ n pa'alɩ ke'eŋo dãana Nawẽnnɛ sũ-isgre la pʋam.

ref: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
text: Yʋ'ʋrɛ n gʋlsɛ a fuugo zuo, la a gere zuo yetɩ: «Na'adõma Naba, la zuudãandõma Zuudãana.»

ref: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
text: Tɩ mam yẽ malɛka n ze wẽntulle pʋam n tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yele bõn-ẽgla wuu n ẽgrɩ saazuum yetɩ: «Wa'am-ya wa lagsɛ Nawẽnnɛ di-kãtɛ la zẽ'am.

ref: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
text: Ya wʋn di la na'adõma nẽnnɔ, la kɛ'ɛndõma, la sʋdaasɩ nẽnnɔ, la wiiri, la wi-bã'arba nẽnnɔ, la nẽrba wuu, burkĩndõma, la yamsɩ, la kɛ'ɛndõma, la tarɩmdõma wuu nẽnnɔ.»

ref: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ dũŋa la, la dũnia na'adõma, la ba zɛbzɛbrɩba n lagsɛ taaba tɩ ba zɛbɛ la sɛka n bã wemõa, la a zɛbzɛbrɩba la.

ref: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
text: Tɩ ba yõkɛ dũŋa la, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la n daan ẽŋɛ bõn-yãlma dũŋa la nifum pã'asɛ sɛba n to'oge dũŋa la dãalgɔ, dee nã'asɛ a bõnɔ tɩ ba mãalɛ la. Ba lobe dũŋa, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la wuu la vɔɔsɩ basɛ bugum mõgrɛ n diti kidibre pʋam.

ref: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
text: Tɩ sʋ'a la n ze'ele we-bã'ara la nõorʋm yɛsra la, kʋ sɛba n weege la. Tɩ bõn-ẽgla wuu di ba nẽnnɔ na tɩgɛ.

ref: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
text: Tɩ mam yẽ na'am bĩmbĩna, tɩ ba bɔ nẽr-sɛba n zĩ ba zuto la ke'eŋo tɩ ba di sarɩya. Tɩ mam yẽ nẽr-sɛba tɩ ba daan wige ba zuto, ban bɔ Azezi sɩra, dee mõolɛ Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga la sɩɩsɩ. Nẽr-bãma daan ka nã'asɛ dũŋa la, la a bõnɔ na tɩ ba mãalɛ la, la ba ka to'oge a dãalgɔ ba dĩa zuto bɩɩ ba nu'usi zuto. Ba vo'oge mɛ lagɩm na Krisi di na'am yʋʋma tʋsrɛ la pʋam.

ref: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
text: Nẽr-sɛba n wʋn po vo'oge pɔspɔsɩ kũm vo'ore la, tarɩ zu-yẽlga, bãma de la Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba. Kũm bʋyi dãana la ka tã wʋn ẽŋɛ ba sɛla. Bãma n wʋn dɛna Nawẽnnɛ la Krisi kãabgɔ kẽma, la ba wʋn lagɩm na Krisi di na'am yʋʋma tʋsrɛ. Ba yãŋɛ Asũtãana

ref: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
text: Tɩ a yese pã'asɛ dũnia so'olʋm buuri wuu nẽrba, Gɔɔgɩ la Magɔɔgɩ. A wʋn lagsɛ ba mɛ tɩ ba zɛbɛ zɛbrɛ. Ba wʋn lagsɛ zo'oge wʋ tẽntõnbĩ'isgo n boe ko-kãtɛ nõorʋm se'em na.

ref: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
text: Ba wʋn yese mɛ dũnia wuu lagsɛ kaagɛ Nawẽnnɛ nẽrba n boe zẽ'e-sɛka, tẽn-sɛka tɩ Nawẽnnɛ nõŋɛ la. La bugum n wʋn ze'ele Nawẽnnɛ yire sige di ba.

ref: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
text: Tɩ ba wʋn yõkɛ Asũtãana n pã'asrɩ dũnia nẽrba la lobe basɛ bugum mõgrɛ n diti kidibre pʋam, zẽ'e-sɛka tɩ ba daan põn lobe dũŋa, la põmpɔrŋɔ nõtɔ'ɔsa la. Bilam ba wʋn nãmsra la wʋntɛɛŋa la yʋ'ʋŋɔ wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Nawẽnnɛ di ba'asgɔ sarɩya

ref: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
text: Tɩ mam le yẽ na'am bĩmbĩnne kãtɛ n de pɛɛlga, la sɛka n zĩ a zuo. Tɩ tẽŋa la saazuum zoe fõrgɛ a nẽŋam, tɩ ba ka le yẽ ba.

ref: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ nẽr-sɛba n ki la, kɛ'ɛndõma, la tarɩmdõma n ze na'am bĩmbĩnne na nẽŋam. Tɩ ba lagsɛ gõnnɔ. La ba lakɛ la gõŋɔ ayẽma n de vɔm gõŋɔ. Tɩ ba di sɛba n ki la sarɩya masɛ wʋ ba tõoma n de se'em, wʋ lan gʋlsɛ gõnnɔ na pʋam se'em na.

ref: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
text: Tɩ ko-kãtɛ basɛ sɛba n ki a pʋam na, tɩ kũm, la kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa mẽ basɛ nẽr-sɛba n ki bɔna ba zẽ'am na. La ba di ba wuu sarɩya masɛ wʋ ban tõm se'em na.

ref: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
text: Tɩ ba lobe kũm, la kɩ'ɩn-tẽŋa basɛ bugum mõgrɛ la pʋam. Bugum mõgrɛ la de la kũm bʋyi dãana.

ref: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
text: Sɛka yʋ'ʋrɛ n ka gʋlsɛ vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam, ba wʋn lobe ẽ basɛ la bugum mõgrɛ la pʋam.

ref: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
text: Tɩ mam yẽ saazuum paalga, la tẽn-paalga. Pɔspɔsɩ saazuum, la pɔspɔsɩ tẽŋa la bɔɩ mɛ, la ko-kãtɛ mẽ ka le bɔna.

ref: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
text: Tɩ mam wõm kõa n ze'ele na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am tɔgɛ ke'enke'em yetɩ: «Nawẽnnɛ kẽ'a zẽ'am boe la nẽrsaalba tẽŋasʋka. A wʋn kẽ'era la ba lɛ, tɩ ba dɛna a nẽrba. Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n wʋn bɔna la ba lɛ.

ref: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
text: A wʋn duuse ba nintãm wuu. La kũm kãn le bɔna, sũ-sã'aŋɔ la kaasga, bɩɩ toogo kãn le bɔna. Yel-kɛgsɩ la wuu tole mɛ.»

ref: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
text: Tɩ sɛka n zĩ na'am bĩmbĩnne zuo la yele yetɩ: «Bɩsɛ, mam ɩtɩ sɛla woo la paalsɩ.» Dee yele mam yetɩ: «Gʋlsɛ wãna, se'ere n soe la, mam yetɔga wã de la sɩra dee masɛ tɩ ba bɔ sɩra.»

ref: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
text: Dee le yele mam yetɩ: «La ẽŋɛ ba'asɛ mɛ. Mam de la Alfa, la Omega, sɛla woo pĩ'iluŋo, la ba'asgɔ. Koyũuro n tarɩ sɛka, mam wʋn bɔ ẽ ko'om n bulli n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm zãŋa tɩ a yũ.

ref: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Nẽr-sɛka n yãŋɛ, a wʋn to'oge la bõn-bãma mam zẽ'am. Mam wʋn dɛna a Wẽnnaam, tɩ a mẽ dɛna mam bia.

ref: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
text: La sãn dɛna dabeto, la sɛba n ka bɔ sɩra, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la nẽr-kʋʋrba, la yalsɩ dõma, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tɩɩm tõoma, la sɛba n nã'asrɩ bõndõma tɩ ba mãalɛ, la põmpɔrndõma la wuu, bãma zẽ'a de la bugum mõgrɛ n diti kidibre pʋam. Bɛla de la kũm bʋyi dãana.» Zerizalɛm tẽn-paalga

ref: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
text: Tɩ malɛkadõma bayopɔɩ la n daan zãlɩ laasɩ bayopɔɩ n pɩrɛ la tooro bayopɔɩ n de ba'asgɔ tooro la, ayɩla wa'am wa yele mam yetɩ: «Wa'am tɩ mam wʋn pa'alɛ fʋ pɔka la n yetɩ a ele la, Pebila la pɔga la.»

ref: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
text: Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa bɔna la mam, tɩ malɛka la tarɩ mam zom tãn-kãtɛ n wɔgɛ zuo. La a pa'alɛ mam na Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa n de Zerizalɛm n ze'ele saazuum Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am sigra,

ref: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
text: n yẽgrɩ la Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa. A sõŋa la yẽgrɩ wʋ kug-sõŋɔ n tarɩ yõorɔ, ãna wʋ kugre tɩ ba wi'iri tɩ zaspɩ, dee yẽgra wʋ bɩsga tɩ ba wi'iri kristalɩ.

ref: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Tẽŋa la tarɩ la lal-kãtɛ n wɔgɛ gilge ẽ, tara nõa pia la ayi. Tɩ malɛkadõma pia la ayi gu'ura nõa la. Ɩsrayɛlɩ buuri pia la ayi la yʋ'ʋra n gʋlsɛ nõa la zuto.

ref: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
text: Nõa batã n boe lalga la dapoore bɔba, tɩ batã bɔna zʋʋgɔ bɔba, tɩ batã bɔna gɔbga bɔba, tɩ batã bɔna zanõa bɔba.

ref: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
text: Tẽŋa la lalga ẽebɛ la kuga pia la ayi zuto. Tɩ Azezi n de Pebila la Tõntõnɩba pia la ayi la yʋ'ʋra gʋlsɛ kug-bãma zuto.

ref: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
text: Malɛk-sɛka n daan tɔgɛ la mam na, tarɩ la sãlma dɔɔgɔ tɩ ba tarɩ makra, tɩ a makɛ tẽŋa la, la a nõa, la a lalga la.

ref: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
text: Tẽŋa la de la tẽn-wẽkɔ tɩ a lalsɩ banaasɩ la wuu zõna taaba. Tɩ malɛka la dɩkɛ dɔɔgɔ la makɛ tẽŋa la. Tɩ a yɔlŋɔ dɛna sɩtaadɩ tʋspia la ayi. A woglʋm la a yɔlŋɔ, la a saazuum woko zõn na taaba.

ref: Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about
text: La a makɛ lalga la mɛ, tɩ a woglʋm dɛna kãntɩɩsɩ kɔbga la pinaasɩ la anaasɩ. A kãntɩɩsɩ la de la bʋyɩla la nẽrsaala kãntɩɩsɩ.

ref: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Ba dɩkɛ la kug-sõma yʋ'ʋrɛ n de zaspɩ mẽ lalga la. La tẽŋa la mẽŋa de la sãlma mẽŋa mẽŋa n yẽgrɩ ãna wʋ bɩsga la.

ref: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
text: Lalga la, ba dɩkɛ la kug-sõma buuri to'oto'ore n tarɩ yõorɔ ẽebɛ ẽ, tɩ a ãna sõŋa. Ẽbrɛ la pɔspɔsɩ kugre la yʋ'ʋrɛ de la zaspɩ, tɩ bʋyi dãana yʋ'ʋrɛ dɛna safɩɩrɩ, tɩ bʋtã dãana dɛna kalsedʋanɩ, tɩ bʋnaasɩ dãana dɛna emerɔdɩ.

ref: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
text: Tɩ bʋnuu dãana dɛna sadonigsi, tɩ bʋyoobɩ dãana dɛna sardʋanɩ, tɩ bʋyopɔɩ dãana dɛna krizoliti, tɩ bʋnii dãana dɛna berili, tɩ bʋwɛɩ dãana dɛna topaazɩ, tɩ pia dãana dɛna krizoprazɩ, tɩ pia la ayɩla dãana dɛna yasẽntɩ, tɩ pia la ayi dãana dɛna ametisti.

ref: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
text: Mam ka yẽ Wẽndeego tẽŋa la pʋam, se'ere n soe la, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n de ke'eŋo wuu dãana, la Pebila la n boe mĩ.

ref: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
text: Tẽŋa la ka boorɩ wẽntulle, bɩɩ wõrga tɩ ba nẽegɛ ẽ, se'ere n soe la, Nawẽnnɛ na'am pãŋa peelem n nẽerɩ ẽ, tɩ Pebila la dɛna a fɩtla.

ref: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
text: Dũnia so'olʋm wuu nẽrba wʋn sẽnna la a peelem na pʋam. La dũnia na'adõma wʋn tara ba bõntarsõm sẽŋɛ mĩ.

ref: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
text: Tẽŋa la nõa kãn malɩn yʋ, se'ere n soe la, yʋ'ʋŋɔ ka boe mĩ.

ref: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
text: Ba wʋn tarɩ dũnia so'olʋm wuu nã'asgɔ, la ba bõntarsõm sẽŋɛ mĩ.

ref: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
text: Sɛla n de bõn-dẽgrɔ wuu, bɩɩ nẽr-sɛka woo n tõnnɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la põmpɔrndõma kãn kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam. Nẽr-sɛba yʋ'ʋra n gʋlsɛ Pebila la vɔm gõŋɔ la pʋam mã'a n wʋn kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam.

ref: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
text: Tɩ malɛka la pa'alɛ mam ko'om n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm kʋlaaga. Kʋlaaga la yẽgrɩ wʋ bɩsga la. A ze'ele la Nawẽnnɛ, la Pebila na'am bĩmbĩnne na zẽ'am

ref: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
text: zɔta tẽŋa la pʋam pallɛ tẽŋasʋka. Tɩa n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm n boe kʋlaaga la sakʋgrɔ bayi la wɔnna biɛ nõorɛ pia la ayi yʋʋnɛ pʋam. A wɔnnɩ na nõorɛ ayɩla wõrga woo pʋam. Tɩa la võorɔ bɔ'ɔrɩ la dũnia so'olʋm wuu ĩmã'asʋm.

ref: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
text: Sɛla tɩ Nawẽnnɛ kã be'em bɔ ẽ kãn le bɔna tẽŋa la pʋam. Nawẽnnɛ, la Pebila la na'am bĩmbĩnne n wʋn bɔna mĩ. Tɩ Nawẽnnɛ tõntõnɩba wʋn nã'asra ẽ.

ref: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
text: La ba wʋn yẽ a nẽŋa, la a yʋ'ʋrɛ wʋn gʋlsɛ ba dĩa zuto.

ref: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
text: Yʋ'ʋŋɔ kãn le bɔna. Nẽrba kãn bɔɔra tɩ fɩtla, bɩɩ wẽntulle nẽegɛ peelem, Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ n wʋn nẽegɛ peelem bɔ ba. La ba wʋn di la na'am wakatɛ n ka ba'asrɩ. Azezi lemŋo

ref: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
text: Tɩ malɛka la yele mam yetɩ: «Yetɔg-bãna wã wuu de la sɩra, masɛ tɩ ba bɔ sɩra. La de la Zuudãana Nawẽnnɛ mẽŋa n botɩ a nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba tɔ'ɔsra a yetɔga tɔgra la, n tõm a malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa pa'alɛ a tõntõnɩba sɛla n kãn yuuge dee ẽŋɛ.»

ref: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
text: Azezi yetɩ: «Selse-ya! La kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am. Sɛka n sakrɩ gõŋɔ wã yetɔga tɩ ba to'osrɩ Nawẽnnɛ zẽ'am tɔgra la tarɩ zu-yẽlga.»

ref: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
text: La de la mam Azã n wõm dee yẽ bõn-bãma. La mam n wõm dee yẽ ba la, mam ka la dũma tẽŋa malɛka la n pa'alɛ mam bõn-bãma la nẽŋam tɩ m nã'asɛ ẽ.

ref: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
text: Tɩ a yele mam yetɩ: «Da ẽŋɛ bɛla, tɩ mam mẽ de la tõntõnna wʋ fõn, la fʋ sɔbiisi n de nõtɔ'ɔsrɩba, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n sakrɩ gõn-ẽna wã yetɔga la. Nawẽnnɛ n de fʋ nã'asɛ.»

ref: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
text: Sɛka n de tõon-be'ero dãana, a ken tõnna be'em. Sɛka n de tõon-dẽgrɔ dãana, a ken tõnna tõon-dẽgrɔ. La sɛka n ɩtɩ sɛla n masɛ, a ken ɩta sɛla n masɛ. Tɩ sɛka n de pʋpeelem dãana, ken sẽŋɛ nẽŋa la pʋpeelem.»

ref: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
text: Azezi yele yetɩ: «Selse-ya! La kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am. La mam tarɩ la yɔɔrɔ sẽna tɩ m yɔ nẽra woo masɛ wʋ ẽn tõm se'em.

ref: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
text: Mam de la Alfa, la Omega, pɔspɔsɩ dãana, la poorʋm dãana, sɛla woo pĩ'iluŋo, la ba'asgɔ.»

ref: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
text: Sɛba n peege ba futo tarɩ zu-yẽlga. Bãma n wʋn tara nõorɛ tɩ ba di vɔm tɩa la biɛ, la bãma n wʋn doose tẽŋa la nõa kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam.

ref: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
text: La nẽr-sɛba n tõnnɩ tõon-dẽgrɔ, la sɛba n tõnnɩ tɩɩm tõoma, la sɛba n tõnnɩ yalsɩ tõoma, la nẽr-kʋʋrba, la sɛba n nã'asrɩ bõndõma tɩ ba mãalɛ, la nẽr-sɛba wuu n nõŋɛ põmpɔrŋɔ dee parna la, wʋn weege la yẽŋa, ba kãn kẽ tẽŋa la pʋam.

ref: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
text: «Mam Azezi n tõm m malɛka tɩ a wa'am wa tɔgɛ bõn-bãma wuu pa'alɛ m nẽrba. Mam de la naba Adavidi yɩɩŋa, la bewã'arɛ n nẽerɩ.»

ref: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
text: Nawẽnnɛ Sɩa, la pɔka la n mãasʋm a mẽŋa tɩ a ele la yetɩ: «Wa'am!» Sɛka n wõm bɛla, a mẽ yetɩ: «Wa'am!» Koyũuro n tarɩ sɛka, a wa'am, sɛka n boorɩ ko'om n bɔ'ɔrɩ vɔm, a wa'am wa to'oge zãŋa. Ba'asgɔ yetɔga

ref: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
text: Nẽr-sɛka woo n wõm Nawẽnnɛ yetɔga wã n boe gõŋɔ wã pʋam, mam Azã kã'anɩ ẽ yetɩ, nẽr-sɛka sãn pa'asɛ sɛla yetɔga wã pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn botɩ a yẽ tooro sɛba n gʋlsɛ gõŋɔ wã pʋam na.

ref: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
text: La nẽr-sɛka sãn yese sɛla gõŋɔ wã yetɔga pʋam, Nawẽnnɛ wʋn yese a pʋrɛ tɩ a kãn po di vɔm tɩa la biɛ, la a kãn kẽ Nawẽnnɛ tẽŋa la pʋam wʋ lan pa'alɛ se'em gõŋɔ wã pʋam na.

ref: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
text: Sɛka n tɔgɛ yetɔg-bãna bɔ la yetɩ la de la sɩra, a yetɩ: «Asɩra, la kãn yuuge dee tɩ mam wa'am.» Amina! La dɛna bɛla. Zuudãana Azezi, wa'am.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
text: Tɩ Zuudãana Azezi yel-sõnnɛ bɔna la ya wuu.
